eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowds, he went up a mountain. He sat down and his disciples came to him.
local: Yesu na wa nyɛ a nɩyɔɔ, wʊ don tɔ̃ɔ ju, wʊn wa tɩ jɛ̃ baar ka a wʊ poturbo wa a wʊ sɛ̃.

eng: Then the Spirit led Jesus up into the wilderness so that the devil might tempt him.
local: A Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ tara na Yesu kpɛ̃n a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ, ka a tɩɩ tɩ bɛɛr wʊ kaa.

eng: He taught them, saying.
local: A wʊ piel wile ba 'yɛrɛ.

eng: After Jesus had fasted for forty days and forty nights, he was starving.
local: Waa di bon jaa ber lɩjaayi nɩ tãsɔɔ gbulo lɩjaayi ɛ, ka kɔ̃ kpɛ wʊ.

eng: Happy are people who are hopeless, because the kingdom of heaven is theirs.
local: Pʊpɛl lɛ kʊ a yɩn banyãna na ba tara bon jaa ɛ, tɩ bʊɔrɔ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na bʊɔrɔ. Yɩn lɛ so a saaju nãalʊ̃ʊ.

eng: The tempter came to him and said, Since you are Gods Son, command these stones to become bread.
local: A 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Ala ɩ fʊ̃ʊ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Bie, 'yɛr kʊ a kʊsɩbɛ a nyãna ka a lɩɛb bʊ̃diir.

eng: Happy are people who grieve, because they will be made glad.
local: Pʊpɛl lɛ kʊ a yɩn na kõnõ, Nãaŋmɩn na 'maan a yɩ nyãa.

eng: Jesus replied, Its written, People wont live only by bread, but by every word spoken by God .
local: Tɩchɛ ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, A sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, bʊ̃diir tɛɛ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ kʊ nɩrɛ nyɛvʊr ɛ, tɩchɛ 'yɛrbir jaa na yi Nãaŋmɩn nɛɛ pɔ.

eng: Happy are people who are humble, because they will inherit the earth.
local: Pʊpɛl lɛ kʊ a yɩn na siir a yɩ mãɛ. Nãaŋmɩn na kʊ ban bon jaa na be a wɛr pɔ.

eng: The Pharisees and Sadducees came to Jesus. In order to test him they asked him to show them a sign from heaven.
local: A Faraseemɩne nɩ a Saduseemɩne wan ɩka ba bɛɛr Yesu kaa na ba 'yɛrɛ, Wiil sɩ nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ na ɩ jãnɛ na yi a saaju.

eng: After that the devil brought him into the holy city and stood him at the highest point of the temple. He said to him.
local: A bɛɛr kaa sʊɔ lɛ tara wʊn don a Jerusalɛm, tɩ wiil wʊ a puorfʊ jie na do gar a jaa.

eng: Happy are people who are hungry and thirsty for righteousness, because they will be fed until they are full.
local: Pʊpɛl lɛ kʊ banyã kɔ̃ nɩ kɔ̃nyũur na kpɛ yeltorsɩ jũu, ban tɩɩ naa.

eng: But he replied, At evening you say, It will be nice weather because the sky is bright red.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Ala wa ɩ jãanʊɔr lɛ, yɩ ma 'yɛr ɩka a wɛr chaar naa bojũu a saaju mʊ̃ɔ naa.

eng: Since you are Gods Son, throw yourself down; for it is written, I will command my angels concerning you, and they will take you up in their hands so that you wont hit your foot on a stone. .
local: tɩ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Ala ɩ fʊ̃ʊ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Bie, ɔɔ lo a tẽe. A sɛb ɩka, wʊn vɛ̃n ka a wʊ malakasɩ gu fʊ na ba nyɔɔ fʊ nɩ a ba nuur ka fu gbɛr kʊ ŋme kʊsɩr ɛ.

eng: Happy are people who show mercy, because they will receive mercy.
local: Pʊpɛl lɛ kʊ yɩn banyã na kaara nɩrɛ nɩbaal, Nãaŋmɩn mɩ na kãan a yɩ nɩbaal.

eng: And in the morning you say, There will be bad weather today because the sky is cloudy. You know how to make sense of the skys appearance. But you are unable to recognize the signs that point to what the time is.
local: Ala wa ɩ bibio lɛ, yɩ ma 'yɛr ɩka saa na wan a dɩna, a julõjuur sɔb naa ka saa koro. Yɩ ma kãan a saaju tɩ 'yɛr ɩka saa na wan bɩɩ wʊ kʊ̃ wa ɛ, yɩ bɔ̃ɔn a tẽe nɩ a saaju 'yɛr ma 'yɛr, ŋmɩnɛ a yɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ bɔ̃ɔ a jãnsɩ a dɩna ɛ?.

eng: Jesus replied, Again its written, Dont test the Lord your God .
local: Ka a Yesu mɩ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, A mɩ sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, ta bɛɛr a yɩ Nãaŋmɩn kaa ɛ.

eng: Happy are people who have pure hearts, because they will see God.
local: Pʊpɛl lɛ kʊ banyã na nʊ̃ Nãaŋmɩn sɩrɛ, nɩ a ba nyãa wʊ jaa, bala lɛ na nyɛ Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: An evil and unfaithful generation searches for a sign. But it wont receive any sign except Jonahs sign. Then he left them and went away.
local: Yɩn yelbe-ɩrbɛ nɩ sɛ̃sɛ̃nbɛ lɛ bʊɔrɔ ɩka n tʊ̃ nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ ka yɩ kaa, yɩ kʊ̃ nyɛ nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ ka a Jona nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ ba lɛ ɛ. Be lɛ wʊ bar ba tɩ chen.

eng: Then the devil brought him to a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their glory.
local: A tɩɩ lɛ tara wʊn don tɔ̃ɔ ju tɩ wiil wʊ a nãa di jiir a jaa na be ka a wɛr pɔ, na a bʊ̃vɩɛlsɩ lɛ an be.

eng: Happy are people who make peace, because they will be called Gods children.
local: Pʊpɛl lɛ kʊ banyã na bʊɔrɔ nyã'maar, ban bʊɔlɔ ban Nãaŋmɩn bibiir.

eng: When the disciples arrived on the other side of the lake, they had forgotten to bring bread.
local: A poturbo ĩin ba tʊ̃ɔ de paanʊ̃ɔ ɛ, tɩ na ba gɔ̃ɔ a man gɔ̃ɔn.

eng: He said, Ill give you all these if you bow down and worship me.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Ɩn kʊ fʊ naa anyãna a jaa, ala ɩ fʊ gbur dumo puor ma.

eng: Happy are people whose lives are harassed because they are righteous, because the kingdom of heaven is theirs.
local: Pʊpɛl lɛ kʊ yɩn bala nɩbɛ na ge yeltorsɩ jũu, yɩn lɛ so a saaju nãalʊ̃ʊ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Watch out and be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
local: Ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ gu yɩ mãɛ a Faraseemɩne nɩ a Saduseemɩne dãbɩl pɔ.

eng: Jesus responded, Go away, Satan, because its written, You will worship the Lord your God and serve only him .
local: Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Fʊ̃ʊ Satan nyã tɔɔ yi be. A sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, a sɩ Soro Nãaŋmɩn tɛɛ lɛ a sɛɛ ɩka yɩ puoro na yɩ tʊ̃nɔ̃ kʊrɔ a 'lɔ tɛɛ.

eng: Happy are you when people insult you and harass you and speak all kinds of bad and false things about you, all because of me.
local: Pʊpɛl lɛ kʊ yɩn a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ nɩbɛ na tʊrɔ yɩ, na ba gere yɩ, tɩ ŋmara jirsɩ 'yɛrɛ 'yɛr dɛbɛ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ yɩ, a maa jũu.

eng: They discussed this among themselves and said, We didnt bring any bread.
local: Ba piel mana kʊrɔ taa, Sɩn ba waan paanʊ̃ɔ jaa lɛ so.

eng: Be full of joy and be glad, because you have a great reward in heaven. In the same way, people harassed the prophets who came before you.
local: Yɩ tara pʊpɛl, a yɩ sãyar bɛrma naa a saaju, lɛ nɩ wʊ ju lɛ ba dɔ̃ɔ ge a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ na dɔ̃ɔ be be.

eng: The devil left him, and angels came and took care of him.
local: A tɩɩ bar wʊn tɩ chen, tɩchɛ ka a Nãaŋmɩn malakasɩ wa sʊ̃ʊ wʊ.

eng: Jesus knew what they were discussing and said, You people of weak faith! Why are you discussing among yourselves the fact that you dont have any bread?.
local: Yesu na wa bɔ̃ɔn wʊ 'yɛr naa, Yɩn sɔɔfʊ na ba jã bon ɛ, ŋmɩnɛ a yɩ 'yɛrɛ ɩka yaa tara paanʊ̃ɔ ɛ?.

eng: You are the salt of the earth. But if salt loses its saltiness, how will it become salty again? Its good for nothing except to be thrown away and trampled under peoples feet.
local: Yɩ kaara fɩka nyaar a wɛr pɔ, tɩchɛ a nyaar nʊ̃ɔ wa baar, ŋmɩnɛ ban lɛ ɩ wʊ ka wʊ lɛ nʊ̃mɔ̃ go? A maa lɛ tara tɔ̃nɔ̃ kʊ bon jaa ɛ, ba ma kpaar bar ka nɩbɛ nɛɛrɛ nɩ gbɛɛ.

eng: Now when Jesus heard that John was arrested, he went to Galilee.
local: Yesu na wa wõ ɩka ba nyɔɔn Jɔɔn na ba pɔɔ, wʊ chen Galilee.

eng: Dont you understand yet? Dont you remember the five loaves that fed the five thousand and how many baskets of leftovers you gathered?.
local: Yaa nyɛ bɔ̃ɔ a par sɛrɛ bɩɩ? Yaa tɩɛrtɔ a paanʊ̃ɔsɩ anũu a nɩbɛ tur anũu na di faa tɩ yɩ wob a bʊ̃ŋmaar 'yɔ̃ɔ a pɛr pie nɩ ayi bɩɩ? Yaa tɩɛrtɔ a paanʊ̃ɔsɩ anũu a nɩbɛ tur anũu na di faa tɩ yɩ wob a bʊ̃ŋmaar 'yɔ̃ɔ a pɛr pie nɩ ayi bɩɩ?.

eng: You are the light of the world. A city on top of a hill cant be hidden.
local: Yɩ kaara fɩka chãa a wɛr pɔ. Yir ban mɛ tɔ̃ɔ ju kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ sɔɔl ɛ.

eng: He left Nazareth and settled in Capernaum, which lies alongside the sea in the area of Zebulun and Naphtali.
local: Wʊ yin Najaretɩ na wʊ tɩ kpɩɛrɛ be a Kapenahum a man nɛɛ be Jebulun nɩ Naftalɩ wɛr pɔ.

eng: And the seven loaves that fed the four thousand and how many large baskets of leftovers you gathered?.
local: Bɩɩ a paanʊ̃ɔsɩ ayopõi a nɩbɛ tur anaar na di faa tɩ yɩ wob 'yɔ̃ɔ pɛr ayopõi bɩɩ?.

eng: Neither do people light a lamp and put it under a basket. Instead, they put it on top of a lampstand, and it shines on all who are in the house.
local: Nɩrɛ maa chʊm fãtɩn de pɩɛ pɔɔ ɛ tɩchɛ wʊ ma bin a kuorgbe ju kʊ chaar yɔ̃ɔ nɩrɛ jaa a yir pɔ.

eng: This fulfilled what Isaiah the prophet said.
local: Ka a lɛ jaa a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ Isaiya na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr ɩ yelmãɛ.

eng: Dont you know that I wasnt talking about bread? But be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
local: Bo lɛ so ka yaa tɩɛr bɔ̃ɔ ɩka a ba ɩ paanʊ̃ɔ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ ɛ? Yɩ gu yɩ mãɛ a Faraseemɩne nɩ a Saduseemɩne dãbɩl yele pɔ.

eng: In the same way, let your light shine before people, so they can see the good things you do and praise your Father who is in heaven.
local: Lɛ nɩ wʊ ju mɩ lɛ, yɩn lɛ ɩ chãa a yɩ taaba sɛ̃, ka ba na nyɛ a yɩ tʊ̃vɩɛlsɩ na ba pɛɛ a yɩ Sãa Nãaŋmɩn na be a saaju.

eng: Land of Zebulun and land of Naphtali, alongside the sea, across the Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles.
local: Jebulun wɛr, Naftalɩ wɛr, a man sɔr a Jordan man gɔ̃ɔn. Galilee be a nɩyor na kpɩɛrɛ.

eng: Then they understood that he wasnt telling them to be on their guard for yeast used in making bread. No, he was telling them to watch out for the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
local: Be lɛ ba pãa tɩɛr bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a ba ɩ a paanʊ̃ɔ dãbɩl lɛ ɛ tɩchɛ ba bɔ̃ɔn a Faraseemɩne nɩ a Saduseemɩne wiilfʊ.

eng: Dont even begin to think that I have come to do away with the Law and the Prophets. I havent come to do away with them but to fulfill them.
local: Yɩ ta tɩɛr ɩka n wan ɩka n wa sɔ̃ɔ a nɛbinãa bɩɩ, a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ wiilfʊ bar ɛ, n ba wa ɩka n wa sɔ̃ɔ yaa bar ɛ, n wan ɩka n vɛ̃ ka a ɩ yelmãɛ.

eng: Now when Jesus came to the area of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, Who do people say the Human One is.
local: A Yesu na wa kpɛ a Sisariya Filipai paal pɔ, wʊ sʊʊr a wʊ poturbo, An lɛ a nɩbɛ 'yɛr ɩka a maa Nɩsaal Bie ɩ naa.

eng: the people who lived in the dark have seen a great light, and a light has come upon those who lived in the region and in shadow of death.
local: A nɩbɛ na dɔ̃ɔ be libe pɔ nyɛn chãa, nɩbɛ bala na ba dɔ̃ɔ tara nyɛvʊr Nãaŋmɩn pɔ ɛ, nyɛn Nãaŋmɩn chãa kpɛ̃ɛ.

eng: I say to you very seriously that as long as heaven and earth exist, neither the smallest letter nor even the smallest stroke of a pen will be erased from the Law until everything there becomes a reality.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, ɩka a saaju nɩ a wɛr ba wa baar ɛ, a sɛbir bile bʊ̃'yen gba kʊ̃ ir a nɛbinãa pɔ ɛ, tɩ ta a lɛ yele jaa na ɩ yelmãɛ.

eng: They replied, Some say John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others Jeremiah or one of the other prophets.
local: Ka ba 'yɛr, Bamɩne 'yɛr ɩka Jɔɔn Kɔ̃suoro, ka bamɩne mɩ 'yɛr ɩka Elaija lɛ, ka bamɩne mɩ 'yɛr ɩka Jeremiya bɩɩ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ ãsʊɔ lɛ.

eng: From that time Jesus began to announce, Change your hearts and lives! Here comes the kingdom of heaven.
local: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ Yesu piel na wʊ mʊɔlɔ 'yɛrɛ, Yɩ lɩɛb a yɩ befʊ a saaju nãalʊ̃ʊ ta na.

eng: Therefore, whoever ignores one of the least of these commands and teaches others to do the same will be called the lowest in the kingdom of heaven. But whoever keeps these commands and teaches people to keep them will be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
local: A lɛ jũu, nɩrɛ jaa wa sɔ̃ɔ a nɛbinsɩ anyãna ãsʊɔ jaa tɩ wiil a wʊ taaba ka ba mɩ maala a lɛ, na ɩn a bile a saaju nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ, tɩchɛ nyã jaa na maala tɩ wile bamɩne ka ba mɩ maala a lɛ, na ɩn kpɛ̃ɛ a saaju nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.

eng: He said, And what about you? Who do you say that I am.
local: Tɩchɛ a wʊ sʊʊr ba, An lɛ yɩn 'yɛr ɩka n ɩ naa.

eng: As Jesus walked alongside the Galilee Sea, he saw two brothers, Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew, throwing fishing nets into the sea, because they were fishermen.
local: Yesu na wa de a Galilee man gogoro lana, wʊ nyɛn yɛɛr ayi Simon ban bʊɔlɔ Pita nɩ a wʊ yɛbɛ Andiru na lɔɔrɔ lɔyaar 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a kʊ̃ɔ pɔ. Ba dɔ̃ɔ ɩ jãnyɔɔrbɔ.

eng: I say to you that unless your righteousness is greater than the righteousness of the legal experts and the Pharisees, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: N 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ, a yɩ yeltorsɩ ba wa tɩ gar a nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a Faraseemɩne ɛ, yɩn ba be na wa kpɛ a saaju nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ ɛ.

eng: Simon Peter said, You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.
local: Ka Simon Pita 'yɛr, Fʊ̃ʊ lɛ a Nɩ-iraa, a Nãaŋmɩn Vʊr Bie.

eng: Come, follow me, he said, and Ill show you how to fish for people.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ turo ma, n vɛ̃ naa ka yɩ nyɔɔrɔ nɩbɛ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.

eng: You have heard that it was said to those who lived long ago, Dont commit murder, and all who commit murder will be in danger of judgment.
local: Yɩ wõn ban dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr kʊ a sɩ sãakpãmɩne, Ta kʊ nɩrɛ ɛ, nɩrɛ nyã na kʊ nɩrɛ na nyɛn 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ.

eng: Then Jesus replied, Happy are you, Simon son of Jonah, because no human has shown this to you. Rather my Father who is in heaven has shown you.
local: Ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Fʊ ju nʊ̃mɔ̃ na Simon, Jona bie! A ba ɩ nɩsaal nɩ nɩrɛ lɛ ir wiil fʊ ɛ, tɩchɛ a n Sãa na be a saaju lɛ.

eng: Right away, they left their nets and followed him.
local: Ka ba tɔ̃ɔ tãɛ a lɔyaarsɩ bar tɩ na ba turo wʊ.

eng: But I say to you that everyone who is angry with their brother or sister will be in danger of judgment. If they say to their brother or sister, You idiot, they will be in danger of being condemned by the governing council. And if they say, You fool, they will be in danger of fiery hell.
local: Tɩchɛ n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ, ala ɩ fʊ nyɛn na yɛbɛ suur, wʊn nyɛn 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ, ala ɩ fʊ tʊ̃n yɛbɛ na nyɛn 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ a nɩbɛrɛ niem, fʊ̃ʊ wa 'yɛr nɩrɛ ɩka, jɔl nyã, na kpɛ̃n a bũu dagel 'lɔ pɔ.

eng: I tell you that you are Peter. And Ill build my church on this rock. The gates of the underworld wont be able to stand against it.
local: Maa lɛ 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ fʊ, a fʊ̃ʊ Pita, a par lɛ kʊsɩr pie, a fʊ 'yɛr nyãna pɔ lɛ, ɩn tu bʊɔl nɩbɛ a ba wa sɔɔ de ma ka a kpĩi tẽe kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ ba ɛ.

eng: Continuing on, he saw another set of brothers, James the son of Zebedee and his brother John. They were in a boat with Zebedee their father repairing their nets. Jesus called them and.
local: Wʊn lɛ yɩ a be chiine, wʊ lɛ nyɛn yɛɛr ayi, Jemesɩ na ɩ Jebedee bidaba naa a wʊ yɛbɛ Jɔɔn ka ba be a gboro pɔ nɩ a ba sãa Jebedee, maala a ba lɔyaarsɩ ka wʊ bʊɔl ba.

eng: Therefore, if you bring your gift to the altar and there remember that your brother or sister has something against you.
local: Ala wa ɩ fʊ waara nɩ naa a fʊ kʊfʊ, a Nãaŋmɩn bɔɔr lofʊ jie na fʊ wa tɩɛr tɔ ɩka fʊ̃ʊ nɩ a yɛbɛ tara na 'yɛr.

eng: Ill give you the keys of the kingdom of heaven. Anything you fasten on earth will be fastened in heaven. Anything you loosen on earth will be loosened in heaven.
local: Ɩn kʊ fʊn a saaju nãalʊ̃ʊ pãn bie, bon 'lɔ jaa fʊn lũ ka a wɛr pɔ, a mɩn lũn a be a saaju, bon 'lɔ jaa fʊn for bar ka a wɛr pɔ, a mɩn for bar a be a saaju.

eng: immediately they left the boat and their father and followed him.
local: A ba tɔ̃ɔ tɩɛ a gboro bar nɩ a ba sãa tɩ turo wʊ.

eng: leave your gift at the altar and go. First make things right with your brother or sister and then come back and offer your gift.
local: bar a fʊ bon a Nãaŋmɩn bɔɔr lofʊ jie tɩ lɩɛb chen tɩ 'maal a fʊ yɛbɛ tɩ pãa wa de a fʊ bon kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: Then he ordered the disciples not to tell anybody that he was the Christ.
local: Ka wʊ pãa kpãkpãan a wʊ poturbo ɩka ba taa 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ nɩrɛ jaa ɩka 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a Nɩ-iraa ɛ.

eng: Jesus traveled throughout Galilee, teaching in their synagogues. He announced the good news of the kingdom and healed every disease and sickness among the people.
local: Yesu yɔn a Galilee wile ba a lɔ̃ɔfʊ jiir tɩ mʊɔlɔ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ tɩ sãana nɩrɛ jaa baal.

eng: Be sure to make friends quickly with your opponents while you are with them on the way to court. Otherwise, they will haul you before the judge, the judge will turn you over to the officer of the court, and you will be thrown into prison.
local: An ɩ yɩn nɩ a yɩ sãn sʊɔ lɛ chiine a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ jie, yɩ mʊlɔ wʊ a sɔr pɔ, ka lɛ ba lɛ ɛ, wʊn de yɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ a 'yɛr tɔɔrɔ nũu pɔ, ka wʊ de yɩ kʊ a nɩpɔɔraa ka wʊ mɩ pɔɔ yɩ bar.

eng: From that time Jesus began to show his disciples that he had to go to Jerusalem and suffer many things from the elders, chief priests, and legal experts, and that he had to be killed and raised on the third day.
local: An piel a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ a Yesu piel 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ ba ɩka wʊn chen Jerusalɛm tɩ di dɔɔyɛ a nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a nɛɛ wiwiilbe nuur pɔ, ka ba kʊ wʊ, ka a ber ata daar a wʊ lɛ ir ɩ vʊr.

eng: News about him spread throughout Syria. People brought to him all those who had various kinds of diseases, those in pain, those possessed by demons, those with epilepsy, and those who were paralyzed, and he healed them.
local: Ka a wʊ yele yaar a Siriya ka ba tara a baalsɩ ba jaa waan a wʊ sɛ̃, bala a baalsɩ nɩ ãgãn ɔbɔ na nyɔɔ, bala na tara a sɩdɛbɛ, banyã baal tãanaa na nyɔɔ nɩ gbɛrfʊ jaa bʊrɔ, ka wʊ sãa a ba baalsɩ.

eng: I say to you in all seriousness that you wont get out of there until youve paid the very last penny.
local: Ala be a lɛ, faa be na wa yi ka fʊ̃ʊ ba wa yab a sãn baar ɛ.

eng: Then Peter took hold of Jesus and, scolding him, began to correct him: God forbid, Lord! This wont happen to you.
local: Ka Pita tɔɔ wʊ yin chɛ̃chɛ̃ɛ na wʊ tɩ piel 'yɛrɛ, N Soro, Nãaŋmɩn kʊ sɔɔ ka a yele nyã ɩ fʊ ɛ.

eng: Large crowds followed him from Galilee, the Decapolis, Jerusalem, Judea, and from the areas beyond the Jordan River.
local: Nɩyɔɔ yin Galilee, nɩ Dekapolisɩ, nɩ Jerusalɛm, nɩ Judiya nɩ a Jordan man gɔ̃ɔn wa turo wʊ.

eng: You have heard that it was said, Dont commit adultery.
local: Yɩ dɔ̃ɔ wõ ban 'yɛr ɩka ta gãn nɩrɛ pɔɔ bɩɩ sɩrɛ ɛ.

eng: But he turned to Peter and said, Get behind me, Satan. You are a stone that could make me stumble, for you are not thinking Gods thoughts but human thoughts.
local: Tɩchɛ a Yesu lɩɛb 'yɛr kʊ a Pita, Tɔɔ lan ka tɩ chɛ ma. Fʊ ɩn Satan, na fʊ ɩ gbɛr ŋme bon kʊ ma. Faa tara Nãaŋmɩn bonsɩ tɩɛr ɛ, tɩchɛ nɩsaal bonsɩ tɩɛr lɛ fʊ tara.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, All who want to come after me must sayno to themselves, take up their cross, and follow me.
local: Ka a Yesu pãa 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ poturbo, Ala ɩ ãsʊɔ jaa bʊɔrɔ na ɩka wʊ ɩ a n poturo, wʊ 'laar a wʊ mãɛ tɩ 'mɔɔ a wʊ daa tuo turo nɩ ma.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me will find them.
local: A nɩrɛ 'lɔ na bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ faa a wʊ nyɛvʊr na bɔr wʊ naa, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ mɩ na bɔr a wʊ nyɛvʊr a maa jũu, na nyɛ wʊ naa.

eng: Why would people gain the whole world but lose their lives? What will people give in exchange for their lives?.
local: Ala ɩ nɩrɛ tara naa a wɛr ju bonsɩ a jaa, tɩ bɔr a wʊ nyɛvʊr, bo tʊ̃nɔ̃ lɛ wʊn nyɛ? Bɩɩ bo lɛ wʊn de tɛɛn a wʊ nyɛvʊr?.

eng: For the Human One is about to come with the majesty of his Father with his angels. And then he will repay each one for what that person has done.
local: A maa Nɩsaal Bie na waan naa Nãaŋmɩn tɩɩr nɩ a wʊ malakasɩ wa yab nɩrɛ jaa bon 'lɔ wʊn maal.

eng: I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see the Human One coming in his kingdom.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka a yɩ bamɩne na ara a ka kʊ̃ kpi ɛ, tɩ na yɩ nyɛ a maa Nɩsaal Bie na waara a n nãa difʊ jie.

eng: But I say to you that every man who looks at a woman lustfully has already committed adultery in his heart.
local: Tɩchɛ n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ, nɩrɛ nyã jaa na wa kaa pɔɔ jaa tãɩɩ tɩ bʊɔrɔ na gãn wʊ, a nɩrɛ 'lɔ gãn wʊ naa a wʊ tɩɛr pɔ.

eng: And if your right eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out and throw it away. Its better that you lose a part of your body than that your whole body be thrown into hell.
local: Ala ɩ a fʊ nũsʊ̃ɔ nɩbir vɛ̃ naa ka fʊ ɩ yelbier, 'loo wʊ bar, a fʊ ãgãn lõboor ãsʊɔ na ba be be ɛ, sa naa a fʊ ãgãn wʊ jaa na kpɛ bũu pɔ.

eng: And if your right hand causes you to fall into sin, chop it off and throw it away. Its better that you lose a part of your body than that your whole body go into hell.
local: Ala ɩ a fʊ nũsʊ̃ɔ nũu mɩ lɛ vɛ̃ ka fʊ ɩ yelbier ɛ, ŋmaa wʊ 'lɔ bar, a fʊ ãgãn lõboor ãsʊɔ na ba be be ɛ, sa naa a fʊ ãgãn wʊ jaa na kpɛ bũu pɔ.

eng: It was said, Whoever divorces his wife must give her a divorce certificate.
local: A mɩ dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr ɩka, nɩrɛ nyã jaa na tɔr a wʊ pɔɔ bar, wʊ kʊ wʊ a pɔɔ bar gãn.

eng: But I say to you that whoever divorces his wife except for sexual unfaithfulness forces her to commit adultery. And whoever marries a divorced woman commits adultery.
local: Tɩchɛ n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, nɩrɛ nyã jaa na tɔr a wʊ pɔɔ bar, ala baa ɩ wʊ sɛ̃nɛ naa ɛ, vɛ̃n kʊ ɩ sɛ̃sɛ̃nɛ, nɩrɛ nyã jaa na kul a pɔɔ 'lɔ mɩ ɩn sɛ̃sɛ̃nɛ.

eng: Again you have heard that it was said to those who lived long ago: Dont make a false solemn pledge, but you should follow through on what you have pledged to the Lord.
local: Yɩ mɩ lɛ wõn ban dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr kʊ a sɩ sãakpãmɩne, Yɩ taa sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a nɛɛ 'lɔ yɩn põl 'yɔ̃ɔ a yɩ mãɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ yɩ tara a lɛ yɩn põl 'yɔ̃ɔ a sɩ Soro.

eng: But I say to you that you must not pledge at all. You must not pledge by heaven, because its Gods throne.
local: Tɩchɛ n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ, yɩ ta pɔ jaa ɛ, yɩ ta pɔn a saaju gba ɛ, bojũu Nãaŋmɩn jaa jie lɛ.

eng: You must not pledge by the earth, because its Gods footstool. You must not pledge by Jerusalem, because its the city of the great king.
local: bɩɩ a wɛr ju, a wʊ gbɛr dɔɔlfʊ jie lɛ bɩɩ Jerusalɛm ɛ, bojũu be lɛ a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ kpɩɛrɛ.

eng: And you must not pledge by your head, because you cant turn one hair white or black.
local: Ta pɔn a fʊ ju ɛ, fʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ vɛ̃ ka a fʊ jukɔɔl ãsʊɔ pɛl bɩɩ sɔb ɛ.

eng: At that time Herod the ruler heard the news about Jesus.
local: A ber ala pɔ, a Hɛrɔd na ɩ a Galilee nãa wõn a Yesu yele.

eng: Let your yes mean yes, and your no mean no. Anything more than this comes from the evil one.
local: Yɩ vɛ̃ ka a yɩ ɔ̃ɔ ɩ ɔ̃ɔ ka a yɩ kai ɩ kai, bon 'lɔ na gar a nɩtɔ̃ yin a dɛɛr sʊɔ sɛ̃.

eng: He said to his servants, This is John the Baptist. Hes been raised from the dead. This is why these miraculous powers are at work through him.
local: na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ, Jɔɔn Kɔ̃suor lɛ tɔ̃ɔ ir ɩ vʊr, ala lɛ so ka wʊ tara a kpɛ̃ɔ tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ anyãna.

eng: You have heard that it was said, An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth .
local: Yɩ dɔ̃ɔ wõ ban 'yɛr ɩka nɩbir na yãan nɩbir sãn, ka nyɩm mɩ yaa nyɩm sãn.

eng: Herod had arrested John, bound him, and put him in prison because of Herodias, the wife of Herods brother Philip.
local: A Nãa Hɛrɔd vɛ̃n ka ba nyɔɔ Jɔɔn lũ 'yɔ̃ɔ nɩpɔɔ die pɔ a wʊ yɛbɛ Filip pɔɔ Hɛrɔdiyas jũu.

eng: But I say to you that you must not oppose those who want to hurt you. If people slap you on your right cheek, you must turn the left cheek to them as well.
local: Tɩchɛ maa 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ, ta pii a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na sɔ̃ɔ fʊ sɔr ɛ. Tɩchɛ nɩrɛ wa 'ma fʊ a tʊkpal nyã pɔ, lɩɛb a nyã lɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ.

eng: Thats because John told Herod, Its against the law for you to marry her.
local: Bojũu Jɔɔn ma 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ wʊ ɩka, A ba tara sɔr ka fʊ kul wʊ ɛ.

eng: When they wish to haul you to court and take your shirt, let them have your coat too.
local: Ka nɩrɛ wa bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ sɛb fʊ nãa yir tɩ de a fʊ fuu, kʊ wʊ a 'lɔ fʊn su, na fʊ yaa a pʊɔm bon pʊɔ wʊ.

eng: Although Herod wanted to kill him, he feared the crowd because they thought John was a prophet.
local: Hɛrɔd ta bʊɔrɔ na a wʊ kʊ fʊ tɩ joro a Juu nɩbɛ dãbãɛ bojũu ba sɔɔn ɩka wʊ ɩn Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ.

eng: When they force you to go one mile, go with them two.
local: Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ wa fɩr fʊ ɩka fʊ de a wʊ tuor tuo chiin niem bãlãa, tuo chiin tɛɛr jaa.

eng: But at Herods birthday party Herodias daughter danced in front of the guests and thrilled Herod.
local: A Hɛrɔd dɔɔfʊ bɩbir dãɛn daar, a Hɛrɔdiyas bipɔɔ sɛn bãnɛ a nɩyɔɔ niem, ka a Hɛrɔd pʊɔ pɛl.

eng: Give to those who ask, and dont refuse those who wish to borrow from you.
local: Kʊ nɩrɛ jaa na sʊɔr fʊ, ta mɔ̃ɔ nɩrɛ nyã mɩ na bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ pɛ̃ɛ a fʊ bon ɛ.

eng: Then he swore to give her anything she asked.
local: yɔɔ jaa ka wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ nɛɛ kʊ a pɔɔbile, Sʊɔr ma bon jaa fʊn bʊɔrɔ ɩn kʊ fʊ naa.

eng: You have heard that it was said, You must love your neighbor and hate your enemy.
local: Yɩ wõn ban dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr ɩka, yɩ nʊ̃ a yɩ tɔ sʊɔ tɩ 'laar a yɩ dɔ̃sʊɔ.

eng: At her mothers urging, the girl said, Give me the head of John the Baptist here on a plate.
local: A wʊ ma wiil wʊn a bon 'lɔ wʊn na sʊɔr, ka wʊ 'yɛr, N bʊɔrɔ na ɩka fʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ a Jɔɔn Kɔ̃suor ju a la'yɛlma pɔ kʊ ma a pʊ̃pãanyãna.

eng: But I say to you, love your enemies and pray for those who harass you.
local: Tɩchɛ maa 'lɔ 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ, nʊ̃ a yɩ dɔ̃dẽme tɩ sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn kʊrɔ a bala na gere yɩ.

eng: Although the king was upset, because of his solemn pledge and his guests he commanded that they give it to her.
local: A nãa pʊɔ sɔ̃ɔ naa, tɩchɛ wʊn faa 'yɔ̃ɔ a nɛɛ a nɩyɔɔ niem ɩka a lɛ a pɔɔbile na bʊɔrɔ a ɩ.

eng: so that you will be acting as children of your Father who is in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both the evil and the good and sends rain on both the righteous and the unrighteous.
local: ka yɩ na ɩ Nãaŋmɩn bibiir bojũu 'lɔ lɛ ma vɛ̃ ka a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ pur 'yɔ̃ɔ a nɩdɛbɛ nɩ a nɩsʊ̃n tɩ na wʊ ma vɛ̃ ka saa wa 'yɔ̃ɔ a bala na tor nɩ a bala na ba tor ɛ.

eng: If you love only those who love you, what reward do you have? Dont even the tax collectors do the same?.
local: Yɩn nʊ̃ a bala na nʊ̃ yɩ tɛɛ, bo sãyar lɛ yɩn nyɛ? A lõpodierbe maa mɩ ɩ a lɛ bɩɩ?.

eng: Then he had John beheaded in prison.
local: Wʊ tʊ̃n ka ba ŋmaa a Jɔɔn ju a nɩpɔɔ die pɔ.

eng: And if you greet only your brothers and sisters, what more are you doing? Dont even the Gentiles do the same?.
local: Yɩn ma puoro a bala tɛɛ na ɩ a yɩ nɩbɛ, bo tɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ be be? A bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, maa mɩ ɩ a lɛ bɩɩ?.

eng: They brought his head on a plate and gave it to the young woman, and she brought it to her mother.
local: Ba 'yɔ̃ɔn a ju a la'yɛlma pɔ wa kʊ a pɔɔbile, ka wʊ de kʊ a wʊ ma.

eng: But Johns disciples came and took his body and buried it. Then they went and told Jesus what had happened.
local: Jɔɔn poturbo wan wa 'mɔɔ a ãgãn tɩ ũu tɩ na ba tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a Yesu.

eng: Therefore, just as your heavenly Father is complete in showing love to everyone, so also you must be complete.
local: Yɩ ɩ nɩmɩn a lɛ a yɩ sãa Nãaŋmɩn na be a saaju kaar.

eng: When Jesus heard about John, he withdrew in a boat to a deserted place by himself. When the crowds learned this, they followed him on foot from the cities.
local: A Yesu na wa wõ a nɩtɔ̃, wʊ kpɛ̃n a gboro chen jiyuo 'lɔ wʊ yõ. Tɩchɛ a nɩyɔɔ na wa wõ a nɩtɔ̃ ba yin a tẽn ala pɔ bɩɛr wʊ nɩ gbɛɛ chen a be.

eng: When Jesus arrived and saw a large crowd, he had compassion for them and healed those who were sick.
local: Wʊn wa yi a gboro pɔ, wʊ nyɛn nɩyɔɔ ka nɩbaal kpɛ wʊ tɩchɛ ka wʊ sãa a ba baalsɩ.

eng: That evening his disciples came and said to him, This is an isolated place and its getting late. Send the crowds away so they can go into the villages and buy food for themselves.
local: Mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ na wa mʊrɔ, a poturbo wa 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, A ka ɩn mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ ka a sɔ̃ɔ mɩ po, bar a nɩbɛ sɔr ka ba chen a tẽbil pɔ, tɩ da bʊ̃diir di.

eng: But Jesus said to them, Theres no need to send them away. You give them something to eat.
local: Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, A ba sɛɛ ɩka ba chen ɛ, yɩ kʊ ba bʊ̃kɔ̃ɔ ka ba di.

eng: They replied, We have nothing here except five loaves of bread and two fish.
local: Ka ba 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Paanʊ̃ɔsɩ anũu nɩ jãmɛ ayi tɛɛ lɛ sɩ tara.

eng: He said, Bring them here to me.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ de wa kʊ ma.

eng: He ordered the crowds to sit down on the grass. He took the five loaves of bread and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them and broke the loaves apart and gave them to his disciples. Then the disciples gave them to the crowds.
local: Wʊ vɛ̃n ka a nɩyɔɔ banyãna jɛ̃ tẽe. Wʊ den a paanʊ̃ɔsɩ anũu nɩ a jãmɛ ayi ala dʊl kaa saaju tɩ puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan tɩ na wʊ ŋma de kʊ a poturbo ɩka ba de põ a nɩbɛ. Ka a poturbo de põ a nɩbɛ.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full, and they filled twelve baskets with the leftovers.
local: Ba jaa din tɩɩ tɩchɛ ka a poturbo wob a bʊ̃ŋmaarsɩ ala na chɛ sɛɛ pɛr pie nɩ ayi.

eng: About five thousand men plus women and children had eaten.
local: A bala na di tɩɩ tɛɛ ɩn dɔɔr tur anũu ka pɔbɔ nɩ bibiir ba pʊɔ ɛ.

eng: Right then, Jesus made the disciples get into the boat and go ahead to the other side of the lake while he dismissed the crowds.
local: Ajienaa lɛ, wʊ vɛ̃n ka a poturbo kpɛ a gboro gɔ̃ɔ a man gɔ̃ɔn tɩchɛ ka wʊ vɛ̃ ka a nɩbɛ yaar.

eng: When he sent them away, he went up onto a mountain by himself to pray. Evening came and he was alone.
local: Wʊn wa wɛl a nɩbɛ baar, wʊ don a tɔ̃ɔ ju ɩka wʊ tɩ sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn. Jãanʊɔr na wa ta, wʊ chɛnɛ na be a be a wʊ yõ.

eng: Meanwhile, the boat, fighting a strong headwind, was being battered by the waves and was already far away from land.
local: Tɩchɛ a kɔ̃sɛbɛ ŋmen a gboro dãdaal chiin tɛɛr ka wʊ jãan a man nɛɛ, bojũu a kɔ̃sɛbɛ fɔ̃ɔ gar ba naa.

eng: Very early in the morning he came to his disciples, walking on the lake.
local: A wɛr na wa chaara wa la la la ba nyɛn a Yesu na chiine a kʊ̃ɔ ju waara a ba sɛ̃.

eng: When the disciples saw him walking on the lake, they were terrified and said, Its a ghost! They were so frightened they screamed.
local: A poturbo na wa nyɛ a Yesu na chiine a kʊ̃ɔ ju waara a ba sɛ̃, dãbãɛ kpɛ ba naa, ka ba kõ 'yɛr, Kpĩin lɛ.

eng: Just then Jesus spoke to them, Be encouraged! Its me. Dont be afraid.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ tara kãnyir, maa lɛ, yɩ ta joro dãbãɛ ɛ.

eng: Peter replied, Lord, if its you, order me to come to you on the water.
local: Pita 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, N Soro, ala ɩ fʊ̃ʊ lɛ vɛ̃ ka chiine a kʊ̃ɔ ju wa a fʊ sɛ̃.

eng: And Jesus said, Come. Then Peter got out of the boat and was walking on the water toward Jesus.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr wʊ, Wa, ka Pita yi a gboro pɔ piel chiine a kʊ̃ɔ ju waara a Yesu sɛ̃.

eng: But when Peter saw the strong wind, he became frightened. As he began to sink, he shouted, Lord, rescue me.
local: Pita na nyɛ a sɛsɛbɛ na furo yɔɔ dãbãɛ kpɛ wʊ naa, ka wʊ piel mʊrɔ a kʊ̃ɔ pɔ, ka wʊ bʊɔl, N Soro, faa ma.

eng: Jesus immediately reached out and grabbed him, saying, You man of weak faith! Why did you begin to have doubts.
local: Ajienaa lɛ, a Yesu tur a wʊ nũu nyɔɔ wʊ tɩ 'yɛr, Fʊ̃ʊ sɔɔfʊ na ba jã bon ɛ, bo so ka fʊ baala.

eng: When they got into the boat, the wind settled down.
local: Ban wa kpɛ a gboro pɔ baar ka a sɛsɛbɛ ŋmaa jaa jomm.

eng: Then those in the boat worshipped Jesus and said, You must be Gods Son.
local: A bala na be a gboro pɔ gbur dumo puor wʊ tɩ 'yɛrɛ, Yelmãɛ, fʊ̃ʊ lɛ ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Bie.

eng: When they had crossed the lake, they landed at Gennesaret.
local: Ban wa gɔ̃ɔ a man baar ba chen a Genesaretɩ wɛr pɔ.

eng: When the people who lived in that place recognized him, they sent word throughout that whole region, and they brought to him everyone who was sick.
local: A tẽe 'lɔ dẽme na wa wõ a wʊ yele ba 'yɛr lʊɔr a paal 'lɔ wʊ jaa na ba waan bala jaa an ɔɔrɔ.

eng: Then they begged him that they might just touch the edge of his clothes. Everyone who touched him was cured.
local: na ba wa sʊɔrɔ ɩka ba sɩɩr a wʊ fuu nɛɛ, a bala jaa na sɩɩr a fuu nɛɛ sa naa.

eng: Be careful that you dont practice your religion in front of people to draw their attention. If you do, you will have no reward from your Father who is in heaven.
local: Yɩ bɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ sʊ̃, na yɩ taa mʊɔlɔ a yɩ yeltorsɩ a nɩbɛ niem ɛ, ka ba na bɔ̃ɔn ɛ, yɩn wa ɩrɛ a lɛ, yɩ kʊ̃ nyɛ sãyar a yɩ sãa Nãaŋmɩn na be a saaju sɛ̃ ɛ.

eng: Whenever you give to the poor, dont blow your trumpet as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets so that they may get praise from people. I assure you, thats the only reward theyll get.
local: A lɛ jũu, fʊ̃ʊ wa de bon kʊ nɩrɛ na ba tara ɛ, ta vɛ̃ ka nɩbɛ bɔ̃ɔn ɛ, fɩka lɛ a nɛɛr ayi dẽme na ma maala a lɔ̃ɔfʊ yie nɩ a tẽn pɔ ɛ, ka nɩbɛ na pɛɛ ba ɛ. Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, bala nyɛn a ba sãyar baar.

eng: When Jesus finished teaching his twelve disciples, he went on from there to teach and preach in their cities.
local: Yesu na wa wiil a wʊ poturbo pie nɩ ayi baar, wʊ yin a be chen a Juu nɩbɛ tẽn tɩ wile na wʊ mʊɔlɔ a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ a tẽn ala na be a Galilee paal pɔ.

eng: But when you give to the poor, dont let your left hand know what your right hand is doing.
local: A lɛ jũu, fʊ̃ʊ wa de bon kʊ nɩrɛ na ba tara ɛ, ta vɛ̃ ka a gubaa nũu bɔ̃ɔn a bon 'lɔ a nũsʊ̃ɔ na maala ɛ.

eng: In those days John the Baptist appeared in the desert of Judea announcing.
local: Sɔ̃ɔ kɔ̃ɔ lɛ a Jɔɔn kɔ̃suor mʊɔlɔ a Judiya mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ.

eng: Now when John heard in prison about the things the Christ was doing, he sent word by his disciples to Jesus, asking.
local: Jɔɔn na be a nɩpɔɔ die pɔ, tɩ wa wõ lɛ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto na maala, wʊ tʊ̃n a wʊ poturbo bamɩne.

eng: Boarding a boat, Jesus crossed to the other side of the lake and went to his own city.
local: Yesu na wa kpɛ a gboro pɔ, wʊ gɔ̃ɔn a man chen a wʊ mãɛ tẽe be a Kapenahum.

eng: so that you may give to the poor in secret. Your Father who sees what you do in secret will reward you.
local: ka nɩrɛ ta bɔ̃ɔn a bon fʊn maala ɛ, tɩchɛ a fʊ Sãa Nãaŋmɩn na nyɛrɛ yelsɔɔlsɩ na kʊ fʊn a sãyar.

eng: He called his twelve disciples and gave them authority over unclean spirits to throw them out and to heal every disease and every sickness.
local: Yesu bʊɔl a wʊ poturbo pie nɩ ayi na wʊ kʊ ba kpɛ̃ɔ ɩka ba dii a sɩdɛbɛ ka a yi, na ba sãana nɩ baal jaa bʊrɔ.

eng: Change your hearts and lives! Here comes the kingdom of heaven.
local: 'yɛrɛ, Yɩ lɩɛb a yɩ befʊ, a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ ta na.

eng: A record of the ancestors of Jesus Christ, son of David, son of Abraham.
local: A Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto sãakpãmɩne nɩ nyã, wʊ yin a Nãa David nɩ Abraham bal pɔ.

eng: Abraham was the father of Isaac. Isaac was the father of Jacob. Jacob was the father of Judah and his brothers.
local: Abraham lɛ ɩ Isaakɩ sãa, ka Isaakɩ mɩ ɩ Jekɔb sãa, ka Jekɔb mɩ dɔ̃ɔ ɩ Juda sãa nɩ a wʊ yɛɛr.

eng: Judah was the father of Perez and Zerah, whose mother was Tamar. Perez was the father of Hezron. Hezron was the father of Aram.
local: ka Juda mɩ ɩ Perejɩ nɩ Jera sãa, ka ba ma di Tamar, nɩ Perejɩ na ɩ Hejoron sãa, ka Hejoron mɩ ɩ Aram sãa.

eng: Aram was the father of Amminadab. Amminadab was the father of Nahshon. Nahshon was the father of Salmon.
local: ka Aram mɩ ɩ Aminadab sãa, ka Aminadab mɩ ɩ Nason sãa, ka Nason mɩ ɩ Salmon sãa.

eng: Salmon was the father of Boaz, whose mother was Rahab. Boaz was the father of Obed, whose mother was Ruth. Obed was the father of Jesse.
local: ka Salmon mɩ ɩ Boajɩ sãa, ka a wʊ ma di Worahab, ka Boajɩ mɩ ɩ Obed sãa, a wʊ ma lɛ Wʊruf, ka Obed mɩ ɩ Jesɩ sãa.

eng: Jesse was the father of David the king. David was the father of Solomon, whose mother had been the wife of Uriah.
local: ka Jesɩ mɩ ɩ a Nãa David sãa. David lɛ ɩ a Solomon sãa, ka a wʊ ma dɔ̃ɔ ɩ a Yurɩya pɔɔ.

eng: Solomon was the father of Rehoboam. Rehoboam was the father of Abijah. Abijah was the father of Asaph.
local: Ka a Solomon mɩ ɩ Wʊrehobom sãa, ka Wʊrehobom mɩ ɩ Abija sãa, ka Abija mɩ ɩ Asa sãa.

eng: Here are the names of the twelve apostles: first, Simon, who is called Peter; and Andrew his brother; James the son of Zebedee; and John his brother.
local: A poturbo pie nɩ ayi na ɩ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ yoe lɛ a anyãna, Simon ban bʊɔlɔ Pita, nɩ a wʊ yɛbɛ Andiru, Jemesɩ na ɩ Jebedee bidaba, nɩ a wʊ yɛbɛ Jɔɔn.

eng: Philip; and Bartholomew; Thomas; and Matthew the tax collector; James the son of Alphaeus; and Thaddaeus.
local: Filip nɩ Batolomi, Tomasɩ nɩ Matiu na ɩ a lõpodiere. Jemesɩ na ɩ Alfayasɩ bidaba nɩ Tadius.

eng: Simon the Cananaean; and Judas, who betrayed Jesus.
local: Simon na mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir nɩ a wʊ wɛr yele, nɩ Judas Isɩkariyot 'lɔ lɛ wa gar 'yɔ̃ɔ a Yesu.

eng: People brought to him a man who was paralyzed, lying on a cot. When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the man who was paralyzed, Be encouraged, my child, your sins are forgiven.
local: Dɔɔr bamɩne tuon daba nɩ fɔbaa nuur nɩ a gbɛɛ na kpi waan wa kpɛ̃n wʊ gaal a Yesu niem. Yesu na wa nyɛ lɛ ban sɔɔ de wʊ, ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a baalaa, N bie ta joro dãbãɛ ɛ, a fʊ yeldɛbɛ vɛ̃n bar.

eng: After Jesus was born in Bethlehem in the territory of Judea during the rule of King Herod, magi came from the east to Jerusalem.
local: Yesu ban wa dɔɔ Bɛtɩlɛhɛm tẽe a Judiya paal pɔ a Nãa Hɛrɔd sɔ̃ɔ, yɛ̃ dẽme yin be a Jerusalɛm sapar wa.

eng: Are you the one who is to come, or should we look for another.
local: ɩka ba tɩ sʊʊr, Fʊ̃ʊ lɛ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ nɩ na wa bɩɩ, sɩ chɛlɛ nɩyuo.

eng: At that time Jesus went through the wheat fields on the Sabbath. His disciples were hungry so they were picking heads of wheat and eating them.
local: A ba kor ɛ tɩchɛ ka Yesu kpɛ a pʊɔ sãlsɔɔ tala a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar. Kɔ̃ kpɛ̃n a wʊ poturbo, ka ba piel fɔrɔ a bʊ̃bie ɔɔrɔ.

eng: Asaph was the father of Jehoshaphat. Jehoshaphat was the father of Joram. Joram was the father of Uzziah.
local: ka Asa mɩ ɩ Jehosafatɩ sãa, ka a Jehosafatɩ mɩ ɩ Jehoram sãa, ka Jehoram mɩ ɩ Yujiya sãa.

eng: When you pray, dont be like hypocrites. They love to pray standing in the synagogues and on the street corners so that people will see them. I assure you, thats the only reward theyll get.
local: Yɩn maa wa sʊɔrɔ a Nãaŋmɩn, yɩ ta ma kaara a nɛɛr ayi dẽme kaar ɛ, ba ma nʊ̃n ɩka ba puoro a lɔ̃ɔfʊ yie pɔ, nɩ a tẽbɛrɛ pɔ, nɩ a sɔchara pɔ, ka nɩbɛ na nyɛ ba. Yelmãɛ jaa lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, ɩka ba nyɛn a ba sãyar baar.

eng: He was the one of whom Isaiah the prophet spoke when he said: The voice of one shouting in the wilderness, Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight. .
local: Jɔɔn nyã 'yɛr lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ Isaiya dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr a wʊ 'yɛr, Kɔkɔr kɔ̃ɔ lɛ gɔ̃nɔ̃ 'yɛrɛ be a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ, chɔɔr a sɩ Soro sɔr, maal a wʊ sɔbie ka a tor.

eng: Jesus sent these twelve out and commanded them, Dont go among the Gentiles or into a Samaritan city.
local: A poturbo pie nɩ ayi banyã lɛ a Yesu tʊ̃ nɩ a kpãafʊ anyãna. Yɩ ta kpɛ a nɩyor bɩɩ a Samariya tẽn pɔ ɛ.

eng: Then Pharisees and legal experts came to Jesus from Jerusalem and said.
local: A Faraseemɩne nɩ a nɛɛ wiwiilbe bamɩne na yi a Jerusalɛm wan a Yesu sɛ̃ wa sʊʊr wʊ.

eng: Some legal experts said among themselves, This man is insulting God.
local: Ka a nɛɛ wiwiilbe bamɩne pãa jɛ̃ bʊlɔ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ pʊʊr pɔ, A daba nyã fere na sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ Nãaŋmɩn yuor.

eng: But Jesus knew what they were thinking and said, Why do you fill your minds with evil things?.
local: Yesu bɔ̃ɔn a lɛ ban tɩɛr, ka wʊ sʊʊr ba, Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ yɩ tɩɛrɛ dɛɛr a yɩ pʊʊr pɔ?.

eng: Jesus responded, Go, report to John what you hear and see.
local: Ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ lɩɛb kul tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a Jɔɔn, a lɛ yɩn wõ tɩ nyɛ.

eng: When the Pharisees saw this, they said to him, Look, your disciples are breaking the Sabbath law.
local: A Faraseemɩne na wa nyɛ a lɛ, ba sʊʊr a Yesu, Fʊ nyɛn lɛ na ba tu a nɛbinãa a fʊ poturbo na ɩrɛ a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar ɔ.

eng: John wore clothes made of camels hair, with a leather belt around his waist. He ate locusts and wild honey.
local: Nyɔɔma kɔɔlʊ fuu lɛ Jɔɔn ma su tɩ de gãn a lũn a sɩɛ. Soor nɩ sɩkãa lɛ ɩ a wʊ bʊ̃diir.

eng: Go instead to the lost sheep, the people of Israel.
local: tɩchɛ yɩ chen a Isɩral nɩbɛ bala na bɔr a piir kaar sɛ̃.

eng: But when you pray, go to your room, shut the door, and pray to your Father who is present in that secret place. Your Father who sees what you do in secret will reward you.
local: Tɩchɛ fʊ̃ʊ maa wa sʊɔrɔ a Sãa Nãaŋmɩn nyã sɩn ba nyɛrɛ ɛ, kpɛ a fʊ die pɔ, tɩ pɔɔ a pãn, tɩ sʊɔr a Sãa Nãaŋmɩn na nyɛrɛ bon jaa, na yab fʊn a sãyar.

eng: They asked, Where is the newborn king of the Jews? Weve seen his star in the east, and weve come to honor him.
local: na ba wa sʊrɔ, Nyɩnɛ lɛ a bie be ban dɔɔ ka wʊ ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ nãa? Sɩ nyɛ naa a wʊ ŋmɛrbir a sapar ɩka sɩ wa puor wʊ.

eng: People from Jerusalem, throughout Judea, and all around the Jordan River came to him.
local: Nɩyɔɔ yin a Jerusalɛm nɩ Judiya chiine a wʊ sɛ̃ nɩ a bala na yi a Jordan man koor.

eng: As they confessed their sins, he baptized them in the Jordan River.
local: Ba woro naa a ba yeldɛbɛ tɩ wʊ suoro ba a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ be a Jordan man pɔ.

eng: Many Pharisees and Sadducees came to be baptized by John. He said to them, You children of snakes! Who warned you to escape from the angry judgment that is coming soon?.
local: Jɔɔn na wa nyɛ a Faraseemɩne nɩ a Saduseemɩne na waara a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ sofʊ jie, ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩn waa bibiir, an n 'yɛr kʊ yɩ ɩka yɩ jo yi a Nãaŋmɩn suur na waara mɔ̃?.

eng: Why are your disciples breaking the elders rules handed down to us? They dont ritually purify their hands by washing before they eat.
local: Ŋmɩnɛ a fʊ poturbo ba turo a sɩ sãakpãmɩne wiilfʊ ɛ? Ba maa pɛɛ a ba nuur tɩ di ɛ.

eng: Which is easier—to say, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up and walk?.
local: 'Yɛr buor sʊɔ lɛ ɩ kɔ̃mɔ̃kɔ̃mɔ̃? N 'yɛr ɩka a wʊ yeldɛbɛ vɛ̃n bar bɩɩ n 'yɛr ɩka wʊ ir chiine?.

eng: But so you will know that the Human One has authority on the earth to forgive sins—he said to the man who was paralyzed—Get up, take your cot, and go home.
local: Tɩchɛ pʊ̃pãanyã ɩn wiil yɩ naa ka yɩ bɔ̃ɔn ɩka maa Nɩsaal Bie tara na kpɛ̃ɔ a wɛr pɔ, na vɛ̃ nɩrɛ yeldɛbɛ bar. Tɩ pãa 'yɛr kʊ a gbɛrɛ, Ir 'mɔɔ a fʊ bʊ̃gãnaa kulo nɩ.

eng: The man got up and went home.
local: Ka a daba vaa ir kulo.

eng: Uzziah was the father of Jotham. Jotham was the father of Ahaz. Ahaz was the father of Hezekiah.
local: ka Yujiya mɩ ɩ Jotam sãa, ka Jotam mɩ ɩ Ahajɩ sãa, ka Ahajɩ mɩ ɩ Hɛjɩkiya sãa.

eng: But he said to them, Havent you read what David did when he and those with him were hungry?.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yaa kar a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ nyɛ a lɛ a Nãa David na dɔ̃ɔ ɩ nɩ a wʊ nɩbɛ a kɔ̃ na kpɛ ɛ?.

eng: When the crowds saw what had happened, they were afraid and praised God, who had given such authority to human beings.
local: A nɩbɛ na nyɛ a lɛ nɛɛ 'maa ban ka ba pɛɛrɛ Nãaŋmɩn na kʊ nɩsaal a kpɛ̃ɔ nyã.

eng: Produce fruit that shows you have changed your hearts and lives.
local: Yɩ vɛ̃ ka a yɩ lɛ befʊ wiil ɩka yɩ lɩɛb a yɩ tɩɛr.

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you break the command of God by keeping the rules handed down to you?.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Ŋmɩnɛ ka yɩ sɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn nɛbinsɩ tɩ na yɩ turo a yɩ mãɛ wiilfʊ?.

eng: When you pray, dont pour out a flood of empty words, as the Gentiles do. They think that by saying many words theyll be heard.
local: Yɩn maa wa sʊɔrɔ a Nãaŋmɩn, yɩ ta ma 'yɛrɛ 'yɛrbie ala na ba tara tɔ̃nɔ̃ ɛ, lɛ lɛ a bala na ba bɔ̃ɔ Nãaŋmɩn ma maala, ba tɩɛr ɩka Nãaŋmɩn na wõn a ba sʊɔrfʊ a ba 'yɛrbiyɔɔ jũu.

eng: Dont be like them, because your Father knows what you need before you ask.
local: Yɩ ta kaara a ba kaar ɛ, a yɩ Sãa den nie bɔ̃ɔn bon 'lɔ yɩn bʊɔrɔ.

eng: Pray like this: Our Father who is in heaven, uphold the holiness of your name.
local: Nɩtɔ̃ lɛ yɩn ma sʊɔr a Nãaŋmɩn. Sɩ Sãa na be a saaju. Sɩ kʊ naa a fʊ yuor tɩɩr.

eng: Bring in your kingdom so that your will is done on earth as its done in heaven.
local: A fʊ nãalʊ̃ʊ wʊ wa. A fʊ bɔfʊ wʊ ɩ a wɛr pɔ, nɩtãa lɛ a fʊ bɔfʊ na ɩrɛ be a saaju.

eng: Hezekiah was the father of Manasseh. Manasseh was the father of Amos. Amos was the father of Josiah.
local: ka Hɛjɩkiya mɩ ɩ Manasɛ sãa, ka Manasɛ mɩ ɩ Amɔn sãa, ka Amɔn mɩ ɩ Josiya sãa.

eng: As Jesus continued on from there, he saw a man named Matthew sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. He said to him, Follow me, and he got up and followed him.
local: Yesu na wa yi a be chiine, wʊ nyɛn daba na di Matiu na jãnɛ a lõpoo defʊ jie, ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Ir turo ma, ka a Matiu ir turo wʊ.

eng: He went into Gods house and broke the law by eating the bread of the presence, which only the priests were allowed to eat.
local: Wʊ kpɛ̃n a Nãaŋmɩn yir ka 'lɔ nɩ a wʊ nɩbɛ tɩ de a paanʊ̃ɔ 'lɔ ban ben kʊ Nãaŋmɩn na ba 'wɔb na ba tara sɔr ɛ, ka ala ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrloro tɛɛ ba lɛ ɛ.

eng: For God said, Honor your father and your mother, and The person who speaks against father or mother will certainly be put to death .
local: Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr naa, 'Yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a fʊ sãa nɩ a fʊ ma, ka Nɩrɛ jaa wa pɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ a wʊ sãa bɩɩ a wʊ ma, ba kʊ wʊ.

eng: And dont even think about saying to yourselves, Abraham is our father. I tell you that God is able to raise up Abrahams children from these stones.
local: Yɩ ta chɛnɛ ŋmara jir 'yɔ̃ɔn yɩ mãɛ ɩka, Abraham lɛ ɩ a sɩ sãa ɛ. N 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka Nãaŋmɩn na tʊ̃ɔn maal a kʊsɩbɛ anyãna ka a lɩɛb ɩ a Abraham bibiir.

eng: When King Herod heard this, he was troubled, and everyone in Jerusalem was troubled with him.
local: A Nãa Hɛrɔd na wa wõ a lɛ wʊ pʊɔ sɔ̃ɔ naa, ka Jerusalɛm nɩyɔɔ pʊʊr mɩ sɔ̃ɔ.

eng: Those who were blind are able to see . Those who were crippled are walking. People with skin diseases are cleansed. Those who were deaf now hear. Those who were dead are raised up. The poor have good news proclaimed to them .
local: Jɔ̃n nyɛrɛ naa, ka a gbɛrsɩ mɩ chiine, ka kɔ̃kɔ̃n mɩ sa, ka wõn mɩ wõnõ jaa chɛchɛ, ka kũusɩ jɛɔ, ka 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ mɩ ta a nɔ̃n dẽme.

eng: As you go, make this announcement: The kingdom of heaven has come near.
local: Yɩ chen tɩ mʊɔl a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ na yɩ 'yɛrɛ, a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ ta na.

eng: Now when Jesus had come down from the mountain, large crowds followed him.
local: Yesu na wa yɩ a tɔ̃ɔ ju suu nɩyɔɔ turo wʊ naa.

eng: Give us the bread we need for today.
local: Kʊ sɩ a dɩna bʊ̃diir ka a sɛɛn sɩ.

eng: Josiah was the father of Jechoniah and his brothers. This was at the time of the exile to Babylon.
local: ka Josiya mɩ ɩ Jekoniya sãa, nɩ a wʊ yɛɛr a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ ban bar a ba tẽe tɩ chen tɩ kpɩɛrɛ Babilon tẽe.

eng: After the exile to Babylon: Jechoniah was the father of Shealtiel. Shealtiel was the father of Zerubbabel.
local: Ban wa tɩ be a Babilon tẽe pɔ, Jekoniya lɛ ɩ a Sealtɩɛl sãa, ka Sealtɩɛl ɩ Jerubabel sãa.

eng: Zerubbabel was the father of Abiud. Abiud was the father of Eliakim. Eliakim was the father of Azor.
local: ka Jerubabel mɩ ɩ Abiud sãa, ka Abiud mɩ ɩ Eliyakim sãa, ka Eliyakim mɩ ɩ Ajor sãa.

eng: But you say, If you tell your father or mother,Everything Im expected to contribute to you Im giving to God as a gift,then you dont have to honor your father.
local: Tɩchɛ yɩn 'yɛr ɩka nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na 'yɛr a wʊ sãa bɩɩ ma ɩka, sʊ̃ʊfʊ 'lɔ jaa ɩn tara naa kʊ fʊ, n den kʊ Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: The ax is already at the root of the trees. Therefore, every tree that doesnt produce good fruit will be chopped down and tossed into the fire.
local: A 'lar dɔɔl a tɩɛ pĩlem gu a ŋmaafʊ, tɩɛ jaa ba wa wɔ̃nɔ̃ wɔ̃ sʊ̃n ɛ ban ŋmaa wʊn lɔb 'yɔ̃ɔ bũu pɔ.

eng: He gathered all the chief priests and the legal experts and asked them where the Christ was to be born.
local: be lɛ wʊ bʊɔl a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a nɛɛ wiwiilbe sʊʊr bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ bɔ̃ɔ a jie 'lɔ ban na dɔɔ a Nɩ-iraa.

eng: Heal the sick, raise the dead, cleanse those with skin diseases, and throw out demons. You received without having to pay. Therefore, give without demanding payment.
local: Yɩ sãana a baalsɩ, na yɩ sãnɛ a kũusɩ, na yɩ sãana a kɔ̃kɔ̃mɔ̃ baalsɩ na yɩn maal ba ka ba ɩ nɩsʊ̃n, na yɩ dire a sɩdɛbɛ ka a yire. Yɩ nyɛn pɔrɔ, yɩ mɩ tɩ tɩr pɔrɔ.

eng: Happy are those who dont stumble and fall because of me.
local: Pʊpɛl sʊɔ lɛ a 'lɔ na ba baala ma ɛ.

eng: A man with a skin disease came, kneeled before him, and said, Lord, if you want, you can make me clean.
local: a nɩyɔɔ na turo wʊ, ka daba ãsʊɔ na tara kɔ̃kɔ̃mɔ̃ baal wa gbur dumo a wʊ niem tɩ 'yɛr, N Soro fʊ̃ʊ sɔɔ sãa ma.

eng: Forgive us for the ways we have wronged you, just as we also forgive those who have wronged us.
local: Vɛ̃ a sɩ yeldɛbɛ bar, nɩtãa lɛ sɩn ma vɛ̃ kʊ a sɩ taaba yeldɛbɛ bar ba.

eng: And dont lead us into temptation, but rescue us from the evil one.
local: Ta sɔɔ ka bɛɛr kaa nyɔɔ sɩ ɛ, faa sɩ yin a dɛɛr sʊɔ nũu pɔ.

eng: If you forgive others their sins, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.
local: Yɩn wa vɛ̃ a yɩ taaba yeldɛbɛ bar ba, a yɩ Sãa Nãaŋmɩn na be a saaju mɩ na vɛ̃n bar yɩ.

eng: But if you dont forgive others, neither will your Father forgive your sins.
local: Yɩn ba wa vɛ̃ a yɩ taaba yeldɛbɛ kʊ ba ɛ, a yɩ Sãa Nãaŋmɩn mɩ kʊ̃ vɛ̃ a yɩ yeldɛbɛ kʊ yɩ ɛ.

eng: Or havent you read in the Law that on the Sabbath the priests in the temple treat the Sabbath as any other day and are still innocent?.
local: Bɩɩ yaa kar a Mosesɩ nɛbinãa pɔ lɛ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlorbo a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar a puorfʊ yir pɔ na ma tʊ̃ tɩ na baa bʊɔlɔ a lɛ bier ɛ?.

eng: So you do away with Gods Law for the sake of the rules that have been handed down to you.
local: Tɩchɛ na waa de a wʊ bʊ̃tarsɩ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ nɩ a a wʊ sãa ɛ, yɩn ɩ a lɛ, yɩ sɔ̃ɔn Nãaŋmɩn nɛɛ, tɩ na yɩ turo a yɩ mãɛ binfʊ.

eng: I baptize with water those of you who have changed your hearts and lives. The one who is coming after me is stronger than I am. Im not worthy to carry his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire.
local: Maa 'lɔ suoro yɩn nɩ kʊ̃ɔ ka yɩ na lɩɛb a yɩ befʊ, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ na tara kpɛ̃ɔ gar ma ben a n puor waara, a wʊ nafɔɔr gba ba sɛɛ ɩka n tuo ɛ. 'Lɔ lɛ na de a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ nɩ bũu son yɩ.

eng: They said, In Bethlehem of Judea, for this is what the prophet wrote.
local: Ka ba 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Bɛtɩlɛhɛm na be Judiya, lɛ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ dɔ̃ɔ sɛb bin.

eng: You, Bethlehem, land of Judah, by no means are you least among the rulers of Judah, because from you will come one who governs, who will shepherd my people Israel.
local: Fʊ̃ʊ Bɛtɩlɛhɛm na be a Judiya paal pɔ, fʊ̃ʊ lɛ ɩ jaa pʊʊrbile a Juda kpãɛn dẽme pɔ, fʊ pɔ lɛ a kpɛ̃ɔ sʊɔ na yi kaara a n nɩbɛ Isɩral. .

eng: When Johns disciples had gone, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What did you go out to the wilderness to see? A stalk blowing in the wind?.
local: Ban wa chen baar, Yesu piel 'yɛrɛ a Jɔɔn 'yɛr kʊrɔ a nɩyɔɔ, Bo lɛ yɩ dɔ̃ɔ kpɛ a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ ɩka yɩ tɩ kaa? A jɩmur a sɛsɛbɛ na dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ bɩɩ?.

eng: Jesus reached out his hand and touched him, saying, I do want to. Become clean. Instantly his skin disease was cleansed.
local: Ka Yesu tur a wʊ nũu sɩɩr a daba tɩ 'yɛr, N sɔɔ naa, sa. Ajienaa ka a daba kɔ̃kɔ̃mɔ̃ baal bar wʊ.

eng: That day Jesus went out of the house and sat down beside the lake.
local: A daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ Yesu yin a yir pɔ tɩ jãnɛ a man kõkoor.

eng: Azor was the father of Zadok. Zadok was the father of Achim. Achim was the father of Eliud.
local: ka Ajor mɩ ɩ Jadɔk sãa, ka Jadɔk mɩ ɩ Achɩm sãa, ka Achɩm mɩ ɩ Ɛliud sãa.

eng: As Jesus sat down to eat in Matthews house, many tax collectors and sinners joined Jesus and his disciples at the table.
local: Ka ba 'mataa kul a wʊ yir tɩ jɛ̃ dire ka a lõpodiere nɩ a yelbe-ɩrbɛ yɔɔ wa pʊɔ ba nɩ a Yesu poturbo ka ba 'mataa dire.

eng: And when you fast, dont put on a sad face like the hypocrites. They distort their faces so people will know they are fasting. I assure you that they have their reward.
local: Yɩn wa gal kɔ̃, yɩ ta sɔ̃ a yɩ nin a nɛɛr ayi dẽme kaar ɛ, ba ma sɔ̃n a ba nin ka a ba taaba nyɛ bɔ̃ɔ ka ba gal kɔ̃. Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka ba nyɛn a ba sãyar baar.

eng: But I tell you that something greater than the temple is here.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, nɩrɛ kɔ̃ɔ ben ka na gar a puorfʊ yir.

eng: Hypocrites! Isaiah really knew what he was talking about when he prophesied about you.
local: Yɩn nɛɛr ayi dẽme! Isaiya 'yɛr yelmãɛ nɩ wʊn dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr ɩka.

eng: The shovel he uses to sift the wheat from the husks is in his hands. He will clean out his threshing area and bring the wheat into his barn. But he will burn the husks with a fire that cant be put out.
local: A ŋmayɩɛlaa ben a wʊ nũu pɔ, ka wʊ na yɩɛl a mʊ̃ɩ a vafʊ jie na wʊ kpaar a bie 'yɔ̃ɔ a boor pɔ tɩ chʊm a sɔ̃sɔɔr nɩ bũu 'lɔ na ba tara kpĩinfʊ bar.

eng: Workers deserve to be fed, so dont gather gold or silver or copper coins for your money belts to take on your trips.
local: Yɩ ta de sɛlma ɛ bɩɩ diere libie 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a yɩ wʊʊr pɔ ɛ.

eng: Then Herod secretly called for the magi and found out from them the time when the star had first appeared.
local: Be lɛ ka Hɛrɔd sɔɔl bʊɔl a yɛ̃ dẽme bala sʊʊr ba a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ a ŋmɛrbir na pur wa.

eng: What did you go out to see? A man dressed up in refined clothes? Look, those who wear refined clothes are in royal palaces.
local: Kai, ka bo lɛ yɩ chen ɩka yɩ tɩ kaa? Daba na su bʊ̃vɩɛl lɛ yɩ chen ɩka yɩ tɩ kaa bɩɩ? Nyɛ a bala na ma su bʊ̃vɩɛl ɛ, nãmɩne yie lɛ ba ma be.

eng: When you fast, brush your hair and wash your face.
local: Yɩn wa gal kɔ̃, yɩ lor a yɩ nie maal a yɩ mãɛ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Dont say anything to anyone. Instead, go and show yourself to the priest and offer the gift that Moses commanded. This will be a testimony to them.
local: Be lɛ ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, taa 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ nɩrɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ chen tɩ wiil a fʊ mãɛ kʊ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrloro na fʊ chiin a fʊ kʊfʊ tɩ ir a nɛɛ 'lɔ a Mosesɩ na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr ka a di dãasɩɛ.

eng: Eliud was the father of Eleazar. Eleazar was the father of Matthan. Matthan was the father of Jacob.
local: ka Ɛliud mɩ ɩ Elɩyajar sãa, ka Elɩyajar mɩ ɩ Matan sãa, ka Matan mɩ ɩ Jekɔb sãa.

eng: Jacob was the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary—of whom Jesus was born, who is called the Christ.
local: Jekɔb mɩ lɛ ɩ a Josefʊ sãa na ɩ Mɛɛr sɩrɛ 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a Yesu ma, a Yesu nyã lɛ ɩ a Nɩ-iraa.

eng: At that time Jesus came from Galilee to the Jordan River so that John would baptize him.
local: Yesu yin a be a Galilee chen a Jordan man pɔ, ka a Jɔɔn na tɩ so wʊ a kʊ̃ɔ.

eng: This people honors me with their lips, but their hearts are far away from me.
local: A nɩbɛ banyãna den a ba nɛɛr 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ nɩ ma tɩchɛ a ba nyãn pɔ, ba tɔɔn jãan ma.

eng: If you had known what this means, I want mercy and not sacrifice , you wouldnt have condemned the innocent.
local: A Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr a, Nɩbaal lɛ n bʊɔrɔ, a ba ɩ dʊ̃n yɩn kʊ chʊm kʊ ma ɛ. Yɩn ta bɔ̃ɔ a yele nyã par, yɩ kʊ naa bʊɔlɔ nɩbɛ na ba maal dɛɛr ɛ, ɩka bier lɛ ba maal ɛ.

eng: Dont judge, so that you wont be judged.
local: Yɩ ta tɔɔr nɩrɛ jaa 'yɛr ɛ, ka Nãaŋmɩn mɩ wa tɔɔr a yɩ 'yɛr ɛ.

eng: Such large crowds gathered around him that he climbed into a boat and sat down. The whole crowd was standing on the shore.
local: A nɩyɔɔ na lɔ̃ɔ jũu wʊ kpɛ̃n jɛ̃ a gboro pɔ, tɩchɛ ka a nɩyɔɔ ara a man kõkoor.

eng: Then you wont look like you are fasting to people, but only to your Father who is present in that secret place. Your Father who sees in secret will reward you.
local: ka nɩbɛ ta bɔ̃ɔn yɩn gal kɔ̃ ɛ, tɩchɛ a yɩ Sãa Nãaŋmɩn nyã sɩn ba nyɛrɛ ɛ, a Sãa Nãaŋmɩn nyã na nyɛrɛ yelsɔɔlsɩ na yab yɩn a sãyar.

eng: He sent them to Bethlehem, saying, Go and search carefully for the child. When youve found him, report to me so that I too may go and honor him.
local: Be lɛ wʊ tara ba chiin Bɛtɩlɛhɛm tɩ 'yɛr, Yɩ chen tɩ yɔbɔ a bie kaa nɛ nɛ, yin wa nyɛ wʊ yɩ lɩɛb wa 'yɛr kʊ ma ka n mɩ chen tɩ puor wʊ.

eng: What did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
local: Tɩchɛ bo lɛ yɩ chen ɩka yɩ tɩ kaa? Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ bɩɩ? Ɔ̃ɔ, n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka wʊ gar Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ.

eng: Dont take a backpack for the road or two shirts or sandals or a walking stick.
local: bɩɩ 'mɔɔ wʊɔ tara a sɔr pɔ ɛ, bɩɩ 'mɔɔ fuusɩ ayi ɛ, bɩɩ de nafɔbɔ pʊ̃ɔ ɛ, bɩɩ dadiiraa ɛ, bojũu tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ jaa sɛɛn naa wʊ sãyar.

eng: When Jesus went to Capernaum, a centurion approached.
local: Yesu na wa kpɛ a Kapenahum a sojasɩ nɩdiere chen a wʊ sɛ̃ tɩ sʊɔr wʊ 'yɛr.

eng: But when the Pharisees saw this, they said to his disciples, Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners.
local: A Faraseemɩne na wa nyɛ a nɩtɔ̃ ba sʊʊr a Yesu poturbo, Bo lɛ so ka a yɩ wiwile 'mataa dire nɩ a lõpodierbe nɩ a yelbe-ɩrbɛ.

eng: When they heard the king, they went; and look, the star they had seen in the east went ahead of them until it stood over the place where the child was.
local: Ban wa chɛl a nãa 'yɛr baar, ba yin lo a sɔr chiine, a ŋmɛrbir 'lɔ ban nyɛ a sapar de ban nie tɩ ara a jie 'lɔ a bie na be.

eng: So there were fourteen generations from Abraham to David, fourteen generations from David to the exile to Babylon, and fourteen generations from the exile to Babylon to the Christ.
local: An yi Abraham tɩ ta a Nãa David, a Yesu sãakpãmɩne ɩ naa pie nɩ anaar, an yi Nãa David tɩ tãn a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ a ba dɔ̃dɔ̃mɔ̃sɩ na nyɔɔr ba chiin a Babilon wɛr pɔ ba mɩ ɩ naa pie nɩ anaar, an lɛ yi a ba gbãgbaar ɩfʊ a Babilon wɛr pɔ tɩ tãn a Nɩ-iraa a ba mɩ ɩ pie nɩ anaar.

eng: He said many things to them in parables: A farmer went out to scatter seed.
local: Sʊkpar lɛ wʊ lɔɔrɔ wile nɩ ba yelyɔɔ, Yɩ chɛlɛ, kʊɔraa lɛ yin wʊ bʊ̃bʊrɔ bʊrfʊ jie.

eng: John tried to stop him and said, I need to be baptized by you, yet you come to me.
local: Jɔɔn ba ta bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ so wʊ ɛ, na wʊ 'yɛr fʊ̃ʊ lɛ sɛɛ ɩka fʊ so ma tɩchɛ ka fʊ lɛ wa a n sɛ̃ go?.

eng: This is how the birth of Jesus Christ took place. When Mary his mother was engaged to Joseph, before they were married, she became pregnant by the Holy Spirit.
local: Nɩtɔ̃ lɛ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto dɔɔfʊ yele tu. A wʊ ma Mɛɛr ŋmaan na Josefʊ baraa tɩchɛ baa kul taa sɛrɛ ɛ, ka Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ vɛ̃ ka wʊ tara pʊɔ.

eng: pleading with him, Lord, my servant is flat on his back at home, paralyzed, and his suffering is awful.
local: N Soro, a n tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ lɛ gã a yir ba tʊɔrɔ ire ɛ, na wʊ wõnõ a ãgãn tuo.

eng: The Human One is Lord of the Sabbath.
local: A maa Nɩsaal Bie lɛ ɩ a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Soro.

eng: Whatever city or village you go into, find somebody in it who is worthy and stay there until you go on your way.
local: Yɩn wa kpɛ tẽe jaa pɔ, yɩ sʊʊr kaa nɩrɛ 'lɔ nɩ na de yɩ, yɩ kpɩɛrɛ a wʊ 'yɛr na yɩ wa tɩ yɩn a be.

eng: Their worship of me is empty since they teach instructions that are human rules .
local: A ba puorfʊ ba tara tɔ̃nɔ̃ kʊ ma ɛ, nɩsaal wiilfʊ lɛ ba wile. .

eng: Youll receive the same judgment you give. Whatever you deal out will be dealt out to you.
local: Nãaŋmɩn na tɔɔr a yɩ 'yɛr nɩtãa lɛ yɩn tɔɔr a yɩ tɔ sʊɔ 'yɛr. Lɛ fʊn man tɩr, lɛ mɩ lɛ ban man kʊ fʊ.

eng: Stop collecting treasures for your own benefit on earth, where moth and rust eat them and where thieves break in and steal them.
local: Yɩ ta bine a yɩ nãbonsɩ ka a wɛr ju be kpolo, nɩ sɔrɔ, nɩ na sɔ̃ɔ bar ɛ, nɩ be nãnyisɩ nɩ naa kpɛ ju ɛ.

eng: When Jesus heard it, he said, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do.
local: Yesu wõn a lɛ ban 'yɛr, ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Nɩrɛ na kpɛ̃mɛ ba chiine tɩtɩlɛ jie ɛ, tɩchɛ baalaa lɛ ma chen.

eng: He is the one of whom it is written: Look, Im sending my messenger before you, who will prepare your way before you .
local: A nyã yele lɛ sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka ɩn tʊ̃ naa a n tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ ka wʊ de a fʊ nie na wʊ tɩ chɔɔr a fʊ sɔr kʊ fʊ.

eng: When they saw the star, they were filled with joy.
local: Ban wa nyɛ ka a ŋmɛrbir ara nʊ̃ɔ kpɛ ban yɔɔ jaa.

eng: Joseph her husband was a righteous man. Because he didnt want to humiliate her, he decided to call off their engagement quietly.
local: A wʊ baraa Josefʊ na ɩ nɩtor jũu, ba bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ su wʊ vĩ nɩyɔɔ pɔ ɛ, ka wʊ tɩɛr ɩka wʊ sɔɔl bar wʊ jaa jomm.

eng: Jesus left that place and went into their synagogue.
local: Yesu yin a be chen a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir pɔ.

eng: A man with a withered hand was there. Wanting to bring charges against Jesus, they asked, Does the Law allow a person to heal on the Sabbath.
local: tɩ nyɛ daba kɔ̃ɔ nũu na kpi. Ka ba sʊʊr wʊ, A tara na sɔr ɩka fʊ sãa baal a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar ɔ? Ba sʊʊr a lɛ bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba nyɛ a wʊ pɔ 'yɛr 'yɛr.

eng: Jesus replied, Who among you has a sheep that falls into a pit on the Sabbath and will not take hold of it and pull it out?.
local: Yesu sʊʊr naa, Ala ɩ a yɩ ãsʊɔ tara na pɛr ka wʊ lo bulo pɔ a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar, ŋmɛ lɛ fʊn ɩ? A ba ɩ fʊn ir wʊn a bɔɔ pɔ ɛ?.

eng: How much more valuable is a person than a sheep! So the Law allows a person to do what is good on the Sabbath.
local: A nɩsaal gar pɛr. A lɛ jũu a sɩ nɛbinãa bar sɔr ɩka fʊ maal vɩla kʊ nɩrɛ a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar.

eng: Then Jesus said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did and it was made healthy, just like the other one.
local: Wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a daba 'lɔ nũu na kpi, Tur a fʊ nũu. A wʊ tur ka a nũu wʊ jaa sa kaara a nũu nyã sʊɔ kaar.

eng: The Pharisees went out and met in order to find a way to destroy Jesus.
local: A Faraseemɩne yin na ba piel guoro a Yesu kʊfʊ.

eng: When you go into a house, say, Peace.
local: Yɩn wa kpɛ a yir, yɩ puor a nɩbɛ.

eng: If the house is worthy, give it your blessing of peace. But if the house isnt worthy, take back your blessing.
local: A yir 'lɔ nɩbɛ wa sɔɔ de yɩ, yɩ vɛ̃ ka a yɩ nyã'maar be a ba sɛ̃, tɩchɛ bala ba wa sɔɔ de yɩ ɛ, yɩ lɩɛb a yɩ nyã'maar.

eng: If anyone refuses to welcome you or listen to your words, shake the dust off your feet as you leave that house or city.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa baa wa sɔɔ de yɩ ɛ bɩɩ chɛl a yɩ 'yɛr ɛ, yɩ kpoor a yɩ gbɛɛ tã-uul bar yɩn wa yire be a tẽe 'lɔ bɩɩ a yir 'lɔ pɔ.

eng: I assure you that it will be more bearable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrah on Judgment Day than it will be for that city.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, Nãaŋmɩn na kãan a Sodom nɩbɛ nɩ a Gomora nɩbɛ nɩbaal a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ bɩbir gar a nɩbɛ bala a tẽe 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ pɔ.

eng: Look, Im sending you as sheep among wolves. Therefore, be wise as snakes and innocent as doves.
local: Yɩ nyɛ, n tʊ̃nɔ̃ yɩ naa fɩka piir na kpɛ jubɛrɛ pɔ kaar, yɩ ɩ yɛ̃n dẽme waba kaar, na yɩ kaara fɩka ŋmama na ba maala dɛɛr ɛ.

eng: Watch out for people—because they will hand you over to councils and they will beat you in their synagogues.
local: Yɩ gu a yɩ mãɛ, ban nyɔɔr yɩn chiin tɩ kʊ a nɩbɛrɛ na ba fɔb yɩ a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ jiir.

eng: They will haul you in front of governors and even kings because of me so that you may give your testimony to them and to the Gentiles.
local: ban fɩr yɩn chiin a paal nɩbɛrɛ jie nɩ a nãmɩne yie a n yuor jũu, ka yɩ na di dãasɩɛ kʊ a bala na ɩ a nɩyor.

eng: Whenever they hand you over, dont worry about how to speak or what you will say, because what you can say will be given to you at that moment.
local: Ba la wa nyɔɔr yɩ chiin a 'yɛr tɔɔr fʊ jie, yɩ ta vɛ̃ ka a wõnõ yɩ a lɛ yɩn tɩ 'yɛrɛ ɛ nɩ a lɛ yɩn na 'yɛr ɛ; bon 'lɔ yɩn na 'yɛr Nãaŋmɩn na wiil yɩ naa a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ nɔ̃.

eng: You arent doing the talking, but the Spirit of my Father is doing the talking through you.
local: A ba ɩ yɩn lɛ na 'yɛr ɛ tɩchɛ a yɩ Sãa Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na wiil yɩn a lɛ yɩn na 'yɛr.

eng: Brothers and sisters will hand each other over to be executed. A father will turn his child in. Children will defy their parents and have them executed.
local: Yɛbɛ na gar 'yɔ̃ɔ yɛbɛ ka ba kʊ wʊ, ka a sãa mɩ gar 'yɔ̃ɔ a wʊ bie. Bibiir kʊ lɛ wõnõ a ba sãamɩne toor ɛ, na ba vɛ̃ ka ba kʊ ba.

eng: Everyone will hate you on account of my name. But whoever stands firm until the end will be saved.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa na 'laar yɩ naa yɩn sɔɔ ma jũu, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ na sɔɔ ara tɩ ta a baaraa, na nyɛn faafʊ.

eng: Why do you see the splinter thats in your brothers or sisters eye, but dont notice the log in your own eye?.
local: Ŋmɩnɛ a fʊ nyɛ dabile na be a fʊ yɛbɛ nɩbir pɔ tɩ ba chɛlɛ a dakpɛ̃ɛ na be a fʊ̃ʊ nɩbir pɔ ɛ?.

eng: How can you say to your brother or sister, Let me take the splinter out of your eye, when theres a log in your eye?.
local: Bɩɩ ŋmɩnɛ fʊn ɩ 'yɛr kʊ a fʊ yɛbɛ vɛ̃ ka ir a dabile na be a fʊ nɩbir pɔ bar tɩ ba chɛlɛ a dakpɛ̃ɛ na be a fʊ nɩbir pɔ ɛ?.

eng: Jesus knew what they intended to do, so he went away from there. Large crowds followed him, and he healed them all.
local: Yesu na wa bɔ̃ɔn a lɛ ban piel guoro ɩka ba ɩ wʊ, wʊ yin a jie 'lɔ ka nɩyɔɔ jaa turo wʊ. Wʊ sãan a bala an ɔɔrɔ.

eng: They entered the house and saw the child with Mary his mother. Falling to their knees, they honored him. Then they opened their treasure chests and presented him with gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh.
local: Ban kpɛ a yir pɔ, ba nyɛn a bie nɩ a wʊ ma Mɛɛr, na ba gbur dumo puor wʊ. Be lɛ ba tũ a ba wʊʊr pɔ ir kʊfʊsɩ na ɩ sɛlma, nyɩnyɩbɩ kãa nyũu nʊ̃ɔ nɩ kãnyũu ala ban bʊɔlɔ sɩmura kʊ wʊ.

eng: I assure you that no one who has ever been born is greater than John the Baptist. Yet whoever is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka bala jaa pɔɔ na dɔɔ ka a wɛr pɔ, ba ɩ kpɛ̃ɛ gar a Jɔɔn kɔ̃suor ɛ, tɩchɛ 'lɔ mɩ na ɩ a pʊʊr a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ gar wʊ naa.

eng: Whenever they harass you in one city, escape to the next, because I assure that you will not go through all the cities of Israel before the Human One comes.
local: A tẽe 'lɔ nɩbɛ wa gere yɩ, yɩ yi a be fɔɔ chen tẽyuo; yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, yɩ kʊ yɔ a Isɩral tẽn bar ɛ, tɩchɛ ka a Nɩsaal Bie wa ɛ.

eng: Instead, collect treasures for yourselves in heaven, where moth and rust dont eat them and where thieves dont break in and steal them.
local: Tɩchɛ yɩ bʊɔrɔ nãbonsɩ bine a saaju, be lɛ a kpolo nɩ sɔrɔ na kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ sɔ̃ɔ bar ɛ, nɩ be a nãnyisɩ na kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ kpɛ ju ɛ.

eng: Jesus responded, Ill come and heal him.
local: Ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Ɩ̃n wa sa naa a wʊ baal kʊ wʊ.

eng: Jesus answered, Allow me to be baptized now. This is necessary to fulfill all righteousness. So John agreed to baptize Jesus.
local: Tɩchɛ ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Vɛ̃ ka a ɩ a lɛ a pʊ̃pãanyã, a vɩɛl na ɩka sɩ ɩ a lɛ jaa Nãaŋmɩn na bʊɔrɔ. Ka Jɔɔn sɔɔ lɛ a Yesu na 'yɛr.

eng: But he ordered them not to spread the word about him.
local: Tɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba ɩka ba ta 'yɛr a wʊ yele kʊ nɩrɛ jaa ɛ.

eng: Disciples arent greater than their teacher, and slaves arent greater than their master.
local: Jãjãna ba gar a wʊ wiwile ɛ bɩɩ ka gbãgbaa gar a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ ɛ.

eng: Because they were warned in a dream not to return to Herod, they went back to their own country by another route.
local: Tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn dɔ̃ɔ kpãa ba a jãn pɔ, ɩka ba ta lɛ lɩɛb chen a Nãa Hɛrɔd sɛ̃ ɛ, ka ba tu jiyuo kul a ba tẽe.

eng: so that what was spoken through Isaiah the prophet might be fulfilled.
local: Wʊ maal a nɩtɔ̃, ka a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr tu a 'yɛ'yɛrɛ Isaiya na ɩ yelmãɛ.

eng: As he was scattering seed, some fell on the path, and birds came and ate it.
local: Wʊ tɩ yaara na yʊɔrɔ nɩ ka amɩne lo sɔr pɔ, ka lubil tuur di.

eng: Look, my Servant whom I chose, the one I love, in whom I find great pleasure . Ill put my Spirit upon him, and hell announce judgment to the Gentiles.
local: A n tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ lɛ a nyã ɩn kaa ir, a 'lɔ n na nʊ̃, a 'lɔ a n pʊɔ na pɛlʊ. Ɩn yɔ̃ɔ wʊn a n Sɩɛ, ka wʊ mʊɔl a n 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ yele kʊ a nɩbɛ ba jaa.

eng: From the days of John the Baptist until now the kingdom of heaven is violently attacked as violent people seize it.
local: An yi a Jɔɔn Kɔ̃suoro sɔ̃ɔ wa tɩ tãn a dɩna a Nãaŋmɩn Nãalʊ̃ʊ nʊ̃ɔnɔ̃ naa ka a kpãɛn dẽme sɔ̃ɔ wʊ.

eng: But the centurion replied, Lord, I dont deserve to have you come under my roof. Just say the word and my servant will be healed.
local: Ka a sojasɩ nɩdiere 'yɛr, N Soro n ba sɛɛ ɩka fʊ wa a n yir ɛ, 'yɛr a 'yɛrbir tɛɛ, a n tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ na sa naa.

eng: Jesus called the crowd near and said to them, Listen and understand.
local: Yesu bʊɔl a nɩyɔɔ a ba wa a wʊ sɛ̃, ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ chɛlɛ na yɩ bɔ̃ɔ a par.

eng: Go and learn what this means: I want mercy and not sacrifice . I didnt come to call righteous people, but sinners.
local: Tɩchɛ yɩ kul tɩ tɩɛr a yele nyã kaa, N bʊɔrɔ na nɩbaal a ba ɩ dʊ̃n bɔɔr lofʊ ɛ. N ba wa ɩka n wa bʊɔl a bala na tor ɛ, tɩchɛ n wan ɩka n wa bʊɔl a yelbe-ɩrbɛ.

eng: Other seed fell on rocky ground where the soil was shallow. They sprouted immediately because the soil wasnt deep.
local: A bʊ̃bie amɩne mɩ lon kʊsɩr pãn ju naa bul tɩ kpi bojũu tãn ba waa ɛ.

eng: As he was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream and said, Joseph son of David, dont be afraid to take Mary as your wife, because the child she carries was conceived by the Holy Spirit.
local: Wʊn wa tɩɛr a yele nyã maalfʊ, ka a Nãaŋmɩn malaka wa a wʊ sɛ̃ jãn pɔ wa 'yɛr, Josefʊ, David bie, ta joro dãbãɛ ɩka fʊ de a Mɛɛr ka wʊ ɩ a fʊ pɔɔ ɛ, a bie na be a wʊ pʊɔm yin a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ kpɛ̃ɔ pɔ.

eng: All the Prophets and the Law prophesied until John came.
local: A Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ ba jaa nɩ a nɛbinãa dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ 'yɛr tɩ tãn a Jɔɔn sɔ̃ɔ.

eng: If you are willing to accept it, he is Elijah who is to come.
local: Ala ɩ yɩn sɔɔn de, a anyãna 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a Elaija nɩ naa lɩɛb wa.

eng: When Jesus was baptized, he immediately came up out of the water. Heaven was opened to him, and he saw the Spirit of God coming down like a dove and resting on him.
local: A lɛ wʊn wa so a Yesu a kʊ̃ɔ baar, a wʊ yi a kʊ̃ɔ pɔ, a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ wʊ nyɛ a saaju na yuo a wʊ nyɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na kaara ŋmam kaar suu wa mar a Yesu ju.

eng: He wont argue or shout, and nobody will hear his voice in the streets.
local: Wʊ kʊ tara nɛchɩrɛ bɩɩ ŋmiere chɛlsɩ bɩɩ pɔɔrɔ gɔ̃mɔ̃ yõo ɛ.

eng: Where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
local: A jie 'lɔ a fʊ nãbonsɩ na be, be lɛ a fʊ tɩɛr mɩ ma be.

eng: Its enough for disciples to be like their teacher and slaves like their master. If they have called the head of the house Beelzebul, its certain that they will call the members of his household by even worse names.
local: A sɛɛn ɩka a jãjãna wʊ nyɛtãan a wʊ wiwile, ka a gbãgbaa mɩ nyɛtãan a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ. Ɩ ba bʊɔlɔ na a yir sʊɔ Beeljebul, yodɛɛr buor lɛ ban pãa bʊɔlɔ a yir dẽme!.

eng: You deceive yourself! First take the log out of your eye, and then youll see clearly to take the splinter out of your brothers or sisters eye.
local: Yɩn nɛɛr ayi dẽme, de nie ir a dakpɛ̃ɛ a fʊ nɩbir pɔ bar na fʊ pãa nyɛrɛ tʊ̃ɔ ir a dabile a fʊ yɛbɛ nɩbir pɔ mɩ bar.

eng: At that time Johns disciples came and asked Jesus, Why do we and the Pharisees frequently fast, but your disciples never fast.
local: Ka a Jɔɔn poturbo wa a wʊ sɛ̃ wa sʊrɔ, Ŋmɩnɛ a sɩn nɩ a Faraseemɩne ma gal kɔ̃ tɩchɛ ka a fʊ poturbo 'lɔ maa gal kɔ̃ ɛ.

eng: Its not what goes into the mouth that contaminates a person in Gods sight. Its what comes out of the mouth that contaminates the person.
local: A ba ɩ bon 'lɔ na ma tu a nɩsaal nɛɛ pɔ kpɛ lɛ ma sɔ̃ɔ wʊ ɛ, tɩchɛ bon 'lɔ na ma yɩ a nɩsaal nɛɛ pɔ lɛ ma sɔ̃ɔ wʊ.

eng: She will give birth to a son, and you will call him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.
local: Wʊn dɔɔn dabile ka fʊ na bʊɔlɔ a wʊ yuor Yesu, 'lɔ lɛ na faa a wʊ nɩbɛ yin a ba yeldɛbɛ pɔ.

eng: Now all of this took place so that what the Lord had spoken through the prophet would be fulfilled.
local: Anyã a jaa ɩ naa ka a wiil a sɩ Soro 'yɛrbie wʊn dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr tu a wʊ 'yɛ'yɛrɛ pɔ ɩ yelmãɛ.

eng: When the magi had departed, an angel from the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, Get up. Take the child and his mother and escape to Egypt. Stay there until I tell you, for Herod will soon search for the child in order to kill him.
local: A yɛ̃ bɔ̃ɔnbɔ na wa chen baar, a Nãaŋmɩn malaka wan Josefʊ sɛ̃ jãn pɔ wa 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Vaa ir 'mɔɔ a bie nɩ a wʊ ma, na fʊ jo chiin be Ijipitɩ wɛr pɔ, na fʊ tɩ be a be wa tɩ tãn a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ ɩn na 'yɛr kʊ fʊ, bojũu a Hɛrɔd nɩ a wʊ nɩbɛ bʊɔrɔ na a bie kʊfʊ.

eng: A voice from heaven said, This is my Son whom I dearly love; I find happiness in him.
local: Tɩ a kɔkɔr yɩ a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ a saaju 'yɛrɛ, A anyãna lɛ ɩ a n binʊ̃nãa ka a n 'yɔ̃ɔ ma 'maa nɩ wʊ.

eng: He wont break a bent stalk, and he wont snuff out a smoldering wick, until he makes justice win.
local: Wʊn siir wʊ mãɛ kʊ a bala na siir a ba mãɛ, tɩ na wʊ maal vɩla kʊ a bala na ba tara sʊ̃nɔ̃ ɛ. Wʊn ara na kpɛ̃ɔ tɩ tãn a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ wʊn na vɛ̃ ka a nɩbɛ sɔɔ de a Nãaŋmɩn yeltorsɩ.

eng: Im a man under authority, with soldiers under me. I say to one, Go, and he goes, and to another, Come, and he comes. I say to my servant, Do this, and the servant does it.
local: A maa mãɛ tara na nɩbɛrɛ ɩn joro dãbãɛ, naa mɩ tara a sojasɩ ka ba mɩ be a n pĩlem, maa 'yɛr kʊ nyã ɩka wʊ, chen, wʊ ma chen naa, bɩɩ ãsʊɔ ɩka, wa, wʊ ma wa naa, bɩɩ ka n 'yɛr kʊ a n gbãgbaa, maal nyã, wʊ ma maal naa.

eng: Therefore, dont be afraid of those people because nothing is hidden that wont be revealed, and nothing secret that wont be brought out into the open.
local: Yɩ ta joro nɩbɛ dãbãɛ ɛ, bon jaa ba be be pɔɔ ka ba kʊ̃ yuo ɛ, bɩɩ bon jaa ba be be sɔɔl ka ba kʊ̃ bɔ̃ɔn ɛ.

eng: But when the sun came up, it scorched the plants, and they dried up because they had no roots.
local: Tɩchɛ a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ na wa nyãa, a bʊ̃bulo jʊɔl naa kpi, bojũu a ba ba nyɩbɛ ɛ.

eng: The eye is the lamp of the body. Therefore, if your eye is healthy, your whole body will be full of light.
local: A fʊ nɩbir lɛ ɩ a fʊ ãgãn chãa. A fʊ nɩbir wa ɩ a fʊ ãgãn wʊ jaa ma sɛɛn na chãa.

eng: Dont give holy things to dogs, and dont throw your pearls in front of pigs. They will stomp on the pearls, then turn around and attack you.
local: Ta de bon na ɩ chɛchɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ baar ɛ, bɩɩ lɔb a fʊ bʊ̃vɩɛlsɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ dor ɛ, ban nɛb na nɩ gbɛɛ tɩ lɩɛb nyɔɔ fʊ.

eng: Jesus responded, The wedding guests cant mourn while the groom is still with them, can they? But the days will come when the groom will be taken away from them, and then theyll fast.
local: Ka a Yesu sʊʊr ba, Fʊn tʊ̃ɔ maal a bala ban bʊɔl a pɔɔ paalaa jie ka ba tara pʊsɔ̃ɔ a pɔɔ paalaa sɩrɛ na chɛnɛ be a ba sɛ̃ ɔ, ban tʊ̃ɔ naa? Sɔ̃ɔ waara naa ka a nɩbɛ na wa nyɔɔ ma chiin, a ber ala pɔ lɛ a n poturbo pʊɔ na sɔ̃ɔ ka ba gal kɔ̃.

eng: Then the disciples came and said to him, Do you know that the Pharisees were offended by what you just said.
local: A poturbo wa 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Fʊ bɔ̃ɔn naa an kpɛ a Faraseemɩne ban wõ a 'yɛr nyã fʊn 'yɛr ɔ.

eng: Let the person who has ears, hear.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na tara toor wʊ chɛlɛ.

eng: Look! A virgin will become pregnant and give birth to a son, And they will call him, Emmanuel.
local: Nyɛ a pɔɔsarbile na tara na pʊɔ dɔɔ dabile, ban 'yɔ̃ɔn a wʊ yuor Emanuwɛl, a par lɛ, Nãaŋmɩn ben a sɩ sɛ̃.

eng: Joseph got up and, during the night, took the child and his mother to Egypt.
local: Ka a Josefʊ ir 'mɔɔ a bie nɩ a wʊ ma a tãsɔɔ bãɩn jo chiin be a Ijipitɩ.

eng: What I say to you in the darkness, tell in the light; and what you hear whispered, announce from the rooftops.
local: Bon 'lɔ n 'yɛr kʊ yɩ libe pɔ yɩ 'yɛr wʊ chãa pɔ, bon 'lɔ yɩn wʊ ka ba bʊl 'yɛr yɔ̃ɔ pʊɔm 'yɛr mʊɔl wʊ a gaɩ jur.

eng: And the Gentiles will put their hope in his name.
local: A wʊ yuor pɔ lɛ a nɩbɛ ba jaa na tara tɩɛrtɔ fʊ jie.

eng: When Jesus heard this, he was impressed and said to the people following him, I say to you with all seriousness that even in Israel I havent found faith like this.
local: Yesu na wa wõ a nɩtɔ̃, nɛɛ 'maa wʊ naa ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a bala na turo wʊ, Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ. A Isɩral nɩbɛ pɔ gba, n ba nyɛ a sɔɔfʊ nyã tɔ ɛ.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorny plants grew and choked them.
local: Ka amɩne mɩ lo gʊʊr pɔ 'mataa bul ka a gʊʊr nʊ̃ɔ fãɩn ba.

eng: Ask, and you will receive. Search, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened to you.
local: Yɩ sʊɔr, Nãaŋmɩn na kʊ yɩ naa. Yɩ yɔbɔ, yɩn nyɛ naa, yɩ kpoor a pãn, wʊn yuo naa.

eng: Other seed fell on good soil and bore fruit, in one case a yield of one hundred to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of thirty to one.
local: Tɩchɛ amɩne mɩ lon kɔjɔ̃ pɔ bul dɔɔ jur kɔbaa, amɩne lɩjaata amɩne lɩjɛr nɩ pie.

eng: But if your eye is bad, your whole body will be full of darkness. If then the light in you is darkness, how terrible that darkness will be!.
local: Tɩchɛ a fʊ nɩbir ba wa ɩ ɛ, a fʊ ãgãn wʊ jaa ma ɩn libe. Ala ɩ a chãa 'lɔ na be a fʊ pɔ ɩn libe, nyɛ lɛ a libe na jã.

eng: Jesus replied, Every plant that my heavenly Father didnt plant will be pulled up.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr, Tɩɛ 'lɔ jaa a n Sãa na be a saaju na ba sɛl ɛ, ɩn vʊ̃ɔ wʊn bar.

eng: When Joseph woke up, he did just as an angel from God commanded and took Mary as his wife.
local: Josefʊ na wa jɛɔ a gʊ̃ɔ pɔ, wʊ ɩn a lɛ jaa a Nãaŋmɩn malaka na 'yɛr ɩka wʊ ɩ. Ka wʊ de a Mɛɛr ka wʊ ɩ a wʊ pɔɔ.

eng: I say to you that there are many who will come from east and west and sit down to eat with Abraham and Isaac and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven.
local: N 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka nɩyɔɔ na yin jie jaa wa na ba wa lɔ̃ɔ din a Abraham nɩ a Isaakɩ nɩ a Jekɔb a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.

eng: For everyone who asks, receives. Whoever seeks, finds. And to everyone who knocks, the door is opened.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na sʊɔr na nyɛ naa, ka a 'lɔ mɩ na yɔbɔ nyɛ, ka a pãn mɩ yuo kʊ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na kpoor.

eng: Dont be afraid of those who kill the body but cant kill the soul. Instead, be afraid of the one who can destroy both body and soul in hell.
local: Yɩ ta joro a bala na kʊ a ãgãn tɩ kʊ tʊ̃ɔ kʊ a sɩɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ yɩ joro a 'lɔ nɩ na tʊ̃ɔ kʊ a sɩɛ nɩ a ãgãn a bũu dagel 'lɔ pɔ.

eng: Everyone who has ears should pay attention.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na tara toor wʊ chɛlɛ.

eng: No one can serve two masters. Either you will hate the one and love the other, or you will be loyal to the one and have contempt for the other. You cannot serve God and wealth.
local: Nɩrɛ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ tʊ̃ nɩbɛrɛ ayi tʊ̃mɔ̃ ɛ. Wʊn nʊ̃n ãsʊɔ, tɩ 'laar ãsʊɔ, bɩɩ wõnõ a ãsʊɔ toor, tɩ ba wõnõ ãsʊɔ toor ɛ. Yɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ tʊ̃ kʊ Nãaŋmɩn nɩ libie ɛ.

eng: They brought to Jesus a demon-possessed man who was blind and unable to speak. Jesus healed him so that he could both speak and see.
local: Ba waan na daba na ɩ jɔ̃ɔ tɩ ɩ wõo na tara sɩdɛbɛ Yesu sɛ̃. Wʊ sãn a daba baal, ka wʊ nyɛrɛ tɩ lɛ 'yɛrɛ jaa chɛchɛ.

eng: Leave the Pharisees alone. They are blind people who are guides to blind people. But if a blind person leads another blind person, they will both fall into a ditch.
local: Yɩ bar ba, jɔ̃n lɛ tɔɔrɔ jɔ̃n. Jɔ̃ɔ wa tɔɔrɔ a wʊ jɔ̃ɔ tɔ sʊɔ ba jaa ma lon bɔɔ pɔ.

eng: To what will I compare this generation? It is like a child sitting in the marketplaces calling out to others.
local: Tɩchɛ bo lɛ ɩn de a dɩna nɩbɛ man? Ba nyɛntãan bibiir na ma jãnɛ a ba dãɛn jiir na ba bʊɔlɔ taa.

eng: Arent two sparrows sold for a small coin? But not one of them will fall to the ground without your Father knowing about it already.
local: Ba ma dãn tiitasɩ ayi kobsɩ ayi. Tɩchɛ ãsʊɔ kʊ lo tẽe ɛ, ala baa ɩ a yɩ Sãa Nãaŋmɩn bɔfʊ lɛ ɛ.

eng: He stayed there until Herod died. This fulfilled what the Lord had spoken through the prophet: I have called my son out of Egypt .
local: Be lɛ wʊ tɩ jãnɛ tɩ tãn a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ a Nãa Hɛrɔd na wa kpi. A yelsɩ anyã ɩ naa lɛ ka a na ɩ yelmãɛ ɩka a lɛ a sɩ Soro 'yɛ'yɛrɛ na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr ɩka, Ijipitɩ pɔ lɛ ɩn bʊɔl a n bie yin.

eng: But he didnt have sexual relations with her until she gave birth to a son. Joseph called him Jesus.
local: tɩchɛ baa lɔɔ sɔ̃ɔ ɛ, tɩ tãn a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ wʊn dɔɔ a dabile, na wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ a wʊ yuor Yesu.

eng: Jesus disciples came and said to him, Why do you use parables when you speak to the crowds.
local: A poturbo wan a wʊ sɛ̃ wa sʊʊr, Bo lɛ so fʊn ma lɔɔrɔ a sʊkpar kʊrɔ ba.

eng: We played the flute for you and you didnt dance. We sang a funeral song and you didnt mourn.
local: sɩ pɛɛl a ɩlɛ tɩ yaa sɛɛrɛ bãnɛ ɛ, na sɩ kõnõ kõyiel yaa ŋmara nɩbikʊ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: Who among you will give your children a stone when they ask for bread?.
local: A yɩn na ɩ a sãamɩne, buor sʊɔ bie lɛ na sʊɔr a wʊ sãa bʊ̃diir, ka wʊ de kʊsɩr kʊ wʊ?.

eng: No one sews a piece of new, unshrunk cloth on old clothes because the patch tears away the cloth and makes a worse tear.
local: Nɩrɛ maa ŋmaa fɔpaalaa sɛ choo fɔkoraa pɔ ɛ, 'lɔ be ɩ a lɛ a fɔpaalaa na tɔɔn a fɔkoraa chɩɛr. Ka a chɩɛrfʊ na ɩ dɛɛr.

eng: When Herod knew the magi had fooled him, he grew very angry. He sent soldiers to kill all the children in Bethlehem and in all the surrounding territory who were two years old and younger, according to the time that he had learned from the magi.
local: A Nãa Hɛrɔd na wa bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a yɛ̃ dẽme banyã na yɩ a sapar bɛl wʊ naa a suur kpɛ wʊ a wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ sojasɩ ɩka ba chen tɩ kʊ dabile jaa na be a Bɛtɩlɛhɛm na ta yuom ayi bɩɩ na ba ta sɛrɛ ɛ, yi a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ jaa wʊn sʊʊr bɔ̃ɔn yi a yɛ̃ bɔ̃ɔnbɔ sɛ̃.

eng: All the crowds were amazed and said, This man couldnt be the Son of David, could he.
local: Nɛɛr 'maan a nɩyɔɔ ka ba sʊrɔ taa ɩka, kaa 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a Nãa David Bie bɩɩ?.

eng: Even the hairs of your head are all counted.
local: Nãaŋmɩn sɔr a yɩ jukɔɔl a jaa bɔ̃ɔ.

eng: Then Peter spoke up, Explain this riddle to us.
local: Tɩchɛ ka Pita 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Wiil a sʊkpa nyã par kʊ sɩ.
