eng: Therefore, I say to you, dont worry about your life, what youll eat or what youll drink, or about your body, what youll wear. Isnt life more than food and the body more than clothes?.
local: A lɛ jũu, n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, yɩ ta vɛ̃ ka a yɩ nyɛvʊr yele wõnõ yɩ ka yɩ 'yɛrɛ ɩka, bo lɛ yɩn di, bɩɩ bo lɛ yɩn nyũ ɛ. Bɩɩ waala ɩka, bo lɛ yɩn su pɔɔ a ãgãn ɛ. A nyɛvʊr ba fɛr gar a bʊ̃diir ɛ, ka a ãgãn mɩ fɛr gar a bʊ̃sursɩ ɛ?.

eng: But the children of the kingdom will be thrown outside into the darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: tɩchɛ a bala na so a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ, wʊn lɔb ban bar yõo a jilibe pɔ, be lɛ ban kõnõ ɔɔrɔ nɩ nyɩmɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Because they havent received the secrets of the kingdom of heaven, but you have.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩn 'lɔ ɩn vɛ̃ naa ka yɩ bɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ yelsɔɔlsɩ tɩchɛ a bala na chɛ sʊkpar lɛ n lɔɔrɔ kʊrɔ ba.

eng: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, He has a demon.
local: A Jɔɔn na wa, waa dire ɛ, na waa lɛ nyuro go ɛ, ka ba 'yɛr ɩka sɩdɛɛr lɛ wʊ ɩ.

eng: Yet the Human One came eating and drinking, and they say, Look, a glutton and a drunk, a friend of tax collectors and sinners. But wisdom is proved to be right by her works.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Nɩsaal Bie wa na wʊ ma dire tɩ nyuro ka ba 'yɛr ɩka di dire nɩ dãburo lɛ wʊ ɩ, na wʊ ɩ a lõpodierbe nɩ a yelbe-ɩrbɛ baraa. A ma wiil a lɛ Nãaŋmɩn na tor, bala wa nyɛ lɛ a wʊ nɩbɛ na kpɩɛrɛ nɩ yɛ̃.

eng: Then he began to scold the cities where he had done his greatest miracles because they didnt change their hearts and lives.
local: Yesu pʊɔ sɔ̃ɔn naa a tẽn bala nɩbɛ a be a wʊ nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ na tʊ̃, tɩchɛ ka baa sɔɔ de wʊ ɛ.

eng: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. If they did, the wineskins would burst, the wine would spill, and the wineskins would be ruined. Instead, people pour new wine into new wineskins so that both are kept safe.
local: Bɩɩ nɩrɛ ma den dãbãɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ dãa wɔkoraa pɔ ɛ, 'lɔ be ɩ a lɛ a dãbãɩn na pur naa a wʊɔ ka a dãa kpaar ka a wʊɔ mɩ sɔ̃ɔ. Ba ma den dãbãɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ a dãa wɔpaalaa pɔ, ka a ta sɔ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: How terrible it will be for you, Chorazin! How terrible it will be for you, Bethsaida! For if the miracles done among you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have changed their hearts and lives and put on funeral clothes and ashes a long time ago.
local: Nɛbier ben a yɩn Korajin dẽme sɛ̃. Nɛbier mɩ ben a yɩn Betɩsaida dẽme sɛ̃. A a ɩ a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ ala ɩn tʊ̃ a yɩ jie, mɩ ta tʊ̃n be a lɛ a Tire nɩ a Sidon tẽn pɔ, ban taa lɩɛb a ba tɩɛr na ba su bʊ̃jarsɩ jɛn tãpɩɛl pɔ.

eng: When the Pharisees heard, they said, This man throws out demons only by the authority of Beelzebul, the ruler of the demons.
local: Tɩchɛ a Faraseemɩne na wa wõ a lɛ ba 'yɛr naa, Wʊ dire na sɩdɛbɛ ka a yire bojũu, a sɩdɛbɛ nãa Beeljebul lɛ kʊ wʊ a kpɛ̃ɔ ka wʊ dire nɩ a sɩdɛbɛ.

eng: Jesus said, Dont you understand yet?.
local: Be lɛ wʊ 'yɛr, A yɩn mɩ na ɩ a n poturbo ba bɔ̃ɔ a par ɛ?.

eng: Jesus said to the centurion, Go; it will be done for you just as you have believed. And his servant was healed that very moment.
local: Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a sojasɩ nɩdiere, Kulo, a ɩ a lɛ kʊ fʊ, fʊn tara a sɔɔfʊ jũu. A tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ sa naa a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ pɔ.

eng: While Jesus was speaking to them, a ruler came and knelt in front of him, saying, My daughter has just died. But come and place your hand on her, and shell live.
local: A Yesu na chɛnɛ 'yɛrɛ a anyãna kʊrɔ ba, a lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir nɩdiere wa gbur dumo a wʊ niem tɩ 'yɛr, A n bipɔɔ lɛ kpi ajienaa, wa lɩɛb a fʊ nuur sɩɩr wʊ tɩ wʊn sa naa.

eng: This fulfilled the word spoken through Jeremiah the prophet.
local: A lɛ jaa a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ Jeremiya na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr ɩ yelmãɛ.

eng: A voice was heard in Ramah, weeping and much grieving. Rachel weeping for her children, and she did not want to be comforted, because they were no more.
local: Ba wõn kɔkɔr be a Wurama tẽe pɔ, kõyiel nɩ wuur kpãɛn, Wurachɛl lɛ kõnõ a wʊ bibiir 'yɔ̃ɔ. Ba kpi na, waa lɛ bʊɔrɔ 'maalfʊ ɛ bojũu baa lɛ chɛnɛ go ɛ.

eng: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt.
local: Hɛrɔd na wa kpi, Nãaŋmɩn malaka wan Josefʊ sɛ̃ be a Ijipitɩ jãn pɔ wa 'yɛr.

eng: Get up, the angel said, and take the child and his mother and go to the land of Israel. Those who were trying to kill the child are dead.
local: Vaa ir 'mɔɔ a bie nɩ a wʊ ma na fʊ chiin a Isɩral wɛr pɔ, a bala na dɔ̃ɔ bʊɔrɔ a bie kʊfʊ kpi naa.

eng: Joseph got up, took the child and his mother, and went to the land of Israel.
local: Josefʊ vaan ir 'mɔɔ a bie nɩ a wʊ ma chiin a Isɩral wɛr pɔ.

eng: But when he heard that Archelaus ruled over Judea in place of his father Herod, Joseph was afraid to go there. Having been warned in a dream, he went to the area of Galilee.
local: Tɩchɛ wʊn wa wõ ɩka Archelaus lɛ di a nãa a Judiya a wʊ sãa kũu puor, wʊ joro na dãbãɛ ɩka wʊ lɛ chen a be. Ka a Nãaŋmɩn wa kpãa wʊ a jãn pɔ ɩka wʊ chen be Galilee paal pɔ.

eng: He settled in a city called Nazareth so that what was spoken through the prophets might be fulfilled: He will be called a Nazarene.
local: Be lɛ wʊ chen Najaretɩ tɩ kpɩɛrɛ a be, ka a lɛ Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr ɩ yelmãɛ, Ban bʊɔl wʊ naa Najaretɩ nɩrɛ.

eng: Jesus went home with Peter and saw Peters mother-in-law lying in bed with a fever.
local: Yesu na kpɛ a Pita yir, wʊ nyɛn a Pita dãɛ ma ãgãn na tɔ kʊ gã.

eng: But I say to you that Tyre and Sidon will be better off on Judgment Day than you.
local: Tɩchɛ n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ daar Nãaŋmɩn na kãan a Tire nɩ a Sidon nɩbɛ nɩbaal gar a yɩn.

eng: Because Jesus knew what they were thinking, he replied, Every kingdom involved in civil war becomes a wasteland. Every city or house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Yesu na bɔ̃ɔn a lɛ ban tɩɛrɛ, a wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Tẽe 'lɔ na ŋma dẽdẽme jɛɛrɛ taa kʊ kor ɛ. Tẽe 'lɔ bɩɩ yir kpɛ̃ɛ 'lɔ na ŋma dẽdẽme jɛɛrɛ taa kʊ ara ɛ.

eng: Look at the birds in the sky. They dont sow seed or harvest grain or gather crops into barns. Yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Arent you worth much more than they are?.
local: Yɩ kaa a lubil na be a saaju. Ba maa bʊr ɛ, bɩɩ ŋma ɛ, bɩɩ tara boor ma bin ɛ, tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn lɛ guolo ba. Yaa fɛr gar a lubil bɩɩ?.

eng: So Jesus and his disciples got up and went with him.
local: Ka a Yesu nɩ a wʊ poturbo ir turo wʊ.

eng: Or give them a snake when they ask for fish?.
local: Bɩɩ ka a bie sʊɔr jãm, ka wʊ de waa kʊ wʊ?.

eng: For those who have will receive more and they will have more than enough. But as for those who dont have, even the little they have will be taken away from them.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na tara Nãaŋmɩn tɩɛr, Nãaŋmɩn na vɛ̃n ka a nɩnyɩnɛ bɔ̃ɔfʊ pʊɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ, tɩchɛ a bala jaa na ba bʊɔrɔ a wʊ bɔ̃ɔfʊ ɛ, Nãaŋmɩn na pɔɔn a ba tɩɛr.

eng: Dont you know that everything that goes into the mouth enters the stomach and goes out into the sewer?.
local: Yaa bɔ̃ɔ ka bon 'lɔ jaa na tu a nɛɛ pɔ kpɛ a pʊɔ pɔ ma tun a par bɔɔ pɔ yi bɩɩ?.

eng: Dont be afraid. You are worth more than many sparrows.
local: A lɛ jũu, yɩ ta joro dãbãɛ ɛ, yɩ tara na tɔ̃nɔ̃ gar a tiitasɩ.

eng: He touched her hand, and the fever left her. Then she got up and served them.
local: Wʊ sɩɩr a pɔɔ nũu, ka a ãgãn tʊl baar a wʊ ir maal bʊ̃diir kʊ wʊ.

eng: If Satan throws out Satan, he is at war with himself. How then can his kingdom endure?.
local: Ala ɩ a Satan diin Satan kʊ yi na wʊ ŋma dẽdẽme ŋmɛ lɛ a wʊ nãalʊ̃ʊ na ɩ ara?.

eng: Who among you by worrying can add a single moment to your life?.
local: A yɩ buor sʊɔ lɛ na waal na wʊ tʊ̃ɔ de bɩbir bʊ̃'yen pʊɔ a wʊ nyɛvʊr pɔ?.

eng: Then a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years came up behind Jesus and touched the hem of his clothes.
local: Ban wa de a sɔr chiine ka pɔɔ na lo sɩɛ suoro faara yuom pie nɩ ayi, wʊ gʊɔr a Yesu puor tɩ sɩɩr a fuu nɛɛ.

eng: If you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your heavenly Father give good things to those who ask him.
local: Ala ɩ a yɩn na ɩ a yelbe-ɩrbɛ bɔ̃ɔn bʊ̃vɩla ma maal kʊ a yɩ bibiir, ŋmɩnɛ lɛ an ɩ ka a yɩ Sãa na be a saaju, kʊ̃ de bʊ̃vɩɛl kʊ a bala na sʊɔr wʊ ɛ?.

eng: But what goes out of the mouth comes from the heart. And thats what contaminates a person in Gods sight.
local: Tɩchɛ bon 'lɔ na ma yi a nɛɛ pɔ, yin a tɩɛr pɔ, anyãna lɛ ma sɔ̃ɔ a nɩsaal.

eng: And if I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul, then by whose authority do your followers throw them out? Therefore, they will be your judges.
local: Ala ɩ Beeljebul lɛ kʊ ma a kpɛ̃ɔ ka dire nɩ a sɩdɛbɛ, tɩ a bala na ɩ a yɩ poturbo, bo lɛ ba tara dire nɩ a sɩdɛbɛ? A lɛ jũu bala lɛ na ɩ a yɩ 'yɛr tɔɔrbɔ.

eng: This is why I speak to the crowds in parables: although they see, they dont really see; and although they hear, they dont really hear or understand.
local: A par nɩ nyã ɩn lɔɔrɔ a sʊkpar kʊrɔ ba, ba ma kaa naa tɩ maa nyɛ ɛ, na ba ma wõ tɩ maa bɔ̃ɔ a par ɛ.

eng: And you, Capernaum, will you be honored by being raised up to heaven? No, you will be thrown down to the place of the dead. After all, if the miracles that were done among you had been done in Sodom, it would still be here today.
local: A yɩn Kapenahum dẽme, ban 'mɔɔ yɩn don saaju bɩɩ? Kai, ban suun yɩn a kpĩisɩ tẽe. Ala ɩ a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ na tʊ̃ a yɩ pɔ mɩ ta tʊ̃n a Sodom pɔ, a tẽe anyãna naa chɛnɛ na wa tɔ dɩna.

eng: And why do you worry about clothes? Notice how the lilies in the field grow. They dont wear themselves out with work, and they dont spin cloth.
local: Ka bo lɛ vɛ̃ ka yɩ tɩɛrɛ a bʊ̃sursɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ? Yɩ kaa nyɛ a mɔpuul lɛ an ma nʊ̃ɔ, ba maa tʊ̃ ɛ, bɩɩ wob fuu su ɛ.

eng: That evening people brought to Jesus many who were demon-possessed. He threw the spirits out with just a word. He healed everyone who was sick.
local: A jãanʊɔr 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ ba waan na nɩyɔɔ a sɩdɛbɛ na tara a Yesu sɛ̃, ka wʊ de a 'yɛrbir diin a sɩdɛbɛ ka a yi tɩ sãa a bala jaa an ɔɔrɔ.

eng: What Isaiah prophesied has become completely true for them: You will hear, to be sure, but never understand; and you will certainly see but never recognize what you are seeing .
local: Bala 'lɔ an ɩn yelmãɛ ɩka a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ Isaiya na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr ɩka, A nɩbɛ banyã na ma wõ naa tɩ ba kʊ̃ bɔ̃ɔ a par ɛ, ban ma kaa naa tɩ ba kʊ̃ nyɛ ɛ.

eng: Therefore, everyone who acknowledges me before people, I also will acknowledge before my Father who is in heaven.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa na wa 'yɛr ɩka wʊ bɔ̃ɔ ma naa a nɩbɛ niem, 'lɔ lɛ n mɩ na 'yɛr ɩka n bɔ̃ɔ naa a n Sãa na be a saaju niem.

eng: She thought, If I only touch his robe Ill be healed.
local: bojũu wʊ tɩɛr a wʊ pʊɔm ɩka 'lɔ sɩɩr a Yesu fuu wʊn sa naa.

eng: Out of the heart come evil thoughts, murders, adultery, sexual sins, thefts, false testimonies, and insults.
local: A nɩsaal tɩɛr pɔ lɛ a tɩɛr dɛbɛ ma yi, nɩrɛ kʊfʊ, pɔɔdeyele, sɛ̃sɛ̃yele, nãnyiyele, jir dãasɩɛ, nɩ a gagaryele ma be.

eng: But if I throw out demons by the power of Gods Spirit, then Gods kingdom has already overtaken you.
local: Ala ɩ Satan diin Satan kʊ yi na wʊ ŋma dẽdẽme ŋmɛ lɛ a wʊ nãalʊ̃ʊ na ɩ ara? Ala ɩ Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ lɛ kʊ ma a kpɛ̃ɔ ka dire nɩ a sɩdɛbɛ ka a yire wiil ɩka a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ ta yɩ naa.

eng: But I say to you that even Solomon in all of his splendor wasnt dressed like one of these.
local: a Nãa Solomon gba nɩ a wʊ tɩɩr wʊ jaa ba dɔ̃ɔ su fuu ka wʊ vɩɛlɛ gar a mɔpuul a nyãna ɛ.

eng: But I say to you that it will be better for the land of Sodom on the Judgment Day than it will be for you.
local: Tɩchɛ n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ daar Nãaŋmɩn na kãan Sodom nɩbɛ nɩbaal gar a yɩn.

eng: Therefore, you should treat people in the same way that you want people to treat you; this is the Law and the Prophets.
local: A lɛ yɩn ma bʊɔrɔ ɩka a nɩbɛ ba ma maala kʊrɔ yɩ, yɩ mɩ ma maala a lɛ ban bʊɔrɔ kʊrɔ ba, a nyã lɛ ɩ a Nɛbinãa nɩ a 'Yɛ'yɛrbɛ Wiilfʊ.

eng: If God dresses grass in the field so beautifully, even though its alive today and tomorrow its thrown into the furnace, wont God do much more for you, you people of weak faith?.
local: Ala ɩ Nãaŋmɩn maal a mɔpuul ka a vɩɛlɛ, tɩchɛ a maa kor ɛ. Nɩbɛ ma chʊm ka a di. A Nãaŋmɩn na ma maal a mɔpuul a nɩtɔ̃, wʊ kaara a yɩn na ɩ a wʊ nɩbɛ gar a mɔpuul anyãna. A yɩ sɔɔfʊ lɛ ba jã bon ɛ.

eng: This happened so that what Isaiah the prophet said would be fulfilled: He is the one who took our illnesses and carried away our diseases.
local: Ka a lɛ jaa a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ Isaiya na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr ɩ yelmãɛ, Ɩka wʊ den a sɩn baalsɩ a jaa bar.

eng: For this peoples senses have become calloused, and theyve become hard of hearing, and theyve shut their eyes so that they wont see with their eyes or hear with their ears or understand with their minds, and change their hearts and lives that I may heal them .
local: A nɩbɛ banyã nyãn dar naa ka a ba tobo pɔɔ baa wõnõ ɛ, na ba ũu a ba nɩbie, ka lɛ ba lɛ ɛ, ban taa kãan naa a ba nɩbie na ba wõn a ba tobo, na ba bɔ̃ɔ a par, na ba lɩɛb wa ka n sãa ba.

eng: These contaminate a person in Gods sight. But eating without washing hands doesnt contaminate in Gods sight.
local: A anyãna lɛ a bonsɩ ala na ma sɔ̃ɔ a nɩsaal tɩchɛ nɩrɛ na din nũdɛbɛ ba sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ wʊ ɛ.

eng: But everyone who denies me before people, I also will deny before my Father who is in heaven.
local: tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ na chiir ma a nɩbɛ niem, 'lɔ lɛ n mɩ na chiir a n Sãa na be a saaju niem.

eng: When Jesus turned and saw her, he said, Be encouraged, daughter. Your faith has healed you. And the woman was healed from that time on.
local: Yesu na lɩɛb nyɛ a pɔɔ wʊ 'yɛr naa, Ta joro dãbãɛ ɛ n yɛpɔɔ, a fʊ sɔɔfʊ sãa fʊ naa. A dẽdẽe daa a pɔɔ jãɩ ŋmaa naa.

eng: Therefore, dont worry and say, What are we going to eat? or What are we going to drink? or What are we going to wear.
local: A lɛ jũu, yɩ ta waala 'yɛrɛ ɩka, bo lɛ yɩn di bɩɩ nyũ ɛ, bɩɩ bo lɛ yɩn su ɛ?.

eng: Can people go into a house that belongs to a strong man and steal his possessions, unless they first tie up the strong man? Then they can rob his house.
local: Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ nɩrɛ na tʊ̃ɔ ɩ kar kpɛ gãdaa yir de a wʊ bonsɩ, 'lɔ ba de nie lũ a gãdaa nyã tɩ de a wʊ bonsɩ ɛ?.

eng: Happy are your eyes because they see. Happy are your ears because they hear.
local: Tɩchɛ yɩn 'lɔ, yɩ ɩn bɔɔsʊ̃n dẽme, a yɩ nɩbie na nyɛ, ka a yɩ tobo mɩ wõ.

eng: At that time Jesus said, I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because youve hidden these things from the wise and intelligent and have shown them to babies.
local: A daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ Yesu 'yɛr naa, N dãn a n Sãa Nãaŋmɩn na ɩ a saaju nɩ a tẽe wʊ jaa Soro, bojũu fʊ sɔɔl a yele anyãna a yɛ̃n dẽme sɛ̃ tɩ ir wiil bibiir.

eng: From there, Jesus went to the regions of Tyre and Sidon.
local: Yesu yin a be chen a Tire paal nɩ Sidon paal pɔ.

eng: A Canaanite woman from those territories came out and shouted, Show me mercy, Son of David. My daughter is suffering terribly from demon possession.
local: Be lɛ a pɔɔ na yi a Kanaan paal pɔ yi piel ŋmiere chɛlsɩ, N Soro kaa a n nɩbaal David Bie, a sɩdɛɛr lɛ tara a n bipɔɔ.

eng: But he didnt respond to her at all. His disciples came and urged him, Send her away; she keeps shouting out after us.
local: Tɩchɛ a Yesu ba 'yɛr 'yɛr jaa kʊ wʊ ɛ. Ka a wʊ poturbo wa 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Dii wʊ bar, wʊ pɔɔrɔ sɩn gɔ̃mɔ̃.

eng: Whoever isnt with me is against me, and whoever doesnt gather with me scatters.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ba be a n sɛ̃ ɛ, tara man nɩ 'yɛr, nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ba pʊɔ ma a sɩ kaal lɔ̃ɔ taa ɛ wʊ ma yɛ̃yɛl bar.

eng: I assure you that many prophets and righteous people wanted to see what you see and hear what you hear, but they didnt.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ yɔɔ nɩ nɩtorsɩ yɔɔ ta bʊɔrɔ na ɩka ba nyɛ bon 'lɔ yɩn nyɛ tɩchɛ baa nyɛ ɛ, na ba ta wõ bon 'lɔ yɩn wõ tɩchɛ baa tʊ̃ɔ wõ ɛ.

eng: Dont think that Ive come to bring peace to the earth. I havent come to bring peace but a sword.
local: Yɩ ta tɩɛr ɩka nyã'maar lɛ n waan a wɛr pɔ ɛ; n ba waan nyã'maar ɛ tɩchɛ ootaa lɛ n waan.

eng: Go in through the narrow gate. The gate that leads to destruction is broad and the road wide, so many people enter through it.
local: Yɩ tu a dɔ̃dɔr fɩɩl pɔ kpɛ, bojũu a dɔ̃dɔr yal sɔr ɩn jaa kɔ̃mɔ̃kɔ̃mɔ̃ tɩ ɩ sɔ̃ɔfʊ sɔr tɩchɛ ka nɩyɔɔ turo.

eng: Ive come to turn a man against his father, a daughter against her mother, and a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law .
local: N wan ɩka n wa vɛ̃ ka Bie nɩ sãa ootaa, ka bipɔɔ oo a ma, pɔɔ na oo naa a wʊ bidaba pɔɔ ka a bidaba pɔɔ mɩ oo a wʊ sɩrɛ ma.

eng: Peoples enemies are members of their own households .
local: Yir dẽme na lɩɛb ɩ dɔ̃dẽme.

eng: Those who love father or mother more than me arent worthy of me. Those who love son or daughter more than me arent worthy of me.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa na nʊ̃ a wʊ sãa bɩɩ a wʊ ma gar ma wʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ ɩ a n poturo ɛ, nɩrɛ jaa mɩ na nʊ̃ a wʊ bie bɩɩ bipɔɔ gar ma, wʊ kʊ tʊ̃ɔ ɩ a n poturo ɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Ive been sent only to the lost sheep, the people of Israel.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr, A piir ala na bɔr a Isɩral nɩbɛ pɔ tɛɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ ba tʊ̃ ma.

eng: Indeed, Father, this brings you happiness.
local: Ɔ̃ɔ, n Sãa lɛ lɛ ɩ a fʊ pʊpɛl ka fʊ maal.

eng: Therefore, I tell you that people will be forgiven for every sin and insult to God. But insulting the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven.
local: A lɛ jũu n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka nɩbɛ na nyɛn yelbier jaa nɩ 'yɛrdɛɛr jaa ban 'yɛr vɛ̃ kʊ fʊ, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ na 'yɛr 'yɛrdɛɛr 'yɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ kʊ nyɛ yelbier vɛ̃ kʊ fʊ ɛ.

eng: Consider then the parable of the farmer.
local: Yɩ pãa chɛlɛ a kʊɔraa sʊkpa par.

eng: When Jesus went into the rulers house, he saw the flute players and the distressed crowd.
local: A Yesu na wa kpɛ a lɔ̃ɔfʊ nɩdiere yir na wʊ nyɛ a ɩɩlpɛɛlbɛ nɩ a nɩyɔɔ na pɔɔrɔ gɔ̃mɔ̃.

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowd, he ordered his disciples to go over to the other side of the lake.
local: Yesu na wa nyɛ a nɩyɔɔ na ŋmaa jilʊ̃ wʊ, ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ poturbo yɩ gɔ̃ɔ a man gɔ̃ɔn.

eng: Gentiles long for all these things. Your heavenly Father knows that you need them.
local: A bala na ba puor ɛ, lɛ de a ba tɩɛr 'yɔ̃ɔ a anyãna pɔ, a yɩ Sãa bɔ̃ɔ naa ɩka yɩ bʊɔrɔ naa a bonsɩ anyãna a jaa.

eng: Those who dont pick up their crosses and follow me arent worthy of me.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ mɩ na ba de a wʊ daa tuo turo nɩ ma ɛ, wʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ ɩ a n poturo ɛ.

eng: But she knelt before him and said, Lord, help me.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a pɔɔ wa gbur dumo a wʊ niem 'yɛr, N Soro, sʊ̃ʊ ma.

eng: And whoever speaks a word against the Human One will be forgiven. But whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven, not in this age or in the age that is coming.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na 'yɛr 'yɛrdɛɛr 'yɔ̃ɔ a maa Nɩsaal Bie na nyɛn yelbier vɛ̃ kʊfʊ, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ na 'yɛr 'yɛrdɛɛr 'yɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ kʊ nyɛ yelbier vɛ̃ kʊ fʊ ɛ. Dɩna nɩ daar kɔ̃ɔ.

eng: But the gate that leads to life is narrow and the road difficult, so few people find it.
local: A dɔ̃dɔr ɩn jaa fɩɩl ka nyɛvʊr sɔr mɩ ɩ jaa kãrakãra ka nɩbɛ bãlãa na tʊ̃ɔ tu.

eng: My Father has handed all things over to me. No one knows the Son except the Father. And nobody knows the Father except the Son and anyone to whom the Son wants to reveal him.
local: N Sãa den a yele a jaa kʊ, nɩrɛ jaa ba bɔ̃ɔ a Bie ɛ, ka a Sãa ba lɛ ɛ, nɩrɛ jaa mɩ ba bɔ̃ɔ a Sãa ɛ, ka a Bie ba lɛ ɛ, nɩ a bala a Nãaŋmɩn Bie na ir, na wiil ban a Sãa.

eng: He said, Go away, because the little girl isnt dead but is asleep; but they laughed at him.
local: ka wʊ 'yɛr, Yɩ tɔɔ chen be a pɔɔbile ba kpi ɛ, wʊ gur naa. Ka ba laara wʊ.

eng: Those who find their lives will lose them, and those who lose their lives because of me will find them.
local: Bala na bʊɔrɔ a ba nyɛvʊr na bɔr wʊ naa tɩchɛ bala mɩ na bɔr a ba nyɛvʊr a maa jũu na nyɛ wʊ naa.

eng: He replied, It is not good to take the childrens bread and toss it to dogs.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, A ba sɛɛ ɩka fʊ de bibiir bʊ̃diir lɔb 'yɔ̃ɔ baar ɛ.

eng: A legal expert came and said to him, Teacher, Ill follow you wherever you go.
local: A nɛɛ wiwiilbe ãsʊɔ tɔɔn ta wʊ tɩ 'yɛr, Wiwile, jie jaa fʊn chiine ɩn turo fʊ naa.

eng: After he had sent the crowd away, Jesus went in and touched her hand, and the little girl rose up.
local: A nɩyɔɔ na wa yɩ a yõo baar, wʊ kpɛ̃n tɩ nyɔɔ a wʊ nũu, ka a pɔɔbile vaa ir.

eng: Whenever people hear the word about the kingdom and dont understand it, the evil one comes and carries off what was planted in their hearts. This is the seed that was sown on the path.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na wõ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ 'yɛr tɩ ba bɔ̃ɔ a par ɛ, a dɛɛr sʊɔ ma wan wa ir a bon 'lɔ na bʊr a wʊ nyãa pɔ, a bʊ̃bie anyã lɛ, a ala na lo a sɔr pɔ.

eng: Instead, desire first and foremost Gods kingdom and Gods righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.
local: Yɩ de nie bɔ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ nɩ a wʊ yeltorsɩ, tɩchɛ a anyãna a jaa, wʊn den pʊɔ yɩ.

eng: Either consider the tree good and its fruit good, or consider the tree rotten and its fruit rotten. A tree is known by its fruit.
local: Ala ɩ tɩɛ vɩɛlɛ na wʊ ma wɔ̃n wɔ̃vɩɛl, ala ɩ tɩɛ ɩn tɩdɛɛr wʊ ma wɔ̃n wɔ̃dɛbɛ. Tɩɛ jaa a wʊ wɔ̃mɔ̃ lɛ ba ma de bɔ̃ɔn wʊ.

eng: Come to me, all you who are struggling hard and carrying heavy loads, and I will give you rest.
local: Yɩ wa a n sɛ̃, yɩn banyã na bal na yɩ tuo tuor ka a wõnõ yɩ, ɩn too yɩn a yɩ pɛ̃n.

eng: Watch out for false prophets. They come to you dressed like sheep, but inside they are vicious wolves.
local: Yɩ bɔ̃ɔn a jire 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ, ba ma bal ba mãɛ piir kaar tɩchɛ ba ɩn mɔdʊ̃ be be na sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃.

eng: News about this spread throughout that whole region.
local: Ka a yele ŋme yaar a paal 'lɔ wʊ jaa.

eng: Jesus replied, Foxes have dens, and the birds in the sky have nests, but the Human One has no place to lay his head.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Mɔbaar tara naa bɔɔr ka lubil mɩ tara choor tɩchɛ maa Nɩsaal Bie ba tara jie na bin a n ju ɛ.

eng: She said, Yes, Lord. But even the dogs eat the crumbs that fall off their masters table.
local: Ka a pɔɔ 'yɛr, Ɔ̃ɔ, n Soro, a baar gba ma dire na a bʊ̃di pʊr pʊr ala na ma ŋmara loro a ba nɩkpɛ̃ɛ dakɔɔ pĩlem.

eng: Those who receive you are also receiving me, and those who receive me are receiving the one who sent me.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na sɔɔ de yɩ, sɔɔn de ma, nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa mɩ na sɔɔ de ma sɔɔn de a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na tʊ̃ ma.

eng: As for the seed that was spread on rocky ground, this refers to people who hear the word and immediately receive it joyfully.
local: Ala mɩ na lo a kʊsɩr pãn ju lɛ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na wõ a 'yɛr sɔɔ de fɔfɔɔ jaa nɩ pʊpɛl.

eng: Because they have no roots, they last for only a little while. When they experience distress or abuse because of the word, they immediately fall away.
local: tɩchɛ a nɩrɛ nyã ba ba nyɩbɛ ɛ, na wʊ di kãnyir ara bãlãa, a lɛ jũu wõm bɩɩ bɛɛr kaa wa pɔɔ wʊ a 'yɛr jũu, a nɩrɛ nyã ma lo naa.

eng: Put on my yoke, and learn from me. Im gentle and humble. And you will find rest for yourselves.
local: Yɩ de a n daa tuo na yɩ jãn yi a n sɛ̃, bojũu n ɩn jomm sʊɔ, naa siir a n mãɛ a n nyãa pɔ, yɩn nyɛn pɛ̃nfʊ kʊ a yɩ sɩɩr.

eng: You will know them by their fruit. Do people get bunches of grapes from thorny weeds, or do they get figs from thistles?.
local: A ba wɔ̃mɔ̃ lɛ yɩn bɔ̃ɔn a lɛ ban be. Ba ma pɔr 'wɔ̃rɔ gʊtɩɛ ju bɩɩ pɔr kɔ̃kɔ̃ɔ nyaartɩɛ ju ɔ?.

eng: As Jesus departed, two blind men followed him, crying out, Show us mercy, Son of David.
local: Yesu na wa yi a be chiine, jɔ̃n ayi turo wʊ naa, na ba gboolo bʊɔlɔ, Kaa sɩ nɩbaal, David Bie.

eng: Children of snakes! How can you speak good things while you are evil? What fills the heart comes out of the mouth.
local: Yɩn waa bibiir! Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ yɩn tʊ̃ɔ 'yɛr 'yɛr vɩɛlsɩ ala ɩ yɩ ɩn yelbe-ɩrbɛ? A ala na sɛɛ a pʊɔ lɛ a nɛɛ ma 'yɛr.

eng: Jesus answered, Woman, you have great faith. It will be just as you wish. And right then her daughter was healed.
local: Ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a pɔɔ, Pɔɔ, a fʊ sɔɔfʊ bɛrma naa! A ɩ kʊ fʊ a lɛ fʊn bʊɔrɔ. A wʊ bipɔɔ sa naa a dẽdẽe daa 'lɔ̃nɔ̃.

eng: Those who receive a prophet as a prophet will receive a prophets reward. Those who receive a righteous person as a righteous person will receive a righteous persons reward.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na sɔɔ de a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ wʊn ɩ Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ jũu na nyɛn a wʊ sãyar. Nɩrɛ jaa mɩ na sɔɔ de nɩrɛ na ɩ nɩtor wʊn ɩ nɩtor jũu na nyɛn a nɩtor sãyar.

eng: Another man, one of his disciples, said to him, Lord, first let me go and bury my father.
local: Ka a wʊ poturo ãsʊɔ mɩ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, N Soro kʊ ma sɔr ka n kul tɩ ũu a n sãa baar.

eng: Therefore, stop worrying about tomorrow, because tomorrow will worry about itself. Each day has enough trouble of its own.
local: A lɛ jũu, yɩ ta waala a bio 'yɔ̃ɔ ɛ, bio lɛ na waala a wʊ mãɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ. Bɩbir jaa tara na a wʊ yelwõnsɩ.

eng: But Jesus said to him, Follow me, and let the dead bury their own dead.
local: Tɩchɛ ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a daba, Turo ma tɩ bar ka a kũn ũno a ba mãɛ kũn.

eng: In the same way, every good tree produces good fruit, and every rotten tree produces bad fruit.
local: Lɛ mɩ lɛ tɩsʊ̃ jaa ma wɔ̃n wɔ̃sʊ̃n ka tɩdɛɛr mɩ ma wɔ̃ wɔ̃dɛbɛ.

eng: Good people bring out good things from their good treasure. But evil people bring out evil things from their evil treasure.
local: A nɩsʊ̃ a yelsʊ̃n ala na be a wʊ pʊɔm lɛ wʊ ma 'yɛr, ka a nɩdɛɛr mɩ ma 'yɛr a yeldɛbɛ ala na be a wʊ pʊɔm.

eng: My yoke is easy to bear, and my burden is light.
local: Bojũu a n daa ɩn kɔ̃mɔ̃kɔ̃mɔ̃, ka yɩn tʊ̃ɔ tuo a tuor ala ɩn na kʊ yɩ.

eng: When Jesus got into a boat, his disciples followed him.
local: Yesu na wa kpɛ a gboro pɔ a wʊ poturbo mɩ tu wʊn kpɛ.

eng: A huge storm arose on the lake so that waves were sloshing over the boat. But Jesus was asleep.
local: Kɔ̃sɛbɛ ir a man pɔ, ka a gboro sɛɛ kʊ̃ɔ ɩrɛ na mʊr tɩchɛ ka a Yesu gã gur.

eng: I assure you that everybody who gives even a cup of cold water to these little ones because they are my disciples will certainly be rewarded.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na kʊ a bʊ̃bile banyãna ãsʊɔ kɔ̃'maar nɩ ŋmele wʊ̃n ɩ a n poturbo jũu, yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka a nɩnyɩnɛ kʊ̃ faa a wʊ sãyar ɛ.

eng: A good tree cant produce bad fruit. And a rotten tree cant produce good fruit.
local: Tɩsʊ̃ ba wɔ̃nɔ̃ wɔ̃dɛbɛ ɛ bɩɩ tɩdɛɛr mɩ ba wɔ̃nɔ̃ wɔ̃sʊ̃n ɛ.

eng: As for the seed that was spread among thorny plants, this refers to those who hear the word, but the worries of this life and the false appeal of wealth choke the word, and it bears no fruit.
local: A bʊ̃bie ala mɩ na lo a gʊʊr pɔ, 'lɔ lɛ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na wõ a 'yɛr tɩ nʊ̃ a wɛr ju nyɛvʊr yelwõnsɩ nɩ a wɛr ju bʊ̃bʊɔrsɩ fãɩn a 'yɛr ka waa dɔɔ bie ɛ.

eng: I tell you that people will have to answer on Judgment Day for every useless word they speak.
local: Yelmãɛ jaa lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ daar, nɩrɛ jaa na man 'yɛrdɛɛr 'lɔ jaa bʊrɔ na dɔ̃ɔ yi a wʊ nɛɛ pɔ.

eng: When he came into the house, the blind men approached him. Jesus said to them, Do you believe I can do this? Yes, Lord, they replied.
local: Wʊn wa kpɛ a yir, a jɔ̃n wan a wʊ sɛ̃, ka Yesu sʊʊr ba, Yɩ sɔɔn de ɩka ɩn tʊ̃ɔ sa a yɩ baal ɔ? Ka ba 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Ɔ̃ɔ, sɩ Soro.

eng: Jesus moved on from there along the shore of the Galilee Sea. He went up a mountain and sat down.
local: Yesu na wa yi a be, wʊ den a Galilee man yal kõkoor 'lana, na wʊ tɩ do tɔ̃ɔ ju jãnɛ.

eng: They came and woke him, saying, Lord, rescue us! Were going to drown.
local: Be lɛ ba chen tɩ sãɩ wʊ na ba 'yɛr, Sɩ Soro faa sɩ, a kʊ̃ɔ dire sɩ naa.

eng: Every tree that doesnt produce good fruit is chopped down and thrown into the fire.
local: Tɩɛ jaa baa wa wɔ̃nɔ̃ wɔ̃sʊ̃n ɛ, ban ŋmaa wʊn lɔb 'yɔ̃ɔ bũu pɔ.

eng: As for what was planted on good soil, this refers to those who hear and understand, and bear fruit and produce—in one case a yield of one hundred to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of thirty to one.
local: Tɩchɛ a bʊ̃bie ala na lo a kɔjɔ̃ pɔ lɛ a nɩrɛ nyã na wõ a 'yɛr bɔ̃ɔ a par, 'lɔ lɛ wɔ̃ wɔ̃mɔ̃ dɔɔ jur, amɩne kɔbaa, amɩne lɩjaata amɩne mɩ lɩjɛr nɩ pie.

eng: By your words you will be either judged innocent or condemned as guilty.
local: A fʊ 'yɛrbie lɛ na wiil a lɛ fʊn be, naa wiil a fʊ sɔ̃ɔnaa.

eng: He said to them, Why are you afraid, you people of weak faith? Then he got up and gave orders to the winds and the lake, and there was a great calm.
local: Ka wʊ sʊʊr ba, Bo lɛ so ka yɩ joro a dãbãɛ? A yɩ sɔɔfʊ lɛ ba jã bon ɛ. Ka a Yesu ir tãn ka a sɛsɛbɛ na ta furo ŋmaa vʊʊr ka a kʊ̃ɔ mɩ na ta ɔɔrɔ lo jaa jomm.

eng: Large crowds came to him, including those who were paralyzed, blind, injured, and unable to speak, and many others. They laid them at his feet, and he healed them.
local: A nɩyɔɔ tara na a nɩbɛ gbɛɛ na kpi kpi, nɩ jɔ̃n, nɩ a gbɛrsɩ, nɩ a wõn 'matãan baalsɩ yɔɔ waan a wʊ sɛ̃. Ba wa bin ban a wʊ niem a wʊ sãa a ba jaa baalsɩ.

eng: Then Jesus touched their eyes and said, It will happen for you just as you have believed.
local: Ka wʊ sɩɩr a ba nɩbie tɩ 'yɛr, A yɩ sɔɔfʊ jũu yɩn nyɛrɛ naa.

eng: Therefore, you will know them by their fruit.
local: A ba wɔ̃mɔ̃ lɛ yɩn de bɔ̃ɔn ba.

eng: At that time some of the legal experts and the Pharisees requested of Jesus, Teacher, we would like to see a sign from you.
local: Be lɛ ka a nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a Faraseemɩne bamɩne 'yɛr, Wiwile, sɩ bʊɔrɔ na ɩka sɩ nyɛ nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ na ɩ jãnɛ a fʊ jie.

eng: Jesus told them another parable: The kingdom of heaven is like someone who planted good seed in his field.
local: Yesu lɛ lɔb a sʊkpa ãsʊɔ kʊ ba, Nɩtɔ̃ lɛ ɩn de a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ man. Nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ lɛ bʊr bʊ̃bivɩɛl a wʊ pʊɔ pɔ.

eng: While people were sleeping, an enemy came and planted weeds among the wheat and went away.
local: nɩrɛ jaa na wa gã gur baar, a dɔ̃sʊɔ wan wa bʊr mɔɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ a chi pɔ tɩ na wʊ kul.

eng: When the stalks sprouted and bore grain, then the weeds also appeared.
local: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ a chi na wa tɩ bulo ka a mɔɩ mɩ bul puro.

eng: The servants of the landowner came and said to him, Master, didnt you plant good seed in your field? Then how is it that it has weeds.
local: A daba tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ wan wa sʊʊr wʊ, Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, a ba ɩ bʊ̃bivɩɛl lɛ fʊ bʊr a pʊɔ pɔ ɛ; tɩ nyɛ a mɔɩ mɩ yi.

eng: An enemy has done this, he answered. The servants said to him, Do you want us to go and gather them.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Dɔ̃sʊɔ lɛ tʊ̃ a lɛ. A tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ sʊʊr wʊ, Fʊ bʊɔrɔ na ɩka sɩ chen tɩ vɔ̃ a mɔɩ bar bɩɩ.

eng: But the landowner said, No, because if you gather the weeds, youll pull up the wheat along with them.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Kai, yɩn be vɔ̃nɔ̃ a mɔɩ, yɩn wa vɔ̃n a chi mɩ bar.

eng: Let both grow side by side until the harvest. And at harvesttime Ill say to the harvesters,First gather the weeds and tie them together in bundles to be burned. But bring the wheat into my barn.
local: Yɩ bar ka a 'mataa nʊ̃ɔ tɩ tãn a ŋmafʊ daar, ɩn 'yɛr kʊ a ŋmaŋmarba ka ba dɔ̃ɔ vɔ̃ a mɔɩ lũ lũ chʊm bar tɩ ŋma a chi 'yɔ̃ɔ a n boor pɔ. .

eng: He told another parable to them: The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed that someone took and planted in his field.
local: Yesu lɛ lɔb a sʊkpa ãsʊɔ kʊ ba go, A Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ kaara naa fɩka kɔ̃kɔ̃ɔ bir nɩrɛ na de bʊr a wʊ pʊɔ pɔ.

eng: Its the smallest of all seeds. But when its grown, its the largest of all vegetable plants. It becomes a tree so that the birds in the sky come and nest in its branches.
local: 'Lɔ lɛ a bʊ̃bie a jaa pɔ bʊ̃bile tɩchɛ 'lɔ wa nʊ̃ɔ, 'lɔ lɛ ma ɩ a tɩɩr a jaa pɔ tɩkpɛ̃ɛ, ka lubil ma 'yɔ̃ɔ choor a wʊ ju.

eng: He told them another parable: The kingdom of heaven is like yeast, which a woman took and hid in a bushel of wheat flour until the yeast had worked its way through all the dough.
local: Wʊ lɛ lɔb a sʊkpa ãsʊɔ kʊ ba, A Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ kaara naa fɩka dãbɩl pɔɔ na de 'yɔ̃ɔ jɔ̃ yɔɔ pɔ ka a ir vʊ̃.

eng: Jesus said all these things to the crowds in parables, and he spoke to them only in parables.
local: Yesu den sʊkpar 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ nɩ a nɩyɔɔ a yelsɩ anyã, wʊ maa 'yɛr 'yɛr kʊ ba ka waa lɔb sʊkpa ɛ.

eng: This was to fulfill what the prophet spoke: Ill speak in parables; Ill declare what has been hidden since the beginning of the world.
local: A nyã lɛ na wiil ɩka a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr ɩn yelmãɛ, Ɩka ɩn ma den a n nɛɛ lɔɔrɔ nɩ sʊkpar naa wiil yɩ yelsɩ ala na sɔɔl a wɛr pielfʊ daar.

eng: Jesus left the crowds and went into the house. His disciples came to him and said, Explain to us the parable of the weeds in the field.
local: Wʊ yin a nɩyɔɔ pɔ kpɛ a yir, ka a wʊ poturbo wa a wʊ sɛ̃ wa 'yɛrɛ, wiil sɩ a mɔɩ na be a pʊɔ pɔ sʊkpa par.

eng: Jesus replied, The one who plants the good seed is the Human One.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, A nɩrɛ 'lɔ na bʊr a bʊ̃bivɩɛl lɛ a maa Nɩsaal Bie.

eng: The field is the world. And the good seeds are the followers of the kingdom. But the weeds are the followers of the evil one.
local: A pʊɔ lɛ ɩ a wɛr. A bʊ̃bivɩɛl lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ bibiir. A mɔɩ lɛ a bala na ɩ a dɛɛr sʊɔ bibiir.

eng: The enemy who planted them is the devil. The harvest is the end of the present age. The harvesters are the angels.
local: A dɔ̃sʊɔ 'lɔ mɩ na bʊr lɛ ɩ a tɩɩ. A ŋmafʊ lɛ ɩ a wɛr baarfʊ daar, a ŋmaŋmarba lɛ ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn malakasɩ.

eng: Just as people gather weeds and burn them in the fire, so it will be at the end of the present age.
local: Lɛ ban na chʊm a mɔɩ bũu pɔ ka a di, lɛ mɩ lɛ na ɩ a wɛr baarfʊ daar.

eng: Not everybody who says to me, Lord, Lord, will get into the kingdom of heaven. Only those who do the will of my Father who is in heaven will enter.
local: A ba ɩ nɩrɛ jaa na bʊɔlɔ ɩka n Soro, n Soro, lɛ na kpɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ nyã na maala a lɛ a n Sãa Nãaŋmɩn na be a saaju na bʊɔrɔ.

eng: The people were amazed and said, What kind of person is this? Even the winds and the lake obey him.
local: Nɛɛ 'maan a nɩbɛ ka ba sʊrɔ taa, nɩ an lɛ a nyã ka a sɛsɛbɛ nɩ a kʊ̃ɔ tu a wʊ nɛɛ?.

eng: But he replied, An evil and unfaithful generation searches for a sign, but it wont receive any sign except Jonahs sign.
local: Tɩchɛ ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, A yɩn banyãna na ɩ a putudẽme nɩ sɛ̃sɛ̃nbɛ tɩ bʊɔrɔ ɩka n tʊ̃ nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ ka a na ɩ jãnɛ, tɩchɛ yɩ kʊ̃ nyɛ ãsʊɔ jaa ɛ ka a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ Jona jãnɛ ba lɛ ɛ.

eng: Their eyes were opened. Then Jesus sternly warned them, Make sure nobody knows about this.
local: Ka a ba nɩbie tɔ̃ɔ yuo yuor. Tɩchɛ ka Yesu kpãkpãan ba ɩka ba taa vɛ̃ ka nɩrɛ jaa bɔ̃ɔn ɛ.

eng: So the crowd was amazed when they saw those who had been unable to speak talking, and the paralyzed cured, and the injured walking, and the blind seeing. And they praised the God of Israel.
local: Nɛɛr 'maan a nɩbɛ ban wõ ka a wõn 'yɛrɛ, ka a gbɛrsɩ sa chiine ka a bala a gbɛɛ nɩ na kpi kpi mɩ sa ka a jɔ̃n mɩ nyɛrɛ. Ka ba dãna a Isɩral nɩbɛ Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: The Human One will send his angels, and they will gather out of his kingdom all things that cause people to fall away and all people who sin.
local: A maa Nɩsaal Bie na tʊ̃n a n malakasɩ ka ba wa tuur a nɩsʊ̃n a nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ, tɩchɛ a bala jaa na vɛ̃ ka a nɩbɛ maala yelbier nɩ yelbe-ɩrbɛ ba jaa 'mataa bar.

eng: When Jesus arrived on the other side of the lake in the country of the Gadarenes, two men who were demon-possessed came from among the tombs to meet him. They were so violent that nobody could travel on that road.
local: Yesu na wa ta a Gadara paal pɔ, dɔɔr ayi na tara sɩdɛbɛ yɩn a yar par tuor wʊ. Ban ɩ a nɩbebe jũu, nɩrɛ jaa maa 'la tu a sɔr 'lɔ ɛ.

eng: They cried out, What are you going to do with us, Son of God? Have you come to torture us before the time of judgment.
local: Ban wa nyɛ a Yesu ba ŋmen chɛlma tɩ 'yɛrɛ, Bo lɛ fʊ bʊɔrɔ ka a sɩ sɛ̃, fʊ̃ʊ Nãaŋmɩn Bie? Fʊ wan ɩka fʊ wa ge sɩ tɩchɛ ka a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ bɩbir wa ta bɩɩ.

eng: Far off in the distance a large herd of pigs was feeding.
local: Do yɔɔ mɩ dɔ̃ɔ ara gbʊr ba dire.

eng: The demons pleaded with him, If you throw us out, send us into the herd of pigs.
local: Ka a sɩdɛbɛ sʊɔr wʊ, Ala ɩ fʊn dii sɩn bar, sɔɔ ka sɩ chen tɩ kpɛ a dor anyã pɔ.

eng: Then he said to the demons, Go away, and they came out and went into the pigs. The whole herd rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ chen! Be lɛ a sɩdɛbɛ yi naa kpɛ a dor pɔ, ka a dor a jaa jo tĩgbil suu tɩ kpɛ a man kʊ̃ɔ pɔ gbʊ̃m kpi kpi.

eng: Those who tended the pigs ran into the city and told everything that had happened to the demon-possessed men.
local: A bala na kaara a dor jon kul, ban wa tɩ kpɩɛrɛ a tẽe pɔ, ba man bon 'lɔ jaa na ɩ a bala na tara a sɩdɛbɛ nɩ a lɛ jaa na ɩ a dor.

eng: Then the whole city came out and met Jesus. When they saw him, they pleaded with him to leave their region.
local: A tẽe wʊ jaa nɩbɛ yɩn wa a Yesu sɛ̃; ban wa nyɛ wʊ ba sʊɔr wʊn ɩka wʊ yɩ a ba wɛr pɔ.

eng: But they went out and spread the word about him throughout that whole region.
local: Tɩchɛ ba yin tɩ ŋme a wʊ yele yaar a tẽe 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ wʊ jaa.

eng: He will throw them into a burning furnace. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Ka ba lɔb ba 'yɔ̃ɔ bũu dagel pɔ, be lɛ ban kõnõ ɔɔrɔ nɩ nyɩmɛ.

eng: Just as Jonah was in the whales belly for three days and three nights , so the Human One will be in the heart of the earth for three days and three nights.
local: Nɩtãa lɛ a Jona na dɔ̃ɔ gã a jãnele pʊɔm ber ata nɩ tãsɔɔ gbulo ata lɛ mɩ lɛ a Maa Nɩsaal Bie mɩ na gã a yaa pɔ ber ata nɩ tãsɔɔ gbulo ata.

eng: On the Judgment Day, many people will say to me, Lord, Lord, didnt we prophesy in your name and expel demons in your name and do lots of miracles in your name.
local: A bɩbir 'lɔ daar, nɩyɔɔ na 'yɛr kʊ ma n Soro, n Soro, saa 'yɛr Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr a fʊ yuor pɔ ɛ, na sɩ diin a sɩdɛbɛ ka a yɩ a fʊ yuor pɔ ɛ, na sɩ tʊ̃ nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ a fʊ yuor pɔ ɛ?.

eng: Now Jesus called his disciples and said, I feel sorry for the crowd because they have been with me for three days and have nothing to eat. I dont want to send them away hungry for fear they wont have enough strength to travel.
local: Yesu bʊɔl a wʊ poturbo wa 'yɛr kʊ ba, Nɩbaal kpɛ ma naa nɩ a nɩyɔɔ banyãna bojũu ba ben a n sɛ̃ ber ata tɩ ba di bon jaa ɛ, n ba bʊɔrɔ ɩka n vɛ̃ ka ba lɩɛb chiine kɔ̃ ɛ, amɩne ban wa lon sɔr pɔ.

eng: As they were leaving, people brought to him a man who was demon-possessed and unable to speak.
local: A jɔ̃n ayi bala na wa lɩɛb chen baar, ba tara na daba na tara sɩdɛbɛ na wʊ ɩ wõo waan a Yesu sɛ̃.

eng: When Jesus had thrown out the demon, the man who couldnt speak began to talk. The crowds were amazed and said, Nothing like this has ever been seen in Israel.
local: A sɩdɛbɛ na wa yɩ a daba pɔ baar, wʊ piel 'yɛrɛ, ka nɛɛr 'maa a nɩyɔɔ ka ba 'yɛrɛ, Saa dɔ̃ɔ nyɛ a nɩtɔ̃ a Isɩral jaa ɛ.

eng: His disciples replied, Where are we going to get enough food in this wilderness to satisfy such a big crowd.
local: Ka a wʊ poturbo 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Nyɛ lɛ sɩn nyɛ paanʊ̃ɔ a dãlpʊɔ nyã pɔ tʊ̃ɔ kãan a nɩyɔɔ banyãna.

eng: Then Ill tell them, Ive never known you. Get away from me, you people who do wrong.
local: A daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ ɩn 'yɛr kʊ ba, n ba bɔ̃ɔ yɩ ɛ, yɩ tɔɔ chen be, yɩn yelbe-ɩrbɛ.

eng: Jesus said, How much bread do you have? They responded, Seven loaves and a few fish.
local: Ka Yesu sʊʊr ba, Paanʊ̃ɔ aŋmɩnɛ lɛ yɩ tara? Ka ba 'yɛr, Ayopõi nɩ jãmɛ bãlãa.

eng: Everybody who hears these words of mine and puts them into practice is like a wise builder who built a house on bedrock.
local: A lɛ jũu, nɩrɛ jaa na wõ a n 'yɛrbie anyãna tɩ maala a lɛ na kaara fɩka yɛ̃ sʊɔ na mɛ a wʊ yir dɔɔl pie ju.

eng: The rain fell, the floods came, and the wind blew and beat against that house. It didnt fall because it was firmly set on bedrock.
local: Saa wa naa, ka a kʊ̃ɔ yɩ yaar jilʊ̃ a yir ka a sɛsɛbɛ fu ŋme a yir tɩchɛ waa lo ɛ, bojũu wʊ ara na kʊsɩr ju.

eng: But everybody who hears these words of mine and doesnt put them into practice will be like a fool who built a house on sand.
local: Tɩchɛ, nɩrɛ jaa na wõ a n 'yɛrbie anyãna tɩ na waa maal ɛ, kaara fɩka jɔl na mɛ a wʊ yir dɔɔl bire ju.

eng: He told the crowd to sit on the ground.
local: Wʊ vɛ̃n ka a nɩbɛ jãnɛ tẽe.

eng: He took the seven loaves of bread and the fish. After he gave thanks, he broke them into pieces and gave them to the disciples, and the disciples gave them to the crowds.
local: Ka wʊ pãa de a paanʊ̃ɔsɩ ayopõi nɩ a jãbãlãa ala na wʊ puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan, tɩ na wʊ ŋma de kʊ a wʊ poturbo, ka ba de põ a nɩbɛ.

eng: Then the righteous will shine like the sun in their Fathers kingdom. Those who have ears should hear.
local: A bala na tor na chaar naa mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ kaar a ba Sãa nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ. Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na tara tobo wʊ chɛlɛ.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a treasure that somebody hid in a field, which someone else found and covered up. Full of joy, the finder sold everything and bought that field.
local: A Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ kaara naa fɩka nɩrɛ na nyɛ nãbonsɩ pʊɔ pɔ 'mɔɔ sɔɔl tɩchɛ ka a wʊ pʊɔ pɛl, ka wʊ tɩ da a wʊ bonsɩ a jaa wʊn tara tɩ dãn a puo.

eng: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a merchant in search of fine pearls.
local: A Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ lɛ kaara naa fɩka nɩrɛ na yʊɔrɔ bʊɔrɔ kʊsɩvɩɛl.

eng: The citizens of Nineveh will stand up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it as guilty, because they changed their hearts and lives in response to Jonahs preaching. And look, someone greater than Jonah is here.
local: A 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ daar Ninive nɩbɛ na yin 'yɔ̃ɔ a yɩn dɩna nɩbɛ 'yɛr, bojũu ba lɩɛb a ba tɩɛr a Jona na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr kʊ ba, tɩchɛ yɩ kaa, a 'lɔ na gar a Jona ben ka.

eng: The queen of the South will be raised up by God at the judgment with this generation and condemn it because she came from a distant land to hear Solomons wisdom. And look, someone greater than Solomon is here.
local: A pɔɔ nãa na yin be a par a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ daar na wʊ wa 'yɔ̃ɔ a yɩn dɩna nɩbɛ 'yɛr, bojũu wʊ dɔ̃ɔ yi be a tẽtɛɛr wa chɛl a Nãa Solomon yɛ̃ 'yɛr, tɩchɛ yɩ kaa a 'lɔ na gar a Solomon ben ka.

eng: But the Pharisees said, He throws out demons with the authority of the ruler of demons.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Faraseemɩne 'yɛr, A sɩdɛbɛ nãa lɛ kʊ wʊ a kpɛ̃ɔ ka wʊ dire nɩ a sɩdɛbɛ ka a yire.

eng: Jesus traveled among all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues, announcing the good news of the kingdom, and healing every disease and every sickness.
local: Yesu yɔn a tẽbɛrɛ nɩ a tẽbil a jaa pɔ, wile a nɩbɛ a lɔ̃ɔfʊ yie pɔ, tɩ mʊɔlɔ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ sãana nɩ baal jaa bʊrɔ.

eng: When he found one very precious pearl, he went and sold all that he owned and bought it.
local: wʊn wa nyɛ a ãsʊɔ daa na be tuo, wʊ tɩ dãn a wʊ bonsɩ a jaa tɩ dãn.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full. The disciples collected seven baskets full of leftovers.
local: Ba jaa din tɩɩ, ka a poturbo wob a bʊ̃ŋmaar 'yɔ̃ɔ pɛr ayopõi.

eng: Four thousand men ate, plus women and children.
local: A bala na di ɩn dɔɔr tur anaar, ka pɔbɔ nɩ bibiir nũu ba pʊɔ ɛ.

eng: The rain fell, the floods came, and the wind blew and beat against that house. It fell and was completely destroyed.
local: Saa wa naa, ka a kʊ̃ɔ yɩ yaar jilʊ̃ a yir, ka a sɛsɛbɛ fu ŋme a yir kʊ lo ka a wʊ lofʊ ɩ dɛɛr.

eng: When Jesus finished these words, the crowds were amazed at his teaching.
local: Yesu na wa 'yɛr a 'yɛrbie anyãna baar nɛɛ 'maan a nɩyɔɔ nɩ a wʊ wiilfʊ.

eng: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a net that people threw into the lake and gathered all kinds of fish.
local: A Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ lɛ nyɛntãan fɩka lɔyaar ban lɔb 'yɔ̃ɔ man pɔ, kʊ nyɔɔr jãdẽdẽme.

eng: When an unclean spirit leaves a person, it wanders through dry places looking for a place to rest. But it doesnt find any.
local: Sɩdɛɛr wa yɩ nɩrɛ pɔ, wʊ ma chen dãlpʊʊr pɔ tɩ bʊɔrɔ pɛ̃nfʊ jie 'lɔ baa wa nyɛ ɛ.

eng: After dismissing the crowds, Jesus got into the boat and came to the region of Magadan.
local: Wʊn wa vɛ̃ ka a nɩbɛ yaar baar wʊ kpɛ̃n a gboro na wʊ chen Magadan paal pɔ.

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowds, he had compassion for them because they were troubled and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
local: Wʊn wa nyɛ a nɩyɔɔ, nɩbaal kpɛ wʊ naa bojũu baa bɔ̃ɔ a ba ju tɩɛr ɛ, na baa mɩ lɛ tara sʊ̃nɔ̃ ɛ, fɩka piir na ba tara chɩnɛ ɛ.

eng: because he was teaching them like someone with authority and not like their legal experts.
local: bojũu wʊ wile ban nɩ kpɛ̃ɔ gar a ba nɛbinãa wiwiilbe.

eng: When it was full, they pulled it to the shore, where they sat down and put the good fish together into containers. But the bad fish they threw away.
local: A lɔyaar na wa sɛɛ ba tɔɔn yin a gogoro ju, tɩ jãnɛ tuur a jãsʊ̃n 'yɔ̃ɔ a pɛr pɔ, tɩ lɔb a jãdɛbɛ bar.

eng: Then it says, Ill go back to the house I left. When it arrives, it finds the place vacant, cleaned up, and decorated.
local: wʊ ma 'yɛr naa ɩn lɩɛb kul a yir 'lɔ pɔ ɩn dɔ̃ɔ yɩ. Wʊ wa nyɛn a yir ka wʊ ɩ yirkpolo na wʊ vɩɛlɛ.

eng: Then he said to his disciples, The size of the harvest is bigger than you can imagine, but there are few workers.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ poturbo, A pʊɔ bɛrma naa tɩchɛ ka a ŋmaŋmarba ba waa ɛ.

eng: Thats the way it will be at the end of the present age. The angels will go out and separate the evil people from the righteous people.
local: Lɛ lɛ an be a wɛr baarfʊ daar. A Nãaŋmɩn na tʊ̃n a wʊ malakasɩ ka ba wa tuur a nɩdɛbɛ yin a nɩmɩn pɔ.

eng: Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more evil than itself. They go in and make their home there. That person is worse off at the end than at the beginning. This is the way it will be also for this evil generation.
local: ka wʊ chen tɩ bʊɔl sɩdɛbɛ ayopõi na tara a putuo gar wʊ ka ba tɩ kpɩɛrɛ a be. Ka a nɩrɛ nyã pãa ɩ dɛɛr gar a lɛ wʊn dɔ̃ɔ be. Lɛ lɛ an kaara a yɩn putudẽme a dɩna.

eng: Therefore, plead with the Lord of the harvest to send out workers for his harvest.
local: a lɛ jũu yɩ sʊɔr a pʊɔ sʊɔ ka wʊ bar tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ ka ba wa a pʊɔ pɔ.

eng: While Jesus was speaking to the crowds, his mother and brothers stood outside trying to speak with him.
local: Wʊn chɛnɛ 'yɛrɛ nɩ a nɩyɔɔ, a wʊ ma nɩ a wʊ yɛɛr wa ben yõo bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba nyɛ wʊ.

eng: and will throw the evil ones into a burning furnace. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: tɩ na ba lɔb a nɩdɛbɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ a bũu dagel pɔ, be lɛ ban kõnõ tɩ ɔɔrɔ nyɩmɛ.

eng: Someone said to him, Look, your mother and brothers are outside wanting to speak with you.
local: Nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Nyɛ a fʊ ma nɩ a fʊ yɛɛr lɛ be yõo bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba nyɛ fʊ.

eng: Have you understood all these things?Jesus asked. They said to him, Yes.
local: Yɩ bɔ̃ɔn a yelsɩ anyã na a jaa par ɔ? Ka ba 'yɛr, Ɔ̃ɔ.

eng: Then he said to them, Therefore, every legal expert who has been trained as a disciple for the kingdom of heaven is like the head of a household who brings old and new things out of their treasure chest.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, A nɛɛ wile ãsʊɔ jaa ban wiil ka wʊ bɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ yele kaara naa fɩka yir sʊɔ na de bʊ̃paalaa nɩ bʊ̃koro yin a wʊ boor pɔ.

eng: When Jesus finished these parables, he departed.
local: Yesu na wa wiil a sʊkpar anyã par, wʊ yin a be.

eng: When he came to his hometown, he taught the people in their synagogue. They were surprised and said, Where did he get this wisdom? Where did he get the power to work miracles?.
local: Wʊ wan a wʊ mãɛ paal pɔ tɩ piel wile a nɩbɛ a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ jiir, ka nɛɛr 'maa ba ka ba 'yɛr, Nyɛ lɛ a daba nyã nyɛ a yɛ̃ nyã nɩ a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ a nyã?.

eng: Isnt he the carpenters son? Isnt his mother named Mary? Arent James, Joseph, Simon, and Judas his brothers?.
local: A ba ɩ a mɩmɩɛrɛ bidaba lɛ ɛ? A ba ɩ a wʊ ma lɛ ba bʊɔlɔ a Mɛɛr ɛ? A ba ɩ a wʊ yɛɛr nɩ Jemesɩ nɩ Josefʊ nɩ Simon nɩ Judas ɛ?.

eng: And his sisters, arent they here with us? Where did this man get all this.
local: A ba ɩ sɩn nɩ a wʊ yɛpɔbɔ ba jaa lɛ 'mataa be a ka ɛ? Nyɩnɛ lɛ a daba nyã nyɛ a kpɛ̃ɔ nyã.

eng: They were repulsed by him and fell into sin. But Jesus said to them, Prophets are honored everywhere except in their own hometowns and in their own households.
local: Tɩ na ba tɔr a wʊ yele. Tɩchɛ ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ nyɛrɛ na 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ jie jaa tɩchɛ a wʊ mãɛ tẽe nɩ a wʊ mãɛ yir pɔ, wʊ maa nyɛ 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ ɛ.

eng: He was unable to do many miracles there because of their disbelief.
local: Ban ba sɔɔ de wʊ jũu, waa tʊ̃ɔ tʊ̃ nɛ'maa tʊ̃yɔɔ a be ɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Who is my mother? Who are my brothers.
local: Tɩchɛ Yesu sʊʊr a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na 'yɛr a 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, An lɛ a ɩ ma nɩ ba boo lɛ ɩ a n yɛɛr.

eng: He stretched out his hand toward his disciples and said, Look, here are my mother and my brothers.
local: Wʊ lɩɛb tur a wʊ poturbo nũu tɩ 'yɛr, Nyɛ a n ma nɩ a n yɛɛr!.

eng: Whoever does the will of my Father who is in heaven is my brother, sister, and mother.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na maala a lɛ a n Sãa na be a saaju na bʊɔrɔ lɛ ɩ a n yɛɛr nɩ a n ma.

eng: When Jesus finished saying these things, he left Galilee and came to the area of Judea on the east side of the Jordan.
local: Yesu na wa 'yɛr a anyã baar, wʊ yin a Galilee chen Judiya paal pɔ, a Jordan man gɔ̃ɔn.

eng: Large crowds followed him, and he healed them.
local: Nɩyɔɔ turo wʊ naa, ka wʊ sãa a ba baalsɩ a be.

eng: Some Pharisees came to him. In order to test him, they said, Does the Law allow a man to divorce his wife for just any reason.
local: Faraseemɩne bamɩne wan a wʊ sɛ̃ ɩka ba wa bɛɛr wʊ kaa, na ba sʊʊr wʊ, Sɔr ben be ɩka daba wʊ bar a wʊ pɔɔ yele kɔ̃ɔ jũu bɩɩ.

eng: Jesus answered, Havent you read that at the beginning the creator made them male and female?.
local: Ka Yesu 'yɛr, Yaa kar a be a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na maal ba a pielfʊ daar Maal daba nɩ pɔɔ .

eng: And God said, Because of this a man should leave his father and mother and be joined together with his wife, and the two will be one flesh .
local: na wʊ 'yɛr, A lɛ jũu, daba na bar a wʊ sãa nɩ a wʊ ma, tɩchɛ ka 'lɔ nɩ a wʊ pɔɔ ɩ bʊ̃'yen, ka ba jaa ayi ɩ ãgãn bʊ̃'yen?.

eng: So they are no longer two but one flesh. Therefore, humans must not pull apart what God has put together.
local: Baa lɛ chɛnɛ ɩ ayi ɛ, tɩchɛ ba ɩn ãgãn bʊ̃'yen. A lɛ jũu, bon 'lɔ Nãaŋmɩn na 'mataa, nɩrɛ wʊ ta oor aa ɛ.

eng: The Pharisees said to him, Then why did Moses command us to give a divorce certificate and divorce her? .
local: Ka ba sʊʊr wʊ, Ŋmɩnɛ ka a Mosesɩ 'yɛr ɩka sɩ kʊ wʊ pɔɔ bar gãn, tɩ bar wʊ.

eng: Jesus replied, Moses allowed you to divorce your wives because your hearts are unyielding. But it wasnt that way from the beginning.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Bojũu a yɩ nyãkpãɛn jũu lɛ so ka a Mosesɩ bar a sɔr ɩka, yɩ bar a yɩ pɔbɔ, tɩchɛ a pielfʊ daar a ba dɔ̃ɔ be a lɛ ɛ.

eng: I say to you that whoever divorces his wife, except for sexual unfaithfulness, and marries another woman commits adultery.
local: A lɛ jũu, n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ, nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na bar a wʊ pɔɔ, ka ala ba ɩ sɛ̃fʊ jũu tɩ kul pɔɔ yuo, wʊ sɔ̃ɔn a kul taa.

eng: His disciples said to him, If thats the way things are between a man and his wife, then its better not to marry.
local: A wʊ poturbo 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, ala ɩ lɛ lɛ a be a daba nɩ a wʊ pɔɔ, a ba sɛɛ ɩka nɩrɛ wʊ kul pɔɔ ɛ.

eng: He replied, Not everybody can accept this teaching, but only those who have received the ability to accept it.
local: Tɩchɛ ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Nɩrɛ jaa kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ sɔɔ a wiilfʊ nyã ka a bala a Nãaŋmɩn na vɛ̃ a ba bɔ̃ɔ a par tɛɛ ba lɛ ɛ.

eng: For there are eunuchs who have been eunuchs from birth. And there are eunuchs who have been made eunuchs by other people. And there are eunuchs who have made themselves eunuchs because of the kingdom of heaven. Those who can accept it should accept it.
local: Bamɩne ben be na ba dɔɔr ɛ, an ɩ lɛ lɛ ba dɔɔ ba ka be, ka bamɩne mɩ a ba taaba lɛ maal ba ka baa dɔɔr ɛ, ka bamɩne mɩ tɔr a kul taa a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ jũu, 'lɔ jaa nɩ na tʊ̃ɔ sɔɔ a wiilfʊ nyã, wʊ sɔɔ.

eng: Some people brought children to Jesus so that he would place his hands on them and pray. But the disciples scolded them.
local: Nɩbɛ waan na bibiir ɩka wʊ dɔɔl ba a wʊ nũu na wʊ sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn kʊ ba. Tɩchɛ a poturbo mɩɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ a bala na waan ba.

eng: Allow the children to come to me, Jesus said. Dont forbid them, because the kingdom of heaven belongs to people like these children.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Yɩ bar a bibiir sɔr ka ba wa a n sɛ̃, yɩ ta pii ba sɔr ɛ, a banyã taaba lɛ so a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ.

eng: Then he blessed the children and went away from there.
local: Wʊ dɔɔl ban a wʊ nuur sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn kʊ ba, tɩ chen.

eng: A man approached him and said, Teacher, what good thing must I do to have eternal life.
local: Be lɛ ka nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ wa a wʊ sɛ̃ wa sʊʊr, Wiwile, bo yelvɩla lɛ ɩn ɩ naa nyɛ nyɛvʊr na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ.

eng: Jesus said, Why do you ask me about what is good? Theres only one who is good. If you want to enter eternal life, keep the commandments.
local: Ka a Yesu sʊʊr, Ŋmɩnɛ a fʊ sʊrɔ ma bon 'lɔ na ɩ vɩla? Nɩbʊ̃'yen tɛɛ lɛ ɩ nɩbie. Ala ɩ fʊ bʊɔrɔ na ɩka fʊ nyɛ nyɛvʊr turo a nɛbinsɩ.

eng: The man said, Which ones? Then Jesus said, Dont commit murder. Dont commit adultery. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony .
local: Ka daba sʊʊr wʊ, A boo? Ka Yesu 'yɛr, Ta kʊ nɩrɛ ɛ, ta sɛ̃ ɛ, ta ju ɛ, ta di jir dãasɩɛ ɛ.

eng: Honor your father and mother, and love your neighbor as you love yourself .
local: 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a fʊ sãa nɩ a fʊ ma, na fʊ mɩ lɛ nʊ̃ a fʊ tɔ sʊɔ a fʊ mãɛ kaar.

eng: The young man replied, Ive kept all these. What am I still missing.
local: Ka a daba 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, N tu naa anyãna a jaa, bo lɛ chɛ ma.

eng: Jesus said, If you want to be complete, go, sell what you own, and give the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come follow me.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Ala ɩ fʊ bʊɔrɔ na ɩka fʊ ɩ chɛchɛ, tɩ de a fʊ bʊ̃tarsɩ a jaa da, na fʊ de a libie kʊ a nɔ̃n dẽme, fʊn nyɛn a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ tɩ na fʊ wa turo ma.

eng: But when the young man heard this, he went away saddened, because he had many possessions.
local: A daba na wa wõ a nɩtɔ̃, wʊ chiin naa pʊsɔ̃ɔ bojũu wʊ tara na bʊ̃tarsɩ.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, I assure you that it will be very hard for a rich person to enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: Ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ poturbo, Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ yɩ, an ɩn kpɛ̃ɔ tara sʊɔ na kpɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.

eng: In fact, its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Sɩrɛ jaa lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ go, an ɩn kɔ̃mɔ̃kɔ̃mɔ̃ ka nyɔɔma na kpɛ kparpĩi bɔɔ pɔ gar tara sʊɔ na kpɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.

eng: When his disciples heard this, they were stunned. Then who can be saved? they asked.
local: A poturbo na wa wõ a nɩtɔ̃, nɛɛ 'maa ba naa ka ba sʊrɔ, An lɛ na pãa nyɛ faafʊ.

eng: Jesus looked at them carefully and said, Its impossible for human beings. But all things are possible for God.
local: Ka Yesu kaa ba tɩ 'yɛr, Ala na ɩ tuo nɩrɛ sɛ̃, Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ yele jaa ba ɩ tuo ɛ.

eng: Then Peter replied, Look, weve left everything and followed you. What will we have.
local: A Pita 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Nyɛ sɩn bar bonsɩ a jaa tɩ wa turo fʊ? Bo sãyar lɛ sɩn nyɛ.

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you who have followed me that, when everything is made new, when the Human One sits on his magnificent throne, you also will sit on twelve thrones overseeing the twelve tribes of Israel.
local: Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, a bonsɩ a jaa bʊ̃paalaa lɩɛbfʊ daar, a maa Nɩsaal Bie na jãnɛ naa a tɩɩr nãalʊ̃ʊ dakɔɔ ju. Ka a yɩn bala mɩ na turo ma mɩ jãnɛ a nãa dakɔɔ pie nɩ ayi ju, tɔɔrɔ a Isɩral bala pie nɩ ayi 'yɛr.

eng: And all who have left houses, brothers, sisters, father, mother, children, or farms because of my name will receive one hundred times more and will inherit eternal life.
local: Nɩrɛ nyã jaa na bar a wʊ yir bɩɩ yɛɛr bɩɩ yɛpɔbɔ, bɩɩ sãa, bɩɩ ma, bɩɩ bibiir, bɩɩ puo a maa jũu na nyɛn dɔɔltaa gbɛɛ kɔbaa tɩ tara nyɛvʊr na ba tara baar fʊ ɛ.

eng: But many who are first will be last. And many who are last will be first.
local: Tɩchɛ a nɩyɔɔ na de a nie na wa ben puor, tɩchɛ ka a puor dẽme nɩ wa de a nie.

eng: At that time the disciples came to Jesus and asked, Who is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
local: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ a poturbo wa a Yesu sɛ̃ na ba wa sʊʊr, An lɛ ɩ kpɛ̃ɛ gar ba jaa a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.

eng: Then he called a little child over to sit among the disciples.
local: Wʊ bʊɔl bibile na wʊ vɛ̃ a wʊ ara a ba pɔ.

eng: and said, I assure you that if you dont turn your lives around and become like this little child, you will definitely not enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: tɩ wʊ 'yɛr, Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, yɩn baa lɩɛb na yɩ kaara fɩka bibiir kaar ɛ, yɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ kpɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ ɛ.

eng: Those who humble themselves like this little child will be the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
local: A lɛ jũu, nɩrɛ 'lɔ na sɩɩr wʊ mãɛ a bibile nyãna kaar, lɛ ɩ a kpɛ̃ɛ gar ba jaa a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.

eng: Whoever welcomes one such child in my name welcomes me.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na de a bibile nyãna a n jũu, maa lɛ wʊ de.

eng: As for whoever causes these little ones who believe in me to trip and fall into sin, it would be better for them to have a huge stone hung around their necks and be drowned in the bottom of the lake.
local: Tɩchɛ, ala ɩ ãsʊɔ jaa mɩ vɛ̃ naa a bʊ̃bile nyãna na sɔɔ ma ɩ yelbier, a nɩnyɩnɛ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃, bala 'mɔɔ nɩɛr lũ yɔɔl a wʊ nyĩe pɔ daa 'yɔ̃ɔ a man pɔ, ala sa.

eng: How terrible it is for the world because of the things that cause people to trip and fall into sin! Such things have to happen, but how terrible it is for the person who causes those things to happen!.
local: Nɛbier lɛ kʊ a wɛr, a bɛɛr kaa na vɛ̃ ka ba ɩ yelbier. Bɛɛr kaa na wa naa, tɩchɛ nɛbier lɛ kʊ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ an tu wa!.

eng: If your hand or your foot causes you to fall into sin, chop it off and throw it away. Its better to enter into life crippled or lame than to be thrown into the eternal fire with two hands or two feet.
local: Ala ɩ a fʊ nũu bɩɩ gbɛr vɛ̃ naa ka fʊ ɩ yelbier, ŋmaa wʊ lɔb bar. Fʊn tɩ kpɛ a nyɛvʊr pɔ nɩ nũŋmaa bɩɩ gbɛŋmaa sãn fʊn tara a nuur nɩ a gbɛɛ a jaa ayi tɩ na fʊ tɩ kpɛ a wõm jie a be a bũu na ba kpĩine ɛ.

eng: If your eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out and throw it away. Its better to enter into life with one eye than to be cast into a burning hell with two eyes.
local: Ala ɩ a fʊ nɩbir mɩ vɛ̃ naa ka fʊ ɩ yelbier, 'loo lɔb bar. Fʊn tɩ kpɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ nɩ nɩbifɩɛ sa naa fʊn tara a nɩbie ayi tɩ na fʊ tɩ kpɛ wõm jie.

eng: Be careful that you dont look down on one of these little ones. I say to you that their angels in heaven are always looking into the face of my Father who is in heaven.
local: Yɩ bɔ̃ɔn sʊ̃ na yɩ taa kaara a bʊ̃bil banyãna ãsʊɔ pɔrɔ ɛ, yelmãɛ jaa lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ a ba malakasɩ ben a saaju ma kaara a n Sãa niem.

eng: What do you think? If someone had one hundred sheep and one of them wandered off, wouldnt he leave the ninety-nine on the hillsides and go in search for the one that wandered off?.
local: Bo lɛ yɩ tɩɛr? Ala ɩ a nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ tara na piir kɔbaa, ka bʊ̃'yen wa bɔr, a ba ɩ wʊn bar a kɔbaa forbir ala a pɛchiin baa pɔ, tɩ yʊɔrɔ bʊɔrɔ a bʊ̃'yen 'lɔ tɩ tãn a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ jaa wʊn na tɩ nyɛ wʊ bɩɩ?.

eng: If he finds it, I assure you that he is happier about having that one sheep than about the ninety-nine who didnt wander off.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, 'lɔ wa nyɛ wʊ, nʊ̃ɔ ma kpɛ wʊ naa gar a kɔbaa forbir ala na ba bɔr ɛ.

eng: In the same way, my Father who is in heaven doesnt want to lose one of these little ones.
local: Lɛ nɩ wʊ ju mɩ lɛ ɩka a ba ɩ a yɩ Sãa saaju bɔfʊ ɩka a bʊ̃bil banyãna ãsʊɔ wʊ bɔr ɛ.

eng: If your brother or sister sins against you, go and correct them when you are alone together. If they listen to you, then youve won over your brother or sister.
local: Ala ɩ a fʊ yɛbɛ ãsʊɔ ɩ fʊn yelbier, chen tɩ wiil wʊ a wʊ sɔ̃ɔnaa fʊ̃ʊ nɩ a 'lɔ tɛɛ. 'Lɔ wa sɔɔ a wʊ bɔrfʊ, yɩ lɛ ɩn yɛɛr go.

eng: But if they wont listen, take with you one or two others so that every word may be established by the mouth of two or three witnesses .
local: Tɩchɛ 'lɔ ba wa sɔɔ a fʊ 'yɛr ɛ, tara nɩbʊ̃'yen bɩɩ nɩbɛ ayi chiin, ka a nɩbɛ ayi bɩɩ nɩbɛ ata di a 'yɛr dãasɩɛ.

eng: But if they still wont pay attention, report it to the church. If they wont pay attention even to the church, treat them as you would a Gentile and tax collector.
local: 'Lɔ wa tɔr a nɩbɛ bala niem, 'yɛr kʊ a pupuorbiir, ka 'lɔ wa chɛnɛ tɔr na waa tu a ba nɛɛ ɛ, fʊ maal wʊ fɩka a lɛ fʊn na maal a nɩyuo, bɩɩ a lõpodier kaar.

eng: I assure you that whatever you fasten on earth will be fastened in heaven. And whatever you loosen on earth will be loosened in heaven.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, bon 'lɔ jaa yɩn lũ ka a wɛr pɔ, mɩ lũn a saaju bon 'lɔ yɩn lor bar a wɛr pɔ a mɩ lor bar a saaju.

eng: Again I assure you that if two of you agree on earth about anything you ask, then my Father who is in heaven will do it for you.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, a yɩ nɩbɛ ayi tara sɔɔfʊ sʊɔr bon jaa, a n Sãa na be a saaju na kʊ yɩ naa.

eng: For where two or three are gathered in my name, Im there with them.
local: Jie 'lɔ nɩbɛ ayi bɩɩ ata na ara nɩ a maa yuor, n ben a ba pɔ.

eng: Then Peter said to Jesus, Lord, how many times should I forgive my brother or sister who sins against me? Should I forgive as many as seven times.
local: Pita wan wa sʊʊr wʊ, N Soro, ala ɩ a n tɔ sʊɔ ɩ man yelbier gbɛɛ aŋmɩnɛ lɛ ɩn vɛ̃ bar wʊ? Gbɛɛ ayopõi bɩɩ.

eng: Jesus said, Not just seven times, but rather as many as seventy-seven times.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, N 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ fʊ a ba ɩ gbɛɛ ayopõi ɛ, tɩchɛ gbɛɛ lɩjaata nɩ pie nɩ ayopõi gbɛɛ ayopõi .

eng: Therefore, the kingdom of heaven is like a king who wanted to settle accounts with his servants.
local: A lɛ jũu a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ na nyɛntaa fɩka nãa na bʊɔr ɩka 'lɔ nɩ a wʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ ba guor a ba libie kaa.

eng: When he began to settle accounts, they brought to him a servant who owed him ten thousand bags of gold.
local: Wʊn wa tɩ piel guoro a libie yele ba nyɔɔn ãsʊɔ na di wʊ turo pie sãn waan a wʊ sɛ̃.

eng: Because the servant didnt have enough to pay it back, the master ordered that he should be sold, along with his wife and children and everything he had, and that the proceeds should be used as payment.
local: wʊn kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ yab a sãn ɛ, a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ 'yɛr ɩka ba da 'lɔ nɩ a wʊ pɔɔ nɩ a bibiir nɩ a wʊ bonsɩ a jaa yab a sãn.

eng: But the servant fell down, kneeled before him, and said, Please, be patient with me, and Ill pay you back.
local: Ka a tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ lo gbur dumo a wʊ niem lɔɔrɔ nuur 'yɛrɛ, Tara nɩ ma kãnyir, ɩn yab a fʊ sãma a jaa.

eng: The master had compassion on that servant, released him, and forgave the loan.
local: A nɩkpɛ̃ɛ kãan a wʊ nɩbaal tɩ vɛ̃ a wʊ sãma kʊ wʊ, tɩ bar wʊ.

eng: When that servant went out, he found one of his fellow servants who owed him one hundred coins. He grabbed him around the throat and said, Pay me back what you owe me.
local: Tɩchɛ, a daba nyã nɩ wʊ ju na wa yɩ a yõo nyɛ a wʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃tɔ sʊɔ na di wʊ toofar kɔbaa sãn, wʊ chɩm a daba kɔkɔr pɔ tɩ 'yɛr, Yab ma a sãn fʊn di.

eng: Then his fellow servant fell down and begged him, Be patient with me, and Ill pay you back.
local: Ka a tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ nyã mɩ lo gbur dumo tɩ sʊɔrɔ, Tara nɩ ma kãnyir, tɩ ɩn yab fʊn a sãn.

eng: But he refused. Instead, he threw him into prison until he paid back his debt.
local: Ka wʊ tɔr, tɩ nyɔɔ wʊ chiin tɩ pɔɔ tɩ tãn a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ wʊn yab a sãn.

eng: When his fellow servants saw what happened, they were deeply offended. They came and told their master all that happened.
local: A wʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃taaba na wa nyɛ a lɛ wʊn ɩ, ba pʊɔ sɔ̃ɔ naa, ka ba chen tɩ man a jaa kʊ a nɩkpɛ̃ɛ a lɛ jaa na ɩ.

eng: His master called the first servant and said, You wicked servant! I forgave you all that debt because you appealed to me.
local: Ka a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ bʊɔl wʊ wa sʊʊr wʊ, Fʊ̃ʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃putuo sʊɔ. Fʊn sʊɔr ma jũu lɛ so ka n vɛ̃ a sãn bar fʊ.

eng: Shouldnt you also have mercy on your fellow servant, just as I had mercy on you.
local: Fʊ mɩ kʊ̃ naa kaa a fʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃tɔ sʊɔ nyã nɩbaal nɩtãa a lɛ ɩn kaa a fʊ nɩbaal ɛ.

eng: His master was furious and handed him over to the guard responsible for punishing prisoners, until he had paid the whole debt.
local: A wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ suur ir naa ka wʊ de wʊ tɩr ɩka ba tɩ pɔɔ wʊ ge wʊ tɩ tãn a daar 'lɔ wʊn yab a sãn a jaa.

eng: My heavenly Father will also do the same to you if you dont forgive your brother or sister from your heart.
local: Lɛ mɩ lɛ a n Sãa na be a saaju na ɩ yɩ mɩ, yɩn baa vɛ̃ a yɩ yɛbɛ yelbier bar ɛ.

eng: Six days later Jesus took Peter, James, and John his brother, and brought them to the top of a very high mountain.
local: A ber ayʊɔb daar, Yesu tara na Pita nɩ Jemesɩ nɩ a wʊ yɛbɛ Jɔɔn don tɔ̃wõo ju a ba tɛɛ.

eng: He was transformed in front of them. His face shone like the sun, and his clothes became as white as light.
local: Wʊ chil wʊ mãɛ a ba niem, ka a wʊ nie nyɩlɛ mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ kaar, ka a fuu pɛl nyɩlɛ bũu kaar.

eng: Moses and Elijah appeared to them, talking with Jesus.
local: Mosesɩ nɩ Elaija mɩ wa dũun yɩ ara a wʊ sɛ̃, ka ba wa 'mataa 'yɛrɛ.

eng: Peter reacted to all of this by saying to Jesus, Lord, its good that were here. If you want, Ill make three shrines: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.
local: Pita 'yɛr kʊ Yesu, N Soro, a vɩla na ka sɩn kpɩɛrɛ a ka. Ala ɩ fʊ sɔɔ naa, ɩn 'yɔ̃ɔn sɛɛr ata a ka na, ka fʊ so a ãsʊɔ, ka Mosesɩ mɩ so a ãsʊɔ, ka Elaija mɩ so a ãsʊɔ.

eng: While he was still speaking, look, a bright cloud overshadowed them. A voice from the cloud said, This is my Son whom I dearly love. I am very pleased with him. Listen to him.
local: Wʊn chɛnɛ 'yɛrɛ ka a julõjupɩla wa pɔɔ ba naa, ka Nãaŋmɩn kɔkɔr 'yɛr be a julõjuur pɔ, Nyã lɛ a n Binʊ̃nãa, a n pʊɔ pɛl wʊ naa, yɩ chɛlɛ a wʊ 'yɛr.

eng: Hearing this, the disciples fell on their faces, filled with awe.
local: A poturbo na wa wõ a nɩtɔ̃ dãbãɛ kpɛ ba naa ka ba lo kpa tẽe.

eng: But Jesus came and touched them. Get up, he said. Dont be afraid.
local: Tɩchɛ ka Yesu wa sɩɩr ba tɩ 'yɛrɛ, Yɩ ir, yɩ ta joro dãbãɛ ɛ.

eng: When they looked up, they saw no one except Jesus.
local: Ban wa dʊɔ kaa, baa nyɛ nɩrɛ jaa ka a Yesu tɛɛ ba lɛ ɛ.

eng: As they were coming down the mountain, Jesus commanded them, Dont tell anybody about the vision until the Human One is raised from the dead.
local: Ban wa yi a tɔ̃ɔ ju suro, Yesu kpãa ba naa, Yɩ taa 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ nɩrɛ jaa a bon 'lɔ yɩn nyɛ ɛ, tɩ tãn a daar 'lɔ a maa Nɩsaal Bie na lɩɛb ir a kũu pɔ.

eng: The disciples asked, Then why do the legal experts say that Elijah must first come.
local: Ka a wʊ poturbo sʊʊr wʊ, Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ a ɩ ka a nɛɛ wiwiilbe 'yɛr ɩka Elaija lɛ na de nie wa.

eng: Jesus responded, Elijah does come first and will restore all things.
local: Ka Yesu 'yɛr, Sɩrɛ jaa lɛ, ɩka Elaija wa naa wa chɔɔr a bonsɩ a jaa.

eng: In fact, I tell you that Elijah has already come, and they didnt know him. But they did to him whatever they wanted. In the same way the Human One is also going to suffer at their hands.
local: Tɩchɛ yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka Elaija den nie wa ka baa bɔ̃ɔ wʊ ɛ, tɩchɛ ba ɩ wʊn a lɛ na pɛl a ba pʊɔ. Lɛ mɩ lɛ a maa Nɩsaal Bie mɩ na di a dɔɔyɛ a ba nuur pɔ.

eng: Then the disciples realized he was telling them about John the Baptist.
local: Be lɛ a poturbo pãa bɔ̃ɔn ɩka Jɔɔn Kɔ̃suoro 'yɛr lɛ wʊ 'yɛrɛ.

eng: When they came to the crowd, a man met Jesus. He knelt before him.
local: Yesu nɩ a wʊ poturbo na wa chen a nɩyɔɔ sɛ̃, daba wa gbur dumo a wʊ niem.

eng: saying, Lord, show mercy to my son. He is epileptic and suffers terribly, for he often falls into the fire or the water.
local: tɩ 'yɛr, N Soro, kaa a n bie nɩbaal, kpũgul lɛ wʊ loro na wʊ dire dɔɔyɛ yɔɔ, sɔ̃ɔ ãsʊɔ wʊ ma lon bũu pɔ bɩɩ kʊ̃ɔ pɔ.

eng: I brought him to your disciples, but they couldnt heal him.
local: N tara wʊn waan a fʊ poturbo sɛ̃, tɩchɛ baa tʊ̃ɔ sãa wʊ ɛ.

eng: Jesus answered, You faithless and crooked generation, how long will I be with you? How long will I put up with you? Bring the boy here to me.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Yɩn dɩna nɩbɛ babãma dẽme, a yɩ sɔɔfʊ na ba jã bon ɛ, n be a yɩ sɛ̃ naa tɩ tãn dabor? N tara nɩ yɩ kãnyir jã ŋmɩnɛ? Tɩ 'yɛr a daba, Tɔɔ a bie wa kʊ ma.

eng: Then Jesus spoke harshly to the demon. And it came out of the child, who was healed from that time on.
local: Yesu tãn a sɩdɛɛr ka wʊ yi a bie pɔ, a dẽdẽe daa ka a bie sa.

eng: Then the disciples came to Jesus in private and said, Why couldnt we throw the demon out.
local: A poturbo wan a Yesu tɛɛ sɛ̃ wa sʊʊr, Ŋmɩnɛ a ɩ ka sɩn ba tʊ̃ɔ dii a sɩdɛɛr bar ɛ.

eng: Because you have little faith, he said. I assure you that if you have faith the size of a mustard seed, you could say to this mountain, Go from here to there, and it will go. There will be nothing that you cant do.
local: Wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Bojũu a yɩ sɔɔfʊ ba jã bon ɛ. Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, yɩn tara sɔɔfʊ kʊ jã a kɔ̃kɔ̃ɔ bir, yɩn 'yɛr kʊ a tɔ̃ɔ nyãna, ir a ka chen a be; wʊn ir chen. Tɩ yele jaa kʊ ɩ kpɛ̃ɔ kʊ yɩ ɛ.

eng: When the disciples came together in Galilee, Jesus said to them, The Human One is about to be delivered over into human hands.
local: Ban wa lɔ̃ɔ taa a Galilee, Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Ban gar 'yɔ̃ɔ a maa Nɩsaal Bie ka n di dɔɔyɛ a nɩbɛ nuur pɔ.

eng: They will kill him. But he will be raised on the third day. And they were heartbroken.
local: Ban kʊ maa naa, ka a ber ata daar ka n lɩɛb ir ɩ vʊr. Ban wõ a nɩtɔ̃, ba pʊʊr sɔ̃ɔ naa.

eng: When they came to Capernaum, the people who collected the half-shekel temple tax came to Peter and said, Doesnt your teacher pay the temple tax.
local: A Yesu nɩ a wʊ poturbo na wa ta a Kapenahum, a puorfʊ yir lõpodierbe wa 'yɛr a Pita, A yɩ wiwile maa yab a puorfʊ yir lõpoo bɩɩ.

eng: Yes, he said. But when they came into the house, Jesus spoke to Peter first.What do you think, Simon? From whom do earthly kings collect taxes, from their children or from strangers.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr, Ɔ̃ɔ, wʊ ma yab naa. Pita na wa ta a yir, Yesu lɛ de nie sʊʊr Simon, Bo lɛ fʊ tɩɛr? Ba boo lɛ ma yab a lõpoo kʊ a wɛr ju nãmɩne? A ba bibiir lɛ bɩɩ nɩyor lɛ ma yab?.

eng: From strangers, he said. Jesus said to him, Then the children dont have to pay.
local: Pita na 'yɛr ɩka nɩyor sɛ̃, lɛ Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, A lɛ a bibiir son ba mãɛ.

eng: But just so we dont offend them, go to the lake, throw out a fishing line and hook, and take the first fish you catch. When you open its mouth, you will find a shekel coin. Take it and pay the tax for both of us.
local: Tɩchɛ saa bʊɔrɔ ɩka sɩ sɔ̃ɔ a nɩbɛ banyãna pʊɔ ɛ, yɩ chen a man pɔ tɩ lɔb a jãpãɩ, yin wa ir a jãdɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ yɩ yuo a nɛɛ, yɩn nyɛn libir, yɩ de ya a sɩ lõpoo. De a jãm 'lɔ fʊn dɔ̃ɔ nie nyɔɔ, fʊ̃ʊ wa haa a wʊ nɛɛ fʊn nyɛn libibir, de a 'lɔ yab a sɩ lõpoo.

eng: Then Jesus spoke to the crowds and his disciples.
local: Yesu pãa 'yɛr kʊ a nɩyɔɔ bala nɩ a wʊ poturbo.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out early in the morning to hire workers for his vineyard.
local: Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ nyɛntãan fɩka daba kɔ̃ɔ na dɔ̃ɔ ŋmaa ir bibio jaa pipi yi ɩka wʊ tɩ bɔ nɩbɛ ka ba chen tɩ kɔ wʊ puo pɔ.

eng: The legal experts and the Pharisees sit on Moses seat.
local: A nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a Faraseemɩne jãnɛ na a Mosesɩ dakɔɔ ju.

eng: After he agreed with the workers to pay them a denarion, he sent them into his vineyard.
local: Wʊ sɔɔ naa ɩka wʊn yab ba naa a lɛ ban na de a bɩbir gbul ka wʊ tara ba chiin a wʊ puo pɔ.

eng: Therefore, you must take care to do everything they say. But dont do what they do.
local: A lɛ jũu, yɩ maala a lɛ ban wile tɩ na yɩ turo, tɩ yɩ ta maala a lɛ ban maala ɛ, bojũu ba maa turo a lɛ ban wile ɛ.

eng: Then he went out around nine in the morning and saw others standing around the marketplace doing nothing.
local: A mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ nɩ lɛ wa do bãlãa wʊ yɩn tɩ nyɛ a nɩbɛ bamɩne na ara a daa pɔ na baa maala bon jaa ɛ.

eng: For they tie together heavy packs that are impossible to carry. They put them on the shoulders of others, but are unwilling to lift a finger to move them.
local: Ba ma lũn tuor tɩɩr tuo nɩbɛ, tɩ maa sɔɔ de a ba nubir sɩɩr ɛ.

eng: He said to them, You also go into the vineyard, and Ill pay you whatever is right.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ mɩ chen a n puo pɔ tɩ ɩn yab yɩn naa a lɛ jaa na sɛɛn.

eng: Everything they do, they do to be noticed by others. They make extra-wide prayer bands for their arms and long tassels for their clothes.
local: Yele jaa ban tʊ̃nɔ̃, ba ma bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka nɩbɛ ba nyɛ ba, na ba ma lũ a fɔɔr a ba jur pɔ, tɩ na ba ma su fuusɩ ban ma sɛ bonsɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ jaa vɛvɛɛ.

eng: And they went. Again around noon and then at three in the afternoon, he did the same thing.
local: Ka ba sɔɔ chen. Tɩ wʊ lɛ yɩ go a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ na wa vɩrʊ̃ gar ju sãlsɔɔ nɩ a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ na wa suro a tɩɩr pɔ na wʊ tɩ bɔ a nɩbɛ bamɩne mɩ.

eng: They love to sit in places of honor at banquets and in the synagogues.
local: Bala wa lɔ̃ɔ taa dire, a ji'yɔ̃ɔnsɩ jie lɛ ba ma nʊ̃ ɩka ba jãnɛ, nɩ a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ jiir a jibɛrɛ jiir.

eng: Around five in the afternoon he went and found others standing around, and he said to them, Why are you just standing around here doing nothing all day long.
local: A jãanɔ'maar sɔ̃ɔ, wʊ lɛ yin tɩ nyɛ bamɩne mɩ na chɛnɛ jɛ̃ pɔrɔ jaa. Ka wʊ sʊʊr ba, Bo lɛ so a dɩna yɩn jãnɛ ba maala bon jaa ɛ.

eng: They love to be greeted with honor in the markets and to be addressed as Rabbi.
local: Ala tɩ na ba ma bʊɔrɔ ɩka bama puoro ba a dar pɔ, na ba ma bʊɔrɔ ɩka nɩbɛ ba bʊɔlɔ ba, Wiwiilbe.

eng: Because nobody has hired us, they replied. He responded, You also go into the vineyard.
local: A ba 'yɛr, Nɩrɛ jaa ba sʊɔr sɩ kɔb ɛ. Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ wa a sɩ chen a n puo pɔ a yɩ tɩ kɔ.

eng: When evening came, the owner of the vineyard said to his manager, Call the workers and give them their wages, beginning with the last ones hired and moving on finally to the first.
local: Jãanʊɔr na wa ta, a puo sʊɔ 'yɛr kʊ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na kaara a wʊ puo, Bʊɔl a tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ kʊ ba a ba libie, de nie piel a bala na bɩɛr wa tɩ tãn a bala na dɔ̃ɔ nie wa.

eng: When those who were hired at five in the afternoon came, each one received a denarion.
local: A bala na wa bɩɛr wa ãsʊɔ jaa den a bɩbir gbul libir.

eng: Now when those hired first came, they thought they would receive more. But each of them also received a denarion.
local: A dɔ̃ɔ nie dẽme na wa tɩ wa, ba tɩɛr ɩka ban nyɛn gar a lɛ, tɩchɛ a wʊ kʊ ãsʊɔ jaa mɩ bɩbir gbul libir.

eng: When they received it, they grumbled against the landowner.
local: Tɩchɛ ban wa de a lɛ baar, ba vʊ̃n nɛɛr 'yɔ̃ɔ a puo sʊɔ.

eng: These who were hired last worked one hour, and they received the same pay as we did even though we had to work the whole day in the hot sun.
local: na ba gɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a puo sʊɔ 'yɛrɛ, A nɩbɛ banyãna wa bɩɛr wa tɩ fʊ yab sɩ jãtaa, nɩ sɩn na ŋmaa ir nɩ ɔɔr wa tʊ̃ lɛ̃ɛɛ ka mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ ŋme sɩ tɩ mʊrʊ̃.

eng: But he replied to one of them, Friend, I did you no wrong. Didnt I agree to pay you a denarion?.
local: Ka wʊ lɩɛb sʊʊr a ba ãsʊɔ, N badaba, n ba ɩ fʊ dɛɛr ɛ, a ba ɩ yɩn lɛ sɔɔ ɩka yɩn den a bɩbir gbul libir ɛ?.

eng: Take what belongs to you and go. I want to give to this one who was hired last the same as I give to you.
local: De a fʊ libir chiine. Maa lɛ nʊ̃ naa kʊ a puor dẽme nɩtãa a lɛ ɩn kʊ yɩ.

eng: Dont I have the right to do what I want with what belongs to me? Or are you resentful because Im generous.
local: N ba tara sɔr na de a n libie ɩn a lɛ ɩn bʊɔrɔ bɩɩ? Bɩɩ fʊ tara nyuur nɩ a n maal vɩla.

eng: But you shouldnt be called Rabbi , because you have one teacher, and all of you are brothers and sisters.
local: Yɩ ta sɔɔ ka ba bʊɔlɔ yɩ, Wiwiilbe ɛ, nɩkpɛ̃ɛ bʊ̃'yen tɛɛ lɛ yɩ tara. Yɩ jaa ɩn yɛɛr.

eng: So those who are last will be first. And those who are first will be last.
local: A lɛ jũu, a puor dẽme na wa ben niem ka a nie dẽme mɩ wa be a puor.

eng: Dont call anybody on earth your father, because you have one Father, who is heavenly.
local: Yɩ ta bʊɔl nɩrɛ jaa yɩ, sãa a wɛr pɔ ɛ, bojũu yɩ tara na Sãa bʊ̃'yen na be a saaju.

eng: As Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, he took the Twelve aside by themselves on the road. He told them.
local: Yesu na wa pãa duoro a Jerusalɛm, wʊ tara na a poturbo pie nɩ ayi yin chɛ̃chɛ̃ɛ na wʊ tɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba.

eng: Dont be called teacher , because Christ is your one teacher.
local: Bɩɩ yɩ ta bʊɔl nɩrɛ jaa wiwile ɛ, bojũu yɩ tara na Wiwile bʊ̃'yen tɛɛ na ɩ a Nɩ-iraa.

eng: Look, we are going up to Jerusalem. The Human One will be handed over to the chief priests and legal experts. They will condemn him to death.
local: Nyɛ, sɩ ben duoro a Jerusalɛm ka ban tɩ de a maa Nɩsaal Bie 'yɔ̃ɔ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a nɛɛ wiwiilbe nuur pɔ.

eng: But the one who is greatest among you will be your servant.
local: A 'lɔ na bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ ɩ a yɩ kpɛ̃ɛ wʊ ɩ a yɩ tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃.

eng: They will hand him over to the Gentiles to be ridiculed, tortured, and crucified. But he will be raised on the third day.
local: Ban de man kʊ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, ka ba la ma, tʊrɔ ma, chɩrɛ nataar 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ma, na ba ge ma, tɩ kʊ ma, ka a ber ata daar ka n ir ɩ vʊr go.

eng: Then the mother of Zebedees sons came to Jesus along with her sons. Bowing before him, she asked a favor of him.
local: Be lɛ ka Jebedee pɔɔ tara a wʊ bibiir ayi waan a wʊ sɛ̃ wa lo gbur dumo a wʊ niem na wʊ sʊɔrɔ bon kɔ̃ɔ.

eng: All who lift themselves up will be brought low. But all who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na 'mɔɔrɔ a wʊ mãɛ ire nɩ, Nãaŋmɩn na suun wʊn pĩlem tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ mɩ na siir wʊ mãɛ Nãaŋmɩn na 'mɔɔ wʊn ka wʊ do.

eng: What do you want? he asked. She responded, Say that these two sons of mine will sit, one on your right hand and one on your left, in your kingdom.
local: Ka a Yesu sʊʊr wʊ, Bo lɛ fʊ bʊɔrɔ? Ka wʊ 'yɛr, Vɛ̃ ka a n bibiir banyã ayi ka ãsʊɔ jãnɛ a fʊ nũsʊ̃ɔ ka ãsʊɔ mɩ jãnɛ a fʊ gʊbaa jie a fʊ nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You shut people out of the kingdom of heaven. You dont enter yourselves, and you wont allow those who want to enter to do so.
local: Tɩchɛ nɛbier ben a yɩn nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a Faraseemɩne na ɩ a nɛɛr ayi dẽme sɛ̃. Yɩ pɔɔn a nɩbɛ var bar a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ. Yɩn mãɛ ba kpɩɛrɛ ɛ, tɩ na yɩ pɔɔ var a bala na bʊɔrɔ a kpɛfʊ bar.

eng: Jesus replied, You dont know what youre asking! Can you drink from the cup that Im about to drink from? They said to him, We can.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Faa bɔ̃ɔ a bon 'lɔ fʊn sʊɔrɔ ɛ. Ban tʊ̃ɔ nyũn a dɔɔyɛ ŋmele 'lɔ ɩn na nyũn ɔ? Ka ba 'yɛr, Sɩn tʊ̃ɔ naa.

eng: How terrible it will be for you, legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You travel over sea and land to make one convert. But when theyve been converted, they become twice the child of hell you are.
local: Nɛbier ben a yɩn nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a Faraseemɩne na ɩ a nɛɛr ayi dẽme sɛ̃. Yɩ ma yɔn jitɛbɛ nɩ a man pɔ na yɩ tɩ 'yɛr lɩɛb nɩrɛ tɩɛr, yɩn wa tʊ̃ɔ wʊ ka wʊ kaara a yɩ kaar, yɩ ma lɩɛb wʊ naa ka wʊn di dɔɔyɛ a bũu dagel pɔ a yɩ kaar.

eng: He said to them, You will drink from my cup, but to sit at my right or left hand isnt mine to give. It belongs to those for whom my Father prepared it.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩn tʊ̃ɔ nyũn a n ŋmele tɩchɛ maa kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ 'yɛr a buor sʊɔ na jãnɛ a n nũsʊ̃ɔ bɩɩ a n gʊbaa jie ɛ, ka a bala a n Sãa na chɔɔr kʊ tɛɛ ba lɛ ɛ.

eng: How terrible it will be for you blind guides who say, If people swear by the temple, its nothing. But if people swear by the gold in the temple, they are obligated to do what they swore.
local: Nɛbier ben a bala na ɩ jɔ̃n tɩ na ba tɔɔrɔ a jɔ̃n, na ba ma 'yɛr ɩka, Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na pɔn a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir a ba ɩ tuo ɛ, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ na pɔn a sɛlma 'lɔ na be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir a lũ wʊ naa.

eng: You foolish and blind people! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold holy?.
local: Yɩ ɩn jɔ̃n na yɩ ɩ jɔlɔ. Buor lɛ ɩ kpɛ̃ɛ, a sɛlma bɩɩ a Nãaŋmɩn yir 'lɔ na maal a sɛlma ka wʊ ɩ a chɛchɛ?.

eng: Now when the other ten disciples heard about this, they became angry with the two brothers.
local: A poturbo pie bala na wa wõ a nɩtɔ̃, ba nyɛn naa a yɛɛr ayi bala suur.

eng: But Jesus called them over and said, You know that those who rule the Gentiles show off their authority over them and their high-ranking officials order them around.
local: Ka a Yesu bʊɔl ba waan wa 'yɛr, Yɩ bɔ̃ɔn na ɩka a bala na kaara a nɩbɛ bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ lɛ kaara ba bon jaa pɔ, ka a ba nɩbɛrɛ tʊ̃ɔ ba.

eng: You say, If people swear by the altar, its nothing. But if they swear by the gift on the altar, they are obligated to do what they swore.
