local: Yɩ mɩ 'yɛr naa, Ala ɩ nɩrɛ pɔn na a Nãaŋmɩn bɔɔr lofʊ jie, a ba tuo ɛ, tɩchɛ ala ɩ nɩrɛ pɔn na a Nãaŋmɩn kʊfʊ 'lɔ na dɔɔl a jie, a lũ wʊ naa.

eng: You blind people! Which is greater, the gift or the altar that makes the gift holy?.
local: Yɩn jɔ̃n banyã. Buor lɛ ɩ kpɛ̃ɛ, a kʊfʊ bɩɩ a Nãaŋmɩn bɔɔr lofʊ jie na ma vɛ̃ ka wʊ ɩ chɛchɛ?.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Whoever wants to be great among you will be your servant.
local: A kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ be a lɛ a yɩ pɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ na bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ ɩ a yɩ kpɛ̃ɛ, wʊ ɩ a yɩ tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃.

eng: Whoever wants to be first among you will be your slave.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ mɩ na bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ ɩ a yɩ nɩdiere, wʊ ɩ a yɩ gbãgbaa.

eng: Therefore, those who swear by the altar swear by it and by everything thats on it.
local: A lɛ jũu, nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na pɔn a Nãaŋmɩn bɔɔr lofʊ jie, pɔn wʊ naa nɩ bon jaa na dɔɔl a wʊ ju.

eng: Those who swear by the temple swear by it and by everything thats part of it.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ mɩ na pɔn a Nãaŋmɩn yir, pɔn wʊ naa nɩ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na jãnɛ a be.

eng: just as the Human One didnt come to be served but rather to serve and to give his life to liberate many people.
local: Nɩtãa lɛ a maa Nɩsaal Bie na ba wa ɩka ba tʊ̃nɔ̃ kʊrɔ ma ɛ, tɩchɛ ka wʊ ɩ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ na wʊ de a n nyɛvʊr faan nɩyɔɔ yelbier bar.

eng: As Jesus and his disciples were going out of Jericho a large crowd followed him.
local: Yesu nɩ a wʊ poturbo na wa yire a Jeriko nɩyɔɔ turo wʊ naa.

eng: Those who swear by heaven swear by Gods throne and by the one who sits on it.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ mɩ na pɔn a saaju, pɔn naa a Nãaŋmɩn nãkɔɔ nɩ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ mɩ na jãnɛ a be.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You give to God a tenth of mint, dill, and cumin, but you forget about the more important matters of the Law: justice, peace, and faith. You ought to give a tenth but without forgetting about those more important matters.
local: Nɛbier ben a yɩn nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a Faraseemɩne na ɩ a nɛɛr ayi dẽme sɛ̃. Yɩ ma ir a bonsɩ pɔ jiir pie pɔ bʊ̃'yen lɔ̃ɔn badʊʊ gba vaar kʊ Nãaŋmɩn, tɩ na yɩ tɔr a Nãaŋmɩn nɛɛr ala na tɩrɛ bar na ɩ yelmãɛ sɔr tufʊ, nɩbaal kaafʊ nɩ yelmãɛ tarfʊ. A anyã lɛ a sɛɛ ɩka yɩ turo tɩ na yɩ taa ire amɩne bara ɛ.

eng: When two blind men sitting along the road heard that Jesus was passing by, they shouted, Show us mercy, Lord, Son of David.
local: Ka a jɔ̃n ayi jãnɛ a sɔr kõkoor. Ban wa wõ ɩka Yesu lɛ tala, ka ba gboolo, Sɩ Soro, David Bie wooo ɩ, kaa a sɩ nɩbaal.

eng: Now the crowd scolded them and told them to be quiet. But they shouted even louder, Show us mercy, Lord, Son of David.
local: Ka a nɩyɔɔ 'yɛr ɩka ba bar gɔ̃mɔ̃ tɩchɛ ka ba chɛnɛ gboolo, Sɩ Soro, David Bie wooo ɩ, kaa a sɩ nɩbaal.

eng: You blind guides! You filter out an ant but swallow a camel.
local: Yɩn jɔ̃tɔɔrbɔ ba nyã. Yɩ ma chuor a jãajubil bar tɩ ma vɔl a nyɔɔma.

eng: Jesus stopped in his tracks and called to them. What do you want me to do for you? he asked.
local: Yesu ara na tɩ bʊɔl ba sʊrɔ, Bo lɛ yɩ bʊɔrɔ ɩka n maal kʊ yɩ.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You clean the outside of the cup and plate, but inside they are full of violence and pleasure seeking.
local: Nɛbier ben a yɩn nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a Faraseemɩne na ɩ a nɛɛr ayi dẽme sɛ̃. Yɩ ma pɛɛn a ŋmanyuraa nɩ a ladiraa a yõo tɩchɛ ka a pʊɔ sɛɛn bɔgar nɩ nyuur.

eng: Lord, we want to see, they replied.
local: Ka ba 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Sɩ Soro, sɩ bʊɔrɔ na ɩka sɩ nyɛrɛ.

eng: Blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of the cup so that the outside of the cup will be clean too.
local: Yɩn Faraseemɩne na ɩ jɔ̃n. Yɩ ma dɔ̃ɔ pɛɛ a ŋmanyuraa pʊɔ tɩchɛ ka a yõo mɩ pãa pɛɛ a yõo ka a vɩɛlɛ.

eng: Jesus had compassion on them and touched their eyes. Immediately they were able to see, and they followed him.
local: Nɩbaal kpɛ̃n a Yesu, ka wʊ sɩɩr a ba nɩbie, ka ba nyɛrɛ na ba turo wʊ.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You are like whitewashed tombs. They look beautiful on the outside. But inside they are full of dead bones and all kinds of filth.
local: Nɛbier ben a yɩn nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a Faraseemɩne na ɩ a nɛɛr ayi dẽme sɛ̃. Yɩ kaara fɩka yar ban pãɛ ka a vɩɛlɛ, tɩchɛ ka a pʊɔ pɔ tara nɩbɛ kɔbɔ nɩ dɛɛr jaa bʊrɔ.

eng: Now Jesus left the temple and was going away. His disciples came to point out to him the temple buildings.
local: Yesu na wa yi a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir pɔ na wʊ chiine, a wʊ poturbo turo wile ɩka wʊ nyɛ lɛ ban mɛ a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir ka wʊ kaara.

eng: He responded, Do you see all these things? I assure that no stone will be left on another. Everything will be demolished.
local: Be lɛ a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Mɩ yɩ mɩ nyɛ naa anyãna a jaa? Yelmãɛ jaa lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, a kʊsɩvɩɛl anyãna ãsʊɔ jaa kʊ lɛ wa chɛnɛ dɔɔl a wʊ tɔ ju ɛ. Ban wa dãn a jaa lɔb.

eng: Now while Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came to him privately and said, Tell us, when will these things happen? What will be the sign of your coming and the end of the age.
local: Yesu na wa do jãnɛ a Olive Tɔ̃ɔju, a poturbo wan a wʊ sɛ̃ a ba tɛɛ wa sʊʊr wʊ, 'Yɛr kʊ sɩ a daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ a anyãna nɩ na ɩ. Bo lɛ na ɩ a jãnɛ a fʊ waa sɔ̃ɔ nɩ a wɛr baarfʊ daar.

eng: Jesus replied, Watch out that no one deceives you.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ bɔ̃ɔn sʊ̃ ka nɩrɛ jaa ta wa bɛl yɩ ɛ.

eng: Many will come in my name, saying, Im the Christ. They will deceive many people.
local: Bojũu, nɩyɔɔ na de naa maa yuor waan wa 'yɛrɛ, Maa lɛ a Nɩ-iraa, na ban bɛl nɩyɔɔ.

eng: You will hear about wars and reports of wars. Dont be alarmed. These things must happen, but this isnt the end yet.
local: Yɩn wa wõn tam, ka ba 'yɛrɛ a tam 'yɛr, yɩ ta mɩrɛ ɛ. A lɛ na ɩ naa, tɩchɛ a wɛr baarfʊ chɛnɛ na be puor.

eng: Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other, and there will be famines and earthquakes in all sorts of places.
local: Paal dẽdẽme na yin tam, ka a nãmɩne mɩ yɩ tam ka kɔ̃ mɩ lo ka a tẽe jiir amɩne dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃.

eng: But all these things are just the beginning of the sufferings associated with the end.
local: A dɔɔyɛ anyãna, an kaara fɩka a pɔɔ na ma dɔɔrɔ ka a wõnõ wʊ.

eng: They will arrest you, abuse you, and they will kill you. All nations will hate you on account of my name.
local: Ban pãa nyɔɔr yɩn chiin na ba tɩ pɔb yɩ na ba kʊ yɩ, ka a paalsɩ a jaa 'laar yɩ, yɩn sɔɔ ma jũu.

eng: In the same way you look righteous to people. But inside you are full of pretense and rebellion.
local: Lɛ nɩ wʊ ju mɩ lɛ a yɩn a yõo, yɩ ɩn nɩtor a yɩ taaba sɛ̃, tɩchɛ a yɩ pʊɔm, yɩ tara na nɛɛr ayi nɩ putuyele.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You build tombs for the prophets and decorate the graves of the righteous.
local: Nɛbier ben a yɩn nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a Faraseemɩne na ɩ a nɛɛr ayi dẽme sɛ̃. Yɩ mɛn a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ yar, tɩ na chɔɔr a bala na tor yar.

eng: At that time many will fall away. They will betray each other and hate each other.
local: Nɩyɔɔ na bar a ba sɔɔfʊ, na ba gar 'yɔ̃ɔ taa na ba 'laar taa.

eng: You say, If we had lived in our ancestors days, we wouldnt have joined them in killing the prophets.
local: tɩ na yɩ 'yɛr ɩka, Sɩn ta be be a sɩ sãakpãmɩne sɔ̃ɔ, sɩ kʊ̃ naa ta pʊɔ ba kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ ɛ.

eng: Many false prophets will appear and deceive many people.
local: Jir ŋmarba 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ na wan wa 'yɛrɛ bɛlɛ nɩyɔɔ.

eng: Because disobedience will expand, the love of many will grow cold.
local: Nɩyɔɔ nɩ na tɔr Nãaŋmɩn nɛɛ, ba kʊ̃ nʊ̃ taa ɛ.

eng: You testify against yourselves that you are children of those who murdered the prophets.
local: A nɩtɔ̃ jũu yɩ din a yɩ mãɛ dãasɩɛ ɩka yɩ ɩn a nɩbɛ bala na kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ bibiir.

eng: Go ahead, complete what your ancestors did.
local: Yɩ baar baar a lɛ a yɩ sãakpãmɩne na dɔ̃ɔ ɩ.

eng: But the one who endures to the end will be delivered.
local: Tɩchɛ nɩrɛ nyã na ara tɩ tãn a baaraa Nãaŋmɩn na faa wʊ naa.

eng: You snakes! You children of snakes! How will you be able to escape the judgment of hell?.
local: Yɩn waa bibiir, ŋmɩnɛ yɩn ɩrɛ wa chãɛ a bũu dagel kũu.

eng: Therefore, look, Im sending you prophets, wise people, and legal experts. Some of them you will kill and crucify. And some you will beat in your synagogues and chase from city to city.
local: A lɛ jũu ɩn tʊ̃n Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ, yɛ̃n dẽme, nɩ a wiwiilbe, ka yɩ kʊ bamɩne yɔɔl a daa ju, yɩn pɔɔn bamɩne na yɩ fɔb ba a yɩ lɔ̃ɔfʊ jiir, tɩ na yɩ pãa tulo yʊɔrɔ bʊɔrɔ ba a tẽn a jaa.

eng: Therefore, upon you will come all the righteous blood that has been poured out on the earth, from the blood of that righteous man Abel to the blood of Zechariah the son of Barachiah, whom you killed between the temple and the altar.
local: Ka a na di yɩ a nɩbɛ bala na ɩ a nɩtor ba bɔ̃ɔ yele jaa yɩn kʊ, piel a Abel jãɩ sɔ̃ɔ tɩ tãn a Jekariya bidaba Barakiya ban kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn bɔɔr lofʊ jie nɩ a puorfʊ jie chara pɔ.

eng: I assure you that all these things will come upon this generation.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, a yɩn dɩna nɩbɛ na nyɛn gefʊ a yele anyãna jũu.

eng: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! You who kill the prophets and stone those who were sent to you. How often I wanted to gather your people together, just as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you didnt want that.
local: Pa yɩn Jerusalɛm nɩbɛ, a yɩn na ma kʊrɔ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ na yɩ lɔɔrɔ a bala a Nãaŋmɩn na tʊ̃ a yɩ sɛ̃ nɩ kʊsɩbɛ, nyɛ lɛ an ma ɩrɛ ma vɔlaa jã ka n ma bʊɔrɔ ɩka n kaal a fʊ bibiir 'mataa fɩka nʊ̃ɔ na ma ũun a wʊ bil, tɩchɛ ka faa bʊɔrɔ ɩka fʊ maal a lɛ ɛ.

eng: Look, your house is left to you deserted.
local: A lɛ jũu n bar a fʊ tẽe kʊ fʊ ka wʊ ɩ dakpolo.

eng: I tell you, you wont see me until you say, Blessings on the one who comes in the Lords name .
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka yɩ kʊ̃ lɛ nyɛ ma go ɛ, wa tɩ tãn yɩn na 'yɛr ɩka, Nãaŋmɩn wʊ sʊ̃ʊ a 'lɔ wʊn tʊ̃. .

eng: This gospel of the kingdom will be proclaimed throughout the world as a testimony to all the nations. Then the end will come.
local: Ban mʊɔl a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyã a wɛr wʊ jaa ka a ɩ dãasɩɛ a paalsɩ a jaa pɔ, tɩchɛ ka a wɛr pãa baar.

eng: When you see the disgusting and destructive thing that Daniel talked about standing in the holy place.
local: Yɩn wa nyɛ naa a yele 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn na 'laar na waara nɩ sɔ̃ɔfʊ ka wʊ be a jichɛchɛ 'lɔ nɩtãa lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ Danɩɛl na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr. Vɛ̃ ka a nyã na kara bɔ̃ɔ a par.

eng: then those in Judea must escape to the mountains.
local: Yɩn wa nyɛ a lɛ a nɩbɛ bala na kpɩɛrɛ a Juda, ba jo do a tɔ̃ɔ ju.

eng: Those on the roof shouldnt come down to grab things from their houses.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa na be gar ju, wʊ ta suu a die pɔ tɩ de bon jaa ɛ.

eng: Those in the field shouldnt come back to grab their clothes.
local: 'Lɔ mɩ na be a puo pɔ wʊ ta kul a yir tɩ de a wʊ fuu ɛ.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant and for women who are nursing their children.
local: A bɩbir nyãna wa vɩɛ an ɩn tuo kʊ a pɔɔpʊʊr nɩ a bala na tara a bi'yɛnsɩ.

eng: Pray that it doesnt happen in winter or on the Sabbath day.
local: Yɩ sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn ka a yɩ jofʊ ta ɩ sãɛ ɛ, bɩɩ a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar ɛ.

eng: There will be great suffering such as the world has never before seen and will never again see.
local: Nãaŋmɩn na maal a wɛr wa tãn a pʊ̃pãanyã, baa di a dɔɔyɛ nyã bʊrɔ ɛ, bɩɩ dɔɔyɛ kʊ̃ lɛ wa jã wʊ go ɛ.

eng: If that time werent shortened, nobody would be rescued. But for the sake of the ones whom God chose, that time will be cut short.
local: Ala ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn ba ŋmaa a ber ala bar ɛ, nɩrɛ kʊ̃ lɛ chɛnɛ go ɛ. Tɩchɛ a bala wʊn chɛl ir jũu wʊn ŋmaan a ber bar.

eng: Then if somebody says to you, Look, heres the Christ, or Hes over here, dont believe it.
local: A lɛ daar nɩrɛ wa 'yɛr yɩ ɩka, Nyɛ a Nɩ-iraa, bɩɩ 'Lɔ nɩ nyã, yɩ ta sɔɔ de ɛ.

eng: False christs and false prophets will appear, and they will offer great signs and wonders in order to deceive, if possible, even those whom God has chosen.
local: Bojũu jirsɩ Nɩ-irsɩ nɩ jirsɩ 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ na wa naa wa tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ na ba bɛl nɩbɛ bamɩne ala ɩ ba tʊ̃ɔ naa ban 'mantaa bɛl a bala a Nãaŋmɩn na chɛl ir.

eng: Look, Ive told you ahead of time.
local: Yɩ nyɛ, n den nie 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ.

eng: So if they say to you, Look, hes in the desert, dont go out. And if they say, Look, hes in the rooms deep inside the house, dont believe it.
local: A lɛ jũu, bala wa 'yɛr kʊ yɩ ɩka, Nyɛ 'lɔ nɩ nyã a dãlpʊɔ ju, yɩ ta yi a be ɛ. Bala wa 'yɛr nyɛ, 'lɔ nɩ nyã a die pɔ, yɩ ta sɔɔ ɛ.

eng: Just as the lightning flashes from the east to the west, so it will be with the coming of the Human One.
local: A lɛ a saa nyɩɩrfʊ na ma yi a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ purfʊ jie na wʊ chaar 'yɔ̃ɔ a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ mʊrfʊ jie, lɛ lɛ a maa Nɩsaal Bie waa mɩ na kaara.

eng: The vultures gather wherever theres a dead body.
local: Jie 'lɔ jaa bʊ̃kũu na be, be lɛ a sɩdumo ma 'laara.

eng: Now immediately after the suffering of that time the sun will become dark, and the moon wont give its light. The stars will fall from the sky and the planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken.
local: Ajienaa, a ber anyãna a dɔɔyɛ baarfʊ puor, A mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ na lii naa, ka a chuu mɩ kʊ̃ lɛ chaar ɛ, ka a ŋmɛrbie pɔrɔ yire a saaju loro, ka a saaju bonsɩ a jaa dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃.

eng: Then the sign of the Human One will appear in the sky. At that time all the tribes of the earth will be full of sadness, and they will see the Human One coming in the heavenly clouds with power and great splendor.
local: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ ban nyɛ a maa Nɩsaal Bie na waara a julõjuur pɔ, ka a paalsɩ a jaa na be ka a wɛr pɔ kõnõ. Ka ba nyɛ a maa Nɩsaal Bie na yi a saaju a julõjuur pɔ, waara nɩ kpɛ̃ɔ nɩ tɩɩr.

eng: He will send his angels with the sound of a great trumpet, and they will gather his chosen ones from the four corners of the earth, from one end of the sky to the other.
local: Wʊn tʊ̃n a wʊ malakasɩ ka ba pɛɛl a ɩɩl kpɛ̃ɔ jaa na ba bʊɔl a nɩbɛ bala wʊn chɛl ir a wɛr wʊ jaa 'mataa.

eng: Learn this parable from the fig tree. After its branch becomes tender and it sprouts new leaves, you know that summer is near.
local: Yɩ de a pɔlpɔl tɩɛr yele jãn yɛ̃. 'Lɔ wa tʊɔrɔ a vaar, yɩ bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a tʊl sɔɔ gbʊr naa.

eng: In the same way, when you see all these things, you know that the Human One is near, at the door.
local: A lɛ nɩ wʊ ju mɩ lɛ, yɩn wa nyɛ a yelsɩ anyãna, yɩ bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a maa Nɩsaal Bie waa gbʊr naa jaa yɛ̃yɛ̃.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until all these things happen.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, a pʊ̃pãanyã nɩbɛ kʊ̃ kpi baar ɛ, wa tɩ tãn a yelsɩ anyãna na ɩ.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: A saaju nɩ a wɛr na wa baaraa, tɩchɛ maa 'yɛrbie 'lɔ kʊ̃ wa baar ɛ.

eng: But nobody knows when that day or hour will come, not the heavenly angels and not the Son. Only the Father knows.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa ba bɔ̃ɔ a bɩbir nyã ɛ, bɩɩ a sɔ̃ɔ ɛ, a malakasɩ mɩ gba na be a saaju ba bɔ̃ɔ ɛ, bɩɩ a Bie ɛ, ka a Sãa tɛɛ ba lɛ bɔ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: As it was in the time of Noah, so it will be at the coming of the Human One.
local: A lɛ an dɔ̃ɔ kaara a Nowa sɔ̃ɔ, lɛ lɛ an mɩ na kaara a maa Nɩsaal Bie waa sɔ̃ɔ.

eng: In those days before the flood, people were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day Noah entered the ark.
local: Bojũu sɛrɛ, tɩchɛ ka a kɔ̃paalaa wa nɩbɛ dire na, tɩ nyuro, ka dɔɔr nɩ pɔbɔ kulo taa, tɩ tãn a bɩbir 'lɔ a Nowa na kpɛ a daka bulomtum pɔ.

eng: They didnt know what was happening until the flood came and swept them all away. The coming of the Human One will be like that.
local: Baa bɔ̃ɔn ɛ, tɩ tãn a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ a kɔ̃paalaa na wa di ba, lɛ mɩ lɛ an kaara a maa Nɩsaal Bie waa daar.

eng: At that time there will be two men in the field. One will be taken and the other left.
local: Nɩbɛ ayi na ben puo pɔ, ka a Nãaŋmɩn wa de a ãsʊɔ chiin tɩ bar a ãsʊɔ.

eng: Two women will be grinding at the mill. One will be taken and the other left.
local: Pɔbɔ ayi na 'mantaa nɩɛnɛ jɔ̃ ka a Nãaŋmɩn wa de a ãsʊɔ chiin tɩ bar a ãsʊɔ.

eng: Therefore, stay alert! You dont know what day the Lord is coming.
local: A lɛ jũu yɩ kaara sʊ̃, bojũu yaa bɔ̃ɔ a bɩbir 'lɔ a yɩ Soro nɩ na wa ɛ.

eng: But you understand that if the head of the house knew at what time the thief would come, he would keep alert and wouldnt allow the thief to break into his house.
local: Tɩchɛ yɩ bɔ̃ɔn a yele anyã par, a yir sʊɔ ta bɔ̃ɔn a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ a nãnyie na waara a tãsɔɔ, wʊ kʊ̃ naa gur ɛ, ka a nãnyie wa kar kpɛ wʊ a yir ɛ.

eng: Therefore, you also should be prepared, because the Human One will come at a time you dont know.
local: A lɛ jũu, yɩ mɩ guro a yɩ mãɛ, bojũu a maa Nɩsaal Bie na wan a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ yɩn kʊ̃ bɔ̃ɔn ɛ.

eng: Who then are the faithful and wise servants whom their master puts in charge of giving food at the right time to those who live in his house?.
local: An lɛ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ɩ a yɛ̃ nɩ a yelmãɛ sʊɔ a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na 'yɔ̃ɔ ka wʊ kaara wʊ a yir na wʊ kʊrɔ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ a ba bʊ̃diir a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ wʊn 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ?.

eng: Happy are those servants whom the master finds fulfilling their responsibilities when he comes.
local: An ɩn bɔɔsʊ̃n kʊ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ nyãna a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na wa nyɛ ka wʊ ɩrɛ a lɛ.

eng: I assure you that he will put them in charge of all his possessions.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, wʊn vɛ̃n ka wʊ kaara a wʊ bonsɩ a jaa.

eng: But suppose those bad servants should say to themselves, My master wont come until later.
local: Tɩchɛ ala ɩ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ nyãna ɩn putusʊɔ na wʊ tɩɛr a wʊ nyãa pɔ ɩka, a n nɩkpɛ̃ɛ kor naa.

eng: And suppose they began to beat their fellow servants and to eat and drink with the drunks?.
local: tɩ piel gere a tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ bala na be a yir tɩ pãa ma di na wʊ ma pʊɔ a dãnyurbo pɔ.

eng: The master of those servants will come on a day when they are not expecting him, at a time they couldnt predict.
local: a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na wa dũu wʊ naa a bɩbir 'lɔ wʊn ba tɩɛr nɩ a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ wʊn ba bɔ̃ɔn ɛ.

eng: He will cut them in pieces and put them in a place with the hypocrites. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Wʊn ŋma wʊn gbʊl gbʊl 'yɔ̃ɔ a nɛɛr ayi dẽme pɔ, be lɛ ban kõnõ tɩ ɔɔrɔ nyɩmɛ.

eng: When they approached Jerusalem and came to Bethphage on the Mount of Olives, Jesus gave two disciples a task.
local: Ban wa gbʊrɔ a Jerusalɛm nɩ a Betefagɩ a Olive Tɩɩr Tɔ̃ɔ ju, Yesu tʊ̃n a wʊ poturbo ayi.

eng: He said to them, Go into the village over there. As soon as you enter, you will find a donkey tied up and a colt with it. Untie them and bring them to me.
local: na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ chen a tẽbile 'lɔ na be a yɩ niem, yɩn tɩ nyɛn bʊ̃ɔ ban lũ ka a bʊ̃pɔl ara kɔɔ wʊ, yɩ for yaa waan.

eng: At that time the kingdom of heaven will be like ten young bridesmaids who took their lamps and went out to meet the groom.
local: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃, a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ na kaara fɩka pɔɔsarsɩ pie na de a ba fãtɩnɛ ɩka ba tɩ tuor a pɔɔ paalaa nɩ a wʊ sɩrɛ.

eng: If anyone says anything to you, say that their master needs them. He sent them off right away.
local: Ala ɩ nɩrɛ wa sʊʊr yɩ naa, Ŋmɩnɛ a yɩ foro a bʊ̃ɔ? Yɩ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, A Sɩ Soro lɛ bʊɔrɔ yaa. Wʊn bar sɔr ka yɩ foraa waan fɔɔ jaa.

eng: Now five of them were wise, and the other five were foolish.
local: Ka a ba anũu ɩ jɔlɔ, tɩchɛ ka a ba anũu mɩ ɩ yɛ̃ dẽme.

eng: The foolish ones took their lamps but didnt bring oil for them.
local: A jɔlɔ bala den a ba fãtɩnɛ tɩchɛ baa de kãa pʊɔ ɛ.

eng: But the wise ones took their lamps and also brought containers of oil.
local: tɩchɛ ka a ba anũu bala mɩ na ɩ a yɛ̃ dẽme, de kãa pʊɔ a ba fãtɩnɛ pɔ.

eng: When the groom was late in coming, they all became drowsy and went to sleep.
local: A pɔɔ paalaa sɩrɛ na wa kor a waa jũu, gʊ̃ɔ kpɛ ba naa ka ba gã gur.

eng: But at midnight there was a cry, Look, the groom! Come out to meet him.
local: A tãsɔɔ bãɩn lɛ ba ŋme chɛlma tãna 'yɛrɛ, A pɔɔ paalaa sɩrɛ waara, yɩ yi wa tuor wʊ yooo.

eng: Then all those bridesmaids got up and prepared their lamps.
local: A pɔɔsarsɩ pie banyãna vaan ir tɩɛ tɩɛ a ba gonsɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ a ba fãtɩnɛ pɔ.

eng: But the foolish bridesmaids said to the wise ones, Give us some of your oil, because our lamps have gone out.
local: A jɔlɔ bala 'yɛr kʊ a yɛ̃ dẽme, Yɩ pʊr a yɩ kãa amɩne kʊ sɩ, a sɩ bonsɩ kpĩine naa.

eng: But the wise bridesmaids replied, No, because if we share with you, there wont be enough for our lamps and yours. We have a better idea. You go to those who sell oil and buy some for yourselves.
local: Be lɛ ka a yɛ̃ dẽme bala 'yɛr kʊ ba, Kai, a kʊ̃ ta sɩ nɩ a yɩn ɛ. Yɩ chen a be ban daara na yɩ tɩ da.

eng: Now this happened to fulfill what the prophet said.
local: A yele anyã ɩ na ka a na ɩ yelmãɛ a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr na wʊ 'yɛrɛ.

eng: But while they were gone to buy oil, the groom came. Those who were ready went with him into the wedding. Then the door was shut.
local: Tɩchɛ, ban lɩɛb chiine a dafʊ jie, a pɔɔ paalaa sɩrɛ wa naa, ka a bala jaa na chɔɔr 'mataa kpɛ a pɔɔ paalaa kulfʊ jie, tɩ na ba nyɔɔ a pãn pɔɔ.

eng: Later the other bridesmaids came and said, Lord, lord, open the door for us.
local: A jɔlɔ anũu bala mɩ wa bɩɛr wa na ba mɩ wa kpooro a pãn 'yɛr, Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, yuo a pãn ka sɩ kpɛ.

eng: But he replied, I tell you the truth, I dont know you.
local: Tɩchɛ ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yelmãɛ jaa, n ba bɔ̃ɔ yɩ ɛ.

eng: Therefore, keep alert, because you dont know the day or the hour.
local: A lɛ jũu yɩ gu yɩ mãɛ bojũu yaa bɔ̃ɔ a bɩbir bɩɩ a sɔ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a man who was leaving on a trip. He called his servants and handed his possessions over to them.
local: A mɩ lɛ nyɛntãan go fɩka daba kɔ̃ɔ na bʊɔrɔ yɔfʊ na wʊ bʊɔl a wʊ gbãgbaar 'mataa de a wʊ bonsɩ põ ba.

eng: To one he gave five valuable coins, and to another he gave two, and to another he gave one. He gave to each servant according to that servants ability. Then he left on his journey.
local: Wʊ kʊn ãsʊɔ sɛlbie anũu, na wʊ kʊ ãsʊɔ mɩ sɛlbie ayi na wʊ kʊ ãsʊɔ mɩ sɛlbir bʊ̃'yen. Wʊ ma kʊn ãsʊɔ jaa man a wʊ fɔ̃ɔ, tɩ na wʊ yɔ.

eng: After the man left, the servant who had five valuable coins took them and went to work doing business with them. He gained five more.
local: A 'lɔ na de a sɛlbie anũu tɩ yar nyɛ sɛlbie anũu tʊ̃nɔ̃ dɔɔl.

eng: In the same way, the one who had two valuable coins gained two more.
local: A 'lɔ mɩ na de a sɛlbie ayi tɩ yar mɩ nyɛ sɛlbie ayi tʊ̃nɔ̃ dɔɔl.

eng: Say to Daughter Zion, Look, your king is coming to you, humble and riding on a donkey, and on a colt the donkeys offspring. .
local: 'Yɛr kʊ a Jerusalɛm nɩbɛ, Nyɛ, a yɩ nãa waara na a yɩ sɛ̃. Wʊ siir wʊ mãɛ na wʊ do bʊ̃ɔ. Bʊ̃pɔl bile lɛ wʊ do waara nɩ. .

eng: The disciples went and did just as Jesus had ordered them.
local: A poturbo chen tɩ tʊ̃ a lɛ a Yesu na 'yɛr ɩka ba ɩ.

eng: They brought the donkey and the colt and laid their clothes on them. Then he sat on them.
local: Ba waan na a bʊ̃ɔ nɩ a bʊ̃pɔl bile na ba de a ba fɔɔr 'yɩɛr dɔɔl, ka wʊ do jãnɛ a bʊ̃pɔl ju.

eng: Now a large crowd spread their clothes on the road. Others cut palm branches off the trees and spread them on the road.
local: Nɩyɔɔ 'yɩɛr a ba fɔɔr a sɔr pɔ ka bamɩne mɩ 'mɛl vaɩ 'yɩɛr a sɔr pɔ.

eng: The crowds in front of him and behind him shouted, Hosanna to the Son of David! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest.
local: A nɩyɔɔ bala na be a niem nɩ a bala na turo a puor chɩlɛ na 'yɛrɛ, Yãan yooo, Nãa David Bie! Nãaŋmɩn wʊ sʊ̃ʊ a 'lɔ wʊn tʊ̃! Yãan yooo, dãnfʊ wʊ ta a saaju Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: And when Jesus entered Jerusalem, the whole city was stirred up. Who is this? they asked.
local: Wʊn wa kpɛ a Jerusalɛm tẽe pɔ, a nɩbɛ ba jaa ɩrɛ na yulõbʊmm, na ba sʊrɔ, An lɛ a nyã.

eng: The crowds answered, Its the prophet Jesus from Nazareth in Galilee.
local: Ka a nɩyɔɔ bala na turo wʊ 'yɛr, Nyã lɛ ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ, Yesu na yi a Najaretɩ na be Galilee.

eng: Then Jesus went into the temple and threw out all those who were selling and buying there. He pushed over the tables used for currency exchange and the chairs of those who sold doves.
local: Yesu kpɛ naa a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir bɛchaar pɔ, na wʊ dire a bala na chiin a bonsɩ tɩ daara nɩ a bala na daara baraa, tɩ na wʊ dara a libitɛɛrbɛ tɛɛbʊlsɩ nɩ a bala na daara a papar dakɔɔr lɔɔrɔ.

eng: He said to them, Its written, My house will be called a house of prayer. But youve made it a hideout for crooks.
local: Wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, A sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, A n yir na ɩ naa Nãaŋmɩn sʊɔrfʊ jie, tɩchɛ Ka yɩn maal wʊ ka wʊ ɩ nãnyisɩ sɔɔlfʊ jie. .

eng: People who were blind and lame came to Jesus in the temple, and he healed them.
local: Jɔ̃n nɩ gbɛrsɩ chen a be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir ka wʊ sãa ba.

eng: But when the chief priests and legal experts saw the amazing things he was doing and the children shouting in the temple, Hosanna to the Son of David! they were angry.
local: Tɩchɛ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a nɛɛ wiwiilbe na nyɛ a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ wʊn tʊ̃ nɩ a bibiir na ɩrɛ a gɔ̃mɔ̃ a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir pɔ ɩka, Yãan yooo! Nãa David Bie. Ka a ba pʊʊr sɔ̃ɔ.

eng: They said to Jesus, Do you hear what these children are saying? Yes, he answered. Havent you ever read, From the mouths of babies and infants youve arranged praise for yourself? .
local: Ba sʊɔr a Yesu, Fʊ wõn a lɛ a bibiir banyãna na 'yɛrɛ ɔ? Ka wʊ 'yɛr, Ɔ̃ɔ, yaa dɔ̃ɔ kar ɩka, A bibiir nɩ bipɩlɛ nɛgãma pɔ lɛ yɩ bʊɔrɔ pɛɛfʊ ɛ? .

eng: Then he left them and went out of the city to Bethany and spent the night there.
local: Wʊ bar ba naa tɩ yi chen a Betanɩ tẽe pɔ tɩ gã a be.

eng: Early in the morning as Jesus was returning to the city, he was hungry.
local: Wʊn wa lɩɛb chen a tẽkpɛ̃ɛ pɔ a bibio, kɔ̃ kpɛ wʊ naa.

eng: He saw a fig tree along the road, but when he came to it, he found nothing except leaves. Then he said to it, Youll never again bear fruit! The fig tree dried up at once.
local: Wʊ nyɛn kɔ̃kɔ̃ɔ tɩɛ na ara a sɔr kõkoor, a wʊ chen tɩ ta ka wɔ̃mɔ̃ ba be be ɛ, tɩ wʊ ɩ va nara tɛɛ. Ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a tɩɛ, Fʊ kʊ̃ lɛ wɔ̃ wɔ̃mɔ̃ go ɛ! Ka a tɩɛ juɔl kpi ajienaa.

eng: When the disciples saw it, they were amazed. How did the fig tree dry up so fast? they asked.
local: A poturbo na wa nyɛ a tɩɛ na kpi, nɛɛr 'maa ba naa ka ba 'yɛrɛ, Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ a tɩɛ ɩ juɔl ajienaa a lɛ.

eng: But the servant who had received the one valuable coin dug a hole in the ground and buried his masters money.
local: Tɩchɛ a 'lɔ na de a sɛlbir bʊ̃'yen tɩ tuun bɔɔ de a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ sɛlbir ũu.

eng: Now after a long time the master of those servants returned and settled accounts with them.
local: An wa kor bãlãa ka a gbãgbaar bala nɩkpɛ̃ɛ wa wa ɩka ba guor a sɛlbie yele.

eng: The one who had received five valuable coins came forward with five additional coins. He said, Master, you gave me five valuable coins. Look, Ive gained five more.
local: A 'lɔ na de a sɛlbie anũu waan na sɛlbie anũu wa 'yɛr, Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, sɛlbie anũu lɛ fʊ dɔ̃ɔ kʊ ma, nyɛ sɛlbie anũu tɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ n nyɛ dɔɔl.

eng: His master replied, Excellent! You are a good and faithful servant! Youve been faithful over a little. Ill put you in charge of much. Come, celebrate with me.
local: Ka a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Fʊ tʊ̃n vɩla, gbãgbamãɛ lɛ fʊ ɩ. Fʊ tara na yelmãɛ a bʊ̃bile pɔ, ɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ fʊn ka fʊ kaara bʊ̃yɔɔ. Wa pʊɔ ma ka sɩ 'mataa dire a nʊ̃ɔ.

eng: The second servant also came forward and said, Master, you gave me two valuable coins. Look, Ive gained two more.
local: A 'lɔ mɩ wʊn kʊ a sɛlbie ayi wa 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, Fʊ dɔ̃ɔ kʊ ma sɛlbie ayi, nyɛ sɛlbie ayi tʊ̃nɔ̃ lɛ n nyɛ dɔɔl.

eng: His master replied, Well done! You are a good and faithful servant. Youve been faithful over a little. Ill put you in charge of much. Come, celebrate with me.
local: Ka a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Fʊ tʊ̃n vɩla, gbãgbamãɛ lɛ fʊ ɩ. Fʊ tara na yelmãɛ a bʊ̃bile pɔ, ɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ fʊn ka fʊ kaara bʊ̃yɔɔ. Wa pʊɔ ma ka sɩ 'mataa dire a nʊ̃ɔ.

eng: Now the one who had received one valuable coin came and said, Master, I knew that you are a hard man. You harvest grain where you havent sown. You gather crops where you havent spread seed.
local: A 'lɔ mɩ wʊn kʊ a sɛlbir bʊ̃'yen wa 'yɛr naa, Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, n bɔ̃ɔn na fʊn ɩ nɩbikõo sʊɔ na fʊ ma ŋma a be fʊn ba bʊr ɛ, na fʊ ma wob a be fʊn maa yaar bar ɛ.

eng: So I was afraid. And I hid my valuable coin in the ground. Here, you have whats yours.
local: Ala lɛ so ka dãbãɛ kpɛ ma ka de a fʊ sɛlbir tɩ ũu bar. Nyɛ a fʊ sɛlbir.

eng: Jesus responded, I assure you that if you have faith and dont doubt, you will not only do what was done to the fig tree. You will even say to this mountain, Be lifted up and thrown into the lake. And it will happen.
local: Ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, yɩn tara sɔɔfʊ na yaa baal ɛ, a ba ɩ a lɛ na tʊ̃ kʊ a tɩɛ nyã tɛɛ lɛ yɩn tʊ̃ ɛ, tɩchɛ yɩn gba 'yɛr kʊ a tɔ̃ɔ nyãna, Ir suu a man pɔ, tɩ wʊ̃n ir naa.

eng: His master replied, You evil and lazy servant! You knew that I harvest grain where I havent sown and that I gather crops where I havent spread seed?.
local: Ka a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Fʊ̃ʊ gbãgbaputuo nɩ koro sʊɔ nyã. Fʊn ben bɔ̃ɔn ɩka n ma ŋman a be ɩn maa bʊr ɛ, naa wob a jie 'lɔ ɩn maa yaar bar ɛ.

eng: If you have faith, you will receive whatever you pray for.
local: Yɩn wa sʊɔr bon jaa tɩ tara sɔɔfʊ, yɩn nyɛ naa.

eng: In that case, you should have turned my money over to the bankers so that when I returned, you could give me what belonged to me with interest.
local: a ta sɛɛn ɩka fʊ ta de a n sɛlbir tɩ yɔ̃ɔ a libie yir pɔ, ka maa ta wa lɩɛb wa ka n nyɛ tʊ̃nɔ̃ dɔɔl ɛ?.

eng: Therefore, take from him the valuable coin and give it to the one who has ten coins.
local: Yɩ faa a sɛlbir bʊ̃'yen a wʊ sɛ̃ na yɩ kʊ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na tara a sɛlbie pie.

eng: Those who have much will receive more, and they will have more than they need. But as for those who dont have much, even the little bit they have will be taken away from them.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na tara yɔɔ, ban kʊ wʊn yɔɔ pʊɔ ka ba tara yɔɔ tɩchɛ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ba tara bon jaa ɛ, a bãlãa 'lɔ wʊn tara gba ban faan yin a wʊ sɛ̃.

eng: Now take the worthless servant and throw him out into the farthest darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: A gbãgbaburbur nyã 'lɔ, yɩ de wʊ lɔb bar yõo be lɛ ban kõnõ tɩ ɔɔrɔ nyɩmɛ.

eng: Now when the Human One comes in his majesty and all his angels are with him, he will sit on his majestic throne.
local: A maa Nɩsaal Bie wa wa a n tɩɩr pɔ, nɩ a n malakasɩ ba jaa, ɩn wa jãnɛ naa, a n tɩɩr nãalʊ̃ʊ dakɔɔ ju.

eng: All the nations will be gathered in front of him. He will separate them from each other, just as a shepherd separates the sheep from the goats.
local: A paalsɩ a jaa na pãa lɔ̃ɔ taa a n niem, ka n pãa oor a nɩbɛ nɩtãa lɛ pɛchinɛ na ma oor a piir nɩ a bʊʊr.

eng: He will put the sheep on his right side. But the goats he will put on his left.
local: Ɩn oon a piir bar a n nũsʊ̃ɔ jie, tɩchɛ ka a bʊʊr mɩ ara a n gʊbaa jie.

eng: Then the king will say to those on his right, Come, you who will receive good things from my Father. Inherit the kingdom that was prepared for you before the world began.
local: Ka n pãa 'yɛr kʊ a bala na be a n nũsʊ̃ɔ jie, Yɩ wa, yɩn a n Sãa Nãaŋmɩn na sʊ̃ʊ. Yɩ wa kpɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ, de a yɩ nãbomo wʊn chɔɔr bin gun yɩ a wɛr pielfʊ sɔ̃ɔ.

eng: I was hungry and you gave me food to eat. I was thirsty and you gave me a drink. I was a stranger and you welcomed me.
local: Bojũu kɔ̃ dɔ̃ɔ kpɛ ma, ka yɩ kʊ ma ka n di, kɔ̃nyũur dɔ̃ɔ kpɛ ma, ka yɩ kʊ ma kʊ̃ɔ ka n nyũ, n dɔ̃ɔ ɩ sãan ka yɩ ir ma sãan.

eng: I was naked and you gave me clothes to wear. I was sick and you took care of me. I was in prison and you visited me.
local: N ba dɔ̃ɔ tara bʊ̃sursɩ ɛ, ka yɩ kʊ ma fuu ka n su, n dɔ̃ɔ bɩɛrɛ ka yɩ sãa ma. N dɔ̃ɔ be pɔɔfʊ die pɔ, ka yɩ wa piir ma kaa.

eng: Then those who are righteous will reply to him, Lord, when did we see you hungry and feed you, or thirsty and give you a drink?.
local: A nɩmɩn na pãa lɩɛb sʊʊr wʊ, Sɩ Soro, nyɩnɛ lɛ sɩ nyɛ fʊ, ka a kɔ̃ kpɛ fʊ, ka sɩ kʊ fʊ bʊ̃diir a fʊ di, bɩɩ ka kɔ̃nyũur kpɛ fʊ, ka sɩ kʊ fʊ kʊ̃ɔ a fʊ nyũ?.

eng: When did we see you as a stranger and welcome you, or naked and give you clothes to wear?.
local: Sɔ̃ɔ buor lɛ sɩ nyɛ fʊ, ka fʊ ɩ sãan ka sɩ ir fʊ a sãan, bɩɩ ka faa tara bʊ̃sursɩ ɛ, ka sɩ kʊ fʊ fuu a fʊ su?.

eng: When did we see you sick or in prison and visit you.
local: Sɔ̃ɔ buor lɛ sɩ nyɛ fʊ, ka fʊ bɩɛrɛ, ka sɩ tɩ piir fʊ kaa, bɩɩ sɔ̃ɔ buor lɛ ba nyɔɔ fʊ pɔɔ ka sɩ chen tɩ piir fʊ kaa.

eng: Then the king will reply to them, I assure you that when you have done it for one of the least of these brothers and sisters of mine, you have done it for me.
local: A nãa na 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, a lɛ yɩn dɔ̃ɔ maal kʊ a nɩba'yɔ̃ɔnsɩ bala na ɩ a n sofʊ, maa lɛ yɩ maal kʊ.

eng: Then he will say to those on his left, Get away from me, you who will receive terrible things. Go into the unending fire that has been prepared for the devil and his angels.
local: Tɩchɛ na wʊ pãa 'yɛr kʊ a bala mɩ na be a wʊ gʊbaa jie, Pɔ 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ turo yɩ naa, yɩ tɔɔ chen be na yɩ tɩ kpɛ a bũu 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn na gbɔɔ gun a tɩɩ nɩ a wʊ malakasɩ.

eng: I was hungry and you didnt give me food to eat. I was thirsty and you didnt give me anything to drink.
local: Bojũu kɔ̃ dɔ̃ɔ kpɛ ma, ka yaa kʊ ma bon jaa ka n di ɛ. Kɔ̃nyũur dɔ̃ɔ kpɛ ma, ka yaa kʊ ma kʊ̃ɔ ka n nyũ ɛ.

eng: I was a stranger and you didnt welcome me. I was naked and you didnt give me clothes to wear. I was sick and in prison, and you didnt visit me.
local: N dɔ̃ɔ ɩ sãan, ka yaa kaa ma sãan kaa fʊ ɛ, n ba tara bon jaa na su ɛ, ka yaa kʊ ma fuu ka su ɛ, ka n bɩɛrɛ, bɩɩ be pɔɔfʊ die pɔ, ka yaa wa piir ma kaa ɛ.

eng: Then they will reply, Lord, when did we see you hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in prison and didnt do anything to help you.
local: Ka ba pãa mɩ lɩɛb sʊʊr wʊ, Sɩ Soro, sɔ̃ɔ buor lɛ sɩ nyɛ fʊ, ka a kɔ̃ kpɛ fʊ, bɩɩ ka a kɔ̃nyũur kpɛ fʊ, bɩɩ ka fʊ ɩ sãan, bɩɩ ka faa tara bon jaa na su ɛ, bɩɩ ka fʊ bɩɛrɛ, bɩɩ ka fʊ be pɔɔfʊ die pɔ, ka saa kaa fʊ ɛ.

eng: Then he will answer, I assure you that when you havent done it for one of the least of these, you havent done it for me.
local: Wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, yɩn ba maal kʊ a nɩba'yɔ̃ɔnsɩ bala ãsʊɔ ɛ, maa lɛ yaa maal kʊ ɛ.

eng: And they will go away into eternal punishment. But the righteous ones will go into eternal life.
local: A nɩbɛ banyãna na chen a dɔɔyɛ 'lɔ na ba tara baarfʊ jie, tɩchɛ ka a nɩmɩn mɩ chen a nyɛvʊr na ba tara baarfʊ jie.

eng: When Jesus entered the temple, the chief priests and elders of the people came to him as he was teaching. They asked, What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority.
local: Yesu na wa tɩ kpɛ a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir bɛchaar pɔ na wʊ wile, ka a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Juu nɩbɛrɛ wa a wʊ sɛ̃ a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ wʊn wile wa sʊʊr wʊ, Bo kpɛ̃ɔ lɛ fʊ de tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ a anyã nɩ an lɛ kʊ fʊ a sɔr ɩka fʊ tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ a anyã.

eng: Jesus replied, I have a question for you. If you tell me the answer, Ill tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, N mɩ na sʊʊr yɩn sʊʊrfʊ bʊ̃'yen. Yɩn wa tʊ̃ɔ wiil ma a par, n mɩ na wiil yɩ naa a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na kʊ ma a kpɛ̃ɔ ka tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ a anyãna.

eng: Where did John get his authority to baptize? Did he get it from heaven or from humans? They argued among themselves, If we say from heaven, hell say to us, Then why didnt you believe him.
local: A Jɔɔn na dɔ̃ɔ suoro a nɩbɛ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ, Nãaŋmɩn lɛ kʊ wʊ a sɔr bɩɩ nɩbɛ lɛ kʊ wʊ a sɔr? Yɩ 'yɛr n kaa? Ka ba piel chɩrɛ taa, Sɩn be 'yɛr ɩka Nãaŋmɩn lɛ tʊ̃ wʊ wʊn sʊʊr sɩ naa ka bo lɛ so ka saa sɔɔ de wʊ ɛ?.

eng: But we cant say from humans because were afraid of the crowd, since everyone thinks John was a prophet.
local: Tɩchɛ sɩn mɩ 'yɛr ɩka nɩrɛ lɛ tʊ̃ wʊ a ba ɩ ɛ, sɩ joro naa a nɩbɛ dãbãɛ bojũu nɩrɛ jaa sɔɔ naa ɩka a Jɔɔn ɩn Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ.

eng: Then they replied, We dont know. Jesus also said to them, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Be lɛ a ba 'yɛr kʊ a Yesu, Saa bɔ̃ɔ ɛ. Ka a Yesu mɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba, N mɩ kʊ̃ 'yɛr kʊ yɩ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na kʊ ma a kpɛ̃ɔ ka n tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ a anyãna ɛ.

eng: Jesus responded by speaking again in parables.
local: Yesu lɛ lɔɔrɔ na sʊkpar kʊrɔ a nɩbɛ na wʊ 'yɛrɛ.

eng: What do you think? A man had two sons. Now he came to the first and said, Son, go and work in the vineyard today.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ yɩ tɩɛr? Daba lɛ tara bibiir ayi, na wʊ chen tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a dɔ̃ɔ sʊɔ, N Bie chen tɩ tʊ̃ a n puo tʊ̃mɔ̃ a dɩna.

eng: No, I dont want to, he replied. But later he changed his mind and went.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr, N kʊ̃ chen ɛ, tɩchɛ na wʊ ta sɔ̃ɔ lɩɛb a wʊ tɩɛr na wʊ chen.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a king who prepared a wedding party for his son.
local: A Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ nyɛntãan fɩka nãa kɔ̃ɔ na maal a wʊ bidaba na kulo pɔɔ paalaa bʊ̃diir.

eng: He sent his servants to call those invited to the wedding party. But they didnt want to come.
local: Wʊ tʊ̃n a wʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ ɩka ba tɩ 'yɛr a bala wʊn bʊɔl a pɔɔ paalaa jie, tɩchɛ a bala wʊn bʊɔl ka ba tɔr.

eng: Again he sent other servants and said to them, Tell those who have been invited,Look, the meal is all prepared. Ive butchered the oxen and the fattened cattle. Now everythings ready. Come to the wedding party.
local: Wʊ lɛ tʊ̃n nɩyor ɩka ba tɩ 'yɛr a bala wʊn bʊɔl, Nyɛ, n maal a pɔɔ paalaa bʊ̃diir baar, n kʊn a n nadar nɩ a napɔl ka yele jaa chɔɔr baar, yɩ wa a pɔɔ paalaa jie.

eng: But they paid no attention and went away—some to their fields, others to their businesses.
local: Tɩchɛ ka ba tɔr ka nyã jaa ma chen wʊ chenfʊ jie, ka ãsʊɔ mɩ chen a wʊ puo pɔ, ka ãsʊɔ mɩ chen a wʊ yar jie.

eng: The father said the same thing to the other son, who replied, Yes, sir. But he didnt go.
local: A sãa lɛ chen tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a ayi sʊɔ a lɛ nɩ wʊ ju mɩ, ka wʊ 'yɛr, N chen naa, tɩ na wʊ tɔr.

eng: Which one of these two did his fathers will? They said, The first one. Jesus said to them, I assure you that tax collectors and prostitutes are entering Gods kingdom ahead of you.
local: A bibiir banyãna ayi, buor sʊɔ lɛ tʊ̃ a wʊ sãa bɔfʊ? Ka ba 'yɛr, A dɔ̃ɔ sʊɔ lɛ. Ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yelmãɛ jaa lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ a lõpodierbe nɩ a pɔɔ baar na dɔ̃ɔ yɩn nie kpɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.

eng: For John came to you on the righteous road, and you didnt believe him. But tax collectors and prostitutes believed him. Yet even after you saw this, you didnt change your hearts and lives and you didnt believe him.
local: Jɔɔn Kɔ̃suoro wan wa wile yɩ a yelmãɛ sɔr ka yaa sɔɔ de wʊ ɛ, tɩchɛ a lõpodierbe nɩ a pɔɔ baar sɔɔn de wʊ, yɩn sɔɔ wa nyɛ gba yaa lɩɛb a yɩ tɩɛr sɔɔ de wʊ ɛ.

eng: Listen to another parable. There was a landowner who planted a vineyard. He put a fence around it, dug a winepress in it, and built a tower. Then he rented it to tenant farmers and took a trip.
local: Yɩ lɛ chɛlɛ a sʊkpa ãsʊɔ. Daba lɛ kɔ wʊ puo na wʊ. Wʊ wɔɔn a puo wʊ jaa jilʊ̃, na wʊ tuu bɔɔ ka ban ma pɔr a wɔ̃mɔ̃ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ na ba nɛŋmɛr ir a kʊ̃ɔ. Wʊ mɛn jie ãsʊɔ na do ka nɩrɛ na ma do jãnɛ a be kaara a puo. Wʊ den a puo kʊ nɩbɛ ɩka ba kaara tɩ na wʊ yɔ chen paal yuo pɔ.

eng: When it was time for harvest, he sent his servants to the tenant farmers to collect his fruit.
local: A pɔrfʊ sɔ̃ɔ na wa ta, wʊ tʊ̃n a wʊ gbãgbaar ɩka ba chen a nɩbɛ bala sɛ̃ tɩ pɔr a tɩɛ wɔ̃mɔ̃.

eng: But the tenant farmers grabbed his servants. They beat some of them, and some of them they killed. Some of them they stoned to death.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a nɩbɛ bala nyɔɔ a wʊ gbãgbaar ŋme a ãsʊɔ, na ba kʊ a ãsʊɔ tɩ na ba lɔb a ãsʊɔ nɩ kʊsɩbɛ kʊ.

eng: Again he sent other servants, more than the first group. They treated them in the same way.
local: Be lɛ wʊ lɛ tʊ̃ a gbãgbaar bamɩne ka ba ge ba a lɛ nɛ nɩ wʊ ju.

eng: Finally he sent his son to them. They will respect my son, he said.
local: A baaraa lɛ wʊ tʊ̃ a wʊ mãɛ bie a ba sɛ̃ na wʊ 'yɛr, Ban 'yɔ̃ɔ naa a n bie.

eng: But when the tenant farmers saw the son, they said to each other, This is the heir. Come on, lets kill him and well have his inheritance.
local: Tɩchɛ a pokaarba na wa nyɛ a bie, ba 'yɛr kʊ taa, Nyã lɛ ɩ a bʊ̃dire, yɩ ɩ a sɩ kʊ wʊ tɩ de a bonsɩ.

eng: They grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
local: Be lɛ ba nyɔɔ wʊ kʊ lɔb bar a puo kõkoor.

eng: When the owner of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those tenant farmers.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã a puo sʊɔ wa wa, ŋmɩnɛ lɛ wʊn ɩ a pokaarba banyãna.

eng: They said, He will totally destroy those wicked farmers and rent the vineyard to other tenant farmers who will give him the fruit when its ready.
local: Ka ba 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Wʊn wan wa kʊ a pokaarba banyã ba jaa tɩ de a puo kʊ nɩyor, a bala na ma sɔɔ ka ba põ a puo bonsɩ a ŋmafʊ sɔ̃ɔ.

eng: The rest of them grabbed his servants, abused them, and killed them.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a bala na chɛ nyɔɔr a wʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ ge ba tɩ na ba kʊ ba.

eng: The king was angry. He sent his soldiers to destroy those murderers and set their city on fire.
local: A nãa suur ir naa ka wʊ tʊ̃ a wʊ sojasɩ ɩka ba tɩ kʊ a nɩkʊrbɔ bala tɩ chʊm a ba tẽe.

eng: Then he said to his servants, The wedding party is prepared, but those who were invited werent worthy.
local: Tɩ na wʊ pãa 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ, Yele jaa chɔɔr baar, tɩchɛ a bala ɩn bʊɔl ba sɛɛ ɛ.

eng: Therefore, go to the roads on the edge of town and invite everyone you find to the wedding party.
local: Yɩ yi a tẽe pɔ tɩ bʊɔl nɩrɛ jaa yɩn nyɛ ka wʊ wa a pɔɔ paalaa jie.

eng: Then those servants went to the roads and gathered everyone they found, both evil and good. The wedding party was full of guests.
local: Lɛ lɛ, a tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ yin a tẽe pɔ tɩ bʊɔl nɩrɛ jaa ban nyɛ, a nɩmɩn nɩ a nɩdɛbɛ ka a yir wa sɛɛn nɩbɛ wʊ jaa.

eng: Now when the king came in and saw the guests, he spotted a man who wasnt wearing wedding clothes.
local: Tɩchɛ a nãa na wa kpɛ ɩka wʊ kaa a nɩbɛ, wʊ nyɛn daba ãsʊɔ na ba su a pɔɔbɩɛl fuu ɛ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Havent you ever read in the scriptures, The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone. The Lord has done this, and its amazing in our eyes?.
local: Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yaa kar a jie nyã a Nãaŋmɩn gãn na 'yɛr ɩka a, A kʊsɩr 'lɔ a mɩmɩɛrbɛ na tɔr bar lɛ ɩ a kʊsɩr ju. A sɩ Soro lɛ tʊ̃ a nɩtɔ̃ ka a sɔb sɩ a nɩbie.

eng: He said to him, Friend, how did you get in here without wedding clothes? But he was speechless.
local: Ka wʊ suur wʊ, N badaba, ŋmɩnɛ fʊ ɩrɛ wa kpɛ a ka tɩ ba su a pɔɔ paalaa fuu ɛ? Ka a daba kpal a nɛɛ.

eng: Therefore, I tell you that Gods kingdom will be taken away from you and will be given to a people who produce its fruit.
local: A lɛ jũu n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ, Nãaŋmɩn na faan a wʊ nãalʊ̃ʊ yin a yɩ sɛ̃ kʊ a nɩyor bala na sɔɔ wʊ.

eng: Whoever falls on this stone will be crushed. And the stone will crush the person it falls on.
local: Nɩrɛ nyã na lo a kʊsɩr nyã ju na pʊrpʊr a, tɩchɛ a wʊ mɩ na kʊ nɩrɛ nyã ju wʊn lo dɔɔl.

eng: Now when the chief priests and the Pharisees heard the parable, they knew Jesus was talking about them.
local: A Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Faraseemɩne na wa wõ a wʊ sʊkpar anyãna, ba bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka bala lɛ wʊ lɔɔrɔ a sʊkpar gbɛɛrɛ nɩ.

eng: They were trying to arrest him, but they feared the crowds, who thought he was a prophet.
local: Ka ba bʊɔrɔ na sɔr ɩka ba nyɔɔ wʊ, tɩ na ba joro a nɩyɔɔ dãbãɛ bojũu ba nyɛn ka wʊ ɩ Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ.

eng: Then the king said to his servants, Tie his hands and feet and throw him out into the farthest darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Ka a nãa 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ, Yɩ lũ a wʊ gbɛɛ naa nuur na yɩ lɔb wʊ bar yõo a libe pɔ, be lɛ nɩbɛ na kõnõ tɩ ɔɔrɔ nyɩmɛ.

eng: Early in the morning all the chief priests and the elders of the people reached the decision to have Jesus put to death.
local: A wɛr na wa chaar bibio, a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ ba jaa lɔ̃ɔ taa guor lɛ ban na ɩ kʊ a Yesu.

eng: Many people are invited, but few people are chosen.
local: Nɩyɔɔ lɛ ba bʊɔl, tɩchɛ bãlãa lɛ ban chɛl ir.

eng: After the Sabbath, at dawn on the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary came to look at the tomb.
local: A Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir na wa baar, ka a na ɩ a Pɛ̃nfʊ divɩɛ bio daar a wɛr na wa chaara wa la la la, Mɛɛr na yi a Magdala nɩ a Mɛɛr ãsʊɔ yin chen ɩka ba tɩ kaa a yaa.

eng: They bound him, led him away, and turned him over to Pilate the governor.
local: Ba lũ wʊn tara chiin a paal Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Pilate sɛ̃.

eng: When Judas, who betrayed Jesus, saw that Jesus was condemned to die, he felt deep regret. He returned the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and elders, and.
local: Judas na gar 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ na wa nyɛ ɩka ba tɔɔr a Yesu 'yɛr guor a wʊ kʊfʊ, wʊ pʊɔ sɔ̃ɔ naa ka wʊ lɛ lɩɛb a libibie lɩjɛr nɩ pie na wʊ tɩ kʊ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ.

eng: Then the Pharisees met together to find a way to trap Jesus in his words.
local: Be lɛ ka a Faraseemɩne guor taa na ba wa bɛɛr Yesu kaa na ba na nyɔɔ wʊ a wʊ 'yɛrbie pɔ.

eng: Look, there was a great earthquake, for an angel from the Lord came down from heaven. Coming to the stone, he rolled it away and sat on it.
local: Be lɛ ka a tẽe dãm kpɛ̃ɔ jaa, ka a ɩ Nãaŋmɩn malaka lɛ yi a saaju suu wa bil a kʊsɩr bar a yaa nɛɛ tɩ do jãnɛ.

eng: They sent their disciples, along with the supporters of Herod, to him. Teacher, they said, we know that you are genuine and that you teach Gods way as it really is. We know that you are not swayed by peoples opinions, because you dont show favoritism.
local: Be lɛ ba tʊ̃ a ba poturbo nɩ a nɩbɛ bamɩne na yi a Nãa Hɛrɔd sɛ̃, na ba 'yɛr, Wiwile, sɩ bɔ̃ɔ naa ɩka fʊ ɩn yelmãɛ sʊɔ, na fʊ wile Nãaŋmɩn sɔr nɩ yelmãɛ, bojũu faa kaara lɛ nɩbɛ na 'yɛrɛ bɩɩ oor ɛ.

eng: said, I did wrong because I betrayed an innocent man. But they said, What is that to us? Thats your problem.
local: Tɩ na wʊ 'yɛr, N maal yelbier nɩ ɩn gar 'yɔ̃ɔ nɩrɛ na ba maal yelbier jaa ɛ. Tɩchɛ ka ba 'yɛr, Sɩ baka ba lɛ ɛ? Fʊ 'yɛr a ɩ.

eng: Now his face was like lightning and his clothes as white as snow.
local: A wʊ nie nyɩlɛ saa nyɩɩrfʊ kaar, ka a wʊ bʊ̃sursɩ pɛl jaa pɩlpɩl.

eng: So tell us what you think: Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not.
local: 'Yɛr kʊ sɩ a lɛ fʊn tɩɛr. A tara na sɔr ɩka sɩ yab a lõpoo kʊ a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Siisa bɩɩ sɩ ta yab ɛ.

eng: Knowing their evil motives, Jesus replied, Why do you test me, you hypocrites?.
local: Tɩchɛ a Yesu na bɔ̃ɔn a ba pʊtɩdɛɛr jũu, ka ka wʊ sʊʊr ba, Yɩn nɛɛr ayi dẽme. Bo lɛ yɩ bʊɔrɔ ɩka yɩ bɛɛr ma kaa?.

eng: The guards were so terrified of him that they shook with fear and became like dead men.
local: Dãbãɛ kpɛ̃n a bala na ara kaara, ka ba gĩem ara kũn kaar.

eng: Judas threw the silver pieces into the temple and left. Then he went and hanged himself.
local: Wʊ den a libie lɔb bar a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir pɔ, tɩ na wʊ yi tɩ yɔɔl miir.

eng: The chief priests picked up the silver pieces and said, According to the Law its not right to put this money in the treasury. Since it was used to pay for someones life, its unclean.
local: Ka a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ wob a libie na ba 'yɛr ɩka a ba sɛɛ ɩka sɩ de yaa 'yɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir libie pɔ ɛ, bojũu a ɩn jãɩ libie.

eng: But the angel said to the women, Dont be afraid. I know that you are looking for Jesus who was crucified.
local: A malaka 'yɛr kʊ a pɔbɔ, Yɩ ta joro dãbãɛ ɛ, n bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka a Yesu 'lɔ ban kpa a daa ju lɛ yɩ bʊɔrɔ.

eng: Show me the coin used to pay the tax. And they brought him a denarion.
local: Yɩ wiil ma a libir n kaa. Be lɛ ba de a libikur waan.

eng: So they decided to use it to buy the potters field where strangers could be buried.
local: Ban wa guor taa baar, ba den a libie dãn a lamɩɛr wɛr a jie ban ma ũno sãanba.

eng: He isnt here, because hes been raised from the dead, just as he said. Come, see the place where they laid him.
local: Waa be ka ɛ. Wʊ ir naa ɩ vʊr nɩtãa a lɛ wʊn dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr. Yɩ wa muul kaa a jie wʊn gã.

eng: Thats why that field is called Field of Blood to this very day.
local: Lɛ lɛ so ka nɩbɛ bʊɔlɔ a jie nyã, Jãɩ Wɛr, wa tãn dɩna.

eng: Whose image and inscription is this? he asked.
local: Ka wʊ sʊʊr ba, An foto nɩ an yuor lɛ be a libir pɔ.

eng: Now hurry, go and tell his disciples, Hes been raised from the dead. Hes going on ahead of you to Galilee. You will see him there. Ive given the message to you.
local: Yɩ chen fɔɔ jaa na yɩ tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ poturbo, Wʊ lɩɛb ir a kũu pɔ, na wʊ de yɩ nie chiine a Galilee. Be lɛ yɩn tɩ nyɛ wʊ. N pãa 'yɛr kʊ yɩ.

eng: This fulfilled the words of Jeremiah the prophet: And I took the thirty pieces of silver, the price for the one whose price had been set by some of the Israelites.
local: A ɩn yelmãɛ nɩ a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ Jeremiya na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr, Ba den a libibie lɩjɛr nɩ pie a lɛ a Isɩral nɩbɛ na sɔɔ de.

eng: and I gave them for the potters field, as the Lord commanded me .
local: na ba dãn a lamɩɛr wɛr nɩtãa lɛ a sɩ Soro na 'yɛr.

eng: With great fear and excitement, they hurried away from the tomb and ran to tell his disciples.
local: A pɔbɔ banyãna yin a yaa par nɩ dãbãɛ jo fɔɔ jaa nɩ pʊpɛl chen ɩka ba tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ poturbo.

eng: Jesus was brought before the governor. The governor said, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Yesu na wa ara a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Pilate niem, wʊ sʊʊr wʊ naa, Fʊ̃ʊ lɛ ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ nãa ɔ? Ka a Yesu 'yɛr Ɔ̃ɔ, a lɛ nɛ fʊn 'yɛr lɛ.

eng: But he didnt answer when the chief priests and elders accused him.
local: A bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ na sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a wʊ yuor, waa 'yɛr 'yɛr jaa ɛ.

eng: But Jesus met them and greeted them. They came and grabbed his feet and worshipped him.
local: Be lɛ ka ajienaa, ka a ɩ Yesu lɛ tuor puor ba, ka ba lo kpa a wʊ niem nyɔɔ a wʊ gbɛɛ puor wʊ.

eng: Caesars, they replied. Then he said, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God.
local: Ka ba 'yɛr, A Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Siisa lɛ. Lɛ lɛ ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ de a 'lɔ na ɩ a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Siisa sofʊ na yɩ kʊ wʊ, na yɩ mɩ de a 'lɔ na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn sofʊ mɩ kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Dont be afraid. Go and tell my brothers that I am going into Galilee. They will see me there.
local: Be lɛ a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, yɩ ta joro dãbãɛ ɛ, Yɩ tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a n yɛɛr ɩka ba chen a Galilee, be lɛ ban tɩ nyɛ ma.

eng: Then Pilate said, Dont you hear the testimony they bring against you.
local: Be lɛ a Pilate sʊʊr wʊ, faa 'yɛrɛ 'yɛr jaa wɛ? Nyɛ lɛ ban sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a fʊ yuor jã?.

eng: But he didnt answer, not even a single word. So the governor was greatly amazed.
local: Tɩchɛ a Yesu ba haa nɛɛ sɔɔ wʊ nɩ a ala a jaa ban 'yɛrɛ dɔɔlɔ wʊ ɛ, ka a 'maa a Pilate nɛɛ.

eng: Now as the women were on their way, some of the guards came into the city and told the chief priests everything that had happened.
local: A pɔbɔ na wa chiine, a sojasɩ bamɩne na dɔ̃ɔ guro a yaa kpɛ̃n a tẽkpɛ̃ɛ pɔ, tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ a lɛ jaa na ɩ.

eng: When they heard this they were astonished, and they departed.
local: Ban wa wõ a nɩtɔ̃, nɛɛ 'maa ba naa ka ba bar wʊ tɩ chen jaa jomm.

eng: It was customary during the festival for the governor to release to the crowd one prisoner, whomever they might choose.
local: A lɔ̃ɔ difʊ sɔ̃ɔ wa ta, ba ma 'yɛr ɩka a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Pilate wʊ ir nɩrɛ 'lɔ a nɩbɛ na bʊɔrɔ a pɔɔ fʊ die pɔ bar.

eng: They met with the elders and decided to give a large sum of money to the soldiers.
local: A bɔɔrlo nãa nɩ a wʊ nɩbɛrɛ na wa lɔ̃ɔ taa, ba guor taa de libiyɔɔ kʊ a sojasɩ.

eng: At that time there was a well-known prisoner named Jesus Barabbas.
local: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃, ba pɔɔn daputuo sʊɔ kɔ̃ɔ nɩrɛ jaa na bɔ̃ɔ ka wʊ di Barabasɩ.

eng: They told them, Say that Jesus disciples came at night and stole his body while you were sleeping.
local: tɩ na ba 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ 'yɛr ɩka, A wʊ poturbo lɛ wa ju a wʊ ãgãn a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ sɩn chɛnɛ gã guro.

eng: That same day Sadducees, who deny that there is a resurrection, came to Jesus.
local: A bɩbir 'lɔ̃nɔ̃, a Saduseemɩne na 'yɛr ɩka a kũu pɔ irfʊ ba be be ɛ, ba wan a Yesu sɛ̃ wa sʊʊr wʊ.

eng: When the crowd had come together, Pilate asked them, Whom would you like me to release to you, Jesus Barabbas or Jesus who is called Christ.
local: A lɛ a nɩyɔɔ na wa lɔ̃ɔ taa, Pilate sʊʊr ba naa, An lɛ yɩ bʊɔrɔ ɩka n yuo kʊ yɩ, Barabasɩ bɩɩ Yesu, a 'lɔ ban bʊɔlɔ a Nɩ-iraa.

eng: And if the governor hears about this, we will take care of it with him so you will have nothing to worry about.
local: Ala ɩ a 'yɛr nyã wa lon a Nãa Pilate toor pɔ, sɩn maal wʊ naa ka a wʊ pʊɔ pɛl a sɩ ir yɩ yin wõm pɔ.

eng: He knew that the leaders of the people had handed him over because of jealousy.
local: Bojũu wʊ bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka a nyuur ban tara nɩ a Yesu lɛ so ban nyɔɔ wʊ waan a wʊ sɛ̃.

eng: They asked, Teacher, Moses said, If a man who doesnt have children dies, his brother must marry his wife and produce children for his brother.
local: Wiwile, Mosesɩ sɛb ɩka yɛbɛ wa kpi tɩ bar pɔɔ na ba tara bie ɛ, a wʊ yɛbɛ wʊ kul a pɔɔkʊɔr 'lɔ na wʊ dɔɔ bibiir kʊ a wʊ yɛbɛ 'lɔ na kpi.

eng: So the soldiers took the money and did as they were told. And this report has spread throughout all Judea to this very day.
local: A sojasɩ den a libie tɩ na ba ɩ a lɛ ban 'yɛr ɩka ba ɩ. A Juu nɩbɛ ma chɛnɛ na 'yɛr a yele nyãna a dɩna.

eng: Now there were seven brothers among us. The first one married, then died. Because he had no children he left his widow to his brother.
local: Yɛɛr ayopõi lɛ dɔ̃ɔ be a ka a sɩ sɛ̃ ka a ba kpɛ̃ɛ kul pɔɔ na waa dɔɔ bie ɛ, tɩ kpi tɩchɛ a pɔɔkʊɔr bar ka a yɛbɛ de kul.

eng: The same thing happened with the second brother and the third, and in fact with all seven brothers.
local: Ka a ayi sʊɔ mɩ de a pɔɔkʊɔr kul baa mɩ dɔɔ bie, tɩchɛ ka wʊ mɩ kpi. A lɛ nɛ nɩ wʊ ju mɩ lɛ ata tɩ tãn a ayopõi sʊɔ.

eng: While he was serving as judge, his wife sent this message to him, Leave that righteous man alone. Ive suffered much today in a dream because of him.
local: A Pilate na wa jãnɛ a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ dakɔɔ ju, a wʊ pɔɔ tʊ̃n 'yɛr wʊ, Taa ɩrɛ wa ɩrɛ a daba 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ na ba maal bon jaa yele jaa ɛ, bojũu jãa, n din dɔɔyɛ jãn pɔ nɩ a wʊ yele.

eng: Now the eleven disciples went to Galilee, to the mountain where Jesus told them to go.
local: A poturbo pie nɩ bʊ̃'yen chen a Galilee a tɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ ju a Yesu na wiil ba ɩka ba do.

eng: But the chief priests and the elders persuaded the crowds to ask for Barabbas and kill Jesus.
local: Tɩchɛ a bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ 'yɛr kʊ a nɩyɔɔ ɩka ba sʊɔr ka wʊ yuo Barabasɩ bar, tɩ kʊ a Yesu.

eng: The governor said, Which of the two do you want me to release to you? Barabbas, they replied.
local: Ka a Nãa Pilate lɛ bɔ̃ɔ sʊʊr ba go, A nɩbɛ banyã ayi, buor lɛ yɩ bʊɔrɔ ɩka n yuo kʊ yɩ? Ka ba 'yɛr, Barabasɩ.

eng: When they saw him, they worshipped him, but some doubted.
local: Ban wa nyɛ wʊ, ba gbur dumo na ba puor wʊ tɩchɛ ka bamɩne chɛnɛ baala.

eng: Pilate said, Then what should I do with Jesus who is called Christ? They all said, Crucify him.
local: Ka a Nãa Pilate sʊʊr ba, Tɩ n ɩ ŋmɩnɛ nɩ a 'lɔ yɩn bʊɔlɔ a Nɩ-iraa? Ka ba jaa 'yɛr, Kpa wʊ a daa ju.

eng: But he said, Why? What wrong has he done? They shouted even louder, Crucify him.
local: Ka wʊ sʊʊr ba, Bo yelbier lɛ wʊ ɩ? Bo dɛɛr lɛ wʊ maal? Tɩchɛ ka ba tãna kpɛ̃ɔ jaa 'yɛrɛ, Kpa wʊ a daa ju.

eng: Jesus came near and spoke to them, Ive received all authority in heaven and on earth.
local: Ka a Yesu wa 'yɛr kʊ ba, A n Sãa den a kpãɛn ala a jaa na be a saaju, nɩ a kpãɛn ala a jaa na be a wɛr pɔ kʊ ma.

eng: Pilate saw that he was getting nowhere and that a riot was starting. So he took water and washed his hands in front of the crowd. Im innocent of this mans blood, he said. Its your problem.
local: A Pilate na wa nyɛ bɔ̃ɔn ɩka wʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ yele jaa ɩ ɛ, tɩchɛ ka a nɩbɛ wɩlɛwɩlɛ jaa tɩ gar, ka wʊ de kʊ̃ɔ pɛɛn a wʊ nuur a nɩyɔɔ niem, tɩ na wʊ 'yɛr, Maa ba pʊɔ a daba nyã kũu pɔ ɛ. Yɩn sɛ̃ lɛ a be.

eng: All the people replied, Let his blood be on us and on our children.
local: Be lɛ ka a nɩbɛ ba jaa 'mataa 'yɛr, Vɛ̃ ka a wʊ jãɩ dɔɔl a sɩn nɩ a sɩ bibiir ju.

eng: Therefore, go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit.
local: A lɛ jũu, yɩ yɔ a wɛr wʊ jaa na yɩ tɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ paalsɩ ka ba wa turo ma, na yɩ suoro ba a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ, a Sãa yuor, nɩ a Bie yuor, nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ yuor 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: Finally, the woman died.
local: Ba jaa na wa kul a pɔɔkʊɔr nyã lʊɔr taa baar a pɔɔkʊɔr nyã mɩ baar kpi.

eng: At the resurrection, which of the seven brothers will be her husband? They were all married to her.
local: Bala wa ir yi a kũu pɔ, a nɩbɛ banyã ayopõi an pɔɔ, lɛ wʊn ɩ, an ɩ ba jaa kul wʊn lʊɔr taa.

eng: teaching them to obey everything that Ive commanded you. Look, I myself will be with you every day until the end of this present age.
local: tɩ na yɩ wile ba ka ba sɔɔ de, tɩ turo bon jaa ɩn 'yɛr kʊ yɩ. Yɩ bɔ̃ɔn ɩka ɩn ma ben a yɩ sɛ̃ sɔ̃ɔ jaa, tɩ tãn a wɛr baarfʊ daar.

eng: Jesus responded, You are wrong because you dont know either the scriptures or Gods power.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yaa nyɛ yɩn bɔr, bojũu yaa bɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn gãma nɩ a wʊ kpɛ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: Then he released Barabbas to them. He had Jesus whipped, then handed him over to be crucified.
local: Be lɛ wʊ yuo a Barabasɩ kʊ ba. Wʊ vɛ̃ naa ka ba fɔb a Yesu tɩ wʊ de wʊ kʊ a sojasɩ ka ban tɩ kpa a daa ju.

eng: The governors soldiers took Jesus into the governors house, and they gathered the whole company of soldiers around him.
local: Be lɛ ka a sojasɩ tara a Yesu tɩ kpɛ̃n a nãa yir kpɛ̃ɛ 'lɔ pɔ, tɩ bʊɔl a ba taaba sojasɩ lɔ̃ɔ taa.

eng: They stripped him and put a red military coat on him.
local: Ba yaar a wʊ bonsɩ bar tɩ de fuu jɩɛ su wʊ.

eng: They twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on his head. They put a stick in his right hand. Then they bowed down in front of him and mocked him, saying, Hey! King of the Jews.
local: na ba lɛ de gʊʊr wob nãa jupil kaar su wʊ. Ba den dãgbal 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ a nũsʊ̃ɔ pɔ, na ba gbur dumo a wʊ niem laara wʊ, Yãan yooo, Juu nɩbɛ nãa.

eng: After they spit on him, they took the stick and struck his head again and again.
local: Ba ma chɩr nataar 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ tɩ de daa ŋmen wʊ a ju pɔ.

eng: When they finished mocking him, they stripped him of the military coat and put his own clothes back on him. They led him away to crucify him.
local: Ban wa la wʊ a nɩtɔ̃ wa baar, ba yãan a fuu jie 'lɔ bar tɩ de a wʊ mãɛ fuu su wʊ, na ba pãa de wʊ chiin ɩka ba tɩ kpa a daa ju.

eng: As they were going out, they found Simon, a man from Cyrene. They forced him to carry his cross.
local: Ban wa yi a sɔr pɔ, ba nyɛn daba na yi Sirenɩ ka a wʊ yuor di Simon, ka ba nyɔɔ wʊ fɩr wʊ ka wʊ tuo a Yesu daa.

eng: At the resurrection people wont marry nor will they be given in marriage. Instead, they will be like angels from God.
local: Nɩbɛ wa ir a kũu pɔ, ba kʊ̃ lɛ kul taa ɛ, tɩ ban kaara fɩka malakasɩ na be a saaju.

eng: As for the resurrection of the dead, havent you read what God told you.
local: Tɩchɛ a kũu pɔ ir fʊ yele, yaa kar a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr kʊ yɩ ɩka.

eng: Im the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob? He isnt the God of the dead but of the living.
local: n ɩ naa a Abraham Nãaŋmɩn nɩ a Isaakɩ Nãaŋmɩn nɩ a Jekɔb Nãaŋmɩn ɛ? Wʊ ɩn Nãaŋmɩn vʊr, waa ɩ Nãaŋmɩn kũu ɛ.

eng: Now when the crowd heard this, they were astonished at his teaching.
local: A nɩbɛ na wa wõ a nɩtɔ̃ nɛɛr 'maa ba naa nɩ a wʊ wiilfʊ.

eng: When the Pharisees heard that Jesus had left the Sadducees speechless, they met together.
local: A Faraseemɩne na wa wõ ɩka Yesu 'yɛr tʊ̃ɔ a Saduseemɩne, ba mɩ lɔ̃ɔn taa.

eng: One of them, a legal expert, tested him.
local: Ka a ba ãsʊɔ na ɩ a nɛɛ wiwile sʊʊr 'yɛrbir bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ bɛɛr a Yesu kaa.

eng: Teacher, what is the greatest commandment in the Law.
local: Wiwile, A Mosesɩ nɛbinsɩ a jaa pɔ, buor lɛ ɩ kpɛ̃ɛ.

eng: He replied, You must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, and with all your mind.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Nʊ̃ a fʊ Soro Nãaŋmɩn nɩ a fʊ nyãa wʊ jaa, nɩ a fʊ nyɛvʊr wʊ jaa, nɩ a fʊ tɩɛr wʊ jaa.

eng: When they came to a place called Golgotha, which means Skull Place.
local: Ba tɩ tãn jie ban bʊɔlɔ Gɔlgɔta, a par lɛ ɩka, Julaa Jie.

eng: This is the first and greatest commandment.
local: Nyã lɛ ɩ a kpɛ̃ɛ nɩ a nɛdɔ̃ɔ.

eng: And the second is like it: You must love your neighbor as you love yourself .
local: A ayi sʊɔ mɩ nɩ nyã, Nʊ̃ a fʊ tɔ sʊɔ a fʊ kaar.

eng: All the Law and the Prophets depend on these two commands.
local: A Mosesɩ nɛɛr a jaa nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ wiilfʊ kpãkpãana na a binfʊ nyãna.

eng: Now as the Pharisees were gathering, Jesus asked them.
local: A Faraseemɩne na wa 'mataa, Yesu sʊʊr ba naa.

eng: What do you think about the Christ? Whose son is he? Davids son, they replied.
local: Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ yɩ tɩɛr a Nɩ-iraa yele? An bie lɛ wʊ ɩ? Ka ba 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, David bie lɛ.

eng: He said, Then how is it that David, inspired by the Holy Spirit, called him Lord when he said.
local: Ka a Yesu sʊʊr ba, Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ ka a nãa David a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na dɔ̃ɔ kpɛ, a wʊ bʊɔl wʊ ɩka, N Soro? Bojũu wʊ 'yɛr naa ɩka.

eng: The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side until I turn your enemies into your footstool?.
local: A Soro 'yɛr kʊ a n Soro, Jãnɛ a n nũsʊ̃ɔ jie, ka n wa de a fʊ dɔ̃dẽme ka ba ɩ a fʊ gbɛɛ dɔɔlfʊ jie. .

eng: If David calls him Lord, how can he be Davids son.
local: Ala ɩ David bʊɔl wʊn wʊ ɩka, N Soro, ŋmɩnɛ wʊn ɩrɛ wa ɩ a wʊ bie.

eng: Nobody was able to answer him. And from that day forward nobody dared to ask him anything.
local: An yɩ a daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ chiine, nɩrɛ jaa ba lɛ haa nɛɛ 'yɛr 'yɛr ɛ bɩɩ lɛ 'la sʊʊr sʊʊrfʊ jaa go ɛ.

eng: they gave Jesus wine mixed with vinegar to drink. But after tasting it, he didnt want to drink it.
local: Ba den tãɩtuo 'yɔ̃ɔ dãa pɔ ɩka a Yesu wʊ nyũ, tɩ wʊn wa lɛm kaa, wʊ tɔr bar.

eng: After they crucified him, they divided up his clothes among them by drawing lots.
local: Ban wa kpa wʊ a daa ju baar, ba põ naa a wʊ fɔɔr ŋmen gbãm bɔ̃ɔn ala ãsʊɔ jaa nɩ na di.

eng: They sat there, guarding him.
local: tɩ na ba jãnɛ a jie pire wʊ.

eng: They placed above his head the charge against him. It read, This is Jesus, the king of the Jews.
local: Ba sɛb a yele 'lɔ jũu ban kʊn wʊ mar a daa ju ɩka, NYƐ A YESU, A JUU NƖBƐ NÃA.

eng: They crucified with him two outlaws, one on his right side and one on his left.
local: Ba mɩ kpan nɩfarba ayi a dabãbãnsɩ mɩ ju, ka ãsʊɔ be a Yesu nũsʊ̃ɔ jie ka ãsʊɔ mɩ be a wʊ gubaa jie.

eng: Those who were walking by insulted Jesus, shaking their heads.
local: A bala na tala a be tʊrɔ wʊ, na ba vavire jur.

eng: and saying, So you were going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, were you? Save yourself! If you are Gods Son, come down from the cross.
local: 'yɛrɛ, A fʊ̃ʊ na 'yɛr ɩka fʊn ŋmɛr a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir tɩ de ber ata mɛn, faa a fʊ mãɛ. Ala ɩ fʊ ɩ naa a Nãaŋmɩn Bie, wa suu a daa ju.

eng: In the same way, the chief priests, along with the legal experts and the elders, were making fun of him, saying.
local: Lɛ nɩ wʊ ju mɩ lɛ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ mɩ laara wʊ.

eng: He saved others, but he cant save himself. Hes the king of Israel, so let him come down from the cross now. Then well believe in him.
local: na ba 'yɛrɛ, Wʊ faan nɩbɛ bamɩne, tɩ wʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ faa a wʊ mãɛ ɛ. 'Lɔ lɛ ɩ a Isɩral Nãa, wʊ wa suu a daa ju a pʊ̃pãanyãna, ka sɩ sɔɔ de wʊ.

eng: He trusts in God, so let God deliver him now if he wants to. He said, Im Gods Son.
local: Wʊ sɔɔn Nãaŋmɩn. Vɛ̃ ka Nãaŋmɩn faa wʊ a pʊ̃pãanyãna, bojũu wʊ 'yɛr naa ɩka wʊ ɩn Nãaŋmɩn Bie.

eng: The outlaws who were crucified with him insulted him in the same way.
local: Lɛ nɩ wʊ ju mɩ lɛ a nɩfarba ayi bala mɩ ban kpa gbʊr wʊ, mɩ tʊ wʊ.

eng: From noon until three in the afternoon the whole earth was dark.
local: A mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ na wa ara gar ju sãlsɔɔ, a wɛr wʊ jaa sɔb naa tɩ tãn a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ balfʊ sɔ̃ɔ.

eng: At about three Jesus cried out with a loud shout, Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani , which means, My God, my God, why have you left me.
local: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ a Yesu chɩɩr kpɛ̃ɔ jaa na wʊ 'yɛr, Ɛloɩ, Ɛloɩ, lama sabakatanɩ. A par lɛ ɩka N Nãaŋmɩn, n Nãaŋmɩn, bo lɛ so fʊn lɩɛb puor 'yɔ̃ɔ ma.

eng: After hearing him, some standing there said, Hes calling Elijah.
local: A nɩbɛ bala na ara gbʊr wʊ, na wa wõ a lɛ wʊn 'yɛr, ka ba 'yɛr ɩka, Elaija lɛ wʊ bʊɔlɔ.

eng: One of them ran over, took a sponge full of vinegar, and put it on a pole. He offered it to Jesus to drink.
local: Ajienaa ka a daba ãsʊɔ jo chen tɩ 'mɔɔ sapɔ 'lɔr a dãmiir amɩne pɔ, na wʊ char daa pɔ, de kʊ a Yesu ɩka wʊ mʊʊr.

eng: But the rest of them said, Lets see if Elijah will come and save him.
local: Tɩchɛ ka bamɩne 'yɛr, Yɩ bar a sɩ kaara, Elaija na wa yɔɔ wʊ naa bɩɩ.

eng: Again Jesus cried out with a loud shout. Then he died.
local: Be lɛ a Yesu chɩɩr kpɛ̃ɔ jaa tɩ ŋmaa a vʊʊr.

eng: Look, the curtain of the sanctuary was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth shook, the rocks split.
local: Ka a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir pɔ, ban lũ fɔbaa chɩɛr a saaju tɩ gbem a par. Ka a tẽe dãm, ka a kʊsɩbɛ mɩ ŋmɛr ŋmɛr.

eng: and the bodies of many holy people who had died were raised.
local: A yar mɩ yuo yuor, ka a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ bamɩne na dɔ̃ɔ kpi, jɛɔ.

eng: After Jesus resurrection they came out of their graves and went into the holy city where they appeared to many people.
local: Ban wa yi a ba yar pɔ a lɛ, ka a Yesu na wa ir a kũu pɔ baar, ka ba pãa kpɛ a tẽchɛchɛ pɔ, ka nɩyɔɔ jaa tɩ nyɛ ba.

eng: When the centurion and those with him who were guarding Jesus saw the earthquake and what had just happened, they were filled with awe and said, This was certainly Gods Son.
local: A sojasɩ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ nɩ a bala wʊn ara nɩ na ba pire a Yesu, nyɛn a wɛr na dãm nɩ a lɛ jaa na ɩ, ka dãbãɛ kpɛ ba, ka ba 'yɛr, Yelmãɛ jaa lɛ, a daba nyã ɩn a Nãaŋmɩn Bie.

eng: Many women were watching from a distance. They had followed Jesus from Galilee to serve him.
local: Pɔɔ yɔɔ mɩ dɔ̃ɔ be a be ara tɛɛr jaa kaara, bala lɛ dɔ̃ɔ tu wʊ yin a Galilee na ba sʊ̃nɔ̃ wʊ.

eng: Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James and Joseph, and the mother of Zebedees sons.
local: A pɔbɔ banyãna pɔ lɛ Mɛɛr na yi Magdala, nɩ Mɛɛr na ɩ Jemesɩ nɩ Josefʊ ma, nɩ a Jebedee bibiir ma.

eng: That evening a man named Joseph came. He was a rich man from Arimathea who had become a disciple of Jesus.
local: A jãanʊɔr na wa ta, a taraa sʊɔ kɔ̃ɔ na yɩ Arimatɩya ban bʊɔlɔ Josefʊ, wʊ mɩ dɔ̃ɔ ɩ a Yesu poturo.

eng: He came to Pilate and asked for Jesus body. Pilate gave him permission to take it.
local: Wʊ chen a Pilate sɛ̃ tɩ sʊɔr a Yesu ãgãn. Ka a Pilate bar sɔr ka ba yɔɔ a Yesu kʊ wʊ.

eng: Joseph took the body, wrapped it in a clean linen cloth.
local: A Josefʊ yɔɔn a ãgãn pil fɔpɩla.

eng: and laid it in his own new tomb, which he had carved out of the rock. After he rolled a large stone at the door of the tomb, he went away.
local: kul tɩ ũu a wʊ mãɛ yaa paalaa pɔ wʊn chɩ a kʊsɩr pɔ. Wʊ bil kʊsɩr wa pɔɔ a yaa nɛɛ tɩ na wʊ kul.

eng: Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were there, sitting in front of the tomb.
local: Mɛɛr na yi Magdala nɩ a Mɛɛr ãsʊɔ ben be jãnɛ a yaa puor.

eng: The next day, which was the day after Preparation Day, the chief priests and the Pharisees gathered before Pilate.
local: A Chɔɔrfʊ Bɩbir ka a wɛr na chaar ka a ɩ a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir lɛ, a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Faraseemɩne chen tɩ 'yɛr a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Pilate.

eng: They said, Sir, we remember that while that deceiver was still alive he said, After three days I will arise.
local: Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, sɩ lɛ tɩɛr a lɛ a jir ŋmara 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr wʊn dɔ̃ɔ chɛnɛ, Ɩka ber ata daar ɩn lɛ lɩɛb ir ɩ vʊr.

eng: Therefore, order the grave to be sealed until the third day. Otherwise, his disciples may come and steal the body and tell the people, Hes been raised from the dead. This last deception will be worse than the first.
local: A lɛ jũu vɛ̃ ka ba gu a yaa tɩ tãn a ber ata daar, ka ala ba ɩ lɛ ɛ, a wʊ poturbo na chen tɩ ju a ãgãn a yaa pɔ, tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a nɩbɛ ɩka, 'lɔ lɛ ir a kũu pɔ, ka a bɛlfʊ taa ɩ dɛɛr gar a lɛ wʊn dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr ɛ.

eng: Pilate replied, You have soldiers for guard duty. Go and make it as secure as you know how.
local: Ka a Pilate 'yɛr, Yɩ de gugurbo ka ba tɩ gu a yaa a lɛ yɩn bɔ̃ɔ.

eng: Then they went and secured the tomb by sealing the stone and posting the guard.
local: Be lɛ ba tara a gugurbo chiin ka ba tɩ bɛ a yaa bɔɔ pɔɔ, tɩ na ba 'yɔ̃ɔ a kʊsɩr jãnɛ, tɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ ka a gugurbo ara gu.

eng: When Jesus finished speaking all these words, he said to his disciples.
local: A Yesu na wa 'yɛr a anyãna a jaa baar, wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ poturbo.

eng: You know that the Passover is two days from now. And the Human One will be handed over to be crucified.
local: Yɩ bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a chɛn ber ayi ka a Gõl Bar Difʊ na ta, ka ba na de a maa Nɩsaal Bie tɩr ka ba kpa ma a daa ju.

eng: Then the chief priests and elders of the people gathered in the courtyard of Caiaphas the high priest.
local: A Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ lɔ̃ɔ taa a bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ yir na di Kayafasɩ.

eng: They were plotting to arrest Jesus by cunning tricks and to kill him.
local: na ba bʊɔrɔ vuo ban na tu nyɔɔ a Yesu na ba kʊ.

eng: But they agreed that it shouldnt happen during the feast so there wouldnt be an uproar among the people.
local: Tɩchɛ ka ba 'yɛr, a ta ɩ a Gõl Bar Difʊ sɔ̃ɔ ɛ, ka jɛɛr taa ɩrɛ wa ir a chara pɔ ɛ.

eng: When Jesus was at Bethany visiting the house of Simon, who had a skin disease.
local: Yesu na wa be a Betanɩ a Simon 'lɔ na dɔ̃ɔ ɩ a kɔ̃kɔ̃mɔ̃ yir.

eng: a woman came to him with a vase made of alabaster containing very expensive perfume. She poured it on Jesus head while he was sitting at dinner.
local: ban wa jɛ̃ dire, pɔɔ kɔ̃ɔ waan na pãrtɔvɩla nɩ kãnyũu kɔ̃ɔ dafʊ na ba wõnõ ɛ, na wʊ kpaar dɔɔl a Yesu ju.

eng: Now when the disciples saw it they were angry and said, Why this waste?.
local: A poturbo na wa nyɛ a lɛ, ba nyɛn suur na ba 'yɛr, Bo lɛ so ka wʊ sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a kãnyũu nyã bar a nɩtɔ̃?.

eng: This perfume could have been sold for a lot of money and given to the poor.
local: Fʊn taa den a kãnyũu anyãna da libiyɔɔ de kʊ a nɔ̃n dẽme.

eng: But Jesus knew what they were thinking. He said, Why do you make trouble for the woman? Shes done a good thing for me.
local: Yesu na bɔ̃ɔn a ba tɩɛr, wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Bãa 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ yɩ mii 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ? Wʊ maal vɩla kʊ ma.

eng: You always have the poor with you, but you wont always have me.
local: A nɔ̃n dẽme 'lɔ, yɩn nɩ bala ben ka tɩchɛ maa kʊ be a yɩ sɛ̃ sɔ̃ɔ jaa ɛ.

eng: By pouring this perfume over my body shes prepared me to be buried.
local: Wʊn kpaar a kãnyũu anyãna 'yɔ̃ɔ ma, wʊ maal a lɛ chɔɔr ma gun a n ũufʊ.

eng: I tell you the truth that wherever in the whole world this good news is announced, what shes done will also be told in memory of her.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, jie jaa ban wa mʊɔl a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyãna a wɛr wʊ jaa pɔ, ban 'yɛr naa a lɛ a pɔɔ nyã na ɩ na ba chɛnɛ tara a wʊ tɩɛr.

eng: Then one of the Twelve, who was called Judas Iscariot, went to the chief priests.
local: A Pie Nɩ Ayi bala ãsʊɔ ban bʊɔlɔ Judas Isɩkariyot chen a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ sɛ̃.

eng: and said, What will you give me if I turn Jesus over to you? They paid him thirty pieces of silver.
local: tɩ sʊʊr ba, Bo lɛ yɩn kʊ ma ala ɩ n de wʊn 'yɔ̃ɔ a yɩ nuur pɔ? Ba sɔr sɛlbie lɩjɛr nɩ pie kʊ wʊ.

eng: From that time on he was looking for an opportunity to turn him in.
local: An yɩ a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ wʊ bʊɔrɔ na vuo wʊn na tu de a Yesu 'yɔ̃ɔ a ba nuur pɔ.

eng: On the first day of the Festival of Unleavened Bread, the disciples came to Jesus and said, Where do you want us to prepare for you to eat the Passover meal.
local: A bɩbir dɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ ɩ naa a Paanʊ̃kõo Lɔ̃ɔfʊ, ka a Yesu poturbo wa sʊʊr wʊ, Nyɩnɛ lɛ fʊ bʊɔrɔ ɩka sɩ chen tɩ chɔɔr a Gõl Bar nyã Difʊ bar ka sɩ tɩ di.

eng: He replied, Go into the city, to a certain man, and say, The teacher says,My time is near. Im going to celebrate the Passover with my disciples at your house.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ chen a tẽe pɔ, daba kɔ̃ɔ sɛ̃ na yɩ tɩ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, A wiwile 'yɛr naa, a n sɔ̃ɔ gbʊr naa, ka maa nɩ a n poturbo na di a Gõl Bar Difʊ a fʊ yir. .

eng: The disciples did just as Jesus instructed them. They prepared the Passover.
local: A poturbo ɩn a lɛ jaa a Yesu na 'yɛr ɩka ba ɩ. Ka ba maal a Gõl Bar bʊ̃diir.

eng: That evening he took his place at the table with the twelve disciples.
local: A jãanʊɔr na wa ta, Yesu nɩ a wʊ poturbo Pie nɩ Ayi wa jɛ̃n jil a bʊ̃diir na di.

eng: As they were eating he said, I assure you that one of you will betray me.
local: Ban wa dire, wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, a yɩ ãsʊɔ na gar 'yɔ̃ɔ ma.

eng: Deeply saddened, each one said to him, Im not the one, am I, Lord.
local: Be lɛ ka ba jaa pʊʊr sɔ̃ɔ ka ba sʊrɔ wʊ bʊ̃'yen 'yen, An ɩn maa lɛ ɔ, n Soro.

eng: He replied, The one who will betray me is the one who dips his hand with me into this bowl.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, A 'lɔ ɩn 'mataa tun ala bʊ̃'yen pɔ lɛ na gar 'yɔ̃ɔ ma.

eng: The Human One goes to his death just as it is written about him. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays the Human One! It would have been better for him if he had never been born.
local: A maa Nɩsaal Bie na kpi naa nɩtãa lɛ ban sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ. Tɩchɛ nɛbier ben a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na gar 'yɔ̃ɔ a maa Nɩsaal Bie wʊn din dɔɔyɛ gar nɩrɛ jaa. Bala ta ba dɔɔ wʊ ɛ, ala sa.

eng: Now Judas, who would betray him, replied, Its not me, is it, Rabbi? Jesus answered, You said it.
local: Judas a 'lɔ na gar 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ sʊʊr, An ɩn maa lɛ ɔ, Wiwile? Ka a Yesu sɔɔ, Ɔ̃ɔ, fʊ̃ʊ lɛ.

eng: While they were eating, Jesus took bread, blessed it, broke it, and gave it to the disciples and said, Take and eat. This is my body.
local: Ban wa dire, Yesu den a paanʊ̃ɔ puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan na wʊ ŋma de kʊ a wʊ poturbo, tɩ na wʊ 'yɛr, Yɩ de 'wɔb, a maa ãgãn lɛ.

eng: He took a cup, gave thanks, and gave it to them, saying, Drink from this, all of you.
local: Wʊ lɛ den a ŋmele 'lɔ ban nyuro nɩ, na wʊ puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan tɩ lɛ de kʊ ba, Yɩ jaa yɩ de nyũ.

eng: This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many so that their sins may be forgiven.
local: A anyãna lɛ ɩ a n jãɩ na ɩ a nɛtɩraa ɩn tara nɩ yɩ na tara kpɛ̃ɔ. An kpaar na a faa nɩyɔɔ ka yelbier vɛ̃ kʊfʊ be be.

eng: I tell you, I wont drink wine again until that day when I drink it in a new way with you in my Fathers kingdom.
local: N 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, n kʊ̃ lɛ nyũ a dãa anyãna ɛ, wa tɩ tãn a daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ ɩn na 'mataa nyũn yɩ a dãpaalaa a n Sãa nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.

eng: Then, after singing songs of praise, they went to the Mount of Olives.
local: Ban wa yiel a yiel dãn Nãaŋmɩn, ba suun chen a Olive Tɩɩr Tɔ̃ɔ ju.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, Tonight you will all fall away because of me. This is because it is written, I will hit the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock will go off in all directions .
local: Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ jaa na jon a tãsɔɔ nyãna tɩ bar ma a n yõ, a maa jũu. A sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, Ɩn ŋme naa pɛchinɛ kʊ ka a piir yɛ̃yɛl.

eng: But after Im raised up, Ill go before you to Galilee.
local: Tɩchɛ maa wa ir a kũu pɔ, ɩn dɔ̃ɔ yɩn nie chen a Galilee.

eng: Peter replied, If everyone else stumbles because of you, Ill never stumble.
local: Ka a Pita 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Ala ɩ ba jaa sɔɔn bar fʊ a fʊ jũu, maa 'lɔ kʊ̃ bar fʊ ɛ.

eng: Jesus said to him, I assure you that, before the rooster crows tonight, you will deny me three times.
local: Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a Pita, Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ fʊ, ɩka a tãsɔɔ nyãna a nɔrdɔ̃ɔ kʊ̃ kõ ɛ, tɩchɛ ka fʊ chiir ma gbɛɛ ata.

eng: Peter said, Even if I must die alongside you, I wont deny you. All the disciples said the same thing.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Pita 'yɛr kʊ wʊ ɩka, Ala ɩ maa nɩ fʊ̃ʊ na 'mataa kpi gba, tɩ n kʊ̃ 'yɛr ɩka n ba bɔ̃ɔ fʊ ɛ. Ka a poturbo bala mɩ na chɛ 'yɛr a lɛ nɛ.

eng: Then Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane. He said to the disciples, Stay here while I go and pray over there.
local: Yesu nɩ a wʊ poturbo chen a jie 'lɔ ban bʊɔlɔ a Getɩsemanɩ, ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ jãnɛ a kana tɩchɛ ka n chen tɩ sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: When he took Peter and Zebedees two sons, he began to feel sad and anxious.
local: Be lɛ wʊ tara Pita nɩ Jebedee bibiir ayi ka a wʊ pʊɔ sɔ̃ɔ yɔɔ jaa ka tɩɛr nyɔɔ wʊ.

eng: Then he said to them, Im very sad. Its as if Im dying. Stay here and keep alert with me.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, N tɩɛr sɔ̃ɔ naa, tɩ nyɛtãan kũn, yɩ jãnɛ a ka na gu ma.

eng: Then he went a short distance farther and fell on his face and prayed, My Father, if its possible, take this cup of suffering away from me. However—not what I want but what you want.
local: Wʊ chen a niem bãlãa tɩ lo kpa tẽe sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛrɛ, N Sãa ala ɩ a tara naa ɩfʊ kɔ̃ɔ, vɛ̃ ka a dɔɔyɛ ŋmele nyãna po ma. Tɩchɛ a ta ɩ lɛ maa na bʊɔrɔ ɛ, tɩ a ɩ a lɛ fʊ̃ʊ na bʊɔrɔ.

eng: He came back to the disciples and found them sleeping. He said to Peter, Couldnt you stay alert one hour with me?.
local: Wʊ lɩɛb wa a poturbo sɛ̃ wa nyɛ ka ba guro. Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, A mɔ̃ yɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ jɛ̃ bãlãa jaa gu ma ɛ?.

eng: Stay alert and pray so that you wont give in to temptation. The spirit is eager, but the flesh is weak.
local: Yɩ jɛɔ chɛlɛ, na yɩ sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn ka a bɛɛr kaa ta wa nyɔɔ yɩ ɛ. A ba tɩɛr bʊɔrɔ naa tɩchɛ ka a ba ãgãma ba tʊɔr ɛ.

eng: A second time he went away and prayed, My Father, if its not possible that this cup be taken away unless I drink it, then let it be what you want.
local: Wʊ lɛ chen a gbɛɛ ayi sʊɔ tɩ sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn. N Sãa, ala ɩ a dɔɔyɛ ŋmele nyã kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ po ma ɛ, ka maa ba nyũn wʊ ɛ, fʊ̃ʊ bɔfʊ wʊ ɩ.

eng: Again he came and found them sleeping. Their eyes were heavy with sleep.
local: Wʊ lɛ wan wa nyɛ ka ba chɛnɛ guro, bojũu gʊ̃ɔ kpɛ ba naa.

eng: But he left them and again went and prayed the same words for the third time.
local: Be lɛ wʊ lɛ bar ba tɩ chen a gbɛɛ ata sʊɔ tɩ sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn a lɛ nɛ nɩ wʊ ju.

eng: Then he came to his disciples and said to them, Will you sleep and rest all night? Look, the time has come for the Human One to be betrayed into the hands of sinners.
local: Wʊ lɩɛb wa a poturbo sɛ̃ wa sʊʊr ba, Yɩ chɛnɛ na gã guro na yɩ pɛnɛ wɛ? Nyɛ, a sɔ̃ɔ ta naa ka ban gar 'yɔ̃ɔ a maa Nɩsaal Bie de wʊ kʊ a yelbe-ɩrbɛ.

eng: Get up. Lets go. Look, here comes my betrayer.
local: Yɩ vaa ir ka sɩ chen. Yɩ nyɛ a gagaraa na waara.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, came. With him was a large crowd carrying swords and clubs. They had been sent by the chief priests and elders of the people.
local: A Yesu na chɛnɛ 'yɛrɛ, ka Judas na ɩ a wʊ poturbo Pie nɩ Ayi ãsʊɔ, tɩ daasãa nɩ nɩbɛ na yi a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ sɛ̃ waara nɩ sɔbɛrɛ nɩ dakʊrsɩ a wʊ sɛ̃.

eng: His betrayer had given them a sign: Arrest the man I kiss.
local: A Judas dɔ̃ɔ man ba ɩka, a 'lɔ wʊn tɩ puor tɩ mʊʊr a kɔtɩr 'lɔ lɛ, yɩ nyɔɔ wʊ.

eng: Just then he came to Jesus and said, Hello, Rabbi. Then he kissed him.
local: Ban wa tɩ ta, Judas pɔɔn chen tɩ 'yɛr, Wiwile, n puor fʊ naa, tɩ mʊʊr a Yesu kɔtɩr.

eng: But Jesus said to him, Friend, do what you came to do. Then they came and grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
local: Ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, N badaba, tʊ̃ a lɛ fʊn bʊɔrɔ ɩka fʊ tʊ̃. Be lɛ ba wa nyɔɔ a Yesu.

eng: One of those with Jesus reached for his sword. Striking the high priests slave, he cut off his ear.
local: Be lɛ ka a Yesu poturbo ãsʊɔ na ara gbʊr wʊ vʊ̃ɔ a sʊɔ chen a Juu dẽme bɔɔrlo nãa gbãgbaa toor ŋmaa.

eng: Then Jesus said to him, Put the sword back into its place. All those who use the sword will die by the sword.
local: Be lɛ ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, De a sʊɔ su a sʊkpa pɔ, bala na jɛɛrɛ nɩ a sʊɔ na kpin a sʊɔ kũu.

eng: Or do you think that Im not able to ask my Father and he will send to me more than twelve battle groups of angels right away?.
local: Fʊ tɩɛr ɩka n kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ bʊɔl a n Sãa ka wʊ sʊ̃ʊ ma nɩ a malakasɩ gbulo pie nɩ ayi bɩɩ?.

eng: But if I did that, how would the scriptures be fulfilled that say this must happen.
local: Tɩchɛ ŋmɩnɛ lɛ an ɩrɛ wa ɩ yelmãɛ a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn gãma na 'yɛr ɩka a nɩtɔ̃ lɛ na ɩ.

eng: Then Jesus said to the crowds, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, like a thief? Day after day, I sat in the temple teaching, but you didnt arrest me.
