local: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃, a Yesu sʊʊr a nɩyɔɔ bala, A mɔ̃ nɩbier lɛ n ɩ ka yɩ waan sʊʊr nɩ dakʊrsɩ a n nyɔɔ fʊ jie wɛ? Bɩbir jaa lɛ n ma jãnɛ wile yɩ a puorfʊ yir bɛchaar pɔ, tɩ yaa nyɔɔ ma ɛ.

eng: But all this has happened so that what the prophets said in the scriptures might be fulfilled. Then all the disciples left Jesus and ran away.
local: Tɩchɛ a ɩ naa a lɛ ka a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr a Nãaŋmɩn gãma pɔ ɩ yelmãɛ. Be lɛ ka a Yesu poturbo ba jaa bar wʊ tɩ jo.

eng: Those who arrested Jesus led him to Caiaphas the high priest. The legal experts and the elders had gathered there.
local: A bala na nyɔɔ a Yesu, chiin wʊ naa a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nãa Kayafasɩ sɛ̃, ka a nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ ba jaa wa lɔ̃ɔ taa.

eng: Peter followed him from a distance until he came to the high priests courtyard. He entered that area and sat outside with the officers to see how it would turn out.
local: Tɩchɛ ka Pita be a puor jaa turo, na wʊ tɩ kpɛ a bɔɔrlo nãa yir bɛchaar pɔ, tɩ lɔ̃ɔ jãnɛ nɩ a gugurbo, ɩka wʊ nyɛ lɛ an na baar.

eng: The chief priests and the whole council were looking for false testimony against Jesus so that they could put him to death.
local: Ka a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ nɩbɛrɛ pãa bʊɔrɔ jir ŋmarba ka ba na ŋmaa jire 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ, ka ba na tu a be kʊ a Yesu.

eng: They didnt find anything they could use from the many false witnesses who were willing to come forward. But finally they found two.
local: Tɩchɛ baa nyɛ yele jaa a wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ ɛ. Nɩyɔɔ daan yi ŋma jiryɔɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ, tɩchɛ ka a ba 'yɛrbie ɩ jirsɩ. A baaraa nɩbɛ ayi daan yi.

eng: who said, This man said, I can destroy Gods temple and rebuild it in three days.
local: na ba 'yɛr ɩka, A daba nyãna lɛ 'yɛr ɩka, Ɩn tʊ̃ɔ ŋmɛr a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir nyãna bar tɩ de ber ata lɛ mɛn wʊ. .

eng: Then the high priest stood and said to Jesus, Arent you going to respond to the testimony these people have brought against you.
local: A be lɛ ka a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nãa ir ara tɩ sʊʊr a Yesu, Faa tara 'yɛr jaa na 'yɛr bɩɩ? Bo dãasɩɛ lɛ a anyã ban dire dɔɔlɔ fʊ.

eng: But Jesus was silent. The high priest said, By the living God, I demand that you tell us whether you are the Christ, Gods Son.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Yesu ɩ jaa gbul. Be lɛ ka a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nãa 'yɛr, N sʊrɔ fʊ naa a Nãaŋmɩn nyã na ɩ a vʊr niem, ɩka fʊ 'yɛr kʊ sɩ, ala ɩ fʊ̃ʊ lɛ ɩ a Nɩ-iraa na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Bie.

eng: You said it, Jesus replied. But I say to you that from now on youll see the Human One sitting on the right side of the Almighty and coming on the heavenly clouds .
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Ɔ̃ɔ, a lɛ nɛ fʊn 'yɛr lɛ. Tɩchɛ yɩ jaa lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ ɩka daar kɔ̃ɔ, yɩn nyɛn a maa Nɩsaal Bie na jãnɛ a Kpɛ̃ɔ Sʊɔ nũsʊ̃ɔ jie. Yɩ mɩ na lɛ nyɛ ma naa ka n yɩ a julõjuur pɔ waara.

eng: Then the high priest tore his clothes and said, Hes insulting God! Why do we need any more witnesses? Look, youve heard his insult against God.
local: Ka a bɔɔrlo nãa tɔɔ a fuu chɩɛr tɩ 'yɛr, Wʊ sɔ̃ɔn Nãaŋmɩn yuor. Bo lɛ so sɩn chɛnɛ bʊɔrɔ dãasɩdirbe? Yɩ wõn wʊ tʊ Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: What do you think? And they answered, He deserves to die.
local: Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ yɩ tɩɛr? Ka ba 'yɛr, Wʊ sɛɛn kũu.

eng: Then they spit in his face and beat him. They hit him.
local: Ba chir nataar 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ a nie pɔ, tɩ ma gbul nũu ŋmen wʊ, ka bamɩne mɩ ma 'ma wʊ.

eng: and said, Prophesy for us, Christ! Who hit you.
local: tɩ sʊʊr, Nɩ-iraa, ala ɩ fʊ bɔ̃ɔn yele jaa wiil sɩ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ŋme fʊ.

eng: Meanwhile, Peter was sitting outside in the courtyard. A servant woman came and said to him, You were also with Jesus the Galilean.
local: Pita jãnɛ naa a yõo a bɛchaar pɔ. Ka a gbãgbapɔɔbile ãsʊɔ wa a wʊ sɛ̃ wa 'yɛr, Fʊ mɩ dɔ̃ɔ turo a Yesu na yi a Galilee.

eng: But he denied it in front of all of them, saying, I dont know what you are talking about.
local: Tɩchɛ ka wʊ chiir a ba jaa niem tɩ 'yɛr, N ba bɔ̃ɔ a 'yɛr 'lɔ par fʊn 'yɛrɛ ɛ.

eng: When he went over to the gate, another woman saw him and said to those who were there, This man was with Jesus, the man from Nazareth.
local: Wʊn wa tɩ ara a dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ, a pɔɔbile ãsʊɔ nyɛ wʊ naa na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a nɩbɛ na ara a be, A daba nyã mɩ dɔ̃ɔ turo a Yesu na yi a Najaretɩ.

eng: With a solemn pledge, he denied it again, saying, I dont know the man.
local: Be lɛ a Pita lɛ chiir pɔ tɩ 'yɛr ɩka, N ba bɔ̃ɔ a daba nyãna ɛ.

eng: A short time later those standing there came and said to Peter, You must be one of them. The way you talk gives you away.
local: A ba kor ɛ, ka a bala na ara gbʊr a Pita mɩ 'yɛr, Sɩrɛ jaa lɛ, fʊ ɩ naa a ba ãsʊɔ. A fʊ kɔkɔr yin fʊn chãa pɔ.

eng: Then he cursed and swore, I dont know the man! At that very moment the rooster crowed.
local: Be lɛ ka a Pita pɔrɔ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a wʊ mãɛ tɩ mɩ pɔrɔ kʊrɔ ba 'yɛrɛ ɩka, N ba bɔ̃ɔ a daba nyãna ɛ. Ka a nɔraa mɩ tɔ̃ɔ kõ.

eng: Peter remembered Jesus words, Before the rooster crows you will deny me three times.And Peter went out and cried uncontrollably.
local: Be lɛ ka Pita tɩɛr tɔ a lɛ a Yesu na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr, Fʊn chiir ma naa gbɛɛ ata tɩchɛ ka a nɔraa kõ. Ka a Pita ir yi a yõo tɩ kõ nɩbaal kõn.

eng: The beginning of the good news about Jesus Christ, Gods Son.
local: Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Bie 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ pielfʊ.

eng: happened just as it was written about in the prophecy of Isaiah: Look, I am sending my messenger before you . He will prepare your way.
local: 'Yɛ'yɛrɛ kɔ̃ɔ yuor na di Isaiya sɛb naa, Nyɛ, ɩn tʊ̃ naa a n tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ ka wʊ de nie tɩ chɔɔr a sɔr kʊ fʊ.

eng: a voice shouting in the wilderness: Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight .
local: Kɔkɔr tãna naa a dãlpʊɔ ju, Chɔɔr a sɔr kʊ a sɩ Soro na fʊ maal a wʊ sɔr ka wʊ tor. .

eng: John the Baptist was in the wilderness calling for people to be baptized to show that they were changing their hearts and lives and wanted God to forgive their sins.
local: Lɛ lɛ a Jɔɔn wa, na wʊ wa suoro a nɩbɛ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ be a dãlpʊɔ ju, na wʊ mʊɔl 'yɛr, Yɩ lɩɛb yi a yɩ tɩɛr dɛɛr pɔ so a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ, ka wʊ vɛ̃ a yelbebe kʊ yɩ.

eng: Everyone in Judea and all the people of Jerusalem went out to the Jordan River and were being baptized by John as they confessed their sins.
local: Ka a nɩbɛ yire a tẽn ala a jaa na be a Judiya wɛr pɔ, ka a bamɩne mɩ yire a Jerusalɛm. Ba chire naa a ba pʊʊr 'yɛrɛ a ba yelbebe kʊrɔ wʊ tɩchɛ ka wʊ suoro ba jaa a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ a Jordan man pɔ.

eng: John wore clothes made of camels hair, with a leather belt around his waist. He ate locusts and wild honey.
local: Nyɔɔma kɔɔlʊ fuu lɛ Jɔɔn ma su tɩ de gãn lũn a sɩɛ. Soor nɩ sɩkãa lɛ ɩ a wʊ bʊ̃diir.

eng: He announced, One stronger than I am is coming after me. Im not even worthy to bend over and loosen the strap of his sandals.
local: Wʊ 'yɛr naa, N puor sʊɔ ben be, na wʊ gar ma. A wʊ nafɔɔr mibe gba n ba sɛɛ ɩka n guun for ɛ.

eng: After a few days, Jesus went back to Capernaum, and people heard that he was at home.
local: A anyã puor, Yesu lɩɛb wa a Kapenahum, ka nɩbɛ wõ ka wʊ be naa a yir.

eng: I baptize you with water, but he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.
local: Maa suoro yɩ naa nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ tɩchɛ 'lɔ na so yɩ naa nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ.

eng: So many gathered that there was no longer space, not even near the door. Jesus was speaking the word to them.
local: Nɩyɔɔ wan wa duo na ba sɛɛ a die wa tɩ yin a yõo. Ka wa 'yɛrɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr kʊrɔ ba.

eng: Some people arrived, and four of them were bringing to him a man who was paralyzed.
local: Be lɛ ka daba ãsʊɔ mɩ bɩɛrɛ, a wʊ nuur nɩ a wʊ gbɛɛ kpi naa, ka nɩbɛ anaar jɛ̃jɛl wʊ na ba waan a Yesu sɛ̃.

eng: They couldnt carry him through the crowd, so they tore off part of the roof above where Jesus was. When they had made an opening, they lowered the mat on which the paralyzed man was lying.
local: Ka ba wa ta, ka jie ba be be a ba na tu kpɛ̃n a be a Yesu na be ɛ, a nɩyɔɔ jũu. Be lɛ ba do a gar ju na ba tɩ ŋmaa puo a jie 'lɔ a Yesu na ara, tɩ na ba de a daba nɩ a fɔbaa 'lɔ ban 'mɔɔn wʊ waan tur bar ka wʊ suu tɩ gã a Yesu niem.

eng: When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, Child, your sins are forgiven.
local: Be lɛ a Yesu na wa nyɛ a lɛ ban sɔɔ de wʊ, wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a dabaala, N bie, n vɛ̃ naa fʊ yelbebe bar.

eng: Some legal experts were sitting there, muttering among themselves.
local: Ka be lɛ ka a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe bamɩne na be be tɩɛrɛ.

eng: Why does he speak this way? Hes insulting God. Only the one God can forgive sins.
local: Ka nyɛ daba na ara fere na wʊ sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ Nãaŋmɩn yuor tɛɛ. An lɛ na tʊ̃ɔ nɩrɛ yelbebe vɛ̃ bar, ka Nãaŋmɩn tɛɛ ba lɛ ɛ.

eng: Jesus immediately recognized what they were discussing, and he said to them, Why do you fill your minds with these questions?.
local: Ajienaa Yesu bɔ̃ɔn naa a lɛ ban tɩɛrɛ a ba pʊʊr pɔ, ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Bãa lɛ yɩ tɩɛrɛ a lɛ a yɩ pʊʊr pɔ?.

eng: Which is easier—to say to a paralyzed person, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up, take up your bed, and walk?.
local: Buor sʊɔ lɛ ɩ kɔ̃mɔ̃kɔ̃mɔ̃, n 'yɛr ɩka n vɛ̃ naa wʊ yelbebe bar, bɩɩ, n 'yɛr ɩka a daba nyã wʊ ir, 'mɔɔ a wʊ bʊ̃gãnaa, na wʊ kulo nɩ?.

eng: But so you will know that the Human One has authority on the earth to forgive sins—he said to the man who was paralyzed.
local: N bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka yɩ bɔ̃ɔ ɩka a Nɩsaal Bie tara naa kpɛ̃ɔ a wɛr pɔ na vɛ̃ nɩrɛ yelbebe kʊ wʊ. Be lɛ wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a daba 'lɔ nuur nɩ a gbɛɛ na kpi.

eng: Get up, take your mat, and go home.
local: Fʊ̃ʊ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ, ir, na fʊ 'mɔɔ a bʊ̃gãnaa, na fʊ kulo nɩ.

eng: Jesus raised him up, and right away he picked up his mat and walked out in front of everybody. They were all amazed and praised God, saying, Weve never seen anything like this.
local: Be lɛ, ka wʊ vaa ir 'mɔɔ a bʊ̃gãnaa yin a nɩbɛ ba jaa niem. Nɛɛr 'maa ba naa ka ba dãna Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛrɛ, Saa dɔ̃ɔ nyɛ a lɛ ɛ.

eng: About that time, Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
local: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ Yesu yi naa Najaretɩ na be a Galilee wɛr pɔ wa, ka a Jɔɔn so wʊ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ a Jordan man pɔ.

eng: Jesus went out beside the lake again. The whole crowd came to him, and he began to teach them.
local: Yesu lɩɛb chen a man nɛɛ ka nɩyɔɔ wa a wʊ sɛ̃ ka wʊ wile ba.

eng: While he was coming up out of the water, Jesus saw heaven splitting open and the Spirit, like a dove, coming down on him.
local: A Yesu na wa yire a kʊ̃ɔ pɔ, wʊ nyɛ naa a saaju na tɔɔ yuo ka a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ wa suro fɩka papa wa kpɛ a Yesu pɔ.

eng: As he continued along, he saw Levi, Alphaeus son, sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. Jesus said to him, Follow me. Levi got up and followed him.
local: A Yesu na wa chiine wʊ nyɛ naa a Alfayasɩ bie, Levi, ka wʊ jãnɛ a lõpo defʊ jie, ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Ir turo ma, ka wʊ ir turo wʊ.

eng: And there was a voice from heaven: You are my Son, whom I dearly love; in you I find happiness.
local: Tɩ a kɔkɔr yi a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ 'yɛrɛ, Fʊ̃ʊ lɛ ɩ a n binʊ̃nãa, ka a n 'yɔ̃ɔ ma 'maa nɩ fʊ.

eng: At once the Spirit forced Jesus out into the wilderness.
local: Ka a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ de wʊ fɔɔ jaa chiin a dãlpʊɔ ju.

eng: He was in the wilderness for forty days, tempted by Satan. He was among the wild animals, and the angels took care of him.
local: ka wʊ tɩ be be ber lɩjaayi ka a Satan bɛɛr wʊ kaa. 'Lɔ nɩ a mɔdʊ̃n lɛ dɔ̃ɔ be be, ka a malakasɩ wa sʊ̃nɔ̃ wʊ.

eng: After John was arrested, Jesus came into Galilee announcing Gods good news.
local: Jɔɔn ban wa nyɔɔ pɔɔ baar, Yesu chen naa a Galilee na wʊ tɩ mʊɔlɔ 'yɛrɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ.

eng: saying, Now is the time! Here comes Gods kingdom! Change your hearts and lives, and trust this good news.
local: Wʊ 'yɛr naa, A sɔ̃ɔ ta naa ka a Nãaŋmɩn di a wʊ nãalʊ̃ʊ. Yɩ lɩɛb yi a tɩɛr dɛɛr pɔ na yɩ sɔɔ de a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ.

eng: As Jesus passed alongside the Galilee Sea, he saw two brothers, Simon and Andrew, throwing fishing nets into the sea, for they were fishermen.
local: A lɛ a Yesu na wa de a Galilee man lana, wʊ nyɛ naa Simon nɩ a wʊ yɛbile Andiru ka ba lɔɔrɔ lɔyaar, jãnyɔɔrbɔ lɛ ba ɩ.

eng: Come, follow me, he said, and Ill show you how to fish for people.
local: ka wʊ bʊɔl ba, Yɩ wa turo ma, ka n lɩɛb yɩ ka yɩ nyɔɔrɔ nɩbɛ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.

eng: Right away, they left their nets and followed him.
local: Ka ba tɔ̃ɔ tɩɛ a jãmɛ nyɔɔrfʊ bar tɩ turo wʊ.

eng: After going a little farther, he saw James and John, Zebedees sons, in their boat repairing the fishing nets.
local: Wʊn wa chen a niem bãlãa, wʊ nyɛ naa Jemesɩ nɩ Jɔɔn, Jebedee bibiir, a ba be a gboro pɔ chɔɔrɔ a lɔyaarsɩ.

eng: At that very moment he called them. They followed him, leaving their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired workers.
local: Wʊn wa nyɛ ba, wʊ bʊɔl ba naa, ka ba bar a ba sãa nɩ a gboro nɩ a bala ban yara tɩ turo a Yesu.

eng: Jesus and his followers went into Capernaum. Immediately on the Sabbath Jesus entered the synagogue and started teaching.
local: Be lɛ ba wa chen a Kapenahum ka a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir wa ta, ka a Yesu chen tɩ kpɛ a lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie tɩ piel wile.

eng: The people were amazed by his teaching, for he was teaching them with authority, not like the legal experts.
local: A nɩbɛ nɛɛr 'maa naa nɩ a wʊ wiilfʊ, bojũu wʊ wile ba naa nɩ Nãaŋmɩn sɔr. Waa ɩ fɩka a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe ɛ.

eng: Suddenly, there in the synagogue, a person with an evil spirit screamed.
local: A sɩdɛɛr sʊɔ kɔ̃ɔ mɩ chen tɩ kpɛ a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie tãna 'yɛrɛ nɩ.

eng: What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are. You are the holy one from God.
local: Bãa lɛ a fʊ̃ʊ Yesu na yi a Najaretɩ bʊɔrɔ a sɩ sɛ̃? Fʊ wa naa ɩka fʊ wa sɔ̃ɔ sɩ bar bɩɩ? N bɔ̃ɔ naa a nɩrɛ 'lɔ fʊn ɩ, fʊ ɩ naa a chɛchɛ 'lɔ na yi a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃.

eng: Jesus sat down to eat at Levis house. Many tax collectors and sinners were eating with Jesus and his disciples. Indeed, many of them had become his followers.
local: A lõpodierbe nɩ a yelbetʊ̃nbɔ yɔɔ turo naa a Yesu, ka 'lɔ nɩ a bamɩne nɩ a wʊ poturbo 'mataa dire a Levi yir.

eng: Silence! Jesus said, speaking harshly to the demon. Come out of him.
local: Ka a Yesu tãn wʊ, Pɔɔ a fʊ nɛɛ tɩ wa yi a daba pɔ.

eng: When some of the legal experts from among the Pharisees saw that he was eating with sinners and tax collectors, they asked his disciples, Why is he eating with sinners and tax collectors.
local: A Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe na ɩ a Faraseemɩne nyɛ a lɛ wʊn jãnɛ nɩ a lõpodierbe nɩ a yelbetʊ̃nbɔ 'mataa dire, ka ba sʊʊr a wʊ poturbo, Bãa lɛ so a daba nyã na 'mataa dire nɩ a lõpodierbe nɩ a yelbetʊ̃nbɔ.

eng: The unclean spirit shook him and screamed, then it came out.
local: Ka a sɩdɛɛr daa a daba lɔb tẽe nɩtãa kpũgul loro tɩ ŋme chɛlma tɩ wa yi a daba pɔ.

eng: When Jesus heard it, he said to them, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do. I didnt come to call righteous people, but sinners.
local: Yesu wõ naa a lɛ ban 'yɛr, ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Nɩrɛ na kpɛ̃mɛ waa chiine tɩtɩlɛ jie ɛ. Baalaa lɛ ma chen. N ba wa ɩka n wa bʊɔl a nɩtorsɩ ɛ. N wa naa ɩka n wa bʊɔl a yelbebe tʊ̃nbɔ ka ba wa lɩɛb a tɩɛr.

eng: Everyone was shaken and questioned among themselves, Whats this? A new teaching with authority! He even commands unclean spirits and they obey him.
local: Ka a nɛɛr 'maa a nɩbɛ ka ba lɩɛb sʊrɔ taa, Bo lɛ? Wiil paal bo mɩ nɩ nyã, na tara kpɛ̃ɔ lɛ! Wʊ ma 'yɛr 'yɛr, ka a sɩdɛbɛ ma tu a wʊ nɛɛ.

eng: Johns disciples and the Pharisees had a habit of fasting. Some people asked Jesus, Why do Johns disciples and the Pharisees disciples fast, but yours dont.
local: Jɔɔn poturbo nɩ a Faraseemɩne nɩbɛ gal kɔ̃, ka nɩbɛ wa sʊʊr a Yesu, Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ a Jɔɔn poturbo nɩ a Farasee poturbo ma gal kɔ̃, tɩchɛ ka a fʊ̃ʊ poturbo maa gal kɔ̃ ɛ.

eng: Jesus said, The wedding guests cant fast while the groom is with them, can they? As long as they have the groom with them, they cant fast.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ an ɩ ka nɩbɛ be a pɔɔ paalaa sɩrɛ jie tɩ tʊ̃ɔ kɔ̃ gal? Wʊn chɛnɛ be a ba sɛ̃, ba kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: But the days will come when the groom will be taken away from them, and then they will fast.
local: A daar 'lɔ ban wa de a pɔɔ paalaa sɩrɛ, ban gal a kɔ̃.

eng: No one sews a piece of new, unshrunk cloth on old clothes; otherwise, the patch tears away from it, the new from the old, and makes a worse tear.
local: Nɩrɛ maa ŋmaa fɔpaalaa sɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ fɔkoraa pɔ ɛ. 'Lɔ be sɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ a lɛ, a fɔpaalaa na tɔɔ naa a fɔkoraa chɩɛr.

eng: No one pours new wine into old leather wineskins; otherwise, the wine would burst the wineskins and the wine would be lost and the wineskins destroyed. But new wine is for new wineskins.
local: Nɩrɛ maa de dãbãɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ wɔkoraa pɔ ɛ. 'Lɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ, a wʊɔ na pur naa, ka a dãa nɩ a wʊɔ sɔ̃ɔ. Wʊ ma 'yɔ̃ɔ naa dãbãɩn wɔpaalaa pɔ.

eng: Jesus went through the wheat fields on the Sabbath. As the disciples made their way, they were picking the heads of wheat.
local: A Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar, Yesu nɩ a wʊ poturbo kpɛ̃n tala puo sãlsɔɔ, ka a wʊ poturbo piel fɔrɔ a bʊ̃bie.

eng: The Pharisees said to Jesus, Look! Why are they breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Ka a Faraseemɩne 'yɛr kʊ a Yesu, Nyɛ lɛ a fʊ poturbo na ɩrɛ. Bo lɛ so ka ba tʊ̃nɔ̃ a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir? A ba tara sɔr ɛ.

eng: He said to them, Havent you ever read what David did when he was in need, when he and those with him were hungry?.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yaa bɔ̃ɔ a par a lɛ yɩn kar kaa a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ a nãa David nɩ a wʊ nɩbɛ na ba dɔ̃ɔ tara bon na di ɛ!.

eng: During the time when Abiathar was high priest, David went into Gods house and ate the bread of the presence, which only the priests were allowed to eat. He also gave bread to those who were with him.
local: A ber ala pɔ Abiyata lɛ ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, kɔ̃ dɔ̃ɔ kpɛ ba, ka wʊ kpɛ a Nãaŋmɩn yir, ka a Abiyata de a paanʊ̃ɔ ala ban bin kʊ Nãaŋmɩn kʊ wʊ, ka wʊ 'wɔb. A paanʊ̃ɔ ala a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlorbo tɛɛ lɛ ma 'wɔb, a ba tara sɔr ɩka nɩyuo wʊ 'wɔb ɛ, tɩchɛ ka David 'wɔb tɩ de kʊ a bala na be a wʊ sɛ̃, ka ba mɩ 'wɔb.

eng: Then he said, The Sabbath was created for humans; humans werent created for the Sabbath.
local: Be lɛ ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, A Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir ben be a nɩbɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ, a ba ɩ a nɩbɛ ben be a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir 'yɔ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: This is why the Human One is Lord even over the Sabbath.
local: Ala lɛ so ka a Nɩsaal Bie ɩ a Soro lɔ̃ɔn a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir gba.

eng: Right away the news about him spread throughout the entire region of Galilee.
local: Ka a wʊ yele yaar a Galilee wɛr wʊ jaa.

eng: After leaving the synagogue, Jesus, James, and John went home with Simon and Andrew.
local: Ban wa yi a lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie, 'lɔ nɩ Jemesɩ nɩ Jɔɔn chen naa a Simon nɩ Andiru yir.

eng: Simons mother-in-law was in bed, sick with a fever, and they told Jesus about her at once.
local: ka a ãgãn tɔ a Simon dãɛ pɔɔ ka ba 'yɛr kʊ a Yesu.

eng: He went to her, took her by the hand, and raised her up. The fever left her, and she served them.
local: Yesu kpɛ naa a wʊ sɛ̃ tɩ nyɔɔ a wʊ nũu sʊ̃ʊ wʊ ka wʊ ir. A ãgãn tʊl baar wʊ, ka wʊ ir maal bʊ̃diir kʊ ba.

eng: That evening, at sunset, people brought to Jesus those who were sick or demon-possessed.
local: A jãanʊɔr 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ na wa mʊr, a nɩbɛ waan na baalsɩ nɩ a bala a sɩdɛbɛ na tara a Yesu sɛ̃.

eng: The whole town gathered near the door.
local: Ka a tẽe nɩbɛ ba jaa wa lɔ̃ɔ a dɔ̃dɔ̃rʊ.

eng: He healed many who were sick with all kinds of diseases, and he threw out many demons. But he didnt let the demons speak, because they recognized him.
local: ka a Yesu sãa nɩyɔɔ na tara baal jaa bʊrɔ. Wʊ mɩ dii naa a sɩdɛbɛ yɔɔ ka a yi, tɩ maa sɔɔ ka a sɩdɛbɛ 'yɛr 'yɛr ɛ, bojũu ba bɔ̃ɔ naa a nɩrɛ 'lɔ wʊn ɩ.

eng: Early in the morning, well before sunrise, Jesus rose and went to a deserted place where he could be alone in prayer.
local: A wɛr na chaara a bibio pipi tɩ chɛnɛ sɔb, Yesu ir na wʊ yi a yir na wʊ chen a jie ãsʊɔ ɩka wʊ tɩ sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: Simon and those with him tracked him down.
local: Simon nɩ a wʊ taaba mɩ yin bʊɔrɔ wʊ na ba wa nyɛ wʊ.

eng: When they found him, they told him, Everyones looking for you.
local: na ba 'yɛr, Nɩbɛ ba jaa yʊɔrɔ na bʊɔrɔ fʊ.

eng: He replied, Lets head in the other direction, to the nearby villages, so that I can preach there too. Thats why Ive come.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Yɩ ɩ ka sɩ chen jiyuo, a tẽn anyã na gbʊr, ka n tɩ mʊɔl 'yɛr a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr a be, ala 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ n wa.

eng: He traveled throughout Galilee, preaching in their synagogues and throwing out demons.
local: Wʊ yɔ naa a Galilee wɛr wʊ jaa na wʊ 'yɛrɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ yie pɔ tɩ na wʊ dire a sɩdɛbɛ mɩ ka a yire.

eng: A man with a skin disease approached Jesus, fell to his knees, and begged, If you want, you can make me clean.
local: Ka a daba ãsʊɔ mɩ na ɩ kɔ̃kɔ̃mɔ̃ wa gbur dumo sʊɔr wʊ, Fʊ̃ʊ sɔɔ, sãa ma.

eng: Incensed, Jesus reached out his hand, touched him, and said, I do want to. Be clean.
local: Ka nɩbaal kpɛ a Yesu ka wʊ tur a wʊ nũu sɩɩr a daba tɩ 'yɛr, N sɔɔ naa. Sa.

eng: Instantly, the skin disease left him, and he was clean.
local: Ajienaa ka a kɔ̃kɔ̃mɔ̃ sa.

eng: Sternly, Jesus sent him away.
local: Yesu kpãa wʊ naa kpãkpãan wʊ ɩka.

eng: saying, Dont say anything to anyone. Instead, go and show yourself to the priest and offer the sacrifice for your cleansing that Moses commanded. This will be a testimony to them.
local: Taa ɩrɛ wa 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ nɩrɛ ɛ. Chen tɩ wiil a fʊ mãɛ kʊ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrloro tɩ ir a nɛɛ 'lɔ a Mosesɩ na 'yɛr ka a ɩ dãasɩɛ.

eng: Instead, he went out and started talking freely and spreading the news so that Jesus wasnt able to enter a town openly. He remained outside in deserted places, but people came to him from everywhere.
local: Tɩchɛ a daba na wa yi wʊ piel naa na wʊ ŋme a Yesu yele yaar, ka a lɛ jũu Yesu ba lɛ tʊ̃ɔ yʊɔrɔ chãa pɔ a tẽe pɔ ɛ. Wʊ ma be naa a tẽe kõkoor, a nɩbɛ chɛnɛ yire jiir jaa waara a wʊ sɛ̃.

eng: Jesus returned to the synagogue. A man with a withered hand was there.
local: Sɔ̃ɔ ãsʊɔ go Yesu chen tɩ kpɛ a lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie, ka a daba kɔ̃ɔ mɩ be be ka a wʊ nũpɛl kpi.

eng: Wanting to bring charges against Jesus, they were watching Jesus closely to see if he would heal on the Sabbath.
local: Ka a nɩbɛ bamɩne na be be bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba gar a Yesu, be lɛ ba tɔɔ gbʊr kaara wʊ. Ka wʊn sãa naa a daba nũu baal a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir bɩɩ.

eng: He said to the man with the withered hand, Step up where people can see you.
local: Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a daba nũpɛl na kpi, Ir ara a sɩ jaa nyɛ fʊ.

eng: Then he said to them, Is it legal on the Sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save life or to kill? But they said nothing.
local: Be lɛ ka a Yesu sʊʊr ba, Tʊ̃buor sʊɔ lɛ tara sɔr a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir daar ɩka fʊ tʊ̃, a fʊ maal vɩla bɩɩ dɛɛr, fʊ faa nyɛvʊr bɩɩ fʊ kʊ wʊ? Ka ba ɩ jaa gbulo gbulo.

eng: Looking around at them with anger, deeply grieved at their unyielding hearts, he said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did, and his hand was made healthy.
local: Wʊ kaa naa ba jaa gʊɔr nɩ suur, ka a wʊ pʊɔ sɔ̃ɔ a ba bachɛlfʊ jũu. Wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a daba 'lɔ nũpɛl na kpi, Tur a fʊ nũu. Wʊ tur naa ka a nũpɛl wʊ jaa sa.

eng: At that, the Pharisees got together with the supporters of Herod to plan how to destroy Jesus.
local: Be lɛ ka a Faraseemɩne yi chen a Hɛrɔd nɩbɛ sɛ̃, a ba tɩ 'mataa guoro a Yesu, bʊɔrɔ sɔr 'lɔ ban na tu kʊ wʊ.

eng: Jesus left with his disciples and went to the lake. A large crowd followed him because they had heard what he was doing. They were from Galilee.
local: Yesu nɩ a wʊ poturbo yin chen a man pɔ ka a nɩyɔɔ na yi Galilee wɛr tu ba.

eng: Judea, Jerusalem, Idumea, beyond the Jordan, and the area surrounding Tyre and Sidon.
local: Nɩyɔyɔɔ mɩ yi naa Judiya wɛr, Jerusalɛm, Idumea wɛr nɩ a Jordan man gɔ̃ɔn, nɩ a tẽn na be Tire nɩ Sidon wɛr pɔ, wa a wʊ sɛ̃ bojũu ba wõ naa a lɛ jaa wʊn tʊ̃.

eng: Jesus told his disciples to get a small boat ready for him so the crowd wouldnt crush him.
local: A nɩyɔɔ jũu, Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ poturbo, Yɩ kaa nyɛ gboro kʊ ma ka n sɛr bãlãa. Wʊ 'yɛr naa a lɛ bojũu waa bʊɔrɔ ɩka a nɩbɛ ba mɩl pɔɔ wʊ ɛ.

eng: He had healed so many people that everyone who was sick pushed forward so that they could touch him.
local: A nɩbɛ yɔɔ wʊn sãa lɛ so, a baalsɩ dara taa bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba sɩɩr wʊ.

eng: Whenever the evil spirits saw him, they fell down at his feet and shouted, You are Gods Son.
local: A sɩdɛbɛ na ma wa nyɛ a Yesu ba ma da naa a nɩrɛ 'lɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ ban be lɔb tẽe tɩ tãna 'yɛrɛ nɩ, Fʊ̃ʊ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Bie.

eng: But he strictly ordered them not to reveal who he was.
local: Tɩchɛ wʊ kpaal ban jũu, ka ba taa ɩrɛ wa 'yɛrɛ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ wʊn ɩ ɛ.

eng: Jesus went up on a mountain and called those he wanted, and they came to him.
local: Yesu do naa a tɔ̃ɔ ju tɩ bʊɔl a bala wʊn bʊɔrɔ ka ba do wa a wʊ sɛ̃.

eng: He appointed twelve and called them apostles. He appointed them to be with him, to be sent out to preach.
local: A ba pɔ lɛ wʊ kaa ir pie nɩ ayi, bʊɔlɔ ba nɩtʊ̃nsɩ, ka ba na be a wʊ sɛ̃, ka wʊ ma tʊ̃ ba ka ba chen tɩ mʊɔl 'yɛr a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr.

eng: and to have authority to throw out demons.
local: tɩ tara kpɛ̃ɔ dire nɩ sɩdɛbɛ ka a yire.

eng: He appointed twelve: Peter, a name he gave Simon.
local: A nɩbɛ pie nɩ ayi bala wʊn ir yoe lɛ a anyã. Simon, wʊn lɛ kʊ wʊ yopaalaa Pita.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, whom he nicknamed Boanerges, which means sons of Thunder.
local: Jemesɩ, nɩ a wʊ yɛbile Jɔɔn, a ba sãa yuor lɛ Jebedee, wʊ bʊɔlɔ ba naa Bɔɔnɛgasɩ, a par lɛ sa tãna bibiir.

eng: Jesus began to teach beside the lake again. Such a large crowd gathered that he climbed into a boat there on the lake. He sat in the boat while the whole crowd was nearby on the shore.
local: Sɔ̃ɔ kɔ̃ɔ go, Yesu piel wile a man nɛɛ. A nɩyɔyɔɔ na wa lɔ̃ɔ jũu, wʊ kpɛ naa a gboro pɔ jãnɛ, tɩchɛ ka a nɩbɛ ara kɔɔ a kʊ̃ɔ nɛɛ.

eng: and Andrew; Philip; Bartholomew; Matthew; Thomas; James, Alphaeus son; Thaddaeus; Simon the Cananaean.
local: Andiru, Filip, Batolomi, Matiu, Tomasɩ, Jemesɩ na ɩ Alfayasɩ bidaba, Tadius, Simon na mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir nɩ a wʊ wɛr yele.

eng: He said many things to them in parables. While teaching them, he said.
local: Sʊkpar lɛ wʊ lɔɔrɔ wile nɩ ba yelyɔɔ, sʊkpa ãsʊɔ nɩ nyã:.

eng: and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed Jesus.
local: nɩ a Judas Isɩkariyot, 'lɔ lɛ na wa de a Yesu tɩr.

eng: Listen to this! A farmer went out to scatter seed.
local: Yɩ chɛlɛ! Kʊɔraa lɛ yi chen ɩka wʊ tɩ yaar a wʊ bʊ̃bʊrɔ.

eng: Jesus entered a house. A crowd gathered again so that it was impossible for him and his followers even to eat.
local: A Yesu na lɩɛb wa a yir, nɩyɔɔ wa naa wa lɔ̃ɔ go, ka 'lɔ nɩ a wʊ poturbo ba tara jaa gba na di ɛ.

eng: When his family heard what was happening, they came to take control of him. They were saying, Hes out of his mind.
local: Be lɛ a wʊ yir dẽme na wa wõ a lɛ, ba chen naa ɩka ba tɩ ka wʊ, bojũu ba 'yɛr naa ɩka, Burbur kpɛ wʊ naa.

eng: As he was scattering seed, some fell on the path; and the birds came and ate it.
local: Wʊ yaar naa ka amɩne lo a sɔr pɔ ka a lubil wa tuur di.

eng: Other seed fell on rocky ground where the soil was shallow. They sprouted immediately because the soil wasnt deep.
local: Ka amɩne mɩ lo a jie 'lɔ a kʊsɩbɛ na waa gar a tãna, na a bul fɔɔ, a tãna na ba tɩrɛ jũu.

eng: The legal experts came down from Jerusalem. Over and over they charged, Hes possessed by Beelzebul. He throws out demons with the authority of the ruler of demons.
local: Ka a Mosesɩ Nɛɛr wiwiilbe bamɩne na yi a Jerusalɛm mɩ 'yɛrɛ, A Beeljebul, lɛ pɔb wʊ! A sɩdɛbɛ nãa lɛ wʊ ma de dire nɩ a sɩdɛbɛ ka a yire.

eng: When the sun came up, it scorched the plants; and they dried up because they had no roots.
local: Tɩchɛ a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ na wa nyãa, a jʊɔl naa na a kpi, bojũu a ba tara nyɩbɛ ɛ.

eng: When Jesus called them together he spoke to them in a parable: How can Satan throw Satan out?.
local: Yesu bʊɔl naa a nɩbɛ na wʊ lɔb sʊkpar kʊ ba, Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ a Satan na ɩ lɛ dii a Satan?.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorny plants grew and choked the seeds, and they produced nothing.
local: Amɩne mɩ tɩ lo naa gʊʊr pɔ, tɩchɛ ka a gʊʊr bul fãɩn naa tara, a ba tʊ̃ɔ dɔɔ ɛ.

eng: A kingdom involved in civil war will collapse.
local: Ala ɩ nãa ir jɛɛrɛ nɩ a wʊ mãɛ nãalʊ̃ʊ, ŋmɩnɛ lɛ wʊn ɩ ara?.

eng: Other seed fell into good soil and bore fruit. Upon growing and increasing, the seed produced in one case a yield of thirty to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of one hundred to one.
local: Ka amɩne mɩ tɩ lo a kɔjɔ̃ vɩla pɔ, na a dɔɔ yɔɔ. Ka amɩne dɔɔ a lɩjɛr nɩ pie, amɩne lɩjaata ka amɩne mɩ kɔbaa.

eng: He said, Whoever has ears to listen should pay attention.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Nɩrɛ jaa na tara toor, wʊ chɛlɛ.

eng: When they were alone, the people around Jesus, along with the Twelve, asked him about the parables.
local: A nɩyɔyɔɔ bala na wa chen tɩ chɛ a nɩbɛ bala na turo wʊ nɩ a wʊ poturbo pie nɩ a ayi bala wʊn ir, ka a ba sʊʊr wʊ a sʊkpar par.

eng: And a house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Yir nɩbɛ mɩ ir jɛɛrɛ taa, a yir 'lɔ nɩbɛ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ 'mataa kpɛ ɛ.

eng: If Satan rebels against himself and is divided, then he cant endure. Hes done for.
local: Ala ɩ a Satan ir naa na wʊ jɛɛrɛ nɩ a wʊ mãɛ, wʊ mɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ ara ɛ, a wʊ baaraa lɛ.

eng: He said to them, The secret of Gods kingdom has been given to you, but to those who are outside everything comes in parables.
local: Be lɛ ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩn 'lɔ Nãaŋmɩn kʊ yɩ naa a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ yelsɔɔlaa, tɩchɛ a banyã 'lɔ na ba pʊɔ ɛ, a yelsɩ a jaa ɩ naa sʊkpar kʊ ba.

eng: No one gets into the house of a strong person and steals anything without first tying up the strong person. Only then can the house be burglarized.
local: Yelmãɛ, nɩrɛ jaa kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ kpɛ gãdaa yir tɩ ju a wʊ bonsɩ ka waa de nie lũ wʊ ɛ. 'Lɔ wa lũ wʊ a lɛ wa baar, wʊn pãa tʊ̃ɔ naa a wʊ yir bonsɩ ju.

eng: I assure you that human beings will be forgiven for everything, for all sins and insults of every kind.
local: Sɩrɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, yeldɛbɛ nɩ ferfʊ a jaa nɩbɛ na fere sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ nɩ Nãaŋmɩn yuor ban nyɛn vɛ̃ kʊfʊ.

eng: This is so that they can look and see but have no insight, and they can hear but not understand. Otherwise, they might turn their lives around and be forgiven.
local: A lɛ jũu, Ba ma kãan lɛ̃ɛɛ tɩ ba maa nyɛ ɛ, na ba ma chɛl lɛ̃ɛɛ tɩ ba maa wõ ɛ. Ala ba ɩ lɛ ɛ ban lɩɛb a ba tɩɛr ka Nãaŋmɩn vɛ̃ kʊ ba. .

eng: Dont you understand this parable? Then how will you understand all the parables?.
local: Ka Yesu sʊʊr ba, Yaa bɔ̃ɔ a sʊkpa nyã par ɛ? Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ yɩn ɩ wa bɔ̃ɔ sʊkpa jaa par?.

eng: But whoever insults the Holy Spirit will never be forgiven. That person is guilty of a sin with consequences that last forever.
local: Tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ na fere nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ kʊ̃ nyɛ vɛ̃ kʊfʊ ɛ, bojũu wʊ lo naa a yeldɛɛr 'lɔ pɔ, na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ.

eng: The farmer scatters the word.
local: Yɩ kaa, a kʊɔraa lɛ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na yaara a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr.

eng: He said this because the legal experts were saying, Hes possessed by an evil spirit.
local: Ban 'yɛr ɩka wʊ tara naa a sɩdɛɛr lɛ so ka wʊ 'yɛr a lɛ.

eng: This is the meaning of the seed that fell on the path: When the word is scattered and people hear it, right away Satan comes and steals the word that was planted in them.
local: Nɩbɛ bamɩne kaara naa fɩka a bʊ̃bie ala ban yaar ka a lo a sɔr pɔ. Bala wa wõ a 'yɛr wa baar, a Satan ma wa naa, wa de a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛrbir ban bʊr a ba nyãa pɔ.

eng: His mother and brothers arrived. They stood outside and sent word to him, calling for him.
local: Be lɛ ka a Yesu ma nɩ a wʊ yɛɛr wa. Ba ara naa a yõo tɩ tʊ̃ nɩrɛ ka wʊ bʊɔl wʊ.

eng: A crowd was seated around him, and those sent to him said, Look, your mother, brothers, and sisters are outside looking for you.
local: Nɩyɔɔ jãnɛ naa jilʊ̃ a Yesu tɩ ba 'yɛr wʊ, A fʊ ma nɩ a fʊ yɛɛr be naa a yõo bʊɔrɔ fʊ.

eng: He replied, Who is my mother? Who are my brothers.
local: Ka wʊ sʊʊr ba, An lɛ ɩ a n ma nɩ a n yɛɛr.

eng: Looking around at those seated around him in a circle, he said, Look, here are my mother and my brothers.
local: Be lɛ wʊ kaa a bala na jãnɛ jilʊ̃ wʊ tɩ 'yɛr, Banyã lɛ ɩ a n ma nɩ a n yɛɛr!.

eng: Whoever does Gods will is my brother, sister, and mother.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na turo a Nãaŋmɩn sɔr, 'lɔ lɛ a n yɛbɛ nɩ a n yɛpɔɔ nɩ a n ma.

eng: Heres the meaning of the seed that fell on rocky ground: When people hear the word, they immediately receive it joyfully.
local: Ala mɩ na lo a jie 'lɔ a kʊsɩbɛ na waa gar a tãna ɩ naa a bala na wõ a 'yɛr na ba ma sɔɔ de fɔɔ jaa nɩ pʊpɛl.

eng: Because they have no roots, they last for only a little while. When they experience distress or abuse because of the word, they immediately fall away.
local: tɩchɛ baa tara nyɩbɛ ɛ, ba maa kor ɛ. A 'yɛr jũu, wõm bɩɩ gefʊ wa wa a ba sɛ̃, ba ma lɩɛb puor fɔɔ jaa.

eng: Others are like the seed scattered among the thorny plants. These are the ones who have heard the word.
local: A ala mɩ na lo a gʊʊr pɔ ɩ naa a bala na wõ a 'yɛr.

eng: but the worries of this life, the false appeal of wealth, and the desire for more things break in and choke the word, and it bears no fruit.
local: tɩchɛ ka a ãgãn nyã wõm, nɩ a bʊ̃tarsɩ bɛlfʊ, nɩ a bʊ̃bʊɔrsɩ fãɩn a 'yɛr, ka waa tʊ̃ɔ dɔɔ ɛ.

eng: The seed scattered on good soil are those who hear the word and embrace it. They bear fruit, in one case a yield of thirty to one, in another case sixty to one, and in another case one hundred to one.
local: A anyã mɩ na lo a kɔjɔ̃ pɔ ɩ naa a bala na wõ a 'yɛr na ba sɔɔ de na ba dɔɔ jur, ka amɩne ɩ lɩjɛr nɩ pie, amɩne lɩjaata, amɩne mɩ kɔbaa.

eng: Jesus said to them, Does anyone bring in a lamp in order to put it under a basket or a bed? Shouldnt it be placed on a lampstand?.
local: Wʊ sʊʊr ba naa, Ba ma chʊm fãtɩn na ba de laa kpa pɔɔ bɩɩ de su gado pĩlem bɩɩ? A ba ɩ a jie 'lɔ wʊn na chaar nɩrɛ jaa lɛ ba ma dɔɔl ɛ?.

eng: Everything hidden will be revealed, and everything secret will come out into the open.
local: Bon jaa na pɔɔ ban yuo wʊ naa, ka ala a jaa mɩ na sɔɔl yi chãa pɔ.

eng: Whoever has ears to listen should pay attention.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa na tara toor, wʊ chɛlɛ.

eng: He said to them, Listen carefully! God will evaluate you with the same standard you use to evaluate others. Indeed, you will receive even more.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ bɔ̃ɔ chɛlɛ sʊ̃ a lɛ yɩn wõnõ. A lɛ fʊn man tɩr, lɛ mɩ lɛ ban man kʊ fʊ, tɩ dɔɔl gba.

eng: Those who have will receive more, but as for those who dont have, even what they dont have will be taken away from them.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa na tara, Nãaŋmɩn ma vɛ̃ naa ka wʊ nyɛrɛ dɔɔlɔ, nɩrɛ nyã mɩ na ba tara ɛ, a bãlãa ala gba wʊn tara, Nãaŋmɩn na de naa a wʊ sɛ̃.

eng: Then Jesus said, This is what Gods kingdom is like. Its as though someone scatters seed on the ground.
local: Tɩ lɛ bɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr kʊ ba, A Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ nɩtɔ̃ lɛ wʊ kaara. Daba ma yaar a wʊ bʊ̃bʊrɔ bar.

eng: then sleeps and wakes night and day. The seed sprouts and grows, but the farmer doesnt know how.
local: Tãsɔɔ nɩ mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ, ka wʊ gãn bɩɩ wʊ ir naa, a bʊ̃bie ma bulaa naa nʊ̃ɔ, tɩchɛ a wʊ mãɛ gba maa bɔ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: The earth produces crops all by itself, first the stalk, then the head, then the full head of grain.
local: A bʊ̃bie ma bul naa a tẽe naa tɔ vaar tɩ dɔɔ jur, bɩ a bie.

eng: Whenever the crop is ready, the farmer goes out to cut the grain because its harvesttime.
local: Ala wa bɩ baar, wʊ ma chiin nɩ sʊɔ tɩ ŋman bojũu a ŋmab sɔ̃ɔ ta naa.

eng: He continued, Whats a good image for Gods kingdom? What parable can I use to explain it?.
local: Ka Yesu lɛ 'yɛr, Bo lɛ sɩn de man a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ bɩɩ sʊkpa buor lɛ sɩn lɔb gbɛb wʊ?.

eng: Consider a mustard seed. When scattered on the ground, its the smallest of all the seeds on the earth.
local: Wʊ kaara fɩka a kɔ̃kɔ̃ɔ bir, 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a bʊ̃bie a jaa pɔ bʊ̃bile.

eng: but when its planted, it grows and becomes the largest of all vegetable plants. It produces such large branches that the birds in the sky are able to nest in its shade.
local: Tɩchɛ wʊ ma bul ɩ tɩkpɛ̃ɛ na wʊ tɛ wilbɛrɛ, ka a lubil ma wa mara a wʊ 'maar pɔ.

eng: With many such parables he continued to give them the word, as much as they were able to hear.
local: Yesu de naa sʊkpayɔɔ na nyɛtaa na wʊ 'yɛr a 'yɛr kʊ ba, a lɛ an na ɩ ka ba tʊ̃ɔ bɔ̃ɔ a par.

eng: He spoke to them only in parables, then explained everything to his disciples when he was alone with them.
local: Sʊkpar tɛɛ lɛ wʊ ma de 'yɛrɛ nɩ a 'yɛr kʊrɔ ba. Tɩchɛ ala wa ɩ 'lɔ nɩ a wʊ poturbo tɛɛ lɛ, wʊ ma wiil naa a jaa par kʊ ba.

eng: Later that day, when evening came, Jesus said to them, Lets cross over to the other side of the lake.
local: A daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ jãanʊɔr, wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ poturbo, Yɩ ɩ a sɩ gɔ̃ɔ chen a man gɔ̃ɔn.

eng: They left the crowd and took him in the boat just as he was. Other boats followed along.
local: Ba bar naa a nɩyɔyɔɔ bala tɩchɛ ka a wʊ poturbo tara wʊ, ka ba 'matãan a gborsɩ amɩne gɔ̃ɔnɔ̃.

eng: Gale-force winds arose, and waves crashed against the boat so that the boat was swamped.
local: Ka a man kʊ̃ɔ sɛkpɛ̃ɛ 'lɔ ire ka a kʊ̃ɔ kpɛ a gboro pɔ ɩrɛ na sɛɛ.

eng: But Jesus was in the rear of the boat, sleeping on a pillow. They woke him up and said, Teacher, dont you care that were drowning.
local: tɩchɛ ka a Yesu be a gboro par gã kɔɔl wʊ ju guro. Ka a wʊ poturbo sãɩ wʊ na ba sʊrɔ wʊ, Wiwile, a ba bã fʊ sɩn mʊrɔ bɩɩ.

eng: He got up and gave orders to the wind, and he said to the lake, Silence! Be still! The wind settled down and there was a great calm.
local: Be lɛ ka wʊ ir tãn a sɛsɛbɛ nɩ a man, Yɩ ŋmaa vʊʊr! Yɩ ara a lɛ nɛ! Ka a sɛsɛbɛ ŋmaa vʊʊr ka a man ara jomm.

eng: Jesus asked them, Why are you frightened? Dont you have faith yet.
local: Ka wʊ sʊʊr a wʊ poturbo, Bo lɛ so ka yɩ joro a dãbãɛ? Yɩ chɛnɛ na ba sɔɔ ma bɩɩ.

eng: Overcome with awe, they said to each other, Who then is this? Even the wind and the sea obey him.
local: Ka a dãbãɛ kpɛ ba ka ba lɩɛb sʊrɔ taa, An lɛ a nyã ka a sɛsɛbɛ nɩ a man tu a wʊ nɛɛ.

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to the other side of the lake, to the region of the Gerasenes.
local: Yesu nɩ a wʊ poturbo gɔ̃ɔ naa a man, a Gerasenɩ nɩbɛ wɛr pɔ.

eng: As soon as Jesus got out of the boat, a man possessed by an evil spirit came out of the tombs.
local: Yesu na wa yi a gboro pɔ, daba na gele yi naa a yar par wa tuor wʊ.

eng: This man lived among the tombs, and no one was ever strong enough to restrain him, even with a chain.
local: A gẽgelaa nyã kpɩɛrɛ naa a yar par ka nɩrɛ jaa ba be be na tʊ̃ɔ lũ wʊ nɩ bɔ̃jɔrsɩ gba ɛ.

eng: He had been secured many times with leg irons and chains, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one was tough enough to control him.
local: Ba ma lũ naa a wʊ gbɛɛ nɩ a nuur tɩchɛ ka wʊ ŋma, tɩ kar a kue ala bar. Nɩrɛ jaa ba be be na tʊ̃ɔ wʊ ɛ.

eng: Night and day in the tombs and the hills, he would howl and cut himself with stones.
local: Tãsɔɔ nɩ mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ, wʊ ma be naa a yar par nɩ a tɔ̃n ju tãna tɩ ŋmara a wʊ mãɛ nɩ kʊsɩbɛ.

eng: When he saw Jesus from far away, he ran and knelt before him.
local: Wʊn wa nyɛ a Yesu na be tɛɛr, wʊ jo naa tɩ lo gbur dumo a wʊ niem.

eng: shouting, What have you to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? Swear to God that you wont torture me.
local: tɩ na wʊ ŋme chɛlma 'yɛrɛ, Bo fʊ bʊɔrɔ ɩka fʊ ɩ ma? Fʊ ɩ naa a Nãaŋmɩn nyã na gar a jaa Bie pɔn Nãaŋmɩn ɩka fʊ kʊ̃ ge ma ɛ.

eng: He said this because Jesus had already commanded him, Unclean spirit, come out of the man.
local: Bojũu Yesu dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ wʊ ɩka, A fʊ̃ʊ sɩdɛɛr wa yi a daba pɔ.

eng: Jesus asked him, What is your name? He responded, Legion is my name, because we are many.
local: Be lɛ ka Yesu sʊʊr wʊ, Bãa a fʊ yuor di? Ka wʊ 'yɛr, Sɩyɔɔ, bojũu sɩ waa naa.

eng: They pleaded with Jesus not to send them out of that region.
local: Ka wʊ sʊɔr a Yesu yãayãabɩlɛ ɩka wʊ ta dii wʊ bar a jie 'lɔ ɛ.

eng: A large herd of pigs was feeding on the hillside.
local: Doyɔɔ mɩ ara na gbʊr a tɔ̃ɔ, dire.

eng: Send us into the pigs! they begged. Let us go into the pigs.
local: Ka a sɩdɛbɛ sʊɔr a Yesu, Ir sɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ a dor ala pɔ na fʊ sɔɔ ka sɩ tɩ kpɛ a ba pɔ.

eng: Jesus gave them permission, so the unclean spirits left the man and went into the pigs. Then the herd of about two thousand pigs rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: Wʊ sɔɔ naa ka a sɩdɛbɛ yi tɩ kpɛ a dor ala. A ɩ naa dor tur ayi ka a jaa tĩgbil suu tɩ kpɛ a man pɔ gbʊ̃m kpi kpi.

eng: Those who tended the pigs ran away and told the story in the city and in the countryside. People came to see what had happened.
local: A bala na kaara a dor jon kul a tẽe pɔ nɩ a tẽbil ala na gbʊr tɩ 'yɛr a lɛ na ɩ. Ka a nɩbɛ yi chen ɩka ba tɩ kaa.

eng: They came to Jesus and saw the man who used to be demon-possessed. They saw the very man who had been filled with many demons sitting there fully dressed and completely sane, and they were filled with awe.
local: Ba wa naa a Yesu sɛ̃ wa nyɛ a daba 'lɔ na dɔ̃ɔ tara a sɩdɛyɔɔ gele ka wʊ chɩ su bonsɩ jãnɛ, ka a dãbãɛ kpɛ ba.

eng: Those who had actually seen what had happened to the demon-possessed man told the others about the pigs.
local: A bala na nyɛ 'yɛr kʊ a nɩbɛ a lɛ na ɩ a daba na dɔ̃ɔ tara a sɩdɛbɛ nɩ a dor 'yɛr.

eng: Then they pleaded with Jesus to leave their region.
local: ka a nɩbɛ piel sʊɔrɔ a Yesu ka wʊ yi a ba wɛr pɔ.

eng: While he was climbing into the boat, the one who had been demon-possessed pleaded with Jesus to let him come along as one of his disciples.
local: Yesu na wa kpɩɛrɛ a gboro pɔ ka a daba 'lɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ a sɩdɛbɛ na yi sʊɔr wʊ ɩka wʊ ɩ a ba 'mataa chen.

eng: But Jesus wouldnt allow it. Go home to your own people, Jesus said, and tell them what the Lord has done for you and how he has shown you mercy.
local: Ka a Yesu ba sɔɔ ɛ, tɩ 'yɛr wʊ, Kul a fʊ yir dẽme sɛ̃ tɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba a lɛ a Soro na ɩ kʊ fʊ, nɩ a lɛ wʊn jo a fʊ nɩbaal.

eng: The man went away and began to proclaim in the Ten Cities all that Jesus had done for him, and everyone was amazed.
local: A daba chen na wʊ tɩ piel 'yɛrɛ a lɛ a Yesu na maal kʊ wʊ a wɛr 'lɔ pɔ ban bʊɔlɔ a Dekapolisɩ. Ka a nɛɛr 'maa a nɩbɛ.

eng: Jesus crossed the lake again, and on the other side a large crowd gathered around him on the shore.
local: Yesu na wa gɔ̃ɔ a man gɔ̃ɔn go, nɩyɔyɔɔ lɔ̃ɔ naa na ba jilʊ̃ wʊ. Wʊ chɛnɛ na ara a man nɛɛ.

eng: Jairus, one of the synagogue leaders, came forward. When he saw Jesus, he fell at his feet.
local: ka a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie nɩkpɛ̃ɛ ãsʊɔ ban bʊɔlɔ Jairusɩ wa a be. Wʊn wa nyɛ a Yesu.

eng: and pleaded with him, My daughter is about to die. Please, come and place your hands on her so that she can be healed and live.
local: wʊ gbur dumo a Yesu niem tɩ sʊɔr wʊ, Yãayãabɩlɛ, a n bipɔɔbile lɛ ɩ baal ɩrɛ na kpi. Wa dɔɔl wʊ a fʊ nũu ka wʊ sa ta kpi ɛ.

eng: So Jesus went with him. A swarm of people were following Jesus, crowding in on him.
local: Ka a Yesu nɩ a daba 'mataa chiine. Nɩyɔyɔɔ turo naa dara taa tɩɛrɛ wʊ.

eng: A woman was there who had been bleeding for twelve years.
local: Pɔɔ kɔ̃ɔ mɩ ben be lo sɩɛ suoro faara yuomo pie nɩ ayi.

eng: She had suffered a lot under the care of many doctors, and had spent everything she had without getting any better. In fact, she had gotten worse.
local: Wʊ bal di dɔɔyɛ yɔɔ a tɩtɩɩlbɛ jie na wʊ sɔ̃ɔ bon jaa wʊn tara tɩ ba sa ɛ. Ka a baal tʊ̃ɔnɔ̃ wʊ.

eng: Because she had heard about Jesus, she came up behind him in the crowd and touched his clothes.
local: Wʊn wa wõ a Yesu yele, wʊ tu naa a puor tɩ sɩɩr a wʊ fuu.

eng: She was thinking, If I can just touch his clothes, Ill be healed.
local: bojũu wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ mãɛ. Maa tʊ̃ɔ sɩɩr a wʊ fuu gba, ɩn sa naa, na n pɛ̃n.

eng: Her bleeding stopped immediately, and she sensed in her body that her illness had been healed.
local: Wʊn wa sɩɩr a Yesu fuu nɛɛ wa baar, a jãɩ ŋmaa naa a dẽdẽe daa ka wʊ bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a wʊ wʊʊr baar naa.

eng: At that very moment, Jesus recognized that power had gone out from him. He turned around in the crowd and said, Who touched my clothes.
local: Ka a Yesu mɩ bɔ̃ɔn a fɔɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ a kpɛ̃ɔ na yi a wʊ sɛ̃. Ka a wʊ lɩɛb a nɩyɔɔ bala pɔ sʊʊr, An lɛ sɩɩr a n fuu.

eng: His disciples said to him, Dont you see the crowd pressing against you? Yet you ask, Who touched me.
local: Ka a wʊ poturbo 'yɛr, Nyɛ a lɛ a nɩyɔyɔɔ banyã na dara taa tɩɛrɛ fʊ, tɩchɛ a fʊ sʊrɔ a sʊɔ 'lɔ na sɩɩr fʊ.

eng: But Jesus looked around carefully to see who had done it.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Yesu chɛnɛ kaara gʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ nyɛ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na sɩɩr wʊ.

eng: The woman, full of fear and trembling, came forward. Knowing what had happened to her, she fell down in front of Jesus and told him the whole truth.
local: Ka a pɔɔ na bɔ̃ɔn a lɛ na ɩ, dãbãɛ kpɛ wʊ naa ka wʊ mɩrɛ na wʊ wa gbur dumo a Yesu niem tɩ 'yɛr a lɛ jaa na ɩ.

eng: He responded, Daughter, your faith has healed you; go in peace, healed from your disease.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, N yɛpɔɔ, fʊn sɔɔ ma jũu, fʊ sa naa. Kulo nɩ ãgãn 'maar, a fʊ dɔɔyɛ baar naa.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking with her, messengers came from the synagogue leaders house, saying to Jairus, Your daughter has died. Why bother the teacher any longer.
local: A Yesu ba 'yɛr baar ɛ, tɩchɛ nɩbɛ kɔ̃ɔ yi a Jairusɩ yir wa 'yɛr wʊ, A fʊ bie kpi naa. Bãa 'yɔ̃ɔ fʊ dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a wiwile go.

eng: But Jesus overheard their report and said to the synagogue leader, Dont be afraid; just keep trusting.
local: Yesu ba chɛlɛ a lɛ ban 'yɛrɛ ɛ, tɩ na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a daba, Taa joro dãbãɛ ɛ. Sɔɔ ma dɛ.

eng: He didnt allow anyone to follow him except Peter, James, and John, James brother.
local: Yesu ba sɔɔ ka nɩrɛ jaa tu wʊ ɛ, ka Pita nɩ Jemesɩ nɩ a wʊ yɛbile Jɔɔn tɛɛ ba lɛ ɛ.

eng: They came to the synagogue leaders house, and he saw a commotion, with people crying and wailing loudly.
local: Ban wa ta a Jairusɩ yir, Yesu nyɛ naa a nɩbɛ na kõnõ ŋmiere chɛlsɩ.

eng: He went in and said to them, Whats all this commotion and crying about? The child isnt dead. Shes only sleeping.
local: Ka wʊ kpɛ tɩ sʊʊr ba, Bãa lɛ yɩ kõnõ ŋmiere nɩ a chɛlsɩ a lɛ? A bie ba kpi ɛ. Wʊ gur naa.

eng: They laughed at him, but he threw them all out. Then, taking the childs parents and his disciples with him, he went to the room where the child was.
local: A ba laara tʊrɔ nɩ wʊ. Wʊ dii naa a nɩbɛ ba jaa bar ka ba yi tɩ wʊ tara a bie sãa nɩ a ma nɩ a wʊ poturbo bala wʊn 'mataa chiin, a ba kpɛ a jie 'lɔ a bikũu na be.

eng: Taking her hand, he said to her, Talitha koum , which means, Young woman, get up.
local: Ka wʊ tɩ nyɔɔ a pɔɔbile nũu tɩ 'yɛr, Talita kum! A par lɛ a Yesu nɩbɛ kɔkɔr pɔ, Pɔɔbile, ir.

eng: Suddenly the young woman got up and began to walk around. She was years old. They were shocked!.
local: Ka wʊ vaa ir chiine. Yuomo pie nɩ ayi bie lɛ. Ka a yele 'maa ba nɛɛ.

eng: He gave them strict orders that no one should know what had happened. Then he told them to give her something to eat.
local: Yesu kpaal ba naa jũu ka ba taa vɛ̃ ka a nɩrɛ jaa wõ naa ɛ. Tɩ 'yɛr ɩka ba kʊ a pɔɔbile bʊ̃diir ka wʊ di.

eng: Jesus left that place and came to his hometown. His disciples followed him.
local: Yesu yi naa a be ka a wʊ poturbo tu wʊ ka a ba 'mataa kul a Najaretɩ na ɩ a wʊ tẽe.

eng: On the Sabbath, he began to teach in the synagogue. Many who heard him were surprised. Where did this man get all this? Whats this wisdom hes been given? What about the powerful acts accomplished through him?.
local: Ka a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir wa ta, ka wʊ kpɛ be a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie tɩ piel wile, ka a nɩyɔɔ bala na wõ nɛɛr 'maa. Ka ba sʊrɔ, Nyɩnɛ lɛ a daba nyã nyɛ a bɔ̃ɔfʊ nyã? Yɛ̃ buor lɛ a nyã wʊn tara na wʊ gba tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃?.

eng: Isnt this the carpenter? Isnt he Marys son and the brother of James, Joses, Judas, and Simon? Arent his sisters here with us? They were repulsed by him and fell into sin.
local: A ba ɩ a mɩmɩɛrɛ lɛ ɛ? A ba ɩ a Mɛɛr bie lɛ ɛ, a wʊ yɛbil ɩ Jemesɩ nɩ Josesɩ nɩ Judas nɩ Simon ɛ? Ka a wʊ yɛpɔbɔ mɩ be ka a sɩ sɛ̃ ɛ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Prophets are honored everywhere except in their own hometowns, among their relatives, and in their own households.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ nyɛrɛ na 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ jie jaa, tɩchɛ a wʊ mãɛ tẽe nɩ a wʊ yir dẽme nɩ a wʊ nɩbɛ sɛ̃ lɛ waa nyɛrɛ 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ ɛ.

eng: He was unable to do any miracles there, except that he placed his hands on a few sick people and healed them.
local: Waa tʊ̃ɔ tʊ̃ nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ yɔɔ a be ɛ, tɩ wʊ dɔɔl a wʊ nũu baalsɩ bãlãa a ba sa.

eng: He was appalled by their disbelief. Then Jesus traveled through the surrounding villages teaching.
local: Yesu nɛɛ 'maa wʊ naa bojũu baa sɔɔ de wʊ ɛ. Wʊ yɔn gʊɔr a tẽn na wʊ wile.

eng: He called for the Twelve and sent them out in pairs. He gave them authority over unclean spirits.
local: Wʊ bʊɔl naa a wʊ poturbo Pie nɩ Ayi bala wʊn dɔ̃ɔ ir, ka ba wa a wʊ sɛ̃ ka wʊ tʊ̃ ba ayir yir, na wʊ kʊ ba kpɛ̃ɔ ka ba tʊ̃ɔ sɩdɛbɛ.

eng: He instructed them to take nothing for the journey except a walking stick—no bread, no bags, and no money in their belts.
local: Wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ ta de bon jaa ɛ, yɩ tara dadiirsɩ tɛɛ. Yɩ ta tara bʊ̃diir ɛ, bɩɩ wʊʊr ɛ, bɩɩ yɔ̃ɔ libie a yɩ sɩɩr gãma pɔ ɛ.

eng: He told them to wear sandals but not to put on two shirts.
local: Yɩ ãsʊɔ jaa wʊ su nafɔbɔ nɩ fuu bʊ̃'yen tɛɛ.

eng: He said, Whatever house you enter, remain there until you leave that place.
local: Yir jaa pɔ yɩn wa kpɛ, yɩ be a be tɩ ta a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ yɩn wa yi a tẽe 'lɔ pɔ.

eng: If a place doesnt welcome you or listen to you, as you leave, shake the dust off your feet as a witness against them.
local: Tẽe jaa pɔ yɩn wa kpɛ ka baa de yɩ ɛ, bɩɩ sɔɔ a 'yɛr ɛ, yɩn wa yire yɩ kpoor a yɩ gbɛɛ tã-uul ka a di a ba dɛɛr dãasɩɛ.

eng: So they went out and proclaimed that people should change their hearts and lives.
local: Ba yin chen tɩ mʊɔlɔ 'yɛrɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr ɩka a nɩbɛ ba lɩɛb ba tɩɛr.

eng: They cast out many demons, and they anointed many sick people with olive oil and healed them.
local: Ba diin a sɩdɛbɛ ka a yi, tɩ 'lɩɩ baalsɩ kãa ka ba sa.

eng: Herod the king heard about these things, because the name of Jesus had become well-known. Some were saying, John the Baptist has been raised from the dead, and this is why miraculous powers are at work through him.
local: A nãa Hɛrɔd mɩ wõ naa a anyã, bojũu nɩrɛ jaa wõ naa a Yesu yuor. Ka a bamɩne 'yɛrɛ, A Jɔɔn 'lɔ na ɩ a kɔ̃suoro, 'lɔ lɛ tɔ̃ɔ yi a yaa pɔ, ala lɛ so ka wʊ tʊ̃nɔ̃ a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ a lɛ.

eng: Others were saying, He is Elijah. Still others were saying, He is a prophet like one of the ancient prophets.
local: Ka a banyã mɩ 'yɛrɛ, Elaija lɛ. Ka a bamɩne mɩ chɛnɛ 'yɛrɛ, Wʊ ɩ naa a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ ãsʊɔ nɩtãa a bala na dɔ̃ɔ be be a kõro jaa.

eng: But when Herod heard these rumors, he said, John, whom I beheaded, has been raised to life.
local: Tɩchɛ a Hɛrɔd na wa wõ, wʊ 'yɛr naa, A Jɔɔn 'lɔ ɩn vɛ̃ ka ba ŋmaa a wʊ ju lɛ, yi.

eng: He said this because Herod himself had arranged to have John arrested and put in prison because of Herodias, the wife of Herods brother Philip. Herod had married her.
local: A Hɛrɔd mãɛ lɛ dɔ̃ɔ vɛ̃ ka ba nyɔɔ a Jɔɔn waan, wa lũ 'yɔ̃ɔ a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ. Wʊ ɩ naa a nɩtɔ̃, a wʊ yɛbile Filip pɔɔ Hɛrɔdiyas wʊn de jũu.

eng: but John told Herod, Its against the law for you to marry your brothers wife.
local: Jɔɔn 'yɛr kʊ a Hɛrɔd, A ba tara sɔr ɩka fʊ de a fʊ yɛbil vʊr pɔɔ kul ɛ.

eng: So Herodias had it in for John. She wanted to kill him, but she couldnt.
local: Ka a Hɛrɔdiyas tara nɩ a Jɔɔn jɛɛr bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ kʊ wʊ, tɩchɛ waa tʊ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: This was because Herod respected John. He regarded him as a righteous and holy person, so he protected him. Johns words greatly confused Herod, yet he enjoyed listening to him.
local: A Hɛrɔd bɔ̃ɔ naa a Jɔɔn na ɩ nɩsʊ̃ nɩ chɛchɛ, na wʊ joro a Jɔɔn dãbãɛ, na wʊ gu wʊ tara. A Hɛrɔd wa wõ a Jɔɔn 'yɛr a ma sɔb wʊn nɩbir, tɩchɛ wʊ ma bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka wʊ chɛlɛ a wʊ 'yɛr.

eng: Finally, the time was right. It was on one of Herods birthdays, when he had prepared a feast for his high-ranking officials and military officers and Galilees leading residents.
local: Tɩchɛ sɔr wa yuon kʊ a Hɛrɔdiyas a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ a nãa na wa maala a wʊ dɔɔfʊ bɩbir yele. A wʊ dɔɔfʊ bɩbir nyãna daar, wʊ bʊɔl naa a nɩbɛrɛ bala na turo a wʊ puor, nɩ a sojasɩ nɩbɛrɛ, nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ na be a Galilee pɔ.

eng: Herods daughter Herodias came in and danced, thrilling Herod and his dinner guests. The king said to the young woman, Ask me whatever you wish, and I will give it to you.
local: A Hɛrɔdiyas bipɔɔ wa kpɛ naa, na wʊ sɛ a bãnɛ ka a pɛl a nãa Hɛrɔd nɩ a wʊ nɩbɛrɛ bala ba jaa pʊɔ. Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a pɔɔbile, Sʊɔr ma bon jaa fʊn bʊɔrɔ, ɩn kʊ fʊ naa.

eng: Then he swore to her, Whatever you ask I will give to you, even as much as half of my kingdom.
local: Be lɛ wʊ põl kʊ a pɔɔbile, Bon jaa fʊn sʊɔr ala ɩ a n nãalʊ̃ʊ ŋmaa gba lɛ, n na kʊ fʊ naa.

eng: She left the banquet hall and said to her mother, What should I ask for? John the Baptists head, Herodias replied.
local: Ka wʊ yi chen tɩ sʊʊr a wʊ ma, Bo lɛ ɩn sʊɔr? Ka a wʊ ma 'yɛr wʊ, Tɩ sʊɔr a Jɔɔn Kɔ̃suor ju.

eng: Hurrying back to the ruler, she made her request: I want you to give me John the Baptists head on a plate, right this minute.
local: Ka a pɔɔbile yi pɔɔ fɔɔ lɛ kpɛ a nãa sɛ̃ tɩ 'yɛr, N bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka fʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ a Jɔɔn Kɔ̃suor ju a la'yɛlma pɔ kʊ ma a pʊ̃pãanyã.

eng: Although the king was upset, because of his solemn pledge and his guests, he didnt want to refuse her.
local: A nãa pʊɔ sɔ̃ɔ naa, tɩchɛ a nɩbɛ bala wʊn bʊɔl na wʊn põl kʊ a pɔɔbile jũu, waa bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ tɔr ɛ.

eng: So he ordered a guard to bring Johns head. The guard went to the prison, cut off Johns head.
local: Ka wʊ tʊ̃ a nɩkʊrɔ ãsʊɔ a dẽdẽe daa ɩka wʊ chen tɩ ŋmaa a Jɔɔn ju waan. A daba chen tɩ ŋmaa a Jɔɔn ju a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ.

eng: brought his head on a plate, and gave it to the young woman, and she gave it to her mother.
local: 'yɔ̃ɔ a la'yɛlma pɔ waan, wa kʊ a pɔɔbile ka wʊ de kʊ a wʊ ma.

eng: When Johns disciples heard what had happened, they came and took his dead body and laid it in a tomb.
local: A Jɔɔn poturbo na wõ a lɛ, ba chen tɩ 'mɔɔ wʊ na ba ũu.

eng: The apostles returned to Jesus and told him everything they had done and taught.
local: A Yesu nɩtʊ̃nsɩ lɩɛb wa lɔ̃ɔ taa a wʊ sɛ̃, na ba wa man a lɛ jaa ban tʊ̃ tɩ wiil a nɩbɛ, na ba kʊ a Yesu.

eng: Many people were coming and going, so there was no time to eat. He said to the apostles, Come by yourselves to a secluded place and rest for a while.
local: A nɩbɛ yɔɔ na waara tɩ chiine jũu, jaa gba ba be be a ban di ɛ. Be lɛ ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ wa a sɩn tɛɛ chen a jie 'lɔ a nɩbɛ na ba be ɛ, a yɩ tɩ jãnɛ pɛ̃n.

eng: They departed in a boat by themselves for a deserted place.
local: Ba kpɛ naa a gboro bala tɛɛ chen a jie 'lɔ̃nɔ̃.

eng: Many people saw them leaving and recognized them, so they ran ahead from all the cities and arrived before them.
local: Tɩchɛ a nɩbɛ na wa nyɛ ban chiine ba bɔ̃ɔ ba naa. A ba yi a tẽn a jaa de nie jon gbɛɛ tɩ ara a be.

eng: When Jesus arrived and saw a large crowd, he had compassion on them because they were like sheep without a shepherd. Then he began to teach them many things.
local: A Yesu na wa tɩ ta, na wʊ nyɛ a nɩyɔɔ a ba ɩrɛ nɩbaal a wʊ sɛ̃, ban kaara fɩka piir na ba tara chɩnɛ jũu, wʊ piel wile ba yelyɔɔ.

eng: Late in the day, his disciples came to him and said, This is an isolated place, and its already late in the day.
local: Mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ na wa mʊrɔ a wʊ poturbo wa naa wa 'yɛr kʊ a Yesu, A ka ɩ naa mɔgɔ̃ɔ, tɩ a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ mɩ mʊrɔ;.

eng: Send them away so that they can go to the surrounding countryside and villages and buy something to eat for themselves.
local: wɛl a nɩbɛ bar a ba chen a por nɩ a tẽn na gbʊr tɩ bɔ bʊ̃diir da di.

eng: He replied, You give them something to eat. But they said to him, Should we go off and buy bread worth almost eight months pay and give it to them to eat.
local: Ka be lɛ a Yesu 'yɛr sɔɔ ba, Yɩn mãɛ yɩ kʊ ba bʊ̃kɔ̃ɔ a ba di. Ka a poturbo 'yɛr ɩka, Nyɩnɛ lɛ sɩn nyɛ a libie anyãna? Nɩbʊ̃'yen ŋmɛrsɩ aniin sãyar gba! Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ sɩn ɩ kʊ ba jaa bʊ̃diir.

eng: He said to them, How much bread do you have? Take a look. After checking, they said, Five loaves of bread and two fish.
local: A be lɛ a Yesu sʊʊr ba, Paanʊ̃ɔsɩ aŋmɩnɛ lɛ yɩ tara? Yɩ chen tɩ kaa. Be lɛ ba chen tɩ kaa tɩ wa 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Paanʊ̃ɔsɩ anũu, nɩ jãmɛ ayi.

eng: He directed the disciples to seat all the people in groups as though they were having a banquet on the green grass.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a poturbo a ba ɩ a nɩbɛ ba jaa a ba jɛ̃ gbulsɩ a mɔ'yɔlɔ ju.

eng: They sat down in groups of hundreds and fifties.
local: Be lɛ a nɩbɛ jãnɛ jãnɛ, a gbulsɩ gbulsɩ. Bamɩne jãnɛ naa kɔɔr kɔɔr, ka bamɩne mɩ jãnɛ lɩjaayi nɩ piir.

eng: He took the five loaves and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them, broke the loaves into pieces, and gave them to his disciples to set before the people. He also divided the two fish among them all.
local: A Yesu de naa a paanʊ̃ɔsɩ anũu nɩ a jãmɛ ayi ala na wʊ kaa saaju tɩ dʊl puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan. Wʊ de naa a paanʊ̃ɔsɩ ŋma ŋma tɩ de kʊ a wʊ poturbo tɩ 'yɛr, Yɩ de põ a nɩbɛ. Tɩ lɛ de a jãmɛ ayi ala mɩ ŋma põ a nɩbɛ.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full.
local: A nɩbɛ ba jaa din, na ba tɩɩ.

eng: They filled twelve baskets with the leftover pieces of bread and fish.
local: tɩchɛ ka a poturbo wob a bʊ̃ŋmaara 'yɔ̃ɔ pɛr pie nɩ ayi.

eng: About five thousand had eaten.
local: A nɩbɛ bala na dɔ̃ɔ di a bʊ̃diir ala, dɔɔr tɛɛ ɩ naa tur anũu.

eng: Right then, Jesus made his disciples get into a boat and go ahead to the other side of the lake, toward Bethsaida, while he dismissed the crowd.
local: Ajienaa, ka a Yesu 'yɔ̃ɔ a wʊ poturbo a ba kpɛ a gboro na ba de nie chiine a Betɩsaida tɩ wʊ na wɛl a nɩbɛ bar.

eng: After saying good-bye to them, Jesus went up onto a mountain to pray.
local: Wʊn wa bar ba wa baar, wʊ do naa a tɔ̃ɔ ju ɩka wʊ tɩ puor.

eng: Evening came and the boat was in the middle of the lake, but he was alone on the land.
local: A wɛr na wa sɔɔrɔ, a gboro be naa a man sãlsɔɔ, tɩchɛ ka wʊ be a wɛyõo, 'lɔ tɛɛ.

eng: He saw his disciples struggling. They were trying to row forward, but the wind was blowing against them. Very early in the morning, he came to them, walking on the lake. He intended to pass by them.
local: Wʊ nyɛ naa a wʊ poturbo ka ba mɔ̃nɔ̃ a gboro faara bojũu a sɛsɛbɛ furo na tuoro ba. A wɛr na wa gbʊrɔ a chaarfʊ, wʊ yin chiine a kʊ̃ɔ ju waara a ba sɛ̃, ta bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ po ba.

eng: When they saw him walking on the lake, they thought he was a ghost and they screamed.
local: Ban wa nyɛ wʊn chiine a kʊ̃ɔ ju a lɛ, ba tɩɛr ɩka kpĩin lɛ, na ba ŋme chɛlsɩ.

eng: Seeing him was terrifying to all of them. Just then he spoke to them, Be encouraged! Its me. Dont be afraid.
local: bojũu ba jaa nyɛ wʊ naa ka dãbãɛ kpɛ ba. Ajienaa, ka a Yesu tãn, Yɩ nyɔɔ nyãa! Maa lɛ! Yɩ taa joro dãbãɛ ɛ.

eng: He got into the boat, and the wind settled down. His disciples were so baffled they were beside themselves.
local: Tɩ pãa kpɛ a gboro pɔ ka a sɛsɛbɛ ŋmaa vʊʊr. Ka a nɛɛr 'maa ba yɔɔ jaa.

eng: Thats because they hadnt understood about the loaves. Their minds had been closed so that they resisted Gods ways.
local: Baa dɔ̃ɔ bɔ̃ɔ a paanʊ̃ɔsɩ ala par ɛ, bojũu a ba ju ba wõnõ nyaar ɛ.

eng: When Jesus and his disciples had crossed the lake, they landed at Gennesaret, anchored the boat.
local: Ban wa gɔ̃ɔ a man do lũ a gboro a Genesaretɩ gɔ̃ɔn.

eng: and came ashore. People immediately recognized Jesus.
local: tɩ tɔ̃ɔ yi a gboro pɔ, nɩbɛ nyɛ naa a Yesu bɔ̃ɔ.

eng: and ran around that whole region bringing sick people on their mats to wherever they heard he was.
local: A nɩbɛ jon yɔ a wɛr 'lɔ wʊ jaa na ba tuo a ba baalsɩ nɩ sɔ̃n chiin jie jaa ban wõ ɩka wʊ be naa.

eng: Wherever he went—villages, cities, or farming communities—they would place the sick in the marketplaces and beg him to allow them to touch even the hem of his clothing. Everyone who touched him was healed.
local: Jie jaa wʊn chen, tẽkpɛ̃ɛ pɔ bɩɩ, tẽbile pɔ bɩɩ, ka a tẽe kõkoor lɛ̃ɛɛ, ba ma gaal naa a ba baalsɩ a chãa pɔ. Ba ma sʊɔrɔ naa a Yesu ɩka wʊ sɔɔ ka ba sɩɩr a wʊ fuu nɛɛ gba, bala jaa na sɩɩr, ma sa naa.

eng: The Pharisees and some legal experts from Jerusalem gathered around Jesus.
local: A Faraseemɩne nɩ a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe bamɩne yi naa a Jerusalɛm wa lɔ̃ɔ a Yesu sɛ̃.

eng: They saw some of his disciples eating food with unclean hands. They were eating without first ritually purifying their hands through washing.
local: na ba nyɛ a wʊ poturbo ka ba dire nɩ nũdɛbɛ. Ka a lɛ wiil ɩka baa pɛɛ a ba nuur a, Puorfʊ pɛɛfʊ 'lɔ ɛ.

eng: The Pharisees and all the Jews dont eat without first washing their hands carefully. This is a way of observing the rules handed down by the elders.
local: A Faraseemɩne nɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ba maa di ka bala ba pɛɛ a ba puorfʊ pɛɛfʊ 'lɔ ɛ, na ba turo a lɛ a ba nɩbɛrɛ na bin.

eng: Upon returning from the marketplace, they dont eat without first immersing themselves. They observe many other rules that have been handed down, such as the washing of cups, jugs, pans, and sleeping mats.
local: Bala wa yi daa wa, ba maa di ka bala ba pɛɛ a pɛɛfʊ 'lɔ ɛ. Ban turo a ba bin yɔɔ ala jũu, ba ma pɛɛn a ŋmama, dʊʊr nɩ a kulaar.

eng: So the Pharisees and legal experts asked Jesus, Why are your disciples not living according to the rules handed down by the elders but instead eat food with ritually unclean hands.
local: Ala lɛ so ka a Faraseemɩne nɩ a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe sʊʊr a Yesu, Bo lɛ so ka a fʊ poturbo ba turo a lɛ a sɩ nɩbɛrɛ na bin ɛ, na baa pɛɛ a ba nuur ɛ tɩ dire nɩ nũdɛbɛ.

eng: He replied, Isaiah really knew what he was talking about when he prophesied about you hypocrites. He wrote, This people honors me with their lips, but their hearts are far away from me.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Sɩrɛ jaa lɛ a Isaiya dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr a yɩn nɛɛr ayi dẽme 'yɛr na wʊ sɛb. Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr naa, A yɩn nɩbɛ banyãna 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ma naa nɩ a yɩ nɛɛ, tɩchɛ ka a yɩ pʊtɩɛr jãa a n sɛ̃.

eng: Their worship of me is empty since they teach instructions that are human words.
local: Pɔrɔ lɛ yɩ puoro ma, bojũu yɩ wile naa nɩsaal nɛbinsɩ tɩ tɩɛr ɩka maa nɛɛ lɛ.

eng: You ignore Gods commandment while holding on to rules created by humans and handed down to you.
local: Yɩ bar naa a Nãaŋmɩn Nɛɛ tɩ turo a lɛ a yɩ nɩbɛrɛ na bin.

eng: Jesus continued, Clearly, you are experts at rejecting Gods commandment in order to establish these rules.
local: A ba ɩ ka yɩ de yɛ̃ bar a Nãaŋmɩn Nɛɛ tufʊ tɩ turo a lɛ a yɩn mãɛ na bin ɛ.

eng: Moses said, Honor your father and your mother, and The person who speaks against father or mother will certainly be put to death .
local: Mosesɩ 'yɛr naa a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ, 'Yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a fʊ sãa nɩ a fʊ ma. Na wʊ mɩ lɛ 'yɛr, Ɩ̃sʊɔ jaa wa pɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ a wʊ sãa bɩɩ a wʊ ma, ba kʊ wʊ.

eng: But you say, If you tell your father or mother,Everything Im expected to contribute to you is corban .
local: Tɩchɛ ka yɩn 'yɛr ɩka a sɛɛ na, nɩrɛ wa 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ sãa bɩɩ a wʊ ma, Bon jaa ɩn naa kʊ fʊ, n den kʊ Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: then you are no longer required to care for your father or mother.
local: Yɩ maa lɛ vɛ̃ ka wʊ maal bon jaa kʊ a wʊ sãa bɩɩ a wʊ ma ɛ.

eng: In this way you do away with Gods word in favor of the rules handed down to you, which you pass on to others. And you do a lot of other things just like that.
local: Yɩn ɩrɛ a nɩtɔ̃ jũu, ka yɩ kaara a Nãaŋmɩn Nɛɛ pɔrɔ tɩ turo a lɛ yɩn mãɛ na bin. Yelyɔɔ mɩ ben be yɩn ɩrɛ nɩ nyɛtaa a nɩtɔ̃.

eng: Then Jesus called the crowd again and said, Listen to me, all of you, and understand.
local: A Yesu lɛ bɔ̃ɔ bʊɔl a nɩyɔɔ ka ba lɩɛb wa a wʊ sɛ̃. Ka wʊ 'yɛr, Yɩ ãsʊɔ jaa wʊ chɛl wõ ɩn 'yɛrɛ tɩ na yɩ bɔ̃ɔ a par.

eng: Nothing outside of a person can enter and contaminate a person in Gods sight; rather, the things that come out of a person contaminate the person.
local: Bon jaa ba be ka a yõo ka nɩrɛ na de di ka a bʊ̃nyɩnɛ vɛ̃ ka wʊ dɛɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ bon 'lɔ na yi a nɩrɛ pʊtɩɛr pɔ lɛ na vɛ̃ ka wʊ dɛɛ.

eng: After leaving the crowd, he entered a house where his disciples asked him about that riddle.
local: Sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ wʊn wa bar a nɩyɔɔ bala tɩ kpɛ a yir, a wʊ poturbo sʊʊr wʊ naa a sʊkpa par.

eng: He said to them, Dont you understand either? Dont you know that nothing from the outside that enters a person has the power to contaminate?.
local: Ka a Yesu sʊʊr ba, Yɩ mɩ ɩ naa a nɩbɛ bala na ba bɔ̃ɔ a par bɩɩ? Yaa bɔ̃ɔ ɩka bon jaa ba be be ka nɩrɛ na de di na wʊ dɛɛ ɛ.

eng: Thats because it doesnt enter into the heart but into the stomach, and it goes out into the sewer. By saying this, Jesus declared that no food could contaminate a person in Gods sight.
local: bojũu wʊ maa kpɛ a wʊ tɩɛr pɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ pʊɔm lɛ wʊ ma suu na wʊ tɩ yi. Yesu 'yɛr a nɩtɔ̃ na wʊ wiil ɩka a bʊ̃diir a jaa sɛɛn difʊ.

eng: Its what comes out of a person that contaminates someone in Gods sight, he said.
local: Na wʊ mɩ lɛ 'yɛr ɩka, Bon nyã na yi a nɩrɛ pʊtɩɛr pɔ lɛ ma tʊ̃ɔ vɛ̃ ka wʊ dɛɛ.

eng: Its from the inside, from the human heart, that evil thoughts come: sexual sins, thefts, murders.
local: Ala na ma yi a nɩrɛ pʊtɩɛr pɔ lɛ, tɩɛr dɛbɛ, nɩ a sɛ̃sɛ̃yele, nɩ a nãnyuur, nɩ nɩrɛ kʊfʊ, nɩ sɛ̃fʊ.

eng: adultery, greed, evil actions, deceit, unrestrained immorality, envy, insults, arrogance, and foolishness.
local: nɩ a nyuur, nɩ a putuo, nɩ jire, nɩ kakaba, nɩ bɔbier, nɩ yosɔ̃ɔ, nɩ pɔlyele nɩ a jɔlyele.

eng: All these evil things come from the inside and contaminate a person in Gods sight.
local: A yeldɛbɛ anyãna a jaa lɛ ma yi a nɩrɛ pʊtɩɛr pɔ, vɛ̃ ka wʊ dɛɛ.

eng: Jesus left that place and went into the region of Tyre. He didnt want anyone to know that he had entered a house, but he couldnt hide.
local: Be lɛ ka a Yesu yi a be na wʊ chen a Tire wɛr pɔ. Wʊ tɩ kpɛ naa a yir ãsʊɔ pɔ, tɩ waa bʊɔrɔ ɩka nɩrɛ jaa wʊ bɔ̃ɔ ka wʊ ben be ɛ. Tɩchɛ wʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ sɔɔl ɛ.

eng: In fact, a woman whose young daughter was possessed by an unclean spirit heard about him right away. She came and fell at his feet.
local: Ala a pɔɔ na tara wʊ bipɔɔ ka a sɩdɛɛr tara, ajienaa wʊn wʊ wõ a Yesu 'yɛr, wʊ wan wa lo gbur dumo a wʊ niem.

eng: The woman was Greek, Syrophoenician by birth. She begged Jesus to throw the demon out of her daughter.
local: A pɔɔ ba dɔ̃ɔ ɩ a Juu nɩrɛ ɛ, na wʊ dɔ̃ɔ yi be a Siriya Fonisiya paal pɔ. Wʊ sʊɔr naa a Yesu ɩka, wʊ dii a sɩdɛɛr bar a wʊ bipɔɔ pɔ.

eng: He responded, The children have to be fed first. It isnt right to take the childrens bread and toss it to the dogs.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Bar ka a bibiir di a lɛ ban bʊɔrɔ, taa de a bibiir bʊ̃diir kʊ baar ɛ.

eng: But she answered, Lord, even the dogs under the table eat the childrens crumbs.
local: Ka a pɔɔ mɩ 'yɛr, Sɩrɛ lɛ, n Soro, tɩchɛ a baar mɩ ma dire naa a bʊ̃ pʊr pʊr ala a bibiir na ma dire, tɩchɛ ka a loro a tẽe.

eng: Good answer! he said. Go on home. The demon has already left your daughter.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, A nɩtɔ̃ fʊn 'yɛr jũu, kulo. A sɩdɛɛr bar naa a fʊ bipɔɔ.

eng: When she returned to her house, she found the child lying on the bed and the demon gone.
local: Wʊ kul tɩ nyɛ a bie na gã a gado ju, tɩ a sɩdɛɛr yi.

eng: After leaving the region of Tyre, Jesus went through Sidon toward the Galilee Sea through the region of the Ten Cities.
local: Yesu lɛ yi naa a Tire wɛr pɔ, na wʊ tu a Sidon suu a Galilee man pɔ, na wʊ tɩ chen a Dekapolisɩ paal pɔ.

eng: Some people brought to him a man who was deaf and could hardly speak, and they begged him to place his hand on the man for healing.
local: Be lɛ ka nɩbɛ bamɩne waan daba na ɩ wõo, na waa tʊ̃ɔnɔ̃ 'yɛrɛ sʊ̃ ɛ. Ba sʊɔr a Yesu ka wʊ sɩɩr a daba nɩ a wʊ nũu na wʊ sãa wʊ.

eng: Jesus took him away from the crowd by himself and put his fingers in the mans ears. Then he spit and touched the mans tongue.
local: Ka Yesu tara a daba yin chɛ̃chɛ̃ɛ, na wʊ de a wʊ nubie su a daba tobɔɔr pɔ, be lɛ wʊ chɩr nataar tɩ sɩɩr a daba jãlbir.

eng: Looking into heaven, Jesus sighed deeply and said, Ephphatha, which means, Open up.
local: Na wʊ dʊl kaa a saaju tɩ vʊʊr na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a daba, Efata!, a par lɛ a Yesu nɩbɛ kɔkɔr pɔ, yuo.

eng: At once, his ears opened, his twisted tongue was released, and he began to speak clearly.
local: Ajienaa ka a daba tobo yuo ka a jãlbir lɩɛb ka wʊ piel 'yɛrɛ jaa chɛchɛ.

eng: Jesus gave the people strict orders not to tell anyone. But the more he tried to silence them, the more eagerly they shared the news.
local: Yesu kpaal ban jũu ɩka ba taa ɩrɛ wa 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ nɩrɛ jaa ɛ. Tɩchɛ a lɛ wʊn kpãa ba lɛ lɛ ba mɩ 'yɛrɛ yʊɔrɔ nɩ a jie wʊ jaa.

eng: People were overcome with wonder, saying, He does everything well! He even makes the deaf to hear and gives speech to those who cant speak.
local: A nɩbɛ nɛɛ 'maan yɔɔ jaa ka ba 'yɛrɛ, Wʊ ɩn yele jaa sʊ̃. Wʊ gba ma vɛ̃ naa ka wõo wõnõ, tɩ jãlbir lɩɛb 'yɛrɛ chɛchɛ.

eng: In those days there was another large crowd with nothing to eat. Jesus called his disciples and told them.
local: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ pɔ, nɩyɔɔ lɛ lɔ̃ɔn taa go a Yesu sɛ̃, tɩ baa tara bon na di ɛ. Yesu bʊɔl a wʊ poturbo ka ba wa a wʊ sɛ̃ ka wʊ 'yɛr.

eng: I feel sorry for the crowd because they have been with me for three days and have nothing to eat.
local: A nɩbɛ banyã ɩrɛ naa nɩbaal, bojũu ber ata nɩ nyã ban be a n sɛ̃, baa lɛ tara bon na di ɛ.

eng: If I send them away hungry to their homes, they wont have enough strength to travel, for some have come a long distance.
local: Maa mɩ bar ba ka ba kulo nɩ a kɔ̃, ban tɩ lon sɔr pɔ, bojũu bamɩne yin tɛɛr.

eng: His disciples responded, How can anyone get enough food in this wilderness to satisfy these people.
local: Ka a wʊ poturbo 'yɛr, Tɩchɛ nyɩnɛ lɛ a kanaa a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ nɩrɛ na nyɛ bʊ̃diir kʊ ba.

eng: Jesus asked, How much bread do you have? They said, Seven loaves.
local: Ka a Yesu sʊʊr ba, Paanʊ̃ɔsɩ aŋmɩnɛ lɛ yɩ tara? Ka ba 'yɛr, Paanʊ̃ɔsɩ ayopõi.

eng: He told the crowd to sit on the ground. He took the seven loaves, gave thanks, broke them apart, and gave them to his disciples to distribute; and they gave the bread to the crowd.
local: Wʊ 'yɛr naa a nɩbɛ, ka ba jãnɛ tẽe, tɩ na wʊ de a paanʊ̃ɔsɩ ayopõi ala puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan, tɩ ŋma ŋma de kʊ a wʊ poturbo ka ba de põ a nɩbɛ.

eng: They also had a few fish. He said a blessing over them, then gave them to the disciples to hand out also.
local: Ba tara naa a jãbil amɩne, ka wʊ de a ala mɩ puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan, tɩ 'yɛr ɩka a poturbo ba de põ a nɩbɛ.

eng: They ate until they were full. They collected seven baskets full of leftovers.
local: Ba di naa tɩɩ tɩ chɛ. Ka a wʊ poturbo wob a bʊ̃ŋmaara ala ban di faa 'yɔ̃ɔ pɛr ayopõi.

eng: This was a crowd of about four thousand people! Jesus sent them away.
local: Ba ɩ naa tur anaar. Ka wʊ bar ba a sɔr.

eng: then got into a boat with his disciples and went over to the region of Dalmanutha.
local: Ajienaa, 'lɔ nɩ a wʊ poturbo 'mantaa kpɛ a gboro pɔ, na ba chen a Dalmanuta paal pɔ.

eng: The Pharisees showed up and began to argue with Jesus. To test him, they asked for a sign from heaven.
local: Be lɛ a Faraseemɩne wa sʊrɔ wʊ yelsʊʊrsɩ, bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba bɛɛr wʊ kaa, na ba 'yɛr ɩka wʊ wiil ba jãnɛ kɔ̃ɔ ka ba bɔ̃ɔ ɩka wʊ yin a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃.

eng: With an impatient sigh, Jesus said, Why does this generation look for a sign? I assure you that no sign will be given to it.
local: Ka wʊ vʊʊr lɛ fãa tɩ 'yɛr, Bo lɛ so ka a yɩn pʊ̃pãanyã nɩbɛ bʊɔrɔ jãnɛ ɩka yɩ nyɛ? Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, yɩ kʊ̃ nyɛ jãnɛ jaa ɛ.

eng: Leaving them, he got back in the boat and crossed to the other side of the lake.
local: Wʊ bar ba naa tɩ kpɛ a gboro gɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a man gɔ̃ɔn.

eng: Jesus disciples had forgotten to bring any bread, so they had only one loaf with them in the boat.
local: A wʊ poturbo ĩin naa ba tara paanʊ̃ɔ ɛ, tɩ tara bʊ̃'yen tɛɛ a gboro pɔ.

eng: He gave them strict orders: Watch out and be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees as well as the yeast of Herod.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ bɔ̃ɔn a Faraseemɩne paanʊ̃ɔ dãbɩl nɩ a Hɛrɔd paanʊ̃ɔ dãbɩl mɩ.

eng: The disciples discussed this among themselves, He said this because we have no bread.
local: Ka a wʊ poturbo guor taa 'yɛrɛ, Sɩn ba tara a paanʊ̃ɔ lɛ so.

eng: Jesus knew what they were discussing and said, Why are you talking about the fact that you dont have any bread? Dont you grasp what has happened? Dont you understand? Are your hearts so resistant to what God is doing?.
local: Yesu na bɔ̃ɔn a lɛ ban 'yɛrɛ, wʊ sʊʊr ba naa, Bãa 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ yɩ 'yɛrɛ ɩka yaa tara paanʊ̃ɔ ɛ? Yaa bɔ̃ɔ a par sɛrɛ ɛ? A yɩ nyãa kpɛ̃mɛ na mɩ?.

eng: Dont you have eyes? Why cant you see? Dont you have ears? Why cant you hear? Dont you remember?.
local: Yɩ tara na nɩbie tɩ ba nyɛrɛ ɛ, na yɩ tara tobo tɩ ba wõnõ ɛ. Yɩ ĩin bɩɩ?.

eng: When I broke five loaves of bread for those five thousand people, how many baskets full of leftovers did you gather? They answered, Twelve.
local: A paanʊ̃ɔsɩ anũu ɩn dɔ̃ɔ de ŋma kʊ a dɔɔr tur anũu, pɛr aŋmɩnɛ lɛ yɩ wob a bʊ̃ŋmaar na chɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ? Ka ba 'yɛr, Pɛr pie nɩ ayi.

eng: And when I broke seven loaves of bread for those four thousand people, how many baskets full of leftovers did you gather? They answered, Seven.
local: Ka a Yesu lɛ bɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr, Ɩn lɛ de a paanʊ̃ɔsɩ ayopõi na n ŋma kʊ a nɩbɛ tur anaar, pɛr aŋmɩnɛ lɛ yɩ lɛ wob 'yɔ̃ɔ? Ka ba 'yɛr, Pɛr ayopõi.

eng: Jesus said to them, And you still dont understand.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yaa bɔ̃ɔ a par sɛrɛ ɛ.

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to Bethsaida. Some people brought a blind man to Jesus and begged him to touch and heal him.
local: Ba wa ta na a Betɩsaida tẽe pɔ, ka a nɩbɛ bamɩne tara jɔ̃ɔ waan a Yesu sɛ̃ wa sʊɔr wʊ, ka wʊ sɩɩr a jɔ̃ɔ ka wʊ nyɛrɛ.

eng: Taking the blind mans hand, Jesus led him out of the village. After spitting on his eyes and laying his hands on the man, he asked him, Do you see anything.
local: Yesu nyɔɔ naa a jɔ̃ɔ nũu tɩ tɔɔ wʊ yin a tẽe chɛchɛ. Wʊ tɩ chɩr nataar 'yɔ̃ɔ a daba nɩbie pɔ tɩ dɔɔl wʊ a nũu tɩ sʊʊr wʊ, Fʊ nyɛn bon bɩɩ.

eng: The man looked up and said, I see people. They look like trees, only they are walking around.
local: Ka a daba dʊɔ kaa tɩ 'yɛr, N nyɛn nɩbɛ na kaara tɩɩr kaar na ba vire yʊɔrɔ.

eng: Then Jesus placed his hands on the mans eyes again. He looked with his eyes wide open, his sight was restored, and he could see everything clearly.
local: Ka a Yesu lɛ de a wʊ nuur pɔɔ a daba nɩbie, ka a wʊ nɩbie yuo, ka wʊ nyɛrɛ pãa nyɛ bomo a jaa chɛchɛ.

eng: Then Jesus sent him home, saying, Dont go into the village.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Kulo, ta lɛ kpɛ a tẽe pɔ ɛ.

eng: Jesus and his disciples went into the villages near Caesarea Philippi. On the way he asked his disciples, Who do people say that I am.
local: Yesu nɩ a wʊ poturbo chen naa a tẽn ala naa gbʊr Sisariya Filipai. A sɔr pɔ ban be chiine, a Yesu sʊʊr ba naa, An lɛ a nɩbɛ 'yɛrɛ ɩka n ɩ naa.

eng: They told him, Some say John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others one of the prophets.
local: Ka ba 'yɛr, Bamɩne 'yɛr naa ɩka fʊ̃ʊ lɛ a Jɔɔn 'lɔ na dɔ̃ɔ suoro a nɩbɛ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ. Ka bamɩne mɩ 'yɛr ɩka Elaija. Ka a bamɩne mɩ chɛnɛ 'yɛrɛ ɩka fʊ ɩ naa Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ ãsʊɔ na dɔ̃ɔ be be.

eng: He asked them, And what about you? Who do you say that I am? Peter answered, You are the Christ.
local: Ka a Yesu sʊʊr ba, Tɩchɛ an lɛ yɩ mɩ 'yɛr ɩka n ɩ naa? Ka a Pita 'yɛr, Fʊ̃ʊ lɛ a Nɩ-iraa, a Nãaŋmɩn na tʊ̃ ɩka fʊ faa a wʊ nɩbɛ.

eng: Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone about him.
local: Ka a Yesu kpãa ba, Yɩ taa ɩrɛ wa 'yɛrɛ a n 'yɛr kʊrɔ nɩrɛ jaa ɛ.

eng: Then Jesus began to teach his disciples: The Human One must suffer many things and be rejected by the elders, chief priests, and the legal experts, and be killed, and then, after three days, rise from the dead.
local: Yesu piel wile ba na wʊ 'yɛrɛ ɩka a Nɩsaal Bie na din dɔɔyɛ yele yɔɔ pɔ, ka a Juu nɩbɛrɛ, nɩ a ba bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe tɔr wʊ bar, na ba kʊ wʊ. Tɩchɛ a ber ata daar wʊn ir naa a kũu pɔ ɩ vʊr.

eng: He said this plainly. But Peter took hold of Jesus and, scolding him, began to correct him.
local: Wʊ 'yɛr naa anyã chãa pɔ, ka a Pita tara wʊ yin chɛ̃chɛ̃ɛ tɩ piel mii 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ.

eng: Jesus turned and looked at his disciples, then sternly corrected Peter: Get behind me, Satan. You are not thinking Gods thoughts but human thoughts.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Yesu lɩɛb kaa a wʊ poturbo tɩ mɩ mii 'yɔ̃ɔ a Pita 'yɛrɛ, Tɔɔ chen be, Satan! A fʊ tɩɛr ɩn nɩrɛ tɩɛr, waa ɩ Nãaŋmɩn tɩɛr ɛ.

eng: After calling the crowd together with his disciples, Jesus said to them, All who want to come after me must say no to themselves, take up their cross, and follow me.
local: Be lɛ wʊ bʊɔl a nɩyɔɔ nɩ a poturbo ka ba wa a wʊ sɛ̃, ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ turo ma, a nɩnyɩnɛ na 'laara a wʊ mãɛ tɩ de a daa na ɩ dɔɔyɛ nɩ kũu, tɩ turo ma.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me and because of the good news will save them.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na bʊɔrɔ a wʊ nyɛvʊr, a wʊ nyɛvʊr na bɔr na, tɩ nɩrɛ 'lɔ na sɔɔ a wʊ nyɛvʊr bɔrfʊ a maa nɩ a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyã jũu, na faa wʊ naa.

eng: Why would people gain the whole world but lose their lives?.
local: Ala ɩ nɩrɛ tara naa a wɛr nyã bonsɩ a jaa tɩ a wʊ sɩɛ wɛ, bo tɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ be be?.

eng: What will people give in exchange for their lives?.
local: Bɩɩ bo lɛ nɩrɛ na de tɛɛn a wʊ sɩɛ?.

eng: Whoever is ashamed of me and my words in this unfaithful and sinful generation, the Human One will be ashamed of that person when he comes in the Fathers glory with the holy angels.
local: Ala ɩ nɩrɛ mɩ nyɛ naa a maa nɩ a n 'yɛrbie ka a ɩ vã yele a nɩbɛ banyã na tɔr Nãaŋmɩn bar, nɩ a putudẽme sɛ̃, a Nɩsaal Bie mɩ na kaa naa a nɩnyɩnɛ 'lɔ ka wʊ ɩ vĩ sʊɔ a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ wʊn wa lɩɛb wa nɩ a wʊ Sãa tɩɩr 'matãan a malaka chɛchɛsɩ.

eng: Jesus left that place and went beyond the Jordan and into the region of Judea. Crowds gathered around him again and, as usual, he taught them.
local: Yesu pãa yin a jie 'lɔ na wa chen a Judiya paal, na wʊ tɩ gɔ̃ɔ a Jordan man. Nɩyɔɔ lɛ wa naa a wʊ sɛ̃ go, ka wʊ wile ba nɩtãa a lɛ wʊn ma wile ba.

eng: Some Pharisees came and, trying to test him, they asked, Does the Law allow a man to divorce his wife.
local: A Faraseemɩne bamɩne wa naa ɩka ba wa bɛɛr wʊ kaa, na ba sʊʊr wʊ, Sɔr ben be ka daba na tɔr a wʊ pɔɔ bar bɩɩ.

eng: Jesus answered, What did Moses command you.
local: Ka a Yesu mɩ sʊʊr ba, Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ a Mosesɩ dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr.

eng: They said, Moses allowed a man to write a divorce certificate and to divorce his wife.
local: Ka ba 'yɛr, Mosesɩ bar sɔr ɩka daba wʊ sɛb dãasɩɛ gãn tɩ na wʊ tɔr a wʊ pɔɔ bar.

eng: Jesus said to them, He wrote this commandment for you because of your unyielding hearts.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, A yɩ nyãkpãɛn jũu lɛ so ka wʊ sɛb a lɛ.

eng: At the beginning of creation, God made them male and female .
local: Tɩchɛ a wɛr pielfʊ, Nãaŋmɩn maal daba nɩ pɔɔ.

eng: Because of this, a man should leave his father and mother and be joined together with his wife.
local: A lɛ jũu, daba na bar a wʊ sãa nɩ a wʊ ma tɩchɛ ka 'lɔ nɩ a wʊ pɔɔ ɩ bʊ̃'yen.

eng: and the two will be one flesh. So they are no longer two but one flesh.
local: ka ba jaa ayi ɩ ãgãn bʊ̃'yen. Baa lɛ ɩ ayi ɛ, tɩ ɩ bʊ̃'yen.

eng: Therefore, humans must not pull apart what God has put together.
local: A lɛ jũu, bon 'lɔ jaa Nãaŋmɩn na 'mataa, nɩrɛ wʊ ta nyɔɔ wɛl ɛ.

eng: Inside the house, the disciples asked him again about this.
local: Ban wa lɩɛb kul a yir, a Yesu poturbo sʊʊr wʊ naa a 'yɛr nyã go.

eng: He said to them, Whoever divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery against her.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Daba jaa na tɔr wʊ pɔɔ bar tɩ kul pɔɔ yuo, wʊ maal yelbier.

eng: and if a wife divorces her husband and marries another, she commits adultery.
local: Lɛ mɩ lɛ, pɔɔ wa tɔr a wʊ sɩrɛ bar tɩ kul dayuo wʊ mɩ maal yelbier.

eng: People were bringing children to Jesus so that he would bless them. But the disciples scolded them.
local: Nɩbɛ dɔ̃ɔ waara nɩ a ba bibiir ɩka Yesu wʊ dɔɔl ba a wʊ nũu, tɩchɛ ka a wʊ poturbo mii 'yɔ̃ɔ ba.

eng: When Jesus saw this, he grew angry and said to them, Allow the children to come to me. Dont forbid them, because Gods kingdom belongs to people like these children.
local: Yesu na wa nyɛ a lɛ a wʊ poturbo na ɩ, wʊ nyɛn suur na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ bar a bibiir ka ba wa a n sɛ̃, na yɩ ta ŋmaa ba sɔr ɛ. A ba nyãna taaba lɛ so a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt welcome Gods kingdom like a child will never enter it.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, nɩrɛ jaa na ba sɔɔ de a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ nɩtãa a bibile ɛ, wʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ kpɛ be ɛ.

eng: Then he hugged the children and blessed them.
local: Be lɛ wʊ 'mɔɔr a bibiir, tɩ dɔɔl ba nuur na wʊ sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ ba.
