eng: As Jesus continued down the road, a man ran up, knelt before him, and asked, Good Teacher, what must I do to obtain eternal life.
local: Yesu na wa de a sɔr chiine, daba jon var gbur dumo a Yesu niem tɩ sʊʊr wʊ, Wiwiilmãɛ, bo lɛ ɩn maal, naa nyɛ nyɛvʊr na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you call me good? No one is good except the one God.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Bãa 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ fʊ bʊɔl ma nɩmãɛ? Nɩrɛ jaa ba ɩ mãɛ ɛ, ka Nãaŋmɩn tɛɛ ba lɛ ɛ.

eng: You know the commandments: Dont commit murder. Dont commit adultery. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony . Dont cheat. Honor your father and mother .
local: Fʊ bɔ̃ɔ naa a Nãaŋmɩn Nɛɛ. Taa kʊ nɩrɛ ɛ. Taa sɛ̃nɛ ɛ. Taa juro ɛ. Taa dire dãasɩjirɩ ɛ. Taa fɔlɔ ɛ. 'Yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a fʊ sãa nɩ a fʊ ma.

eng: Teacher, he responded, Ive kept all of these things since I was a boy.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr, Wiwile, n bile sɔ̃ɔ jaa lɛ n turo a Nɛɛ anyãna wa tãn a dɩna.

eng: Jesus looked at him carefully and loved him. He said, You are lacking one thing. Go, sell what you own, and give the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come, follow me.
local: Yesu kaa naa a daba nɩ nʊ̃fʊ, tɩ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, A chɛ fʊn yelbʊ̃'yen. Kul tɩ da a fʊ bonsɩ a jaa de põ nɔ̃n dẽme, ala lɛ fʊn tara bʊ̃mãɛ a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃. Tɩ na fʊ pãa wa turo ma.

eng: But the man was dismayed at this statement and went away saddened, because he had many possessions.
local: A 'yɛr nyã vɛ̃ naa ka a daba sɔ̃ nie na wʊ kul pʊsɔ̃ɔ, bojũu tara sʊɔ lɛ wʊ ɩ.

eng: Looking around, Jesus said to his disciples, It will be very hard for the wealthy to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Be lɛ ka a Yesu kaa gʊɔr na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ poturbo, A tara sʊɔ kpɛfʊ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ ɩn kpɛ̃ɔ.

eng: His words startled the disciples, so Jesus told them again, Children, its difficult to enter Gods kingdom!.
local: A 'yɛrbie anyãna vɛ̃ naa ka ba nɛɛr 'maa, tɩchɛ ka wʊ lɛ 'yɛr go, Bibiir, a ɩn kpɛ̃ɔ kʊ nɩrɛ jaa na na kpɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ!.

eng: Its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: An ɩn kɔ̃mɔ̃kɔ̃mɔ̃, nyɔɔma na na kpɛ kparpĩi bɔɔ pɔ gar tara sʊɔ na na kpɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.

eng: They were shocked even more and said to each other, Then who can be saved.
local: Ka a poturbo pãa ɩ chĩcham, tɩ lɩɛb sʊrɔ taa, An lɛ na pãa tʊ̃ɔ nyɛ faafʊ.

eng: Jesus looked at them carefully and said, Its impossible with human beings, but not with God. All things are possible for God.
local: Ka a Yesu kaa ba tɩ 'yɛr, Nɩrɛ sɛ̃ 'lɔ, a ɩn tuo, tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ ba lɛ ɛ. Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ yele jaa ba ɩ tuo ɛ.

eng: Peter said to him, Look, weve left everything and followed you.
local: Be lɛ Pita 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Nyɛ, sɩn bar bon jaa tɩ wa turo fʊ.

eng: Jesus said, I assure you that anyone who has left house, brothers, sisters, mother, father, children, or farms because of me and because of the good news.
local: Ka Yesu 'yɛr, Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na bar a wʊ yir bɩɩ yɛɛr, bɩɩ yɛpɔbɔ bɩɩ a wʊ sãa bɩɩ a wʊ ma, bɩɩ bibiir bɩɩ wɛr, tɩ 'la maa nɩ a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyã jũu.

eng: will receive one hundred times as much now in this life—houses, brothers, sisters, mothers, children, and farms —and in the coming age, eternal life.
local: wʊn nyɛn yɔɔ a dɩna. Wʊn nyɛn yie, yɛɛr, yɛpɔbɔ, mamɩne, bibiir nɩ wɛr dɔɔl gbɛɛ kɔbaa, nɩ a gefʊ! Tɩ a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ na waara, ka wʊ nyɛ nyɛvʊr na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ.

eng: But many who are first will be last. And many who are last will be first.
local: Tɩchɛ a nɩyɔɔ bala na de nie na wa ben puor, ka a nɩyɔɔ bala na be a puor mɩ wa de a nie.

eng: Jesus and his disciples were on the road, going up to Jerusalem, with Jesus in the lead. The disciples were amazed while the others following behind were afraid. Taking the Twelve aside again, he told them what was about to happen to him.
local: Ba dɔ̃ɔ de a sɔr duoro a Jerusalɛm ka Yesu de a nie, tɩ a wʊ poturbo nɛɛr 'maa ka bala mɩ na turo joro dãbãɛ. Wʊ tɔɔ naa a Pie Nɩ Ayi yin chɛ̃chɛ̃ 'yɛr kʊ ba a lɛn na wa a wʊ sɛ̃.

eng: Look! he said. Were going up to Jerusalem. The Human One will be handed over to the chief priests and the legal experts. They will condemn him to death and hand him over to the Gentiles.
local: Yesu 'yɛr naa, Sɩn chiine a Jerusalɛm tẽe pɔ, be lɛ ban tɩ de a Nɩsaal Bie kʊ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe, ka ba ɩka wʊ sɛɛn kũu, na ba nyɔɔ wʊ kʊ a nɩbɛ na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ.

eng: They will ridicule him, spit on him, torture him, and kill him. After three days, he will rise up.
local: A banyãna na la wʊ naa, na ba chɩr nataar 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ, na ba fɔb wʊ tɩ kʊ wʊ. Ka a ber ata daar wʊn ir naa a kũu pɔ ɩ vʊr.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, came to Jesus and said, Teacher, we want you to do for us whatever we ask.
local: Be lɛ a Jebedee bibiir Jemesɩ nɩ Jɔɔn tɔɔ gbʊr a Yesu na ba 'yɛr, Wiwile, sɩ bʊɔrɔ na ɩka sɩ sʊɔr fʊ yele kɔ̃ɔ ka fʊ maal kʊ sɩ.

eng: What do you want me to do for you? he asked.
local: Ka a Yesu sʊʊr ba, Bãa lɛ yɩ bʊɔrɔ ɩka n maal kʊ yɩ.

eng: They said, Allow one of us to sit on your right and the other on your left when you enter your glory.
local: Ka ba 'yɛr, Vɛ̃ ka a sɩ ãsʊɔ jãnɛ a fʊ nũsʊ̃ɔ loor, ka 'yen mɩ jãnɛ a fʊ gʊba loor a fʊ nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, You dont know what youre asking! Can you drink the cup I drink or receive the baptism I receive.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yaa bɔ̃ɔ a bon yɩn sʊɔrɔ ɛ. Yɩn tʊ̃ɔ nyũn a dɔɔyɛ ŋmele 'lɔ ɩn wa nyũn, bɩɩ kpɛ a dɔɔyɛ 'lɔ pɔ ɩn wa kpɛ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ sofʊ kaar ɔ.

eng: We can, they answered. Jesus said, You will drink the cup I drink and receive the baptism I receive.
local: Ka a ba 'yɛr, Ɔ̃ɔ. Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Yɩn nyũn a ŋmele 'lɔ ɩn na nyũn, na yɩ so a kʊ̃ɔ 'lɔ ɩn na so.

eng: but to sit at my right or left hand isnt mine to give. It belongs to those for whom it has been prepared.
local: tɩchɛ a n nũsʊ̃ɔ loor nɩ a n gʊba loor 'lɔ n ba tara sɔr ɩka n kʊ yɩ ɛ. Nãaŋmɩn tɛɛ lɛ tara sɔr ɩka wʊ de a jiir anyãna kʊ a bala wʊn chɔɔr.

eng: Now when the other ten disciples heard about this, they became angry with James and John.
local: A poturbo pie bala na chɛ na wa wõ a yele, ba nyɛn suur 'yɔ̃ɔ a Jemesɩ nɩ a Jɔɔn.

eng: Jesus called them over and said, You know that the ones who are considered the rulers by the Gentiles show off their authority over them and their high-ranking officials order them around.
local: Ka a Yesu bʊɔl ba jaa 'mataa tɩ na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ bɔ̃ɔn naa a bala na ɩ a nãmɩne a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, ma fɩrɛ naa a nɩbɛ, ka a nɩbɛrɛ ma wile a ba kpãɛn.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Whoever wants to be great among you will be your servant.
local: A ba ɩ lɛ lɛ a yɩ sɛ̃ ɛ. Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ ɩ a yɩ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, wʊ ɩ a yɩ jaa tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃.

eng: Whoever wants to be first among you will be the slave of all.
local: ka a 'lɔ mɩ na bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ ɩ a nɩdiere, wʊ ɩ a yɩ jaa gbãgbaa.

eng: for the Human One didnt come to be served but rather to serve and to give his life to liberate many people.
local: A Nɩsaal Bie mɩ gba ba wa, ɩka ba wa tʊ̃nɔ̃ kʊrɔ wʊ ɛ, tɩchɛ wʊ wa naa ɩka wʊ wa tʊ̃ kʊ ba, na wʊ de a wʊ nyɛvʊr lɩɛ faan nɩyɔɔ.

eng: Jesus and his followers came into Jericho. As Jesus was leaving Jericho, together with his disciples and a sizable crowd, a blind beggar named Bartimaeus, Timaeus son, was sitting beside the road.
local: Ba pãa wa naa a Jeriko. Ban wa kpɛ a Jeriko tɩ yire, nɩyɔɔ turo naa Yesu nɩ a wʊ poturbo, ka a jɔ̃ɔ kɔ̃ɔ ban bʊɔlɔ Batimayus, Timeyus bidaba, jãnɛ a sɔr kõkoor sʊɔrɔ bomo.

eng: When he heard that Jesus of Nazareth was there, he began to shout, Jesus, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Wʊn wa wõ ka a Yesu na yi a Najaretɩ lɛ, ka wʊ piel gboolo, Yesu, David Bie, kaa n nɩbaal.

eng: Many scolded him, telling him to be quiet, but he shouted even louder, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Ka a nɩbɛ bamɩne ɩka wʊ pɔɔ a nɛɛ, tɩchɛ ka wʊ pãa chɛnɛ mal gboolo, David Bie, kaa n nɩbaal.

eng: Jesus stopped and said, Call him forward. They called the blind man, Be encouraged! Get up! Hes calling you.
local: Be lɛ a Yesu tɛɛ ara tɩ 'yɛr, Yɩ bʊɔl wʊ. Ka ba bʊɔl a jɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr wʊ, 'Maa nyãa! Ir ara, wʊ bʊɔlɔ fʊ naa.

eng: Throwing his coat to the side, he jumped up and came to Jesus.
local: Ka wʊ vaa ir lɔb a fɔbaa bar tɩ wa a Yesu sɛ̃.

eng: Jesus asked him, What do you want me to do for you? The blind man said, Teacher, I want to see.
local: Ka a Yesu sʊʊr wʊ, Bãa fʊ bʊɔrɔ ɩka n maal kʊ fʊ? Ka wʊ 'yɛr, Wiwile, n bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka n nyɛrɛ.

eng: Jesus said, Go, your faith has healed you. At once he was able to see, and he began to follow Jesus on the way.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Chiine! Fʊ sa naa, fʊn sɔɔ ma jũu. Ajienaa, wʊ nyɛrɛ naa, na wʊ turo a Yesu ka ba de a sɔr.

eng: When Jesus and his followers approached Jerusalem, they came to Bethphage and Bethany at the Mount of Olives. Jesus gave two disciples a task.
local: A lɛ ban wa gbʊrɔ a Jerusalɛm, ba ta naa a tẽn ayi, Betefagɩ nɩ Betanɩ na be a Olive tɔ̃ɔ par, ka Yesu tʊ̃ wʊ poturbo ayi.

eng: saying to them, Go into the village over there. As soon as you enter it, you will find tied up there a colt that no one has ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
local: Wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ chen a tẽbile 'lɔ na be a yɩ niem, Yɩn wa kpɩɛrɛ a tẽe pɔ, yɩn nyɛn bʊ̃bile nɩrɛ jaa na ba do wʊ sɛrɛ ɛ, ban lũ. Yɩ for waan.

eng: If anyone says to you, Why are you doing this? say, Its master needs it, and he will send it back right away.
local: Ka nɩrɛ wa sʊʊr yɩ ɩka bãa lɛ yɩ ɩrɛ a lɛ, yɩ 'yɛr wʊ ɩka, a yɩ Soro lɛ bʊɔrɔ wʊ, tɩ wʊn lɩɛb waan fɔɔ.

eng: They went and found a colt tied to a gate outside on the street, and they untied it.
local: Ba chen tɩ nyɛ a bʊ̃bile ban lũ a yõo dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ. Ban wa foro.

eng: Some people standing around said to them, What are you doing, untying the colt.
local: nɩbɛ bamɩne na ara a be sʊʊr ba naa, Bãa lɛ yɩ foro a bʊ̃bile ɩrɛ nɩ.

eng: They told them just what Jesus said, and they left them alone.
local: Ka ba 'yɛr a lɛ a Yesu na ɩka ba 'yɛr, ka a nɩbɛ bar ba.

eng: They brought the colt to Jesus and threw their clothes upon it, and he sat on it.
local: Be lɛ ba waan a bʊ̃bile wa kʊ a Yesu, na ba de a ba fɔɔr 'yɩɛr dɔɔl a bʊ̃bile ju, ka wʊ do jãnɛ.

eng: Many people spread out their clothes on the road while others spread branches cut from the fields.
local: Nɩyɔɔ na bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba 'yɔ̃ɔ a Yesu jũu, ba den fɔɔr 'yɩɛr a sɔr pɔ, ka bamɩne mɩ kar vaɩ a por pɔ wa 'yɩɛr a sɔr pɔ.

eng: Those in front of him and those following were shouting, Hosanna! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord!.
local: Ka a bala na be a niem nɩ a bala na be a puor, pãa chɩlɛ gɔ̃nɔ̃ 'yɛrɛ, Yãan yooo! Nãaŋmɩn wʊ sʊ̃ʊ a 'lɔ wʊn tʊ̃!.

eng: Blessings on the coming kingdom of our ancestor David! Hosanna in the highest.
local: Nãaŋmɩn wʊ sʊ̃ʊ a sɩ sãa David nãalʊ̃ʊ na waara! Yãan yooo! Sɩ dãn Nãaŋmɩn yɔɔ jaa.

eng: Jesus entered Jerusalem and went into the temple. After he looked around at everything, because it was already late in the evening, he returned to Bethany with the Twelve.
local: Yesu na wa ta a Jerusalɛm, wʊ chen tɩ kpɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Puorfʊ Yir kaa a bomo a jaa. A wɛr na sɔɔrɔ jũu, 'lɔ nɩ a wʊ poturbo pie nɩ ayi yin chen a Betanɩ.

eng: The next day, after leaving Bethany, Jesus was hungry.
local: A wɛr na wa chaar ka a Yesu nɩ a wʊ poturbo lɩɛb yi a Betanɩ waara a Jerusalɛm, kɔ̃ kpɛ naa a Yesu.

eng: From far away, he noticed a fig tree in leaf, so he went to see if he could find anything on it. When he came to it, he found nothing except leaves, since it wasnt the season for figs.
local: Be lɛ wʊ nyɛ a kɔ̃kɔ̃ɔ tɩɛ na tɔ vaar ara a niem, ka wʊ chen ɩka wʊ tɩ kaa wɔ̃mɔ̃ ben be bɩɩ. Wʊ chen tɩ ta ka a ɩ wɔ̃mɔ̃ ba be be ɛ, vaar tɛɛ lɛ, bojũu a ba ɩ a wɔ̃mɔ̃ sɔ̃ɔ lɛ ɛ.

eng: So he said to it, No one will ever again eat your fruit! His disciples heard this.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a tɩɛ, Nɩrɛ jaa kʊ̃ lɛ wa pɔr a fʊ wɔ̃mɔ̃ di ɛ. A wʊ poturbo wõ naa a lɛ wʊn 'yɛr.

eng: They came into Jerusalem. After entering the temple, he threw out those who were selling and buying there. He pushed over the tables used for currency exchange and the chairs of those who sold doves.
local: Ban wa ta a Jerusalɛm, Yesu chen tɩ kpɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Puorfʊ Yir bɛchaar pɔ dire a nɩbɛ bala na chiin a bonsɩ tɩ daara nɩ a bala na daara. Wʊ daa naa libitɛɛrbɛ tɛɛbʊlsɩ lɩɛb kpa, tɩ da a bala mɩ na daara a papasɩ dakɔɔr lɔb.

eng: He didnt allow anyone to carry anything through the temple.
local: Waa sɔɔ ka a nɩrɛ jaa tuo bon jaa tun a bɛchaar 'lɔ pɔ ɛ.

eng: He taught them, Hasnt it been written, My house will be called a house of prayer for all nations? But youve turned it into a hideout for crooks .
local: Be lɛ ka wʊ pãa wile ba na wʊ 'yɛr, A Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr naa a wʊ gãn pɔ, N yir ɩ naa a yir a nɩbʊrɔ ba jaa na wa puor ma, tɩchɛ ka a yɩn 'lɔ maal wʊ a nɩfarba jie.

eng: The chief priests and legal experts heard this and tried to find a way to destroy him. They regarded him as dangerous because the whole crowd was enthralled at his teaching.
local: Ka a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ wa wõ a nɩtɔ̃ bʊɔrɔ sɔr 'lɔ ban tu kʊ wʊ, tɩ na ba joro wʊ dãbãɛ bojũu a nɩyɔɔ nɛɛr 'maa ba naa a wʊ wiilfʊ pɔ.

eng: When it was evening, Jesus and his disciples went outside the city.
local: A jãanʊɔr na wa ta, Yesu nɩ a wʊ poturo lɩɛb chen a Betanɩ.

eng: Early in the morning, as Jesus and his disciples were walking along, they saw the fig tree withered from the root up.
local: A wɛr na wa chaar bibio ka ba lɩɛb chiine a Jerusalɛm, ba nyɛ naa ka ɩ a kɔ̃kɔ̃tɩɛ na kpi lɔ̃ɔn a nyɩbɛ.

eng: Peter remembered and said to Jesus, Rabbi, look how the fig tree you cursed has dried up.
local: Be lɛ ka a Pita tɩɛr a lɛ a Yesu na 'yɛr kʊ a tɩɛ, na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Wiwile, nyɛ a tɩɛ 'lɔ fʊn pɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ! Wʊ kpi naa.

eng: Jesus responded to them, Have faith in God!.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ sɔɔ de Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: I assure you that whoever says to this mountain, Be lifted up and thrown into the sea—and doesnt waver but believes that what is said will really happen—it will happen.
local: Sɩrɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, ala ɩ yɩ ãsʊɔ sɔɔ naa de Nãaŋmɩn na waa baala a wʊ tɩɛr pɔ ɛ, 'lɔ gba na tʊ̃ɔ 'yɛr kʊ a tɔ̃ɔ nyãna, Ir tɩ lo a man pɔ, a Nãaŋmɩn na sɔɔ naa ka a lɛ ɩ.

eng: Therefore I say to you, whatever you pray and ask for, believe that you will receive it, and it will be so for you.
local: Ala lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, fʊ̃ʊ puor Nãaŋmɩn sʊɔr bon jaa tɩ sɔɔ ɩka, wʊn ɩn kʊ fʊ, wʊn ɩ naa.

eng: And whenever you stand up to pray, if you have something against anyone, forgive so that your Father in heaven may forgive you your wrongdoings.
local: Fʊ̃ʊ wa ɩka fʊ puor, tɩ tara nɩrɛ nɩ 'yɛr, vɛ̃ kʊ a nɩnyɩnɛ. A fʊ Sãa na be a saaju mɩ na vɛ̃ naa a fʊ yelbier kʊ fʊ.

eng: Jesus and his disciples entered Jerusalem again. As Jesus was walking around the temple, the chief priests, legal experts, and elders came to him.
local: Ba lɛ wa naa a Jerusalɛm go, ka a Yesu tɩ kpɛ yʊɔrɔ a Nãaŋmɩn Puorfʊ Yir bɛchaar pɔ. A Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ wa naa a wʊ sɛ̃.

eng: They asked, What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority to do them.
local: Ba wa sʊʊr wʊ naa, An lɛ kʊ fʊ a sɔr ka fʊ tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ a anyã? Fʊ tara naa a sɔr ɩka fʊ tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ a anyã ɔ.

eng: Jesus said to them, I have a question for you. Give me an answer, then Ill tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, N sʊʊr yɩ sʊʊrfʊ bʊ̃'yen. Yɩn wa tʊ̃ɔ wiil ma a par, n mɩ na wiil yɩ naa a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na kʊ ma a sɔr ka n tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ a anyã.

eng: Was Johns baptism of heavenly or of human origin? Answer me.
local: Jɔɔn na dɔ̃ɔ suoro a nɩbɛ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ, Nãaŋmɩn lɛ kʊ wʊ a sɔr bɩɩ, nɩbɛ lɛ kʊ wʊ a sɔr? Yɩ 'yɛr n kaa.

eng: They argued among themselves, If we say, Its of heavenly origin, hell say, Then why didnt you believe him.
local: A ba lɩɛb chɩrɛ taa, Sɩn be 'yɛr ɩka Nãaŋmɩn lɛ tʊ̃ wʊ, wʊn sʊʊr sɩ naa, ka bo lɛ so ka saa sɔɔ wʊ ɛ?.

eng: But we cant say, Its of earthly origin. They said this because they were afraid of the crowd, because they all thought John was a prophet.
local: Tɩchɛ sɩn mɩ 'yɛr ɩka nɩrɛ lɛ tʊ̃ wʊ, a ba ɩ ɛ. Ba joro naa a nɩbɛ dãbãɛ, bojũu nɩrɛ jaa sɔɔ naa ɩka a Jɔɔn ɩn Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ.

eng: They answered Jesus, We dont know. Jesus replied, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Lɛ lɛ ba 'yɛr kʊ a Yesu, Saa bɔ̃ɔ ɛ. Ka a Yesu mɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba, N mɩ kʊ̃ 'yɛr kʊ yɩ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na kʊ ma a sɔr ka n tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ a anyãna ɛ.

eng: Jesus continued, I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see Gods kingdom arrive in power.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, bamɩne ara naa a kana na ba kʊ̃ kpi ɛ, tɩchɛ na ba nyɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ na na wa nɩ kpɛ̃ɔ.

eng: Six days later Jesus took Peter, James, and John, and brought them to the top of a very high mountain where they were alone. He was transformed in front of them.
local: A ber ayʊɔb puor, Yesu tara naa Pita nɩ Jemesɩ nɩ Jɔɔn tɛɛ na wʊ de ba nie ka ba 'mataa do tɔ̃wõo kɔ̃ɔ ju. Be lɛ wʊ tɩ chil a ba niem.

eng: and his clothes were amazingly bright, brighter than if they had been bleached white.
local: A wʊ fuu pɛl nyɩlɛ gar bʊ̃pɩla jaa nɩrɛ na maal a ka a wɛr pɔ.

eng: Elijah and Moses appeared and were talking with Jesus.
local: Ka a Elaija nɩ a Mosesɩ, wa daa yi a ba sɛ̃. Ka bala nɩ a Yesu 'mataa 'yɛrɛ.

eng: Peter reacted to all of this by saying to Jesus, Rabbi, its good that were here. Lets make three shrines—one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.
local: Ka a Pita 'yɛr kʊ a Yesu, Wiwile, a sɛɛ naa sɩn be a ka. Sɔɔ, ka sɩ maal sɛɛr ata: fʊ̃ʊ ãsʊɔ, Mosesɩ ãsʊɔ, Elaija mɩ ãsʊɔ.

eng: He said this because he didnt know how to respond, for the three of them were terrified.
local: Pita ba bɔ̃ɔ ŋmɩnɛ wʊ na 'yɛr ɛ, bojũu dãbãɛ kpɛ ba naa.

eng: Then a cloud overshadowed them, and a voice spoke from the cloud, This is my Son, whom I dearly love. Listen to him.
local: Ka a julõjuur wa pɔɔ ba, tɩ kɔkɔr 'yɛr a be a julõjuur pɔ, N binʊ̃nãa lɛ a nyãna. Yɩ chɛlɛ a wʊ 'yɛr.

eng: Suddenly, looking around, they no longer saw anyone with them except Jesus.
local: Ajienaa ban ɩka ba kaa, baa lɛ nyɛ nɩrɛ jaa ɛ, tɩ nyɛ a Yesu tɛɛ.

eng: As they were coming down the mountain, he ordered them not to tell anyone what they had seen until after the Human One had risen from the dead.
local: Ban wa yi a tɔ̃ɔ ju suro, ka a Yesu kpãa ba ɩka ba taa wa 'yɛrɛ a lɛ ban nyɛ kʊrɔ nɩrɛ jaa ɛ, wa tɩ tãn a lɛ a Nɩsaal Bie na ir yi a wʊ kũu pɔ.

eng: So they kept it to themselves, wondering, Whats this rising from the dead.
local: Baa 'yɛr kʊ nɩrɛ jaa ɛ, tɩ 'yɛrɛ na ba sʊrɔ taa a kũu pɔ irfʊ par.

eng: They asked Jesus, Why do the legal experts say that Elijah must come first.
local: Ka ba sʊʊr wʊ, Bo lɛ so ka a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe 'yɛr ɩka Elaija lɛ na de nie wa.

eng: He answered, Elijah does come first to restore all things. Why was it written that the Human One would suffer many things and be rejected?.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Sɩrɛ jaa lɛ a Elaija de nie wa chɔɔr a yele jaa. Tɩ ba mɩ sɛb ɩka a Nɩsaal Bie na din dɔɔyɛ yɔɔ ka ba tɔr wʊ bar.

eng: In fact, I tell you that Elijah has come, but they did to him whatever they wanted, just as it was written about him.
local: Tɩchɛ n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka a Elaija wa naa ka ba ɩ a lɛ jaa ban bʊɔrɔ kʊ wʊ nɩtãa a lɛ ban sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ.

eng: When Jesus, Peter, James, and John approached the other disciples, they saw a large crowd surrounding them and legal experts arguing with them.
local: Ban wa ta a poturbo ŋmaa bala sɛ̃, ba nyɛn nɩyɔɔ na ara jilʊ̃ ba ka a bala nɩ a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe chɩrɛ taa.

eng: Suddenly the whole crowd caught sight of Jesus. They ran to greet him, overcome with excitement.
local: Ka nɩbɛ na wa nyɛ a Yesu na waara, ka ba nɛɛ 'maa ka ba jo tuor puor wʊ.

eng: Jesus asked them, What are you arguing about.
local: Ka a Yesu sʊʊr a wʊ poturbo, Bo lɛ yɩ chɩrɛ taa a lɛ.

eng: Someone from the crowd responded, Teacher, I brought my son to you, since he has a spirit that doesnt allow him to speak.
local: Ka nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ 'yɛr a be a nɩyɔɔ pɔ, Wiwile, n bidaba lɛ sɩdɛɛr nyɔɔ ka wʊ maa tʊ̃ɔ 'yɛr ɛ, ka n waan a fʊ sɛ̃.

eng: Wherever it overpowers him, it throws him into a fit. He foams at the mouth, grinds his teeth, and stiffens up. So I spoke to your disciples to see if they could throw it out, but they couldnt.
local: 'Lɔ wa ir wʊ, wʊ ma daa wʊn lɔb tẽe ka wʊ furo a natafuul tɩ ɔɔrɔ a nyɩmɛ tɩ tãa. Ka n wa sʊɔr a fʊ poturbo ɩka ba dii a sɩdɛɛr nyãna bar, ka baa tʊ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: Jesus answered them, You faithless generation, how long will I be with you? How long will I put up with you? Bring him to me.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Aa, a yɩn nɩbɛ banyã na ba tara sɔɔfʊ ɛ, n be a yɩ sɛ̃ kor ta ŋmɩnɛ? N tara kãnyir ŋmɩnɛ kʊ yɩ? Waan a bie wa kʊ ma.

eng: They brought him. When the spirit saw Jesus, it immediately threw the boy into a fit. He fell on the ground and rolled around, foaming at the mouth.
local: Ka ba waan a bie. A sɩɛ na wa nyɛ a Yesu, wʊ ir tãa a bie ka wʊ lo tẽe, mɩrɛ tɩ bile tɩ furo a natafuul.

eng: Jesus asked his father, How long has this been going on? He said, Since he was a child.
local: Be lɛ a Yesu sʊʊr a bie sãa, Sɔ̃ɔ buor lɛ a piel wʊ a nɩtɔ̃? Ka a sãa 'yɛr, A bibile daar jaa lɛ a piel wʊ.

eng: It has often thrown him into a fire or into water trying to kill him. If you can do anything, help us! Show us compassion.
local: Wʊ ma daa wʊn lɔb bũu pɔ bɩɩ kʊ̃ɔ pɔ bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ kpi. Ala ɩ fʊn tʊ̃ɔ ɩ yele kɔ̃ɔ, kaa a sɩ nɩbaal tɩ na fʊ sʊ̃ʊ sɩ.

eng: Jesus said to him, If you can do anything? All things are possible for the one who has faith.
local: Ka a Yesu mɩ 'yɛr, Bo lɛ so ka a fʊ 'yɛr ɩka ala ɩ ɩn tʊ̃ɔ naa? Yele jaa ɩn kɔ̃mɔ̃kɔ̃mɔ̃ kʊ a bala na sɔɔ de ma.

eng: At that the boys father cried out, I have faith; help my lack of faith.
local: Ajienaa lɛ a bie sãa chɩɩr, N sɔɔn de fʊ! Sʊ̃ʊ ma, a n sɔɔfʊ ba bɛrma ɛ.

eng: Noticing that the crowd had surged together, Jesus spoke harshly to the unclean spirit, Mute and deaf spirit, I command you to come out of him and never enter him again.
local: Yesu na wa nyɛ a nɩyɔɔ na joro lɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ taa a jie, wʊ tãn a sɩdɛɛr, Fʊ̃ʊ sɩdɛɛr na ma vɛ̃ ka nɩbɛ ɩ wõn, wa yi a bie pɔ na fʊ taa lɛ ɩrɛ wa kpɩɛrɛ a wʊ pɔ go ɛ.

eng: After screaming and shaking the boy horribly, the spirit came out. The boy seemed to be dead; in fact, several people said that he had died.
local: Ka a sɩɛ ŋme chɛlma tɩ dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a bie yɔɔ tɩ yi, ka a bie gã kaara fɩka kũu. Ka a nɩbɛ 'yɛr, Wʊ kpi naa.

eng: But Jesus took his hand, lifted him up, and he arose.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Yesu nyɔɔ a wʊ nũu tɔɔ, ka wʊ ir ara.

eng: After Jesus went into a house, his disciples asked him privately, Why couldnt we throw this spirit out.
local: Yesu na wa kpɛ a yir 'lɔ nɩ a wʊ poturbo tɛɛ, ka ba sʊʊr wʊ, Bãa lɛ so ka sɩn ba tʊ̃ɔ a sɩdɛɛr nyã dii bar ɛ.

eng: Jesus answered, Throwing this kind of spirit out requires prayer.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Nãaŋmɩn sʊɔrfʊ tɛɛ lɛ na vɛ̃ a fʊ dii a sɩɛ nyã ka wʊ yi.

eng: From there Jesus and his followers went through Galilee, but he didnt want anyone to know it.
local: Ba yin a jie 'lɔ tɩ tu a Galilee tal. Yesu ba bʊɔrɔ ɩka nɩrɛ jaa wʊ bɔ̃ɔ a jie ban be ɛ.

eng: This was because he was teaching his disciples, The Human One will be delivered into human hands. They will kill him. Three days after he is killed he will rise up.
local: bojũu wʊ wile naa a wʊ poturbo, Ban de naa a Nɩsaal Bie kʊ a nɩbɛ, ka ba kʊ wʊ. Ka a ber ata daar wʊn ir naa, na wʊ tara nyɛvʊr.

eng: But they didnt understand this kind of talk, and they were afraid to ask him.
local: Tɩchɛ baa bɔ̃ɔ a par a lɛ wʊn 'yɛrɛ ɛ, tɩ joro dãbãɛ ɩka ba sʊʊr wʊ.

eng: They entered Capernaum. When they had come into a house, he asked them, What were you arguing about during the journey.
local: Yesu nɩ a wʊ poturbo wa ta na a Kapenahum. Ban wa kpɛ a yir, a Yesu sʊʊr ba naa, Bãa 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ yɩ chɩrɛ taa a sɔr pɔ a lɛ.

eng: They didnt respond, since on the way they had been debating with each other about who was the greatest.
local: Ka ba ɩ jaa gbulo, bojũu a sɔr pɔ ba chɩrɛ na taa ɩka an lɛ ɩ a kpɛ̃ɛ.

eng: He sat down, called the Twelve, and said to them, Whoever wants to be first must be least of all and the servant of all.
local: Yesu na wa jãnɛ wʊ bʊɔl naa a wʊ poturbo Pie nɩ Ayi na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, A yɩ ãsʊɔ wa bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ de nie, a nɩnyɩnɛ 'lɔ wʊ ɩ a yɩ jaa bile, na wʊ ɩ a yɩ jaa tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃.

eng: Jesus reached for a little child, placed him among the Twelve, and embraced him. Then he said.
local: Yesu vɛ̃ naa ka bibile kɔ̃ɔ ir ara a ba jaa sãlsɔɔ. Wʊ 'mɔɔn a bie tɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba.

eng: Whoever welcomes one of these children in my name welcomes me; and whoever welcomes me isnt actually welcoming me but rather the one who sent me.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na de a bibiir banyãna taaba a n jũu, maa lɛ wʊ de. Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa mɩ na de ma, a ba ɩ maa lɛ wʊ de ɛ, tɩchɛ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na tʊ̃ ma lɛ wʊ de.

eng: John said to Jesus, Teacher, we saw someone throwing demons out in your name, and we tried to stop him because he wasnt following us.
local: Ka a Jɔɔn 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Wiwile, sɩ nyɛ naa nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ mɩ na de a fʊ yuor na wʊ dire nɩ a sɩdɛbɛ ka a yire. Ka sɩ 'yɛr ɩka wʊ bar bojũu waa ɩ a sɩ ãsʊɔ ɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Dont stop him. No one who does powerful acts in my name can quickly turn around and curse me.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ ta dii wʊ bar ɛ. Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na de a n yuor tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ kʊ̃ 'yɛr ma 'yɛrdɛɛr go ɛ.

eng: Whoever isnt against us is for us.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa na ba jɛɛr nɩ sɩ ɛ, pʊɔ sɩ naa.

eng: I assure you that whoever gives you a cup of water to drink because you belong to Christ will certainly be rewarded.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na kʊ yɩ kɔ̃'maar nɩ ŋmele, yɩn ɩ a maa sofʊ jũu, a nɩnyɩnɛ kʊ̃ wa faa a wʊ sãyar ɛ.

eng: As for whoever causes these little ones who believe in me to trip and fall into sin, it would be better for them to have a huge stone hung around their necks and to be thrown into the lake.
local: Ala ɩ ãsʊɔ jaa mɩ vɛ̃ naa a bʊ̃bil banyãna ãsʊɔ na sɔɔ ma ɩ yelbier, a nɩnyɩnɛ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃, bala 'mɔɔ nɩɛr lũ yɔɔl a wʊ nyĩe pɔ daa 'yɔ̃ɔ a man pɔ, ala sa.

eng: If your hand causes you to fall into sin, chop it off. Its better for you to enter into life crippled than to go away with two hands into the fire of hell, which cant be put out.
local: Ala ɩ a fʊ nũu vɛ̃ naa ka fʊ ɩ yelbier, ŋmaa bar. Fʊn tɩ kpɛ nyɛvʊr pɔ nɩ nũŋmaa sa naa fʊn tara a nuur a jaa ayi tɩ tɩ kpɛ a wõm jie a be a bũu na ba kpĩine ɛ.

eng: If your foot causes you to fall into sin, chop it off. Its better for you to enter life lame than to be thrown into hell with two feet.
local: Ala ɩ a fʊ gbɛr mɩ vɛ̃ naa ka fʊ ɩ yelbier, ŋmaa bar fʊn tɩ kpɛ nyɛvʊr pɔ nɩ gbɛŋmaa sa naa fʊn tara a gbɛɛ a jaa ayi tɩ tɩ kpɛ a wõm jie.

eng: If your eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out. Its better for you to enter Gods kingdom with one eye than to be thrown into hell with two.
local: Ala ɩ a fʊ nɩbir mɩ vɛ̃ naa ka fʊ ɩ yelbier, 'loo bar. Fʊn tɩ kpɛ Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ nɩ nɩbifɩɛ sa naa fʊn tara a nɩbie ayi tɩ tɩ kpɛ a wõm jie.

eng: Thats a place where worms dont die and the fire never goes out .
local: Be lɛ a Bufulo ba kpire ɛ, ka a bũu mɩ ba kpĩine ɛ.

eng: Everyone will be salted with fire.
local: Dɔɔyɛ difʊ na lɩɛb nɩrɛ jaa nɩtãa lɛ nyaar na ma wõ bʊ̃diir ka a nʊ̃mɔ̃.

eng: Salt is good; but if salt loses its saltiness, how will it become salty again? Maintain salt among yourselves and keep peace with each other.
local: Nyaar ɩ naa, tɩchɛ a nʊ̃ɔ wa baar, ban lɛ bʊɔl wʊn nyaar ɔ? Yɩ ɩ nyaar, na yɩ tara nyã'maar kʊ taa.

eng: As Jesus left the temple, one of his disciples said to him, Teacher, look! What awesome stones and buildings.
local: Yesu na wa yire a Nãaŋmɩn Puorfʊ Yir pɔ, a wʊ poturo ãsʊɔ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Wiwile, nyɛ a kʊsɩbɛ ban mɛn a yir kpɛ̃ɛ nyã ka wʊ kaara ka.

eng: Jesus spoke to them in parables. A man planted a vineyard, put a fence around it, dug a pit for the winepress, and built a tower. Then he rented it to tenant farmers and took a trip.
local: Ka a Yesu lɛ piel 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ ba nɩ sʊkpa. Wʊ 'yɛr naa, Daba lɛ kɔ wʊ puo na wʊ bʊr tɩwɔ̃nsɩ. Wʊ wɔɔ naa a puo wʊ jaa jilʊ̃, na wʊ tuu bɔɔ ka ban ma pɔr a wɔ̃mɔ̃ 'yɔ̃ɔ na ba nɛŋmɛr ir a kʊ̃ɔ. Wʊ mɛn jie ãsʊɔ na do ka nɩrɛ na ma do jɛ̃ a be kaara a puo. Wʊ de naa a puo na wʊ kʊ nɩbɛ ɩka ba kaara tɩ na wʊ yɔ chen paal yuo pɔ.

eng: Jesus responded, Do you see these enormous buildings? Not even one stone will be left upon another. All will be demolished.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr sɔɔ wʊ, Mɩ fʊ nyɛ naa a lɛ ban mɛ a yir kpɛ̃ɛ nyãna? A kʊsɩvɩɛl anyãna 'yen jaa kʊ̃ lɛ wa chɛnɛ dɔɔl wʊ tɔ ju ɛ. Ban dãn a jaa lɔb.

eng: When it was time, he sent a servant to collect from the tenants his share of the fruit of the vineyard.
local: A wɔ̃fʊ sɔ̃ɔ wa ta naa ka wʊ tʊ̃ a wʊ gbãgbaa ãsʊɔ ɩka wʊ chen a nɩbɛ bala sɛ̃ tɩ de a wɔ̃mɔ̃ amɩne.

eng: Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives across from the temple. Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked him privately.
local: Yesu na wa jãnɛ a Olive Tɩɩr Tɔ̃ɔ ju, a Nãaŋmɩn Puorfʊ Yir nidaa, Pita nɩ Jemesɩ nɩ Jɔɔn nɩ Andiru sʊʊr wʊ naa a ba tɛɛ.

eng: But they grabbed the servant, beat him, and sent him away empty-handed.
local: Tɩchɛ ka ba nyɔɔ wʊ ŋme bar ka wʊ kul nũjaal.

eng: Tell us, when will these things happen? What sign will show that all these things are about to come to an end.
local: 'Yɛr kʊ sɩ a daar 'lɔ a anyã na ɩ. Bo lɛ na ɩ a jãnɛ sɛrɛ tɩ chɛ ka a wa ɩ.

eng: Again the landowner sent another servant to them, but they struck him on the head and treated him disgracefully.
local: Be lɛ wʊ lɛ tʊ̃ a gbãgbaa ãsʊɔ ka ba lɛ ŋme wʊ a ju puo tɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ vĩ.

eng: Jesus said, Watch out that no one deceives you.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ bɔ̃ɔn ka nɩrɛ jaa ta wa bɛl yɩ ɛ!.

eng: He sent another one; that one they killed. The landlord sent many other servants, but the tenants beat some and killed others.
local: Wʊ lɛ tʊ̃n ãsʊɔ ka ba kʊ a 'lɔ. Ka wʊ lɛ bɔ̃ɔ tʊ̃ bamɩne, ka ba pɔb bamɩne tɩ kʊ a bamɩne.

eng: Many people will come in my name, saying, Im the one! They will deceive many people.
local: Nɩyɔɔ na de naa maa yuor waan wa 'yɛrɛ, Maa lɛ a Yesu, na ba wa bɛlɛ nɩyɔɔ.

eng: Now the landowner had one son whom he loved dearly. He sent him last, thinking, They will respect my son.
local: Wʊ chen nɩbʊ̃'yen wʊn na tʊ̃, 'lɔ lɛ a wʊ bibʊ̃'yen wʊn nʊ̃. 'Lɔ lɛ wʊ wa tʊ̃ a baaraa jaa na wʊ 'yɛr, Ban 'yɔ̃ɔ naa a n bie.

eng: When you hear of wars and reports of wars, dont be alarmed. These things must happen, but this isnt the end yet.
local: Yɩn wa wõn tam, ka ba 'yɛrɛ a tam 'yɛr, yɩ taa mɩrɛ ɛ. A lɛ na ɩ naa, tɩchɛ a wɛr baarfʊ chɛnɛ na be puor.

eng: But those tenant farmers said to each other, This is the heir. Lets kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.
local: Tɩchɛ a pokaarba na wa nyɛ a bie, ba 'yɛr kʊ taa, A nyã lɛ ɩ a bʊ̃dire! Yɩ ɩ a sɩ kʊ wʊ, tɩ chɛ ka a bonsɩ ɩ a sɩ sofʊ.

eng: Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other, and there will be earthquakes and famines in all sorts of places. These things are just the beginning of the sufferings associated with the end.
local: Nɩbʊr dẽdẽme na yin tam, ka a nãmɩne mɩ yi tam. Ka a tẽgãn dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ jiir yɔɔ, ka kɔ̃ mɩ lo. A dɔɔyɛ anyãna lɛ ɩ a pielfʊ.

eng: They grabbed him, killed him, and threw him out of the vineyard.
local: Be lɛ ba nyɔɔ wʊ kʊ lɔb bar a puo kõkoor.

eng: Watch out for yourselves. People will hand you over to the councils. You will be beaten in the synagogues. You will stand before governors and kings because of me so that you can testify before them.
local: Yɩ bɔ̃ɔn, ɩka ban ma nyɔɔr yɩn chiin a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ jie, na ba lɛ pɔb yɩ, a lɔ̃ɔfʊ jiir. A maa yɩn sɔɔ jũu, ban tara yɩn chiin a tẽe nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a nãmɩne jie, ɩka ba tɩ tɔɔr a yɩ 'yɛr, tɩ yɩn tu naa a be 'yɛr a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ kʊ ba.

eng: So what will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others.
local: Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ yɩ tɩɛr ɩka a puo sʊɔ na ɩ naa? Wʊn wan wa kʊ a pokaarba banyãna ba jaa tɩ de a puo kʊ nɩyor!.

eng: First, the good news must be proclaimed to all the nations.
local: A 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyã na den nie ta a nɩbʊrɔ ba jaa.

eng: Havent you read this scripture, The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone.
local: Yaa kar a jie nyã a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɛ? Wʊ 'yɛr ɩka, A kʊsɩr 'lɔ a mɩmɩɛrbɛ na tɔr bar lɛ ɩ a kʊsɩr ju.

eng: The Lord has done this, and its amazing in our eyes? .
local: A sɩ Soro lɛ tʊ̃ a nɩtɔ̃ ka sɔb sɩ a nɩbie. .

eng: When they haul you in and hand you over, dont worry ahead of time about what to answer or say. Instead, say whatever is given to you at that moment, for you arent doing the speaking but the Holy Spirit is.
local: Bala wa nyɔɔr yɩ chiin a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ jie, yɩ ta vɛ̃ ka tɩɛr nyɔɔ yɩ a lɛ yɩn tɩ 'yɛr ɛ. Lɛ jaa a Nãaŋmɩn na wiil yɩ a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ sɔ̃ɔ, 'yɛr wʊ. A ba ɩ yɩn lɛ 'yɛr ɛ, tɩchɛ a 'yɛrbie yi naa a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ sɛ̃.

eng: Brothers and sisters will hand each other over to death. A father will turn in his children. Children will rise up against their parents and have them executed.
local: Yɛbɛ na gar 'yɔ̃ɔ yɛbɛ, ka ba kʊ wʊ, ka a sãa mɩ gar 'yɔ̃ɔ bie, ka ba kʊ wʊ. Bibiir kʊ̃ lɛ wõnõ a ba sãamɩne toor ɛ, na ba vɛ̃ ka ba kʊ a ba sãamɩne.

eng: They wanted to arrest Jesus because they knew that he had told the parable against them. But they were afraid of the crowd, so they left him and went away.
local: Be lɛ a Juu nɩbɛrɛ bʊɔrɔ sɔr ɩka ba nyɔɔ a Yesu, bojũu ba bɔ̃ɔ naa ɩka bala lɛ wʊ lɔb a sʊkpa gbɛb. Tɩchɛ ba joro naa a nɩyɔɔ bala dãbãɛ, na ba bar wʊ tɩ lɩɛb chen.

eng: They sent some of the Pharisees and supporters of Herod to trap him in his words.
local: Ba lɛ tʊ̃ naa a Faraseemɩne bamɩne nɩ nɩbɛ bamɩne na yi a nãa Hɛrɔd sɛ̃, ka ba wa bɛr Yesu nɛɛ nyɔɔ wʊ, a wʊ 'yɛrbie pɔ.

eng: Everyone will hate you because of my name. But whoever stands firm until the end will be saved.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa na 'laar yɩ naa, yɩn sɔɔ ma jũu, tɩ 'lɔ na sɔɔ tɩ ta a baaraa, na nyɛn Nãaŋmɩn faafʊ.

eng: When you see the disgusting and destructive thing standing where it shouldnt be , then those in Judea must escape to the mountains.
local: Yɩn nyɛ naa a yele 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn na 'laar na be a jie 'lɔ an ba sɛɛ ɛ, na wʊ sɔ̃ɔ a jie, na wʊ vɛ̃ ka a wʊ suur dire. A fʊ̃ʊ nyã na kara bɔ̃ɔ a par. Yɩn wa nyɛ a lɛ, nɩbɛ na be a Judiya ba jo do a tɔ̃ɔ ju.

eng: They came to him and said, Teacher, we know that youre genuine and you dont worry about what people think. You dont show favoritism but teach Gods way as it really is. Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not? Should we pay taxes or not.
local: Ba wa na wa 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Wiwile, sɩ bɔ̃ɔ naa ɩka fʊ ɩn yelmãɛ sʊɔ. Nɩbɛ 'yɛrbie maa tʊ̃ɔ a fʊ pʊtɩɛr lɩɛb ɛ, bojũu fʊ maa chɛlɛ a lɛ nɩrɛ jaa na be ɛ. Tɩchɛ fʊ wile na a Nãaŋmɩn sɔr nɩ yelmãɛ. A tara naa sɔr ɩka sɩ yab a lõpo kʊ a nãkpɛ̃ɛ Siisa bɩɩ?.

eng: Those on the roof shouldnt come down or enter their houses to grab anything.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa na be gar ju, ta suu a die pɔ tɩ de bon jaa ɛ.

eng: Those in the field shouldnt come back to grab their clothes.
local: 'Lɔ mɩ na be a puo pɔ, ta lɛ chen tɩ de a wʊ fɔbaa ɛ.

eng: Since Jesus recognized their deceit, he said to them, Why are you testing me? Bring me a coin. Show it to me.
local: Sɩ yab bɩɩ sɩ ta yab ɛ? Tɩchɛ ka a Yesu bɔ̃ɔn a ba nɛbɛr yele ban waan, na wʊ sʊʊr ba, Bo lɛ yɩ bʊɔrɔ ɩka yɩ bɛɛr ma kaa? Yɩ de a libir ãsʊɔ waan ka n kaa.

eng: And they brought one. He said to them, Whose image and inscription is this? Caesars, they replied.
local: Be lɛ ba de a libir kur ãsʊɔ waan, ka wʊ sʊʊr ba, An foto nɩ a yuor lɛ be a libir pɔ? Ka ba 'yɛr, Siisa lɛ.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant and for women who are nursing their children.
local: A bɩbir nyãna an ɩn tuo kʊ a pɔɔpʊʊr nɩ a bala na tara a bi'yɛnsɩ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God. His reply left them overcome with wonder.
local: Lɛ lɛ ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ de a 'lɔ na ɩ a Siisa sofʊ na yɩ yab wʊ, na yɩ mɩ de a 'lɔ na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn sofʊ na yɩ mɩ kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn. Ka ba faa tɩ nyɔɔr a nɛɛr ara nɩ.

eng: Pray that it doesnt happen in winter.
local: Yɩ sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn ka a ta wa ɩ uon ɛ.

eng: In those days there will be great suffering such as the world has never before seen and will never again see.
local: A ber ala pɔ, nɩbɛ na din dɔɔyɛ yɔɔ. Nãaŋmɩn na maal a wɛr baa di a dɔɔyɛ nyã bʊrɔ ɛ, tɩ ba mɩ kʊ̃ lɛ wa di wʊ go ɛ.

eng: Sadducees, who deny that there is a resurrection, came to Jesus and asked.
local: A nɩbɛ bamɩne ban bʊɔlɔ a Saduseemɩne na 'yɛr ɩka kũu pɔ irfʊ ba be be ɛ, mɩ wa naa wa sʊʊr wʊ.

eng: If the Lord hadnt shortened that time, no one would be rescued. But for the sake of the chosen ones, the ones whom God chose, he has cut short the time.
local: Ala ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn ba ŋmaa a ber ala bar ɛ, nɩrɛ jaa kʊ̃ chɛ ɛ. Tɩchɛ a bala wʊn chɛl ir jũu, wʊ ŋmaan bar.

eng: Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a mans brother dies , leaving a widow but no children, the brother must marry the widow and raise up children for his brother.
local: Wiwile, Mosesɩ sɛb kʊ sɩ ɩka nɩrɛ yɛbɛ wa kpi tɩ bar pɔɔ na ba tara bie ɛ, a wʊ yɛbɛ wʊ kul a pɔɔkʊɔr 'lɔ na wʊ dɔɔ bibiir kʊ a wʊ yɛbɛ 'lɔ na kpi.

eng: Then if someone says to you, Look, heres the Christ, or There he is, dont believe it.
local: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃, nɩrɛ wa 'yɛr kʊ yɩ ɩka yɩ nyɛ a Nɩ-iraa a ka, bɩɩ kasɛn lɛ wʊ be, yɩ taa sɔɔrɔ ɛ.

eng: False christs and false prophets will appear, and they will offer signs and wonders in order to deceive, if possible, those whom God has chosen.
local: Bojũu a jire Nɩ-iraa nɩ a jire 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ na wa na wa tʊ̃nɔ̃ a lɛ a nɩbɛ na ba dɔ̃ɔ nyɛ ɛ, na ba maala yelbɛrɛ fɩka Nãaŋmɩn lɛ kʊ ba a kpɛ̃ɔ. Na ba bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba bɛl a bala a Nãaŋmɩn na chɛl ir, ala ɩ ban tʊ̃ɔ naa.

eng: Now there were seven brothers. The first one married a woman; when he died, he left no children.
local: Yɛɛr ayopõi lɛ dɔ̃ɔ be be, ka ba kpɛ̃ɛ kul pɔɔ kpi tɩ chɛ bar, waa tara bie ɛ.

eng: The second married her and died without leaving any children. The third did the same.
local: Ka a wʊ turo de a pɔɔkʊɔr kul, baa mɩ dɔɔ bie ɛ, tɩchɛ ka wʊ mɩ kpi. A lɛ nɛ nɩ wʊ ju mɩ lɛ a turo ata sʊɔ.

eng: But you, watch out! Ive told you everything ahead of time.
local: Yɩ bɔ̃ɔn! N den nie 'yɛr kʊ yɩ.

eng: None of the seven left any children. Finally, the woman died.
local: Yelmãɛ jaa a yɛɛr ayopõi banyãna ba jaa kulnaa a pɔɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃, tɩ waa dɔɔ bie ɛ, a baaraa jaa ka a pɔɔ mɩ kpi.

eng: In those days, after the suffering of that time, the sun will become dark, and the moon wont give its light.
local: Tɩchɛ a dɔɔyɛ nyã baarfʊ sɔ̃ɔ, Mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ na lii naa, ka a ŋmɛraa kʊ̃ mɩ lɛ chaar ɛ.

eng: At the resurrection, when they all rise up, whose wife will she be? All seven were married to her.
local: Bala wa ir yi a kũu pɔ, an pɔɔ lɛ wʊn ɩ, an ɩ ba jaa kul wʊn lʊɔr taa.

eng: The stars will fall from the sky, and the planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken.
local: Ka a ŋmɛrbie pɔrɔ yire a saaju loro, tɩ a saaju wʊ jaa dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃.

eng: Jesus said to them, Isnt this the reason you are wrong, because you dont know either the scriptures or Gods power?.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Yaa nyɛ yɩn bɔr, bojũu yaa bɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn sɛbɛ nɩ a wʊ kpɛ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: Then they will see the Human One coming in the clouds with great power and splendor.
local: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃, ban nyɛn a Nɩsaal Bie na waara a julõjuur pɔ, nɩ kpɛ̃ɔ nɩ tɩɩr.

eng: When people rise from the dead, they wont marry nor will they be given in marriage. Instead, they will be like Gods angels.
local: Nɩbɛ wa ir a kũu pɔ ba kʊ̃ lɛ kul taa ɛ, tɩ ban kaara naa fɩka a malakasɩ, baa kulo taa ɛ.

eng: Then he will send the angels and gather together his chosen people from the four corners of the earth, from the end of the earth to the end of heaven.
local: Wʊn tʊ̃ naa a wʊ malakasɩ, ka ba wa lɔ̃ɔ taa a bala wʊn chɛl ir a wɛr wʊ jaa, tɩ yin a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ purfʊ jie tɩ tɔ a mʊrfʊ jie.

eng: As for the resurrection from the dead, havent you read in the scroll from Moses, in the passage about the burning bush, how God said to Moses, I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob?.
local: A kũu pɔ irfʊ yele yɩ kar a Mosesɩ gãn pɔ a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ wʊn nyɛ a tɩbile na 'lɩrɛ bũu, ka a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, N ɩ naa a Abraham Nãaŋmɩn nɩ a Isaakɩ Nãaŋmɩn nɩ a Jekɔb Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: Learn this parable from the fig tree. After its branch becomes tender and it sprouts new leaves, you know that summer is near.
local: Yɩ de a pɔlpɔl tɩɛ yele jãn yɛ̃. 'Lɔ wa tʊɔrɔ a vaar yɩ bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a tʊl sɔ̃ɔ gbʊr naa.

eng: He isnt the God of the dead but of the living. You are seriously mistaken.
local: Wʊ ɩ naa vʊr Nãaŋmɩn, waa ɩ kũn Nãaŋmɩn ɛ. Yɩ tul naa.

eng: In the same way, when you see these things happening, you know that hes near, at the door.
local: A lɛ mɩ nɩ wʊ ju lɛ, yɩn wa nyɛ a lɛ n 'yɛr kʊ yɩ na ɩrɛ, yɩ bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a sɔ̃ɔ gbʊr naa jaa yɛ̃yɛ̃.

eng: One of the legal experts heard their dispute and saw how well Jesus answered them. He came over and asked him, Which commandment is the most important of all.
local: A Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wile ãsʊɔ wa ara na chɛl a lɛ a Yesu nɩ a nɩbɛ na 'mataa 'yɛrɛ. Ka wʊ nyɛ ka a Yesu 'yɛr nʊ̃mɔ̃. Ka wʊ mɩ sʊʊr a Yesu, A Nãaŋmɩn nɛɛr a jaa pɔ, buor sʊɔ lɛ ɩ kpɛ̃ɛ.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until all these things happen.
local: N 'yɛrɛ naa yelmãɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, ɩka a pʊ̃pãanyã nɩbɛ kʊ̃ kpi baar ɛ, tɩ yɩ nyɛ a yele anyãna ka a ɩ.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: A saaju nɩ a wɛr na wa baar naa tɩchɛ maa 'yɛrbie dɔ̃ɔ kʊ̃ wa baar ɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, The most important one is Israel, listen! Our God is the one Lord.
local: Ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, A 'lɔ na ɩ kpɛ̃ɛ gar a jaa nɩ nyã, A Isɩral nɩbɛ, yɩ chɛlɛ! A sɩ Soro Nãaŋmɩn, wʊ ɩ naa Soro bʊ̃'yen tɛɛ.

eng: But nobody knows when that day or hour will come, not the angels in heaven and not the Son. Only the Father knows.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa ba bɔ̃ɔ a bɩbir nyã ɛ, bɩɩ a sɔ̃ɔ ɛ, a malakasɩ gba na be a saaju ba bɔ̃ɔ ɛ, a Bie mɩ ba bɔ̃ɔ ɛ, a Sãa tɛɛ lɛ bɔ̃ɔ.

eng: Watch out! Stay alert! You dont know when the time is coming.
local: Yɩ gu yɩ mãɛ vɩla! Yɩ kaar sʊ̃! Yaa bɔ̃ɔ a sɔ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: and you must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, with all your mind, and with all your strength.
local: Nʊ̃ a fʊ Soro Nãaŋmɩn nɩ a fʊ nyãa, nɩ a fʊ nyɛvʊr wʊ jaa, nɩ a fʊ tɩɛr wʊ jaa, nɩ a fʊ kpɛ̃ɔ wʊ jaa.

eng: It is as if someone took a trip, left the household behind, and put the servants in charge, giving each one a job to do, and told the doorkeeper to stay alert.
local: An kaar naa fɩka yir sʊɔ na wa yʊɔrɔ tɩ de a wʊ yir 'yɔ̃ɔ a wʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ nuur pɔ, na wʊ kʊ ãsʊɔ jaa nɩ wʊ tʊ̃mɔ̃, tɩ mɩ 'yɛr kʊ a dɔ̃dɔr guro ka wʊ kaara.

eng: The second is this, You will love your neighbor as yourself . No other commandment is greater than these.
local: A 'lɔ na tu nɩ nyã, Nʊ̃ a fʊ tɔ sʊɔ nɩtãa lɛ fʊn nʊ̃ a fʊ mãɛ. Nɛɛ jaa ba be be ɩ kpɛ gar a anyã ɛ.

eng: Therefore, stay alert! You dont know when the head of the household will come, whether in the evening or at midnight, or when the rooster crows in the early morning or at daybreak.
local: A lɛ jũu yɩ kaara tɩ guro wʊ, bojũu yaa bɔ̃ɔ a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ a yir sʊɔ na na lɩɛb wa ɛ. Amɩne kɔ̃ɔ a wa ɩ jãanʊɔr, bɩɩ tãsɔɔ bãɩn, bɩɩ nɔrsɩ na kõnõ, bɩɩ bibio pipi.

eng: Dont let him show up when you werent expecting and find you sleeping.
local: Yɩ ta wa vɛ̃ ka wʊ wa dũu yɩ a yɩ be gʊ̃ɔ pɔ ɛ.

eng: The legal expert said to him, Well said, Teacher. You have truthfully said that God is one and there is no other besides him.
local: Ka a daba 'yɛr, Wiwile, fʊ 'yɛr yelmãɛ, a lɛ fʊn 'yɛr ɩka Nãaŋmɩn bʊ̃'yen tɛɛ lɛ be be, ka ãsʊɔ jaa ba lɛ be be pʊɔ wʊ ɛ.

eng: What I say to you, I say to all: Stay alert.
local: A lɛ n 'yɛr kʊ yɩ, nɩrɛ jaa lɛ n 'yɛr kʊ yɩ kaar sʊ̃.

eng: And to love God with all of the heart, a full understanding, and all of ones strength, and to love ones neighbor as oneself is much more important than all kinds of entirely burned offerings and sacrifices.
local: A fʊ nʊ̃ wʊ nɩ a fʊ nyãa, nɩ a fʊ tɩɛr wʊ jaa, nɩ a fʊ kpɛ̃ɔ wʊ jaa, na fʊ lɛ nʊ̃ a fʊ tɔ sʊɔ nɩtãa a fʊ mãɛ, a yele anyãna tufʊ ɩn gar dʊ̃n fʊn kʊ chʊm kʊ Nãaŋmɩn nɩ bɔɔrlorsɩ a jaa.

eng: When Jesus saw that he had answered with wisdom, he said to him, You arent far from Gods kingdom. After that, no one dared to ask him any more questions.
local: Yesu na nyɛ a lɛ a daba na 'yɛr nɩ yɛ̃ ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Faa jãa nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ ɛ. An yi a be chiine nɩrɛ jaa ba lɛ 'la sʊʊr wʊ sʊʊrfʊ go ɛ.

eng: While Jesus was teaching in the temple, he said, Why do the legal experts say that the Christ is Davids son?.
local: A lɛ a Yesu na wile a Puorfʊ Yir bɛchaar pɔ, wʊ sʊʊr naa a nɩbɛ, Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ a ta ka a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe 'yɛr ɩka David bie lɛ a Nɩ-iraa?.

eng: David himself, inspired by the Holy Spirit, said, The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side until I turn your enemies into your footstool.
local: A ba ɩ a David mãɛ pɔ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ, ka wʊ 'yɛr ɩka, A n Soro Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr kʊ a n Soro nyã wʊn ir, Jãnɛ a n nũsʊ̃ɔ loor ka n wa de a fʊ dɔ̃dẽme bin a fʊ gbɛɛ pĩlem. .

eng: David himself calls him Lord, so how can he be Davids son? The large crowd listened to him with delight.
local: Ala ɩ David mãɛ bʊɔl wʊ naa a wʊ Soro, ŋmɩnɛ lɛ wʊn lɛ ɩ a wʊ bie tɛɛ? Ka a nɩyɔɔ bala ara chɛlɛ wʊ nɩ pʊpɛl.

eng: As he was teaching, he said, Watch out for the legal experts. They like to walk around in long robes. They want to be greeted with honor in the markets.
local: Yesu wile naa tɩ na wʊ kpãa ba, Yɩ gu yɩ mãɛ a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe sɛ̃, ba ma su naa a fuu vɛɛsɩ ala yʊɔrɔ ɩka ba puor ba a dar pɔ.

eng: They long for places of honor in the synagogues and at banquets.
local: Bala be wa kpɛ a lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie, a niem lɛ ba ma jãnɛ.

eng: They are the ones who cheat widows out of their homes, and to show off they say long prayers. They will be judged most harshly.
local: Ba mɩ ma fɔlɔ naa a pɔɔkɔɛ, na ba mɩ ma puor Nãaŋmɩn kor, bʊɔrɔ ɩka nɩbɛ ba kaara ba. A nɩbɛ banyãna 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ na kpɛ̃mɛ naa.

eng: Jesus sat across from the collection box for the temple treasury and observed how the crowd gave their money. Many rich people were throwing in lots of money.
local: Yesu jɛ̃n gbʊr a jie ban 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a libie a Nãaŋmɩn Puorfʊ Yir pɔ, na wa kaara a lɛ a nɩyɔɔ na 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a ba libie. Taraa dẽme yɔɔ jaa lɔɔrɔ naa yɔɔ.

eng: One poor widow came forward and put in two small copper coins worth a penny.
local: Be lɛ ka a pɔɔkɔnɔ̃ɔ sʊɔ mɩ be be na wʊ mɩ waan libikue ayi na ɩ kobo wa 'yɔ̃ɔ a libie pɔ.

eng: Jesus called his disciples to him and said, I assure you that this poor widow has put in more than everyone whos been putting money in the treasury.
local: Ka Yesu bʊɔl a wʊ poturbo ka ba tɔɔ gbʊr wʊ ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, a pɔɔkɔnɔ̃ɔ sʊɔ nyã lɔb gar ba jaa.

eng: All of them are giving out of their spare change. But she from her hopeless poverty has given everything she had, even what she needed to live on.
local: A tara dẽme ma 'yɔ̃ɔn tɩ chɛ a libiyɔɔ amɩne, tɩchɛ a pɔɔkɔnɔ̃ɔ sʊɔ nyã 'lɔ, a jaa lɛ wʊ de 'yɔ̃ɔ. Waa lɛ tara bon jaa go ɛ.

eng: It was two days before Passover and the Festival of Unleavened Bread. The chief priests and legal experts through cunning tricks were searching for a way to arrest Jesus and kill him.
local: A chen ber ayi ka ban di a Gõl Bar Difʊ nɩ a Paanʊ̃kõo Lɔ̃ɔfʊ, ka a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe bʊɔrɔ vuo 'lɔ ban na tu sɔɔl nyɔɔ a Yesu kʊ.

eng: But they agreed that it shouldnt happen during the festival; otherwise, there would be an uproar among the people.
local: Tɩchɛ ka ba 'yɛr, A ta ɩ a lɔ̃ɔfʊ sɔ̃ɔ ɛ, ka a jɛɛr taa ir a chara pɔ ɛ.

eng: Jesus was at Bethany visiting the house of Simon, who had a skin disease. During dinner, a woman came in with a vase made of alabaster and containing very expensive perfume of pure nard. She broke open the vase and poured the perfume on his head.
local: Yesu na wa be a Betanɩ a Simon 'lɔ na dɔ̃ɔ ɩ a kɔ̃kɔn yir, ka ba 'mataa dire, pɔɔ kɔ̃ɔ wa waan na pãrtɔvɩla nɩ kãnyũu kɔ̃ɔ dafʊ na ba wõnõ ɛ. A pɔɔ kpɩr naa a pãrtʊɔ nyĩe tɩ kpaar a kãnyũu ala dɔɔl a Yesu ju.

eng: Some grew angry. They said to each other, Why waste the perfume?.
local: Ka a nɩbɛ bala na be be nyɛ suur tɩ 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ taa, Bo lɛ so ka wʊ sɔ̃ɔ a kãnyũu nyã bar a nɩtɔ̃?.

eng: This perfume could have been sold for almost a years pay and the money given to the poor. And they scolded her.
local: Wʊn taa den da nɩbʊ̃'yen yuon gbul sãyar libie na wʊ de kʊ nɔ̃n dẽme. Tɩ mii 'yɔ̃ɔ a pɔɔ.

eng: Jesus said, Leave her alone. Why do you make trouble for her? She has done a good thing for me.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ bar wʊ! Bãa 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ yɩ mii 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ? Wʊ maal vɩla kʊ ma.

eng: You always have the poor with you; and whenever you want, you can do something good for them. But you wont always have me.
local: A nɔ̃n dẽme 'lɔ, yɩn nɩ bala ben ka. Yɩn tʊ̃ɔ sʊ̃ʊ ba sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ jaa yɩn bʊɔrɔ, tɩchɛ maa kʊ̃ be a yɩ sɛ̃ sɔ̃ɔ jaa ɛ.

eng: She has done what she could. She has anointed my body ahead of time for burial.
local: Wʊ ɩ naa a lɛ wʊn tʊ̃ɔ. Wʊ kpaar a kãnyũu 'yɔ̃ɔ ma tɩ chɛ ka n ba kpi sɛrɛ ɛ, na wʊ chɔɔr ma guro nɩ a n ũufʊ.

eng: I tell you the truth that, wherever in the whole world the good news is announced, what shes done will also be told in memory of her.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, jie jaa ban wa 'yɛr a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyãna a wɛr wʊ jaa pɔ, ban 'yɛr naa a lɛ a pɔɔ nyã na ɩ na ba chɛnɛ tara a wʊ tɩɛr.

eng: Judas Iscariot, one of the Twelve, went to the chief priests to give Jesus up to them.
local: A Pie nɩ Ayi bala wʊn kaa ir ãsʊɔ ban bʊɔlɔ a Judas Isɩkariyot chen a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ sɛ̃ tɩ 'yɛr ba ɩka wʊ bʊɔrɔ na ɩka wʊ de a Yesu 'yɔ̃ɔ a ba nuur pɔ.

eng: When they heard it, they were delighted and promised to give him money. So he started looking for an opportunity to turn him in.
local: Ban wa wõ a lɛ wʊn 'yɛr ka a nʊ̃mɔ̃ ba, ka ba 'yɛr ɩka ban kʊ wʊn libie. Ka a Judas pãa bʊɔrɔ vuo 'lɔ wʊn na tu de a Yesu kʊ ba.

eng: On the first day of the Festival of Unleavened Bread, when the Passover lamb was sacrificed, the disciples said to Jesus, Where do you want us to prepare for you to eat the Passover meal.
local: A bɩbir dɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ ɩ naa a Paanʊ̃kõo Lɔ̃ɔfʊ, ka ban na kʊ a pele na ba di a Gõl Bar Difʊ, Yesu poturbo sʊʊr wʊ naa, Nyɩnɛ lɛ fʊ bʊɔrɔ ɩka sɩ chen tɩ chɔɔr a Gõl Bar nyã bʊ̃diir a fʊ tɩ di.

eng: He sent two of his disciples and said to them, Go into the city. A man carrying a water jar will meet you. Follow him.
local: Wʊ tʊ̃ naa a wʊ poturbo ayi, na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ chen a tẽe pɔ, daba na tuo kʊ̃ɔ nɩ dʊʊ na tuor yɩ naa. Yɩ turo wʊ.

eng: Wherever he enters, say to the owner of the house, The teacher asks,Where is my guest room where I can eat the Passover meal with my disciples.
local: A yir 'lɔ pɔ wʊn wa tɩ kpɛ, yɩ sʊʊr a yir sʊɔ, A Wiwile ɩka sɩ wa sʊʊr fʊ, Nyɩnɛ lɛ a 'lɔ nɩ a wʊ poturbo na wa 'mataa di a Gõl Bar Difʊ? .

eng: When the Sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices so that they could go and anoint Jesus dead body.
local: A Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir na wa baar, Mɛɛr na yi a Magdala, nɩ Salome, nɩ a Mɛɛr na ɩ a Jemesɩ ma, ba dãn a kãnyũur ala ɩka ba chiin tɩ jɛ a Yesu ãgãn.

eng: He will show you a large room upstairs already furnished. Prepare for us there.
local: Wʊn wiil yɩ naa bɔɔpɩɛ ban chɔɔr. Yɩ maal a bʊ̃diir a be.

eng: Very early on the first day of the week, just after sunrise, they came to the tomb.
local: A Pɛ̃nfʊ divɩɛ bio daar, bibio mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ na pur baar, ba chen naa a yaa par.

eng: The disciples left, came into the city, found everything just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover meal.
local: Ba yin chen a tẽe tɩ nyɛ a lɛ jaa a Yesu na 'yɛr kʊ ba. Ka ba maal a Gõl Bar bʊ̃diir.

eng: That evening, Jesus arrived with the Twelve.
local: Jãanʊɔr na wa ta, Yesu nɩ a wʊ poturbo Pie nɩ Ayi wa naa.

eng: They were saying to each other, Whos going to roll the stone away from the entrance for us.
local: A kʊsɩr na pɔɔ a yaa nɛɛ ba gbʊr ɛ, ka ba sʊrɔ taa, An lɛ na tɩ bil a kʊsɩr bar sɩ a yaa nɛɛ.

eng: During the meal, Jesus said, I assure you that one of you will betray me—someone eating with me.
local: Ban wa dire, wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, a yɩ ãsʊɔ na tɩ gar 'yɔ̃ɔ ma; ãsʊɔ sɩn 'mataa dire nɩ a labʊ̃'yen pɔ.

eng: When they looked up, they saw that the stone had been rolled away.
local: Tɩchɛ ban dʊɔ kaa a ba nidaa, a kʊsɩr 'lɔ bil yi a yaa nɛɛ.

eng: Deeply saddened, they asked him, one by one, Its not me, is it.
local: Be lɛ ka ba jaa pʊɔ sɔ̃ɔ ka ba sʊrɔ wa bʊ̃'yen 'yen, Maa lɛ ɔ.

eng: Jesus answered, Its one of the Twelve, one who is dipping bread with me into this bowl.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, A yɩ Pie nɩ Ayi ãsʊɔ lɛ! A yɩ ãsʊɔ sɩn 'mataa tũno a labʊ̃'yen pɔ lɛ.

eng: Going into the tomb, they saw a young man in a white robe seated on the right side; and they were startled.
local: Ban tɩ kpɩɛrɛ a yaa pɔ, ba nyɛn pɔlbile na su fuu pɩla jãnɛ nɩ a nũsʊ̃ɔ loor, ka pɩla ɔɔ ba.

eng: The Human One goes to his death just as it is written about him. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays the Human One! It would have been better for him if he had never been born.
local: A Nɩsaal Bie na kpi naa nɩtãa lɛ ban sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ. Tɩchɛ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na gar 'yɔ̃ɔ a Nɩsaal Bie na din dɔɔyɛ gar nɩrɛ jaa! Ka bala ta ba dɔɔ wʊ ɛ, ala sa.

eng: But he said to them, Dont be alarmed! You are looking for Jesus of Nazareth, who was crucified. He has been raised. He isnt here. Look, heres the place where they laid him.
local: A pɔlbile 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ ta vɛ̃ ka yɩ pɩla ɔɔrɔ ɛ. Yɩ bʊɔrɔ naa a Yesu 'lɔ na yi a Najaretɩ ban kpa a daa ju. Wʊ ir naa ɩ vʊr! Waa be a ka ɛ! Yɩ nyɛ a jie 'lɔ ban gaal wʊ.

eng: Go, tell his disciples, especially Peter, that he is going ahead of you into Galilee. You will see him there, just as he told you.
local: Yɩ lɩɛb kul tɩ 'yɛr a wʊ poturbo nɩ a Pita ɩka, wʊ de yɩn nie chiine a Galilee, be lɛ yɩn tɩ nyɛ wʊ, nɩtãa a lɛ wʊn dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr kʊ yɩ.

eng: Overcome with terror and dread, they fled from the tomb. They said nothing to anyone, because they were afraid. .
local: Chĩcham kpɛ ba naa ka ba nɛɛ 'maa, ka ba jo yi a yaa par. Baa 'yɛr 'yɛr kʊ nɩrɛ jaa ɛ, bojũu dãbãɛ kpɛ ba naa.

eng: While they were eating, Jesus took bread, blessed it, broke it, and gave it to them, and said, Take; this is my body.
local: Ban wa dire ka a Yesu de a paanʊ̃ɔ puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan, tɩ ŋmaa de kʊ a wʊ poturbo, na wʊ 'yɛr, Yɩ de, a n ãgãn lɛ.

eng: They promptly reported all of the young mans instructions to those who were with Peter. Afterward, through the work of his disciples, Jesus sent out, from the east to the west, the sacred and undying message of eternal salvation. Amen. .
local: A Pɛ̃nfʊ divɩɛ bio daar bibio, Yesu na wa ir a kũu pɔ, wʊ den nie dũu a Mɛɛr na yi a Magdala, a 'lɔ pɔ wʊn dɔ̃ɔ dii a sɩdɛbɛ ayopõi bar ka a yi.

eng: He took a cup, gave thanks, and gave it to them, and they all drank from it.
local: Wʊ lɛ den a ŋmele 'lɔ ban nyuro nɩ, na wʊ puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan tɩ lɛ de kʊ ba, ka ba jaa nyũ.

eng: She went and reported to the ones who had been with him, who were mourning and weeping.
local: A Mɛɛr chen tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a bala na dɔ̃ɔ be a Yesu sɛ̃, a bala a nie na sɔb ka baa kõnõ.

eng: He said to them, This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many.
local: Be lɛ a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, N jãɩ lɛ a anyã na vɛ̃ a Nãaŋmɩn nɛtɩraa na tara kpɛ̃ɔ, an chir naa, na a faa nɩyɔɔ.

eng: But even after they heard the news, they didnt believe that Jesus was alive and that Mary had seen him.
local: Ban wa wõ ka wʊ 'yɛr ɩka a Yesu ir naa kũu pɔ ɩ vʊr, ka wʊ nyɛ wʊ, baa sɔɔ de ɛ.

eng: I assure you that I wont drink wine again until that day when I drink it in a new way in Gods kingdom.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, n kʊ̃ lɛ nyũ a dãa anyãna ɛ, wa tɩ tãn a daar 'lɔ ɩn na nyũ a bʊ̃paalaa a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.

eng: After that he appeared in a different form to two of them who were walking along in the countryside.
local: A anyã puor lɛ, a Yesu lɩɛb nɩyuo na wʊ dũu a ba ayi na de sɔr chiine a tẽe ãsʊɔ pɔ.

eng: After singing songs of praise, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
local: Ban wa yiel yiel dãn Nãaŋmɩn, ba suun chen a Olive Tɩɩr Tɔ̃ɔ ju.

eng: When they returned, they reported it to the others, but they didnt believe them.
local: Ba lɩɛb chen a Jerusalɛm tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a ba taaba, tɩchɛ baa sɔɔ ɛ.

eng: Jesus said to them, You will all falter in your faithfulness to me. It is written, I will hit the shepherd, and the sheep will go off in all directions .
local: Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ jaa na jon tɩ bar ma, nɩtãa lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na 'yɛr a wʊ gãn pɔ, Ɩn ŋme naa pɛchinɛ kʊ ka a piir yɛ̃yɛl.

eng: Finally he appeared to the eleven while they were eating. Jesus criticized their unbelief and stubbornness because they didnt believe those who saw him after he was raised up.
local: A ba kor ɛ, tɩ a Yesu lɛ dũu a Pie nɩ Bʊ̃'yen na 'mataa dire. Wʊ miin 'yɔ̃ɔ ba a ba ba sɔɔfʊ pɔ, nɩ a ba nyãkpãɛn jũu, bojũu baa sɔɔ de a bala na nyɛ wʊn ir a kũu pɔ 'yɛr.

eng: But after Im raised up, I will go before you to Galilee.
local: Tɩchɛ maa wa ir a kũu pɔ, ɩn de yɩn nie chen a Galilee.

eng: He said to them, Go into the whole world and proclaim the good news to every creature.
local: Wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ yɔ a wɛr wʊ jaa, na yɩ mʊɔl 'yɛr a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyã kʊ a nɩbʊrɔ ba jaa.

eng: Peter said to him, Even if everyone else stumbles, I wont.
local: Ka a Pita chɛ nyãa 'yɛr, Ala ɩ gba, ba jaa sɔɔn bar fʊ tɩ maa 'lɔ kʊ̃ bar fʊ ɛ.

eng: Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved, but whoever doesnt believe will be condemned.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na sɔɔ de a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyã, ka ba so wʊ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ, wʊn nyɛn a Nãaŋmɩn faafʊ, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na ba sɔɔ de a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyã ɛ, a nɩnyɩnɛ na wɛ naa.

eng: But Jesus said to him, I assure you that on this very night, before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times.
local: Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a Pita, Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ fʊ, dɩna tãsɔɔ, a nɔrdɔ̃ɔ kʊ̃ kõ gbɛɛ ayi sɛrɛ ɛ, tɩ fʊ 'yɛr gbɛɛ ata ɩka faa bɔ̃ɔ ma ɛ.

eng: These signs will be associated with those who believe: they will throw out demons in my name. They will speak in new languages.
local: A jãnsɩ anyã na turo naa bala na sɔɔ. Ban de naa n yuor diin sɩdɛbɛ ka a yi, na ba lɛ bɔ̃ɔ 'yɛrbʊlsɩ 'yɛrfʊ.

eng: But Peter insisted, If I must die alongside you, I wont deny you. And they all said the same thing.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Pita 'yɛr kpɛ̃ɔ jaa ɩka, Maa nɩ fʊ̃ʊ na 'mantaa kpi gba, tɩ n kʊ̃ 'yɛr ɩka n ba bɔ̃ɔ fʊ ɛ. Ka a bala mɩ na chɛ 'yɛr a lɛ nɛ.

eng: They will pick up snakes with their hands. If they drink anything poisonous, it will not hurt them. They will place their hands on the sick, and they will get well.
local: Ban ma 'mɔɔr waba. Bala wa nyũ bon na tara lɔɔ, tɩ a kʊ̃ ɩ ba yele ɛ. Baalaa ban dɔɔl a ba nũu, wʊn sa naa.

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to a place called Gethsemane. Jesus said to them, Sit here while I pray.
local: Yesu nɩ a wʊ poturbo chen naa a jie 'lɔ ban bʊɔlɔ Getɩsemanɩ, ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ jãnɛ a kana, tɩ ka n chen tɩ puor Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: After the Lord Jesus spoke to them, he was lifted up into heaven and sat down on the right side of God.
local: A Soro Yesu na wa 'yɛr a nɩtɔ̃ kʊ a wʊ poturbo, Nãaŋmɩn vɛ̃ naa ka wʊ do a saaju tɩ jãnɛ a wʊ nũsʊ̃ɔ loor.

eng: He took Peter, James, and John along with him. He began to feel despair and was anxious.
local: Be lɛ wʊ tara Pita nɩ Jemesɩ nɩ Jɔɔn 'mataa chiin ka a wʊ pʊɔ sɔ̃ɔ yɔɔ jaa, ka tɩɛr nyɔɔ wʊ yɔɔ.

eng: But they went out and proclaimed the message everywhere. The Lord worked with them, confirming the word by the signs associated with them.
local: Be lɛ ka a poturbo yi chen tɩ mʊɔlɔ a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ jie jaa. Be lɛ ka a Soro 'mataa tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ ba, ka a wʊ nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ anyã ban tʊ̃nɔ̃ wiil ɩka a wʊ 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ ɩn yelmãɛ.

eng: He said to them, Im very sad. Its as if Im dying. Stay here and keep alert.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, N tɩɛr sɔ̃ɔ naa, tɩ nyɛtãan kũu. Yɩ jãnɛ a kana gu ma tɩ na yɩ taa guro ɛ.

eng: Then he went a short distance farther and fell to the ground. He prayed that, if possible, he might be spared the time of suffering.
local: Wʊ chen naa a niem bãlãa tɩ lo kpa tẽe sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn ɩka ala ɩ a tara naa ɩfʊ kɔ̃ɔ, wʊ vɛ̃ ka a sɔ̃ɔ nyãna po wʊ.

eng: He said, Abba, Father, for you all things are possible. Take this cup of suffering away from me. However—not what I want but what you want.
local: Wʊ 'yɛr naa, N Sãa, yele jaa ba gar a fʊ maalfʊ ɛ. De a dɔɔyɛ ŋmele nyãna bar a n sɛ̃, tɩchɛ a ta tu maa bɔfʊ ɛ, a tu a lɛ fʊ̃ʊ na bʊɔrɔ.

eng: He came and found them sleeping. He said to Peter, Simon, are you asleep? Couldnt you stay alert for one hour?.
local: Wʊ lɩɛb chen a poturbo sɛ̃ tɩ nyɛ ka a ɩ ba gur naa. Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a Simon Pita, Simon, fʊ guro naa ɔ? A fʊ kʊ̃ lɛ tʊ̃ɔ jɛ̃ bãlãa gu ma ɛ?.

eng: Stay alert and pray so that you wont give in to temptation. The spirit is eager, but the flesh is weak.
local: Yɩ jɛ̃ gu chɛlɛ, na yɩ sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn ka a bɛɛrkaa ta wa tʊ̃ɔ yɩ ɛ. A yɩ tɩɛr 'lɔ bʊɔrɔ naa tɩchɛ ka a yɩ ãgãma ba tʊɔr ɛ.

eng: Again, he left them and prayed, repeating the same words.
local: Wʊ lɛ chen naa tɩ puor a Nãaŋmɩn a lɛ nɛ nɩ wʊ ju.

eng: And, again, when he came back, he found them sleeping, for they couldnt keep their eyes open, and they didnt know how to respond to him.
local: Wʊ lɩɛb wa nyɛ ba go ka a ɩ ba gur naa bojũu gʊ̃ɔ kpɛ ba naa. Baa bɔ̃ɔ ŋmɩnɛ ban lɛ 'yɛr kʊ a Yesu ɛ.

eng: He came a third time and said to them, Will you sleep and rest all night? Thats enough! The time has come for the Human One to be betrayed into the hands of sinners.
local: Wʊ lɩɛb wa a ba sɛ̃ a gbɛɛ ata sɔ̃ɔ wa sʊʊr ba, Yɩ chɛnɛ na gã guro na yɩ pɛ̃nɛ ɔ? A sɛɛ naa! A sɔ̃ɔ ta naa. Nyɛ, ba gar 'yɔ̃ɔ a Nɩsaal Bie kʊ a yelbe-ɩrbɛ!.

eng: Get up! Lets go! Look, here comes my betrayer.
local: Yɩ vaa ir, ka sɩ chen a ba sɛ̃. Nyɛ, a n gagaraa na waara.

eng: Suddenly, while Jesus was still speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, came with a mob carrying swords and clubs. They had been sent by the chief priests, legal experts, and elders.
local: Yesu na chɛnɛ 'yɛrɛ lɛ ka Judas na ɩ a wʊ poturo pie nɩ ayi ãsʊɔ tɩ daasãa, nɩ nɩbɛ na tara sɔbɛrɛ nɩ dakʊrsɩ waara nɩ a Yesu nyɔɔfʊ jie. A Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ lɛ tʊ̃ ba.

eng: His betrayer had given them a sign: Arrest the man I kiss, and take him away under guard.
local: Judas dɔ̃ɔ wiil a nɩbɛ mana ɩka, A 'lɔ ɩn na tɩ puor mʊʊr a kɔtɩr yɩ nyɔɔ a 'lɔ, na yɩ bɔ̃ɔ tara wʊ chiin.

eng: As soon as he got there, Judas said to Jesus, Rabbi! Then he kissed him.
local: Ban wa ta, Judas pɔɔn chen a Yesu sɛ̃ tɩ 'yɛr, Wiwile! Tɩ puor wʊ nɩ mʊʊrfʊ.

eng: Then they came and grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
local: Ka a bala wʊn chiin nyɔɔ a Yesu.

eng: One of the bystanders drew a sword and struck the high priests slave and cut off his ear.
local: Be lɛ ka a bala ãsʊɔ na ara gbʊr a Yesu vʊ̃ɔ a wʊ sʊɔ chɛn a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nãa gbãgbaa toor ŋmaa.

eng: Jesus responded, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, like an outlaw?.
local: Ka a Yesu sʊʊr ba, A mɔ̃ nɩbier lɛ n ɩ ka yɩ waan sɔbɛrɛ, nɩ dakʊrsɩ a n nyɔɔfʊ jie ɔ?.

eng: Day after day, I was with you, teaching in the temple, but you didnt arrest me. But let the scriptures be fulfilled.
local: Bɩbir jaa lɛ n ma dɔ̃ɔ be a yɩ sɛ̃, na n wile yɩ a Puorfʊ Yir pɔ, tɩ yaa nyɔɔ ma ɛ. Tɩchɛ a ɩ naa a lɛ ɩka a tu a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn gãn na 'yɛr.

eng: And all his disciples left him and ran away.
local: Be lɛ a Yesu poturbo ba jaa bar wʊ tɩ jo.

eng: One young man, a disciple, was wearing nothing but a linen cloth. They grabbed him.
local: Pɔlbile kɔ̃ɔ mɩ na vaar fɔbaa tɛɛ turo nɩ a Yesu, ba nyɔɔ wʊ naa.

eng: but he left the linen cloth behind and ran away naked.
local: Ka wʊ mɩɩr a fɔbaa 'yɔ̃ɔ ba tɩ jon sal'mʊɔr.

eng: They led Jesus away to the high priest, and all the chief priests, elders, and legal experts gathered.
local: Be lɛ ba chiin a Yesu a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nãa yir, ka a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ ba jaa nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ 'matãan a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe, na ɩ a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ nɩbɛrɛ, wa lɔ̃ɔ taa.

eng: Peter followed him from a distance, right into the high priests courtyard. He was sitting with the guards, warming himself by the fire.
local: Pita mɩ turo naa a puor jaa na wʊ wa tɩ kpɛ a bɔɔrlo nãa yir bɛchaar pɔ, na wʊ 'mataa jãnɛ nɩ a bala na kara a Puorfʊ Yir uoro bũu.

eng: The chief priests and the whole Sanhedrin were looking for testimony against Jesus in order to put him to death, but they couldnt find any.
local: Ka a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ nɩbɛrɛ na chɛ bʊɔrɔ yele kɔ̃ɔ na wiil ɩka Yesu sɛɛn kũu, tɩ baa nyɛ ɛ.

eng: Many brought false testimony against him, but they contradicted each other.
local: Nɩyɔɔ mɩ ŋman jirsɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ, tɩchɛ a ba 'yɛrbie ŋma taa poe.

eng: Some stood to offer false witness against him, saying.
local: Ka a bamɩne mɩ ir ŋmaa jire 'yɛr ɩka.

eng: We heard him saying, I will destroy this temple, constructed by humans, and within three days I will build another, one not made by humans.
local: A ba sɛ̃, wʊ 'yɛr naa ɩka, A Puorfʊ Yir nyãna a nɩrɛ nũu na mɛ, ɩn ŋmɛr wʊ naa tɩ na n de ber ata mɛn 'lɔ na ba ɩ nɩrɛ nũu bʊ̃mɩɛraa ɛ. .

eng: But their testimonies didnt agree even on this point.
local: Tɩchɛ a ba 'yɛr mɩ ba ɩ bʊ̃'yen ɛ.

eng: Then the high priest stood up in the middle of the gathering and examined Jesus. Arent you going to respond to the testimony these people have brought against you.
local: Be lɛ ka a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nãa ir ara tɩ sʊʊr a Yesu, Faa 'yɛrɛ 'yɛr sɔɔrɔ ba bɩɩ? Bo dãasɩɛsɩ lɛ anyãna ban dire dɔɔlɔ fʊ. A ɩn yelmãɛ ɔ.

eng: But Jesus was silent and didnt answer. Again, the high priest asked, Are you the Christ, the Son of the blessed one.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Yesu ɩ jaa gbul, waa 'yɛr sɔɔ wʊ ɛ. Ka a bɔɔrlo nãa lɛ sʊʊr wʊ go, Fʊ̃ʊ lɛ ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn 'lɔ sɩn 'Yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ Bie na ɩ a Nɩ-iraa ɔ.

eng: Jesus said, I am. And you will see the Human One sitting on the right side of the Almighty and coming on the heavenly clouds.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Maa lɛ. Yɩn nyɛ naa a Nɩsaal Bie na jãnɛ a Tʊ̃ɔfʊ Sʊɔ nũsʊ̃ɔ loor. Yɩ mɩ na lɛ nyɛn wʊn waara a julõjuur pɔ.

eng: Then the high priest tore his clothes and said, Why do we need any more witnesses?.
local: Ka a bɔɔrlo nãa tɔɔ a fuu chɩɛr nɩ suur, tɩ sʊʊr, Bãa lɛ sɩn lɛ bʊɔrɔ nɩrɛ ɩka wʊ lɛ 'yɛr a wʊ 'yɛr go?.

eng: Youve heard his insult against God. What do you think? They all condemned him. He deserves to die.
local: Yɩn mãɛ wõ naa a lɛ wʊn fere. Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ yɩ tɩɛr? Ka ba jaa 'yɛr ɩka wʊ sɛɛn kũu.

eng: Some began to spit on him. Some covered his face and hit him, saying, Prophesy! Then the guards took him and beat him.
local: Be lɛ a bamɩne piel chɩrɛ nataar 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ wʊ, ka a bamɩne mɩ pɔɔ a wʊ nɩbie tɩ ŋme wʊ, tɩ sʊʊr wʊ, Wiil sɩ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ŋme fʊ. Ka a bala mɩ na guro a Puorfʊ Yir mɩ ŋme ŋme wʊ.

eng: Meanwhile, Peter was below in the courtyard. A woman, one of the high priests servants, approached.
local: Pita jãnɛ naa a be a bɛchaar pɔ, ka a bɔɔrlo nãa gbãgbapɔɔbile ãsʊɔ wa wa.

eng: and saw Peter warming himself by the fire. She stared at him and said, You were also with the Nazarene, Jesus.
local: Wʊn wa nyɛ a Pita na uoro a bũu, wʊ kaa wʊ naa lɛ̃ɛɛ tɩ 'yɛr, Fʊ mɩ dɔ̃ɔ turo a Yesu na yi a Najaretɩ.

eng: But he denied it, saying, I dont know what youre talking about. I dont understand what youre saying. And he went outside into the outer courtyard. A rooster crowed.
local: Ka a Pita chɩɩr tɩ 'yɛr, N ba bɔ̃ɔ a lɛ fʊn 'yɛrɛ par ɛ. Tɩ na wʊ tɔɔ chen tɩ ara a dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ.

eng: The female servant saw him and began a second time to say to those standing around, This man is one of them.
local: A pɔɔbile na wa nyɛ a Pita a be, wʊ lɛ 'yɛr kʊ a nɩbɛ na ara a be, A nyã mɩ ɩ naa a ba ãsʊɔ.

eng: But he denied it again. A short time later, those standing around again said to Peter, You must be one of them, because you are also a Galilean.
local: Ka a Pita lɛ chɩɩr go. A ba kor ɛ, tɩ a bala na ara gbʊr a Pita mɩ 'yɛr, Sɩrɛ jaa lɛ, fʊ ɩ naa a ba ãsʊɔ, bojũu Galilee lɛ fʊ mɩ yi.

eng: But he cursed and swore, I dont know this man youre talking about.
local: Ka a Pita pɔrɔ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a wʊ mãɛ, tɩ pɔrɔ kʊrɔ ba 'yɛrɛ ɩka, N ba bɔ̃ɔ a daba nyã 'yɛr yɩn 'yɛr ɛ.

eng: At that very moment, a rooster crowed a second time. Peter remembered what Jesus told him, Before a rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times. And he broke down, sobbing.
local: Ajienaa lɛ ka a nɔraa mɩ tɔ̃ɔ kõ a gbɛɛ ayi sʊɔ. Ka a Pita pãa tɩɛrtɔ a lɛ a Yesu na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Fʊn chɩɩr ma naa gbɛɛ ata, tɩ ka a nɔrdɔ̃ɔ kõ gbɛɛ ayi. Pita na wa tɩɛr bɔ̃ɔn a lɛ, ka wʊ kõ yɔɔ jaa.

eng: At daybreak, the chief priests—with the elders, legal experts, and the whole Sanhedrin—formed a plan. They bound Jesus, led him away, and turned him over to Pilate.
local: A wɛr na wa chaar bibio, a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ ba jaa nɩ a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ nɩbɛrɛ na chɛ, lɔ̃ɔn guor taa lũ a Yesu tara wʊ chiin tɩ kʊ a nãa Pilate.

eng: In those days Caesar Augustus declared that everyone throughout the empire should be enrolled in the tax lists.
local: A ber ala pɔ, a paal Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Siisa Agustus 'yɛr naa ɩka ba sɔr a nɩbɛ ba jaa na be a Worom paal pɔ.

eng: This first enrollment occurred when Quirinius governed Syria.
local: A sɔrfʊ nyã lɛ ɩ a sɔrdɔ̃ɔ ban sɔr a nɩbɛ ka a Kiriniyus mɩ ɩ a Siriya nãa.

eng: Everyone went to their own cities to be enrolled.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa kul a wʊ mãɛ tẽe ka ban tɩ sɔr wʊ.

eng: Pilate questioned him, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Ka a Pilate sʊʊr wʊ, Fʊ̃ʊ lɛ ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ nãa ɔ? Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Ɔ̃ɔ, a ɩn a lɛ fʊn 'yɛr.

eng: The chief priests were accusing him of many things.
local: Ka a bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a Yesu yuor.

eng: Pilate asked him again, Arent you going to answer? What about all these accusations.
local: Be lɛ a Pilate sʊʊr a Yesu, Faa 'yɛrɛ 'yɛr jaa ɛ, nyɛ lɛ ban sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ fʊ a yuor jã.

eng: But Jesus gave no more answers, so that Pilate marveled.
local: Tɩchɛ Yesu ba haa nɛɛ ɛ, ka a 'maa a Pilate nɛɛ.

eng: During the festival, Pilate released one prisoner to them, whomever they requested.
local: A mɩ ɩ naa a ba tʊ̃mɔ̃ lɛ ka bala wa lɔ̃ɔ taa dire a Gõl Bar Difʊ, a ba nãa ma ir nɩrɛ 'lɔ a nɩbɛ na bʊɔrɔ a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ bar.

eng: A man named Barabbas was locked up with the rebels who had committed murder during an uprising.
local: Barabasɩ mɩ dɔ̃ɔ pʊɔ a nɩbɛ bamɩne pɔ, na dɔ̃ɔ ir jɛɛr nɩ a paal nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na ba kʊ nɩbɛ, ka ba nyɔɔr ba pɔɔ.

eng: The crowd pushed forward and asked Pilate to release someone, as he regularly did.
local: Nɩyɔɔ wa naa a Pilate sɛ̃ wa sʊɔr wʊ ɩka, wʊ ɩ kʊ ba a lɛ wʊn ma ɩ.

eng: Pilate answered them, Do you want me to release to you the king of the Jews.
local: Ka a Pilate sʊʊr ba, Yɩ bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka n ir a Juu nɩbɛ nãa kʊ yɩ bɩɩ.

eng: He knew that the chief priests had handed him over because of jealousy.
local: Pilate bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka a nyuur ban tara nɩ a Yesu lɛ vɛ̃ ban nyɔɔ wʊ waan a wʊ sɛ̃.

eng: But the chief priests stirred up the crowd to have him release Barabbas to them instead.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ dʊ̃ a nɩbɛ toor ɩka, ba 'yɛr ɩka, wʊ yuo a Barabasɩ bar.

eng: Pilate replied, Then what do you want me to do with the one you call king of the Jews.
local: Ka a Pilate sʊʊr ba go, Tɩ n ɩ ŋmɩnɛ nɩ a 'lɔ yɩn bʊɔlɔ a Juu nɩbɛ nãa.

eng: They shouted back, Crucify him.
local: Ka ba tãn, Kpa wʊ a daa ju.

eng: Pilate said to them, Why? What wrong has he done? They shouted even louder, Crucify him.
local: Be lɛ ka a Pilate sʊʊr ba, Bãa wʊ ɩ? Bo tʊ̃dɛɛr lɛ wʊ tʊ̃? Tɩchɛ ka ba tãna kpɛ̃ɔ jaa, Kpa wʊ a daa ju.

eng: Pilate wanted to satisfy the crowd, so he released Barabbas to them. He had Jesus whipped, then handed him over to be crucified.
local: Pilate na bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ pɛl a nɩbɛ pʊɔ jũu, wʊ yuo naa a Barabasɩ kʊ ba. Wʊ vɛ̃ naa ka ba fɔb a Yesu yɔɔ jaa, tɩ de wʊ kʊ a sojasɩ ɩka, ba tɩ kpa wʊ a daa ju, ka wʊ kpi.

eng: The soldiers led Jesus away into the courtyard of the palace known as the governors headquarters, and they called together the whole company of soldiers.
local: Be lɛ a sojasɩ tara a Yesu kpɛ̃n a nãa yir kpɛ̃ɛ 'lɔ pɔ, tɩ bʊɔl a ba taaba sojasɩ lɔ̃ɔ taa.

eng: They dressed him up in a purple robe and twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on him.
local: Ba den fuu jɩɛ nãa fuu kaar su wʊ, na ba lɛ de gʊʊr vil nãa jupil kaar su wʊ.

eng: They saluted him, Hey! King of the Jews.
local: Tɩ pãa piel laara wʊ, Yãan yooo, Juu nɩbɛ nãa.

eng: Again and again, they struck his head with a stick. They spit on him and knelt before him to honor him.
local: Tɩ ma de daa ŋmen wʊ a jũu tɩ chɩrɛ nataar 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ wʊ. Ba gbur dumo a wʊ niem puoro wʊ.

eng: When they finished mocking him, they stripped him of the purple robe and put his own clothes back on him. Then they led him out to crucify him.
local: Ban wa la wʊ a nɩtɔ̃ wa baar, ba yaa naa a fuu 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ bar, tɩ de a wʊ mãɛ fuu su wʊ, na ba pãa de wʊ chiin ɩka ba tɩ kpa wʊ a daa ju.

eng: Simon, a man from Cyrene, Alexander and Rufus father, was coming in from the countryside. They forced him to carry his cross.
local: A sɔr pɔ, ba nyɛn Simon na yi Sirenɩ tẽe pɔ, Aleksanda nɩ Worofʊs sãa lɛ. Mʊɔ pɔ lɛ wʊ yi tala, ka ba nyɔɔ wʊ fɩr ka wʊ de a Yesu daa tuo.

eng: They brought Jesus to the place called Golgotha, which means Skull Place.
local: Ba chiin naa a Yesu tɩ tãn a be ban bʊɔlɔ a Yesu nɩbɛ kɔkɔr pɔ, Gɔlgɔta. A par lɛ ɩka, Julaa Jie.

eng: They tried to give him wine mixed with myrrh, but he didnt take it.
local: Ka ba pãa de tãɩ kɔ̃ɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ a dãa pɔ, ɩka wʊ nyũ, tɩ wʊ tɔr.

eng: They crucified him. They divided up his clothes, drawing lots for them to determine who would take what.
local: Be lɛ ba kpa wʊ a daa ju. Ba põ naa a wʊ fɔɔr na ba ŋme gbãm bɔ̃ɔ a ala ãsʊɔ jaa na na de.

eng: It was nine in the morning when they crucified him.
local: Bibipɔl lɛ ba kpa wʊ.

eng: The notice of the formal charge against him was written, The king of the Jews.
local: Ba sɛb naa a yele 'lɔ jũu ban kʊn wʊ mar a daa jũu ɩka, A JUU NƖBƐ NÃA.

eng: They crucified two outlaws with him, one on his right and one on his left.
local: Ba mɩ kpan nãnyisɩ ayi a dabãbãnsɩ mɩ ju, ka ãsʊɔ be a Yesu nũsʊ̃ɔ loor ka a ãsʊɔ mɩ be a wʊ gʊba loor.

eng: People walking by insulted him, shaking their heads and saying, Ha! So you were going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, were you?.
local: A bala na tala a be a wʊ kpafʊ jie tʊrɔ wʊ, vavire a ba jur 'yɛrɛ, Wooo, a fʊ̃ʊ nyã na 'yɛr ɩka fʊn sɔ̃ɔ naa a Nãaŋmɩn Puorfʊ Yir nyã bar tɩ mɛ wʊ a ber ata.

eng: Save yourself and come down from that cross.
local: wa suu a daa ju, na fʊ faa a fʊ mãɛ.

eng: In the same way, the chief priests were making fun of him among themselves, together with the legal experts. He saved others, they said, but he cant save himself.
local: Lɛ nɩ wʊ ju lɛ, a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe mɩ laara wʊ, na ba 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ taa, Nyɛ, wʊ faan bamɩne, tɩ wʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ a wʊ mãɛ faa ɛ!.

eng: Since Joseph belonged to Davids house and family line, he went up from the city of Nazareth in Galilee to Davids city, called Bethlehem, in Judea.
local: Ala lɛ so ka a Josefʊ mɩ na yi a Najaretɩ tẽe na be a Galilee wɛr pɔ, na wʊ chen a Judiya wɛr pɔ, a Nãa David tẽe ban bʊɔlɔ a Bɛtɩlɛhɛm, bojũu a Nãa David yir lɛ wʊ yi tɩ lɛ ɩ a wʊ bal nɩrɛ.

eng: He went to be enrolled together with Mary, who was promised to him in marriage and who was pregnant.
local: Josefʊ tara na a Mɛɛr na tara a pʊɔ wʊn bʊɔrɔ a kulfʊ 'mataa chiin ka ban tɩ sɔr ba.

eng: Let the Christ, the king of Israel, come down from the cross. Then well see and believe. Even those who had been crucified with Jesus insulted him.
local: Vɛ̃ a 'lɔ na 'yɛr ɩka, 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a Nɩ-iraa, na wʊ ɩ a Isɩral nɩbɛ nãa, suu a daa ju a sɩ kaa, tɩ na sɩ pãa sɔɔ. A bala mɩ ban kpa gbʊr wʊ, lɛ lɛ ba mɩ tʊ wʊ.

eng: While they were there, the time came for Mary to have her baby.
local: A lɛ ban be a Bɛtɩlɛhɛm ka a Mɛɛr bie dɔɔfʊ daar ta.

eng: She gave birth to her firstborn child, a son, wrapped him snugly, and laid him in a manger, because there was no place for them in the guestroom.
local: Ka wʊ dɔɔ a pɔɔsarbie ka wʊ ɩ dabile a wʊ vil wʊ a fɔbaa gaal a dʊ̃n mʊɔr 'wɔfʊ jie bojũu baa nyɛ jie na kpɛ a sãanba kpɛfʊ jie ɛ.

eng: Nearby shepherds were living in the fields, guarding their sheep at night.
local: Pɛchiinbɛ mɩ dɔ̃ɔ gbʊr a tẽe na ba gãn a ba piir be a mʊ̃ɔ pɔ kaara.

eng: The Lords angel stood before them, the Lords glory shone around them, and they were terrified.
local: Ka a Nãaŋmɩn malaka yi a saaju wa a ba sɛ̃, ka Nãaŋmɩn tɩɩr chaar pɔɔ ba, ka dãbãɛ kpɛ ba.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid! Look! I bring good news to you—wonderful, joyous news for all people.
local: Tɩchɛ a malaka 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ ta joro dãbãɛ ɛ, 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ lɛ n waan a yɩ sɛ̃, an ɩn chɩlfʊ kpɛ̃ɛ kʊ a nɩbɛ ba jaa.

eng: Your savior is born today in Davids city. He is Christ the Lord.
local: A dɩna tãsɔɔ ba dɔɔ naa a Fafaara a yɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ a Nãa David tẽe pɔ. Wʊ ɩ naa a sɩ Soro nɩ a Nɩ-iraa.

eng: This is a sign for you: you will find a newborn baby wrapped snugly and lying in a manger.
local: Jãnɛ yɩn na de bɔ̃ɔn wʊ nɩ nyã. Yɩn nyɛ naa a bipɩla ban vil a fɔbaa gaal wʊ a dʊ̃n mʊɔr 'wɔfʊ jie.

eng: Suddenly a great assembly of the heavenly forces was with the angel praising God. They said.
local: Ajienaa a saaju malakasɩ yɔɔ wa naa wa 'matãan a malaka 'lɔ a ba dãna a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛrɛ nɩ.

eng: Glory to God in heaven, and on earth peace among those whom he favors.
local: Tɩɩr wʊ ta a Nãaŋmɩn na be a saaju, ka nyã'maar mɩ be a nɩbɛ bala na pɛlɛ a wʊ pʊɔ a wɛr pɔ.

eng: When the angels returned to heaven, the shepherds said to each other, Lets go right now to Bethlehem and see whats happened. Lets confirm what the Lord has revealed to us.
local: A malakasɩ na wa bar ba tɩ lɩɛb do a saaju, ka a pɛchiinbɛ 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ taa, Yɩ ɩ a sɩ chen a Bɛtɩlɛhɛm tẽe pɔ, tɩ kaa a yelnyɩnɛ na ɩ ka a Nãaŋmɩn tʊ̃ 'yɛr sɩ.

eng: They went quickly and found Mary and Joseph, and the baby lying in the manger.
local: Be lɛ ba pɔɔ chen tɩ nyɛ a Mɛɛr nɩ a Josefʊ nɩ a bipɩla na gã a dʊ̃n mʊɔr 'wɔfʊ jie.

eng: When they saw this, they reported what they had been told about this child.
local: A lɛ ban wa nyɛ wʊ, ba 'yɛr naa a lɛ a malaka na 'yɛr kʊ ba a bibile yele.

eng: Everyone who heard it was amazed at what the shepherds told them.
local: A bala ba jaa na wõ a lɛ a pɛchiinbɛ na 'yɛr a bie 'yɛr kʊ ba, ka nɛɛr 'maa ba jaa.

eng: Mary committed these things to memory and considered them carefully.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Mɛɛr 'yɔ̃ɔ a anyãna a wʊ pʊɔm tara tɩɛrɛ.

eng: The shepherds returned home, glorifying and praising God for all they had heard and seen. Everything happened just as they had been told.
local: A pɛchiinbɛ lɩɛb chen a piir jie tɩ dãna Nãaŋmɩn na ba pɛɛrɛ wʊ a ala a jaa ban wõ tɩ nyɛ a lɛ a malaka na 'yɛr kʊ ba.

eng: When eight days had passed, Jesus parents circumcised him and gave him the name Jesus. This was the name given to him by the angel before he was conceived.
local: A ber aniin na wa ta, ba ŋmaa wʊn a yʊɔr tɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ a yuor Yesu, a yuor nyã lɛ a malaka 'yɛr kʊ a Mɛɛr tɩ ka wʊ wa tara a pʊɔ.

eng: When the time came for their ritual cleansing, in accordance with the Law from Moses, they brought Jesus up to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord. .
local: Mɛɛr na wa baar a padɔɔr a lɛ a Mosesɩ nɛɛ na 'yɛr, Josefʊ nɩ a Mɛɛr den a bie chiin a Jerusalɛm na tɩ kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: Its written in the Law of the Lord, Every firstborn male will be dedicated to the Lord.
local: Nɩtãa lɛ a Soro na 'yɛr, Yɩ de dabil dɔ̃ɔ jaa kʊ a Soro.

eng: They offered a sacrifice in keeping with whats stated in the Law of the Lord, A pair of turtledoves or two young pigeons .
local: Ba den a bɔɔrlo bomo ala a Soro na 'yɛr ɩka, Ŋmama ayi bɩɩ papa pɔlɔ ayi tɩ pɔl a ba mãɛ.
