eng: A man named Simeon was in Jerusalem. He was righteous and devout. He eagerly anticipated the restoration of Israel, and the Holy Spirit rested on him.
local: Daba kɔ̃ɔ dɔ̃ɔ be a Jerusalɛm ban bʊɔlɔ Simeon na wʊ ɩ nɩtor na waa baala Nãaŋmɩn ɛ. Wʊ mɩ dɔ̃ɔ chɛlɛ a Isɩral nɩbɛ faafʊ bɩbir ka a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ be a wʊ sɛ̃.

eng: The Holy Spirit revealed to him that he wouldnt die before he had seen the Lords Christ.
local: A Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ ɩka wʊn nyɛ naa a Nɩ-iraa 'lɔ a Soro na tʊ̃ nɩ nɩbir tɩ kpi.

eng: Led by the Spirit, he went into the temple area. Meanwhile, Jesus parents brought the child to the temple so that they could do what was customary under the Law.
local: A Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ vɛ̃n ka Simeon chen be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir bɛchaar pɔ, ka a ɩ a Josefʊ nɩ a Mɛɛr mɩ waan naa a ba bie Yesu a be na wa ir a nɛɛ.

eng: From noon until three in the afternoon the whole earth was dark.
local: A mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ na wa ara a gar ju sãlsɔɔ, a wɛr wʊ jaa sɔb naa wa tɩ tãn a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ balfʊ sɔ̃ɔ.

eng: At three, Jesus cried out with a loud shout, Eloi, eloi, lama sabachthani, which means, My God, my God, why have you left me.
local: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ pɔ lɛ, ka a Yesu chɩɩr bʊɔl, Eloi, eloi, lama sabakatanɩ. A par lɛ a Yesu nɩbɛ kɔkɔr pɔ ɩka, N Nãaŋmɩn, n Nãaŋmɩn, bo lɛ so ka fʊ lɩɛb puor 'yɔ̃ɔ ma.

eng: After hearing him, some standing there said, Look! Hes calling Elijah.
local: A bala na ara gbʊr a be wõ na ba 'yɛr, Yɩ chɛlɛ! Elaija lɛ wʊ bʊɔlɔ.

eng: Someone ran, filled a sponge with sour wine, and put it on a pole. He offered it to Jesus to drink, saying, Lets see if Elijah will come to take him down.
local: Ka a daba ãsʊɔ jo chen tɩ 'mɔɔ sapɔ 'lɔr a dãmiir amɩne pɔ, na wʊ chab daa tur a Yesu ɩka wʊ mʊʊr, tɩ na wʊ lɛ 'yɛr, Yɩ vɛ̃ a sɩ kaara, Elaija na wa yɔɔ wʊ naa bɩɩ.

eng: But Jesus let out a loud cry and died.
local: Be lɛ a Yesu chɩɩr tɩ ŋmaa a vʊʊr.

eng: The curtain of the sanctuary was torn in two from top to bottom.
local: Ka a Juu Nɩbɛ Puorfʊ Yir pɔ, ban lũ a fɔbaa var a die chɩɛr a saaju tɩ gbem a par.

eng: When the centurion, who stood facing Jesus, saw how he died, he said, This man was certainly Gods Son.
local: A sojasɩ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na ara a be a Yesu niem na wõ a wʊ chɩɩrfʊ, tɩ nyɛ a lɛ mɩ wʊn ŋmaa a vʊʊr, wʊ 'yɛr naa, Yelmãɛ jaa, a daba nyã ɩ naa a Nãaŋmɩn Bie.

eng: Some women were watching from a distance, including Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of James and Joses, and Salome.
local: A pɔbɔ bamɩne lɔ̃ɔn Mɛɛr na yi Magdala tẽe pɔ, nɩ Salome, nɩ a Mɛɛr na ɩ a Jemesɩ bile, nɩ Josesɩ ma mɩ ara na tɛɛr jaa tɩ kaar wʊ.

eng: Simeon took Jesus in his arms and praised God. He said.
local: Ka a Simeon de a bie 'yɔ̃ɔ a wʊ nũu pɔ tɩ puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan 'yɛrɛ.

eng: When Jesus was in Galilee, these women had followed and supported him, along with many other women who had come to Jerusalem with him.
local: A Galilee pɔ, a pɔbɔ banyãna lɛ turo wʊ, na ba sʊ̃nɔ̃ wʊ. Ka a pɔbɔ bamɩne mɩ na 'matãan wʊ wa a Jerusalɛm, mɩ be be.

eng: Now, master, let your servant go in peace according to your word.
local: Tʊ̃ɔnɔ̃ sʊɔ, nɩtãa lɛ fʊn tɩr a fʊ nɛɛ kʊ a maa na ɩ a fʊ gbãgbaa, fʊn tʊ̃ɔ bar ma sɔr nɩ nyã'maar.

eng: Since it was late in the afternoon on Preparation Day, just before the Sabbath.
local: A daar 'lɔ ɩ naa a ba Chɔɔrfʊ Bɩbir ban na chɔɔr a ba mãɛ, bojũu a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ wa mʊr, a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir piel naa. A jãanʊɔr na wa tara.

eng: because my eyes have seen your salvation.
local: Bojũu n nɩbir nyɛ naa a fafaara.

eng: Joseph from Arimathea dared to approach Pilate and ask for Jesus body.
local: a Josefʊ na yi a Arimatɩya tẽe pɔ, na ɩ a nɩkpɛ̃ɛ ãsʊɔ ban 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃, tɩ mɩ dɔ̃ɔ chɛlɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Nãalʊ̃ʊ, nyɔɔn nyãa chen a Pilate sɛ̃, tɩ sʊɔr a Yesu ãgãn ɩka wʊ ũu.

eng: You prepared this salvation in the presence of all peoples.
local: 'lɔ fʊn chɔɔr ka nɩrɛ jaa nyɛ wʊ.

eng: Pilate wondered if Jesus was already dead. He called the centurion and asked him whether Jesus had already died.
local: Pilate na wa wõ ɩka a Yesu kpi naa, a 'maa wʊn nɛɛ. Be lɛ wʊ bʊɔl a sojasɩ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ ka wʊ wa, ka wʊ sʊʊr wʊ, A Yesu kpin bɩɩ.

eng: Its a light for revelation to the Gentiles and a glory for your people Israel.
local: Wʊn ɩn chãa wiil a nɩbɛ bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, tɩ na wʊ ɩ tɩɩr kʊ a fʊ nɩbɛ Isɩral.

eng: His father and mother were amazed by what was said about him.
local: Ka a Yesu sãa nɩ a ma nɛɛ 'maa a lɛ a Simeon na 'yɛr a wʊ yele.

eng: When he learned from the centurion that Jesus was dead, Pilate gave the dead body to Joseph.
local: Ka a sojasɩ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ 'yɛr, Ɔ̃ɔ, wʊ kpi naa. Ka a Pilate sɔɔ ɩka a Josefʊ wʊ tɩ yɔɔ a Yesu ãgãn.

eng: Simeon blessed them and said to Mary his mother, This boy is assigned to be the cause of the falling and rising of many in Israel and to be a sign that generates opposition.
local: Ka a Simeon pãa 'yɛr nɛvɩla 'yɔ̃ɔ ba, tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a Mɛɛr na ɩ a ma, A bie nyãna lɛ Nãaŋmɩn kʊ a Isɩral nɩbɛ, ka ba yɔɔ na ŋme gbɛr lo tɩ ir, na wʊ ɩ jãnɛ ka ban 'yɛrɛ kpɛrɛ nɩ wʊ.

eng: He bought a linen cloth, took Jesus down from the cross, wrapped him in the cloth, and laid him in a tomb that had been carved out of rock. He rolled a stone against the entrance to the tomb.
local: Josefʊ dãn fɔbaa na wʊ tɩ yɔɔ a Yesu ãgãn vil wʊ chiin tɩ ũu, a yaa ban chɩ kʊsɩr pɔ. Ba bil kʊsɩr wa pɔɔ a yaa nɛɛ.

eng: so that the inner thoughts of many will be revealed. And a sword will pierce your innermost being too.
local: Nɩbɛ yɔɔ pʊtɩɛr na gãn chãa pɔ, ka a fʊ̃ʊ mɩ pʊɔ sɔ̃ɔ fɩka sʊɔ ban chɔr fʊ.

eng: Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joses saw where he was buried.
local: A Mɛɛr na yi a Magdala, nɩ a Mɛɛr na ɩ a Josesɩ ma, nyɛ naa a jie 'lɔ ban ũu wʊ.

eng: There was also a prophet, Anna the daughter of Phanuel, who belonged to the tribe of Asher. She was very old. After she married, she lived with her husband for seven years.
local: Pɔɔ na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ ben be ban bʊɔlɔ Ana na wʊ ɩ Fanuwɛl bie na wʊ ɩ Aserɩ bal nɩrɛ, na wʊ ɩ pɔɔ nyɔ̃ɔ. Wʊ kul sɩrɛ maal yuomo ayopõi.

eng: She was now an -year-old widow. She never left the temple area but worshipped God with fasting and prayer night and day.
local: Ka a sɩrɛ kpi tɩ wʊ ɩ a pɔɔkʊɔr maal yuomo lɩjaanaar nɩ anaar . Waa gala a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir ɛ, tɩchɛ wʊ ma ben be na wʊ ma gal bʊ̃diir tɩ sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn tãsɔɔ nɩ a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ.

eng: She approached at that very moment and began to praise God and to speak about Jesus to everyone who was looking forward to the redemption of Jerusalem.
local: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ wʊ wa a ba sɛ̃ tɩ dãn Nãaŋmɩn tɩ na wʊ 'yɛr a bie 'yɛr kʊ a bala jaa na chɛlɛ a Jerusalɛm faafʊ bɩbir.

eng: When Mary and Joseph had completed everything required by the Law of the Lord, they returned to their hometown, Nazareth in Galilee.
local: A lɛ a Josefʊ nɩ a Mɛɛr na wa ir a nɛɛ maal a lɛ a Soro na bin, ba lɩɛb kul a Najaretɩ na ɩ a ba tẽe na be a Galilee wɛr pɔ.

eng: The child grew up and became strong. He was filled with wisdom, and Gods favor was on him.
local: Be lɛ a bie tɩ nʊ̃ɔnɔ̃ nɩ fɔ̃ɔ na wʊ sɛɛn yɛ̃ ka a Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ be a wʊ sɛ̃.

eng: Each year his parents went to Jerusalem for the Passover Festival.
local: Yuon jaa a sãa naa ma ma chen a Jerusalɛm tɩ lɔ̃ɔ na ba di a Gõl Bar Difʊ.

eng: When he was years old, they went up to Jerusalem according to their custom.
local: Yesu na wa nyɛ yuomo pie nɩ ayi ba tara wʊn don a Difʊ nyã jie nɩtãa lɛ ban ma ɩrɛ.

eng: After the festival was over, they were returning home, but the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem. His parents didnt know it.
local: A Gõl Bar Difʊ ber na wa baar, ba lɩɛb kulo a ba tẽe tɩchɛ a Yesu bar a Jerusalɛm ka baa bɔ̃ɔn ɛ.

eng: Supposing that he was among their band of travelers, they journeyed on for a full day while looking for him among their family and friends.
local: Ba tɩɛr ɩka wʊ taa pɔɔ a ba nɩbɛ kulo nɩ tɩ chen bɩbir bʊ̃'yen baa nyɛ wʊ ɛ, na ba pãa yʊɔrɔ bʊɔrɔ wʊ a ba nɩbɛ nɩ a ba nɩbɔ̃ɔnsɩ nɩ a ba barmɩne sɛ̃.

eng: When they didnt find Jesus, they returned to Jerusalem to look for him.
local: Ban ba wa nyɛ wʊ ɛ, ba lɩɛb chen a Jerusalɛm a wʊ bɔfʊ jie.

eng: After three days they found him in the temple. He was sitting among the teachers, listening to them and putting questions to them.
local: A ber ata daar lɛ a ba tɩ nyɛ wʊ a be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir bɛchaar pɔ, ka wʊ jãnɛ a wiwiilbe chara pɔ chɛlɛ a lɛ ban 'yɛrɛ na wʊ sʊrɔ ba sʊʊrfʊsɩ.

eng: Everyone who heard him was amazed by his understanding and his answers.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa na wõ a lɛ wʊn ire a 'yɛrsɩ par a chɔ̃ chɔ̃ nɛɛ 'maa ba naa.

eng: When his parents saw him, they were shocked. His mother said, Child, why have you treated us like this? Listen! Your father and I have been worried. Weve been looking for you.
local: A sãa nɩ a ma na wa nyɛ wʊ a ba nɛɛ mɩ 'maa ba naa ka a ma sʊʊr, Pa n bie, bo lɛ fʊ maal sɩ a nɩtɔ̃, ka maa nɩ a fʊ sãa yɔbɔ fʊ lɛ̃ɛɛ wa faa.

eng: Jesus replied, Why were you looking for me? Didnt you know that it was necessary for me to be in my Fathers house.
local: Ka Yesu sʊʊr a ma, Bãa 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ yɩ yʊɔrɔ bʊɔrɔ ma? Yaa bɔ̃ɔ ɩka ɩn be naa a n Sãa yir ɛ.

eng: But they didnt understand what he said to them.
local: Tɩchɛ baa bɔ̃ɔ a anyãna wʊn 'yɛr kʊ ba par ɛ.

eng: Jesus went down to Nazareth with them and was obedient to them. His mother cherished every word in her heart.
local: A wʊ ir pɔɔ ba, a ba pãa kul a Najaretɩ a wʊ tɩ wõnõ a ba toor, tɩchɛ ka a ma 'yɔ̃ɔ a 'yɛr anyãna a jaa a wʊ pʊɔm.

eng: Jesus matured in wisdom and years, and in favor with God and with people.
local: Ka a Yesu nʊ̃ɔnɔ̃ nɩ yɛ̃ nɩ a kpɛ̃ɛ ka wʊ yele nʊ̃mɔ̃ a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ nɩ a nɩbɛ sɛ̃.

eng: In the fifteenth year of the rule of the emperor Tiberius—when Pontius Pilate was governor over Judea and Herod was ruler over Galilee, his brother Philip was ruler over Ituraea and Trachonitis, and Lysanias was ruler over Abilene.
local: Yuon pie nɩ anũu lɛ a Tiberiyus Siisa di a nãkpɛ̃ɛ ka a Pontius Pilate mɩ ɩ a Judiya paal nɩkpɛ̃ɛ ka a Hɛrɔd mɩ di a Galilee nãa ka a wʊ yɛbɛ Filip mɩ ɩ a Ituriya nɩ a Tɩrakonɩtɩs nãa. Ka a Lisaniyasɩ mɩ di a Abilenɩ nãa.

eng: during the high priesthood of Annas and Caiaphas—Gods word came to John son of Zechariah in the wilderness.
local: Anasɩ nɩ Kayafasɩ bala lɛ ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ. Ka a Nãaŋmɩn de a wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a Jekariya bidaba Jɔɔn be a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ.

eng: John went throughout the region of the Jordan River, calling for people to be baptized to show that they were changing their hearts and lives and wanted God to forgive their sins.
local: Wʊ yɔn a tẽn ala a jaa na jilʊ̃ a Jordan man na wʊ 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ a nɩbɛ, Yɩ lɩɛb a yɩ tɩɛr na yɩ so a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ, ka Nãaŋmɩn vɛ̃ a yɩ yeldɛbɛ kʊ yɩ.

eng: This is just as it was written in the scroll of the words of Isaiah the prophet, A voice crying out in the wilderness: Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight.
local: Nɩtãa lɛ a Isaiya na sɛb a wʊ gãn pɔ, Nɩrɛ 'yɛrɛ naa be a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ, Yɩ chɔɔr a sɩ Soro sɔr, na yɩ maal a wʊ sɔr ka wʊ tor.

eng: Every valley will be filled, and every mountain and hill will be leveled. The crooked will be made straight and the rough places made smooth.
local: Na yɩ 'mʊr a kʊlsɩ, na yɩ ŋmɛr a tɔ̃n nɩ a gbẽgbẽnsɩ ka a jãtaa. Yɩ maal a sɔgɔ̃rsɩ a jaa ka a tor, na yɩ maal a sɔr nyara nyara ka wʊ sal.

eng: All humanity will see Gods salvation. .
local: Nɩsaal bʊrɔ ba jaa na nyɛn Nãaŋmɩn faafʊ. .

eng: Then John said to the crowds who came to be baptized by him, You children of snakes! Who warned you to escape from the angry judgment that is coming soon?.
local: Ka a nɩyɔɔ bala na waara a Jɔɔn sɛ̃ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ sofʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ, ka wʊ sʊʊr ba, Yɩn waa bibiir, an lɛ 'yɛr kʊ yɩ ɩka yɩ jo yi a Nãaŋmɩn suur na waara a yɩ sɛ̃?.

eng: Produce fruit that shows you have changed your hearts and lives. And dont even think about saying to yourselves, Abraham is our father. I tell you that God is able to raise up Abrahams children from these stones.
local: Yɩ vɛ̃ ka yɩ befʊ wiil ɩka yɩ lɩɛb a yɩ tɩɛr. Yɩ ta chɛnɛ 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ a yɩ mãɛ ɩka, Abraham lɛ ɩ a sɩ sãa, n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka Nãaŋmɩn na tʊ̃ɔn maal a kusɩbɛ anyãna a ba lɩɛb ɩ a Abraham bibiir.

eng: The ax is already at the root of the trees. Therefore, every tree that doesnt produce good fruit will be chopped down and tossed into the fire.
local: A 'lar dɔɔl a tɩɛ nyɩbɛ ju, tɩɛ jaa ba wa wɔ̃nɔ̃ wɔ̃ sʊ̃n ɛ, ban ŋmaa wʊn lɔb 'yɔ̃ɔ bũu pɔ.

eng: The crowds asked him, What then should we do.
local: Ka a nɩyɔɔ bala sʊʊr wʊ, Ŋmɩnɛ sɩn pãa ɩ.

eng: He answered, Whoever has two shirts must share with the one who has none, and whoever has food must do the same.
local: Ka Jɔɔn 'yɛr kʊ ba, Ɩ̃sʊɔ jaa na tara fuusɩ ayi, wʊ ir ãsʊɔ kʊ a wʊ tɔ sʊɔ na ba tara ɛ, nyã mɩ na tara bʊ̃diir wʊ ɩ a lɛ.

eng: Even tax collectors came to be baptized. They said to him, Teacher, what should we do.
local: Lõpodierbe mɩ wan a Jɔɔn sɛ̃ ɩka wʊ so ba a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ, na ba mɩ sʊʊr wʊ, Wiwile sɩ mɩ ɩ ŋmɩnɛ.

eng: He replied, Collect no more than you are authorized to collect.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ ta ma de dɔɔl a lɛ ban ɩka yɩ de ɛ.

eng: Soldiers asked, What about us? What should we do? He answered, Dont cheat or harass anyone, and be satisfied with your pay.
local: Sojasɩ bamɩne mɩ wan wa sʊʊr wʊ, Sɩn mɩ, sɩ ɩ ŋmɩnɛ? Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ ta fɩr nɩrɛ de libir ɛ, yɩ ta mɩ ŋmaa jir kara dɔɔl nɩrɛ ɛ, na yɩ vɛ̃ ka a yɩ sãyar sɛɛn yɩ.

eng: The people were filled with expectation, and everyone wondered whether John might be the Christ.
local: Ka a nɩbɛ mɩ na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ toor chɛlɛ, pãa tɩɛrɛ ɩka a Jɔɔn lɛ tɔ̃ɔ ɩ a Nɩ-iraa.

eng: John replied to them all, I baptize you with water, but the one who is more powerful than me is coming. Im not worthy to loosen the strap of his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.
local: A Jɔɔn 'yɛr kʊ ba, Maa 'lɔ kʊ̃ɔ lɛ n suoro yɩ, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ na gar ma na wa naa, a wʊ nafɔɔr mibe gba, n ba sɛɛ ɩka n for ɛ. 'Lɔ lɛ na de Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ nɩ bũu son yɩ.

eng: The shovel he uses to sift the wheat from the husks is in his hands. He will clean out his threshing area and bring the wheat into his barn. But he will burn the husks with a fire that cant be put out.
local: A ŋmayɩɛl naa ben a wʊ nũu pɔ, a wʊ na yɩɛl a mʊ̃ɩ a vafʊ jie na wʊ kpaar a bie 'yɔ̃ɔ a boor pɔ tɩ chʊm a sɔ̃sɔɔr nɩ bũu 'lɔ na ba tara kpĩinfʊ bar.

eng: With many other words John appealed to them, proclaiming good news to the people.
local: Jɔɔn kpãa ban nɩ yelyɔɔ na wʊ 'yɛr a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr kʊ ba.

eng: But Herod the ruler had been criticized harshly by John because of Herodias, Herods brothers wife, and because of all the evil he had done.
local: Tɩchɛ a nãa Hɛrɔd na de a wʊ yɛbɛ pɔɔ Hɛrɔdiyas kul, Jɔɔn 'yɛr wʊ naa ɩka a ba tara sɔr ka wʊ kul wʊ ɛ, tɩ 'yɛr wʊ a dɛbɛ amɩne wʊn ɩ.

eng: He added this to the list of his evil deeds: he locked John up in prison.
local: Hɛrɔd lɛ maal dɛɛr wʊn vɛ̃ ka ba nyɔɔ Jɔɔn tɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ.

eng: When everyone was being baptized, Jesus also was baptized. While he was praying, heaven was opened.
local: Ban ba nyɔɔ a Jɔɔn pɔɔ sɛrɛ ɛ, wʊ so naa a nɩbɛ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ na wʊ mɩ so a Yesu a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ. Ka a Yesu sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn ka a saaju yuo.

eng: and the Holy Spirit came down on him in bodily form like a dove. And there was a voice from heaven: You are my Son, whom I dearly love; in you I find happiness.
local: Ka a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ de papa ãgãn suu wa 'mar a Yesu ju, tɩ a kɔkɔr yi be a saaju 'yɛr, Fʊ̃ʊ lɛ ɩ a n Bidanʊ̃naa ka fʊ yele pɛl a n pʊɔ.

eng: Jesus was about years old when he began his ministry. People supposed that he was the son of Joseph son of Heli.
local: Yesu nyɛn yuon lɩjɛr nɩ pie tɩ piel a wʊ tʊ̃mɔ̃, ka nɩbɛ tɩɛr ɩka wʊ ɩ naa a Josefʊ bidaba, na ɩ a Heli bidaba.

eng: son of Matthat son of Levi son of Melchi son of Jannai son of Joseph.
local: Matatɩ bidaba, Levi bidaba, Melki bidaba, Janai bidaba, Josefʊ bidaba.

eng: son of Mattathias son of Amos son of Nahum son of Esli son of Naggai.
local: Matatiyasɩ bidaba, Amosɩ bidaba, Nahum bidaba, Esili bidaba, Nagai bidaba.

eng: son of Maath son of Mattathias son of Semein son of Josech son of Joda.
local: Maat bidaba, Matatiyasɩ bidaba, Semeyin bidaba, Josekɩ bidaba, Joda bidaba.

eng: son of Joanan son of Rhesa son of Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel son of Neri.
local: Joanan bidaba, Aresa bidaba, Jerubabel bidaba, Sealtɩɛl bidaba, Neri bidaba.

eng: son of Melchi son of Addi son of Cosam son of Elmadam son of Er.
local: Melki bidaba, Adi bidaba, Kosam bidaba, Elmadam bidaba, Erɩ bidaba.

eng: son of Joshua son of Eliezer son of Jorim son of Matthat son of Levi.
local: Josua bidaba, Eliyeja bidaba, Jorim bidaba, Matatɩ bidaba, Levi bidaba.

eng: son of Simeon son of Judah son of Joseph son of Jonam son of Eliakim.
local: Simeon bidaba, Juda bidaba, Josefʊ bidaba, Jonam bidaba, Eliyakim bidaba.

eng: son of Melea son of Menna son of Mattatha son of Nathan son of David.
local: Melea bidaba, Mena bidaba, Matata bidaba, Natan bidaba, David bidaba.

eng: son of Jesse son of Obed son of Boaz son of Sala son of Nahshon.
local: Jesɩ bidaba, Obed bidaba, Boajɩ bidaba, Salmon bidaba, Nason bidaba.

eng: son of Amminadab son of Admin son of Arni son of Hezron son of Perez son of Judah.
local: Aminadab bidaba, Aram bidaba, Hejoron bidaba, Perejɩ bidaba, Juda bidaba.

eng: son of Jacob son of Isaac son of Abraham son of Terah son of Nahor.
local: Jekɔb bidaba, Isaakɩ bidaba, Abraham bidaba, Tera bidaba, Nahor bidaba.

eng: son of Serug son of Reu son of Peleg son of Eber son of Shelah.
local: Serugu bidaba, Wɛru bidaba, Peleg bidaba, Eber bidaba, Sela bidaba.

eng: son of Cainan son of Arphaxad son of Shem son of Noah son of Lamech.
local: Kainan bidaba, Afakadɩ bidaba, Sem bidaba, Nowa bidaba, Lamekɩ bidaba.

eng: son of Methuselah son of Enoch son of Jared son of Mahalalel son of Cainan.
local: Metusela bidaba, Enok bidaba, Jared bidaba, Mahalaleel bidaba, Kenan bidaba.

eng: son of Enos son of Seth son of Adam son of God.
local: Enosɩ bidaba, Setɩ bidaba, Adama bidaba, Nãaŋmɩn bidaba.

eng: Many people have already applied themselves to the task of compiling an account of the events that have been fulfilled among us.
local: Nɩtãa lɛ a nɩyɔɔ na mɔ̃ man a lɛ na ɩ a sɩ ber anyã pɔ.

eng: They used what the original eyewitnesses and servants of the word handed down to us.
local: a lɛ ɩn yelmãɛ, ba dɔ̃ɔ be be na ba nyɛn nɩbir a pielfʊ, na ba ɩ a 'yɛr nyã gbãgbaar.

eng: Now, after having investigated everything carefully from the beginning, I have also decided to write a carefully ordered account for you, most honorable Theophilus.
local: A lɛ jũu n baraa Teofilusɩ, n mɩ mɔ̃n vɩɛr a 'yɛr tɩ tãn a pielfʊ nyɛ ka a ɩ yelmãɛ lɛ, ka n bʊɔrɔ ɩka n sɛb a lɛ na tu taa kʊ fʊ.

eng: I want you to have confidence in the soundness of the instruction you have received.
local: Ka fʊ na tʊ̃ɔ bɔ̃ɔ ɩka a lɛ ban wiil fʊ ɩn yelmãɛ.

eng: During the rule of King Herod of Judea there was a priest named Zechariah who belonged to the priestly division of Abijah. His wife Elizabeth was a descendant of Aaron.
local: A Judiya Nãa Hɛrɔd sɔ̃ɔ, a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrloro ãsʊɔ ben be ban bʊɔlɔ Jekariya wʊ pʊɔ naa a Abija gbul pɔ ka a wʊ pɔɔ yuor di Elijabetɩ na yi a Aaron bʊrɔ pɔ.

eng: They were both righteous before God, blameless in their observance of all the Lords commandments and regulations.
local: Ba jaa ayi yele tor Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ ka ba turo a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nɛɛ na 'yɛr na baa tara chɛfʊ ɛ.

eng: They had no children because Elizabeth was unable to become pregnant and they both were very old.
local: Tɩchɛ baa tara bie ɛ, bojũu a Elijabetɩ abbaa ka 'lɔ naa sɩrɛ jaa nyɔ̃ɔ.

eng: One day Zechariah was serving as a priest before God because his priestly division was on duty.
local: A mɩ lʊɔr wa ta a Jekariya ka wʊ̃n tʊ̃ a bɔɔr lofʊ tʊ̃mɔ̃ kʊ Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: Following the customs of priestly service, he was chosen by lottery to go into the Lords sanctuary and burn incense.
local: Gbãm lɛ ba gba ir wʊ nɩtãa lɛ a ba bɔɔrlo tʊ̃mɔ̃ na be, ka wʊn ma kpɛ a puorfʊ yir tɩ chʊm a tãɩ nyũu vɩla ala.

eng: All the people who gathered to worship were praying outside during this hour of incense offering.
local: Ka a sɔ̃ɔ na wa ta ɩka wʊ chʊm a tãɩ nyũu vɩla anyãna, a nɩbɛ ara naa a yõo puoro.

eng: An angel from the Lord appeared to him, standing to the right of the altar of incense.
local: Be lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn malaka wa dũu wʊ be a tãɩ nyũu vɩla chʊmfʊ jie a nũsʊ̃ɔ jie.

eng: When Zechariah saw the angel, he was startled and overcome with fear.
local: Jekariya na wa nyɛ wʊ a lɛ, pɩla ɔɔ wʊ naa ka dãbãɛ kpɛ wʊ.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Zechariah. Your prayers have been heard. Your wife Elizabeth will give birth to your son and you must name him John.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a malaka 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Jekariya ta joro dãbãɛ ɛ, Nãaŋmɩn wõn a fʊ sʊɔrfʊ, a fʊ pɔɔ Elijabetɩ na dɔɔn dabile ka fʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ a wʊ yuor Jɔɔn.

eng: He will be a joy and delight to you, and many people will rejoice at his birth.
local: A wʊ yele na ɩn nʊ̃ɔ kpɛ fʊ ka fʊ chɩlɛ ka nɩyɔɔ mɩ chɩlɛ a wʊ dɔɔfʊ jũu.

eng: for he will be great in the Lords eyes. He must not drink wine and liquor. He will be filled with the Holy Spirit even before his birth.
local: An ɩ wʊn ɩn kpɛ̃ɛ a Nãaŋmɩn niem, wʊ kʊ̃ nyũ dãa ban 'yɔ̃ɔ dãbɩl ɛ, bojũu Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na sɛɛ wʊn be a ma pʊɔm.

eng: He will bring many Israelites back to the Lord their God.
local: Wʊn lɩɛb naa a Isɩral nɩbɛ yɔɔ jie waan a ba Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃.

eng: He will go forth before the Lord, equipped with the spirit and power of Elijah. He will turn the hearts of fathers back to their children, and he will turn the disobedient to righteous patterns of thinking. He will make ready a people prepared for the Lord.
local: Wʊn den a sɩ Soro nie nɩ a sɩɛ nɩ a kpɛ̃ɔ 'lɔ na dɔ̃ɔ be a Elaija 'yɔ̃ɔ, na wʊ lɩɛb sãamɩne ka ba tara a ba bibiir tɩɛr. Ka bawõnsɩ mɩ nyɛ yɛ̃ na ba maala yeltorsɩ na wʊ vɛ̃ ka nɩbɛ mɩ chɔɔr ba mãɛ gun a sɩ Soro.

eng: Zechariah said to the angel, How can I be sure of this? My wife and I are very old.
local: Ka a Jekariya sʊʊr a malaka, Ŋmɩnɛ ɩn ɩrɛ wa sɔɔ a nɩtɔ̃ fʊn 'yɛr? Maa nɩ n pɔɔ jaa kor naa.

eng: The angel replied, I am Gabriel. I stand in Gods presence. I was sent to speak to you and to bring this good news to you.
local: Ka a malaka 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Maa lɛ a Gabɩrɛl na ma ara a Nãaŋmɩn niem, 'lɔ lɛ tʊ̃ ma nɩ a 'yɛr nyã ɩka n wa 'yɛr a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyã kʊ fʊ.

eng: Know this: What I have spoken will come true at the proper time. But because you didnt believe, you will remain silent, unable to speak until the day when these things happen.
local: A lɛ jũu fʊn ɩn wõo fʊ kʊ̃ lɛ tʊ̃ɔ 'yɛr 'yɛr ɛ, wa tãn a bɩbir 'lɔ a anyãna na ɩ bojũu faa sɔɔ de a 'yɛr ala na wa ɩ a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ na sɛɛ ɛ.

eng: Meanwhile, the people were waiting for Zechariah, and they wondered why he was in the sanctuary for such a long time.
local: A nɩbɛ bala na be a yõo chɛlɛ a Jekariya wʊ̃n kor a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir, ka chĩcham kpɛ ba.

eng: When he came out, he was unable to speak to them. They realized he had seen a vision in the temple, for he gestured to them and couldnt speak.
local: Be lɛ ka wʊ wa yi tɩ wʊ kʊ̃ lɛ tʊ̃ɔ 'yɛr 'yɛr ɛ, ka a nɩbɛ bɔ̃ɔn ɩka wʊ nyɛn nyɛfʊ kɔ̃ɔ bojũu nuur kaalfʊ lɛ wʊ mana nɩ ba tɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ 'yɛr ɛ.

eng: When he completed the days of his priestly service, he returned home.
local: A lɛ a wʊ tʊ̃mɔ̃ bɩbie na wa baar be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir, wʊ lɩɛb kul a wʊ yir.

eng: Afterward, his wife Elizabeth became pregnant. She kept to herself for five months, saying.
local: A anyã puor lɛ ka wʊ pɔɔ Elijabetɩ tara pʊɔ na wʊ sɔɔl ŋmɛrsɩ anũu.

eng: This is the Lords doing. He has shown his favor to me by removing my disgrace among other people.
local: Na wʊ pãa 'yɛrɛ, Nyɛ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na maal ma, a wʊ maal vɩla bɩbir vɩɛ naa ka wʊ ir ma a vĩ yin a nɩbɛ niem bar.

eng: When Elizabeth was six months pregnant, God sent the angel Gabriel to Nazareth, a city in Galilee.
local: Elijabetɩ pʊɔ na wa ta ŋmɛrsɩ ayʊɔb, Nãaŋmɩn tʊ̃ naa a Gabɩrɛl a tẽe na be a Galilee paal pɔ ban bʊɔlɔ Najaretɩ.

eng: to a virgin who was engaged to a man named Joseph, a descendant of Davids house. The virgins name was Mary.
local: be lɛ pɔɔsarbile kɔ̃ɔ be, ka daba ban bʊɔlɔ Josefʊ na ɩ a Nãa David yɔ̃ɔ ŋmaa baraa na kul ka a pɔɔbile yuor di Mɛɛr.

eng: When the angel came to her, he said, Rejoice, favored one! The Lord is with you.
local: A malaka chen a wʊ sɛ̃ tɩ 'yɛr, N puorfʊ naa, fʊ ɩn nɩ'yɔ̃ɔ naa, Nãaŋmɩn ben a fʊ sɛ̃.

eng: She was confused by these words and wondered what kind of greeting this might be.
local: Ka dãbãɛ nyɔɔ a Mɛɛr nɩ a puorfʊ nyã bʊrɔ, na wʊ sʊrɔ wʊ mãɛ a puorfʊ nyã par.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Mary. God is honoring you.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a malaka 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Ta joro dãbãɛ ɛ, fʊ ɩn nɩ'yɔ̃ɔ naa Nãaŋmɩn niem.

eng: Look! You will conceive and give birth to a son, and you will name him Jesus.
local: Fʊn tara na pʊɔ dɔɔ dabile, na fʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ a wʊ yuor Yesu.

eng: He will be great and he will be called the Son of the Most High. The Lord God will give him the throne of David his father.
local: Wʊn ɩn kpɛ̃ɛ, ka ba bʊɔlɔ wʊ a Nãaŋmɩn na be a Saaju Bie. A sɩ Soro Nãaŋmɩn na den a wʊ sãa David nãalʊ̃ʊ kʊ wʊ.

eng: He will rule over Jacobs house forever, and there will be no end to his kingdom.
local: 'Lɔ lɛ na di a Jekɔb yir nãa jaa kpo kpo, a wʊ nãa kʊ̃ wa baar ɛ.

eng: Then Mary said to the angel, How will this happen since I havent had sexual relations with a man.
local: Ka a Mɛɛr sʊʊr a malaka, N ba ta daba sɛrɛ ɛ, tɩ ŋmɩnɛ ɩn ɩrɛ wa dɔɔ a bie.

eng: The angel replied, The Holy Spirit will come over you and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. Therefore, the one who is to be born will be holy. He will be called Gods Son.
local: Ka a malaka 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na wa kpɛ fʊ naa, ka a Nãaŋmɩn kpɛ̃ɔ wa ger pɔɔ fʊ a bichɛchɛ 'lɔ fʊn na dɔɔ, ban bʊɔlɔ wʊ̃n Nãaŋmɩn Bie.

eng: Look, even in her old age, your relative Elizabeth has conceived a son. This woman who was labeled unable to conceive is now six months pregnant.
local: Nyɛ a fʊ yɛbɛ Elijabetɩ gba na ɩ a pɔɔ abbaa na wʊ nyɔ̃ɔ tɩ wʊ tara na pʊɔ na dɔɔ bie, chuur ayʊɔb nɩ nyã wʊn gal.

eng: Nothing is impossible for God.
local: Yele jaa ba ɩ tuo Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ ɛ.

eng: Then Mary said, I am the Lords servant. Let it be with me just as you have said. Then the angel left her.
local: Be lɛ a Mɛɛr 'yɛr, Nyɛ, Nãaŋmɩn gbãgbaa lɛ n ɩ, a ɩ a lɛ kʊ ma fʊn 'yɛr? Ka a malaka bar wʊ tɩ chen.

eng: Mary got up and hurried to a city in the Judean highlands.
local: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ a Mɛɛr pɔɔ yi do a tɔ̃ɔ ju a Judiya tẽe pɔ.

eng: She entered Zechariahs home and greeted Elizabeth.
local: Wʊ tɩ kpɛ naa a Jekariya yir na wʊ puor a Elijabetɩ.

eng: When Elizabeth heard Marys greeting, the child leaped in her womb, and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit.
local: Elijabetɩ na wõ a Mɛɛr puorfʊ ka a bie ɔɔ lɩɛb be a wʊ pʊɔm ka a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ sɛɛ a Elijabetɩ.

eng: With a loud voice she blurted out, God has blessed you above all women, and he has blessed the child you carry.
local: Ka wʊ ŋme chɛlma 'yɛrɛ, Nãaŋmɩn sʊ̃ʊ fʊn gar a pɔbɔ ba jaa, na wʊ mɩ sʊ̃ʊ a bie 'lɔ na be a fʊ pʊɔm.

eng: Why do I have this honor, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?.
local: Tɩchɛ nyɛ 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ na kaara, ka a n Soro ma wa a n sɛ̃?.

eng: As soon as I heard your greeting, the baby in my womb jumped for joy.
local: Ɩ̃n wa wõ a fʊ puorfʊ a bie na gã a n pʊɔm ɔɔn lɩɛb nɩ pʊpɛl.

eng: Happy is she who believed that the Lord would fulfill the promises he made to her.
local: Pʊpɛl sʊɔ lɛ fʊ ɩ an ɩ fʊ sɔɔn de a lɛ a n Soro na tʊ̃, ka ba wa 'yɛr fʊ ɩka an ɩ naa a lɛ.

eng: Mary said, With all my heart I glorify the Lord!.
local: Ka a Mɛɛr 'yɛr, N pɛɛn Nãaŋmɩn nɩ a pʊɔ wʊ jaa.

eng: In the depths of who I am I rejoice in God my savior.
local: A n sɩɛ chɩlɛ naa a Nãaŋmɩn na ɩ a n Fafaara 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: He has looked with favor on the low status of his servant. Look! From now on, everyone will consider me highly favored.
local: An ɩ wʊ tɩɛrtɔ a wʊ gbãgbapɔɔbile mãɛ siirfʊ. An yi dɩna chiine, a dɔɔ tu taa ba jaa na bʊɔlɔ ma naa bɔɔsʊ̃n sʊɔ.

eng: because the mighty one has done great things for me. Holy is his name.
local: An ɩ a Tʊ̃ɔfʊ Sʊɔ lɛ maal a lɛ kʊ ma. A wʊ yuor ɩn chɛchɛ.

eng: He shows mercy to everyone, from one generation to the next, who honors him as God.
local: A wʊ nɩbaal be naa a bala na joro wʊ dãbãɛ sɛ̃, yi a yɔn tɩ tãn a yɔn suulsɩ.

eng: He has shown strength with his arm. He has scattered those with arrogant thoughts and proud inclinations.
local: A wʊ nũu tʊ̃n tʊ̃bɛrɛ, wʊ yɛ̃yɛl naa a bala na bʊɔrɔ a yuor a ba tɩɛr pɔ bar.

eng: He has pulled the powerful down from their thrones and lifted up the lowly.
local: Wʊ dɔɔn nãmɩne a ba nãa dakɔɔr jur bar, tɩchɛ na wʊ 'mɔɔr a bala na siir a ba mãɛ ka ba do saaju.

eng: He has filled the hungry with good things and sent the rich away empty-handed.
local: Wʊ kʊ naa a kɔ̃ dẽme bʊ̃sʊ̃n, tɩ dii a tara dẽme bar nũjaalsɩ.

eng: He has come to the aid of his servant Israel, remembering his mercy.
local: Wʊ sʊ̃ʊn a wʊ gbãgbaa Isɩral tɩ tɩɛrtɔ ɩka wʊ kaa Abraham nɩ a wʊ yɔn nɩbaal bɩbir jaa, nɩtãa lɛ wʊn dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr kʊ a sɩ sãamɩne.

eng: Mary stayed with Elizabeth about three months, and then returned to her home.
local: Mɛɛr jãnɛ na a Elijabetɩ sɛ̃ ŋmɛrsɩ ata tɩ lɩɛb kul a wʊ tẽe.

eng: When the time came for Elizabeth to have her child, she gave birth to a boy.
local: Elijabetɩ dɔɔfʊ bɩbir wa vɩɛ naa ka wʊ dɔɔ dabile.

eng: Her neighbors and relatives celebrated with her because they had heard that the Lord had shown her great mercy.
local: Ka a bala na kpɩɛrɛ gbʊr a Elijabetɩ nɩ a wʊ yir dẽme na wõ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na maal vɩla kʊ wʊ ba pʊɔ mɩ pɛl naa a wʊ kaar.

eng: On the eighth day, it came time to circumcise the child. They wanted to name him Zechariah because that was his fathers name.
local: A ber aniin daar ba wa naa ɩka ba wa ŋmaa a bie yʊɔr, na ba ta bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba de a sãa yuor 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ bʊɔlɔ a Jekariya.

eng: But his mother replied, No, his name will be John.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a ma ba sɔɔ ɛ, tɩ 'yɛr, A bie yuor din Jɔɔn.

eng: They said to her, None of your relatives have that name.
local: A ba 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Fʊ bal nɩrɛ jaa ba di a yuor nyã ɛ.

eng: Then they began gesturing to his father to see what he wanted to call him.
local: Tɩ na ba pãa 'lɩɩr sʊʊr a sãa a yobuor wʊn bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba 'yɔ̃ɔ a bie.

eng: After asking for a tablet, he surprised everyone by writing, His name is John.
local: Ka wʊ man ba ɩka ba kʊ wʊ bon ka wʊ sɛb, wʊn ben wa de a bon na wʊ sɛb, A bie yuor din Jɔɔn. Ka nɛɛr 'maa ba jaa.

eng: At that moment, Zechariah was able to speak again, and he began praising God.
local: Ajienaa lɛ a Jekariya nɛɛ mɩ 'laa ka a jãlbir bal ka wʊ tʊ̃ɔ 'yɛrɛ na wʊ pɛɛrɛ Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: All their neighbors were filled with awe, and everyone throughout the Judean highlands talked about what had happened.
local: Ka dãbãɛ kpɛ a bala jaa na gbʊr a be ka nɩbɛ 'yɛrɛ ka a yaar de a Judiya tẽn ala na be a tɔ̃n ju.

eng: All who heard about this considered it carefully. They said, What then will this child be? Indeed, the Lords power was with him.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa na wõ a nɩtɔ̃ a ma 'maa wʊn nɛɛ ka wʊ ma sʊʊr, Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ a bie nyã na wa kaara? Bojũu a Soro nũu dɔɔl naa a bie.

eng: Johns father Zechariah was filled with the Holy Spirit and prophesied.
local: Jekariya na ɩ a sãa, Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ sɛɛ wʊ naa ka wʊ 'yɛrɛ.

eng: Bless the Lord God of Israel because he has come to help and has delivered his people.
local: Pɛɛfʊ wʊ ta a Isɩral Nãaŋmɩn, bojũu wʊ wa naa wa faa a wʊ nɩbɛ.

eng: He has raised up a mighty savior for us in his servant Davids house.
local: Wʊ kʊ sɩn gãdaa ɩka wʊ faa sɩ na yi a wʊ gbãgbaa Nãa David yir.

eng: just as he said through the mouths of his holy prophets long ago.
local: nɩtãa lɛ wʊn dɔ̃ɔ tu a wʊ 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ chɛchɛ bala sɛ̃ a kõr jaa ka ba 'yɛr.

eng: He has brought salvation from our enemies and from the power of all those who hate us.
local: ɩka wʊ faa sɩ a sɩ dɔ̃dẽme sɛ̃, na wʊ faa sɩ a bala na laar sɩ nuur pɔ.

eng: He has shown the mercy promised to our ancestors, and remembered his holy covenant.
local: Wʊ maal a nɩtɔ̃ ɩka wʊ wiil nɩbaal kʊ a sɩ sãamɩne, na wʊ lɛ tɩɛr a wʊ nɛtɩr chɛchɛ 'lɔ.

eng: the solemn pledge he made to our ancestor Abraham. He has granted.
local: a lɛ wʊn pɔ kʊ a sɩ sãakpã Abraham.

eng: that we would be rescued from the power of our enemies so that we could serve him without fear.
local: Wʊ pɔn 'yɛr ɩka, wʊn faa sɩ naa a sɩ dɔ̃dẽme nuur pɔ, ka sɩ puoro wʊ kʊ̃ lɛ joro dãbãɛ ɛ.

eng: in holiness and righteousness in Gods eyes, for as long as we live.
local: na sɩ ɩ chɛchɛ nɩ nɩtorsɩ a wʊ niem a ber a jaa.

eng: You, child, will be called a prophet of the Most High, for you will go before the Lord to prepare his way.
local: A fʊ̃ʊ na ɩ a n bie ban bʊɔlɔ fʊ naa a saaju Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ, an ɩ fʊ̃ʊ lɛ na de a sɩ Soro nie chɔɔr a wʊ sɔr.

eng: You will tell his people how to be saved through the forgiveness of their sins.
local: na fʊ vɛ̃ ka wʊ nɩbɛ bɔ̃ɔ a faafʊ sɔr, na ba nyɛ yelbier vɛ̃ kʊfʊ.

eng: Because of our Gods deep compassion, the dawn from heaven will break upon us.
local: Nãaŋmɩn na tara nɩ sɩ nɩbaal jũu, wʊ vɛ̃ naa ka a fafaara pur chaar 'yɔ̃ɔ sɩ yi a saaju.

eng: to give light to those who are sitting in darkness and in the shadow of death, to guide us on the path of peace.
local: na wʊ chaar 'yɔ̃ɔ a bala na kpɩɛrɛ a libe pɔ, tɩ be a kũu gãagiel pĩlem, na wʊ wiil a sɩ gbɛɛ ka a de nyã'maar sɔr.

eng: The child grew up, becoming strong in character. He was in the wilderness until he began his public ministry to Israel.
local: A bie nʊ̃ɔ naa ka a sɩɛ kpɛ̃ɔ be a wʊ sɛ̃. Mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ lɛ wʊ kpɩɛrɛ tɩ tãn a bɩbir 'lɔ wʊn na wiil a wʊ mãɛ kʊ a Isɩral nɩbɛ.

eng: Jesus returned from the Jordan River full of the Holy Spirit, and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness.
local: Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ sɛɛn a Yesu na wʊ lɩɛb wʊ yin a Jordan man jie kpɛ̃n wʊ a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ.

eng: There he was tempted for forty days by the devil. He ate nothing during those days and afterward Jesus was starving.
local: Be lɛ a tɩɩ bɛɛr wʊ kaa ber lɩjaayi, waa di bon jaa a ber anyãna pɔ ɛ, ka a kɔ̃ kpɛ wʊ.

eng: The devil said to him, Since you are Gods Son, command this stone to become a loaf of bread.
local: A tɩɩ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Ala ɩ fʊ̃ʊ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Bie, 'yɛr kʊ a kʊsɩbɛ anyãna, ka a lɩɛb bʊ̃diir.

eng: Jesus replied, Its written, People wont live only by bread .
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ ɩka, a sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, Bʊ̃diir tɛɛ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ kʊ nɩrɛ nyɛvʊr ɛ.

eng: Next the devil led him to a high place and showed him in a single instant all the kingdoms of the world.
local: A tɩɩ lɛ tara wʊn don tɔ̃ɔ ju tɩ wiil wʊ a nãa di jiir a jaa na be ka a wɛr pɔ.

eng: The devil said, I will give you this whole domain and the glory of all these kingdoms. Its been entrusted to me and I can give it to anyone I want.
local: tɩ 'yɛr, Ɩn kʊ fʊ naa anyãna a jaa fɔ̃ɔ nɩ a ba nãyele, maa lɛ ba de a ba fɔ̃ɔ kʊ, ka n mɩ na de kʊ nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa ɩn bʊɔrɔ.

eng: Therefore, if you will worship me, it will all be yours.
local: Ala ɩ, fʊ gbur dumo puor ma fʊ̃ʊ lɛ so.

eng: Jesus answered, Its written, You will worship the Lord your God and serve only him. .
local: Ka Yesu 'yɛr wʊ, A sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, Nãaŋmɩn tɛɛ lɛ a sɛɛ ɩka fʊ puor, na fʊ tʊ̃nɔ̃ kʊrɔ a 'lɔ tɛɛ.

eng: The devil brought him into Jerusalem and stood him at the highest point of the temple. He said to him, Since you are Gods Son, throw yourself down from here.
local: A tɩɩ lɛ tara wʊn chiin a Jerusalɛm ka wʊ tɩ do a puorfʊ yir jie 'lɔ na do na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a Yesu, Ala ɩ fʊ̃ʊ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Bie ɔɔ lo a tẽe.

eng: for its written: He will command his angels concerning you, to protect you.
local: bojũu a sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, Nãaŋmɩn na vɛ̃ naa ka a wʊ malakasɩ wa gu fʊ taa lɛɛ.

eng: and they will take you up in their hands so that you wont hit your foot on a stone. .
local: na ba nyɔɔ fʊ nɩ a ba nuur ka a fʊ gbɛr kʊ̃ ŋme kʊsɩr ɛ. .

eng: Jesus answered, Its been said, Dont test the Lord your God. .
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a tɩɩ, a mɩ sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, Ta bɛɛr a fʊ Nãaŋmɩn kaa ɛ.

eng: After finishing every temptation, the devil departed from him until the next opportunity.
local: A tɩɩ na wa baar a bɛɛr kaa anyãna wʊ bar wʊn tɩ chen, tɩ lɛ guro nɩ sɔ̃ɔ kɔ̃ɔ wʊn lɛ wa nyɛ jie.

eng: Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit to Galilee, and news about him spread throughout the whole countryside.
local: Yesu lɩɛb kul a Galilee nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ kpɛ̃ɔ ka a wʊ yele yaar a wɛr 'lɔ wʊ jaa.

eng: He taught in their synagogues and was praised by everyone.
local: Wʊ tɩ wile naa a nɩbɛ a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ jiir ka a nɩrɛ jaa pɛɛrɛ wʊ.

eng: Jesus went to Nazareth, where he had been raised. On the Sabbath he went to the synagogue as he normally did and stood up to read.
local: Yesu lɩɛb chen a Najaretɩ be wʊn nʊ̃ɔ, a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar wʊ tɩ kpɛ̃n a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir an ɩ lɛ lɛ wʊ ma ɩrɛ na wʊ ir ara na wʊn kar gãn.

eng: The synagogue assistant gave him the scroll from the prophet Isaiah. He unrolled the scroll and found the place where it was written.
local: Ka ba de Isaiya gãn kʊ wʊ, ka wʊ nyɔɔ 'yɩɛr a jie 'lɔ ban sɛb.

eng: The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because the Lord has anointed me . He has sent me to preach good news to the poor, to proclaim release to the prisoners and recovery of sight to the blind, to liberate the oppressed.
local: Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ be naa a n 'yɔ̃ɔ, bojũu wʊ ir ma naa ɩka n tɩ 'yɛr a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ kʊ a nɔ̃n dẽme, naa mɩ tɩ 'yɛr kʊ gbãgbaar ka ban so ba mãɛ, naa 'yɛr kʊ jɔ̃n ka ba yuo, naa 'yɛr faa a bala ban gere.

eng: and to proclaim the year of the Lords favor.
local: naa 'yɛr a Nãaŋmɩn maal vɩla yuon kʊ ba.

eng: He rolled up the scroll, gave it back to the synagogue assistant, and sat down. Every eye in the synagogue was fixed on him.
local: Be lɛ wʊ pilʊ̃ a gãn de kʊ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ tɩ jãnɛ, ka a nɩbɛ ba jaa na be a lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir kpa a nɩbie 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ.

eng: He began to explain to them, Today, this scripture has been fulfilled just as you heard it.
local: Ka wʊ piel 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ ba, Dɩna a lɛ yɩn wõ a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ, a ɩ naa a lɛ.

eng: Everyone was raving about Jesus, so impressed were they by the gracious words flowing from his lips. They said, This is Josephs son, isnt it.
local: Ba jaa 'yɛrɛ naa a wʊ sʊ̃ 'yɛr ka ba nɛɛr 'maa a 'yɛr vɩɛlsɩ na yire a wʊ nɛɛ pɔ ka ba sʊrɔ taa, A ba ɩ a Josefʊ bidaba lɛ a nyã ɛ.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Undoubtedly, you will quote this saying to me: Doctor, heal yourself. Do here in your hometown what weve heard you did in Capernaum.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Sɩrɛ jaa lɛ, yɩn sʊʊr ma naa nɩ a sʊkpa nyã, Tɩtɩlɛ, tɩɩl a fʊ mãɛ, maal a ala fʊn maal a Kapenahum ka a fʊ tẽe. .

eng: He said, I assure you that no prophet is welcome in the prophets hometown.
local: A wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, ba maa de Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ a wʊ mãɛ tẽe pɔ ɛ.

eng: And I can assure you that there were many widows in Israel during Elijahs time, when it didnt rain for three and a half years and there was a great food shortage in the land.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka pɔɔkɔyɔɔ ben a Isɩral nɩbɛ wɛr pɔ a Elaija sɔ̃ɔ, a war bũu 'lɔ na dɔ̃ɔ ŋmaa a yuom ata nɩ ŋmɛrsɩ ayʊɔb a wɛr wʊ jaa.

eng: Yet Elijah was sent to none of them but only to a widow in the city of Zarephath in the region of Sidon.
local: Tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn ba tʊ̃ a Elaija a pɔɔkɔɛ ba nyã ãsʊɔ jaa sɛ̃ ɛ, tɩ tʊ̃ wʊ a pɔɔkʊɔr na be a Jarefat na be a Sidon wɛr pɔ.

eng: There were also many persons with skin diseases in Israel during the time of the prophet Elisha, but none of them were cleansed. Instead, Naaman the Syrian was cleansed.
local: A Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ Elisa sɔ̃ɔ, kɔ̃kɔ̃n mɩ ben a Isɩral nɩbɛ wɛr pɔ tɩ ãsʊɔ jaa ba sa ɛ, ka Naaman na yi a Siriya wɛr pɔ ba lɛ ɛ.

eng: When they heard this, everyone in the synagogue was filled with anger.
local: A nɩyɔɔ bala na be a lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir 'lɔ pɔ, ka suur kpɛ ba jaa.

eng: They rose up and ran him out of town. They led him to the crest of the hill on which their town had been built so that they could throw him off the cliff.
local: Ba ir da wʊ yin a tẽe pɔ don a tɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ ju a ba tẽe na mɛ bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba daa wʊ lɔb a tɔ̃ɔ koro pɔ.

eng: But he passed through the crowd and went on his way.
local: Tɩchɛ ka wʊ fʊɔl yi a nɩyɔɔ bala sãlsɔɔ a lɛ na wʊ chen.

eng: Jesus went down to the city of Capernaum in Galilee and taught the people each Sabbath.
local: Be lɛ wʊ suu chen a Kapenahum na be a Galilee wɛr pɔ, ka a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar ka wʊ piel wile a nɩbɛ.

eng: They were amazed by his teaching because he delivered his message with authority.
local: Ka a wʊ wiilfʊ 'maa ba nɛɛ bojũu a wʊ 'yɛr tara na kpɛ̃ɔ.

eng: A man in the synagogue had the spirit of an unclean demon. He screamed.
local: Daba mɩ ben a be a lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir na tara a dɛɛr sʊɔ, ka a sɩdɛɛr be a wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ, a wʊ ŋme chɛlma sʊʊr.

eng: Hey! What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are. You are the holy one from God.
local: Ai, a fʊ̃ʊ Yesu na yi a Najaretɩ, bo fʊ bʊɔrɔ ka a sɩ sɛ̃? Fʊ wa naa ɩka fʊ wa sɔ̃ɔ sɩ bar bɩɩ? N bɔ̃ɔ naa a nɩnyɩnɛ fʊn ɩ, fʊ ɩ naa a Nãaŋmɩn nɩchɛchɛ 'lɔ.

eng: Silence! Jesus said, speaking harshly to the demon. Come out of him! The demon threw the man down before them, then came out of him without harming him.
local: Ka Yesu tãn wʊ, Pɔɔ a nɛɛ tɩ wa yi a daba 'yɔ̃ɔ. A sɩdɛɛr daan a daba lɔb a nɩbɛ niem tɩ na wʊ yi a daba 'yɔ̃ɔ tɩ waa 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ baal ɛ.

eng: They were all shaken and said to each other, What kind of word is this, that he can command unclean spirits with authority and power, and they leave.
local: Ka a nɛɛr 'maa a nɩbɛ ka ba sʊrɔ taa, Wiilbuor mɩ nɩ nyã? Wʊ den kpɛ̃ɔ nɩ tʊ̃ɔfʊ 'yɛr kʊ a sɩdɛbɛ ka a yi.

eng: Reports about him spread everywhere in the surrounding region.
local: Ka a wʊ yele yaar de a tẽn ala na jilʊ̃ a be.

eng: After leaving the synagogue, Jesus went home with Simon. Simons mother-in-law was sick with a high fever, and the family asked Jesus to help her.
local: Yesu yi naa a lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir na wʊ chen a Simon yir. Ka ãgãn tɔ a Simon dãɛ ma ka ba sʊɔr a Yesu ɩka wʊ sʊ̃ʊ wʊ.

eng: He bent over her and spoke harshly to the fever, and it left her. She got up at once and served them.
local: Ka wʊ guun ger a pɔɔ na wʊ tãn a ãgãn tʊl ka wʊ bar a pɔɔ, ka wʊ sa na wʊ ir maal bʊ̃diir kʊ ba.

eng: When the sun was setting, everyone brought to Jesus relatives and acquaintances with all kinds of diseases. Placing his hands on each of them, he healed them.
local: A mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ na wa mʊrɔ nɩbɛ waan naa ba baalsɩ a Yesu sɛ̃, ka wʊ dɔɔl ba a wʊ nũu na wʊ sãa baal jaa bʊrɔ ban waan.

eng: Demons also came out of many people. They screamed, You are Gods Son. But he spoke harshly to them and wouldnt allow them to speak because they recognized that he was the Christ.
local: Sɩdɛbɛ yin a nɩyɔɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ tãna 'yɛrɛ, Fʊ ɩn Nãaŋmɩn Bie. Tɩchɛ ka a Yesu tãn ba ka ba ta 'yɛrɛ ɛ, an ɩ ba bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a Nɩ-iraa.

eng: When daybreak arrived, Jesus went to a deserted place. The crowds were looking for him. When they found him, they tried to keep him from leaving them.
local: A wɛr na wa chaar Yesu yin tɩ jãnɛ a jie ãsʊɔ wʊ yõ, ka a nɩbɛ yɔbɔ wʊ tɩ nyɛ a jie wʊn be ba lɛ bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ bar ba tɩ chen ɛ.

eng: But he said to them, I must preach the good news of Gods kingdom in other cities too, for this is why I was sent.
local: Tɩchɛ ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, A sɛɛ naa ɩka n 'yɛr a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ 'yɛr kʊ a tẽn anyã mɩ bojũu a lɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn tʊ̃ ma.

eng: So he continued preaching in the Judean synagogues.
local: Tɩ na wʊ chɛnɛ 'yɛrɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr be a a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ yie a Juu nɩbɛ tẽe pɔ.

eng: One Sabbath, as Jesus was going through the wheat fields, his disciples were picking the heads of wheat, rubbing them in their hands, and eating them.
local: A Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir ãsʊɔ daar, Yesu nɩ a wʊ poturbo kpɛ̃n tala a puo sãlsɔɔ, ka a wʊ poturbo piel fɔrɔ a bʊ̃bie na ba ŋmɩɛnɛ nɩ a nuur ɔɔrɔ.

eng: Some Pharisees said, Why are you breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Ka a Farasee bamɩne sʊʊr ba, Bo lɛ yɩ ɩrɛ a lɛ na ba tara sɔr a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar ɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Havent you read what David and his companions did when they were hungry?.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yaa kar a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ nyɛ a lɛ a David na dɔ̃ɔ ɩ nɩ a wʊ nɩbɛ a kɔ̃ na kpɛ ba ɛ?.

eng: He broke the Law by going into Gods house and eating the bread of the presence, which only the priests can eat. He also gave some of the bread to his companions.
local: Wʊ kpɛ̃n a Nãaŋmɩn yir tɩ de a paanʊ̃ɔ ala ban bin kʊ Nãaŋmɩn na wʊ 'wɔb an ba tara sɔr ɛ, ka a Nãaŋmɩn bɔɔrlorbo tɛɛ ba lɛ ɛ. Tɩ de amɩne mɩ kʊ a wʊ nɩbɛ.

eng: Then he said to them, The Human Oneis Lord of the Sabbath.
local: Ka a Yesu pãa 'yɛr kʊ ba, Maa Nɩsaal Bie lɛ ɩ a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Soro.

eng: On another Sabbath, Jesus entered a synagogue to teach. A man was there whose right hand was withered.
local: A Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar ãsʊɔ, wʊ ben a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir na wʊ wile, ka a daba ãsʊɔ be be a nũsʊ̃ɔ na kpi.

eng: The legal experts and the Pharisees were watching him closely to see if he would heal on the Sabbath. They were looking for a reason to bring charges against him.
local: A Faraseemɩne nɩ a nɛɛ wiwiilbe bʊɔrɔ sɔr na gar 'yɔ̃ɔ a Yesu, lɛ lɛ ba tɔɔ gbʊr kaara wʊ ɩka ba kaa ala ɩ wʊn sãan a baal a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir ɔ.

eng: Jesus knew their thoughts, so he said to the man with the withered hand, Get up and stand in front of everyone. He got up and stood there.
local: Tɩchɛ Yesu bɔ̃ɔn a lɛ ban tɩɛrɛ na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a daba 'lɔ nũu na kpi, Ir ara ka nɩrɛ jaa nyɛ fʊ. Ka a daba ir ara.

eng: Jesus said to the legal experts and Pharisees, Heres a question for you: Is it legal on the Sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save life or to destroy it.
local: Ka a Yesu pãa 'yɛr kʊ ba, N sʊʊr yɩ kaa, bo lɛ sɛɛ ɩka a maal a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar, fʊ maal vɩla bɩɩ fʊ maal dɛɛr? Fʊ kʊ nɩrɛ bɩɩ fʊ faa nɩrɛ, bɩɩ fʊ sɔ̃ɔ bar.

eng: Looking around at them all, he said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did and his hand was made healthy.
local: Wʊ kãan ba jaa gʊɔr tɩ 'yɛr a daba, Tur a fʊ nũu. A wʊ tur ka a nũu wʊ jaa sa.

eng: They were furious and began talking with each other about what to do to Jesus.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a suur kpɛ ba ka ba guoro taa a lɛ ban na ɩ a Yesu.

eng: During that time, Jesus went out to the mountain to pray, and he prayed to God all night long.
local: A ber ala ãsʊɔ pɔ, Yesu don a tɔ̃ɔ ju Nãaŋmɩn sʊɔrfʊ jie, wʊ sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn a tãsɔɔ 'lɔ wʊ jaa.

eng: At daybreak, he called together his disciples. He chose twelve of them whom he called apostles.
local: A wɛr na wa chaar a bibio, wʊ bʊɔl naa a poturbo ka ba wa a wʊ sɛ̃, ka wʊ ir a ba pɔ pie nɩ ayi bʊɔlɔ ba nɩtʊ̃nsɩ.

eng: Simon, whom he named Peter; his brother Andrew; James; John; Philip; Bartholomew.
local: A bala wʊn ir lɛ, Simon wʊn lɛ kʊ yopaalaa Pita, nɩ a wʊ yɛbɛ Andiru, Jemesɩ, Jɔɔn, Filip, Batolomi.

eng: Matthew; Thomas; James the son of Alphaeus; Simon, who was called a zealot.
local: Matiu, Tomasɩ, Jemesɩ na ɩ Alfayasɩ bidaba, Simon na mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir nɩ a wʊ wɛr yele.

eng: Judas the son of James; and Judas Iscariot, who became a traitor.
local: Jemesɩ bidaba Judas, nɩ a Judas Isɩkariyot 'lɔ lɛ wa ɩ a gagaraa.

eng: Jesus came down from the mountain with them and stood on a large area of level ground. A great company of his disciples and a huge crowd of people from all around Judea and Jerusalem and the area around Tyre and Sidon joined him there.
local: Yesu tara ban suun ka ba tɩ ara a jie 'lɔ na jãtaa, kaa poturbo yɔɔ be be, ka a nɩyɔɔ mɩ yi Judiya, Jerusalɛm, ka bamɩne mɩ yi a Tire tẽe nɩ a Sidon tẽe na be a man koor.

eng: They came to hear him and to be healed from their diseases, and those bothered by unclean spirits were healed.
local: Ba wa naa ɩka ba wa chɛl a wʊ 'yɛr tɩ wʊ sãa a ba baalsɩ. Wʊ sãan a bala ba jaa na tara a sɩdɛbɛ.

eng: The whole crowd wanted to touch him, because power was going out from him and he was healing everyone.
local: Ka a nɩyɔɔ bala dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ taa bʊɔrɔ a Yesu sɩɩrfʊ bojũu kpɛ̃ɔ yire naa a wʊ pɔ sãana a ba jaa baalsɩ.

eng: Jesus raised his eyes to his disciples and said: Happy are you who are poor, because Gods kingdom is yours.
local: Yesu kaara naa a wʊ poturbo tɩ 'yɛr, Pʊpɛl lɛ kʊ a yɩn banyãna na ɩ a nɔ̃n dẽme. Yɩn lɛ so a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ.

eng: Happy are you who hunger now, because you will be satisfied. Happy are you who weep now, because you will laugh.
local: Pʊpɛl lɛ kʊ a yɩn a kɔ̃ na kpɛ pʊ̃pãanyã yɩn lɛ na wa tɩɩ. Pʊpɛl lɛ kʊ a yɩn na kõnõ a pʊ̃pãanyã, yɩn wa laara naa.

eng: Happy are you when people hate you, reject you, insult you, and condemn your name as evil because of the Human One.
local: Pʊpɛl lɛ kʊ yɩ a nɩbɛ na wa 'laar yɩ, na ba oo yɩ bar, tɩ tʊrɔ yɩ na ba sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a yɩ yuor fɩka bʊ̃dɛɛr a maa Nɩsaal Bie jũu.

eng: Rejoice when that happens! Leap for joy because you have a great reward in heaven. Their ancestors did the same things to the prophets.
local: A bɩbir nyã wa vɩɛ yɩ chɩlɛ nɩ pʊpɛl, bojũu a yɩ sãyar waa naa na be a saaju. Lɛ lɛ a ba sãakpãmɩne dɔ̃ɔ maal a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ.

eng: But how terrible for you who are rich, because you have already received your comfort.
local: Nɛbier pɔɔn a yɩn na ɩ a tara dẽme, yɩn 'lɔ nyɛn a yɩ 'maalfʊ.

eng: How terrible for you who have plenty now, because you will be hungry. How terrible for you who laugh now, because you will mourn and weep.
local: Nɛbier kpɛ̃n a yɩn na tɩɩ a pʊ̃pãanyã kɔ̃ na kpɛ yɩ naa. Nɛbier kpɛ̃n a yɩn na laara a pʊ̃pãanyã, yɩn lɛ nɩbaal na wa kpɛ a yɩ kõnõ.

eng: How terrible for you when all speak well of you. Their ancestors did the same things to the false prophets.
local: Nɛbier kpɛ̃n a yɩn nɩbɛ jaa na bʊɔlɔ yɩ yo sʊ̃, lɛ lɛ a ba sãakpãmɩne mɩ dɔ̃ɔ bʊɔlɔ a jir 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ a yosʊ̃n.

eng: But I say to you who are willing to hear: Love your enemies. Do good to those who hate you.
local: Tɩchɛ n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ a yɩn na chɛlɛ a n 'yɛr, yɩ nʊ̃ a yɩ dɔ̃dɔ̃mɔ̃sɩ na yɩ maal vɩla kʊ a bala na 'laar yɩ.

eng: Bless those who curse you. Pray for those who mistreat you.
local: Yɩ 'yɛr nɛvɩla 'yɔ̃ɔ a bala na pɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ yɩ, na yɩ sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn kʊrɔ a bala na gere yɩ.

eng: If someone slaps you on the cheek, offer the other one as well. If someone takes your coat, dont withhold your shirt either.
local: Nɩrɛ wa 'ma fʊ tʊkpal pɔ, lɛ lɩɛb a bʊ̃'yen ka wʊ lɛ 'ma, nɩrɛ wa de a fʊ fuu kpɛ̃ɛ ta mɔ̃ɔ wʊ a fuu bile ɛ.

eng: Give to everyone who asks and dont demand your things back from those who take them.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa wa sʊɔr fʊ bon de kʊ wʊ, nɩrɛ wa de a fʊ bon taa lɛ wa sʊrɔ wʊ go ɛ.

eng: Treat people in the same way that you want them to treat you.
local: A lɛ yɩn bʊɔrɔ ɩka a nɩbɛ ba maal kʊ yɩ, yɩ mɩ maal a lɛ kʊ ba.

eng: If you love those who love you, why should you be commended? Even sinners love those who love them.
local: Ala ɩ yɩ nʊ̃ naa a bala na nʊ̃ yɩ, yobuor lɛ yɩ nyɛ? A yelbetʊ̃nbɔ gba mɩ nʊ̃n a bala na nʊ̃ ba.

eng: If you do good to those who do good to you, why should you be commended? Even sinners do that.
local: Ala ɩ yɩ maala na vɩla kʊrɔ a bala na maal yɩ a vɩla, yobuor lɛ yɩn nyɛ? A yelbetʊ̃nbɔ gba mɩ ɩrɛ naa a lɛ.

eng: If you lend to those from whom you expect repayment, why should you be commended? Even sinners lend to sinners expecting to be paid back in full.
local: Ka ala ɩ fʊ pɛ̃ɛn a bala fʊn tɩɛr ɩka ban wa yab fʊ naa, yobuor lɛ yɩn wa nyɛ? A yelbetʊ̃nbɔ ma pɛ̃ɛn a ba taaba tɩɛrɛ ɩka ban wa yab a jaa.

eng: Instead, love your enemies, do good, and lend expecting nothing in return. If you do, you will have a great reward. You will be acting the way children of the Most High act, for he is kind to ungrateful and wicked people.
local: Tɩchɛ yɩ nʊ̃ a yɩ dɔ̃dɔ̃mɔ̃sɩ na yɩ maal vɩla kʊ ba, yɩ pɛ̃ɛ ba na yɩ taa chɛlɛ ɩka ba lɛ wa yab yɩ ɛ. Ala lɛ a yɩ sãyar na waa, ka yɩ na ɩ a saaju Nãaŋmɩn bibiir, bojũu 'lɔ lɛ maala vɩla kʊrɔ a nɩhayasɩ nɩ putudẽme.

eng: Be compassionate just as your Father is compassionate.
local: Yɩ ɩ nɩbibaal dẽme nɩtãa lɛ a yɩ Sãa na ɩ a nɩbibaal sʊɔ.

eng: Dont judge, and you wont be judged. Dont condemn, and you wont be condemned. Forgive, and you will be forgiven.
local: Yɩ ta tɔɔr nɩrɛ 'yɛr ɛ, ka Nãaŋmɩn mɩ wa tɔɔr a yɩ 'yɛr ɛ. Yɩ ta 'yɔ̃ɔ nɩrɛ 'yɛr ɛ, ka Nãaŋmɩn mɩ ta 'yɔ̃ɔ yɩ 'yɛr ɛ. Yɩ vɛ̃ kʊ nɩrɛ ka Nãaŋmɩn mɩ vɛ̃ kʊ yɩ.

eng: Give, and it will be given to you. A good portion—packed down, firmly shaken, and overflowing—will fall into your lap. The portion you give will determine the portion you receive in return.
local: Yɩ de tɩr, Nãaŋmɩn mɩn kʊ yɩ naa, man vɩla tɩ dib 'yɔ̃ɔ tɩ yɛ yɛɛ ka a yaara, an kpaar dɔɔl a yɩ gbɛɛ ju. A bon 'lɔ yɩn man tɩr, 'lɔ mɩ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na man kʊ yɩ.

eng: Jesus also told them a riddle. A blind person cant lead another blind person, right? Wont they both fall into a ditch?.
local: Sʊkpa nyã lɛ a Yesu lɛ lɔb kʊ ba, Jɔ̃ɔ na tʊ̃ɔn jɔ̃ɔ tɔɔ? Ba jaa ayi kʊ̃ lo bɔɔ pɔ ɛ?.

eng: Disciples arent greater than their teacher, but whoever is fully prepared will be like their teacher.
local: Nɩrɛ na jãna wʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ gar a wʊ wiwile ɛ, tɩ 'lɔ wa jãn baar wʊn tʊ̃ɔ kaara a wʊ wiwile kaar.

eng: Why do you see the splinter in your brothers or sisters eye but dont notice the log in your own eye?.
local: Ŋmɩnɛ a fʊ kaara a dabile na be a fʊ yɛbɛ nɩbir pɔ, tɩ ba chɛlɛ a dakpɛ̃ɛ na be a fʊ̃ʊ nɩbir pɔ ɛ?.

eng: How can you say to your brother or sister, Brother, Sister, let me take the splinter out of your eye, when you dont see the log in your own eye? You deceive yourselves! First take the log out of your eye, and then you will see clearly to take the splinter out of your brothers or sisters eye.
local: Bɩɩ Ŋmɩnɛ a ɩ ka fʊ 'yɛr kʊ a fʊ yɛbɛ, n yɛbɛ vɛ̃ ka n ir a a dabile bar a fʊ nɩbir pɔ, tɩ ba ire a dakpɛ̃ɛ na be a fʊ̃ʊ nɩbir pɔ ɛ? Fʊ̃ʊ nɩbɛlɛ nyã, de nie ir a dakpɛ̃ɛ a fʊ nɩbir pɔ bar na fʊ pãa nyɛrɛ tʊ̃ɔ ir a dabile a fʊ yɛbɛ nɩbir pɔ bar.

eng: A good tree doesnt produce bad fruit, nor does a bad tree produce good fruit.
local: Tɩsʊ̃ ba wɔ̃nɔ̃ bidɛbɛ ɛ, bɩɩ tɩdɛɛr mɩ ba wɔ̃nɔ̃ bisʊ̃n ɛ.

eng: Each tree is known by its own fruit. People dont gather figs from thorny plants, nor do they pick grapes from prickly bushes.
local: Tɩɛ jaa a wʊ wɔ̃mɔ̃ lɛ ba ma de bɔ̃ɔn wʊ. Ba maa pɔr kɔ̃kɔ̃ɔ gʊtɩɛ ju ɛ, bɩɩ pɔr 'wɔ̃rɔ tamiir tɩɛ ju ɛ.

eng: A good person produces good from the good treasury of the inner self, while an evil person produces evil from the evil treasury of the inner self. The inner self overflows with words that are spoken.
local: Nɩsʊ̃ a yelsʊ̃ ala na be a wʊ pʊɔm lɛ wʊ ma 'yɛrɛ, ka a nɩdɛɛr mɩ ma 'yɛrɛ a yeldɛbɛ ala na be a wʊ pʊɔm. Ala na sɛɛ a pʊɔ lɛ a nɛɛ ma 'yɛrɛ.

eng: Why do you call me Lord, Lord and dont do what I say?.
local: Ŋmɩnɛ a yɩ bʊɔlɔ ma ɩka Soro, tɩ na yaa turo a lɛ ɩn 'yɛr ɛ?.

eng: Ill show what its like when someone comes to me, hears my words, and puts them into practice.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa na wa a n sɛ̃ na wʊ wõ a n 'yɛr tɩ maala, ɩn wiil yɩ naa a lɛ wʊn be.

eng: Its like a person building a house by digging deep and laying the foundation on bedrock. When the flood came, the rising water smashed against that house, but the water couldnt shake the house because it was well built.
local: Wʊ kaara fɩka nɩrɛ na mɩɛrɛ yir na wʊ tuu a dachin par kparaa bɔɔ tɩ pɔɔn kʊsɩr na mɛ a dachin dɔɔl. Kʊ̃ɔ wa yaar yi jilʊ̃ a yir, ka a kɔ̃sɛbɛ ŋmiere a yir tɩ waa dam ɛ, bojũu ba mɛ wʊn sʊ̃.

eng: But those who dont put into practice what they hear are like a person who built a house without a foundation. The floodwater smashed against it and it collapsed instantly. It was completely destroyed.
local: Tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ na wõ a n 'yɛr tɩ na waa maala ɛ, wʊ kaara fɩka nɩrɛ na mɛ yir dɔɔl a tãn mʊɔr ju, waa tuu tɩ mɛ ɛ. A kɔ̃sɛbɛ wa yin ŋme a dachin, ka a dachin wʊ jaa lo ŋmɛr.

eng: One day Jesus was standing beside Lake Gennesaret when the crowd pressed in around him to hear Gods word.
local: Bɩbir ãsʊɔ Yesu be naa a Genesaretɩ man nɛɛ, ka nɩyɔɔ ara jilʊ̃ wʊ chɛlɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr.

eng: Jesus saw two boats sitting by the lake. The fishermen had gone ashore and were washing their nets.
local: Wʊ nyɛn gborsɩ ayi a gogoro nɛɛ a jãnyɔɔrbɔ na bar tɩ suu pɛɛrɛ a ba lɔyaarsɩ.

eng: Jesus boarded one of the boats, the one that belonged to Simon, then asked him to row out a little distance from the shore. Jesus sat down and taught the crowds from the boat.
local: Ka wʊ kpɛ a gboro ãsʊɔ pɔ na ɩ Simon bon tɩ sʊɔr wʊ ɩka wʊ daa wʊ ka wʊ jãan a gogoro bãlãa. Wʊ pãa jãnɛ naa a gboro pɔ tɩ wile a nɩbɛ.

eng: When he finished speaking to the crowds, he said to Simon, Row out farther, into the deep water, and drop your nets for a catch.
local: Wʊn wa baar a 'yɛr ka wʊ 'yɛr a Simon, Daa a gboro chiin a kɔ̃die pɔ na fʊ tɩ lɔb a fʊ lɔyaar.

eng: Simon replied, Master, weve worked hard all night and caught nothing. But because you say so, Ill drop the nets.
local: Ka a Simon 'yɛr, Kpãkpãana, tãsɔɔ jaa lɛ sɩ lɔb lɛ̃ɛɛ tɩ saa nyɔɔ bon ɛ, tɩchɛ fʊn 'yɛr go, ɩn lɔbaa.

eng: So they dropped the nets and their catch was so huge that their nets were splitting.
local: Ban wa lɔb, ba nyɔɔr jãyɔɔ ka a lɔyaarsɩ ɩrɛ na chɩɛr.

eng: They signaled for their partners in the other boat to come and help them. They filled both boats so full that they were about to sink.
local: Ka ba 'lɩɩr a ba taaba na be a gboro ãsʊɔ pɔ, ka ba wa sʊ̃ʊ ba. Ba wa naa ka a gborsɩ a jaa ayi wa sɛɛ ka ba ɩrɛ na mʊr.

eng: When Simon Peter saw the catch, he fell at Jesus knees and said, Leave me, Lord, for Im a sinner.
local: Simon Pita na wa nyɛ a lɛ, wʊ lon kpa a Yesu niem tɩ 'yɛr, N Soro sɛr chen tɩ bar ma yelbe-ɩrɛ lɛ n ɩ.

eng: Peter and those with him were overcome with amazement because of the number of fish they caught.
local: A jãyɔɔ anyã ban nyɔɔr jũu, 'lɔ nɩ a wʊ taaba nɛɛr 'maa naa.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, were Simons partners and they were amazed too. Jesus said to Simon, Dont be afraid. From now on, you will be fishing for people.
local: Lɛ lɛ a Jemesɩ nɩ Jɔɔn na ɩ a Jebedee bibiir na ɩ a Simon taaba. Ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a Simon, Ta joro dãbãɛ ɛ, an yi dɩna chiine fʊn nyɔɔrɔ na nɩbɛ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.

eng: As soon as they brought the boats to the shore, they left everything and followed Jesus.
local: Be lɛ ba tɔɔ a gborsɩ waan a gogoro nɛɛ wa bar bomo a jaa tɩ turo a Yesu.

eng: Jesus was in one of the towns where there was also a man covered with a skin disease. When he saw Jesus, he fell on his face and begged, Lord, if you want, you can make me clean.
local: Yesu na wa be a tẽe ãsʊɔ pɔ, daba kɔ̃kɔ̃mɔ̃ baal na de a ãgãn wʊ jaa nyɛn a Yesu na wʊ lo kpa tẽe a Yesu niem tɩ sʊɔr wʊ, N Soro, fʊ̃ʊ sɔɔ, sãa ma.

eng: Jesus reached out his hand, touched him, and said, I do want to. Be clean. Instantly, the skin disease left him.
local: Ka Yesu tur a wʊ nũu sɩɩr a daba tɩ 'yɛr, N sɔɔ naa sa. Ajienaa ka a kɔ̃kɔ̃mɔ̃ baal bar wʊ.

eng: Jesus ordered him not to tell anyone. Instead, Jesus said, go and show yourself to the priest and make an offering for your cleansing, as Moses instructed. This will be a testimony to them.
local: Be lɛ a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Taa 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ nɩrɛ ɛ, tɩ chen tɩ wiil a fʊ mãɛ kʊ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrloro na fʊ ir a nɛɛ 'lɔ a Mosesɩ na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr ka a di dãasɩɛ ɩka fʊ ɩn nɩsʊ̃.

eng: News of him spread even more and huge crowds gathered to listen and to be healed from their illnesses.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a wʊ yele chɛnɛ ŋme yaar a jie wʊ jaa ka a nɩyɔɔ wa chɛlɛ a wʊ 'yɛr ka wʊ sãa a ba baalsɩ.

eng: But Jesus would withdraw to deserted places for prayer.
local: Tɩchɛ wʊ ma chɛnɛ na tɔɔ wʊ mãɛ yin chɛ̃chɛ̃ tɩ sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: One day when Jesus was teaching, Pharisees and legal experts were sitting nearby. They had come from every village in Galilee and Judea, and from Jerusalem. Now the power of the Lord was with Jesus to heal.
local: Bɩbir ãsʊɔ Yesu na wile ka a ɩ a Faraseemɩne nɩ a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe na yi a Galilee tẽbil a jaa nɩ a Judiya ka bamɩne yi a Jerusalɛm mɩ ben be jãnɛ. Ka a Soro kʊ a Yesu kpɛ̃ɔ ɩka wʊ sãan baalsɩ.

eng: Some men were bringing a man who was paralyzed, lying on a cot. They wanted to carry him in and place him before Jesus.
local: Dɔɔr bamɩne tuon nɩrɛ nɩ sɔ̃ɔ a nuur nɩ a gbɛɛ na kpi waan bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba kpɛ̃n wʊ tɩ gaal a Yesu niem.

eng: but they couldnt reach him because of the crowd. So they took him up on the roof and lowered him—cot and all—through the roof tiles into the crowded room in front of Jesus.
local: Ban ba wa nyɛrɛ sɔr a nɩyɔɔ jũu, ba don naa gar ju tɩ yuo bɔɔ tur bar ka wʊ suun a sɔ̃ɔ a nɩbɛ sãlsɔɔ tɩ gã a Yesu niem.

eng: When Jesus saw their faith, he said, Friend, your sins are forgiven.
local: Yesu na nyɛ a lɛ ban sɔɔ de, ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a baalaa, N baraa, a fʊ yeldɛbɛ vɛ̃n bar.

eng: The legal experts and Pharisees began to mutter among themselves, Who is this who insults God? Only God can forgive sins.
local: Ka a nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a Faraseemɩne pãa jɛ̃ tɩɛrɛ, An lɛ a nyã na jãnɛ fere sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a Nãaŋmɩn yuor a nɩtɔ̃? An lɛ na tʊ̃ɔ nɩrɛ yelbier vɛ̃ bar ka Nãaŋmɩn tɛɛ ba lɛ ɛ.

eng: Jesus recognized what they were discussing and responded, Why do you fill your minds with these questions?.
local: Yesu bɔ̃ɔn a lɛ ban tɩɛrɛ ka wʊ sʊʊr ba, Bo lɛ yɩ tɩɛrɛ a anyã a lɛ a yɩ pʊɔm?.

eng: Which is easier—to say, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up and walk?.
local: 'Yɛr buor lɛ ɩ kɔ̃mɔ̃kɔ̃mɔ̃? N 'yɛr ɩka a wʊ yeldɛbɛ vɛ̃n bar bɩɩ n 'yɛr ɩka wʊ ir chiine?.

eng: But so that you will know that the Human Onehas authority on the earth to forgive sins—Jesus now spoke to the man who was paralyzed,I say to you, get up, take your cot, and go home.
local: Tɩchɛ nɩtɔ̃ lɛ yɩn bɔ̃ɔ ɩka a maa Nɩsaal Bie tara na kpɛ̃ɔ a wɛr pɔ na vɛ̃ yeldɛbɛ bar. Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a dagbɛrɛ, Fʊ̃ʊ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ nɩ ir 'mɔɔ a fʊ sɔ̃ɔ kulo nɩ.

eng: Right away, the man stood before them, picked up his cot, and went home, praising God.
local: Ajienaa ka wʊ vaa ir ara a ba niem, 'mɔɔ a wʊ bʊ̃gãnaa pɛɛrɛ Nãaŋmɩn kulo nɩ.

eng: All the people were beside themselves with wonder. Filled with awe, they glorified God, saying, Weve seen unimaginable things today.
local: Ka nɩrɛ jaa nɛɛ 'maa ka ba dãna Nãaŋmɩn. Ka dãbãɛ kpɛ ba ka ba 'yɛrɛ, Sɩ nyɛn yelkal bala dɩna.

eng: Afterward, Jesus went out and saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. Jesus said to him, Follow me.
local: A anyã puor Yesu yin tɩ nyɛ lõpodiere ban bʊɔlɔ Levi na jãnɛ diere wʊ lõposɩ, ka wʊ bʊɔl wʊ, Ir turo ma.

eng: Levi got up, left everything behind, and followed him.
local: Ka a Levi vaa ir bar bomo a jaa tɩ turo wʊ.

eng: Then Levi threw a great banquet for Jesus in his home. A large number of tax collectors and others sat down to eat with them.
local: Levi maal bʊ̃diir kʊ a Yesu a be a wʊ yir na wʊ bʊɔl a taaba lõpodierbe nɩ nɩyɔɔ ka ba wa 'mataa dire.

eng: The Pharisees and their legal experts grumbled against his disciples. They said, Why do you eat and drink with tax collectors and sinners.
local: Tɩchɛ a Faraseemɩne nɩ a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe hũhũnõ nɩ a yele nyã sʊrɔ a Yesu poturbo, Bo lɛ so ka yɩ 'mataa dire nɩ a lõpodierbe nɩ a tʊ̃dɛbɛ tʊ̃nbɔ.

eng: Jesus answered, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do.
local: Ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, A ba ɩ nɩrɛ na kpɛ̃mɛ lɛ chiine tɩtɩlɛ jie ɛ, tɩchɛ baalaa lɛ ma chen.

eng: I didnt come to call righteous people but sinners to change their hearts and lives.
local: N ba wa ɩka n wa bʊɔl a nɩtorsɩ ɛ, n wa naa ɩka n wa bʊɔl a tʊ̃dɛbɛ tʊ̃nbɔ ka ba wa lɩɛb tɩɛr.

eng: Some people said to Jesus, The disciples of John fast often and pray frequently. The disciples of the Pharisees do the same, but your disciples are always eating and drinking.
local: Ka ba lɛ sʊʊr a Yesu, Ŋmɩnɛ a ɩ ka Jɔɔn poturbo nɩ a Faraseemɩne poturbo ma jɛ̃n kɔ̃ sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn, tɩchɛ ka a fʊ̃ʊ poturbo ma tɔ̃ɔ kpal a dib nɩ a nyũfʊ a lɛ a tɔ̃tɔ̃.

eng: Jesus replied, You cant make the wedding guests fast while the groom is with them, can you?.
local: Ka a Yesu sʊʊr ba, Fʊn tʊ̃ɔ maal a bala ban bʊɔl a pɔɔ paalaa jie ka ba jɛ̃n kɔ̃ a pɔɔ paalaa sɩrɛ na chɛnɛ be a ba sɛ̃ ɔ?.

eng: The days will come when the groom will be taken from them, and then they will fast.
local: Tɩchɛ bɩbir kɔ̃ɔ na wa vɩɛ naa ka a pɔɔ paalaa sɩrɛ yi a ba sɛ̃, a ber ala pɔ 'lɔ ban jɛ̃n nɩ kɔ̃.

eng: Then he told them a parable. No one tears a patch from a new garment to patch an old garment. Otherwise, the new garment would be ruined, and the new patch wouldnt match the old garment.
local: Sʊkpa nyã lɛ wʊ lɔb kʊ ba, Nɩrɛ maa ŋmaa fɔpaalaa sɛ choo fɔkoraa pɔ ɛ, 'lɔ be ɩ a lɛ a fɔpaalaa na chɩɛraa a fɔkoraa, bʊ̃paalaa nɩ bʊ̃koraa kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ 'mataa ɛ.

eng: Nobody pours new wine into old wineskins. If they did, the new wine would burst the wineskins, the wine would spill, and the wineskins would be ruined.
local: Nɩrɛ maa 'yɔ̃ɔ dãbãɩn a dãa wɔkoraa pɔ ɛ, 'lɔ be ɩ a lɛ a dãbãɩn na pur naa a wʊɔ ka a dãa kpaar ka a wʊɔ mɩ sɔ̃ɔ.

eng: Instead, new wine must be put into new wineskins.
local: Ba ma den dãbãɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ a dãa wɔpaalaa pɔ.

eng: No one who drinks a well-aged wine wants new wine, but says, The well-aged wine is better.
local: Nɩrɛ wa nyũ a dãgaa wʊ maa lɛ bʊɔrɔ a dãbãɩn ɛ, wʊ ma 'yɛr ɩka a dãgaa ɩn gar a dãbãɩn.

eng: After Jesus finished presenting all his words among the people, he entered Capernaum.
local: A lɛ a nɩbɛ na wõ a 'yɛrsɩ anyãna a Yesu na 'yɛr kʊ ba baar wʊ chen a Kapenahum.

eng: A centurion had a servant who was very important to him, but the servant was ill and about to die.
local: Be lɛ a soja nɩdiere gbãgbaa wʊn nʊ̃ yɔɔ jaa bɩɛrɛ ɩrɛ na ŋmaa vʊʊr.

eng: When the centurion heard about Jesus, he sent some Jewish elders to Jesus to ask him to come and heal his servant.
local: Soja nɩdiere na wõ a Yesu yele, wʊ tʊ̃n a Juu nɩbɛ nɩdierbe bamɩne ka ba tɩ sʊɔr a Yesu ka wʊ wa sãa a wʊ gbãgbaa.

eng: When they came to Jesus, they earnestly pleaded with Jesus. He deserves to have you do this for him, they said.
local: Ba wan a Yesu sɛ̃ wa sʊɔr wʊ yãayãabɩlɛ 'yɛrɛ, A daba nyã sɛɛ naa ɩka fʊ tɩ sʊ̃ʊ wʊ.

eng: He loves our people and he built our synagogue for us.
local: bojũu wʊ nʊ̃n a sɩ nɩbɛ na vɛ̃ ka ba mɛ a sɩ lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie.

eng: Jesus went with them. He had almost reached the house when the centurion sent friends to say to Jesus, Lord, dont be bothered. I dont deserve to have you come under my roof.
local: Yesu pɔɔ ba naa ka ba 'mataa chiine tɩ gbʊrɔ a daba yir, ka wʊ tʊ̃ a wʊ barmɩne ka ba tuor 'yɛr a Yesu ɩka. N Soro, ta bal a fʊ mãɛ ɛ, bojũu n ba sɛɛ ka fʊn wa kpɛ a n yir ɛ.

eng: In fact, I didnt even consider myself worthy to come to you. Just say the word and my servant will be healed.
local: Ala lɛ so gba maa mãɛ na ba 'la wa a fʊ sɛ̃ ɛ. Tɩchɛ 'yɛr bir tɛɛ an sãan a n gbãgbaa.

eng: Im also a man appointed under authority, with soldiers under me. I say to one, Go, and he goes, and to another, Come, and he comes. I say to my servant, Do this, and the servant does it.
local: Bojũu maa mãɛ tara na nɩbɛrɛ ɩn joro dãbãɛ, naa mɩ tara a sojasɩ ka ba mɩ be a n pĩlem. Maa 'yɛr kʊ ãsʊɔ ɩka wʊ, chen wʊ ma chen naa, bɩɩ ãsʊɔ ɩka wʊ wa, wʊ ma wa naa. N ma 'yɛr kʊ a n gbãgbaa, maal anyã, ka wʊ ma maal.

eng: When Jesus heard these words, he was impressed with the centurion. He turned to the crowd following him and said, I tell you, even in Israel I havent found faith like this.
local: A lɛ a Yesu na wõ a nɩtɔ̃ a daba nyã na ba ɩ a Juu nɩrɛ na 'yɛr, ka nɛ'maa wʊ, ka wʊ lɩɛb 'yɛr kʊ a nɩyɔɔ bala. A Isɩral nɩbɛ pɔ gba, n ba nyɛ a sɔɔfʊ nyã bʊrɔ ɛ.

eng: When the centurions friends returned to his house, they found the servant restored to health.
local: Ka a wʊ barmɩne bala wʊn tʊ̃ lɩɛb kpɛ a yir tɩ nyɛ ka a gbãgbaa sa.

eng: A little later Jesus went to a city called Nain. His disciples and a great crowd traveled with him.
local: Ala lɛ a wɛr chaar ka Yesu nɩ a wʊ poturbo 'matãan nɩyɔɔ na turo wʊ ka ba 'mantaa chen a tẽe ban bʊɔlɔ Nain.

eng: As he approached the city gate, a dead man was being carried out. He was his mothers only son, and she was a widow. A large crowd from the city was with her.
local: Wʊn wa gbʊr a tẽe dɔ̃dɔr kpɩɛraa ba tuon kũu wa yin na ɩ a pɔɔ bidabʊ̃'yen tɩ wʊ ɩ pɔɔkʊɔr. Ka a tẽe 'lɔ nɩbɛ yɔɔ mɩ pʊɔ wʊ.

eng: When he saw her, the Lord had compassion for her and said, Dont cry.
local: A lɛ a Soro na wa nyɛ a pɔɔ, nɩbaal kpɛ wʊ naa ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a pɔɔ, Ta kõnõ ɛ.

eng: He stepped forward and touched the stretcher on which the dead man was being carried. Those carrying him stood still. Jesus said, Young man, I say to you, get up.
local: Tɩ na wʊ chen tɩ sɩɩr a kũu ban tuo, ka a bala na tuo a ba ara. Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Pɔlbile, fʊ̃ʊ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ nɩ ir.

eng: The dead man sat up and began to speak, and Jesus gave him to his mother.
local: Ka a kũu vaa ir jãnɛ tɩ piel na wʊ 'yɛrɛ ka Yesu de wʊ kʊ a ma.

eng: Awestruck, everyone praised God. A great prophet has appeared among us, they said. God has come to help his people.
local: Dãbãɛ kpɛ̃n ba jaa ka ba pɛɛrɛ Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛrɛ nɩ, Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrkpɛ̃ɛ ãsʊɔ lɛ yi sɩ, Nãaŋmɩn wan ɩka wʊ wa sʊ̃ʊ a wʊ nɩbɛ.

eng: This news about Jesus spread throughout Judea and the surrounding region.
local: Ka a Yesu yele ŋme yaar a Judiya paal wʊ jaa nɩ a tẽn ala na gbʊr.

eng: Johns disciples informed him about all these things. John called two of his disciples.
local: Jɔɔn poturbo tɩ man a yele anyã a jaa kʊ wʊ, ka wʊ bʊɔl a ba ayi.

eng: and sent them to the Lord. They were to ask him, Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for someone else.
local: na wʊ tʊ̃ ba a Soro sɛ̃ ka ba tɩ sʊʊr wʊ kaa, Fʊ̃ʊ ɩ a 'lɔ na wa bɩɩ sɩ chɛlɛ nɩyuo.

eng: When they reached Jesus, they said, John the Baptist sent us to you. He asks, Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for someone else.
local: Ka a nɩbɛ bala wa a Yesu sɛ̃ wa 'yɛr, A Jɔɔn kɔ̃suor lɛ tʊ̃ sɩ a fʊ sɛ̃ ɩka sɩ wa sʊʊr fʊ kaa, fʊ̃ʊ lɛ ɩ a 'lɔ na wa bɩɩ sɩ chɛlɛ nɩyuo.

eng: Right then, Jesus healed many of their diseases, illnesses, and evil spirits, and he gave sight to a number of blind people.
local: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ mɩ lɛ Yesu sãa baalsɩ, bala na tara a sɩdɛbɛ na wʊ yuon jɔ̃n nɩbie.

eng: Then he replied to Johns disciples, Go, report to John what you have seen and heard. Those who were blind are able to see. Those who were crippled now walk. People with skin diseases are cleansed. Those who were deaf now hear. Those who were dead are raised up. And good news is preached to the poor.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a bala a Jɔɔn na tʊ̃, Yɩ lɩɛb kul tɩ man kʊ a Jɔɔn a lɛ yɩn nyɛ tɩ wõ, jɔ̃n nyɛrɛ naa ka gbɛrsɩ chiine ka kɔ̃kɔ̃n sa, ka wõn wõnõ, ka kũusɩ ir ɩ vʊɛ, ka a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ mɩ ta a nɔ̃n dẽme.

eng: Happy is anyone who doesnt stumble along the way because of me.
local: Pʊpɛl sʊɔ lɛ a 'lɔ a n yele na ba bãr wʊ lɔb ɛ.

eng: After Johns messengers were gone, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John. What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A stalk blowing in the wind?.
local: A bala a Jɔɔn na tʊ̃ na wa chen baar, Yesu piel 'yɛrɛ a Jɔɔn 'yɛr kʊrɔ a nɩyɔɔ bala, Bo lɛ yɩ dɔ̃ɔ kpɛ a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ a kaafʊ jie? A jɩmur a sɛsɛbɛ na dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ bɩɩ?.

eng: What did you go out to see? A man dressed up in refined clothes? Look, those who dress in fashionable clothes and live in luxury are in royal palaces.
local: Ala ba ɩ lɛ ɛ, bo yɩ chen tɩ nyɛ? Daba na su bʊ̃pɩɛl ɔ? Kai, a bala na suro a bʊ̃sʊ̃n, na ba dire a bʊ̃sʊ̃n nãa yir lɛ ba ma be.

eng: What did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
local: Tɩchɛ bo lɛ yɩ tɩ nyɛ? Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ ɔ. Ɔ̃ɔ, n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka wʊ gar Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ.

eng: He is the one of whom its written: Look, Im sending my messenger before you, who will prepare your way before you .
local: A anyã yele lɛ sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, Ɩn tʊ̃ naa a n tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ ka wʊ de a fʊ nie na wʊ tɩ chɔɔr a fʊ sɔr kʊ fʊ.

eng: I tell you that no greater human being has ever been born than John. Yet whoever is least in Gods kingdom is greater than he.
local: N 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka bala pɔɔ na dɔɔ ka a wɛr pɔ ba ɩ kpɛ̃ɛ gar a Jɔɔn ɛ, tɩchɛ 'lɔ mɩ na ɩ jaa pʊʊr a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ gar wʊ naa.

eng: Everyone who heard this, including the tax collectors, acknowledged Gods justice because they had been baptized by John.
local: Ka a nɩbɛ ba jaa lɔ̃ɔn a lõpodierbe gba na wõ a lɛ a Yesu na 'yɛr, ba sɔɔ naa ɩka Nãaŋmɩn yele tor naa, na ba sɔɔ ka a Jɔɔn so ba a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ.

eng: But the Pharisees and legal experts rejected Gods will for themselves because they hadnt been baptized by John.
local: Tɩchɛ a Faraseemɩne nɩ a bala na bɔ̃ɔ a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ, bala mãɛ tɔr naa a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na bʊɔrɔ, bojũu baa sɔɔ ka a Jɔɔn so ba a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: To what will I compare the people of this generation? Jesus asked. What are they like?.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Ba boo lɛ ɩn de a dɩna nɩbɛ naa man? Bo lɛ ba nyɛtãan?.

eng: They are like children sitting in the marketplace calling out to each other, We played the flute for you and you didnt dance. We sang a funeral song and you didnt cry.
local: Ba nyɛntãan bibiir na jãnɛ daa pɔ bʊɔlɔ taa, Sɩ pɛɛlɛ naa a laa 'lɩr tɩ yaa sɛɛrɛ bãnɛ ɛ, na sɩ kõnõ kõyiel tɩ yaa ŋmara nɩbikʊ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: John the Baptist came neither eating bread nor drinking wine, and you say, He has a demon.
local: A Jɔɔn kɔ̃suor na wa, waa dire bʊ̃diir ɛ, waa nyuro dãa ɛ, ka yɩ 'yɛr ɩka sɩdɛɛr lɛ wʊ tara.

eng: Yet the Human Onecame eating and drinking, and you say, Look, a glutton and a drunk, a friend of tax collectors and sinners.
local: Ka a maa Nɩsaal Bie wa na n dire na n nyuro ka yɩ lɛ 'yɛr, Wʊ dire na ba gala ɛ, na wʊ nyuro dãa, na wʊ ɩ a lõpodierbe nɩ a tʊ̃dɛtʊ̃nbɔ baraa.

eng: But wisdom is proved to be right by all her descendants.
local: Tɩchɛ a yɛ̃ ma wiil a lɛ wʊn tor a wʊ bibiir pɔ.

eng: One of the Pharisees invited Jesus to eat with him. After he entered the Pharisees home, he took his place at the table.
local: A Farasee nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ bʊɔl a Yesu ɩka wʊ wa di bʊ̃diir a wʊ yir, ka wʊ chen a Farasee nɩrɛ 'lɔ yir tɩ jãnɛ a bʊ̃diir difʊ jie.

eng: Meanwhile, a woman from the city, a sinner, discovered that Jesus was dining in the Pharisees house. She brought perfumed oil in a vase made of alabaster.
local: Pɔɔ ben a tẽe pɔ na wʊ tʊ̃nɔ̃ tʊ̃dɛbɛ, wʊn wa wõ ɩka a Yesu ben a Farasee yir, wʊ den a kãnyũvɩla na sɛɛ pãrtɔbile na wʊ chiin.

eng: Standing behind him at his feet and crying, she began to wet his feet with her tears. She wiped them with her hair, kissed them, and poured the oil on them.
local: Wʊ tɩ kpɛ̃n naa na wʊ ara a Yesu gbɛɛ par kõnõ. Wʊ soor a nɩbikʊ̃ɔ 'maan a Yesu gbɛɛ tɩ nyɔɔ a jukɔɔl fɩɛl bar, tɩ mʊʊr a gbɛɛ tɩ kpaar a kãnyũvɩla ala 'yɔ̃ɔ a Yesu gbɛɛ pɔ.

eng: When the Pharisee who had invited Jesus saw what was happening, he said to himself, If this man were a prophet, he would know what kind of woman is touching him. He would know that she is a sinner.
local: Ka a Farasee nɩrɛ 'lɔ na bʊɔl wʊ ka wʊ tɩ dire, na nyɛ a lɛ ka wʊ tɩɛr, Ala ɩ a daba nyãna ta ɩn Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ wʊn taa bɔ̃ɔn naa a pɔɔ nyãna tɔ sʊɔ na sɩrɛ wʊ, wʊ tʊ̃nɔ̃ na tʊ̃dɛbɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Simon, I have something to say to you. Teacher, speak, he said.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr sɔɔ wʊ, Simon, n tara na 'yɛr na sʊʊr fʊ. Ka a Simon 'yɛr, Wiwile, sʊʊr ma.

eng: A certain lender had two debtors. One owed enough money to pay five hundred people for a days work.The other owed enough money for fifty.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Nɩbɛ ayi kɔ̃ɔ lɛ tɩ pɛ̃ɛ nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ libie, ãsʊɔ pɛ̃ɛn kɔɔr anũu ka ãsʊɔ mɩ pɛ̃ɛ lɩjaayi nɩ pie .

eng: When they couldnt pay, the lender forgave the debts of them both. Which of them will love him more.
local: A nɩbɛ ayi banyãna ãsʊɔ jaa ba tara a libie na yab wʊ ɛ, ka a 'lɔ na so a libie 'yɛr, Yɩ ta lɛ yab go ɛ, a sãn baar naa. Buor sʊɔ lɛ na nʊ̃ wʊ yɔɔ.

eng: Simon replied, I suppose the one who had the largest debt canceled. Jesus said, You have judged correctly.
local: Ka a Simon 'yɛr, N tɩɛr ɩka a 'lɔ sãn na waa lɛ. Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Sɩrɛ fʊ 'yɛr.

eng: Jesus turned to the woman and said to Simon, Do you see this woman? When I entered your home, you didnt give me water for my feet, but she wet my feet with tears and wiped them with her hair.
local: Yesu lɩɛb tor a pɔɔ tɩ pãa 'yɛr kʊ a Simon, Fʊ nyɛn a pɔɔ nyãna? Ɩn wa kpɛ a fʊ yir, faa kʊ ma kʊ̃ɔ ɩka n pɛɛ gbɛɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ a pɔɔ pɛɛn a n gbɛɛ nɩ a wʊ nɩbikʊ̃ɔ tɩ de a wʊ jukɔɔl fɩɛl bar.

eng: You didnt greet me with a kiss, but she hasnt stopped kissing my feet since I came in.
local: Faa sʊʊr ma kpãɛn ɛ, tɩchɛ a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ jaa ɩn wa kpɛ a pɔɔ nyã ba bar ma kpãɛn sʊʊrfʊ ɛ.

eng: You didnt anoint my head with oil, but she has poured perfumed oil on my feet.
local: Faa pur kãa 'yɔ̃ɔ ma a ju pɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ a pɔɔ kpaar a kãnyũvɩla 'yɔ̃ɔ ma a gbɛɛ pɔ.

eng: This is why I tell you that her many sins have been forgiven; so she has shown great love. The one who is forgiven little loves little.
local: A lɛ jũu n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ fʊ ɩka a wʊ tʊ̃dɛbɛ ala a jaa vɛ̃n kʊ wʊ, bojũu a wʊ nʊ̃fʊ waa naa. Tɩchɛ a 'lɔ Nãaŋmɩn na vɛ̃ jaa pʊʊr kʊ a wʊ nʊ̃fʊ mɩ ɩn jaa pʊʊr.

eng: Then Jesus said to her, Your sins are forgiven.
local: A Yesu pãa 'yɛr kʊ a pɔɔ, A fʊ yeldɛbɛ vɛ̃n bar.

eng: The other table guests began to say among themselves, Who is this person that even forgives sins.
local: Ka a bala na lɔ̃ɔ jɛ̃n wʊ 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ taa, An lɛ anyã, na wʊ mɩ ma vɛ̃ nɩrɛ yeldɛɛr bar.

eng: Jesus said to the woman, Your faith has saved you. Go in peace.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a pɔɔ, A fʊ sɔɔfʊ faa fʊ naa, chiine nɩ nyã'maar.

eng: Soon afterward, Jesus traveled through the cities and villages, preaching and proclaiming the good news of Gods kingdom. The Twelve were with him.
local: A anyãna puor, Yesu yɔn a tẽbɛrɛ nɩ a tẽbil 'yɛrɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ 'yɛr kʊrɔ ba ka a wʊ nɩtʊ̃nsɩ Pie nɩ Ayi pʊɔ wʊ.

eng: along with some women who had been healed of evil spirits and sicknesses. Among them were Mary Magdalene.
local: Ka a pɔbɔ bamɩne mɩ pʊɔ wʊ, bala lɛ wʊ dɔ̃ɔ dii a sɩdɛbɛ ka a yi na wʊ sãa a ba baalsɩ, ãsʊɔ lɛ ba bʊɔlɔ Mɛɛr na yi a Magdala 'lɔ lɛ wʊ dii a sɩdɛbɛ ayopõi ka a yi.

eng: Joanna , Susanna, and many others who provided for them out of their resources.
local: Joana lɛ a ãsʊɔ na ɩ a Kuja na kaara a Hɛrɔd yir pɔɔ, Susana lɛ a ãsʊɔ nɩ a pɔbɔ yɔɔ bamɩne. Banyãna lɛ de a ba bomo sʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ a Yesu nɩ a wʊ poturbo.

eng: Jesus called the Twelve together and he gave them power and authority over all demons and to heal sicknesses.
local: Yesu na wa bʊɔl a wʊ nɩtʊ̃nsɩ pie nɩ ayi lɔ̃ɔ taa, wʊ kʊ ban kpɛ̃ɔ nɩ tʊ̃ɔfʊ ka ban diin sɩdɛbɛ ka a yi tɩ sãa baalsɩ.

eng: He sent them out to proclaim Gods kingdom and to heal the sick.
local: Wʊ tʊ̃ ba naa ka ba chen tɩ 'yɛr a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ 'yɛr tɩ sãa baal.

eng: He told them, Take nothing for the journey—no walking stick, no bag, no bread, no money, not even an extra shirt.
local: Wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ taa tara bon jaa chiine ɛ. Yɩ ta de dãgbal ɛ, bɩɩ wʊɔ ɛ, bɩɩ bʊ̃diir ɛ, bɩɩ libie ɛ, bɩɩ de fuusɩ ayi ɛ.

eng: Whatever house you enter, remain there until you leave that place.
local: Yir jaa pɔ yɩn tɩ kpɛ, yɩ jãnɛ a be tɩ tãn a daar 'lɔ yɩn na yi a tẽe 'lɔ pɔ.

eng: When a great crowd was gathering and people were coming to Jesus from one city after another, he spoke to them in a parable.
local: Nɩyɔɔ yin tẽn tẽn waara a Yesu sɛ̃, a nɩyɔɔ banyãna na wa duo, sʊkpa nyã lɛ wʊ lɔb kʊ ba.

eng: A farmer went out to scatter his seed. As he was scattering it, some fell on the path where it was crushed, and the birds in the sky came and ate it.
local: Kʊɔraa lɛ yin wʊ bʊ̃bʊrɔ bʊrfʊ jie. Wʊ tɩ yaara naa yʊɔrɔ nɩ ka amɩne tɩ lo sɔr pɔ, ka ba nɛ nɛb ka lubil wa tuur di.

eng: Other seed fell on rock. As it grew, it dried up because it had no moisture.
local: Ka amɩne mɩ tɩ lo kʊsɩr pãn ju naa bul tɩ kpi bojũu tãn ba be be ɛ.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorns grew with the plants and choked them.
local: Ka amɩne mɩ lo gʊʊr pɔ 'mataa bul ka a gʊʊr nʊ̃ɔ fãɩn ba.

eng: Still other seed landed on good soil. When it grew, it produced one hundred times more grain than was scattered. As he said this, he called out, Everyone who has ears should pay attention.
local: Tɩchɛ ka amɩne mɩ lo a kɔjɔ̃ pɔ bul dɔɔ jur kɔɔkɔɔr naa gar a lɛ wʊn bʊr. Yesu na wa 'yɛr a nɩtɔ̃ baar, wʊ 'yɛr kpɛ̃ɔ jaa, Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na tara tuor wõnõ nɩ wʊ chɛlɛ.

eng: His disciples asked him what this parable meant.
local: Ka a wʊ poturbo sʊʊr wʊ a sʊkpa nyã par.
