eng: He said, You have been given the mysteries of Gods kingdom, but these mysteries come to everyone else in parables so that when they see, they cant see, and when they hear, they cant understand .
local: A wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩn 'lɔ, n vɛ̃ naa ka yɩ bɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ yelsɔɔlsɩ, tɩchɛ a bala na chɛ, sʊkpar lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ ba, Ba ma kaa naa tɩ maa nyɛ ɛ, na ba ma wõnõ tɩ ba bɔ̃ɔ a par ɛ.

eng: The parable means this: The seed is Gods word.
local: A sʊkpa par nɩ nyã. Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr lɛ ɩ a bʊ̃bie.

eng: Wherever they dont welcome you, as you leave that city, shake the dust off your feet as a witness against them.
local: Ala ɩ nɩbɛ ba wa de yɩ ɛ, yɩ yi a tẽe 'lɔ pɔ, tɩ yɩn wa yire yɩ pĩ pĩ a yɩ gbɛɛ tã-uul ka a di a ba dãasɩɛ.

eng: They departed and went through the villages proclaiming the good news and healing people everywhere.
local: Be lɛ ba yi lʊɔrɔ a tẽn tẽn 'yɛrɛ a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyã sãa na nɩ a nɩbɛ baalsɩ a jie wʊ jaa.

eng: Herod the ruler heard about everything that was happening. He was confused because some people were saying that John had been raised from the dead.
local: A Nãa Hɛrɔd na wõ a nɩtɔ̃ na ɩrɛ, ka tɩɛr nyɔɔ wʊ, bojũu bamɩne 'yɛrɛ naa ɩka Jɔɔn lɛ tɔ̃ɔ ir ɩ vʊr.

eng: others that Elijah had appeared, and still others that one of the ancient prophets had come back to life.
local: Ka bamɩne mɩ 'yɛrɛ ɩka a Elaija lɛ tɔ̃ɔ yi, ka bamɩne mɩ chɛnɛ 'yɛrɛ ɩka a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ ãsʊɔ lɛ tɔ̃ɔ ir ɩ vʊr.

eng: Herod said, I beheaded John, so now who am I hearing about? Herod wanted to see him.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Hɛrɔd 'yɛr, N ŋmaan Jɔɔn ju, tɩ an 'yɛr lɛ a nyã ɩn wõnõ? Na wʊ mʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ nyɛ a Yesu.

eng: When the apostles returned, they described for Jesus what they had done. Taking them with him, Jesus withdrew privately to a city called Bethsaida.
local: A nɩtʊ̃nsɩ lɩɛb wa na ba wa man kʊ a Yesu a lɛ ban tʊ̃. Ka wʊ pãa tara a bala tɛɛ ka ba 'mataa chen a tẽe ban bʊɔlɔ Betɩsaida.

eng: When the crowds figured it out, they followed him. He welcomed them, spoke to them about Gods kingdom, and healed those who were sick.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a nɩyɔɔ chɛnɛ bɔ̃ɔn na ba bɩɛr chen a jie. Wʊ de ba naa na wʊ 'yɛr a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, tɩ sãa a bala na bʊɔrɔ a sãafʊ.

eng: When the day was almost over, the Twelve came to him and said, Send the crowd away so that they can go to the nearby villages and countryside and find lodging and food, because we are in a deserted place.
local: Mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ na wa mʊrɔ, a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ pie nɩ ayi tɩ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Wɛl a nɩbɛ bar a ba chen a tẽbil anyã nɩ a por anyã na gbʊr na ba tɩ bɔ gaa jie nɩ bʊ̃diir di bojũu a ka ɩn mɔgɔ̃ɔ.

eng: He replied, You give them something to eat. But they said, We have no more than five loaves of bread and two fish—unless we go and buy food for all these people.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr sɔɔ ba, Yɩn lɛ na kʊ ba bʊ̃kɔ̃ɔ ka ba di. Ka ba 'yɛr, Paanʊ̃ɔsɩ anũu nɩ jãbie ayi tɛɛ lɛ sɩ tara, ka a ba ɩ sɩ chen tɩ da bʊ̃diir wa kʊ a nɩyɔɔ ba nyãna ɛ.

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Seat them in groups of about fifty.
local: A nɩyɔɔ bala, dɔɔr tɛɛ ɩn tur anũu . Tɩchɛ ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a poturbo, Yɩ ŋma a nɩbɛ ka ba jãnɛ jãnɛ gbulsɩ lɩjaayi nɩ pie pie.

eng: They did so, and everyone was seated.
local: A poturbo ɩn a lɛ ka nɩrɛ jaa jɛ̃ tẽe.

eng: He took the five loaves and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them, and broke them and gave them to the disciples to set before the crowd.
local: Yesu den a paanʊ̃ɔsɩ a nũu nɩ a jãmɛ ayi ala dʊl kaa saaju tɩ puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan, tɩ na wʊ ŋma ŋma, de kʊ a poturbo ɩka ba de põ a nɩbɛ.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full, and the disciples filled twelve baskets with the leftovers.
local: Ba jaa din tɩɩ, ka a poturbo wob a bʊ̃ŋmaar ala na chɛ sɛɛ pɛr pie nɩ ayi.

eng: Once when Jesus was praying by himself, the disciples joined him, and he asked them, Who do the crowds say that I am.
local: Daar kɔ̃ɔ lɛ a Yesu tɛɛ wa sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn ka wʊ poturbo be be ka wʊ sʊʊr ba, An lɛ a nɩbɛ 'yɛrɛ ɩka n ɩ naa.

eng: They answered, John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others that one of the ancient prophets has come back to life.
local: Ka ba 'yɛr, Bamɩne 'yɛr ɩka fʊ ɩn a Jɔɔn 'lɔ na dɔ̃ɔ suoro a nɩbɛ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ, ka bamɩne mɩ 'yɛr ɩka Elaija lɛ, ka bamɩne mɩ 'yɛr ɩka a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ ãsʊɔ kɔ̃ɔ lɛ lɩɛb wa.

eng: He asked them, And what about you? Who do you say that I am? Peter answered, The Christ sent from God.
local: Ka wʊ sʊʊr ba, Tɩchɛ yɩn mɩ, an lɛ yɩ 'yɛr ɩka n ɩ naa? Ka a Pita 'yɛr, Fʊ ɩ naa a Nɩ-iraa yi a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃.

eng: Jesus gave them strict orders not to tell this to anyone.
local: Ka a Yesu kpãkpãan ba ɩka ba taa 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ nɩrɛ jaa ɛ.

eng: He said, The Human Onemust suffer many things and be rejected—by the elders, chief priests, and the legal experts—and be killed and be raised on the third day.
local: Tɩ na wʊ 'yɛr, A maa Nɩsaal Bie na din dɔɔyɛ yɔɔ ka a nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Mosesɩ nɛɛ wiwiilbe tɔr ma bar, ka ba kʊ ma, ka a ber ata daar ka n ir ɩ vʊr.

eng: The seed on the path are those who hear, but then the devil comes and steals the word from their hearts so that they wont believe and be saved.
local: A ala na lo a sɔr pɔ ɩn a bala na wõ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr, tɩchɛ ka a tɩɩ wa ir naa a ba nyãa pɔ, ka ba ta sɔɔ de nyɛ faafʊ ɛ.

eng: Jesus said to everyone, All who want to come after me must say no to themselves, take up their cross daily, and follow me.
local: Be lɛ wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba jaa, Ala ɩ nɩrɛ na turo ma naa, wʊ 'laar a wʊ mãɛ tɩ tuo a wʊ daa turo nɩ ma.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me will save them.
local: Bojũu nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ faa a wʊ nyɛvʊr a wʊ nyɛvʊr na bɔr naa, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na lɔb a wʊ nyɛvʊr bar a maa jũu na faa wʊ naa.

eng: What advantage do people have if they gain the whole world for themselves yet perish or lose their lives?.
local: Ala ɩ nɩrɛ tara naa a wɛr wʊ jaa bonsɩ tɩ bɔr bɩɩ ka a wʊ sɩɛ wɛ, bo tɔ̃nɔ̃ wʊ nyɛ?.

eng: Whoever is ashamed of me and my words, the Human Onewill be ashamed of that person when he comes in his glory and in the glory of the Father and of the holy angels.
local: Ala ɩ nɩrɛ nyɛ naa maa nɩ a n 'yɛr ka a ɩ vĩ yele a wʊ sɛ̃, a maa Nɩsaal Bie mɩ na kaa naa nɩnyɩnɛ ka wʊ ɩ vĩ sʊɔ maa wa lɩɛb waara nɩ a n tɩɩr nɩ a Sãa tɩɩr nɩ a malaka chɛchɛ bala.

eng: I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see Gods kingdom.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka a yɩ bamɩne gba ara naa kana kʊ̃ kpi ɛ, tɩ na ban nyɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ.

eng: About eight days after Jesus said these things, he took Peter, John, and James, and went up on a mountain to pray.
local: Yesu na 'yɛr a 'yɛrbie anyãna baar a maal ber aniin tɩ wʊ tara Pita, Jɔɔn nɩ Jemesɩ don a tɔ̃ɔ ju Nãaŋmɩn sʊɔrfʊ jie.

eng: As he was praying, the appearance of his face changed and his clothes flashed white like lightning.
local: Wʊn tɩ sʊɔrɔ a Nãaŋmɩn ka a nie lɩɛb, ka a fuu pɛl nyɩlɛ bũu 'lɩraa kaar.

eng: Two men, Moses and Elijah, were talking with him.
local: Ka a dɔɔr ayi, Mosesɩ nɩ Elaija wa naa a wʊ sɛ̃.

eng: The seed on the rock are those who receive the word joyfully when they hear it, but they have no root. They believe for a while but fall away when they are tempted.
local: Ala mɩ na lo a kʊsɩr pãn ju ɩn a bala na wõ a 'yɛr den pʊpɛl, tɩchɛ baa tara nyɩbɛ ɛ. Ba sɔɔn ara bãlãa tɩchɛ bɛɛr kaa wa wa ba ma lo naa.

eng: They were clothed with heavenly splendor and spoke about Jesus departure, which he would achieve in Jerusalem.
local: Ba wa yin naa a saaju tɩɩr 'yɛrɛ nɩ a Yesu. Ba 'mantaa 'yɛrɛ a kũu a Yesu na tɩ kpi a Jerusalɛm 'yɛr.

eng: Peter and those with him were almost overcome by sleep, but they managed to stay awake and saw his glory as well as the two men with him.
local: Gʊ̃ɔ kpɛ̃n a Pita nɩ a wʊ taaba, tɩchɛ ban wa jɔɔ ir ba nyɛn a wʊ tɩɩr nɩ a dɔɔr ayi bala a ba ara a wʊ sɛ̃.

eng: As the two men were about to leave Jesus, Peter said to him, Master, its good that were here. We should construct three shrines: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah—but he didnt know what he was saying.
local: A dɔɔr bala na lɩɛb chiine ka Pita 'yɛr, N Soro, a sɛɛn ka sɩn kpɩɛrɛ a ka. Bar a sɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ sɛɛr ata, fʊ̃ʊ so ãsʊɔ, Mosesɩ ãsʊɔ Elaija mɩ ãsʊɔ. Waa bɔ̃ɔ a lɛ wʊn 'yɛrɛ ɛ.

eng: Peter was still speaking when a cloud overshadowed them. As they entered the cloud, they were overcome with awe.
local: Wʊn chɛnɛ 'yɛrɛ a julõjuur wa pɔɔn ba jaa ka a dãbãɛ kpɛ a Pita, Jɔɔn nɩ Jemesɩ.

eng: Then a voice from the cloud said, This is my Son, my chosen one. Listen to him.
local: Ka a kɔkɔr yi be a julõjuur pɔ 'yɛrɛ, Nyã lɛ ɩ a n Bie ɩn ir, yɩ chɛlɛ a wʊ 'yɛr.

eng: Even as the voice spoke, Jesus was found alone. They were speechless and at the time told no one what they had seen.
local: A kɔkɔr na wa 'yɛr baar, ba nyɛn a Yesu tɛɛ na ara. Ka a poturbo ba 'la man kʊ nɩrɛ a lɛ ban nyɛ ɛ.

eng: The next day, when Jesus, Peter, John, and James had come down from the mountain, a large crowd met Jesus.
local: A wɛr na wa chaar, ba yin a tɔ̃ɔ ju suu, a nɩyɔɔ wa lɔ̃ɔ a wʊ sɛ̃.

eng: A man from the crowd shouted, Teacher, I beg you to take a look at my son, my only child.
local: Ka a daba bʊɔl be a nɩyɔɔ bala pɔ, Wiwile, n sʊɔrɔ fʊ naa, kaa a n bidaba, 'lɔ tɛɛ lɛ ɩ a n bie.

eng: Look, a spirit seizes him and, without any warning, he screams. It shakes him and causes him to foam at the mouth. It tortures him and rarely leaves him alone.
local: Sɩɛ kɔ̃ɔ lɛ tara wʊ, ma ire wʊ ba gala ɛ. Wʊ maa ŋmen a chɛlma tɩ tãanaa tɩ furo a natafuul a nɛɛ pɔ. Wʊ maa bara wʊ ɛ, na wʊ bʊɔrɔ a wʊ sɔ̃ɔfʊ.

eng: I begged your disciples to throw it out, but they couldnt.
local: N sʊɔr a fʊ poturbo ɩka ba dii a sɩɛ nyã bar tɩchɛ baa tʊ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: Jesus answered, You faithless and crooked generation, how long will I be with you and put up with you? Bring your son here.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Yɩn babãma dẽme na ba tara sɔɔfʊ ɛ, n be a yɩ sɛ̃ naa tara yɩ nɩ kãnyir tɩ tãn dabor? Tɩ 'yɛr a daba, Tɔɔ a bie wa kʊ ma.

eng: While he was coming, the demon threw him down and shook him violently. Jesus spoke harshly to the unclean spirit, healed the child, and gave him back to his father.
local: A bie na chɛnɛ waara ka a sɩdɛɛr ir a bie tãa lɔb a tẽe, tɩchɛ ka a a Yesu tãn a sɩdɛɛr na wʊ sãa a bie de kʊ a sãa.

eng: Everyone was overwhelmed by Gods greatness. While everyone was marveling at everything he was doing, Jesus said to his disciples.
local: Nɩbɛ ba jaa nɛ'maa naa ban nyɛ a Nãaŋmɩn kpɛ̃ɔ. A nɩbɛ na chɛnɛ kpire a lɛ a Yesu na ɩ, tɩ wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a poturbo.

eng: Take these words to heart: the Human Oneis about to be delivered into human hands.
local: Yɩ chɛlɛ a lɛ ɩn 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, ban gar naa maa Nɩsaal Bie 'yɔ̃ɔ a nɩbɛ nuur pɔ.

eng: They didnt understand this statement. Its meaning was hidden from them so they couldnt grasp it. And they were afraid to ask him about it.
local: Tɩchɛ baa bɔ̃ɔ a anyãna wʊn 'yɛr par ɛ, a lii ba naa, ka ba bɔ̃ɔ faa, tɩ joro dãbãɛ ba 'la sʊʊr wʊ ɛ.

eng: An argument arose among the disciples about which of them was the greatest.
local: Nɛchɩrɛ ir naa a Yesu poturbo pɔ ɩka a ba buor sʊɔ lɛ na ɩ a ba kpɛ̃ɛ.

eng: Aware of their deepest thoughts, Jesus took a little child and had the child stand beside him.
local: Yesu na bɔ̃ɔ a ba tɩɛr, wʊ tɔɔn bibile wa lan a wʊ lõboor.

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Whoever welcomes this child in my name welcomes me. Whoever welcomes me, welcomes the one who sent me. Whoever is least among you all is the greatest.
local: Tɩ pãa 'yɛr kʊ ba, Nɩrɛ jaa na de a bibile nyãna a maa jũu maa lɛ wʊ de. Nɩrɛ jaa mɩ na de ma wʊ mɩ den a 'lɔ na tʊ̃ ma. Bojũu nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ɩ a yɩ jaa bile, 'lɔ lɛ ɩ kpɛ̃ɛ gar yɩ jaa.

eng: John replied, Master, we saw someone throwing demons out in your name, and we tried to stop him because he isnt in our group of followers.
local: Ka a Jɔɔn 'yɛr, N Soro, sɩ nyɛn nɩrɛ na de a fʊ yuor dire nɩ a sɩdɛbɛ ka a yire, ka sɩ 'yɛr wʊ ɩka wʊ ta ɩrɛ a lɛ ɛ, bojũu waa ɩ a sɩ ãsʊɔ ɛ.

eng: But Jesus replied, Dont stop him, because whoever isnt against you is for you.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Yɩ ta ŋmaa wʊ sɔr ɛ, bojũu nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ba kpɛrɛ yɩ ɛ, wʊ pʊɔ yɩ naa.

eng: As the time approached when Jesus was to be taken up into heaven, he determined to go to Jerusalem.
local: Lɛ a sɔ̃ɔ na wa gbʊrɔ ka Yesu na lɩɛb do a saaju, wʊ ŋmen nyãa a Jerusalɛm chenfʊ.

eng: He sent messengers on ahead of him. Along the way, they entered a Samaritan village to prepare for his arrival.
local: Wʊ den nie tʊ̃ nɩbɛ ka ba de nie chen a Samariya tẽbile ãsʊɔ pɔ, na ban tɩ chɔɔr a jie bar wʊ.

eng: but the Samaritan villagers refused to welcome him because he was determined to go to Jerusalem.
local: Tɩchɛ a tẽe 'lɔ nɩbɛ ba sɔɔ de wʊ ɛ, bojũu Jerusalɛm lɛ wʊ chiine.

eng: When the disciples James and John saw this, they said, Lord, do you want us to call fire down from heaven to consume them.
local: Ka a wʊ poturbo Jemesɩ nɩ Jɔɔn na wa nyɛ a lɛ, ka ba sʊʊr a Yesu, Sɩ Soro, fʊ bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka sɩ bʊɔl bũu ka ayi saaju suu wa di ba bɩɩ.

eng: But Jesus turned and spoke sternly to them.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Yesu lɩɛb tãn ba ɩka ba ta ɩ a lɛ ɛ.

eng: and they went on to another village.
local: Tɩ na ba chen tẽyuo pɔ.

eng: As Jesus and his disciples traveled along the road, someone said to him, I will follow you wherever you go.
local: A sɔr ban chɛnɛ de chiine ka daba ãsʊɔ 'yɛr kʊ a Yesu, Jie jaa fʊn chiine ɩn turo fʊ naa.

eng: Jesus replied, Foxes have dens and the birds in the sky have nests, but the Human Onehas no place to lay his head.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Mɔbaar tara naa bɔɔr ka a lubil mɩ tara choor, tɩchɛ maa Nɩsaal Bie ba tara jie na bin a n ju ɛ.

eng: Then Jesus said to someone else, Follow me. He replied, Lord, first let me go and bury my father.
local: Wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a daba ãsʊɔ, Turo ma. Tɩchɛ ka a daba 'yɛr, N Soro, bar ma sɔr ka n kul tɩ ũu a n sãa baar.

eng: Jesus said to him, Let the dead bury their own dead. But you go and spread the news of Gods kingdom.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a daba, Bar ka a kũn ũno ba mãɛ kũn tɩchɛ fʊ̃ʊ 'lɔ, chen tɩ mʊɔlɔ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ 'yɛr.

eng: Someone else said to Jesus, I will follow you, Lord, but first let me say good-bye to those in my house.
local: Ka ãsʊɔ mɩ 'yɛr, N Soro, ɩn turo fʊ naa tɩ bar ma sɔr ka n kul tɩ 'yɛr a n yir dẽme.

eng: Jesus said to him, No one who puts a hand on the plow and looks back is fit for Gods kingdom.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Nɩrɛ na nyɔɔ niin kuur tɩ lɩɛb kaara a puor ba sɛɛn a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ tʊ̃mɔ̃ ɛ.

eng: As for the seed that fell among thorny plants, these are the ones who, as they go about their lives, are choked by the concerns, riches, and pleasures of life, and their fruit never matures.
local: A bʊ̃bie ala na lo a gʊʊr pɔ ɩn a bala na wõ a 'yɛr, tɩchɛ ka a ãgãn nyã vɔlaa nɩ bʊ̃tarsɩ nɩ a bʊ̃bʊɔrsɩ fãɩn ba ka baa tʊ̃ɔ dɔɔ bie ɛ.

eng: The seed that fell on good soil are those who hear the word and commit themselves to it with a good and upright heart. Through their resolve, they bear fruit.
local: Tɩchɛ a bʊ̃bie ala na lo a kɔjɔ̃ pɔ, ɩn a bala na wõ a 'yɛr na ba sɔɔ den nyãa ara nɩ pʊpɛl, ka a kãnyir vɛ̃ ka ba dɔɔ jur.

eng: No one lights a lamp and then covers it with a bowl or puts it under a bed. Instead, they put it on top of a lampstand so that those who enter can see the light.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa maa chʊm fãtɩn de laa kpa pɔɔ ɛ, bɩɩ de su gado pĩlem ɛ. Tɩchɛ wʊ ma den dɔɔl a dɔɔlfʊ jie ka a bala na kpɩɛrɛ nyɛ a chãa.

eng: Nothing is hidden that wont be exposed. Nor is anything concealed that wont be made known and brought to the light.
local: Bojũu bon jaa ba be be na sɔɔl ka ba kʊ̃ nyɛ wʊ ɛ, bɩɩ bon jaa ba be be sɔɔl ka ba kʊ̃ bɔ̃ɔn ɛ, bɩɩ kʊ̃ yi chãa pɔ ɛ.

eng: Therefore, listen carefully. Those who have will receive more, but as for those who dont have, even what they seem to have will be taken away from them.
local: A lɛ jũu, yɩ bɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ sʊ̃ a lɛ yɩn chɛlɛ. Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na bɔ̃ɔ a par Nãaŋmɩn na lɛ kʊ wʊn dɔɔl, nɩrɛ 'lɔ mɩ na ba bɔ̃ɔ a par ɛ, Nãaŋmɩn na den a bãlãa 'lɔ wʊn tɩɛr ɩka wʊ bɔ̃ɔn a par.

eng: Jesus mother and brothers came to him but were unable to reach him because of the crowd.
local: Yesu ma nɩ a wʊ yɛɛr wan ɩka ba wa nyɛ wʊ, tɩchɛ a nɩyɔɔ jũu ba kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ kpɛ ɛ.

eng: Someone told him, Your mother and brothers are standing outside, wanting to see you.
local: Ka nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ 'yɛr wʊ, A fʊ ma nɩ a fʊ yɛɛr lɛ be a yõo bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba nyɛ fʊ.

eng: He replied, My mother and brothers are those who listen to Gods word and do it.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr, N ma nɩ a n yɛɛr lɛ a bala na wõ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr na ba turo.

eng: One day Jesus and his disciples boarded a boat. He said to them, Lets cross over to the other side of the lake. So they set sail.
local: Bɩbir ãsʊɔ lɛ a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ poturbo, Yɩ ɩ a sɩ gɔ̃ɔ a kasɛn a manyal gɔ̃ɔn. Be lɛ ba kpɛ a gboro na ba gɔ̃ɔnɔ̃.

eng: While they were sailing, he fell asleep. Gale-force winds swept down on the lake. The boat was filling up with water and they were in danger.
local: Ban gɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ tɩ wʊ gã gur. Ka a kɔ̃sɛkpɛ̃ɛ ire a man pɔ ka a kʊ̃ɔ ɔɔrɔ kpɩɛrɛ a gboro pɔ, ka ba ɩrɛ na mʊr.

eng: So they went and woke Jesus, shouting, Master, Master, were going to drown! But he got up and gave orders to the wind and the violent waves. The storm died down and it was calm.
local: Ka a poturbo tɩ sãɩ wʊ 'yɛrɛ, Kpãkpãana, a kʊ̃ɔ na di sɩ naa. Ka a Yesu ir tãn a sɛsɛbɛ nɩ a kʊ̃ɔ na ɔɔrɔ, ka a sɛsɛbɛ ŋmaa vʊʊr ka a kʊ̃ɔ mɩ lo jaa jomm.

eng: He said to his disciples, Where is your faith? Filled with awe and wonder, they said to each other, Who is this? He commands even the winds and the water, and they obey him.
local: Tɩ pãa sʊʊr a wʊ poturbo, Nyɩnɛ a yɩ sɔɔfʊ be? Ka ba mɩ de dãbãɛ sʊrɔ nɩ taa, Nɩ an lɛ anyã, na 'yɛr ka a sɛsɛbɛ nɩ kʊ̃ɔ tu a wʊ nɛɛ.

eng: Jesus and his disciples sailed to the Gerasenes land, which is across the lake from Galilee.
local: Ba yin a Galilee gɔ̃ɔn na ba gɔ̃ɔ a Gerasenɩ gɔ̃ɔn.

eng: As soon as Jesus got out of the boat, a certain man met him. The man was from the city and was possessed by demons. For a long time, he had lived among the tombs, naked and homeless.
local: Yesu na wa yire a gogoro ju wʊ tuor daba na tara sɩdɛbɛ na wʊ gele yi a tẽe 'lɔ pɔ. Bojũu a kor wʊn yi a yir na wʊ maa su fuu ɛ, tɩ na wʊ gãna a yar par.

eng: When he saw Jesus, he shrieked and fell down before him. Then he shouted, What have you to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I beg you, dont torture me.
local: Wʊn wa nyɛ a Yesu wʊ ŋmen chɛlma tɩ lo a Yesu niem na wʊ tãna 'yɛrɛ nɩ, Bo lɛ fʊ bʊɔrɔ ka a n sɛ̃, a fʊ̃ʊ na ɩ a saaju Nãaŋmɩn Bie? N sʊɔrɔ fʊ naa, ta ge ma ɛ.

eng: He said this because Jesus had already commanded the unclean spirit to come out of the man. Many times it had taken possession of him, so he would be bound with leg irons and chains and placed under guard. But he would break his restraints, and the demon would force him into the wilderness.
local: Bojũu Yesu dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr kʊ a sɩdɛɛr ɩka wʊ yi a daba 'yɔ̃ɔ. Gbɛɛ yɔɔ jaa lɛ a ma ir a daba ka ba ma gbar a nuur nɩ a gbɛɛ nɩ bɔ̃jɔrsɩ kaara wʊ, tɩ wʊ ma kar a bɔ̃jɔrsɩ bar ka a sɩdɛɛr ma dii wʊ bar be a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ wʊ yõ.

eng: Jesus asked him, What is your name? Legion, he replied, because many demons had entered him.
local: Ka a Yesu sʊʊr wʊ, A fʊ yuor lɛ di bo? Ka wʊ 'yɛr, Nɩyɔɔ. Bojũu sɩdɛyɔɔ lɛ kpɛ wʊ.

eng: They pleaded with him not to order them to go back into the abyss.
local: Ka a sɩdɛbɛ sʊɔrɔ a Yesu ɩka wʊ ta dii ba 'yɔ̃ɔ be a bɔɔ fulom 'lɔ pɔ ɛ.

eng: A large herd of pigs was feeding on the hillside. The demons begged Jesus to let them go into the pigs. Jesus gave them permission.
local: Doyɔɔ mɩ dɔ̃ɔ ara be a tɔ̃ɔ kõkoor dire, ka a sɩdɛbɛ sʊɔr a Yesu ɩka wʊ sɔɔ ka ba yi tɩ kpɛ a dor ala pɔ, ka wʊ sɔɔ kʊ ba sɔr.

eng: and the demons left the man and entered the pigs. The herd rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: A sɩdɛbɛ yin a daba pɔ tɩ kpɛ a dor pɔ, ka a tĩgbil suu tɩ kpɛ a man kʊ̃ɔ pɔ tɩ gbʊ̃m kpi kpi.

eng: When those who tended the pigs saw what happened, they ran away and told the story in the city and in the countryside.
local: A bala na kaara a dor na wa nyɛ a lɛ na ɩ, ba jon kul a tẽe pɔ, nɩ a tẽbil ala na gbʊr tɩ 'yɛr a lɛ na ɩ.

eng: People came to see what had happened. They came to Jesus and found the man from whom the demons had gone. He was sitting at Jesus feet, fully dressed and completely sane. They were filled with awe.
local: Ka a nɩbɛ yi chen ɩka ba tɩ kaa a lɛ na ɩ. Ba chen a Yesu sɛ̃ tɩ nyɛ a dagẽgelaa a sɩdɛbɛ na yi tɩ chɛ bar ka wʊ chɩ na wʊ su a fuu jãnɛ a Yesu gbɛɛ par, ka a dãbãɛ kpɛ ba.

eng: Those people who had actually seen what had happened told them how the demon-possessed man had been delivered.
local: A nɩbɛ bala na dɔ̃ɔ nyɛ a lɛ an ɩ ka a gẽgelaa sa pãa mana kʊrɔ a nɩbɛ.

eng: Then everyone gathered from the region of the Gerasenes asked Jesus to leave their area because they were overcome with fear. So he got into the boat and returned across the lake.
local: Ka a Gerasenɩ paal nɩbɛ ba jaa sʊɔrɔ a Yesu ɩka wʊ yi a be, bojũu dãbãɛ kpɛ ba naa. Lɛ lɛ wʊ kpɛ a gboro yi a be chen.

eng: The man from whom the demons had gone begged to come along with Jesus as one of his disciples. Jesus sent him away, saying.
local: Ka a daba 'lɔ pɔ a sɩdɛbɛ na yi sʊɔr a Yesu ɩka ba 'mataa chen, tɩchɛ a Yesu lɩɛb wʊ bar tɩ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ.

eng: Return home and tell the story of what God has done for you. So he went throughout the city proclaiming what Jesus had done for him.
local: Lɩɛb kul a fʊ yir tɩ man a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na ɩ kʊ fʊ. Lɛ lɛ ka a daba kul a tẽe pɔ tɩ mana kʊrɔ a nɩbɛ a lɛ a Yesu na ɩ kʊ wʊ.

eng: When Jesus returned, the crowd welcomed him, for they had been waiting for him.
local: Yesu na wa lɩɛb wa, a nɩyɔɔ tuor de wʊ, bojũu ba dɔ̃ɔ chɛlɛ wʊ.

eng: A man named Jairus, who was a synagogue leader, came and fell at Jesus feet. He pleaded with Jesus to come to his house.
local: Ka daba ban bʊɔlɔ Jairusɩ na ɩ a lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir nɩdier, wa gbur dumo a Yesu niem sʊɔrɔ wʊ yãayãabɩlɛ ɩka wʊ wa a wʊ yir.

eng: because his only daughter, a twelve-year-old, was dying. As Jesus moved forward, he faced smothering crowds.
local: bojũu a wʊ bipɔɔ bʊ̃'yen tɛɛ na nyɛ yuom pie nɩ ayi lɛ kpire. Yesu na de a sɔr chiine ka a nɩyɔɔ mɩl pɔɔ wʊ.

eng: A woman was there who had been bleeding for twelve years. She had spent her entire livelihood on doctors, but no one could heal her.
local: Ka pɔɔ mɩ be be na lo sɩɛ suoro faara yuom pie nɩ ayi, nɩrɛ jaa ba tʊ̃ɔ wʊ sãa ɛ.

eng: She came up behind him and touched the hem of his clothes, and at once her bleeding stopped.
local: A wʊ turo a puor tɩ sɩɩr a Yesu fuu nɛɛ ka a jãɩ ŋmaa ajienaa.

eng: Who touched me? Jesus asked. When everyone denied it, Peter said, Master, the crowds are surrounding you and pressing in on you.
local: Ka a Yesu sʊʊr, An lɛ sɩɩr ma? Ka ba jaa chiir ɩka baa sɩɩr wʊ ɛ, tɩchɛ ka a Pita 'yɛr, N Soro, a nɩbɛ waa naa na dara taa tɩɛr fʊ.

eng: But Jesus said, Someone touched me. I know that power has gone out from me.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Nɩrɛ kɔ̃ɔ lɛ sɩɩr ma na wʊ sa ka n bɔ̃ɔn.

eng: When the woman saw that she couldnt escape notice, she came trembling and fell before Jesus. In front of everyone, she explained why she had touched him and how she had been immediately healed.
local: A pɔɔ na wa nyɛ ka ɩ wʊ kʊ̃ lɛ tʊ̃ɔ sɔɔl ɛ, wʊ mɩrɛ naa wa lo gã a Yesu niem. A nɩyɔɔ bala niem tɩ na wʊ 'yɛr a lɛ jũu wʊn sɩɩr wʊ na wʊ sa ajienaa.

eng: Daughter, your faith has healed you, Jesus said. Go in peace.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, N bipɔɔ, a fʊ sɔɔfʊ sa fʊ naa, kulo nɩ nyã'maar.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, someone came from the synagogue leaders house, saying to Jairus, Your daughter has died. Dont bother the teacher any longer.
local: Yesu na chɛnɛ 'yɛrɛ, nɩrɛ kɔ̃ɔ yin a lɔ̃ɔfʊ nɩdier Jairusɩ yir wa 'yɛrɛ, A fʊ bipɔɔ kpi naa ta lɛ fɩrɛ a wiwile go ɛ.

eng: When Jesus heard this, he responded, Dont be afraid; just keep trusting, and she will be healed.
local: Yesu na wõ a lɛ ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a Jairusɩ, Ta joro dãbãɛ ɛ. Sɔɔ ma dɛ. Wʊn sa naa.

eng: When he came to the house, he didnt allow anyone to enter with him except Peter, John, and James, and the childs father and mother.
local: Wʊn wa ta a Jairusɩ yir waa sɔɔ ka nɩyuo kpɛ be ɛ, tɩ tara Pita, Jɔɔn nɩ Jemesɩ nɩ a bi sãa nɩ a ma kpɛ̃n.

eng: They were all crying and mourning for her, but Jesus said, Dont cry. She isnt dead. Shes only sleeping.
local: Ka a nɩyɔɔ bala na kõnõ ŋmiere a chɛlsɩ, Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ bar a kõn waa kpi ɛ, wʊ gur naa.

eng: They laughed at him because they knew she was dead.
local: Ka ba laara wʊ ban bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a bie kpi naa.

eng: Taking her hand, Jesus called out, Child, get up.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Yesu nyɔɔ a bie nũu tɩ 'yɛr, N bie, ir.

eng: Her life returned and she got up at once. He directed them to give her something to eat.
local: Ka wʊ sɩɛ lɩɛb wa kpɛ ka wʊ vaa ir. Ka a Yesu pãa 'yɛr, yɩ kʊ wʊ bʊ̃kɔ̃ɔ ka wʊ di.

eng: Her parents were beside themselves with joy, but he ordered them to tell no one what had happened.
local: Ka a sãa naa ma nyɔɔ nɛɛ, tɩchɛ ka a Yesu kpãkpãan ba ɩka ba taa ɩrɛ wa 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ nɩrɛ jaa a lɛ na ɩ ɛ.

eng: After these things, the Lord commissioned seventy-two others and sent them on ahead in pairs to every city and place he was about to go.
local: A nyã puor Yesu lɛ ir naa nɩbɛ lɩjaata nɩ pie nɩ ayi bamɩne go, na wʊ tʊ̃ ba ayir yir ka ba de nie chen a tẽn nɩ a jiir ala a jaa wʊn na yɔ.

eng: He said to them, The harvest is bigger than you can imagine, but there are few workers. Therefore, plead with the Lord of the harvest to send out workers for his harvest.
local: Wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, A puo bɛr ma naa, tɩchɛ ka a bala na ŋmara ba waa ɛ, a lɛ jũu yɩ sʊɔr a puo sɔɔ ka wʊ waan tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ wa 'yɔ̃ɔ a puo pɔ.

eng: Go! Be warned, though, that Im sending you out as lambs among wolves.
local: Yɩ chen, yɩ kaara na fɩka piir ɩn tʊ̃nɔ̃ ka yɩn tɩ kpɛ gbõgborosɩ pɔ.

eng: Carry no wallet, no bag, and no sandals. Dont even greet anyone along the way.
local: Yɩ taa de libie ɛ, bɩɩ wʊɔ ɛ, bɩɩ na fɔɔr ɛ, na yɩ puor nɩrɛ taa wa jaa a sɔr pɔ ɛ.

eng: Whenever you enter a house, first say, May peace be on this house.
local: Yir jaa pɔ yɩn wa kpɛ, yɩ de nie 'yɛr, Nyã'maar wʊ be a yir nyã pɔ.

eng: If anyone there shares Gods peace, then your peace will rest on that person. If not, your blessing will return to you.
local: Ala ɩ nyã'maar sʊɔ ben a yir pɔ, a yɩ nyã'maar na be naa a wʊ sɛ̃, ala ba ɩ lɛ ɛ, an lɩɛb wa a yɩ sɛ̃.

eng: Remain in this house, eating and drinking whatever they set before you, for workers deserve their pay. Dont move from house to house.
local: Yɩ jãnɛ a yir nyɩnɛ pɔ dire na yɩ nyuro bon jaa ban wa kʊ yɩ, tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ sãyar sɛɛn wʊ naa. Yɩ taa lʊɔrɔ yie yʊɔrɔ nɩ ɛ.

eng: Whenever you enter a city and its people welcome you, eat what they set before you.
local: Tẽe jaa pɔ yɩn wa kpɛ, ka ba de yɩ, yɩ dire bon jaa ban kʊ yɩ.

eng: Heal the sick who are there, and say to them, Gods kingdom has come upon you.
local: Yɩ sãana a baalsɩ bala na be be tɩ na yɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba, A Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ gbʊr yɩ naa.

eng: Whenever you enter a city and the people dont welcome you, go out into the streets and say.
local: Tɩchɛ tẽe jaa pɔ yɩn wa kpɛ, ka baa de yɩ ɛ, yɩ yi de a tẽe sãlsɔɔ tɩ 'yɛr.

eng: As a complaint against you, we brush off the dust of your city that has collected on our feet. But know this: Gods kingdom has come to you.
local: A yɩ tẽe tã-uul gba na mar a sɩ gbɛɛ, sɩ kpoor 'yɔ̃ɔ yɩ. Yɩ bɔ̃ɔn a nyãna ɩka a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ gbʊr naa.

eng: I assure you that Sodom will be better off on Judgment Day than that city.
local: N 'yɛr kʊ yɩ ɩka a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ bɩbir, Sodom na sa naa a tẽe 'lɔ̃nɔ̃.

eng: How terrible it will be for you, Chorazin. How terrible it will be for you, Bethsaida. If the miracles done among you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have changed their hearts and lives long ago. They would have sat around in funeral clothes and ashes.
local: Nɛbier ben a yɩn Korajin dẽme sɛ̃, nɛbier mɩ ben a yɩn Betɩsaida dẽme mɩ sɛ̃. A nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ ala ɩn tʊ̃ a yɩ jie, ala ɩ a mɩ ta tʊ̃n a lɛ a Tire nɩ Sidon tẽe pɔ, ban taa sun bʊ̃jarsɩ jɛ̃n tãpɩɛl pɔ lɩɛb ba tɩɛr.

eng: But Tyre and Sidon will be better off at the judgment than you.
local: Tɩchɛ a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ daar, a Tire nɩ a Sidon na sa yɩ naa.

eng: And you, Capernaum, will you be honored by being raised up to heaven? No, you will be cast down to the place of the dead.
local: A yɩn Kapenahum dẽme, ban 'mɔɔ yɩn don saaju bɩɩ? Kai, ban suun yɩ naa kpĩisɩ tẽe.

eng: Whoever listens to you listens to me. Whoever rejects you rejects me. Whoever rejects me rejects the one who sent me.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na chɛlɛ a yɩn na ɩ a n poturbo na chɛl naa a n 'yɛr, nɩrɛ 'lɔ na tɔr yɩ mɩ tɔr ma naa. Tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ na tɔr ma mɩ tɔr naa a 'lɔ na tʊ̃ ma.

eng: The seventy-two returned joyously, saying, Lord, even the demons submit themselves to us in your name.
local: A nɩbɛ lɩjaata nɩ pie nɩ ayi bala wʊn tʊ̃ lɩɛb wa a wʊ sɛ̃ chɩlɛ 'yɛrɛ, Sɩ Soro, a fʊ yuor jũu a sɩdɛbɛ tu naa a sɩ nɛɛ na be yire a nɩbɛ pɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, I saw Satan fall from heaven like lightning.
local: Ka Yesu 'yɛr sɩrɛ jaa lɛ, N nyɛn Satan na yi saaju lo fɩka saa na nyɩɩr.

eng: Look, I have given you authority to crush snakes and scorpions underfoot. I have given you authority over all the power of the enemy. Nothing will harm you.
local: N kʊ yɩn kpɛ̃ɔ ka yɩn nɛɛrɛ waba nɩ lɔ̃n na yɩ tʊ̃ɔ a yɩ dɔ̃dɔ̃mɔ̃ Satan, bon jaa kʊ̃ ɩ yɩ ɛ.

eng: Nevertheless, dont rejoice because the spirits submit to you. Rejoice instead that your names are written in heaven.
local: Yɩ ta chɩlɛ a sɩdɛbɛ na tu a yɩ nɛɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ yɩ chɩlɛ a yɩ yuor sɛb bin be a saaju.

eng: At that very moment, Jesus overflowed with joy from the Holy Spirit and said, I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because youve hidden these things from the wise and intelligent and shown them to babies. Indeed, Father, this brings you happiness.
local: A daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ nʊ̃ɔ kpɛ a Yesu ka wʊ 'yɛr, N dãn a fʊ̃ʊ n Sãa na ɩ a saaju nɩ a tẽe Soro, bojũu fʊ̃ʊ lɛ sɔɔl a yele anyãna bar ka a bala na ɩ a yɛ̃ dẽme nɩ a bala na jãn ba bɔ̃ɔ ɛ, tɩ na fʊ de wiil kʊ bibiir. Ɔ̃ɔ, n Sãa lɛ lɛ nʊ̃mɔ̃ fʊ ka fʊ maal.

eng: My Father has handed all things over to me. No one knows who the Son is except the Father, or who the Father is except the Son and anyone to whom the Son wants to reveal him.
local: Maa lɛ a n Sãa kʊ a yele a jaa, nɩrɛ jaa ba bɔ̃ɔ a Bie ka a Sãa ba lɛ ɛ. Nɩrɛ jaa mɩ ba bɔ̃ɔ a Sãa ka a Bie ba lɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ a bala a maa Nãaŋmɩn Bie na ir, na wiil naa a sãa kʊ ba. Ka ba bɔ̃ɔ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na be.

eng: Turning to the disciples, he said privately, Happy are the eyes that see what you see.
local: Tɩ pãa lɩɛb 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ poturbo tɛɛ, Pʊpɛl dẽme lɛ a bala nɩbir na nyɛ a anyã yɩn nyɛ.

eng: I assure you that many prophets and kings wanted to see what you see and hear what you hear, but they didnt.
local: Bojũu n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ nɩ a nãmɩne dɔ̃ɔ bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba nyɛ a anyã yɩn nyɛ, tɩ ba nyɛ ɛ, na ba ta wõ a anyã yɩn wõ tɩ ba wõ ɛ.

eng: A legal expert stood up to test Jesus. Teacher, he said, what must I do to gain eternal life.
local: Nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ na bɔ̃ɔ a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka wʊ bɛɛr a Yesu kaa na wʊ ir sʊʊr, Wiwile, ŋmɩnɛ lɛ ɩn ɩ naa nyɛ nyɛvʊr kpo kpo.

eng: Jesus replied, What is written in the Law? How do you interpret it.
local: Ka a Yesu sʊʊr wʊ, Bo ba sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ bin? Ŋmɩnɛ fʊ ma kar.

eng: He responded, You must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, with all your strength, and with all your mind, and love your neighbor as yourself .
local: Ka a daba 'yɛr, Nʊ̃ a fʊ Nãaŋmɩn nɩ a fʊ nyãa wʊ jaa, nɩ a fʊ nyɛvʊr wʊ jaa, nɩ a fʊ kpɛ̃ɔ wʊ jaa, nɩ a fʊ tɩɛr wʊ jaa. Na fʊ nʊ̃ a fʊ tɔ sʊɔ nɩtãa a fʊ mãɛ.

eng: Jesus said to him, You have answered correctly. Do this and you will live.
local: Be lɛ a Yesu 'yɛr, Fʊ 'yɛr vɩla, tɩ ɩrɛ a lɛ, fʊn ben be kpo kpo.

eng: But the legal expert wanted to prove that he was right, so he said to Jesus, And who is my neighbor.
local: Tɩchɛ wʊn ba ɩrɛ a lɛ ɛ, tɩ bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ sɔɔl a wʊ mãɛ bar jũu, wʊ sʊʊr naa Yesu, Nɩbuor lɛ ɩ a n tɔ sʊɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, A man went down from Jerusalem to Jericho. He encountered thieves, who stripped him naked, beat him up, and left him near death.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Daba kɔ̃ɔ lɛ dɔ̃ɔ yi Jerusalɛm chiine Jeriko ka a nãnyisɩ tɩ var wʊ be a sɔr pɔ. Ba yaar naa fuusɩ tɩ ŋme ŋme wʊ, ka wʊ vɔɔ chɛ bãlãa naa kpi, ka ba bar wʊ tɩ chen.

eng: Now it just so happened that a priest was also going down the same road. When he saw the injured man, he crossed over to the other side of the road and went on his way.
local: A Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrloro ãsʊɔ mɩ de a sɔr 'lɔ̃nɔ̃, wʊn tɩ nyɛ a daba, wʊ gõl bar tɩ chen.

eng: Likewise, a Levite came by that spot, saw the injured man, and crossed over to the other side of the road and went on his way.
local: Ka a Nãaŋmɩn Puorfʊ yir tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ mɩ waara wa nyɛ, na wʊ mɩ gõl tu jiyuo chen.

eng: A Samaritan, who was on a journey, came to where the man was. But when he saw him, he was moved with compassion.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ na ɩ a Samariya nɩrɛ mɩ tu a sɔr 'lɔ a daba na gã, wʊn wa nyɛ wʊ, nɩbaal kpɛ wʊ naa.

eng: The Samaritan went to him and bandaged his wounds, tending them with oil and wine. Then he placed the wounded man on his own donkey, took him to an inn, and took care of him.
local: Ka wʊ tɔɔ ta a daba pʊr dãa pɛɛn a natɩɛ bar, tɩ maal kãa fɔ, tɩ de fɔbaa lũ pɔɔ. Na wʊ 'mɔɔ a daba dɔɔl a wʊ bʊ̃ɔ ju chiin a sãanba gaa jie tɩ gãn.

eng: The next day, he took two full days worth of wages and gave them to the innkeeper. He said, Take care of him, and when I return, I will pay you back for any additional costs.
local: Wɛr na wa chaar, wʊ den a kpãna libikue ayi kʊ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na kaara a sãanba tɩ 'yɛr, Kaara wʊ, maa wa lɩɛb gɔr, ala ba wa ta ɛ, ɩn lɛ yab kʊ fʊ. .

eng: What do you think? Which one of these three was a neighbor to the man who encountered thieves.
local: Ka a Yesu sʊʊr a daba, A nɩbɛ ata banyãna, buor sʊɔ lɛ ɩ a daba nyã, a nãnyisɩ na ŋme tɔ sʊɔ.

eng: Then the legal expert said, The one who demonstrated mercy toward him. Jesus told him, Go and do likewise.
local: A wʊ 'yɛr, Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na kaa a wʊ nɩbaal. Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Mɩ chen tɩ maal a lɛ nɛ.

eng: While Jesus and his disciples were traveling, Jesus entered a village where a woman named Martha welcomed him as a guest.
local: Yesu nɩ a wʊ poturbo na chiine ba vɩɛn kpɛ tẽbile kɔ̃ɔ pɔ, ka pɔɔ ban bʊɔlɔ Mata ka wʊ de ba na ir ba sãan.

eng: She had a sister named Mary, who sat at the Lords feet and listened to his message.
local: A Mata tara na yɛbɛ ban bʊɔlɔ Mɛɛr, ka 'lɔ jãnɛ a Soro gbɛɛ par chɛlɛ a lɛ wʊn 'yɛrɛ.

eng: By contrast, Martha was preoccupied with getting everything ready for their meal. So Martha came to him and said, Lord, dont you care that my sister has left me to prepare the table all by myself? Tell her to help me.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Mata wʊ yõ sõsoro nɩ a tʊ̃mɔ̃, na wʊ wa tɔɔ ta a Yesu sɛ̃ 'yɛr, N Soro, a ba bã fʊ a n yɛbɛ na bar a tʊ̃mɔ̃ 'yɔ̃ɔ ma ka n sõsoro nɩ ɛ? 'Yɛr wʊ ka wʊ ir wa sʊ̃ʊ ma.

eng: The Lord answered, Martha, Martha, you are worried and distracted by many things.
local: Ka a Soro 'yɛr, Mata, Mata fʊ fɩrɛ na fʊ mãɛ ka suur kʊrɔ fʊ.

eng: One thing is necessary. Mary has chosen the better part. It wont be taken away from her.
local: Tɩchɛ yelbʊ̃'yen tɛɛ lɛ fɛr, Mɛɛr ir naa a bʊ̃sʊ̃ nɩrɛ na kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ faa wʊ yin a wʊ sɛ̃ ɛ.

eng: Some who were present on that occasion told Jesus about the Galileans whom Pilate had killed while they were offering sacrifices.
local: Nɩbɛ bamɩne mɩ ben be a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃, na ba pãa mana kʊrɔ a Yesu a lɛ a paal nɩkpɛ̃ɛ Pilate na dɔ̃ɔ kʊ a Galilee dɔɔr bamɩne, a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ ban be a bɔɔr lofʊ jie.

eng: He replied, Do you think the suffering of these Galileans proves that they were more sinful than all the other Galileans?.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ tɩɛr ɩka bala lɛ maal dɛɛr gar a Galilee nɩbɛ ba jaa, ala lɛ so ban di a dɔɔyɛ nyã bɩɩ?.

eng: No, I tell you, but unless you change your hearts and lives, you will die just as they did.
local: A ba ɩ lɛ lɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka yɩn ba wa lɩɛb a yɩ tɩɛr ɛ, yɩ mɩ na wɛ naa.

eng: What about those eighteen people who were killed when the tower of Siloam fell on them? Do you think that they were more guilty of wrongdoing than everyone else who lives in Jerusalem?.
local: Bɩɩ a nɩbɛ pie nɩ aniin bala a jiwõo 'lɔ na kar lo kʊ a Siloam, yɩ tɩɛr ɩka bala lɛ maal yeldɛɛr gar a bala jaa na kpɩɛrɛ a Jerusalɛm bɩɩ?.

eng: No, I tell you, but unless you change your hearts and lives, you will die just as they did.
local: N 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka lɛ ba lɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ yɩn mɩ baa lɩɛb a yɩ tɩɛr ɛ, yɩ mɩ na wɛ naa.

eng: Jesus told this parable: A man owned a fig tree planted in his vineyard. He came looking for fruit on it and found none.
local: A sʊkpa nyã lɛ Yesu pãa lɔb kʊ ba, Daba kɔ̃ɔ lɛ tara kɔ̃kɔ̃tɩɛ a wʊ puo pɔ, a wʊ wa chen ɩka wʊ tɩ kaa a tɩɛ wɔ̃n bɩɩ, tɩchɛ a tɩɛ ba wɔ̃ ɛ.

eng: He said to his gardener, Look, Ive come looking for fruit on this fig tree for the past three years, and Ive never found any. Cut it down! Why should it continue depleting the soils nutrients.
local: Be lɛ wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ wʊn vɛ̃ ka wʊ kaara a puo, Bojũu, yuom ata nɩ nyã ɩn yɔ ka, ma bʊɔrɔ a wɔ̃mɔ̃ ɩka n pɔr tɩ ma wa bɔ faa. Ŋmaa wʊ lɔb, ŋmɩnɛ an ɩ ka wʊ pii a wɛr ara nɩ.

eng: The gardener responded, Lord, give it one more year, and I will dig around it and give it fertilizer.
local: Ka a 'lɔ na kaara a puo 'yɛr, Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, bar wʊ ka n tuu jilʊ̃ 'yɔ̃ɔ pʊɔr ka yuon waara.

eng: Maybe it will produce fruit next year; if not, then you can cut it down.
local: 'lɔ wa wɔ̃ go, tɩ 'lɔ ba wa wɔ̃ ɛ, ka n ŋmaa wʊ lɔb. .

eng: Jesus was teaching in one of the synagogues on the Sabbath.
local: A Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar Yesu ben a lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir ãsʊɔ pɔ wile ba.

eng: A woman was there who had been disabled by a spirit for eighteen years. She was bent over and couldnt stand up straight.
local: be lɛ a pɔɔ be a sɩdɛbɛ na maal ka wʊ kab gʊ̃mɔ̃ yuom pie nɩ aniin , wʊ maa tʊ̃ɔ dɔl ɛ.

eng: When he saw her, Jesus called her to him and said, Woman, you are set free from your sickness.
local: Yesu na nyɛ a pɔɔ a lɛ, wʊ bʊɔl wʊ naa ka wʊ wa a wʊ sɛ̃, ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Pɔɔ, a fʊ baal bar fʊ naa.

eng: He placed his hands on her and she straightened up at once and praised God.
local: Tɩ na wʊ pãa de a wʊ nũu dɔɔl a pɔɔ, ka a pɔɔ dɔl ajienaa na wʊ pɛɛrɛ a Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: The synagogue leader, incensed that Jesus had healed on the Sabbath, responded, There are six days during which work is permitted. Come and be healed on those days, not on the Sabbath day.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a suur kpɛ a lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir nɩdier Yesu na sãa a baalaa a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar jũu, ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a nɩbɛ, Bibie ayʊɔb ben be ka nɩrɛ na de tʊ̃n tʊ̃mɔ̃, yɩ ma wa ka wʊ sãana yɩ a ber ala pɔ, yɩ ta ma waara a Pɛ̃nfʊ Daar ɛ.

eng: The Lord replied, Hypocrites! Dont each of you on the Sabbath untie your ox or donkey from its stall and lead it out to get a drink?.
local: Ka a Soro 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Yɩn nɛɛr ayi dẽme banyãna, a yɩ buor sʊɔ lɛ maa yuo a wʊ niin bɩɩ a wʊ bon chiin tɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ kʊ̃ɔ a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir ɛ?.

eng: Then isnt it necessary that this woman, a daughter of Abraham, bound by Satan for eighteen long years, be set free from her bondage on the Sabbath day.
local: Ka a pɔɔ nyãna na ɩ a Abraham bipɔɔ, a tɩɩ na lũ yuom pie nɩ aniin , a ba sɛɛ ɩka wʊ for a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar bɩɩ.

eng: When he said these things, all his opponents were put to shame, but all those in the crowd rejoiced at all the extraordinary things he was doing.
local: Wʊn wa 'yɛr a nɩtɔ̃ ka a vĩ kpɛ a bala jaa na bɛrɛ wʊ a nɛɛ, tɩchɛ ka a nɩbɛ chɩlɛ a tʊ̃vɩɛlɛ ala wʊn tʊ̃.

eng: Jesus asked, What is Gods kingdom like? To what can I compare it?.
local: A Yesu pãa 'yɛr, Bo lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ nyɛtãan? Bo lɛ ɩn de man?.

eng: Its like a mustard seed that someone took and planted in a garden. It grew and developed into a tree and the birds in the sky nested in its branches.
local: Wʊ kaara fɩka kɔ̃kɔ̃ɔ bir nɩrɛ na de bʊr a puo pɔ ka wʊ bul na wʊ ɩ tɩɛ, ka a lubil wa 'yɔ̃ɔ choor a wilsɩ ju.

eng: Again he said, To what can I compare Gods kingdom?.
local: Wʊ lɛ sʊʊr ban go, Bo lɛ ɩn de a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ man?.

eng: Its like yeast, which a woman took and hid in a bushel of wheat flour until the yeast had worked its way through the whole.
local: Wʊ kaara fɩka dãbɩl pɔɔ na de 'yɔ̃ɔ jɔ̃ yɔɔ pɔ ka a ir vʊ̃.

eng: Jesus traveled through cities and villages, teaching and making his way to Jerusalem.
local: Yesu wile naa nɩbɛ be a tẽbɛrɛ nɩ a tẽbil ala pɔ tɩ na wʊn chen Jerusalɛm.

eng: Someone said to him, Lord, will only a few be saved? Jesus said to them.
local: Be lɛ a nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ sʊʊr wʊ, N Soro, nɩbɛ bãlãa tɛɛ lɛ na nyɛ faafʊ bɩɩ? Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba.

eng: Make every effort to enter through the narrow gate. Many, I tell you, will try to enter and wont be able to.
local: Yɩ mɔ̃ tu a dɔ̃dɔ̃ bile pɔ kpɛ, n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka nɩyɔɔ lɛ na mʊɔrɔ bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba kpɛ tɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ kpɛ ɛ.

eng: Once the owner of the house gets up and shuts the door, then you will stand outside and knock on the door, saying, Lord, open the door for us. He will reply, I dont know you or where you are from.
local: A yir sʊɔ na wa ir pɔɔ a pãn var yɩ bar be a yõo, ka yɩ ara kpooro sʊɔrɔ, Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, yuo sɩ a pãn. Tɩchɛ ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ yɩ, N ba bɔ̃ɔ yɩ ɛ, bɩɩ a jie yɩn yi ɛ.

eng: Then you will begin to say, We ate and drank in your presence, and you taught in our streets.
local: Ka yɩ pãa 'yɛrɛ, Sɩn nɩ fʊ̃ʊ jaa 'mantaa di tɩ nyũ, a fʊ wiil sɩ be a sɩ tẽe sãlsɔɔ.

eng: He will respond, I dont know you or where you are from. Go away from me, all you evildoers.
local: Tɩchɛ ka wʊn 'yɛr, N ba bɔ̃ɔ yɩ ɛ, bɩɩ a jie yɩn yi ɛ, yɩ tɔɔ yi tɩ chɛ ma a yɩn na tʊ̃nɔ̃ a dɛbɛ.

eng: There will be weeping and grinding of teeth when you see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in Gods kingdom, but you yourselves will be thrown out.
local: Be lɛ yɩn kõnõ ɔɔrɔ a nyɩmɛ, yɩn wa nyɛ a Abraham, Isaakɩ nɩ Jekɔb nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ ba jaa be a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ, tɩchɛ ka a yɩn 'lɔ be a yõo.

eng: People will come from east and west, north and south, and sit down to eat in Gods kingdom.
local: Nɩbɛ na yin a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ purfʊ jie, a mʊrfʊ jie, a jũu nɩ a par na ba wa jãnɛ dire be a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.

eng: Look! Those who are last will be first and those who are first will be last.
local: Sɩrɛ jaa, nɩbɛ ben puor tɩ na ban wa de nie, ka a bala mɩ na be a niem mɩ wa be a puor.

eng: At that time, some Pharisees approached Jesus and said, Go! Get away from here, because Herod wants to kill you.
local: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ pɔ lɛ a Faraseemɩne bamɩne wa a Yesu sɛ̃ wa 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Yi a ka chen jiyuo. A Hɛrɔd bʊɔrɔ na a fʊ kʊfʊ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Go, tell that fox, Look, Im throwing out demons and healing people today and tomorrow, and on the third day I will complete my work.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ chen tɩ 'yɛr a mɔbaa 'lɔ̃nɔ̃, ɩn dii naa a sɩdɛbɛ ka a yi ka n sãa a nɩbɛ baalsɩ a dɩna nɩ bio ka a dayere daar ka n baar a n tʊ̃mɔ̃.

eng: However, its necessary for me to travel today, tomorrow, and the next day because its impossible for a prophet to be killed outside of Jerusalem.
local: Lɛ jaa an ɩ, ɩn chɛnɛ na chiine a dɩna nɩ bio nɩ a dayere. Sɩrɛ jaa lɛ, Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ kʊ̃ kpi ka Jerusalɛm ba lɛ ɛ.

eng: Jerusalem, Jerusalem, you who kill the prophets and stone those who were sent to you! How often I have wanted to gather your people just as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you didnt want that.
local: Gboo yɩn Jerusalɛm dẽme, yɩn lɛ ma kʊrɔ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ tɩ lɔɔrɔ a bala Nãaŋmɩn na tʊ̃ a yɩ sɛ̃ nɩ kʊsɩbɛ. N ma bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka n kaal a fʊ bibiir 'mataa nɩtãa lɛ nʊ̃ɔ na ma kaal a wʊ bil 'mataa a wʊ pɩrɛ pɔ, tɩ yɩ maa sɔɔ ɛ.

eng: Look, your house is abandoned. I tell you, you wont see me until the time comes when you say, Blessings on the one who comes in the Lords name .
local: Yɩ nyɛ, a yɩ yir na lɩɛb dakpolo, tɩ ka n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka yɩ kõ lɛ nyɛ ma go ɛ, tɩ tãn a daar 'lɔ yɩn na 'yɛr, Nãaŋmɩn wʊ sʊ̃ʊ a 'lɔ wʊn tʊ̃! .

eng: When a crowd of thousands upon thousands had gathered so that they were crushing each other, Jesus began to speak first to his disciples: Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees—I mean, the mismatch between their hearts and lives.
local: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ pɔ lɛ, a nɩyɔɔ turo turo tire taa na ba nɛɛrɛ taa, ka Yesu piel kpãana a wʊ poturbo na wʊ 'yɛrɛ, Yɩ gu yɩ mãɛ a Faraseemɩne dãbɩl pɔ, ala lɛ ɩ a ba nɛɛr ayi.

eng: Nothing is hidden that wont be revealed, and nothing is secret that wont be brought out into the open.
local: Bon jaa ba be be na pɔɔ naa kʊ̃ wa yuo ɛ, bɩɩ sɔɔl ka ba kʊ̃ bɔ̃ɔn ɛ.

eng: Therefore, whatever you have said in the darkness will be heard in the light, and whatever you have whispered in the rooms deep inside the house will be announced from the rooftops.
local: Ala yɩn 'yɛr libe pɔ, na yin chãa pɔ, ka ba wõ naa, ka a ala yɩn bʊl 'yɛr be a die pɔ, ban mʊɔl 'yɛr be a gar ju.

eng: Jesus was praying in a certain place. When he finished, one of his disciples said, Lord, teach us to pray, just as John taught his disciples.
local: Bɩbir ãsʊɔ Yesu ben a jie kɔ̃ɔ na wʊ sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn, wʊn wa sʊɔr baar, ka a wʊ poturbo ãsʊɔ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Sɩ Soro, wiil sɩ a Nãaŋmɩn sʊɔrfʊ, nɩtãa lɛ a Jɔɔn na wiil a wʊ poturbo.

eng: I tell you, my friends, dont be terrified by those who can kill the body but after that can do nothing more.
local: N 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ a yɩn na ɩ a n barmɩne ɩka, yɩ ta joro a bala na kʊ a yɩ ãgãn ɛ, tɩ na ba kʊ̃ lɛ tʊ̃ɔ yele jaa ɩ ɛ.

eng: Jesus told them, When you pray, say: Father, uphold the holiness of your name. Bring in your kingdom.
local: A wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩn ma wa sʊɔrɔ a Nãaŋmɩn yɩ 'yɛr, Sɩ Sãa, sɩ kʊ naa a fʊ yuor tɩɩr. A fʊ nãalʊ̃ʊ wʊ wa.

eng: Ill show you whom you should fear: fear the one who, after you have been killed, has the authority to throw you into hell. Indeed, I tell you, thats the one you should fear.
local: Tɩchɛ ɩn wiil yɩ naa a 'lɔ yɩn na joro. Yɩ joro a 'lɔ na tʊ̃ɔ kʊ yɩ, tɩ lɛ tʊ̃ɔ lɔb yɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ a dɔɔyɛ jie. Ɔ̃ɔ, 'lɔ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ ɩka yɩ joro.

eng: Give us the bread we need for today.
local: Na fʊ kʊ sɩ a sɩ bɩbir jaa bʊ̃diir.

eng: Arent five sparrows sold for two small coins?Yet not one of them is overlooked by God.
local: A ba ɩ kobsɩ ayi lɛ ba ma dãn tiitasɩ anũu ɛ? Tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn maa ĩin a ba bʊ̃'yen bar ɛ.

eng: Forgive us our sins, for we also forgive everyone who has wronged us. And dont lead us into temptation.
local: Vɛ̃ a sɩ maal dɛbɛ bar, bojũu sɩ mɩ vɛ̃n kʊ a bala jaa na maal sɩ a dɛɛr. Ta sɔɔ ka bɛɛr kaa nyɔɔ sɩ ɛ.

eng: Even the hairs on your head are all counted. Dont be afraid. You are worth more than many sparrows.
local: Sɩrɛ jaa, Nãaŋmɩn sɔr a yɩ jukɔɔl a jaa bɔ̃ɔ, yɩ ta joro dãbãɛ ɛ, yɩ gar tiita yɔɔ.

eng: He also said to them, Imagine that one of you has a friend and you go to that friend in the middle of the night. Imagine saying, Friend, loan me three loaves of bread.
local: Be lɛ wʊ pãa 'yɛr kʊ ba. Ala ɩ a yɩ ãsʊɔ tara na baraa na wʊ wa ir chen a wʊ sɛ̃ tãsɔɔ bãɩn tɩ 'yɛr, Pɛ̃ɛ ma jɔ̃ nɩ ŋmele.

eng: I tell you, everyone who acknowledges me before humans, the Human Onewill acknowledge before Gods angels.
local: N 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na wa 'yɛr chã a nɩbɛ niem, ɩka wʊ bɔ̃ɔ naa a maa Nɩsaal Bie, 'lɔ lɛ n mɩ na 'yɛr chã ɩka, n bɔ̃ɔ wʊ naa a Nãaŋmɩn malakasɩ niem.

eng: because a friend of mine on a journey has arrived and I have nothing to set before him.
local: bojũu n baraa ãsʊɔ lɛ wa suu a tãsɔɔ nyãna tɩ n ba tara bon na maal kʊ wʊ ɛ.

eng: But the one who rejects me before others will be rejected before Gods angels.
local: Tɩchɛ 'lɔ mɩ na chiir ma a nɩbɛ niem, 'lɔ lɛ n mɩ na chiir a Nãaŋmɩn malakasɩ niem.

eng: Imagine further that he answers from within the house, Dont bother me. The door is already locked, and my children and I are in bed. I cant get up to give you anything.
local: Ka wʊ gã be a die pɔ tɩ 'yɛr, Ta dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ma ɛ, a pãn pɔɔ naa ka maa naa bibiir be gã, n kʊ̃ lɛ tʊ̃ɔ ir kʊ fʊ bon go ɛ.

eng: Anyone who speaks a word against the Human Onewill be forgiven, but whoever insults the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa na 'yɛr 'yɛr kpɛ̃n a maa Nɩsaal Bie na chɛnɛ na nyɛ vɛ̃ kʊ fʊ, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ na 'yɛr 'yɛrdɛɛr 'yɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ kʊ̃ nyɛ vɛ̃ kʊ fʊ ɛ.

eng: I assure you, even if he wouldnt get up and help because of his friendship, he will get up and give his friend whatever he needs because of his friends brashness.
local: N 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ, a wʊ baraa na ba sɔɔ ir kʊ wʊ a jɔ̃ ɛ, tɩchɛ wʊn chɛnɛ ara kpãkpãana, wʊn wa ir kʊ wʊ jã a lɛ wʊn bʊɔrɔ.

eng: When they bring you before the synagogues, rulers, and authorities, dont worry about how to defend yourself or what you should say.
local: Bala wa nyɔɔr yɩ chiin a lɔ̃ɔfʊ yie nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a kpãɛn dẽme sɛ̃, yɩ ta vɛ̃ ka a ɩ yɩ tɩɛr a lɛ yɩn na tɩ 'yɛr faa yɩ mãɛ ɛ, bɩɩ a lɛ yɩn na 'yɛr ɛ.

eng: And I tell you: Ask and you will receive. Seek and you will find. Knock and the door will be opened to you.
local: Lɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, yɩ sʊɔr, Nãaŋmɩn na kʊ yɩ naa. Yɩ kaa yɩn nyɛ naa, yɩ kpoor a pãn wʊn yuo naa.

eng: The Holy Spirit will tell you at that very moment what you must say.
local: Bojũu a daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na wiil yɩ naa a lɛ yɩn na 'yɛr.

eng: Everyone who asks, receives. Whoever seeks, finds. To everyone who knocks, the door is opened.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na sʊɔr, wʊn nyɛ naa, ka a 'lɔ mɩ na kaara nyɛ, ka a pãn mɩ yuo kʊ a 'lɔ na kpoor.

eng: Someone from the crowd said to him, Teacher, tell my brother to divide the inheritance with me.
local: Ka nɩrɛ kɔ̃ɔ na be a nɩyɔɔ bala pɔ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Wiwile 'yɛr a n yɛbɛ ka wʊ ɩ a sɩ põ a kpĩi bonsɩ.

eng: Which father among you would give a snake to your child if the child asked for a fish?.
local: A yɩn na ɩ a sãamɩne, buor sʊɔ bie lɛ na sʊɔr wʊ jãm ka wʊ de waa kʊ wʊ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Man, who appointed me as judge or referee between you and your brother.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr wʊ, Daba, an 'yɔ̃ɔ ma ɩka n ɩ 'yɛr tɔɔrɔ naa põnõ yɩ kpĩi bomo.

eng: If a child asked for an egg, what father would give the child a scorpion?.
local: Bɩɩ sʊɔr najɛl ka wʊ de lɔ̃ɔ kʊ wʊ?.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Watch out! Guard yourself against all kinds of greed. After all, ones life isnt determined by ones possessions, even when someone is very wealthy.
local: Tɩ na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ kaara sʊ̃, na yɩ gu yɩ mãɛ a bʊ̃yɔɔ bɔfʊ pɔ, nɩrɛ bʊ̃yɔɔ ba kʊrɔ wʊ nyɛvʊr ɛ.

eng: If you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will the heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to those who ask him.
local: Ala ɩ a yɩn gba na ɩ nɩdɛbɛ bɔ̃ɔn vɩla ma ɩ kʊ a yɩ bibiir, ŋmɩnɛ lɛ an ɩ ka yɩ Sãa na be a saaju kʊ̃ de a Sɩɛ kʊ a bala na sʊɔr wʊ ɛ.

eng: Then he told them a parable: A certain rich mans land produced a bountiful crop.
local: Be lɛ wʊ lɔb a sʊkpa nyã kʊ ba, Danãa kɔ̃ɔ lɛ dɔ̃ɔ be be, na wʊ kɔ chɛ yɔɔ jaa a wʊ puo pɔ.

eng: Jesus was throwing out a demon that causes muteness. When the demon was gone, the man who couldnt speak began to talk. The crowds were amazed.
local: Yesu dire naa a sɩdɛɛr na be daba yɔ̃ɔ vɛ̃ ka wʊ ɩ wõo. A sɩdɛɛr na wa yi a daba 'yɔ̃ɔ, ka wʊ tʊ̃ɔ 'yɛrɛ, ka nɛ'maa a nɩbɛ.

eng: He said to himself, What will I do? I have no place to store my harvest!.
local: Na wʊ piel tɩɛrɛ ɩka ŋmɩnɛ lɛ wʊn ɩ, Ɩn ba tara jie na bin a bonsɩ ɛ.

eng: But some of them said, He throws out demons with the authority of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a bamɩne 'yɛrɛ, A sɩdɛbɛ nãa Beeljebul lɛ wʊ de dire nɩ a sɩdɛbɛ.

eng: Then he thought, Heres what Ill do. Ill tear down my barns and build bigger ones. Thats where Ill store all my grain and goods.
local: Nɩtɔ̃ lɛ ɩn ɩ, Ɩn ŋmɛr a bobo anyãna bar, tɩ naa mɛ bʊ̃bɛrɛ naa su a chi 'yɔ̃ɔn a bonsɩ a be.

eng: Others were testing him, seeking a sign from heaven.
local: Ka a bamɩne mɩ bɛɛr wʊ kaara na ba bʊɔrɔ jãnɛ na yi saaju.

eng: Ill say to myself, You have stored up plenty of goods, enough for several years. Take it easy! Eat, drink, and enjoy yourself.
local: Tɩ pãa 'yɛr kʊ n mãɛ, ãgãn, pɛnɛ, n tara na bʊ̃yɔɔ ɩn bin kʊ n mãɛ yuon kʊ̃ bɔ̃ɔ sɔr ɛ. Ɩn dire naa, na n nyuro, na n chɩlɛ.

eng: Because Jesus knew what they were thinking, he said to them, Every kingdom involved in civil war becomes a wasteland, and a house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Yesu bɔ̃ɔn a lɛ ban tɩɛrɛ na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Nãa jaa na jɛɛrɛ nɩ a wʊ mãɛ a wʊ nãa na sɔ̃ɔ naa. Yir nɩbɛ mɩ na ir jɛɛrɛ taa kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ 'mataa kpɛ ɛ.

eng: But God said to him, Fool, tonight you will die. Now who will get the things you have prepared for yourself.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Jɔl nyã, fʊn kpin a tãsɔɔ nyãna, tɩ a bonsɩ ala fʊn bin kʊ a fʊ mãɛ, an lɛ na de.

eng: If Satan is at war with himself, how will his kingdom endure? I ask this because you say that I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul.
local: Lɛ mɩ lɛ, ala ɩ a Satan lɩɛb jɛɛrɛ nɩ a wʊ mãɛ, ŋmɩnɛ a wʊ nãalʊ̃ʊ na ɩ ara? N 'yɛr naa nɩtɔ̃ bojũu yɩ 'yɛr ɩka a Beeljebul lɛ n de dire nɩ a sɩdɛbɛ.

eng: This is the way it will be for those who hoard things for themselves and arent rich toward God.
local: Nɩtɔ̃ lɛ an be nɩrɛ jaa na bin bonsɩ kʊ a wʊ mãɛ, tɩ ba ɩ tara sʊɔ Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ ɛ.

eng: If I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul, then by whose authority do your followers throw them out? Therefore, they will be your judges.
local: Ala ɩ a Beeljebul lɛ n tara dire nɩ a sɩdɛbɛ, tɩ a bala na ɩ a yɩ poturbo bo lɛ ba tara dire nɩ a sɩdɛbɛ? A lɛ jũu, bala lɛ na ɩ a yɩ 'yɛr tɔɔrbɔ.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, Therefore, I say to you, dont worry about your life, what you will eat, or about your body, what you will wear.
local: Kaa Yesu pãa 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ poturbo, A lɛ jũu, n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, yɩ ta waala a yɩ nyɛvʊr 'yɔ̃ɔ ɛ, 'yɛrɛ ɩka bo lɛ yɩn di ɛ. Bɩɩ waala ɩka bo lɛ yɩn su pɔɔ a ãgãn ɛ.

eng: But if I throw out demons by the powerof God, then Gods kingdom has already overtaken you.
local: Tɩchɛ ala ɩ Nãaŋmɩn kpɛ̃ɔ lɛ n dire nɩ a sɩdɛbɛ ka a yire wiil yɩn ɩka a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ wa naa a yɩ sɛ̃.

eng: There is more to life than food and more to the body than clothing.
local: A nyɛvʊr gar naa a bʊ̃diir, ka a ãgãn mɩ gar a fɔɔr.

eng: When a strong man, fully armed, guards his own palace, his possessions are secure.
local: Gãdaa na gu a wʊ yir nɩ jɛɛr bomo, a wʊ bonsɩ maa dam ɛ.

eng: But as soon as a stronger one attacks and overpowers him, the stronger one takes away the armor he had trusted and divides the stolen goods.
local: Tɩchɛ ala ɩ nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ wa wa naa na wʊ kpɛ̃mɛ gar wʊ, wʊn faan a wʊ jɛɛr bomo ala wʊn dɩɛl tɩ de a wʊ bonsɩ põn nɩbɛ.

eng: Consider the ravens: they neither plant nor harvest, they have no silo or barn, yet God feeds them. You are worth so much more than birds!.
local: Yɩ kaa a gagasɩ, ba maa bʊr ɛ, bɩɩ ŋma ɛ, bɩɩ tara boor ma bin ɛ, tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn lɛ guolo ba. Yɩn, yɩ gar a lubil.

eng: Who among you by worrying can add a single moment to your life?.
local: A yɩ buor sʊɔ lɛ na waal na wʊ wa de bɩbir bʊ̃'yen pʊɔ a wʊ nyɛvʊr pɔ?.

eng: If you cant do such a small thing, why worry about the rest?.
local: Ala ɩ yɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ a yelbile nyãna ɩ ɛ, ŋmɩnɛ a yɩ tɩɛrɛ a ala na chɛ?.

eng: Whoever isnt with me is against me, and whoever doesnt gather with me, scatters.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ba be a n sɛ̃ ɛ, wʊ tara man nɩ 'yɛr, nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ba pʊɔ ma a sɩ kaala lɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ taa ɛ, wʊ ma yɛ̃yɛl bar.

eng: Notice how the lilies grow. They dont wear themselves out with work, and they dont spin cloth. But I say to you that even Solomon in all his splendor wasnt dressed like one of these.
local: Yɩ kaa nyɛ a lɛ a bonsɩ puul na ma nʊ̃ɔ. Ba maa tʊ̃ ɛ, bɩɩ wob fuu su ɛ, tɩchɛ a nãa Solomon gba nɩ a wʊ bʊ̃tarsɩ ba dɔ̃ɔ su fuu ka wʊ vɩɛlɛ gar naa ɛ.

eng: When an unclean spirit leaves a person, it wanders through dry places looking for a place to rest. But it doesnt find any. Then it says, Ill go back to the house I left.
local: Sɩdɛɛr ban wa dii bar ka wʊ yi nɩrɛ pɔ, wʊ ma chen dal pʊɔ ju tɩ bʊɔrɔ pɛ̃nfʊ jie, na waa nyɛ ɛ, na wʊ pãa wa 'yɛr, Ɩn lɩɛb kul a nɩrɛ 'lɔ pɔ ɩn yi tɩ chɛ bar.

eng: If God dresses grass in the field so beautifully, even though its alive today and tomorrow its thrown into the furnace, how much more will God do for you, you people of weak faith!.
local: Ala ɩ nɩtɔ̃ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn ma pãɛ a bonsɩ puul a mʊ̃ɔ pɔ ka a vɩɛlɛ a dɩna tɩchɛ ka a wɛr chaar ka a bũu di, wʊ kʊ̃ pãɛ a yɩn gar a lɛ ɛ? Yɩn sɔɔfʊ na ba jã bon ɛ.

eng: When it arrives, it finds the house cleaned up and decorated.
local: Wʊn wa lɩɛb wa, wʊ nyɛn a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na kaara yir kaar ban pɩɩr tɩ chɔɔr wʊ sʊ̃.

eng: Dont chase after what you will eat and what you will drink. Stop worrying.
local: Yɩ ta ir nyãa 'yɔ̃ɔ a ala yɩn na di bɩɩ nyũ pɔ ɛ, yɩ ta vɛ̃ ka a yɩ pɩla ɔɔrɔ ɛ.

eng: All the nations of the world long for these things. Your Father knows that you need them.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa, ala puor lɛ ba turo, tɩchɛ a yɩ Sãa bɔ̃ɔ naa ɩka yɩ bʊɔrɔ naa a bonsɩ anyãna.

eng: Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more evil than itself. They go in and make their home there. That person is worse off at the end than at the beginning.
local: Ka wʊ pãa tɩ bɔ sɩɩr ayopõi na tara putuo gar wʊ ka ba chen tɩ kpɩɛrɛ a be. Ka a daba pãa ɩ dɛɛr gar a lɛ wʊn dɔ̃ɔ be.

eng: Instead, desire his kingdom and these things will be given to you as well.
local: Yɩ de nie bɔ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ, ka anyãna a jaa wʊn den pʊɔ yɩ.

eng: While Jesus was saying these things, a certain woman in the crowd spoke up: Happy is the mother who gave birth to you and who nursed you.
local: A nyãna lɛ a Yesu 'yɛrɛ ka a pɔɔ ŋme chɛlma be a nɩyɔɔ pɔ tɩ 'yɛr, Pʊpɛl lɛ kʊ a fʊ ma 'lɔ na dɔɔ fʊ, nɩ a wʊ bɩr fʊn 'yɛn.

eng: Dont be afraid, little flock, because your Father delights in giving you the kingdom.
local: Yɩn piir na ba jã bon ɛ, yɩ ta joro dãbãɛ ɛ, bojũu a yɩ Sãa pʊɔ pɛl naa wʊn kʊ yɩ a wʊ nãalʊ̃ʊ.

eng: But he said, Happy rather are those who hear Gods word and put it into practice.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Lɛ ba lɛ ɛ, a bala na wõ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr na ba turo, bala lɛ ɩ a pʊpɛl dẽme.

eng: Sell your possessions and give to those in need. Make for yourselves wallets that dont wear out—a treasure in heaven that never runs out. No thief comes near there, and no moth destroys.
local: Yɩ da a yɩ bonsɩ de a libie põ nɔ̃n dẽme, yɩn tara naa a libie wʊʊr ala na ba sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ɛ. Ka a yɩ bʊ̃tarsɩ be a saaju, naa kʊ̃ wur ɛ. Nãnyie kʊ̃ gbʊr be ɛ, kpolo ba be be na mʊʊ ɛ.

eng: When the crowds grew, Jesus said, This generation is an evil generation. It looks for a sign, but no sign will be given to it except Jonahs sign.
local: A nɩbɛ na waara 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ka Yesu 'yɛr, A dɩna nɩbɛ banyãna ɩn putudẽme tɩ bʊɔrɔ nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ ka a na ɩ jãnɛ, tɩchɛ ba kʊ̃ nyɛ ãsʊɔ jaa ɛ, ka a Jona jãnɛ ba lɛ ɛ.

eng: Where your treasure is, there your heart will be too.
local: Bojũu, a jie a yɩ bʊ̃tarsɩ na be, be lɛ a yɩ nyãa mɩ ma gã.

eng: Just as Jonah became a sign to the people of Nineveh, so the Human Onewill be a sign to this generation.
local: Bojũu, lɛ a Jona na dɔ̃ɔ ɩ jãnɛ kʊ a Ninive dẽme, lɛ mɩ lɛ a maa Nɩsaal Bie na ɩ a jãnɛ kʊ a dɩna nɩbɛ.

eng: Be dressed for service and keep your lamps lit.
local: Yɩ chɔɔr yɩ mãɛ gu tɩ chʊm a yɩ fãtɩnsɩ ka a lɩrɛ.

eng: The queen of the South will rise up at the judgment with the people of this generation and condemn them, because she came from a distant land to hear Solomons wisdom. And look, someone greater than Solomon is here.
local: A Siba Pɔɔ Nãa na yin a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ daar na wʊ wa 'yɔ̃ɔ a yɩn dɩna nɩbɛ 'yɛr, bojũu wʊ dɔ̃ɔ yi be a tẽtɛɛr wa chɛl a Nãa Solomon yɛ̃ 'yɛr, tɩchɛ yɩ kaa a 'lɔ na gar a Solomon ben ka.

eng: Be like people waiting for their master to come home from a wedding celebration, who can immediately open the door for him when he arrives and knocks on the door.
local: Yɩ kaara fɩka tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ na chɛlɛ a ba nɩkpɛ̃ɛ ka wʊn yi a pɔɔ kulfʊ jie wa. 'Lɔ wa kpoor a pãn ban yuo wʊn a pãn ajienaa ka wʊ kpɛ.

eng: The people of Nineveh will rise up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it, because they changed their hearts and lives in response to Jonahs preaching—and one greater than Jonah is here.
local: A 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ daar Ninive nɩbɛ na ir 'yɔ̃ɔ a yɩn dɩna nɩbɛ 'yɛr, bojũu ba lɩɛb ba tɩɛr a Jona na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr kʊ ba, yɩ kaa a 'lɔ na gar a Jona ben ka.

eng: Happy are those servants whom the master finds waiting up when he arrives. I assure you that, when he arrives, he will dress himself to serve, seat them at the table as honored guests, and wait on them.
local: An ɩn bɔɔsʊ̃n kʊ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ banyãna a ba nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na wa nyɛ ba ka ba chɛnɛ jɛ̃ gu wʊ. Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka wʊn chɔɔr naa a wʊ mãɛ ka ba 'mataa jãnɛ a bʊ̃diir jie ka wʊ põ ba a bʊ̃diir.

eng: People dont light a lamp and then put it in a closet or under a basket. Rather, they place the lamp on a lampstand so that those who enter the house can see the light.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa ba be na chʊm fãtɩn na wʊ sɔɔl bar ɛ, bɩɩ de laa kpa pɔɔ ɛ. Wʊ ma dɔɔl bar a dɔɔl fʊ jie, ka a bala na kpɩɛrɛ nyɛ a chãa.

eng: Happy are those whom he finds alert, even if he comes at midnight or just before dawn.
local: An ɩn bɔɔsʊ̃n kʊ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ bala a ba nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na wa nyɛ ban chɔɔr, ala ɩ gba ka tãsɔɔ põn lɛ bɩɩ, ka nɔrsɩ kõnõ naa.

eng: Your eye is the lamp of your body. When your eye is healthy, your whole body is full of light. But when your eye is bad, your whole body is full of darkness.
local: A fʊ nɩbir ɩn a ãgãn fãtɩn. A fʊ nɩbie wa ɩ, a fʊ ãgãn wʊ jaa ma sɛɛn nɩ chãa. Tɩchɛ ala ba wa ɩ ɛ, a fʊ ãgãn wʊ jaa ma ɩn libe.

eng: Therefore, see to it that the light in you isnt darkness.
local: Yɩ kaara sʊ̃ ka a chãa 'lɔ na be a yɩ pɔ, taa lɩɛb libe ɛ.

eng: But know this, if the homeowner had known what time the thief was coming, he wouldnt have allowed his home to be broken into.
local: Tɩchɛ yɩ bɔ̃ɔ a par, ɩka ala ɩ a yir sʊɔ bɔ̃ɔ naa a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ a nãnyie na waara, wʊ kʊ̃ sɔɔ ka a nãnyie kar a pãn tɩ kpɛ a yir ɛ.

eng: If your whole body is full of light—with no part darkened—then it will be as full of light as when a lamp shines brightly on you.
local: A lɛ jũu, ala ɩ a chãa sɛɛn a fʊ ãgãn wʊ jaa ka a libe bãlãa jaa ba be be ɛ, wʊ jaa na ɩn chãa nɩtãa lɛ a fãtɩn na ma chaar fʊ a wʊ chãa.

eng: You also must be ready, because the Human Oneis coming at a time when you dont expect him.
local: A yɩn mɩ yɩ chɔɔr gu, bojũu a maa Nɩsaal Bie na lɩɛb wa a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ yɩn ba lɛ tɩɛrɛ ma ɛ.

eng: Peter said, Lord, are you telling this parable for us or for everyone.
local: Ka a Pita sʊʊr wʊ, N Soro, sɩn lɛ fʊ lɔb a sʊkpa nyã kʊ bɩɩ nɩrɛ jaa lɛ.

eng: While Jesus was speaking, a Pharisee invited him to share a meal with him, so Jesus went and took his place at the table.
local: Yesu na wa 'yɛr baar, Farasee nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ bʊɔl wʊ naa ka wʊ tɩ di bʊ̃diir, lɛ lɛ wʊ chen tɩ kpɛ jãnɛ a bʊ̃diir par.

eng: The Lord replied, Who are the faithful and wise managers whom the master will put in charge of his household servants, to give them their food at the proper time?.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, An lɛ pãa ɩ a yelmãɛ nɩ a yɛ̃ sʊɔ, a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na 'yɔ̃ɔ ka wʊ kaara wʊ a yir, na wʊ kʊrɔ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ a ba bʊ̃diir a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ na sɛɛ?.

eng: When the Pharisee saw that Jesus didnt ritually purify his hands by washing before the meal, he was astonished.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Farasee nɩrɛ jʊrɔ a Yesu ka a ɩ waa pɛɛ a ba pɛɛfʊ ɛ, ka nɛɛ 'maa wʊ.

eng: The Lord said to him, Now, you Pharisees clean the outside of the cup and platter, but your insides are stuffed with greed and wickedness.
local: Be lɛ a Soro pãa 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Faa nyɛ, a yɩn Faraseemɩne ma pɛɛr naa a ŋmama nɩ a laar dũkur, tɩchɛ ka a yɩ pʊɔ sɛɛn fɔlfʊ nɩ putuo.

eng: Happy are the servants whom the master finds fulfilling their responsibilities when he comes.
local: An ɩn bɔɔsʊ̃n kʊ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ nyãna a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na wa nyɛ wʊn ɩrɛ a lɛ.

eng: Foolish people! Didnt the one who made the outside also make the inside?.
local: Jɔlɔ banyã, a ba ɩ a 'lɔ na maal a yõo 'lɔ lɛ mɩ maal a pʊɔ ɛ?.

eng: I assure you that the master will put them in charge of all his possessions.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, wʊn vɛ̃n ka wʊ kaara a wʊ bonsɩ a jaa.

eng: But suppose that these servants should say to themselves, My master is taking his time about coming. And suppose they began to beat the servants, both men and women, and to eat, drink, and get drunk.
local: Tɩchɛ ala ɩ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ nyãna tɩɛr a wʊ nyãa pɔ ɩka, a n nɩkpɛ̃ɛ kor a waa, tɩ piel pɔɔrɔ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ bala na be a yir, tɩ pãa ma di na wʊ nyũ buu.

eng: Therefore, give to those in need from the core of who you are and you will be clean all over.
local: Tɩchɛ a ala na be a laa pʊɔm, yɩ de kʊ a nɔ̃n dẽme ka a bonsɩ a jaa pãa vɩɛlɛ kʊ yɩ.

eng: The master of those servants would come on a day when they werent expecting him, at a time they couldnt predict. The master will cut them into pieces and assign them a place with the unfaithful.
local: A tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ nyãna, a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na wa dũu wʊ naa a bɩbir 'lɔ wʊn ba tɩɛrɛ nɩ a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ wʊn ba bɔ̃ɔ ɛ. Wʊn ŋma wʊn jaa gbʊl gbʊl, tɩ lɔb wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ a bala na ba sɔɔ de pɔ ɛ.

eng: How terrible for you Pharisees! You give a tenth of your mint, rue, and garden herbs of all kinds, while neglecting justice and love for God. These you ought to have done without neglecting the others.
local: Nɛbier ben a yɩn Faraseemɩne sɛ̃, bojũu bʊ̃bile jaa yɩn da lɔ̃ɔn badʊʊ gba vaar, yɩ ma ir a da ŋmaa tɩ na yɩ ĩin vɩla maalfʊ nɩ Nãaŋmɩn nʊ̃fʊ bar. Ala lɛ yɩn taa irɛ na yɩ ta ĩin a ala na de nie bar ba maal ɛ.

eng: That servant who knew his masters will but didnt prepare for it or act on it will be beaten severely.
local: A tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ 'lɔ na bɔ̃ɔ lɛ a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na bʊɔrɔ tɩ na waa chɔɔr tʊ̃n a lɛ a nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na bʊɔrɔ ɛ, wʊn nyɛn ŋmefʊ yɔɔ.

eng: How terrible for you Pharisees! You love the most prominent seats in the synagogues and respectful greetings in the marketplaces.
local: Nɛbier ben a yɩn Faraseemɩne sɛ̃, bojũu yɩ nʊ̃ naa a nɩbɛrɛ jaa jie a yɩ lɔ̃ɔfʊ yie pɔ na yɩ mɩ nʊ̃ a daa pɔ puorfʊ.

eng: The one who didnt know the masters will but who did things deserving punishment will be beaten only a little. Much will be demanded from everyone who has been given much, and from the one who has been entrusted with much, even more will be asked.
local: Tɩchɛ a 'lɔ na ba bɔ̃ɔ ɛ, tɩ maal bɔr ban ŋme wʊn jaa bãlãa. Nɩrɛ 'lɔ ban kʊ yɔɔ ban bʊɔrɔ na yɔɔ yi a wʊ sɛ̃, fʊ̃ʊ nũu pɔ ban 'yɔ̃ɔ yɔɔ, fʊ̃ʊ lɛ ban sʊʊr yɔɔ.

eng: How terrible for you! You are like unmarked graves, and people walk on them without recognizing it.
local: Nɛbier ben a yɩ sɛ̃, bojũu yɩ kaara naa fɩka yar nɩbɛ na ba bɔ̃ɔ ɛ, tɩ do yʊɔrɔ.

eng: I came to cast fire upon the earth. How I wish that it was already ablaze!.
local: Bũu lɛ n waan ɩka n wa 'yɔ̃ɔ a wɛr pɔ, ɩn taa bʊɔrɔ naa ala ɩ a ben ta 'lɩrɛ.

eng: One of the legal experts responded, Teacher, when you say these things, you are insulting us too.
local: Ka a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwile ãsʊɔ 'yɛr, Wiwile, a anyãna fʊn 'yɛr, sɩ jaa lɛ fʊ 'mataa tʊrɔ.

eng: I have a baptism I must experience. How I am distressed until its completed!.
local: Tɩchɛ n chɛnɛ tara yele ɩn na ɩ Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ sofʊ kaar ka a ɩ ma tɩɛr tɩ tãn ɩn na wa baar.

eng: Do you think that I have come to bring peace to the earth? No, I tell you, I have come instead to bring division.
local: Yɩ maa tãan nɛbʊ̃'yen yele lɛ n waan a wɛr pɔ bɩɩ? Kai, n 'yɛr kʊ yɩ, tɩchɛ ootaa lɛ n waan.

eng: From now on, a household of five will be divided—three against two and two against three.
local: An yi a dɩna nɩbɛ anũu na kpɩɛrɛ yir na ŋman jiir ayi. Ka ata jɛɛrɛ nɩ a ayi ka ayi mɩ jɛɛrɛ nɩ a ata.

eng: Father will square off against son and son against father; mother against daughter and daughter against mother; and mother-in-law against daughter-in-law and daughter-in-law against mother-in-law.
local: Ban ooro nɩ taa, sãa na oon bie ka a bie mɩ oo a sãa, ka a ma oo a bipɔɔ ka a bipɔɔ mɩ oo a ma. Pɔɔ na oo naa a wʊ bidaba pɔɔ, ka a bidaba pɔɔ mɩ oo a wʊ sɩrɛ ma.

eng: Jesus also said to the crowds, When you see a cloud forming in the west, you immediately say, Its going to rain. And indeed it does.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a nɩyɔɔ bala, Yɩn wa nyɛ julõjuur na ire a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ mʊrfʊ jie yɩ ma 'yɛr ɩka saa na wa naa, ka a ma ɩ a lɛ.

eng: And when a south wind blows, you say, A heat wave is coming. And it does.
local: Yɩn wa nyɛ sɛsɛbɛ mɩ na furo duoro jũu, yɩ ma 'yɛr ɩka tʊl na wa naa. Ka a ma ɩ a lɛ.

eng: Hypocrites! You know how to interpret conditions on earth and in the sky. How is it that you dont know how to interpret the present time?.
local: A yɩn nɛɛr ayi dẽme banyãna. Yɩ bɔ̃ɔn a tẽe nɩ a saaju 'yɛr ma 'yɛr, ŋmɩnɛ a yɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ a dɩna 'yɛr 'yɛr ɛ?.

eng: And why dont you judge for yourselves what is right?.
local: Bo lɛ so ka yɩn mãɛ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ tɔɔr a lɛ na tor ɛ?.

eng: As you are going to court with your accuser, make your best effort to reach a settlement along the way. Otherwise, your accuser may bring you before the judge, and the judge hand you over to the officer, and the officer throw you into prison.
local: An ɩ a yɩn nɩ a yɩ sãn sʊɔ lɛ 'mataa chiine, yɩ mʊlɔ wʊ a sɔr pɔ, ka lɛ ba lɛ ɛ, wʊn de yɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ a 'yɛr tɔɔrɔ nũu pɔ, ka wʊ de yɩ kʊ a nɩpɔɔrɔ ka wʊ mɩ pɔɔ yɩ bar.

eng: I tell you, you wont get out of there until you have paid the very last cent.
local: N 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, ala be ɩ a lɛ, faa be na wa yi ka faa yab a fʊ sãn baar ɛ.

eng: Jesus said, How terrible for you legal experts too! You load people down with impossible burdens and you refuse to lift a single finger to help them.
local: Be lɛ a Yesu 'yɛr wʊ, A yɩn Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɛbier ben a yɩ sɛ̃, bojũu yɩ ma tuon nɩbɛ tuor na tɩrɛ tɩ na yɩ maa ir a yɩ nubir gba tɩɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ ba ɛ.

eng: How terrible for you! You built memorials to the prophets, whom your ancestors killed.
local: Nɛbier ben a yɩ sɛ̃, bojũu a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ bala a yɩ sãamɩne na kʊ, yɩn lɛ mɛ a ba yar.

eng: In this way, you testify that you approve of your ancestors deeds. They killed the prophets, and you build memorials!.
local: A lɛ yɩn maal din dãasɩɛ ɩka a yɩ sãamɩne lɛ. Bala lɛ kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ ka yɩ mɩ mɛ a ba yar.

eng: Therefore, Gods wisdom has said, I will send prophets and apostles to them and they will harass and kill some of them.
local: A nɩtɔ̃ jũu lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na ɩ yɛ̃ sʊɔ 'yɛr, N tʊ̃n a n 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ nɩ a n nɩtʊ̃nsɩ a ba sɛ̃, ka ban ir bamɩne kʊ tɩ ge bamɩne.

eng: As a result, this generation will be charged with the murder of all the prophets since the beginning of time.
local: A lɛ jũu, yele tuon a yɩn dɩna nɩbɛ. A Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ bala ban kʊ a kõr jaa, yɩn lɛ na yab a ba sãn.

eng: This includes the murder of every prophet—from Abel to Zechariah—who was killed between the altar and the holy place. Yes, Im telling you, this generation will be charged with it.
local: Piel a Abel jãɩ sɔ̃ɔ tɩ tãn a Jekariya ban kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn bɔɔr lofʊ jie nɩ a puorfʊ jie chara pɔ. Ɔ̃ɔ, n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka a yɩn dɩna nɩbɛ lɛ na yab a jaa sãn.

eng: How terrible for you legal experts! You snatched away the key of knowledge. You didnt enter yourselves, and you stood in the way of those who were entering.
local: Nɛbier ben a yɩn Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe sɛ̃, bojũu yɩn lɛ tara a bɔ̃ɔfʊ pãn bir, yɩn mãɛ ba kpɩɛrɛ ɛ, tɩ pɔɔ var a bala na bʊɔrɔ a kpɛfʊ.

eng: As he left there, the legal experts and Pharisees began to resent him deeply and to ask him pointed questions about many things.
local: Yesu yin a be wʊn 'yɛr a anyãna, ka a Faraseemɩne nɩ a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe kpãkpãana wʊ nɩ suur sʊrɔ nɩ wʊ 'yɛr yɔɔ.

eng: They plotted against him, trying to trap him in his words.
local: Na ba bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ 'yɛr 'yɛr lɩɛb nyɔɔ wʊ mãɛ.

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Things that cause people to trip and fall into sin must happen, but how terrible it is for the person through whom they happen.
local: Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ poturbo, Yelsɩ ala na vɛ̃ ka nɩrɛ ɩ bier ben be na wa, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ jie an tu wa ben pɔ'yɔ̃ɔ fʊ pɔ.

eng: All the tax collectors and sinners were gathering around Jesus to listen to him.
local: A lõpodierbe nɩ a tʊ̃dɛtʊ̃nbɔ lɔ̃ɔn taa na chɛl a Yesu 'yɛr.

eng: It would be better for them to be thrown into a lake with a large stone hung around their neck than to cause one of these little ones to trip and fall into sin.
local: A nɩnyɩnɛ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃, bala 'mɔɔ nɩɛr lũ yɔɔl a nyĩe pɔ daa lɔb a man kʊ̃ɔ pɔ, ala sa wʊn na vɛ̃ ka a bʊ̃bil banyãna ãsʊɔ ɩ bier.

eng: The Pharisees and legal experts were grumbling, saying, This man welcomes sinners and eats with them.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Faraseemɩne nɩ a Mosesɩ nɛɛ wiwiilbe hũhũnõ 'yɛrɛ, A daba nyã diere naa tʊ̃dɛtʊ̃nbɔ ka ba 'mataa dire.

eng: Watch yourselves! If your brother or sister sins, warn them to stop. If they change their hearts and lives, forgive them.
local: Yɩ guro yɩ mãɛ, ala ɩ fʊ yɛbɛ maal fʊn dɛɛr gɔ̃ wʊ, 'lɔ wa de a bɔrfʊ vɛ̃ kʊ wʊ.

eng: Jesus told them this parable.
local: A Yesu pãa lɔb a sʊkpa nyã kʊ ba.

eng: Even if someone sins against you seven times in one day and returns to you seven times and says, I am changing my ways, you must forgive that person.
local: Ala ɩ a bɩbir bʊ̃'yen pɔ lɛ wʊ maal fʊ dɛɛr gbɛɛ ayopõi tɩ mɩ lɩɛb wa a gbɛɛ ayopõi wa de bɔrfʊ, vɛ̃ kʊ wʊ.

eng: Suppose someone among you had one hundred sheep and lost one of them. Wouldnt he leave the other ninety-nine in the pasture and search for the lost one until he finds it?.
local: Ala ɩ a yɩ ãsʊɔ tara na piir kɔbaa ka a bʊ̃'yen wa bɔr, a ba ɩ wʊn bar a kɔbaa forbir ala a pɛchiin baa pɔ, tɩ yʊɔrɔ bʊɔrɔ a bʊ̃'yen 'lɔ tɩ tãn a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ jaa wʊn wa tɩ nyɛ wʊ bɩɩ?.

eng: The apostles said to the Lord, Increase our faith.
local: Ka a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ 'yɛr, Sɩ Soro, pʊɔ sɩ sɔɔfʊ.

eng: The Lord replied, If you had faith the size of a mustard seed, you could say to this mulberry tree, Be uprooted and planted in the sea, and it would obey you.
local: Ka a Soro 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩn tara sɔɔfʊ jaa pʊʊr fɩka kɔ̃kɔ̃ɔ bir, yɩn tʊ̃ɔ 'yɛr kʊ a tɩkpɛ̃ɛ nyãna, Vʊ̃ɔ tɩ ba man pɔ, ka wʊ tu a lɛ yɩn 'yɛr.

eng: Would any of you say to your servant, who had just come in from the field after plowing or tending sheep, Come! Sit down for dinner?.
local: Ala ɩ a yɩ ãsʊɔ tara na gbãgbaa ka wʊ kʊɔrɔ bɩɩ ŋmaara piir, 'lɔ wa yi a tʊ̃mɔ̃ jie wa, fʊn 'yɛr wʊ naa ɩka wʊ wa jãnɛ di ɔ?.

eng: Wouldnt you say instead, Fix my dinner. Put on the clothes of a table servant and wait on me while I eat and drink. After that, you can eat and drink?.
local: A ba ɩ fʊn 'yɛr wʊ naa, Maal a bʊ̃diir fɔɔ na fʊ chɔɔr a fʊ mãɛ wa ara gu ma, ka n di tɩ nyũ baar ka fʊ mɩ pãa di tɩ nyũ ɛ.

eng: You wont thank the servant because the servant did what you asked, will you?.
local: Wʊn puor naa a gbãgbaa nyãna wʊn 'yɔ̃ɔ ka wʊ maal bɩɩ?.

eng: And when he finds it, he is thrilled and places it on his shoulders.
local: 'Lɔ wa nyɛ a pɛr nʊ̃ɔ ma kpɛ wʊ naa ka wʊ 'mɔɔ dɔɔl bɔɔ ju.

eng: In the same way, when you have done everything required of you, you should say, We servants deserve no special praise. We have only done our duty.
local: A yɩn mɩ lɛ lɛ, yɩn wa maal ala a jaa Nãaŋmɩn na 'yɔ̃ɔ yɩ, yɩn 'yɛr naa, Saa sɛɛn ɛ, gbãgbaar lɛ sɩ ɩ na sɩ maal a lɛ na ɩ a sɩ tʊ̃mɔ̃. .

eng: When he arrives home, he calls together his friends and neighbors, saying to them, Celebrate with me because Ive found my lost sheep.
local: kulo nɩ a yir, tɩ pãa bʊɔlɔ a wʊ barmɩne nɩ a wʊ taaba lɔ̃ɔ taa tɩ 'yɛr, Yɩ pʊɔ ma chɩlɛ, bojũu n nyɛ naa a n pɛr 'lɔ na bɔr.

eng: On the way to Jerusalem, Jesus traveled along the border between Samaria and Galilee.
local: Yesu den a sɔr chiine a Jerusalɛm, wʊ kpɛ̃n a Samariya nɩ a Galilee chara pɔ.

eng: In the same way, I tell you, there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who changes both heart and life than over ninety-nine righteous people who have no need to change their hearts and lives.
local: N 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka a lɛ nɛ nɩ wʊ ju mɩ lɛ, chɩl kpɛ̃ɛ na ben a saaju a tʊ̃dɛtʊ̃nɔ̃ bʊ̃'yen na lɩɛb a wʊ tɩɛr gar a kɔbaa forbir bala na ba tara tɩɛr lɩɛb fʊ ɛ.

eng: As he entered a village, ten men with skin diseases approached him. Keeping their distance from him.
local: Na wʊ chiine kpɛ a tẽbile ãsʊɔ pɔ, ka a kɔ̃kɔ̃n pie tuor wʊ a be. Ba ara na tɛɛr jaa.

eng: Or what woman, if she owns ten silver coins and loses one of them, wont light a lamp and sweep the house, searching her home carefully until she finds it?.
local: Bɩɩ pɔɔ buor lɛ tara libibie pie ka ãsʊɔ bɔr, wʊ kʊ̃ chʊm bũu den saar pɩɩr a die wʊ jaa na wʊ wa tɩ nyɛ wʊ bɩɩ?.

eng: they raised their voices and said, Jesus, Master, show us mercy.
local: tɩ gboolo bʊɔlɔ, Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ Yesu, kaa sɩ nɩbaal.

eng: When she finds it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, Celebrate with me because Ive found my lost coin.
local: 'Lɔ wa nyɛ wʊ, wʊ ma bʊɔl a wʊ barmɩne nɩ a wʊ taaba lɔ̃ɔ taa tɩ 'yɛr, Yɩ pʊɔ ma chɩlɛ, n nyɛ naa a n libir na bɔr.

eng: When Jesus saw them, he said, Go, show yourselves to the priests. As they left, they were cleansed.
local: Wʊn nyɛ ba a lɛ, na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ chen tɩ wiil a yɩ mãɛ kʊ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlorbo. Ban chen a lɛ, ka a ɩ ba sa naa.

eng: In the same way, I tell you, joy breaks out in the presence of Gods angels over one sinner who changes both heart and life.
local: Lɛ nɛ nɩ wʊ ju mɩ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, nʊ̃ɔ mɩ na kpɛ naa a Nãaŋmɩn malakasɩ ala ɩ a tʊ̃dɛtʊ̃nɔ̃ bʊ̃'yen lɩɛb a wʊ tɩɛr.

eng: One of them, when he saw that he had been healed, returned and praised God with a loud voice.
local: A ba ãsʊɔ na nyɛ ka a ɩ wʊ sa naa, wʊ lɩɛb wa de nɛkpɛ̃ɛ na wʊ pɛɛrɛ nɩ Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: Jesus said, A certain man had two sons.
local: Yesu chɛnɛ na 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ ba, Daba lɛ tara wʊ dabil ayi.

eng: He fell on his face at Jesus feet and thanked him. He was a Samaritan.
local: Wʊ lon kpa a Yesu gbɛɛ par tɩ puor wʊ, Samariya lɛ wʊ yi.

eng: The younger son said to his father, Father, give me my share of the inheritance. Then the father divided his estate between them.
local: Ka a bile sʊɔ wa 'yɛr kʊ a sãa, N sãa, põ a fʊ kpĩi bomo ir a n bon kʊ ma. Ka a sãa de a wʊ kpĩi bomo põ ba.

eng: Jesus replied, Werent ten cleansed? Where are the other nine?.
local: Ka a Yesu sʊʊr, A ba ɩ pie lɛ sa ɛ? Ka nyɩnɛ a pie forbir be?.

eng: Soon afterward, the younger son gathered everything together and took a trip to a land far away. There, he wasted his wealth through extravagant living.
local: A ba kor ɛ, ka a bile 'lɔ lɔ̃ɔ taa a wʊ bonsɩ a jaa yi chiin tẽyuo pɔ, na wʊ tɩ bũbur a bonsɩ sɔ̃ɔ a jaa bar.

eng: No one returned to praise God except this foreigner.
local: Ɩ̃sʊɔ jaa ba lɩɛb wa ɩka wʊ wa puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan ka a sãan nyã ba lɛ ɛ.

eng: When he had used up his resources, a severe food shortage arose in that country and he began to be in need.
local: Wʊn wa sɔ̃ɔ a jaa baar, ka a kɔ̃ lo a tẽe 'lɔ pɔ, ka a wõm piel kpɛ wʊ.

eng: Then Jesus said to him, Get up and go. Your faith has healed you.
local: Tɩ pãa 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Ir kulo, a fʊ sɔɔfʊ sa fʊ naa.

eng: He hired himself out to one of the citizens of that country, who sent him into his fields to feed pigs.
local: Wʊ chen a tẽe 'lɔ pɔ nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ yir tɩ bɔ tʊ̃mɔ̃, ka a nɩrɛ 'lɔ ma tʊ̃ wʊ ka wʊ chen a puo pɔ tɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ a wʊ dor bʊ̃diir.

eng: Pharisees asked Jesus when Gods kingdom was coming. He replied, Gods kingdom isnt coming with signs that are easily noticed.
local: A Faraseemɩne lɛ sʊʊr naa Yesu ɩka dabor lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ na wa, ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ kʊ̃ wa ka ba bɔ̃ɔn ɛ.

eng: He longed to eat his fill from what the pigs ate, but no one gave him anything.
local: A jɔ̃ pɛbɛ ala ma ɩrɛ wʊn vɔlaa ka wʊ bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ 'wɔb tɩɩ, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ jaa maa kʊ wʊ bon ɛ.

eng: Nor will people say, Look, here it is! or There it is! Dont you see? Gods kingdom is already among you.
local: Bɩɩ ka nɩbɛ 'yɛr, yɩ nyɛ wʊ ka, bɩɩ yɩ nyɛ wʊ kasɛn, bojũu Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ ben a yɩ pɔ.

eng: When he came to his senses, he said, How many of my fathers hired hands have more than enough food, but Im starving to death!.
