local: Ka a yɛ̃ wa wʊ ka wʊ 'yɛr, N sãa tara na tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ a ŋmɩnɛ a ba ma di bʊ̃diir faa, tɩchɛ ka a maa wa kpire nɩ kɔ̃ ka?.

eng: Then Jesus said to the disciples, The time will come when you will long to see one of the days of the Human One,and you wont see it.
local: Tɩ pãa 'yɛr kʊ a poturbo, Sɔ̃ɔ kɔ̃ɔ waara na a yɩn wa bʊɔrɔ ɩka yɩ nyɛ a maa Nɩsaal Bie nɩ nɩbir, tɩchɛ yɩ kʊ̃ nyɛ ma ɛ.

eng: I will get up and go to my father, and say to him,Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you.
local: Ɩn lɩɛb kul a n sãa sɛ̃ naa tɩ 'yɛr wʊ, n maal dɛɛr Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ nɩ a fʊ sɛ̃.

eng: People will say to you, Look there! or Look here! Dont leave or go chasing after them.
local: Nɩbɛ na 'yɛr yɩ naa, Kasɛn lɛ wʊ be, bɩɩ Ka lɛ wʊ be. Yɩ taa ɩrɛ wa joro turo ba chiine be ɛ.

eng: I no longer deserve to be called your son. Take me on as one of your hired hands.
local: N ba lɛ chɛnɛ sɛɛ ka ban bʊɔl ma a fʊ bie ɛ, maal ma ka n ɩ a fʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ ãsʊɔ.

eng: The Human Onewill appear on his day in the same way that a flash of lightning lights up the sky from one end to the other.
local: A maa Nɩsaal Bie bɩbir na kaara naa fɩka a saa na ma nyɩɩr a saaju yi a lõboor nyã tɩ gbem a lõboor ãsʊɔ.

eng: So he got up and went to his father. While he was still a long way off, his father saw him and was moved with compassion. His father ran to him, hugged him, and kissed him.
local: Lɛ wʊ ir kulo a sãa sɛ̃. Tɩchɛ wʊn chɛnɛ be a nɩdaa waara, a sãa nyɛ wʊ naa ka nɩbaal kpɛ wʊ ka wʊ jotuor a bie kõr tɩ puor wʊ.

eng: However, first he must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.
local: Tɩchɛ ɩn den nie di dɔɔyɛ yele yɔɔ jaa pɔ, ka a dɩna nɩbɛ tɔr ma.

eng: Then his son said, Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you. I no longer deserve to be called your son.
local: Ka a bidaba 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, N sãa, n maal dɛɛr Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ nɩ a fʊ sɛ̃, n ba lɛ sɛɛn ka ban bʊɔl ma a fʊ bie go ɛ.

eng: As it was in the days of Noah, so it will be during the days of the Human One.
local: Lɛ an dɔ̃ɔ kaara a Nowa sɔ̃ɔ, lɛ mɩ lɛ an kaara a maa Nɩsaal Bie sɔ̃ɔ.

eng: But the father said to his servants, Quickly, bring out the best robe and put it on him! Put a ring on his finger and sandals on his feet!.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a sãa 'yɛr kʊ a gbãgbaar, Yɩ pɔɔ fɔɔ tɩ de fuu nyã na vɩɛlɛ wa su wʊ, na yɩ de bɔ̃bile 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ a nubir pɔ, na yɩ de nafɔbɔ wa su wʊ.

eng: People were eating, drinking, marrying, and being given in marriage until the day Noah entered the ark and the flood came and destroyed them all.
local: Nɩbɛ dire naa na ba nyuro ka dɔɔr nɩ pɔbɔ kulo taa, tɩ tãn a bɩbir 'lɔ a Nowa na kpɛ a gbor kpɛ̃ɛ pɔ, ka a kʊ̃ɔ paal wa sɔ̃ɔ ba jaa bar.

eng: Fetch the fattened calf and slaughter it. We must celebrate with feasting.
local: Yɩ tɩ kaa nele na 'wɔr na yɩ waan wa kʊ ka sɩ 'wɔb tɩ chɩlɛ.

eng: Likewise in the days of Lot, people were eating, drinking, buying, selling, planting, and building.
local: Lɛ mɩ lɛ a kaara a Lotɩ sɔ̃ɔ, nɩbɛ dire naa, na ba nyuro, na ba daara lɩɛb daara, na ba bʊrɔ na ba mɩɛrɛ nɩ yie.

eng: But on the day Lot left Sodom, fire and sulfur rained down from heaven and destroyed them all.
local: Tɩchɛ a bɩbir 'lɔ a Lotɩ nɩ wa yi a Sodom bũu nɩ kʊsɩlirsɩ yin saaju lo pɔɔ a nɩbɛ ka ba jaa kpi.

eng: because this son of mine was dead and has come back to life! He was lost and is found! And they began to celebrate.
local: Bojũu a n bidaba nyãna dɔ̃ɔ kpi tɩ lɛ ir ɩ vʊr, wʊ dɔ̃ɔ bɔr tɩ sɩ lɛ nyɛ wʊ. Ka ba piel chɩlɛ.

eng: Thats the way it will be on the day the Human Oneis revealed.
local: Lɛ lɛ a maa Nɩsaal Bie bɩbir mɩ na kaara.

eng: Now his older son was in the field. Coming in from the field, he approached the house and heard music and dancing.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a bidakpɛ̃ɛ 'lɔ be a puo pɔ, na wʊ wa ir waara, wʊn wa gbʊrɔ a yir wʊ wõn bãnɛ nɩ chɛlsɩ.

eng: Jesus also said to the disciples, A certain rich man heard that his household manager was wasting his estate.
local: Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ poturbo ɩka, Taraa sʊ̃ɔ kɔ̃ɔ lɛ dɔ̃ɔ be be, na wʊ tara nɩrɛ na kaara wʊ a bonsɩ, na wʊ pʊ̃pʊrɔ bara.

eng: He called the manager in and said to him, What is this I hear about you? Give me a report of your administration because you can no longer serve as my manager.
local: Ka wʊ bʊɔl wʊ waan wa sʊʊr, N wõn ban 'yɛrɛ a fʊ 'yɛr yʊɔrɔ nɩ, man a lɛ fʊn tʊ̃ kʊ ma, bojũu fʊ kʊ̃ lɛ tʊ̃ɔ tʊ̃ a tʊ̃mɔ̃ ɛ.

eng: He called one of the servants and asked what was going on.
local: Be lɛ wʊ bʊɔl a gbãgbaa ãsʊɔ na wʊ sʊʊr a yelnyɩnɛ na be be.

eng: On that day, those on the roof, whose possessions are in the house, shouldnt come down to grab them. Likewise, those in the field shouldnt turn back.
local: A bɩbir 'lɔ̃nɔ̃, nɩrɛ na be a wʊ gar ju, ka a wʊ bonsɩ be a die pɔ, wʊ kʊ̃ suu tɩ de ɛ. Lɛ mɩ lɛ nɩrɛ na be puo pɔ kʊ̃ kul tɩ de bon ɛ.

eng: The household manager said to himself, What will I do now that my master is firing me as his manager? Im not strong enough to dig and too proud to beg.
local: Ka a tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ mãɛ, Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ ɩn pãa ɩ a pʊ̃pãanyã? A n nɩkpɛ̃ɛ faa man a tʊ̃mɔ̃, n mɩ ba lɛ kpɛ̃mɛ na tʊ̃ɔ kɔ ɛ, a mɩ na ɩn vĩ ɩn na sʊɔrɔ.

eng: Remember Lots wife!.
local: Yɩ tɩɛr a Lotɩ pɔɔ.

eng: Whoever tries to preserve their life will lose it, but whoever loses their life will preserve it.
local: Ɩ̃sʊɔ jaa na bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ faa a wa nyɛvʊr na bɔr wʊ naa, nɩrɛ 'lɔ na lɔb a wʊ nyɛvʊr bar, wʊn tara wʊ naa.

eng: I tell you, on that night two people will be in the same bed: one will be taken and the other left.
local: N 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka a tãsɔɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃, nɩbɛ ayi na gãn gaa jie, ka a Nãaŋmɩn wa de bʊ̃'yen tɩ chɛ bʊ̃'yen bar.

eng: Two women will be grinding grain together: one will be taken and the other left.
local: Ka a pɔbɔ ayi nɩɛnɛ jɔ̃, ka a Nãaŋmɩn de bʊ̃'yen tɩ chɛ bʊ̃'yen bar.

eng: The disciples asked, Where, Lord? Jesus said, The vultures gather wherever theres a dead body.
local: Ka ba sʊʊr wʊ, Sɩ Soro, nyɩnɛ? Ka wʊ 'yɛr, Jie bʊ̃kũu na be, be lɛ sɩdumosɩ ma loro.

eng: The servant replied, Your brother has arrived, and your father has slaughtered the fattened calf because he received his son back safe and sound.
local: Ka a gbãgbaa 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, A fʊ yɛbile lɛ wa ka a fʊ sãa ɩ a ba kʊ nel'wɔr jaa, bojũu wʊ nyɛn a wʊ bie na lɩɛb wa nɩ lãafɩɛ tɩ chɛnɛ kpɛ̃mɛ.

eng: I know what Ill do so that, when I am removed from my management position, people will welcome me into their houses.
local: N bɔ̃ɔ lɛ ɩn na ɩ maa ba lɛ wa tara tʊ̃mɔ̃ ɛ, tɩchɛ ka nɩbɛ chɛnɛ de ma a ba yie.

eng: Then the older son was furious and didnt want to enter in, but his father came out and begged him.
local: A suur kpɛ a yɛkarma ka wʊ jɛ, waa kpɛ ɛ. Ka a sãa yi 'maala wʊ ɩka wʊ kpɛ.

eng: He answered his father, Look, Ive served you all these years, and I never disobeyed your instruction. Yet youve never given me as much as a young goat so I could celebrate with my friends.
local: Tɩchɛ ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a sãa, Nyɛ lɛ ɩn wõnõ a fʊ toor naa tʊ̃nɔ̃ kʊrɔ fʊ a yuom anyãna a jaa, n ba gɔ̃ɔ fʊ nɛɛ jaa ɛ. Tɩchɛ faa mɩ wa kʊ ma bule gba ɩka, n mɩ kʊ ka maa nɩ a n barmɩne mɩ 'mataa di tɩ chɩlɛ ɛ.

eng: But when this son of yours returned, after gobbling up your estate on prostitutes, you slaughtered the fattened calf for him.
local: Tɩchɛ a fʊ bibawõno nyãna na de a fʊ bonsɩ yin ka a 'lɔ nɩ a pɔɔ baar tɩ 'mataa sɔ̃ɔ bar, tɩ lɩɛb wa a yir ka fʊ ir kʊ nel'wɔr kʊ wʊ.

eng: One by one, the manager sent for each person who owed his master money. He said to the first, How much do you owe my master.
local: Be lɛ wʊ bʊɔl a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ sãma dẽme ba jaa na wʊ sʊʊr a 'lɔ na de nie, A ŋmɩnɛ lɛ fʊ di a n nɩkpɛ̃ɛ sãn.

eng: Then his father said, Son, you are always with me, and everything I have is yours.
local: Ka a sãa 'yɛr, Fʊ̃ʊ 'lɔ, fʊ ben a n sɛ̃, ka a bomo a jaa ɩn tara ɩ a fʊ bonsɩ.

eng: He said, Nine hundred gallons of olive oil.The manager said to him, Take your contract, sit down quickly, and write four hundred fifty gallons.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr, Kãlaar kɔbaa lɛ n di a sãn. Ka a 'lɔ na kaara a bonsɩ 'yɛr, De a gãn nyãna jɛn fɔɔ jaa na fʊ sɛb lɩjaayi nɩ pie .

eng: But we had to celebrate and be glad because this brother of yours was dead and is alive. He was lost and is found.
local: Tɩchɛ a sɛɛ naa ka sɩn dãɛnɛ na sɩ chɩlɛ bojũu a fʊ yɛbile nyãna dɔ̃ɔ kpi tɩ lɛ ɩ vʊr go, na wʊ dɔ̃ɔ bɔr tɩ sɩ lɛ nyɛ wʊ. .

eng: Then the manager said to another, How much do you owe? He said, One thousand bushels of wheat.He said, Take your contract and write eight hundred.
local: Tɩ na wʊ lɛ sʊʊr a ayi sʊɔ, A ŋmɩnɛ fʊ mɩ di a n nɩkpɛ̃ɛ sãn? Ka wʊ 'yɛr, Chi bɔtɔr kɔbaa sãn lɛ n di. Ka wʊ 'yɛr, De a fʊ gãn na fʊ sɛb lɩjaa naar 'yɔ̃ɔ .

eng: The master commended the dishonest manager because he acted cleverly. People who belong to this world are more clever in dealing with their peers than are people who belong to the light.
local: Ka a nɩkpɛ̃ɛ dãn a tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ nyãna na ba tara yelmãɛ ɛ, bojũu wʊ tɩɛr nɩdaar. A wɛr nyã nɩbɛ tara na yɛ̃ a ba taaba jie gar a chãa nɩbɛ.

eng: I tell you, use worldly wealth to make friends for yourselves so that when its gone, you will be welcomed into the eternal homes.
local: N 'yɛr na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, yɩ de a wɛr nyã bʊ̃tarsɩ na yɩ ŋman barmɩne ala wa baar, Nãaŋmɩn na de yɩn kpɛ̃n a yir kpo kpo 'lɔ pɔ.

eng: Whoever is faithful with little is also faithful with much, and the one who is dishonest with little is also dishonest with much.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na tara yelmãɛ nɩ a bʊ̃bile, wʊn tara naa yelmãɛ nɩ a bʊ̃yɔɔ. Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa mɩ na ba tara yelmãɛ nɩ a bʊ̃bile ɛ, wʊ kʊ̃ mɩ tara yelmãɛ nɩ a bʊ̃yɔɔ ɛ.

eng: If you havent been faithful with worldly wealth, who will trust you with true riches?.
local: Ala ɩ yaa tara yelmãɛ nɩ a wɛr bʊ̃tarsɩ ɛ, an lɛ na sɔɔ de a yelmãɛ bomo kʊ yɩ?.

eng: If you havent been faithful with someone elses property, who will give you your own?.
local: Ala ɩ yaa tara yelmãɛ nɩ nɩrɛ kɔ̃ɔ bon ɛ, an lɛ na kʊ yɩ a yɩ bon?.

eng: No household servant can serve two masters. Either you will hate the one and love the other, or you will be loyal to the one and have contempt for the other. You cannot serve God and wealth.
local: Gbãgbaa kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ tʊ̃ kʊ nɩbɛrɛ ayi ɛ, wʊn nʊ̃n ãsʊɔ tɩ 'laar ãsʊɔ, bɩɩ wʊn wõnõ na ãsʊɔ toor tɩ ba wõnõ ãsʊɔ toor ɛ. Yɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ tʊ̃ kʊ Nãaŋmɩn nɩ libie ɛ.

eng: The Pharisees, who were money-lovers, heard all this and sneered at Jesus.
local: A Faraseemɩne na nʊ̃ libie na wõ a lɛ a Yesu na 'yɛr, ka ba laara wʊ.

eng: He said to them, You are the ones who justify yourselves before other people, but God knows your hearts. What is highly valued by people is deeply offensive to God.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ ma maal yɩ mãɛ nɩtorsɩ a nɩbɛ niem, tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn bɔ̃ɔn a yɩ tɩɛr. Bon 'lɔ nɩbɛ na 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ɩn Nãaŋmɩn bʊ̃chiiraa.

eng: Until John, there was only the Law and the Prophets. Since then, the good news of Gods kingdom is preached, and everyone is urged to enter it.
local: A Mosesɩ Nɛɛ nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ 'yɛr wa tãn a Jɔɔn sɔ̃ɔ. An yi a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ wʊ jaa, ba 'yɛr naa a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ 'yɛr sʊ̃n ka a ãsʊɔ jaa mʊɔrɔ wʊ sɔr, ɩka wʊ kpɛ.

eng: Its easier for heaven and earth to pass away than for the smallest stroke of a pen in the Law to drop out.
local: An ɩn kɔ̃mɔ̃kɔ̃mɔ̃ kʊ a saaju nɩ a tẽe ka a po tal, gar a Nɛɛ 'yɛr bir na yi lo.

eng: Any man who divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery, and a man who marries a woman divorced from her husband commits adultery.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa na dii a wʊ pɔɔ bar tɩ kul pɔɔ yuo, wʊ maal dɛɛr, ka a daba 'lɔ mɩ na kul a pɔɔ a sɩrɛ na dii bar, mɩ maal dɛɛr.

eng: There was a certain rich man who clothed himself in purple and fine linen, and who feasted luxuriously every day.
local: Tara sʊɔ kɔ̃ɔ lɛ dɔ̃ɔ be be, na wʊ ma su kparsɩ nɩ fuu pɩɛl na wʊ dire bʊ̃nʊ̃n bɩbir jaa.

eng: At his gate lay a certain poor man named Lazarus who was covered with sores.
local: Ka a sʊsʊɔrɔ kɔ̃ɔ yuor na di Lajarusɩ mɩ be be na ɩ natɩnara ma jãnɛ a tara sʊɔ dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ.

eng: Lazarus longed to eat the crumbs that fell from the rich mans table. Instead, dogs would come and lick his sores.
local: Na wʊ ma bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ nyɛ a bʊ̃di pʊr pʊr ala a tara sʊɔ na dire ka a ŋmara loro, ka a baar gba ma wa lɛnɛ a wʊ natɩɛ.

eng: The poor man died and was carried by angels to Abrahams side. The rich man also died and was buried.
local: A sʊsʊɔrɔ nyãna wa kpi naa, ka a malakasɩ wa de wʊ chiine tɩ bin a Abraham sɛ̃. Ka a tara sʊɔ 'lɔ mɩ wa kpi ka ba ũu wʊ.

eng: While being tormented in the place of the dead, he looked up and saw Abraham at a distance with Lazarus at his side.
local: A be a kpĩisɩ tẽe, ka a ɩ dɔɔyɛ jie lɛ, ka wʊ gbã nyɛ Abraham tɛɛr jaa ka a Lajarusɩ jãnɛ a wʊ lõboor.

eng: He shouted, Father Abraham, have mercy on me. Send Lazarus to dip the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue, because Im suffering in this flame.
local: Be lɛ wʊ bʊɔl, N sãa Abraham, kaa n nɩbaal tɩ na fʊ tʊ̃ a Lajarusɩ ka wʊ 'me kʊ̃ɔ nɩ nubir wa 'maan ma jãlbir, bojũu a wõnõ ma naa ka a bũu pɔ.

eng: But Abraham said, Child, remember that during your lifetime you received good things, whereas Lazarus received terrible things. Now Lazarus is being comforted and you are in great pain.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Abraham 'yɛr, N bie, tɩɛr kaa fʊn dɔ̃ɔ be a wɛr pɔ dire nʊ̃ɔ, tɩchɛ ka a Lajarusɩ mɩ dire dɔɔyɛ, pʊ̃pãanyã, wʊ mɩ dire naa a nʊ̃ɔ tɩchɛ ka fʊ mɩ dire a dɔɔyɛ.

eng: Moreover, a great crevasse has been fixed between us and you. Those who wish to cross over from here to you cannot. Neither can anyone cross from there to us.
local: A nyã gba ba ɩ yele ɛ, a bɔɔ kpɛ̃ɛ nyã na be a sɩ chara pɔ, ka a bala na bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba yi a ka tɩ a yɩ sɛ̃ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ ɛ, ãsʊɔ jaa kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ yi a be tɩ mɩ wa a sɩ sɛ̃ ɛ.

eng: The rich man said, Then I beg you, Father, send Lazarus to my fathers house.
local: Ka wʊ lɔb nũu 'yɛr, Yãayãaabɩlɛ lɛ n sʊɔrɔ, n sãa, tʊ̃ a Lajarusɩ ka wʊ kul a be a sãa yir.

eng: I have five brothers. He needs to warn them so that they dont come to this place of agony.
local: n tara na yɛɛr anũu wʊ tɩ kpãa ba ka ba taa ɩrɛ mɩ wa wa a dɔɔyɛ nyã pɔ ɛ.

eng: Abraham replied, They have Moses and the Prophets. They must listen to them.
local: Ka Abraham 'yɛr, Mosesɩ nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ a lɛ ban sɛb bin ben a ba sɛ̃, ba chɛlɛ a ala ban 'yɛrɛ.

eng: The rich man said, No, Father Abraham! But if someone from the dead goes to them, they will change their hearts and lives.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr, Kai n sãa Abraham, ala ɩ nɩrɛ lɛ ir a kũu pɔ tɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba ban lɩɛb a ba tɩɛr.

eng: Abraham said, If they dont listen to Moses and the Prophets, then neither will they be persuaded if someone rises from the dead.
local: Ka a Abraham 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Ala ɩ ba kʊ̃ chɛl a lɛ a Mosesɩ nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ na 'yɛr lɩɛb a ba tɩɛr ɛ, nɩrɛ sɔɔ ir kũu pɔ tɩ 'yɛr an ɩn pɔrɔ. .

eng: One Sabbath, when Jesus went to share a meal in the home of one of the leaders of the Pharisees, they were watching him closely.
local: A Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir ãsʊɔ daar, Yesu chen a Faraseemɩne nɩdiere ãsʊɔ yir difʊ jie ka ba jʊrɔ wʊ.

eng: A man suffering from an abnormal swelling of the body was there.
local: Be lɛ ka daba kɔ̃ɔ na tara a baal vʊ̃ naa ara a wʊ niem.

eng: Jesus asked the lawyers and Pharisees, Does the Law allow healing on the Sabbath or not.
local: Ka a Yesu sʊʊr a Faraseemɩne nɩ a Mosesɩ nɛɛ bɔ̃ɔnbɔ, A tara na sɔr ka fʊn sãa nɩrɛ na bɩɛrɛ a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir bɩɩ a ba tara sɔr ɛ.

eng: But they said nothing. Jesus took hold of the sick man, cured him, and then let him go.
local: Tɩchɛ ka ba ɩ jaa gbulo, ka Yesu de a nũu dɔɔl a daba sãa a wʊ baal tɩ bar wʊ sɔr.

eng: He said to them, Suppose your child or ox fell into a ditch on the Sabbath day. Wouldnt you immediately pull it out.
local: Tɩ na wʊ pãa sʊʊr ba, Ala ɩ a yɩ ãsʊɔ bie bɩɩ a wʊ nadar lon bulo pɔ a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir, wʊ kʊ̃ chen tɩ ir wʊ fɔfɔɔ jaa bɩɩ.

eng: But they had no response.
local: Baa lɛ tara 'yɛr na 'yɛr ɛ.

eng: When Jesus noticed how the guests sought out the best seats at the table, he told them a parable.
local: Yesu nyɛn a nɩbɛ bala ban bʊɔl ɩka ba wa di, ka ba tuur a nɩbɛrɛ jaa jiir jãnɛ, ka wʊ lɔb a sʊkpa nyã kʊ ba.

eng: When someone invites you to a wedding celebration, dont take your seat in the place of honor. Someone more highly regarded than you could have been invited by your host.
local: Nɩrɛ wa bʊɔl fʊ pɔɔ kulfʊ jie, fʊ̃ʊ wa chen ta tɩ jãnɛ a nɩbɛrɛ jaa jie ɛ, amɩne wʊ bʊɔl nɩrɛ na gar fʊ ka wʊn wa.

eng: The host who invited both of you will come and say to you, Give your seat to this other person. Embarrassed, you will take your seat in the least important place.
local: Ala wa ɩ a lɛ, wʊn wa 'yɛr fʊ naa, Ir, ka a daba nyã jãnɛ a fʊ jaa jie. Vĩ lɛ fʊn ir tɩ jãnɛ nɩ a puor.

eng: Instead, when you receive an invitation, go and sit in the least important place. When your host approaches you, he will say, Friend, move up here to a better seat. Then you will be honored in the presence of all your fellow guests.
local: Tɩchɛ bala wa bʊɔl fʊ, tɩ jãnɛ a puor, ka a 'lɔ na bʊɔl fʊ wa 'yɛr, N badaba wa jãnɛ ka a niem, be lɛ fʊn pãa vʊ̃ a fʊ taaba niem.

eng: All who lift themselves up will be brought low, and those who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Bojũu, nɩrɛ jaa na 'mɔɔrɔ wʊ mãɛ saaju, Nãaŋmɩn na siir wʊ naa, 'lɔ mɩ na siir a wʊ mãɛ, Nãaŋmɩn na 'mɔɔ wʊn saaju.

eng: Then Jesus said to the person who had invited him, When you host a lunch or dinner, dont invite your friends, your brothers and sisters, your relatives, or rich neighbors. If you do, they will invite you in return and that will be your reward.
local: Ka Yesu pãa 'yɛr kʊ a 'lɔ na bʊɔl wʊ, Fʊ̃ʊ wa bʊɔl nɩrɛ ɩka wʊ wa di, taa wa bʊɔlɔ a fʊ barmɩne, bɩɩ a fʊ yɛɛr, bɩɩ a fʊ bal nɩbɛ, bɩɩ a fʊ tɔ dẽme bala na ɩ tara dẽme ɛ. Ala ɩ fʊ ɩn a lɛ, ban bʊɔl fʊn daar kɔ̃ɔ tɩ yaa a sãn.

eng: Instead, when you give a banquet, invite the poor, crippled, lame, and blind.
local: Tɩchɛ fʊ̃ʊ wa bʊɔlɔ nɩbɛ ɩka ba wa di, bʊɔl a nɔ̃n dẽme, nɩ a gbɛrsɩ, nɩ a nɩbaalsɩ, nɩ a jɔ̃n.

eng: And you will be blessed because they cant repay you. Instead, you will be repaid when the just are resurrected.
local: Fʊ̃ʊ lɛ Nãaŋmɩn na sʊ̃ʊ. Ban ba tara bon na yab fʊ ɛ, fʊ̃ʊ lɛ na nyɛ sãyar a lɛ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ na wa ir a kũu pɔ ɩ vʊ ɛ.

eng: When one of the dinner guests heard Jesus remarks, he said to Jesus, Happy are those who will feast in Gods kingdom.
local: Ka a bala na lɔ̃ɔ dire ãsʊɔ na wõ a nɩtɔ̃, na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a Yesu, An ɩn bɔɔsʊ̃n kʊ a 'lɔ na wa jãnɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ dire.

eng: Jesus replied, A certain man hosted a large dinner and invited many people.
local: Be lɛ a Yesu lɔb a sʊkpa nyã, Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ kɔ̃ɔ lɛ dɔ̃ɔ chɔɔrɔ na wʊ bʊɔlɔ nɩyɔɔ ɩka ba wa di.

eng: When it was time for the dinner to begin, he sent his servant to tell the invited guests, Come! The dinner is now ready.
local: A sɔ̃ɔ na wa ta, wʊ tʊ̃n a wʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ ɩka wʊ chen tɩ 'yɛr a bala wʊn bʊɔl ɩka wʊ maal yele a jaa baar.

eng: One by one, they all began to make excuses. The first one told him, I bought a farm and must go and see it. Please excuse me.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a ba ãsʊɔ jaa 'yɛr ɩka ba kʊ̃ nyɛ jie ɛ. Ka a 'lɔ ban dɔ̃ɔ ta ka wʊ 'yɛr, N dãn popaalaa naa chiine ɩka n tɩ kaa, n kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ tɩ ɛ.

eng: Another said, I bought five teams of oxen, and Im going to check on them. Please excuse me.
local: Ka a ãsʊɔ mɩ 'yɛr, N dãn narkʊɔrsɩ pie naa jãna n kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ tɩ ɛ.

eng: Another said, I just got married, so I cant come.
local: Ka a ãsʊɔ mɩ 'yɛr, Pɔɔ lɛ n de n kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ tɩ be ɛ.

eng: When he returned, the servant reported these excuses to his master. The master of the house became angry and said to his servant, Go quickly to the citys streets, the busy ones and the side streets, and bring the poor, crippled, blind, and lame.
local: A tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ lɩɛb wa 'yɛr a nɩtɔ̃ kʊ a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, ka a suur kpɛ a nɩkpɛ̃ɛ ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃, Lɩɛb chen fɔfɔɔ jaa a tẽe sãlsɔɔ nɩ a kõkorsɩ pɔ, na fʊ tɩ bʊɔl a nɔ̃n dẽme, a gbɛrsɩ, jɔ̃n nɩ a nɩbaalsɩ waan.

eng: The servant said, Master, your instructions have been followed and there is still room.
local: Ka a tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ wa 'yɛr, Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, n ɩ naa a lɛ fʊn 'yɛr ɩka n ɩ, tɩchɛ ka a jie chɛnɛ be be.

eng: The master said to the servant, Go to the highways and back alleys and urge people to come in so that my house will be filled.
local: Be lɛ a nɩkpɛ̃ɛ pãa lɛ 'yɛr kʊ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃, Yi chen a sɔɩ pɔ, nɩ a sɛɛr pɔ tɩ bʊɔl nɩbɛ waan ka n yir wa sɛɛ.

eng: I tell you, not one of those who were invited will taste my dinner.
local: N 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka ãsʊɔ jaa kʊ̃ lɛ̃ maa ɛ. .

eng: Large crowds were traveling with Jesus. Turning to them, he said.
local: Yesu nɩ a nɩyɔɔ 'mantaa chiine, ka wʊ lɩɛb kaa ba tɩ 'yɛr.

eng: Whoever comes to me and doesnt hate father and mother, spouse and children, and brothers and sisters—yes, even ones own life—cannot be my disciple.
local: Ala ɩ nɩrɛ na wa naa a n sɛ̃, na waa 'laar a wʊ sãa nɩ a wʊ ma, nɩ a wʊ pɔɔ, a wʊ bibiir, a wʊ yɛɛr nɩ a wʊ yɛpɔbɔ 'matãan a wʊ mãɛ nyɛvʊr ɛ, wʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ ɩ a n poturo ɛ.

eng: Whoever doesnt carry their own cross and follow me cannot be my disciple.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ mɩ na ba de a wʊ daa tuo turo nɩ ma ɛ, wʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ ɩ a n poturo ɛ.

eng: If one of you wanted to build a tower, wouldnt you first sit down and calculate the cost, to determine whether you have enough money to complete it?.
local: Ala ɩ a yɩ ãsʊɔ bʊɔrɔ na yir kpɛ̃ɛ mɛfʊ, a ba ɩ wʊn den nie jãnɛ guor kaa ala ɩ wʊ tara naa a libie na tʊ̃ɔ mɛn baar ɛ?.

eng: Otherwise, when you have laid the foundation but couldnt finish the tower, all who see it will begin to belittle you.
local: Na wʊ taa kpar a yir tɩ ba tʊ̃ɔ baar ɛ, ka a wʊ taaba nyɛ laara wʊ.

eng: They will say, Heres the person who began construction and couldnt complete it.
local: 'yɛrɛ, Jaal piel a mɛfʊ tɩ na waa baar ɛ.

eng: Or what king would go to war against another king without first sitting down to consider whether his ten thousand soldiers could go up against the twenty thousand coming against him?.
local: Bɩɩ nãbuor lɛ bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ tɩ jɛb a wʊ nãa tɔ sʊɔ na waa de nie jãnɛ kaa ɩka wʊn tʊ̃ɔ de a wʊ nɩbɛ turo pie tɩ jɛb a 'lɔ na waara nɩ a wʊ nɩbɛ turo lɩjɛr ɔ?.

eng: And if he didnt think he could win, he would send a representative to discuss terms of peace while his enemy was still a long way off.
local: Ala ɩ wʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ ɛ, wʊn tʊ̃n nɩbɛ ka ba chen tɩ 'maal a nãa 'lɔ na chɛnɛ be a tɛɛr.

eng: In the same way, none of you who are unwilling to give up all of your possessions can be my disciple.
local: Lɛ mɩ lɛ, a yɩ ãsʊɔ jaa baa bar a wʊ bonsɩ a jaa ɛ, wʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ ɩ a n poturo ɛ.

eng: Salt is good. But if salt loses its flavor, how will it become salty again?.
local: Nyaar ɩ naa, tɩchɛ a wʊ nʊ̃ɔ wa baar, ŋmɩnɛ ban lɛ ɩ wʊ ka wʊ lɛ nʊ̃mɔ̃ go?.

eng: It has no value, neither for the soil nor for the manure pile. People throw it away. Whoever has ears to hear should pay attention.
local: Wa vɩɛl kʊ a tãpuor tãna ɛ, ban lɔb bar. Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na tara toor wõnõ, vɛ̃ ka wʊ wõ naa.

eng: Jesus entered Jericho and was passing through town.
local: Yesu kpɛ̃n a Jeriko tẽe pɔ tala.

eng: A man there named Zacchaeus, a ruler among tax collectors, was rich.
local: Ka a daba kɔ̃ɔ be be ban bʊɔlɔ Jakiyas, 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a lõpodierbe nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na wʊ ɩ tara sʊɔ.

eng: He was trying to see who Jesus was, but, being a short man, he couldnt because of the crowd.
local: Wʊ bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka wʊ nyɛ Yesu nɩ nɩbir, tɩchɛ ka a nɩbɛ waa, a wʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ nyɛ wʊ ɛ, bojũu nɩŋmaa lɛ wʊ ɩ.

eng: So he ran ahead and climbed up a sycamore tree so he could see Jesus, who was about to pass that way.
local: Be lɛ wʊ de nie jo chen tɩ do kɔ̃kɔ̃ɔ tɩɛ ju ɩka wʊ nyɛ a Yesu an ɩ a be lɛ wʊ tu tala.

eng: When Jesus came to that spot, he looked up and said, Zacchaeus, come down at once. I must stay in your home today.
local: Yesu na wa ta a tɩɛ pĩlem, wʊ dʊɔn bʊɔl wʊ, Jakiyas wa suu fɔɔ, a fʊ yir lɛ ɩn gã dɩna.

eng: So Zacchaeus came down at once, happy to welcome Jesus.
local: Ka a Jakiyas suu fɔɔ jaa na wʊ tuor de a Yesu nɩ pʊpɛl.

eng: Everyone who saw this grumbled, saying, He has gone to be the guest of a sinner.
local: A nɩbɛ ba jaa nyɛn a lɛ na ba piel hũhũnõ 'yɛrɛ, Wʊ ben na tɩ kpɛ a yelbe-ɩrɛ nyãna sãan.

eng: Zacchaeus stopped and said to the Lord, Look, Lord, I give half of my possessions to the poor. And if I have cheated anyone, I repay them four times as much.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Jakiyas ir ara tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a Yesu, Nyɛ n Soro, ɩn põ naa a n bʊ̃tarsɩ ŋmaa, naa kʊ a nɔ̃n dẽme, ala ɩ n mɩ fɔl nɩrɛ jaa bon, ɩn yab a lɛ dɔɔl gbɛɛ anaar.

eng: Jesus said to him, Today, salvation has come to this household because he too is a son of Abraham.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Dɩna faafʊ wan a yir nyã pɔ, bojũu a daba nyã mɩ ɩn a Abraham bie.

eng: The Human Onecame to seek and save the lost.
local: Bojũu a maa Nɩsaal Bie wa naa ɩka n wa bɔ na n faa a bala na bɔr.

eng: As they listened to this, Jesus told them another parable because he was near Jerusalem and they thought Gods kingdom would appear right away.
local: Ban chɛlɛ a anyãna ka wʊ lɔb sʊkpa kʊ ba, bojũu ba gbʊrɔ naa a Jerusalɛm ka nɩbɛ tɩɛr ɩka a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ na tɔɔn daa yi ajienaa.

eng: He said, A certain man who was born into royalty went to a distant land to receive his kingdom and then return.
local: Wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Nãbie ãsʊɔ lɛ ir na chen a tẽtɛɛr ãsʊɔ ka ba tɩ su wʊ nãa, ka wʊ lɩɛb wa.

eng: He called together ten servants and gave each of them money worth four months wages.He said, Do business with this until I return.
local: Na wʊ bʊɔl a wʊ gbãgbaar pie na wʊ de a sɛlbie pie na wʊ kʊ ba tɩ 'yɛr, Yɩ de a libie anyãna tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ, wa tãn a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ ɩn na lɩɛb wa.

eng: His citizens hated him, so they sent a representative after him who said, We dont want this man to be our king.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a wʊ tẽe dẽme 'laar wʊ, ir nɩbɛ tʊ̃ wʊn chen baar ka ba tɩ 'yɛr ɩka, Baa bʊɔrɔ a daba nyã ɩka wʊ wa ɩ a ba nãa ɛ.

eng: After receiving his kingdom, he returned and called the servants to whom he had given the money to find out how much they had earned.
local: Wʊ din a nãa na wʊ lɩɛb wa a wʊ tẽe, wʊ wa tʊ̃n bʊɔl a wʊ gbãgbaar bala wʊn kʊ a libie ka ba wa a wʊ sʊʊr kaa a lɛ ban tʊ̃n a libie.

eng: The first servant came forward and said, Your money has earned a return of one thousand percent.
local: A 'lɔ na de nie wa 'yɛr naa, Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, a fʊ libir dɔɔn pie dɔɔl.

eng: The king replied, Excellent! You are a good servant. Because you have been faithful in a small matter, you will have authority over ten cities.
local: Ka a nɩkpɛ̃ɛ 'yɛr, Fʊ maal vɩla fʊn ben tara yelmãɛ nɩ a bʊ̃bile nyã, ɩn vɛ̃ naa ka fʊ kaara tẽn pie.

eng: The second servant came and said, Master, your money has made a return of five hundred percent.
local: A ayi sʊɔ wa 'yɛr, Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, a fʊ libir mɩ dɔɔn anũu dɔɔl.

eng: To this one, the king said, You will have authority over five cities.
local: Ka a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ 'yɛr, Mɩ kaara tẽn anũu.

eng: Another servant came and said, Master, here is your money. I wrapped it up in a scarf for safekeeping.
local: Ka ata sʊɔ mɩ wa 'yɛr, Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ nyɛ a fʊ libir ɩn lũ 'yɔ̃ɔ fɔbaa pɔ bin.

eng: I was afraid of you because you are a stern man. You withdraw what you havent deposited and you harvest what you havent planted.
local: N joro fʊn dãbãɛ, bojũu a fʊ yele kpɛ̃mɛ naa. Fʊ ma den a jie 'lɔ fʊn ba bin ɛ, na fʊ ŋma a jie 'lɔ fʊn ba bʊr ɛ.

eng: The king replied, I will judge you by the words of your own mouth, you worthless servant! You knew, did you, that Im a stern man, withdrawing what I didnt deposit, and harvesting what I didnt plant?.
local: Ka a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ 'yɛr, A fʊ nɛɛ 'yɛrbie lɛ ɩn de tɔɔr a fʊ 'yɛr, gbãgbadɛɛr lɛ fʊ ɩ. Fʊ̃ʊ mãɛ bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka a n yele kpɛ̃mɛ naa, ma de a be ɩn ba bin ɛ, na n ma ŋmara a jie 'lɔ ɩn ba bʊr ɛ.

eng: Why then didnt you put my money in the bank? Then when I arrived, at least I could have gotten it back with interest.
local: Bo lɛ so ka faa de a n libir tɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ a be a libie binfʊ jie ɛ, ɩn ben ta wa, n kʊ̃ naa de ka bon dɔɔl ɛ.

eng: He said to his attendants, Take his money and give it to the one who has ten times as much.
local: Tɩ pãa 'yɛr kʊ a bala na ara a be, Yɩ faa a libir 'lɔ a wʊ sɛ̃, na yɩ kʊ a 'lɔ na tara a libie pie.

eng: But Master, they said, he already has ten times as much.
local: Ka ba 'yɛr, Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, pie lɛ wʊ tara.

eng: He replied, I say to you that everyone who has will be given more, but from those who have nothing, even what they have will be taken away.
local: Wʊ 'yɛr naa, N 'yɛrɛ yɩ naa ɩka nɩrɛ 'lɔ na tara wʊn lɛ nyɛn dɔɔl, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ba tara ɛ, tɩ tara bãlãa ban den a bãlãa ala.

eng: As for my enemies who dont want me as their king, bring them here and slaughter them before me.
local: Tɩchɛ a n dɔ̃dẽme bala na ba bʊɔrɔ ma ɩka n ɩ a ba nãa ɛ, yɩ nyɔɔr ba waan wa kʊ a ka a n niem. .

eng: After Jesus said this, he continued on ahead, going up to Jerusalem.
local: Yesu na 'yɛr a anyãna baar wʊ de ban nie ka ba duoro a Jerusalɛm.

eng: As Jesus came to Bethphage and Bethany on the Mount of Olives, he gave two disciples a task.
local: A lɛ wʊn wa gbʊrɔ a Betefagɩ nɩ a Betanɩ, tɔ̃ɔ ben be ka ba bʊɔlɔ Olive Tɩɩr Tɔ̃ɔ, wʊ tʊ̃ naa a wʊ poturbo ayi tɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba.

eng: He said, Go into the village over there. When you enter it, you will find tied up there a colt that no one has ever ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
local: Yɩ chen a tẽbile 'lɔ a yɩ niem, yɩn wa kpɩɛrɛ a tẽe pɔ yɩn nyɛn bʊ̃pɔlbile ban lũ, baa do wʊ jaa sɛrɛ ɛ, yɩ for wʊ waan.

eng: If anyone asks, Why are you untying it? just say, Its master needs it.
local: Ala ɩ nɩrɛ wa sʊʊr yɩ naa, Ŋmɩnɛ a yɩ foro a bʊ̃pɔlbile? Yɩ 'yɛr wʊ, A sɩ Soro lɛ bʊɔrɔ wʊ. .

eng: Those who had been sent found it exactly as he had said.
local: A bala wʊn tʊ̃ chen tɩ nyɛ a lɛ nɛ wʊn 'yɛr ba.

eng: As they were untying the colt, its owners said to them, Why are you untying the colt.
local: Ban wa foro a bʊ̃pɔlbile, a bʊ̃pɔlbile dẽme sʊʊr ba naa, Ŋmɩnɛ a yɩ foro a bʊ̃pɔlbile.

eng: They replied, Its master needs it.
local: A ba 'yɛr, A sɩ Soro lɛ bʊɔrɔ wʊ.

eng: They brought it to Jesus, threw their clothes on the colt, and lifted Jesus onto it.
local: Ba waan naa a bʊ̃pɔlbile wa kʊ a Yesu, na ba 'yɩɛr a ba fɔɔr dɔɔl a bʊ̃pɔlbile ju, tɩ nyɔɔ a Yesu ka wʊ do jãnɛ.

eng: As Jesus rode along, they spread their clothes on the road.
local: A lɛ wʊn chiine, lɛ mɩ lɛ a nɩbɛ 'yɩɛrɛ a fɔɔr den a sɔr.

eng: As Jesus approached the road leading down from the Mount of Olives, the whole throng of his disciples began rejoicing. They praised God with a loud voice because of all the mighty things they had seen.
local: Wʊn chen wa gbʊrɔ a jie a sɔr na suu a Olive Tɩɩr Tɔ̃ɔ ju, a nɩyɔɔ bala na turo wʊ piel chɩlɛ nɩ nɛkpɛ̃ɛ na ba pɛɛrɛ Nãaŋmɩn a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ ala a jaa ban nyɛ jũu.

eng: They said, Blessings on the king who comes in the name of the Lord. Peace in heaven and glory in the highest heavens.
local: Nãaŋmɩn wʊ sʊ̃ʊ a nãa 'lɔ wʊn tʊ̃! Nyã'maar ben a saaju, ka a tɩɩr be a saaju jaa saaju.

eng: Some of the Pharisees from the crowd said to Jesus, Teacher, scold your disciples! Tell them to stop.
local: Ka a Faraseemɩne na be a nɩyɔɔ bala pɔ, 'yɛr kʊ a Yesu, Wiwile tãn a fʊ poturbo a ba bar a gɔ̃n.

eng: He answered, I tell you, if they were silent, the stones would shout.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Ala ɩ ba bar a gɔ̃n, kʊsɩbɛ na pɔɔrɔ naa gɔ̃mɔ̃.

eng: As Jesus came to the city and observed it, he wept over it.
local: Yesu na wa tara a Jerusalɛm na wʊ nyɛ a tẽe wʊ kõn kʊ wʊ.

eng: He said, If only you knew on this of all days the things that lead to peace. But now they are hidden from your eyes.
local: tɩ 'yɛr, Ala ɩ a fʊ̃ʊ, fʊ̃ʊ gba ta bɔ̃ɔ a bon 'lɔ a dɩna bɩbir nɩ taa waan nyã'maar, tɩchɛ a sɔɔl naa a fʊ sɛ̃.

eng: The time will come when your enemies will build fortifications around you, encircle you, and attack you from all sides.
local: A bɩbir na wa vɩɛ yɩ naa, ka a yɩ dɔ̃dẽme wa jilʊ̃ yɩ gʊɔr gʊɔr a jie wʊ jaa.

eng: They will crush you completely, you and the people within you. They wont leave one stone on top of another within you, because you didnt recognize the time of your gracious visit from God.
local: Ban nɛb yɩn ŋmɛr nɩ a yɩ bibiir nɩ a yɩ tẽe ba kʊ̃ wa bar kʊsɩr ka wʊ dɔɔl wʊ tɔ ju ɛ, bojũu yaa tɩɛr tɔ a sɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn na waara a yɩ sɛ̃ ɛ.

eng: When Jesus entered the temple, he threw out those who were selling things there.
local: Wʊ pãa tɩ kpɛ naa a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir bɛchaar pɔ na wʊ piel dire a bala na daara bara.

eng: He said to them, Its written, My house will be a house of prayer, but you have made it a hideout for crooks. .
local: Na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, A sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, A n yir na ɩ naa a puorfʊ jie, tɩchɛ ka a yɩn maal wʊ a nãnyisɩ sɔɔlfʊ jie. .

eng: Jesus was teaching daily in the temple. The chief priests, the legal experts, and the foremost leaders among the people were seeking to kill him.
local: Bɩbir jaa lɛ wʊ wile ba be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir, tɩchɛ ka a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ bʊɔrɔ a wʊ kʊfʊ.

eng: However, they couldnt find a way to do it because all the people were enthralled with what they heard.
local: Tɩchɛ baa bɔ̃ɔ lɛ ban na ɩ ɛ, bojũu nɩbɛ ba jaa 'yɔ̃ɔn toor chɛlɛ a wʊ 'yɛr.

eng: Jesus was telling them a parable about their need to pray continuously and not to be discouraged.
local: Yesu pãa lɔb sʊkpa kʊ a wʊ poturbo na wʊ wiil ba a lɛ ban na sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn taa baala ɛ.

eng: He said, In a certain city there was a judge who neither feared God nor respected people.
local: Wʊ 'yɛr naa, A tẽe kɔ̃ɔ pɔ, 'yɛr tɔɔrɔ kɔ̃ɔ dɔ̃ɔ be be, waa joro Nãaŋmɩn dãbãɛ ɛ, waa mɩ kaara nɩrɛ nɩbaal ɛ.

eng: In that city there was a widow who kept coming to him, asking, Give me justice in this case against my adversary.
local: Ka a pɔɔ kʊɔr kɔ̃ɔ mɩ be be a tẽe pɔ, na wʊ ma waara a wʊ sɛ̃ wa 'yɛrɛ, 'Yɛr faa ma a n sãn sʊɔ sɛ̃.

eng: For a while he refused but finally said to himself, I dont fear God or respect people.
local: Bojũu, gbɛɛ aŋmɩnɛ jaa lɛ wʊ ma tɔr, tɩ wa 'yɛr kʊ wʊ mãɛ, N gba ba joro Nãaŋmɩn dãbãɛ ɛ, bɩɩ kaara nɩrɛ nɩbaal lɛ.

eng: but I will give this widow justice because she keeps bothering me. Otherwise, there will be no end to her coming here and embarrassing me.
local: Tɩchɛ bojũu, a pɔɔ kʊɔr nyãna na yʊɔrɔ dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ma, ɩn tɔɔr a wʊ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ ka wʊ ta yʊɔrɔ bala ma ɛ. .

eng: The Lord said, Listen to what the unjust judge says.
local: Ka a Soro 'yɛr, Yɩ nyɛ lɛ a 'yɛr tɔɔr filefile nyã na 'yɛr.

eng: Wont God provide justice to his chosen people who cry out to him day and night? Will he be slow to help them?.
local: Nãaŋmɩn mɩ kʊ̃ 'yɛr faa a wʊ nɩ-irsɩ na kõnõ kʊrɔ wʊ mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ nɩ tãsɔɔ jaa ɛ? Bɩɩ wʊn lɩɛrɛ ban bara?.

eng: I tell you, he will give them justice quickly. But when the Human Onecomes, will he find faithfulness on earth.
local: N 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka Nãaŋmɩn na tɔɔr a ba 'yɛr fɔɔ jaa kʊ ba. A maa Nɩsaal Bie wa lɩɛb wa a wɛr pɔ, ɩn nyɛn nɩbɛ ka ba tara sɔɔfʊ ɔ.

eng: Jesus told this parable to certain people who had convinced themselves that they were righteous and who looked on everyone else with disgust.
local: Yesu lɛ lɔb sʊkpa de man a nɩbɛ bamɩne na tɩɛr ɩka ba ɩn nɩtorsɩ tɩ kaara a ba taaba pɔrɔ.

eng: Two people went up to the temple to pray. One was a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.
local: Nɩbɛ ayi lɛ dɔ̃ɔ do a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir ɩka ba tɩ sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn. Ɩ̃sʊɔ ɩn Farasee nɩrɛ, ka a ãsʊɔ mɩ ɩ lõpodiere.

eng: The Pharisee stood and prayed about himself with these words, God, I thank you that Im not like everyone else—crooks, evildoers, adulterers—or even like this tax collector.
local: Ka a Farasee nɩrɛ 'lɔ ir ara, tɩ sʊɔrɔ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛrɛ nɩ, Nãaŋmɩn n puor fʊn yãan, nɩ ɩn ba kaara a nɩbɛ bamɩne kaar ɛ, nɩfarba, tʊ̃dɛtʊ̃nbɔ, sɛ̃sɛ̃nbɛ, bɩɩ a lõpodiere nyã kaar gba ɛ.

eng: I fast twice a week. I give a tenth of everything I receive.
local: Maa 'lɔ, n ma gal kɔ̃ gbɛɛ ayi daa jaa pɔ, n bonsɩ pie jaa pɔ, n ma ir naa a bʊ̃'yen.

eng: But the tax collector stood at a distance. He wouldnt even lift his eyes to look toward heaven. Rather, he struck his chest and said, God, show mercy to me, a sinner.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a lõpodiere 'lɔ mɩ ara tɛɛr jaa na wʊ 'wor nuur, waa 'la dʊɔ kaa saaju ɛ, tɩ na wʊ 'yɛr, Nãaŋmɩn kaa n nɩbaal, yelbe-ɩrɛ lɛ n ɩ.

eng: I tell you, this person went down to his home justified rather than the Pharisee. All who lift themselves up will be brought low, and those who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: A lõpodiere nyã yele tor naa a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃, ka wʊ lɩɛb kul gar, a Farasee nɩrɛ 'lɔ. Bojũu nɩrɛ jaa na 'mɔɔrɔ wʊ mãɛ saaju, Nãaŋmɩn na siir wʊ naa, tɩ nɩrɛ nyã mɩ na siir a wʊ mãɛ, Nãaŋmɩn na 'mɔɔ wʊn saaju.

eng: People were bringing babies to Jesus so that he would bless them. When the disciples saw this, they scolded them.
local: Nɩbɛ dɔ̃ɔ waara nɩ bibibil a Yesu sɛ̃ ɩka wʊ sɩɩr ba, ka a poturbo na nyɛ a lɛ na ba mĩi 'yɔ̃ɔ ba.

eng: Then Jesus called them to him and said, Allow the children to come to me. Dont forbid them, because Gods kingdom belongs to people like these children.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Yesu bʊɔl a bibiir ka ba wa a wʊ sɛ̃, ka wʊ 'yɛr, Yɩ bar a bibiir sɔr ka ba wa a n sɛ̃, na yɩ ta ŋmaa ba sɔr ɛ, bojũu banyãna taaba lɛ so a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt welcome Gods kingdom like a child will never enter it.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, ãsʊɔ jaa ba wa de a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ nɩtãa a bibibile nyãna kaar ɛ, wʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ kpɛ be ɛ.

eng: A certain ruler asked Jesus, Good Teacher, what must I do to obtain eternal life.
local: A nɩdiere kɔ̃ɔ sʊʊr a Yesu, Wiwiilsʊ̃, bo lɛ ɩn ɩ naa nyɛ nyɛvʊr na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you call me good? No one is good except the one God.
local: Ka a Yesu sʊʊr wʊ, Bãa 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ fʊ bʊɔl ma nɩsʊ̃? Nɩrɛ jaa ba ɩ nɩsʊ̃ ka Nãaŋmɩn tɛɛ ba lɛ ɛ.

eng: You know the commandments: Dont commit adultery. Dont murder. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony. Honor your father and mother. .
local: Fʊ bɔ̃ɔ naa a Nãaŋmɩn Nɛɛ. Taa sɛ̃nɛ ɛ, taa kʊrɔ nɩrɛ ɛ, taa juro ɛ, taa ŋmaa jir kara 'yɔ̃ɔ nɩrɛ ɛ, na fʊ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a fʊ sãa nɩ a fʊ ma.

eng: Then the ruler said, Ive kept all of these things since I was a boy.
local: Ka a daba 'yɛr, A anyãna a jaa lɛ n turo a n bile sɔ̃ɔ wa tãn dɩna.

eng: When Jesus heard this, he said, Theres one more thing. Sell everything you own and distribute the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come, follow me.
local: Yesu na wõ a nɩtɔ̃ ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, A chɛ fʊn yelbʊ̃'yen, tɩ da a fʊ bonsɩ a jaa, de põ nɔ̃n dẽme ka a fʊ tara bomo be a saaju, tɩ na fʊ pãa wa turo ma.

eng: When he heard these words, the man became sad because he was extremely rich.
local: A daba na wõ a nɩtɔ̃ wʊ pʊɔ sɔ̃ɔ naa, bojũu tara sʊɔ lɛ wʊ ɩ.

eng: When Jesus saw this, he said, Its very hard for the wealthy to enter Gods kingdom!.
local: Yesu kaa wʊn tɩ 'yɛr, An ɩn kpɛ̃ɔ a tara sʊɔ na kpɛ Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.

eng: Its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Sɩrɛ jaa lɛ, an ɩn kɔ̃mɔ̃kɔ̃mɔ̃ nyɔɔma na kpɛ kparpĩi bɔɔ pɔ, gar tara sʊɔ na kpɛ Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.

eng: Those who heard this said, Then who can be saved.
local: Ka a bala na wõ a nɩtɔ̃ sʊʊr a Yesu, An lɛ na pãa nyɛ faafʊ.

eng: Jesus replied, What is impossible for humans is possible for God.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Ala na ɩ tuo nɩrɛ sɛ̃, Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ yele jaa ba ɩ tuo ɛ.

eng: Peter said, Look, we left everything we own and followed you.
local: A Pita 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Sɩn bar bonsɩ a jaa, tɩ wa turo fʊ.

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you that anyone who has left house, husband, wife, brothers, sisters, parents, or children because of Gods kingdom.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, nɩrɛ na bar a wʊ yir, bɩɩ pɔɔ, bɩɩ yɛɛr, bɩɩ a wʊ sãa naa ma, bɩɩ bibiir a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ jũu.

eng: will receive many times more in this age and eternal life in the coming age.
local: wʊn nyɛn dɔɔl taa a dɩna nɩ daar kɔ̃ɔ na wa lɔɔn nyɛvʊr na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ.

eng: Jesus took the Twelve aside and said, Look, were going up to Jerusalem, and everything written about the Human Oneby the prophets will be accomplished.
local: Yesu tɔɔn a Pie nɩ Ayi yin chɛ̃chɛ̃ɛ, tɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Sɩ ben duoro a Jerusalɛm, ala a jaa a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ na sɛb 'yɛr a maa Nɩsaal Bie 'yɛr, an tɩ ɩ naa.

eng: He will be handed over to the Gentiles. He will be ridiculed, mistreated, and spit on.
local: Ban tɩ nyɔɔ man kʊ a nɩbɛ bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, ka ba la ma, tʊrɔ ma, na ba chɩr nataar 'yɔ̃ɔ ma, na ba ge ma, tɩ kʊ ma.

eng: After torturing him, they will kill him. On the third day, he will rise up.
local: Ka a ber ata daar ka n ir ɩ vʊr go.

eng: But the Twelve understood none of these words. The meaning of this message was hidden from them and they didnt grasp what he was saying.
local: A poturbo ba bɔ̃ɔ a anyãna wʊn 'yɛr par bãlãa jaa ɛ. A liin ba jaa, baa bɔ̃ɔ a ala wʊn 'yɛrɛ ɛ.

eng: As Jesus came to Jericho, a certain blind man was sitting beside the road begging.
local: Yesu na wa gbʊrɔ a Jeriko, jɔ̃ɔ jɛ̃n be a sɔr kõkoor na wʊ sʊɔrɔ.

eng: When the man heard the crowd passing by, he asked what was happening.
local: Wʊn wa wõ a nɩyɔɔ na chiine tala, a wʊ sʊrɔ, ɩka bo lɛ ɩrɛ.

eng: They told him, Jesus the Nazarene is passing by.
local: Ka ba 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Yesu na yi Najaretɩ lɛ tala.

eng: The blind man shouted, Jesus, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: A wʊ gboolo, Yesu, David Bidaba, kaa a n nɩbaal.

eng: Those leading the procession scolded him, telling him to be quiet, but he shouted even louder, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Ka a bala na de a nie tãn wʊ 'yɛrɛ pɔɔ a nɛɛ, tɩchɛ ka wʊ chɛnɛ gboolo bʊɔlɔ, Yesu, David Bidaba, kaa n nɩbaal.

eng: Jesus stopped and called for the man to be brought to him. When he was present Jesus asked.
local: Ka Yesu tɛɛ ara tɩ 'yɛr ɩka ba tara a daba waan. Lɛ a jɔ̃ɔ na wa gbʊr a Yesu ka wʊ sʊʊr wʊ.

eng: What do you want me to do for you? He said, Lord, I want to see.
local: Bo lɛ fʊ bʊɔrɔ ɩka n ɩ kʊ fʊ? Ka a jɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr, N Soro n bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka n nyɛrɛ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Receive your sight! Your faith has healed you.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Nyɛrɛ a fʊ sɔɔfʊ sa fʊ naa.

eng: At once he was able to see, and he began to follow Jesus, praising God. When all the people saw it, they praised God too.
local: Ajienaa ka a ɩ wʊ nyɛrɛ naa, na wʊ dãna Nãaŋmɩn turo nɩ a Yesu. A nɩbɛ ba jaa na wa nyɛ a lɛ, ba mɩ dãna naa a Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: On one of the days when Jesus was teaching the people in the temple and proclaiming the good news, the chief priests, legal experts, and elders approached him.
local: A bɩbir ãsʊɔ Yesu ben a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir bɛchaar pɔ, wile a nɩbɛ 'yɛrɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ kʊrɔ ba. Ka a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ wa a wʊ sɛ̃.

eng: They said, Tell us: What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority.
local: Na ba sʊʊr wʊ, 'Yɛr kʊ sɩ a jie fʊn nyɛ a sɔr maala nɩ a anyãna, an kʊ fʊ a sɔr nyã.

eng: He replied, I have a question for you. Tell me.
local: Ka a Yesu mɩ 'yɛr, N tara na sʊʊrfʊ na sʊʊr yɩ.

eng: Was Johns baptism of heavenly or of human origin.
local: A Jɔɔn na dɔ̃ɔ suoro a nɩbɛ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ an lɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ, Nãaŋmɩn lɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ bɩɩ nɩrɛ lɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ.

eng: They discussed among themselves, If we say, Its of heavenly origin, hell say, Why didnt you believe him.
local: A ba lɩɛb guor taa 'yɛrɛ, Ala ɩ sɩ ben 'yɛr ɩka Nãaŋmɩn lɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ, wʊn lɩɛb sʊʊr sɩ, ŋmɩnɛ a saa sɔɔ de wʊ ɛ?.

eng: But if we say, Its of human origin, all the people will stone us to death because they are convinced that John was a prophet.
local: Tɩchɛ sɩn mɩ 'yɛr ɩka nɩrɛ lɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ, a nɩbɛ ba jaa na lɔb sɩn nɩ kʊsɩbɛ, bojũu ba jaa sɔɔn de ɩka Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ lɛ wʊ ɩ.

eng: They answered that they didnt know where it came from.
local: Be lɛ a ba 'yɛr, Saa bɔ̃ɔ a jie an yi ɛ.

eng: Then Jesus replied, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: A Yesu mɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba, N mɩ kʊ̃ 'yɛr kʊ yɩ a jie ɩn nyɛ a sɔr maala nɩ a anyãna ɛ.

eng: Jesus told the people this parable: A certain man planted a vineyard, rented it to tenant farmers, and went on a trip for a long time.
local: Yesu chɛnɛ na 'yɛrɛ nɩ a nɩbɛ na wʊ lɔb a sʊkpa nyã kʊ ba, Daba lɛ bʊr a tɩwɔ̃nsɩ de kʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ a ba kaara, tɩ yɔ chen tɛɛr na wʊ tɩ kor be jaa.

eng: When it was time, he sent a servant to collect from the tenants his share of the fruit of the vineyard. But the tenants sent him away, beaten and empty-handed.
local: Ka a tɩɩr wɔ̃mɔ̃ mʊɔfʊ sɔ̃ɔ wa ta ka wʊ ir a tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ ãsʊɔ tʊ̃ a bala na kaara wʊ a puo ka ba pɔr a wɔ̃mɔ̃ amɩne tɩr. Tɩchɛ ka a nɩbɛ bala ŋme wʊ bar ka wʊ kul nũjaal.

eng: The man sent another servant. But they beat him, treated him disgracefully, and sent him away empty-handed as well.
local: Ka wʊ lɛ tʊ̃ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ ãsʊɔ go, tɩchɛ a 'lɔ mɩ ba lɛ ŋme ŋme naa tɩ tu tu wʊ bar, ka wʊ mɩ lɩɛb kul a nũjaala.

eng: He sent a third servant. They wounded this servant and threw him out.
local: Ka wʊ lɛ maal tʊ̃ a ata sʊɔ ka ba ŋme a 'lɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ baal 'mɔɔ lɔb bar a yõo.

eng: The owner of the vineyard said, What should I do? Ill send my son, whom I love dearly. Perhaps they will respect him.
local: A puo sʊɔ pãa 'yɛr, Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ ɩn ɩ? Ɩn tʊ̃n a n bidanʊ̃naa amɩne sɛ̃ ban jo wʊn vĩ.

eng: But when they saw him, they said to each other, This is the heir. Lets kill him so the inheritance will be ours.
local: Tɩchɛ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ na wa nyɛ a bidaba na waara a ba guor taa 'yɛr, Nyã lɛ ɩ a bʊ̃dire, yɩ ɩ a sɩ kʊ wʊ bar tɩ de a bonsɩ.

eng: They threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. What will the owner of the vineyard do to them?.
local: Na ba nyɔɔ wʊ vu yin a puo kõkoor na ba tɩ kʊ wʊ. Bo lɛ a puo sʊɔ na wa ɩ a nɩbɛ banyãna?.

eng: He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others. When the people heard this, they said, May this never happen.
local: Wʊn wa kʊ naa a nɩbɛ banyãna bar, tɩ de a puo kʊ nɩyor. Lɛ a nɩbɛ na wa wõ a 'yɛrbir nyã a ba 'yɛr, A kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ ɩ a lɛ ɛ.

eng: Staring at them, Jesus said, Then what is the meaning of this text of scripture: The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone?.
local: Ka a Yesu kaa ba a lɛ a tãɩɩ tɩ sʊʊr, Ala ɩ a kʊ̃ ɩ a lɛ ɛ, a par nɩ bo an sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, A kʊsɩr 'lɔ a mɩmɩɛrbɛ na tɔr lɛ ɩ a kʊsɩr ju.

eng: Everyone who falls on that stone will be crushed. And the stone will crush the person it falls on.
local: Ɩ̃sʊɔ jaa wa lo a kʊsɩr nyã ju na kar kara, tɩchɛ 'lɔ mɩ wa lo dib ãsʊɔ wʊn pʊrpʊr wʊ naa.

eng: The legal experts and chief priests wanted to arrest him right then because they knew he had told this parable against them. But they feared the people.
local: Ka a Mosesɩ nɛɛ wiwiilbe nɩ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ kaara sɔr 'lɔ ban na tu nyɔɔ wʊ fɔɔ, bojũu ba bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka bala lɛ wʊ lɔb a sʊkpa gbɛb, tɩchɛ na ba joro a nɩbɛ dãbãɛ.

eng: The legal experts and chief priests were watching Jesus closely and sent spies who pretended to be sincere. They wanted to trap him in his words so they could hand him over to the jurisdiction and authority of the governor.
local: Ba jʊrɔ naa Yesu, na ba ir nɩbɛ tʊ̃ ka ba tɩ maala fɩka nɩsʊ̃n tɩ jʊrɔ wʊ. Ba bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka ba wõ a wʊ nɛɛ 'yɛrbie de nyɔɔn wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ kpɛ̃ɔ sʊɔ nũu pɔ, na ɩ a paal nɩkpɛ̃ɛ.

eng: They asked him, Teacher, we know that you are correct in what you say and teach. You dont show favoritism but teach Gods way as it really is.
local: A nɛbɛr nɩbɛ banyãna sʊʊr wʊ naa, Wiwile sɩ bɔ̃ɔ naa ɩka fʊ ma 'yɛr naa na fʊ wiil a lɛ na tor, faa 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ nɩbɛ gar taa ɛ, tɩchɛ na fʊ wile a nɩbɛ Nãaŋmɩn sɔr nɩ yelmãɛ.

eng: Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not.
local: Sɔr ben be a sɩn yab a lõpo kʊ a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Siisa bɩɩ sɔr ba be be ɛ.

eng: Since Jesus recognized their deception, he said to them.
local: Yesu nyɛn a ba yɛ̃ na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba.

eng: Show me a coin.Whose image and inscription does it have on it? Caesars, they replied.
local: Yɩ wiil ma a libikur. An ju ŋmaa nɩ a wʊ yuor lɛ be be.

eng: He said to them, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God.
local: A ba 'yɛr, A Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Siisa lɛ. A wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba. Yɩ kʊ a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Siisa a ala na ɩ a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Siisa bon na yɩ mɩ de a ala na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn bon mɩ kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: They couldnt trap him in his words in front of the people. Astonished by his answer, they were speechless.
local: Ba bɛr wʊn a nɛɛ faa a anyãna wʊn 'yɛr a chãsara, ka nɛɛr 'maa ba nɩ a 'yɛr nyãna wʊn 'yɛr, a ba ɩ gbulo gbulo.

eng: Some Sadducees, who deny that theres a resurrection, came to Jesus and asked.
local: A Saduseemɩne na 'yɛr ɩka a kũu pɔ irfʊ ba be be ɛ, bamɩne wan a Yesu sɛ̃ wa sʊʊr wʊ.

eng: Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a mans brother dies leaving a widow but no children, the brother must marry the widow and raise up children for his brother .
local: Wiwile, Mosesɩ sɛb 'yɔ̃ɔ a wʊ gãn pɔ kʊ sɩ ɩka, ala ɩ daba kul pɔɔ na wʊ wa kpi, tɩchɛ ka a pɔɔ ba dɔɔ bie ɛ, a yɛbɛ wʊ de a pɔɔ kʊɔr kul na wʊ dɔɔ bie kʊ a yɛbɛ.

eng: Now there were seven brothers. The first man married a woman and then died childless.
local: Yɛɛr ayopõi lɛ dɔ̃ɔ be be, ka a ba kpɛ̃ɛ kul pɔɔ na wʊ kpi, tɩ baa dɔɔ bie ɛ.

eng: The second.
local: Ka a wʊ turo.

eng: and then the third brother married her. Eventually all seven married her, and they all died without leaving any children.
local: nɩ a ata sʊɔ mɩ kul a pɔɔ, tɩ kpi, lɛ a yɛɛr ayopõi bala mɩ, kul a pɔɔ tɩ kpi baa dɔɔ bie ɛ.

eng: Finally, the woman died too.
local: A baaraa jaa ka a pɔɔ mɩ kpi.

eng: In the resurrection, whose wife will she be? All seven were married to her.
local: An be a lɛ, bala wa ir a kũu pɔ, buor sʊɔ pɔɔ lɛ wʊn ɩ, an ɩ ba jaa ayopõi kul wʊ naa.

eng: Jesus said to them, People who belong to this age marry and are given in marriage.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Nɩbɛ na be ka a wɛr pɔ, kulo nɩ taa.

eng: But those who are considered worthy to participate in that age, that is, in the age of the resurrection from the dead, wont marry nor will they be given in marriage.
local: Tɩchɛ a bala na sɛɛn na ir a kũu pɔ a bɩbir 'lɔ, bala ba kulo taa ɛ.

eng: They can no longer die, because they are like angels and are Gods children since they share in the resurrection.
local: Bala kʊ̃ lɛ kpi go ɛ, nɩtãa lɛ a malakasɩ na ba kpire ɛ. Ba ɩ naa Nãaŋmɩn bibiir an ɩ bala lɛ ɩ a kũu pɔ irfʊ bibiir.

eng: Even Moses demonstrated that the dead are raised—in the passage about the burning bush, when he speaks of the Lord as the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob .
local: A kũu pɔ irfʊ, Mosesɩ mɩ wiil sɩ naa ɩka a ɩn yelmãɛ a lɛ wʊn sɛb a wʊ gãn pɔ, a Nãaŋmɩn na 'yɛrɛ nɩ wʊ a tɩbile na 'lɩrɛ a bũu, ka wʊ sɛb ɩka Nãaŋmɩn bʊɔl wʊ mãɛ, Abraham, Isaakɩ nɩ a Jekɔb Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: He isnt the God of the dead but of the living. To him they are all alive.
local: Waa ɩ kũn Nãaŋmɩn ɛ, tɩchɛ wʊ ɩn a vʊɛ Nãaŋmɩn a wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ ba jaa ɩ vʊɛ.

eng: Some of the legal experts responded, Teacher, you have answered well.
local: Ka a Mosesɩ nɛɛ wiwiilbe bamɩne 'yɛr, Wiwile, fʊ 'yɛr vɩla.

eng: No one dared to ask him anything else.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa ba lɛ 'la sʊʊr wʊ go ɛ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Why do they say that the Christ is Davids son?.
local: Yesu pãa sʊʊr ba naa, Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ a ɩ ka a nɩbɛ 'yɛrɛ ɩka a Kɩrɩsɩto ɩn a Nãa David Bidaba?.

eng: David himself says in the scroll of Psalms, The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side.
local: A Nãa David mãɛ 'yɛr a wʊ yiel gãn pɔ, A n Soro Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr kʊ a n Soro nyã wʊn ir, Jãnɛ a n nũsʊ̃ɔ jie.

eng: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet.
local: ka n wa de a fʊ dɔ̃dẽme bin a fʊ gbɛɛ pĩlem. .

eng: Since David calls him Lord, how can he be Davids son.
local: A Nãa David bʊɔl wʊ naa a wʊ Soro, ŋmɩnɛ wʊn lɛ ɩrɛ wa ɩ a wʊ bie.

eng: In the presence of all the people, Jesus said to his disciples.
local: A nɩbɛ na chɛlɛ a lɛ wʊn 'yɛrɛ, ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a poturbo.

eng: Watch out for the legal experts. They like to walk around in long robes. They love being greeted with honor in the markets. They long for the places of honor in the synagogues and at banquets.
local: Yɩ gu yɩ mãɛ a Mosesɩ nɛɛ wiwiilbe 'yɔ̃ɔ. Ba nʊ̃ naa fuu vɛɛsɩ sufʊ, na ba nʊ̃ ɩka ba puoro ba a dar pɔ. A ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ yie a niem lɛ ba ma jãnɛ na ba jãnɛ a jibɛrɛ jie bala wa lɔ̃ɔ dire.

eng: They are the ones who cheat widows out of their homes, and to show off they say long prayers. They will be judged most harshly.
local: Bala lɛ ma fɔlɔ a pɔɔ kɔɛ yie na ba ma sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn kor bʊɔrɔ ɩka a nɩbɛ ba kaara ba. Ban nyɛn ge dɛɛr.

eng: Looking up, Jesus saw rich people throwing their gifts into the collection box for the temple treasury.
local: Yesu dʊɔn kaa nyɛ a tara dẽme na 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a ba libie be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir.

eng: He also saw a poor widow throw in two small copper coins worth a penny.
local: Wʊ nyɛn pɔɔ kʊɔr nɔ̃ɔ sʊɔ mɩ na 'yɔ̃ɔ kobsɩ ayi.

eng: He said, I assure you that this poor widow has put in more than them all.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka a pɔɔ kʊɔr nɔ̃ɔ sʊɔ nyã 'yɔ̃ɔn gar ba jaa.

eng: All of them are giving out of their spare change. But she from her hopeless poverty has given everything she had to live on.
local: A nɩbɛ banyãna ma ir 'yɔ̃ɔ tɩ chɛ a libie amɩne, tɩchɛ a pɔɔ nyã bon jaa wʊn tara lɛ wʊ de 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: Some people were talking about the temple, how it was decorated with beautiful stones and ornaments dedicated to God. Jesus said.
local: A Yesu poturbo bamɩne 'yɛrɛ na a lɛ ban maal a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir nɩ a kʊsɩr vɩɛlsɩ, nɩ a kʊfʊsɩ ban kʊ Nãaŋmɩn. Tɩchɛ ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba.

eng: As for the things you are admiring, the time is coming when not even one stone will be left upon another. All will be demolished.
local: A kʊsɩbɛ anyãna yɩn nyɛ a kana, sɔ̃ɔ waara naa, ãsʊɔ jaa kʊ̃ dɔɔl a wʊ tɔ ju ɛ, a jaa na wɛl gã tẽe.

eng: They asked him, Teacher, when will these things happen? What sign will show that these things are about to happen.
local: Ka ba sʊʊr wʊ, Wiwile, dabor lɛ an ɩ? Bo lɛ na ɩ a jãnɛ ala wa bʊɔrɔ a ɩfʊ.

eng: Jesus said, Watch out that you arent deceived. Many will come in my name, saying, Im the one! and Its time! Dont follow them.
local: A wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ gu yɩ mãɛ ka nɩrɛ taa bɛl yɩ ɛ. Bojũu nɩyɔɔ lɛ na de a n yuor waan wa 'yɛrɛ ɩka, Maa lɛ ba ɩ, ka a ɩ, Sɔ̃ɔ gbʊr naa. Yɩ taa turo ba ɛ.

eng: When you hear of wars and rebellions, dont be alarmed. These things must happen first, but the end wont happen immediately.
local: Yɩn wa wõ jɛɛr nɩ faa taa, yɩ ta vɛ̃ ka dãbãɛ kpɛ yɩ ɛ. A anyãna lɛ na de nie wa, tɩchɛ a ba ɩ a baarfʊ lɛ ɛ.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other.
local: Tɩ pãa 'yɛr kʊ ba, Nɩbɛ na ir tɩ jɛɛr nɩ a tẽe ãsʊɔ nɩbɛ, ka a nãmɩne mɩ ir jɛɛrɛ taa.

eng: There will be great earthquakes and wide-scale food shortages and epidemics. There will also be terrifying sights and great signs in the sky.
local: A tẽe na mɩr naa a jie wʊ jaa ka a kɔ̃ lo ka a baal dɛbɛ de jiir a jaa ka a dãbãɛ nɩ jãnɛ yi saaju.

eng: But before all this occurs, they will take you into custody and harass you because of your faith. They will hand you over to synagogues and prisons, and you will be brought before kings and governors because of my name.
local: Sɛrɛ tɩchɛ ka anyãna na wa ɩ ban ge yɩ naa, na ba nyɔɔr yɩ chiin a lɔ̃ɔfʊ jiir na ba fɩr yɩ chiin tɩ pɔɔ. Ban fɩr yɩn chiin a nãmɩne yie nɩ a paal nɩbɛrɛ jie a n yuor jũu.

eng: This will provide you with an opportunity to testify.
local: A anyãna na kʊ yɩn sɔr ka yɩ di a n dãasɩɛ kʊ ba.

eng: Make up your minds not to prepare your defense in advance.
local: Tɩchɛ yɩ ta ɩ ka a ɩ yɩ tɩɛrɛ a lɛ yɩn na tɩ 'yɛr faa yɩ mãɛ ɛ.

eng: Ill give you words and wisdom that none of your opponents will be able to counter or contradict.
local: Bojũu maa lɛ na kʊ yɩ a 'yɛr nɩ a yɛ̃ ka a yɩ sãma dẽme kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ nyɛ jie chɩɩr yɩ ɛ.

eng: You will be betrayed by your parents, brothers and sisters, relatives, and friends. They will execute some of you.
local: A yɩ sãamɩne nɩ a yɩ mamɩne, yɛɛr nɩ a yɩ bal nɩbɛ nɩ a yɩ barmɩne na gar 'yɔ̃ɔ yɩ ka ba kʊ a yɩ bamɩne.

eng: Everyone will hate you because of my name.
local: Nɩbɛ ba jaa na 'laar yɩ naa a maa jũu.

eng: Still, not a hair on your heads will be lost.
local: Tɩchɛ a yɩ jukɔɔlbir kʊ̃ wa wɛ ɛ.

eng: By holding fast, you will gain your lives.
local: Yɩn chɛnɛ sɔɔ Nãaŋmɩn nɩ yelmãɛ yɩn faan yɩ mãɛ.

eng: When you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then you will know that its destruction is close at hand.
local: Yɩn wa nyɛ a sojasɩ a ba wa ŋmaa jilʊ̃ a Jerusalɛm, yɩ bɔ̃ɔ ɩka a wʊ wɛlfʊ gbʊr jaa yɛ̃yɛ̃.

eng: At that time, those in Judea must flee to the mountains, those in the city must escape, and those in the countryside must not enter the city.
local: Ka a bala na pãa be a Judiya ba joro duoro a tɔ̃n jur. A bala mɩ na be a a Jerusalɛm tẽe pɔ, yɩ joro yire, ka a bala mɩ na be a tẽe kõkoor ba ta lɛ kpɛ a tẽe pɔ go ɛ.

eng: These are the days of punishment, when everything written will find its fulfillment.
local: A sɔ̃ɔ nyãna lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na kpãa a Isɩral nɩbɛ, a kpãafʊ nyã na ɩn a lɛ ban sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant or for women who are nursing their children. There will be great agony on the earth and angry judgment on this people.
local: A bɩbir nyã na ɩn wõm kʊ a pɔɔ pʊʊr nɩ a bipɩlɛ dẽme, dɔɔyɛ kpɛ̃ɛ na ben a wɛr pɔ, bojũu Nãaŋmɩn suur lɛ dire a wʊ nɩbɛ.

eng: They will fall by the edge of the sword and be taken away as captives among all nations. Jerusalem will be plundered by Gentiles until the times of the Gentiles are concluded.
local: Sɔkpɛ̃ɛ lɛ ban kʊn ba, tɩ nyɔɔr ba nɩpɔɔrsɩ kaar kʊ a paalsɩ. Ka a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ na nɛɛrɛ naa a Jerusalɛm wa tɩ tãn a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn na 'yɔ̃ɔ kʊ ba.

eng: There will be signs in the sun, moon, and stars. On the earth, there will be dismay among nations in their confusion over the roaring of the sea and surging waves.
local: Yɩn nyɛn jãnsɩ a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ pɔ, ŋmɛra pɔ nɩ a ŋmɛrbie pɔ. Tuo na pɔɔn nɩbʊrɔ jaa na be ka wɛr ju, ka dãbãɛ kpɛ a nɩbɛ, a man kpɛ̃ɛ kʊ̃ɔ na kara na a ɔɔrɔ.

eng: The planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken, causing people to faint from fear and foreboding of what is coming upon the world.
local: Dãbãɛ nyã na vɛ̃ naa ka a nɩbɛ pɛl, bojũu ba bɔ̃ɔn naa a dɔɔyɛ nyã na waara a wɛr pɔ, a saaju bomo a jaa na dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ naa.

eng: Then they will see the Human Onecoming on a cloud with power and great splendor.
local: A daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ ban nyɛ a maa Nɩsaal Bie na yi a julõjuur pɔ, waara nɩ a kpɛ̃ɔ nɩ a tɩɩr kpɛ̃ɛ.

eng: Now when these things begin to happen, stand up straight and raise your heads, because your redemption is near.
local: A yele anyãna wa ɩrɛ, yɩ ir ara tɩ jɛɛr a yɩ jur, bojũu a yɩ faafʊ gbʊrɔ naa.

eng: Jesus told them a parable: Look at the fig tree and all the trees.
local: Sʊkpa nyã lɛ a Yesu lɔb kʊ ba, na wa 'yɛr, Yɩ kaa a pɔlpɔl tɩɛ nɩ a tɩɩr a jaa.

eng: When they sprout leaves, you can see for yourselves and know that summer is near.
local: Yɩn wa nyɛ ka a tʊɔrɔ a vaar yɩ ma bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka tʊl tara naa.

eng: In the same way, when you see these things happening, you know that Gods kingdom is near.
local: A lɛ mɩ lɛ, yɩn wa nyɛ a yelsɩ anyãna ka a ɩrɛ, yɩ bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ gbʊr naa.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until everything has happened.
local: Yelmãɛ jaa lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka nɩsaal jaa kʊ̃ kpi baar ɛ, tɩchɛ ka a yelsɩ anyãna ɩ ɛ.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: A saaju nɩ a tẽe, a jaa na wa baaraa, tɩchɛ a maa 'yɛrbie kʊ̃ wa baar ɛ.

eng: Take care that your hearts arent dulled by drinking parties, drunkenness, and the anxieties of day-to-day life. Dont let that day fall upon you unexpectedly.
local: Yɩ guro yɩ mãɛ, ka a yɩ nyãa taa pɔɔ ɛ, nɩ dãa kuolfʊ nɩ a ãgãn nyã bʊ̃bʊɔrsɩ ka a bɩbir nyã taa wa dũu yɩ fɩka chɛbaa na tɛb ɛ.

eng: like a trap. It will come upon everyone who lives on the face of the whole earth.
local: Bojũu a lɛ na ɩ ɩn na wa, pɔɔn nɩrɛ jaa na be ka a wɛr nyã pɔ.

eng: Stay alert at all times, praying that you are strong enough to escape everything that is about to happen and to stand before the Human One.
local: Yɩ kaara na yɩ sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn, na yɩ tʊ̃ɔ jo yi a ala a jaa na wa wa ɩ pɔ, na yɩn tʊ̃ɔ ara a maa Nɩsaal Bie niem.

eng: Every day Jesus was teaching in the temple, but he spent each night on the Mount of Olives.
local: A Yesu ma wile naa a nɩbɛ be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir, tɩchɛ ka a tãsɔɔ ka wʊ ma do a tɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ ban bʊɔlɔ a Olive Tɩɩr Tɔ̃ɔ tɩ gã a be.

eng: All the people rose early in the morning to hear him in the temple area.
local: Ka a nɩbɛ ma chɛnɛ ŋmaa ir a bibio wa be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir ɩka ba wa chɛl a wʊ 'yɛr.

eng: The Festival of Unleavened Bread, which is called Passover, was approaching.
local: A Paanʊ̃kõo Lɔ̃ɔfʊ ban bʊɔlɔ a Gõl Bar Difʊ gbʊrɔ naa.

eng: The chief priests and the legal experts were looking for a way to kill Jesus, because they were afraid of the people.
local: Ka a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe bʊɔrɔ sɔr 'lɔ ban na tu nyɔɔ a Yesu jaa jom, bojũu ba joro naa a nɩbɛ dãbãɛ.

eng: Then Satan entered Judas, called Iscariot, who was one of the Twelve.
local: Ka a Satan pãa kpɛ a Judas na yi a Isɩkariyot na ɩ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ pie nɩ ayi ãsʊɔ.

eng: He went out and discussed with the chief priests and the officers of the temple guard how he could hand Jesus over to them.
local: Wʊ ir chen a bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir gugurbo sɛ̃, ka ba tɩ man taa lɛ wʊn na ɩ, ka ba nyɔɔ a Yesu.

eng: They were delighted and arranged payment for him.
local: Ba pʊɔ pɛl naa ka ba sɔɔ ɩka ban kʊ wʊ naa libie.

eng: He agreed and began looking for an opportunity to hand Jesus over to them—a time when the crowds would be absent.
local: Judas sɔɔ naa na wʊ kaara bʊɔrɔ sɔr 'lɔ wʊn na nyɔɔ a Yesu kʊ ba ka a nɩyɔɔ ba be be ɛ.

eng: The Day of Unleavened Bread arrived, when the Passover had to be sacrificed.
local: A Paanʊ̃kõo bɩbir vɩɛ naa, ka ban kʊ a Gõl Bar Difʊ pɛr.

eng: Jesus sent Peter and John with this task: Go and prepare for us to eat the Passover meal.
local: Yesu tʊ̃n a Pita nɩ Jɔɔn na wʊ 'yɛr, Yɩ chen tɩ chɔɔr jie ka sɩ chen tɩ di a Gõl Bar Difʊ.

eng: They said to him, Where do you want us to prepare it.
local: Ka ba sʊʊr wʊ, Nyɩnɛ lɛ fʊ bʊɔrɔ ɩka sɩ chen tɩ chɔɔr.

eng: Jesus replied, When you go into the city, a man carrying a water jar will meet you. Follow him to the house he enters.
local: A wʊ 'yɛr, Yɩn wa tɩ kpɛ a tẽe pɔ, yɩn tuor daba na tuo kʊ̃ɔ nɩ yuor yɩ turo wʊ tɩ kpɛ̃n a yir 'lɔ pɔ wʊn kpɛ.

eng: Say to the owner of the house, The teacher says to you,Where is the guestroom where I can eat the Passover meal with my disciples.
local: Na yɩ tɩ 'yɛr a yir sʊɔ, a wiwile lɛ 'yɛr ɩka sɩ wa sʊʊr fʊ kaa, ka nyɩnɛ lɛ a sãanba die be, ka 'lɔ naa wʊ poturbo na wa di a Gõl Bar Difʊ?.

eng: He will show you a large upstairs room, already furnished. Make preparations there.
local: Wʊn wiil yɩn bɔɔpɩɛ dikpɛ̃ɛ ban chɔɔr yɩ maal a bʊ̃diir a be.

eng: They went and found everything just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover meal.
local: Ba chen tɩ nyɛ a lɛ nɛ a Yesu na 'yɛr na ba chɔɔr a bʊ̃diir a be.

eng: When the time came, Jesus took his place at the table, and the apostles joined him.
local: A sɔ̃ɔ na wa ta Yesu nɩ a wʊ nɩtʊ̃nsɩ wa jãnɛ naa a bʊ̃diir par.

eng: He said to them, I have earnestly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer.
local: Be lɛ wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, N bʊɔrɔ na yɔɔ jaa ɩka n 'mataa din yɩ a Gõl Bar nyã bʊ̃diir tɩ na n di a dɔɔyɛ.

eng: I tell you, I wont eat it until it is fulfilled in Gods kingdom.
local: Bojũu n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka n kʊ̃ lɛ di yaa go ɛ, wa tɩ tãn yele jaa na maal baar a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.

eng: After taking a cup and giving thanks, he said, Take this and share it among yourselves.
local: Wʊn wa de a dãa naa ŋmele wʊ puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ nɩtʊ̃nsɩ, Yɩ de a anyã nyũ lʊɔr taa.

eng: I tell you that from now on I wont drink from the fruit of the vine until Gods kingdom has come.
local: Bojũu n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka n kʊ̃ lɛ nyũ a dãa anyãna go ɛ, tɩ tãn a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ waa daar.

eng: After taking the bread and giving thanks, he broke it and gave it to them, saying, This is my body, which is given for you. Do this in remembrance of me.
local: Wʊ lɛ den a paanʊ̃ɔ puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan tɩ na wʊ ŋma de kʊ ba tɩ 'yɛr, A maa ãgãn lɛ a nyãna ɩn diere kʊrɔ yɩ, yɩ maala a nɩtɔ̃ tɩɛrɛ nɩ ma.

eng: In the same way, he took the cup after the meal and said, This cup is the new covenant by my blood, which is poured out for you.
local: Lɛ nɩ wʊ ju lɛ ban wa di baar wʊ den a ŋmele tɩ 'yɛr, A ŋmele nyã ɩn a nɛtɩr paalaa na ɩ a maa jãɩ na kpaar a yɩ jũu.

eng: But look! My betrayer is with me; his hand is on this table.
local: Tɩchɛ a 'lɔ na garʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ ma lɛ n 'mataa dire nɩ a bʊ̃diir.

eng: The Human Onegoes just as it has been determined. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays him.
local: A maa Nɩsaal Bie na chen naa nɩtãa lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na bin, tɩchɛ nɛbier ben a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na garʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ ma.

eng: They began to argue among themselves about which of them it could possibly be who would do this.
local: A ba piel sʊrɔ taa ɩka a ba buor sʊɔ lɛ na pãa tʊ̃ a nɩtɔ̃.

eng: An argument broke out among the disciples over which one of them should be regarded as the greatest.
local: Ka nɛchɩrɛ mɩ ir a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ pɔ, a ba chɩrɛ taa bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba bɔ̃ɔ buor sʊɔ lɛ na ɩ a ba jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ.

eng: But Jesus said to them, The kings of the Gentiles rule over their subjects, and those in authority over them are called friends of the people.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, A bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ nãmɩne ɛ, ma fɩrɛ naa a nɩbɛ, ka a bala mɩ na wile a ba kpãɛn bʊɔlɔ ba mãɛ, nɩsʊ̃nbɔ.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Instead, the greatest among you must become like a person of lower status and the leader like a servant.
local: Tɩchɛ yɩn 'lɔ a ba ɩ lɛ lɛ ɛ, a 'lɔ na ɩ a yɩ kpɛ̃ɛ, 'lɔ lɛ na ɩ a yɩ jaa bibile, a 'lɔ na kaara yɩ, 'lɔ lɛ na tʊ̃nɔ̃ kʊrɔ yɩ.

eng: So which one is greater, the one who is seated at the table or the one who serves at the table? Isnt it the one who is seated at the table? But I am among you as one who serves.
local: Nɩbuor sʊɔ lɛ ɩ kpɛ̃ɛ, a 'lɔ na jãnɛ a bʊ̃diir par, bɩɩ a 'lɔ na põnõ? A ba ɩ a 'lɔ na jãnɛ a bʊ̃diir par ɛ? Tɩchɛ a maa na ɩ a yɩ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ ben a yɩ sɛ̃, ɩ a yɩ bʊ̃ põnõ.

eng: You are the ones who have continued with me in my trials.
local: Yɩn lɛ ɩ a nɩbɛ bala na chɛnɛ ara gu ma a n wõm pɔ.

eng: And I confer royal power on you just as my Father granted royal power to me.
local: N kʊ yɩn a nãalʊ̃ʊ nɩtãa lɛ a n Sãa na kʊ ma a nãalʊ̃ʊ.

eng: Thus you will eat and drink at my table in my kingdom, and you will sit on thrones overseeing the twelve tribes of Israel.
local: Ka maa nɩ yɩn lɔ̃ɔ di tɩ nyũ be a n nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ, na yɩ jãnɛ a nãa dakɔɔ ju tɔɔrɔ a Isɩral balsɩ pie nɩ ayi 'yɛr.

eng: Simon, Simon, look! Satan has asserted the right to sift you all like wheat.
local: Simon, Simon, a Satan sʊɔr sɔr Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ ɩka wʊ yɩɛl yɩ chi kaar.

eng: However, I have prayed for you that your faith wont fail. When you have returned, strengthen your brothers and sisters.
local: Tɩchɛ a fʊ̃ʊ Pita n sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn kʊ fʊ ɩka, a fʊ sɔɔfʊ ta bʊ̃bʊr ɛ, a fʊ̃ʊ wa lɩɛb puor, fʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ a fʊ taaba ãkpãɛn.

eng: Peter responded, Lord, Im ready to go with you, both to prison and to death.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Simon 'yɛr, N Soro, n chɔɔr naa ɩka maa nɩ fʊ̃ʊ lɛ na lɔ̃ɔ kpɛ a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ, na sɩ 'mataa kpi.

eng: Jesus replied, I tell you, Peter, the rooster wont crow today before you have denied three times that you know me.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr, A fʊ̃ʊ Pita, sɛrɛ ka a nɔraa kõ a dɩna fʊn chɩɩr gbɛɛ ata ɩka faa bɔ̃ɔ ma ɛ.

eng: Jesus said to them, When I sent you out without a wallet, bag, or sandals, you didnt lack anything, did you? They said, Nothing.
local: Be lɛ a Yesu pãa sʊʊr ba, Ɩn dɔ̃ɔ tʊ̃ yɩ a nũjaala, yaa tara libie ɛ, bɩɩ wʊɔ nɩ nafɔɔr ɛ, yɩ dɔ̃ɔ bɔ bon faa bɩɩ? A ba 'yɛr ɩka baa bɔ bon jaa faa ɛ.

eng: Then he said to them, But now, whoever has a wallet must take it, and likewise a bag. And those who dont own a sword must sell their clothes and buy one.
local: A wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Tɩchɛ pʊ̃pãanyã, fʊ̃ʊ nyã na tara libie de, fʊ̃ʊ nyã mɩ na tara wʊɔ de, fʊ̃ʊ nyã na ba wa tara sɔkpɛ̃ɛ ɛ, de a fʊ fuu da na fʊ dãn a sɔkpɛ̃ɛ.

eng: I tell you that this scripture must be fulfilled in relation to me: And he was counted among criminals .Indeed, whats written about me is nearing completion.
local: A sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, Ba sɔr wʊn 'yɔ̃ɔ a tʊ̃dɛbɛ dẽme pɔ. N 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka maa lɛ ban maal a lɛ nɛ, ɔ̃ɔ, a lɛ ban sɛb a n yele a gbʊrɔ na tafʊ.

eng: They said to him, Lord, look, here are two swords. He replied, Enough of that.
local: Ka a poturbo 'yɛr, Sɩ Soro nyɛ sɔbɛrɛ ayi. A wʊ 'yɛr, A sɛɛ naa.

eng: Jesus left and made his way to the Mount of Olives, as was his custom, and the disciples followed him.
local: Yesu yin duoro a Olive Tɩɩr Tɔ̃ɔ ju, nɩtãa lɛ wʊn ma ɩrɛ ka a wʊ poturbo turo wʊ.

eng: When he arrived, he said to them, Pray that you wont give in to temptation.
local: Wʊn wa ta a jie wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn na yɩ taa lo a bɛɛr kaa pɔ ɛ.

eng: He withdrew from them about a stones throw, knelt down, and prayed.
local: Wʊ tɔɔn jãa ba bãlãa fɩka kʊsɩr fʊn na lɔb ka wʊ ta, na wʊ gbʊr dumo sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛrɛ.

eng: He said, Father, if its your will, take this cup of suffering away from me. However, not my will but your will must be done.
local: N Sãa, ala ɩ fʊ bʊɔrɔ naa, de a dɔɔyɛ ŋmele nyãna bar ma, a ɩ a fʊ bɔfʊ a ta ɩ a maa bɔfʊ ɛ.

eng: Then a heavenly angel appeared to him and strengthened him.
local: Be lɛ a malaka yi be a saaju wa a wʊ sɛ̃ wa 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ ãkpɛ̃ɔ.

eng: He was in anguish and prayed even more earnestly. His sweat became like drops of blood falling on the ground.
local: A nɩbaal na kpɛ wʊ a lɛ, ka wʊ sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn kpɛ̃ɔ jaa ka a nyĩe hãna wʊ loro tẽe fɩka jãɩ.

eng: When he got up from praying, he went to the disciples. He found them asleep, overcome by grief.
local: Wʊn wa ir a Nãaŋmɩn sʊɔrfʊ jie lɩɛb chen tɩ nyɛ ka a wʊ poturbo gã gur nɩ pʊsɔ̃ɔ.

eng: He said to them, Why are you sleeping? Get up and pray so that you wont give in to temptation.
local: Ka wʊ sʊʊr ba, Ŋmɩnɛ a yɩ gã guro? Yɩ ir sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn na yɩ taa lo a bɛɛr kaa pɔ ɛ.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, a crowd appeared, and the one called Judas, one of the Twelve, was leading them. He approached Jesus to kiss him.
local: Wʊn chɛnɛ 'yɛrɛ a lɛ ka a ɩ a nɩyɔɔ daan sãa, ka a 'lɔ ban bʊɔlɔ a Judas a Pie Nɩ Ayi ãsʊɔ lɛ de ba a nie. Wʊ tɔɔn ta a Yesu puor wʊ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Judas, would you betray the Human Onewith a kiss.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Yesu sʊʊr wʊ, Judas, fʊ ben de puorfʊ garʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ a maa Nɩsaal Bie.

eng: When those around him recognized what was about to happen, they said, Lord, should we fight with our swords.
local: Yesu poturbo na nyɛ a lɛ na ɩrɛ ka ba sʊʊr wʊ, Sɩ Soro, sɩ chɩɛrɛ nɩ a sɔbɛrɛ bɩɩ.

eng: One of them struck the high priests servant, cutting off his right ear.
local: Ka a ba ãsʊɔ chɛ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ gbãgbaa nũsʊ̃ɔ toor ŋmaa.

eng: Jesus responded, Stop! No more of this! He touched the slaves ear and healed him.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Yɩ ta lɛ maal a lɛ ɛ. Tɩ na wʊ sɩɩr a daba toor ka wʊ choo.

eng: Then Jesus said to the chief priests, the officers of the temple guard, and the elders who had come to get him, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, as though I were a thief?.
local: A Yesu pãa sʊʊr a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir gugurbo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Juu nɩbɛ nɩdierbe, A mɔ̃ nɩbier lɛ n ɩ ka yɩ waan sɔbɛrɛ nɩ dakʊrsɩ a n nyɔɔfʊ jie bɩɩ?.

eng: Day after day I was with you in the temple, but you didnt arrest me. But this is your time, when darkness rules.
local: Maa nɩ yɩn lɛ be be bɩbir jaa be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir bɛchaar pɔ, yaa nyɔɔ ma ɛ, tɩchɛ a yɩ bɩbir vɩɛ naa ka a libe mɩ di nãa.

eng: After they arrested Jesus, they led him away and brought him to the high priests house. Peter followed from a distance.
local: Ba nyɔɔ naa Yesu, na ba chiine a bɔɔrlo nãa yir, ka a Pita mɩ be a puor na wʊ turo.

eng: When they lit a fire in the middle of the courtyard and sat down together, Peter sat among them.
local: Ban tɩ ta ba gbɔɔn bũu be a bɛchaar sãlsɔɔ 'mataa jãnɛ ka a Pita mɩ tɩ pʊɔ ba ka ba 'mataa jãnɛ.

eng: Then a servant woman saw him sitting in the firelight. She stared at him and said, This man was with him too.
local: Ka a gbãgbapɔɔbile nyɛ wʊ a bũu na chaar na wʊ kaara wʊ jaa tãɩɩ tɩ wa 'yɛr, A daba nyã mɩ ɩn a wʊ poturo ãsʊɔ.

eng: But Peter denied it, saying, Woman, I dont know him.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Pita chiir 'yɛr, Pɔɔ, n ba bɔ̃ɔ wʊ ɛ.

eng: A little while later, someone else saw him and said, You are one of them too. But Peter said, Man, Im not.
local: A ba kor ɛ ka a nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ nyɛ wʊ na wʊ 'yɛr, Fʊ mɩ ɩn a ba ãsʊɔ. Ka a Pita 'yɛr, Daba, n ba ɩ a ba ãsʊɔ ɛ.

eng: An hour or so later, someone else insisted, This man must have been with him, because he is a Galilean too.
local: A ba lɛ kor ɛ, ka a nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ mɩ 'yɛr, A ba ɩ baal ɛ, a daba nyã ɩn a ba ãsʊɔ bojũu Galilee lɛ wʊ yi.

eng: Peter responded, Man, I dont know what you are talking about! At that very moment, while he was still speaking, a rooster crowed.
local: Ka a Pita 'yɛr, Daba, n ba bɔ̃ɔ a ala fʊn 'yɛrɛ ɛ. Ka a nɔraa mɩ tɔ̃ɔ kõ a lɛ nɛ.

eng: The Lord turned and looked straight at Peter, and Peter remembered the Lords words: Before a rooster crows today, you will deny me three times.
local: Ka a Yesu lɩɛb na wʊ kaa a Pita, a wʊ tɔ̃ɔ tɩɛr tɔ a lɛ a Yesu na 'yɛr kʊ wʊ ɩka, Sɛrɛ tɩchɛ ka a nɔraa kõ a dɩna fʊn chiir man gbɛɛ ata.

eng: And Peter went out and cried uncontrollably.
local: Ka a Pita ir yi a yõo na wʊ tɩ kõ nɩbaal kõn.

eng: The men who were holding Jesus in custody taunted him while they beat him.
local: A dɔɔr bala na nyɔɔ a Yesu, piel na ba ɩrɛ wʊ laara tɩ ŋmiere wʊ.

eng: They blindfolded him and asked him repeatedly, Prophesy! Who hit you.
local: Ba pɔɔ wʊ naa nɩbie tɩ sʊʊr, Bɔ̃ɔ na fʊ 'yɛr kʊ sɩ a nɩnyɩnɛ na ŋme fʊ.

eng: Insulting him, they said many other horrible things against him.
local: Ba lɛ den 'yɛrbidɛbɛ yɔɔ tʊ tʊn wʊ.

eng: As morning came, the elders of the people, both chief priests and legal experts, came together, and Jesus was brought before their council.
local: A wɛr na wa chaar, a Juu nɩbɛ nɩdierbe nɩ a ba bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ, nɩ a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe lɔ̃ɔn taa, ka ba tara a Yesu waan a be.

eng: They said, If you are the Christ, tell us! He answered, If I tell you, you wont believe.
local: A ba sʊʊr wʊ, Ala ɩ fʊ̃ʊ lɛ a Nɩ-iraa 'lɔ, 'yɛr kʊ sɩ. Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Maa 'yɛr, yɩ kʊ̃ sɔɔ ɛ.

eng: And if I ask you a question, you wont answer.
local: Ala ɩ n sʊʊr yɩn 'yɛr yɩ kʊ̃ haa ma nɛɛ ɛ.

eng: But from now on, the Human One will be seated on the right side of the power of God. .
local: Tɩchɛ an yi a dɩna chiine, a maa Nɩsaal Bie na jãnɛ naa Nãaŋmɩn kpɛ̃ɔ sɔɔ nũsʊ̃ɔ jie.

eng: They all said, Are you Gods Son, then? He replied, You say that I am.
local: Ka ba sʊʊr wʊ, Fʊ̃ʊ lɛ ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Bie ɔ? Kaa a Yesu 'yɛr, Yɩn lɛ 'yɛr ɩka lɛ n ɩ.

eng: Then they said, Why do we need further testimony? Weve heard it from his own lips.
local: Ka ba pãa 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ taa, Bãa 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ sɩ lɛ bʊɔrɔ dãasɩɛ go? Sɩ wõn yi a wʊ mãɛ nɛɛ pɔ.

eng: The whole assembly got up and led Jesus to Pilate and.
local: Be lɛ a nɩyɔɔ bala pãa tara a Yesu chiin a be a paal nɩkpɛ̃ɛ Pilate sɛ̃.

eng: began to accuse him. They said, We have found this man misleading our people, opposing the payment of taxes to Caesar, and claiming that he is the Christ, a king.
local: Na ba tɩ piel 'yɛrɛ a 'yɛrsɩ anyãna dɔɔlɔ wʊ, Sɩ nyɛn a daba nyãna ka wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a nɩbɛ 'yɛr, a ba ta yab a lõpo kʊ a Nãa Siisa ɛ, na wʊ lɛ 'yɛr ɩka 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a Nɩ-iraa na ɩ a nãa.

eng: Pilate asked him, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Ka a Pilate sʊʊr a Yesu, Fʊ̃ʊ lɛ ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ nãa ɔ? Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Ɔ̃ɔ, fʊ̃ʊ baa 'yɛr.

eng: Then Pilate said to the chief priests and the crowds, I find no legal basis for action against this man.
local: Ka a Pilate pãa 'yɛr kʊ a bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a nɩbɛ, N ba nyɛ chɛfʊ jaa a daba nyã jie ɛ.

eng: But they objected strenuously, saying, He agitates the people with his teaching throughout Judea—starting from Galilee all the way here.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a nɩbɛ tɔr tɩ chɛnɛ 'yɛrɛ, Wiilfʊ nyãna lɛ wʊ tara dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ nɩ a nɩbɛ be a Judiya. Galilee lɛ wʊ piel a yele nyãna be wa tãn a ka.

eng: Hearing this, Pilate asked if the man was a Galilean.
local: Pilate na wõ a nɩtɔ̃ ka wʊ sʊrɔ, Galilee lɛ a daba yi bɩɩ?.

eng: When he learned that Jesus was from Herods district, Pilate sent him to Herod, who was also in Jerusalem at that time.
local: Wʊn wa bɔ̃ɔn ka a Hɛrɔd paal nɩrɛ lɛ, wʊ vɛ̃ naa ka ba tara wʊ chiin tɩ kʊ a Hɛrɔd, ka ɩ a Jerusalɛm lɛ wʊ mɩ be a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃.

eng: Herod was very glad to see Jesus, for he had heard about Jesus and had wanted to see him for quite some time. He was hoping to see Jesus perform some sign.
local: Hɛrɔd na wa nyɛ a Yesu, wʊ pʊɔ pɛl naa, bojũu a kor wʊn wõ a Yesu yele na wʊ bʊɔrɔ a wʊ nyɛfʊ, ka wʊ tʊ̃ a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃.

eng: Herod questioned Jesus at length, but Jesus didnt respond to him.
local: Na wʊ sʊʊr a Yesu sʊʊr yɔɔ tɩ waa haa wʊ nɛɛ ɛ.

eng: The chief priests and the legal experts were there, fiercely accusing Jesus.
local: Ka a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe na ara a be 'yɛrɛ 'yɛr kpãɛn dɔɔlɔ wʊ.

eng: Herod and his soldiers treated Jesus with contempt. Herod mocked him by dressing Jesus in elegant clothes and sent him back to Pilate.
local: Hɛrɔd nɩ a wʊ sojasɩ maal a Yesu dɛɛr na ba ɩrɛ wʊ laara. Ba su wʊ naa a nãa fuu tɩ lɩɛb wʊ chiin a Pilate sɛ̃.

eng: Pilate and Herod became friends with each other that day. Before this, they had been enemies.
local: Hɛrɔd nɩ a Pilate dɔ̃ɔ tara taa nɩ 'yɛr, tɩ a daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ ba maal a 'yɛr.

eng: Then Pilate called together the chief priests, the rulers, and the people.
local: Be lɛ a Pilate bʊɔl a bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ, nɩ a tẽe nɩbɛrɛ, nɩ a nɩbɛ ba jaa lɔ̃ɔ taa.

eng: He said to them, You brought this man before me as one who was misleading the people. I have questioned him in your presence and found nothing in this mans conduct that provides a legal basis for the charges you have brought against him.
local: Na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩn lɛ waan a daba nyãna a n sɛ̃ wa 'yɛr ɩka wʊ sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ naa a nɩbɛ. N vɩɛr naa a wʊ 'yɛr a yɩ jaa niem ka a ala yɩn 'yɛr dɔɔl wʊ ba ɩ yelmãɛ ɛ.

eng: Neither did Herod, because Herod returned him to us. Hes done nothing that deserves death.
local: Hɛrɔd mɩ ba nyɛ yele jaa a wʊ jie ɛ, ala lɛ so ka wʊ lɛ lɩɛb wʊ wa kʊ sɩ, yɩn mãɛ nyɛ naa wʊn ba maal yele jaa na sɛɛn kũu ɛ.

eng: Therefore, Ill have him whipped, then let him go.
local: A lɛ jũu ɩn vɛ̃ naa ka ba fɔb wʊ tɩchɛ kaa n bar wʊ.

eng: But with one voice they shouted, Away with this man! Release Barabbas to us. .
local: Ka ba jaa ŋme chɛl daa 'yɛrɛ, Ŋmaa daba nyã ju tɩ na fʊ yuo a Barabasɩ na fʊ kʊ sɩ.

eng: Barabbas had been thrown into prison because of a riot that had occurred in the city, and for murder.
local: Ba pɔɔ naa a Barabasɩ, bojũu 'yɛr dɔ̃ɔ lo be a tẽe pɔ, ka wʊ kʊ nɩrɛ.

eng: Pilate addressed them again because he wanted to release Jesus.
local: Pilate na bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ bar a Yesu wʊ lɛ 'yɛr kʊ a nɩbɛ go.

eng: They kept shouting out, Crucify him! Crucify him.
local: Tɩchɛ ka ba chɛnɛ ŋmiere a chɛlsɩ 'yɛrɛ, Kpa wʊ a daa ju, kpa wʊ a daa ju.

eng: For the third time, Pilate said to them, Why? What wrong has he done? Ive found no legal basis for the death penalty in his case. Therefore, I will have him whipped, then let him go.
local: Gbɛɛ ata lɛ wʊ sʊʊr ba, Bo? Bãa dɛɛr lɛ wʊ ɩ? Maa ba nyɛ dɛɛr jaa wʊn sɛɛn kũu ɛ. A lɛ jũu ɩn vɛ̃n ka ba fɔb wʊ tɩ bar wʊ.

eng: But they were adamant, shouting their demand that Jesus be crucified. Their voices won out.
local: Tɩchɛ ka ba chɛnɛ tãna bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ kpa wʊ a daa ju, ka a ba gɔ̃mɔ̃ wa tʊ̃ɔ wʊ.

eng: Pilate issued his decision to grant their request.
local: Lɛ lɛ a Pilate 'yɛr ɩka wʊn ɩ naa a lɛ ban 'yɛr.
