eng: He released the one they asked for, who had been thrown into prison because of a riot and murder. But he handed Jesus over to their will.
local: A Barabasɩ 'lɔ na dɔ̃ɔ dam a tẽe na wʊ kʊn nɩrɛ ka ba nyɔɔ wʊ pɔɔ, wʊ yuo wʊn bar tɩ kʊ ba a Yesu.

eng: As they led Jesus away, they grabbed Simon, a man from Cyrene, who was coming in from the countryside. They put the cross on his back and made him carry it behind Jesus.
local: A lɛ ban chiine a Yesu, ba nyɔɔn daba yuor na di Simon, Sirenɩ lɛ wʊ yi na wʊ yi a yõo tɩ kpɩɛrɛ a tẽe pɔ. Ba fɩr wʊn de a Yesu daa tuo wʊ ka wʊ turo nɩ wʊ.

eng: A huge crowd of people followed Jesus, including women, who were mourning and wailing for him.
local: Nɩyɔɔ turo wʊ naa lɔ̃ɔn pɔbɔ, bala lɛ kõnõ waala kʊrɔ wʊ.

eng: Jesus turned to the women and said, Daughters of Jerusalem, dont cry for me. Rather, cry for yourselves and your children.
local: Ka a Yesu lɩɛb 'yɛr kʊ ba, Jerusalɛm pɔɔbil, yɩ ta kõnõ kʊrɔ ma ɛ, yɩ kõnõ kʊrɔ a yɩ mãɛ nɩ a yɩ bibiir.

eng: The time will come when they will say, Happy are those who are unable to become pregnant, the wombs that never gave birth, and the breasts that never nursed a child.
local: Bojũu bɩbir kɔ̃ɔ na wa vɩɛ naa ka yɩ 'yɛr, Pʊpɛl lɛ kʊ a pɔbɔ bala na ab nɩ pɔɔ 'lɔ jaa na ba dɔɔ bie ɛ, bɩɩ bɩrɛ ala jaa na ba 'yɛn kaa ɛ.

eng: Then they will say to the mountains, Fall on us, and to the hills, Cover us.
local: A ban pãa 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ a tɔ̃bɛrɛ, Yɩ lo pɔɔ sɩ, na yɩ ũu sɩ. .

eng: If they do these things when the tree is green, what will happen when it is dry.
local: Ala ɩ nɩbɛ maala naa a nɩtɔ̃nɔ̃ kʊ a tɩɛ na chɛnɛ ɩ a 'maar, ŋmɩnɛ ban ɩ wʊ 'lɔ wa ko.

eng: They also led two other criminals to be executed with Jesus.
local: Dɔɔr ayi na maal dɛɛr, ka ba nyɔɔr ba 'matãan a Yesu chiine a kʊfʊ jie.

eng: When they arrived at the place called The Skull, they crucified him, along with the criminals, one on his right and the other on his left.
local: Ba chiin ban tɩ tãn a jie ban bʊɔlɔ Julaa, be lɛ ba kpa ãsʊɔ jaa a wʊ daa ju, ka a nɩdɛbɛ ayi bala ka a ãsʊɔ be a Yesu nũsʊ̃ɔ jie, tɩchɛ ka a ãsʊɔ mɩ be a wʊ gʊbaa jie.

eng: Jesus said, Father, forgive them, for they dont know what theyre doing. They drew lots as a way of dividing up his clothing.
local: Be lɛ a Yesu 'yɛr, N Sãa, vɛ̃ kʊ ba, baa bɔ̃ɔ a lɛ ban ɩrɛ ɛ. Gbãm lɛ ba gba na ba põn a wʊ fuusɩ.

eng: The people were standing around watching, but the leaders sneered at him, saying, He saved others. Let him save himself if he really is the Christ sent from God, the chosen one.
local: Ka a nɩbɛ ara kaara wʊ, ka a Juu nɩbɛ nɩbɛrɛ ara kpɛrɛ wʊ na ba 'yɛrɛ, Wʊ ma faan bamɩne, vɛ̃ a wʊ faa a wʊ mãɛ ala ɩ 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a Nɩ-iraa 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn na ir.

eng: The soldiers also mocked him. They came up to him, offering him sour wine.
local: Ka a sojasɩ mɩ wa ɩrɛ wʊ laara, na ba de a dãmiir kʊ wʊ.

eng: and saying, If you really are the king of the Jews, save yourself.
local: Tɩ na ba 'yɛrɛ, Ala ɩ fʊ̃ʊ lɛ ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ nãa, faa a fʊ mãɛ.

eng: Above his head was a notice of the formal charge against him. It read This is the king of the Jews.
local: Ba sɛb sɛbie mar be a wʊ ju pɔ ka a 'yɛr, Nyãna lɛ ɩ a Juu Nɩbɛ Nãa.

eng: One of the criminals hanging next to Jesus insulted him: Arent you the Christ? Save yourself and us.
local: A nɩdɛbɛ bala ban lɔ̃ɔ kpan wʊ, ãsʊɔ tʊrɔ wʊn 'yɛrɛ, A ba ɩ fʊ̃ʊ lɛ ɩ a Nɩ-iraa ɛ? Faa a fʊ mãɛ tɩ mɩ faa sɩ.

eng: Responding, the other criminal spoke harshly to him, Dont you fear God, seeing that youve also been sentenced to die?.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a wʊ tɔ sʊɔ 'lɔ gɔ̃nɔ̃ wʊ 'yɛrɛ, Faa joro Nãaŋmɩn dãbãɛ ɛ, an ɩ a kũu 'lɔ lɛ fʊ mɩ kpire.

eng: We are rightly condemned, for we are receiving the appropriate sentence for what we did. But this man has done nothing wrong.
local: Sɩn gefʊ sɛɛn sɩ naa, sɩ nyɛn a lɛ na sɛɛ sɩn maal, tɩchɛ a daba nyã 'lɔ ba maal dɛɛr jaa ɛ.

eng: Then he said, Jesus, remember me when you come into your kingdom.
local: Tɩ pãa 'yɛr kʊ a Yesu, Tɩɛr tɔ ma fʊ̃ʊ wa kpɛ a fu nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, I assure you that today you will be with me in paradise.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ fʊ, dɩna maa nɩ fʊ̃ʊ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn tẽvɩla pɔ.

eng: It was now about noon, and darkness covered the whole earth until about three oclock.
local: A mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ dɔ̃ɔ gbʊrɔ a garju sãlsɔɔ tɩchɛ ka a chãa ɩ jaa gbɛb, ka a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ wa tɩ vɩr.

eng: while the sun stopped shining. Then the curtain in the sanctuary tore down the middle.
local: A mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ ba lɛ chaar go ɛ, ka a fɔbaa 'lɔ ban lũ var a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir die pɔ 'mar ayi.

eng: Crying out in a loud voice, Jesus said, Father, into your hands I entrust my life . After he said this, he breathed for the last time.
local: Be lɛ a Yesu tãn kpɛ̃ɔ jaa 'yɛr, N Sãa, fʊ nũu pɔ lɛ n de a n sɩɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ. Wʊn wa 'yɛr a nɩtɔ̃ baar, tɩ vʊʊr a wʊ vʊʊr baaraa.

eng: When the centurion saw what happened, he praised God, saying, Its really true: this man was righteous.
local: A sojasɩ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na nyɛ a lɛ na ɩ wʊ dãn Nãaŋmɩn tɩ 'yɛr, Sɩrɛ jaa a daba nyã ɩn nɩtor.

eng: All the crowds who had come together to see this event returned to their homes beating their chests after seeing what had happened.
local: Ka a nɩbɛ ba jaa na lɔ̃ɔ taa a be nyɛn a lɛ na ɩ ka ba lɩɛb pʊsɔ̃ɔ, na ba waala kulo.

eng: And everyone who knew him, including the women who had followed him from Galilee, stood at a distance observing these things.
local: Tɩchɛ a wʊ nɩbɔ̃ɔnsɩ pʊɔn a pɔbɔ bala na tu wʊ yi a Galilee ara tɛɛr tɩ kaara a lɛ na ɩrɛ.

eng: Now there was a man named Joseph who was a member of the council. He was a good and righteous man.
local: Daba ãsʊɔ mɩ ben be ban bʊɔlɔ Josefʊ, wʊ pʊɔ naa a nɩbɛrɛ pɔ, na wʊ ɩ nɩsʊ̃ nɩ nɩtor.

eng: He hadnt agreed with the plan and actions of the council. He was from the Jewish city of Arimathea and eagerly anticipated Gods kingdom.
local: Wʊ tɔr naa a lɛ ban 'yɛr nɩ a lɛ ban ɩrɛ. Wʊ yi naa a Judiya paal pɔ tẽe ban bʊɔlɔ Arimatɩya, wʊ mɩ dɔ̃ɔ chɛlɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ.

eng: This man went to Pilate and asked for Jesus body.
local: 'Lɔ lɛ chen a Pilate sɛ̃ tɩ sʊɔr a Yesu ãgãn ɩka wʊ ũu.

eng: Taking it down, he wrapped it in a linen cloth and laid it in a tomb carved out of the rock, in which no one had ever been buried.
local: Na wʊ pãa yɔɔ a kũu suun tɩ vil wʊ a fɔpɩla pɔɔ tɩ ũu wʊ a yaa ban chɩ kʊsɩr pɔ, ban ba ũu nɩrɛ sɛrɛ ɛ.

eng: It was the Preparation Day for the Sabbath, and the Sabbath was quickly approaching.
local: A daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ ba mɩ chɔɔrɔ ka a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir na piel.

eng: The women who had come with Jesus from Galilee followed Joseph. They saw the tomb and how Jesus body was laid in it.
local: A pɔbɔ bala nɩ a Yesu na 'mataa yi a Galilee wa, ba tu naa a Josefʊ tɩ nyɛ a yaa nɩ a lɛ ban ũu a Yesu.

eng: then they went away and prepared fragrant spices and perfumed oils. They rested on the Sabbath, in keeping with the commandment.
local: Tɩ na ba pãa lɩɛb kul tɩ chɔɔr a tãɩ nɩ a kãnyũu vɩla na tɩ fɔ a Yesu ãgãn, tɩchɛ a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir jũu, baa dam ɛ, ba pɛ̃n naa a lɛ a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ na 'yɛr.

eng: In the beginning was the Word and the Word was with God and the Word was God.
local: A pielfʊ daar a 'lɔ ban bʊɔlɔ a 'Yɛr ben be, ka a 'Yɛr be a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃, ka a 'Yɛr ɩ Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: The Word was with God in the beginning.
local: Wʊ dɔ̃ɔ be a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ a pielfʊ daar.

eng: Everything came into being through the Word, and without the Word nothing came into being. What came into being.
local: A 'Yɛr nyã lɛ Nãaŋmɩn maal bomo a jaa. Bon jaa ba maal ka a ba tu a 'Yɛr nyã pɔ ɛ.

eng: through the Word was life, and the life was the light for all people.
local: A wʊ pɔ lɛ nyɛvʊr be, a wʊ nyɛvʊr nyã lɛ kʊrɔ bomo a jaa chãa.

eng: The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness doesnt extinguish the light.
local: A chãa nyã lɛ chaara a libe pɔ, ka libe ba tʊ̃ɔ wʊ kpĩin ɛ.

eng: A man named John was sent from God.
local: Nãaŋmɩn tʊ̃n daba na di Jɔɔn.

eng: He came as a witness to testify concerning the light, so that through him everyone would believe in the light.
local: 'Lɔ lɛ wa di a dãasɩɛ na wʊ 'yɛr a chãa nyã 'yɛr, ka a nɩrɛ jaa na tu a wʊ sɛ̃ sɔɔ de a chãa.

eng: He himself wasnt the light, but his mission was to testify concerning the light.
local: Jɔɔn ba ɩ a chãa ɛ, tɩ wʊ wa naa ɩka wʊ wa 'yɛr a chãa 'yɛr.

eng: The true light that shines on all people was coming into the world.
local: A yelmãɛ chãa nyã lɛ chaara 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ nɩrɛ jaa na waara a wɛr pɔ.

eng: The light was in the world, and the world came into being through the light, but the world didnt recognize the light.
local: A 'yɛr dɔ̃ɔ be a wɛr pɔ, ka a Nãaŋmɩn gba maal a wɛr tu a wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ, tɩchɛ ãsʊɔ jaa ba bɔ̃ɔ wʊ ɛ.

eng: The light came to his own people, and his own people didnt welcome him.
local: Wʊ wa naa a wʊ mãɛ paal pɔ, tɩchɛ a wʊ mãɛ nɩbɛ ba de wʊ ɛ.

eng: But those who did welcome him, those who believed in his name, he authorized to become Gods children.
local: Tɩchɛ nɩbɛ bamɩne de wʊ naa, na ba sɔɔ de a wʊ yuor, ka wʊ kʊ ba sɔr ka ba ɩ Nãaŋmɩn bibiir.

eng: born not from blood nor from human desire or passion, but born from God.
local: Baa ɩ Nãaŋmɩn bibiir nɩrɛ dɔɔfʊ kaar ɛ, bɩɩ nɩrɛ bɔfʊ pɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn mãɛ lɛ maal ba a wʊ bibiir.

eng: The Word became flesh and made his home among us. We have seen his glory, glory like that of a fathers only son, full of grace and truth.
local: A 'yɛr wa ɩ naa nɩrɛ na wʊ jãnɛ a sɩ sɛ̃. Sɩ nyɛ naa a wʊ tɩɩr, a tɩɩr nyã na ɩ a Sãa Bidabʊ̃'yen tɛɛ. 'Lɔ lɛ yi a Sãa sɛ̃ wa, na wʊ tara wãɛfʊ nɩ yelmãɛ.

eng: John testified about him, crying out, This is the one of whom I said, He who comes after me is greater than me because he existed before me.
local: Jɔɔn 'yɛr a wʊ 'yɛr, na wʊ tãna 'yɛrɛ, Nyã lɛ n 'yɛr yɩ ɩka wʊn wa naa, 'lɔ na be a n puor waara gar ma naa, bojũu wʊ dɔ̃ɔ be be tɩ ba wa dɔɔ ma.

eng: From his fullness we have all received grace upon grace.
local: A wãɛfʊ sɛɛ wʊ naa, ka a wʊ maal vɩla lʊɔr sɩ jaa bʊ̃'yen 'yen dɔɔlɔ taa waara a sɩ sɛ̃.

eng: as the Law was given through Moses, so grace and truth came into being through Jesus Christ.
local: Bojũu, Mosesɩ lɛ dɔ̃ɔ waan a nɛɛ, tɩchɛ Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto mɩ lɛ waan a wãɛfʊ nɩ yelmãɛ.

eng: No one has ever seen God. God the only Son, who is at the Fathers side, has made God known.
local: Nɩbʊ̃'yen jaa ba nyɛ Nãaŋmɩn ɛ, tɩchɛ a Bidabʊ̃'yen tɛɛ 'lɔ na be a Sãa lõboor lɛ wiil sɩ a lɛ Nãaŋmɩn na be.

eng: This is Johns testimony when the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem sent priests and Levites to ask him, Who are you.
local: A nɩbɛrɛ na be a Jerusalɛm tʊ̃n a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlorbo nɩ a Juu nɩbɛ puorfʊ jie sʊ̃sʊ̃nbɔ ka ba tɩ sʊʊr Jɔɔn, nɩbuor lɛ wʊ ɩ.

eng: John confessed , Im not the Christ.
local: Ka a Jɔɔn 'yɛr ba, N ba ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Nɩ-iraa 'lɔ wʊn tʊ̃ ɩka wʊ wa faa a wʊ nɩbɛ ɛ.

eng: They asked him, Then who are you? Are you Elijah? John said, Im not. Are you the prophet? John answered, No.
local: Ka ba lɛ sʊʊr wʊ ɩka 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a Elaija bɩɩ? Ka wʊ 'yɛr, N ba ɩ wʊ ɛ. Ka ba lɛ sʊʊr wʊ ka 'lɔ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ ɔ? Ka wʊ ɩka, Kai.

eng: They asked, Who are you? We need to give an answer to those who sent us. What do you say about yourself.
local: Ka ba sʊʊr wʊ, Tɩ an lɛ fʊ pãa ɩ? A sɩn tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a bala na tʊ̃ sɩ, 'yɛr sɩ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ fʊn ɩ.

eng: John replied, I am a voice crying out in the wilderness, Make the Lords path straight, just as the prophet Isaiah said.
local: Jɔɔn den a lɛ a Isaiya na dɔ̃ɔ sɛb na wʊ 'yɛr, Maa lɛ a kɔkɔr 'lɔ na tãna a dãlpʊɔ pɔ, ɩka yɩ chɔɔr a Soro sɔr.

eng: Those sent by the Pharisees.
local: A Faraseemɩne tʊ̃n nɩbɛ bamɩne a Jɔɔn sɛ̃.

eng: asked, Why do you baptize if you arent the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the prophet.
local: Ba wa sʊʊr wʊ naa, Ala ɩ fʊ̃ʊ baa ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Nɩ-iraa ɛ, bɩɩ a Elaija ɛ, bɩɩ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ ãsʊɔ ɛ, ŋmɩnɛ a fʊ suoro a nɩbɛ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ.

eng: John answered, I baptize with water. Someone greater stands among you, whom you dont recognize.
local: Be lɛ a Jɔɔn 'yɛr ba, Maa den kʊ̃ɔ suoro nɩ a nɩbɛ, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ kɔ̃ɔ ben a yɩ pɔ, yɩn ba bɔ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: He comes after me, but Im not worthy to untie his sandal straps.
local: An ɩ maa lɛ de nie wa gba, n ba sɛɛ ɩka n for a wʊ nafɔbɔ mibe gba ɛ.

eng: This encounter took place across the Jordan in Bethany where John was baptizing.
local: Jɔɔn be naa a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ puorfʊ jie suoro a nɩbɛ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ, a Jordan man gɔ̃ɔn na be a Betanɩ, be lɛ wʊ 'yɛr a 'yɛr anyãna.

eng: The next day John saw Jesus coming toward him and said, Look! The Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!.
local: Wɛr na wa chaar, Jɔɔn nyɛn a Yesu na tor wʊ waara ka wʊ 'yɛr, Nyɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Pɛraabile, 'lɔ lɛ na de a wɛr nɩbɛ yeldɛbɛ bar.

eng: This is the one about whom I said, He who comes after me is really greater than me because he existed before me.
local: Wʊ ɩ naa a 'lɔ ɩn 'yɛr ɩka, nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ na wa naa, wʊ gar ma naa, bojũu 'lɔ dɔ̃ɔ be be tɩ ba wa dɔɔ ma.

eng: Even I didnt recognize him, but I came baptizing with water so that he might be made known to Israel.
local: Maa ba bɔ̃ɔ a nɩnyɩnɛ wʊn ɩ ɛ, tɩ naa wa suoro yɩ nɩ kʊ̃ɔ ka bala jaa na be a Isɩral na nyɛ wʊ.

eng: John testified, I saw the Spirit coming down from heaven like a dove, and it rested on him.
local: A Jɔɔn dãasɩɛ nɩ nyã, N ben be nyɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na yi saaju fɩka ŋmam, wa 'mar wʊ, ka a Sɩɛ be a wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: Even I didnt recognize him, but the one who sent me to baptize with water said to me, The one on whom you see the Spirit coming down and resting is the one who baptizes with the Holy Spirit.
local: N ba dɔ̃ɔ bɔ̃ɔ a nɩnyɩnɛ wʊn ɩ ɛ. A 'lɔ na tʊ̃ ma ɩka n wa suoro a nɩbɛ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ 'yɛr kʊ ma, A nɩrɛ 'lɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ fʊn wa nyɛ a Sɩɛ na suu wa 'mar na waa yi ɛ, 'lɔ lɛ na suoro a nɩbɛ nɩ a Sɩɛ.

eng: I have seen and testified that this one is Gods Son.
local: N nyɛn a anyã na ɩ, 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka 'lɔ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Bidabʊ̃'yen.

eng: The next day John was standing again with two of his disciples.
local: A wɛr na chaar go, Jɔɔn ara na a be nɩ a wʊ poturbo ayi.

eng: When he saw Jesus walking along he said, Look! The Lamb of God.
local: Be lɛ wʊ nyɛ Yesu na tala, ka wʊ 'yɛr, Yɩ nyɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Pɛraabile.

eng: The two disciples heard what he said, and they followed Jesus.
local: A Jɔɔn poturbo ayi bala na wõ a lɛ wʊn 'yɛr, ka ba turo a Yesu chiine.

eng: When Jesus turned and saw them following, he asked, What are you looking for? They said, Rabbi , where are you staying.
local: Yesu na wa lɩɛb nyɛ ba, wʊ sʊʊr ba naa, Bo yɩ bʊɔrɔ? Ka ba 'yɛr, Wiwile nyɩnɛ fʊ kpɩɛrɛ.

eng: He replied, Come and see. So they went and saw where he was staying, and they remained with him that day. It was about four oclock in the afternoon.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Yɩ wa bɔ̃ɔ a jie. Ba jãnɛ naa a wʊ sɛ̃, bojũu mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ bal baar a daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃.

eng: One of the two disciples who heard what John said and followed Jesus was Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter.
local: A nɩbɛ ayi bala na wõ a lɛ Jɔɔn na 'yɛr ka ba turo a Yesu, ãsʊɔ lɛ Andiru na ɩ a Simon Pita yɛbɛ.

eng: He first found his own brother Simon and said to him, We have found the Messiah .
local: Lɛ a Andiru na de nie ɩ lɛ, wʊ bɔ naa a wʊ yɛbɛ Simon 'yɛr wʊ, Sɩ nyɛn a Nɩ-iraa 'lɔ na wa faa a wʊ nɩbɛ ban bʊɔlɔ a Kɩrɩsɩto.

eng: He led him to Jesus. Jesus looked at him and said, You are Simon, son of John. You will be called Cephas .
local: Andiru tara naa a yɛbɛ waan a Yesu sɛ̃. Yesu na wa nyɛ wʊ, ka wʊ 'yɛr, Jɔɔn bidaba Simon, ban bʊɔlɔ fʊ naa Sɩfasɩ. A wʊ par lɛ Pita.

eng: The next day Jesus wanted to go into Galilee, and he found Philip. Jesus said to him, Follow me.
local: A ber anaar daar, Yesu chiine naa a Galilee, be lɛ wʊ nyɛ Filip na wʊ bʊɔl wʊ ɩka wʊ wa turo wʊ.

eng: Philip was from Bethsaida, the hometown of Andrew and Peter.
local: A Filip yin Betɩsaida, Andiru nɩ Pita tẽe.

eng: Philip found Nathanael and said to him, We have found the one Moses wrote about in the Law and the Prophets: Jesus, Josephs son, from Nazareth.
local: Filip mɩ chen tɩ nyɛ Natanɩɛl 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Sɩ nyɛn a 'lɔ a Mosesɩ nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ na sɛb a wʊ yele, 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a Yesu na yi a Najaretɩ, Josefʊ bidaba.

eng: Nathanael responded, Can anything from Nazareth be good? Philip said, Come and see.
local: Be lɛ a Natanɩɛl sʊʊr wʊ, Yelsʊ̃ na tʊ̃ɔ yi a Najaretɩ tẽe ɔ? Ka a Filip 'yɛr, Wa kaa.

eng: Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him and said about him, Here is a genuine Israelite in whom there is no deceit.
local: Yesu na wa nyɛ a Natanɩɛl na tor wʊ waara, wʊ 'yɛr naa, Yɩ nyɛ a sɩ sãakpã Isɩral yɔ̃ɔ na ba tara bɛlfʊ ɛ.

eng: Nathanael asked him, How do you know me? Jesus answered, Before Philip called you, I saw you under the fig tree.
local: Ka a Natanɩɛl sʊʊr a Yesu, Ŋmɩnɛ fʊ ɩrɛ wa bɔ̃ɔ ma? Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Filip na wa bʊɔlɔ fʊ, ka n nyɛ fʊ naa a kɔ̃kɔ̃ɔ tɩɛ pĩlem.

eng: Nathanael replied, Rabbi, you are Gods Son. You are the king of Israel.
local: Be lɛ a Natanɩɛl 'yɛr, Wiwile, fʊ ɩn Nãaŋmɩn Bie nɩ a Isɩral Nãa.

eng: Jesus answered, Do you believe because I told you that I saw you under the fig tree? You will see greater things than these!.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr sɔɔ wʊ, Fʊ sɔɔ ma naa, bojũu ɩn 'yɛr ɩka n nyɛ fʊ naa a kɔ̃kɔ̃ɔ tɩɛ pĩlem ɔ? Fʊn nyɛn yelbɛrɛ ka a gar lɛ.

eng: I assure you that you will see heaven open and Gods angels going up to heaven and down to earth on the Human One.
local: Na wʊ 'yɛr, sɩrɛ jaa lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ fʊ ɩka fʊn nyɛn saaju na yuo ka a Nãaŋmɩn malakasɩ suro tɩ duoro a maa Nɩsaal Bie 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: On the third day there was a wedding in Cana of Galilee. Jesus mother was there, and.
local: A ber a nyã puor, Yesu ma Mɛɛr be naa a pɔɔ ban kulo a tẽe na be a Galilee paal pɔ, ka ba bʊɔlɔ Keena.

eng: Jesus and his disciples were also invited to the celebration.
local: Ba bʊɔl Yesu nɩ a wʊ poturbo ka ba mɩ be be a pɔɔ kulfʊ jie.

eng: When the wine ran out, Jesus mother said to him, They dont have any wine.
local: A dãa na wa baar, Mɛɛr 'yɛr kʊ Yesu, A nɩbɛ dãa baar naa.

eng: Jesus replied, Woman, what does that have to do with me? My time hasnt come yet.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, N ma, n sɔ̃ɔ ba ta ɛ, ta wiil ma a lɛ ɩn na ɩ ɛ.

eng: His mother told the servants, Do whatever he tells you.
local: Mɛɛr lɩɛb 'yɛr kʊ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ, Yɩ ɩ lɛ Jaa a Yesu na wa 'yɛr ɩka yɩ ɩ.

eng: Nearby were six stone water jars used for the Jewish cleansing ritual, each able to hold about twenty or thirty gallons.
local: Kʊsɩɛ dʊʊr ayʊɔb ben be, be lɛ a nɩbɛ ma ɔ̃ɔ a kʊ̃ɔ pɛɛ a ba mãɛ lɛ a ba puorfʊ na ɩka ba ɩ. Yoi anaar bɩɩ anũu lɛ ma sɛɛ ãsʊɔ jaa.

eng: Jesus said to the servants, Fill the jars with water, and they filled them to the brim.
local: Yesu 'yɛr a tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ ɩka ba ɔ̃ɔ kʊ̃ɔ sɛɛ a dʊʊr.

eng: Then he told them, Now draw some from them and take it to the headwaiter, and they did.
local: An wa sɛɛ ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ pãa ɔ̃ɔ a kʊ̃ɔ amɩne kʊ a nɩkpɛ̃ɛ 'lɔ na kaara a pɔɔ paalaa yele. Ka a tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ ɩ a lɛ a Yesu na 'yɛr ba.

eng: The headwaiter tasted the water that had become wine. He didnt know where it came from, though the servants who had drawn the water knew. The headwaiter called the groom.
local: Ka a nɩkpɛ̃ɛ 'lɔ na wa nyũ a kʊ̃ɔ ala na lɩɛb ɩ dãa, waa bɔ̃ɔ a jie a dãa na yi ɛ. A tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ 'lɔ bɔ̃ɔ naa. Be lɛ wʊ bʊɔl a pɔɔ paalaa sɩrɛ yin chɛ̃chɛ̃ɛ.

eng: and said, Everyone serves the good wine first. They bring out the second-rate wine only when the guests are drinking freely. You kept the good wine until now.
local: na wʊ 'yɛr, Nɩbɛ ma den nie waan a dãnʊ̃ɔ, bala wa nyũ tɩɩ baar, ka ba pãa waan a ala na ba bɔ̃ɔ nʊ̃mɔ̃ ɛ. Tɩchɛ fʊ̃ʊ 'lɔ bin a dãnʊ̃ɔ bar wa ta a baaraa.

eng: This was the first miraculous sign that Jesus did in Cana of Galilee. He revealed his glory, and his disciples believed in him.
local: A nɛ'maa yele nyã lɛ de nie Yesu na ɩ a Keena na be a Galilee paal pɔ. Be lɛ Yesu wiil a wʊ tɩɩr, ka a wʊ poturbo sɔɔ de wʊ.

eng: After this, Jesus and his mother, his brothers, and his disciples went down to Capernaum and stayed there for a few days.
local: A nyã puor, 'lɔ naa ma nɩ a wʊ yɛbil naa poturbo 'mantaa chen Kapenahum ɩka ba tɩ jɛ̃ bãlãa.

eng: It was nearly time for the Jewish Passover, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
local: A kʊ̃ lɛ kor ɛ, ka a Juu nɩbɛ Gõl Bar Difʊ ta, Yesu don a Jerusalɛm.

eng: He found in the temple those who were selling cattle, sheep, and doves, as well as those involved in exchanging currency sitting there.
local: Be lɛ wʊ tɩ nyɛ nɩbɛ na daara niin, piir nɩ papar, a Juu nɩbɛ puorfʊ yir bɛchaar pɔ. Wʊ nyɛn bamɩne mɩ na jɛ̃n tɛɛbʊlsɩ tɛɛrɛ libie.

eng: He made a whip from ropes and chased them all out of the temple, including the cattle and the sheep. He scattered the coins and overturned the tables of those who exchanged currency.
local: Wʊ den miir maal kpaara diin ba bar a puorfʊ yir bɛchaar pɔ, nɩ a ba piir nɩ a niin. Wʊ tɩɛ naa a libitɛɛrbɛ tɛɛbʊlsɩ lɔb ka a libie yɛ̃yɛl.

eng: He said to the dove sellers, Get these things out of here! Dont make my Fathers house a place of business.
local: Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a bala na daara a papar, Yɩ yin a papar a ka, na yɩ ta maal ma a Sãa yir daa difʊ jie ɛ.

eng: His disciples remembered that it is written, Passion for your house consumes me.
local: Ka a poturbo pãa tɩɛr tɔ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn gãn na 'yɛr, Lɛ ɩn nʊ̃ a fʊ yir 'moro man fɩka bũu.

eng: Then the Jewish leaders asked him, By what authority are you doing these things? What miraculous sign will you show us.
local: A Juu nɩbɛrɛ pãa sʊʊr a Yesu, Jãnɛ buor lɛ fʊn maal wiil sɩ ka a ɩ fʊ tara na sɔr fʊn maal a lɛ.

eng: Jesus answered, Destroy this temple and in three days Ill raise it up.
local: Ka Yesu 'yɛr, Yɩ ŋmɛr a puorfʊ yir nyãna, tɩ ka n de ber ata mɛn wʊ go.

eng: The Jewish leaders replied, It took forty-six years to build this temple, and you will raise it up in three days.
local: Ka a nɩbɛrɛ 'yɛr, Yuom lɩjaayi nɩ ayʊɔb lɛ ba de mɛn a puorfʊ yir nyã, ŋmɩnɛ fʊ̃ʊ na ɩ de ber ata tɛɛ mɛn wʊ.

eng: But the temple Jesus was talking about was his body.
local: Tɩchɛ a puorfʊ yir nyã a Yesu na 'yɛrɛ, ɩ naa a wʊ ãgãn lɛ wʊ 'yɛrɛ.

eng: After he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered what he had said, and they believed the scripture and the word that Jesus had spoken.
local: Yesu na wa ir a kũu pɔ, ka a wʊ poturbo tɩɛr yuo a lɛ wʊn 'yɛr, na ba pãa sɔɔ de lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn gãn na 'yɛr nɩ lɛ mɩ a Yesu na 'yɛr.

eng: While Jesus was in Jerusalem for the Passover Festival, many believed in his name because they saw the miraculous signs that he did.
local: A be a Jerusalɛm a Gõl Bar Difʊ na wa ta, nɩyɔɔ sɔɔn de a wʊ yuor, bojũu ba nyɛn a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ wʊn tʊ̃.

eng: But Jesus didnt trust himself to them because he knew all people.
local: Tɩchɛ Yesu na bɔ̃ɔ nɩrɛ jaa tɩɛr, waa sɔɔ de wʊ mãɛ kʊ ba ɛ.

eng: He didnt need anyone to tell him about human nature, for he knew what human nature was.
local: A ba ɩ nɩrɛ lɛ na 'yɛr wʊ lɛ nɩbɛ na be ɛ, bojũu wʊ bɔ̃ɔn nɩrɛ jaa tɩɛr.

eng: Very early in the morning on the first day of the week, the women went to the tomb, bringing the fragrant spices they had prepared.
local: A Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir divɩɛ bio bibio, a pɔbɔ den a kãnyũur vɩla ala ban chɔɔr chiin a be a yaa par.

eng: They found the stone rolled away from the tomb.
local: Ba tɩ nyɛn ka a kʊsɩr bil yi a yaa nɛɛ pɔ.

eng: but when they went in, they didnt find the body of the Lord Jesus.
local: Ka ba kpɛ tɩchɛ baa nyɛ a Yesu ãgãn ɛ.

eng: They didnt know what to make of this. Suddenly, two men were standing beside them in gleaming bright clothing.
local: A ba ara bẽne a lɛ nɛ ka a dɔɔr ayi wa dũu ba ka a ba bʊ̃sursɩ pɛl saa nyɩɩrfʊ kaar.

eng: The women were frightened and bowed their faces toward the ground, but the men said to them, Why do you look for the living among the dead?.
local: Ka a dãbãɛ kpɛ a pɔbɔ, ka ba kpa kpa jur a tẽe, tɩchɛ ka a dɔɔr sʊʊr ba, Bo so ka yɩ bʊɔrɔ a vʊr a kũusɩ pɔ?.

eng: He isnt here, but has been raised. Remember what he told you while he was still in Galilee.
local: Waa be ka ɛ. Wʊ ir naa. Yɩ tɩɛr a lɛ wʊn 'yɛr kʊ yɩ a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ wʊn be a yɩ sɛ̃, be a Galilee.

eng: that the Human One must be handed over to sinners, be crucified, and on the third day rise again.
local: Ban nyɔɔ naa a maa Nɩsaal Bie 'yɔ̃ɔ a nɩdɛbɛ nuur pɔ, ka ba kʊ ma a daa ju, tɩchɛ a ber ata daar, ka n lɛ ir ɩ vʊr. .

eng: Then they remembered his words.
local: Be lɛ ba pãa tɩɛr tɔ a wʊ 'yɛrbie.

eng: When they returned from the tomb, they reported all these things to the eleven and all the others.
local: Ban wa yi a yaa par kul, ba tɩ man a anyãna kʊ a Pie Nɩ Bʊ̃'yen nɩ nɩrɛ jaa.

eng: It was Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and the other women with them who told these things to the apostles.
local: Mɛɛr na yi Magdala, nɩ Joana nɩ a Jemesɩ ma Mɛɛr nɩ a bamɩne na pʊɔ ba lɛ man a anyãna kʊ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ.

eng: Their words struck the apostles as nonsense, and they didnt believe the women.
local: Tɩchɛ baa sɔɔ de a pɔbɔ 'yɛr ɛ, bojũu a ba 'yɛr nyɛntãan jire.

eng: But Peter ran to the tomb. When he bent over to look inside, he saw only the linen cloth. Then he returned home, wondering what had happened.
local: An be a lɛ ka Pita vaa ir jo chen a yaa par, na wʊ tɩ guun muul kaa na wʊ nyɛ a fɔpɩla na for gã wʊ yõ, ka wʊ lɩɛb yi tɩ bẽne a lɛ na ɩ.

eng: On that same day, two disciples were traveling to a village called Emmaus, about seven miles from Jerusalem.
local: A daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ a wʊ poturbo ayi chiine naa a tẽbile ãsʊɔ ban bʊɔlɔ Emayusɩ na ba jãan a Jerusalɛm ɛ.

eng: They were talking to each other about everything that had happened.
local: Ka ba pãa 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ taa a lɛ jaa na ɩ.

eng: While they were discussing these things, Jesus himself arrived and joined them on their journey.
local: A lɛ ban 'yɛrɛ na ba mana a anyãna kʊrɔ taa, ka a Yesu mãɛ wa pʊɔ ba a ba 'mataa chiine.

eng: They were prevented from recognizing him.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a lɩɩ ba a baa bɔ̃ɔ wʊ ɛ.

eng: He said to them, What are you talking about as you walk along? They stopped, their faces downcast.
local: Ka wʊ sʊʊr ba, Bo lɛ yɩ chiine mana kʊrɔ taa a lɛ mɔ̃? Ka a nɩbaal kpɛ ba a ba tɛɛ ara.

eng: The one named Cleopas replied, Are you the only visitor to Jerusalem who is unaware of the things that have taken place there over the last few days.
local: Ka ba ãsʊɔ yuor na di Kɩleopasɩ sʊʊr wʊ, Fʊ̃ʊ tɛɛ lɛ ɩ sãan be a Jerusalɛm na faa bɔ̃ɔ lɛ na ɩ a be a ber ayi anyãna ɛ.

eng: He said to them, What things? They said to him, The things about Jesus of Nazareth. Because of his powerful deeds and words, he was recognized by God and all the people as a prophet.
local: A wʊ sʊʊr, Bo yele? Ka ba 'yɛr, A Yesu na yi a Najaretɩ, Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ lɛ wʊ ɩ, ka a wʊ 'yɛr nɩ a wʊ maalfʊ kpɛ̃mɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nɩ a nɩbɛ niem.

eng: But our chief priests and our leaders handed him over to be sentenced to death, and they crucified him.
local: A bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a sɩ nɩbɛrɛ nyɔɔ wʊn tɩr ɩka ba kʊ, ka ba kpa wʊ a daa ju.

eng: We had hoped he was the one who would redeem Israel. All these things happened three days ago.
local: Tɩchɛ 'lɔ lɛ sɩn mɩ tɩɛrɛ ɩka wʊn faan a Isɩral nɩbɛ. A ala na ɩ dɛɛr jaa nɩ nyã, ber ata nɩ nyã a lɛ na ɩ.

eng: But theres more: Some women from our group have left us stunned. They went to the tomb early this morning.
local: A sɩ pɔbɔ bamɩne lɛ wa 'maa sɩ nɛɛ, a bibio nyãna lɛ ba chen a yaa par.

eng: and didnt find his body. They came to us saying that they had even seen a vision of angels who told them he is alive.
local: tɩchɛ baa tɩ nyɛ a wʊ ãgãn ɛ. Na ba wa 'yɛr kʊ sɩ ɩka ba nyɛn malakasɩ ka ba 'yɛr ɩka wʊ ir ɩ vʊr.

eng: Some of those who were with us went to the tomb and found things just as the women said. They didnt see him.
local: Ka a sɩ taaba bamɩne pãa mɩ chen a yaa par tɩ nyɛ ka ɩ a lɛ nɛ a pɔbɔ na 'yɛr lɛ, tɩchɛ a 'lɔ mãɛ baa nyɛ wʊ ɛ.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, You foolish people! Your dull minds keep you from believing all that the prophets talked about.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ yɩ faa tɩɛr lɛ, na yaa tʊ̃ɔ tɩɛr tɔ na yɩ sɔɔ de a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ na 'yɛr ɛ?.

eng: Wasnt it necessary for the Christ to suffer these things and then enter into his glory.
local: A ba ɩ a Nɩ-iraa na din a dɔɔyɛ anyãna tɩ na wʊ pãa kpɛ a wʊ tɩɩr pɔ ɛ.

eng: Then he interpreted for them the things written about himself in all the scriptures, starting with Moses and going through all the Prophets.
local: Wʊ piel naa Mosesɩ nɩ a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ ba jaa, na wʊ pãa wiil ba a par a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn gãn na 'yɛr a wʊ mãɛ 'yɛr.

eng: When they came to Emmaus, he acted as if he was going on ahead.
local: A lɛ ban wa tara a tẽe 'lɔ pɔ ban chiine, a Yesu po naa fɩka tɛɛr wʊn chiine.

eng: But they urged him, saying, Stay with us. Its nearly evening, and the day is almost over. So he went in to stay with them.
local: Tɩchɛ ka ba sʊɔr wʊ yãayãabɩlɛ 'yɛrɛ, Lɩɛb kpɛ wa gã a sɩ sɛ̃, bojũu mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ 'mʊr baar. Ka wʊ lɩɛb kpɛ na gã a ba sɛ̃.

eng: After he took his seat at the table with them, he took the bread, blessed and broke it, and gave it to them.
local: Be lɛ a ba tɩ 'mataa jãnɛ a bʊ̃diir par na di, ka wʊ de a paanʊ̃ɔ na puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan, tɩ piel ŋmara põnõ ba.

eng: Their eyes were opened and they recognized him, but he disappeared from their sight.
local: Ka a ba nɩbie pãa yuo, ka ba bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a Yesu lɛ, tɩ wʊ bɔr a ba niem.

eng: They said to each other, Werent our hearts on fire when he spoke to us along the road and when he explained the scriptures for us.
local: Ka ba 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ taa, Ala lɛ so wʊn 'yɛrɛ nɩ sɩ a sɔr pɔ ka a nʊ̃ɔ kpɛ sɩ a lɛ, wʊn wiil sɩ a lɛ na sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ.

eng: They got up right then and returned to Jerusalem. They found the eleven and their companions gathered together.
local: Be lɛ ba vaa ir fɔɔ jaa lɩɛb chen a Jerusalɛm tɩ nyɛ a Pie Nɩ Bʊ̃'yen nɩ a Yesu poturbo bamɩne na lɔ̃ɔ taa.

eng: They were saying to each other, The Lord really has risen! He appeared to Simon.
local: Na ba 'yɛr kʊ ba, Sɩrɛ jaa lɛ a sɩ Soro ir ɩ vʊr, na wʊ ir a wʊ mãɛ wiil a Simon.

eng: Then the two disciples described what had happened along the road and how Jesus was made known to them as he broke the bread.
local: A ba ayi bala pãa man a lɛ na ɩ a sɔr pɔ nɩ a lɛ ban de bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a Yesu lɛ wʊn wa ŋma a paanʊ̃ɔ.

eng: While they were saying these things, Jesus himself stood among them and said, Peace be with you.
local: A anyãna ban chɛnɛ 'yɛrɛ ka a Yesu mãɛ wa ara a ba sɛ̃ tɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Nyã'maar wʊ be a yɩ sɛ̃.

eng: They were terrified and afraid. They thought they were seeing a ghost.
local: Ka a dãbãɛ kpɛ ba a ba mii a lɛ a gbɛ gbɛ, tɩɛr ɩka kpĩin lɛ ba nyɛ.

eng: He said to them, Why are you startled? Why are doubts arising in your hearts?.
local: A wʊ sʊʊr ba, Bo lɛ so ka a dãbãɛ kpɛ yɩ a lɛ, a nɩ bo lɛ so ka yɩ baala a lɛ?.

eng: Look at my hands and my feet. Its really me! Touch me and see, for a ghost doesnt have flesh and bones like you see I have.
local: Yɩ kaa nyɛ a n nuur nɩ a gbɛɛ, maa mãɛ lɛ, yɩ sɩɩr ma kaa, kpĩin ba lɛ ɛ. Kpĩin maa tara ãgãn nʊ̃n nɩ kɔɔr nɩtãa lɛ yɩn nyɛ ma ɩn tara ɛ.

eng: As he said this, he showed them his hands and feet.
local: Wʊn 'yɛr a nɩtɔ̃ baar, wʊ wiil ban a wʊ nuur nɩ a wʊ gbɛɛ.

eng: Because they were wondering and questioning in the midst of their happiness, he said to them, Do you have anything to eat.
local: Tɩchɛ baa chɛnɛ sɔɔ de sɛrɛ ɛ, nʊ̃ɔ kpɛ ba naa ka a nɛɛr 'maa ba, ka wʊ sʊʊr ba, Yaa tara bon na kʊ ma ka n di ɛ.

eng: They gave him a piece of baked fish.
local: Be lɛ ba kʊ wʊ jãŋmaa ban wɔ̃.

eng: Taking it, he ate it in front of them.
local: ka wʊ 'wɔb be a ba niem.

eng: Jesus said to them, These are my words that I spoke to you while I was still with you—that everything written about me in the Law from Moses, the Prophets, and the Psalms must be fulfilled.
local: Wʊ pãa 'yɛr kʊ ba, A anyãna lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩn dɔ̃ɔ chɛnɛ be a yɩ sɛ̃. Yele a jaa na sɛb a Mosesɩ nɛɛ pɔ, nɩ a ala na sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ gãn pɔ, nɩ a yiel gãn pɔ, a n jũu na ɩ naa lɛ.

eng: Then he opened their minds to understand the scriptures.
local: Be lɛ wʊ pãa yuo a ba tɩɛr ka ba tʊ̃ɔ bɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr ala na sɛb a wʊ gãn pɔ.

eng: He said to them, This is what is written: the Christ will suffer and rise from the dead on the third day.
local: Wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, A anyãna lɛ sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ, a Nɩ-iraa na din dɔɔyɛ na wʊ kpi na wʊ lɛ ir ɩ vʊr a ber ata daar.

eng: and a change of heart and life for the forgiveness of sins must be preached in his name to all nations, beginning from Jerusalem.
local: Ban den a wʊ yuor mʊɔl kʊ nɩbʊrɔ ba jaa, ka ãsʊɔ jaa wa lɩɛb a wʊ tɩɛr, Nãaŋmɩn na vɛ̃ naa a wʊ yeldɛɛr bar, Jerusalɛm lɛ an piel.

eng: You are witnesses of these things.
local: Yɩn lɛ ɩ a anyãna dãasɩɛ dẽme.

eng: Look, Im sending to you what my Father promised, but you are to stay in the city until you have been furnished with heavenly power.
local: A maa na tɩ tʊ̃ naa a 'lɔ a n Sãa na tɩr a nɛɛ kʊ yɩ, tɩchɛ yɩ chɛnɛ jãnɛ a Jerusalɛm, ka a kpɛ̃ɔ 'lɔ wa yi a saaju wa pɔɔ yɩ.

eng: He led them out as far as Bethany, where he lifted his hands and blessed them.
local: Wʊ tara ban yin tɩ gbʊrɔ nɩ a Betanɩ na wʊ tur a nuur saaju sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ ba.

eng: As he blessed them, he left them and was taken up to heaven.
local: A lɛ wʊn sʊɔr a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ ba, wʊ bar ba naa ka Nãaŋmɩn 'mɔɔ wʊ don be a saaju.

eng: They worshipped him and returned to Jerusalem overwhelmed with joy.
local: A ba pãa gbʊr dumo puor wʊ tɩ lɩɛb pʊpɛl kul a Jerusalɛm.

eng: And they were continuously in the temple praising God.
local: Bɩbir jaa lɛ ba ma chen be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir tɩ dãna a Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: Jesus learned that the Pharisees had heard that he was making more disciples and baptizing more than John .
local: Yesu bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka a Faraseemɩne wõ naa wʊn nyɛrɛ nɩbɛ suoro ba a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ, ka a bala na turo wʊ gar a bala na turo a Jɔɔn.

eng: although Jesus disciples were baptizing, not Jesus himself.
local: Tɩchɛ Yesu poturbo lɛ dɔ̃ɔ suoro a nɩbɛ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ, a ba ɩ Yesu mãɛ lɛ ɛ.

eng: Therefore, he left Judea and went back to Galilee.
local: Yesu bar naa Judiya tɩ lɩɛb chiine a Galilee go.

eng: Jesus had to go through Samaria.
local: Ka a ɩ Samariya lɛ a sɛɛ ɩka wʊ tu.

eng: He came to a Samaritan city called Sychar, which was near the land Jacob had given to his son Joseph.
local: A sɔr 'lɔ wʊn tu tɩ yi naa a Saika tẽe pɔ, na wʊ gbʊr a wɛr 'lɔ a Jekɔb na dɔ̃ɔ de kʊ a wʊ bidaba Josefʊ a kõr jaa.

eng: Jacobs well was there. Jesus was tired from his journey, so he sat down at the well. It was about noon.
local: A lɔ-ɔ̃ɔ a Jekɔb na dɔ̃ɔ tuu chɛnɛ na be be, ka a Yesu jɛ̃ gbʊr a lɔ-ɔ̃ɔ bojũu wʊ chen bal. Mɔ̃tɔ̃tuo sɔ̃ɔ lɛ.

eng: A Samaritan woman came to the well to draw water. Jesus said to her, Give me some water to drink.
local: Ka a Samariya pɔɔ suu a lɔ-ɔ̃ɔ pɔ kʊ̃ɔ ɔ̃ɔfʊ jie. Ka a Yesu sʊɔr wʊ, Pɔɔ yãayãabɩlɛ, ɔ̃ɔ a kʊ̃ɔ kʊ ma ka n nyũ.

eng: His disciples had gone into the city to buy him some food.
local: A Yesu poturbo mɩ kpɛ̃n a tẽe pɔ ɩka ba tɩ da bʊ̃diir.

eng: The Samaritan woman asked, Why do you, a Jewish man, ask for something to drink from me, a Samaritan woman.
local: Ka a Samariya pɔɔ 'yɛr, Fʊ̃ʊ Juu nɩrɛ, maa mɩ Samariya pɔɔ, taa ɩ ŋmɩnɛ ka fʊ ɩka n kʊ fʊ kʊ̃ɔ ka fʊ nyũ, sɩn nɩ Juu nɩbɛ na ba mʊɔrɔ taa bũu ɛ?.

eng: Jesus responded, If you recognized Gods gift and who is saying to you, Give me some water to drink, you would be asking him and he would give you living water.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr wʊ, Faa bɔ̃ɔ a bon a Nãaŋmɩn na bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ kʊ fʊ ɛ. Faa mɩ bɔ̃ɔ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na sʊɔrɔ fʊ a kʊ̃ɔ ɩka wʊ nyũ ɛ, fʊ̃ʊ ta bɔ̃ɔ, fʊn taa sʊɔr man a kʊ̃ɔ 'lɔ na kʊrɔ nyɛvʊr.

eng: The woman said to him, Sir, you dont have a bucket and the well is deep. Where would you get this living water?.
local: A pɔɔ 'yɛr, Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, faa tara bʊ̃-ɔ̃ɔnaa ɛ, ka a lɔ-ɔ̃ɔ mɩ yulʊ̃, nyɩnɛ lɛ fʊn nyɛ a kʊ̃ɔ ala na kʊrɔ a nyɛvʊr?.

eng: You arent greater than our father Jacob, are you? He gave this well to us, and he drank from it himself, as did his sons and his livestock.
local: A sɩ sãakpã Jekɔb lɛ tuu a lɔ-ɔ̃ɔ nyãna kʊ sɩ, ka wʊ yɔn nɩ a wʊ dʊ̃n nyuro. Fʊ̃ʊ bɛrma gar a Jekɔb bɩɩ.

eng: Jesus answered, Everyone who drinks this water will be thirsty again.
local: Ka Yesu 'yɛr, Ɩ̃sʊɔ jaa na nyũ a kʊ̃ɔ nyãna, kɔ̃nyũur na kpɛ wʊ̃n go.

eng: but whoever drinks from the water that I will give will never be thirsty again. The water that I give will become in those who drink it a spring of water that bubbles up into eternal life.
local: Tɩchɛ ãsʊɔ jaa na nyũ a kʊ̃ɔ ala ɩn na kʊ wʊ, kɔ̃nyũur kʊ̃ lɛ kpɛ wʊ go ɛ. A kʊ̃ɔ ala ɩn na kʊ wʊ kaara kɔ̃bulaa na yire, naa kʊrɔ nyɛvʊr na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ.

eng: The woman said to him, Sir, give me this water, so that I will never be thirsty and will never need to come here to draw water.
local: Ka a pɔɔ 'yɛr, Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ yãayãabɩlɛ, kʊ ma a kʊ̃ɔ ala ka n nyũ, ka a kɔ̃nyũur ta lɛ nyɔɔ ma ka n suu a lɔ-ɔ̃ɔ nyã pɔ go ɛ.

eng: Jesus said to her, Go, get your husband, and come back here.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr wʊ, Kul tɩ tara a fʊ sɩrɛ waan.

eng: The woman replied, I dont have a husband. You are right to say, I dont have a husband, Jesus answered.
local: Ka a pɔɔ 'yɛr, N ma tara sɩrɛ ɛ. A Yesu 'yɛr, Yelmãɛ fʊ 'yɛr ɩka faa tara sɩrɛ ɛ.

eng: Youve had five husbands, and the man you are with now isnt your husband. Youve spoken the truth.
local: Fʊ dɔ̃ɔ kul sɩrbɛ anũu, tɩ a daba 'lɔ sɛ̃ fʊn be a pʊ̃pãanyã ba ɩ a fʊ sɩrɛ ɛ.

eng: The woman said, Sir, I see that you are a prophet.
local: Ka a pɔɔ 'yɛr, Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ n nyɛ naa a fʊ ɩ Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ.

eng: Our ancestors worshipped on this mountain, but you and your people say that it is necessary to worship in Jerusalem.
local: A sɩ sãakpãmɩne, a tɔ̃ɔ nyã ju lɛ ba ma do tɩ puor a Nãaŋmɩn tɩchɛ a yɩn Juu nɩbɛ 'yɛr ɩka Jerusalɛm tɛɛ lɛ ɩ a puorfʊ jie.

eng: Jesus said to her, Believe me, woman, the time is coming when you and your people will worship the Father neither on this mountain nor in Jerusalem.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Pɔɔ sɔɔ de ma, sɔ̃ɔ waara an kʊ̃ ɩ a tɔ̃ɔ nyã ju bɩɩ Jerusalɛm yɩn do tɩ puor a Sãa Nãaŋmɩn go ɛ.

eng: You and your people worship what you dont know; we worship what we know because salvation is from the Jews.
local: A yɩn Samariya nɩbɛ ba bɔ̃ɔ a 'lɔ yɩn puoro ɛ, a sɩn Juu nɩbɛ bɔ̃ɔ naa a Nãaŋmɩn 'lɔ sɩn puoro. Bojũu a sɩ jie lɛ Nãaŋmɩn na tu faa nɩbɛ.

eng: But the time is coming—and is here!—when true worshippers will worship in spirit and truth. The Father looks for those who worship him this way.
local: Tɩchɛ a sɔ̃ɔ waara, wʊ gɔr ta baar, a bala na puoro a Nãaŋmɩn sɩrɛ jaa, a sɩɛ na wiil ba naa ka ba puor a Sãa Nãaŋmɩn nɩ yelmãɛ. Ba nyãna puorfʊ lɛ a Sãa Nãaŋmɩn bʊɔrɔ.

eng: God is spirit, and it is necessary to worship God in spirit and truth.
local: Nãaŋmɩn ɩn sɩɛ, bala na puoro wʊ a sɩɛ pɔ wʊn wiil ba naa ka ba puor wʊ nɩ yelmãɛ pɔ.

eng: The woman said, I know that the Messiah is coming, the one who is called the Christ. When he comes, he will teach everything to us.
local: Ka a pɔɔ 'yɛr, N bɔ̃ɔ naa ɩka a Nɩ-iraa na wa naa, a 'lɔ sɩn na bʊɔlɔ a Kɩrɩsɩto. 'Lɔ wa wa, wʊn wiil sɩ naa yele a jaa par.

eng: Jesus said to her, I Am—the one who speaks with you.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a pɔɔ, Maa lɛ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ nɩ fʊ̃ʊ na 'yɛrɛ a pʊ̃pãanyã.

eng: Just then, Jesus disciples arrived and were shocked that he was talking with a woman. But no one asked, What do you want? or Why are you talking with her.
local: Ka a ɩ a poturbo mɩ tɔ̃ɔ ta nyɛ wʊn 'yɛrɛ nɩ a pɔɔ ka a nɛɛr 'maa ba. Tɩchɛ ba ãsʊɔ jaa ba 'la sʊʊr wʊ bo lɛ wʊ bʊɔrɔ bɩɩ bãa 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ wʊ 'yɛrɛ nɩ a pɔɔ ɛ.

eng: The woman put down her water jar and went into the city. She said to the people.
local: A jie lɛ a pɔɔ bar a yuor tɩ jo kul a tẽe pɔ tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a nɩbɛ.

eng: Come and see a man who has told me everything Ive done! Could this man be the Christ.
local: Yɩ wa nyɛ daba na 'yɛr ma ala a jaa n na dɔ̃ɔ maal. Kaa 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a Nɩ-iraa naa 'lɔ bɩɩ.

eng: They left the city and were on their way to see Jesus.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa na be a tẽe pɔ yin chen na tɩ kaa a Yesu.

eng: In the meantime the disciples spoke to Jesus, saying, Rabbi, eat.
local: An wa ɩrɛ a lɛ ka a poturbo sʊɔr a Yesu. Wiwile yãayãabɩlɛ, di bʊ̃diir.

eng: Jesus said to them, I have food to eat that you dont know about.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, N tara na bʊ̃diir yɩn ba bɔ̃ɔ a yele ɛ.

eng: The disciples asked each other, Has someone brought him food.
local: Ka wʊ poturbo piel sʊrɔ taa, Nɩrɛ kɔ̃ɔ lɛ tɔ̃ɔ waan bon kɔ̃ɔ wa kʊ wʊ ka wʊ di bɩɩ.

eng: Jesus said to them, I am fed by doing the will of the one who sent me and by completing his work.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr, N bʊ̃diir lɛ ka n ɩ a lɛ Nãaŋmɩn na bʊɔrɔ, 'lɔ lɛ tʊ̃ ma, ka n baar a tʊ̃mɔ̃ 'lɔ wʊn kʊ ma ɩka n tʊ̃.

eng: Dont you have a saying, Four more months and then its time for harvest? Look, I tell you: open your eyes and notice that the fields are already ripe for the harvest.
local: Yɩn 'lɔ 'yɛr naa ɩka a chɛn ŋmɛrsɩ anaar ka a ŋmab ta, maa 'yɛr kʊ yɩ ɩka, yɩ kaa yɩn nyɛn a por bonsɩ na mʊ̃ɔ sɛɛ ŋmab.

eng: Those who harvest are receiving their pay and gathering fruit for eternal life so that those who sow and those who harvest can celebrate together.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã gba, a tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ bala na ŋmara nyɛrɛ naa a ba sãyar, ban lɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a bʊ̃ŋmarsɩ waara nɩ nyɛvʊr na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃. Ka a bala na bʊr a bʊ̃bie nɩ a ŋmaŋmarba pãa 'mataa chɩlɛ.

eng: This is a true saying, that one sows and another harvests.
local: A sʊkpa na ɩ yelmãɛ ɩka, bamɩne lɛ bʊr ka bamɩne mɩ ŋma.

eng: I have sent you to harvest what you didnt work hard for; others worked hard, and you will share in their hard work.
local: Maa tʊ̃nɔ̃ yɩ naa ɩka, yɩ chen tɩ ŋma a puo 'lɔ pɔ a bamɩne na tʊ̃ hãn.

eng: Many Samaritans in that city believed in Jesus because of the womans word when she testified, He told me everything Ive ever done.
local: Nɩyɔɔ na yi a Samariya tẽe pɔ sɔɔn de Yesu, bojũu a pɔɔ 'yɛr kʊ ba ɩka, A daba nyã 'yɛr naa a lɛ jaa n na dɔ̃ɔ maal.

eng: So when the Samaritans came to Jesus, they asked him to stay with them, and he stayed there two days.
local: Ba wan wa sʊɔr wʊ ɩka wʊ jãnɛ a ba tẽe, ka wʊ jɛ̃ be ber ayi.

eng: Many more believed because of his word.
local: A Samariya tẽe dẽme yɔɔ jie mɩ lɛ sɔɔn de Yesu bojũu ba wʊn a lɛ wʊn 'yɛr.

eng: and they said to the woman, We no longer believe because of what you said, for we have heard for ourselves and know that this one is truly the savior of the world.
local: Be a ba 'yɛr kʊ a pɔɔ, A ba ɩ lɛ fʊn 'yɛr kʊ sɩ jũu sɩ sɔɔ de a Yesu ɛ, sɩn mãɛ wõ naa a lɛ wʊn 'yɛr kʊ sɩ, ka sɩ bɔ̃ɔ ɩka 'lɔ lɛ na faa a wɛr nɩbɛ.

eng: After two days Jesus left for Galilee. .
local: A ber ayi na po, wʊ yin a be na wʊ chen Galilee.

eng: Jesus himself had testified that prophets have no honor in their own country.
local: Yesu 'yɛr naa, Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ nyɛrɛ na 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ jie jaa, tɩchɛ a ba tẽe dẽme ba 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ba ɛ.

eng: When he came to Galilee, the Galileans welcomed him because they had seen all the things he had done in Jerusalem during the festival, for they also had been at the festival.
local: Wʊn wa ta a Galilee, a nɩbɛ de wʊn nuur ayi a be, bojũu ba mɩ dɔ̃ɔ chen a Jerusalɛm a Gõl Bar Difʊ jie tɩ nyɛ a lɛ jaa wʊn maal.

eng: He returned to Cana in Galilee where he had turned the water into wine. In Capernaum there was a certain royal official whose son was sick.
local: Yesu na wa be a Galilee, wʊ lɩɛb chen a Keena tẽe pɔ be wʊn dɔ̃ɔ lɩɛb a kʊ̃ɔ ka a ɩ a dãa. Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ kɔ̃ɔ ben Kapenahum ka a wʊ bie bɩɛrɛ.

eng: When he heard that Jesus was coming from Judea to Galilee, he went out to meet him and asked Jesus if he would come and heal his son, for his son was about to die.
local: Wʊn wa wõ ɩka a Yesu yin a Galilee wa a Judiya, wʊ chen tɩ sʊɔr wʊ ɩka wʊ sãa a wʊ bie na kpire.

eng: Jesus said to him, Unless you see miraculous signs and wonders, you wont believe.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, Yɩn baa nyɛ jãnɛ nɩ nɛ'maa yele ɛ, yɩ kʊ̃ sɔɔ de ɛ.

eng: The royal official said to him, Lord, come before my son dies.
local: Ka a daba 'yɛr, Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, yãayãabɩlɛ, wa ka a n bie ta kpi ɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Go home. Your son lives. The man believed the word that Jesus spoke to him and set out for his home.
local: A Yesu pãa 'yɛr, A fʊ bie sa naa, kulo a yir. A daba sɔɔn de a lɛ a Yesu na 'yɛr na wʊ lɩɛb kulo a yir.

eng: While he was on his way, his servants were already coming to meet him. They said, Your son lives.
local: Ka a tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ na yi a nɩkpɛ̃ɛ yir tuor wʊ a sɔr pɔ 'yɛr, A fʊ bie sa naa.

eng: So he asked them at what time his son had started to get better. And they said, The fever left him yesterday at about one oclock in the afternoon.
local: A wʊ sʊʊr ba a sɔ̃ɔ buor a bie na sa, ka ba 'yɛr, Jãa mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ na vɩrʊ̃ lɛ a ãgãn tul bar wʊ.

eng: Then the father realized that this was the hour when Jesus had said to him, Your son lives. And he and his entire household believed in Jesus.
local: Ka a bie sãa tɩɛr tɔ an ɩ a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ vɩr naa a jãa, a Yesu na 'yɛr, A fʊ bie sa naa. A daba naa bala ba jaa na be a wʊ yir sɔɔn de a Yesu.

eng: This was the second miraculous sign Jesus did while going from Judea to Galilee.
local: A nɛ'maa yelsɩ ayi lɛ anyã Yesu na tʊ̃ wʊn yi a Judiya na wʊ chen a Galilee.

eng: After this there was a Jewish festival, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
local: A anyã puor, a Juu nɩbɛ tara na lɔ̃ɔ di ka a Yesu chen a be a Jerusalɛm.

eng: In Jerusalem near the Sheep Gate in the north city wall is a pool with the Aramaic name Bethsaida. It had five covered porches.
local: A tẽe pɔ, gbatarbile ben be ka a jɛ̃bɛrsɩ anũu be be. A jie gbʊr a be ban bʊɔlɔ a piir dɔ̃dɔr, ka a Juu nɩbɛ kɔkɔr pɔ a ba bʊɔlɔ Betesda.

eng: and a crowd of people who were sick, blind, lame, and paralyzed sat there.
local: A jɛ̃bɛrsɩ ala pɔ baal bʊrsɩ ben be, jɔ̃n, bamɩne gbɛɛ na kpi kpi nɩ gbɛrsɩ ka ba gã kɔɔ a kʊ̃ɔ.

eng: A certain man was there who had been sick for thirty-eight years.
local: A gbatarbile 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ koor mɩ lɛ nɩbaala na bɛ yuom lɩjɛr nɩ pie nɩ aniin na wʊ gã a be.

eng: When Jesus saw him lying there, knowing that he had already been there a long time, he asked him, Do you want to get well.
local: Yesu na wa nyɛ a daba, wʊ bɔ̃ɔn naa wʊn gbɛr kor, ka wʊ sʊʊr wʊ, Fʊ bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka fʊ sa bɩɩ.

eng: The sick man answered him, Sir, I dont have anyone who can put me in the water when it is stirred up. When Im trying to get to it, someone else has gotten in ahead of me.
local: Ka a daba 'yɛr, Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, n ba tara nɩrɛ na 'mɔɔ ma 'yɔ̃ɔ a kʊ̃ɔ pɔ 'lɔ wa dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ɛ, n ma mʊɔrɔ naa ɩka n kpɛ, tɩchɛ ka a nɩrɛ ma dɔ̃ɔ ma nie kpɛ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Get up! Pick up your mat and walk.
local: A Yesu pãa 'yɛr wʊ, 'Mɔɔ a fʊ bʊ̃gãnaa chiine.

eng: Immediately the man was well, and he picked up his mat and walked. Now that day was the Sabbath.
local: Ajienaa ka a daba sa, na wʊ 'mɔɔ a sɔ̃ɔ chiine. A Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir daar lɛ a anyãna ɩ.

eng: The Jewish leaders said to the man who had been healed, Its the Sabbath; you arent allowed to carry your mat.
local: A Juu nɩbɛrɛ na wa nyɛ a daba na tuo a sɔ̃ɔ, ka ba 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir lɛ, nɩrɛ jaa ba tara sɔr ɩka wʊ tuo a wʊ sɔ̃ɔ a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir ɛ.

eng: He answered, The man who made me well said to me, Pick up your mat and walk.
local: Tɩchɛ ka wʊ 'yɛr, A daba 'lɔ na sa ma lɛ 'yɛr kʊ ma ɩka n 'mɔɔ a sɔ̃ɔ chiine.

eng: They inquired, Who is this man who said to you, Pick it up and walk.
local: Ka ba sʊʊr wʊ, Nɩbuor lɛ 'yɛr fʊ ɩka fʊ 'mɔɔ a sɔ̃ɔ tuo chiine.

eng: The man who had been cured didnt know who it was, because Jesus had slipped away from the crowd gathered there.
local: A daba ba dɔ̃ɔ bɔ̃ɔ a Yesu a nɩbuor wʊn ɩ ɛ, Yesu kpɛ̃n a nɩyɔɔ bala pɔ.

eng: Later Jesus found him in the temple and said, See! You have been made well. Dont sin anymore in case something worse happens to you.
local: A nyã puor, a Yesu nyɛn a daba a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir pɔ na wʊ 'yɛr wʊ, Fʊ pãa sãn pʊ̃pãanyã, taa wa ɩrɛ yeldɛɛr go ɛ, ka amɩne wa pɔɔ fʊ gar a lɛ ɛ.

eng: The man went and proclaimed to the Jewish leaders that Jesus was the man who had made him well.
local: Ka a daba chen tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a Juu nɩbɛrɛ, Yesu lɛ ɩ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na sa ma.

eng: As a result, the Jewish leaders were harassing Jesus, since he had done these things on the Sabbath.
local: Ba piel bʊɔrɔ sɔɩ na ɩ Yesu yele bojũu wʊ ma tʊ̃nɔ̃ naa a anyãna a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar.

eng: Jesus replied, My Father is still working, and I am working too.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr ba, N Sãa ba vɛ̃ anyãna tʊ̃fʊ ɛ, ala lɛ so ka n mɩ tʊ̃nɔ̃.

eng: For this reason the Jewish leaders wanted even more to kill him—not only because he was doing away with the Sabbath but also because he called God his own Father, thereby making himself equal with God.
local: A nɩbɛrɛ tara na yele ayi ban bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba kõn Yesu. Wʊ sɔ̃ɔ naa a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir yele, tɩchɛ a dɛɛr 'lɔ jaa lɛ wʊn 'yɛr ɩka Nãaŋmɩn ɩ naa a wʊ Sãa, na wʊ de a wʊ mãɛ ka 'lɔ nɩ Nãaŋmɩn ɩ bʊ̃'yen.

eng: Jesus responded to the Jewish leaders, I assure you that the Son cant do anything by himself except what he sees the Father doing. Whatever the Father does, the Son does likewise.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a nɩbɛ, Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka a Bie kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ maal bon jaa ɛ. Wʊn tʊ̃ɔ ɩ a lɛ tɛɛ wʊn nyɛ a Sãa na maala, bojũu a Bie ma maal naa a lɛ nɛ wʊn nyɛ a Sãa na maala.

eng: The Father loves the Son and shows him everything that he does. He will show him greater works than these so that you will marvel.
local: A Sãa nʊ̃n a Bie na wʊ wiil wʊ a yele a jaa wʊn maal. A Sãa gba na wiil wʊn yelbɛrɛ ka yɩ nɛɛr 'maa.

eng: As the Father raises the dead and gives life, so too does the Son give life to whomever he wishes.
local: A lɛ a Sãa na ma sãɩ a kũu na wʊ kʊ wʊ nyɛvʊr, lɛ lɛ a Bie mɩ na kʊ a ãsʊɔ jaa wʊn bʊɔrɔ a nyɛvʊr.

eng: The Father doesnt judge anyone, but he has given all judgment to the Son.
local: A Sãa gba ba tɔɔrɔ nɩrɛ jaa 'yɛr ɛ, tɩchɛ wʊ ɩka a Bie wʊ tɔɔr nɩrɛ jaa 'yɛr.

eng: so that everyone will honor the Son just as they honor the Father. Whoever doesnt honor the Son doesnt honor the Father who sent him.
local: A Sãa bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka ba 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a Bie nɩtãa lɛ ban 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ wʊ. Ɩ̃sʊɔ jaa wa tɔr ba 'yɔ̃ɔ a Bie ɛ, lɛ mɩ lɛ wʊ tɔr, ba 'yɔ̃ɔ a Sãa na tʊ̃ wʊ ɛ.

eng: I assure you that whoever hears my word and believes in the one who sent me has eternal life and wont come under judgment but has passed from death into life.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, ãsʊɔ jaa na wõ a lɛ ɩn na 'yɛr tɩ sɔɔ de a 'lɔ na tʊ̃ ma, tara na nyɛvʊr 'lɔ na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ, Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃. Ba kʊ̃ 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ 'yɛr ɛ. Wʊ yin kũu pɔ, tɩ kpɛ nyɛvʊr pɔ.

eng: I assure you that the time is coming—and is here!—when the dead will hear the voice of Gods Son, and those who hear it will live.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, sɔ̃ɔ waara, wʊ ben ta ka a kũn na wõ a Nãaŋmɩn Bie kɔkɔr. A bala na wõ naa, na ir tara nyɛvʊr.

eng: Just as the Father has life in himself, so he has granted the Son to have life in himself.
local: Bojũu a Sãa tara na kpɛ̃ɔ kʊrɔ nyɛvʊr. Wʊ den a kpɛ̃ɔ 'lɔ kʊ a Bie ka wʊ mɩ tara nyɛvʊr.

eng: He gives the Son authority to judge, because he is the Human One.
local: Wʊ kʊ wʊn sɔr ɩka wʊ tɔɔr nɩrɛ jaa 'yɛr, bojũu wʊ ɩ naa Nɩsaal Bie.

eng: Dont be surprised by this, because the time is coming when all who are in their graves will hear his voice.
local: Yɩ ta vɛ̃ ka a 'maa yɩ nɛɛ ɛ. A sɔ̃ɔ na wa naa, ka a kũn wõ a Nɩsaal Bie kɔkɔr.

eng: Those who did good things will come out into the resurrection of life, and those who did wicked things into the resurrection of judgment.
local: na ba yire a ba yar pɔ. Ɩ̃sʊɔ jaa na maal vɩla, na yin kpɛ nyɛvʊr pɔ, ka a ãsʊɔ jaa mɩ na maal dɛɛr na yin kpɛ sɔ̃ɔfʊ pɔ.

eng: I cant do anything by myself. Whatever I hear, I judge, and my judgment is just. I dont seek my own will but the will of the one who sent me.
local: N kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ maal bon jaa nɩ maa mãɛ kpɛ̃ɔ ɛ. 'Lɔ lɛ 'yɛr ma a lɛ ɩn na tɔɔrɔ ka a n tɔɔrfʊ tara sɔr bojũu n turo naa a wʊ nɛɛ, a ba ɩ n ɩrɛ na lɛ ɩn bʊɔrɔ ɛ.

eng: If I testify about myself, my testimony isnt true.
local: Maa 'yɛrɛ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a n mãɛ, sɔr jaa ba be be na wiil ɩka yelmãɛ lɛ ɛ.

eng: There is someone else who testifies about me, and I know his testimony about me is true.
local: Nɩrɛ kɔ̃ɔ ben be na 'yɛrɛ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ma, ka n mɩ bɔ̃ɔ ɩka a lɛ wʊn 'yɛrɛ ɩn yelmãɛ.

eng: You sent a delegation to John, and he testified to the truth.
local: Yɩ tʊ̃n nɩbɛ a Jɔɔn sɛ̃ ka wʊ tɩ 'yɛr ba a yelmãɛ.

eng: Although I dont accept human testimony, I say these things so that you can be saved.
local: N ba dɩɛl lɛ nɩbɛ na 'yɛrɛ a n 'yɛr ɛ, tɩchɛ n 'yɛrɛ yɩ naa anyãna ka yɩ na tʊ̃ɔ nyɛ faafʊ.

eng: John was a burning and shining lamp, and, at least for a while, you were willing to celebrate in his light.
local: Jɔɔn dɔ̃ɔ ɩ fɩka fãtɩn na chaar yɔ̃ɔ ka yɩ pʊɔ pɛl, a yɩ chɩl a chãa nyã pɔ bãlãa.

eng: I have a witness greater than Johns testimony. The Father has given me works to do so that I might complete them. These works I do testify about me that the Father sent me.
local: Bon kɔ̃ɔ na tɩrɛ gar a lɛ a Jɔɔn na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr a n 'yɛr, naa tʊ̃mɔ̃ ala a Sãa na kʊ ma ɩka n tʊ̃. A anyãna a jaa 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ ma, naa wiil ɩka a Sãa lɛ tʊ̃ ma.

eng: And the Father who sent me testifies about me. You have never even heard his voice or seen his form.
local: A Sãa mɩ na tʊ̃ ma 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ ma, tɩchɛ yaa wõ a wʊ kɔkɔr bɩɩ nyɛ a wʊ nie jaa ɛ.

eng: and you dont have his word dwelling with you because you dont believe the one whom he has sent.
local: Yaa sɔɔ a wʊ 'yɛr ɛ, bojũu yɩ tɔr na yaa sɔɔ de a 'lɔ wʊn tʊ̃ ɛ.

eng: Examine the scriptures, since you think that in them you have eternal life. They also testify about me.
local: Yɩ ma chɛ̃pɛ̃ naa a Nãaŋmɩn gãma, bojũu yɩ tɩɛr naa ɩka be lɛ yɩn nyɛ nyɛvʊr na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ. Maa 'yɛr lɛ a gãma anyãna 'yɛrɛ.

eng: yet you dont want to come to me so that you can have life.
local: Tɩchɛ yɩ tɔr naa na wa a n sɛ̃ wa nyɛ nyɛvʊr 'lɔ na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃.

eng: I dont accept praise from people.
local: N baka ba be nɩrɛ pɛɛfʊ pɔ ɛ.

eng: but I know you, that you dont have Gods love in you.
local: Tɩchɛ n bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka a yɩ ãsʊɔ jaa ba nʊ̃ Nãaŋmɩn ɛ.

eng: I have come in my Fathers name, and you dont receive me. If others come in their own name, you receive them.
local: Maa waan naa a n Sãa yuor, ka yaa de ma ɛ. Tɩchɛ yɩn den nɩbɛ na waan ba mãɛ yuor.

eng: How can you believe when you receive praise from each other but dont seek the praise that comes from the only God?.
local: Ŋmɩnɛ yɩn ɩrɛ wa sɔɔ? Yɩn na bʊɔrɔ ɩka a yɩ barmɩne ba pɛɛrɛ yɩ, ka yɩ baka ba be a pɛɛfʊ 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn tɛɛ na kʊ yɩ ɛ.

eng: Dont think that I will accuse you before the Father. Your accuser is Moses, the one in whom your hope rests.
local: Tɩchɛ, yɩ taa tɩɛr ɩka ɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ yɩn 'yɛr a Sãa niem ɛ. Yɩ den a yɩ tɩɛr dɔɔl Mosesɩ, 'lɔ nɩ wʊ ju lɛ na 'yɔ̃ɔ yɩ 'yɛr.

eng: If you believed Moses, you would believe me, because Moses wrote about me.
local: Maa 'yɛr lɛ a Mosesɩ na dɔ̃ɔ sɛb, ala ɩ yɩn ta sɔɔ wʊ, yɩn taa sɔɔ ma naa.

eng: If you dont believe the writings of Moses, how will you believe my words.
local: Tɩchɛ yɩn ba sɔɔ a lɛ a Mosesɩ na dɔ̃ɔ sɛb ɛ, ŋmɩnɛ yɩn ɩrɛ wa sɔɔ a lɛ ɩn 'yɛr.

eng: There was a Pharisee named Nicodemus, a Jewish leader.
local: Daba dɔ̃ɔ be be a Faraseemɩne pɔ, ka a wʊ yuor di Nikodimasɩ, wʊ mɩ ɩ naa a Juu nɩbɛ nɩdiere ãsʊɔ.

eng: He came to Jesus at night and said to him, Rabbi, we know that you are a teacher who has come from God, for no one could do these miraculous signs that you do unless God is with him.
local: Wʊ dɔ̃ɔ chen a Yesu sɛ̃ tãsɔɔ na wʊ tɩ 'yɛr, Wiwile, sɩ bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka Nãaŋmɩn lɛ tʊ̃ fʊ ɩka, fʊ wa wiil sɩ. Fʊ kʊ̃ naa tʊ̃ɔ a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ anyãna tʊ̃ ɛ, ka Nãaŋmɩn ba be a fʊ sɛ̃ ɛ.

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you, unless someone is born anew, its not possible to see Gods kingdom.
local: Ka a Yesu haa 'yɛr, Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ fʊ, fʊ̃ʊ ba nyɛ dɔɔ paalaa ɛ, fʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ kpɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ ɛ.

eng: Nicodemus asked, How is it possible for an adult to be born? Its impossible to enter the mothers womb for a second time and be born, isnt it.
local: A Nikodimasɩ sʊʊr wʊ, Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ ban lɛ ɩ dɔɔ nɩkoraa gbɛɛ ayi? Wʊ kʊ̃ lɛ tʊ̃ɔ kpɛ a wʊ ma pʊɔ pɔ ka wʊ lɛ bɔ̃ɔ dɔɔ wʊ go ɛ.

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you, unless someone is born of water and the Spirit, its not possible to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ fʊ, fʊ̃ʊ na kpɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ, ka baa dɔɔ wʊ nɩ a kʊ̃ɔ nɩ a Sɩɛ ɛ.

eng: Whatever is born of the flesh is flesh, and whatever is born of the Spirit is spirit.
local: A 'lɔ a nɩrɛ na dɔɔ ɩ naa nɩrɛ bie, Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ mɩ lɛ na tʊ̃ɔ nɩrɛ lɩɛb ka wʊ ɩ Nãaŋmɩn bie.

eng: Dont be surprised that I said to you, You must be born anew.
local: Ta ɩ a nɛɛ 'maa fʊ ɩn 'yɛr ɩka a sɛɛ naa ka ba dɔɔfʊ dɔɔ paalaa ɛ.

eng: Gods Spirit blows wherever it wishes. You hear its sound, but you dont know where it comes from or where it is going. Its the same with everyone who is born of the Spirit.
local: Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ tɛɛ lɛ tɩrɛ a nyɛvʊr paalaa. A Sɩɛ kaar naa sɛsɛbɛ na furo chiine jie 'lɔ wʊn bʊɔrɔ, fʊn tʊ̃ɔn wõ a dɔɔn, tɩchɛ fʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ bɔ̃ɔ a jie wʊn yi ɛ, bɩɩ a jie wʊn chiine ɛ.

eng: Nicodemus said, How are these things possible.
local: Ka a Nikodimasɩ sʊʊr wʊ, Ŋmɩnɛ an ɩ wa ɩ a lɛ.

eng: Jesus answered, You are a teacher of Israel and you dont know these things?.
local: Ka a Yesu haa 'yɛr, Ŋmɩnɛ fʊ ɩ a Isɩral nɩbɛ wiwile tɩ ba bɔ̃ɔ a anyãna ɛ?.

eng: I assure you that we speak about what we know and testify about what we have seen, but you dont receive our testimony.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ fʊ, sɩ 'yɛrɛ naa ala sɩn bɔ̃ɔ, na sɩ di a ala sɩn 'yɛrɛ dãasɩɛ, tɩchɛ yɩ ãsʊɔ jaa ba de a lɛ sɩn 'yɛr ɛ.

eng: If I have told you about earthly things and you dont believe, how will you believe if I tell you about heavenly things?.
local: Ala ɩ yaa sɔɔ de a wɛr nyã bonsɩ 'yɛr ɛ, ŋmɩnɛ yɩn ɩrɛ wa sɔɔ de ala ɩ, n 'yɛr naa saaju bonsɩ 'yɛr?.

eng: No one has gone up to heaven except the one who came down from heaven, the Human One.
local: Nɩrɛ ba do saaju jaa ɛ, ka a Nɩsaal Bie na yi a be wa tɛɛ ba lɛ ɛ.

eng: Just as Moses lifted up the snake in the wilderness, so must the Human One be lifted up.
local: A Nɩsaal Bie na 'mɔɔn saaju nɩtãa lɛ a Mosesɩ na maal a kur jɩɛ 'mɔɔ a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ.

eng: so that everyone who believes in him will have eternal life.
local: Ka nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na pãa sɔɔ de a Nɩsaal Bie na tara na nyɛvʊr 'lɔ na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ, Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃.

eng: God so loved the world that he gave his only Son, so that everyone who believes in him wont perish but will have eternal life.
local: Nãaŋmɩn nʊ̃ naa a nɩbɛ na be ka a wɛr pɔ yɔɔ jaa, na wʊ de a wʊ Bidabʊ̃'yen tɛɛ kʊ sɩ, nɩrɛ jaa wa sɔɔ de wʊ, wʊ kʊ̃ wɛ ɛ, tɩ tara a nyɛvʊr 'lɔ na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ, Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃.

eng: God didnt send his Son into the world to judge the world, but that the world might be saved through him.
local: Bojũu, Nãaŋmɩn ba tʊ̃ a wʊ Bidabʊ̃'yen nyã a wɛr pɔ ɩka wʊ wa sɔ̃ɔ a nɩbɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ wʊ wa naa ɩka wʊ wa faa ba.

eng: Whoever believes in him isnt judged; whoever doesnt believe in him is already judged, because they dont believe in the name of Gods only Son.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na wa sɔɔ de a Nãaŋmɩn Bidabʊ̃'yen nyã, wʊ kʊ̃ sɔ̃ɔ wʊ ɛ, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na ba wa sɔɔ de wʊ ɛ, wʊn sɔ̃ɔ wʊn bar, bojũu waa sɔɔ de a Nãaŋmɩn Bidabʊ̃'yen tɛɛ nyã yuor ɛ.

eng: This is the basis for judgment: The light came into the world, and people loved darkness more than the light, for their actions are evil.
local: A baaraa nɩ nyã, chãa wan ka a wɛr pɔ, tɩchɛ a nɩbɛ nʊ̃n libe gar a chãa, bojũu a ba maalfʊ ɩn bier.

eng: All who do wicked things hate the light and dont come to the light for fear that their actions will be exposed to the light.
local: A nɩbɛ bala na ɩrɛ a bier 'laar chãa, ba kʊ̃ sɔɔ yi chãa pɔ ɛ, bojũu a chãa na wiil naa a lɛ ban be.

eng: Whoever does the truth comes to the light so that it can be seen that their actions were done in God.
local: Tɩchɛ ãsʊɔ jaa na be be nɩ yelmãɛ ma wan chãa pɔ, bojũu ba bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka a ba taaba ba bɔ̃ɔ ɩka Nãaŋmɩn lɛ maala a lɛ ban maala.

eng: After this Jesus and his disciples went into Judea, where he spent some time with them and was baptizing.
local: A nyã puor, Yesu nɩ a wʊ poturbo 'mantaa chen Judiya be lɛ wʊ jɛ̃n ba bãlãa, na wʊ suoro nɩbɛ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ.

eng: John was baptizing at Aenon near Salem because there was a lot of water there, and people were coming to him and being baptized. .
local: Jɔɔn mɩ dɔ̃ɔ suoro a nɩbɛ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ be a Ainon, a be ba jãa naa Salim ɛ, bojũu kʊ̃ɔ dɔ̃ɔ be be yɔɔ. Nɩbɛ dɔ̃ɔ chiine a wʊ sɛ̃ ka wʊ suoro ba a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ.

eng: John hadnt yet been thrown into prison.
local: Baa na nyɔɔ a Jɔɔn pɔɔ sɛrɛ ɛ.

eng: A debate started between Johns disciples and a certain Jew about cleansing rituals.
local: Jɔɔn poturbo bamɩne nɩ a Juu nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ chɩrɛ nɩ taa nɩ a ãgãn pɛɛfʊ yele.

eng: They came to John and said, Rabbi, look! The man who was with you across the Jordan, the one about whom you testified, is baptizing and everyone is flocking to him.
local: Ba chen a Jɔɔn sɛ̃ tɩ 'yɛr, Wiwile, fʊ 'yɛr a daba 'lɔ sɛ̃ fʊn dɔ̃ɔ be a Jordan man gɔ̃ɔn. Wʊ suoro na nɩbɛ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ ka nɩrɛ jaa chiine a wʊ sɛ̃.

eng: John replied, No one can receive anything unless it is given from heaven.
local: Ka Jɔɔn haa 'yɛr, Nɩrɛ jaa kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ ɩ bon jaa ɛ, ka Nãaŋmɩn na be a saaju baa sɔɔ ɛ.

eng: You yourselves can testify that I said that Im not the Christ but that Im the one sent before him.
local: Yɩn mãɛ chɛnɛ na tɩɛr a ŋmɩnɛ ɩn dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr yɩ ɩka n ba ɩ a Nɩ-iraa ɛ. Tɩchɛ wʊ tʊ̃ ma naa ka n de a wʊ nie.

eng: The groom is the one who is getting married. The friend of the groom stands close by and, when he hears him, is overjoyed at the grooms voice. Therefore, my joy is now complete.
local: A 'lɔ na kulo a pɔɔ, 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a pɔɔ sʊɔ. A wʊ bɩbɩɛlɛ pʊɔ ma pɛl naa wʊn ara a be wõnõ a pɔɔ sʊɔ kɔkɔr. Ala lɛ so ka a n pʊɔ mɩ pɛl.

eng: He must increase and I must decrease.
local: A sɛɛ naa ɩka Yesu wʊ do, tɩchɛ ka maa suu.

eng: The one who comes from above is above all things. The one who is from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks as one from the earth. The one who comes from heaven is above all things.
local: Nãaŋmɩn Bie nyã lɛ yi a saaju, 'lɔ lɛ gar ba jaa, nɩrɛ na yi ka a tẽe ɩn a tẽe tʊɔr na wʊ ma 'yɛrɛ a tẽe bomo 'yɛr. A 'lɔ na yi a saaju wa gar a bomo a jaa.

eng: He testifies to what he has seen and heard, but no one accepts his testimony.
local: Wʊ ma 'yɛr naa a lɛ wʊn nyɛ nɩ a lɛ wʊn wõ tɩ ãsʊɔ jaa maa sɔɔ de wʊ ɛ.

eng: Whoever accepts his testimony confirms that God is true.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na wa sɔɔ de, a wiil naa ɩka Nãaŋmɩn ɩ naa yelmãɛ sʊɔ.

eng: The one whom God sent speaks Gods words because God gives the Spirit generously.
local: Bojũu, Nãaŋmɩn tʊ̃ naa a wʊ Bie ka wʊ wa 'yɛr a wʊ 'yɛr. Nãaŋmɩn vɛ̃ naa ka a wʊ Sɩɛ sɛɛ wʊ.

eng: The Father loves the Son and gives everything into his hands.
local: A Sãa nʊ̃ naa Bie na wʊ de yele a jaa 'yɔ̃ɔ a wʊ nũu pɔ.

eng: Whoever believes in the Son has eternal life. Whoever doesnt believe in the Son wont see life, but the angry judgment of God remains on them.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa na sɔɔ de a Bie tara na nyɛvʊr 'lɔ na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ, Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ na tɔr wʊ, ba tara a nyɛvʊr nyã ɛ, tɩ Nãaŋmɩn suur be a wʊ sɛ̃.

eng: And Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Yesu mɩ do a Olive Tɩɩr Tɔ̃ɔ ju.

eng: Early in the morning he returned to the temple. All the people gathered around him, and he sat down and taught them.
local: Wɛr na wa chaar bibio pipi, Yesu lɩɛb wa kpɛ a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir bɛchaar pɔ, be lɛ a nɩbɛ ŋmaa jilʊ̃ wʊ tɩ wʊ jãnɛ wile ba.

eng: The legal experts and Pharisees brought a woman caught in adultery. Placing her in the center of the group.
local: Be lɛ a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe, nɩ a Faraseemɩne wa 'mataa waan pɔɔ ban nyɔɔ na gãn daba. Ba vɛ̃n ka a pɔɔ ir ara a ba jaa niem.

eng: they said to Jesus, Teacher, this woman was caught in the act of committing adultery.
local: tɩ ba 'yɛr kʊ a Yesu, Wiwile, a pɔɔ nyãna lɛ gãn daba ka sɩ nyɔɔ.

eng: In the Law, Moses commanded us to stone women like this. What do you say.
local: A Mosesɩ Nɛɛ 'lɔ 'yɛr ɩka sɩ lɔb wʊ nɩ kʊsɩɛ kʊ.

eng: They said this to test him, because they wanted a reason to bring an accusation against him. Jesus bent down and wrote on the ground with his finger.
local: Ba 'yɛr naa a lɛ bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba bɛɛr Yesu kaa na ba tʊ̃ɔ nyɛ 'yɛr, 'yɛr a wʊ yele. Tɩchɛ ka a Yesu guun de a nubir sɛɛrɛ nɩ a tẽe.

eng: They continued to question him, so he stood up and replied, Whoever hasnt sinned should throw the first stone.
local: Tɩ ba chɛnɛ sʊrɔ wʊ, ka wʊ dɔl 'yɛr kʊ ba, Ala ɩ a yɩ ãsʊɔ jaa ben ka ba maal dɛɛr ɛ, a nɩnyɩnɛ wʊ de nie lɔb a pɔɔ nɩ a kʊsɩr.

eng: Bending down again, he wrote on the ground.
local: Tɩ na wʊ lɛ guun sɛɛrɛ a tẽe go.

eng: Those who heard him went away, one by one, beginning with the elders. Finally, only Jesus and the woman were left in the middle of the crowd.
local: A anyãna ban wõ, ba piel yire bʊ̃'yen 'yen, a danyɔ̃n lɛ de nie. A wa tɩ chɛ naa a Yesu nɩ a pɔɔ tɛɛ na ara a be.

eng: Jesus stood up and said to her, Woman, where are they? Is there no one to condemn you.
local: Yesu dɔl ir sʊʊr a pɔɔ, Nyɛ ba be? Ɩ̃sʊɔ jaa ba 'yɔ̃ɔ fʊ yele ɛ.

eng: She said, No one, sir. Jesus said, Neither do I condemn you. Go, and from now on, dont sin anymore.
local: Ka a pɔɔ 'yɛr, Kai nɩkpɛ̃ɛ. Ka Yesu mɩ pãa 'yɛr wʊ, Maa mɩ kʊ̃ 'yɔ̃ɔ fʊ yele ɛ, chiine, taa lɛ wa maala yeldɛɛr go ɛ.

eng: Jesus spoke to the people again, saying, I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me wont walk in darkness but will have the light of life.
local: Yesu lɛ bɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr kʊ a nɩbɛ na wʊ 'yɛr, Maa lɛ a wɛr nyã chãa, nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na turo ma kʊ̃ lɛ chen libe pɔ ɛ, tɩ wʊn tara na nyɛvʊr na ɩ chãa.

eng: Then the Pharisees said to him, Because you are testifying about yourself, your testimony isnt valid.
local: Ka a Faraseemɩne chɩrɛ wʊ 'yɛrɛ, Fʊ bʊɔrɔ na yuor 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ fʊ mãɛ, ka a fʊ yobɔ ɩ pɔrɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, Even if I testify about myself, my testimony is true, since I know where I came from and where Im going. You dont know where I come from or where Im going.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Ala ɩ n bʊɔrɔ naa yuor 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a n mãɛ, a n yobɔ tara na sɔr. Bojũu n bɔ̃ɔ naa jie ɩn yi wa, naa lɛ bɔ̃ɔ a jie ɩn chiine. Tɩchɛ yɩn, yaa bɔ̃ɔ a jie ɩn yi wa ɛ, yaa lɛ bɔ̃ɔ a jie ɩn chiine ɛ.

eng: You judge according to human standards, but I judge no one.
local: Yɩ den nɩsaal tɩɛrsɩ tɔɔrɔ nɩ 'yɛr, maa ba tɔɔrɔ nɩrɛ jaa 'yɛr ɛ.

eng: Even if I do judge, my judgment is truthful, because Im not alone. My judgments come from me and from the Father who sent me.
local: Tɩchɛ ala ɩ ɩn tɔɔr naa 'yɛr, a lɛ ɩn na tɔɔr na tor naa bojũu n ba ɩ n yõ ɛ, a Sãa na tʊ̃ ma pʊɔ ma naa.

eng: In your Law it is written that the witness of two people is true.
local: A sɛb a yɩ nɛɛr pɔ ɩka, nɩbɛ ayi 'yɛr ɩn yelmãɛ.

eng: I am one witness concerning myself, and the Father who sent me is the other.
local: N 'yɛrɛ na 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a n mãɛ, ka a Sãa mɩ na tʊ̃ ma 'yɛrɛ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ma.

eng: They asked him, Where is your Father? Jesus answered, You dont know me and you dont know my Father. If you knew me, you would also know my Father.
local: Ka ba sʊʊr wʊ, Nyɩnɛ a fʊ Sãa be? Ka Yesu 'yɛr Yaa bɔ̃ɔ ma ɛ, bɩɩ a n Sãa ɛ, ala ɩ yɩ ta bɔ̃ɔ ma naa, yɩn taa bɔ̃ɔ naa n Sãa mɩ.

eng: He spoke these words while he was teaching in the temple area known as the treasury. No one arrested him, because his time hadnt yet come.
local: Yesu 'yɛr naa a 'yɛr nyã wʊn wile ba a puorfʊ yir bɛchaar pɔ gbʊr a libie lɔb fʊ jie. Tɩ nɩrɛ jaa ba nyɔɔ wʊ ɛ, an ɩ a wʊ bɩbir ba vɩɛ ɛ.

eng: Jesus continued, Im going away. You will look for me, and you will die in your sin. Where Im going, you cant come.
local: Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba go, N chiine na, yɩn wa yʊɔrɔ na bʊɔrɔ ma tɩ na yɩ kpin a yɩ yelbier. Jie ɩn chiine, yɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ wa be ɛ.

eng: The Jewish leaders said, He isnt going to kill himself, is he? Is that why he said, Where Im going, you cant come.
local: Ka a Juu nɩbɛ pãa sʊrɔ taa, Wʊn kʊn wʊ mãɛ bɩɩ? Ala lɛ so wʊn 'yɛr ɩka, A jie ɩn chiine yɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ wa be ɛ. .

eng: He said to them, You are from below; Im from above. You are from this world; Im not from this world.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Yesu chɛnɛ 'yɛrɛ, Yɩn yin ka tẽe, maa yin saaju. A wɛr nyã pɔ lɛ yɩ yi, maa mɩ ba yi a wɛr nyã pɔ ɛ.

eng: This is why I told you that you would die in your sins. If you dont believe that I Am, you will die in your sins.
local: N 'yɛr kʊ yɩ ɩka yɩn kpin naa yɩ yelbier, ala ɩ yaa sɔɔ de ma nɩ a lɛ ɩn 'yɛr ɩka n ɩ naa ɛ, sɩrɛ jaa lɛ, yɩn kpin naa yɩ yelbier.

eng: Who are you? they asked. Jesus replied, Im exactly who I have claimed to be from the beginning.
local: Ka a nɩbɛ sʊʊr wʊ, An fʊ ɩ? A Yesu 'yɛr ba, N ɩ naa a 'lɔ ɩn 'yɛr kʊ yɩ a kõr jaa.

eng: I have many things to say in judgment concerning you. The one who sent me is true, and what I have heard from him I tell the world.
local: N tara na yɔɔ ɩn taa 'yɛr tɔɔr a yɩ 'yɛr, tɩchɛ a 'lɔ na tʊ̃ ma ɩn yelmãɛ sʊɔ, ala ɩn wõ yi a wʊ sɛ̃ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ a wɛr nɩbɛ.

eng: They didnt know he was speaking about his Father.
local: Baa bɔ̃ɔ a par ɩka a wʊ Sãa 'yɛr lɛ wʊ dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛrɛ ɛ.

eng: So Jesus said to them, When the Human One is lifted up, then you will know that I Am. Then you will know that I do nothing on my own, but I say just what the Father has taught me.
local: Be a Yesu 'yɛr, Yɩn wa 'mɔɔ a Nɩsaal Bie a saaju yɩn bɔ̃ɔ naa a nɩrɛ 'lɔ ɩn ɩ, na yɩ mɩ bɔ̃ɔ ɩka n ba ɩrɛ yele nɩ maa kpɛ̃ɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ n 'yɛrɛ naa a lɛ a Sãa na wiil ma.

eng: He who sent me is with me. He doesnt leave me by myself, because I always do what makes him happy.
local: A 'lɔ na tʊ̃ ma be naa n sɛ̃, waa bar ma n yõ ɛ, bojũu n ma ɩrɛ naa lɛ na pɛlɛ a wʊ pʊɔ.

eng: While Jesus was saying these things, many people came to believe in him.
local: Wʊn 'yɛrɛ a lɛ tɩ nɩyɔɔ sɔɔn de wʊ.

eng: Jesus said to the Jews who believed in him, You are truly my disciples if you remain faithful to my teaching.
local: Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a Juu nɩbɛ bala na sɔɔ de wʊ, Ala ɩ yɩ sɔɔn turo a lɛ ɩn wiil yɩ, yɩn ɩ naa a n poturbo yelmãɛ.

eng: Then you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.
local: Yɩn bɔ̃ɔn a yelmãɛ, ka a yelmãɛ for yɩ bar ka yɩ so yɩ mãɛ.

eng: They responded, We are Abrahams children; weve never been anyones slaves. How can you say that we will be set free.
local: Ka ba 'yɛr, Sɩn ɩ naa Abraham yɔn, na saa ɩ nɩrɛ jaa gbãgbaar ɛ, ŋmɩnɛ lɛ fʊ 'yɛr ɩka ban for sɩn bar ka sɩ so sɩ mãɛ.

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you that everyone who sins is a slave to sin.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, nɩrɛ jaa na maal dɛɛr ɩn a dɛɛr gbãgbaa.

eng: A slave isnt a permanent member of the household, but a son is.
local: Gbãgbaa maa be a yir kor ɛ, a bie 'lɔ ma ben be kor.

eng: Therefore, if the Son makes you free, you really will be free.
local: Ala wa ɩ a Bie for fʊn bar, fʊn son fʊ mɩ sɩrɛ jaa.

eng: I know that you are Abrahams children, yet you want to kill me because you dont welcome my teaching.
local: N bɔ̃ɔn na yɩn ɩ Abraham yɔn tɩ bʊɔrɔ ɩka yɩ kʊ ma, bojũu yaa sɔɔ a n 'yɛr ɛ.

eng: Im telling you what Ive seen when I am with the Father, but you are doing what youve heard from your father.
local: Maa 'yɛrɛ yɩ naa a lɛ ɩn nyɛ a n Sãa sɛ̃, ka yɩn mɩ ɩrɛ a lɛ yɩn wõ a yɩ sãa sɛ̃.

eng: They replied, Our father is Abraham. Jesus responded, If you were Abrahams children, you would do Abrahams works.
local: Ka ba 'yɛr, Abraham lɛ ɩ a sɩ sãa. Be a Yesu 'yɛr, Ala ɩ Abraham bibiir lɛ yɩ ta ɩ, yɩn taa maal a lɛ a Abraham na dɔ̃ɔ maal.

eng: Instead, you want to kill me, though I am the one who has spoken the truth I heard from God. Abraham didnt do this.
local: Yɩ ben bʊɔrɔ ɩka yɩ kʊ ma a nɩrɛ na 'yɛr yɩ a yelmãɛ ɩn wõ a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃. Abraham ba dɔ̃ɔ maal lɛ ɛ.

eng: You are doing your fathers works. They said, Our ancestry isnt in question! The only Father we have is God.
local: Yɩ ɩrɛ naa a lɛ a yɩ sãa mãɛ na dɔ̃ɔ ɩ. Ka ba 'yɛr, Saa ɩ sɛ̃sɛ̃ bibiir ɛ, sɩn Sãa tɛɛ sɩn tara lɛ Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: Jesus replied, If God were your Father, you would love me, for I came from God. Here I am. I havent come on my own. God sent me.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Ala ɩ Nãaŋmɩn lɛ ta ɩ a yɩ Sãa, yɩn taa nʊ̃ ma naa. Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ lɛ n yi naa be be ka a pʊ̃pãanyã. N ba waan a n mãɛ yuor ɛ, 'lɔ lɛ tʊ̃ ma.

eng: Why dont you understand what Im saying? Its because you cant really hear my words.
local: Bo lɛ so ka a lɛ ɩn 'yɛr ba chaar kʊ yɩ ɛ? Bojũu yaa tʊ̃ɔ wõ a lɛ ɩn 'yɛr ɛ.

eng: Your father is the devil. You are his children, and you want to do what your father wants. He was a murderer from the beginning. He has never stood for the truth, because theres no truth in him. Whenever that liar speaks, he speaks according to his own nature, because hes a liar and the father of liars.
local: A yɩ sãa ɩn tɩɩ, a yɩ bʊɔrɔ ɩka yɩ maal a lɛ wʊn bʊɔrɔ. An yi a pielfʊ jaa nɩkʊrɔ lɛ wʊ ɩ, waa tara yelmãɛ ɛ, yelmãɛ bãlãa jaa ba be a wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ ɛ. 'Lɔ wa ŋmaara a jire, lɛ wʊ be, jir ŋmara lɛ wʊ ɩ, na wʊ ɩ a jie a jaa sãa.

eng: Because I speak the truth, you dont believe me.
local: Tɩchɛ maa na 'yɛrɛ a yelmãɛ yaa sɔɔ de ma ɛ.

eng: Who among you can show Im guilty of sin? Since I speak the truth, why dont you believe me?.
local: A yɩ ãsʊɔ na 'yɛr ɩka wʊ nyɛn ɩn maal dɛɛr ɔ? Ala ɩ yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ, bo lɛ so ka yaa sɔɔ de ma ɛ?.

eng: Gods children listen to Gods words. You dont listen to me because you arent Gods children.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ɩ Nãaŋmɩn tʊɔr wõnõ naa a lɛ wʊn 'yɛrɛ. Yɩn ba ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn tʊɔr ɛ, yaa wõnõ a lɛ wʊn 'yɛrɛ ɛ.

eng: The Jewish opposition answered, We were right to say that you are a Samaritan and have a demon, werent we.
local: Ka a Juu nɩbɛ 'yɛr, Yelmãɛ lɛ sɩ 'yɛrɛ ɩka Samariya lɛ fʊ yi na fʊ tara a sɩdɛɛr.

eng: I dont have a demon, Jesus replied. But I honor my Father and you dishonor me.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr, Maa ba sɩdɛɛr ɛ, tɩchɛ n 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ naa a n Sãa, tɩ yɩn ba 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ma ɛ.

eng: Im not trying to bring glory to myself. Theres one who is seeking to glorify me, and hes the judge.
local: N ba bʊɔrɔ tɩɩr kʊrɔ a n mãɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ kɔ̃ɔ bʊɔrɔ naa a lɛ. 'Lɔ Lɛ ɩ a 'yɛr tɔɔrɔ.

eng: I assure you that whoever keeps my word will never die.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, ala ɩ nɩrɛ turo naa maa 'yɛr, wʊ kʊ̃ lɛ bɔ̃ɔ kũu ɛ.

eng: The Jewish opposition said to Jesus, Now we know that you have a demon. Abraham and the prophets died, yet you say, Whoever keeps my word will never die.
local: A anyãna lɛ ka a Juu nɩbɛ pãa 'yɛrɛ, Pʊ̃pãanyã, sɩ bɔ̃ɔn naa fʊn tara a sɩdɛɛr. Abraham kpi naa, ka a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ kpi, tɩchɛ ka fʊ 'yɛr ɩka ala ɩ nɩrɛ turo naa a fʊ 'yɛr wʊ kʊ̃ bɔ̃ɔ kũu ɛ.

eng: Are you greater than our father Abraham? He died and the prophets died, so who do you make yourself out to be.
local: Fʊ̃ʊ bɛrma gar a sɩ sãa Abraham bɩɩ? Wʊ kpi naa, ka a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ mɩ kpi. An fʊ tɩɛr ɩka fʊ pãa ɩ naa.

eng: Jesus answered, If I glorify myself, my glory is meaningless. My Father, who you say is your God, is the one who glorifies me.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Ala ɩ n ben 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a n mãɛ tɩɩr, a n tɩɩr na ɩn pɔrɔ. A n Sãa 'lɔ yɩn 'yɛrɛ ɩka a yɩ Nãaŋmɩn lɛ, 'lɔ lɛ kʊrɔ ma a tɩɩr.

eng: You dont know him, but I do. If I said I didnt know him, I would be like you, a liar. But I do know him, and I keep his word.
local: Yɩn ba bɔ̃ɔ wʊ gba ɛ, maa bɔ̃ɔ wʊ naa. Ala ɩ n 'yɛr naa ɩka n ba bɔ̃ɔ wʊ ɛ, ɩn ɩ naa jir ŋmara a yɩ kaar. Tɩchɛ maa bɔ̃ɔ wʊ naa, naa turo a wʊ 'yɛr bie.

eng: Your father Abraham was overjoyed that he would see my day. He saw it and was happy.
local: A yɩ sãa Abraham dɔ̃ɔ chɩlɛ tɩɛr ɩka wʊn taa nyɛ naa a n bɩbir. Wʊ nyɛn ka nʊ̃ɔ nyɔɔ wʊ.

eng: You arent even years old! the Jewish opposition replied. How can you say that you have seen Abraham.
local: Ka a Juu nɩbɛ sʊʊr wʊ, Faa ta yuom lɩjaayi nɩ pie sɛrɛ ɛ, tɩ na fʊ nyɛ Abraham.

eng: I assure you, Jesus replied, before Abraham was, I Am.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, sɛrɛ tɩ ba wa dɔɔ Abraham, ka maa dɔ̃ɔ be be.

eng: So they picked up stones to throw at him, but Jesus hid himself and left the temple.
local: A nyãna, ka ba wob kʊsɩbɛ ɩka ba lɔb wʊ kʊ, tɩchɛ ka a Yesu sɔɔl fʊɔl na wʊ yi a be a ba puorfʊ yir pɔ.

eng: As Jesus walked along, he saw a man who was blind from birth.
local: Yesu na chiine a lɛ, wʊ nyɛn daba ban dɔɔ ka wʊ ɩ jɔ̃ɔ.

eng: Jesus disciples asked, Rabbi, who sinned so that he was born blind, this man or his parents.
local: Ka a poturbo sʊʊr wʊ, Wiwile, an lɛ maal dɛɛr, a dajɔ̃ɔ bɩɩ a wʊ sãa naa ma lɛ maal dɛɛr, a dajɔ̃ɔ bɩɩ a wʊ sãa naa ma lɛ maal a dɛɛr dɔɔn wʊ, ka wʊ ɩ a jɔ̃ɔ.

eng: Jesus answered, Neither he nor his parents. This happened so that Gods mighty works might be displayed in him.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, A daba nyã ba maal dɛɛr ɛ, a wʊ sãa naa ma mɩ ba maal dɛɛr ɛ, tɩchɛ a ɩn a lɛ ka Nãaŋmɩn tʊ̃mɔ̃ na tʊ̃ a wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: While its daytime, we must do the works of him who sent me. Night is coming when no one can work.
local: An chɛnɛ ɩ mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ, sɩn tʊ̃ naa a 'lɔ na tʊ̃ ma tʊ̃mɔ̃. Wɛr na sɔb naa ka nɩrɛ kʊ̃ lɛ tʊ̃ɔ tʊ̃mɔ̃ tʊ̃ ɛ.

eng: While I am in the world, I am the light of the world.
local: Ɩn chɛnɛ be a wɛr pɔ, maa lɛ a wɛr chãa.

eng: After he said this, he spit on the ground, made mud with the saliva, and smeared the mud on the mans eyes.
local: Wʊn wa 'yɛr a lɛ baar, wʊ chir nataar bun tãn a tẽe na wʊ bɛ a daba nɩbie.

eng: Jesus said to him, Go, wash in the pool of Siloam . So the man went away and washed. When he returned, he could see.
local: Tɩ na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a daba, Chen, tɩ pɛɛ a Siloam kʊ̃ɔ pɔ. A Siloam par lɛ, Tʊ̃fʊ. Wʊ chen tɩ pɛɛ nyɛrɛ wa a yir.

eng: The mans neighbors and those who used to see him when he was a beggar said, Isnt this the man who used to sit and beg.
local: Ka a nɩbɔ̃ɔnsɩ bala na dɔ̃ɔ ma nyɛ wʊ na sʊɔrɔ, ka ba sʊrɔ taa, A ba ɩ a daba nyãna lɛ ma sʊɔrɔ ɛ.

eng: Some said, It is, and others said, No, its someone who looks like him. But the man said, Yes, its me.
local: Bamɩne 'yɛr ɩka 'lɔ lɛ, ka bamɩne mɩ 'yɛr, Nɩnyɛtaa lɛ. Tɩchɛ ka wʊ 'yɛr, Maa lɛ.

eng: So they asked him, How are you now able to see.
local: Ka ba sʊʊr wʊ, Ŋmɩnɛ a nɩbie ɩrɛ wa yuo fʊ.

eng: He answered, The man they call Jesus made mud, smeared it on my eyes, and said, Go to the pool of Siloam and wash. So I went and washed, and then I could see.
local: Ka a daba 'yɛr kʊ ba. Daba ban bʊɔlɔ Yesu lɛ bu tãn bɛ ma a nɩbie tɩ 'yɛr ma ɩka n chen a Siloam tɩ pɛɛ. Lɛ lɛ n chen tɩ pɛɛ naa nyɛrɛ.

eng: They asked, Where is this man? He replied, I dont know.
local: Ka ba sʊʊr wʊ, Nyɩnɛ lɛ wʊ be? Ka wʊ 'yɛr, N ba bɔ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: Then they led the man who had been born blind to the Pharisees.
local: Be lɛ ba tara a daba 'lɔ na dɔ̃ɔ ɩ a jɔ̃ɔ chiin a Faraseemɩne sɛ̃.

eng: Now Jesus made the mud and smeared it on the mans eyes on a Sabbath day.
local: A bɩbir 'lɔ a Yesu na dɔ̃ɔ bu a tãn na wʊ yuon a daba nɩbie ɩn a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar.

eng: So Pharisees also asked him how he was able to see. The man told them, He put mud on my eyes, I washed, and now I see.
local: Ka Faraseemɩne sʊʊr a daba lɛ wʊn ɩrɛ wa nyɛrɛ. Ka wʊ 'yɛr, Tãn lɛ wʊ bɛ ma a nɩbie ka n pɛɛ be nyɛrɛ.

eng: Some Pharisees said, This man isnt from God, because he breaks the Sabbath law. Others said, How can a sinner do miraculous signs like these? So they were divided.
local: Ka a Faraseemɩne bamɩne 'yɛr, A daba nyã ba yi Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ ɛ, waa sɔ̃nɔ̃ a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir ɛ. Tɩchɛ ka a bamɩne mɩ sʊʊr, Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ yelbe-ɩrɛ na tʊ̃ɔ tʊ̃ a nɛ'maa yele nyã? Na ba ŋma jiir ayi.

eng: Some of the Pharisees questioned the man who had been born blind again: What do you have to say about him, since he healed your eyes? He replied, Hes a prophet.
local: Ka ba lɩɛb sʊʊr a daba, Bo fʊ tara na 'yɛr a wʊ 'yɛr? Fʊ nɩbie ba lɛ wʊ yuo. Ka a daba 'yɛr, Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ lɛ wʊ ɩ.

eng: The Jewish leaders didnt believe the man had been blind and received his sight until they called for his parents.
local: Ka a Juu nɩbɛrɛ ba sɔɔ de ɛ ɩka a daba dɔ̃ɔ jɔ̃ɔ tɩ wa nyɛrɛ ɛ, ka ba bʊɔl a sãa naa ma.

eng: The Jewish leaders asked them, Is this your son? Are you saying he was born blind? How can he now see.
local: Ba sʊʊr ba naa, A yɩ bie lɛ a nyã ɔ? A nyã lɛ yɩ dɔɔ ka wʊ ɩ a jɔ̃ɔ? Ka ŋmɩnɛ wʊ ɩrɛ wa nyɛrɛ.

eng: His parents answered, We know he is our son. We know he was born blind.
