local: Ka a sãa naa ma 'yɛr, Sɩ bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka sɩ bie lɛ, a sɩ dɔɔ wʊ ka wʊ ɩ jɔ̃ɔ.

eng: But we dont know how he now sees, and we dont know who healed his eyes. Ask him. Hes old enough to speak for himself.
local: Tɩchɛ lɛ wʊn ɩrɛ wa nyɛrɛ a pʊ̃pãanyã saa bɔ̃ɔ ɛ, bɩɩ nɩrɛ 'lɔ na yuo wʊ a nɩbie saa bɔ̃ɔ ɛ. Waa ɩ bibile ɛ, yɩ sʊʊr wʊ, wʊn 'yɛr kʊ wʊ mãɛ.

eng: His parents said this because they feared the Jewish authorities. This is because the Jewish authorities had already decided that whoever confessed Jesus to be the Christ would be expelled from the synagogue.
local: A sãa naa ma 'yɛr naa nɩtɔ̃ bojũu ba joro naa a Juu nɩbɛ dãbãɛ, ba den nie 'yɛr ɩka nɩrɛ jaa wa 'yɛr ɩka a Yesu lɛ a Nɩ-iraa 'lɔ, ka ban dii wʊn bar a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie.

eng: Thats why his parents said, Hes old enough. Ask him.
local: Ala lɛ so ka a sãa naa ma 'yɛr, Waa ɩ bibile ɛ, yɩ sʊʊr wʊ.

eng: Therefore, they called a second time for the man who had been born blind and said to him, Give glory to God. We know this man is a sinner.
local: A Faraseemɩne lɛ bʊɔl naa a daba 'lɔ na dɔ̃ɔ ɩ a jɔ̃ɔ a gbɛɛ ayi sʊɔ 'yɛr wʊ, 'Yɛr yelmãɛ kʊ Nãaŋmɩn, sɩn bɔ̃ɔn naa a daba nyã na ɩ yelbe-ɩrɛ.

eng: The man answered, I dont know whether hes a sinner. Heres what I do know: I was blind and now I see.
local: Ka a daba 'yɛr, Ala ɩ wʊ ɩn yelbe-ɩrɛ bɩɩ waa ɩ ɛ, n ba bɔ̃ɔ ɛ, yelbʊ̃'yen ɩn bɔ̃ɔ nɩ nyã. N dɔ̃ɔ ɩ jɔ̃ɔ tɩchɛ pʊ̃pãanyã, n nyɛrɛ naa.

eng: They questioned him: What did he do to you? How did he heal your eyes.
local: Be lɛ ba sʊʊr wʊ, Bo lɛ wʊ ɩ fʊ? Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ wʊ ɩ yuo fʊ a nɩbie.

eng: He replied, I already told you, and you didnt listen. Why do you want to hear it again? Do you want to become his disciples too.
local: Ka a daba 'yɛr, N ba dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr tɩ yaa chɛlɛ ɛ. Bo yɩ bʊɔrɔ ɩka yɩ lɛ wõ ka a ɩ go? Yɩ bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka yɩ mɩ lɩɛb a wʊ poturbo bɩɩ.

eng: They insulted him: You are his disciple, but we are Moses disciples.
local: A ba tʊrɔ wʊ 'yɛrɛ, Fʊ̃ʊ lɛ ɩ a wʊ poturo, sɩn ɩ naa Mosesɩ poturbo.

eng: We know that God spoke to Moses, but we dont know where this man is from.
local: Sɩ bɔ̃ɔn na ɩka Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr 'yɛr kʊ Mosesɩ, tɩchɛ a daba 'lɔ saa bɔ̃ɔ jie wʊn yi gba ɛ.

eng: The man answered, This is incredible! You dont know where he is from, yet he healed my eyes!.
local: Ka a daba 'yɛr, A 'maa man nɛɛ yɩn ba bɔ̃ɔ jie wʊn yi ɛ, tɩ yuo ma a nɩbie.

eng: We know that God doesnt listen to sinners. God listens to anyone who is devout and does Gods will.
local: Sɩ bɔ̃ɔ naa ɩka Nãaŋmɩn ba chɛlɛ a yelbe-ɩrbɛ ɛ. Wʊ ma chɛlɛ naa a bala na joro wʊ tɩ maala a lɛ wʊn bʊɔrɔ.

eng: No one has ever heard of a healing of the eyes of someone born blind.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa ba dɔ̃ɔ wõ ɩka ba yuon nɩrɛ ban lɔ̃ɔ dɔɔn jɔ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: If this man wasnt from God, he couldnt do this.
local: Ala ɩ a daba nyã ba ta yi Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ ɛ, wʊ kʊ̃ naa tʊ̃ɔ bon jaa maal ɛ.

eng: They responded, You were born completely in sin! How is it that you dare to teach us? Then they expelled him.
local: Ka a Juu nɩbɛrɛ 'yɛr kʊ a daba, A fʊ̃ʊ, yelbier pɔ lɛ ba dɔɔ fʊ, ŋmɩnɛ fʊn ɩrɛ wa wiil sɩ, tɩ dii wʊ bar a ba pɔ.

eng: Jesus heard they had expelled the man born blind. Finding him, Jesus said, Do you believe in the Human One.
local: Yesu na wõ ɩka ba dii naa a daba bar, wʊ bɔ wʊn wa nyɛ wʊ, na wʊ sʊʊr wʊ, Fʊ sɔɔn de a Nɩsaal Bie ɔ.

eng: He answered, Who is he, sir? I want to believe in him.
local: Ka a daba sʊʊr wʊ, Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ an lɛ, wiil ma ka n sɔɔ de wʊ.

eng: Jesus said, You have seen him. In fact, he is the one speaking with you.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr, Fʊ nyɛ wʊ naa pʊ̃pãanyã, sɩrɛ jaa, 'lɔ lɛ 'yɛrɛ nɩ fʊ.

eng: The man said, Lord, I believe. And he worshipped Jesus.
local: Ka a daba pãa 'yɛr, N Soro n sɔɔn de, tɩ gbur dumo puor wʊ.

eng: Jesus said, I have come into the world to exercise judgment so that those who dont see can see and those who see will become blind.
local: Be lɛ a Yesu 'yɛr, 'Yɛr tɔɔrfʊ jie lɛ n wa ka a wɛr pɔ. Jɔ̃n na nyɛrɛ naa ka nɩbinyɛrɛ mɩ lɩɛb ɩ jɔ̃n.

eng: Some Pharisees who were with him heard what he said and asked, Surely we arent blind, are we.
local: Ka a Faraseemɩne bamɩne na be a wʊ sɛ̃ wõ a lɛ na ba sʊʊr, Bo ya! A sɩn mɩ ɩn a jɔ̃n ɔ.

eng: Jesus said to them, If you were blind, you wouldnt have any sin, but now that you say, We see, your sin remains.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr ba, Ala ɩ yɩ ta ɩn jɔ̃n, yɩ kʊ naa tara chɛfʊ a dɛɛr pɔ ɛ. Tɩchɛ, yɩn ben 'yɛr ɩka yɩ nyɛrɛ naa, yɩ chɛnɛ na be a yɩ dɛɛr pɔ.

eng: After this Jesus went across the Galilee Sea .
local: A anyã puor, Yesu gɔ̃ɔ naa a Galilee man gɔ̃ɔn be ban bʊɔlɔ a Tiberiyas.

eng: A large crowd followed him, because they had seen the miraculous signs he had done among the sick.
local: Nɩyɔɔ tu wʊn chen ban nyɛ wʊn tʊ̃ nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ na wʊ sãa baalsɩ.

eng: Jesus went up a mountain and sat there with his disciples.
local: Ka a Yesu pãa do a tɔ̃ɔ ju, na wʊ tɩ jãnɛ nɩ a wʊ poturbo.

eng: It was nearly time for Passover, the Jewish festival.
local: Ka a Juu nɩbɛ Gõl Bar Difʊ mɩ gbʊr.

eng: Jesus looked up and saw the large crowd coming toward him. He asked Philip, Where will we buy food to feed these people.
local: Yesu na wa nyɛ a nɩyɔɔ ka ba waara a wʊ sɛ̃, wʊ sʊʊr Filip, Nyɩnɛ lɛ sɩn nyɛ bʊ̃diir kʊ a nɩbɛ banyãna.

eng: Jesus said this to test him, for he already knew what he was going to do.
local: Wʊ sʊʊr a nɩtɔ̃ ɩka wʊ bɛɛr a Filip kaa, tɩ bɔ̃ɔn a lɛ wʊn na ɩ.

eng: Philip replied, More than a half years salary worth of food wouldnt be enough for each person to have even a little bit.
local: Ka a Filip 'yɛr, Faa bɔ̃ɔ ka an den ŋmɛrsɩ aniin sãyar a fʊn dãn paanʊ̃ɔ jaa pʊʊr kʊ a nɩbɛ banyãna ɛ.

eng: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peters brother, said.
local: Andiru na ɩ a Simon Pita yɛbɛ, a Yesu poturo ãsʊɔ lɛ, ka wʊ 'yɛr.

eng: A youth here has five barley loaves and two fish. But what good is that for a crowd like this.
local: Dabile ben ka na wʊ tara a chi jɔ̃n paanʊ̃ɔ anũu nɩ jãmɛ ayi, tɩ bo an maal a nɩyɔɔ banyãna.

eng: Jesus said, Have the people sit down. There was plenty of grass there. They sat down, about five thousand of them.
local: Mʊɔr pɔɔn a tẽe wʊ jaa, ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a poturbo ka ba ɩ a nɩbɛ jãnɛ jãnɛ. A nɩbɛ bala dɔ̃ɔ ɩ tur anũu .

eng: Then Jesus took the bread. When he had given thanks, he distributed it to those who were sitting there. He did the same with the fish, each getting as much as they wanted.
local: Yesu den a paanʊ̃ɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ a wʊ nũu pɔ tɩ puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan, tɩ de a paanʊ̃ɔ põ a nɩbɛ, na wʊ lɛ de a jãmɛ mɩ põ ba.

eng: When they had plenty to eat, he said to his disciples, Gather up the leftover pieces, so that nothing will be wasted.
local: Ka a nɩbɛ di lɛ ban bʊɔrɔ, ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a poturbo ɩka ba wob a bʊ̃ŋmaara ala na chɛ, ka a bon jaa ta wɛ ɛ.

eng: So they gathered them and filled twelve baskets with the pieces of the five barley loaves that had been left over by those who had eaten.
local: Lɛ lɛ ba wob a ala na chɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ pɛr pie nɩ ayi a chi paanʊ̃ɔ anũu ala.

eng: When the people saw that he had done a miraculous sign, they said, This is truly the prophet who is coming into the world.
local: A nɩbɛ na wa nyɛ a anyã baar lɛ a Yesu na tʊ̃ a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃, ba piel 'yɛrɛ, A nyã lɛ tɔ̃ɔ ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ 'lɔ na wa a wɛr pɔ.

eng: Jesus understood that they were about to come and force him to be their king, so he took refuge again, alone on a mountain.
local: Yesu na wa bɔ̃ɔn ɩka ba bʊɔrɔ naa na fɩr wʊ ka wʊ ɩ a ba nãa, wʊ don a tɔ̃ɔ ju wʊ yõ.

eng: When evening came, Jesus disciples went down to the lake.
local: A jãanʊɔr 'lɔ̃nɔ̃, a Yesu poturbo lɩɛb suu a man pɔ.

eng: They got into a boat and were crossing the lake to Capernaum. It was already getting dark and Jesus hadnt come to them yet.
local: Na ba tɩ kpɛ a gboro pɔ piel gɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a Kapenahum. A jãanɔ'maar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ Yesu ba wa a ba sɛ̃ sɛrɛ ɛ.

eng: The water was getting rough because a strong wind was blowing.
local: A sɛsɛkpɛ̃ɛ furo na ka a kʊ̃ɔ dãdaala.

eng: When the wind had driven them out for about three or four miles, they saw Jesus walking on the water. He was approaching the boat and they were afraid.
local: Ka a poturbo na wa mɔ̃ kpɛ sɔɔ jaa, ba nyɛn a Yesu na chiine a kʊ̃ɔ ju waara a gboro par ka dãbãɛ kpɛ ba.

eng: He said to them, I Am. Dont be afraid.
local: Tɩchɛ ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Maa lɛ, yɩ ta joro dãbãɛ ɛ.

eng: Then they wanted to take him into the boat, and just then the boat reached the land where they had been heading.
local: Ka ba ta ɩka ba de wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ a gboro pɔ, tɩ ajienaa ka a gboro mɩ ta a gogoro nɛɛ a jie 'lɔ ban chiine.

eng: The next day the crowd that remained on the other side of the lake realized that only one boat had been there. They knew Jesus hadnt gone with his disciples, but that the disciples had gone alone.
local: A wɛr na wa chaar, a nɩyɔɔ bala na jãnɛ a man gɔ̃ɔn bɔ̃ɔn na ɩka gboro bʊ̃'yen tɛɛ lɛ dɔ̃ɔ be be. Ka Yesu naa poturbo ba 'mataa kpɛ wʊ ɛ, tɩchɛ bala tɛɛ lɛ chiine.

eng: Some boats came from Tiberias, near the place where they had eaten the bread over which the Lord had given thanks.
local: Ka gboi amɩne na yi Tiberiyas wa ara gbʊr a be a Yesu na dɔ̃ɔ puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan tɩ ka a nɩbɛ di a bʊ̃diir.

eng: When the crowd saw that neither Jesus nor his disciples were there, they got into the boats and came to Capernaum looking for Jesus.
local: A nɩbɛ na wa nyɛ ka a Yesu bɩɩ a wʊ poturbo ba be be ɛ, ba kpɛ̃n a gboi ala chiine a Kapenahum na tɩ bɔ a Yesu.

eng: When they found him on the other side of the lake, they asked him, Rabbi, when did you get here.
local: Ban wa tɩ nyɛ wʊ a manyal gɔ̃ɔn, ba sʊʊr wʊ naa, Wiwile, dabor fʊ wa ka.

eng: Jesus replied, I assure you that you are looking for me not because you saw miraculous signs but because you ate all the food you wanted.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊ̃rɔ yɩ, yɩ yʊɔrɔ na bʊɔrɔ ma a ba ɩ a nɛ 'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ yɩn nyɛ ɩn tʊ̃ ɛ, tɩchɛ a bʊ̃diir yɩn di tɩɩ jũu.

eng: Dont work for the food that doesnt last but for the food that endures for eternal life, which the Human One will give you. God the Father has confirmed him as his agent to give life.
local: Yɩ ta tʊ̃nɔ̃ bʊɔrɔ bʊ̃diir anyã na sɔ̃ɔ ɛ, tɩ yɩ tʊ̃nɔ̃ bʊɔrɔ bʊ̃diir anyã na kʊ yɩ nyɛvʊr 'lɔ na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ, ala lɛ a maa Nɩsaal Bie na kʊ yɩ. Bojũu 'lɔ lɛ a Sãa Nãaŋmɩn kʊ sɔr ɩka wʊ ɩ a lɛ.

eng: They asked, What must we do in order to accomplish what God requires.
local: Be lɛ ba sʊʊr wʊ, Bãa lɛ sɩn na ɩ na sɩ tʊ̃ a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na bʊɔrɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, This is what God requires, that you believe in him whom God sent.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Nãaŋmɩn tʊ̃mɔ̃ nɩ nyã ɩka yɩ sɔɔ de a 'lɔ wʊn tʊ̃.

eng: They asked, What miraculous sign will you do, that we can see and believe you? What will you do?.
local: Lɛ lɛ ba sʊʊr wʊ, Nɛ'maa tʊ̃buor lɛ fʊn tʊ̃ ka sɩ nyɛ tɩ sɔɔ de fʊ? Bo lɛ fʊn ɩ?.

eng: Our ancestors ate manna in the wilderness, just as it is written, He gave them bread from heaven to eat .
local: A sɩ sãakpãmɩne din a maana a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ nɩtãa lɛ an sɛb. Wʊ kʊ ba naa a bʊ̃diir na yi saaju ka ba di.

eng: Jesus told them, I assure you, it wasnt Moses who gave the bread from heaven to you, but my Father gives you the true bread from heaven.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, a ba ɩ Mosesɩ lɛ dɔ̃ɔ kʊ yɩ a bʊ̃diir ala na yi a saaju ɛ, tɩchɛ a n Sãa lɛ dɔ̃ɔ kʊ yɩ a yelmãɛ bʊ̃diir ala na yi a saaju.

eng: The bread of God is the one who comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.
local: A Nãaŋmɩn bʊ̃diir ɩ naa a 'lɔ na yi a saaju wa, na wʊ wa kʊrɔ a wɛr nɩbɛ nyɛvʊr.

eng: They said, Sir, give us this bread all the time.
local: Ka ba 'yɛr, Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, an yi a dɩna tɩ chiine, kʊrɔ sɩ a bʊ̃diir ala.

eng: Jesus replied, I am the bread of life. Whoever comes to me will never go hungry, and whoever believes in me will never be thirsty.
local: Be a Yesu pãa 'yɛr, Maa lɛ a bʊ̃diir ala na kʊrɔ nyɛvʊr, 'lɔ jaa na wa n sɛ̃ kʊ̃ lɛ chiin kɔ̃ ɛ. 'Lɔ jaa mɩ na sɔɔ de ma, kɔ̃nyũur kʊ̃ lɛ kpɛ wʊ ɛ.

eng: But I told you that you have seen me and still dont believe.
local: Tɩchɛ a lɛ n na 'yɛr kʊ yɩ, yɩ nyɛ ma naa tɩ yaa sɔɔ de ma ɛ.

eng: Everyone whom the Father gives to me will come to me, and I wont send away anyone who comes to me.
local: Bala ba jaa a Sãa na kʊ ma, ban wan a n sɛ̃. Ɩ̃sʊɔ jaa mɩ wa wa a n sɛ̃, n kʊ̃ dii wʊ bar ɛ.

eng: I have come down from heaven not to do my will, but the will of him who sent me.
local: Bojũu, n ba yi a saaju wa ɩka n wa ɩ a n bɔfʊ ɛ, tɩ n wa naa ɩka n wa ɩ a Sãa bɔfʊ. 'Lɔ lɛ tʊ̃ ma.

eng: This is the will of the one who sent me, that I wont lose anything he has given me, but I will raise it up at the last day.
local: A nyã lɛ ɩ a 'lɔ na tʊ̃ ma bɔfʊ, ka n taa ɩrɛ wa vɛ̃ ka bala jaa wʊn kʊ ma bɔr ɛ, tɩchɛ ka n sãɩ ba, ka ba ir ɩ vʊɛ a ber baaraa daar.

eng: This is my Fathers will: that all who see the Son and believe in him will have eternal life, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Bojũu, a n Sãa bɔfʊ nɩ nyã ɩka, nɩrɛ jaa na kaa a Bie na wʊ sɔɔ de wʊ, ban tara naa a nyɛvʊr 'lɔ na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ, ka n sãɩ ba, ka ba ir ɩ vʊɛ a ber baaraa daar.

eng: The Jewish opposition grumbled about him because he said, I am the bread that came down from heaven.
local: Ka a Juu nɩbɛ piel hũhũnõ bojũu a Yesu 'yɛr ɩka, 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a bʊ̃diir 'lɔ na yi a saaju wa.

eng: They asked, Isnt this Jesus, Josephs son, whose mother and father we know? How can he now say, I have come down from heaven.
local: Ka ba 'yɛrɛ, A ba ɩ a Yesu lɛ a nyã ɛ, a Josefʊ bidaba, a wʊ sãa nɩ a wʊ ma sɩn bɔ̃ɔ ɛ? Ŋmɩnɛ wʊ ɩrɛ wa 'yɛr ɩka saaju lɛ n yi wa.

eng: Jesus responded, Dont grumble among yourselves.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr ba, Yɩ bar a hũhũfʊ.

eng: No one can come to me unless they are drawn to me by the Father who sent me, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ wa a n sɛ̃ ɛ, ka a Sãa na tʊ̃ ma baa tɔɔ wʊ waan ɛ, ka n sãɩ wʊ ka wʊ ir ɩ vʊr a ber baaraa daar.

eng: It is written in the Prophets, And they will all be taught by God. Everyone who has listened to the Father and learned from him comes to me.
local: A Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ ãsʊɔ dɔ̃ɔ sɛb, Nãaŋmɩn na wiil ba jaa. Ɩ̃sʊɔ jaa na chɛlɛ a Sãa sɛ̃, na wʊ jãn yi a wʊ sɛ̃, na wa naa a n sɛ̃.

eng: No one has seen the Father except the one who is from God. He has seen the Father.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa ba nyɛ a Sãa ɛ, a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na yi a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃, 'lɔ tɛɛ lɛ nyɛ a Sãa.

eng: I assure you, whoever believes has eternal life.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, nɩrɛ 'lɔ na sɔɔ de ma tara na nyɛvʊr 'lɔ na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ, Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃.

eng: I am the bread of life.
local: Maa lɛ ɩ a bʊ̃diir ala na tara nyɛvʊr.

eng: Your ancestors ate manna in the wilderness and they died.
local: A yɩ sãakpãmɩne dɔ̃ɔ di a maana a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ tɩ chɛnɛ kpi.

eng: This is the bread that comes down from heaven so that whoever eats from it will never die.
local: Tɩchɛ a bʊ̃diir 'lɔ na yi a saaju wa nɩ nyã, 'lɔ lɛ nɩrɛ na di na wʊ kʊ̃ kpi ɛ.

eng: I am the living bread that came down from heaven. Whoever eats this bread will live forever, and the bread that I will give for the life of the world is my flesh.
local: Maa lɛ ɩ a nyɛvʊr bʊ̃diir 'lɔ na yi a saaju wa. Ala ɩ nɩrɛ di naa a bʊ̃diir nyãna, wʊn ben be jaa kpo kpo. A bʊ̃diir nyã ɩ naa a n ãgãn ɩn de kʊrɔ a nɩbɛ na be a wɛr pɔ, ka ba na nyɛ nyɛvʊr.

eng: Then the Jews debated among themselves, asking, How can this man give us his flesh to eat.
local: Ka a Juu nɩbɛ piel chɩrɛ taa nɩ suur na ba 'yɛrɛ, Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ a daba nyãna na ɩrɛ wa kʊ sɩ a wʊ ãgãn ka sɩ 'wɔb.

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you, unless you eat the flesh of the Human One and drink his blood, you have no life in you.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ. Yɩn baa 'wɔb a Nɩsaal Bie ãgãn na yɩ nyũ a wʊ jãɩ ɛ, yaa tara nyɛvʊr ɛ.

eng: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na 'wɔb a n ãgãn na wʊ nyũ a n jãɩ tara na a nyɛvʊr 'lɔ na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ, tɩ ka n sãɩ wʊ a wʊ ir ɩ vʊr a ber baaraa daar.

eng: My flesh is true food and my blood is true drink.
local: A n ãgãn ɩ naa bʊ̃dimãɛ, ka n jãɩ mɩ ɩ bʊ̃nyũmãɛ.

eng: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood remains in me and I in them.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na 'wɔb a n ãgãn na wʊ nyũ a n jãɩ, be naa a n 'yɔ̃ɔ ka n mɩ be a wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: As the living Father sent me, and I live because of the Father, so whoever eats me lives because of me.
local: Nɩtãa lɛ a Sãa na ɩ a nyɛvʊr sʊɔ na tʊ̃ ma ka n be be a Sãa jũu, nɩrɛ 'lɔ mɩ na 'wɔb ma mɩ na ben be a maa jũu.

eng: This is the bread that came down from heaven. It isnt like the bread your ancestors ate, and then they died. Whoever eats this bread will live forever.
local: A bʊ̃diir nyã lɛ yi a saaju wa. Sɩ sãakpãmɩne dɔ̃ɔ di a maana tɩ kpi, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ na di a bʊ̃diir nyã na ben be kpo kpo.

eng: Jesus said these things while he was teaching in the synagogue in Capernaum.
local: Yesu na dɔ̃ɔ wile ba a Juu nɩbɛ lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir a Kapenahum ala lɛ wʊ 'yɛr a nyãna.

eng: Many of his disciples who heard this said, This message is harsh. Who can hear it.
local: Yesu poturbo yɔɔ na wa wõ a lɛ wʊn 'yɛr, ka ba 'yɛr, A fʊ wiilfʊ nyã kpɛ̃mɛ na kpɛ, an lɛ na tʊ̃ɔ de.

eng: Jesus knew that the disciples were grumbling about this and he said to them, Does this offend you?.
local: Yesu na bɔ̃ɔn a lɛ a poturbo na hũhũnõ, ka wʊ sʊʊr ba, A nɩtɔ̃ chɛ yɩn bɩɩ?.

eng: What if you were to see the Human One going up where he was before?.
local: Ŋmɩnɛ an pãa kaara, yɩn wa nyɛ a Nɩsaal Bie na duoro a jie 'lɔ wʊn dɔ̃ɔ be?.

eng: The Spirit is the one who gives life and the flesh doesnt help at all. The words I have spoken to you are spirit and life.
local: 'Lɔ na kʊrɔ a nɩrɛ nyɛvʊr ɩ naa Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ. Nɩrɛ mãɛ fɔ̃ɔ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ maal bon jaa ɛ. A 'yɛr ala ɩn 'yɛr yɩ, ɩ naa Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ 'yɛr, naa ɩ Nyɛvʊr.

eng: Yet some of you dont believe.Jesus knew from the beginning who wouldnt believe and the one who would betray him.
local: Tɩchɛ a yɩ bamɩne ba sɔɔ ɛ. Bojũu, Yesu bɔ̃ɔn naa a pielfʊ a 'lɔ na ba sɔɔ de wʊ ɛ, nɩ a 'lɔ na gar 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ.

eng: He said,For this reason I said to you that none can come to me unless the Father enables them to do so.
local: Be lɛ wʊ 'yɛr, Nɩtɔ̃ lɛ so ka n 'yɛr ɩka ãsʊɔ jaa kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ wa a n sɛ̃ ɛ, ka a Sãa baa waan wʊ ɛ.

eng: At this, many of his disciples turned away and no longer accompanied him.
local: An yi a daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ a poturbo yɔɔ ŋmaan yi na baa lɛ turo wʊ go ɛ.

eng: Jesus asked the Twelve, Do you also want to leave.
local: Ka a Yesu sʊʊr a Pie Nɩ Ayi bala, A yɩn mɩ, yɩ bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka yi ŋmaa yi bɩɩ.

eng: Simon Peter answered, Lord, where would we go? You have the words of eternal life.
local: Be a Simon Pita 'yɛr, N Soro, an sɛ̃ lɛ sɩn chen? An ɩ fʊ̃ʊ tɛɛ lɛ tara a nyɛvʊr 'lɔ na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ.

eng: We believe and know that you are Gods holy one.
local: Sɩ sɔɔn de na sɩ bɔ̃ɔ ɩka fʊ ɩ naa a chɛchɛ sʊɔ 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn na tʊ̃.

eng: Jesus replied, Didnt I choose you twelve? Yet one of you is a devil.
local: Ka a Yesu haa 'yɛr, A ba ɩ maa lɛ ir a yɩ Pie Nɩ Ayi ɛ? Tɩchɛ ka a yɩ ãsʊɔ ɩ Satan tʊɔr.

eng: He was speaking of Judas, Simon Iscariots son, for he, one of the Twelve, was going to betray him.
local: Wʊ 'yɛrɛ naa a Judas na ɩ a Pie Nɩ Ayi ãsʊɔ na wa gar 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ. Wʊ ɩ naa a Simon bidaba na yi Isɩkariyot.

eng: After this Jesus traveled throughout Galilee. He didnt want to travel in Judea, because the Jewish authorities wanted to kill him.
local: A anyã puor, Yesu yi naa Judiya tɩ yʊɔrɔ a Galilee paal pɔ, bojũu a Juu nɩbɛ bʊɔrɔ naa a wʊ kʊfʊ.

eng: When it was almost time for the Jewish Festival of Booths.
local: Tɩchɛ a Juu nɩbɛ sɛɛr pɔ lɔ̃ɔ di bɩbir na wa gbʊrɔ.

eng: Jesus brothers said to him, Leave Galilee. Go to Judea so that your disciples can see the amazing works that you do.
local: a Yesu yɛbil 'yɛr wʊ naa, Yi a ka chen a Judiya ka a fʊ poturbo mɩ tɩ nyɛ a fʊ nɛ 'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃.

eng: Those who want to be known publicly dont do things secretly. Since you can do these things, show yourself to the world.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa na bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba bʊɔlɔ a wʊ yuor ba sɔɔlɔ ɛ. Fʊn maala a nyãna, wiil a fʊ mãɛ ka nɩbɛ jaa nyɛ fʊ.

eng: His brothers said this because even they didnt believe in him.
local: Bojũu a wʊ mãɛ yɛbil ba sɔɔ de wʊ gba ɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, For you, anytime is fine. But my time hasnt come yet.
local: A lɛ jũu, a Yesu 'yɛr ba, A n bɩbir ba vɩɛ ɛ, yɩn bɩbir vɩɛ naa.

eng: The world cant hate you. It hates me, though, because I testify that its works are evil.
local: A wɛr nɩbɛ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ 'laar yɩ ɛ, tɩchɛ ba 'laar ma naa bojũu n 'yɛr ba naa ɩka a lɛ ban ɩrɛ ɩn dɛɛr.

eng: You go up to the festival. Im not going to this one because my time hasnt yet come.
local: Yɩn, yɩ chiine a lɔ̃ɔ di jie. Maa ba chiine a lɔ̃ɔ di jie sɛrɛ ɛ, bojũu n bɩbir ba vɩɛ ɛ.

eng: Having said this, he stayed in Galilee.
local: Wʊ 'yɛr naa lɛ tɩ chɛnɛ jãnɛ a Galilee.

eng: However, after his brothers left for the festival, he went too—not openly but in secret.
local: A wʊ yɛbil na wa chen a lɔ̃ɔ di jie baar ka wʊ mɩ ir sɔɔl chen.

eng: The Jewish leaders were looking for Jesus at the festival. They kept asking, Where is he.
local: A lɔ̃ɔ di jie lɛ a Juu nɩbɛ jʊrɔ wʊ tɩ sʊrɔ taa, Nyɩnɛ lɛ a daba 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ be.

eng: The crowds were murmuring about him. Hes a good man, some said, but others were saying, No, he tricks the people.
local: A nɩyɔɔ bala bʊlɔ na 'yɛrɛ kʊ̃rɔ taa, A Yesu ɩn nɩsʊ̃. Ka bamɩne mɩ 'yɛrɛ, Nɩbɛlɛ lɛ wʊ ɩ.

eng: No one spoke about him publicly, though, for fear of the Jewish authorities.
local: Tɩchɛ baa 'lara 'yɛrɛ ka a yire chãa pɔ ɛ, bojũu ba joro naa a Juu nɩbɛrɛ dãbãɛ.

eng: Halfway through the festival, Jesus went up to the temple and started to teach.
local: A lɔ̃ɔfʊ na wa põ jãtaa, Yesu don kpɛ a Juu nɩbɛ puorfʊ jie na wʊ tɩ piel wile.

eng: Astonished, the Jewish leaders asked, Hes never been taught! How has he mastered the Law.
local: Ka a nɛɛr 'maa a Juu nɩbɛrɛ ka ba sʊrɔ taa, Ŋmɩnɛ a daba nyã ɩrɛ wa bɔ̃ɔ a nɩtɔ̃ wʊn ba jãn ɛ.

eng: Jesus responded, My teaching isnt mine but comes from the one who sent me.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, A wiilfʊ nyã ba yi a n sɛ̃ ɛ, a yi naa a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na tʊ̃ ma sɛ̃.

eng: Whoever wants to do Gods will can tell whether my teaching is from God or whether I speak on my own.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na kaa ir ɩka wʊn ɩ naa Nãaŋmɩn bɔfʊ, wʊn bɔ̃ɔ naa ɩka a wiilfʊ nyã yin Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ bɩɩ n mãɛ lɛ n wile.

eng: Those who speak on their own seek glory for themselves. Those who seek the glory of him who sent me are people of truth; theres no falsehood in them.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na 'yɛrɛ a wʊ mãɛ 'yɛr, bʊɔrɔ na 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ kʊrɔ a wʊ mãɛ. Tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ na tʊ̃nɔ̃ bʊɔrɔ 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ kʊrɔ a 'lɔ na tʊ̃ wʊ, a nɩnyɩnɛ ɩn yelmãɛ sʊɔ ka a bɛlfʊ ba be a wʊ jie ɛ.

eng: Didnt Moses give you the Law? Yet none of you keep the Law. Why do you want to kill me.
local: Mosesɩ baa dɔ̃ɔ bin a Nɛɛ kʊ yɩ ɛ? Tɩ yɩ ãsʊɔ jaa ba tʊ̃ɔ tu ɛ. Bo 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ yɩ bʊɔrɔ ɩka yɩ kʊ ma.

eng: The crowd answered, You have a demon. Who wants to kill you.
local: Ka a nɩyɔɔ bala 'yɛr, Sɩdɛɛr lɛ fʊ tara, an bʊɔrɔ fʊ kʊfʊ.

eng: Jesus replied, I did one work, and you were all astonished.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Gbɛbʊ̃'yen tɛɛ lɛ n be tʊ̃, tɩchɛ ka a be 'maa yɩ a nɛɛ a nɩtɔ̃?.

eng: Because Moses gave you the commandment about circumcision , you circumcise a man on the Sabbath.
local: Mosesɩ lɛ dɔ̃ɔ wiil yɩ a yʊɔr ŋmaafʊ an ba ɩ 'lɔ sɛ̃ lɛ a dɔ̃ɔ yi ɛ, tɩ yi a sãakpãmɩne sɛ̃, ka ba ma ŋmaa yʊɔr a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar.

eng: If a man can be circumcised on the Sabbath without breaking Moses Law, why are you angry with me because I made an entire man well on the Sabbath?.
local: Ala ɩ yɩn tʊ̃ɔ ŋmaa bibile yʊɔr a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar tɩ ka a Mosesɩ nɛɛ 'lɔ wʊn bin ba sɔ̃ɔ ɛ, bo lɛ so ka yɩ nyɛ suur 'yɔ̃ɔ ma nɩrɛ ɩn sa a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar?.

eng: Dont judge according to appearances. Judge with right judgment.
local: Yɩ ta bʊ̃nɔ̃ tɔɔr ɛ, tɩ yɩ tɔɔrɔ a lɛ na tor.

eng: Some people from Jerusalem said, Isnt he the one they want to kill?.
local: Nɩbɛ bamɩne na yi Jerusalɛm piel sʊrɔ taa, A ba ɩ a daba nyã lɛ ba bʊɔrɔ a kʊfʊ ɛ.

eng: Here he is, speaking in public, yet they arent saying anything to him. Could it be that our leaders actually think he is the Christ?.
local: nyɛ wʊn ara 'yɛrɛ chãa pɔ, ka baa 'yɛrɛ 'yɛr ɛ? A nɩbɛrɛ bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka 'lɔ lɛ a Nɩ-iraa bɩɩ?.

eng: We know where he is from, but when the Christ comes, no one will know where he is from.
local: Tɩchɛ sɩn bɔ̃ɔ naa a jie 'lɔ a daba nyãna na yi. A Nɩ-iraa 'lɔ wa wa, nɩrɛ jaa kʊ̃ bɔ̃ɔ a jie wʊn na yi ɛ.

eng: While Jesus was teaching in the temple, he exclaimed, You know me and where I am from. I havent come on my own. The one who sent me is true, and you dont know him.
local: Yesu chɛnɛ na wile a be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir bɛchaar pɔ, na wʊ tãna 'yɛrɛ, Ɔ̃ɔ yɩ bɔ̃ɔ ma naa, na yɩ lɛ bɔ̃ɔ a jie 'lɔ n na yi. N ba be ka nɩ n mãɛ kpɛ̃ɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na tʊ̃ ma ɩn yelmãɛ sʊɔ, yɩn ba bɔ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: I know him because I am from him and he sent me.
local: Tɩchɛ maa bɔ̃ɔ wʊ naa, wʊ sɛ̃ lɛ n yi, 'lɔ lɛ tʊ̃ ma.

eng: So they wanted to seize Jesus, but they couldnt because his time hadnt yet come.
local: A anyã lɛ ba ta bʊɔrɔ a wʊ nyɔɔfʊ, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ jaa ba lɩɛb wʊ nũu sɩɩr wʊ ɛ, bojũu a wʊ bɩbir ba vɩɛ ɛ.

eng: Many from that crowd believed in Jesus. They said, When the Christ comes, will he do more miraculous signs than this man does.
local: A nɩyɔɔ bala pɔ, bamɩne chɛnɛ na sɔɔ de wʊ na ba 'yɛrɛ, A Nɩ-iraa 'lɔ wa wa, wʊn tʊ̃n nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ gar a daba nyã bɩɩ.

eng: The Pharisees heard the crowd whispering such things about Jesus, and the chief priests and Pharisees sent guards to arrest him.
local: Faraseemɩne wõn a lɛ a nɩyɔɔ bala na bʊlɔ 'yɛrɛ a Yesu 'yɛr, ka a bala naa Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ tʊ̃ a puorfʊ yir kakaarba ka ba tɩ nyɔɔ wʊ waan.

eng: Therefore, Jesus said, Im still with you for a little while before I go to the one who sent me.
local: Be a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, N be naa yɩ sɛ̃ bãlãa, tɩ naa pãa lɛ lɩɛb chen a 'lɔ na tʊ̃ ma sɛ̃.

eng: You will look for me, but you wont find me, and where I am you cant come.
local: Yɩn wa yʊɔrɔ na bʊɔrɔ ma, tɩchɛ yɩ kʊ̃ nyɛ ma ɛ, a be n na be yɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ wa be ɛ.

eng: The Jewish opposition asked each other, Where does he intend to go that we cant find him? Surely he doesnt intend to go where our people have been scattered and are living among the Greeks! He isnt going to teach the Greeks, is he?.
local: Ka a Juu nɩbɛ pãa sʊrɔ taa, Nyɩnɛ lɛ a daba nyã na pãa chen ka sɩ kʊ̃ nyɛ wʊ ɛ? Wʊn chen naa be a sɩ nɩbɛ na yaar jɛr a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ tɩ wile ba bɩɩ?.

eng: What does he mean when he says, You will look for me, but you wont find me, and where I am you cant come.
local: Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ wʊ 'yɛr ɩka, yɩn wa yʊɔrɔ na bʊɔrɔ ma tɩ yɩ kʊ̃ nyɛ ma ɛ, a be n na be yɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ wa be ɛ.

eng: On the last and most important day of the festival, Jesus stood up and shouted, All who are thirsty should come to me!.
local: A bɩbir baaraa daar na ɩ a lɔ̃ɔ di bɩbir kpɛ̃ɛ, Yesu ir ara tɩ tãn 'yɛr, Kɔ̃nyũur wa kpɛ nɩrɛ, wʊ wa a n sɛ̃ wa nyũ.

eng: All who believe in me should drink! As the scriptures said concerning me, Rivers of living water will flow out from within him. .
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na sɔɔ de ma nɩtãa lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn gãn na 'yɛr, nyɛvʊr kʊ̃ɔ na joro na yire a wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: Jesus said this concerning the Spirit. Those who believed in him would soon receive the Spirit, but they hadnt experienced the Spirit yet since Jesus hadnt yet been glorified.
local: Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ 'yɛr lɛ wʊ dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛrɛ, a bala na sɔɔ de wʊ ka wʊn kʊ ba. A Sɩɛ ba dɔ̃ɔ wa ɛ, an ɩ a Yesu ba nyɛ a tɩɩr sɛrɛ ɛ.

eng: When some in the crowd heard these words, they said, This man is truly the prophet.
local: A nɩbɛ na wa wõ a lɛ wʊn 'yɛr, ka bamɩne 'yɛr, A daba nyã ɩn Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ yelmãɛ.

eng: Others said, Hes the Christ. But others said, The Christ cant come from Galilee, can he?.
local: Ka bamɩne mɩ 'yɛrɛ, A nyã lɛ ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Nɩ-iraa. Ka bamɩne mɩ 'yɛr, Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ a Nɩ-iraa 'lɔ na ɩrɛ wa yi Galilee?.

eng: Didnt the scripture say that the Christ comes from Davids family and from Bethlehem, Davids village.
local: Nãaŋmɩn gãn ba 'yɛr ɩka David yɔ̃ɔ lɛ wʊn ɩ na wʊ yi Bɛtɩlɛhɛm, a tẽe 'lɔ a David na dɔ̃ɔ kpɩɛrɛ ɛ.

eng: So the crowd was divided over Jesus.
local: Ka a nɩbɛ ŋma dẽ dẽ a Yesu juu.

eng: Some wanted to arrest him, but no one grabbed him.
local: Bamɩne mɩ ta bʊɔrɔ na ɩka ba nyɔɔ wʊ tɩchɛ nɩrɛ jaa ba 'la sɩɩr wʊ nɩ wʊ nũu ɛ.

eng: The guards returned to the chief priests and Pharisees, who asked, Why didnt you bring him.
local: A puorfʊ yir gugurbo lɩɛb chen a Farasee nɩ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ sɛ̃, ka ba sʊʊr ba, Ŋmɩnɛ wʊ ɩ ka yaa waan wʊ ɛ.

eng: The guards answered, No one has ever spoken the way he does.
local: Ka a bala ban tʊ̃, ka ba 'yɛr, Nɩrɛ jaa ba 'yɛr 'yɛr a daba nyã kaar ɛ.

eng: The Pharisees replied, Have you too been deceived?.
local: Ka a Faraseemɩne sʊʊr ba, Wʊ mɩ ben bɛl yɩ wɛ?.

eng: Have any of the leaders believed in him? Has any Pharisee?.
local: Yɩ nyɛn a Faraseemɩne nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ ka ba sɔɔ de wʊ?.

eng: No, only this crowd, which doesnt know the Law. And they are under Gods curse.
local: Kai, tɩchɛ a nɩyɔɔ ba nyãna ba bɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn nɛɛ ɛ, ba ben pɔ 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ pɔ gba.

eng: Nicodemus, who was one of them and had come to Jesus earlier, said.
local: Nikodimasɩ na dɔ̃ɔ chen a Yesu sɛ̃ mɩ ɩ naa a ba ãsʊɔ sʊʊr ba.

eng: Our Law doesnt judge someone without first hearing him and learning what he is doing, does it.
local: A nɛɛ ba kʊ sɩ sɔr ɩka sɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ nɩrɛ yele ka saa wõ a lɛ wʊn 'yɛr bɩɩ maala ɛ.

eng: They answered him, You are not from Galilee too, are you? Look it up and you will see that the prophet doesnt come from Galilee.
local: Ka ba sʊʊr wʊ, Fʊ mɩ yin Galilee bɩɩ? Jʊʊr kaa, a la ɩ fʊ nyɛn Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ na yi Galilee.

eng: They each went to their own homes.
local: Ka ba jaa yaar kul a ba yie.

eng: A certain man, Lazarus, was ill. He was from Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha. .
local: Daba ban bʊɔlɔ Lajarusɩ dɔ̃ɔ bɩɛrɛ, wʊ ɩn Mɛɛr nɩ Mata yɛdaba na ba kpɩɛrɛ a Betanɩ tẽe pɔ.

eng: This was the Mary who anointed the Lord with fragrant oil and wiped his feet with her hair. Her brother Lazarus was ill.
local: A Mɛɛr nyã na dɔ̃ɔ kpaar a kãnyũu 'yɔ̃ɔ a Yesu tɩ de a wʊ jukɔɔl fɩɛl, a wʊ yɛdaba Lajarusɩ lɛ a nyã na bɩɛrɛ.

eng: So the sisters sent word to Jesus, saying, Lord, the one whom you love is ill.
local: Ka a yɛpɔbɔ tʊ̃ 'yɛr a Yesu, Sɩ Soro, a fʊ baraa bɩɛrɛ naa.

eng: When he heard this, Jesus said, This illness isnt fatal. Its for the glory of God so that Gods Son can be glorified through it.
local: Yesu na wõ a lɛ, a wʊ 'yɛr, A baal nyã ba ɩ kũu baal ɛ, tɩ an wiil Nãaŋmɩn tɩɩr tɩ mɩ vɛ̃ ka Nãaŋmɩn Bie yuor yi.

eng: Jesus loved Martha, her sister, and Lazarus.
local: Yesu nʊ̃n Mata nɩ a wʊ yɛbɛ nɩ a ba yɛdaba Lajarusɩ.

eng: When he heard that Lazarus was ill, he stayed where he was. After two days.
local: Tɩchɛ wʊn wõ a Lajarusɩ baal tɩ na wʊ chɛnɛ jãnɛ a be wʊn be ber ayi.

eng: he said to his disciples, Lets return to Judea again.
local: Tɩ pãa 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ poturbo, Yɩ ɩ a sɩ lɩɛb chen a Judiya.

eng: The disciples replied, Rabbi, the Jewish opposition wants to stone you, but you want to go back.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a poturbo 'yɛr, Wiwile, daar yɛ̃ yɛ̃ nyãna ba lɛ a Juu nɩbɛrɛ ta bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba lɔb fʊ ɛ, tɩchɛ a fʊ lɛ chiine a be go.

eng: Jesus answered, Arent there twelve hours in the day? Whoever walks in the day doesnt stumble because they see the light of the world.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, A ba ɩ a bɩbir tara na a wʊ mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ chaarfʊ sɔ̃ɔ ɛ? Nɩrɛ wa chiine a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ wʊ maa ŋme gbɛr ɛ, wʊ nyɛ naa a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ chãa.

eng: But whoever walks in the night does stumble because the light isnt in them.
local: Bojũu ala ɩ tãsɔɔ lɛ wʊ chiine, wʊn ŋmen gbɛr, bojũu waa tara chãa ɛ.

eng: He continued, Our friend Lazarus is sleeping, but I am going in order to wake him up.
local: Wʊn wa 'yɛr a lɛ baar, wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, A sɩ baraa Lajarusɩ gur naa, tɩchɛ ɩn chen tɩ sãɩ wʊ.

eng: The disciples said, Lord, if hes sleeping, he will get well.
local: Ka a wʊ poturbo 'yɛr, Sɩ Soro, ala ɩ wʊ gur naa, wʊn ir naa.

eng: They thought Jesus meant that Lazarus was in a deep sleep, but Jesus had spoken about Lazarus death.
local: Yesu 'lɔ 'yɛr naa ɩka wʊ kpi naa, tɩchɛ ka a poturbo 'lɔ tɩɛr ɩka a gʊ̃ɔ sʊ̃ɔ lɛ wʊ 'yɛrɛ.

eng: Jesus told them plainly, Lazarus has died.
local: Ala lɛ so a Yesu pãa 'yɛr ka a chaar ɩka, Lajarusɩ kpi naa.

eng: For your sakes, Im glad I wasnt there so that you can believe. Lets go to him.
local: A yɩ jũu, a nʊ̃mɔ̃ ma naa ɩn ba be be ɛ, ka yɩ na sɔɔ de ma. Tɩchɛ, yɩ ɩ a sɩ chen a wʊ sɛ̃.

eng: Then Thomas said to the other disciples, Let us go too so that we may die with Jesus.
local: A wʊ poturo ãsʊɔ ban bʊɔlɔ Tomasɩ na ba lɛ bʊɔlɔ wʊ Naa 'yɛr kʊ a bala na chɛ, Yɩ ɩ a sɩ 'mataa chiin wʊ mɩ tɩ kpi.

eng: When Jesus arrived, he found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb for four days.
local: Yesu na wa ta a be, wʊ nyɛn ka a ɩ ba ũu naa Lajarusɩ maal ber anaar.

eng: Bethany was a little less than two miles from Jerusalem.
local: Betanɩ nɩ a Jerusalɛm mɩ ba jãa ɛ.

eng: Many Jews had come to comfort Martha and Mary after their brothers death.
local: Ka a Juu nɩbɛ yɔɔ chen ɩka ba tɩ puor a Mata nɩ a Mɛɛr a ba yɛdaba kuor.

eng: When Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she went to meet him, while Mary remained in the house.
local: Mata na wa wõ ɩka Yesu waara naa, wʊ yin tɩ tuor wʊ, tɩchɛ kaa Mɛɛr 'lɔ jãnɛ a yir.

eng: Martha said to Jesus, Lord, if you had been here, my brother wouldnt have died.
local: Be lɛ a Mata 'yɛr kʊ a Yesu, N Soro, fʊ̃ʊ ta dɔ̃ɔ be ka, a n yɛdaba kʊ̃ naa kpi ɛ.

eng: Even now I know that whatever you ask God, God will give you.
local: Tɩchɛ n bɔ̃ɔ naa pʊ̃pãanyã gba ɩka, bon jaa fʊn sʊɔr a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃, wʊn kʊ fʊ naa.

eng: Jesus told her, Your brother will rise again.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, A fʊ yɛdaba na ir naa a kũu pɔ ɩ vʊr.

eng: Martha replied, I know that he will rise in the resurrection on the last day.
local: Be lɛ a Mata 'yɛr kʊ a Yesu, N bɔ̃ɔn na wʊn na ir a kũn irfʊ daar, a ber baaraa sɔ̃ɔ.

eng: Jesus said to her, I am the resurrection and the life. Whoever believes in me will live, even though they die.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Maa lɛ ɩ a kũu pɔ irfʊ, nɩ nyɛvʊr, nɩrɛ 'lɔ na sɔɔ de ma, 'lɔ kpi gba, tɩ wʊn tara na nyɛvʊr.

eng: Everyone who lives and believes in me will never die. Do you believe this.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ba kpi ɛ, tɩ na wʊ sɔɔ de ma, kʊ̃ lɛ kpi ɛ, fʊ sɔɔ naa a lɛ ɔ.

eng: She replied, Yes, Lord, I believe that you are the Christ, Gods Son, the one who is coming into the world.
local: Ka a Mata 'yɛr, Ɔ̃ɔ, n Soro, n sɔɔn de ɩka fʊ̃ʊ lɛ ɩ a Nɩ-iraa na ɩ Nãaŋmɩn Bie, 'lɔn na wa ka a wɛr pɔ.

eng: After she said this, she went and spoke privately to her sister Mary, The teacher is here and hes calling for you.
local: Wʊn wa 'yɛr a lɛ baar wʊ lɩɛb kul tɩ bʊl 'yɛr kʊ a yɛbile Mɛɛr, A wiwile ben ka tɩ sʊrɔ fʊ.

eng: When Mary heard this, she got up quickly and went to Jesus.
local: A Mɛɛr na wa wõ a lɛ, ka wʊ vaa ir chen a Yesu sɛ̃.

eng: He hadnt entered the village but was still in the place where Martha had met him.
local: Yesu ba kpɛ a tẽe pɔ sɛrɛ ɛ, tɩ chɛnɛ ara a be a Mata na tuor wʊ.

eng: When the Jews who were comforting Mary in the house saw her get up quickly and leave, they followed her. They assumed she was going to mourn at the tomb.
local: A Juu nɩbɛ bala na wa a kuor puorfʊ jie be a Mɛɛr sɛ̃ a yir, na nyɛ a lɛ wʊn vaa ir yire, ka ba turo wʊ tɩɛr ɩka a yaa par lɛ wʊ chiine a kõn jie.

eng: When Mary arrived where Jesus was and saw him, she fell at his feet and said, Lord, if you had been here, my brother wouldnt have died.
local: Mɛɛr na tɩ ta a jie nyɛ a Yesu a be, wʊ gbur dumo a Yesu niem tɩ 'yɛr, N Soro, fʊ̃ʊ ta dɔ̃ɔ be ka, a n yɛdaba kʊ̃ naa kpi ɛ.

eng: When Jesus saw her crying and the Jews who had come with her crying also, he was deeply disturbed and troubled.
local: Yesu na nyɛ a lɛ wʊn kõnõ ka a Juu nɩbɛ bala na bɩɛl wʊ mɩ kõnõ, nɩbaal kpɛ wʊ naa ka a suur nyɔɔ wʊ.

eng: He asked, Where have you laid him? They replied, Lord, come and see.
local: A wʊ sʊʊr ba, Nyɩnɛ yɩ ũu wʊ? Ka ba 'yɛr, N Soro, wa nyɛ a jie.

eng: Jesus began to cry.
local: Ka Yesu kõ.

eng: The Jews said, See how much he loved him.
local: Ka a Juu nɩbɛ pãa 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ taa, Nyɛ lɛ wʊn nʊ̃ a Lajarusɩ.

eng: But some of them said, He healed the eyes of the man born blind. Couldnt he have kept Lazarus from dying.
local: Tɩchɛ ka bamɩne pãa 'yɛr, Wʊn dɔ̃ɔ yuo a jɔ̃ɔ nɩbie, kaa ɩ wʊ kʊ̃ naa tʊ̃ɔ vɛ̃ ka a Lajarusɩ ta kpi bɩɩ.

eng: Jesus was deeply disturbed again when he came to the tomb. It was a cave, and a stone covered the entrance.
local: Ka nɩbaal kpɛ a Yesu, ka wʊ tɔɔ ta a yaa par. Kʊsɩr bɔɔ lɛ, ka ba 'mɔɔ a kʊsɩr ãsʊɔ pɔɔ a nɛɛ.

eng: Jesus said, Remove the stone. Martha, the sister of the dead man, said, Lord, the smell will be awful! Hes been dead four days.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr, Yɩ bil a kʊsɩr bar. Tɩchɛ a Mata an ɩ a wʊ yɛdaba lɛ kpi, ka wʊ 'yɛr, N Soro, wʊn nyuro naa, ber anaar nɩ dɩna ban ũu wʊ.

eng: Jesus replied, Didnt I tell you that if you believe, you will see Gods glory.
local: Ka Yesu 'yɛr, N ba 'yɛr fʊ ɩka fʊ̃ʊ sɔɔ de, fʊn nyɛn Nãaŋmɩn tɩɩr ɛ.

eng: So they removed the stone. Jesus looked up and said, Father, thank you for hearing me.
local: Be lɛ ba bil a kʊsɩr bar, a Yesu kaa saaju tɩ 'yɛr, N Sãa, fʊ son puorfʊ, fʊn wõ a n sʊɔrfʊ.

eng: I know you always hear me. I say this for the benefit of the crowd standing here so that they will believe that you sent me.
local: N bɔ̃ɔn naa fʊn wõnõ a n sʊɔrfʊ, tɩchɛ n 'yɛr naa ka a na sʊ̃ʊ a nɩbɛ banyãna na ara a ka, a ba sɔɔ de ɩka, fʊ̃ʊ lɛ tʊ̃ ma.

eng: Having said this, Jesus shouted with a loud voice, Lazarus, come out.
local: Yesu na 'yɛr a nɩtɔ̃ wʊ bʊɔl kpɛ̃ɔ jaa, Lajarusɩ, wa yi.

eng: The dead man came out, his feet bound and his hands tied, and his face covered with a cloth. Jesus said to them, Untie him and let him go.
local: Ka a kũu yi, ka a gbɛɛ naa nuur ban vil a fɔpɩɛl pɔɔ ka a fɔbaa mɩ pɔɔ a ju. A Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ yaar a kũu fɔɔr bar wʊ ka wʊ chen.

eng: Therefore, many of the Jews who came with Mary and saw what Jesus did believed in him.
local: Ala lɛ so ka a Juu nɩbɛ bala na wa ɩka ba wa puor a Mɛɛr, nyɛn a lɛ a Yesu na ɩ, a ba sɔɔ wʊ.

eng: But some of them went to the Pharisees and told them what Jesus had done.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a bamɩne chen a Faraseemɩne sɛ̃ tɩ man a lɛ a Yesu na maal.

eng: Then the chief priests and Pharisees called together the council and said, What are we going to do? This man is doing many miraculous signs!.
local: Be lɛ a Faraseemɩne nɩ a bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ bʊɔl taa lɔ̃ɔfʊ na ba 'yɛr, Ŋmɩnɛ sɩn ɩ, a daba nyã na ben tʊ̃nɔ̃ a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ a nɩtɔ̃?.

eng: If we let him go on like this, everyone will believe in him. Then the Romans will come and take away both our temple and our people.
local: Ala ɩ sɩn be bar wʊ ka wʊ maala lɛ, nɩrɛ jaa na sɔɔn a wʊ yele, a Worom nɩbɛ wa wõ a yele ban wa wɛl sɩ naa a jisʊ̃ nɩ a tẽe bar.

eng: One of them, Caiaphas, who was high priest that year, told them, You dont know anything!.
local: Ka a ba ãsʊɔ ban bʊɔlɔ Kayafasɩ na ɩ a bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ a yuon 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ 'yɛr, Yaa tara tɩɛr jaa ɛ.

eng: You dont see that it is better for you that one man die for the people rather than the whole nation be destroyed.
local: Yaa bɔ̃ɔ ɩka a sɛɛ naa ka nɩbʊ̃'yen na kpi kʊ a nɩyɔɔ gar a nɩyɔɔ ba jaa na wɛ ɛ.

eng: He didnt say this on his own. As high priest that year, he prophesied that Jesus would soon die for the nation.
local: A nyã ba yi a wʊ mãɛ sɛ̃ ka wʊ 'yɛr ɛ, tɩchɛ wʊn ɩ a bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ a yuon 'lɔ, ala lɛ wʊ de nie dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr ɩka a Yesu na kpin kʊ a Juu nɩbʊrɔ ba jaa.

eng: and not only for the nation. Jesus would also die so that Gods children scattered everywhere would be gathered together as one.
local: A ba ɩ bala tɛɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ ka wʊ lɔ̃ɔ taa a Nãaŋmɩn bibiir bala na yɛ̃yɛl na wʊ maal ba a ba ɩ bʊ̃'yen.

eng: From that day on they plotted to kill him.
local: An yi a daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ ba bʊɔrɔ na sɔr na kʊ a Yesu.

eng: Therefore, Jesus was no longer active in public ministry among the Jewish leaders. Instead, he left Jerusalem and went to a place near the wilderness, to a city called Ephraim, where he stayed with his disciples.
local: A lɛ juu Yesu ba lɛ yʊɔrɔ chãa pɔ a Juu nɩbɛ jie ɛ, tɩ chen jiyuo na gbʊr a mɔgɔ̃ɔ na wʊ tɩ kpɩɛrɛ a tẽbile ban bʊɔlɔ Eferɛm, be lɛ wʊ tɩ 'mataa jɛ̃n a wʊ poturbo.

eng: It was almost time for the Jewish Passover, and many people went from the countryside up to Jerusalem to purify themselves through ritual washing before the Passover.
local: A Juu nɩbɛ Gõl Bar Difʊ gbʊr naa, ka ba yɔɔ yi a tẽbil do a Jerusalɛm tɩ pɔlɔ a ba mãɛ chɛlɛ nɩ a bɩbir.

eng: They were looking for Jesus. As they spoke to each other in the temple, they said, What do you think? He wont come to the festival, will he.
local: Ba kaara na bʊɔrɔ a Yesu a be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir, sʊrɔ taa, Ŋmɩnɛ yɩ tɩɛr, wʊn wa naa a lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie bɩɩ.

eng: The chief priests and Pharisees had given orders that anyone who knew where he was should report it, so they could arrest him.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Faraseemɩne 'yɛr, Nɩrɛ jaa wa bɔ̃ɔ a jie a Yesu na be, wʊ 'yɛr ka ba tɩ nyɔɔ wʊ.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt enter into the sheep pen through the gate but climbs over the wall is a thief and an outlaw.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na maa tu a piir jɔɔ dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ tɩ do a dachin kpɛ, ɩn nãnyie nɩ nɩfara.

eng: The one who enters through the gate is the shepherd of the sheep.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na tu a dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ kpɛ 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a pɛchinɛ.

eng: The guard at the gate opens the gate for him, and the sheep listen to his voice. He calls his own sheep by name and leads them out.
local: A dɔ̃dɔ̃guro ma yuon a pãn kʊ a pɛchinɛ, ka a piir chɛlɛ a wʊ kɔkɔr. Wʊ maa bʊɔl naa a wʊ piir yoe, na wʊ ŋmaa ba yin.

eng: Whenever he has gathered all of his sheep, he goes before them and they follow him, because they know his voice.
local: 'Lɔ wa ŋmaa a wʊ piir a jaa ka a yi, wʊ ma den nie ka a turo wʊ, bojũu ba bɔ̃ɔ naa a wʊ kɔkɔr.

eng: They wont follow a stranger but will run away because they dont know the strangers voice.
local: Tɩchɛ ba kʊ̃ wa tu sãan jaa ɛ, ban jon yi a wʊ sɛ̃ bojũu baa bɔ̃ɔ sãan kɔkɔr ɛ.

eng: Those who heard Jesus use this analogy didnt understand what he was saying.
local: Yesu lɔb a sʊkpa nyã tɩchɛ baa bɔ̃ɔ a par a lɛ wʊn 'yɛrɛ ba ɛ.

eng: So Jesus spoke again, I assure you that I am the gate of the sheep.
local: A lɛ jũu lɛ a Yesu 'yɛr ba go, Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, maa lɛ a piir dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ.

eng: All who came before me were thieves and outlaws, but the sheep didnt listen to them.
local: A bala ba jaa na dɔ̃ɔ ma nie wa ɩn nãnyisɩ nɩ nɩfarba, tɩchɛ a piir ba chɛlɛ ba ɛ.

eng: I am the gate. Whoever enters through me will be saved. They will come in and go out and find pasture.
local: Maa lɛ a dɔ̃dɔr, nɩrɛ jaa na tu ma kpɛ, wʊn nyɛn faafʊ. Wʊn ma kpɛ̃n tɩ yi na wʊ nyɛrɛ bʊ̃diir.

eng: The thief enters only to steal, kill, and destroy. I came so that they could have life—indeed, so that they could live life to the fullest.
local: Nãnyie ma wa naa ɩka wʊ wa ju, na wʊ kʊ, tɩ sɔ̃ɔ bar. Maa wa naa ka ban nyɛ nyɛvʊr, na ba nyɛ wʊ tɩ põn bar.

eng: I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.
local: Maa lɛ a pɛchiin sʊ̃, a pɛchiin sʊ̃ sɔɔn kpi a wʊ piir 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: When the hired hand sees the wolf coming, he leaves the sheep and runs away. Thats because he isnt the shepherd; the sheep arent really his. So the wolf attacks the sheep and scatters them.
local: A pɛchinɛ 'lɔ ban yara, waa so a piir ɛ. 'Lɔ wa nyɛ gbõgboro na waara, wʊ ma tɛɛn a piir bar tɩ jo, ka a gbõgboro yɛ̃yɛl a piir tɩ nyɔɔr amɩne.

eng: Hes only a hired hand and the sheep dont matter to him.
local: Wʊ ma jon a lɛ an ɩ libie tʊ̃mɔ̃ lɛ wʊ tʊ̃nɔ̃, a piir yele ba bã wʊ ɛ.

eng: I am the good shepherd. I know my own sheep and they know me.
local: Maa ɩ naa a pɛchiin sʊ̃, n bɔ̃ɔ naa a n piir, ka n piir mɩ bɔ̃ɔ ma.

eng: just as the Father knows me and I know the Father. I give up my life for the sheep.
local: Nɩtãa lɛ a Sãa na bɔ̃ɔ ma ka n mɩ bɔ̃ɔ a Sãa, ɩn sɔɔn kpi a piir 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: I have other sheep that dont belong to this sheep pen. I must lead them too. They will listen to my voice and there will be one flock, with one shepherd.
local: N tara na piir amɩne na ba be a jɔɔ nyã pɔ ɛ, ɩn waan ba naa, ka ba mɩ wa chɛlɛ a n kɔkɔr, ka ba ɩ pɛgbul bʊ̃'yen na ba tara pɛchiin bʊ̃'yen.

eng: This is why the Father loves me: I give up my life so that I can take it up again.
local: A n Sãa na nʊ̃ ma par nɩ nyã, n sɔɔ naa na kpi tɩ lɛ ir ɩ vʊr go.

eng: No one takes it from me, but I give it up because I want to. I have the right to give it up, and I have the right to take it up again. I received this commandment from my Father.
local: Nɩrɛ baa faara wʊ ɛ, tɩchɛ maa mãɛ lɛ sɔɔ na kpi. N tara na kpɛ̃ɔ na bar a nyɛvʊr naa lɛ tara kpɛ̃ɔ na de wʊ go. A nyã lɛ a n Sãa 'yɛr ma ɩka n ɩ.

eng: There was another division among the Jews because of Jesus words.
local: A 'yɛr nyã lɛ vɛ̃ ka a Juu nɩbɛ ŋma dẽ dẽ.

eng: Many of them said, He has a demon and has lost his mind. Why listen to him.
local: Ka a ba yɔɔ 'yɛrɛ ɩka, Sɩdɛɛr lɛ wʊ tara, na wʊ gele, bo 'yɔ̃ɔ yɩ chɛlɛ a lɛ wʊn 'yɛrɛ.

eng: Others said, These arent the words of someone who has a demon. Can a demon heal the eyes of people who are blind.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a bamɩne 'yɛrɛ, A nyãna ba ɩ nɩrɛ na tara sɩdɛɛr 'yɛr ɛ, a sɩdɛɛr na tʊ̃ɔn yuo jɔ̃ɔ nɩbie ɔ.

eng: The time came for the Festival of Dedication in Jerusalem. It was winter.
local: A ɩ naa uon ka ba maala a ba puorfʊ yir Pɔlfʊ lɔ̃ɔ di be a Jerusalɛm.

eng: and Jesus was in the temple, walking in the covered porch named for Solomon.
local: Ka Yesu be be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir bɛchaar ãsʊɔ pɔ, na wʊ yʊɔrɔ a be ban bʊɔlɔ a Solomon Jɛ̃bɛr.

eng: The Jewish opposition circled around him and asked, How long will you test our patience? If you are the Christ, tell us plainly.
local: Be a Juu nɩbɛrɛ bamɩne ŋmaa jilʊ̃ wʊ sʊrɔ, A baalfʊ nyã fʊn 'yɔ̃ɔ sɩ, dabor an wa baar? Ala ɩ fʊ̃ʊ lɛ ɩ a Nɩ-iraa 'lɔ, 'yɛr kʊ sɩ chãa pɔ.

eng: Jesus answered, I have told you, but you dont believe. The works I do in my Fathers name testify about me.
local: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, N dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr yɩ, yɩn lɛ ba sɔɔ de ɛ. A nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ ɩn tʊ̃ nɩ a n Sãa yuor, 'yɛr naa n 'yɛr.

eng: but you dont believe because you dont belong to my sheep.
local: Tɩchɛ yɩn lɛ ba sɔɔ de ɛ, bojũu yaa ɩ a n piir ɛ.

eng: My sheep listen to my voice. I know them and they follow me.
local: A n piir ma chɛlɛ naa a n kɔkɔr, n bɔ̃ɔ ba naa ka ba ma turo ma.

eng: I give them eternal life. They will never die, and no one will snatch them from my hand.
local: N kʊ ban nyɛvʊr na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ. Ba kʊ̃ wɛ ɛ, nɩrɛ jaa kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ ba 'loo faa a n nũu pɔ ɛ.

eng: My Father, who has given them to me, is greater than all, and no one is able to snatch them from my Fathers hand.
local: A n Sãa 'lɔ na de ba kʊ ma, wʊ gar bomo a jaa. Nɩrɛ jaa kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ ba 'loo faa a n Sãa nũu pɔ ɛ.

eng: I and the Father are one.
local: Maa naa n Sãa jaa ɩn bʊ̃'yen.

eng: Again the Jewish opposition picked up stones in order to stone him.
local: Ka a Juu nɩbɛrɛ wob kʊsɩbɛ ɩka ba lɔb wʊ go.

eng: Jesus responded, I have shown you many good works from the Father. For which of those works do you stone me.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Yesu sʊʊr ba, N tʊ̃n nɛ'maa tʊ̃yɔɔ yi a Sãa sɛ̃, buor sʊɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ yɩ pãa be lɔɔrɔ ma.

eng: The Jewish opposition answered, We dont stone you for a good work but for insulting God. You are human, yet you make yourself out to be God.
local: Ka ba 'yɛr, Saa lɔɔrɔ fʊ a anyã ãsʊɔ jaa 'yɔ̃ɔ ɛ, tɩ fʊn fere sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a Nãaŋmɩn yuor, tɩ mɩ ɩ nɩsaal.

eng: Jesus replied, Isnt it written in your Law, I have said, you are gods?.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr ba, A ba sɛb a yɩ Nɛɛ pɔ ɩka, N 'yɛr naa ɩka, yɩ ɩn ŋmɩmɛ ɛ.

eng: Scripture calls those to whom Gods word came gods , and scripture cant be abolished.
local: Ala ɩ Nãaŋmɩn bʊɔl naa a bala a wʊ 'yɛr, na wa a ba sɛ̃, ŋmɩmɛ, Nãaŋmɩn sɛɛr ba be na wa sɔ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: So how can you say that the one whom the Father has made holy and sent into the world insults God because he said, I am Gods Son?.
local: Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ a 'lɔ a Sãa na kaa ir a wʊ bɔfʊ pɔ, na wʊ tʊ̃ ka a wɛr pɔ. Bo lɛ so ka yaa bara ma ɛ, bojũu n 'yɛr ɩka Nãaŋmɩn Bie lɛ n ɩ?.

eng: If I dont do the works of my Father, dont believe me.
local: Yɩ taa sɔɔ de ma ɛ, ala ɩ n ba maala a lɛ a Sãa na maala ɛ.

eng: But if I do them, and you dont believe me, believe the works so that you can know and recognize that the Father is in me and I am in the Father.
local: Tɩchɛ ala ɩ n tʊ̃ naa, yɩn gba ba sɔɔ de ma ɛ, yɩ sɔɔ de a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃, yɩn bɔ̃ɔn a par ɩka a Sãa ben a n 'yɔ̃ɔ ka n mɩ be a Sãa 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: Again, they wanted to arrest him, but he escaped from them.
local: Ka ba lɛ bʊɔrɔ a wʊ nyɔɔfʊ go, tɩ chɛ ka wʊ fʊɔl yi.

eng: Jesus went back across the Jordan to the place where John had baptized at first, and he stayed there.
local: Be lɛ a Yesu lɩɛb gɔ̃ɔ a Jordan man gɔ̃ɔn, a jie 'lɔ a Jɔɔn na dɔ̃ɔ suoro a nɩbɛ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ na wʊ jɛ̃ a be.

eng: Many people came to him. John didnt do any miraculous signs, they said, but everything John said about this man was true.
local: ka nɩyɔɔ wa a wʊ sɛ̃ wa 'yɛrɛ, Jɔɔn gba ba dɔ̃ɔ tʊ̃ nɛ 'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ ɛ, a ala a jaa wʊn 'yɛr a daba nyã 'yɛr, ɩn yelmãɛ.

eng: Many believed in Jesus there.
local: Be lɛ a nɩyɔɔ sɔɔ de wʊ.

eng: Six days before Passover, Jesus came to Bethany, home of Lazarus, whom Jesus had raised from the dead.
local: A chɛn ber ayʊɔb ka a Gõl Bar Difʊ bɩbir ta. Yesu chen a Betanɩ na ɩ a Lajarusɩ 'lɔ na kpi ka wʊ sãɩ kpɩɛrɛ.

eng: Lazarus and his sisters hosted a dinner for him. Martha served and Lazarus was among those who joined him at the table.
local: Ba maal bʊ̃diir kʊ wʊ a be, ka a Mata põnõ a bʊ̃diir. Lajarusɩ pʊɔ naa a nɩbɛ bala na lɔ̃ɔ jɛ̃n Yesu a bʊ̃diir jie.

eng: Then Mary took an extraordinary amount, almost three-quarters of a pound, of very expensive perfume made of pure nard. She anointed Jesus feet with it, then wiped his feet dry with her hair. The house was filled with the aroma of the perfume.
local: Be lɛ Mɛɛr de kãnyũu vɩla daa na ɩ tuo wa pʊr 'yɔ̃ɔ a Yesu gbɛɛ pɔ, tɩ lɩɛb a jukɔɔl fɩɛlɛ nɩ ka a nyũu de a yir wʊ jaa.

eng: Judas Iscariot, one of his disciples , complained.
local: Tɩchɛ a wʊ poturo ãsʊɔ ban bʊɔlɔ Judas na yi a Isɩkariyot, 'lɔ lɛ na wa gar 'yɔ̃ɔ a Yesu sʊʊr.

eng: This perfume was worth a years wages! Why wasnt it sold and the money given to the poor? .
local: Bo lɛ so ka baa da a kãnyũu nyãna ɛ, a libie na tãn nɩrɛ yuon gbul sãyar na ba de kʊ nɔ̃n dẽme ɛ.

eng: He said this not because he cared about the poor but because he was a thief. He carried the money bag and would take what was in it.
local: A nɔ̃n dẽme yele ba bã wʊ ɛ, bojũu nãnyie lɛ wʊ ɩ an ɩ 'lɔ lɛ tara a ba libie wʊɔ, na wʊ ma juro a ala ban 'yɔ̃ɔ a be sʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ wʊ mãɛ.

eng: Then Jesus said, Leave her alone. This perfume was to be used in preparation for my burial, and this is how she has used it.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr, Bar a pɔɔ jaa jomm, wʊ bin a kãnyũu nyãna gun a n ũufʊ bɩbir.

eng: You will always have the poor among you, but you wont always have me.
local: A nɔ̃n dẽme na ben a yɩ sɛ̃ bɩbir jaa, tɩchɛ maa kʊ̃ be a yɩ sɛ̃ bɩbir jaa ɛ.

eng: Many Jews learned that he was there. They came not only because of Jesus but also to see Lazarus, whom he had raised from the dead.
local: A Juu yɔɔ jie na wõ ɩka Yesu be naa a Betanɩ, ba wa naa a ba ɩ a 'lɔ tɛɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ ba bʊɔrɔ na ɩka ba nyɛ a Lajarusɩ 'lɔ wʊn sãɩ a kũu pɔ, ka wʊ ir ɩ vʊr.

eng: The chief priests decided that they would kill Lazarus too.
local: A lɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ, a bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ mɩ guoro nɩ taa ɩka ba mɩn kʊ naa a Lajarusɩ.

eng: It was because of Lazarus that many of the Jews had deserted them and come to believe in Jesus.
local: A wʊ jũu lɛ a Juu nɩbɛ yɔɔ jie tɔr a ba nɩbɛrɛ wiilfʊ tɩ sɔɔrɔ a Yesu.

eng: The next day the great crowd that had come for the festival heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem.
local: Wɛr na wa chaar, a nɩyɔɔ bala na wa a Gõl Bar Difʊ jie, wõ naa ɩka Yesu ben a sɔr pɔ waara a Jerusalɛm.

eng: They took palm branches and went out to meet him. They shouted, Hosanna! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord! Blessings on the king of Israel.
local: Ba 'mɛl bɛ vaɩ yi tuoro nɩ wʊ pãa chɩlɛ gɔ̃nɔ̃, Yãan yooo, Nãaŋmɩn wʊ sʊ̃ʊ a 'lɔ wʊn tʊ̃. Nãaŋmɩn wʊ sʊ̃ʊ a 'lɔ na ɩ a Isɩral nãa.

eng: Jesus found a young donkey and sat on it, just as it is written.
local: Yesu nyɛn a bʊ̃pɔl na wʊ do jãnɛ nɩtãa lɛ an sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ.

eng: Dont be afraid, Daughter Zion. Look! Your king is coming , sitting on a donkeys colt .
local: A yɩn Jerusalɛm nɩbɛ, yɩ ta joro dãbãɛ ɛ, Yɩ nyɛ a yɩ nãa na do bʊ̃pɔl waara nɩ.

eng: His disciples didnt understand these things at first. After he was glorified, they remembered that these things had been written about him and that they had done these things to him.
local: A wʊ poturbo ba dɔ̃ɔ bɔ̃ɔ a anyãna a jaa par ɛ, tɩ a Yesu na wa ir ɩ vʊr, ba tɩɛr yuo ɩka anyãna lɛ ba dɔ̃ɔ sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ a wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ ka ba mɩ maal a lɛ kʊ wʊ.

eng: The crowd who had been with him when he called Lazarus out of the tomb and raised him from the dead were testifying about him.
local: A nɩyɔɔ bala na dɔ̃ɔ be a wʊ sɛ̃ na ba nyɛ a lɛ wʊn bʊɔl a Lajarusɩ ka wʊ yi a yaa pɔ ɩ vʊr, ba chɛnɛ na mana kʊrɔ a nɩbɛ.

eng: Thats why the crowd came to meet him, because they had heard about this miraculous sign that he had done.
local: Ala lɛ so ka a nɩyɔɔ yi tuoro wʊ, bojũu ba wõ naa ɩka wʊ tʊ̃n nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃.

eng: Therefore, the Pharisees said to each other, See! Youve accomplished nothing! Look! The whole world is following him.
local: Be lɛ ka a Faraseemɩne pãa 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ taa, Yɩ nyɛ, saa lɛ tʊɔrɔ bon jaa maala ɛ. Nyɛ, a nɩbɛ ba jaa ben turo wʊ.

eng: Some Greeks were among those who had come up to worship at the festival.
local: A nɩbɛ bamɩne mɩ na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, pʊɔ naa bala na do a puorfʊ jie a Jerusalɛm a Gõl Bar Difʊ sɔ̃ɔ.

eng: They came to Philip, who was from Bethsaida in Galilee, and made a request: Sir, we want to see Jesus.
local: Ba chen a Filip na yi a Betɩsaida na be a Galilee wɛr pɔ sɛ̃ tɩ 'yɛr, Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, sɩ bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka sɩ nyɛ a Yesu.

eng: Philip told Andrew, and Andrew and Philip told Jesus.
local: Filip chen tɩ 'yɛr kʊ Andiru ka ba 'mataa chen tɩ 'yɛr a Yesu.

eng: Jesus replied, The time has come for the Human One to be glorified.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr ba, A sɔ̃ɔ ta naa ka Nãaŋmɩn na kʊ a Nɩsaal Bie tɩɩr.

eng: I assure you that unless a grain of wheat falls into the earth and dies, it can only be a single seed. But if it dies, it bears much fruit.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, chibir baa ũu tẽe ɛ, wʊn chɛnɛ na ɩ a chibir bʊ̃'yen. Tɩchɛ 'lɔ wa lo tẽe kpi, wʊ ma dɔɔn chibiyɔɔ.

eng: Those who love their lives will lose them, and those who hate their lives in this world will keep them forever.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na nʊ̃ a wʊ nyɛvʊr na bɔr wʊ naa, tɩchɛ 'lɔ na 'laar a wʊ nyɛvʊr a wɛr nyã pɔ, wʊn tara na a nyɛvʊr 'lɔ na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ.

eng: Whoever serves me must follow me. Wherever I am, there my servant will also be. My Father will honor whoever serves me.
local: Ala ɩ ãsʊɔ jaa bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka wʊ sʊ̃ʊ ma tʊ̃, wʊ turo ma, jie jaa ɩn be a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na sʊ̃nɔ̃ ma tʊ̃nɔ̃ mɩ na ben be. Ka a n Sãa 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a 'lɔ na sʊ̃nɔ̃ ma tʊ̃nɔ̃.

eng: Now I am deeply troubled. What should I say? Father, save me from this time? No, for this is the reason I have come to this time.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã, n pʊɔ sɔ̃ɔ naa, tɩ n 'yɛr bo? N 'yɛr ɩka a Sãa wʊ faa ma a sɔ̃ɔ nyãna pɔ bɩɩ? Kai, a sɔ̃ɔ nyãna 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ n wa.

eng: Father, glorify your name! Then a voice came from heaven, I have glorified it, and I will glorify it again.
local: Sãa, kʊ a fʊ yuor tɩɩr. Be lɛ a kɔkɔr pãa 'yɛr yi saaju, N dɔ̃ɔ kʊ wʊ a tɩɩr, ɩn lɛ kʊ wʊ naa a tɩɩr go.

eng: The crowd standing there heard and said, Its thunder. Others said, An angel spoke to him.
local: Ka a nɩyɔɔ bala na ara a be wõ naa na ba 'yɛr, saa lɛ tãna, ka bamɩne mɩ 'yɛr malaka ãsʊɔ lɛ 'yɛrɛ nɩ wʊ.

eng: Jesus replied, This voice wasnt for my benefit but for yours.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr ba, A ba ɩ maa 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ a kɔkɔr 'yɛr ɛ, tɩ yɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ.

eng: Now is the time for judgment of this world. Now this worlds ruler will be thrown out.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã a sɔ̃ɔ ta naa ka a wɛr nyã 'yɛr na tɔɔr. Pʊ̃pãanyã, ban ir naa wɛr nyã nãa Satan bar.

eng: When I am lifted up from the earth, I will draw everyone to me. .
local: Tɩchɛ maa, bala wa 'mɔɔ ma a saaju, ɩn tɔɔn nɩbɛ ba jaa waan a n sɛ̃.

eng: He said this to show how he was going to die.
local: Wʊ 'yɛr a lɛ na wʊ wiil kũu 'lɔ tɔ wʊn na kpi.

eng: The crowd responded, We have heard from the Law that the Christ remains forever. How can you say that the Human One must be lifted up? Who is this Human One.
local: Ka a nɩyɔɔ bala 'yɛr, Sɩ wõn yi a Nɛɛ pɔ ɩka, a Nɩ-iraa naa 'lɔ na ben be kpo kpo, tɩ ŋmɩnɛ a ɩ a fʊ 'yɛr ɩka ban 'mɔɔn a Nɩsaal Bie a saaju? An lɛ a Nɩsaal Bie 'lɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, The light is with you for only a little while. Walk while you have the light so that darkness doesnt overtake you. Those who walk in the darkness dont know where they are going.
local: A Yesu pãa 'yɛr ba, Ber bãlãa lɛ a chãa na be a yɩ sɛ̃, yɩ chiine a chãa pɔ wʊn chɛnɛ be be ka a libe taa pɔɔ yɩ ɛ. Nɩrɛ na be libe pɔ ba bɔ̃ɔ a jie wʊn chiine ɛ.

eng: As long as you have the light, believe in the light so that you might become people whose lives are determined by the light. After Jesus said these things, he went away and hid from them.
local: Yɩ sɔɔ de a chãa, yɩn chɛnɛ tara wʊ na yɩ tʊ̃ɔ ɩ a chãa bibiir. Yesu na wa 'yɛr a lɛ baar, wʊ yin chen a baa nyɛ wʊ ɛ.

eng: Jesus had done many miraculous signs before the people, but they didnt believe in him.
local: Yesu gba, wʊn tʊ̃ a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ a ba sɛ̃, tɩ baa chɛnɛ sɔɔ de wʊ ɛ.

eng: This was to fulfill the word of the prophet Isaiah: Lord, who has believed through our message? To whom is the arm of the Lord fully revealed?.
local: Ka a Isaiya 'yɛr ɩ yelmãɛ. Lɛ wʊn 'yɛr a kõr jaa, Sɩ Soro an lɛ sɔɔ de a sɩ 'yɛr, an lɛ a Soro mɩ wiil a wʊ kpɛ̃ɔ.

eng: Isaiah explains why they couldnt believe.
local: Ban ba tʊɔrɔ sɔɔrɔ ɛ, bojũu a Isaiya 'yɛr jie ãsʊɔ.

eng: He made their eyes blind and closed their minds so that they might not see with their eyes, understand with their minds, and turn their lives around— and I would heal them.
local: Wʊ jɔ̃ɔn a ba nɩbie bar. Na wʊ pɔɔ a ba tɩɛr bar, baa lɛ nyɛrɛ ɛ. Ba kʊ̃ mɩ lɛ bɔ̃ɔ a par a ba tɩɛr pɔ ɛ, na ba lɩɛb wa a n sɛ̃ ka n sãa ba ɛ.

eng: Isaiah said these things because he saw Jesus glory; he spoke about Jesus.
local: Isaiya 'yɛr naa nɩtɔ̃, bojũu wʊ dɔ̃ɔ nyɛ a Yesu tɩɩr na wʊ 'yɛrɛ a wʊ 'yɛr.

eng: Even so, many leaders believed in him, but they wouldnt acknowledge their faith because they feared that the Pharisees would expel them from the synagogue.
local: An sɔɔ be a lɛ tɩ nɩbɛrɛ yɔɔ bamɩne sɔɔn de wʊ. Tɩchɛ a Faraseemɩne dãbãɛ jũu baa 'lara 'yɛrɛ ɛ, ka ba taa dii ba bar a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie ɛ.

eng: They believed, but they loved human praise more than Gods glory.
local: Bojũu ba nʊ̃n nɩrɛ pɛɛfʊ gar Nãaŋmɩn pɛɛfʊ.

eng: Jesus shouted, Whoever believes in me doesnt believe in me but in the one who sent me.
local: Be lɛ a Yesu 'yɛr kpɛ̃ɔ jaa, Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na sɔɔ de ma a ba ɩ maa tɛɛ lɛ wʊ sɔɔ de ɛ, tɩchɛ wʊ sɔɔn de a 'lɔ na tʊ̃ ma.

eng: Whoever sees me sees the one who sent me.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na nyɛ ma mɩ nyɛn a 'lɔ na tʊ̃ ma.

eng: I have come as a light into the world so that everyone who believes in me wont live in darkness.
local: N wan a wɛr pɔ nɩtãa chãa, ka nɩrɛ 'lɔ na sɔɔ de ma kʊ̃ lɛ be libe pɔ ɛ.

eng: If people hear my words and dont keep them, I dont judge them. I didnt come to judge the world but to save it.
local: A nɩrɛ 'lɔ na wõ a n 'yɛr, tɩ na waa tu ɛ, n kʊ̃ tɔɔr a wʊ 'yɛr ɛ. N ba wa ɩka n wa tɔɔr a wɛr 'yɛr ɛ, tɩ n wa faa wʊ.

eng: Whoever rejects me and doesnt receive my words will be judged at the last day by the word I have spoken.
local: A 'yɛr tɔɔrɔ ben be, nɩrɛ 'lɔ na tɔr ma na waa de a n 'yɛr bie ɛ, a 'yɛr 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ nɩ wʊ ju ɩn 'yɛr lɛ na 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ a 'yɛr a ber baaraa daar.

eng: I dont speak on my own, but the Father who sent me commanded me regarding what I should speak and say.
local: N ba 'yɛrɛ n mãɛ 'yɛr ɛ, tɩchɛ n 'yɛr naa a lɛ a Sãa na tʊ̃ ma 'yɛr ma ɩka n 'yɛr.

eng: I know that his commandment is eternal life. Therefore, whatever I say is just as the Father has said to me.
local: N bɔ̃ɔ naa ɩka a wʊ 'yɛr kʊrɔ na nyɛvʊr na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ. Ala lɛ so ka n 'yɛr yɩ a lɛ nɛ a Sãa na 'yɛr.

eng: Before the Festival of Passover, Jesus knew that his time had come to leave this world and go to the Father. Having loved his own who were in the world, he loved them fully.
local: A kʊ̃ lɛ kor ɛ, tɩ a Gõl Bar Difʊ ta, Yesu bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka a wʊ bɩbir vɩɛ naa ka wʊn yi ka a wɛr nyã pɔ lɩɛb chen a Sãa sɛ̃. Wʊ nʊ̃ naa bala na ɩ a wʊ tʊɔr a wɛr nyã pɔ, wʊ nʊ̃ ban tɩ tãn a wʊ kũu.

eng: Jesus and his disciples were sharing the evening meal. The devil had already provoked Judas, Simon Iscariots son, to betray Jesus.
local: Ba põn a jãanʊɔr bʊ̃diir, ka a ɩ tɩɩ de nie kpɛ Judas na ɩ Simon na yi Isɩkariyot bidaba ka wʊ tɩ gar 'yɔ̃ɔ a Yesu.

eng: Jesus knew the Father had given everything into his hands and that he had come from God and was returning to God.
local: Yesu bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka a Sãa den bomo a jaa 'yɔ̃ɔ a wʊ nũu pɔ, an ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ lɛ wʊ yi na wʊ lɛ lɩɛb chiine a wʊ sɛ̃.

eng: So he got up from the table and took off his robes. Picking up a linen towel, he tied it around his waist.
local: Be lɛ wʊ bar a bʊ̃diir tɩ ir na wʊ yaa a fuu bar tɩ de fɔfɩɛlaa sɛ a sɩɛ pɔ.

eng: Then he poured water into a washbasin and began to wash the disciples feet, drying them with the towel he was wearing.
local: Na wʊ pʊr kʊ̃ɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ la'yɛlma pɔ piel pɛɛrɛ a wʊ poturbo gbɛɛ tɩ ma fɩɛl a fɔfɩɛlaa 'lɔ wʊn sɛ a sɩɛ pɔ bar.

eng: When Jesus came to Simon Peter, Peter said to him, Lord, are you going to wash my feet.
local: Wʊn wa ta a Simon Pita ka wʊ 'yɛr, N Soro, fʊ̃ʊ lɛ na pɛɛ a maa gbɛɛ ɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, You dont understand what Im doing now, but you will understand later.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr wʊ, Faa bɔ̃ɔ a lɛ ɩn ɩrɛ a pʊ̃pãanyã ɛ, tɩchɛ fʊn wa bɔ̃ɔn daar kɔ̃ɔ.

eng: No! Peter said. You will never wash my feet! Jesus replied, Unless I wash you, you wont have a place with me.
local: Ka a Pita 'yɛr, Kai, faa be na wa pɛɛ a n gbɛɛ ɛ. A Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Maa ba pɛɛ a fʊ gbɛɛ ɛ, saa lɛ be taa ɛ.

eng: Simon Peter said, Lord, not only my feet but also my hands and my head.
local: Ka Simon Pita pãa 'yɛr, N Soro ala be lɛ, a ta ɩ a gbɛɛ tɛɛ ɛ, pɛɛ a nuur nɩ a ju mɩ.

eng: Jesus responded, Those who have bathed need only to have their feet washed, because they are completely clean. You disciples are clean, but not every one of you.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr, Nɩrɛ na so kʊ̃ɔ baar gbɛɛ tɛɛ lɛ wʊ ma wa pɛɛ, a wʊ ãgãn wʊ jaa yi naa. A yɩn mɩ yi naa an ba ɩ yɩ jaa lɛ ɛ.

eng: He knew who would betray him. Thats why he said, Not every one of you is clean.
local: Yesu bɔ̃ɔn a lɔn na gar 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ, ala lɛ so ka wʊ 'yɛr ɩka a ba ɩ ba jaa lɛ yi ɛ.

eng: After he washed the disciples feet, he put on his robes and returned to his place at the table. He said to them, Do you know what Ive done for you?.
local: Wʊn wa pɛɛ a ba jaa gbɛɛ baar, wʊ lɛ den a fuu su tɩ jãnɛ nɩ a wʊ jie, tɩ sʊʊr ba, Yɩ bɔ̃ɔn a nɩtɔ̃ ɩn ɩ par ɔ?.

eng: You call me Teacher and Lord, and you speak correctly, because I am.
local: Yɩ ma bʊɔl ma naa Wiwile bɩɩ Soro, yelmãɛ yɩ 'yɛr an ɩ a lɛ n ɩ.

eng: If I, your Lord and teacher, have washed your feet, you too must wash each others feet.
local: Ala ɩ maa na ɩ a yɩ Soro nɩ a yɩ Wiwile pɛɛn a yɩ gbɛɛ, yɩ mɩ pɛɛrɛ taa gbɛɛ.

eng: I have given you an example: Just as I have done, you also must do.
local: N maal wiil yɩ ɩka yɩ maala a lɛ ɩn ɩ kʊ yɩ.

eng: I assure you, servants arent greater than their master, nor are those who are sent greater than the one who sent them.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, gbãgbaa ba ɩ kpɛ̃ɛ gar a wʊ soro ɛ. Tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ mɩ ba ɩ kpɛ̃ɛ gar a 'lɔ na tʊ̃ wʊ ɛ.

eng: Since you know these things, you will be happy if you do them.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã yɩn bɔ̃ɔ a anyãna, an ɩn sʊ̃ʊfʊ ala ɩ yɩ ɩrɛ yaa naa.

eng: Im not speaking about all of you. I know those whom Ive chosen. But this is to fulfill the scripture, The one who eats my bread has turned against me.
local: Yɩ jaa ba lɛ n 'yɛrɛ ɛ, n bɔ̃ɔ naa a bala ɩn ir. Tɩchɛ lɛ an sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ na ɩ naa a lɛ an sɛb, A 'lɔ ɩn lɔ̃ɔ dire nɩ na lɩɛb ɩ ma bier.

eng: Im telling you this now, before it happens, so that when it does happen you will believe that I Am.
local: N 'yɛr yɩn a pʊ̃pãanyã tɩchɛ ka a ba ta sɛrɛ ɛ, ala wa ta ka yɩ sɔɔ de ma ɩka Maa lɛ.

eng: I assure you that whoever receives someone I send receives me, and whoever receives me receives the one who sent me.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, nɩrɛ 'lɔ na sɔɔ de a 'lɔ ɩn tʊ̃, maa lɛ wʊ sɔɔ de. Nɩrɛ 'lɔ mɩ na sɔɔ de ma mɩ sɔɔn de a 'lɔ na tʊ̃ ma.

eng: After he said these things, Jesus was deeply disturbed and testified, I assure you, one of you will betray me.
local: Yesu na 'yɛr a nɩtɔ̃ baar a wʊ pʊɔ sɔ̃ɔ naa ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, a yɩ ãsʊɔ na tɩ gar 'yɔ̃ɔ ma.

eng: His disciples looked at each other, confused about which of them he was talking about.
local: Ka a poturbo kaara taa bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba bɔ̃ɔ a buor sɔɔ wʊn 'yɛrɛ.

eng: One of the disciples, the one whom Jesus loved, was at Jesus side.
local: A Yesu poturo ãsʊɔ wʊn nʊ̃ na jɛ̃ gbɛb wʊ.

eng: Simon Peter nodded at him to get him to ask Jesus who he was talking about.
local: 'lɔ lɛ a Simon Pita 'lɩɩr 'yɛr, Sʊʊr wʊ kaa, an lɛ wʊ 'yɛrɛ.

eng: Leaning back toward Jesus, this disciple asked, Lord, who is it.
local: Ka a poturo 'lɔ dɩɛl a Yesu tɩ sʊʊr wʊ, N Soro, an lɛ fʊ 'yɛrɛ.

eng: Jesus answered, Its the one to whom I will give this piece of bread once I have dipped into the bowl.Then he dipped the piece of bread and gave it to Judas, Simon Iscariots son.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr, A 'lɔ ɩn na ŋmaa a paanʊ̃ɔ suun a jɩɛr kʊ, 'lɔ lɛ. Wʊ ŋmaa naa a paanʊ̃ɔ suun a jɩɛr de kʊ a Judas na yi Isɩkariyot na ɩ Simon bie.

eng: After Judas took the bread, Satan entered into him. Jesus told him, What you are about to do, do quickly.
local: Judas na 'wɔb a paanʊ̃ɔ baar ka Satan kpɛ wʊ. Ka Yesu 'yɛr wʊ, A lɛ fʊn bʊɔrɔ ɩka fʊ maal, pɔɔ fɔɔ maal.

eng: No one sitting at the table understood why Jesus said this to him.
local: Tɩchɛ a bala na be a bʊ̃diir jie, ãsʊɔ jaa ba bɔ̃ɔ a par a lɛ Yesu na 'yɛr kʊ wʊ ɛ.

eng: Some thought that, since Judas kept the money bag, Jesus told him, Go, buy what we need for the feast, or that he should give something to the poor.
local: An ɩ a Judas lɛ tara a ba libie wʊɔ, ka bamɩne tɩɛr ɩka Yesu tɔ̃ɔ 'yɛrɛ wʊ ɩka wʊ da bon kɔ̃ɔ waan a ba wa maal a lɔ̃ɔ di jie bɩɩ wʊ kʊ nɔ̃n dẽme bon kɔ̃ɔ.

eng: So when Judas took the bread, he left immediately. And it was night.
local: A lɛ a Judas na wa de a paanʊ̃ɔ baar wʊ yi naa, tãsɔɔ mɩ lɛ.

eng: When Judas was gone, Jesus said, Now the Human One has been glorified, and God has been glorified in him.
local: Wʊn wa yi baar, Yesu 'yɛr naa, Pʊ̃pãanyã maa Nɩsaal Bie nyɛn tɩɩr ka Nãaŋmɩn mɩ nyɛ tɩɩr tu a wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: If God has been glorified in him, God will also glorify the Human One in himself and will glorify him immediately.
local: Ala ɩ Nãaŋmɩn nyɛn tɩɩr tu a wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ, Nãaŋmɩn na kʊ naa a Bie tɩɩr, wʊn maal naa lɛ ajienaa.

eng: Little children, Im with you for a little while longer. You will look for me—but, just as I told the Jewish leaders, I also tell you now—Where Im going, you cant come.
local: N bibiir, n kʊ̃ lɛ be a yɩ sɛ̃ kor ɛ. Yɩn wa yʊɔrɔ na bʊɔrɔ ma tɩ a lɛ ɩn 'yɛr kʊ a Juu nɩbɛ, lɛ mɩ lɛ n 'yɛr kʊ yɩ a pʊ̃pãanyã. A jie 'lɔ ɩn chiine yɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ wa be ɛ.

eng: I give you a new commandment: Love each other. Just as I have loved you, so you also must love each other.
local: Bin paalaa lɛ n kʊrɔ yɩ, yɩ nʊ̃ taa nɩtãa lɛ ɩn nʊ̃ yɩ. Yɩ mɩ nʊ̃ taa.

eng: This is how everyone will know that you are my disciples, when you love each other.
local: Yɩn wa nʊ̃ taa, nɩbɛ ba jaa na bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka yɩn lɛ ɩ a n poturbo.

eng: Simon Peter said to Jesus, Lord, where are you going? Jesus answered, Where I am going, you cant follow me now, but you will follow later.
local: Ka a Simon Pita sʊʊr wʊ, N Soro, nyɩnɛ fʊ chiine? A Yesu 'yɛr, A jie ɩn chiine, fʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ tu a pʊ̃pãanyã ɛ, tɩchɛ daar kɔ̃ɔ fʊn tu naa.

eng: Peter asked, Lord, why cant I follow you now? Ill give up my life for you.
local: Ka a Pita sʊʊr wʊ, N Soro, bo lɛ so ka n kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ tu fʊ a pʊ̃pãanyã ɛ? Ɩn sɔɔn kpi a fʊ jũu.

eng: Jesus replied, Will you give up your life for me? I assure you that you will deny me three times before the rooster crows.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr wʊ, Fʊn sɔɔn kpi kʊ ma? Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ fʊ ɩka nɔraa kʊ̃ kõ sɛrɛ ɛ, tɩ fʊ chiir gbɛɛ ata ɩka faa bɔ̃ɔ ma ɛ.

eng: Dont be troubled. Trust in God. Trust also in me.
local: Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a poturbo, Yɩ ta vɛ̃ ka a yɩ pʊtɩɛr vavire ɛ, yɩ sɔɔ de Nãaŋmɩn na yɩ mɩ sɔɔ de ma.

eng: My Fathers house has room to spare. If that werent the case, would I have told you that Im going to prepare a place for you?.
local: De yɔɔ be na a n Sãa yir, ala ba ta ɩ lɛ ɛ, n kʊ̃ naa 'yɛr kʊ yɩ ɛ. N chiine naa na tɩ chɔɔr a jie kʊ yɩ.

eng: When I go to prepare a place for you, I will return and take you to be with me so that where I am you will be too.
local: Maa wa chen tɩ chɔɔr a jie baar, ɩn lɩɛb wa de yɩ ka yɩ tɩ be a n sɛ̃, ka yɩ mɩ tɩ be a jie 'lɔ ɩn be.

eng: You know the way to the place Im going.
local: Yɩ bɔ̃ɔn a jie 'lɔ ɩn chiine sɔr.

eng: Thomas asked, Lord, we dont know where you are going. How can we know the way.
local: Ka Tomasɩ sʊʊr wʊ, N Soro, sɩn ba bɔ̃ɔ a jie fʊn chiine ɛ, ŋmɩnɛ sɩn ɩrɛ wa bɔ̃ɔ a sɔr.

eng: Jesus answered, I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr, Maa lɛ a sɔr, a yelmãɛ, nɩ a nyɛvʊr. Nɩrɛ jaa kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ chen a Sãa sɛ̃, na waa tu a n jie ɛ.

eng: If you have really known me, you will also know the Father. From now on you know him and have seen him.
local: Ala ɩ yɩ bɔ̃ɔ ma naa, yɩn bɔ̃ɔn a Sãa mɩ. An be yi a dɩna yɩ bɔ̃ɔ wʊ naa, na yɩ nyɛ wʊ mɩ.

eng: Philip said, Lord, show us the Father; that will be enough for us.
local: Ka Filip 'yɛr, N Soro, wiil sɩ a Sãa a lɛ na sɛɛ sɩ naa.

eng: Jesus replied, Dont you know me, Philip, even after I have been with you all this time? Whoever has seen me has seen the Father. How can you say, Show us the Father?.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr, Filip, a mɔ̃ faa bɔ̃ɔ ma ɔ, a kõr jaa ɩn be a yɩ sɛ̃? Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na nyɛ ma nyɛn a Sãa. Ŋmɩnɛ fʊ 'yɛr ɩka n wiil yɩ a Sãa?.

eng: Dont you believe that I am in the Father and the Father is in me? The words I have spoken to you I dont speak on my own. The Father who dwells in me does his works.
local: Faa sɔɔ de ɩka n ben a Sãa 'yɔ̃ɔ ka a Sãa mɩ be a n 'yɔ̃ɔ ɛ? A 'yɛrbie ala ɩn 'yɛr kʊ yɩ a ba ɩ maa mãɛ 'yɛr ɛ, tɩ a Sãa na be a n 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ tʊ̃nɔ̃ a wʊ tʊ̃mɔ̃.

eng: Trust me when I say that I am in the Father and the Father is in me, or at least believe on account of the works themselves.
local: Yɩ sɔɔ de a lɛ ɩn 'yɛr ɩka n be naa a Sãa 'yɔ̃ɔ ka a Sãa mɩ be a n 'yɔ̃ɔ, bɩɩ yɩ sɔɔ de a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ ala.

eng: I assure you that whoever believes in me will do the works that I do. They will do even greater works than these because I am going to the Father.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na sɔɔ de ma, wʊn tʊ̃nɔ̃ naa a tʊ̃mɔ̃ ala ɩn tʊ̃nɔ̃. Wʊn tʊ̃n tʊ̃bɛrɛ gar anyãna, bojũu n chiine naa a Sãa sɛ̃.

eng: I will do whatever you ask for in my name, so that the Father can be glorified in the Son.
local: Bon jaa yɩn de a yuor sʊɔr, ɩn maal wʊn kʊ yɩ, ka a Sãa nyɛ tɩɩr tu a Bie 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: When you ask me for anything in my name, I will do it.
local: Yɩ de a n yuor sʊɔr ma bon jaa, ɩn maal wʊ naa.

eng: If you love me, you will keep my commandments.
local: Ala ɩ yɩ nʊ̃ ma naa, yɩn tu naa a lɛ ɩn 'yɛr.

eng: I will ask the Father, and he will send another Companion, who will be with you forever.
local: Ka n sʊɔr a Sãa ka wʊ kʊ yɩ a Sʊ̃nɔ̃ ãsʊɔ, ka wʊ be a yɩ sɛ̃ jaa kpo kpo.

eng: This Companion is the Spirit of Truth, whom the world cant receive because it neither sees him nor recognizes him. You know him, because he lives with you and will be with you.
local: A yelmãɛ Sɩɛ 'lɔ, a wɛr nɩbɛ na kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ de wʊ ɛ, bojũu baa nyɛ wʊ ɛ, bɩɩ bɔ̃ɔ wʊ ɛ. Tɩchɛ yɩn 'lɔ bɔ̃ɔ wʊ naa an ɩ wʊ kpɩɛrɛ naa a yɩ sɛ̃, na wʊn be a yɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: I wont leave you as orphans. I will come to you.
local: N kʊ̃ bar yɩ nɩtãa kpɩɩr ɛ, ɩn lɩɛb wa a yɩ sɛ̃.

eng: Soon the world will no longer see me, but you will see me. Because I live, you will live too.
local: A chɛn bãlãa a wɛr nɩbɛ kʊ̃ lɛ nyɛ ma go ɛ, tɩchɛ yɩn 'lɔ na nyɛ ma naa. Bojũu ɩn tara na nyɛvʊr ka yɩn mɩ na a nyɛvʊr.

eng: On that day you will know that I am in my Father, you are in me, and I am in you.
local: A bɩbir 'lɔ wa vɩɛ, yɩn bɔ̃ɔ ɩka n be naa a n Sãa 'yɔ̃ɔ, ka yɩ mɩ be a n 'yɔ̃ɔ, ka n mɩ be a yɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: Whoever has my commandments and keeps them loves me. Whoever loves me will be loved by my Father, and I will love them and reveal myself to them.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na de a n 'yɛr na wʊ turo, 'lɔ lɛ ɩ nɩrɛ 'lɔ na nʊ̃ ma. 'Lɔ jaa mɩ na nʊ̃ ma, a n Sãa mɩ na nʊ̃ wʊ naa, ka maa mɩ nʊ̃ a nɩnyɩnɛ, naa wiil wʊ a n mãɛ.

eng: Judas asked, Lord, why are you about to reveal yourself to us and not to the world.
local: Be lɛ ka a Judas, a ba ɩ a Judas 'lɔ na yi a Isɩkariyot ɛ, 'lɔ lɛ 'yɛr, Tɩchɛ, n Soro bo lɛ so ka fʊ wiil a fʊ mãɛ kʊ sɩ, tɩ na faa wile a fʊ mãɛ kʊrɔ a wɛr nɩbɛ ɛ.

eng: Jesus answered, Whoever loves me will keep my word. My Father will love them, and we will come to them and make our home with them.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr, Ala ɩ ãsʊɔ jaa nʊ̃ ma naa, wʊn turo naa a n wiilfʊ. A n Sãa na nʊ̃ wʊ naa, ka sɩ wa be a wʊ sɛ̃.

eng: Whoever doesnt love me doesnt keep my words. The word that you hear isnt mine. It is the word of the Father who sent me.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ba nʊ̃ ma ɛ, wʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ tu a n wiilfʊ ɛ. A 'yɛrbie anyãna yɩn wõ ba ɩ maa mãɛ 'yɛr ɛ, a yin a Sãa na tʊ̃ ma sɛ̃.

eng: I have spoken these things to you while I am with you.
local: A anyãna a jaa lɛ n 'yɛr kʊ yɩ, ɩn chɛnɛ be a yɩ sɛ̃.

eng: The Companion, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, will teach you everything and will remind you of everything I told you.
local: Tɩchɛ a Sʊ̃nɔ̃ 'lɔ na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ, 'lɔ lɛ a Sãa na tʊ̃n a n yuor. Wʊn wiil yɩ naa a yele a jaa, na wʊ mɩ jɛɔ yɩ ala a jaa ɩn dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr yɩ.

eng: Peace I leave with you. My peace I give you. I give to you not as the world gives. Dont be troubled or afraid.
local: Nyã'maar pɔ lɛ n bar yɩ, a maa mãɛ nyã'maar lɛ n bar kʊ yɩ. N ba kʊ yɩ nɩtãa lɛ a wɛr nɩbɛ na ma kʊ ɛ. Yɩ ta vɛ̃ ka yɩ tɩɛr vavire ɛ, ka yɩ joro dãbãɛ ɛ.

eng: You have heard me tell you, Im going away and returning to you. If you loved me, you would be happy that I am going to the Father, because the Father is greater than me.
local: Yɩ wõn a lɛ ɩn 'yɛr ɩka n chiine naa tɩ ɩn lɩɛb wa a yɩ sɛ̃. Ala ɩ yɩ ta nʊ̃ ma naa, yɩ pʊɔ naa pɛl naa ɩn chiine a Sãa sɛ̃ an ɩ a n Sãa lɛ bɛrma gar ma.

eng: I have told you before it happens so that when it happens you will believe.
local: N den nie 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, tɩchɛ ka a na wa ɩ, ala wa ɩ, yɩn sɔɔn de.

eng: I wont say much more to you because this worlds ruler is coming. He has nothing on me.
local: Maa nɩ yɩn kʊ̃ lɛ 'yɛr kor ɛ, bojũu a wɛr nyã nãa Satan waara naa, tɩ waa tara kpɛ̃ɔ a n 'yɔ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: Rather, he comes so that the world will know that I love the Father and do just as the Father has commanded me. Get up. Were leaving this place.
local: Tɩchɛ a wɛr nɩbɛ na bɔ̃ɔn ɩka n nʊ̃ naa n Sãa naa maal a lɛ nɛ a n Sãa na 'yɔ̃ɔ ma. Yɩ ir a sɩ chen.

eng: I am the true vine, and my Father is the vineyard keeper.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr, Maa lɛ ɩ a tɩwɔ̃n sʊ̃ ka a n Sãa mɩ ɩ a tɩkaara.

eng: He removes any of my branches that dont produce fruit, and he trims any branch that produces fruit so that it will produce even more fruit.
local: Wʊ ma ŋman a wilsɩ ala na be a n 'yɔ̃ɔ tɩ ba wɔ̃nɔ̃ ɛ. A wilsɩ ala mɩ na wɔ̃nɔ̃, wʊ ma chɔɔr 'mɛl bar ka a wɔ̃ yɔɔ.

eng: You are already trimmed because of the word I have spoken to you.
local: Yɩn 'lɔ, a 'yɛr ɩn 'yɛr kʊ yɩ chɔɔr yɩ naa.

eng: Remain in me, and I will remain in you. A branch cant produce fruit by itself, but must remain in the vine. Likewise, you cant produce fruit unless you remain in me.
local: Yɩ mar a n 'yɔ̃ɔ, ka n mɩ mar a yɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ. Wile jaa kʊ̃ wɔ̃ wɔ̃mɔ̃ a wʊ mãɛ ɛ, ka waa mar a tɩwɔ̃naa 'yɔ̃ɔ ɛ. Yɩn mɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ wɔ̃mɔ̃ wɔ̃ ɛ. Yɩn mɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ wɔ̃mɔ̃ wɔ̃ ɛ, ka yaa mar a n 'yɔ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: I am the vine; you are the branches. If you remain in me and I in you, then you will produce much fruit. Without me, you cant do anything.
local: Maa lɛ ɩ a tɩɛ, yɩn mɩ lɛ a wilsɩ, ala ɩ nɩrɛ be naa a n 'yɔ̃ɔ ka n mɩ be a wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ, wʊn wɔ̃n wɔ̃mɔ̃ yɔɔ. Maa ba be a yɩ sɛ̃ ɛ, yɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ bon jaa ɩ ɛ.

eng: If you dont remain in me, you will be like a branch that is thrown out and dries up. Those branches are gathered up, thrown into a fire, and burned.
local: Ala ɩ ãsʊɔ jaa ba be a n 'yɔ̃ɔ ɛ, wʊ nyɛntãan a wile ban lɔb bar ka wʊ ko. Ba ma wob a wilbie anyãna lɔb 'yɔ̃ɔ bũu pɔ ka a di.

eng: If you remain in me and my words remain in you, ask for whatever you want and it will be done for you.
local: Ala ɩ yɩ be naa a n 'yɔ̃ɔ, ka a n 'yɛr be a yɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ, yɩ sʊɔr bon jaa yɩn bʊɔrɔ, yɩn nyɛ naa.

eng: My Father is glorified when you produce much fruit and in this way prove that you are my disciples.
local: A nyã lɛ ɩ a n Sãa tɩɩr yɩn wa wɔ̃nɔ̃ wɔ̃mɔ̃ yɔɔ, a mɩ nɩ wiil naa ɩka yɩ ɩ naa n poturbo.

eng: As the Father loved me, I too have loved you. Remain in my love.
local: Lɛ a Sãa na nʊ̃ ma, lɛ mɩ lɛ n nʊ̃ yɩ. Yɩ ara a n nʊ̃fʊ pɔ.

eng: If you keep my commandments, you will remain in my love, just as I kept my Fathers commandments and remain in his love.
local: Ala ɩ yɩ tu naa a lɛ ɩn 'yɛr, yɩn be naa a n nʊ̃fʊ pɔ, nɩtãa lɛ ɩn tu a n Sãa 'yɛr naa be a wʊ nʊ̃fʊ pɔ.

eng: I have said these things to you so that my joy will be in you and your joy will be complete.
local: A anyãna lɛ n 'yɛr kʊ yɩ ka n pʊpɛl be a yɩ sɛ̃, ka a yɩ chɩlfʊ mɩ sɛɛ.

eng: This is my commandment: love each other just as I have loved you.
local: A n 'yɛr ɩn kʊrɔ yɩ nɩ nyã. Yɩ nʊ̃ taa nɩtãa lɛ ɩn nʊ̃ yɩ.

eng: No one has greater love than to give up ones life for ones friends.
local: A nʊ̃fʊ 'lɔ na gar a nʊ̃fʊ a jaa lɛ nɩrɛ na sɔɔ kpi kʊ a wʊ barmɩne.

eng: You are my friends if you do what I command you.
local: Yɩn lɛ ɩ a n barmɩne ala ɩ yɩ ɩrɛ naa a lɛ ɩn 'yɛr.

eng: I dont call you servants any longer, because servants dont know what their master is doing. Instead, I call you friends, because everything I heard from my Father I have made known to you.
local: N ba lɛ bʊɔlɔ yɩ gbãgbaar go ɛ, bojũu tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ ba bɔ̃ɔ lɛ a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na ɩrɛ ɛ. N bʊɔlɔ yɩ naa n barmɩne, bojũu n 'yɛr yɩn a lɛ jaa ɩn wõ yi a n Sãa sɛ̃.

eng: You didnt choose me, but I chose you and appointed you so that you could go and produce fruit and so that your fruit could last. As a result, whatever you ask the Father in my name, he will give you.
local: Yɩn baa ir ma ɛ, tɩchɛ maa lɛ ir yɩ ɩka yɩ tɩ wɔ̃ wɔ̃mɔ̃, a wɔ̃mɔ̃ ala na kʊ̃ sɔ̃ɔ ɛ. A Sãa na pãa kʊ yɩ naa bon jaa yɩn sʊɔr a n yuor pɔ.

eng: I give you these commandments so that you can love each other.
local: A nyãna lɛ n 'yɛr ɩka, yɩ nʊ̃ taa.

eng: If the world hates you, know that it hated me first.
local: Ala wa ɩ a wɛr nɩbɛ 'laar yɩ naa, yɩ bɔ̃ɔ ɩka ba dɔ̃ɔ de nie 'laar ma.

eng: If you belonged to the world, the world would love you as its own. However, I have chosen you out of the world, and you dont belong to the world. This is why the world hates you.
local: Ala ɩ yɩ ta ɩ naa a wɛr nɩbɛ, a wɛr nɩbɛ naa nʊ̃ yɩ naa nɩtãa a wʊ bon. Yaa ɩ a wɛr nyã tʊɔr ɛ. Tɩchɛ an ɩ n oo yɩn ir a wɛr nɩbɛ pɔ, ala lɛ so a ba 'laar yɩ.
