eng: Remember what I told you, Servants arent greater than their master. If the world harassed me, it will harass you too. If it kept my word, it will also keep yours.
local: Yɩ tɩɛr tɔ a 'yɛr 'lɔ ɩn 'yɛr kʊ yɩ. Gbãgbaa ba ɩ kpɛ̃ɛ gar a wʊ soro ɛ. Ala ɩ ba ge ma naa, ba mɩ na ge yɩ naa. Ala ɩ ba tu naa a n wiilfʊ, ba mɩ a n tu naa a yɩ wiilfʊ mɩ.

eng: The world will do all these things to you on account of my name, because it doesnt know the one who sent me.
local: Ban maal yɩ naa a nɩtɔ̃nɔ̃ a n yuor jũu, bojũu baa bɔ̃ɔ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na tʊ̃ ma ɛ.

eng: If I hadnt come and spoken to the people of this world, they wouldnt be sinners. But now they have no excuse for their sin.
local: Ala ɩ n ba ta wa 'yɛr 'yɛr kʊ ba ɛ, ba kʊ̃ naa tara yeldɛɛr ɛ, pʊ̃pãanyã baa tara a ba yeldɛɛr lɩɛb tunfʊ jie ɛ.

eng: Whoever hates me also hates the Father.
local: A 'lɔ na 'laar ma mɩ 'laara a n Sãa mɩ.

eng: If I hadnt done works among them that no one else had done, they wouldnt be sinners. But now they have seen and hated both me and my Father.
local: Maa ta ba tʊ̃tʊ̃mɔ̃ na ba sɔ̃ɔ nɩrɛ jaa na ba ɩ ɛ, ba kʊ̃ naa tara yeldɛɛr ɛ. Tɩchɛ pʊ̃pãanyã ba nyɛn a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ ɩn tʊ̃, tɩ chɛnɛ 'laar ma nɩ a n Sãa.

eng: This fulfills the word written in their Law, They hated me without a reason.
local: Tɩchɛ a anyã ɩ naa ka a ɩ yelmãɛ a lɛ an sɛb a Nɛɛ pɔ, Ba 'laar man pɔrɔ.

eng: When the Companion comes, whom I will send from the Father—the Spirit of Truth who proceeds from the Father—he will testify about me.
local: A Sʊ̃nɔ̃ na wa na, a 'lɔ na yi a Sãa sɛ̃. A yelmãɛ Sɩɛ 'lɔ wʊ yin a Sãa sɛ̃, wʊn 'yɛr yɩ naa a n 'yɛr.

eng: You will testify too, because you have been with me from the beginning.
local: Yɩ mɩ na 'yɛr naa a n 'yɛr bojũu yɩ ben a n sɛ̃ a pielfʊ jaa.

eng: I have said these things to you so that you wont fall away.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr, A anyãna a jaa lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, ka yɩ taa bar yi a sɔɔfʊ pɔ ɛ.

eng: They will expel you from the synagogue. The time is coming when those who kill you will think that they are doing a service to God.
local: Ban dii yɩn bar a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie. Bɩbir kɔ̃ɔ na vɩɛ naa, ka ba kʊ yɩ tɩ tɩɛr ɩka Nãaŋmɩn tʊ̃mɔ̃ lɛ ba tʊ̃.

eng: They will do these things because they dont know the Father or me.
local: Ban ɩ naa anyãna, bojũu baa bɔ̃ɔ a Sãa ɛ, bɩɩ a maa ɛ.

eng: But I have said these things to you so that when their time comes, you will remember that I told you about them. I didnt say these things to you from the beginning, because I was with you.
local: N 'yɛrɛ yɩ naa anyãna ka a bɩbir wa vɩɛ yɩn tɩɛr tɔ, ɩka n dɔ̃ɔ kpãa yɩ. N ba dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr kʊ yɩ ɛ, bojũu n be naa a yɩ sɛ̃.

eng: But now I go away to the one who sent me. None of you ask me, Where are you going.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã n chiine naa a 'lɔ na tʊ̃ ma sɛ̃, tɩchɛ a ãsʊɔ jaa ba sʊʊr ma a jie ɩn chiine ɛ.

eng: Yet because I have said these things to you, you are filled with sorrow.
local: A anyãna ɩn 'yɛr kʊ yɩ jũu lɛ a yɩ pʊʊr sɔ̃ɔ ɔ?.

eng: I assure you that it is better for you that I go away. If I dont go away, the Companion wont come to you. But if I go, I will send him to you.
local: Tɩchɛ yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, a yɩ jũu a sɛɛ naa ɩka n chen. Maa ba chen ɛ, a Sʊ̃nɔ̃ kʊ̃ wa a yɩ sɛ̃ ɛ, tɩchɛ maa chen ɩn tʊ̃ wʊn ka wʊ wa a yɩ sɛ̃.

eng: When he comes, he will show the world it was wrong about sin, righteousness, and judgment.
local: 'Lɔ wa wa, wʊn wiil a wɛr nɩbɛ a ba bɔ̃ɔ ɩka ba bɔr naa, lɛ a yeldɛɛr nɩ yeltorsɩ na be, nɩ lɛ wʊn na tɔɔr a ba 'yɛr.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about sin because they dont believe in me.
local: Wʊn vɛ̃ naa a ba bɔ̃ɔ a ba yeldɛɛr ban ba sɔɔ de ma ɛ.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about righteousness because Im going to the Father and you wont see me anymore.
local: Ba bɔr naa nɩ a yeltorsɩ, bojũu n chiine naa a Sãa sɛ̃, yɩ kʊ̃ lɛ nyɛ ma go ɛ.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about judgment because this worlds ruler stands condemned.
local: Ba mɩ bɔr naa nɩ a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ yele, bojũu a wɛr nyã nãa Satan 'yɛr tɔɔr naa.

eng: I have much more to say to you, but you cant handle it now.
local: N chɛnɛ na tara 'yɛr yɔɔ nɩ taa 'yɛr yɩ tɩchɛ a pʊ̃pãanyã, yɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ bɔ̃ɔ a par ɛ.

eng: However, when the Spirit of Truth comes, he will guide you in all truth. He wont speak on his own, but will say whatever he hears and will proclaim to you what is to come.
local: Tɩchɛ a yelmãɛ Sɩɛ 'lɔ wa wa, wʊn wiil yɩ naa a yelmãɛ wʊ jaa par. Wʊ kʊ̃ 'yɛr a wʊ mãɛ 'yɛrɛ ɛ, tɩ wʊn 'yɛr naa a lɛ tɛɛ wʊn wõ, na wʊ lɛ 'yɛr yɩ a ala na wa.

eng: He will glorify me, because he will take what is mine and proclaim it to you.
local: Wʊn waan na tɩɩr wa kʊ ma, na wʊ vɛ̃ ka yɩ bɔ̃ɔ a ala na ɩ a n bon.

eng: Everything that the Father has is mine. Thats why I said that the Spirit takes what is mine and will proclaim it to you.
local: Ala a jaa a Sãa na so ɩ naa a n bon. Ala lɛ so ka n 'yɛr ɩka a Sɩɛ na vɛ̃ naa ka yɩ bɔ̃ɔ a ala na ɩ a n bon.

eng: Soon you wont be able to see me; soon after that, you will see me.
local: A chɛn bãlãa yɩ kʊ̃ nyɛ ma go ɛ, ka a pãa lɛ ɩ bãlãa ka yɩ nyɛ ma go.

eng: Some of Jesus disciples said to each other, What does he mean: Soon you wont see me, and soon after that you will see me and Because Im going to the Father?.
local: Ka a poturbo bamɩne sʊrɔ taa, A par nɩ bo wʊn 'yɛr ɛ ɩka, A chɛn bãlãa yɩ kʊ̃ lɛ nyɛ ma go ɛ, ka a pãa lɛ ɩ bãlãa ka yɩ lɛ nyɛ wʊ go, nɩ wʊn lɛ 'yɛr ɩka, n chiine naa a Sãa sɛ̃? .

eng: What does he mean by soon? We dont understand what hes talking about.
local: Ba chɛnɛ na sʊrɔ taa, A chɛn bãlãa par nɩ bo? Sãa bɔ̃ɔ a par a lɛ, wʊn 'yɛrɛ ɛ.

eng: Jesus knew they wanted to ask him, so he said, Are you trying to find out from each other what I meant when I said, Soon you wont see me, and soon after that you will see me?.
local: Yesu bɔ̃ɔn naa ban bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba sʊʊr wʊ ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ sʊrɔ nɩ taa a lɛ ɩn 'yɛr ɩka, A chɛn bãlãa yɩ kʊ̃ nyɛ ma go ɛ, ka a lɛ ɩ bãlãa ka yɩ nyɛ ma go?.

eng: I assure you that you will cry and lament, and the world will be happy. You will be sorrowful, but your sorrow will turn into joy.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka yɩn kõnõ na ka yɩ pʊʊr sɔ̃ɔ, tɩchɛ ka a wɛr nɩbɛ chɩlɛ. Yɩ pʊʊr na sɔ̃ɔ naa, tɩchɛ ka a yɩ pʊsɔ̃ɔ na lɩɛb pʊpɛl.

eng: When a woman gives birth, she has pain because her time has come. But when the child is born, she no longer remembers her distress because of her joy that a child has been born into the world.
local: Pɔɔ wa dɔɔrɔ ãgãn ma chɩrɛ wʊ naa ka a ɔɔrɔ wʊ bojũu a wʊ dɔɔfʊ bɩbir vɩɛ naa. Tɩchɛ 'lɔ wʊ dɔɔ baar, wʊ ma ĩin naa a wʊ dɔɔyɛ bojũu wʊ dɔɔn nɩrɛ a wɛr pɔ.

eng: In the same way, you have sorrow now; but I will see you again, and you will be overjoyed. No one takes away your joy.
local: Lɛ nɩ wʊ ju lɛ a yɩn mɩ, yɩ pʊʊr na sɔ̃ɔ naa a pʊ̃pãanyã, tɩchɛ ɩn lɛ wa nyɛ yɩn go ka yɩ pʊʊl pɛl, ka a nɩrɛ jaa kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ faa wʊ ɛ.

eng: In that day, you wont ask me anything. I assure you that the Father will give you whatever you ask in my name.
local: A bɩbir 'lɔ wa vɩɛ, yɩ kʊ̃ lɛ sʊʊr ma bon go ɛ. Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, a n Sãa na kʊ yɩn bon jaa yɩn sʊɔr a n yuor pɔ.

eng: Up to now, you have asked nothing in my name. Ask and you will receive so that your joy will be complete.
local: An wa ta a pʊ̃pãanyã, yaa de a n yuor sʊɔr bon gba ɛ. Yɩ sʊɔr, yɩn nyɛ naa, ka yɩ pʊpɛl na sɛɛ.

eng: Ive been using figures of speech with you. The time is coming when I will no longer speak to you in such analogies. Instead, I will tell you plainly about the Father.
local: Sʊkpar lɛ n lɔɔrɔ 'yɛrɛ nɩ yɩ a anyãna, a bɩbir wa vɩɛ, n kʊ̃ lɛ lɔb sʊkpar go ɛ, tɩchɛ ɩn 'yɛr yɩ naa a n Sãa 'yɛr chãa pɔ.

eng: In that day you will ask in my name. Im not saying that I will ask the Father on your behalf.
local: A bɩbir 'lɔ̃nɔ̃, yɩn sʊɔr naa nɩ a n yuor. N ba 'yɛrɛ ɩka maa lɛ na sʊɔr a Sãa kʊ yɩ ɛ.

eng: The Father himself loves you, because you have loved me and believed that I came from God.
local: An ɩ a Sãa nʊ̃ yɩ naa bojũu yɩ nʊ̃ ma naa na yɩ sɔɔ de ɩka a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ lɛ n yi.

eng: I left the Father and came into the world. I tell you again: I am leaving the world and returning to the Father.
local: A Sãa sɛ̃ lɛ n yi wa ka a wɛr pɔ, pʊ̃pãanyã n lɛ bara naa a wɛr tɩ lɩɛb chiine a Sãa sɛ̃.

eng: His disciples said, See! Now you speak plainly; you arent using figures of speech.
local: Ka a Yesu poturbo pãa 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Pʊ̃pãanyã fʊ pãa 'yɛr ka a chaar faa lɛ lɔɔrɔ sʊkpar go ɛ.

eng: Now we know that you know everything and you dont need anyone to ask you. Because of this we believe you have come from God.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã sɩ nyɛn ɩka fʊ bɔ̃ɔn yele a jaa, na faa lɛ bʊɔrɔ ɩka nɩrɛ wʊ sʊʊr fʊ sʊʊrfʊsɩ ɛ. Ala lɛ so ka sɩ sɔɔ de ɩka Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ lɛ fʊ yi.

eng: Jesus replied, Now you believe?.
local: Ka a Yesu sʊʊr ba, Yɩ pãa sɔɔn de a pʊ̃pãanyã ɔ?.

eng: Look! A time is coming—and is here!—when each of you will be scattered to your own homes and you will leave me alone. Im not really alone, for the Father is with me.
local: Tɩchɛ bɩbir kɔ̃ɔ na wa vɩɛ naa, wʊ ben ta, ka yɩ jaa na yɛ̃yɛl ka ãsʊɔ jaa kul a wʊ yir tɩ chɛ ma bar a n yõ. Tɩ n ba ɩ a n yõ ɛ, a n Sãa be naa n sɛ̃.

eng: Ive said these things to you so that you will have peace in me. In the world you have distress. But be encouraged! I have conquered the world.
local: N 'yɛr yɩ naa anyãna ka yɩ na tara nyã'maar an ɩ yɩ ben a n 'yɔ̃ɔ. A wɛr nyã pɔ yɩn din dɔɔyɛ, tɩchɛ yɩ nyɔɔ nyãa, bojũu n tʊ̃ɔn a wɛr.

eng: When Jesus finished saying these things, he looked up to heaven and said, Father, the time has come. Glorify your Son, so that the Son can glorify you.
local: A Yesu na wa 'yɛr a anyãna baar, wʊ kãan saaju tɩ 'yɛr, N Sãa, a bɩbir vɩɛ naa, kʊ a fʊ Bie a tɩɩr ka a fʊ Bie mɩ kʊ fʊ a tɩɩr.

eng: You gave him authority over everyone so that he could give eternal life to everyone you gave him.
local: Bojũu, fʊ̃ʊ lɛ kʊ wʊ sɔr ka wʊ tʊ̃ɔ a nɩbɛ ba jaa na wʊ kʊ ba a nyɛvʊr 'lɔ na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ, a bala ba jaa fʊn de kʊ wʊ.

eng: This is eternal life: to know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ whom you sent.
local: A nyɛvʊr 'lɔ na ba tara baarfʊ nɩ nyã, ka ban bɔ̃ɔ a fʊ̃ʊ Nãaŋmɩn tɛɛ, na ɩ yelmãɛ sʊɔ, nɩ a Yesu 'lɔ fʊn tʊ̃.

eng: I have glorified you on earth by finishing the work you gave me to do.
local: N waan na tɩɩr wa kʊ fʊ a ka a wɛr pɔ, naa baar a tʊ̃mɔ̃ ala fʊn kʊ ma ɩka n tʊ̃.

eng: Now, Father, glorify me in your presence with the glory I shared with you before the world was created.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã, n Sãa kʊ ma a tɩɩr a fʊ niem, a tɩɩr 'lɔ ɩn dɔ̃ɔ tara tɩchɛ na fʊ wa maal a wɛr.

eng: I have revealed your name to the people you gave me from this world. They were yours and you gave them to me, and they have kept your word.
local: N wiil fʊn kʊ a bala fʊn de kʊ ma ka a wɛr pɔ, fʊ̃ʊ lɛ dɔ̃ɔ so ba tɩ na fʊ wa de ba kʊ ma, ba tu naa a fʊ 'yɛr.

eng: Now they know that everything you have given me comes from you.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã ba bɔ̃ɔ naa ɩka bon jaa ɩn tara yin a fʊ sɛ̃.

eng: This is because I gave them the words that you gave me, and they received them. They truly understood that I came from you, and they believed that you sent me.
local: Bojũu, a 'yɛr ala fʊn kʊ ma, n 'yɛr kʊ ba, ka ba sɔɔ de. Ba bɔ̃ɔn yelmãɛ jaa ɩka fʊ̃ʊ sɛ̃ lɛ n yi naa wa, na ba sɔɔ de ɩka fʊ̃ʊ lɛ tʊ̃ ma.

eng: Im praying for them. Im not praying for the world but for those you gave me, because they are yours.
local: Bala 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ n sʊɔrɔ fʊ, n ba sʊɔrɔ fʊ kʊrɔ a wɛr nɩbɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ a bala fʊn kʊ ma an ɩ fʊ̃ʊ lɛ so ba.

eng: Everything that is mine is yours and everything that is yours is mine; I have been glorified in them.
local: Bon jaa ɩn tara ɩ naa a fʊ sofʊ, ka bon jaa fʊn mɩ na tara ɩ a n sofʊ. N nyɛn tɩɩr yi a ba jie.

eng: Im no longer in the world, but they are in the world, even as Im coming to you. Holy Father, watch over them in your name, the name you gave me, that they will be one just as we are one.
local: Maa kʊ̃ lɛ be ka a wɛr pɔ go ɛ, tɩchɛ bala 'lɔ chɛnɛ na be ka a wɛr pɔ, n waara a fʊ sɛ̃. Sãa sʊ̃, gu ba nɩ a fʊ yuor kpɛ̃ɔ 'lɔ pɔ, a yuor 'lɔ fʊn kʊ ma ka ban ɩ bʊ̃'yen, nɩtãa lɛ maa nɩ fʊ̃ʊ na ɩ bʊ̃'yen.

eng: When I was with them, I watched over them in your name, the name you gave to me, and I kept them safe. None of them were lost, except the one who was destined for destruction, so that scripture would be fulfilled.
local: Ɩn be a ba sɛ̃, n gu ban naa kpɛ̃ɔ 'lɔ fʊn kʊ ma. Ɩ̃sʊɔ jaa ba bɔr ɛ, ala ba ɩ a 'lɔ tɛɛ an sɛɛ ɩka wʊ bɔr ka a lɛ na sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ na ɩ yelmãɛ.

eng: Now Im coming to you and I say these things while Im in the world so that they can share completely in my joy.
local: N waara naa a fʊ sɛ̃ a pʊ̃pãanyã, tɩchɛ ɩn chɛnɛ be ka a wɛr pɔ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ a anyãna ka ban tara a n pʊpɛl wʊ jaa.

eng: I gave your word to them and the world hated them, because they dont belong to this world, just as I dont belong to this world.
local: N kʊ ban a fʊ 'yɛr, ala lɛ so ka a wɛr nɩbɛ 'laar ba, baa lɛ chɛnɛ pʊɔ ba go ɛ, nɩtãa maa na ba pʊɔ ba ɛ.

eng: Im not asking that you take them out of this world but that you keep them safe from the evil one.
local: N ba sʊɔrɔ fʊ ɩka fʊ ir ba yin ka a wɛr pɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ fʊ guro ba a dɛɛr sʊɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: They dont belong to this world, just as I dont belong to this world.
local: Baa lɛ pʊɔ a wɛr nɩbɛ pɔ ɛ, nɩtãa n mɩ na ba pʊɔ ba ɛ.

eng: Make them holy in the truth; your word is truth.
local: Oo ba nɩ a fʊ yelmãɛ, a fʊ 'yɛr ɩn yelmãɛ.

eng: As you sent me into the world, so I have sent them into the world.
local: Nɩtãa lɛ fʊn tʊ̃ ma a wɛr nɩbɛ sɛ̃, lɛ lɛ n mɩ tʊ̃ ba a wɛr nɩbɛ sɛ̃.

eng: I made myself holy on their behalf so that they also would be made holy in the truth.
local: A ba 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ n oo a n mãɛ kʊ fʊ, ka ba mɩ na oo a ba mãɛ sɩrɛ jaa kʊ fʊ.

eng: Im not praying only for them but also for those who believe in me because of their word.
local: A ba ɩ bala tɛɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ n sʊɔrfʊ ɛ, n mɩ sʊɔrɔ na kʊrɔ a bala na wa sɔɔ de ma tu a ba 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: I pray they will be one, Father, just as you are in me and I am in you. I pray that they also will be in us, so that the world will believe that you sent me.
local: N sʊɔrɔ fʊ naa ɩka fʊ vɛ̃ ka ba jaa ɩ bʊ̃'yen. N Sãa, nɩtãa lɛ fʊn be a n 'yɔ̃ɔ, ka n mɩ be a fʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ. Ba mɩ be a sɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ ka a wɛr nɩbɛ na sɔɔ de ɩka fʊ̃ʊ lɛ tʊ̃ ma.

eng: Ive given them the glory that you gave me so that they can be one just as we are one.
local: N kʊ ban a tɩɩr 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ nɩ wʊ ju fʊn kʊ ma, ka ba na ɩ bʊ̃'yen nɩtãa maa nɩ fʊ̃ʊ na ɩ bʊ̃'yen.

eng: Im in them and you are in me so that they will be made perfectly one. Then the world will know that you sent me and that you have loved them just as you loved me.
local: N ben a ba 'yɔ̃ɔ, ka fʊ mɩ be a n 'yɔ̃ɔ. Ka ba jaa mɩ na 'mataa ɩ bʊ̃'yen ka a wɛr nɩbɛ bɔ̃ɔ ɩka fʊ̃ʊ lɛ tʊ̃ ma na fʊ nʊ̃ ba gba nɩtãa a lɛ fʊn nʊ̃ ma.

eng: Father, I want those you gave me to be with me where I am. Then they can see my glory, which you gave me because you loved me before the creation of the world.
local: N Sãa, n bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka a bala fʊn kʊ ma, ba mɩ be a jie 'lɔ ɩn na be, na ba nyɛ a n tɩɩr. A tɩɩr 'lɔ fʊn de nie kʊ ma, bojũu fʊ nʊ̃ ma naa tɩ na fʊ wa maal a wɛr.

eng: Righteous Father, even the world didnt know you, but Ive known you, and these believers know that you sent me.
local: N Sãa, a fʊ yele tor naa, tɩ a wɛr nɩbɛ ba bɔ̃ɔ fʊ ɛ, maa bɔ̃ɔ fʊ naa, ka a n poturbo mɩ bɔ̃ɔ ɩka fʊ̃ʊ lɛ tʊ̃ ma.

eng: Ive made your name known to them and will continue to make it known so that your love for me will be in them, and I myself will be in them.
local: N vɛ̃n ka ba bɔ̃ɔ fʊ, ɩn chɛnɛ na maal ba ka ba bɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ fʊ pʊɔrɔ ka a nʊ̃fʊ 'lɔ fʊn nʊ̃ ma na be a ba 'yɔ̃ɔ ka n mɩ be a ba 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: After he said these things, Jesus went out with his disciples and crossed over to the other side of the Kidron Valley. He and his disciples entered a garden there.
local: Yesu na sʊɔr a Sãa a nɩtɔ̃ baar, 'lɔ naa poturbo gɔ̃ɔ naa a Kidɩrɔn kʊla gɔ̃ɔn. 'Lɔ nɩ a wʊ poturbo chen a jie a kãa tɩɩr na be.

eng: Judas, his betrayer, also knew the place because Jesus often gathered there with his disciples.
local: Judas na gar 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ mɩ bɔ̃ɔn a jie nyã, bojũu Yesu naa wʊ poturbo ba gala a be lɔ̃ɔfʊ ɛ.

eng: Judas brought a company of soldiers and some guards from the chief priests and Pharisees. They came there carrying lanterns, torches, and weapons.
local: Be lɛ Judas tara a Worom sojasɩ, nɩ a puorfʊ die gugurbo bala a bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Faraseemɩne na tʊ̃ chiin a jie 'lɔ. Ba tara na bũusɩ, fãtɩnsɩ nɩ jɛɛr bomo.

eng: Jesus knew everything that was to happen to him, so he went out and asked, Who are you looking for.
local: Yesu na bɔ̃ɔ lɛ na waara a wʊ sɛ̃, wʊ tuor sʊʊr ba, An yɩ bʊɔrɔ.

eng: They answered, Jesus the Nazarene. He said to them, I Am.
local: Ka ba 'yɛr, Yesu na yi Najaretɩ lɛ sɩ bʊɔrɔ. Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, Maa lɛ. Ka a Judas 'lɔ na gar 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ lɔ̃ɔn ba ara.

eng: When he said, I Am, they shrank back and fell to the ground.
local: Yesu na wa 'yɛr ɩka, Maa lɛ. Ba lɩɛb puor lo lo tẽe.

eng: He asked them again, Who are you looking for? They said, Jesus the Nazarene.
local: Wʊ lɛ sʊʊr ban go, An lɛ yɩ bʊɔrɔ? A ba 'yɛr, Yesu na yi Najaretɩ.

eng: Jesus answered, I told you, I Am. If you are looking for me, then let these people go.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr, N ba 'yɛr yɩ ɩka Maa lɛ. Ala ɩ maa lɛ yɩ bʊɔrɔ, yɩ ɩ ka a nɩbɛ banyã chiine.

eng: This was so that the word he had spoken might be fulfilled: I didnt lose anyone of those whom you gave me.
local: Yesu 'yɛr a nɩtɔ̃ ka a lɛ wʊn dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr ɩ yelmãɛ ɩka, A bala fʊn kʊ ma, n ba vɛ̃ ka ãsʊɔ jaa bɔr ɛ.

eng: Then Simon Peter, who had a sword, drew it and struck the high priests servant, cutting off his right ear.
local: Be lɛ a Simon Pita na tara sʊɔ na wʊ vʊ̃ɔ chɛn a bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ gbãgbaa nũsʊ̃ɔ toor ŋmaa. A gbãgbaa yuor din Malkusɩ.

eng: Jesus told Peter, Put your sword away! Am I not to drink the cup the Father has given me.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a Pita, Su a sʊɔ a sʊkpa pɔ, n ta nyũn a dɔɔyɛ ŋmele 'lɔ a Sãa na kʊ ma bɩɩ.

eng: Then the company of soldiers, the commander, and the guards from the Jewish leaders took Jesus into custody. They bound him.
local: Ka a sojasɩ nɩ a ba nɩkpɛ̃ɛ 'matãan a gugurbo bala ban tʊ̃, nyɔɔ a Yesu na ba lũ.

eng: and led him first to Annas. He was the father-in-law of Caiaphas, the high priest that year. .
local: Ba den nie tara wʊ chiin Anasɩ sɛ̃, wʊ ɩ naa a Kayafasɩ dãɛ na wʊ mɩ ɩ bɔɔrlo nãa a yuon 'lɔ̃nɔ̃.

eng: Caiaphas was the one who had advised the Jewish leaders that it was better for one person to die for the people.
local: A Kayafasɩ nyãna lɛ dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr kʊ a Juu nɩbɛ ɩka a ɩn sʊ̃, ka nɩbʊ̃'yen na kpi kʊ a nɩbɛ.

eng: Simon Peter and another disciple followed Jesus. Because this other disciple was known to the high priest, he went with Jesus into the high priests courtyard.
local: Simon Pita nɩ a poturo ãsʊɔ turo naa a Yesu, bojũu a poturo nyã bɔ̃ɔn a bɔɔrlo nãa ala lɛ so ka wʊ tu a Yesu kpɛ a bɔɔrlo nãa bɛchaar pɔ.

eng: However, Peter stood outside near the gate. Then the other disciple came out and spoke to the woman stationed at the gate, and she brought Peter in.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Pita 'lɔ ara a yõo dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ. A poturo nyã a bɔɔrlo nãa na bɔ̃ɔ, yin wa 'yɛr a pɔɔbile 'lɔ na kaara a dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ tɩ tara a Pita kpɛ̃n.

eng: The servant woman stationed at the gate asked Peter, Arent you one of this mans disciples? Im not, he replied.
local: Ka a pɔɔbile 'lɔ na kaara a dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ, sʊʊr a Pita, A fʊ̃ʊ, faa mɩ ɩ a daba nyã poturo ãsʊɔ ɛ? Ka a Pita 'yɛr, N ba ɩ a wʊ poturo ãsʊɔ ɛ.

eng: The servants and the guards had made a fire because it was cold. They were standing around it, warming themselves. Peter joined them there, standing by the fire and warming himself.
local: Ɔɔr kʊrɔ naa ka a gbãgbaar nɩ a gugurbo bala gbɔɔ saalaa bũu ara uoro ka a Pita mɩ tɩ ara uoro.

eng: Meanwhile, the chief priest questioned Jesus about his disciples and his teaching.
local: A bɔɔrlo nãa sʊʊr naa a Yesu, ba boo lɛ ɩ a wʊ poturbo nɩ bo lɛ wʊ ma wile?.

eng: Jesus answered, Ive spoken openly to the world. Ive always taught in synagogues and in the temple, where all the Jews gather. Ive said nothing in private.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Chãa pɔ lɛ n ma 'yɛrɛ a n 'yɛr kʊrɔ a nɩbɛ. N ma wile ba naa a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie nɩ a ba Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ jiir, be lɛ a Juu nɩbɛ ba jaa ma lɔ̃ɔ. N ba sɔɔl 'yɛr 'yɛr jaa ɛ.

eng: Why ask me? Ask those who heard what I told them. They know what I said.
local: Bo lɛ so ka fʊ sʊrɔ ma a lɛ? Sʊʊr a bala na wõ a n 'yɛr, ba bɔ̃ɔn a lɛ ɩn 'yɛr.

eng: After Jesus spoke, one of the guards standing there slapped Jesus in the face. Is that how you would answer the high priest? he asked.
local: Yesu na 'yɛr a nɩtɔ̃ ka a guguro ãsʊɔ na ara gbʊr wʊ 'ma wʊ tɩ 'yɛr, Lɛ lɛ a sɛɛ ɩka fʊ 'yɛr kʊ a bɔɔrlo nãa ɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, If I speak wrongly, testify about what was wrong. But if I speak correctly, why do you strike me.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr, Ala ɩ a lɛ ɩn 'yɛr n bɔr naa, wiil a n bɔrfʊ jie. Tɩchɛ ala ɩ yelmãɛ lɛ n mɩ 'yɛr, bo 'yɔ̃ɔ fʊ 'ma ma.

eng: Then Annas sent him, bound, to Caiaphas the high priest.
local: Wʊ chɛnɛ na lũ tɩchɛ ka a Anasɩ vɛ̃ a ba tara wʊ chiin a bɔɔrlo nãa Kayafasɩ sɛ̃.

eng: Meanwhile, Simon Peter was still standing with the guards, warming himself. They asked, Arent you one of his disciples? Peter denied it, saying, Im not.
local: A Simon Pita na chɛnɛ ara uoro a bũu, ba sʊʊr wʊ naa, A fʊ̃ʊ, fʊ ɩ naa a daba nyã poturo ãsʊɔ ɔ? Ka Pita chiir 'yɛr, N ba ɩ a wʊ poturo ãsʊɔ ɛ.

eng: A servant of the high priest, a relative of the one whose ear Peter had cut off, said to him, Didnt I see you in the garden with him.
local: A bɔɔrlo nãa gbãgbaa ãsʊɔ, wʊ nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ toor lɛ a 'lɔ a Pita na dɔ̃ɔ chɛ ŋmaa 'yɛr, A ba ɩ n nyɛ fʊ naa fʊn be a wʊ sɛ̃ a kãa tɩɩr jie 'lɔ ɛ.

eng: Peter denied it again, and immediately a rooster crowed.
local: Ka a Pita lɛ chiir, ka a nɔraa mɩ tɔ̃ɔ kõ ajienaa.

eng: The Jewish leaders led Jesus from Caiaphas to the Roman governors palace. It was early in the morning. So that they could eat the Passover, the Jewish leaders wouldnt enter the palace; entering the palace would have made them ritually impure.
local: Be lɛ ba tara a Yesu yin a Kayafasɩ yir bibio pipi na ba chiin a Worom nɩkpɛ̃ɛ yir. A Juu nɩbɛ mãɛ ba kpɛ be ɛ, bojũu baa bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba sɔ̃ɔ ba mãɛ na ba kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ di a Gõl Bar Difʊ ɛ.

eng: So Pilate went out to them and asked, What charge do you bring against this man.
local: Lɛ lɛ a Pilate yi a yõo tɩ sʊʊr ba, Bo yelbier yɩ ɩka a daba nyã ɩ naa.

eng: They answered, If he had done nothing wrong, we wouldnt have handed him over to you.
local: Ka ba 'yɛr, 'Lɔ ba ta maal yelbier ɛ, sɩ kʊ̃ naa tara wʊ waan a fʊ sɛ̃ ɛ.

eng: Pilate responded, Take him yourselves and judge him according to your Law. The Jewish leaders replied, The Law doesnt allow us to kill anyone. .
local: Ka Pilate 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩn mãɛ, yɩ de wʊ tɩ tɔɔr a wʊ 'yɛr, a lɛ a yɩn mãɛ nɛɛ na 'yɛr bin. Tɩchɛ ka a Juu nɩbɛ 'yɛr, Saa tara sɔr na kʊ nɩrɛ ɛ.

eng: This was so that Jesus word might be fulfilled when he indicated how he was going to die.
local: A ɩn a lɛ a Yesu na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr wiil a kũu 'lɔ tɔ wʊn na kpi.

eng: Pilate went back into the palace. He summoned Jesus and asked, Are you the king of the Jews.
local: Pilate lɩɛb kpɛ tɩ bʊɔl a Yesu sʊʊr, Fʊ̃ʊ lɛ ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ nãa bɩɩ.

eng: Jesus answered, Do you say this on your own or have others spoken to you about me.
local: A Yesu sʊʊr wʊ, Fʊ̃ʊ lɛ tɩɛr a lɛ bɩɩ nɩbɛ lɛ 'yɛr a n 'yɛr kʊ fʊ.

eng: Pilate responded, Im not a Jew, am I? Your nation and its chief priests handed you over to me. What have you done.
local: Ka a Pilate 'yɛr, Fʊ tɩɛr ɩka n ɩn Juu nɩrɛ bɩɩ? A fʊ mãɛ nɩbɛ nɩ a bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ baa nyɔɔ fʊ waan a n sɛ̃, bo fʊ̃ʊ ɩ.

eng: Jesus replied, My kingdom doesnt originate from this world. If it did, my guards would fight so that I wouldnt have been arrested by the Jewish leaders. My kingdom isnt from here.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr, A n nãalʊ̃ʊ ba yi ka a wɛr pɔ ɛ, ala ɩ a wɛr pɔ lɛ wʊ dɔ̃ɔ yi, a n poturbo naa jɛb faa ma ka a Juu nɩbɛrɛ ta nyɔɔ ma ɛ. Tɩchɛ a n nãalʊ̃ʊ ba yi a ka ɛ.

eng: So you are a king? Pilate said. Jesus answered, You say that I am a king. I was born and came into the world for this reason: to testify to the truth. Whoever accepts the truth listens to my voice.
local: Ka a Pilate pãa sʊʊr wʊ, Ka ala ɩ a lɛ, nãa lɛ fʊ ɩ wɛ? Ka Yesu 'yɛr, Fʊ 'yɛr vɩla fʊn 'yɛr ɩka nãa lɛ n ɩ, ala 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ ba dɔɔ ma ka n wa ka a wɛr pɔ, naa wa 'yɛr a yelmãɛ 'yɛr. Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na ɩ yelmãɛ sʊɔ chɛlɛ naa lɛ ɩn 'yɛrɛ.

eng: What is truth? Pilate asked. After Pilate said this, he returned to the Jewish leaders and said, I find no grounds for any charge against him.
local: Ka Pilate sʊʊr wʊ, Bo lɛ ɩ a yelmãɛ? Wʊn sʊʊr a nɩtɔ̃ baar, wʊ yin a yõo a Juu nɩbɛrɛ sɛ̃ tɩ 'yɛr, N ba nyɛ yelsɔ̃ɔnaa jaa a wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: You have a custom that I release one prisoner for you at Passover. Do you want me to release for you the king of the Jews.
local: Tɩchɛ a yɩ binfʊ lɛ ɩka n ma ir nɩrɛ a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ kʊ yɩ a Gõl Bar Difʊ daar. Yɩ bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka n yuo a yɩ nãa kʊ yɩ bɩɩ.

eng: They shouted, Not this man! Give us Barabbas.
local: A ba tãna 'yɛrɛ, Kai, saa bʊɔrɔ wʊ ɛ, Barabasɩ lɛ sɩ bʊɔrɔ. Tɩchɛ a Barabasɩ mɩ ɩn nɩkʊrɔ.

eng: Then Pilate had Jesus taken and whipped.
local: Pilate vɛ̃ naa ka ba nyɔɔ a Yesu chiin tɩ fɔb.

eng: The soldiers twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on his head, and dressed him in a purple robe.
local: Ka a sojasɩ de gʊʊr wob jupil su a Yesu, tɩ mɩ de fuu jã ile ile na ba su wʊ.

eng: Over and over they went up to him and said, Greetings, king of the Jews! And they slapped him in the face.
local: Tɩ pãa ma tɔɔ ta a wʊ sɛ̃ 'yɛrɛ, Sɩ puor fʊ naa Juu nɩbɛ nãa, tɩ ma 'ma 'ma wʊ.

eng: Pilate came out of the palace again and said to the Jewish leaders, Look! Im bringing him out to you to let you know that I find no grounds for a charge against him.
local: Pilate lɛ yin wa a Juu nɩbɛrɛ sɛ̃ go wa 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ nyɛ, ɩn vɛ̃n ka ba yi waan wʊ, ka yɩn mãɛ nyɛ ɩka, n ba nyɛ yelbier jaa a wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: When Jesus came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple robe, Pilate said to them, Heres the man.
local: Ban wa yin a Yesu, wʊ sun a gʊʊr jupil nɩ a fuu jã ile ile 'lɔ, ka a Pilate 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ nyɛ a daba.

eng: When the chief priests and their deputies saw him, they shouted out, Crucify, crucify! Pilate told them, You take him and crucify him. I dont find any grounds for a charge against him.
local: A bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a puorfʊ yir gugurbo, ban wa nyɛ a Yesu, ka ba tãna 'yɛrɛ, Kpa wʊ a daa ju. Kpa wʊ a daa ju. Tɩchɛ ka a Pilate 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩn, yɩ de wʊ tɩ kpa wʊ a daa ju, maa 'lɔ n ba nyɛ yelbier jaa a wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: The Jewish leaders replied, We have a Law, and according to this Law he ought to die because he made himself out to be Gods Son.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Juu nɩbɛrɛ 'yɛr, Sɩ tara na nɛɛ, sɩn 'yɛr, ka a nɛɛ 'lɔ ɩka wʊ sɛɛn kũu, bojũu wʊ bʊɔlɔ naa wʊ mãɛ Nãaŋmɩn Bie.

eng: When Pilate heard this word, he was even more afraid.
local: Pilate na wõ a nɩtɔ̃ ka dãbãɛ gba kpɛ wʊ.

eng: He went back into the residence and spoke to Jesus, Where are you from? Jesus didnt answer.
local: Ka wʊ lɩɛb kpɛ na wʊ tɩ sʊʊr a Yesu, Nyɩnɛ fʊ yi? Tɩchɛ Yesu ba haa nɛɛ sɔɔ wʊ ɛ.

eng: So Pilate said, You wont speak to me? Dont you know that I have authority to release you and also to crucify you.
local: A Pilate sʊʊr wʊ, Faa haar ma nɛɛ ɛ? Faa bɔ̃ɔ ɩka n tara na sɔr na vɛ̃ ka ba kʊ fʊ, naa lɛ tara sɔr na faar fʊ bar ɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, You would have no authority over me if it had not been given to you from above. Thats why the one who handed me over to you has the greater sin.
local: Be lɛ a Yesu 'yɛr wʊ, Faa tara kpɛ̃ɔ bãlãa jaa a n jie ɛ, ala ɩ Nãaŋmɩn ba de yin saaju kʊ fʊ ɛ. A lɛ jũu nɩrɛ 'lɔ na nyɔɔ ma wa kʊ fʊ, wʊ lon yeldɛɛr pɔ gar fʊ.

eng: From that moment on, Pilate wanted to release Jesus. However, the Jewish leaders cried out, saying, If you release this man, you arent a friend of the emperor! Anyone who makes himself out to be a king opposes the emperor.
local: An yi a be chiine, ka Pilate pãa mʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ faar a Yesu, tɩchɛ ka a Juu nɩbɛrɛ tãna 'yɛrɛ, Fʊ̃ʊ vɛ̃ ka a daba nyã chen, faa ɩ a Siisa baraa go ɛ. Nɩrɛ jaa na ɩka nãa lɛ wʊ ɩ, wʊ jɛɛrɛ nɩ naa a Siisa.

eng: When Pilate heard these words, he led Jesus out and seated him on the judges bench at the place called Stone Pavement .
local: Pilate na wõ a nɩtɔ̃, wʊ yin naa Yesu tɩ jãnɛ nɩ a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ dakɔɔ ju, a jie ban bʊɔlɔ Kʊsɩpãn. Ka a Juu nɩbɛrɛ mɩ bʊɔlɔ a jie Gabata.

eng: It was about noon on the Preparation Day for the Passover. Pilate said to the Jewish leaders, Heres your king.
local: A Gõl Bar Difʊ bãnsɩ daar mɔ̃tɔ̃tuo sɔ̃ɔ, Pilate 'yɛr kʊ a Juu nɩbɛrɛ, Yɩ nyɛ a yɩ nãa.

eng: The Jewish leaders cried out, Take him away! Take him away! Crucify him! Pilate responded, What? Do you want me to crucify your king? We have no king except the emperor, the chief priests answered.
local: Tɩchɛ ka ba tãna 'yɛrɛ, Kʊ wʊ. Kʊ wʊ. Kpa wʊ yɔɔl a daa ju. Ka a Pilate sʊʊr ba, N kpa a yɩ nãa? A ba 'yɛrɛ, Saa tara nãa jaa ka nãa Siisa ba lɛ ɛ.

eng: Then Pilate handed Jesus over to be crucified. The soldiers took Jesus prisoner.
local: Ka Pilate vɛ̃ a ba nyɔɔ a Yesu chiin na tɩ kpa ka a sojasɩ tara wʊ.

eng: Carrying his cross by himself, he went out to a place called Skull Place .
local: Yesu yin tuo a wʊ mãɛ daa chiin a jie 'lɔ ban bʊɔlɔ a Julaa Jie. Tɩchɛ ka a Juu nɩbɛ mɩ bʊɔlɔ a jie, Gɔlgɔta.

eng: Thats where they crucified him—and two others with him, one on each side and Jesus in the middle.
local: Be lɛ ba kpa wʊ 'matãan nɩbɛ ayi, ka bala be a lõbobo tɩchɛ ka a Yesu be a sãlsɔɔ.

eng: Pilate had a public notice written and posted on the cross. It read Jesus the Nazarene, the king of the Jews.
local: Pilate sɛb sɛbie 'mar a daa ju ɩka, YESU NA YI NAJARETƖ, A JUU NƖBƐ NÃA.

eng: Many of the Jews read this sign, for the place where Jesus was crucified was near the city and it was written in Aramaic, Latin, and Greek.
local: Kɔkɔɛ ata lɛ wʊ sɛb, a Juu nɩbɛ kɔkɔr, Worom nɩbɛ kɔkɔr nɩ a Greek dẽme kɔkɔr. Ka a Juu nɩbɛ yɔɔ kar a sɛbie bojũu a jie ban kpa wʊ ba jãan a yir ɛ.

eng: Therefore, the Jewish chief priests complained to Pilate, Dont write, The king of the Jews but This man said, I am the king of the Jews.
local: Ka a bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ 'yɛr kʊ a Pilate, Ta sɛb ɩka a Juu nɩbɛ nãa ɛ, fʊn taa sɛb ɩka a daba nyã 'yɛr ɩka 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ nãa.

eng: Pilate answered, What Ive written, Ive written.
local: Ka a Pilate 'yɛr, A lɛ ɩn sɛb n sɛb baar.

eng: When the soldiers crucified Jesus, they took his clothes and his sandals, and divided them into four shares, one for each soldier. His shirt was seamless, woven as one piece from the top to the bottom.
local: A sojasɩ na wa kpa a Yesu baar, ba põn a wʊ fuusɩ jiir anaar ka ãsʊɔ jaa 'mɔɔ wʊ bon. A wa chɛn a fuu wõo 'lɔ na ba tara nɛɛ ɛ, bʊ̃bʊ̃'yen lɛ ba wob yin a saaju tɩ baar a gbɛɛ pɔ.

eng: They said to each other, Lets not tear it. Lets cast lots to see who will get it. This was to fulfill the scripture, They divided my clothes among themselves, and they cast lots for my clothing. Thats what the soldiers did.
local: Ka ba 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ taa, Yɩ ta ɩ a sɩ chɩɛr a fuu nyã ɛ, yɩ ɩ a sɩ gba gbãm kaa an lɛ na di? Ba ɩn a lɛ ka a ala na sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ na ɩ yelmãɛ, Ba põn a n fuur tɩ de a n fuu gbãn gbãm. Lɛ lɛ a sojasɩ ɩ.

eng: Jesus mother and his mothers sister, Mary the wife of Clopas, and Mary Magdalene stood near the cross.
local: Yesu ma nɩ a wʊ ma yɛbɛ, Klopasɩ pɔɔ Mɛɛr nɩ a Mɛɛr 'lɔ mɩ na yi a Magdala ara naa be a wʊ daa par.

eng: When Jesus saw his mother and the disciple whom he loved standing nearby, he said to his mother, Woman, here is your son.
local: A Yesu na nyɛ a wʊ ma na ara, ka a poturo 'lɔ wʊn nʊ̃ mɩ ara gbʊr wʊ, ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a ma, A fʊ bie lɛ a 'lɔ.

eng: Then he said to the disciple, Here is your mother. And from that time on, this disciple took her into his home.
local: Na wʊ mɩ 'yɛr kʊ a poturo 'lɔ mɩ, A fʊ ma lɛ. Ala lɛ so ka a poturo 'lɔ tara a Yesu ma kul a wʊ yir.

eng: After this, knowing that everything was already completed, in order to fulfill the scripture, Jesus said, I am thirsty.
local: Yesu bɔ̃ɔn naa pʊ̃pãanyã ɩka yele a jaa pãa baar naa ka a lɛ na sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ, na ɩ yelmãɛ, ka wʊ 'yɛr, Kɔ̃nyũur kpɛ ma naa.

eng: A jar full of sour wine was nearby, so the soldiers soaked a sponge in it, placed it on a hyssop branch, and held it up to his lips.
local: Dʊʊ ben be ka a dãmiir sɛɛ ka ba de sapɔ 'lɔr ir chab urbanyũu daa pɔ tur a Yesu.

eng: When he had received the sour wine, Jesus said, It is completed. Bowing his head, he gave up his life.
local: Wʊn wa mʊʊr a dãmiir ala baar, ka wʊ 'yɛr, A baar naa, tɩ sur a ju na wʊ ŋmaa a vʊʊr.

eng: It was the Preparation Day and the Jewish leaders didnt want the bodies to remain on the cross on the Sabbath, especially since that Sabbath was an important day. So they asked Pilate to have the legs of those crucified broken and the bodies taken down.
local: A bɩbir chiiraa Chɔɔrfʊ Bɩbir Daar lɛ ka a wɛr na chaar ɩ Bɩbir Kpɛ̃ɛ, ala lɛ so ka a Juu nɩbɛ ba bʊɔrɔ ɩka a kũn a chɛnɛ be a daar jur ɛ. Ba sʊɔr a Pilate ɩka wʊ vɛ̃ ka ba kar a ba gbɛɛ tɩ yɔɔ ba.

eng: Therefore, the soldiers came and broke the legs of the two men who were crucified with Jesus.
local: Be lɛ a sojasɩ chen tɩ kar a bala ban lɔ̃ɔ kpan a Yesu gbɛɛ.

eng: When they came to Jesus, they saw that he was already dead so they didnt break his legs.
local: Tɩchɛ ban wa ta a Yesu sɛ̃, ba nyɛn ka a ɩ wʊ kpi naa ka baa kar a wʊ gbɛɛ ɛ.

eng: However, one of the soldiers pierced his side with a spear, and immediately blood and water came out.
local: Ka a sojasɩ ãsʊɔ de a pĩi wõo 'lɔ chɔr a Yesu lõboor ka jãɩ nɩ kʊ̃ɔ yi ajienaa.

eng: The one who saw this has testified, and his testimony is true. He knows that he speaks the truth, and he has testified so that you also can believe.
local: A nɩrɛ 'lɔ na nyɛ a anyãna lɛ di a dãasɩɛ, ka wʊ dãasɩɛ ɩ yelmãɛ. Wʊ bɔ̃ɔ naa ɩka a lɛ wʊn 'yɛr ɩn yelmãɛ, ka yɩ mɩ na sɔɔ de.

eng: These things happened to fulfill the scripture, They wont break any of his bones .
local: A anyãna ɩ naa ka a lɛ na sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩ yelmãɛ ɩka, A wʊ kɔɔr kʊ̃ kab ɛ.

eng: And another scripture says, They will look at him whom they have pierced.
local: Ka ba lɛ sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn jie ãsʊɔ ɩka, Ban kaa naa a 'lɔ ban chɔr.

eng: After this Joseph of Arimathea asked Pilate if he could take away the body of Jesus. Joseph was a disciple of Jesus, but a secret one because he feared the Jewish authorities. Pilate gave him permission, so he came and took the body away.
local: A Josefʊ na yi Arimatɩya, ɩ naa a Yesu poturo, tɩ a Juu nɩbɛrɛ dãbãɛ jũu, waa dɔ̃ɔ vɛ̃ ka ba bɔ̃ɔn ɛ. 'Lɔ lɛ sʊɔr a Yesu a Pilate sɛ̃. Pilate sɔɔ naa ka wʊ tɩ yɔɔ a Yesu.

eng: Nicodemus, the one who at first had come to Jesus at night, was there too. He brought a mixture of myrrh and aloe, nearly seventy-five pounds in all.
local: A 'lɔ nɩ a Nikodimasɩ nyã na dɔ̃ɔ chen a Yesu sɛ̃ a tãsɔɔ pʊɔ wʊ naa, na wʊ mɩ waan a kãnyũur ala ban bʊɔlɔ sɩmura nɩ alosɩ. An tãn nũgbulo lɩjaata nɩ pie nɩ a nũu .

eng: Following Jewish burial customs, they took Jesus body and wrapped it, with the spices, in linen cloths.
local: A ba jaa ayi den a Yesu vil wʊ a fɔpɩla tɩ jɛ wʊ a kãnyũu ala tu a lɛ a Juu nɩbɛ na ma ɩ tɩ ũu a nɩrɛ.

eng: There was a garden in the place where Jesus was crucified, and in the garden was a new tomb in which no one had ever been laid.
local: A jie 'lɔ ban kpa a Yesu, pobile ben be, ka yaa paalaa mɩ ban ba ũu nɩrɛ jaa ɛ, mɩ be be.

eng: Because it was the Jewish Preparation Day and the tomb was nearby, they laid Jesus in it.
local: Bojũu an ɩ a bɩbir chiiraa Chɔɔrfʊ Bɩbir Daar lɛ, tɩchɛ ka a yaa mɩ gbʊr a be jũu, ba ũun a Yesu a be.

eng: Later, Jesus himself appeared again to his disciples at the Sea of Tiberias. This is how it happened.
local: A anyã puor, Yesu lɛ ir wʊ mãɛ wiil kʊ a wʊ poturbo be a Tiberiyas man pɔ. Nɩtɔ̃ lɛ a tu.

eng: Simon Peter, Thomas , Nathanael from Cana in Galilee, Zebedees sons, and two other disciples were together.
local: Simon Pita, Tomasɩ ban bʊɔlɔ Naa, Natanɩɛl na yi Keena na be a Galilee paal pɔ, Jebedee bibiir ayi pʊɔn a poturbo ayi bamɩne 'mantaa.

eng: Simon Peter told them, Im going fishing. They said, Well go with you. They set out in a boat, but throughout the night they caught nothing.
local: Ka a Simon Pita 'yɛr kʊ ba, Maa chiine naa jãmɛ nyɔɔrfʊ jie. Ka ba mɩ 'yɛr, Sɩn 'mantaa chen! Ba yin kpɛ a gboro chen, tɩchɛ a tãsɔɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ baa nyɔɔ bon ɛ.

eng: Early in the morning, Jesus stood on the shore, but the disciples didnt realize it was Jesus.
local: Bibio pipi Yesu ben a gogoro ju ara, tɩchɛ a poturbo ba bɔ̃ɔ ɩka 'lɔ lɛ ɛ.

eng: Jesus called to them, Children, have you caught anything to eat? They answered him, No.
local: Ka wʊ bʊɔl ba sʊʊr, Barmɩne, yaa nyɔɔ jãm ɛ? Ka ba 'yɛr Kai.

eng: He said, Cast your net on the right side of the boat and you will find some. So they did, and there were so many fish that they couldnt haul in the net.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr ba, Yɩ lɔb a lɔyaar 'yɔ̃ɔ a gboro nũsʊ̃ɔ jie, yɩn nyɔɔr amɩne. Ban lɔb a lɔyaar 'yɔ̃ɔ, baa lɛ tʊ̃ɔnɔ̃ tɔɔrɔ ɛ, bojũu wʊ nyɔɔr jãyɔɔ.

eng: Then the disciple whom Jesus loved said to Peter, Its the Lord! When Simon Peter heard it was the Lord, he wrapped his coat around himself and jumped into the water.
local: Ka a poturo 'lɔ a Yesu na nʊ̃ 'yɛr kʊ a Simon Pita, A sɩ Soro lɛ. A lɛ a Pita na wõ ɩka, A sɩ Soro lɛ, wʊ sun a fuu wʊn dɔ̃ɔ yaa bar na wʊ ɔɔ lon a kʊ̃ɔ pɔ.

eng: Early in the morning of the first day of the week, while it was still dark, Mary Magdalene came to the tomb and saw that the stone had been taken away from the tomb.
local: A daa gbul bɩbir dɔ̃ɔ daar wɛr na chaara wa la la la, Mɛɛr na yi a Magdala chen naa a yaa par tɩ nyɛ ka ɩ a kʊsɩr 'lɔ ban pɔɔ a yaa yuo naa.

eng: The other disciples followed in the boat, dragging the net full of fish, for they werent far from shore, only about one hundred yards.
local: Ka a poturbo ba nyã na chɛ turo nɩ a gboro tɔɔrɔ a lɔyaar na sɛɛn a jãmɛ. Baa jãan a gogoro ɛ, an ɩn gbɛɛ kɔɔr ata .

eng: When they landed, they saw a fire there, with fish on it, and some bread.
local: Ban wa do a gogoro ju, ba nyɛn saalaa bũu jãmɛ na be be nɩ paanʊ̃ɔ.

eng: She ran to Simon Peter and the other disciple, the one whom Jesus loved, and said, They have taken the Lord from the tomb, and we dont know where theyve put him.
local: Wʊ jon chen a Simon Pita nɩ a poturo 'lɔ a Yesu na nʊ̃ sɛ̃ tɩ 'yɛr, Ba ir naa a Soro ãgãn a yaa pɔ, ka saa bɔ̃ɔ a jie ban bin wʊ ɛ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Bring some of the fish that youve just caught.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ ir a jãmɛ ala yɩn nyɔɔr a pʊ̃pãanyã amɩne waan.

eng: Peter and the other disciple left to go to the tomb.
local: Ka a Pita nɩ a poturo yi na chen a yaa par.

eng: Simon Peter got up and pulled the net to shore. It was full of large fish, one hundred fifty-three of them. Yet the net hadnt torn, even with so many fish.
local: Ka a Simon Pita kpɛ a gboro pɔ, tɩ tɔɔ a lɔyaar na sɛɛn a jãbɛrɛ don a gogoro ju. A ɩn jãmɛ kɔbaa lɩjaayi nɩ pie nɩ ata , an ɩ a yɔɔ a lɛ, tɩchɛ a lɔyaar ba chɩɛr ɛ.

eng: They were running together, but the other disciple ran faster than Peter and was the first to arrive at the tomb.
local: Ba jaa 'mantaa joro, tɩchɛ ka a poturo 'lɔ jo vɩɛ a Pita bar tɩ de nie ta a yaa par.

eng: Jesus said to them, Come and have breakfast. None of the disciples could bring themselves to ask him, Who are you? They knew it was the Lord.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ wa suor nɛɛ. Tɩ a poturbo ãsʊɔ jaa ba 'la sʊʊr wʊ ɩka, An lɛ fʊ ɩ? Ba jaa bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka a Soro lɛ.

eng: Bending down to take a look, he saw the linen cloths lying there, but he didnt go in.
local: Na wʊ muul nyɛ a fɔpɩla 'lɔ na gã, tɩ waa kpɛ ɛ.

eng: Jesus came, took the bread, and gave it to them. He did the same with the fish.
local: Yesu ir a paanʊ̃ɔ na wʊ kʊ ba, tɩ lɛ ir a jãmɛ mɩ kʊ ba.

eng: Following him, Simon Peter entered the tomb and saw the linen cloths lying there.
local: Be lɛ ka a Pita na turo a puor mɩ ta, na wʊ kpɛ a yaa pɔ, na wʊ nyɛ a fɔpɩla mɩ na gã.

eng: This was now the third time Jesus appeared to his disciples after he was raised from the dead.
local: A nyã lɛ ɩ gbɛɛ ata Yesu na ir a wʊ mãɛ wiil a poturbo wʊn ir a kũu pɔ ɩ vʊr.

eng: He also saw the face cloth that had been on Jesus head. It wasnt with the other clothes but was folded up in its own place.
local: Ka a fɔ-ũu naa 'lɔ ban vil a Yesu ju mɩ pil yi a fɔpɩla pɔ gã wʊ yõ.

eng: When they finished eating, Jesus asked Simon Peter, Simon son of John, do you love me more than these? Simon replied, Yes, Lord, you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Feed my lambs.
local: Ban di baar ka Yesu sʊʊr a Simon Pita, Simon, Jɔɔn bie, fʊ nʊ̃ man gar a banyãna ɔ? Ka wʊ 'yɛr, Ɔ̃ɔ, n Soro, fʊ bɔ̃ɔ naa ɩn nʊ̃ fʊ. A Yesu 'yɛr wʊ, Kʊrɔ a n pelsɩ a bʊ̃diir.

eng: Then the other disciple, the one who arrived at the tomb first, also went inside. He saw and believed.
local: A poturo 'lɔ na dɔ̃ɔ de nie ta a yaa par mɩ pãa kpɛ, na wʊ nyɛ a lɛ tɩ sɔɔ de.

eng: Jesus asked a second time, Simon son of John, do you love me? Simon replied, Yes, Lord, you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Take care of my sheep.
local: Yesu lɛ sʊʊr go, Simon, Jɔɔn bie, fʊ nʊ̃ man sɩrɛ jaa? Ka wʊ 'yɛr, Ɔ̃ɔ, n Soro, fʊ bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩn nʊ̃ fʊ. Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Bɔ̃ɔ kaara ma a piir.

eng: They didnt yet understand the scripture that Jesus must rise from the dead.
local: Baa na bɔ̃ɔ a par sɛrɛ ɛ, ɩka lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn gãn na 'yɛr ɩka a Yesu na ir naa a kũu pɔ, ɩ vʊr ɛ.

eng: He asked a third time, Simon son of John, do you love me? Peter was sad that Jesus asked him a third time, Do you love me? He replied, Lord, you know everything; you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Feed my sheep.
local: Yesu lɛ sʊʊr wʊn gbɛɛ ata, Simon Jɔɔn bie, fʊ nʊ̃ ma naa? Ka a Pita pʊɔ sɔ̃ɔ bojũu gbɛɛ ata nɩ nyã a Yesu na sʊʊr wʊ, ɩka wʊ, Nʊ̃ wʊ naa? Tɩ 'yɛr, N Soro fʊ bɔ̃ɔn yele a jaa, fʊ bɔ̃ɔ naa ɩn nʊ̃ fʊ. A Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Kʊrɔ ma a piir bʊ̃diir.

eng: Then the disciples returned to the place where they were staying.
local: A poturbo ayi bala lɩɛb kul a ba taaba sɛ̃.

eng: I assure you that when you were younger you tied your own belt and walked around wherever you wanted. When you grow old, you will stretch out your hands and another will tie your belt and lead you where you dont want to go.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ fʊ, a fʊ pɔlbile daar fʊ mãɛ ma sun fʊ fuusɩ na fʊ chiin jie 'lɔ jaa fʊn bʊɔrɔ. Tɩchɛ fʊ̃ʊ wa kor baar, fʊ ma jãn a nuur ka nɩrɛ kɔ̃ɔ su fʊ a fuu tɩ tɔɔ fʊ chiin a jie 'lɔ fʊn ba bʊɔrɔ ɛ.

eng: Mary stood outside near the tomb, crying. As she cried, she bent down to look into the tomb.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Mɛɛr ara a yaa par kõnõ. Wʊ kõn wa muul kaa a yaa pɔ.

eng: He said this to show the kind of death by which Peter would glorify God. After saying this, Jesus said to Peter, Follow me.
local: Yesu 'yɛr a nɩtɔ̃ na wʊ wiil a kũu 'lɔ tɔ Pita na wa kpi 'yɔ̃ɔ Nãaŋmɩn yuor tɩɩr. Tɩ pãa 'yɛr kʊ a Pita, Turo ma.

eng: She saw two angels dressed in white, seated where the body of Jesus had been, one at the head and one at the foot.
local: na wʊ nyɛ a malakasɩ ayi na su fuu pɩɛl be a yaa pɔ, ka a ãsʊɔ jãnɛ a ju jie, ka a ãsʊɔ mɩ jãnɛ a gbɛɛ jie, a jie ban dɔ̃ɔ gaal a Yesu.

eng: The angels asked her, Woman, why are you crying? She replied, They have taken away my Lord, and I dont know where theyve put him.
local: Ka ba sʊʊr wʊ, Pɔɔ, bãa 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ fʊ kõnõ? A wʊ 'yɛr ba, Ba ir naa a n Soro, ka n ba bɔ̃ɔ a jie ban bin wʊ ɛ.

eng: Peter turned around and saw the disciple whom Jesus loved following them. This was the one who had leaned against Jesus at the meal and asked him, Lord, who is going to betray you.
local: Pita lɩɛb nyɛ a poturo 'lɔ a Yesu na nʊ̃ ka wʊ turo ba. A poturo nyã lɛ dɔ̃ɔ dɩɛl a Yesu lɛ ban dire a di baaraa, na dɔ̃ɔ sʊʊr a Yesu, N Soro, an lɛ na gar 'yɔ̃ɔ fʊ.

eng: When Peter saw this disciple, he said to Jesus, Lord, what about him.
local: Pita na nyɛ wʊn turo ba, ka wʊ sʊʊr, N Soro, tɩ nyã mɩ.

eng: Jesus replied, If I want him to remain until I come, what difference does that make to you? You must follow me.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr, Maa nʊ̃ ɩka wʊ be be tɩ tãn ɩn na lɩɛb wa, a ɩ bo a fʊ sʊrɔ? Fʊ̃ʊ turo ma.

eng: Therefore, the word spread among the brothers and sisters that this disciple wouldnt die. However, Jesus didnt say he wouldnt die, but only, If I want him to remain until I come, what difference does that make to you.
local: A anyãna jũu lɛ a poturbo 'yɛr yʊɔrɔ nɩ ɩka a poturo 'lɔ kʊ̃ kpi ɛ. Tɩchɛ Yesu ba 'yɛr ɩka wʊ kʊ̃ kpi ɛ, wʊ 'yɛr ɩka, Maa nʊ̃ ɩka wʊ be be tɩ tãn ɩn na lɩɛb wa, a ɩ bo a fʊ sʊrɔ.

eng: This is the disciple who testifies concerning these things and who wrote them down. We know that his testimony is true.
local: A poturo nyãna lɛ di a anyãna dãasɩɛ na wʊ sɛb bin, ka sɩ bɔ̃ɔn a wʊ dãasɩɛ na ɩ yelmãɛ.

eng: Jesus did many other things as well. If all of them were recorded, I imagine the world itself wouldnt have enough room for the scrolls that would be written.
local: Yesu tʊ̃n tʊ̃yɔɔ mɩ, ala ɩ a jaa ta sɛb bʊ̃'yen 'yen, a wɛr wʊ jaa kʊ̃ naa tara jie bin a ala na sɛb ɛ.

eng: As soon as she had said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing there, but she didnt know it was Jesus.
local: A nɩtɔ̃ wʊn 'yɛr, tɩ lɩɛb nyɛ a Yesu na ara, tɩchɛ waa bɔ̃ɔ ɩka 'lɔ lɛ ɛ.

eng: Jesus said to her, Woman, why are you crying? Who are you looking for? Thinking he was the gardener, she replied, Sir, if you have carried him away, tell me where you have put him and I will get him.
local: Ka Yesu sʊʊr wʊ, Pɔɔ, bãa 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ fʊ kõnõ? An lɛ fʊ bʊɔrɔ? Mɛɛr tɩɛr naa ɩka a jikaara lɛ, tɩ 'yɛr, Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, ala ɩ fʊ̃ʊ lɛ ir wʊ, wiil ma a jie fʊn bin, ka n chen tɩ 'mɔɔ wʊ.

eng: Jesus said to her, Mary. She turned and said to him in Aramaic, Rabbouni .
local: A Yesu bʊɔl, Mɛɛr! Ka wʊ lɩɛb 'yɛr nɩ a Juu nɩbɛ kɔkɔr, Woroboni. A par lɛ, Wiwile.

eng: Jesus said to her, Dont hold on to me, for I havent yet gone up to my Father. Go to my brothers and sisters and tell them, Im going up to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.
local: A Yesu 'yɛr, Ta gɔɔ ma ɛ, n ba chen a n Sãa sɛ̃ sɛrɛ ɛ. Chen a n poturbo sɛ̃ tɩ 'yɛr ba, n lɩɛb chiine a n Sãa Nãaŋmɩn mɩ na ɩ a yɩ Sãa Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃.

eng: Mary Magdalene left and announced to the disciples, Ive seen the Lord. Then she told them what he said to her.
local: Ka a Mɛɛr na yi a Magdala chen a poturbo sɛ̃ tɩ 'yɛr, N nyɛn a Soro, na wʊ mɩ man a ala a Yesu na 'yɛr kʊ wʊ.

eng: It was still the first day of the week. That evening, while the disciples were behind closed doors because they were afraid of the Jewish authorities, Jesus came and stood among them. He said, Peace be with you.
local: A daa gbul bɩbir dɔ̃ɔ daar jãanʊɔr, a poturbo lɔ̃ɔn taa a die pɔ, tɩ kpal a pãma a Juu nɩbɛ dãbãɛ jũu. A Yesu tɩ kpɛ ara a ba sɛ̃ tɩ puor ba 'yɛr, Nyã'maar wʊ be a yɩ sɛ̃.

eng: After he said this, he showed them his hands and his side. When the disciples saw the Lord, they were filled with joy.
local: Wʊn 'yɛr a nɩtɔ̃, wʊ wiil ba naa a nuur nɩ a lõboor. Nʊ̃ɔ kpɛ̃n a poturbo ban nyɛ a Soro.

eng: Jesus said to them again, Peace be with you. As the Father sent me, so I am sending you.
local: Yesu lɛ 'yɛr kʊ ba go, Nyã'maar wʊ be a yɩ sɛ̃, lɛ a Sãa na tʊ̃ ma, lɛ lɛ n mɩ tʊ̃nɔ̃ yɩ.

eng: Then he breathed on them and said, Receive the Holy Spirit.
local: Tɩ pãa vʊʊr 'yɔ̃ɔ ba tɩ 'yɛr, Yɩ de a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ.

eng: If you forgive anyones sins, they are forgiven; if you dont forgive them, they arent forgiven.
local: Ala ɩ yɩ vɛ̃n nɩrɛ yeldɛɛr bar, Nãaŋmɩn mɩ na vɛ̃n bar wʊ. Ala ɩ yaa mɩ wa vɛ̃ bar ɛ, Nãaŋmɩn mɩ kʊ̃ vɛ̃ bar ɛ.

eng: Thomas, the one called Didymus, one of the Twelve, wasnt with the disciples when Jesus came.
local: Tomasɩ ban lɛ bʊɔlɔ Naa na ɩ a Poturo Pie Nɩ Ayi ãsʊɔ ba dɔ̃ɔ be be a Yesu na wa ɛ.

eng: The other disciples told him, Weve seen the Lord! But he replied, Unless I see the nail marks in his hands, put my finger in the wounds left by the nails, and put my hand into his side, I wont believe.
local: Ka a poturbo bala na be be nyɛ a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Sɩ nyɛn a Soro, tɩ wʊ 'yɛr, Maa ba nyɛ a natɩɛ a wʊ nũu pɔ ɛ, naa de a n nubir tũ a kpafʊ jie ɛ, naa mɩ lɛ de nũu tũ a lõboor bɔɔ pɔ ɛ, n kʊ̃ sɔɔ de jaa ɛ.

eng: After eight days his disciples were again in a house and Thomas was with them. Even though the doors were locked, Jesus entered and stood among them. He said, Peace be with you.
local: A ber aniin daar, a wʊ poturbo ba jaa lɛ lɔ̃ɔn taa a yir pɔ, ka a Tomasɩ mɩ be be. A pãma kpal naa, tɩchɛ ka a Yesu wa ara a ba sɛ̃ puor ba tɩ 'yɛr, Nyã'maar wʊ be a yɩ sɛ̃.

eng: Then he said to Thomas, Put your finger here. Look at my hands. Put your hand into my side. No more disbelief. Believe.
local: Tɩ pãa 'yɛr kʊ a Tomasɩ, Tur a fʊ nubir a ka, nyɛ a n nuur na fʊ tur a fʊ nũu su a n lõboor bɔɔ pɔ, na fʊ bar a baal tɩ sɔɔ de.

eng: Thomas responded to Jesus, My Lord and my God.
local: Ka a Tomasɩ 'yɛr, N Soro nɩ n Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: Jesus replied, Do you believe because you see me? Happy are those who dont see and yet believe.
local: A Yesu pãa sʊʊr wʊ, Fʊn nyɛ ma jũu lɛ fʊ sɔɔ de ɔ? Pʊpɛl dẽme lɛ a bala na ba nyɛ ɛ, tɩ sɔɔ de.

eng: Then Jesus did many other miraculous signs in his disciples presence, signs that arent recorded in this scroll.
local: Yesu tʊ̃n nɛ'maa tʊ̃ yɔɔ a wʊ poturbo niem, ban ba sɛb 'yɔ̃ɔ a gãn nyã pɔ ɛ.

eng: But these things are written so that you will believe that Jesus is the Christ, Gods Son, and that believing, you will have life in his name.
local: Tɩchɛ a anyãna sɛb naa ka yɩn sɔɔ de ɩka, a Yesu lɛ a Nɩ-iraa, na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Bie. Yɩn sɔɔ de jũu, yɩn tara naa nyɛvʊr yin a wʊ yuor pɔ.

eng: Theophilus, the first scroll I wrote concerned everything Jesus did and taught from the beginning.
local: Teofilusɩ a n gãn dɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ pɔ, n sɛb a ala a jaa a Yesu na dɔ̃ɔ piel maal tɩ na wʊ wiil a nɩbɛ.

eng: right up to the day when he was taken up into heaven. Before he was taken up, working in the power of the Holy Spirit, Jesus instructed the apostles he had chosen.
local: Yesu tu naa a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ pɔ na wʊ wiil a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ bala wʊn ir lɛ ban na ɩrɛ tɩ a Nãaŋmɩn de wʊ don a saaju.

eng: After his suffering, he showed them that he was alive with many convincing proofs. He appeared to them over a period of forty days, speaking to them about Gods kingdom.
local: A wʊ dɔɔyɛ difʊ puor, wʊ wiil wʊ mãɛ kʊ a nɩbɛ banyãna, ka a chaar ba nie ɩka wʊ ir naa na wʊ ɩ vʊr. A ber lɩjaayi daar lɛ wʊ wa dũu ba, na wʊ 'yɛr a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ 'yɛr.

eng: While they were eating together, he ordered them not to leave Jerusalem but to wait for what the Father had promised. He said, This is what you heard from me.
local: Wʊn wa lɔ̃ɔn ba ka ba 'mataa dire, ka wʊ kpãkpãan ba nɩ a 'yɛr bir nyã, Yɩ taa ɩrɛ wa yire a Jerusalɛm ɛ, yɩ chɛlɛ a kʊfʊ 'lɔ a n Sãa na põl ka n 'yɛr kʊ yɩ.

eng: John baptized with water, but in only a few days you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.
local: Jɔɔn 'lɔ, kʊ̃ɔ lɛ wʊ de son a nɩbɛ, tɩchɛ yɩn 'lɔ, a kʊ̃ lɛ kor ɛ, Nãaŋmɩn na so yɩ naa nɩ a wʊ Sɩɛ.

eng: As a result, those who had gathered together asked Jesus, Lord, are you going to restore the kingdom to Israel now.
local: Ba lɛ wa lɔ̃ɔn naa a Yesu go, na ba sʊʊr wʊ, Sɩ Soro, a sɔ̃ɔ nyãna pɔ lɛ fʊn lɩɛb a Isɩral nãalʊ̃ʊ kʊ ba bɩɩ.

eng: Jesus replied, It isnt for you to know the times or seasons that the Father has set by his own authority.
local: A wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, A ba ɩ yɩ 'yɛr ɛ, ɩka yɩ bɔ̃ɔ a sɔ̃ɔ bɩɩ a bɩbir a Sãa na bin a wʊ kpɛ̃ɔ pɔ ɛ.

eng: Rather, you will receive power when the Holy Spirit has come upon you, and you will be my witnesses in Jerusalem, in all Judea and Samaria, and to the end of the earth.
local: Tɩchɛ yɩn nyɛn kpɛ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ wa wa a yɩ sɛ̃, ka yɩ ɩ a n dãasɩɛ dẽme, a Jerusalɛm, nɩ a Judiya paal wʊ jaa, nɩ a Samariya paal tɩ tãn a wɛr tɛɛfʊ jie.

eng: After Jesus said these things, as they were watching, he was lifted up and a cloud took him out of their sight.
local: A anyãna lɛ wʊ 'yɛr baar tɩ duoro a saaju a lɛ nɛ a ba nɩbiru ka a julõjuur tɩ pɔɔ wʊ.

eng: While he was going away and as they were staring toward heaven, suddenly two men in white robes stood next to them.
local: Ka ba chɛnɛ dʊl kaara a saaju a lɛ nɛ wʊn duoro, be lɛ a dɔɔr ayi na su fuu pɩɛl wa dũu ba a lɛ nɛ ara a ba sɛ̃.

eng: They said, Galileans, why are you standing here, looking toward heaven? This Jesus, who was taken up from you into heaven, will come in the same way that you saw him go into heaven.
local: Na ba sʊʊr ba, Galilee dɔɔr, bãa lɛ so a yɩ kaara a saaju a lɛ mɔ̃? A Yesu nyãna nɩ wʊ ju yɩn nyɛ na duoro a saaju, na lɩɛb a lɛ nɛ nɩ wʊ ju wa, nɩtãa a lɛ nɛ yɩn nyɛ wʊn duoro a saaju.

eng: Then they returned to Jerusalem from the Mount of Olives, which is near Jerusalem—a sabbath days journey away.
local: A ba yi a tɔ̃ɔ ban bʊɔlɔ a Olive Tɩɩr Tɔ̃ɔ lɩɛb kul a Jerusalɛm na ba jãan a tẽe ɛ, a fʊn tʊ̃ɔ chen a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar.

eng: When they entered the city, they went to the upstairs room where they were staying. Peter, John, James, and Andrew; Philip and Thomas; Bartholomew and Matthew; James, Alphaeus son; Simon the zealot; and Judas, James son.
local: Ban wa ta, ba don a bɔɔpɩɛ pɔ, be lɛ ba dɔ̃ɔ lɔ̃ɔ taa. Ka a bala na dɔ̃ɔ be be lɛ Pita, Jɔɔn, Jemesɩ nɩ Andiru. Filip, Tomasɩ, Batolomi nɩ Matiu nɩ Alfayasɩ bidaba Jemesɩ nɩ Simon na mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir nɩ a wʊ wɛr yele, nɩ a Jemesɩ bidaba Judas.

eng: all were united in their devotion to prayer, along with some women, including Mary the mother of Jesus, and his brothers.
local: A banyãna ba jaa lɛ lɔ̃ɔ taa nɩ nɛbʊ̃'yen sʊɔrɔ a Nãaŋmɩn, ka a pɔbɔ bamɩne pʊɔ ba ka a Yesu yɛɛr nɩ a wʊ ma Mɛɛr mɩ be be.

eng: During this time, the family of believers was a company of about one hundred twenty persons. Peter stood among them and said.
local: Bɩbir ãsʊɔ lɛ a wʊ poturbo wa lɔ̃ɔ taa na ba ɩ nɩbɛ kɔbaa dɔɔl lɩjɛr. Ka Pita ir ara a ba pɔ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, the scripture that the Holy Spirit announced beforehand through David had to be fulfilled. This was the scripture concerning Judas, who became a guide for those who arrested Jesus.
local: tɩ 'yɛr, Yɛɛr, a sɛɛ naa ka sɩn maal a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na dɔ̃ɔ tu a Nãa David nɛɛ pɔ, a wʊ 'yɛr na wʊ sɛb bin a kõr jaa nɩtãa lɛ a Judas nyã na dɔ̃ɔ de a nɩbɛ nie a ba wa nyɔɔ a Yesu.

eng: This happened even though he was one of us and received a share of this ministry. .
local: Wʊ dɔ̃ɔ ɩ a sɩ ãsʊɔ na wʊ pʊɔ sɩ a tʊ̃mɔ̃ nyã pɔ.

eng: In fact, he bought a field with the payment he received for his injustice. Falling headfirst, he burst open in the middle and all his intestines spilled out.
local: Judas putuyar wʊn nyɛ lɛ ɩka wʊ dãn wɛr, be lɛ wʊ chʊr ju pur ka a nyaba a jaa yi.

eng: This became known to everyone living in Jerusalem, so they called that field in their own language Hakeldama, or Field of Blood.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa na be ka a Jerusalɛm lɛ wõ a 'yɛr nyã na ba bʊɔlɔ a jie a ba kɔkɔr pɔ ɩka, Akeldama. A par lɛ, Jãɩ Wɛr.

eng: It is written in the Psalms scroll, Let his home become deserted and let there be no one living in it; and Give his position of leadership to another. .
local: Ka a Pita 'yɛr, Nɩtɔ̃ lɛ a Nãa David sɛb a wʊ yiel gãn pɔ, A wʊ jie wʊ lɩɛb dãlpʊɔ, ka a nɩrɛ jaa taa kpɩɛrɛ be ɛ, ka, A nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ, wʊ de a wʊ jie.

eng: Therefore, we must select one of those who have accompanied us during the whole time the Lord Jesus lived among us.
local: A lɛ jũu, nɩyuo lɛ sɩn ir ka a nɩnyɩnɛ ɩ a 'lɔ na dɔ̃ɔ pʊɔ sɩ 'mataa yʊɔrɔ nɩ a Yesu.

eng: beginning from the baptism of John until the day when Jesus was taken from us. This person must become along with us a witness to his resurrection.
local: Piel a Jɔɔn Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ sofʊ sɔ̃ɔ wa tãn a sɔ̃ɔ nyã a Yesu na yi a sɩ sɛ̃ do a saaju. A nɩrɛ 'lɔ lɛ na pʊɔ sɩ dire a wʊ kũu pɔ irfʊ dãasɩɛ.

eng: So they nominated two: Joseph called Barsabbas, who was also known as Justus, and Matthias.
local: Be lɛ ba tɩɛr 'yɔ̃ɔ nɩbɛ ayi. Josefʊ ban bʊɔlɔ Basabasɩ na ba lɛ bʊɔlɔ wʊ Jusɩtusɩ nɩ Matɩyasɩ.

eng: They prayed, Lord, you know everyones deepest thoughts and desires. Show us clearly which one you have chosen from among these two.
local: Na ba pãa sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛrɛ, Sɩ Soro, fʊ̃ʊ lɛ bɔ̃ɔ nɩrɛ jaa pʊtɩɛr, wiil sɩ a nɩbɛ ayi banyãna ãsʊɔ fʊn na ir.

eng: to take the place of this ministry and apostleship, from which Judas turned away to go to his own place.
local: Ka wʊn de a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ tʊ̃mɔ̃ jie a Judas na bar, tɩ chen a jie wʊn pʊɔ.

eng: When they cast lots, the lot fell on Matthias. He was added to the eleven apostles.
local: A ba pãa gba gbãm ka a gbãbie tɩ ir Matɩyasɩ a wʊ pʊɔ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ pie nɩ bʊ̃'yen pɔ.

eng: When Pentecost Day arrived, they were all together in one place.
local: A Pentikosɩ na wa ta, ba jaa ben a jibʊ̃'yen.

eng: Suddenly a sound from heaven like the howling of a fierce wind filled the entire house where they were sitting.
local: Ajienaa lɛ ka a damfʊ na kaara fɩka sɛsɛkpɛ̃ɛ na furo yi a saaju wa sɛɛ a die 'lɔ pɔ ban jãnɛ.

eng: They saw what seemed to be individual flames of fire alighting on each one of them.
local: Be lɛ ba nyɛ bũu 'lɩraa na ŋmara jaa pʊrpʊr dɔɔlɔ ãsʊɔ jaa bʊ̃'yen 'yenɩ.

eng: They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began to speak in other languages as the Spirit enabled them to speak.
local: Ka ba jaa sɛɛn a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na ba piel 'yɛrɛ kɔkɔyor lɛ a Sɩɛ na kʊ ba.

eng: There were pious Jews from every nation under heaven living in Jerusalem.
local: A Juu nɩbɛ na joro a Nãaŋmɩn dãbãɛ na ba yi a tẽn tẽn wa kpɩɛrɛ a be a Jerusalɛm.

eng: When they heard this sound, a crowd gathered. They were mystified because everyone heard them speaking in their native languages.
local: Ban wa wõ a gɔ̃mɔ̃ nyã, a nɩyɔɔ ba nyãna ba jaa wa lɔ̃ɔn taa na ba ɩ chĩcham, bojũu ãsʊɔ ma wõn ban 'yɛrɛ a wʊ kɔkɔr.

eng: They were surprised and amazed, saying, Look, arent all the people who are speaking Galileans, every one of them?.
local: Ka a ɩ yel dũu naa, na a 'maa ba jaa nɛɛ ka ba sʊrɔ taa, A ba ɩ Galilee dẽme lɛ a banyãna na 'yɛrɛ ɛ?.

eng: How then can each of us hear them speaking in our native language?.
local: Tɩ ŋmɩnɛ a ɩ ka a sɩ ãsʊɔ jaa wõnõ ban 'yɛrɛ nɩ a sɩ kɔkɔɛ?.

eng: Parthians, Medes, and Elamites; as well as residents of Mesopotamia, Judea, and Cappadocia, Pontus and Asia.
local: Parti dẽme, Mede dẽme, nɩ a Elam dẽme. Bamɩne mɩ yin Mesopotamiya, Judiya, Kapadosiya nɩ Pontus nɩ Asɩya.

eng: Phrygia and Pamphylia, Egypt and the regions of Libya bordering Cyrene; and visitors from Rome.
local: Ka bamɩne mɩ yi Fɩrgɩya, nɩ Pamfiliya, nɩ Ijipitɩ, nɩ a Libiya dẽme na gbʊr a Sirenɩ wɛr nɩ a sãanba na yi a Worom.

eng: Cretans and Arabs—we hear them declaring the mighty works of God in our own languages.
local: a Juu nɩbɛ mãɛ nɩ a bala na lɩɛb a Juu nɩbɛ, bamɩne yin Kɩrete nɩ a Arabɩya dẽme. Sɩ wõn ban de a sɩ mãɛ kɔkɔr 'yɛrɛ nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn tʊ̃bɛrɛ wʊn tʊ̃.

eng: They were all surprised and bewildered. Some asked each other, What does this mean.
local: Ka a anyãna sɔb ba nɩbir ka ba nɛɛr 'maa a ba sʊrɔ taa, A anyãna par nɩ bo.

eng: Others jeered at them, saying, Theyre full of new wine.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a bamɩne kpɛrɛ 'yɛrɛ, Dãa lɛ maal ba a lɛ nɛ.

eng: Peter stood with the other eleven apostles. He raised his voice and declared, Judeans and everyone living in Jerusalem! Know this! Listen carefully to my words!.
local: Ka a Pita nɩ a Pie Nɩ Bʊ̃'yen pãa ir ara ka wʊ 'yɛr kpɛ̃ɔ jaa kʊ a nɩbɛ, Juu nɩbɛ nɩ nɩrɛ jaa na kpɩɛrɛ ka a Jerusalɛm, yɩ vɛ̃ ka n wiil yɩ a par. Yɩ chɛlɛ a lɛ ɩn na 'yɛr.

eng: These people arent drunk, as you suspect; after all, its only nine oclock in the morning!.
local: A nɩbɛ banyãna ba nyũ dãa ɛ, nɩtãa a lɛ yɩn tɩɛr ɛ, a chɛnɛ na ɩ bibi'maar.

eng: Rather, this is what was spoken through the prophet Joel.
local: Nɩtɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ Joɛl dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr.

eng: In the last days, God says, I will pour out my Spirit on all people. Your sons and daughters will prophesy. Your young will see visions. Your elders will dream dreams. .
local: Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr ɩka a ber baaraa daar, wʊn kpaaraa a wʊ Sɩɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ a nɩbɛ ba jaa. Ka a yɩ dabil nɩ a pɔɔbil 'yɛrɛ a 'yɛrsɩ ala a Nãaŋmɩn na kʊ ba, ka a yɩ dabil nyɛrɛ nyɛfʊsɩ, ka a yɩ danyɔ̃n mɩ jãna jãnsɩ.

eng: Even upon my servants, men and women, I will pour out my Spirit in those days, and they will prophesy. .
local: A bala gba na ɩ a n gbãgbadɔɔr nɩ a pɔbɔ, ba jaa pɔ lɛ ɩn kpaar a n Sɩɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ ba a ber ala pɔ, ka ba 'yɛrɛ a n 'yɛr.

eng: I will cause wonders to occur in the heavens above and signs on the earth below, blood and fire and a cloud of smoke. .
local: Ɩn wiil ban yelfɛrsɩ na yi saaju ka a jãnsɩ mɩ be ka a tẽe, jãɩ, bũu nɩ jʊʊr na den jie wʊ jaa.

eng: The sun will be changed into darkness, and the moon will be changed into blood, before the great and spectacular day of the Lord comes. .
local: Ka a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ lɩɛb lĩi ka a ŋmɛra mɩ lɩɛb jãɩ kaar tɩchɛ ka a sɩ Soro bɩbir 'lɔn na chaar pãa wa.

eng: And everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved. .
local: Be lɛ nɩrɛ jaa na pãa bʊɔlɔ a sɩ Soro yuor na nyɛn faafʊ.

eng: Fellow Israelites, listen to these words! Jesus the Nazarene was a man whose credentials God proved to you through miracles, wonders, and signs, which God performed through him among you. You yourselves know this.
local: Isɩral nɩbɛ yɩ chɛlɛ ka, A Yesu nyã na yi a Najaretɩ 'lɔ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn ir kʊ yɩ, na wʊ de wʊ tʊ̃n a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃, nɩ tʊ̃fɛrsɩ, nɩ jãnsɩ tʊ̃mɔ̃ a yɩ pɔ, ka yɩ mãɛ bɔ̃ɔn.

eng: In accordance with Gods established plan and foreknowledge, he was betrayed. You, with the help of wicked men, had Jesus killed by nailing him to a cross.
local: Ba nyɔɔn a daba nyãna 'yɔ̃ɔ a yɩ nuur pɔ nɩtãa lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na dɔ̃ɔ tu a wʊ bɔfʊ nɩ a wʊ bɔ̃ɔfʊ pɔ, de nie 'yɛr bin, ka a putudẽme sʊ̃ʊ yɩ ka yɩ kpa wʊ a daa ju a wʊ kpi.

eng: God raised him up! God freed him from deaths dreadful grip, since it was impossible for death to hang on to him.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Nãaŋmɩn sãɩ wʊ a kũu pɔ, na wʊ faa wʊ a kũu wõm pɔ, bojũu a kũu ba tara kpɛ̃ɔ na nyɔɔ wʊ taa lɛ ɛ.

eng: David says about him, I foresaw that the Lord was always with me; because he is at my right hand I wont be shaken. .
local: A Nãa David dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr a wʊ 'yɛr, Bɩbir jaa lɛ n nyɛrɛ a n Soro, bojũu wʊ ben a n nũsʊ̃ɔ jie, n kʊ̃ wa lɔɔ gbɛr ɛ.

eng: Therefore, my heart was glad and my tongue rejoiced. Moreover, my body will live in hope, .
local: Ala lɛ so ka n pʊɔ pɛl ka n jãlbir chɩlɛ, ka a n ãgãn mɩ tara tɩɛr tɔfʊ jie.

eng: because you wont abandon me to the grave, nor permit your holy one to experience decay. .
local: Bojũu fʊ kʊ̃ wa bar ma be a yaa pɔ ɛ, bɩɩ ka fʊ bar a fʊ Chɛchɛ Sʊɔ 'lɔ ka wʊ pʊ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: You have shown me the paths of life; your presence will fill me with happiness. .
local: Fʊ wiil man a nyɛvʊr sɔr, fʊn vɛ̃n ka nʊ̃ɔ kpɛ ma a fʊ niem.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I can speak confidently about the patriarch David. He died and was buried, and his tomb is with us to this very day.
local: N yɛɛr, n 'yɛr chãa pɔ kʊ yɩ ɩka a sɩ sãakpã David kpi naa a ba ũu, a wʊ yaa lɛ anyãna na be ka dɩna.

eng: Because he was a prophet, he knew that God promised him with a solemn pledge to seat one of his descendants on his throne.
local: Tɩchɛ wʊ dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na tɩr nɛɛ kʊ wʊ ɩka, wʊn vɛ̃ naa ka 'lɔ David yɔ̃ɔ jãnɛ a wʊ nãa dakɔɔ ju.

eng: Having seen this beforehand, David spoke about the resurrection of Christ, that he wasnt abandoned to the grave, nor did his body experience decay .
local: David den nie nyɛ a lɛ na be a wʊ niem, ala lɛ so ka wʊ 'yɛr a Kɩrɩsɩto kũu pɔ irfʊ 'yɛr, na wʊ 'yɛr ɩka wʊ kʊ̃ gã be a yaa pɔ ɛ, bɩɩ ka a wʊ ãgãn pʊ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: This Jesus God raised up. We are all witnesses to that fact.
local: Nãaŋmɩn sãɩ naa a Yesu nyãna ka wʊ ir ɩ vʊr, sɩn lɛ ɩ a anyãna a jaa dãasɩɛ dẽme.

eng: He was exalted to Gods right side and received from the Father the promised Holy Spirit. He poured out this Spirit, and you are seeing and hearing the results of his having done so.
local: 'Lɔ lɛ wʊ 'mɔɔ don bin a wʊ nũsʊ̃ɔ jie, ka a Sãa kʊ a Yesu a wʊ Sɩɛ 'lɔ wʊn tɩr a nɛɛ kʊ wʊ. A Sɩɛ nyã lɛ wʊ kpaar 'yɔ̃ɔ sɩ ka yɩ nyɛ tɩ be wõnõ.

eng: David didnt ascend into heaven. Yet he says, The Lord said to my Lord, Sit at my right side, .
local: David ba dɔ̃ɔ do a saaju ɛ, tɩ wʊ 'yɛr naa, A Soro 'yɛr kʊ a n Soro, Jãnɛ a n nũsʊ̃ɔ jie.

eng: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet. .
local: ka n wa de a fʊ dɔ̃dẽme ka ba ɩ a fʊ gbɛɛ dɔɔlfʊ jie. .

eng: Therefore, let all Israel know beyond question that God has made this Jesus, whom you crucified, both Lord and Christ.
local: A lɛ jũu, vɛ̃ ka a Isɩral nɩbɛ ba jaa bɔ̃ɔ sɩrɛ jaa ɩka Nãaŋmɩn maal naa Yesu nyã yɩn kpa a daa ju ka wʊ ɩ a sɩ Soro nɩ a Nɩ-iraa.

eng: When the crowd heard this, they were deeply troubled. They said to Peter and the other apostles, Brothers, what should we do.
local: A nɩbɛ na wõ a nɩtɔ̃, ka a ŋmara ba a nyãn, ka ba sʊʊr a Pita nɩ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ, N yɛɛr, ŋmɩnɛ sɩn ɩ.

eng: Peter replied, Change your hearts and lives. Each of you must be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins. Then you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
local: Ka a Pita 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ lɩɛb a yɩ tɩɛr, ka ba de a Yesu yuor son yɩ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ, ka a yɩ yelbier baar. Ka a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ yɩ a wʊ Sɩɛ.

eng: This promise is for you, your children, and for all who are far away—as many as the Lord our God invites.
local: Yɩn nɩ a yɩ bibiir lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn tɩr a wʊ nɛɛ kʊ, yɩn nɩ a bala ba jaa na be a tɛɛr, nɩ a bala ba jaa a sɩ Soro Nãaŋmɩn na wa bʊɔl.

eng: With many other words he testified to them and encouraged them, saying, Be saved from this perverse generation.
local: Pita den 'yɛr yɔɔ kpãan ba, na wʊ kpãkpãan ba 'yɛrɛ, Yɩ faa yɩ mãɛ yin a dɩna nɩfɩlefɩlesɩ pɔ.

eng: Those who accepted Peters message were baptized. God brought about three thousand people into the community on that day.
local: Ka a bala na sɔɔ de a wʊ 'yɛr, ba so naa a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ, ka nɩbɛ tur ata pʊɔ ba a daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃.

eng: The believers devoted themselves to the apostles teaching, to the community, to their shared meals, and to their prayers.
local: Ba den ba mãɛ kʊ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ wiilfʊ, na ba tara nɛbʊ̃'yen lɔ̃ɔ dire, na ba lɔ̃ɔ puoro a Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: A sense of awe came over everyone. God performed many wonders and signs through the apostles.
local: Dãbãɛ kpɛ̃n nɩrɛ jaa a lɛ ban nyɛ ka a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ tʊ̃ a tʊ̃fɛrsɩ nɩ a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃.

eng: All the believers were united and shared everything.
local: A bala na sɔɔ de, ba jaa lɔ̃ɔn taa jibʊ̃'yen na ba sʊ̃nɔ̃ taa.

eng: They would sell pieces of property and possessions and distribute the proceeds to everyone who needed them.
local: Na ba daara a ba bʊ̃tarsɩ na ba ma põ ir kʊ taa lɛ jaa ãsʊɔ na bʊɔrɔ.

eng: Every day, they met together in the temple and ate in their homes. They shared food with gladness and simplicity.
local: Bɩbir jaa lɛ ba ma lɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ taa be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir bɛchaar pɔ, na ba yʊɔrɔ taa yie ŋmara a paanʊ̃ɔ dire nɩ pʊpɛl nɩ pʊtɩɛr bʊ̃'yen.

eng: They praised God and demonstrated Gods goodness to everyone. The Lord added daily to the community those who were being saved.
local: Ba pɛɛrɛ naa Nãaŋmɩn ka a nɩbɛ ba jaa mɩ ara a ba puor. A sɩ Soro ma vɛ̃ naa ka a bala na nyɛ faafʊ, pʊɔ ba bɩbir jaa.

eng: While Peter and John were speaking to the people, the priests, the captain of the temple guard, and the Sadducees confronted them.
local: A lɛ a Pita nɩ a Jɔɔn na chɛnɛ 'yɛrɛ nɩ a nɩbɛ, a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlorbo nɩ a puorfʊ yir nɩdiere 'matãan a Saduseemɩne wan a ba sɛ̃.

eng: They were incensed that the apostles were teaching the people and announcing that the resurrection of the dead was happening because of Jesus.
local: Ba wa nyɛn suur, bojũu ba wile naa a nɩbɛ ɩka Yesu jũu nɩrɛ jaa na ir naa kũu pɔ.

eng: They seized Peter and John and put them in prison until the next day.
local: Be lɛ ba nyɔɔ a Pita nɩ a Jɔɔn na ba 'yɔ̃ɔ ba pɔɔfʊ die pɔ bar ka wɛr na chaar, bojũu mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ mʊr baar.

eng: Many who heard the word became believers, and their number grew to about five thousand.
local: Tɩchɛ a bala na wõ a lɛ a Pita na 'yɛr a Yesu 'yɛr na ba sɔɔ de na ɩn dɔɔr tur anũu .

eng: The next day the leaders, elders, and legal experts gathered in Jerusalem.
local: A wɛr na wa chaar, a ba nɩdierbe nɩ a ba nɩbɛrɛ 'matãan a Mosesɩ nɛɛ wiwiilbe lɔ̃ɔn taa be a Jerusalɛm.

eng: along with Annas the high priest, Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and others from the high priests family.
local: Ka Anasɩ na ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ nɩ Kayafasɩ, Jɔɔn nɩ Aleksanda 'matãan a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ nɩbɛ bamɩne.

eng: They had Peter and John brought before them and asked, By what power or in what name did you do this.
local: A ba tɩ yuo a Pita nɩ a Jɔɔn waan a ba wa sʊrɔ ba, Kpɛ̃ɔ buor nɩ yobuor lɛ yɩ de tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ a anyãna.

eng: Then Peter, inspired by the Holy Spirit, answered, Leaders of the people and elders.
local: Ka a Pita na sɛɛn a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ 'yɛr kʊ ba, A yɩn nɩdierbe nɩ a yɩn nɩbɛrɛ.

eng: are we being examined today because something good was done for a sick person, a good deed that healed him?.
local: ala ɩ yɩ ben bʊɔl sɩ dɩna ɩka sɩ wa man a maal vɩla nyã na vɛ̃ ka a gbɛrɛ sa.

eng: If so, then you and all the people of Israel need to know that this man stands healthy before you because of the name of Jesus Christ the Nazarene—whom you crucified but whom God raised from the dead.
local: Yɩ pãa bɔ̃ɔ ɩka yaa, a yɩn nɩ nɩrɛ jaa na be a Isɩral, ɩka a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto nyã na yi a Najaretɩ yuor pɔ, a 'lɔ yɩn kʊ a daa ju, tɩchɛ ka a Nãaŋmɩn sãɩ wʊ a kũu pɔ, ka wʊ ɩ vʊr, lɛ sa a daba nyãna na ben ara a kana.

eng: This Jesus is the stone you builders rejected; he has become the cornerstone!.
local: Wʊ ɩ naa A kʊsɩr 'lɔ a yɩn mɩmɩɛrbɛ na tɔr bar, 'lɔ lɛ wa ɩ a kʊsɩr ju.

eng: Salvation can be found in no one else. Throughout the whole world, no other name has been given among humans through which we must be saved.
local: Fafaara jaa ba lɛ be be ɛ, bojũu yuor jaa ba lɛ be ka a tẽe pɔ, na tʊ̃ɔ nɩrɛ faa ɛ.

eng: The council was caught by surprise by the confidence with which Peter and John spoke. After all, they understood that these apostles were uneducated and inexperienced. They also recognized that they had been followers of Jesus.
local: Ban nyɛ a lɛ a Pita nɩ a Jɔɔn na ba joro dãbãɛ ɛ, tɩ mɩ bɔ̃ɔn ban ba jãn gãn chen tɛɛr ɛ, ka nɛ'maa ba. Ka ba tɩɛrtɔ ban dɔ̃ɔ be a Yesu sɛ̃.

eng: However, since the healed man was standing with Peter and John before their own eyes, they had no rebuttal.
local: Tɩchɛ ba mɩ nyɛn a daba 'lɔ na sa 'mataa ara nɩ ba, baa lɛ tara 'yɛr na 'yɛr ɛ.

eng: After ordering them to wait outside, the council members began to confer with each other.
local: Lɛ lɛ ba 'yɛr ba a ba yi chɛ̃chɛ̃ɛ, tɩ ba pãa guoro taa 'yɛrɛ.

eng: What should we do with these men? Everyone living in Jerusalem is aware of the sign performed through them. Its obvious to everyone and we cant deny it.
local: Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ sɩn ɩ a nɩbɛ banyãna? Nɩrɛ jaa na be ka a Jerusalɛm bɔ̃ɔn naa a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ ban tʊ̃ a sɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ chiir ɛ.

eng: To keep it from spreading further among the people, we need to warn them not to speak to anyone in this name.
local: Tɩchɛ ala ɩ sɩn pɔɔ naa a yele nyãna sɔr ka nɩrɛ kʊ̃ lɛ wõ naa ɛ, yɩ ɩ a sɩ fuu ba ka ba taa ɩrɛ wa pʊrɔ a yuor nyãna kʊrɔ nɩrɛ jaa ɛ.

eng: When they called Peter and John back, they demanded that they stop all speaking and teaching in the name of Jesus.
local: Be lɛ ba bʊɔl ba a ba kpɛ ka ba kpãkpãan ba ɩka, ba taa ɩrɛ wa 'yɛrɛ bɩɩ wile nɩ a Yesu yuor go ɛ.

eng: Peter and John responded, Its up to you to determine whether its right before God to obey you rather than God.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Pita nɩ a Jɔɔn 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩn mãɛ yɩ pɛ kaa, a sɛɛn Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ ka sɩn tu a yɩ nɛɛ gar a Nãaŋmɩn bɩɩ?.

eng: As for us, we cant stop speaking about what we have seen and heard.
local: Bojũu sɩn 'lɔ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ bar a 'yɛr sɩn nyɛn nɩbir tɩ wõ 'yɛrfʊ ɛ.

eng: They threatened them further, then released them. Because of public support for Peter and John, they couldnt find a way to punish them. Everyone was praising God for what had happened.
local: Lɛ lɛ ba vuur ba tɩ bar ba a ba chen. Baa bɔ̃ɔ kpãa buor lɛ ban kpãa ba ɛ, bojũu a nɩbɛ ba jaa pɛɛrɛ naa Nãaŋmɩn a lɛ na ɩ.

eng: because the man who had experienced this sign of healing was over years old.
local: Bojũu a daba nyãna na sa a nɛ'maa safʊ nyãna kor gar yuomo lɩjaayi .

eng: After their release, Peter and John returned to the brothers and sisters and reported everything the chief priests and elders had said.
local: Ban wa bar a Pita nɩ a Jɔɔn, ba chen a ba taaba sɛ̃ tɩ man ala a jaa a bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ na 'yɛr kʊ ba.

eng: They listened, then lifted their voices in unison to God, Master, you are the one who created the heaven, the earth, the sea, and everything in them.
local: A ba taaba na wõ a nɩtɔ̃nɔ̃, ba jaa 'mantaa nɩ nɛbʊ̃'yen puor Nãaŋmɩn na ba 'yɛrɛ, Tʊ̃ɔnɔ̃ Sʊɔ, fʊ̃ʊ lɛ maal a saaju nɩ a tẽe nɩ a man kpɛ̃ɛ nɩ bon jaa na be a pɔ.

eng: You are the one who spoke by the Holy Spirit through our ancestor David, your servant: Why did the Gentiles rage , and the peoples plot in vain? .
local: Fʊ̃ʊ lɛ dɔ̃ɔ vɛ̃ ka a fʊ Sɩɛ haa a fʊ gbãgbaa na ɩ a sɩ sãa David nɛɛ ka wʊ 'yɛr, Bo lɛ so ka a paalsɩ nyɛ suur, ka a nɩbɛ ŋmara a dɔɔl pɔrsɩ?.

eng: The kings of the earth took their stand and the rulers gathered together as one against the Lord and against his Christ. .
local: A wɛr nãmɩne tʊɔrɔ naa gbɛchile, ka a nɩbɛrɛ mɩ lɔ̃ɔ taa jɛɛrɛ nɩ a Soro nɩ a fʊ Nɩ-iraa.

eng: Indeed, both Herod and Pontius Pilate, with Gentiles and Israelites, did gather in this city against your holy servant Jesus, whom you anointed.
local: Sɩrɛ jaa lɛ a Hɛrɔd nɩ a Pontius Pilate lɔ̃ɔ taa nɩ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, nɩ a Juu nɩbɛ a tẽe nyãna pɔ, na ba jɛb a fʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃chɛchɛ Yesu, a 'lɔ fʊn ir.

eng: They did what your power and plan had already determined would happen.
local: Ba maal naa a lɛ fʊn kaa ir na ɩ a fʊ pʊtɩɛr, nɩ a fʊ kpɛ̃ɔ, nɩ a fʊ bɔfʊ fʊn dɔ̃ɔ de nie chɔɔr tɩ a wa ɩ.

eng: Now, Lord, take note of their threats and enable your servants to speak your word with complete confidence.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã sɩ Soro, kaa lɛ ban fuu sɩ, tɩ na fʊ sʊ̃ʊ a fʊ gbãgbaar a sɩ mʊɔl a fʊ 'yɛr nɩ 'lafʊ.

eng: Stretch out your hand to bring healing and enable signs and wonders to be performed through the name of Jesus, your holy servant.
local: Tur a fʊ nũu sãan baalsɩ, na fʊ tʊ̃ nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ nɩ a fʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃chɛchɛ Yesu yuor.

eng: After they prayed, the place where they were gathered was shaken. They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began speaking Gods word with confidence.
local: Ban wa puor baar, a jie 'lɔ ban lɔ̃ɔ taa damaa. Ka ba jaa sɛɛn a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ, na ba 'yɛrɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr nɩ 'lafʊ.

eng: The community of believers was one in heart and mind. None of them would say, This is mine! about any of their possessions, but held everything in common.
local: Ka a bala na sɔɔ de, ba jaa tara na tɩɛr bʊ̃'yen nɩ nyãbʊ̃'yen. Ɩ̃sʊɔ jaa maa 'yɛr ɩka a wʊ bonsɩ ɩ naa a 'lɔ tɛɛ bon ɛ, ba ma põn bon jaa ban tara.

eng: The apostles continued to bear powerful witness to the resurrection of the Lord Jesus, and an abundance of grace was at work among them all.
local: A nɩtʊ̃nsɩ den kpɛ̃ɔ mana nɩ a sɩ Soro Yesu kũu pɔ irfʊ 'yɛr, ka a Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ be a ba jaa sɛ̃.

eng: There were no needy persons among them. Those who owned properties or houses would sell them, bring the proceeds from the sales.
local: Ba ãsʊɔ jaa ba dɔ̃ɔ faa bon ɛ, sɔ̃ɔ jaa lɛ a bala na tara por nɩ yie ma da na ba ma waan a libie.

eng: and place them in the care and under the authority of the apostles. Then it was distributed to anyone who was in need.
local: wa bin a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ sɛ̃, ka ba ma põ ir kʊ ãsʊɔ jaa lɛ an sɛɛn wʊ.

eng: Joseph, whom the apostles nicknamed Barnabas , was a Levite from Cyprus.
local: Josefʊ na yi Sipurusɩ na wʊ ɩ a Levi bal nɩrɛ, kaa a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ bʊɔlɔ wʊ Banabasɩ. A par lɛ nɩrɛ na 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ãkpãɛn.

eng: He owned a field, sold it, brought the money, and placed it in the care and under the authority of the apostles.
local: Wʊ dãn a wʊ puo, na wʊ de a libie waan wa bin a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ sɛ̃.

eng: However, a man named Ananias, along with his wife Sapphira, sold a piece of property.
local: Ka a daba ãsʊɔ na be be di Ananias ka a wʊ pɔɔ mɩ di Safira. Ba mɩ den a ba pobile da.

eng: With his wifes knowledge, he withheld some of the proceeds from the sale. He brought the rest and placed it in the care and under the authority of the apostles.
local: Wʊ ir naa libie amɩne bin ka a pɔɔ bɔ̃ɔn, tɩchɛ na wʊ chiin a ŋmaa tɩ bin a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ sɛ̃.

eng: Peter asked, Ananias, how is it that Satan has influenced you to lie to the Holy Spirit by withholding some of the proceeds from the sale of your land?.
local: Ka a Pita sʊʊr a Ananias, Bo lɛ so ka a Satan sɛɛ fʊ a nyãa a fʊ ŋmaa jir kara yɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ, na fʊ ir a libie amɩne a lɛ fʊn da a puo bin?.

eng: Wasnt that property yours to keep? After you sold it, wasnt the money yours to do with whatever you wanted? What made you think of such a thing? You havent lied to other people but to God.
local: A ba ɩ fʊ̃ʊ lɛ so na fʊ de da ɛ? Fʊn wa da baar a ba ɩ fʊ̃ʊ lɛ so a libie ɛ? Bo lɛ ɩ a fʊ tɩɛr a fʊ maal a nɩtɔ̃? Nɩrɛ ba lɛ fʊ ŋmaa a jir nyã 'yɔ̃ɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn lɛ.

eng: When Ananias heard these words, he dropped dead. Everyone who heard this conversation was terrified.
local: Ananias na wõ a nɩtɔ̃nɔ̃ na wʊ lo kpi a jie. Ka a dãbãɛ kpɛ a bala jaa na wõ naa.

eng: Some young men stood up, wrapped up his body, carried him out, and buried him.
local: Ka a pɔlbil ir a ba pɔ 'mɔɔ a kũu lũ tuo yin tɩ ũu.

eng: About three hours later, his wife entered, but she didnt know what had happened to her husband.
local: An wa kor bãlãa ka a wʊ pɔɔ mɩ wa bʊ̃n dɔɔl ba bɔ̃ɔ a lɛ na ɩ ɛ.

eng: Peter asked her, Tell me, did you and your husband receive this price for the field? She responded, Yes, thats the amount.
local: ka a Pita sʊʊr a pɔɔ, A libie a jaa lɛ a anyãna a fʊ̃ʊ nɩ a Ananias na da a puo nyɛ ɔ? 'Yɛr kʊ ma. A wʊ 'yɛr, Ɔ̃ɔ, lɛ lɛ.

eng: He replied, How could you scheme with each other to challenge the Lords Spirit? Look! The feet of those who buried your husband are at the door. They will carry you out too.
local: A Pita 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ a ɩ a yɩ guor taa ɩka yɩ bɛɛr a sɩ Soro Sɩɛ kaa? Nyɛ a nɩbɛ bala a dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ bala lɛ tuo a fʊ sɩrɛ yin tɩ ũu, ba mɩn tuo fʊn yin.

eng: At that very moment, she dropped dead at his feet. When the young men entered and found her dead, they carried her out and buried her with her husband.
local: Ajienaa ka a ɩ wʊ mɩ lon kpi be a Pita niem. A pɔlbil bala na ben wa kpɛ nyɛ ka a ɩ wʊ kpi naa, ka ba tuo wʊ yin tɩ ũu lan a sɩrɛ.

eng: Trepidation and dread seized the whole church and all who heard what had happened.
local: Ka a dãbãɛ kpɛ a pupuorbiir ba jaa nɩ nɩrɛ jaa na wõ naa.

eng: The apostles performed many signs and wonders among the people. They would come together regularly at Solomons Porch.
local: A nɩtʊ̃nsɩ tʊ̃n nɛ'maa tʊ̃ yɔɔ a nɩbɛ sɔ̃ɔ. Ka a bala na sɔɔ de, ba jaa tara na nɛbʊ̃'yen ma lɔ̃ɔ taa a be a Solomon Bɛchaar pɔ.

eng: No one from outside the church dared to join them, even though the people spoke highly of them.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa kpire ba naa, tɩ ãsʊɔ jaa maa 'la lɔ̃ɔn ba ɛ.

eng: Indeed, more and more believers in the Lord, large numbers of both men and women, were added to the church.
local: Tɩchɛ nɩyɔɔ jaa chɛnɛ na waara, ka dɔɔr nɩ pɔbɔ na sɔɔ de a sɩ Soro pʊɔrɔ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃.

eng: As a result, they would even bring the sick out into the main streets and lay them on cots and mats so that at least Peters shadow could fall on some of them as he passed by.
local: A lɛ vɛ̃ naa ka a nɩbɛ yin a ba baalsɩ tɩ gaal ba a gador ju nɩ a sɔ̃n pɔ a sɔr kõkoor chɛlɛ nɩ a Pita, ɩka 'lɔ wa tala a wʊ gãagiel wʊ pɔɔ ba.

eng: Even large numbers of persons from towns around Jerusalem would gather, bringing the sick and those harassed by unclean spirits. Everyone was healed.
local: Nɩyɔɔ yin a tẽbil ala na jilʊ̃ a Jerusalɛm waan a ba baalsɩ wa lɔ̃ɔn 'matãan a bala a sɩdɛbɛ na tara, ka ba jaa sa.
