eng: The high priest, together with his allies, the Sadducees, was overcome with jealousy.
local: Ka a nyuur kpɛ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ nɩ a wʊ taaba bala na pʊɔ a Saduseemɩne gbul pɔ.

eng: They seized the apostles and made a public show of putting them in prison.
local: A ba nyɔɔr a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ pɔɔfʊ die pɔ bar.

eng: An angel from the Lord opened the prison doors during the night and led them out. The angel told them.
local: Tɩchɛ a tãsɔɔ a sɩ Soro tʊ̃ naa a wʊ malaka ka wʊ wa yuo a pɔɔfʊ die pãn na wʊ tɔɔ ba yin.

eng: Go, take your place in the temple, and tell the people everything about this new life.
local: tɩ 'yɛr, Yɩ chen a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir bɛchaar pɔ na yɩ tɩ 'yɛr a nyɛvʊr paalaa nyã 'yɛr kʊ a nɩbɛ.

eng: Early in the morning, they went into the temple as they had been told and began to teach. When the high priest and his colleagues gathered, they convened the Jerusalem Council, that is, the full assembly of Israels elders. They sent word to the prison to have the apostles brought before them.
local: A wɛr na wa chaar ba kpɛ̃n be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir bɛchaar pɔ, nɩtãa lɛ a malaka na 'yɛr ba tɩ piel wile a nɩbɛ. A Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ nɩ a wʊ taaba mɩ wa lɔ̃ɔn taa na ba bʊɔl a Juu nɩbɛ nɩdierbe ba jaa lɔ̃ɔ taa na ba na guor a 'yɛr, na ba pãa tʊ̃ ɩka ba chen a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ tɩ yuo a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ waan.

eng: However, the guards didnt find them in the prison. They returned and reported.
local: Tɩchɛ ban wa tɩ ta a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ baa nyɛ nɩrɛ jaa ɛ. A ba lɩɛb chen tɩ mana kʊrɔ ba.

eng: We found the prison locked and well-secured, with guards standing at the doors, but when we opened the doors we found no one inside.
local: Sɩ chen tɩ nyɛ a pɔɔfʊ die dɔ̃dɔr na chɛnɛ kpal, ka a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ara gu chɛnɛ ara a dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ, tɩchɛ sɩn wa yuo a pãn nɩrɛ jaa ba be be a die pɔ ɛ.

eng: When they received this news, the captain of the temple guard and the chief priests were baffled and wondered what might be happening.
local: Ban wa wõ a anyãna ban man ka a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir guguro nɩkpɛ̃ɛ nɩ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nãmɩne ka a ɩ ba chĩcham, a ba baala baa bɔ̃ɔ lɛn na ɩ ɛ.

eng: Just then, someone arrived and announced, Look! The people you put in prison are standing in the temple and teaching the people.
local: Ka a nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ wa 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ nyɛ a nɩbɛ bala yɩn nyɔɔr pɔɔ, ba ben be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir bɛchaar pɔ wile a nɩbɛ.

eng: Then the captain left with his guards and brought the apostles back. They didnt use force because they were afraid the people would stone them.
local: Ka a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir guguro nɩkpɛ̃ɛ tara a wʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ chiin tɩ tara a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ jaa jom waan. Baa tɩ fɩr ba ɛ, bojũu ba joro na dãbãɛ ɩka a nɩbɛ na lɔb ban nɩ kʊsɩbɛ.

eng: The apostles were brought before the council where the high priest confronted them.
local: Ban wa waan ba, ba vɛ̃n ka ba ara a nɩbɛrɛ niem, ka a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na sʊʊr ba 'yɛr.

eng: In no uncertain terms, we demanded that you not teach in this name. And look at you! You have filled Jerusalem with your teaching. And you are determined to hold us responsible for this mans death.
local: Saa kpãkpãan yɩ jaa 'yɛr ɩka, yɩ taa lɛ ɩrɛ wa pʊrɔ a yuor nyãna wile nɩ nɩrɛ jaa go ɛ? Tɩchɛ a yɩ chɛnɛ tɔr tɩ sɛɛ a Jerusalɛm nɩ a yɩ wiilfʊ nyãna, na yɩ bʊɔrɔ ɩka yɩ de a daba nyãna jãɩ yele dɔɔl sɩ.

eng: Peter and the apostles replied, We must obey God rather than humans!.
local: Ka a Pita nɩ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ bala na chɛ 'yɛr, Sɩn tun Nãaŋmɩn nɛɛ gar nɩsaal.

eng: The God of our ancestors raised Jesus from the dead—whom you killed by hanging him on a tree.
local: A sɩ sãakpãmɩne Nãaŋmɩn lɛ sãɩ a Yesu a kũu pɔ, a 'lɔ yɩn yɔɔl a daa ju ka wʊ kpi.

eng: God has exalted Jesus to his right side as leader and savior so that he could enable Israel to change its heart and life and to find forgiveness for sins.
local: Nãaŋmɩn 'mɔɔ wʊn don bin be a wʊ nũsʊ̃ɔ jie, ka wʊ ɩ Nãa nɩ Fafaara ɩka wʊ kʊ a Isɩral nɩbɛ sɔr ka ba lɩɛb ba tɩɛr, na ba nyɛ vɛ̃ kʊfʊ.

eng: We are witnesses of such things, as is the Holy Spirit, whom God has given to those who obey him.
local: Sɩn lɛ ɩ a anyãna dãasɩɛ dẽme nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ 'lɔ wʊn de kʊ a bala na tu a wʊ nɛɛ.

eng: When the council members heard this, they became furious and wanted to kill the apostles.
local: Ban wa wõ a anyãna, ba suur ir naa ka ba bʊɔrɔ a ba kʊfʊ.

eng: One council member, a Pharisee and teacher of the Law named Gamaliel, well-respected by all the people, stood up and ordered that the men be taken outside for a few moments.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Farasee ãsʊɔ ban bʊɔlɔ Gamaliel na ɩ a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwile, nɩrɛ jaa 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ wʊ naa, ka wʊ ir ara a nɩdierbe pɔ tɩ 'yɛr ɩka, ba vɛ̃ ka a dɔɔr bala yi yõo bãlãa.

eng: He said, Fellow Israelites, consider carefully what you intend to do to these people.
local: Tɩ na wʊ pãa 'yɛr kʊ ba, Isɩral dɔɔr, yɩ bɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ sʊ̃ a lɛ yɩn bʊɔrɔ ɩka yɩ maal a nɩbɛ banyãna.

eng: Some time ago, Theudas appeared, claiming to be somebody, and some four hundred men joined him. After he was killed, all of his followers scattered, and nothing came of that.
local: Sɔ̃ɔ kɔ̃ɔ lɛ a Tiudasɩ dɔ̃ɔ ir 'yɛr gãdaa 'yɛr ɩka nɩkpɛ̃ɛ lɛ wʊ ɩ ka a dɔɔr kɔɔr anaar ir turo wʊ. Ba kʊ wʊ naa ka a nɩbɛ bala yɛ̃yɛl ka a jaa ɩ pɔrɔ.

eng: Afterward, at the time of the census, Judas the Galilean appeared and got some people to follow him in a revolt. He was killed too, and all his followers scattered far and wide.
local: Ala puor lɛ ka a Judas na yi a Galilee a nɩbɛ sɔrfʊ yuon ir nɩyɔɔ ka ba turo wʊ. Ba mɩ kʊ wʊ naa ka a bala jaa na turo wʊ mɩ bũbur.

eng: Heres my recommendation in this case: Distance yourselves from these men. Let them go! If their plan or activity is of human origin, it will end in ruin.
local: A lɛ jũu, maa na 'yɛr yɩ naa ɩka yɩ ir nũu, tɩchɛ a nɩbɛ ba nyã bar. Yɩ bar ba a ba chiine. Ala ɩ a lɛ ban maala ɩn nɩrɛ lɛ bin an ɩn pɔrɔ.

eng: If it originates with God, you wont be able to stop them. Instead, you would actually find yourselves fighting God! The council was convinced by his reasoning.
local: Tɩchɛ ala ɩ Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ lɛ a yi, yaa be jaa na wa tʊ̃ɔ ŋmaa a nɩbɛ banyãna sɔr ɛ, yɩn wa bɔ̃ɔn naa ka a ɩ Nãaŋmɩn mãɛ lɛ yɩ jɛɛrɛ nɩ.

eng: After calling the apostles back, they had them beaten. They ordered them not to speak in the name of Jesus, then let them go.
local: A nɩbɛrɛ tu naa a lɛ a Gamaliel na 'yɛr, tɩ na ba bʊɔl a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ fɔb ba tɩ kpãkpãan ba ɩka ba taa lɛ ɩrɛ wa 'yɛrɛ 'yɛr pʊrɔ nɩ a Yesu yuor go ɛ, tɩ bar ba a ba chen.

eng: The apostles left the council rejoicing because they had been regarded as worthy to suffer disgrace for the sake of the name.
local: A nɩtʊ̃nsɩ na wa yi a nɩbɛrɛ banyãna niem, ba chɩlɛ naa, bojũu ba sɛɛn naa a dɔɔyɛ nɩ a vã difʊ a Yesu yuor jũu.

eng: Every day they continued to teach and proclaim the good news that Jesus is the Christ, both in the temple and in houses.
local: Bɩbir jaa lɛ ba ma be a be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir bɛchaar pɔ wile a nɩbɛ na ba mɩ yʊɔrɔ a yie ba gala ɛ, na ba wile a nɩbɛ tɩ 'yɛrɛ a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ ɩka a Yesu lɛ ɩ a Nɩ-iraa.

eng: Peter and John were going up to the temple at three oclock in the afternoon, the established prayer time.
local: Bɩbir ãsʊɔ Pita nɩ Jɔɔn duoro naa a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir kpɛ̃ɛ pɔ, a puorfʊ jie mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ na gɔr.

eng: Meanwhile, a man crippled since birth was being carried in. Every day, people would place him at the temple gate known as the Beautiful Gate so he could ask for money from those entering the temple.
local: Ka a daba ban lɔ̃ɔ dɔɔn a wʊ gbɛr, a ba ma tuo wʊ tɩ bin a be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ 'lɔ ban bʊɔlɔ, Vɩla. Be lɛ ba ma tuo wʊ tɩ bin bɩbir jaa a wʊ ma sʊɔrɔ a bala na kpɩɛrɛ a Nãaŋmɩn yir bɛchaar pɔ libie.

eng: When he saw Peter and John about to enter, he began to ask them for a gift.
local: Wʊn wa nyɛ a Pita nɩ a Jɔɔn na kpɩɛrɛ, a wʊ sʊɔrɔ ba a libir.

eng: Peter and John stared at him. Peter said, Look at us.
local: Ka a Pita nɩ a Jɔɔn kaara wʊ a lɛ a tãɩɩ, ka a Pita wa 'yɛr, Kaara sɩ.

eng: So the man gazed at them, expecting to receive something from them.
local: Ka a daba tɔ̃ɔ fur a nɩbir a lɛ chir chir chir 'yɔ̃ɔ ba tɩɛrɛ ɩka wʊn nyɛn bon.

eng: Peter said, I dont have any money, but I will give you what I do have. In the name of Jesus Christ the Nazarene, rise up and walk.
local: A Pita pãa 'yɛr, N ba tara libir bɩɩ, sɛlma kʊrɔ fʊ ɛ, tɩchɛ a 'lɔ ɩn tara lɛ ɩn kʊ fʊ. Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto na yi a Najaretɩ yuor pɔ, ir chiine.

eng: Then he grasped the mans right hand and raised him up. At once his feet and ankles became strong.
local: Tɩ na wʊ nyɔɔ a daba nũsʊ̃ɔ sʊ̃ʊ wʊ ka wʊ ir ka a ɩ a gbɛpɛlɛ nɩ a nakɔɔtɩɛ kpɛ̃mɛ naa.

eng: Jumping up, he began to walk around. He entered the temple with them, walking, leaping, and praising God.
local: Ka a daba ɔɔ chʊr a gbɛɛ na wʊ piel chiine, na wʊ pãa tu ba kpɛ̃n be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir bɛchaar pɔ. Be lɛ wʊ tɩ ɔɔrɔ yʊɔrɔ na wʊ pɛɛrɛ a Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: All the people saw him walking and praising God.
local: A nɩbɛ ba jaa na wa nyɛ wʊ, a lɛ wʊn chiine pɛɛrɛ a Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: They recognized him as the same one who used to sit at the temples Beautiful Gate asking for money. They were filled with amazement and surprise at what had happened to him.
local: ba bɔ̃ɔ wʊ naa an ɩ 'lɔ lɛ ma jãnɛ sʊɔrɔ libie be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir dɔ̃dɔr 'lɔ ban bʊɔlɔ a, Vɩla. Ka a ɩ ba chĩcham naa 'maa ba nɛɛ a lɛ na ɩ.

eng: While the healed man clung to Peter and John, all the people rushed toward them at Solomons Porch, completely amazed.
local: An ɩ a daba chɛnɛ na pɔɔ a Pita nɩ a Jɔɔn ka a nɩbɛ nɛɛr 'maa a ba jo wa titaa a be ban bʊɔlɔ a Solomon Bɛchaar pɔ.

eng: Seeing this, Peter addressed the people: You Israelites, why are you amazed at this? Why are you staring at us as if we made him walk by our own power or piety?.
local: Pita na nyɛ a lɛ ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a nɩbɛ, Isɩral dɔɔr woɩ, bãa lɛ so ka a nɛɛr 'maa yɩ a lɛ mɔ̃? Bãa lɛ yɩ fur a nɩbir 'yɔ̃ɔ sɩ fɩka sɩn mãɛ kpɛ̃ɔ bɩɩ sɩn mãɛ maal vɩla lɛ sɩ maal a daba nyã ka wʊ chiine?.

eng: The God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob—the God of our ancestors—has glorified his servant Jesus. This is the one you handed over and denied in Pilates presence, even though he had already decided to release him.
local: A Nãaŋmɩn nyã na ɩ a Abraham, Isaakɩ nɩ a Jekɔb Nãaŋmɩn nɩ a sɩ sãakpãmɩne Nãaŋmɩn lɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ a wʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ Yesu yuor. Yɩn lɛ nyɔɔ wʊ tɩr ɩka ba kʊ wʊ, ka a Pilate ŋme nyãa na bar wʊ tɩ yɩ 'yɛr ɩka yaa bɔ̃ɔ wʊ ɛ.

eng: You rejected the holy and righteous one, and asked that a murderer be released to you instead.
local: Yɩ 'yɛr naa ɩka yaa bɔ̃ɔ a chɛchɛ sʊɔ nɩ a yelmãɛ sʊɔ ɛ, tɩ na yɩ sʊɔr ka ba yuo a nɩkʊrɔ kʊ yɩ.

eng: You killed the author of life, the very one whom God raised from the dead. We are witnesses of this.
local: Yɩn lɛ kʊ a nyɛvʊr sʊɔ, tɩchɛ ka a Nãaŋmɩn sãɩ wʊ a kũu pɔ. Sɩn lɛ nyɛ anyãna nɩ nɩbir.

eng: His name itself has made this man strong. That is, because of faith in Jesus name, God has strengthened this man whom you see and know. The faith that comes through Jesus gave him complete health right before your eyes.
local: A daba nyãna nɩ a sɩn na 'mataa sɔɔ a Yesu lɛ sa a daba nyãna baal yɩn nyɛ na yɩ bɔ̃ɔ, sɩrɛ jaa sɩn sɔɔ de a Yesu yuor lɛ wʊ sa, a daba nyãna yɩn ben nyɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I know you acted in ignorance. So did your rulers.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã, yɛɛr, n bɔ̃ɔ naa ɩka ba bɔ̃ɔ fʊ lɛ vɛ̃ ka yɩ tʊ̃ a lɛ nɛ a yɩ nɩbɛrɛ na dɔ̃ɔ tʊ̃.

eng: But this is how God fulfilled what he foretold through all the prophets: that his Christ would suffer.
local: Tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn ɩ naa a lɛ wʊn dɔ̃ɔ vɛ̃ ka a wʊ 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ 'yɛr ɩka, a wʊ Nɩ-iraa na din dɔɔyɛ.

eng: Change your hearts and lives! Turn back to God so that your sins may be wiped away.
local: Yɩ pãa lɩɛb a yɩ tɩɛr, na yɩ lɩɛb wa a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃, ka wʊ fɩɛl a yɩ yeldɛbɛ bar. A ãkpɛ̃ɔ paalaa yɩn na nyɛ na yin a sɩ Soro sɛ̃.

eng: Then the Lord will provide a season of relief from the distress of this age and he will send Jesus, whom he handpicked to be your Christ.
local: ka wʊ gba na tʊ̃ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'lɔ wʊn dɔ̃ɔ ir bin kʊ yɩ.

eng: Jesus must remain in heaven until the restoration of all things, about which God spoke long ago through his holy prophets.
local: Wʊn chɛnɛ na jãnɛ be a saaju, tɩ tãn a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ Nãaŋmɩn na 'mataa a bonsɩ a jaa nɩtãa lɛ wʊn tɩr a nɛɛ a kõr jaa tu, a wʊ 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ chɛchɛsɩ sɛ̃.

eng: Moses said, The Lord your God will raise up from your own people a prophet like me. Listen to whatever he tells you. .
local: Bojũu Mosesɩ dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr, A Soro Nãaŋmɩn na ir 'yɛ'yɛrɛ a n kaar ka wʊ ɩ a yɩ mãɛ nɩrɛ, a yɩ chɛlɛ a ala a jaa wʊn na 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ.

eng: Whoever doesnt listen to that prophet will be totally cut off from the people. .
local: Ɩ̃sʊɔ jaa ba wa chɛlɛ a lɛ wʊn na 'yɛrɛ ɛ, ban ir wʊ nũu bar a wʊ nɩbɛ pɔ.

eng: All the prophets who spoke—from Samuel forward—announced these days.
local: Sɩrɛ jaa, a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ ba jaa na yi a Samuwɛl sɔ̃ɔ mɩ 'yɛr naa a bɩbie anyãna 'yɛr.

eng: You are the heirs of the prophets and the covenant that God made with your ancestors when he told Abraham, Through your descendants, all the families on earth will be blessed. .
local: Yɩn lɛ ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ banyãna bibiir nɩ a nɛtɩraa 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn na tara nɩ a sɩ sãamɩne. Wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a Abraham, An tu naa a fʊ yɔn pɔ, ka a nɩbɛ ba jaa na be ka a tẽe na nyɛ sʊ̃ʊfʊ.

eng: After God raised his servant, he sent him to you first—to bless you by enabling each of you to turn from your evil ways.
local: Nãaŋmɩn na wa ir a wʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃, yɩn sɛ̃ lɛ wʊ de nie tʊ̃ wʊ ka wʊ tɩ sʊ̃ʊ yɩ na wʊ lɩɛb a yɩ ãsʊɔ jaa yin a wʊ putusɔr pɔ.

eng: About that time, while the number of disciples continued to increase, a complaint arose. Greek-speaking disciples accused the Aramaic-speaking disciples because their widows were being overlooked in the daily food service.
local: A ber ala pɔ, a poturbo na waara pʊɔrɔ a bala ban dɔɔ a paal yuo pɔ, ka baa tʊɔrɔ 'yɛrɛ a Juu kɔkɔr ɛ, hũhũnõ na 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a bala na ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ, bojũu a ba pɔɔkɔɛ maa nyɛ a bɩbir jaa bʊ̃diir ban ma põ ɛ.

eng: The Twelve called a meeting of all the disciples and said, It isnt right for us to set aside proclamation of Gods word in order to serve tables.
local: Be lɛ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ pie nɩ ayi bʊɔl a poturbo ba jaa lɔ̃ɔ taa wa 'yɛr, A ba tara sɔr ka sɩn bar a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr 'yɛrfʊ tɩ põnõ bʊ̃diir ɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, carefully choose seven well-respected men from among you. They must be well-respected and endowed by the Spirit with exceptional wisdom. We will put them in charge of this concern.
local: A lɛ jũu yɛɛr, yɩ kaa a yɩ pɔ na yɩ ir dɔɔr ayopõi, bala yɩn bɔ̃ɔ ka ba sɛɛn a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ tɩ mɩ tara yɛ̃, ka sɩn de a tʊ̃mɔ̃ nyã kʊ ba.

eng: As for us, we will devote ourselves to prayer and the service of proclaiming the word.
local: Tɩchɛ a sɩn 'lɔ mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn sʊɔrfʊ nɩ a wʊ 'yɛr.

eng: This proposal pleased the entire community. They selected Stephen, a man endowed by the Holy Spirit with exceptional faith, Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolaus from Antioch, a convert to Judaism.
local: A 'yɛr nyã pɛl ba jaa na duo a be pʊɔ, ka ba ir a nɩbɛ banyãna. Sɩteven a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na sɛɛ ka wʊ mɩ tara sɔɔfʊ, nɩ Filip, nɩ Porokorusɩ, nɩ Nɩkanor, nɩ Tɩmon, nɩ Pamenasɩ, nɩ Nikolasɩ na yi Antiok na wʊ lɩɛb kpɛ a Juu nɩbɛ puorfʊ pɔ.

eng: The community presented these seven to the apostles, who prayed and laid their hands on them.
local: A nɩbɛ banyãna lɛ ba ir waan a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ sɛ̃, ka ba dɔɔl ba nuur sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn kʊ ba.

eng: Gods word continued to grow. The number of disciples in Jerusalem increased significantly. Even a large group of priests embraced the faith.
local: Ka a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr yaar, ka a poturbo mɩ na be a Jerusalɛm pʊɔrɔ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃. A Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlorbo yɔɔ jie mɩ sɔɔn de a 'yɛr.

eng: Stephen, who stood out among the believers for the way Gods grace was at work in his life and for his exceptional endowment with divine power, was doing great wonders and signs among the people.
local: Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ ben a Sɩteven sɛ̃ ka wʊ tara kpɛ̃ɔ tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ a nɛ'maa tʊ̃yɔɔ a nɩbɛ sɛ̃.

eng: Opposition arose from some who belonged to the so-called Synagogue of Former Slaves. Members from Cyrene, Alexandria, Cilicia, and Asia entered into debate with Stephen.
local: A nɩbɛ bamɩne na yi a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie a ba bʊɔl ba, Mãɛ Sorbo, nɩ a Juu nɩbɛ bamɩne na yi a Sirenɩ nɩ a Aleksandiriya, ka bamɩne mɩ yi a Sɩlɩsɩya nɩ Asɩya. A nɩbɛ banyãna lɛ piel a nɛchɩrɛ na ba chɩrɛ nɩ a Sɩteven.

eng: However, they couldnt resist the wisdom the Spirit gave him as he spoke.
local: Tɩchɛ baa tʊɔrɔ wʊ ɛ, a yɛ̃ 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na kʊ wʊ a wʊ 'yɛrɛ nɩ.

eng: Then they secretly enticed some people to claim, We heard him insult Moses and God.
local: A ba pãa sɔɔl tʊ̃ nɩbɛ bamɩne ka ba ŋmaa jir 'yɛr ɩka, Sɩn lɛ wõ a Sɩteven na fere 'yɛrɛ 'yɛrdɛɛr 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a Mosesɩ nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: They stirred up the people, the elders, and the legal experts. They caught Stephen, dragged him away, and brought him before the Jerusalem Council.
local: Tɩ tɔ a nɩbɛ nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Mosesɩ nɛɛ wiwiilbe 'yɔ̃ɔ ka ba nyɔɔ a Sɩteven chiin a ba 'yɛr tɔɔrbɔ sɛ̃.

eng: Before the council, they presented false witnesses who testified, This man never stops speaking against this holy place and the Law.
local: Be lɛ ba waan jir ŋmarba ka ba wa 'yɛr, A daba nyãna ba gala 'yɛr dɛbɛ wʊn 'yɛrɛ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir chɛchɛ nyãna ɛ, nɩ a Mosesɩ nɛɛ wʊn bin ɛ.

eng: In fact, we heard him say that this man Jesus of Nazareth will destroy this place and alter the customary practices Moses gave us.
local: Sɩn mãɛ lɛ wõ wʊn 'yɛr ɩka a Yesu na yi a Najaretɩ na ŋmɛr a jie nyãna, tɩ na wʊ lɩɛb a binfʊ ala a Mosesɩ na dɔ̃ɔ bin kʊ sɩ.

eng: Everyone seated in the council stared at Stephen, and they saw that his face was radiant, just like an angels.
local: Ka a bala jaa na jãnɛ a be a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ jie, kãan a Sɩteven nie ka wʊ kaara fɩka a malaka nie.

eng: The high priest asked, Are these accusations true.
local: Be lɛ ka a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ sʊʊr wʊ, Sɩrɛ lɛ a anyãna ban 'yɛr bɩɩ.

eng: Stephen responded, Brothers and fathers, listen to me. Our glorious God appeared to our ancestor Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he settled in Haran.
local: Ka a Sɩteven haa 'yɛr, Yɛɛr nɩ sãamɩne, yɩ chɛlɛ. A Nãaŋmɩn tɩɩr sʊɔ dɔ̃ɔ yi a sɩ sãa Abraham lɛ wʊn dɔ̃ɔ chɛnɛ be a Mesopotamiya wɛr pɔ sɛrɛ tɩ wa chen a Haran.

eng: God told him, Leave your homeland and kin, and go to the land that I will show you.
local: A Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Yi a fʊ tẽe na fʊ bar a fʊ nɩbɛ tɩ na fʊ chen wɛr yuo pɔ ɩn na wiil fʊ.

eng: So Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After Abrahams father died, God had him resettle in this land where you now live.
local: A wʊ yi a Chaldea wɛr pɔ na wʊ chen tɩ jãnɛ a Haran wɛr pɔ. A wʊ sãa na wa kpi baar lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn waan wʊ a kana yɩn ben kpɩɛrɛ.

eng: God didnt give him an inheritance here, not even a square foot of land. However, God did promise to give the land as his possession to him and to his descendants, even though Abraham had no child.
local: Nãaŋmɩn ba kʊ wʊ bon jaa a kana ɛ, waa kʊ wʊ jibile jaa gba ɛ, bɩɩ gbɛr nɛfʊ jie ɛ. Tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn tɩr nɛɛ kʊ wʊ ɩka 'lɔ nɩ a wʊ yɔn lɛ na so a jie, tɩ waa tara bie gba ɛ.

eng: God put it this way: His descendants will be strangers in a land that belongs to others, who will enslave them and abuse them for four hundred years. .
local: Nɩtɔ̃ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr kʊ a Abraham, A fʊ yɔn na ɩ naa sãanba paal sãan pɔ, na ba ɩ gbãgbaar ka ba ge ba yuom kɔɔr anaar .

eng: And I will condemn the nation they serve as slaves, God said, and afterward they will leave that land and serve me in this place.
local: Tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr naa, ɩka wʊn yaa naa a sãn a paal 'lɔ na maal ba a gbãgbaar. A anyã puor lɛ ban wa puor ma a jie nyãna.

eng: God gave him the covenant confirmed through circumcision. Accordingly, eight days after Isaacs birth, Abraham circumcised him. Isaac did the same with Jacob, and Jacob with the twelve patriarchs.
local: Nãaŋmɩn tɩr nɛɛ kʊ a Abraham a yʊɔr ŋmaafʊ yele. Abraham na wa dɔɔ a Isaakɩ, wʊ ŋmaa wʊn a yʊɔr a ber aniin daar. Ka a Isaakɩ mɩ dɔɔ a Jekɔb, ka a Jekɔb mɩ dɔɔ a sɩ sãamɩne pie nɩ ayi.

eng: Because the patriarchs were jealous of Joseph, they sold him into slavery in Egypt. God was with him, however.
local: A sɩ sãamɩne banyãna na tara nɩ a ba yɛbile Josefʊ nɩ nyuur jũu, na ba nyɔɔ wʊ da kʊ a Ijipitɩ dẽme. Tɩchɛ ka a Nãaŋmɩn be a wʊ sɛ̃.

eng: and rescued him from all his troubles. The grace and wisdom he gave Joseph were recognized by Pharaoh, king of Egypt, who appointed him ruler over Egypt and over his whole palace.
local: Na wʊ faa wʊ yin a wʊ wõm a jaa pɔ. Wʊ kʊ naa a Josefʊ yɛ̃ na wʊ sʊ̃ʊ wʊ a wʊ nyɛ 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ a Faaro na ɩ a Ijipitɩ nãa niem, na wʊ maal wʊ ka wʊ ɩ a Ijipitɩ nɩ a nãa jie wʊ jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ.

eng: A famine came upon all Egypt and Canaan, and great hardship came with it. Our ancestors had nothing to eat.
local: Ka a kɔ̃ lo Ijipitɩ nɩ a Kanaan wɛr wʊ jaa waan dɔɔyɛ, ka a sɩ sãamɩne ba nyɛrɛ bʊ̃diir ɛ.

eng: When Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there for the first time.
local: Jekɔb na wa wõ ɩka bʊ̃diir ben a be Ijipitɩ, wʊ tʊ̃n a sɩ sãamɩne ka ba chen.

eng: During their second visit, Joseph told his brothers who he was, and Pharaoh learned about Josephs family.
local: A gbɛɛ ayi sʊɔ ban chen lɛ a Josefʊ wiil a wʊ mãɛ kʊ ba, ka a Faaro mɩ pãa bɔ̃ɔ a Josefʊ yir nɩbɛ.

eng: Joseph sent for his father Jacob and all his relatives—seventy-five in all—and invited them to live with him.
local: A anyã puor lɛ a Josefʊ tʊ̃ bʊɔl a wʊ sãa Jekɔb nɩ a wʊ yir dẽme, a ba jaa ɩ nɩbɛ lɩjaata nɩ pie nɩ anũu .

eng: So Jacob went down to Egypt, where he and our ancestors died.
local: Jekɔb chen a be a Ijipitɩ nɩ a sɩ sãamɩne be lɛ ba jaa tɩ kpi.

eng: Their bodies were brought back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had purchased for a certain sum of money from Hamors children, who lived in Shechem.
local: Ba tuo naa a ba ãgãma waan a Sekem wa ũu ba a yaa 'lɔ pɔ a Abraham na da a Hamor bibiir sɛ̃ a be a Sekem.

eng: When it was time for God to keep the promise he made to Abraham, the number of our people in Egypt had greatly expanded.
local: A sɔ̃ɔ na wa gbʊr ka a Nãaŋmɩn na maal a lɛ wʊn tɩr a nɛɛ kʊ a Abraham, a sɩ nɩbɛ na be a Ijipitɩ dɔɔn paal.

eng: But then another king rose to power over Egypt who didnt know anything about Joseph. .
local: Ala lɛ ka a nãyuo wa di na wʊ kaara a Ijipitɩ na waa bɔ̃ɔ a Josefʊ yele ɛ.

eng: He exploited our people and abused our ancestors. He even forced them to abandon their newly born babies so they would die.
local: Wʊ maal naa a sɩ nɩbɛ putuyele na wʊ ge ba, na wʊ ma fɩr a pɔɔ pʊʊr ka bala wa dɔɔ bipɩlɛ ba lɔb ba 'yɔ̃ɔ a kʊ̃ɔ pɔ a ba kpi.

eng: Thats when Moses was born. He was highly favored by God, and for three months his parents cared for him in their home.
local: A sɔ̃ɔ nyãna lɛ ba dɔɔ a Mosesɩ, ka wʊ ɩ bivɩla a Nãaŋmɩn niem. A ba guol wʊ ŋmɛrsɩ ata be a wʊ sãa yir.

eng: After he was abandoned, Pharaohs daughter adopted and cared for him as though he were her own son.
local: Ban wa yin wʊ tɩ bar a yõo, Faaro bipɔɔ de wʊn guol ka wʊ ɩ a wʊ mãɛ bidaba.

eng: Moses learned everything Egyptian wisdom had to offer, and he was a man of powerful words and deeds.
local: Ba jãn naa Mosesɩ a Ijipitɩ tẽe yɛ̃ a jaa, ka wʊ kpɛ̃mɛ a wʊ 'yɛr nɩ a wʊ tʊ̃mɔ̃ pɔ.

eng: When Moses was years old, he decided to visit his family, the Israelites.
local: A lɛ a Mosesɩ na wa ta yuom lɩjaayi , wʊ tɩɛr naa a wʊ yɛɛr Isɩral nɩbɛ na wʊ ɩka wʊ chen tɩ piir ba kaa.

eng: He saw one of them being wronged so he came to his rescue and evened the score by killing the Egyptian.
local: Wʊ tɩ nyɛn a ba ãsʊɔ a Ijipitɩ nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ na gere ka wʊ ŋmaa de na wʊ kʊ a Ijipitɩ nɩrɛ 'lɔ.

eng: He expected his own kin to understand that God was using him to rescue them, but they didnt.
local: Mosesɩ tɩɛr lɛ ɩka a wʊ mãɛ nɩbɛ na bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka 'lɔ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na de faan ba, tɩchɛ baa tɩɛrtɔ a lɛ ɛ.

eng: The next day he came upon some Israelites who were caught up in an argument. He tried to make peace between them by saying, You are brothers! Why are you harming each other.
local: A wɛr na wa chaar, wʊ tɩ nyɛn a Isɩral nɩbɛ ayi mɩ na jɛɛrɛ taa. Ka wʊ 'maal ba tɩ sʊrɔ ba, Yɩn yɛɛr nara, bo lɛ so a yɩ jɛɛrɛ taa.

eng: The one who started the fight against his neighbor pushed Moses aside and said, Who appointed you as our leader and judge? .
local: Tɩchɛ a 'lɔ sʊɔ na tʊ̃ɔ a wʊ tɔ sʊɔ daan a Mosesɩ bar tɩ sʊʊr wʊ, An tʊ̃ fʊ ɩka fʊ wa kaara sɩ na fʊ tɔɔrɔ a sɩ 'yɛr?.

eng: Are you planning to kill me like you killed that Egyptian yesterday? .
local: Fʊ bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka fʊ mɩ kʊ ma nɩtãa lɛ fʊn kʊ a Ijipitɩ nɩrɛ a jãa wɛ.

eng: When Moses heard this, he fled to Midian, where he lived as an immigrant and had two sons.
local: Mosesɩ na wõ a 'yɛr nyãna ka wʊ jo chen tɩ kpɩɛrɛ a Midiyan, be lɛ wʊ be na wʊ wa dɔɔ dabil ayi.

eng: Forty years later, an angel appeared to Moses in the flame of a burning bush in the wilderness near Mount Sinai.
local: Yuom lɩjaayi na wa po, malaka wa lɩɛb bũu lɩrɛ tɩbile pɔ, na wʊ dũu a Mosesɩ be a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ na gbʊr a Sinai tɔ̃ɔ par.

eng: Enthralled by the sight, Moses approached to get a closer look and he heard the Lords voice.
local: Wʊn wa nyɛ a lɛ ka a nɛ'maa wʊ, a wʊ tɔɔ gbʊr ɩka wʊ kaa. Be lɛ wʊ wõ a Soro kɔkɔr na 'yɛr.

eng: I am the God of your ancestors, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. Trembling with fear, Moses didnt dare to investigate any further.
local: 'Maa lɛ ɩ a fʊ sãamɩne Nãaŋmɩn, Abraham, Isaakɩ nɩ a Jekɔb Nãaŋmɩn. Ka a dãbãɛ kpɛ a Mosesɩ waa lɛ 'lara na kaa ɛ.

eng: The Lord continued, Remove the sandals from your feet, for the place where you are standing is holy ground. .
local: Ka a sɩ Soro pãa 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Yaa a fʊ nafɔɔr bar, a jie fʊn ara ɩ naa jichɛchɛ.

eng: I have clearly seen the oppression my people have experienced in Egypt, and I have heard their groaning. I have come down to rescue them. Come! I am sending you to Egypt .
local: N nyɛ naa a n nɩbɛ be a Ijipitɩ pɔ ban gere, n wõ naa ba hʊ̃ʊnfʊ naa suu ɩka n wa faa ba. Wa, ka n tʊ̃ fʊ a be a Ijipitɩ.

eng: This is the same Moses whom they rejected when they asked, Who appointed you as our leader and judge? This is the Moses whom God sent as leader and deliverer. God did this with the help of the angel who appeared before him in the bush.
local: A Mosesɩ nyãna nɩ wʊ ju ban tɔr bar tɩ sʊʊr wʊ, An lɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ fʊ ɩka fʊ kaara sɩ na fʊ tɔɔrɔ a sɩ 'yɛr? 'Lɔ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn mãɛ tʊ̃ a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ a malaka na wa dũu wʊ 'lɩrɛ a bũu a tɩbile pɔ, ɩka wʊ tɩ ɩ a ba nɩkpɛ̃ɛ nɩ a ba fafaara.

eng: This man led them out after he performed wonders and signs in Egypt at the Red Sea and for forty years in the wilderness.
local: 'Lɔ lɛ tɩ de ba nie na wʊ tʊ̃ tʊ̃ yɔɔ be a Ijipitɩ tẽe pɔ, nɩ a Man Jɩɛ par nɩ a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ ban be a yuom lɩjaayi .

eng: This is the Moses who told the Israelites, God will raise up for you a prophet like me from your own people. .
local: A Mosesɩ nyãna nɩ wʊ ju lɛ 'yɛr kʊ a Isɩral nɩbɛ ɩka, Nãaŋmɩn na tʊ̃ naa a 'yɛ'yɛrɛ ãsʊɔ a maa kaar ka wʊ yi a yɩ mãɛ nɩbɛ pɔ.

eng: This is the one who was in the assembly in the wilderness with our ancestors and with the angel who spoke to him on Mount Sinai. He is the one who received life-giving words to give to us.
local: Wʊ dɔ̃ɔ pʊɔ a nɩyɔɔ bala pɔ be a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ, 'matãan a sɩ sãakpãmɩne ka a malaka 'yɛr 'yɛr kʊ wʊ be a Sinai Tɔ̃ɔ par na ɩ nyɛvʊr 'yɛr ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ sɩ.

eng: Hes also the one whom our ancestors refused to obey. Instead, they pushed him aside and, in their thoughts and desires, returned to Egypt.
local: Tɩchɛ a sɩ sãakpãmɩne tɔr wʊ naa, na baa tu a wʊ nɛɛ ɛ. Ba tɔr wʊn bar ka a ba tɩɛr lɩɛb chen be a Ijipitɩ.

eng: They told Aaron, Make us gods that will lead us. As for this Moses who led us out of Egypt, we dont know whats happened to him! .
local: Ba 'yɛr kʊ a Aaron, Maal ŋmɩmɛ kʊ sɩ ka a ma de sɩ nie, a Mosesɩ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ na yin sɩ a Ijipitɩ saa bɔ̃ɔ bo nɩ tara wʊ ɛ.

eng: Thats when they made an idol in the shape of a calf, offered a sacrifice to it, and began to celebrate what they had made with their own hands.
local: A ba mɛ bʊtɩɩ a daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ ka wʊ nyɛtãan nele, na ba waan bomo wa lon bɔɔr a jie na ba dãɛn 'yɔ̃ɔn a ba nũu bʊ̃maalaa nyãna.

eng: So God turned away from them and handed them over to worship the stars in the sky, just as it is written in the scroll of the Prophets: Did you bring sacrifices and offerings to me for forty years in the wilderness, house of Israel? .
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Nãaŋmɩn lɩɛb puor 'yɔ̃ɔ ba, na wʊ bar ba ka ba puoro a saaju bomo. Ka anyãna tɩ tuor a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ ãsʊɔ na dɔ̃ɔ sɛb, wʊ sʊʊr naa, A yɩn Isɩral nɩbɛ, yɩn be a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ a yuom lɩjaayi na yɩ dɔ̃ɔ waan a bɔɔrlo bomo maa lɛ yɩ wa kʊ bɩɩ?.

eng: No! Instead, you took the tent of Moloch with you, and the star of your god Rephan, the images that you made in order to worship them. Therefore, I will send you far away, farther than Babylon. .
local: Kai, tɩ a bʊtɩbɛ ala yɩn kaara lɛ a Molokɩ nɩ ŋmɛrbir ŋmɩn Arefan, a tɩbɛ anyãna lɛ yɩ dɔ̃ɔ maal puoro. A lɛ jũu, ɩn vɛ̃ naa ka ba nyɔɔr yɩ chiin a Babilon puor ka yɩ tɩ kpɩɛrɛ a be.

eng: The tent of testimony was with our ancestors in the wilderness. Moses built it just as he had been instructed by the one who spoke to him and according to the pattern he had seen.
local: A sɩ sãakpãmɩne dɔ̃ɔ maal gãma yir be a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ ka a ɩ dãasɩɛ, ba maal wʊ naa nɩtãa lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na man wiil a Mosesɩ ka wʊ nyɛ.

eng: In time, when they had received the tent, our ancestors carried it with them when, under Joshuas leadership, they took possession of the land from the nations whom God expelled. This tent remained in the land until the time of David.
local: A gãma yir nyã lɛ a sɩ sãakpãmɩne tara ka a Josua de ba a nie ka ba waan, a ba wa faa a wɛr nyã pɔ a nɩyor na dɔ̃ɔ kpɩɛrɛ ka a Nãaŋmɩn dii ba bar. A gãn yir nyã ben be lɛ̃ɛɛ tɩ tãn a Nãa David sɔ̃ɔ.

eng: God approved of David, who asked that he might provide a dwelling place for the God of Jacob.
local: Nãaŋmɩn maal vɩla kʊ wʊ ka wʊ ɩka wʊn mɛn yir kʊ a Jekɔb Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: But it was Solomon who actually built a house for God.
local: Tɩchɛ Solomon lɛ wa mɛ a yir kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: However, the Most High doesnt live in houses built by human hands. As the prophet says.
local: Fʊ̃ʊ kaa, a Nãaŋmɩn nyã na ɩ a saaju sʊɔ, ba kpɩɛrɛ a yie anyã nɩbɛ na mɛ ɛ. Nɩtãa lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ ãsʊɔ na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr.

eng: Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What kind of house will you build for me, says the Lord, or where is my resting place? .
local: Saaju lɛ ɩ a maa nakɔɔ, ka a tẽe ɩ a n gbɛɛ dɔɔlfʊ jie. Ka a sɩ Soro sʊʊr, yir bo tɔ lɛ yɩn mɛ kʊ ma? Bɩɩ nyɩnɛ lɛ na ɩ a n pɛ̃nfʊ jie?.

eng: Didnt I make all these things with my own hand? .
local: A ba ɩ a maa nũu lɛ maal anyãna a jaa ɛ.

eng: You stubborn people! In your thoughts and hearing, you are like those who have had no part in Gods covenant! You continuously set yourself against the Holy Spirit, just like your ancestors did.
local: Yɩn nyãkpãɛn dẽme na ɩ nɩbɛ na ba wõnõ 'yɛr ɛ, yɩ kaara a yɩ sãamɩne kaar. Yɩ maa sɔɔ kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ sɔr ɛ.

eng: Was there a single prophet your ancestors didnt harass? They even killed those who predicted the coming of the righteous one, and youve betrayed and murdered him!.
local: Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ jaa ba be be ka a yɩ sãamɩne ba ge wʊ ɛ. Ba gba kʊ naa bala na de nie mʊɔl 'yɛr a Torfʊ Sʊɔ 'lɔ waa 'yɛr. A pʊ̃pãanyã, yɩn gba lɛ gar 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ na yɩ kʊ wʊ.

eng: You received the Law given by angels, but you havent kept it.
local: Yɩn lɛ de a Nãaŋmɩn nɛɛ wʊn kʊ a malaka a wʊ wa kʊ yɩ ka yɩ de tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ, tɩchɛ a yɩ tɔr bar.

eng: Once the council members heard these words, they were enraged and began to grind their teeth at Stephen.
local: A 'yɛr nyãna ban wa wõ, ka ba suur ir a ba kpar kparaa jur 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ wʊ.

eng: But Stephen, enabled by the Holy Spirit, stared into heaven and saw Gods majesty and Jesus standing at Gods right side.
local: Tɩchɛ a Sɩteven a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na sɛɛ a wʊ dʊl kaa saaju na wʊ nyɛ a Nãaŋmɩn tɩɩr, ka a Yesu ara a Nãaŋmɩn nũsʊ̃ɔ jie.

eng: He exclaimed, Look! I can see heaven on display and the Human One standing at Gods right side.
local: Ka wʊ 'yɛr, Yɩ nyɛ ka, n nyɛ naa saaju na yuo ka a Nɩsaal Bie ara a Nãaŋmɩn nũsʊ̃ɔ jie.

eng: At this, they shrieked and covered their ears. Together, they charged at him.
local: A anyãna ban wõ ba pɔɔn a tobo tɩ ŋme chɛlma na ba tɩ mɩl wʊ.

eng: threw him out of the city, and began to stone him. The witnesses placed their coats in the care of a young man named Saul.
local: na ba tɔɔ wʊ yin a tẽe chɛ̃chɛ̃ɛ tɩ piel lɔɔrɔ wʊ nɩ kʊsɩbɛ. Ka a bala na di dãasɩɛ yaar a ba fuusɩ bin pɔlbile kɔ̃ɔ ban bʊɔlɔ Sɔɔl sɛ̃.

eng: As they battered him with stones, Stephen prayed, Lord Jesus, accept my life.
local: Ban lɔɔrɔ wʊ a lɛ nɛ na kʊ ka wʊ sʊɔr Yesu, N Soro Yesu de a n sãɛ.

eng: Falling to his knees, he shouted, Lord, dont hold this sin against them! Then he died.
local: Wʊ lon gbur dumo tɩ pãa ŋme chɛlma 'yɛr, N Soro taa ɩrɛ wa yaara ba sãn ɛ. A anyãna lɛ wʊ 'yɛr baar tɩ ŋmaa vʊʊr.

eng: Saul was in full agreement with Stephens murder. At that time, the church in Jerusalem began to be subjected to vicious harassment. Everyone except the apostles was scattered throughout the regions of Judea and Samaria.
local: A Sɔɔl mɩ dɔ̃ɔ sɔɔ ɩka ba kʊ a Sɩteven. A daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ jaa lɛ ba piel gere a Nãaŋmɩn puorbo be a Jerusalɛm. Ka ba jaa ŋma yaar a bamɩne chen Judiya a bamɩne mɩ chen Samariya tɩchɛ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ tɛɛ be a Jerusalɛm.

eng: Some pious men buried Stephen and deeply grieved over him.
local: Nɩbɛ bamɩne na joro a Nãaŋmɩn 'mɔɔn a Sɩteven ũu tɩ na ba kõ a kuor.

eng: Saul began to wreak havoc against the church. Entering one house after another, he would drag off both men and women and throw them into prison.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Sɔɔl mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir bur buro a pupuorbiir gbulsɩ bara, na wʊ yʊɔrɔ kpɩɛrɛ a yie nyɔɔrɔ dɔɔr nɩ pɔbɔ tɩ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ.

eng: Those who had been scattered moved on, preaching the good news along the way.
local: A bala na jo ŋma a dẽdẽme chɛnɛ na mʊɔlɔ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr jie jaa ban chen.

eng: Philip went down to a city in Samaria and began to preach Christ to them.
local: Filip chen Samariya tẽe pɔ, na wʊ tɩ 'yɛr ɩka Yesu lɛ a Nɩ-iraa a be.

eng: The crowds were united by what they heard Philip say and the signs they saw him perform, and they gave him their undivided attention.
local: A nɩyɔɔ na wa wõ a lɛ a Filip na 'yɛr tɩ mɩ nyɛ a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ wʊn tʊ̃, a ba yuo toor chɛlɛ a lɛ wʊn 'yɛrɛ.

eng: With loud shrieks, unclean spirits came out of many people, and many who were paralyzed or crippled were healed.
local: A sɩdɛbɛ ala na dɔ̃ɔ pɔɔrɔ a nɩbɛ ŋmen chɛlsɩ wa yin a nɩbɛ pɔ, ka a gbɛkũn nɩ a gbɛrsɩ yɔɔ jaa sa.

eng: There was great rejoicing in that city.
local: Ka a tẽe 'lɔ dẽme ba jaa chɩlɛ.

eng: Before Philips arrival, a certain man named Simon had practiced sorcery in that city and baffled the people of Samaria. He claimed to be a great person.
local: Daba kɔ̃ɔ dɔ̃ɔ be be ban bʊɔlɔ Simon na wʊ ma tʊ̃nɔ̃ tãsɔɔ tʊ̃mɔ̃ ka a 'maa a Samariya nɩbɛ nɛɛ, na wʊ ma wile a wʊ mãɛ ɩka nɩkpɛ̃ɛ lɛ wʊ ɩ.

eng: Everyone, from the least to the greatest, gave him their undivided attention and referred to him as the power of God called Great.
local: A nɩbɛ ba jaa, a bɛrɛ nɩ a bil ba jaa ma yuon toor chɛlɛ wʊ tɩ 'yɛrɛ, A daba nyã 'lɔ kpɛ̃ɔ ɩ naa a gãdaa sʊɔ kpɛ̃ɔ.

eng: He had their attention because he had baffled them with sorcery for a long time.
local: Ba turo wʊ naa bojũu a kor naa wʊn tʊ̃nɔ̃ a tãsɔɔ tʊ̃mɔ̃ ka a 'maa ba nɛɛ.

eng: After they came to believe Philip, who preached the good news about Gods kingdom and the name of Jesus Christ, both men and women were baptized.
local: Tɩchɛ a lɛ ban wa sɔɔ de a lɛ a Filip na 'yɛr a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyã na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ 'yɛr, nɩ a Yesu na ɩ a Nɩ-iraa yuor, dɔɔr nɩ pɔbɔ ba jaa son a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ.

eng: Even Simon himself came to believe and was baptized. Afterward, he became one of Philips supporters. As he saw firsthand the signs and great miracles that were happening, he was astonished.
local: A Simon mɩ sɔɔ naa ka ba so wʊ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ a wʊ turo a Filip yʊɔrɔ, a nɛ'maa tʊ̃yɔɔ wʊn nyɛ wʊn tʊ̃.

eng: When word reached the apostles in Jerusalem that Samaria had accepted Gods word, they commissioned Peter and John to go to Samaria.
local: A nɩtʊ̃nsɩ na be a Jerusalɛm na wa wõ ɩka a Samariya nɩbɛ mɩ sɔɔn de a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr, ba tʊ̃n Pita nɩ Jɔɔn a ba sɛ̃.

eng: Peter and John went down to Samaria where they prayed that the new believers would receive the Holy Spirit. .
local: Ban tɩ ta, ba sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn kʊ a nɩbɛ ka ban nyɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ.

eng: This was because the Holy Spirit had not yet fallen on any of them; they had only been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
local: Bojũu Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ ba kpɛ ãsʊɔ jaa sɛrɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ lɛ ba so ba, nɩ a sɩ Soro Yesu yuor.

eng: So Peter and John laid their hands on them, and they received the Holy Spirit.
local: Ka a Pita nɩ a Jɔɔn pãa dɔɔl ba a nuur a ba nyɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ.

eng: When Simon perceived that the Spirit was given through the laying on of the apostles hands, he offered them money.
local: Simon na nyɛ a lɛ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ na dɔɔl ba a nũu ka ba nyɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ, a wʊ de libie kʊ ba.

eng: He said, Give me this authority too so that anyone on whom I lay my hands will receive the Holy Spirit.
local: tɩ 'yɛr, Yɩ mɩ kʊ ma a kpɛ̃ɔ nyãna ka maa wa dɔɔl nɩrɛ jaa a n nũu, a wʊ mɩ ma nyɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ.

eng: Peter responded, May your money be condemned to hell along with you because you believed you could buy Gods gift with money!.
local: Ka a Pita 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Fʊ̃ʊ nɩ a fʊ libie jaa yɩ wɛ, bojũu fʊ tɩɛr ɩka fʊn tʊ̃ɔ da a Nãaŋmɩn kʊfʊ nɩ libie.

eng: You can have no part or share in Gods word because your heart isnt right with God.
local: Faa tara sɔr na lɔ̃ɔ tʊ̃n sɩ ɛ, bojũu a fʊ nyãa ba tor a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ ɛ.

eng: Therefore, change your heart and life! Turn from your wickedness! Plead with the Lord in the hope that your wicked intent can be forgiven.
local: Lɩɛb a fʊ pututɩɛr nyãna na fʊ sʊɔr a sɩ Soro amɩne sɛ̃ wʊn vɛ̃n kʊ fʊ a fʊ tɩɛr dɛɛr nyãna fʊn tara.

eng: for I see that your bitterness has poisoned you and evil has you in chains.
local: N nyɛn bɔ̃ɔn fʊn ɩ pubier sʊɔ ka a yelbier nyɔɔ fʊ pɔɔ.

eng: Simon replied, All of you, please, plead to the Lord for me so that nothing of what you have said will happen to me.
local: A Simon pãa 'yɛr, Yɩ sʊɔr a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ ma ka ala yɩn 'yɛr taa waara a n sɛ̃ ɛ.

eng: After the apostles had testified and proclaimed the Lords word, they returned to Jerusalem, preaching the good news to many Samaritan villages along the way.
local: A lɛ Pita nɩ a Jɔɔn na wa man 'yɛr a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr baar, ba lɩɛb kulo a Jerusalɛm ba 'yɛrɛ naa a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyãna a tẽbil ala na chɛɛl a Samariya.

eng: An angel from the Lord spoke to Philip, At noon, take the road that leads from Jerusalem to Gaza.
local: Nãaŋmɩn malaka 'yɛr kʊ a Filip, Ir suu de a sɔr 'lɔ na yi a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ tu a Jerusalɛm chen a Gaja.

eng: So he did. Meanwhile, an Ethiopian man was on his way home from Jerusalem, where he had come to worship. He was a eunuch and an official responsible for the entire treasury of Candace.
local: Lɛ lɛ wʊ ir chiine, na wʊ tɩ tuor a Etiyopiya daba ãsʊɔ ban maal ka wʊ ab na wʊ ɩ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ kaara a Etiyopiya nãpɔɔ ban bʊɔlɔ Kandasɩ libie. Jerusalɛm lɛ wʊ chen tɩ puor.

eng: He was reading the prophet Isaiah while sitting in his carriage.
local: na wʊ lɩɛb kulo jãnɛ a wʊ wii torko pɔ na wʊ kara a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ Isaiya gãn.

eng: The Spirit told Philip, Approach this carriage and stay with it.
local: Ka a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ 'yɛr a Filip, Chen tɩ gbʊr a torko par.

eng: Running up to the carriage, Philip heard the man reading the prophet Isaiah. He asked, Do you really understand what you are reading.
local: A Filip jo chen a be a torko par na wʊ wõ a daba na kara a Isaiya gãn a wʊ sʊʊr wʊ, Fʊ bɔ̃ɔ naa lɛ par fʊn kara ɔ.

eng: The man replied, Without someone to guide me, how could I? Then he invited Philip to climb up and sit with him.
local: Ka a daba 'yɛr, Ŋmɩnɛ ɩn ɩrɛ wa bɔ̃ɔ a par, ka nɩrɛ baa wiil ma ɛ? Tɩ na wʊ bʊɔl a Filip ka wʊ do jãnɛ a torko pɔ.

eng: This was the passage of scripture he was reading: Like a sheep he was led to the slaughter and like a lamb before its shearer is silent so he didnt open his mouth. .
local: A anyãna lɛ a da-abbaa 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ dɔ̃ɔ kara a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ, Ba nyɔɔ wʊn chiine fɩka pɛr na tɩ kʊ, Wʊ ɩn jaa gbul fɩka pele ban põnõ a kɔɔl na waa haa nɛɛ ɛ.

eng: In his humiliation justice was taken away from him. Who can tell the story of his descendants because his life was taken from the earth? .
local: Wʊ siir a wʊ mãɛ a lɛ ban ba tɔɔr a wʊ 'yɛr kaa tor ɛ. An lɛ na tʊ̃ɔ 'yɛr a wʊ bal nɩbɛ 'yɛr? A wʊ nyɛvʊr ba tal tɛɛr a ka a wɛr pɔ ɛ.

eng: The eunuch asked Philip, Tell me, about whom does the prophet say this? Is he talking about himself or someone else.
local: Ka a da-abbaa nyã sʊɔr a Filip, Yãayãabɩlɛ, 'yɛr kʊ ma, an 'yɛr lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ nyã 'yɛrɛ? Wʊ mãɛ 'yɛr lɛ wʊ 'yɛrɛ bɩɩ nɩrɛ kɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr lɛ wʊ 'yɛrɛ.

eng: Starting with that passage, Philip proclaimed the good news about Jesus to him.
local: A Filip pãa piel na wʊ de a Nãaŋmɩn gãn 'yɛr nyãna man a Yesu 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ kʊ wʊ.

eng: As they went down the road, they came to some water. The eunuch said, Look! Water! What would keep me from being baptized.
local: Ban 'mataa chiine tɩ ta kʊ̃ɔ kɔ̃ɔ par a sɔr pɔ, ka a da-abbaa 'lɔ 'yɛr, Nyɛ kʊ̃ɔ, bãa lɛ so ka fʊ kʊ̃ so ma a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: He ordered that the carriage halt. Both Philip and the eunuch went down to the water, where Philip baptized him.
local: Tɩ na wʊ tɔɔ a torko a wʊ ara, ka a 'lɔ nɩ a Filip 'mataa kpɛ a kʊ̃ɔ pɔ, ka a Filip so wʊ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ.

eng: When they came up out of the water, the Lords Spirit suddenly took Philip away. The eunuch never saw him again but went on his way rejoicing.
local: Ban wa yi a kʊ̃ɔ pɔ, Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ wa jaa naa a Filip chiin ka a da-abbaa 'lɔ ba lɛ nyɛ wʊ go ɛ. Tɩ de wʊ sɔr chɩlɛ kulo.

eng: Philip found himself in Azotus. He traveled through that area, preaching the good news in all the cities until he reached Caesarea.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Filip tɩ yi naa Ajotusɩ na wʊ 'yɛrɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr a tẽn ala a jaa tɩ tãn Sisariya.

eng: There was a man in Caesarea named Cornelius, a centurion in the Italian Company.
local: Be a Sisariya, daba kɔ̃ɔ dɔ̃ɔ be be a ba bʊɔlɔ wʊ Konɩlɩyɔs, wʊ be naa a sojasɩ gbul 'lɔ pɔ a Itali nɩbɛ na bʊɔlɔ a sojasɩ kɔbaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ.

eng: He and his whole household were pious, Gentile God-worshippers. He gave generously to those in need among the Jewish people and prayed to God constantly.
local: 'Lɔ naa wʊ yir dẽme ba jaa puoro naa na ba joro Nãaŋmɩn dãbãɛ, na wʊ ma kʊrɔ a nɩbɛ bala an wõnõ pɔrɔ, na wʊ mɩ ma sʊɔrɔ a Nãaŋmɩn ba gala ɛ.

eng: About that time King Herod began to harass some who belonged to the church.
local: A sɔ̃ɔ nyãna lɛ a Nãa Hɛrɔd nyɔɔr a pupuorbo bamɩne na wʊn ge.

eng: One day at nearly three oclock in the afternoon, he clearly saw an angel from God in a vision. The angel came to him and said, Cornelius.
local: Bɩbir ãsʊɔ lɛ a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ na wa bal, a wʊ gã jãn jaa chɛchɛ nyɛ a Nãaŋmɩn malaka a wʊ wa ara a wʊ sɛ̃ tɩ bʊɔl wʊ, Konɩlɩyɔs.

eng: Startled, he stared at the angel and replied, What is it, Lord? The angel said, Your prayers and your compassionate acts are like a memorial offering to God.
local: Ka wʊ chaal a lɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ tɩ sʊʊr, Bo lɛ N Soro? Ka a malaka 'yɛr, A fʊ puorfʊ nɩ a kʊfʊ fʊn kʊrɔ a nɔ̃n dẽme don tɩ pɔɔ a Nãaŋmɩn ka wʊ tara a fʊ tɩɛr.

eng: Send messengers to Joppa at once and summon a certain Simon, the one known as Peter.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã, tʊ̃ nɩbɛ ka ba chen a Jopa tɩ tara daba kɔ̃ɔ na di Simon tɩ ba bʊɔlɔ wʊ Pita a wʊ wa.

eng: He is a guest of Simon the tanner, whose house is near the seacoast.
local: Wʊ kpɩɛrɛ naa a daba na nɔ̃nɔ̃ gãma mɩ na di a Simon yir, ka a wʊ yir gbʊr a man kpɛ̃ɛ gogoro.

eng: When the angel who was speaking to him had gone, Cornelius summoned two of his household servants along with a pious soldier from his personal staff.
local: A malaka na wa chen baar, Konɩlɩyɔs bʊɔl naa a wʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ ayi nɩ a soja ãsʊɔ mɩ na puoro na ma sʊ̃nɔ̃ wʊ.

eng: He explained everything to them, then sent them to Joppa.
local: Wʊ man a lɛ jaa na ɩ tɩ tʊ̃ ba a Jopa.

eng: At noon on the following day, as their journey brought them close to the city, Peter went up on the roof to pray.
local: A wɛr na wa chaar mɔ̃tɔ̃tuo sʊ̃ɔ, ka ba be a sɔr pɔ chiine tɩ gbʊrɔ a tẽe, a Pita mɩ don a gar ju Nãaŋmɩn sʊɔrfʊ jie.

eng: He became hungry and wanted to eat. While others were preparing the meal, he had a visionary experience.
local: Ka a kɔ̃ kpɛ wʊ a wʊ bʊɔrɔ bʊ̃kɔ̃ɔ na di, ban chɛnɛ maala a bʊ̃diir ka a dedee kpɛ wʊ.

eng: He saw heaven opened up and something like a large linen sheet being lowered to the earth by its four corners.
local: A wʊ nyɛ saaju na yuo a wʊ nyɛ bʊ̃kɔ̃ɔ fɩka fɔkpɛ̃ɛ na gbũ jiir anaar ka wʊ suro wa gbʊrɔ a tẽe.

eng: Inside the sheet were all kinds of four-legged animals, reptiles, and wild birds.
local: Ka a dʊ̃gbɛɛ anaar a jaa bʊrɔ be be nɩ a tẽe bʊ̃vʊʊrsɩ nɩ a lubilɔɔrsɩ a jaa.

eng: A voice told him, Get up, Peter! Kill and eat.
local: Be lɛ a kɔkɔr pãa 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Pita, ir kʊ 'wɔb.

eng: Peter exclaimed, Absolutely not, Lord! I have never eaten anything impure or unclean.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Pita 'yɛr, Sɩrɛ jaa, kai. N Soro, n ba 'wɔb a dʊ̃dɛbɛ anyãna nɩ a dʊ̃chiirsɩ anyãna ãsʊɔ jaa ɛ.

eng: The voice spoke a second time, Never consider unclean what God has made pure.
local: Ka a kɔkɔr lɛ 'yɛr go, Ta bʊɔl bon jaa bʊ̃dɛɛr 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn na maal a wʊ ɩ bʊ̃sʊ̃ ɛ.

eng: This happened three times, then the object was suddenly pulled back into heaven.
local: Gbɛɛ ata lɛ a ɩ, tɩchɛ ka a fɔbaa lɛ jaa a lɛ nɛ don a saaju ajienaa.

eng: Peter was bewildered about the meaning of the vision. Just then, the messengers sent by Cornelius discovered the whereabouts of Simons house and arrived at the gate.
local: Lɛ a Pita na chɛnɛ pɛrɛ a nyɛfʊ nyã par, ka a dɔɔr bala a Konɩlɩyɔs na tʊ̃ mɩ nyɛn a Simon yir na ba ara a dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ.

eng: He had James, Johns brother, killed with a sword.
local: Wʊ vɛ̃ naa ka ba ŋmaa Jemesɩ na ɩ a Jɔɔn yɛbɛ ju.

eng: When he saw that this pleased the Jews, he arrested Peter as well. This happened during the Festival of Unleavened Bread.
local: A Hɛrɔd nyɛn a lɛ ka a pɛl a Juu nɩbɛ pʊɔ, ka wʊ nyɔɔ a Pita mɩ. A anyãna ɩ naa a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ ban tara a Paanʊ̃kõo Lɔ̃ɔfʊ.

eng: He put Peter in prison, handing him over to four squads of soldiers, sixteen in all, who guarded him. He planned to charge him publicly after the Passover.
local: Wʊn nyɔɔ a Pita a lɛ, a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ lɛ wʊ tɩ pɔɔ wʊ, tɩ na wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ sojasɩ jiir anaar ka ba jʊrɔ taa kaara nɩ wʊ. Bojũu wʊ bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka a Gõl Bar Difʊ wʊ po baar tɩchɛ ka wʊ tɔɔr a wʊ 'yɛr ka nɩrɛ jaa nyɛ.

eng: While Peter was held in prison, the church offered earnest prayer to God for him.
local: Lɛ lɛ ba gu a Pita be a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ, tɩchɛ ka a pupuorbiir mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn kʊrɔ wʊ.

eng: The night before Herod was going to bring Peters case forward, Peter was asleep between two soldiers and bound with two chains, with soldiers guarding the prison entrance.
local: A tãsɔɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ a wɛr na chaar ka a Hɛrɔd na vɛ̃ ka ba waan a Pita ka wʊ tɔɔr a wʊ 'yɛr, bɔ̃jɔrsɩ ayi lɛ ba 'yɔ̃ɔ a Pita gbɛb sojasɩ ayi ka wʊ be a ba sãlsɔɔ gã guro, ka a sojasɩ bamɩne ara a pɔɔfʊ die dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ gu.

eng: Suddenly an angel from the Lord appeared and a light shone in the prison cell. After nudging Peter on his side to awaken him, the angel raised him up and said, Quick! Get up! The chains fell from his wrists.
local: Ajienaa lɛ a sɩ Soro malaka wa kpɛ ara a wʊ sɛ̃ be a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ, ka a die wʊ jaa chaar. Ka a malaka ŋme a Pita jaa 'mɛ 'mɛ 'mɛ, sãɩ wʊ 'yɛr, Pɔɔ fɔɔ jaa ir, ka a bɔ̃jɔrsɩ for a Pita nũ'yɔ̃ɔ pɔ lo.

eng: The angel continued, Get dressed. Put on your sandals. Peter did as he was told. The angel said, Put on your coat and follow me.
local: Be lɛ a malaka 'yɛr kʊ a Pita, Su a fuu nɩ a nafɔbɔ na fʊ 'mɔɔ a fɔbaa vaar na fʊ turo nɩ ma. Ka a Pita ɩ a lɛ a malaka na 'yɛr.

eng: Following the angel, Peter left the prison. However, he didnt realize the angel had actually done all this. He thought he was seeing a vision.
local: Pita tu wʊn a lɛ tɩ yin a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ, tɩchɛ nɩbir ba yuo wʊ a wʊ bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a lɛ a malaka na 'yɔ̃ɔ a wʊ maala ɩn sɩrɛ ɛ, wʊ tɩɛr man tãa jãn lɛ wʊ jãna.

eng: They passed the first and second guards and came to the iron gate leading to the city. It opened for them by itself. After leaving the prison, they proceeded the length of one street, when abruptly the angel was gone.
local: Ba tɩ yin a dɔ̃dɔr dɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ pɔ, na ba lɛ tɩ yi a dɔ̃dɔr ayi sʊɔ pɔ, tɩ pãa ta a yõo dɔ̃dɔr a kur pãn na pɔɔ. Ban wa ta a wʊ yuo wʊ yõ a ba yi. A malaka tara na a Pita den a sɔr dẽdẽe ãsʊɔ chiin bãlãa tɩ bar wʊ tɩ chen ajienaa.

eng: At that, Peter came to his senses and remarked, Now Im certain that the Lord sent his angel and rescued me from Herod and from everything the Jewish people expected.
local: Ka Pita tɩɛr pãa yuo a wʊ 'yɛr, Pʊ̃pãanyã n bɔ̃ɔn naa an ba ɩ baal ɛ, ɩka a Nãaŋmɩn lɛ tʊ̃ a wʊ malaka a wʊ wa 'loo ma faa a Hɛrɔd nũu pɔ, nɩ a lɛ jaa a Juu nɩbɛ na tɩɛr ɩka ba ge ma.

eng: Realizing this, he made his way to Marys house. Many believers had gathered there and were praying.
local: A anyãna wʊn wa tɩɛr yuo, wʊ chen a Mɛɛr na ɩ a Jɔɔn Maakɩ ma yir, be lɛ a pupuorbiir yɔɔ lɔ̃ɔ taa sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn kʊrɔ wʊ.

eng: When Peter knocked at the outer gate, a female servant named Rhoda went to answer.
local: Pita wa kpoor a yõo dɔ̃dɔr pãn, ka a gbãgbaa pɔɔbile ban bʊɔlɔ Woroda chen a pãn yuofʊ jie.

eng: She was so overcome with joy when she recognized Peters voice that she didnt open the gate. Instead, she ran back in and announced that Peter was standing at the gate.
local: Wʊn wa wõ ka a ɩ Pita kɔkɔr lɛ ka a nʊ̃ɔ kpɛ wʊ tɩ põn bar, waa yuo a pãn ɛ, tɩ lɩɛb jo kpɛ a die pɔ tɩ 'yɛrɛ, Pita lɛ be a yõo dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ.

eng: Youve lost your mind! they responded. She stuck by her story with such determination that they began to say, It must be his guardian angel.
local: A ba tãn wʊ pĩpĩi bar tɩ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Gelfʊ baal lɛ kpɛ fʊ! Tɩ wʊ chɛnɛ mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir 'yɛrɛ ɩka sɩrɛ jaa lɛ, ka ba 'yɛr, A wʊ malaka tɔ̃ɔ lɛ.

eng: Calling out, they inquired whether the Simon known as Peter was a guest there.
local: Ba bʊɔl sʊʊr, Daba na di Simon tɩ ba bʊɔlɔ wʊ Pita ka lɛ wʊ kpɩɛrɛ ɔ.

eng: Meanwhile, Peter remained outside, knocking at the gate. They finally opened the gate and saw him there, and they were astounded.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Pita chɛnɛ kpooro a pãn, ka ba tɩ yuo nyɛ ka a ɩ 'lɔ lɛ ka a nɛɛ 'maa ba.

eng: Meanwhile, Saul was still spewing out murderous threats against the Lords disciples. He went to the high priest.
local: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ a Sɔɔl chɛnɛ pʊrɔ a sɩ Soro poturbo ju, na wʊ bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ kʊ ba. Wʊ chen naa a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ sɛ̃.

eng: While Peter was brooding over the vision, the Spirit interrupted him, Look! Three people are looking for you.
local: Lɛ a Pita na chɛnɛ tɩɛrɛ a nyɛfʊ nyãna ka a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Simon, dɔɔr ata lɛ bʊɔrɔ fʊ sʊrɔ.

eng: He gestured with his hand to quiet them down, then recounted how the Lord led him out of prison. He said, Tell this to James and the brothers and sisters. Then he left for another place.
local: Ka a Pita wɩlwɩl ba a nũu a ba bar gɔ̃n tɩchɛ ka wʊ man a lɛ a sɩ Soro na yin wʊ a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ. Wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba ɩka ba tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a Jemesɩ nɩ a yɛɛr, na wʊ bar ba tɩ yi chen jiyuo.

eng: seeking letters to the synagogues in Damascus. If he found persons who belonged to the Way, whether men or women, these letters would authorize him to take them as prisoners to Jerusalem.
local: na wʊ tɩ sʊɔr sɔr de gãn na chiin a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ jiir ala na be a Damaskus ɩka 'lɔ wa tɩ nyɛ nɩrɛ na turo a Sɔr nyã, ka pɔɔ lɛ bɩɩ daba lɛ wʊ tʊ̃ɔ nyɔɔr ba waan a Jerusalɛm.

eng: The next morning the soldiers were flustered about what had happened to Peter.
local: A wɛr na chaar bibio, ka a sojasɩ ɩ chĩcham na ba sʊrɔ taa, Bo lɛ na tara a Pita.

eng: Go downstairs. Dont ask questions; just go with them because I have sent them.
local: Ir suu a ba sɛ̃, taa ɩrɛ wa baala fʊn na lɔ̃ɔ chiin ba ɛ, maa lɛ tʊ̃ ba.

eng: During the journey, as he approached Damascus, suddenly a light from heaven encircled him.
local: Ala lɛ wʊ de a sɔr chiine tɩ gbʊrɔ a Damaskus ka chãa yi be a saaju ajienaa wa nyɩɩr jil wʊ.

eng: Herod called for a thorough search. When Peter didnt turn up, Herod interrogated the guards and had them executed. Afterward, Herod left Judea in order to spend some time in Caesarea.
local: Hɛrɔd vɛ̃n ka ba yɔbɔ wʊ faa na baa nyɛ wʊ ɛ, na wʊ kpãkpãan a sojasɩ mɩ faa tɩ 'yɛr ɩka ba kʊ ba. Ka a Hɛrɔd pãa yi a Judiya na wʊ chen a Sisariya na wʊ tɩ jãnɛ a be bãlãa.

eng: He fell to the ground and heard a voice asking him, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me.
local: Ka wʊ lo tẽe tɩ wõ kɔkɔr na bʊɔlɔ wʊ sʊrɔ, Sɔɔl, Sɔɔl, bo lɛ so a fʊ gere ma a lɛ.

eng: Herod had been furious with the people of Tyre and Sidon for some time. They made a pact to approach him together, since their region depended on the kings realm for its food supply. They persuaded Blastus, the kings personal attendant, to join their cause, then appealed for an end to hostilities.
local: A Hɛrɔd dɔ̃ɔ tara a Tire nɩbɛ nɩ a Sidon nɩbɛ nɩ jɛɛr, ka ba nyɛtaa nɛɛ na wa a nãa 'maalfʊ jie. Blastus na ɩ a nãa gbãgbaa wʊn 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ sɛ̃ lɛ ba tʊ̃ ɩka wʊ 'maal a nãa kʊ ba bojũu a wʊ wɛr pɔ lɛ ba nyɛrɛ a ba bʊ̃diir.

eng: So Peter went downstairs and told them, Im the one you are looking for. Why have you come.
local: Ka a Pita ir suu tɩ nyɛ a nɩbɛ na wʊ 'yɛr, Maa lɛ ɩ a nɩrɛ yɩn bʊɔrɔ, bãa 'yɔ̃ɔ yɩ wa.

eng: Saul asked, Who are you, Lord? I am Jesus, whom you are harassing, came the reply.
local: Ka a Sɔɔl sʊʊr, An lɛ fʊ ɩ Soro? Ka wʊ 'yɛr, Maa lɛ ɩ a Yesu fʊn gere.

eng: On the scheduled day Herod dressed himself in royal attire, seated himself on the throne, and gave a speech to the people.
local: Bɩbir kɔ̃ɔ, a Nãa Hɛrɔd sun a wʊ nafɔɔr jãnɛ nɩ a nãa dakɔɔ ju, na wʊn 'yɛr 'yɛr kʊ a nɩbɛ.

eng: Now get up and enter the city. You will be told what you must do.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã, ir na fʊ kpɛ be a tẽe pɔ, ban wiil fʊ naa a lɛ fʊn na ɩ.

eng: Those assembled kept shouting, over and over, This is a gods voice, not the voice of a mere human.
local: Ka a nɩbɛ ŋmiere chɛlsɩ 'yɛrɛ, Ŋmɩn kɔkɔr lɛ a nyãna na 'yɛrɛ, nɩrɛ ba lɛ ɛ.

eng: They replied, Weve come on behalf of Cornelius, a centurion and righteous man, a God-worshipper who is well-respected by all Jewish people. A holy angel directed him to summon you to his house and to hear what you have to say.
local: A ba 'yɛr, Konɩlɩyɔs na ɩ a sojasɩ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ sɛ̃ lɛ sɩ yi. A wʊ yele tor naa ka wʊ ɩ nɩrɛ na joro Nãaŋmɩn dãbãɛ ka a Juu nɩbɛ ba jaa 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ wʊ. A Nãaŋmɩn malaka wa 'yɛr kʊ wʊ ɩka wʊ bʊɔl fʊ a fʊ wa be a wʊ yir a wʊ wõ lɛ jaa fʊn na man.

eng: Peter invited them into the house as his guests. The next day he got up and went with them, together with some of the believers from Joppa.
local: A Pita pãa tara ba kpɛ̃n tɩ gaal. Ka a wɛr chaar, a bala nɩ a Pita nɩ a pupuorbiir bamɩne na yi a Jopa pʊɔ ba a ba 'mataa chen.

eng: Immediately an angel from the Lord struck Herod down, because he didnt give the honor to God. He was eaten by worms and died.
local: Ajienaa lɛ ka a sɩ Soro malaka wa 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ baal bojũu waa de a 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn ɛ, ka a bufulo 'wɔb a nyaba ŋma ka wʊ kpi.

eng: Those traveling with him stood there speechless; they heard the voice but saw no one.
local: Ka a dɔɔr bala nɩ a Sɔɔl na 'mataa chiine gĩe ara, na ba wõ a dɔɔn, tɩchɛ baa nyɛ nɩrɛ ɛ.

eng: They arrived in Caesarea the following day. Anticipating their arrival, Cornelius had gathered his relatives and close friends.
local: Ba chen ka a wɛr tɩ chaar a ba ta a Sisariya, ka a ɩ a Konɩlɩyɔs mɩ chɛlɛ ba naa na wʊ bʊɔl a wʊ yir dẽme nɩ a wʊ barmɩne ba jaa lɔ̃ɔ taa a ba jɛ̃ gu.

eng: After they picked Saul up from the ground, he opened his eyes but he couldnt see. So they led him by the hand into Damascus.
local: Ka a Sɔɔl ir a tẽe ara na wʊ yuo a nɩbie tɩ waa lɛ nyɛrɛ ɛ. A ba nyɔɔ wʊ a nũu tɔɔ kpɛ̃n be a Damaskus.

eng: Gods word continued to grow and increase.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr chɛnɛ yaara.

eng: As Peter entered the house, Cornelius met him and fell at his feet in order to honor him.
local: Lɛ a Pita na ta kpɩɛrɛ a Konɩlɩyɔs yir wʊ ir tuor a Pita gbur dumo a wʊ niem na wʊ puor wʊ nɩ 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ.

eng: Barnabas and Saul returned to Antioch from Jerusalem after completing their mission, bringing with them John, who was also known as Mark.
local: Lɛ a Banabasɩ nɩ a Sɔɔl na wa tʊ̃ a ba tʊ̃mɔ̃ baar a Jerusalɛm, ba tara naa Jɔɔn Maakɩ 'mataa chiin.

eng: For three days he was blind and neither ate nor drank anything.
local: Wʊ maal ber ata waa lɛ nyɛrɛ ɛ, waa di bʊ̃diir bɩɩ nyũ kʊ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: But Peter lifted him up, saying, Get up! Like you, Im just a human.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Pita nyɔɔ wʊ a wʊ ir, ka wʊ 'yɛr, Ara, a maa mɩ nɩsaal lɛ n mɩ ɩ.

eng: In Damascus there was a certain disciple named Ananias. The Lord spoke to him in a vision, Ananias! He answered, Yes, Lord.
local: Tɩchɛ a poturo ãsʊɔ ben a be Damaskus a ba bʊɔlɔ wʊ Ananias ka a sɩ Soro wa bʊɔl wʊ a jãna pɔ, Ananias. A wʊ sɔɔ, Ɔ̃ɔ, n Soro.

eng: The Lord instructed him, Go to Judas house on Straight Street and ask for a man from Tarsus named Saul. He is praying.
local: Ka a Soro 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Ir de a Sɔr Dẽdẽe chiine na fʊ tɩ ta a Judas yir sʊʊr daba na yi Tasusɩ a ba bʊɔlɔ Sɔɔl, be lɛ wʊ be sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: In a vision he has seen a man named Ananias enter and put his hands on him to restore his sight.
local: A wʊ jãna pɔ, wʊ nyɛn daba ban bʊɔlɔ Ananias na dɔɔl wʊ nũu ka wʊ lɛ nyɛrɛ.

eng: As they continued to talk, Peter went inside and found a large gathering of people.
local: Wʊ 'yɛr wʊ naa lɛ tɩ kpɛ a die pɔ tɩ nyɛ nɩyɔɔ na duo.

eng: Ananias countered, Lord, I have heard many reports about this man. People say he has done horrible things to your holy people in Jerusalem.
local: A Ananias 'yɛr, N Soro, n wõn a daba nyã yelyɔɔ a lɛ wʊn ge a fʊ nɩsʊ̃n a be a Jerusalɛm.

eng: He said to them, You all realize that it is forbidden for a Jew to associate or visit with outsiders. However, God has shown me that I should never call a person impure or unclean.
local: A wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩn mãɛ bɔ̃ɔn naa an ba tara sɔr ɩka a sɩn Juu nɩbɛ ba lɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ nɩ a yɩn bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ. Tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn wiil ma naa ɩka n taa bʊɔl nɩrɛ jaa nɩdɛɛr ɛ, bɩɩ nɩsɔ̃ɔ naa ɛ.

eng: Hes here with authority from the chief priests to arrest everyone who calls on your name.
local: A ka mɩ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ mɩ kʊ wʊn sɔr ɩka wʊ wa nyɔɔr a bala jaa na bʊɔlɔ a fʊ yuor.

eng: For this reason, when you sent for me, I came without objection. I want to know, then, why you sent for me.
local: Ala lɛ so yɩn wa bʊɔl ma n ba baala ɛ, bɩɩ 'yɛr ɩka n kʊ̃ wa ɛ, bo 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ yɩ bʊɔl ma.

eng: The Lord replied, Go! This man is the agent I have chosen to carry my name before Gentiles, kings, and Israelites.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Soro 'yɛr kʊ a Ananias, Chen, a daba nyãna lɛ ɩ a n bʊ̃-iraa ka wʊn tuo a maa yuor chiin a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ pɔ, nɩ a ba nãmɩne sɛ̃, lɔ̃ɔn a Isɩral nɩbɛ.

eng: Cornelius answered, Four days ago at this same time, three oclock in the afternoon, I was praying at home. Suddenly a man in radiant clothing stood before me.
local: Ka a Konɩlɩyɔs 'yɛr, Ber anaar nɩ dɩna ɩn be a die pɔ sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ na bal baar. Ajienaa lɛ ka a ɩ daba na su fuu ka wʊ nyɩlɛ wa ara a n sɛ̃.

eng: I will show him how much he must suffer for the sake of my name.
local: Ɩ̃n wiil wʊ naa a lɛ wʊn na di dɔɔyɛ a n yuor jũu.

eng: He said, Cornelius, God has heard your prayers, and your compassionate acts are like a memorial offering to him.
local: tɩ na wʊ bʊɔl ma, Konɩlɩyɔs, Nãaŋmɩn wõn a fʊ sʊɔrfʊ na wʊ tɩɛr a fʊ kʊfʊ fʊn kʊrɔ a nɔ̃n dẽme.

eng: Ananias went to the house. He placed his hands on Saul and said, Brother Saul, the Lord sent me—Jesus, who appeared to you on the way as you were coming here. He sent me so that you could see again and be filled with the Holy Spirit.
local: Be lɛ a Ananias ir chen tɩ kpɛ be a yir, na wʊ tɩ dɔɔl wʊ a nũu tɩ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, N yɛbɛ Sɔɔl, a sɩ Soro Yesu na yi fʊ be a sɔr pɔ fʊn waara lɛ tʊ̃ ma a fʊ sɛ̃ ka fʊn lɛ nyɛrɛ go, na fʊ sɛɛn a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ.

eng: Therefore, send someone to Joppa and summon Simon, who is known as Peter. He is a guest in the home of Simon the tanner, located near the seacoast.
local: Tʊ̃ nɩrɛ a wʊ chen a Jopa tɩ bʊɔl Simon ban lɛ bʊɔlɔ Pita, wʊ be naa a daba ban bʊɔlɔ a Simon na nɔ̃nɔ̃ gãma ka a yir gbʊr man gogoro.

eng: Instantly, flakes fell from Sauls eyes and he could see again. He got up and was baptized.
local: Ajienaa ka bʊ̃nyɩnsɩ 'lɔɔr a Sɔɔl nɩbie pɔ lo a wʊ lɛ nyɛrɛ go. Wʊ ir naa a ba tɔ̃ɔ so wʊ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ.

eng: I sent for you right away, and you were kind enough to come. Now, here we are, gathered in the presence of God to listen to everything the Lord has directed you to say.
local: Ala lɛ so ka n mɩ tʊ̃ a fɔɔ a lɛ nɛ bʊɔl fʊ, a fʊ mɩ pɛl pʊɔ tɩ wa. Pʊ̃pãanyã, Nãaŋmɩn niem lɛ sɩ jaa jãnɛ tɩ na chɛl bon jaa a sɩ Soro na tʊ̃n fʊ ɩka fʊ wa 'yɛr kʊ sɩ.

eng: After eating, he regained his strength. He stayed with the disciples in Damascus for several days.
local: Wʊn wa di a bʊ̃diir baar wʊ lɛ nyɛn kpɛ̃ɔ, na wʊ jãnɛ a poturbo bala sɛ̃ a be a Damaskus bãlãa.

eng: Peter said, I really am learning that God doesnt show partiality to one group of people over another.
local: Ka a Pita pãa piel na wʊ 'yɛrɛ, Pʊ̃pãanyã n pãa bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka Nãaŋmɩn ba ooro ɛ.

eng: Right away, he began to preach about Jesus in the synagogues. He is Gods Son, he declared.
local: Ajienaa, wʊ piel 'yɛrɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr be a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ jiir, na wʊ 'yɛrɛ ɩka a Yesu ɩ naa Nãaŋmɩn Bie.

eng: Rather, in every nation, whoever worships him and does what is right is acceptable to him.
local: Tɩchɛ wʊ ma den nɩbʊrɔ ba jaa na joro wʊ dãbãɛ tɩ maal a lɛ na tor.

eng: Everyone who heard him was baffled. They questioned each other, Isnt he the one who was wreaking havoc among those in Jerusalem who called on this name? Hadnt he come here to take those same people as prisoners to the chief priests.
local: Bala jaa na wõ a wʊ 'yɛr ka a 'maa ba nɛɛ a ba sʊrɔ, A ba ɩ a daba nyãna lɛ ir gere a bala a be a Jerusalɛm na bʊɔlɔ a yuor nyãna ɛ? Na wʊ wa a ka mɩ ɩka wʊ wa nyɔɔr a bala mɩ chiin tɩ kʊ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ ɛ.

eng: This is the message of peace he sent to the Israelites by proclaiming the good news through Jesus Christ: He is Lord of all!.
local: A 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyã lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn tɩr kʊ a Isɩral nɩbɛ nɩ a nyã'maar 'yɛr na tu a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto na ɩ a bon jaa Soro sɛ̃.

eng: But Saul grew stronger and stronger. He confused the Jews who lived in Damascus by proving that Jesus is the Christ.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Sɔɔl kpɛ̃ɔ chɛnɛ pʊɔrɔ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ka wʊ 'yɛr tʊ̃ɔ a Juu nɩbɛ na kpɩɛrɛ a Damaskus na wʊ wiil ba ɩka a Yesu lɛ ɩ a Nɩ-iraa.

eng: You know what happened throughout Judea, beginning in Galilee after the baptism John preached.
local: Yɩ bɔ̃ɔn naa ala na ɩ a Judiya paal wʊ jaa na piel a Galilee a lɛ a Jɔɔn na wa mʊɔl a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ sofʊ 'yɛr baar.

eng: After this had gone on for some time, the Jews hatched a plot to kill Saul.
local: Ber yɔɔ na wa po, a Juu nɩbɛ guor taa ɩka ba kʊ a Sɔɔl.

eng: You know about Jesus of Nazareth, whom God anointed with the Holy Spirit and endowed with power. Jesus traveled around doing good and healing everyone oppressed by the devil because God was with him.
local: Lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na ir a Yesu na yi Najaretɩ na wʊ de a wʊ Sɩɛ nɩ a wʊ kpɛ̃ɔ kʊ wʊ. Wʊ yɔn a ka wʊ jaa jilʊ̃ na wʊ maala vɩla sãana nɩ baalsɩ nɩ a bala jaa a dɛɛr sʊɔ na tara, bojũu Nãaŋmɩn ben a wʊ sɛ̃.

eng: However, he found out about their scheme. They were keeping watch at the city gates around the clock so they could assassinate him.
local: tɩchɛ kaa a Sɔɔl bɔ̃ɔn a ba guorfʊ. Mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ nɩ tãsɔɔ jaa lɛ ba mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir guro wʊ a tẽe dɔ̃dɔɩ nɛɛr, bʊɔrɔ a wʊ kʊ̃ fʊ.

eng: We are witnesses of everything he did, both in Judea and in Jerusalem. They killed him by hanging him on a tree.
local: Sɩn lɛ nyɛn nɩbir, a ala a jaa wʊn tʊ̃ a Juu nɩbɛ tẽn pɔ, nɩ a Jerusalɛm. Ba nyɔɔ wʊn kpa yɔɔl a daa ju ka wʊ kpi.

eng: But his disciples took him by night and lowered him in a basket through an opening in the city wall.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a bala na turo wʊ 'mɔɔ wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ pɩɛ pɔ tãsɔɔ na ba tur wʊ bar ka wʊ tu a dãpuor dachin pɔ suu.

eng: but God raised him up on the third day and allowed him to be seen.
local: Tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn sãɩ wʊ naa a kũu pɔ, a ber ata daar a wʊ ir ɩ vʊr na wʊ vɛ̃ a ba nyɛ wʊ.

eng: When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to join the disciples, but they were all afraid of him. They didnt believe he was really a disciple.
local: Wʊn tɩ ta Jerusalɛm, wʊ bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka wʊ lɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ nɩ a poturbo tɩchɛ a ba joro wʊ dãbãɛ, baa sɔɔ ɩka wʊ ɩ naa a poturo ãsʊɔ ɛ.

eng: not by everyone but by us. We are witnesses whom God chose beforehand, who ate and drank with him after God raised him from the dead.
local: Nɩbɛ ba jaa ba lɛ nyɛ wʊ ɛ, tɩchɛ a sɩn na ɩ a dãasɩɛ dẽme a Nãaŋmɩn na de nie ir, sɩn nɩ 'lɔ 'mantaa di tɩ nyũ wʊn wa ir a kũu pɔ baar.

eng: Then Barnabas brought Saul to the apostles and told them the story about how Saul saw the Lord on the way and that the Lord had spoken to Saul. He also told them about the confidence with which Saul had preached in the name of Jesus in Damascus.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Banabasɩ tara wʊ chiin be a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ sɛ̃. 'Lɔ lɛ pãa man kʊ ba a lɛ a Sɔɔl na nyɛ a sɩ Soro a sɔr pɔ, ka a Soro 'yɛr 'yɛr kʊ wʊ. Wʊ 'yɛr ɩka a be a Damaskus wʊ mʊɔl 'yɛr a Yesu yuor 'yɛr, na wʊ ta jo dãbãɛ ɛ.

eng: He commanded us to preach to the people and to testify that he is the one whom God appointed as judge of the living and the dead.
local: 'Lɔ lɛ kpãkpãan sɩ ɩka sɩ mʊɔl 'yɛr kʊ a nɩbɛ ɩka 'lɔ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn ir ka wʊ ɩ a vʊɛ nɩ a kpĩisɩ 'yɛr tɔɔrɔ.

eng: After this, Saul moved freely among the disciples in Jerusalem and was speaking with confidence in the name of the Lord.
local: A Sɔɔl jãnɛ a be a ba sɛ̃ na wʊ so wʊ mãɛ yʊɔrɔ be a Jerusalɛm na wʊ mʊɔlɔ a sɩ Soro Yesu yuor 'yɛrɛ ba joro dãbãɛ ɛ.

eng: All the prophets testify about him that everyone who believes in him receives forgiveness of sins through his name.
local: 'Lɔ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ ba jaa 'yɛr a wʊ 'yɛr ɩka, nɩrɛ jaa wa sɔɔ de wʊ, wʊn nyɛn yelbier vɛ̃ kʊfʊ tu a wʊ yuor pɔ.

eng: He got into debates with the Greek-speaking Jews as well, but they tried to kill him.
local: Wʊ ma 'yɛrɛ naa ka a 'lɔ nɩ a Juu nɩbɛ bala ban dɔɔ a paal yuo pɔ ka baa tʊɔrɔ 'yɛrɛ a Juu kɔkɔr ɛ, ma chɩrɛ taa, ka ba bʊɔrɔ a wʊ kʊfʊ.

eng: While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit fell on everyone who heard the word.
local: Lɛ a Pita na chɛnɛ 'yɛrɛ a anyãna, ka a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ wa kpɛ a bala jaa na wõnõ a 'yɛr nyãna.

eng: When the family of believers learned about this, they escorted him down to Caesarea and sent him off to Tarsus.
local: A poturbo na wa wõ a nɩtɔ̃ ba tara wʊn chiin a Sisariya tɩ bar ka wʊ chen Tasusɩ.

eng: The circumcised believers who had come with Peter were astonished that the gift of the Holy Spirit had been poured out even on the Gentiles.
local: Ka a Juu nɩbɛ bala na sɔɔ de na tu a Pita wa, nɛɛ 'maa ba naa a lɛ ban nyɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na pʊr a wʊ Sɩɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ.

eng: Then the church throughout Judea, Galilee, and Samaria enjoyed a time of peace. God strengthened the church, and its life was marked by reverence for the Lord. Encouraged by the Holy Spirit, the church continued to grow in numbers.
local: Damfʊ ba lɛ be a bala na puoro be a Judiya, Galilee nɩ a Samariya, ka a nyã'maar. Ba kpɛ̃mɛ naa ka a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ ba ãkpɛ̃ɔ ka ba nʊ̃ɔnɔ̃ na ba joro a sɩ Soro dãbãɛ.

eng: They heard them speaking in other languages and praising God. Peter asked.
local: A ba wõ ban 'yɛrɛ kɔkɔyor na ba pɛɛrɛ nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn. Ka a Pita pãa 'yɛr.

eng: As Peter toured the whole region, he went to visit Gods holy people in Lydda.
local: Ka a Pita yʊɔrɔ a tẽn na wʊ wa chen Lida ɩka wʊ tɩ piir a Nãaŋmɩn puorbo kaa.

eng: These people have received the Holy Spirit just as we have. Surely no one can stop them from being baptized with water, can they.
local: Nɩrɛ na tʊ̃ɔ ŋmaa a nɩbɛ banyãna sɔr ka ba ta so a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ ɛ? Ba mɩ nyɛn a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ nɩtãa a sɩn.

eng: There he found a man named Aeneas who was paralyzed and had been confined to his bed for eight years.
local: Be lɛ wʊ tɩ nyɛ daba ban bʊɔlɔ Ainɩyas na gbɛr gã yuomo aniin.

eng: He directed that they be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they invited Peter to stay for several days.
local: Wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ ba naa ka ba so a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ nɩ Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto yuor, a ba pãa sʊɔr a Pita ka wʊ be a ba sɛ̃ bãlãa.

eng: Peter said to him, Aeneas, Jesus Christ heals you! Get up and make your bed. At once he got up.
local: Ka a Pita bʊɔl wʊ, Ainɩyas, Yesu yuor pɔ, sa. Ir saaju na fʊ pilʊ̃ a fʊ bʊ̃gãnaa. Ajienaa lɛ a Ainɩyas ir saaju.

eng: Everyone who lived in Lydda and Sharon saw him and turned to the Lord.
local: Ka a Lida nɩbɛ ba jaa nɩ a Saron nɩbɛ na nyɛ a lɛ, a ba lɩɛb puoro a sɩ Soro.

eng: In Joppa there was a disciple named Tabitha . Her life overflowed with good works and compassionate acts on behalf of those in need.
local: Pɔɔ kɔ̃ɔ mɩ na dɔ̃ɔ puoro ben a be Jopa, ka ba bʊɔlɔ wʊ Tabita, a yo bʊ̃'yen 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ ba lɩɛb bʊɔlɔ Dɔɔkas. Wʊ ma maala na vɩla na wʊ sʊ̃nɔ̃ a nɔ̃n dẽme.

eng: About that time, though, she became so ill that she died. After they washed her body, they laid her in an upstairs room.
local: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ wʊ ɩ baal na wʊ kpi, a ba so wʊ a kʊ̃ɔ don gaal be a bɔɔpɩɛ pɔ.

eng: Since Lydda was near Joppa, when the disciples heard that Peter was there, they sent two people to Peter. They urged, Please come right away.
local: Lida nɩ a Jopa mɩ ba jãa ɛ, kaa a pupuorbo wõ ɩka a Pita ben a Lida, a ba tʊ̃ nɩbɛ ayi ka ba tɩ sʊɔr wʊ, Yãayãabɩlɛ a wʊ wa fɔɔ.

eng: Peter went with them. Upon his arrival, he was taken to the upstairs room. All the widows stood beside him, crying as they showed the tunics and other clothing Dorcas made when she was alive.
local: Ka bala naa Pita jaa 'mataa chen, wʊn wa ta ba de wʊn don be a bɔɔpɩɛ die pɔ. A pɔɔkɔɛ ba jaa ara na jilʊ̃ wʊ kõnõ na ba wile a Pita a fuusɩ nɩ a fɔɔr a Dɔɔkas na maal wʊn dɔ̃ɔ chɛnɛ be a ba sɛ̃.

eng: Peter sent everyone out of the room, then knelt and prayed. He turned to the body and said, Tabitha, get up! She opened her eyes, saw Peter, and sat up.
local: Pita diin ba jaa a ba yi a be a die pɔ, tɩ na wʊ gbur dumo sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn, na wʊ lɩɛb tor a kũu tɩ 'yɛr, Tabita ir. A wʊ yuo a nɩbie nyɛ a Pita na wʊ ir jãnɛ.

eng: He gave her his hand and raised her up. Then he called Gods holy people, including the widows, and presented her alive to them.
local: Ka a Pita nyɔɔ wʊ a nũu sʊ̃ʊ wʊ a wʊ ir ara, tɩ pãa bʊɔl a bala na sɔɔ nɩ a pɔɔkɔɛ ka ba kpɛ wa nyɛ a wʊ ɩ nɩ nɩbir.

eng: The news spread throughout Joppa, and many put their faith in the Lord.
local: Ka a yele nyãna yaar a Jopa wʊ jaa ka a nɩbɛ yɔɔ jie wa sɔɔ a sɩ Soro.

eng: Peter stayed for some time in Joppa with a certain tanner named Simon.
local: A Pita jɛ̃n be a Jopa bãlãa a daba kɔ̃ɔ ban bʊɔlɔ Simon na ma nɔ̃nɔ̃ gãma yir.

eng: The apostles and the brothers and sisters throughout Judea heard that even the Gentiles had welcomed Gods word.
local: A nɩtʊ̃nsɩ nɩ a yɛɛr na be a Judiya wõ naa ɩka a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, mɩ sɔɔn a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr.

eng: When Peter went up to Jerusalem, the circumcised believers criticized him.
local: Lɛ a Pita na wa lɩɛb chen a Jerusalɛm a bala na ŋmaa a yʊɔr tɩ sɔɔl mii 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ 'yɛrɛ.

eng: They accused him, You went into the home of the uncircumcised and ate with them.
local: Fʊ ben chen a bala na ba ŋmaa a yʊɔr yir tɩ lɔ̃ɔ din ba wɛ.

eng: Step-by-step, Peter explained what had happened.
local: Ka a Pita pãa piel mana a lɛ jaa na ɩ na wʊ kʊ ba.

eng: I was in the city of Joppa praying when I had a visionary experience. In my vision, I saw something like a large linen sheet being lowered from heaven by its four corners. It came all the way down to me.
local: N ben a Jopa tẽe pɔ naa sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn ka a wa ɩ ma jaa de dee, ka n nyɛ nyɛfʊ kɔ̃ɔ. N nyɛn bʊ̃kɔ̃ɔ fɩka fɔkpɛ̃ɛ ban tur bar ka wʊ yi saaju suu na wʊ gbũ jiir anaar ka wʊ suu wa ara a be ɩn be.

eng: As I stared at it, wondering what it was, I saw four-legged animals—including wild beasts—as well as reptiles and wild birds.
local: Be lɛ n muul kaa naa nyɛ dʊ̃gbɛɛ anaar jaa bʊrɔ na be ka a wɛr pɔ, mɔdʊ̃n, bʊ̃vʊʊrsɩ lɔ̃ɔn a lubil.

eng: I heard a voice say, Get up, Peter! Kill and eat.
local: Ka n wõ kɔkɔr na 'yɛr, Pita ir na fʊ kʊ 'wɔb.

eng: I responded, Absolutely not, Lord! Nothing impure or unclean has ever entered my mouth.
local: Ka n haa 'yɛr, Kai, n Soro bon jaa na ɩ dɛɛr na ba vɩɛlɛ ɛ, ba kpɛ n nɛɛ pɔ jaa ɛ.

eng: The voice from heaven spoke a second time, Never consider unclean what God has made pure.
local: Ka a kɔkɔr lɛ 'yɛr yi a saaju go, Ta bʊɔl bon jaa Nãaŋmɩn na maal a wʊ vɩɛlɛ bʊ̃dɛɛr ɛ.

eng: This happened three times, then everything was pulled back into heaven.
local: A anyãna ɩ naa nɩtɔ̃nɔ̃ gbɛɛ ata tɩ lɛ tɔɔ a jaa don be a saaju go.

eng: At that moment three men who had been sent to me from Caesarea arrived at the house where we were staying.
local: Ajienaa, ka a ɩ a nɩbɛ ata ban tʊ̃ a n sɛ̃ na yi Sisariya mɩ tãn a yir 'lɔ pɔ ɩn kpɩɛrɛ dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ.

eng: The Spirit told me to go with them even though they were Gentiles. These six brothers also went with me, and we entered that mans house.
local: Ka a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ 'yɛr kʊ ma ka n tu ba chen naa taa baala ɛ. Maa nɩ a yɛɛr ayʊɔb banyãna lɛ 'mataa chen na sɩ tɩ kpɛ a daba yir.

eng: He reported to us how he had seen an angel standing in his house and saying, Send to Joppa and summon Simon, who is known as Peter.
local: A wʊ man kʊ sɩ a lɛ a malaka na wa a wʊ yir wa 'yɛr, Tʊ̃ nɩbɛ a Jopa ka ba tɩ bʊɔl daba na di Simon ban lɛ bʊɔlɔ Pita.

eng: He will tell you how you and your entire household can be saved.
local: 'Lɔ lɛ na waan 'yɛr wa 'yɛr kʊ fʊ ka a fʊ̃ʊ nɩ a fʊ yir dẽme ba jaa tu a 'yɛr 'lɔ pɔ nyɛ faafʊ.

eng: When I began to speak, the Holy Spirit fell on them, just as the Spirit fell on us in the beginning.
local: Ala lɛ ka n piel a 'yɛr naa 'yɛrɛ, ka a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ wa a ba sɛ̃ a lɛ wʊn dɔ̃ɔ wa a sɩ sɛ̃ a pielfʊ.

eng: I remembered the Lords words: John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.
local: Ka n pãa tɩɛrtɔ a lɛ a sɩ Soro na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr, Jɔɔn den kʊ̃ɔ son, tɩchɛ yɩn 'lɔ Nãaŋmɩn na den a wʊ Sɩɛ son yɩ.

eng: If God gave them the same gift he gave us who believed in the Lord Jesus Christ, then who am I? Could I stand in Gods way.
local: Ala ɩ Nãaŋmɩn lɛ kʊ ba, a kʊfʊ bʊ̃'yen 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ wʊn dɔ̃ɔ kʊ sɩ a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ sɩn sɔɔ de a sɩ Soro Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto, maa mɩ nɩ ana, na ŋmaa Nãaŋmɩn sɔr.

eng: Once the apostles and other believers heard this, they calmed down. They praised God and concluded, So then God has enabled Gentiles to change their hearts and lives so that they might have new life.
local: Ban wõ a anyãna, baa lɛ haa nɛɛ ɛ, tɩ pɛɛrɛ Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛrɛ, Nãaŋmɩn pãa yuon sɔr kʊ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, ka ban lɩɛb a ba tɩɛr nyɛ nyɛvʊr.

eng: Now those who were scattered as a result of the trouble that occurred because of Stephen traveled as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch. They proclaimed the word only to Jews.
local: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ ban kʊ a Sɩteven ka a pupuorbo bamɩne yɛ̃yɛl tɩ yi Fonisiya, Sipurusɩ nɩ Antiok, na ba mana a 'yɛr kʊrɔ a Juu nɩbɛ tɛɛ.

eng: Among them were some people from Cyprus and Cyrene. They entered Antioch and began to proclaim the good news about the Lord Jesus also to Gentiles.
local: Ka a pupuorbo bamɩne na yi a Sipurusɩ nɩ a Sirenɩ, chen a Antiok na ba mɩ tɩ piel 'yɛrɛ a sɩ Soro Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyã kʊrɔ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ.

eng: The Lords power was with them, and a large number came to believe and turned to the Lord.
local: A sɩ Soro nũu dɔ̃ɔ pʊɔ ba ka a nɩyɔɔ dɔ̃ɔ sɔɔ de na ba lɩɛb turo a sɩ Soro.

eng: When the church in Jerusalem heard about this, they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
local: Ka a 'yɛr nyã lo a pupuorbo na be a Jerusalɛm toor pɔ, a ba ir Banabasɩ tʊ̃ a be Antiok.

eng: When he arrived and saw evidence of Gods grace, he was overjoyed and encouraged everyone to remain fully committed to the Lord.
local: Wʊn tɩ ta nyɛ a lɛ Nãaŋmɩn na wãɛ ba, nʊ̃ɔ kpɛ wʊ naa ka wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ ba ãkpɛ̃ɔ tɩ 'yɛr, ka ba ara nɩ yelmãɛ nɩ a ba nyãa wʊ jaa kʊ a sɩ Soro.

eng: Barnabas responded in this way because he was a good man, whom the Holy Spirit had endowed with exceptional faith. A considerable number of people were added to the Lord.
local: Banabasɩ mɩ dɔ̃ɔ ɩ nɩsʊ̃ na sɛɛn a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na wʊ tara sɔɔfʊ na wʊ mɩ tɔɔ nɩyɔɔ waan a ba wa turo a sɩ Soro.

eng: Barnabas went to Tarsus in search of Saul.
local: Ala puor lɛ ka a Banabasɩ pãa yɩ a be tu Tasusɩ tɩ bʊɔrɔ a Sɔɔl.

eng: When he found him, he brought him to Antioch. They were there for a whole year, meeting with the church and teaching large numbers of people. It was in Antioch where the disciples were first labeled Christians.
local: Wʊn tɩ nyɛ wʊ, ba lɩɛb wa Antiok na ba be be yuon gbul, ka a Banabasɩ naa Sɔɔl wile a nɩyɔɔ bala. Be a Antiok lɛ a pupuorbo nyɛ a yuor, Kɩrɩsɩto turbo.

eng: About that time, some prophets came down from Jerusalem to Antioch.
local: A sɔ̃ɔ nyãna lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ yi be a Jerusalɛm wa Antiok.

eng: One of them, Agabus, stood up and, inspired by the Spirit, predicted that a severe famine would overtake the entire Roman world.
local: A ba ãsʊɔ yuor na di Agabus ir ara, ka Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ kpɛ wʊ a wʊ 'yɛr, Kɔ̃kpɛ̃ɛ na lon a Worom wɛr wʊ jaa. A kɔ̃ nyãna wa lo naa a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ a Kɩlaudiyus na di a nãa.

eng: The disciples decided they would send support to the brothers and sisters in Judea, with everyone contributing to this ministry according to each persons abundance.
local: A poturbo banyãna mɔ̃ naa na ba ir bʊ̃kɔ̃ɔ lɛ a ba fɔ̃ɔ na jã na ba sʊ̃ʊn a yɛɛr bala na be a Judiya.

eng: They sent Barnabas and Saul to take this gift to the elders.
local: Ban wa ir a kʊfʊ anyãna, Banabasɩ nɩ a Sɔɔl lɛ ba tʊ̃n a ba tɩ kʊ a nɩbɛrɛ.

eng: While Apollos was in Corinth, Paul took a route through the interior and came to Ephesus, where he found some disciples.
local: Lɛ a Apolosɩ na chɛnɛ be a Korɩntɩ, Pɔɔl den a mɔgɔ̃ɔ sɔr na wʊ tɩ ta a Efesus tẽe pɔ. Be lɛ wʊ nyɛ a poturbo bamɩne.

eng: He asked them, Did you receive the Holy Spirit when you came to believe? They replied, Weve not even heard that there is a Holy Spirit.
local: na wʊ sʊʊr ba, A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ yɩn sɔɔ de a Yesu, yɩ nyɛn a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ bɩɩ? Ka ba 'yɛr, Kai, saa gba wõ ɩka Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ ben be ɛ.

eng: Then he said, What baptism did you receive, then? They answered, Johns baptism.
local: Ka a Pɔɔl sʊʊr ba, Tɩ kɔ̃buor lɛ yɩ so? Ka ba 'yɛr, Jɔɔn kʊ̃ɔ lɛ sɩ so.

eng: Paul explained, John baptized with a baptism by which people showed they were changing their hearts and lives. It was a baptism that told people about the one who was coming after him. This is the one in whom they were to believe. This one is Jesus.
local: A Pɔɔl 'yɛr kʊ ba, A bala na lɩɛb a ba tɩɛr lɛ a Jɔɔn so ba Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ, tɩ na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a nɩbɛ ɩka ba sɔɔ de a 'lɔ na waara a wʊ puor, 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a Yesu.

eng: After they listened to Paul, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
local: A nɩbɛ na wa wõ a anyãna ba sɔɔ naa ka wʊ so ba a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ, nɩ a sɩ Soro Yesu yuor.

eng: When Paul placed his hands on them, the Holy Spirit came on them, and they began speaking in other languages and prophesying.
local: Ka a Pɔɔl dɔɔl ba a wʊ nuur ka Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ wa kpɛ ba a ba 'yɛrɛ kɔkɔyor, na ba ma piel 'yɛrɛ lɛ Nãaŋmɩn na wiil ba.

eng: Altogether, there were about twelve people.
local: Ban ɩn dɔɔr pie nɩ ayi.
