eng: Paul went to the synagogue and spoke confidently for the next three months. He interacted with those present and offered convincing arguments concerning the nature of Gods kingdom.
local: Ŋmɛrsɩ ata lɛ wʊ be a be na wʊ ma kpɩɛrɛ a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie na wʊ ma kpɩrkpɩrɛ ba wile ba a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ 'yɛr.

eng: Some people had closed their minds, though. They refused to believe and publicly slandered the Way. As a result, Paul left them, took the disciples with him, and continued his daily interactions in Tyrannus lecture hall.
local: Tɩchɛ a bamɩne kpɛ̃mɛ naa a ba nyãa na ba tɔr baa sɔɔ ɛ, tɩ na ba kpɛrɛ a Sɔr. Ka a Pɔɔl bar ba, tɩ na wʊ ma tara a bala na sɔɔ de, ma kpɛ̃n be a Tiranusɩ jãnfʊ diyal 'lɔ pɔ, a ba ma tɩ mana taa.

eng: This went on for two years, so that everyone living in the province of Asia—both Jews and Greeks—heard the Lords word.
local: Lɛ lɛ wʊ tara tɩ tãn yuom ayi, ka a Juu nɩbɛ nɩ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ, bala jaa na be a Asɩya paal pɔ wõn a sɩ Soro 'yɛr.

eng: God was doing unusual miracles through Paul.
local: Nãaŋmɩn den a Pɔɔl tʊ̃n nɛ'maa tʊ̃yɔɔ.

eng: Even the small towels and aprons that had touched his skin were taken to the sick, and their diseases were cured and the evil spirits left them.
local: A Pɔɔl fɔbile wʊn ma fɩɛlɛ nɩ nyĩe nɩ a wʊ pẽpele ala wa sɩɩr baalaa, a baalsɩ ba jaa ma sa naa, ka a sɩdɛbɛ ma yire a nɩbɛ pɔ.

eng: There were some Jews who traveled around throwing out evil spirits. They tried to use the power of the name of the Lord Jesus against some people with evil spirits. They said, In the name of the Jesus whom Paul preaches, I command you.
local: Ka a Juu nɩbɛ bamɩne mɩ yi yʊɔrɔ na dii a sɩdɛbɛ ka a yi na ba fere de a sɩ Soro Yesu yuor 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ a bala na tara a sɩdɛbɛ, A Yesu 'lɔ a Pɔɔl na mʊɔlɔ, a wʊ yuor pɔ lɛ sɩ 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, yɩ wa yi.

eng: The seven sons of Sceva, a Jewish chief priest, were doing this.
local: A Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na di Sekeva dabil ayopõi lɛ dɔ̃ɔ maala a lɛ nɛ.

eng: The evil spirit replied, I know Jesus and Im familiar with Paul, but who are you.
local: Daar kɔ̃ɔ a sɩdɛɛr sʊʊr ba naa, N bɔ̃ɔn Yesu naa bɔ̃ɔ Pɔɔl, tɩchɛ a yɩn mɩ ba boo yɩ ɩ.

eng: The person who had an evil spirit jumped on them and overpowered them all with such force that they ran out of that house naked and wounded.
local: Ka a daba 'lɔ na tara a sɩ dɛɛr pãa ɔɔ nyɔɔr ba jaa, na wʊ ŋme ba jaa pɩrpɩr tɔɔr a ba bonsɩ chɩɛr ka ba jo yi a die pɔ nɩ sal'mɔi.

eng: This became known to the Jews and Greeks living in Ephesus. Everyone was seized with fear and they held the name of the Lord Jesus in the highest regard.
local: A Juu nɩbɛ nɩ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ, na kpɩɛrɛ a be Efesus tẽe pɔ, ba jaa bɔ̃ɔn naa a yele nyãna, ka dãbãɛ kpɛ ba a baa lɛ tara a sɩ Soro Yesu yuor dãɛnɛ nɩ ɛ.

eng: Many of those who had come to believe came, confessing their past practices.
local: Ka a nɩyɔɔ bala na sɔɔ de, yi chãa pɔ, na ba 'yɛrɛ a ba maal dɛbɛ.

eng: This included a number of people who practiced sorcery. They collected their sorcery texts and burned them publicly. The value of those materials was calculated at more than someone might make if they worked for one hundred sixty-five years.
local: A jɩbʊʊrbɔ bala mɩ na sɔɔ, waan naa a ba jɩbʊʊrgãma wa chʊm a nɩbɛ ba jaa niem, ban wa sɔr a gãma ala ban chʊm libie ka a ɩ libipɩla turo ayi dɔɔl lɩjaayi nɩ pie .

eng: In this way the Lords word grew abundantly and strengthened powerfully.
local: A sɔr nyãna lɛ a sɩ Soro 'yɛr yaar de jie wʊ jaa nɩ kpɛ̃ɔ.

eng: Once these things had come to an end, Paul, guided by the Spirit, decided to return to Jerusalem, taking a route that would carry him through the provinces of Macedonia and Achaia. He said, After I have been there, I must visit Rome as well.
local: A anyãna a jaa na wa ɩ, Pɔɔl ŋmen nyãa ɩka wʊn chen a Jerusalɛm na wʊn tu a Masedonɩya nɩ Akaya, Maa wa ta a be baar, ɩn chen a Worom mɩ.

eng: He sent two of his assistants, Timothy and Erastus, to Macedonia, while he remained awhile in the province of Asia.
local: Wʊ tʊ̃ naa a nɩbɛ ayi a bala na sʊ̃nɔ̃ wʊ Timoti nɩ Erastus ka ba chen a Masedonɩya, tɩchɛ ka a 'lɔ jãnɛ be a Asɩya paal pɔ kor bãlãa.

eng: At that time a great disturbance erupted about the Way.
local: A sɔ̃ɔ nyãna lɛ a damfʊ ir a Sɔr tʊfʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: There was a silversmith named Demetrius. He made silver models of Artemis temple, and his business generated a lot of profit for the craftspeople.
local: Sãa kuro kɔ̃ɔ yuor na di Demetirus, wʊ ma den a kpãna na wʊ kur a Atemisɩ puorfʊ yirbil ka a bala na ɩ a tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ nyɛrɛ libie.

eng: He called a meeting with these craftspeople and others working in related trades and said, Friends, you know that we make an easy living from this business.
local: A Demetirus bʊɔl a wʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ ba jaa lɔ̃ɔ taa nɩ a bala jaa mɩ na bɔ̃ɔ a maalfʊ na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɛɛr, yɩ bɔ̃ɔn naa tɔ̃nɔ̃ sɩn nyɛrɛ a tʊ̃mɔ̃ nyã pɔ.

eng: And you can see and hear that this Paul has convinced and misled a lot of people, not only in Ephesus but also throughout most of the province of Asia. He says that gods made by human hands arent really gods.
local: Yɩn mãɛ nyɛ naa na yɩ wõ a lɛ a Pɔɔl nyãna na 'yɛrɛ na wʊ lɩɛrɛ a nɩyɔɔ tɩɛrsɩ a ka Efesus tẽe pɔ, nɩ a Asɩya wɛr wʊ jaa. Wʊ 'yɛr naa ɩka a ŋmɩmɛ ala ban maal nuur ba ɩ ŋmɩmɛ ɛ.

eng: This poses a danger not only by discrediting our trade but also by completely dishonoring the great goddess Artemis. The whole province of Asia—indeed, the entire civilized world—worships her, but her splendor will soon be extinguished.
local: A ba ɩ a sɩ yara na lo tɛɛ lɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ ba kʊ̃ lɛ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a Atemisɩ puorfʊ yir kpɛ̃ɛ go ɛ. Ka a Asɩya nɩ a wɛr wʊ jaa kʊ̃ lɛ puoro a ŋmɩn pɔɔ nyã go ɛ.

eng: Once they heard this, they were beside themselves with anger and began to shout, Great is Artemis of the Ephesians.
local: Ban wõ lɛ a Demetirus na 'yɛr, ka a suur nyɔɔ ba a ba tãna 'yɛrɛ, A sɩn Efesus ŋmɩn Atemisɩ bɛrma naa.

eng: The city was thrown into turmoil. They rushed as one into the theater. They seized Gaius and Aristarchus, Pauls traveling companions from the province of Macedonia.
local: Ajienaa lɛ a tẽe wʊ jaa pɔɔn gɔ̃mɔ̃, ka a nɩbɛ nyɔɔ a Gayus nɩ Aristakus, Pɔɔl tʊ̃tʊ̃taaba lɛ a ba 'mataa yi a Masedonɩya na ba ŋmʊr ba a lɛ chiin a jie 'lɔ a tẽe nɩbɛ na ma lɔ̃ɔ dãɛnɛ.

eng: Paul wanted to appear before the assembly, but the disciples wouldnt allow him.
local: Ka a Pɔɔl mɩ ta bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ daa yi a be a nɩyɔɔ bala pɔ, tɩchɛ ka a poturbo ba sɔɔ ɛ.

eng: Even some officials of the province of Asia, who were Pauls friends, sent word to him, urging him not to risk going into the theater.
local: A nɩbɛrɛ bamɩne gba na yi a paal 'lɔ pɔ na ɩ a Pɔɔl barmɩne, tʊ̃n nɩbɛ ka ba sʊɔr wʊ ɩka wʊ taa ɩrɛ wa yire be a dãɛn jie ɛ.

eng: Meanwhile, the assembly was in a state of confusion. Some shouted one thing, others shouted something else, and most of the crowd didnt know why they had gathered.
local: Burbur kpɛ̃n a nɩyɔɔ bala na duo a be, ka bamɩne 'yɛrɛ turo nɩ ka sɛn, ka bamɩne mɩ 'yɛrɛ turo nɩ jã yuo, ka bamɩne mɩ gba ba bɔ̃ɔ bo 'yɔ̃ɔ ban be be ɛ.

eng: The Jews sent Alexander to the front, and some of the crowd directed their words toward him. He gestured that he wanted to offer a defense before the assembly.
local: Ka a Juu nɩbɛ daa Aleksanda 'yɔ̃ɔ be a niem, ka a nɩyɔɔ bala tãna mana wʊ lɛ wʊn na 'yɛr. Wʊ wɩlwɩl ban nuur ɩka, ba bar gɔ̃n tɩ wʊ 'yɛr a ba kaa.

eng: but when they realized he was a Jew, they all shouted in unison, Great is Artemis of the Ephesians! This continued for about two hours.
local: Tɩchɛ ban wa bɔ̃ɔn ɩka Juu nɩrɛ lɛ wʊ ɩ, ba jaa ŋmen chɛl bʊ̃'yen lɛ̃ɛɛ tɩ na ba 'yɛrɛ, A sɩn Efesus ŋmɩn Atemisɩ bɛrma naa.

eng: The city manager brought order to the crowd and said, People of Ephesus, doesnt everyone know that the city of Ephesus is guardian of the temple of the great Artemis and of her image, which fell from heaven?.
local: A tẽe gãn sɛɛrɛ vɛ̃n ka a nɩyɔɔ bala ŋmaa vʊʊr tɩ wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Efesus dẽme woɩ, a wɛr wʊ jaa bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka a Efesus tẽe lɛ kaara a Atemisɩ kpɛ̃ɛ nyã puorfʊ yir a wʊ kʊsɩr 'lɔ na yi a saaju lo.

eng: Therefore, since these facts are undeniable, you must calm down. Dont be reckless.
local: A nɩtɔ̃nɔ̃ jũu, nɩrɛ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ chɩɩr ɛ, yɩ ɩ jaa gbul na yɩ ta pɔɔrɔ yelsɩ ɛ.

eng: The men you brought here have neither robbed the temple nor slandered our goddess.
local: A nɩbɛ banyãna yɩn waan a kana ba ju bon jaa a puorfʊ yir ɛ, bɩɩ 'yɛr 'yɛrdɛɛr jaa 'yɔ̃ɔ a sɩ ŋmɩn pɔɔ nyã ɛ.

eng: Therefore, if Demetrius and the craftspeople with him have a charge against anyone, the courts are in session and governors are available. They can press charges against each other there.
local: Ala ɩ a Demetirus nɩ a wʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ wa tara nɩrɛ nɩ 'yɛr, a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ bɩbie ben be nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ ban tʊ̃ɔ chen a be tɩ bʊɔl taa.

eng: Additional disputes can be resolved in a legal assembly.
local: Ala ɩ yele kɔ̃ɔ mɩ ben be a yɩ bʊɔrɔ ɩka yɩ 'yɛr, ban tʊ̃ɔ maal a be a nɩyɔɔ ba jaa niem.

eng: As for us, we are in danger of being charged with rioting today, since we cant justify this unruly gathering.
local: A lɛ an sɩ ben 'yɛr pɔ, a damfʊ nyã na dam a dɩna jũu. A 'yɛr nyãna jũu, saa be jaa na tʊ̃ɔ wiil a par ɛ, bojũu a ba tara par ɛ.

eng: After he said this, he dismissed the assembly.
local: A anyãna wʊn 'yɛr baar tɩ na wʊ wɛl a nɩyɔɔ bala bar.

eng: The same thing happened in Iconium. Paul and Barnabas entered the Jewish synagogue and spoke as they had before. As a result, a huge number of Jews and Greeks believed.
local: Be a Ikoniyum lɛ a Pɔɔl nɩ a Banabasɩ ma chen a Juu nɩbɛ lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie, be lɛ ba man a 'yɛr jaa nɛ nɛ nɛ ka a nɩyɔɔ bamɩne na ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ nɩ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, sɔɔ de a 'yɛr.

eng: However, the Jews who rejected the faith stirred up the Gentiles, poisoning their minds against the brothers.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Juu nɩbɛ bala na ba sɔɔ de ɛ, dam a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, sɔ̃ɔ a ba tɩɛr ka baa bara a yɛɛr ɛ.

eng: Nevertheless, Paul and Barnabas stayed there for quite some time, confidently speaking about the Lord. And the Lord confirmed the word about his grace by the signs and wonders he enabled them to perform.
local: Pɔɔl nɩ a Banabasɩ jɛ̃n a be na ba kor bãlãa, na ba 'yɛrɛ a sɩ Soro 'yɛr ba joro dãbãɛ ɛ, ka a sɩ Soro mãɛ wiil ɩka a wʊ wãɛfʊ 'yɛr ɩn yelmãɛ na wʊ sʊ̃ʊ ba a ba tʊ̃ɔ tʊ̃ yelwiilsɩ nɩ nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃.

eng: When the riot was over, Paul sent for the disciples, encouraged them, said good-bye, and left for the province of Macedonia.
local: Lɛ a gɔ̃mɔ̃ na wa baar, Pɔɔl tʊ̃n bʊɔl a poturbo ka ba wa, a wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ ba ãkpãɛn tɩ bar ba tɩ chen a Masedonɩya.

eng: The people of the city were divided—some siding with the Jews, others with the Lords messengers.
local: Ka a tẽdẽme ŋma jiir ayi, a bamɩne pʊɔ a Juu nɩbɛ, ka a bamɩne mɩ pʊɔ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ.

eng: Some people came down from Judea teaching the family of believers, Unless you are circumcised according to the custom weve received from Moses, you cant be saved.
local: Nɩbɛ bamɩne yin be a Judiya suun wa a Antiok wa wile a yɛɛr 'yɛrɛ, Yɩn baa sɔɔ ka ba ŋmaa yɩ a yʊɔr nɩtãa lɛ a Mosesɩ na bin ɛ, yɩ kʊ̃ nyɛ faafʊ ɛ.

eng: Paul and Silas journeyed through Amphipolis and Apollonia, then came to Thessalonica, where there was a Jewish synagogue.
local: Ba tun a Amfipolis nɩ Apoloniya na ba wa a Tɛsalonika, be lɛ a Juu nɩbɛ lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir be.

eng: As was Pauls custom, he entered the synagogue and for three Sabbaths interacted with them on the basis of the scriptures.
local: Lɛ a Pɔɔl na ma irɛ lɛ wʊ chen be a lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir, Pɛ̃nfʊsɩ gbɛɛ ata jaa lɛ wʊ kpɛ a be ka a 'lɔ nɩ a bala vɩɛrɛ a Nãaŋmɩn gãma.

eng: Through his interpretation of the scriptures, he demonstrated that the Christ had to suffer and rise from the dead. He declared, This Jesus whom I proclaim to you is the Christ.
local: Wʊ mana na wile a lɛ an sɛɛ a Nɩ-iraa na di dɔɔyɛ tɩ na wʊ ir a kũu pɔ ɩ vʊr, A Yesu nyãna ɩn ben 'yɛrɛ a wʊ 'yɛr, lɛ ɩ a Nɩ-iraa.

eng: He traveled through that region with a message of encouragement. When he came to Greece.
local: Be lɛ wʊ tu a tẽn ala pɔ na wʊ 'yɛr 'yɛr yɔɔ 'yɔ̃ɔn a bala na puoro ãkpãɛn na wʊ tɩ tãn a Greek wɛr pɔ.

eng: Paul and Barnabas took sides against these Judeans and argued strongly against their position. The church at Antioch appointed Paul, Barnabas, and several others from Antioch to go up to Jerusalem to set this question before the apostles and the elders.
local: A anyãna vɛ̃ naa ka a Pɔɔl nɩ a Banabasɩ nɩ a nɩbɛ mĩi 'yɔ̃ɔ taa ka a ɩ nɛchɩrɛ. Be lɛ a ba ir Pɔɔl nɩ a Banabasɩ nɩ a pupuorbiir bamɩne 'yɔ̃ɔ ɩka ba chen a Jerusalɛm tɩ nyɛ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a 'yɛr nyãna.

eng: Then some Gentiles and Jews, including their leaders, hatched a plot to mistreat and stone Paul and Barnabas.
local: Nɛchɩrɛ lon a Juu nɩbɛ nɩ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ nɩ a ba nɩbɛrɛ chara pɔ, ka ban maal Pɔɔl nɩ a Banabasɩ dɛɛr lɔb ba nɩ kʊsɩbɛ.

eng: Paul reached Derbe, and then Lystra, where there was a disciple named Timothy. He was the son of a believing Jewish woman and a Greek father.
local: Pɔɔl tɩ chen a Debi na wʊ tɩ po chen a Lɩstɩra, be lɛ wʊ tɩ nyɛ a pupuoro ãsʊɔ ban bʊɔlɔ Timoti ka a wʊ ma ɩ Juu nɩrɛ na wʊ puoro, tɩchɛ ka a wʊ sãa ba ɩ a Juu nɩrɛ ɛ.

eng: The church at Antioch included prophets and teachers: Barnabas, Simeon , Lucius from Cyrene, Manaen , and Saul.
local: A Nãaŋmɩn puorbo bala na be a Antiok, ba tara naa bamɩne ka ba 'yɛrɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr ka a bamɩne mɩ ɩ wiwiilbe, Banabasɩ nɩ Simeon ban bʊɔlɔ Nɩsɔɔlaa, Lusiyusɩ na yi a Sirenɩ tẽe pɔ, nɩ Manayen ban dɔ̃ɔ 'mataa guol a Nãa Hɛrɔd, nɩ a Sɔɔl.

eng: The brothers and sisters in Lystra and Iconium spoke well of him.
local: Ka a yɛɛr na be a Lɩstɩra nɩ a Ikoniyum pʊr a Timoti yovɩla.

eng: he stayed for three months. Because the Jews hatched a plot against Paul as he was about to sail for Syria, he decided instead to return through Macedonia.
local: wʊ jɛ̃n a be ŋmɛrsɩ ata. Bojũu a Juu nɩbɛ dɔ̃ɔ guoro taa a wʊ yele, ala lɛ so waa lɛ kpɛ a gboro chen a Siriya ɛ, tɩ na wʊ lɩɛb tu a Masedonɩya sɔr nɩ gbɛɛ.

eng: The church sent this delegation on their way. They traveled through Phoenicia and Samaria, telling stories about the conversion of the Gentiles to everyone. Their reports thrilled the brothers and sisters.
local: A pupuorbiir na wa bar ba a sɔr, ba chen tɩ yi a Fonisiya nɩ a Samariya na ba man a lɛ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, na lɩɛb puoro a Nãaŋmɩn. Ka a yɛɛr ba jaa pʊɔ pɛl nɩ a 'yɛr nyã.

eng: When they learned of it, these two messengers fled to the Lycaonian cities of Lystra and Derbe and the surrounding area.
local: Tɩchɛ a ba bɔ̃ɔn na ba jo chen a tẽn anyãna na be a Likoniya paal pɔ, Lɩstɩra nɩ a Debi nɩ a tẽn ala na jilʊ̃ a be.

eng: Some were convinced and joined Paul and Silas, including a larger number of Greek God-worshippers and quite a few prominent women.
local: Ka a Juu nɩbɛ bamɩne sɔɔ a lɛ na ba pʊɔ a Pɔɔl nɩ a Silasɩ, 'matãan a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ na dɔ̃ɔ puoro a Nãaŋmɩn lɔ̃ɔn pɔɔ 'yɔ̃ɔnsɩ.

eng: As they were worshipping the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said, Appoint Barnabas and Saul to the work I have called them to undertake.
local: A lɛ ban gal a kɔ̃ na ba sʊɔrɔ a sɩ Soro, ka a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ ir a Banabasɩ nɩ a Sɔɔl kʊ ma ka ba tʊ̃ a tʊ̃mɔ̃ 'lɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ ɩn bʊɔl ba.

eng: Paul wanted to take Timothy with him, so he circumcised him. This was because of the Jews who lived in those areas, for they all knew Timothys father was Greek.
local: A Pɔɔl bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ tara wʊ 'mataa chiine na wʊ ŋmaa wʊ a yʊɔr, bojũu a Juu nɩbɛ na kpɩɛrɛ a be bɔ̃ɔn naa a Timoti sãa na ba ɩ a Juu nɩrɛ ɛ.

eng: When they arrived in Jerusalem, the church, the apostles, and the elders all welcomed them. They gave a full report of what God had accomplished through their activity.
local: Ban wa ta Jerusalɛm, a pupuorbiir nɩ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ de ban nuur ayi, ka ba man a lɛ jaa a Nãaŋmɩn na de ba tʊ̃n.

eng: He was accompanied by Sopater, Pyrrhus son from Beroea, Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica, Gaius from Derbe, Timothy, and Tychicus and Trophimus from the province of Asia.
local: Ka a bala na pʊɔ wʊ a ba 'mataa chen lɛ, Sopater a wʊ sãa lɛ Pirihusɩ na yi Beriya, Aristakus nɩ Sekundusɩ mɩ na yi Tɛsalonika, Gayus na yi Debi nɩ Timoti nɩ Tɩchɩkusɩ nɩ a Torofimusɩ na yi a Asɩya paal pɔ.

eng: where they continued to proclaim the good news.
local: Be lɛ ba chɛnɛ mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir na ba mʊɔlɔ a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ.

eng: As Paul and his companions traveled through the cities, they instructed Gentile believers to keep the regulations put in place by the apostles and elders in Jerusalem.
local: A lɛ ban lʊɔrɔ a tẽn, lɛ mɩ lɛ ba mana kʊrɔ ba lɛ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ nɩ a nɩdierbe na be a Jerusalɛm na 'yɛr bin ka nɩbɛ ba turo.

eng: But the Jews became jealous and brought along some thugs who were hanging out in the marketplace. They formed a mob and started a riot in the city. They attacked Jasons house, intending to bring Paul and Silas before the people.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a nyuur kpɛ a Juu nɩbɛ, ka ba bɔ a ba taaba nɩbomo be a daa pɔ lɔ̃ɔ taa, na ba piel gɔ̃mɔ̃ be a tẽe pɔ. Ba tɩ sɛɛn a Jason yir bʊɔrɔ a Pɔɔl nɩ a Silasɩ ɩka ba yin chiin ba a nɩyɔɔ bala niem.

eng: After they fasted and prayed, they laid their hands on these two and sent them off.
local: A lɛ ban gal a kɔ̃ ba di bon jaa ɛ, ba dɔɔl ban a nuur sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn kʊ ba tɩ bar ba sɔr ka ba chen.

eng: So the churches were strengthened in the faith and every day their numbers flourished.
local: Ba 'yɔ̃ɔn a pupuorbiir ãkpãɛn ka a nɩbɛ mɩ waara pʊɔrɔ bɩbir jaa.

eng: Some believers from among the Pharisees stood up and claimed, The Gentiles must be circumcised. They must be required to keep the Law from Moses.
local: Ka a pupuorbiir bamɩne na chɛnɛ tara a Farasee wiilfʊ ir ara tɩ 'yɛr, A nɩyor banyãna, a sɛɛ naa ka ban ŋma a ba yɔɩ na ba tu a lɛ a Mosesɩ na bin.

eng: The apostles and the elders gathered to consider this matter.
local: A nɩtʊ̃nsɩ nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ lɔ̃ɔn taa na ban guor a 'yɛr nyãna.

eng: After much debate, Peter stood and addressed them, Fellow believers, you know that, early on, God chose me from among you as the one through whom the Gentiles would hear the word of the gospel and come to believe.
local: A lɛ ban mana taa tɩ ta sɔ̃ɔ, Pita ir 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɛɛr, yɩ bɔ̃ɔ naa ɩka sɔ̃ɔ kɔ̃ɔ Nãaŋmɩn ir man a yɩ pɔ, ka a bala na ba sɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, mɩ na wõ a faafʊ 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ 'yɛr yi a n nɛɛ pɔ, na ba sɔɔ de.

eng: In Lystra there was a certain man who lacked strength in his legs. He had been crippled since birth and had never walked. Sitting there, he.
local: A be a Lɩstɩra, dagbɛrɛ kɔ̃ɔ ben be ban lɔ̃ɔ dɔɔn ka waa chen kaa jaa ɛ.

eng: Paul and his companions traveled throughout the regions of Phrygia and Galatia because the Holy Spirit kept them from speaking the word in the province of Asia.
local: Pɔɔl nɩ a wʊ taaba tun a Fɩrgɩya nɩ a Galatiya paal pɔ, ka a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ ŋmaa ba sɔr a ba taa mʊɔlɔ a 'yɛr a be a Asɩya ɛ.

eng: They went on ahead and waited for us in Troas.
local: A nɩbɛ banyãna lɛ de nie tɩ chɛlɛ sɩ be a Toroyas.

eng: When they didnt find them, they dragged Jason and some believers before the city officials. They were shouting, These people who have been disturbing the peace throughout the empire have also come here.
local: Tɩchɛ ban ba wa tɩ nyɛ ba ɛ, ba nyɔɔn a Jason nɩ a yɛɛr bamɩne chiin a tẽe nɩbɛrɛ niem tɩ tãna 'yɛrɛ. A dɔɔr banyãna lɛ dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a wɛr wʊ jaa, ba wa tãn ka pʊ̃pãanyã.

eng: What is more, Jason has welcomed them into his home. Every one of them does what is contrary to Caesars decrees by naming someone else as king: Jesus.
local: ka a Jason tuor de ba 'yɔ̃ɔ a wʊ yir. Ba sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ naa a Nãa Siisa chire na ba 'yɛrɛ ɩka nãa ãsʊɔ ben be ban bʊɔlɔ Yesu.

eng: God, who knows peoples deepest thoughts and desires, confirmed this by giving them the Holy Spirit, just as he did to us.
local: Nãaŋmɩn na bɔ̃ɔ nɩrɛ jaa nyãa lɛ wiil ɩka wʊ sɔɔn de ba na wʊ kʊ ba a wʊ Sɩɛ nɩtãa lɛ wʊn kʊ sɩ.

eng: He made no distinction between us and them, but purified their deepest thoughts and desires through faith.
local: Waa maal a sɩn nɩ a bala ka sɩ tɛɛ taa ɛ, wʊ pɛɛn a ba nyãa ban sɔɔ jũu.

eng: Why then are you now challenging God by placing a burden on the shoulders of these disciples that neither we nor our ancestors could bear?.
local: A lɛ jũu, bãa 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ yɩ mʊɔrɔ ɩka yɩ bɛɛr Nãaŋmɩn kaa, na yɩ tuo a poturbo tuor a sɩ sãakpãmɩne na ba dɔ̃ɔ tʊ̃ɔ tuo ɛ?.

eng: We sailed from Philippi after the Festival of Unleavened Bread and met them five days later in Troas, where we stayed for a week.
local: Tɩchɛ a Paanʊ̃kõo Lɔ̃ɔfʊ di na wa baar, sɩ kpɛ̃n a gboro yi be a Filipai tẽe pɔ na sɩ maal ber anũu tɩ na sɩ pɔɔ ba a be Toroyas jãnɛ a be ber ayopõi.

eng: On the first day of the week, as we gathered together for a meal, Paul was holding a discussion with them. Since he was leaving the next day, he continued talking until midnight.
local: A Pɛ̃nfʊ divɩɛ bio daar jãanʊɔr, sɩ jaa lɔ̃ɔn taa na sɩn 'mataa di. Pɔɔl 'yɛrɛ naa a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr kʊrɔ a nɩbɛ bojũu wɛr wa chaar, wʊn chen naa. Wʊ 'yɛr kor jaa ka wɛr tɩ põ.

eng: There were many lamps in the upstairs room where we had gathered.
local: A be a bɔɔpɩɛ pɔ sɩn lɔ̃ɔ ba chʊm fãtɩnsɩ yɔɔ bin.

eng: heard Paul speaking. Paul stared at him and saw that he believed he could be healed.
local: Wʊ chɛlɛ naa lɛ a Pɔɔl na 'yɛrɛ ka a Pɔɔl kaa wʊ jaa tãɩɩ nyɛ na wʊ bɔ̃ɔn wʊn tara sɔɔfʊ na sa.

eng: On the contrary, we believe that we and they are saved in the same way, by the grace of the Lord Jesus.
local: Kai, sɩn sɔɔ naa ɩka a tu naa a sɩ Soro Yesu wãɛfʊ jũu lɛ sɩ nyɛ faafʊ nɩtãa a bala kaar.

eng: When they approached the province of Mysia, they tried to enter the province of Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus wouldnt let them.
local: Ban wa gbʊr a Misiya wɛr pɔ, ba ta mʊɔrɔ naa ɩka ba chen a Bitiniya tɩchɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ ba kʊ ba sɔr ɛ.

eng: After the Holy Spirit sent them on their way, they went down to Seleucia. From there they sailed to Cyprus.
local: A nɩbɛ ayi banyãna den a sɔr ka a Nãaŋmɩn de ba nie suun be a Selusiya na ba tɩ kpɛ a gboro chiine a Sipurusɩ.

eng: This provoked the crowd and the city officials even more.
local: A lɛ a tẽe nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a nɩyɔɔ bala na wõ a 'yɛr nyãna, a dam ba naa.

eng: After Jason and the others posted bail, they released them.
local: A ba pãa 'yɔ̃ɔ a Jason nɩ a yɛɛr bala a ba yab libie tɩ ba bar ba sɔr.

eng: As soon as it was dark, the brothers and sisters sent Paul and Silas on to Beroea. When they arrived, they went to the Jewish synagogue.
local: A lɛ a wɛr na wa tɔ̃ɔ sɔb, ka a yɛɛr bar a Pɔɔl nɩ Silasɩ sɔr ka ba chen be a Beriya. Ban tɩ ta, ba chen be a Juu nɩbɛ lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir.

eng: A young man named Eutychus was sitting in the window. He was sinking into a deep sleep as Paul talked on and on. When he was sound asleep, he fell from the third floor and died.
local: Pɔlbile kɔ̃ɔ yuor na di Yutikus jãnɛ naa a tokoro nɛɛ ka a gʊ̃ɔ kpɛ wʊ. Lɛ a Pɔɔl na 'yɛr kor a wʊ gur jaa chãɩɩ na wʊ yi a be a saaju faa lo a be tẽe, bojũu ba mɛn a bɔɔpɩɛ dɔɔl taa ata ka ba suu 'mɔɔ ka ɩ wʊ kpi naa.

eng: Passing by Mysia, they went down to Troas instead.
local: A ba vɩɛ a Misiya bar tɩ suu chen be a Toroyas.

eng: A vision of a man from Macedonia came to Paul during the night. He stood urging Paul, Come over to Macedonia and help us.
local: A tãsɔɔ lɛ a Pɔɔl nyɛ jãna pɔ, daba na yi Masedonɩya ara sʊɔrɔ wʊ 'yɛrɛ, Gɔ̃ɔ wa ka a Masedonɩya wa sʊ̃ʊ sɩ.

eng: Raising his voice, Paul said, Stand up straight on your feet! He jumped up and began to walk.
local: A wʊ bʊɔl a daba 'yɛr, Ir ara naa fʊ gbɛɛ. Ka a daba vaa ir na wʊ piel chiine.

eng: The entire assembly fell quiet as they listened to Barnabas and Paul describe all the signs and wonders God did among the Gentiles through their activity.
local: Ka a nɩyɔɔ bala na duo ɩ jaa gbulo, tɩ chɛlɛ a lɛ a Pɔɔl nɩ a Banabasɩ na mana a yelfɛrsɩ nɩ a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ a Nãaŋmɩn na de ba tʊ̃n a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ bala pɔ.

eng: The Beroean Jews were more honorable than those in Thessalonica. This was evident in the great eagerness with which they accepted the word and examined the scriptures each day to see whether Paul and Silas teaching was true.
local: A Juu nɩbɛ bala na be a Beriya ɩn chɛchɛlbɛ gar a bala na be a Tɛsalonika, bojũu ba den a 'yɛr nɩ chɩlfʊ na ba vɩɛrɛ a Nãaŋmɩn gãma kaara bɩbir jaa ɩka ba kaa lɛ a Pɔɔl na 'yɛrɛ ɩn yelmãɛ bɩɩ.

eng: Paul went down, fell on him and embraced him, then said, Dont be alarmed. Hes alive.
local: A Pɔɔl suun tɩ lo bib a kũu tɩ kõr wʊ tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a nɩbɛ, Yɩ ta kõnõ ɛ, wʊ chɛnɛ na vʊrɔ.

eng: In Salamis they proclaimed Gods word in the Jewish synagogues. John was with them as their assistant.
local: Lɛ ban tɩ ta a Salamis, ba mʊɔl a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr a be a Juu nɩbɛ lɔ̃ɔfʊ jiir. Ka a Jɔɔn Maakɩ be a ba sɛ̃ na wʊ sʊ̃nɔ̃ ba.

eng: Many came to believe, including a number of reputable Greek women and many Greek men.
local: A Juu nɩbɛ yɔɔ jie sɔɔn de a 'yɛr lɔ̃ɔn a bala na ba ɩ Juu nɩbɛ dɔɔr yɔɔ jie, nɩ a pɔɔ 'yɔ̃ɔnsɩ bamɩne yɔɔ jie.

eng: Seeing what Paul had done, the crowd shouted in the Lycaonian language, The gods have taken human form and come down to visit us.
local: Lɛ a nɩbɛ na wa nyɛ a lɛ a Pɔɔl na ɩ, ka ba ŋme chɛlma nɩ a Likoniya kɔkɔr pɔ, A ŋmɩmɛ lɩɛb nɩbɛ suu wa a sɩ sɛ̃.

eng: When Barnabas and Paul also fell silent, James responded, Fellow believers, listen to me.
local: Ban wa 'yɛr baar ka a Jemesɩ mɩ 'yɛr, Yɛɛr, yɩ chɛlɛ ka.

eng: Simon reported how, in his kindness, God came to the Gentiles in the first place, to raise up from them a people of God.
local: Simon man kʊ sɩ a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na dɔ̃ɔ tɩɛrtɔ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ ɩka wʊn ir nɩbɛ a ba pɔ, kʊ a wʊ mãɛ.

eng: The prophets words agree with this; as it is written.
local: Ka a lɛ na dɔ̃ɔ sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ nɩ a 'yɛr nyã ɩ yelmãɛ.

eng: They traveled throughout the island until they arrived at Paphos. There they found a certain man named Bar-Jesus, a Jew who was a false prophet and practiced sorcery.
local: Ba yɔn a man dõdol wʊ jaa tɩ ta Pafos, be lɛ ba tuor a Juu nɩrɛ jɩbʊrɔ ãsʊɔ na ma ŋmara jirsɩ ka a wʊ yuor di Bar-Yesu.

eng: He kept company with the governor of that province, an intelligent man named Sergius Paulus. The governor sent for Barnabas and Saul since he wanted to hear Gods word.
local: Wʊ dɔ̃ɔ be a Segiyusɩ Pɔɔlusɩ na kaara a dõdol na ɩ yɛ̃ sʊɔ, a tẽkaara nyãna lɛ bʊɔl a Banabasɩ nɩ a Sɔɔl bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ wõ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr.

eng: But Elymas the sorcerer opposed them, trying to steer the governor away from the faith.
local: Tɩchɛ a Elimasɩ na ɩ a jɩbʊrɔ a wʊ yuor par lɛ a lɛ nɛ, ŋmaan a Banabasɩ nɩ a Sɔɔl sɔr ba bʊɔrɔ ɩka a tẽkaara nyãna wʊ sɔɔ a Yesu ɛ.

eng: Immediately after he saw the vision, we prepared to leave for the province of Macedonia, concluding that God had called us to proclaim the good news to them.
local: Pɔɔl na nyɛ a nyɛfʊ nyãna, sɩ ir chɔɔr a lɛ nɛ na sɩ chen a Masedonɩya, na sɩ bɔ̃ɔn ɩka Nãaŋmɩn lɛ bʊɔl sɩ a sɩ tɩ mʊɔl a wʊ 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ kʊ ba.

eng: Then Paul went back upstairs and ate. He talked for a long time—right up until daybreak—then he left.
local: Wʊ pãa don be a bɔɔpɩɛ pɔ, ka wʊ tɩ ŋma a paanʊ̃ɔ ka ba 'mataa di tɩ wʊ lɛ 'yɛr lɛ̃ɛɛ ka wɛr tɩ chaar ka wʊ yi a be chen.

eng: After this I will return, and I will rebuild Davids fallen tent; I will rebuild what has been torn down. I will restore it .
local: A anyã puor, ɩn lɩɛb tɩ mɛ a Nãa David yir 'lɔ na lo. Ɩn mɛn a 'lɔ na ŋmɛr, naa lɛ maal wʊ.

eng: We sailed from Troas straight for Samothrace and came to Neapolis the following day.
local: A Toroyas sɩn yi tɔ̃ɔ kpɛ a gboro a dẽdẽe a lɛ nɛ chen a Samotɩrasɩ, ka a wɛr na wa chaar, sɩ tãn a Neapolis.

eng: The Jews from Thessalonica learned that Paul also proclaimed Gods word in Beroea, so they went there too and were upsetting and disturbing the crowds.
local: A Juu nɩbɛ na be a Tɛsalonika na wa bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a Pɔɔl ben a Beriya mʊɔlɔ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr, ba chen a be tɩ chãgbala a nɩbɛ dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ nɩ ba.

eng: The brothers and sisters sent Paul away to the seacoast at once, but Silas and Timothy remained at Beroea.
local: Ka a yɛɛr mɩ tɔ̃ɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ a Pɔɔl a man sɔr a wʊ chen, tɩchɛ ka a Silasɩ nɩ a Timoti 'lɔ jɛ̃ be a Beriya.

eng: Those who escorted Paul led him as far as Athens, then returned with instructions for Silas and Timothy to come to him as quickly as possible.
local: A nɩbɛ bala na bɩɛl a Pɔɔl ba tɩ tãn wʊn a Atensɩ, bala lɛ wʊ lɩɛb tʊ̃ ɩka a Silasɩ nɩ a Timoti a ba wa fɔɔ a wʊ sɛ̃.

eng: While Paul waited for them in Athens, he was deeply distressed to find that the city was flooded with idols.
local: A lɛ wʊn chɛnɛ chɛlɛ ba be a Atensɩ, suur wa tɩ kpɛ wʊ naa a lɛ wʊn nyɛ a tẽe na sɛɛn a tɩbɛ.

eng: so that the rest of humanity will seek the Lord, even all the Gentiles who belong to me. The Lord says this, the one who does these things .
local: Ka a nɩbɛ bala na chɛ pãa na yɔ bɔ a Soro nɩ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ na bʊɔlɔ a n yuor, lɛ lɛ a sɩ Soro 'yɛr na maal a yele anyãna.

eng: He began to interact with the Jews and Gentile God-worshippers in the synagogue. He also addressed whoever happened to be in the marketplace each day.
local: Lɛ lɛ 'lɔ nɩ a Juu nɩbɛ nɩ a Greek bala na puoro a Nãaŋmɩn ma mana taa be a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir, nɩ a bala wʊn ma pɔɔ a daa pɔ bɩbir jaa nɩ nɩrɛ jaa wʊn tuor.

eng: Empowered by the Holy Spirit, Saul, also known as Paul, glared at Bar-Jesus and.
local: Ka a Sɔɔl nyãna ban bʊɔlɔ a Pɔɔl na sɛɛn a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ kaara a Elimasɩ jaa tãɩɩ tɩ wa 'yɛr kʊ wʊ.

eng: known from earliest times.
local: ka ba bɔ̃ɔn a kõr jaa jaa.

eng: They took the young man away alive, and they were greatly comforted.
local: Tɩchɛ ka pɔlbile ir kpɛ̃mɛ ka ba ãgãn 'maa a ba tara wʊ kul a yir.

eng: We went on to the ship and sailed for Assos, where we intended to take Paul on board. Paul had arranged this, since he intended to make his way there by land.
local: Sɩn den nie kpɛ a gboro na sɩ chiine a Asosɩ, be lɛ sɩn tɩ 'mɔɔ a Pɔɔl, lɛ lɛ wʊ dɔ̃ɔ man sɩ tɩ chiine nɩ gbɛɛ a be.

eng: When he met us at Assos, we took him aboard and went on to Mitylene.
local: Lɛ wʊn tɩ tuor sɩ a be Asosɩ, sɩ 'mɔɔ wʊn 'yɔ̃ɔ a gboro pɔ na sɩ chiine be a Mitilene.

eng: said, You are a deceiver and trickster! You devil! You attack anything that is right! Will you never stop twisting the straight ways of the Lord into crooked paths?.
local: Fʊ ɩn Satan bie na fʊ ɩ bʊ̃sʊ̃n a jaa dɔ̃dɔ̃mɔ̃. A fʊ pʊɔ sɛɛn naa bɛlfʊ nɩ bɛlfʊ 'yɛr bie, fʊ kʊ̃ bar a jir fʊn bʊɔrɔ ɩka fʊ sɔ̃ɔn a sɩ Soro sɔr dẽdẽe bar ɛ?.

eng: Certain Epicurean and Stoic philosophers engaged him in discussion too. Some said, What an amateur! Whats he trying to say? Others remarked, He seems to be a proclaimer of foreign gods.
local: Ka a bɔ̃bɔ̃ɔnbɔ bamɩne na yi a Epikuriya nɩ a Sɩtoyik gbul pɔ piel chɩrɛ nɩ a Pɔɔl. Ka a bamɩne sʊrɔ, Bo lɛ a dabʊlɔbʊlɔ nyãna ara 'yɛrɛ? Ka a bamɩne mɩ 'yɛrɛ, Ŋmɩn sãansɩ kɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr lɛ wʊ 'yɛrɛ. Ba 'yɛrɛ naa nɩtɔ̃ bojũu, Pɔɔl mʊɔlɔ naa a Yesu 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nɩ a kũu pɔ irfʊ 'yɛr.

eng: Listen! The Lords power is set against you. You will be blind for a while, unable even to see the daylight. At once, Bar-Jesus eyes were darkened, and he began to grope about for someone to lead him around by the hand.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã a sɩ Soro na ŋme fʊ naa ka fʊ jɔ̃ɔ tɩ tãn sɔ̃ɔ kɔ̃ɔ, fʊ kʊ̃ lɛ tʊ̃ɔ nyɛ mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ chãa ɛ. Ajienaa lɛ a Elimasɩ nɩbie de, ka a libe pɔɔ wʊ, ka wʊ bɔɔlɔ bʊɔrɔ nɩrɛ na nyɔɔ wʊ nũu.

eng: They referred to Barnabas as Zeus and to Paul as Hermes, since Paul was the main speaker.
local: Ba bʊɔlɔ naa a Banabasɩ Jeyusɩ, na ba bʊɔlɔ a Pɔɔl mɩ Hɛmɩsɩ, bojũu 'lɔ lɛ dɔ̃ɔ ɩ a 'yɛ'yɛrɛ.

eng: Therefore, I conclude that we shouldnt create problems for Gentiles who turn to God.
local: Maa 'yɛr nɩ nyã ɩka sɩ ta vɛ̃ ka a ɩ tuo kʊ a nɩyor banyãna na lɩɛrɛ waara a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ ɛ.

eng: From there we went to Philippi, a city of Macedonias first district and a Roman colony. We stayed in that city several days.
local: Be lɛ sɩ yin gbɛɛ chiin a Filipai, Worom dẽme lɛ dɔ̃ɔ kpɩɛrɛ a be na wʊ mɩ ɩ a Masedonɩya jikpɛ̃ɛ, be lɛ sɩ jãnɛ maal ber aŋmɩnɛ jaa.

eng: On the Sabbath we went outside the city gate to the riverbank, where we thought there might be a place for prayer. We sat down and began to talk with the women who had gathered.
local: A Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar, sɩ yin a tẽe pɔ suu a põn pɔ, be lɛ sɩ tɩɛr ɩka sɩn nyɛn jie ban ma sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn. Sɩ jãnɛ naa be na sɩ piel 'yɛrɛ 'yɛr kʊrɔ pɔbɔ na lɔ̃ɔ a be.

eng: They took him into custody and brought him to the council on Mars Hill. What is this new teaching? Can we learn what you are talking about?.
local: Be lɛ ba pãa bʊɔl wʊ chiin be a lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie a Areyopagusɩ na ba 'yɛr, Fʊn tʊ̃ɔ wiil sɩ a fʊ wiil paalaa nyãna fʊn wile a sɩ bɔ̃ɔ bɩɩ?.

eng: Youve told us some strange things and we want to know what they mean. .
local: 'Yɛr sãan lɛ a nyã fʊn waan a sɩ wõnõ na sɩ bʊɔrɔ ɩka sɩ bɔ̃ɔ a par.

eng: They said this because all Athenians as well as the foreigners who live in Athens used to spend their time doing nothing but talking about or listening to the newest thing.
local: Atensɩ dẽme ba jaa lɔ̃ɔn a sãanba na kpɩɛrɛ a be, baa tara tʊ̃mɔ̃ lɛ pʊɔ a 'yɛr paalaa ban ma 'yɛrɛ na ba chɛlɛ nɩ yelpaalaa na ɩrɛ.

eng: Paul stood up in the middle of the council on Mars Hill and said, People of Athens, I see that you are very religious in every way.
local: Lɛ lɛ a Pɔɔl pãa ir a nɩbɛ na lɔ̃ɔ a Areyopagusɩ na wʊ 'yɛr, Atensɩ dɔɔr, n nyɛn bɔ̃ɔn yɩn nʊ̃ ŋmɩmɛ puorfʊ.

eng: As I was walking through town and carefully observing your objects of worship, I even found an altar with this inscription: To an unknown God. What you worship as unknown, I now proclaim to you.
local: Bojũu ɩn yʊɔrɔ kaara a yɩ tɩbɛ jiir, n tɩ nyɛn a bɔɔr lofʊ jie ãsʊɔ yɩn sɛb 'yɔ̃ɔ, ŊMƖN 'LƆ SƖN BA BƆ̃Ɔ Ɛ. A Nãaŋmɩn 'lɔ gba yɩn mãɛ na puoro tɩ ba bɔ̃ɔ wʊ ɛ, 'lɔ lɛ n waan ɩka n wa mʊɔl kʊ yɩ.

eng: When the governor saw what had taken place, he came to believe, for he was astonished by the teaching about the Lord.
local: A lɛ a tẽkaara na nyɛ a lɛ na ɩ, wʊ sɔɔn de, bojũu nɛɛ 'maa wʊ naa a lɛ ban wile a sɩ Soro yele.

eng: One of those women was Lydia, a Gentile God-worshipper from the city of Thyatira, a dealer in purple cloth. As she listened, the Lord enabled her to embrace Pauls message.
local: A pɔbɔ banyãna na chɛlɛ ãsʊɔ yuor din Lidiya na yara fɔjɩɩr, na wʊ yi a Tayatira tẽe pɔ, wʊ mɩ puoro naa Nãaŋmɩn. A sɩ Soro yuon a wʊ tɩɛr ka wʊ sɔɔ de a lɛ a Pɔɔl na 'yɛr.

eng: Once she and her household were baptized, she urged, Now that you have decided that I am a believer in the Lord, come and stay in my house. And she persuaded us.
local: 'Lɔ nɩ a wʊ yir dẽme ba jaa na wa so a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ, wʊ bʊɔl sɩ naa a sɩ wa a wʊ yir a wʊ wa 'yɛr, Ala ɩ yɩ sɔɔ naa ɩka n sɔɔn de a sɩ Soro, sɩrɛ jaa yɩ wa jɛ̃ a n yir. Lɛ lɛ wʊ mʊlɔ sɩ.

eng: One day, when we were on the way to the place for prayer, we met a slave woman. She had a spirit that enabled her to predict the future. She made a lot of money for her owners through fortune-telling.
local: Daar kɔ̃ɔ sɩn lɛ chiine a jie 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ a Nãaŋmɩn sʊɔrfʊ jie, sɩ tuor gbãgbapɔɔbile na tara a sɩdɛɛr na wʊ ma 'yɛrɛ yele na ɩ daar kɔ̃ɔ. A lɛ wʊn 'yɛrɛ na wʊ nyɛrɛ libiyɔɔ kʊrɔ a bala na so wʊ.

eng: The next day we sailed from there and arrived opposite Chios. On the day after, we sailed to Samos, and on the following day we came to Miletus.
local: Wɛr na wa chaar, sɩ yin a be tɩ ta Kiyosɩ ka a wɛr lɛ bɔ̃ɔ chaar sɩ go kaa ɩ sɩ vɩɛn Samosɩ bar. A ber ata daar lɛ sɩ pãa ta a Miletusɩ.

eng: The priest of Zeus, whose temple was located just outside the city, brought bulls and wreaths to the city gates. Along with the crowds, he wanted to offer sacrifices to them.
local: Ka a Jeyusɩ nyã puorfʊ yir na dɔ̃ɔ ara a tẽe kõkoor bɔɔrloro waan nɩ nadai nɩ a vapuulsɩ bojũu 'lɔ nɩ a nɩyɔɔ bala bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka ba lo bɔɔr kʊ ba.

eng: When the Lords messengers Barnabas and Paul found out about this, they tore their clothes in protest and rushed out into the crowd. They shouted.
local: Tɩchɛ lɛ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ, Banabasɩ nɩ a Pɔɔl na wa wõ a lɛ, ba tɔɔn a ba fuusɩ chɩɛr tɩ jo kpɛ be a nɩyɔɔ bala pɔ tɩ tãna.

eng: People, what are you doing? We are humans too, just like you! We are proclaiming the good news to you: turn to the living God and away from such worthless things. He made the heaven, the earth, the sea, and everything in them. .
local: Nɩbɛ, bãa lɛ yɩ maala a lɛ? Sɩn mɩ ɩn nɩbɛ, nɩsaalba a yɩ kaar. Sɩn waan naa a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyã wa kʊ yɩ, na sɩ 'yɛrɛ yɩ ɩka, yɩ lɩɛb yi a yelpɔrsɩ anyãna pɔ, tɩ turo a Nãaŋmɩn vʊr. 'Lɔ lɛ maal a saaju, nɩ a tẽe, nɩ a man kpɛ̃ɛ, nɩ bon jaa na be be.

eng: Instead, we should write a letter, telling them to avoid the pollution associated with idols, sexual immorality, eating meat from strangled animals, and consuming blood.
local: Tɩchɛ yɩ ɩ a sɩ sɛb kʊ ba ɩka yaa, ba taa dire tɩɩ bon ɛ, na ba taa sɛ̃nɛ ɛ, dʊ̃ɔ na var kpi ba taa ɔɔrɔ ɛ, na ba taa ɔɔrɔ jãɩ ɛ.

eng: After all, Moses has been proclaimed in every city for a long time, and is read aloud every Sabbath in every synagogue.
local: Bojũu, lɛ lɛ a Mosesɩ bin a nɛɛ ka a Juu nɩbɛ ma mʊɔlɔ a 'yɛr nyãna a tẽn a jaa ka ba mɩ ma kar naa a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ jiir a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar jaa.

eng: The apostles and the elders, along with the entire church, agreed to send some delegates chosen from among themselves to Antioch, together with Paul and Barnabas. They selected Judas Barsabbas and Silas, who were leaders among the brothers and sisters.
local: Ka a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ lɔ̃ɔn a pupuorbiir ba jaa pãa kaa ir nɩbɛ a ba pɔ, ka ban 'matãan a Pɔɔl nɩ a Banabasɩ tʊ̃ ka ba chen a Antiok. Judas ban bʊɔlɔ Basabasɩ nɩ Silasɩ lɛ ba ir, a nɩbɛ ayi banyãna mɩ dɔ̃ɔ ɩ nɩbɛrɛ pʊɔ a yɛɛr.

eng: Paul and his companions sailed from Paphos to Perga in Pamphylia. John deserted them there and returned to Jerusalem.
local: Pɔɔl nɩ a wʊ taaba kpɛ̃n a gboro yi a Pafos chen be a Perga na be a Pamfiliya be lɛ a Jɔɔn Maakɩ tɩ bar ba tɩ lɩɛb chen a Jerusalɛm.

eng: She began following Paul and us, shouting, These people are servants of the Most High God! They are proclaiming a way of salvation to you.
local: Ka a pɔɔbile nyãna turo a Pɔɔl na wʊ turo sɩ tãna 'yɛrɛ, A nɩbɛ banyãna ɩ naa a Nãaŋmɩn nyã na be saaju tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ na ba wile yɩ a faafʊ sɔr.

eng: God, who made the world and everything in it, is Lord of heaven and earth. He doesnt live in temples made with human hands.
local: A Nãaŋmɩn 'lɔ na maal a wɛr nɩ a bomo a jaa na be a pɔ, 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a saaju nɩ a tẽe jaa Soro, na waa gãna a yie ala a nɩbɛ na mɛn a ba nuur kʊ wʊ ɛ.

eng: Nor is God served by human hands, as though he needed something, since he is the one who gives life, breath, and everything else.
local: Waa dɩɛl nɩrɛ nũu tʊ̃mɔ̃ ala ɩ wʊ bʊɔrɔ na bon kɔ̃ɔ ɛ, bojũu 'lɔ mãɛ lɛ kʊ nɩrɛ nyɛvʊr, ka wʊ vʊrɔ nɩ bon jaa.

eng: From one person God created every human nation to live on the whole earth, having determined their appointed times and the boundaries of their lands.
local: Dabʊ̃'yen pɔ lɛ wʊ tu na wʊ maal a nɩbʊrsɩ, ka ba kpɩɛrɛ a wɛr wʊ jaa. A wʊ man a sɔ̃ɔsɩ kʊ ba, nɩ a jiir ala ban na kpɩɛrɛ.

eng: In the past, he permitted every nation to go its own way.
local: A kõr jaa wʊ dɔ̃ɔ bar ka nɩrɛ jaa turo a wʊ sɔr.

eng: They went on from Perga and arrived at Antioch in Pisidia. On the Sabbath, they entered and found seats in the synagogue there.
local: Ka ba yi a Perga chen Antiok na gbʊr a Pisidiya, a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir daar ba kpɛ̃n a lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie na ba tɩ jãnɛ.

eng: Paul had decided to sail past Ephesus so that he wouldnt need to spend too much time in the province of Asia. He was hurrying to reach Jerusalem, if possible, by Pentecost Day.
local: Pɔɔl bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka wʊ po a Efesus tẽe bar na wʊ ta gã be a Asɩya paal pɔ ɛ, bojũu wʊ bʊɔrɔ na fɔɔ ɩka 'lɔ tʊ̃ɔ, wʊ ta a Jerusalɛm a Pentikosɩ bɩbir daar.

eng: From Miletus he sent a message to Ephesus calling for the churchs elders to meet him.
local: Pɔɔl na chɛnɛ be a Miletusɩ lɛ wʊ tʊ̃ bʊɔl a bala na puoro a be a Efesus, a ba nɩdierbe ka ba wa.

eng: After the reading of the Law and the Prophets, the synagogue leaders invited them, Brothers, if one of you has a sermon for the people, please speak.
local: Ban wa kar a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ nɩ a ala a Nãaŋmɩn 'Yɛ'yɛrbɛ na sɛb baar, a lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie nɩbɛrɛ sʊʊr naa Pɔɔl nɩ a wʊ taaba, Yɛɛr, yɩn tara 'yɛr na sʊ̃ʊn a nɩbɛ sɩ kʊ yɩn sɔr, yɩ 'yɛr.

eng: They were to carry this letter: The apostles and the elders, to the Gentile brothers and sisters in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia. Greetings!.
local: Bala lɛ ba tʊ̃n a gãn nyãna, A sɩn nɩtʊ̃nsɩ nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ na ɩ a yɩ yɛɛr puor naa a yɩn na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, na ɩ a sɩ yɛɛr be a Antiok nɩ a Siriya nɩ a Sɩlɩsɩya paal pɔ.

eng: When they arrived, he said to them, You know how I lived among you the whole time I was with you, beginning with the first day I arrived in the province of Asia.
local: Ban wa ta, wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ bɔ̃ɔ lɛ ɩn be dɔ̃ɔ kpɩɛrɛ a yɩ sɛ̃, yi a daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ jaa ɩn wa a Asɩya paal pɔ.

eng: She did this for many days. This annoyed Paul so much that he finally turned and said to the spirit, In the name of Jesus Christ, I command you to leave her! It left her at that very moment.
local: Lɛ lɛ a pɔɔbile mar bɩbir jaa ka a Pɔɔl wa vʊʊr faa tɩ lɩɛb 'yɛr kʊ a sɩɛ 'lɔ na be a pɔɔbile pɔ ɩka, Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto yuor pɔ, n 'yɛr kʊ fʊ ɩka fʊ wa yi a pɔɔbile pɔ. Ajienaa ka a sɩɛ yi tɩchɛ wʊ bar.

eng: Nevertheless, he hasnt left himself without a witness. He has blessed you by giving you rain from above as well as seasonal harvests, and satisfying you with food and happiness.
local: Tɩchɛ wʊ ma wiil a lɛ wʊn ɩ maal vɩla sʊɔ, na wʊ ma wiil a wʊ maal vɩla vɛ̃ ka a saa yi saaju waara, ka a bʊ̃bʊɔrsɩ sɔ̃ɔ mɩ be be. Wʊ ma kʊ yɩn a bʊ̃diir yɔɔ ka yɩ pʊɔ pɛl.

eng: God made the nations so they would seek him, perhaps even reach out to him and find him. In fact, God isnt far away from any of us.
local: Nãaŋmɩn maal naa nɩtɔ̃ ka nɩrɛ na tʊ̃ɔ yɔbɔ wʊ, amɩne sɛ̃ ban nyɛ wʊ naa, bojũu waa jãan a sɩ ãsʊɔ jaa ɛ.

eng: Even with these words, they barely kept the crowds from sacrificing to them.
local: A 'yɛr nyãna gba ban 'yɛr, tɩchɛ a chɛnɛ na ɩ tuo ka ban ŋmaa a nɩbɛ sɔr ka ban bar a bɔɔr lofʊ.

eng: Jews from Antioch and Iconium arrived and won the crowds over. They stoned Paul and dragged him out of the city, supposing he was dead.
local: Ka a Juu nɩbɛ bamɩne na pãa yi a Antiok nɩ a Ikoniyum wa tɔ̃n a nɩyɔɔ bala 'yɔ̃ɔ ka ba lɔb a Pɔɔl nɩ kʊsɩbɛ vu yin bar a tẽe kõkoor tɩɛr man tãa wʊ kpi naa.

eng: When the disciples surrounded him, he got up and entered the city again. The following day he left with Barnabas for Derbe.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Nãaŋmɩn puorbo tɩ ŋmaa jilʊ̃ wʊ ara nɩ ka wʊ ir kpɛ a tẽe pɔ. A wɛr na wa chaar ka 'lɔ naa Banabasɩ chen a Debi.

eng: In God we live, move, and exist. As some of your own poets said, We are his offspring.
local: Bojũu, a wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ sɩ chɛnɛ na sɩ be ka a tẽe pɔ, na sɩ yʊɔrɔ nɩtãa lɛ a yɩn mãɛ nɩbɛ bamɩne na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr, A wʊ bibiir lɛ sɩ ɩ.

eng: Therefore, as Gods offspring, we have no need to imagine that the divine being is like a gold, silver, or stone image made by human skill and thought.
local: Sɩn ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn bibiir jũu, yɩ taa ɩ a sɩ tɩɛr ɩka a Nãaŋmɩn nyã kaara naa fɩka bon nɩrɛ na de wʊ yɛ̃ bɩɩ bɔ̃ɔfʊ mɛn, sɛlma, bɩɩ libipɩla, bɩɩ kʊsɩr kaar ɛ.

eng: I served the Lord with great humility and with tears in the midst of trials that came upon me because of the Jews schemes.
local: Mãɛ siirfʊ nɩ nɩbikʊ̃ɔ lɛ n tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ a sɩ Soro tʊ̃mɔ̃, bojũu a Juu nɩbɛ ba maal ma vɩla ɛ.

eng: Paul and Barnabas proclaimed the good news to the people in Derbe and made many disciples. Then they returned to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch, where.
local: Be lɛ ba tɩ mʊɔl a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ a tẽe 'lɔ pɔ, ka a nɩyɔɔ jaa sɔɔ turo a Yesu. A ba pãa lɩɛb chen a Lɩstɩra, Ikoniyum nɩ a Antiok.

eng: Her owners realized that their hope for making money was gone. They grabbed Paul and Silas and dragged them before the officials in the city center.
local: A pɔɔbile sorbo na wa bɔ̃ɔn ɩka baa lɛ nyɛrɛ tɔ̃nɔ̃ a pɔɔbile sɛ̃ ɛ, ba nyɔɔn a Pɔɔl nɩ a Silasɩ tɔɔ chiin be a daa pɔ a nɩbɛrɛ niem.

eng: You know I held back nothing that would be helpful so that I could proclaim to you and teach you both publicly and privately in your homes.
local: Yɩn mãɛ bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka n ba sɔɔl yɩ yele jaa na sʊ̃ʊ yɩ ɛ, tɩchɛ n wile yɩn chãa pɔ, nɩ a yɩ yie pɔ.

eng: Weve heard that some of our number have disturbed you with unsettling words we didnt authorize.
local: Sɩ wõn a bamɩne na yi a sɩ sɛ̃ sɩn ba kʊ ba sɔr ɛ, tɩ vuro yɩ dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ nɩ yɩ 'yɛrɛ.

eng: God overlooks ignorance of these things in times past, but now directs everyone everywhere to change their hearts and lives.
local: A kõr jaa 'lɔ, Nãaŋmɩn lɩɛb puor 'yɔ̃ɔ a sɩ ba bɔ̃ɔfʊ, tɩchɛ pʊ̃pãanyã 'lɔ, wʊ kpãkpãana na nɩrɛ jaa jie jaa ɩka, ba lɩɛb ba tɩɛr.

eng: You know I have testified to both Jews and Greeks that they must change their hearts and lives as they turn to God and have faith in our Lord Jesus.
local: N kpãkpãan naa a Juu nɩbɛ nɩ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, ɩka ba lɩɛb wa a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃, na ba lɩɛb a ba tɩɛr sɔɔ de a sɩ Soro Yesu.

eng: Standing up, Paul gestured with his hand and said, Fellow Israelites and Gentile God-worshippers, please listen to me.
local: Ka a Pɔɔl vaa ir wɩlwɩl ba a nũu tɩ 'yɛrɛ, Isɩral nɩbɛ woɩ, nɩ a yɩn na ba ɩ a Isɩral nɩbɛ ɛ, na puoro a Nãaŋmɩn, yɩ chɛlɛ a lɛ ɩn 'yɛrɛ.

eng: We reached a united decision to select some delegates and send them to you along with our dear friends Barnabas and Paul.
local: Ala lɛ so ka sɩ jaa sɔɔ ir nɩbɛ bamɩne tʊ̃ ka ban wa a yɩ sɛ̃, 'matãan a sɩ barmɩne Pɔɔl nɩ a Banabasɩ.

eng: These people have devoted their lives to the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: a nɩbɛ banyãna lɛ sɔɔ kũu a sɩ Soro Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto yuor jũu.

eng: This is because God has set a day when he intends to judge the world justly by a man he has appointed. God has given proof of this to everyone by raising him from the dead.
local: Bojũu wʊ ir bɩbir bin wʊn na de a daba 'lɔ wʊn ir, na wʊ tɔɔr a wɛr 'yɛr nɩ yeltorsɩ pɔ. Wʊ vɛ̃n ka a chaar kʊ nɩrɛ jaa wʊn sãɩ wʊ a kũu pɔ.

eng: Therefore, we are sending Judas and Silas. They will confirm what we have written.
local: A lɛ jũu lɛ sɩ tʊ̃n a Judas nɩ a Silasɩ ka ban tɩ man a ba nɛɛ a lɛ sɩn sɛb.

eng: The God of this people Israel chose our ancestors. God made them a great people while they lived as strangers in the land of Egypt. With his great power, he led them out of that country.
local: A Nãaŋmɩn nyã a sɩn Isɩral nɩbɛ na puoro lɛ dɔ̃ɔ chɛl ir a sɩ sãakpãmɩne na wʊ vɛ̃ ka ba dɔɔ paal be a Ijipitɩ pɔ. Kpɛ̃ɔ lɛ wʊ de faan ba yin a paal 'lɔ pɔ.

eng: For about forty years, God put up with them in the wilderness.
local: na wʊ bɔ̃ɔ tara ba be a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ yuom lɩjaayi .

eng: When they heard about the resurrection from the dead, some began to ridicule Paul. However, others said, Well hear from you about this again.
local: Ban wa wõ a kũu pɔ irfʊ 'yɛr, bamɩne lan wuol, tɩchɛ ka bamɩne mɩ 'yɛr, Sɩn bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka sɩ lɛ wõ a 'yɛr nyã nɩdaar.

eng: God conquered seven nations in the land of Canaan and gave the Israelites their land as an inheritance.
local: Paal bʊrsɩ ayopõi lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn dii bar be a Kanaan tɩ de a wɛr 'lɔ kʊ a wʊ nɩbɛ ka a ɩ ba sofʊ.

eng: At that, Paul left the council.
local: A lɛ, Pɔɔl bar ba naa a lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie.

eng: When her owners approached the legal authorities, they said, These people are causing an uproar in our city. They are Jews.
local: Ba chiin ban tɩ kʊ a 'yɛr tɔɔrbɔ tɩ 'yɛr, A nɩbɛ banyãna ɩ naa a Juu nɩbɛ na ba wa kpɛ dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ sɩ a tẽe.

eng: they strengthened the disciples and urged them to remain firm in the faith. They told them, If we are to enter Gods kingdom, we must pass through many troubles.
local: tɩ mʊlɔ a pupuorbiir 'yɔ̃ɔn ba ãkpãɛn ka ba ara nɩ yelmãɛ a sɔɔfʊ pɔ, na ba 'yɛrɛ, Dɔɔyɛ yɔɔ pɔ lɛ sɩn tu tɩ kpɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.

eng: Now, compelled by the Spirit, Im going to Jerusalem. I dont know what will happen to me there.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã 'lɔ, Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ 'yɛr man ka n chiine a Jerusalɛm, n ba bɔ̃ɔ lɛn na tɩ pɔɔ ma a be ɛ.

eng: The Holy Spirit has led us to the decision that no burden should be placed on you other than these essentials.
local: A vɩɛlɛ na kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ nɩ a sɩn mɩ sɛ̃, ka sɩ kʊ̃ lɛ tuo yɩ tuor dɔɔl a anyãna ju ɛ.

eng: who promote customs that we Romans cant accept or practice.
local: Ba tara na yelsɩ kɔ̃ɔ an ba tara sɔr kʊ a sɩn Worom nɩbɛ a sɩn sɔɔ bɩɩ maal ɛ.

eng: The crowd joined in the attacks against Paul and Silas, so the authorities ordered that they be stripped of their clothes and beaten with a rod.
local: Ka a nɩyɔɔ bala ir pʊɔ na baa bara a Pɔɔl nɩ a Silasɩ ɛ, ka a 'yɛr tɔɔrbɔ vɛ̃ ka ba yaar a Pɔɔl nɩ a Silasɩ fuusɩ bar tɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ a ba fɔb ba.

eng: When Paul and Silas had been severely beaten, the authorities threw them into prison and ordered the jailer to secure them with great care.
local: A lɛ ban wa fɔb ba sɛɛ, ba nyɔɔr ban 'yɔ̃ɔ a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ, tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a 'lɔ na guro a pɔɔfʊ die, a wʊ bɔ̃ɔ kaara ba.

eng: They appointed elders for each church. With prayer and fasting, they committed these elders to the Lord, in whom they had placed their trust.
local: Pɔɔl nɩ a Banabasɩ ir nɩdierbe kʊ ba a ba kaara a puorfʊ dẽme ãsʊɔ jaa. Ba gal kɔ̃ na ba sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn tɩ de ba 'yɔ̃ɔ a sɩ Soro ban sɔɔ nũu pɔ.

eng: What I do know is that the Holy Spirit testifies to me from city to city that prisons and troubles await me.
local: Yelbʊ̃'yen tɛɛ lɛ n bɔ̃ɔ ɩka, tẽe jaa pɔ, a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ ma vɛ̃n ka n bɔ̃ɔn ɩka die pɔ pɔɔfʊ, nɩ wõm lɛ ɩn tɩ tuor.

eng: But nothing, not even my life, is more important than my completing my mission. This is nothing other than the ministry I received from the Lord Jesus: to testify about the good news of Gods grace.
local: Tɩchɛ a anyãna maa bã ma ɛ, ala ɩ ɩn tʊ̃ɔ jo ta a baaraa naa tʊ̃ a lɛ a sɩ Soro Yesu na kʊ ma a tʊ̃mɔ̃, ɩka n 'yɛr a Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ.

eng: This happened over a period of about four hundred fifty years. After this, he gave them judges until the time of the prophet Samuel.
local: A anyãna a jaa lɔ̃ɔn taa ɩ yuom kɔɔr anaar dɔɔl lɩjaayi nɩ pie . A anyã puor, Nãaŋmɩn kʊ ban 'yɛr tɔɔrbɔ tɩ tãn a Samuwɛl na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ sɔ̃ɔ.

eng: refuse food offered to idols, blood, the meat from strangled animals, and sexual immorality. You will do well to avoid such things. Farewell.
local: Yɩ taa dire tɩɩ bon ɛ, yɩ taa ɔɔrɔ jãɩ ɛ, na yɩ taa ɔɔrɔ dʊ̃ɔ na var kpi nʊ̃n ɛ, bɩɩ sɛ̃nɛ ɛ. Yɩ mɔ̃ na yɩ nyɔɔ yɩ mãɛ a yelsɩ anyãna pɔ. Yɩ jɛ̃ vɩla.

eng: Some people joined him and came to believe, including Dionysius, a member of the council on Mars Hill, a woman named Damaris, and several others.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a nɩbɛ bãlãa turo a Pɔɔl na ba sɔɔ de a 'yɛr, a ba ãsʊɔ lɛ Diyonɩsɩs na ɩ a Areyopagusɩ lɔ̃ɔfʊ nɩrɛ, ka a pɔɔ ãsʊɔ mɩ na pʊɔ yuor di Damarisɩ nɩ bamɩne mɩ na pʊɔ.

eng: After Paul and Barnabas traveled through Pisidia, they came to Pamphylia.
local: Ba kpɛ̃n tala a Pisidiya, ba wa ta naa Pamfiliya.

eng: I know that none of you will see me again—you among whom I traveled and proclaimed the kingdom.
local: Yɩ nyɛ, pʊ̃pãanyã a yɩn bala jie ɩn yɔ naa mʊɔl a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ 'yɛr, yɩ kʊ̃ lɛ nyɛ ma go ɛ.

eng: Therefore, today I testify to you that Im not responsible for anyones fate.
local: A lɛ jũu, n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ a dɩna ɩka ãsʊɔ jaa wa bɔr, n nũu ba pʊɔ be ɛ.

eng: They proclaimed the word in Perga, then went down to Attalia.
local: ban wa mʊɔl a 'yɛr a Perga, ba suun chen be a Ataliya.

eng: The Israelites requested a king, so God gave them Saul, Kishs son, from the tribe of Benjamin, and he served as their king for forty years.
local: Be lɛ ka a nɩbɛ pãa wa sʊɔr Samuwɛl a wʊ bɔ nãa kʊ ba, ka Nãaŋmɩn de a Sɔɔl na ɩ a Kɩsɩ bidaba kʊ ba na ɩ a Benjamin bal nɩrɛ a wʊ ɩ a ba nãa yuom lɩjaayi .

eng: When he received these instructions, he threw them into the innermost cell and secured their feet in stocks.
local: Wʊn wõ a nɩtɔ̃, wʊ tɩɛ ban 'yɔ̃ɔ a die 'lɔ na be a sãlsɔɔ tɩ lũ a ba gbɛɛ mar daa.

eng: When Barnabas, Paul, and the delegates were sent on their way, they went down to Antioch. They gathered the believers and delivered the letter.
local: Ban wa bar a Pɔɔl, nɩ a Banabasɩ, nɩ a Judas nɩ a Silasɩ sɔr, ka ba suu chen be a Antiok, be lɛ ba tɩ lɔ̃ɔ taa a bala na puoro ba jaa na ba de a gãn kʊ ba.

eng: From there they sailed to Antioch, where they had been entrusted by Gods grace to the work they had now completed.
local: Be a Ataliya lɛ ba yi tɩ kpɛ a gboro chen a Antiok. Be lɛ ba dɔ̃ɔ de ba 'yɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn nũu pɔ ka wʊ sʊ̃ʊ ba, a ba tʊ̃ɔ a tʊ̃mɔ̃ a be tʊ̃ baar.

eng: I havent avoided proclaiming the entire plan of God to you.
local: Bojũu n ba dɔ̃ɔ sɔɔl yɩ yele jaa ɛ, tɩ man a lɛ jaa a Nãaŋmɩn na bʊɔrɔ kʊ yɩ.

eng: Around midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God, and the other prisoners were listening to them.
local: A tãsɔɔ bãɩn lɛ a Pɔɔl nɩ a Silasɩ sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn, na ba yiele nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn yiel, ka a ba taaba ban lɔ̃ɔ pɔɔn ba chɛlɛ.

eng: The people read it, delighted with its encouraging message.
local: A nɩbɛ na wa kar a gãn a 'yɔ̃ɔ ban ãkpãɛn ka ba pʊɔ pɛl.

eng: All at once there was such a violent earthquake that it shook the prisons foundations. The doors flew open and everyones chains came loose.
local: Ajienaa ka a tẽe dam kpɛ̃ɔ jaa, ka a pɔɔfʊ yir par kparaa mɩ dam. Ka a pɔɔfʊ der dɔ̃dɔɩ yuo yuor a jaa, ka nɩrɛ jaa bɔ̃jɔrma ban lũn wʊ for for.

eng: Watch yourselves and the whole flock, in which the Holy Spirit has placed you as supervisors, to shepherd Gods church, which he obtained with the death of his own Son.
local: Yɩ gu yɩ mãɛ nɩ a nɩbɛ bala 'yɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na maal yɩ a yɩ ɩ a nɩdierbe. Yɩ kaara a Nãaŋmɩn pupuorbiir bala wʊn dãn a wʊ mãɛ jãɩ.

eng: Judas and Silas were prophets, and they said many things that encouraged and strengthened the brothers and sisters.
local: Judas nɩ a Silasɩ mɩ na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ 'yɛr 'yɛr yɔɔ 'yɔ̃ɔn a yɛɛr ãkpãɛn.

eng: When the jailer awoke and saw the open doors of the prison, he thought the prisoners had escaped, so he drew his sword and was about to kill himself.
local: Ka a 'lɔ na guro a pɔɔfʊ die jɛɔ ir na wʊ nyɛ a lɛ a pɔɔfʊ der dɔ̃dɔɩ na yuor, ka wʊ vʊ̃ɔ sɔɔ ɩka wʊ kʊ̃n wʊ mãɛ, bojũu wʊ man tãa a pɔɔfʊ dẽme yin jo.

eng: After God removed him, he raised up David to be their king. God testified concerning him, I have found David, Jesses son, a man who shares my desires. Whatever my will is, he will do.
local: Wʊn wa ir a Sɔɔl bar, wʊ den a David ka wʊ ɩ a ba nãa. Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr naa, N nyɛn a David na ɩ a Jesɩ bidaba ka a n ãgãn 'maa, wʊn maal yele a jaa ɩn bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ ɩ.

eng: On their arrival, they gathered the church together and reported everything that God had accomplished through their activity, and how God had opened a door of faith for the Gentiles.
local: Ban tɩ tara jaa lɛ ba bʊɔl a pupuorbiir lɔ̃ɔ taa na ba man a lɛ jaa a Nãaŋmɩn na de ba tʊ̃n nɩ a lɛ mɩ wʊn yuo sɔr kʊ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, ka ba tʊ̃ɔ sɔɔ de.

eng: I know that, after my departure, savage wolves will come in among you and wont spare the flock.
local: N bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka maa wa chen baar, nɩbɛ na lɩɛb gbõgboi kpɛ a yɩ pɔ, ba kʊ̃ bar yɩ ɛ.

eng: But Paul shouted loudly, Dont harm yourself! Were all here.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Pɔɔl tãn, Ta ge fʊ mãɛ ɛ, sɩ jaa ben ka.

eng: They stayed with the disciples a long time.
local: Ba jɛ̃n a be kor bãlãa nɩ a poturbo.

eng: Judas and Silas stayed there awhile, then were sent back with a blessing of peace from the brothers and sisters to those who first sent them.
local: Ban wa jɛ̃ a be kor bãlãa, ka a yɛɛr lɛ bar ba sɔr nɩ nyã'maar ka ba lɩɛb chen a bala na tʊ̃ ba sɛ̃.

eng: From this mans descendants, God brought to Israel a savior, Jesus, just as he promised.
local: A Nãa David nyãna bal par pɔ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn ir a Fafaara Yesu kʊ a Isɩral nɩbɛ nɩtãa lɛ wʊn dɔ̃ɔ tɩr a nɛɛ.

eng: Some of your own people will distort the word in order to lure followers after them.
local: Bamɩne na yin a yɩn mãɛ pɔ gba, na ba jɛr a yelmãɛ bɛl nɩbɛ ka ba turo ba.

eng: Paul and Barnabas stayed in Antioch, where, together with many others, they taught and proclaimed the good news of the Lords word.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Pɔɔl nɩ a Banabasɩ jɛ̃ be a Antiok, be lɛ a bala nɩ a ba taaba bamɩne wile tɩ mʊɔlɔ a sɩ Soro 'yɛr.

eng: Before Jesus appearance, John proclaimed to all the Israelites a baptism to show they were changing their hearts and lives.
local: Sɛrɛ tɩchɛ ka a Yesu wa, Jɔɔn mʊɔlɔ naa a tɩɛr lɩɛbfʊ 'yɛr na wʊ suoro a Isɩral nɩbɛ ba jaa a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ.

eng: The jailer called for some lights, rushed in, and fell trembling before Paul and Silas.
local: A wʊ bʊɔl a ba waan fãtɩn wa kʊ wʊ a wʊ pɔɔ kpɛ̃n be a die pɔ, na wʊ tɩ lo gbur dumo mɩrɛ a Pɔɔl nɩ a Silasɩ niem.

eng: Stay alert! Remember that for three years I constantly and tearfully warned each one of you. I never stopped warning you!.
local: A lɛ jũu yɩ guro yɩ mãɛ na yɩ tɩɛr kaa, yuom ata nɩ nyã ɩn ba vɛ̃ a yɩ ãsʊɔ jaa wiilfʊ ɛ, nɩbikʊ̃ɔ lɛ n wile nɩ yɩ mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ nɩ tãsɔɔ.

eng: Now I entrust you to God and the message of his grace, which is able to build you up and give you an inheritance among all whom God has made holy.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã, n de yɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn nũu pɔ, nɩ a wʊ wãɛfʊ 'yɛr bie pɔ, a yɩ na tʊ̃ɔ nʊ̃ɔ a wʊ kʊ yɩ a bʊ̃sʊ̃n ala wʊn tara kʊ a wʊ nɩbɛ.

eng: As John was completing his mission, he said, Who do you think I am? Im not the one you think I am, but he is coming after me. Im not worthy to loosen his sandals.
local: Lɛ a Jɔɔn na wa baaraa a wʊ tʊ̃mɔ̃, wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, An lɛ yɩ tɩɛr ɩka n ɩ naa? N ba ɩ a 'lɔ yɩn tɩɛr ɛ, tɩchɛ wʊ ben a n puor waara, n ba sɛɛ ɩka n for a wʊ nafɔbɔ ɛ.

eng: He led them outside and asked, Honorable masters, what must I do to be rescued.
local: Tɩ pãa ir tara ba yin tɩ sʊʊr, Nɩbɛrɛ, ŋmɩnɛ lɛ ɩn ɩrɛ wa nyɛ faafʊ.

eng: I havent craved anyones silver, gold, or clothing.
local: N ba tara nyuur nɩ ãsʊɔ jaa libir, bɩɩ sɛlma, bɩɩ a fɔɔr ɛ.

eng: Brothers, children of Abrahams family, and you Gentile God-worshippers, the message about this salvation has been sent to us.
local: Maa yɛɛr na ɩ Abraham yɔn nɩ a yɩn na joro a Nãaŋmɩn dãbãɛ na ɩ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, sɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ a faafʊ nyãna wa.

eng: The people in Jerusalem and their leaders didnt recognize Jesus. By condemning him they fulfilled the words of the prophets that are read every Sabbath.
local: Bojũu, a nɩbɛ na be a Jerusalɛm nɩ a ba nɩbɛrɛ ba chɛl a Yesu ɛ, na ba 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ 'yɛr ka a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ na sɛb a ba ma kara a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir jaa ɩ.

eng: Some time later, Paul said to Barnabas, Lets go back and visit all the brothers and sisters in every city where we preached the Lords word. Lets see how they are doing.
local: A mɩn lɛ wa kor bãlãa ka a Pɔɔl 'yɛr kʊ a Banabasɩ, Vɛ̃ ka sɩ lɛ chen tɩ piir a yɛɛr kaa a tẽn ala pɔ sɩn mʊɔl a sɩ Soro 'yɛr na sɩ kaa ŋmɩnɛ lɛ ba irɛ.

eng: Barnabas wanted to take John Mark with them.
local: Ka a Banabasɩ bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ de a Jɔɔn Maakɩ ka ba 'mataa chen.

eng: Paul insisted that they shouldnt take him along, since he had deserted them in Pamphylia and hadnt continued with them in their work.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Pɔɔl ba tɩɛr ɩka a ba tara sɔr ɛ, bojũu wʊ dɔ̃ɔ bar ba be a Pamfiliya ba lɛ pɔɔ ba chen a tʊ̃mɔ̃ jie ɛ.

eng: Even though they didnt find a single legal basis for the death penalty, they asked Pilate to have him executed.
local: Ba gban ba dɔ̃ɔ nyɛ yele jaa na wiil ɩka wʊ sɛɛn kũu ɛ, tɩ ba sʊɔr a Pilate ɩka wʊ vɛ̃ ka ba kʊ a Yesu.

eng: Their argument became so intense that they went their separate ways. Barnabas took Mark and sailed to Cyprus.
local: Be lɛ ba tãn tãn taa na ba ŋma dẽdẽme, ka a Banabasɩ de a Jɔɔn Maakɩ na wʊ kpɛ̃n a gboro chiin be a Sipurusɩ.

eng: You yourselves know that I have provided for my own needs and for those of my companions with my own hands.
local: Yɩn mãɛ bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka a n bʊ̃bʊɔrsɩ, a n nũu pɔ lɛ a yi, naa sʊ̃ʊ a bala ɩn lɔ̃ɔ tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ.

eng: They replied, Believe in the Lord Jesus, and you will be saved—you and your entire household.
local: A ba 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Sɔɔ de a sɩ Soro Yesu, fʊ̃ʊ nɩ a fʊ yir dẽme na nyɛn faafʊ.

eng: When they finished doing everything that had been written about him, they took him down from the cross and laid him in a tomb.
local: Ban wa maal a lɛ jaa ban sɛb a wʊ yele baar, a wʊ poturbo yɔɔ wʊn a daa ju na ba tɩ ũu a yaa pɔ.

eng: Paul chose Silas and left, entrusted by the brothers and sisters to the Lords grace.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Pɔɔl mɩ ir a Silasɩ ka a yɛɛr de ba 'yɔ̃ɔ a sɩ Soro nũu pɔ, ka wʊn sʊ̃ʊ ba.

eng: They spoke the Lords word to him and everyone else in his house.
local: Be lɛ ba pãa 'yɛr a sɩ Soro 'yɛr kʊ wʊ nɩ a bala jaa na be a wʊ yir.

eng: But God raised him from the dead!.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Nãaŋmɩn ir wʊ a kũu pɔ.

eng: In everything I have shown you that, by working hard, we must help the weak. In this way we remember the Lord Jesus words: It is more blessed to give than to receive.
local: Bon jaa ɩn maal, n wiil yɩ naa ɩka a tʊ̃kpãɛn a nɩtɔ̃nɔ̃ pɔ lɛ, sɩn tu na sɩ sʊ̃ʊ a nɩbaalsɩ, yɩ tɩɛrɛ a lɛ a sɩ Soro Yesu na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr, An ɩn bɔɔsʊ̃n kʊ a 'lɔ na de bon kʊ nɩrɛ, gar a 'lɔ na de. .

eng: He traveled through Syria and Cilicia, strengthening the churches.
local: Wʊ tun a Siriya nɩ a Sɩlɩsɩya 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a bala na puoro ãkpãɛn.

eng: Right then, in the middle of the night, the jailer welcomed them and washed their wounds. He and everyone in his household were immediately baptized.
local: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ a pɔɔfʊ die guguro de ba yin a tãsɔɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ na wʊ tɩ pɛɛ a ba natɩɛ tɩchɛ a ba mɩ tɔ̃ɔ so a 'lɔ nɩ a wʊ yir dẽme ba jaa a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ.

eng: After he said these things, he knelt down with all of them to pray.
local: Wʊn 'yɛr a anyãna baar, wʊ gbur dumo ka ba jaa 'mataa sʊɔr a Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: He brought them into his home and gave them a meal. He was overjoyed because he and everyone in his household had come to believe in God.
local: Ka a pɔɔfʊ die guguro tara ba kpɛ̃n a wʊ yir tɩ maal bʊ̃diir kʊ ba, ka nʊ̃ɔ kpɛ a wʊ yir dẽme, bojũu 'lɔ nɩ a wʊ yir dẽme ba jaa sɔɔn de a Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: He appeared over many days to those who had traveled with him from Galilee to Jerusalem. They are now his witnesses to the people.
local: A ber yɔɔ na wa po, a bala wʊn dɔ̃ɔ lɔ̃ɔ yɔn yin a Galilee tɩ tãn a Jerusalɛm, ba nyɛ wʊ naa, bala lɛ ɩ a dãasɩɛ dẽme a pʊ̃pãanyã kʊ a sɩ nɩbɛ.

eng: They cried uncontrollably as everyone embraced and kissed Paul.
local: Ba jaa ŋman nɩbikʊ̃ɔ tɩ na ba kõr kõr wʊ.

eng: We proclaim to you the good news. What God promised to our ancestors.
local: Sɩn 'yɛrɛ naa a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyãna kʊrɔ yɩ, a Nãaŋmɩn na dɔ̃ɔ tɩr a nɛɛ kʊ a sɩ sãakpãmɩne.

eng: They were especially grieved by his statement that they would never see him again. Then they accompanied him to the ship.
local: A lɛ na maal sɔ̃ɔ a ba pʊɔ nɩ nyã, a lɛ wʊn 'yɛr ɩka ba kʊ̃ lɛ nyɛ wʊ go ɛ, tɩ na ba bɩɛl wʊ suun a gboro jie.

eng: The next morning the legal authorities sent the police to the jailer with the order Release those people.
local: Wɛr na wa chaar a 'yɛr tɔɔrbɔ ir a ba tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ tʊ̃ na ba 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ chen tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a 'lɔ na guro a pɔɔfʊ die ɩka, a wʊ yuo a dɔɔr bala bar.

eng: he has fulfilled for us, their children, by raising up Jesus. As it was written in the second psalm, You are my son; today I have become your father. .
local: Nãaŋmɩn maal kʊ a sɩn na ɩ a yɔn a lɛ wʊn dɔ̃ɔ tɩr a nɛɛ kʊ a sɩ sãakpãmɩne, na wʊ sãɩ a Yesu yin a kũu pɔ. Nɩtãa lɛ an sɛb a Yiel Gãma ayi pɔ, Fʊ ɩ naa a maa bie, dɩna n lɩɛb a fʊ sãa.

eng: So the jailer reported this to Paul, informing him, The authorities sent word that you both are to be released. You can leave now. Go in peace.
local: Ka a pɔɔfʊ die guguro 'yɛr kʊ a Pɔɔl, A 'yɛr tɔɔrbɔ tʊ̃n ɩka n yuo a fʊ̃ʊ nɩ a Silasɩ bar. A lɛ pʊ̃pãanyã yɩn tʊ̃ɔ chiine, yɩ chiine nɩ nyã'maar.

eng: After this, Paul left Athens and went to Corinth.
local: A anyã puor, Pɔɔl yin a Atensɩ na wʊ chen be a Korɩntɩ.

eng: God raised Jesus from the dead, never again to be subjected to deaths decay. Therefore, God said, I will give to you the holy and firm promises I made to David. .
local: Lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na sãɩ wʊ a kũu pɔ nɩ nyã, wʊ kʊ̃ lɛ kpi pʊ̃ɔ go ɛ, a 'yɛr nyãna lɛ wʊ 'yɛr, Ɩn kʊ yɩ naa a yelsʊ̃n nɩ a sʊ̃ʊfʊ 'lɔ ɩn tɩr a nɛɛ kʊ a David.

eng: Paul told the police, Even though we are Roman citizens, they beat us publicly without first finding us guilty of a crime, and they threw us into prison. And now they want to send us away secretly? No way! They themselves will have to come and escort us out.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Pɔɔl 'yɛr kʊ a bala ban tʊ̃, Chãa pɔ jaa lɛ ba vɛ̃ a ba fɔb sɩ, baa tɔɔr a sɩ 'yɛr ɛ, sɩ gba ɩ naa a Worom dẽme tɩ ba 'yɔ̃ɔ sɩ a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ. Pʊ̃pãanyã a ba lɛ bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba sɔɔl yuo sɩ bar? Bala mãɛ ba wa yuo sɩ bar.

eng: There he found a Jew named Aquila, a native of Pontus. He had recently come from Italy with his wife Priscilla because Claudius had ordered all Jews to leave Rome. Paul visited with them.
local: Be lɛ wʊ tuor a Juu nɩrɛ na di Akuila na wʊ ɩ Pontus bal nɩrɛ, 'lɔ nɩ a wʊ pɔɔ Pɩrɩsɩla na yi a Itali wa a ba kor ɛ, bojũu a Nãa Kɩlaudiyus 'yɛr naa ɩka a Juu nɩrɛ jaa wʊ yi a Worom. Ka a Pɔɔl chen ɩka wʊ piir ba kaa.

eng: In another place it is said, You will not let your holy one experience deaths decay. .
local: A jie ãsʊɔ lɛ 'yɛr ɩka, Fʊ kʊ̃ vɛ̃ ka a fʊ Chɛchɛ Sʊɔ kpɛ pʊ̃ɔfʊ jie ɛ.

eng: David served Gods purpose in his own generation, then he died and was buried with his ancestors. He experienced deaths decay.
local: A Nãa David na wa tʊ̃ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na bʊɔrɔ a wʊ sɔ̃ɔ, wʊ kpi naa ka ba ũu wʊ be a wʊ sãamɩne yar par, ka a wʊ ãgãn pʊ̃ɔ.

eng: but the one whom God has raised up didnt experience deaths decay.
local: Tɩchɛ a 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn na sãɩ a kũu pɔ, ba kpɛ pʊ̃ɔfʊ jie ɛ.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, know this: Through Jesus we proclaim forgiveness of sins to you. From all those sins from which you couldnt be put in right relationship with God through Moses Law.
local: A lɛ jũu n yɛɛr, n bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka yɩ bɔ̃ɔ ka a tu naa a Yesu 'yɔ̃ɔ ka ba mʊɔl yelbier vɛ̃ kʊfʊ kʊ yɩ.

eng: through Jesus everyone who believes is put in right relationship with God.
local: A tu naa a wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ ɩka nɩrɛ jaa na sɔɔ de, Nãaŋmɩn na faa wʊn yin a ala a jaa a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ na kʊ̃ naa tʊ̃ɔ faa wʊ ɛ.

eng: Take care that the prophets words dont apply to you.
local: Yɩ bɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ sʊ̃, ka a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr taa wa a yɩ sɛ̃ ɛ.

eng: Look, you scoffers , marvel and die . Im going to do work in your day — a work you wont believe even if someone told you .
local: Nyɛ a yɩn na kpɛrɛ, yɩ bʊ̃bʊr na yɩ wɛ, bojũu ɩn maal yele kɔ̃ɔ a yɩ sɔ̃ɔ yɩn ba be na sɔɔ de ɛ, ala ɩ nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ gba 'yɛr kʊ yɩ. .

eng: As Paul and Barnabas were leaving the synagogue, the people urged them to speak about these things again on the next Sabbath.
local: Lɛ a Pɔɔl nɩ a Banabasɩ na wa yire a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie, a nɩbɛ lɛ sʊɔr ba naa ɩka ba lɛ wa a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar wa 'yɛr a 'yɛr nyãna go.

eng: When the people in the synagogue were dismissed, many Jews and devout converts to Judaism accompanied Paul and Barnabas, who urged them to remain faithful to the message of Gods grace.
local: Lɛ a lɔ̃ɔfʊ dẽme na wa yaar, a Juu nɩbɛ yɔɔ jie nɩ a bala na lɩɛb a Juu nɩbɛ turo naa Pɔɔl nɩ a Banabasɩ na ba bʊrɔ nɩ ba kpãkpãana ba 'yɛrɛ ka ba mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ nyã pɔ.

eng: On the next Sabbath, almost everyone in the city gathered to hear the Lords word.
local: A Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir na lɛ wa ta, a tẽdẽme yɔɔ jie tɩ lɔ̃ɔn taa na wõ a sɩ Soro 'yɛr.

eng: When the Jews saw the crowds, they were overcome with jealousy. They argued against what Paul was saying by slandering him.
local: Lɛ a Juu nɩbɛ na nyɛ a nɩyɔɔ ba nyãna, nyuur kpɛ ba naa ka ba tʊrɔ chɩrɛ nɩ lɛ a Pɔɔl na 'yɛrɛ.

eng: Speaking courageously, Paul and Barnabas said, We had to speak Gods word to you first. Since you reject it and show that you are unworthy to receive eternal life, we will turn to the Gentiles.
local: Ka a Pɔɔl nɩ a Banabasɩ ba jo ba dãbãɛ ɛ, tɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Sɩn 'lɔ, sɩn den nie 'yɛr a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr kʊ yɩ. Tɩchɛ yɩn ben tɔr bar na yaa kaa a yɩ mãɛ ɩka yɩ sɛɛn naa a nyɛvʊr na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ, pʊ̃pãanyã 'lɔ sɩn lɩɛb chen a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ sɛ̃.

eng: This is what the Lord commanded us: I have made you a light for the Gentiles, so that you could bring salvation to the end of the earth .
local: Nɩtɔ̃ lɛ a sɩ Soro kpãkpãan sɩ ɩka sɩ ɩ, Ɩn maal fʊ naa ka fʊ ɩ chãa kʊ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, ka fʊn chiin a faafʊ 'yɛr tɩ tãn a tẽe baaraa. .

eng: When the Gentiles heard this, they rejoiced and honored the Lords word. Everyone who was appointed for eternal life believed.
local: A lɛ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, na wõ a 'yɛr ba pʊɔ pɛl naa ka ba de 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ kʊ a sɩ Soro 'yɛr, ka a bala jaa a Nãaŋmɩn na ir ɩka ba nyɛ a nyɛvʊr na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ, sɔɔ de.

eng: and the Lords word was broadcast throughout the entire region.
local: Ka a sɩ Soro 'yɛr yaar a paal 'lɔ wʊ jaa.

eng: The police reported this to the legal authorities, who were alarmed to learn that Paul and Silas were Roman citizens.
local: A tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ na tɩ man a lɛ kʊ a 'yɛr tɔɔrbɔ, ban wa wõ ɩka a Pɔɔl nɩ a Silasɩ ɩ naa Worom dẽme, dãbãɛ kpɛ ba naa.

eng: Because they practiced the same trade, he stayed and worked with them. They all worked with leather.
local: Wʊ tɩ jɛ̃n a be a ba 'mataa tʊ̃nɔ̃ bojũu ba jaa bɔ̃ɔn a gãma tʊ̃mɔ̃.

eng: They came and consoled Paul and Silas, escorting them out of prison and begging them to leave the city.
local: Ka a bala mãɛ pãa chen tɩ 'maal ba yin a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ, tɩ sʊɔr ba ɩka ba yi a tẽe 'lɔ pɔ.

eng: However, the Jews provoked the prominent women among the Gentile God-worshippers, as well as the citys leaders. They instigated others to harass Paul and Barnabas, and threw them out of their district.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Juu nɩbɛ dam a pɔbɔ bala na turo a Juu nɩbɛ puorfʊ tɩ ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, nɩ a tẽe nɩdierbe 'yɔ̃ɔ ka ba dii a Pɔɔl nɩ a Banabasɩ bar a paal 'lɔ pɔ.

eng: Paul and Silas left the prison and made their way to Lydias house where they encouraged the brothers and sisters. Then they left Philippi.
local: Pɔɔl nɩ a Silasɩ na wa yi a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ, ba chen a Lidiya yir, be lɛ ba tɩ tuor a yɛɛr ka ba 'yɔ̃ɔ ba ãkpãɛn tɩ pãa yi a be chen.

eng: Every Sabbath he interacted with people in the synagogue, trying to convince both Jews and Greeks.
local: A Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir Daar jaa Pɔɔl ma chen a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir ma tɩ 'yɛrɛ a 'yɛr bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ lɩɛb a Juu nɩbɛ nɩ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ tɩɛr.

eng: Paul and Barnabas shook the dust from their feet and went to Iconium.
local: Ka ba pãpã a ba gbɛɛ tã-uul 'yɔ̃ɔ ba ka a wiil ɩka Nãaŋmɩn na ge ba naa tɩ na ba chen a Ikoniyum.

eng: Once Silas and Timothy arrived from Macedonia, Paul devoted himself fully to the word, testifying to the Jews that Jesus was the Christ.
local: Lɛ a Silasɩ nɩ a Timoti na wʊ yi a Masedonɩya wa a Korɩntɩ, Pɔɔl den a wʊ sɔ̃ɔ wʊ jaa na wʊ mʊɔlɔ mana kʊrɔ a Juu nɩbɛ ɩka a Yesu lɛ a Nɩ-iraa.

eng: When they opposed and slandered him, he shook the dust from his clothes in protest and said to them, You are responsible for your own fates! Im innocent! From now on Ill go to the Gentiles.
local: Tɩchɛ ka Juu nɩbɛ 'yɛrɛ a Pɔɔl kpõkpoor 'yɛr, a wʊ pãpã a fɔbaa 'yɔ̃ɔ ba tɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba, A yɩ jãɩ a lɩɛb dɔɔl a yɩ ju, a ba lɛ ɩ maa 'yɛr ɛ, an yi dɩna chiine, n chiine naa a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ jie.

eng: He left the synagogue and went next door to the home of Titius Justus, a Gentile God-worshipper.
local: Pɔɔl yin be a lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir na wʊ gʊɔr a daba ãsʊɔ na di Titiyusɩ Jusɩtusɩ na ba ɩ a Juu nɩrɛ ɛ na puoro ka a wʊ yir gbʊr a lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir.

eng: Crispus, the synagogue leader, and his entire household came to believe in the Lord. Many Corinthians believed and were baptized after listening to Paul.
local: Ka a Kɩrɩsɩpusɩ na ɩ a lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir nɩdiere nɩ a wʊ yir dẽme ba jaa sɔɔ de a sɩ Soro 'yɛr nɩ a Korɩntɩ dẽme yɔɔ na wõ a lɛ wʊn 'yɛr, a ba sɔɔ a wʊ so ba jaa a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ.

eng: Because of the abundant presence of the Holy Spirit in their lives, the disciples were overflowing with happiness.
local: Ka a nʊ̃ɔ kpɛ a poturbo bala ka ba sɛɛn a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ.

eng: One night the Lord said to Paul in a vision, Dont be afraid. Continue speaking. Dont be silent.
local: A tãsɔɔ ãsʊɔ, a sɩ Soro 'yɛr 'yɛr kʊ a Pɔɔl jãna pɔ ɩka, Taa joro dãbãɛ ɛ, mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir mʊɔlɔ a 'yɛr ta pɔɔ nɛɛ ɛ.

eng: Im with you and no one who attacks you will harm you, for I have many people in this city.
local: Bojũu, ɩn pʊɔ fʊ naa, nɩrɛ jaa kʊ̃ nyɔɔ fʊ bɩɩ maal fʊ dɛɛr ɛ, bojũu n chɛnɛ na tara nɩyɔɔ a tẽe nyã pɔ.

eng: So he stayed there for eighteen months, teaching Gods word among them.
local: Be lɛ a Pɔɔl jɛ̃ a be a Korɩntɩ yuon gbul nɩ bʊ̃ŋmaa na wʊ wile ba a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr.

eng: Now when Gallio was the governor of the province of Achaia, the Jews united in their opposition against Paul and brought him before the court.
local: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ a Galio ɩ a Akaya nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, ka a Juu nɩbɛ guor taa na ba nyɔɔ a Pɔɔl chiin be a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ jie.

eng: This man is persuading others to worship God unlawfully, they declared.
local: Be lɛ ba tɩ 'yɛr, A daba nyãna lɛ bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ lɩɛb a nɩbɛ tɩɛr ka ba puoro Nãaŋmɩn na ba tu a lɛ a sɩ nɛbinsɩ na 'yɛr ɛ.

eng: Just as Paul was about to speak, Gallio said to the Jews, If there had been some sort of injury or criminal behavior, I would have reason to accept your complaint.
local: Ka a Pɔɔl haa nɛɛ ɩka wʊ 'yɛr 'yɛr, tɩchɛ ka a Galio 'yɛr kʊ a Juu nɩbɛ, A yɩn, ala ta ɩ yeldɛɛr bɩɩ yelbier kɔ̃ɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ, ɩn taa 'maan ãgãn chɛlɛ.

eng: However, since these are squabbles about a message, names, and your own Law, deal with them yourselves. I have no desire to sit in judgment over such things.
local: Tɩchɛ an ben wa lɔ̃ɔ taa nɩ a yɩ 'yɛr bie nɩ yoe 'matãan a yɩ nɛbinãa yɩn mãɛ yɩ maal, maa kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ a 'yɛr nyãna tɔɔr ɛ.

eng: He expelled them from the court.
local: Lɛ lɛ wʊ vɛ̃ a ba dii ba bar be a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ jie.

eng: but everyone seized Sosthenes, the synagogue leader, and gave him a beating in the presence of the governor. None of this mattered to Gallio.
local: A ba lɩɛb nyɔɔ a Sosetenesɩ na ɩ a lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na ba ŋme be a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ yir dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ, tɩchɛ a Galio baka ba be be ɛ.

eng: After Paul stayed in Corinth for some time, he said good-bye to the brothers and sisters. At the Corinthian seaport of Cenchreae he had his head shaved, since he had made a solemn promise. Then, accompanied by Priscilla and Aquila, he sailed away to Syria.
local: Pɔɔl jɛ̃n be a Korɩntɩ tẽe kor bãlãa, tɩ pãa bar a yɛɛr na wʊ kpɛ a gboro chiine a Siriya ka a Pɩrɩsɩla nɩ a Akuila pɔɔ wʊ. Sɛrɛ tɩchɛ ka wʊ chen, wʊ vɛ̃n ka ba põ wʊ a ju be a Senkereya, bojũu wʊ dɔ̃ɔ põl 'yɔ̃ɔ a wʊ mãɛ.

eng: After they arrived in Ephesus, he left Priscilla and Aquila and entered the synagogue and interacted with the Jews.
local: Ba tãn be a Efesus tẽe pɔ, ka a Pɔɔl bar a Pɩrɩsɩla nɩ a Akuila tɩ na wʊ mãɛ chen be a lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie ka a 'lɔ naa Juu nɩbɛ tɩ mana taa.

eng: They asked him to stay longer, but he declined.
local: A ba sʊɔr wʊ ka wʊ jãnɛ a be bãlãa tɩ waa sɔɔ ɛ.

eng: As he said farewell to them, though, he added, God willing, I will return. Then he sailed off from Ephesus.
local: Tɩchɛ wʊn wa chiine wʊ tɩr nɛɛ kʊ ba, Ala ɩ Nãaŋmɩn wa sɔɔ naa, ɩn lɩɛb wa. Be lɛ wʊ kpɛ a gboro yi a be a Efesus tẽe pɔ.

eng: He arrived in Caesarea, went up to Jerusalem and greeted the church, and then went down to Antioch.
local: Wʊn tɩ ta a Sisariya wʊ don be a Jerusalɛm na wʊ tɩ puor a bala na puoro, tɩ pãa suu chen a Antiok.

eng: After some time there he left and traveled from place to place in the region of Galatia and the district of Phrygia, strengthening all the disciples.
local: Pɔɔl na tɩ jɛ̃ a be a Antiok kor bãlãa, wʊ pãa yin yʊɔrɔ a Galatiya paal 'lɔ wʊ jaa nɩ a Fɩrgɩya na wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a poturbo ãkpãɛn.

eng: Meanwhile, a certain Jew named Apollos arrived in Ephesus. He was a native of Alexandria and was well-educated and effective in his use of the scriptures.
local: Ka a Juu nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ yuor na di Apolosɩ yin be a Aleksandiriya na wʊ wa a Efesus tẽe pɔ. Wʊ ɩ naa nɩrɛ na jãn na wʊ bɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn gãma tɩ bɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr sʊ̃.

eng: He had been instructed in the way of the Lord and spoke as one stirred up by the Spirit. He taught accurately the things about Jesus, even though he was aware only of the baptism John proclaimed and practiced.
local: Apolosɩ nyã dɔ̃ɔ jãn a sɩ Soro sɔr defʊ, na wʊ 'yɛrɛ nɩ nɩbijɩɛ na wʊ wile nɩ Yesu yele ka a tor, tɩchɛ ka a ɩ Jɔɔn Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ sofʊ tɛɛ lɛ wʊ bɔ̃ɔ.

eng: He began speaking with confidence in the synagogue. When Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they received him into their circle of friends and explained to him Gods way more accurately.
local: Wʊ piel na wʊ 'yɛrɛ nɩ nyãa be a Juu nɩbɛ lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir. A Pɩrɩsɩla nɩ a Akuila na wa wõ a wʊ 'yɛr, ba bʊɔl wʊn be a ba yir na ba pãa wiil wʊ a Nãaŋmɩn sɔr jaa dẽ dẽ.

eng: When he wanted to travel to Achaia, the brothers and sisters encouraged him and wrote to the disciples so they would open their homes to him. Once he arrived, he was of great help to those who had come to believe through grace.
local: Apolosɩ wa bʊɔrɔ naa Akaya chenfʊ, ka a yɛɛr 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ ãkpɛ̃ɔ tɩ sɛb gãn kʊ a bala na puoro a be a ba de wʊ. Wʊn tɩ ta a be a ɩn sʊ̃ʊfʊ kpɛ̃ɛ kʊ a bala a Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ na vɛ̃ ka ba sɔɔ de a Yesu.

eng: He would vigorously defeat Jewish arguments in public debate, using the scriptures to prove that Jesus was the Christ.
local: Wʊ 'yɛr kpɛ̃ɔ jaa lɔb a Juu nɩbɛ chãa pɔ, na wʊ chɩrɛ ba yuoro nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn gãma wile ba ɩka a Yesu lɛ ɩ a Nɩ-iraa.

eng: After we tore ourselves away from them, we set sail on a straight course to Cos, reaching Rhodes the next day, and then Patara.
local: Sɩn wa bar taa baar, sɩ kpɛ̃n a gboro na sɩ tor a Kos a dẽdẽe a lɛ nɛ, a wɛr na wa chaar ka a ɩ sɩ tãn a Worodesɩ yi a be chen a Patara.

eng: Paul stared at the council and said, Brothers, I have lived my life with an altogether clear conscience right up to this very day.
local: Pɔɔl kãan a nɩbɛrɛ a lɛ a dẽdẽe tɩ 'yɛr, N yɛɛr, n tʊ̃n a lɛ na sɛɛ kʊ Nãaŋmɩn wa ta a dɩna tɩ n nyãa ba dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ɛ.

eng: We found a ship crossing over to Phoenicia, boarded, and put out to sea.
local: Sɩ nyɛn a gboro ãsʊɔ na chiine a Fonisiya ka sɩ kpɛ chiine.

eng: We spotted Cyprus, but passed by it on our left. We sailed on to the province of Syria and landed in Tyre, where the ship was to unload its cargo.
local: Sɩn wa nyɛrɛ a Sipurusɩ tẽe sɩ lan wʊn bar a guba jie tɩ ta a Siriya tẽe pɔ. Tire lɛ sɩ ta ara an ɩ be lɛ a gboro na wɛl a tuor wʊn tuo.

eng: We found the disciples there and stayed with them for a week. Compelled by the Spirit, they kept telling Paul not to go to Jerusalem.
local: Be lɛ sɩ nyɛ a poturbo bamɩne na sɩ jãnɛ a ba sɛ̃ ber ayopõi. Ka a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ tu a ba 'yɔ̃ɔ a ba 'yɛr kʊ a Pɔɔl ɩka wʊ taa chiine a Jerusalɛm ɛ.

eng: When our time had come to an end, we departed. All of them, including women and children, accompanied us out of town where we knelt on the beach and prayed.
local: Tɩchɛ a sɩ bɩbie na wa ta, sɩ yin de a sɩ sɔr. Ka a poturbo bala ba jaa nɩ a ba pɔbɔ nɩ a ba bibiir ba jaa bɩɛl sɩ yin a tẽe pɔ, tɩ tãn a man gogoro nɛɛ, ka sɩ gbur dumo sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: We said good-bye to each other, then we boarded the ship and they returned to their homes.
local: Sɩn wa bar taa baar, sɩ kpɛ̃n a gboro pɔ, tɩ ba mɩ lɩɛb kulo a yir.

eng: The high priest Ananias ordered those standing beside Paul to strike him in the mouth.
local: A anyãna wʊn 'yɛr ka a Ananias na ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ 'yɛr kʊ a bala na ara gbʊr a Pɔɔl ɩka ba 'ma wʊ a nɛɛ pɔ.

eng: Then Paul said to him, God is about to strike you, you whitewashed wall! You sit and judge me according to the Law, yet disobey the Law by ordering that I be struck.
local: Ka a Pɔɔl pãa 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Nãaŋmɩn na 'ma fʊ naa a fʊ̃ʊ na ɩ a tãpɩɛl dachin nyã. Fʊ jɛ̃n a be ɩka fʊ tɔɔr a n 'yɛr a lɛ a nɛɛ na bin tɩ na fʊ̃ʊ mãɛ lɛ sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a nɛɛ, na fʊ 'yɛr ɩka ba 'ma ma.

eng: Those standing near him asked, You dare to insult Gods high priest.
local: A bala na ara gbʊr a Pɔɔl 'yɛr kʊ, Aa! Bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ lɛ fʊ tʊrɔ ɔ.

eng: Paul replied, Brothers, I wasnt aware that he was the high priest. It is written, You will not speak evil about a ruler of your people .
