local: Ka a Pɔɔl 'yɛr, Yɛɛr n ba bɔ̃ɔ ɩka a bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ lɛ wʊ ɩ ɛ, bojũu a sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, Taa ɩrɛ wa 'yɛrɛ 'yɛrdɛɛr 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a fʊ nɩbɛ nɩdier ɛ. .

eng: Knowing that some of them were Sadducees and the others Pharisees, Paul exclaimed in the council, Brothers, Im a Pharisee and a descendant of Pharisees. I am on trial because of my hope in the resurrection of the dead.
local: Pɔɔl na wa bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a Saduseemɩne jɛr naa Faraseemɩne pɔ ka wʊ tãn kpɛ̃ɔ jaa be a nɩbɛrɛ pɔ na wʊ 'yɛr, N yɛɛr, Farasee lɛ n ɩ, ka a Farasee nɩrɛ dɔɔ ma. N ara naa kana a ban tɔɔr a n 'yɛr, bojũu n sɔɔ naa ɩka kũu pɔ irfʊ ben be.

eng: These words aroused a dispute between the Pharisees and Sadducees, and the assembly was divided.
local: A lɛ wʊn wa 'yɛr a nɩtɔ̃nɔ̃ ka a nɛchɩrɛ lo a Faraseemɩne nɩ a Saduseemɩne chara pɔ, ka a lɔ̃ɔfʊ ŋma jiir ayi.

eng: This is because Sadducees say that theres no resurrection, angel, or spirit, but Pharisees affirm them all.
local: Saduseemɩne 'yɛr ɩka kũu pɔ irfʊ ba be be ɛ, malakasɩ nɩ a nɩsɩɩr mɩ ba be be ɛ, tɩchɛ ka a Faraseemɩne 'lɔ sɔɔn ɩka a jaa ben be.

eng: Council members were shouting loudly. Some Pharisees who were legal experts stood up and insisted forcefully, We find nothing wrong with this man! What if a spirit or angel has spoken to him.
local: A gɔ̃mɔ̃ pɔɔfʊ tɩ gar naa ka a Mosesɩ nɛɛ wiwiilbe bamɩne na ɩ a Faraseemɩne ir ara tɩ chɩɩr kpɛ̃ɔ jaa ɩka, Sɩn 'lɔ ba nyɛ a daba nyã chɛfʊ jie ɛ, tɩ ala wa ɩ sɩɛ kɔ̃ɔ bɩɩ malaka lɛ 'yɛr 'yɛr kʊ wʊ.

eng: The dispute became so heated that the commander feared they might tear Paul to pieces. He ordered soldiers to go down and remove him by force from their midst. Then they took him back to the military headquarters.
local: A nɛchɩrɛ wa tɩ gar naa, ka a dãbãɛ kpɛ a sojasɩ ba jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ a wʊ man tãa ban wa tɔɔr a Pɔɔl 'mɛl, ala lɛ so ka wʊ vɛ̃ a sojasɩ suu tɩ faa a Pɔɔl don be a sojasɩ yir.

eng: The following night the Lord stood near Paul and said, Be encouraged! Just as you have testified about me in Jerusalem, so too you must testify in Rome.
local: A tãsɔɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃, a sɩ Soro wa ara na gbʊr a Pɔɔl tɩ 'yɛr, Nyɔɔ nyãa, a lɛ fʊn man a n yelmãɛ a ka a Jerusalɛm mɩ tɩ man a lɛ nɛ a be a Worom tẽe pɔ.

eng: Continuing our voyage, we sailed from Tyre and arrived in Ptolemais. We greeted the brothers and sisters there and spent a day with them.
local: A Tire sɩn be yi, a man pɔ lɛ sɩ chen bãlãa tɩ ta a Tolemaisɩ be lɛ sɩ puor a yɛɛr bamɩne na be be na sɩ gã a be bɩbir bʊ̃'yen.

eng: The next day we left and came to Caesarea. We went to the house of Philip the evangelist, one of the Seven, and stayed with him.
local: Sɩn ir a be chiine sɩ tãn be a Sisariya na sɩ chen a Filip na mʊɔlɔ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ na wʊ mɩ ɩ a nɩbɛ ayopõi bala ãsʊɔ ban dɔ̃ɔ ir be a Jerusalɛm yir.

eng: He had four unmarried daughters who were involved in the work of prophecy.
local: Wʊ dɔ̃ɔ tara pɔɔsarsɩ anaar na ba kul sɩrbɛ ɛ, ka Nãaŋmɩn ma kʊ ba a wʊ 'yɛr a ba 'yɛrɛ.

eng: The next morning some Jewish leaders formulated a plot and solemnly promised that they wouldnt eat or drink until they had killed Paul.
local: Wɛr na wa chaar bibio, a Juu nɩbɛ bamɩne jɛ̃n guor taa na ba pɔ'yɔ̃ɔ a ba mãɛ 'yɛrɛ ɩka ba kʊ̃ di bon jaa ɛ, bɩɩ nyũ bon jaa ɛ, ka bala baa kʊ a Pɔɔl ɛ.

eng: After staying there for several days, a prophet named Agabus came down from Judea.
local: Sɩ jɛ̃n a be kor bãlãa ka a Agabus yi be a Judiya na.

eng: More than forty people were involved in the conspiracy.
local: Ba gar dɔɔr lɩjaayi na guor a guorfʊ nyãna.

eng: They went to the chief priests and elders and said, We have solemnly promised to eat nothing until we have killed Paul.
local: Ba chen a bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ sɛ̃, na ba tɩ 'yɛr, Sɩ bin nɛɛ ɩka sɩ kʊ̃ di bon jaa ɛ, ka sɩn baa kʊ a Pɔɔl baar ɛ.

eng: He came to us, took Pauls belt, tied his own feet and hands, and said, This is what the Holy Spirit says: In Jerusalem the Jews will bind the man who owns this belt, and they will hand him over to the Gentiles.
local: wʊn wa a sɩ sɛ̃. Wʊ den a Pɔɔl sɩɛ gãn na wʊ lũn a wʊ mãɛ nuur nɩ a gbɛɛ tɩ na wʊ 'yɛr. A Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ 'yɛr naa, Nɩtɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ a Juu nɩbɛ na be a Jerusalɛm na lũ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na so a sɩɛ gãn nyãna, na ba 'yɔ̃ɔ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ nuur pɔ. .

eng: When we heard this, we and the local believers urged Paul not to go up to Jerusalem.
local: A lɛ sɩn wõ a nɩtɔ̃nɔ̃ ka a sɩn nɩ a nɩbɛ bala na be a be 'mataa sʊɔr a Pɔɔl ɩka wʊ taa chiine a Jerusalɛm ɛ.

eng: You and the council must explain to the commander that you need Paul brought down to you. Pretend that you want to examine his case more closely. Were prepared to kill him before he arrives.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã, yɩn nɩ a 'yɛr tɔɔrbɔ nɩbɛrɛ yɩ tʊ̃ 'yɛr a sojasɩ ba jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ a wʊ waan a Pɔɔl a yɩ sʊʊr yele kɔ̃ɔ bɔ̃ɔ a par. Sɩn na var gu be a sɔr pɔ kʊ wʊ ka waa ta a be a yɩ sɛ̃ ɛ.

eng: Pauls sister had a son who heard about the ambush and he came to the military headquarters and reported it to Paul.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Pɔɔl arbile wõ a guorfʊ nyã na wʊ chen be a sojasɩ yir na wʊ tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a Pɔɔl.

eng: Paul replied, Why are you doing this? Why are you weeping and breaking my heart? Im ready not only to be arrested but even to die in Jerusalem for the sake of the name of the Lord Jesus.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Pɔɔl 'yɛr, Bãa 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ yɩ kõnõ na yɩ chɩrɛ ma a ãgãn a lɛ? N sɔɔ naa, a ta ɩ a lũfu tɛɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ n kpi be a Jerusalɛm a sɩ Soro Yesu yuor jũu.

eng: Paul called for one of the centurions and said, Take this young man to the commander because he has something to report to him.
local: Ka a Pɔɔl bʊɔl a sojasɩ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ ãsʊɔ na wʊ 'yɛr, Tara a pɔlbile nyãna chiin a yɩ jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ sɛ̃ wʊ tara na 'yɛr na 'yɛr kʊ wʊ.

eng: Since we couldnt talk him out of it, the only thing we could say was, The Lords will be done.
local: A lɛ sɩn sʊɔr wʊ faa sɩ mɩ bar naa tɩ 'yɛr A sɩ Soro bɔfʊ a ɩ.

eng: After this, we got ready and made our way up to Jerusalem.
local: A anyã puor lɛ sɩ chɔɔr na sɩ do be a Jerusalɛm.

eng: Some of the disciples from Caesarea accompanied us and led us to Mnasons home, where we were guests. He was from Cyprus and had been a disciple a long time.
local: Ka a poturbo bala na be a Sisariya bɩɛl sɩ na ba waan sɩ a daba 'lɔ na di a Minason yir be lɛ sɩn wa kpɩɛrɛ, Sipurusɩ tẽe pɔ lɛ wʊ yi tɩ na wʊ ɩ a poturo kor.

eng: When we arrived in Jerusalem, the brothers and sisters welcomed us warmly.
local: A lɛ sɩn wa ta a Jerusalɛm, a yɛɛr de sɩn nuur ayi.

eng: On the next day Paul and the rest of us went to see James. All of the elders were present.
local: Ka a wɛr na wa chaar, sɩn nɩ a Pɔɔl jaa 'mantaa chen ɩka sɩ tɩ nyɛ a Jemesɩ ka a ɩ be lɛ a nɩbɛrɛ ba jaa lɔ̃ɔ taa.

eng: After greeting them, he gave them a detailed report of what God had done among the Gentiles through his ministry.
local: Pɔɔl na puor ba jaa baar, wʊ man naa yele a jaa kʊ ba, a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na de wʊ tʊ̃n be a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ pɔ.

eng: Those who heard this praised God. Then they said to him, Brother, you see how many thousands of Jews have become believers, and all of them keep the Law passionately.
local: A lɛ ban wõ anyãna ba jaa pɛɛn Nãaŋmɩn tɩ na ba pãa 'yɛr kʊ a Pɔɔl, Yɛbɛ faa nyɛ, a Juu nɩbɛ turo turo na sɔɔ de, tɩchɛ ba chɛnɛ na tara nɩbijɩɛ nɩ a nɛbinãa.

eng: He took him to the commander and said, The prisoner Paul asked me to bring this young man to you. He has something to tell you.
local: Lɛ lɛ wʊ tara a pɔlbile chiin a ba nɩkpɛ̃ɛ sɛ̃ na wʊ tɩ 'yɛr, A Pɔɔl na be a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ lɛ tʊ̃ bʊɔl ma na wʊ 'yɛr ɩka n tara a pɔlbile nyãna waan a fʊ sɛ̃, ka wʊ tara na 'yɛr na 'yɛr kʊ fʊ.

eng: The commander took him by the hand and withdrew to a place where they could speak privately. He asked, What do you have to report to me.
local: Ka a sojasɩ ba jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ nyɔɔ a pɔlbile nũu tɔɔ yin chɛ̃chɛ̃ɛ tɩ sʊʊr wʊ, Baa lɛ fʊ bʊɔrɔ ɩka fʊ 'yɛr kʊ ma.

eng: He replied, The Jewish leaders have conspired to ask that you bring Paul down to the council tomorrow. They will pretend that they want to investigate his case more closely.
local: A wʊ 'yɛr, A Juu nɩbɛ guor taa na ban sʊɔr fʊ ka fʊn waan a Pɔɔl a 'yɛr tɔɔrbɔ sɛ̃ a bio ka a kaara fɩka ba bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka ba sʊʊr wʊ yele kɔ̃ɔ bɔ̃ɔ a par.

eng: Dont fall for it! More than forty of them are waiting to ambush him. They have solemnly promised not to eat or drink until they have killed him. They are ready now, awaiting your consent.
local: Ta sɔɔ ɛ, bojũu ba gar nɩbɛ lɩjaayi na var gu wʊ, ba pɔn 'yɔ̃ɔ a ba mãɛ ɩka ba kʊ̃ di bon ɛ, bɩɩ nyũ bon jaa ɛ, ka bala ba kʊ wʊ ɛ. Bala ben chɔɔr baar a pʊ̃pãanyã tɩ chɛlɛ a fʊ nɛɛ.

eng: The commander dismissed the young man, ordering him, Dont tell anyone that you brought this to my attention.
local: Ka a nɩkpɛ̃ɛ bar a pɔlbile sɔr tɩ kpãa wʊ 'yɛr, Taa ɩrɛ wa vɛ̃ ka nɩrɛ jaa bɔ̃ɔn ɩka fʊ wan a n sɛ̃ ɛ.

eng: The commander called two centurions and said, Prepare two hundred soldiers, seventy horsemen, and two hundred spearmen to leave for Caesarea at nine oclock tonight.
local: Tɩ pãa bʊɔl a wʊ nɩbɛrɛ ayi na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ chɔɔr sojasɩ kɔɔr ayi na yɩ lɛ chɔɔr a sojasɩ lɩjaata nɩ pie na bɔ̃ɔ wiisɩ dofʊ, nɩ sojasɩ kɔɔr ayi mɩ na bɔ̃ɔ a pãɩ woor ala lɔbfʊ ka yɩ chen a Sisariya a tãsɔɔ nyãna.

eng: They have been informed that you teach all the Jews who live among the Gentiles to reject Moses, telling them not to circumcise their children nor to live according to our customs.
local: Ba man kʊ ba ɩka fʊ̃ʊ lɛ wile a Juu nɩbɛ bala na kpɩɛrɛ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ pɔ, ka ba taa turo a Mosesɩ ɛ, na fʊ lɛ wile ɩka ba taa ŋmara a ba bibiir yɔɩ ɛ, na ba taa ɩrɛ a lɛ a Juu nɩbɛ na ma ɩrɛ ɛ.

eng: Have horses ready for Paul to ride, so they may take him safely to Governor Felix.
local: Yɩ bɔ a wii ãsʊɔ kʊ a Pɔɔl a wʊ do jãnɛ a yɩ chiin wʊ vɩla tɩ kʊ a paal nɩkpɛ̃ɛ Feliksɩ.

eng: What about this? Without a doubt, they will hear that you have arrived.
local: Ban wõn ɩka fʊ wan a ka, ŋmɩnɛ sɩn ɩ?.

eng: He wrote the following letter.
local: A gãn wʊn sɛb 'lɔ nɩ nyã.

eng: You must therefore do what we tell you. Four men among us have made a solemn promise.
local: Fʊ̃ʊ bʊɔrɔ fʊ tu a lɛ sɩn na 'yɛr. Dɔɔr anaar ben ka a sɩ sɛ̃ na ba põl 'yɔ̃ɔ a ba mãɛ.

eng: Claudius Lysias, to the most honorable Governor Felix: Greetings.
local: Maa Kɩlaudiyus Lisiyasɩ lɛ sɛɛrɛ a gãn nyãna kʊrɔ a fʊ̃ʊ Feliksɩ a paal nɩkpɛ̃ɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ naa, n puor fʊ naa.

eng: Take them with you, go through the purification ritual with them, and pay the cost of having their heads shaved. Everyone will know there is nothing to those reports about you but that you too live a life in keeping with the Law.
local: De a nɩbɛ banyãna na fʊ pʊɔ ba, a yɩ tɩ pɔl a fʊ yab a ba jur põfʊ sãma. Ka nɩrɛ jaa na pãa bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a lɛ ban man 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ ba ɩ yelmãɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ a fʊ̃ʊ mãɛ mɩ turo naa a nɛbinãa.

eng: This man was seized by the Jews and was almost killed by them. I was nearby with a unit of soldiers, and I rescued him when I discovered that he was a Roman citizen.
local: A daba nyãna lɛ a Juu nɩbɛ nyɔɔ na ba ta ɩka ba kʊ, tɩchɛ ɩn wa bɔ̃ɔn ɩka Worom nɩrɛ lɛ wʊ ɩ, n chiin naa a n sojasɩ tɩ faan wʊ.

eng: As for the Gentile believers, we wrote a letter about what we decided, that they avoid food offered to idols, blood, the meat from strangled animals, and sexual immorality.
local: Tɩchɛ a nɩyor bala na sɔɔ de, sɩ sɛb gãn kʊ ba a lɛ sɩn 'yɛr ɩka, ba taa dire tɩbɛ bomo ɛ, bɩɩ ɔɔrɔ jãɩ ɛ, bɩɩ 'wɔb dʊɔ na var kpi ɛ, na ba taa sɛ̃nɛ ɛ.

eng: I wanted to find out why they were accusing him, so I brought him to their council.
local: N ta bʊɔrɔ na ɩka n bɔ̃ɔ a yele 'lɔ jũu ban ba bara wʊ ɛ, naa de wʊ chiin be a 'yɛr tɔɔrbɔ nɩbɛrɛ sɛ̃.

eng: The following day Paul took the men with him and went through the purification ritual with them. He entered the temple and publicly announced the completion of the days of purification, when the offering would be presented for each one of them.
local: Wɛr na wa chaar, Pɔɔl den a dɔɔr anaar bala ka ba 'mataa tɩ pɔl a ba mãɛ. Tɩ na wʊ pãa chen be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir na tɩ 'yɛr a bɩbie ala a ba pɔlfʊ na baar nɩ a bɔɔrlo bomo ala a ba ãsʊɔ jaa na ir.

eng: I discovered that they were accusing him about questions related to their Law. I found no charge deserving of death or imprisonment.
local: Naa tɩ nyɛ ka ɩ a yele ɩ naa a ba nɛbinãa yele lɛ. Tɩchɛ saa lɛ nyɛ yele jaa wʊn maal na wʊ sɛɛn kũu bɩɩ die pɔ pɔɔfʊ ɛ.

eng: When the seven days of purification were almost over, the Jews from the province of Asia saw Paul in the temple. Grabbing him, they threw the whole crowd into confusion by shouting.
local: A lɛ a ber ayopõi na wa gbʊrɔ a baarfʊ, a Juu nɩbɛ bamɩne na yi a Asɩya paal pɔ nyɛn a Pɔɔl a be Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir, na ba dam a nɩyɔɔ bala a ba nyɔɔ wʊ.

eng: When I was informed of a conspiracy against his life, I sent him to you at once and ordered his accusers to bring their case against him before you.
local: Lɛ ban wa man kʊ ma ban guoro a daba nyã yele, ala lɛ so ka n vɛ̃ ka ba tara wʊ a fɔɔ a lɛ nɛ waan a fʊ sɛ̃. N mɩ 'yɛr kʊ a bala na tuoro wʊ a yele ɩka ba wa a fʊ sɛ̃ wa 'yɛr a fʊ mɩ kaa.

eng: Fellow Israelites! Help! This is the man who teaches everyone everywhere against our people, the Law, and this place. Not only that, he has even brought Greeks into the temple and defiled this holy place. .
local: Ba gboolo naa, Isɩral nɩbɛ woɩ, yɩ wa sʊ̃ʊ sɩ yooo. A daba lɛ anyãna na wile a nɩbɛ jie jaa ka ba kpɛrɛ a sɩ nɩbɛ nɩ a nɛbinãa lɔ̃ɔn a jie nyãna. Lɛ tɛɛ gba ba lɛ ɛ, wʊ tara na a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, kpɛ̃n ka a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir na wʊ sɔ̃ɔ a jivɩla nyãna.

eng: Following their orders, the soldiers took Paul during the night and brought him to Antipatris.
local: A sojasɩ tun a lɛ a ba nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na 'yɛr na ba de a Pɔɔl a tãsɔɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ jaa na ba tɩ tãn Antɩpatɩrsɩ tẽe pɔ.

eng: They said this because they had seen Trophimus the Ephesian in the city with him earlier, and they assumed Paul had brought him into the temple.
local: Ba dɔ̃ɔ nyɛ a Torofimusɩ na yi a Efesus nɩ a Pɔɔl a ba tɩɛr man tãa a Pɔɔl tara wʊn kpɛ̃n be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir.

eng: The following day they let the horsemen continue on with Paul while they returned to the military headquarters in Jerusalem.
local: Wɛr na wa chaar, ba vɛ̃n ka a wiir dẽme tara a Pɔɔl chiine, tɩchɛ ka a gbɛɛ dẽme lɩɛb wa a sojasɩ yir.

eng: The entire city was stirred up. The people came rushing, seized Paul, and dragged him out of the temple. Immediately the gates were closed.
local: A tẽdaa wʊ jaa kab, ka a nɩbɛ yire a tẽe wʊ jaa joro waara na ba wa nyɔɔ a Pɔɔl. Ba nyɔɔ wʊn a lɛ tɔɔ yin a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir jie tɩ pɔɔ a pãma ajienaa.

eng: The horsemen entered Caesarea, delivered the letter to the governor, and brought Paul before him.
local: Lɛ ban tɩ ta a Sisariya tẽe pɔ ba den a gãn na ba kʊ a nɩkpɛ̃ɛ tɩ de a Pɔɔl mɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ a wʊ nũu pɔ.

eng: While they were trying to kill him, a report reached the commander of a company of soldiers that all Jerusalem was in a state of confusion.
local: Ka ba ta bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba kʊ a Pɔɔl ka a yele tɩ ta a Worom sojasɩ ba jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ ɩka a Jerusalɛm wʊ jaa dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ naa.

eng: After he read the letter, he asked Paul about his home province. When he learned that he was from Cilicia.
local: Ka a paal nɩkpɛ̃ɛ kar a gãn tɩ na wʊ sʊʊr a Pɔɔl a tẽbuor wʊn yi, wʊn wa bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a Sɩlɩsɩya lɛ wʊ yi.

eng: Without a moments hesitation, he took some soldiers and officers and ran down to the mob. When the mob saw the commander and his soldiers, they stopped beating Paul.
local: A wʊ vaa ir a lɛ na wʊ tara a nɩbɛrɛ bamɩne nɩ a sojasɩ na wʊ suu chiin be a nɩyɔɔ bala pɔ. Lɛ ban wa nyɛ a nɩkpɛ̃ɛ nɩ a wʊ sojasɩ a ba bar a Pɔɔl ŋmefʊ.

eng: the governor said, I will hear your case when your accusers arrive. Then he ordered that Paul be kept in custody in Herods palace.
local: Ka a nɩkpɛ̃ɛ 'yɛr, A bala na tuo fʊ a yele wa ta ala lɛ ɩn wõ a fʊ 'yɛr. Wʊ vɛ̃n ka ba tara a Pɔɔl kpɛ̃n be a yir kpɛ̃ɛ 'lɔ pɔ a Nãa Hɛrɔd na dɔ̃ɔ mɛ tɩ guro wʊ.

eng: When the commander arrived, he arrested Paul and ordered him to be bound with two chains. Only then did he begin to ask who Paul was and what he had done.
local: Lɛ a sojasɩ ba jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na wa ta wʊ tɩ nyɔɔn a Pɔɔl na wʊ vɛ̃ ka ba gbãr wʊ nɩ bɔ̃jɔrsɩ ayi. Tɩ pãa sʊʊr ɩka nɩ ana lɛ, nɩ bo lɛ wʊ maal.

eng: Some in the crowd shouted one thing, others shouted something else. Because of the commotion, he couldnt learn the truth, so he ordered that Paul be taken to the military headquarters.
local: Bamɩne na be a nɩyɔɔ bala pɔ 'yɛrɛ na turo nɩ jiyuo ka bamɩne mɩ 'yɛrɛ turo nɩ jiyuo a sojasɩ ba jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na ba nyɛ a yelmãɛ ɛ, a gɔ̃mɔ̃ jũu, wʊ vɛ̃n ka ba tara a Pɔɔl gʊɔr be a sojasɩ kpɛfʊ jie.

eng: When Paul reached the steps, he had to be carried by the soldiers in order to protect him from the violence of the crowd.
local: Lɛ a Pɔɔl na wa ta a dofʊ jie a nɩbɛ pir pirfʊ jaa waa naa ka a sojasɩ 'mɔɔ a Pɔɔl.

eng: The mob that followed kept screaming, Away with him.
local: Ka a nɩyɔɔ bala na turo tãna 'yɛrɛ, Yɩ kʊ wʊ.

eng: As Paul was about to be taken into the military headquarters, he asked the commander, May I speak with you? He answered, Do you know Greek?.
local: Lɛ a sojasɩ na wa ɩka ba kpɛ̃n a Pɔɔl a be a ba kpɛfʊ jie wʊ sʊɔr naa a sojasɩ ba jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ ɩka, N 'yɛr 'yɛr kɔ̃ɔ kʊ fʊ bɩɩ? Ka a nɩkpɛ̃ɛ sʊʊr wʊ, Fʊn tʊ̃ɔn 'yɛr a Greek kɔkɔr bɩɩ?.

eng: Five days later the high priest Ananias came down with some elders and a lawyer named Tertullus. They pressed charges against Paul before the governor.
local: A ber anũu puor lɛ ka a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ Ananias suun chen be a Sisariya nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ bamɩne, ka a Tɛtulusɩ na ɩ a 'yɛr tɔɔrɔ pʊɔ ba a ba 'mataa chen na tɩ 'yɛr a Pɔɔl 'yɛr a nɩkpɛ̃ɛ niem.

eng: Arent you the Egyptian who started a revolt and led four thousand terrorists into the desert some time ago.
local: A ba ɩ fʊ̃ʊ lɛ a Ijipitɩ nɩrɛ 'lɔ na dɔ̃ɔ ŋmaa a jɛɛr na fʊ tɔɔ dɔɔr turo anaar kpɛ̃n be a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ ɛ.

eng: After the governor summoned Paul, Tertullus began to make his case against him. He declared, Under your leadership, we have experienced substantial peace, and your administration has brought reforms to our nation.
local: Ban bʊɔl a Pɔɔl kpɛ̃n ka a Tɛtulusɩ na man a wʊ yele kʊ a Feliksɩ na wʊ 'yɛrɛ, Sɩ din nʊ̃ɔ kor a fʊ jũu, ka a fʊ yɛ̃ lɩɛr yelvɩɛlsɩ kʊ a fʊ paal.

eng: Always and everywhere, most honorable Felix, we acknowledge this with deep gratitude.
local: Jie jaa nɩ sɔr jaa pɔ fʊ ɩn nɩkpɛ̃vɩla, a sɩ sɔɔ de fʊ nɩ nuur ayi nɩ pʊpɛl.

eng: I dont want to take too much of your time, so I ask that you listen with your usual courtesy to our brief statement of the facts.
local: Tɩchɛ n ba bʊɔrɔ ɩka n dam fʊ yɔɔ ɛ, yelbʊ̃'yen tɛɛ lɛ n bʊɔrɔ ɩka n sʊɔr jaa bãlãa ka fʊ di kãnyir kʊ sɩ.

eng: Paul replied, Im a Jew from Tarsus in Cilicia, a citizen of an important city. Please, let me speak to the people.
local: Ka a Pɔɔl 'yɛr, N ɩ naa Juu nɩrɛ naa yi a Tasusɩ na be a Sɩlɩsɩya paal pɔ, naa yi a tẽe nyãna pɔ nɩrɛ jaa na bɔ̃ɔn, yãayãabɩlɛ sɔɔ ka n 'yɛr 'yɛr kʊ a nɩbɛ.

eng: With the commanders permission, Paul stood on the steps and gestured to the people. When they were quiet, he addressed them in Aramaic.
local: A sojasɩ ba jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ kʊn a Pɔɔl sɔr, ka a wʊ ir ara a dofʊ jie, na wʊ wɩlwɩl a nɩyɔɔ bala a nuur ka ba bar gɔ̃mɔ̃. A lɛ ban wa ŋmaa a vʊʊr wʊ den a ba Juu nɩbɛ kɔkɔr na 'yɛrɛ nɩ.

eng: We have found this man to be a troublemaker who stirs up riots among all the Jews throughout the empire. Hes a ringleader of the Nazarene faction.
local: Sɩ nyɛn a daba nyãna ka wʊ ɩ gɔ̃bʊɔrɔ, a Juu nɩbɛ ba jaa lɛ wʊ ma tɔ a ba jur gbõtaa a wɛr wʊ jaa, na wʊ ɩ a nɩbɛ bala na turo a Najaretɩ nɩrɛ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ nɩdiere.

eng: and even tried to defile the temple. Thats when we arrested him.
local: Na wʊ bʊɔrɔ a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir sɔ̃ɔ barfʊ, ala lɛ so ka sɩ nyɔɔ wʊ.

eng: By examining him yourself, you will be able to verify the allegations we are bringing against him.
local: Fʊ̃ʊ mãɛ fʊ sʊʊr wʊ kaa fʊn nyɛn bɔ̃ɔ ɩka a ala sɩn 'yɛrɛ dɔɔlɔ wʊ ɩn yelmãɛ.

eng: The Jews reinforced the action against Paul, affirming the truth of these accusations.
local: Ka a Juu nɩbɛ ba jaa na be be 'yɛr ɩka a lɛ a Tɛtulusɩ na 'yɛr ɩn yelmãɛ.

eng: The governor nodded at Paul, giving him permission to speak. He responded, I know that you have been judge over this nation for many years, so I gladly offer my own defense.
local: A paal nɩkpɛ̃ɛ kʊn a Pɔɔl sɔr a wʊ 'yɛr, ka a Pɔɔl 'yɛr, N bɔ̃ɔn naa an ɩ yuom yɔɔ lɛ fʊ kaa a paal nyãna, a nʊ̃mɔ̃ ma naa ka n na 'yɛr a n 'yɛr.

eng: You can verify that I went up to worship in Jerusalem no more than twelve days ago.
local: Fʊ̃ʊ sʊʊr ban 'yɛr ɩka a ba gar ber pie nɩ ayi ɩn chen be a Jerusalɛm ɩka n tɩ puor Nãaŋmɩn ɛ.

eng: They didnt find me arguing with anyone in the temple or stirring up a crowd, whether in the synagogue or anywhere else in the city.
local: Ba ãsʊɔ jaa ba nyɛ ma ka n chɩrɛ nɩ nɩrɛ jaa be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir ɛ, bɩɩ dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ nɩyɔɔ be a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ jiir ɛ, bɩɩ a tẽe pɔ jie jaa ɛ.

eng: Nor can they prove to you the allegations they are now bringing against me.
local: A anyãna ban 'yɛrɛ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ma ãsʊɔ jaa kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ wiil ma a yelmãɛ ɛ.

eng: I do admit this to you, that I am a follower of the Way, which they call a faction. Accordingly, I worship the God of our ancestors and believe everything set out in the Law and written in the Prophets.
local: Tɩchɛ n sɔɔ naa ɩka n puoro naa a Nãaŋmɩn 'lɔ a sɩ sãakpãmɩne na dɔ̃ɔ puoro naa lɛ turo a Sɔr 'lɔ ban bʊɔlɔ a sɔr yuo. N sɔɔn de a ala a jaa, na a sɔɔ lɛ a nɛɛ na sɔɔ nɩ a ala a jaa a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ na dɔ̃ɔ sɛb.

eng: The hope I have in God I also share with my accusers, that there will be a resurrection of both the righteous and the unrighteous.
local: N mɩ tara naa a tɩɛrtɔ bʊ̃'yen 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ nɩtãa a ba kaar ɩka nɩsʊ̃n nɩ a putudẽme na ir ɩ vʊɛ.

eng: On account of this, I have committed myself to maintaining a clear conscience before God and with all people.
local: Ala lɛ so ka n mʊɔrɔ sɔ̃ɔ jaa ɩka a n pʊtɩɛr wʊ chaar Nãaŋmɩn nɩ a nɩrɛ niem.

eng: After an absence of several years, I came to Jerusalem to bring gifts for the poor of my nation and to offer sacrifices.
local: N yin kor, tɩ naa lɛ lɩɛb wa a Jerusalɛm nɩ libie ɩka n wa kʊ a nɔ̃n dẽme tɩ naa mɩ lo bɔɔr kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: When they found me in the temple, I was ritually pure. There was no crowd and no disturbance.
local: N dɔ̃ɔ pɔl a n mãɛ baar a lɛ ban nyɛ ma a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir bɛchaar pɔ. Nɩyɔɔ ba dɔ̃ɔ pʊɔ ma ɛ, n mɩ ba dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ damfʊ jaa ɛ.

eng: But there were some Jews from the province of Asia. They should be here making their accusations, if indeed they have something against me.
local: Tɩchɛ a Juu nɩbɛ bamɩne na yi a Asɩya paal pɔ wa, bala mɩ lɛ a ta sɛɛ ɩka ba be ka na ba 'yɛr a n 'yɛr.

eng: In their absence, have these people who are here declare what crime they found when I stood before the Jerusalem Council.
local: Bɩɩ a banyãna na be ka a ba wiil a yelnyɩnɛ ban nyɛ ɩn maal, a lɛ ɩn ara a 'yɛr tɔɔrbɔ niem.

eng: Perhaps it concerns this one statement that I blurted out when I was with them: I am on trial before you today because of the resurrection of the dead.
local: Ala ba ɩ a 'yɛr bir bʊ̃'yen nyãna tɛɛ ɩn 'yɛr kpɛ̃ɔ jaa a lɛ ɩn ara a ba niem naa 'yɛr, A kũn na ir ɩ vʊɛ, naa be ara a yɩ niem a yɩn tɔɔr a n 'yɛr a dɩna. .

eng: Felix, who had an accurate understanding of the Way, adjourned the meeting. He said, When Lysias the commander arrives from Jerusalem, I will decide this case.
local: A Feliksɩ na wa pãa bɔ̃ɔn a Sɔr yele wʊ wɛl a nɩbɛ bar tɩ 'yɛr, A Lisiyasɩ na ɩ a sojasɩ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ wa wa ala lɛ ɩn 'yɛr a fʊ 'yɛr.

eng: He arranged for a centurion to guard Paul. He was to give Paul some freedom, and his friends were not to be hindered in their efforts to provide for him.
local: Wʊ den a Pɔɔl kʊ a sojasɩ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ tɩ 'yɛr ɩka a wʊ ma kʊ wʊ sɔr tɩ sɔɔ ka a Pɔɔl barmɩne ma sʊ̃nɔ̃ wʊ nɩ a wʊ bʊ̃bʊɔrsɩ.

eng: After several days, Felix came with his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, and summoned Paul. He listened to him talk about faith in Christ Jesus.
local: A maal ber aŋmɩnɛ jaa tɩchɛ ka a Feliksɩ tara a wʊ pɔɔ Durusila na ɩ a Juu nɩrɛ waan. Wʊ tʊ̃n ka ba waan a Pɔɔl a wʊ chɛlɛ a lɛ wʊn mana a lɛ fʊn na sɔɔ de a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto.

eng: When he spoke about upright behavior, self-control, and the coming judgment, Felix became fearful and said, Go away for now! When I have time, Ill send for you.
local: Pɔɔl 'yɛr naa a lɛ nɛ wa pʊr yeltorsɩ yuor nɩ mãɛ nyɔɔfʊ nɩ lɛ a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ na waara. Ka a dãbãɛ kpɛ a Feliksɩ a wʊ 'yɛr, Pʊ̃pãanyã a sɛɛn lɛ, chiine maa wa tara jaa ɩn tʊ̃n bʊɔl fʊ.

eng: At the same time, he was hoping that Paul would offer him some money, so he often sent for him and talked with him.
local: Wʊ tɩɛr man taa Pɔɔl na kʊ wʊn libie, na wʊ ma bʊɔlɔ a Pɔɔl jaa tɔ̃tɔ̃ ma 'yɛrɛ nɩ wʊ.

eng: When two years had passed, Felix was succeeded by Porcius Festus. Since Felix wanted to grant a favor to the Jews, he left Paul in prison.
local: Yuom ayi na wa po Pɔsɩyusɩ Festus den a Feliksɩ jie, tɩchɛ a Feliksɩ na bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ pɛl a Juu nɩbɛ pʊɔ jũu, wʊ bar a Pɔɔl a be a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ.

eng: Brothers and fathers, listen now to my defense.
local: N yɛɛr nɩ n sãamɩne yɩ chɛlɛ a n 'yɛr ɩn 'yɛrɛ a pʊ̃pãanyã.

eng: When they heard him address them in Aramaic, they became even more quiet.
local: A lɛ ban wa wõ wʊn 'yɛrɛ nɩ a ba mãɛ Juu nɩbɛ kɔkɔr ka ba jaa ŋma vʊrɔ, tɩ a Pɔɔl pãa 'yɛrɛ.

eng: Paul continued, Im a Jew, born in Tarsus in Cilicia but raised in this city. Under Gamaliels instruction, I was trained in the strict interpretation of our ancestral Law. I am passionately loyal to God, just like you who are gathered here today.
local: Maa ɩn Juu nɩrɛ ka ba dɔɔ ma a Tasusɩ na be a Sɩlɩsɩya paal pɔ, tɩchɛ a kana a Jerusalɛm lɛ ba guol ma. A wiwile Gamaliel pĩlem lɛ n jãnɛ ka wʊ wiil ma a nɛɛr ala a sɩ sãakpãmɩne na dɔ̃ɔ turo ka n mɩ turo a Nãaŋmɩn nɩ a n nyãa wʊ jaa, nɩtãa a yɩ ãsʊɔ jaa na be turo a dɩna.

eng: I harassed those who followed this Way to their death, arresting and delivering both men and women into prison.
local: N gen a nɩbɛ bala na turo a Sɔr nyãna naa kʊ, n nyɔɔr dɔɔr nɩ pɔbɔ jaa 'yɔ̃ɔ a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ.

eng: The high priest and the whole Jerusalem Council can testify about me. I received letters from them, addressed to our associates in Damascus, then went there to bring those who were arrested to Jerusalem so they could be punished.
local: A bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ na tʊ̃ɔn di ma dãasɩɛ, ba sɛ̃ lɛ n de gãma na chiin a ba yɛɛr sɛ̃ be a Damaskus, n chen ɩka n tɩ nyɔɔr a nɩbɛ banyãna waan a Jerusalɛm ka ba kpãa ba.

eng: During that journey, about noon, as I approached Damascus, suddenly a bright light from heaven encircled me.
local: Mɔ̃tɔ̃tuo sɔ̃ɔ lɛ n chiine tɩ gbʊrɔ a Damaskus, ajienaa ka ɩ chãa yin be a saaju na wʊ wa chaar jilʊ̃ ma.

eng: I fell to the ground and heard a voice asking me, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me.
local: Ka a n tɔ̃ɔ lo a tẽe a lɛ nɛ tɩ naa wõ kɔkɔr na sʊrɔ ma, Sɔɔl, Sɔɔl, bo lɛ fʊ gere ma a lɛ.

eng: I answered, Who are you, Lord? I am Jesus the Nazarene, whom you are harassing, he replied.
local: Ka n sʊʊr, Soro, an lɛ fʊ ɩ? Ka a kɔkɔr 'yɛr, Maa lɛ a Yesu na yi a Najaretɩ a 'lɔ fʊn gere.

eng: My traveling companions saw the light, but they didnt hear the voice of the one who spoke to me.
local: A maa nɩ a bala na lɔ̃ɔ chiine ba nyɛn a chãa, tɩchɛ baa bɔ̃ɔ a nɩnyɩnɛ kɔkɔr lɛ wʊn 'yɛrɛ nɩ ma par ɛ.

eng: I asked, What should I do, Lord? Get up, the Lord replied, and go into Damascus. There you will be told everything you have been appointed to do.
local: Ka n sʊʊr, Soro, ŋmɩnɛ lɛ ɩn ɩ? Ka a Soro 'yɛr, Ir na fʊ kpɛ be a Damaskus tẽe pɔ, be lɛ ban tɩ man fʊ a lɛ ɩn bʊɔrɔ ɩka fʊ ɩ.

eng: I couldnt see because of the brightness of that light, so my companions led me by the hand into Damascus.
local: Ka n lɔ̃ɔturbo bala nyɔɔ ma a nũu tɔɔ kpɛ̃n be a Damaskus tẽe pɔ, bojũu a chãa 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ na chaar 'yɔ̃ɔ ma a lɛ nɛ n ba lɛ nyɛrɛ ɛ.

eng: There was a certain man named Ananias. According to the standards of the Law, he was a pious man who enjoyed the respect of all the Jews living there.
local: Daba kɔ̃ɔ yuor na di Ananias wan ɩka wʊ wa nyɛ ma, wʊ ɩn Nãaŋmɩn puoro na wʊ turo a nɛbinãa ka a Juu nɩbɛ ba jaa na kpɩɛrɛ a be 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ wʊ.

eng: He came and stood beside me. Brother Saul, receive your sight! he said. Instantly, I regained my sight and I could see him.
local: Wʊ wa ara na gbɛb ma tɩ 'yɛr, N yɛbɛ Sɔɔl, lɛ nyɛrɛ, ka a ɩ ajienaa ka a ɩ n nyɛ wʊ naa.

eng: He said, The God of our ancestors has selected you to know his will, to see the righteous one, and to hear his voice.
local: Be lɛ wʊ pãa 'yɛr kʊ ma, A sɩ sãakpãmɩne Nãaŋmɩn lɛ ir fʊ ɩka fʊ bɔ̃ɔ a lɛ wʊn bʊɔrɔ, na fʊ lɛ nyɛ a chɛchɛ sʊɔ 'lɔ, na fʊ wõ a 'yɛr bie yi a wʊ nɛɛ pɔ.

eng: You will be his witness to everyone concerning what you have seen and heard.
local: Fʊ̃ʊ lɛ na ɩ a dãasɩɛ sʊɔ na fʊ kʊ a nɩbɛ ba jaa, a ala fʊn nyɛ, nɩ a ala fʊn wõ.

eng: What are you waiting for? Get up, be baptized, and wash away your sins as you call on his name.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã, bo lɛ fʊ lɛ chɛlɛ go? Ir a ba so fʊ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ, ka a na pɛɛ a fʊ yeldɛbɛ bar a fʊ bʊɔlɔ a sɩ Soro yuor.

eng: When I returned to Jerusalem and was praying in the temple, I had a visionary experience.
local: A lɛ ɩn lɩɛb wa a Jerusalɛm naa tɩ sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn be a puorfʊ yir, ka a wa kaara ma jãna kaar.

eng: I saw the Lord speaking to me. Hurry! he said. Leave Jerusalem at once because they wont accept your testimony about me.
local: Ka n nyɛ a sɩ Soro na 'yɛrɛ 'yɛr kʊrɔ ma. Wʊ 'yɛr naa, Pɔɔ fɔɔ na fʊ yi ka a Jerusalɛm ajienaa, bojũu nɩrɛ jaa kʊ̃ sɔɔ a lɛ fʊn na 'yɛr a n 'yɛr ɛ.

eng: I responded, Lord, these people know I used to go from one synagogue to the next, beating those who believe in you and throwing them into prison.
local: Ka n 'yɛr sɔɔ wʊ, N Soro a nɩbɛ banyãna bɔ̃ɔn naa a lɛ ɩn yʊɔrɔ a lɔ̃ɔfʊ jiir naa nyɔɔrɔ ba pɔɔrɔ tɩ ŋmiere a bala na sɔɔ de fʊ.

eng: When Stephen your witness was being killed, I stood there giving my approval, even watching the clothes that belonged to those who were killing him.
local: A daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ ban kʊrɔ a fʊ dãasɩ dire Sɩteven maa nɩ bala jaa ɩn nɛbʊ̃'yen ka n kaara a ba fuusɩ tɩ ba kʊ wʊ.

eng: Then the Lord said to me, Go! I will send you far away to the Gentiles.
local: Ka a sɩ Soro pãa 'yɛr kʊ ma, Chen, ɩn tʊ̃ fʊn tɛɛr jaa be a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ pɔ. .

eng: The crowd listened to Paul until he said this. Then they shouted, Away with this man! Hes not fit to live.
local: A nɩyɔɔ bala chɛlɛ naa lɛ a Pɔɔl na 'yɛrɛ wa tãn a jie wʊn 'yɛr a nɩyor pɔ chenfʊ 'yɛr, a ba pãa tãna 'yɛrɛ, Yɩ ŋmaa wʊ ju, a ba sɛɛ ɩka wʊ lɛ chɛnɛ vʊrɔ ɛ.

eng: As they were screaming, throwing off their garments, and flinging dust into the air.
local: Lɛ lɛ ba pɔɔrɔ a gɔ̃mɔ̃ tɩ lɔɔrɔ a ba fɔɔr ɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a tãna lɔɔrɔ a saaju.

eng: the commander directed that Paul be taken into the military headquarters. He ordered that Paul be questioned under the whip so that he could find out why they were shouting at him like this.
local: Ka a sojasɩ ba jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ vɛ̃ ka ba tara a Pɔɔl kpɛ̃n be a sojasɩ yir. Wʊ 'yɛr ɩka ba fɔb a Pɔɔl a wʊ wiil ba bãa lɛ so a nɩbɛ pɔɔrɔ a gɔ̃mɔ̃ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ wʊ a lɛ.

eng: As they were stretching him out and tying him down with straps, Paul said to the centurion standing there, Can you legally whip a Roman citizen who hasnt been found guilty in court.
local: A lɛ ban lũ a Pɔɔl ɩka ba fɔb, ka wʊ sʊʊr a sojasɩ nɩdier na ara a be, A tara na sɔr ka yɩn fɔb a Worom nɩrɛ na yaa sʊʊr kaa a yelnyɩnɛ wʊn ɩ ɛ.

eng: When the centurion heard this, he went to the commander and reported it. He asked, What are you about to do? This man is a Roman citizen.
local: Lɛ a sojasɩ nɩdier na wa wõ a nɩtɔ̃ wʊ chen a ba jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ sɛ̃ tɩ 'yɛr, Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ fʊn ɩ? A daba nyã ɩn Worom nɩrɛ.

eng: The commander went to Paul and demanded, Tell me! Are you a Roman citizen? He said, Yes.
local: Ka a sojasɩ ba jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ chen a Pɔɔl sɛ̃ tɩ sʊʊr wʊ, 'Yɛr kʊ ma, Worom nɩrɛ lɛ fʊ ɩ ɔ? Ka a Pɔɔl 'yɛr ɩka, Ɔ̃ɔ.

eng: The commander replied, It cost me a lot of money to buy my citizenship. Paul said, Im a citizen by birth.
local: A sojasɩ ba jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ pãa 'yɛr, Maa 'lɔ, libiyɔɔ lɛ n yab tɩ jãnɛ a ka. Tɩchɛ ka a Pɔɔl 'yɛr, Maa 'lɔ, ka lɛ ba dɔɔ ma.

eng: At once those who were about to examine him stepped away. The commander was alarmed when he realized he had bound a Roman citizen.
local: Ka a bala na ara gu a wʊ fɔbfʊ mɩ tɔɔ yaar ajienaa. Dãbãɛ mɩ kpɛ̃n a sojasɩ ba jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ a lɛ wʊn wa bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a Pɔɔl ɩn Worom nɩrɛ tɩchɛ ka wʊ vɛ̃ a ba lũ wʊ.

eng: The commander still wanted to know the truth about why Paul was being accused by the Jews. Therefore, the next day he ordered the chief priests and the entire Jerusalem Council to assemble. Then he took Paul out of prison and had him stand before them.
local: A wɛr na wa chaar, a sojasɩ ba jaa nɩkpɛ̃ɛ bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka wʊ bɔ̃ɔ bãa 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ a Juu nɩbɛ ba bara a Pɔɔl ɛ. Wʊ for wʊn bar tɩ bʊɔl a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a 'yɛr tɔɔrbɔ nɩbɛrɛ ka ba jaa lɔ̃ɔ taa. Tɩ wʊ pãa suu waan a Pɔɔl a wʊ wa ara a ba niem.

eng: When it was determined that we were to sail to Italy, Paul and some other prisoners were placed in the custody of a centurion named Julius of the Imperial Company.
local: An wa ta ɩka sɩn chen a Itali, ba den a Pɔɔl nɩ a bamɩne ban pɔɔ kʊ a sojasɩ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ ban bʊɔlɔ Julius, wʊ ɩ naa a nãkpɛ̃ɛ soja kpɛ̃ɛ ãsʊɔ.

eng: We boarded a ship from Adramyttium that was about to sail for ports along the coast of the province of Asia. So we put out to sea. Aristarchus, a Macedonian from Thessalonica, came with us.
local: Sɩ kpɛ̃n a gboro kpɛ̃ɛ na yi a Adɩramatiyum ka wʊ ir an ɩ Asɩya kõkoor lɛ wʊn lana. Aristakus na ɩ a Masedonɩya paal nɩrɛ na wʊ yi a Tɛsalonika pʊɔ sɩ naa.

eng: The next day we landed in Sidon. Julius treated Paul kindly and permitted him to go to some friends so they could take care of him.
local: A wɛr na wa chaar, sɩ tãn a Sidon tẽe pɔ, ka a Julius maal a Pɔɔl vɩla na wʊ bar wʊ sɔr ka wʊ chen a wʊ barmɩne sɛ̃ ka ba tɩ sʊ̃nɔ̃ wʊ.

eng: From there we sailed off. We passed Cyprus, using the island to shelter us from the headwinds.
local: Be lɛ sɩ lɛ yin a gboro lana a Sipurusɩ bojũu a sɛsɛbɛ furo na tuoro sɩ.

eng: We sailed across the open sea off the coast of Cilicia and Pamphylia, and landed in Myra in Lycia.
local: A lɛ sɩn wa gɔ̃ɔ a man sãlsɔɔ Sɩlɩsɩya nɩ a Pamfiliya kõkoor lɛ sɩ wa ara nɩ a Mira na be a Lisiya wɛr pɔ.

eng: There the centurion found an Alexandrian ship headed for Italy and put us on board.
local: Be lɛ a soja nɩkpɛ̃ɛ nyɛ a Aleksandiriya gboro kpɛ̃ɛ na chiine a Itali ka wʊ wɛl sɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: After many days of slow and difficult sailing, we arrived off the coast of Cnidus. The wind wouldnt allow us to go farther, so we sailed under the shelter of Crete off Salmone.
local: A ber waa naa sɩn maal a sɔr pɔ, jaa vɛ̃ nɛ nɛ nɛ lɛ sɩ chiine ka a wõ sɩ tɩ sɩ tɩ ta a Sɩnidusɩ tẽe pɔ. Lɛ a sɛsɛbɛ na ba sɔɔrɔ a sɩ chiine a dẽdẽe ɛ, a gboro tun sɩ naa a Kɩrete a Salmone tẽe puor.

eng: We sailed along the coast only with difficulty until we came to a place called Good Harbors, near the city of Lasea.
local: A man kõkoor lɛ sɩ lana ka a wõ sɩ tɩ tãn a jie ban bʊɔlɔ a Gboro Juvɩla Ara A Jie na gbʊr a Lasea tẽe.

eng: Much time had been lost, and the voyage was now dangerous since the Day of Reconciliation had already passed. Paul warned them.
local: Sɩ sɔ̃ɔn sɔ̃ɔ yɔɔ a sɔr pɔ a sɛsɛbɛ na furo jũu, ka a gboro chenfʊ mɩ kʊrɔ dãbãɛ bojũu Kɔ̃ Galfʊ sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ mɩ po naa, ka a Pɔɔl kpãana ba 'yɛrɛ.

eng: Men, I see that our voyage will suffer damage and great loss, not only for the cargo and ship but also for our lives.
local: Nɩbɛ, n kãan nyɛ ka a dãbãɛ be a sɩ chenfʊ pɔ, yelyɔɔ na sɔ̃ɔ naa, a gboro tuor nɩ a sɩn mãɛ mɩ.

eng: But the centurion was persuaded more by the ships pilot and captain than by Pauls advice.
local: Tɩchɛ a sojasɩ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ ba chɛlɛ a lɛ a Pɔɔl na 'yɛrɛ ɛ, tɩ sɔɔ a lɛ a gboro mɔ̃nɔ̃ nɩ lɛ a gboro sʊɔ na 'yɛr.

eng: Since the harbor was unsuitable for spending the winter, the majority supported a plan to put out to sea from there. They thought they might reach Phoenix in Crete and spend the winter in its harbor, which faced southwest and northwest.
local: A gboro ara a jie ba sɛɛn ban na ara a be a ɔɔr sɔ̃ɔ ɛ, a ba yɔɔ jaa 'yɛr naa ɩka ba po chiine, tɩɛrɛ ɩka ban tãn a Fonɩksɩ na ba ara a be. A Kɩrete gboro ara a jie lɛ na tor a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ purfʊ jie guba nɩ a nũsʊ̃ɔ jie.

eng: When a gentle south wind began to blow, they thought they could carry out their plan. They pulled up anchor and sailed closely along the coast of Crete.
local: Lɛ a sɛsɛbaalʊ̃ʊ na wa furo yire a guba jie, ba tɩɛr ɩka ba nyɛn a lɛ ban bʊɔrɔ, ba for a kur nɛbie bara tɩ lana nɩ a gboro a Kɩrete gogoro.

eng: Before long, a hurricane-strength wind known as a northeaster swept down from Crete.
local: An wa kor bãlãa, ka a sɛsɛbɛ kpɛ̃ɔ 'lɔ ban bʊɔlɔ a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ purfʊ jie guba sɛsɛbɛ fun yi a be wa.

eng: The ship was caught in the storm and couldnt be turned into the wind. So we gave in to it, and it carried us along.
local: A sɛsɛbɛ tʊ̃ɔn a gboro a waa lɛ tʊ̃ɔnɔ̃ chiine a niem ɛ, ka a sɛsɛbɛ pãa tara sɩ chiine.

eng: After sailing under the shelter of an island called Cauda, we were able to control the lifeboat only with difficulty.
local: A lɛ sɩn wa pã a dõdolbile ãsʊɔ ban bʊɔlɔ Kawuda, a ɩn tuo kʊ sɩ ka sɩn 'mɔɔ a gborbile.

eng: They brought the lifeboat aboard, then began to wrap the ship with cables to hold it together. Fearing they might run aground on the sandbars of the Gulf of Syrtis, they lowered the anchor and let the ship be carried along.
local: Lɛ a nɩbɛ na wa 'mɔɔ a gborbile ka wʊ do, ba den miir lũ gbɛb a gborkpɛ̃ɛ. Sɩ joro na dãbãɛ ɩka sɩ taa ɩrɛ wa tɩ mʊr a Sirtisɩ bire pɔ ɛ, ka ba bar a kur nɛbie ka a gboro pãa tɔɔrɔ.

eng: We were so battered by the violent storm that the next day the men began throwing cargo overboard.
local: A sɛsɛkpɛ̃ɛ nyã mɔ̃n sɩn jaa ka wɛr wa chaar ka ba piel wɛlɛ a tuor lɔɔrɔ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a kʊ̃ɔ pɔ.

eng: On the third day, they picked up the ships gear and hurled it into the sea.
local: A ber ata daar a gboro dẽme mãɛ foro naa a gboro bomo nɩ a ba mãɛ nuur lɔɔrɔ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a kʊ̃ɔ pɔ.

eng: When neither the sun nor the moon appeared for many days and the raging storm continued to pound us, all hope of our being saved from this peril faded.
local: Lɛ a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ nɩ a ŋmarbie na ba lɛ wa nyɩr ɛ, a ber waa naa tɩchɛ ka a sɛkpɛ̃ɛ chɛnɛ ire ka a sɩ chãɛfʊ tɩɛrtɔ jie baar.

eng: For a long time no one had eaten. Paul stood up among them and said, Men, you should have complied with my instructions not to sail from Crete. Then we would have avoided this damage and loss.
local: A ber wa po naa tɩ baa di bon jaa ɛ, ka a Pɔɔl ir ara a ba sãlsɔɔ tɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Nɩbɛ, yɩn ta sɔɔ tu a n nɛɛ ka sɩ ta chɛnɛ be a be Kɩrete a wõm nyãna kʊ̃ naa pɔɔ sɩ ɛ, ka a bonsɩ be wɛ ɛ.

eng: Now I urge you to be encouraged. Not one of your lives will be lost, though we will lose the ship.
local: Tɩchɛ n sʊɔrɔ yɩ naa, yɩ nyɔɔ nyãa bojũu ãsʊɔ jaa kʊ̃ bɔr ɛ, a gboro tɛɛ lɛ na wɛ.

eng: Last night an angel from the God to whom I belong and whom I worship stood beside me.
local: Jãa tãsɔɔ a Nãaŋmɩn nyã na so ka n tʊ̃nɔ̃ a wʊ tʊ̃mɔ̃ tʊ̃n a wʊ malaka ka wʊ wa ara a n lõboor.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Paul! You must stand before Caesar! Indeed, God has also graciously given you everyone sailing with you.
local: na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ma, Pɔɔl taa joro dãbãɛ ɛ, fʊn nyɛn a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Siisa nɩ nɩbir ka wʊ tɔɔr a fʊ 'yɛr. Ka a bala jaa fʊn lɔ̃ɔ chiin Nãaŋmɩn den a ba nyɛvʊr 'yɔ̃ɔ a fʊ nũu pɔ.

eng: Be encouraged, men! I have faith in God that it will be exactly as he told me.
local: A lɛ jũu yɩ nyɔɔ nyãa nɩbɛ, n sɔɔn de Nãaŋmɩn ɩka wʊn ɩ naa a lɛ wʊn 'yɛr kʊ ma.

eng: However, we must run aground on some island.
local: Tɩchɛ sɩn don dõdol kɔ̃ɔ ju ara.

eng: On the fourteenth night, we were being carried across the Adriatic Sea. Around midnight the sailors began to suspect that land was near.
local: A ber pie nɩ anaar daar, a sɛsɛbɛ chɛnɛ na mʊɔrɔ nɩ sɩ be a Adiriya man yal pɔ, a tãsɔɔ bãɩn ka a bala na kaara a gboro tɩɛr man tãa ba gbʊrɔ na gogoro.

eng: They dropped a weighted line to take soundings and found the water to be about one hundred twenty feet deep. After proceeding a little farther, we took soundings again and found the water to be about ninety feet deep.
local: Ba den a bon 'lɔ ban ma mana nɩ a kʊ̃ɔ, man ka a man yulʊ̃fʊ ɩ gbɛɛ kɔbaa dɔɔl lɩjɛr . Ban wa chen bãlãa ba lɛ man kaa ka a yulʊ̃fʊ lɛ ɩ gbɛɛ lɩjaanaar nɩ pie .

eng: Afraid that we might run aground somewhere on the rocks, they hurled out four anchors from the stern and began to pray for daylight.
local: Ba joro na dãbãɛ ɩka a gboro wʊ taa tɔ̃n sɩ kʊsɩr ɛ, a ba wɛl a nɛbie ala anaar na ma nyɔɔ a gboro lɔb 'yɔ̃ɔ a man pɔ tɩ pãa sʊɔrɔ ɩka wɛr wʊ chaar fɔɔ.

eng: The sailors tried to abandon the ship by lowering the lifeboat into the sea, pretending they were going to lower anchors from the bow.
local: Ka a gboro dẽme bʊɔrɔ sɔr ban na yi a gboro pɔ jo na ba for a gborbile bar ka wʊ suu a man pɔ tɩ maala fɩka a nɛbinyɔɔrsɩ ala lɛ ba foro bara a niem.

eng: Paul said to the centurion and his soldiers, Unless they stay in the ship, you cant be saved from peril.
local: A Pɔɔl pãa 'yɛr kʊ a sojasɩ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ nɩ a sojasɩ, Yɩn baa vɛ̃ ka a gboro dẽme banyãna be a gboro pɔ ɛ, sɩ wɛ naa a lɛ nɛ.

eng: The soldiers then cut the ropes to the lifeboat and let it drift away.
local: Ka a sojasɩ ŋmaa a miir 'lɔ na nyɔɔ a gborbile taa lɛ ka wʊ lo.

eng: Just before daybreak, Paul urged everyone to eat. He said, This is the fourteenth day youve lived in suspense, and youve not had even a bite to eat.
local: Lɛ a wɛr na ɩrɛ a wa lalala, Pɔɔl sʊɔr ba jaa na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩ di a bʊ̃diir bojũu ber pie nɩ anaar nɩ nyã yɩn jɛ̃n baal na yaa di bon jaa ɛ.

eng: I urge you to take some food. Your health depends on it. None of you will lose a single hair from his head.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã, n sʊɔrɔ yɩ naa ɩka yɩ di a bʊ̃diir na yɩ tara kpɛ̃ɔ, a yɩ ãsʊɔ jaa jukɔɔlbir kʊ̃ vʊ̃ɔ a wʊ ju pɔ ɛ.

eng: After he said these things, he took bread, gave thanks to God in front of them all, then broke it and began to eat.
local: A anyãna lɛ wʊ 'yɛr baar tɩ na wʊ de a paanʊ̃ɔ amɩne puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan a ba jaa niem, tɩ pãa ŋmaa na wʊ ɔɔrɔ.

eng: Everyone was encouraged and took some food. .
local: Ka a 'yɔ̃ɔ ba jaa ãkpɛ̃ɛ ka a ba mãɛ di a bʊ̃diir amɩne.

eng: In all, there were two hundred seventy-six of us on the ship.
local: A sɩn bala na be a gboro pɔ ɩ naa nɩbɛ kɔɔr ayi dɔɔl lɩjaata nɩ pie nɩ ayʊɔb .

eng: When they had eaten as much as they wanted, they lightened the ship by throwing the grain into the sea.
local: Lɛ ban wa di tɩɩ baar, ba wɛl a bʊ̃diir ala na chɛ lɔb 'yɔ̃ɔ a man pɔ tɩchɛ ka a gboro ɩ dẽfere.

eng: In the morning light they saw a bay with a sandy beach. They didnt know what land it was, but they thought they might possibly be able to run the ship aground.
local: Lɛ a wɛr na wa chaar, baa bɔ̃ɔ a jie ban be ɛ, tɩchɛ na ba nyɛ a jie na gɔr ka a bire be be a ba bʊɔrɔ ɩka ala ɩ ba tʊ̃ɔ naa a ba tɩ ara.

eng: They cut the anchors loose and left them in the sea. At the same time, they untied the ropes that ran back to the rudders. They raised the foresail to catch the wind and made for the beach.
local: Ba ŋman a mibe ala na nyɔɔr a nɛbinyɔɔrsɩ ala bar, na ba mɩ lɛ for a vuulsɩ ala na ma dɛnɛ a gboro tɩ pãa yɛr a fɔbaa bar a gboro niem ka a sɛsɛbɛ pãa tɔɔrɔ ba chiine a bire pɔ.

eng: But they struck a sandbar and the ship ran aground. The bow was stuck and wouldnt move, and the stern was broken into pieces by the force of the waves.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a gboro tɩ tɔ a bire na duo a jie ãsʊɔ, ka a gboro niem mʊr wʊ jaa na wʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ chen ɛ. Ka a kɔ̃sɛbɛ ŋmiere a gboro pãa wɛlɛ.

eng: The soldiers decided to kill the prisoners to keep them from swimming to shore and escaping.
local: Ka a sojasɩ ɩ nɛbʊ̃'yen bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba kʊ a bala ban pɔɔ ka a ãsʊɔ jaa taa dũu yi jo ɛ.

eng: However, the centurion wanted to save Paul, so he stopped them from carrying out their plan. He ordered those who could swim to jump overboard first and head for land.
local: Tɩchɛ a sojasɩ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ na bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ faa a Pɔɔl jũu, waa sɔɔ ɩka ba ɩ a lɛ ɛ. Wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ naa a bala na bɔ̃ɔ a dũufʊ ka ba ɔɔ lo a kʊ̃ɔ pɔ de nie dũu do a gogoro ju.

eng: He ordered the rest to grab hold of planks or debris from the ship. In this way, everyone reached land safely.
local: Ka a bala na chɛ mɩ nyɔɔr a gboro daar bɩɩ a gboro da'mɛlɛ ala, nɩtɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ ba ɩ ka ba ãsʊɔ tɩ jaa do a gogoro ju ka yele ba tara ba ɛ.

eng: Agrippa said to Paul, You may speak for yourself. So Paul gestured with his hand and began his defense.
local: Kaa Agripa pãa 'yɛr kʊ a Pɔɔl, Fʊ tara na sɔr na 'yɛr a fʊ nɛɛ 'yɛr. Pɔɔl tur naa a nũu tɩ piel mana a wʊ mãɛ yele.

eng: King Agrippa, I consider myself especially fortunate that I stand before you today as I offer my defense concerning all the accusations the Jews have brought against me.
local: Nãa Agripa, a ɩ man bɔɔsʊ̃n ɩn ara a fʊ niem a dɩna naa na man a n yele ala a jaa a Juu nɩbɛ na 'yɛr dɔɔl ma.

eng: This is because you understand well all the Jewish customs and controversies. Therefore, I ask you to listen to me patiently.
local: Yelmãɛ jaa lɛ, a fʊ̃ʊ mãɛ jaa bɔ̃ɔn naa a Juu nɩbɛ yelbinsɩ a jaa nɩ a ba nɛchɩrɛ, a lɛ jũu n sʊɔr fʊ naa ka fʊ di kãnyir chɛlɛ ma.

eng: Every Jew knows the way of life I have followed since my youth because, from the beginning, I was among my people and in Jerusalem.
local: A Juu nɩbɛ ba jaa bɔ̃ɔn a lɛ ɩn be a bibile daar jaa, a maa paal pɔ nɩ a ka a Jerusalɛm mɩ.

eng: They have known me for a long time. If they wanted to, they could testify that I followed the way of life set out by the most exacting group of our religion. I am a Pharisee.
local: Ba bɔ̃ɔ man kor na ban tʊ̃ɔ di a lɛ dãasɩɛ ala ɩ ba bʊɔrɔ naa. Ba bɔ̃ɔn ma naa ɩn turo yele jaa nɛ nɛ nɛ a sɩ puorfʊ pɔ, naa ɩ a Farasee nɩrɛ.

eng: Today I am standing trial because of the hope in the promise God gave our ancestors.
local: A pʊ̃pãanyã a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na dɔ̃ɔ tɩr a nɛɛ kʊ a sɩ sãakpãmɩne ka n tara a tɩɛr jũu lɛ so ka n 'yɛr be tɔɔrɔ a dɩna.

eng: This is the promise our twelve tribes hope to receive as they earnestly worship night and day. The Jews are accusing me, King Agrippa, because of this hope!.
local: A nɛ tɩra anyãna jũu lɛ a sɩn bʊrɔ pie nɩ ayi ɩ a sɩ tɩɛrtɔ fʊ jie, ka a na wa ɩ a sɩ mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir puoro a Nãaŋmɩn mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ nɩ tãsɔɔ. A fʊ̃ʊ nãa a tɩɛrtɔ nyãna jũu, a Juu nɩbɛ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ma a 'yɛr.

eng: Why is it inconceivable to you that God raises the dead?.
local: Bo lɛ so ka yɩ tɩɛr ɩka Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ sãɩ kũu ɛ?.

eng: I really thought that I ought to oppose the name of Jesus the Nazarene in every way possible.
local: A maa mɩ dɔ̃ɔ ŋme nyãa naa ɩka ɩn ɩ naa lɛ jaa ɩn na tʊ̃ɔ naa sɔ̃ɔ a Yesu na yi a Najaretɩ yuor.

eng: And thats exactly what I did in Jerusalem. I locked up many of Gods holy people in prison under the authority of the chief priests. When they were condemned to death, I voted against them.
local: Nɩtɔ̃ lɛ n ɩ be a Jerusalɛm. A Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ kʊ man sɔr ka n nyɔɔr a Nãaŋmɩn puorbo yɔɔ naa pɔɔ, bala wa kʊrɔ ba n ma sɔɔ naa.

eng: In one synagogue after another—indeed, in all the synagogues—I would often torture them, compelling them to slander God. My rage bordered on the hysterical as I pursued them, even to foreign cities.
local: Sɔ̃ɔ yɔɔ jaa, n ma chen a be a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ jiir naa tɩ ge ge ba, naa ma fɩrɛ ba 'yɛr 'yɛr dɛbɛ tɩchɛ a maa tʊ̃ɔ ɩ a lɛ ɛ. A lɛ a ba yele na dire ma a suur n ma chen a wɛr yuo pɔ tɩ ge ba a be.

eng: On one such journey, I was going to Damascus with the full authority of the chief priests.
local: Ala jũu lɛ a bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ dɔ̃ɔ kʊ ma a sɔr ka n chiine a Damaskus.

eng: While on the road at midday, King Agrippa, I saw a light from heaven shining around me and my traveling companions. That light was brighter than the sun.
local: Mɔ̃tɔ̃tuo sɔ̃ɔ lɛ fʊ̃ʊ nãa, a lɛ ɩn be a sɔr pɔ, n nyɛn chãa na yi saaju chaar mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ na wʊ jilʊ̃ ma nɩ a bala ɩn lɔ̃ɔ chiine.

eng: We all fell to the ground, and I heard a voice that said to me in Aramaic, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me? Its hard for you to kick against a spear.
local: Sɩ jaa lon tẽe tɩchɛ ka n wõ nɩrɛ na sʊrɔ ma nɩ a Heburu kɔkɔr, Sɔɔl, Sɔɔl bo lɛ fʊ gere ma a lɛ? Fʊ wõn tuo fʊn tɛb a dakʊra.

eng: Then I said, Who are you, Lord? The Lord replied, I am Jesus, whom you are harassing.
local: Be lɛ n pãa sʊʊr, Fʊ̃ʊ nɩ ana, Soro? A wʊ 'yɛr, Maa lɛ a Yesu 'lɔ fʊn gere.

eng: Get up! Stand on your feet! I have appeared to you for this purpose: to appoint you as my servant and witness of what you have seen and what I will show you.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã ir ara, n yi fʊ naa, naa ir fʊ a fʊn ɩ a n tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ na fʊ mʊɔlɔ a ala fʊn nyɛ a dɩna nɩ a ala ɩn na wiil fʊ.

eng: I will rescue you from your own people and from the Gentiles. I am sending you.
local: Ɩn faa fʊ naa a fʊ̃ʊ mãɛ nɩbɛ nuur pɔ, nɩ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ nuur pɔ. Maa lɛ tʊ̃nɔ̃ fʊ.

eng: to open their eyes. Then they can turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God, and receive forgiveness of sins and a place among those who are made holy by faith in me.
local: tɩ yuo a ba nɩbie na fʊ tɔɔ ba yin a libe pɔ, waan a chãa pɔ, na fʊ yin a Satan kpɛ̃ɔ pɔ, waan a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃, a wʊ vɛ̃ a ba yelbier kʊ ba ka ba mɩ nyɛ jie nɩ a bala na sɔɔ de ma, ka n faa ba.

eng: So, King Agrippa, I wasnt disobedient to that heavenly vision.
local: A lɛ jũu, Nãa Agripa, n ba tɔr a nyɛfʊ nyã na yi a saaju ɛ.

eng: Instead, I proclaimed first to those in Damascus and Jerusalem, then to the whole region of Judea and to the Gentiles. My message was that they should change their hearts and lives and turn to God, and that they should demonstrate this change in their behavior.
local: N den nie man kʊ a Damaskus dẽme, nɩ a Jerusalɛm dẽme, nɩ a Judiya wɛr wʊ jaa, nɩ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ mɩ. N mʊɔl kʊ ba ɩka ba lɩɛb a ba tɩɛr, na ba lɩɛb wa Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃, na ba vɛ̃ ka ba tʊ̃mɔ̃ wiil ɩka ba lɩɛb a ba tɩɛr.

eng: Because of this, some Jews seized me in the temple and tried to murder me.
local: Ala lɛ so a Juu nɩbɛ nyɔɔ ma a ba puorfʊ yir bɛchaar pɔ bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba kʊ ma.

eng: God has helped me up to this very day. Therefore, I stand here and bear witness to the lowly and the great. Im saying nothing more than what the Prophets and Moses declared would happen.
local: Tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn lɛ sʊ̃ʊ ma wa tãn a dɩna, ka n be ara na man kʊ nɩrɛ jaa. N ba 'yɛr 'yɛr jaa tɩ põn a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ nɩ a Mosesɩ na 'yɛr ɩka an ɩ naa ɛ.

eng: that the Christ would suffer and that, as the first to rise from the dead, he would proclaim light both to my people and to the Gentiles.
local: ɩka a Nɩ-iraa na din dɔɔyɛ na wʊ mɩ ɩ nɩrɛ na de nie ir a kũu pɔ, na wʊ man a chãa 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ nɩbɛ nɩ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ.

eng: At this point in Pauls defense, Festus declared with a loud voice, Youve lost your mind, Paul! Too much learning is driving you mad.
local: Kana lɛ a Pɔɔl ta ka a Festus kpɛ tãn wʊ tɩ 'yɛr, Pɔɔl a tɩɛr bɔr fʊ naa, a jãn yɔɔ vɛ̃n ka fʊ gele.

eng: But Paul replied, Im not mad, most honorable Festus! Im speaking what is sound and true.
local: Ka a Pɔɔl 'yɛr, Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ Festus n ba gele ɛ, ala ɩn 'yɛrɛ ɩn yelmãɛ nɩ yɛ̃ 'yɛr.

eng: King Agrippa knows about these things, and I have been speaking openly to him. Im certain that none of these things have escaped his attention. This didnt happen secretly or in some out-of-the-way place.
local: A nãa bɔ̃ɔn a yele anyãna ala lɛ so ka n lɔb yãan bar tɩ 'yɛrɛ nɩ wʊ. N sɔɔ naa ɩka a yele anyãna ba sɔɔl wʊ ɛ, bojũu a yele nyã ba ɩ libe pɔ ɛ.

eng: King Agrippa, do you believe the prophets? I know you do.
local: Nãa Agripa, fʊ sɔɔn a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ bɩɩ? N bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka fʊ sɔɔ naa.

eng: Agrippa said to Paul, Are you trying to convince me that, in such a short time, youve made me a Christian.
local: Ka a Nãa Agripa pãa sʊʊr a Pɔɔl, Fʊ tɩɛr man tãa a dɩna tɛɛ lɛ fʊn lɩɛb ma ka n ɩ a Kɩrɩsɩto turo bɩɩ.

eng: Paul responded, Whether it is a short or a long time, I pray to God that not only you but also all who are listening to me today will become like me, except for these chains.
local: A Pɔɔl 'yɛr, Ka dɩna tɛɛ lɛ bɩɩ daar kɔ̃ɔ lɛ yo, n sʊɔrɔ naa Nãaŋmɩn ɩka a ta ɩ a fʊ̃ʊ tɛɛ ɛ, tɩ nɩrɛ jaa na chɛlɛ a 'yɛr nyãna a dɩna ka yɩ kaara a n kaar tɩ na ba taa 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ yɩ a bɔ̃jɔrsɩ anyãna ɛ.

eng: The king stood up, as did the governor, Bernice, and those sitting with them.
local: Ka a nãa vaa ir nɩ a wʊ nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Bernisɩ lɔ̃ɔn a bala wʊn 'mataa jãnɛ nɩ.

eng: As they left, they were saying to each other, This man is doing nothing that deserves death or imprisonment.
local: Ba yin a die pɔ, pãa 'yɛrɛ 'yɛr kʊrɔ taa na ba 'yɛrɛ, A daba nyãna ba maal yele jaa na sɛɛn kũu bɩɩ pɔɔfʊ ɛ.

eng: Agrippa said to Festus, This man could have been released if he hadnt appealed to Caesar.
local: Ka a Agripa 'yɛr kʊ a Festus, Fʊn taa tʊ̃ɔ bar a daba nyãna ala ɩ waa ta sʊɔr sɔr ɩka wʊn chen a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Siisa sɛ̃ ɛ.

eng: Three days after arriving in the province, Festus went up to Jerusalem from Caesarea.
local: Festus na de a wʊ tʊ̃mɔ̃ maal ber ata wʊ yin be a Sisariya chen a Jerusalɛm.

eng: The chief priests and Jewish leaders presented their case against Paul. Appealing to him.
local: Be lɛ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a ba nɩdierbe man a 'yɛr ala ban tara nɩ, a Pɔɔl kʊ wʊ.

eng: they asked as a favor from Festus that he summon Paul to Jerusalem. They were planning to ambush and kill him along the way.
local: Ba sʊɔr a Festus nɩbijɩɛ jaa ɩka a lɛ ban sʊɔrɔ wʊ a wʊ sɔɔ na wʊ vɛ̃ a ba tara a Pɔɔl waan be a Jerusalɛm an ɩ ba chɔɔr ũu gu na kʊ a Pɔɔl a be a sɔr pɔ.

eng: But Festus responded by keeping Paul in Caesarea, since he was to return there very soon himself.
local: Ka a Festus 'yɛr, A Pɔɔl ben a be a Sisariya a maa mãɛ a kʊ̃ kor ɛ, tɩchɛ ka n chen a be.

eng: Some of your leaders can come down with me, he said. If hes done anything wrong, they can bring charges against him.
local: Yɩ vɛ̃ ka a yɩ nɩbɛrɛ bamɩne pʊɔ ma a sɩ 'mataa chen, ala ɩ wʊ maal yeldɛɛr a yɩ 'yɛr n kaa.

eng: He stayed with them for no more than eight or ten days, then went down to Caesarea. The following day he took his seat in the court and ordered that Paul be brought in.
local: Wʊn wa maal a be ber aniin bɩɩ pie wʊ chen a Sisariya, wʊn tɩ gã ir wʊ jãnɛ naa a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ jie tɩ na wʊ 'yɛr ɩka ba waan a Pɔɔl.

eng: When he arrived, many Jews who had come down from Jerusalem surrounded him. They brought serious charges against him, but they couldnt prove them.
local: Ban wa waan a Pɔɔl a Juu nɩbɛ bala na yi a Jerusalɛm wa ara na jilʊ̃ wʊ ŋmara jir kara dɔɔlɔ wʊ tɩchɛ ba maa tʊ̃ɔ wiil a par ɛ.

eng: In his own defense, Paul said, Ive done nothing wrong against the Jewish Law, against the temple, or against Caesar.
local: A Pɔɔl pãa 'yɛr, Maa ba maal dɛɛr jaa ɛ, naa sɔ̃ɔ a Juu nɩbɛ nɛbinãa nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir ɛ, bɩɩ 'yɛr 'yɛrdɛɛr 'yɔ̃ɔ a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Siisa ɛ.

eng: Festus, wanting to put the Jews in his debt, asked Paul, Are you willing to go up to Jerusalem to stand trial before me concerning these things.
local: Festus bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka wʊ pɛl a Juu nɩbɛ pʊɔ na wʊ sʊʊr a Pɔɔl, Fʊ bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka fʊ do a Jerusalɛm ka a 'yɛr anyãna ban 'yɛr dɔɔl fʊ ka n tɩ tɔɔr a be bɩɩ.

eng: Paul replied, Im standing before Caesars court. I ought to be tried here. I have done nothing wrong to the Jews, as you well know.
local: A Pɔɔl 'yɛr, Pʊ̃pãanyã, n ben ara a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Siisa 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ jie a be an sɛɛ ɩka ba tɔɔr a n 'yɛr. N mɩ ba maal yeldɛɛr jaa a Juu nɩbɛ sɛ̃ ɛ, a lɛ a fʊ̃ʊ mãɛ na bɔ̃ɔn.

eng: If Im guilty and have done something that deserves death, then I wont try to avoid death. But if there is nothing to their accusations against me, no one has the authority to hand me over to them. I appeal to Caesar.
local: Maa ta maal dɛɛr naa sɛɛn kũu n kʊ̃ naa tɔr ɩka n kpi ɛ. Tɩchɛ a 'yɛr anyãna a Juu nɩbɛ na 'yɛr dɔɔl ma na ba ɩ yelmãɛ ɛ, nɩrɛ jaa ba tara sɔr na fɩr ma kʊ ba ɛ. Siisa sɛ̃ lɛ ɩn chen ka wʊ tɩ tɔɔr a n 'yɛr.

eng: After Festus conferred with his advisors, he responded, You have appealed to Caesar. To Caesar you will go.
local: Lɛ a Festus nɩ a wʊ nɩbɛrɛ na wa man taa baar wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a Pɔɔl, Fʊ̃ʊ lɛ sʊɔr ɩka a Siisa sɛ̃ lɛ fʊn chen, be lɛ fʊn chen.

eng: After several days had passed, King Agrippa and Bernice arrived in Caesarea to welcome Festus.
local: A kor bãlãa ka a Nãa Agripa nɩ a wʊ yɛpɔɔ Bernisɩ wa be a Sisariya ɩka ba wa puor a Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ Festus.

eng: Since they were staying there for many days, Festus discussed the case against Paul with the king. He said, There is a man whom Felix left in prison.
local: An ɩ ban jɛ̃n a be kor jũu, Festus mana naa a Pɔɔl yele kʊrɔ a Nãa Agripa 'yɛrɛ, Daba kɔ̃ɔ lɛ be a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ a Feliksɩ na pɔɔ.

eng: When I was in Jerusalem, the Jewish chief priests and elders brought charges against him and requested a guilty verdict in his case.
local: A lɛ ɩn chen a Jerusalɛm, a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a ba nɩdierbe den yelyɔɔ dɔɔl wʊ na ba sʊɔr ma ɩka n tɔɔr a wʊ 'yɛr ge wʊ.

eng: I told them it is contrary to Roman practice to hand someone over before they have faced their accusers and had opportunity to offer a defense against the charges.
local: Ka n 'yɛr kʊ ba a Worom nɩbɛ maa tɔɔr nɩrɛ 'yɛr ala ɩ a nɩnyɩnɛ ban 'yɛr a 'yɛr dɔɔl ba wa ara a bala na tara nɩ wʊ a 'yɛr niem na wʊ 'yɛr a wʊ nɛɛ 'yɛr ɛ.

eng: When they came here, I didnt put them off. The very next day I took my seat in the court and ordered that the man be brought before me.
local: Ala lɛ so ka ba pɔɔ ma a sɩ 'mataa wa a ka, n ba wa jɛ̃n a 'yɛr ɛ, a wɛr na wa chaar n jɛ̃ naa na tɔɔr a 'yɛr naa vɛ̃ ka ba waan a daba.

eng: When the accusers took the floor, they didnt charge him with any of the crimes I had expected.
local: A bala na tara nɩ wʊ a 'yɛr na wa ir ara na 'yɛr, baa 'yɛr 'yɛr jaa na ɩ dɛɛr nɩtãa lɛ ɩn tɩɛr ban taa 'yɛr ɛ.

eng: Instead, they quibbled with him about their own religion and about some dead man named Jesus, who Paul claimed was alive.
local: Tɩchɛ a ba 'yɛr tɩ ɩ naa a ba puorfʊ yele lɛ nɩ daba kɔ̃ɔ na di Yesu na kpi ka a Pɔɔl 'yɛr ɩka wʊ ir naa na wʊ ɩ vʊr.

eng: Since I had no idea how to investigate these matters, I asked if he would be willing to go to Jerusalem to stand trial there on these issues.
local: Ka a yele nyã sɔb ma nɩbir, n ba bɔ̃ɔ ŋmɩnɛ ɩn na ɩ vɩɛr a yele nyã ɛ, ala lɛ so ka n sʊʊr wʊ ala ɩ wʊn bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka wʊ chen a Jerusalɛm ka ba tɩ tɔɔr a 'yɛr anyãna a be.

eng: However, Paul appealed that he be held in custody pending a decision from His Majesty the emperor, so I ordered that he be held until I could send him to Caesar.
local: Pɔɔl 'lɔ bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Siisa wʊ tɔɔr a wʊ 'yɛr, n vɛ̃n a ba tara wʊ tɩ tãn a lɛ ɩn na bar sɔr ka ba tara wʊ chiin a Siisa sɛ̃.

eng: Agrippa said to Festus, I want to hear the man myself. Tomorrow, Festus replied, you will hear him.
local: Ka a Nãa Agripa pãa 'yɛr kʊ a Festus, Maa mãɛ naa bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka n wõ a daba mɩ kɔkɔr. Ka a Festus 'yɛr, Bio fʊn wõn a wʊ kɔkɔr.

eng: The next day Agrippa and Bernice came with great fanfare. They entered the auditorium with the military commanders and the citys most prominent men. Festus then ordered that Paul be brought in.
local: A wɛr na wa chaar, a Nãa Agripa nɩ a Bernisɩ wa naa ka a nɩbɛ de 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ kʊ ba ka ba tɩ kpɛ̃n be a dikpɛ̃ɛ 'lɔ pɔ ban ma lɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ nɩ a sojasɩ nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a tẽe nɩbɛrɛ. Ka a Festus vɛ̃ a sojasɩ tara a Pɔɔl waan.

eng: Festus said, King Agrippa and everyone present with us: You see this man! The entire Jewish community, both here and in Jerusalem, has appealed to me concerning him. Theyve been calling for his immediate death.
local: Ka a Festus 'yɛr, Nãa Agripa nɩ a yɩn bala jaa na be ka a sɩ sɛ̃, yɩ nyɛ a daba nyãna, a Juu nɩbɛ ba jaa 'yɛr a wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ma be a Jerusalɛm nɩ a ka a Sisariya tãna 'yɛrɛ ɩka a ba sɛɛ ka wʊ lɛ chɛnɛ go ɛ.

eng: Ive found that he has done nothing deserving death. When he appealed to His Majesty, I decided to send him to Rome.
local: N mɩ ba nyɛ yele jaa wʊn ɩ ka a tara sɔr ka sɩn kʊ wʊ ɛ, tɩchɛ bojũu wʊ sʊɔr sɔr ɩka wʊ chen a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Siisa sɛ̃, n mɩ na bar wʊn sɔr ka wʊ chen a Worom.

eng: I have nothing definite to write to our lord emperor. Therefore, Ive brought him before all of you, and especially before you, King Agrippa, so that after this investigation, I might have something to write.
local: Tɩchɛ n ba tara a wʊ yele ala na sɛɛ ka n na sɛb kʊ a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ ɛ. A lɛ jũu n waan wʊ naa a yɩ jaa niem nɩ a fʊ̃ʊ Nãa Agripa niem a fʊ mɩ jʊʊr a wʊ yele kaa, ka n bɔ̃ɔ a lɛ ɩn na sɛb.

eng: After all, it would be foolish to send a prisoner without specifying the charges against him.
local: Bojũu, a ba sɛɛn ma ɛ, ka n na vɛ̃ ka ba tara nɩrɛ ban pɔɔ chiine naa ba wiil a yelnyɩnɛ wʊn maal ɛ.

eng: After reaching land safely, we learned that the island was called Malta.
local: Sɩ jaa na wa do baar, ba 'yɛr ɩka a dõdol yuor din Malta.

eng: The islanders showed us extraordinary kindness. Because it was rainy and cold, they built a fire and welcomed all of us.
local: A nɩbɛ bala na be a be maal sɩn vɩla, ba gbɔɔn bũu na ba bʊɔl sɩ a sɩ tɩ uoro, bojũu saa lɛ waara ka ɔɔr kʊrɔ.

eng: Paul gathered a bunch of dry sticks and put them on the fire. As he did, a poisonous snake, driven out by the heat, latched on to his hand.
local: Ka a Pɔɔl mɩ vaar delsɩ wa 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a bũu pɔ, ka a dʊ̃pãn na be a delsɩ pɔ, a bũu tʊl kpɛ ka wʊ yi dʊ̃ a Pɔɔl nũu mar taalɛ.

eng: When the islanders saw the snake hanging from his hand, they said to each other, This man must be a murderer! He was rescued from the sea, but the goddess Justice hasnt let him live.
local: Lɛ a gogoro dẽme bala na wa nyɛ a lɛ a waa na mar a Pɔɔl nũu pɔ jɔ̃jɔlɔ, ka ba 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ taa, A daba nyãna na ɩn nɩkʊrɔ, a man na ba di wʊ gba ɛ, tɩchɛ yeltorsɩ kʊ̃ sɔɔ ka wʊ be be ɛ.

eng: Paul shook the snake into the fire and suffered no harm.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Pɔɔl mɩr a waa 'yɔ̃ɔ a bũu pɔ tɩ yele jaa ba tara wʊ ɛ.

eng: They expected him to swell up with fever or suddenly drop dead. After waiting a long time and seeing nothing unusual happen to him, they changed their minds and began to claim that he was a god.
local: A nɩbɛ tɩɛr man tãa a nũu na mɔr wʊ naa bɩɩ a wʊ vaa lo kpi. Tɩchɛ ban chɛl lɛ̃ɛɛ wa faa ka a yele jaa ba ɩrɛ ɛ, ba lɩɛb a ba tɩɛr 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ taa, Ŋmɩn lɛ wʊ ɩ.

eng: Publius, the islands most prominent person, owned a large estate in that area. He welcomed us warmly into his home as his guests for three days.
local: A dõdol 'lɔ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ ban bʊɔlɔ Pubilusɩ tara na jie na gbʊr a be a wʊ de sɩ sãan, na wʊ kʊ sɩ bʊ̃diir ber ata.

eng: Publius father was bedridden, sick with a fever and dysentery. Paul went to see him and prayed. He placed his hand on him and healed him.
local: A Pubilusɩ sãa bɩɛrɛ na gã, ãgãn tʊl nɩ masʊmɔ̃ lɛ wʊ nyɩɛrɛ. Pɔɔl kpɛ̃n tɩ nyɛ wʊ, wʊn wa sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn baar wʊ dɔɔl wʊ naa nũu sãa wʊ.

eng: Once this happened, the rest of the sick on the island came to him and were healed.
local: Lɛ a anyãna na wa ɩ, baalsɩ bala jaa na be a jie 'lɔ mɩ wa naa ka Pɔɔl sãa ba jaa baalsɩ.

eng: They honored us in many ways. When we were getting ready to sail again, they supplied us with what we needed.
local: Ba den 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ kʊ sɩ sɔɩ jaa pɔ, a lɛ sɩn wa chɔɔr na yi, ba kʊ sɩn bon jaa sɩn na bʊɔrɔ.

eng: After three months we put out to sea in a ship that had spent the winter at the island. It was an Alexandrian ship with carvings of the twin gods Castor and Pollux as its figurehead.
local: Sɩ maal a be ŋmɛrsɩ ata tɩ na sɩ lɛ kpɛ a gboro ãsʊɔ na ara a be a sãɛ. A gboro yin Aleksandiriya na wʊ tara ŋmɩn yɩbɛ Kasɩtor nɩ Poluksɩ.

eng: We landed in Syracuse where we stayed three days.
local: Sɩ tɩ ara naa Sɩrakusɩ maal ber ata a be.

eng: From there we sailed to Rhegium. After one day a south wind came up, and we arrived on the second day in Puteoli.
local: Be lɛ a gboro yin sɩ tãn a be a Wuregiyum. Wɛr na wa chaar ka a ɩ a guba jie sɛsɛbɛ furo naa, a ber ayi daar ka sɩ ta a be Puteyoli na sɩ suu a be.

eng: There we found brothers and sisters who urged us to stay with them for a week. In this way we came to Rome.
local: Be lɛ sɩ pɔɔ a Nãaŋmɩn puorbo bamɩne ka ba bʊɔl sɩ ka sɩ tɩ maal daa gbul a ba sɛ̃. Lɛ lɛ a ɩ tɩ sɩ pãa wa ta a Worom.

eng: When the brothers and sisters there heard about us, they came as far as the Forum of Appius and the Three Taverns to meet us. When Paul saw them, he gave thanks to God and was encouraged.
local: A Nãaŋmɩn puorbo na be a Worom na wa wõ ɩka sɩ waara naa, ba yin tuor sɩ tɩ ta Apiyusɩ Daa pɔ na ba pɔɔ sɩ a Sãanba Kpɛfʊ Jiir Ata. A lɛ a Pɔɔl na wa nyɛ a nɩbɛ banyãna wʊ puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan ka a 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ ãkpɛ̃ɔ.

eng: When we entered Rome, Paul was permitted to live by himself, with a soldier guarding him.
local: Lɛ sɩn wa ta a Worom, ba sɔɔ naa ka a Pɔɔl kpɩɛrɛ a wʊ yõ tɩchɛ ka a soja kaara wʊ.

eng: Three days later, Paul called the Jewish leaders together. When they gathered, he said, Brothers, although I have done nothing against our people or the customs of our ancestors, Im a prisoner from Jerusalem. They handed me over to the Romans.
local: Pɔɔl na wa maal ber ata, wʊ bʊɔl naa a Juu nɩbɛ nɩdierbe lɔ̃ɔ taa. Lɛ ban wa duo ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, N yɛɛr, n gba na ba maal yele jaa bɔr a sɩ nɩbɛ sɛ̃ ɛ, bɩɩ 'mab a sɩ sãakpãmɩne yel-ɩrsɩ ɛ, tɩ ba nyɔɔ ma naa be a Jerusalɛm na ba kʊ a Worom nɩbɛ.

eng: who intended to release me after they examined me, because they couldnt find any reason for putting me to death.
local: Ba chɛ̃pɛ̃n a yele faa na ba ta bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba bar ma bojũu n ba maal yele jaa na sɛɛn kũu ɛ.

eng: When the Jews objected, I was forced to appeal to Caesar. Dont think I appealed to Caesar because I had any reason to bring charges against my nation.
local: Tɩchɛ ka a Juu nɩbɛ na ba sɔɔ ɛ, ka n faa tɩ sʊɔr sɔr ka ba chiin ma a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Siisa sɛ̃. A ba ɩ n tara na yele kɔ̃ɔ na de dɔɔl a n nɩbɛ ɛ.

eng: This is why I asked to see you and speak with you: its because of the hope of Israel that I am bound with this chain.
local: A lɛ jũu lɛ so ka n bʊɔrɔ ɩka n nyɛ yɩ naa 'yɛr yɩ. Bojũu a Isɩral nɩbɛ tɩɛrtɔfʊ jie jũu lɛ ba gbar ma nɩ a bɔ̃jɔrsɩ anyãna.

eng: They responded, We havent received any letters about you from Judea, nor have any of our brothers come and reported or said anything bad about you.
local: Ka ba 'yɛr, Saa nyɛ gãn jaa yi a Judiya dẽme sɛ̃ ɛ, ka a sɩ yɛɛr bala mɩ na yi a be wa ba 'yɛr a fʊ 'yɛrdɛɛr jaa kʊ sɩ ɛ.

eng: But we think its important to hear what you think, for we know that people everywhere are speaking against this faction.
local: Tɩchɛ sɩn bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka sɩ bɔ̃ɔ a lɛ fʊn nyɔɔ, bojũu sɩ bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka jie jaa nɩbɛ 'laar naa a yele nyãna.

eng: On the day scheduled for this purpose, many people came to the place where he was staying. From morning until evening, he explained and testified concerning Gods kingdom and tried to convince them about Jesus through appealing to the Law from Moses and the Prophets.
local: Ba 'yɔ̃ɔn bɩbir ban lɛ wa a Pɔɔl sɛ̃, a bɩbir na wa vɩɛ ba wan yɔɔ jaa a be wʊn kpɩɛrɛ. An yi a bibio tɩ tãn a jãanʊɔr na wʊ mana a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ kʊrɔ ba, na wʊ de a Mosesɩ nɛɛ nɩ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ na sɛb bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ lɩɛb a ba tɩɛr ka ba sɔɔ a Yesu.

eng: Some were persuaded by what he said, but others refused to believe.
local: Bamɩne lɩɛb ba tɩɛr a lɛ wʊn 'yɛr, tɩchɛ ka a bamɩne ba sɔɔ de ɛ.

eng: They disagreed with each other and were starting to leave when Paul made one more statement: The Holy Spirit spoke correctly when he said to your ancestors through Isaiah the prophet.
local: Ba chɩrɛ nɩ taa na ba piel yire lɛ a Pɔɔl na wa 'yɛr a wʊ 'yɛr baaraa na wʊ 'yɛr, A Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ 'yɛr yelmãɛ kʊ a yɩ sãakpãmɩne a lɛ wʊn de a wʊ 'yɛ'yɛrɛ Isaiya nɛɛ.

eng: Go to this people and say: You will hear, to be sure, but never understand; and you will certainly see but never recognize what you are seeing. .
local: 'yɛr, Chen a nɩbɛ banyãna sɛ̃ tɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba, Yɩn wõn lɛ̃ɛɛ tɩ yɩ kʊ̃ wa bɔ̃ɔ a par ɛ, yɩn fur nɩbir 'yɔ̃ɔ tɩ yɩ kʊ wa nyɛ bon jaa ɛ.

eng: This peoples senses have become calloused, and theyve become hard of hearing, and theyve shut their eyes so that they wont see with their eyes or hear with their ears or understand with their minds, and change their hearts and lives that I may heal them. .
local: Bojũu a nɩbɛ banyãna nyãn kpɛ̃mɛ naa, baa wõnõ ɛ, ba ma ũun a ba nɩbie, ka lɛ ba lɛ ɛ, ban taa nyɛn naa ba nɩbie na ba wõn a ba tobo bɔ̃ɔ a par na ba lɩɛb wa a n sɛ̃ ka n sãa ba.

eng: Therefore, be certain of this: Gods salvation has been sent to the Gentiles. They will listen.
local: A lɛ jũu, n bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka yɩ bɔ̃ɔ ɩka a Nãaŋmɩn den a wʊ faafʊ 'yɛr tɩ kʊ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ na chɛlɛ naa.

eng: Paul lived in his own rented quarters for two full years and welcomed everyone who came to see him.
local: Pɔɔl yab a yir 'lɔ pɔ wʊn kpɩɛrɛ yuom ayi, tɩ ma de nɩrɛ jaa na ma wa a wʊ sɛ̃.

eng: Unhindered and with complete confidence, he continued to preach Gods kingdom and to teach about the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Yele jaa ba ŋmaa wʊ sɔr ɛ, ka wʊ 'lara na wʊ mʊɔlɔ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ 'yɛr na wʊ wile ba nɩ a sɩ Soro Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɛr.

eng: From Paul, a slave of Christ Jesus, called to be an apostle and set apart for Gods good news.
local: Maa Pɔɔl na ɩ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto gbãgbaa, Nãaŋmɩn na kaa ir bʊɔl ka n ɩ a wʊ nɩtʊ̃nãa naa ɩ a wʊ 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ 'yɛrɛ, lɛ sɛɛrɛ kʊrɔ a yɩn na be a Worom.

eng: God promised this good news about his Son ahead of time through his prophets in the holy scriptures. His Son was descended from David.
local: A 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyã wʊn dɔ̃ɔ tɩr nɛɛ a kõr jaa tu a wʊ 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ sɛ̃ ka ba sɛb 'yɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ.

eng: God promised this good news about his Son ahead of time through his prophets in the holy scriptures. His Son was descended from David.
local: na ɩ a wʊ Bie 'yɛr, ban dɔɔ ka wʊ ɩ a Nãa David bal nɩrɛ.

eng: He was publicly identified as Gods Son with power through his resurrection from the dead, which was based on the Spirit of holiness. This Son is Jesus Christ our Lord.
local: A tu naa a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ pɔ wiil kpɛ̃ɔ ɩka a Nãaŋmɩn Bie lɛ, na wʊ ir yi a kũn pɔ ɩ vʊr, 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto na ɩ a sɩ Soro.

eng: Through him we have received Gods grace and our appointment to be apostles. This was to bring all Gentiles to faithful obedience for his names sake.
local: A tun a wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ nɩ a wʊ yuor jũu, sɩ nyɛn wãɛfʊ nɩ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ tʊ̃mɔ̃ na sɩn bʊɔl nɩrɛ jaa, ka ba wa wõnõ a wʊ toor nɩ sɔɔfʊ.

eng: You who are called by Jesus Christ are also included among these Gentiles.
local: Yɩn mɩ pʊɔn a bala a Nãaŋmɩn na bʊɔl ka yɩ ɩ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto sofʊ.

eng: To those in Rome who are dearly loved by God and called to be Gods people. Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: N sɛɛrɛ na kʊrɔ a yɩn bala jaa na be a Worom a Nãaŋmɩn na nʊ̃, na wʊ bʊɔl yɩ a yɩ ɩ a wʊ nɩbɛ. Wãɛfʊ nɩ nyã'maar na yi a sɩ Sãa Nãaŋmɩn nɩ a sɩ Soro Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto jie a be a yɩ sɛ̃.

eng: First of all, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for all of you, because the news about your faithfulness is being spread throughout the whole world.
local: N den nie puor a maa Nãaŋmɩn yãan a yɩ jaa jũu tu a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ, bojũu a yɩ sɔɔfʊ ŋmen yaar a wɛr wʊ jaa.

eng: I serve God in my spirit by preaching the good news about Gods Son, and God is my witness that I continually mention you.
local: A Nãaŋmɩn nyã ɩn tʊ̃nɔ̃ kʊrɔ nɩ a n nyãa wʊ jaa, naa mʊɔlɔ a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyã na ɩ a wʊ Bie 'yɛr, di man dãasɩɛ lɛ ɩn ma tɩɛrɛ a yɩ yele.

eng: in all my prayers. Im always asking that somehow, by Gods will, I might succeed in visiting you at last.
local: N ma sʊɔrɔ naa Nãaŋmɩn ba gala ɛ, naa sʊɔrɔ ɩka a wʊ bɔfʊ a ɩ, ka wʊ yuo sɔr a pʊ̃pãanyã kʊ ma ka n wa a yɩ sɛ̃.

eng: I really want to see you to pass along some spiritual gift to you so that you can be strengthened.
local: N bʊɔrɔ naa yɩ nyɛfʊ naa na tʊ̃ɔ wiil yɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ kʊfʊ amɩne ka a 'yɔ̃ɔ yɩ ãkpɛ̃ɔ.

eng: What I mean is that we can mutually encourage each other while I am with you. We can be encouraged by the faithfulness we find in each other, both your faithfulness and mine.
local: Lɛ lɛ, a yɩn nɩ maa na pãa nʊ̃ɔ kpɛ̃mɛ a sɩ sɔɔfʊ pɔ.

eng: I want you to know, brothers and sisters, that I planned to visit you many times, although I have been prevented from coming until now. I want to harvest some fruit among you, just as I have done among the other Gentiles.
local: N yɛɛr, n bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka yɩ bɔ̃ɔ a lɛ ɩn mʊɔrɔ sɔ̃ɔ yɔɔ jaa, ɩka n wa a yɩ sɛ̃ tɩchɛ ka a sɔr pɔɔ wa tãn dɩna. Ɩn taa nyɛn a yɩ bamɩne a ba puoraa nɩtãa lɛ ɩn nyɛ a yɩ bamɩne na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, tɩ puoro.

eng: I have a responsibility both to Greeks and to those who dont speak Greek, both to the wise and to the foolish.
local: N din a Juu nɩbɛ nɩ a bala mɩ na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, nɩ a yɛ̃ dẽme nɩ a jɔlɔ sãn.

eng: Thats why Im ready to preach the gospel also to you who are in Rome.
local: Ala lɛ so ka n mʊɔrɔ ɩka n mʊɔl a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ kʊ a yɩn mɩ na be a Worom.

eng: Im not ashamed of the gospel: it is Gods own power for salvation to all who have faith in God, to the Jew first and also to the Greek.
local: Vã ba kʊrɔ ma nɩ a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyãna 'yɛrfʊ ɛ, bojũu a ɩ naa a Nãaŋmɩn kpɛ̃ɔ wʊn de faara nɩ nɩrɛ jaa na sɔɔ, a Juu nɩbɛ lɛ de nie, ka bala mɩ na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, mɩ tu.

eng: Gods righteousness is being revealed in the gospel, from faithfulness for faith, as it is written, The righteous person will live by faith. .
local: Bojũu a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyã na wiil nɩrɛ lɛ wʊn na tor Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃, torfʊ 'lɔ na yi sɔɔfʊ pɔ, yi a pielfʊ tɩ tãn a baaraa nɩtãa lɛ an sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ, Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na tara sɔɔfʊ, tor naa, wʊn tara na nyɛvʊr na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ.

eng: Gods wrath is being revealed from heaven against all the ungodly behavior and the injustice of human beings who silence the truth with injustice.
local: Nãaŋmɩn vɛ̃n ka a wʊ suur yele yi a saaju gã chãa pɔ, ɩka wʊ nyɛn suur 'yɔ̃ɔ a bala na ba puoro ɛ, nɩ putudẽme bala na pɔɔ a yelmãɛ nɩ a ba putuyele.

eng: This is because what is known about God should be plain to them because God made it plain to them.
local: An ɩ a ala ban na de bɔ̃ɔn a Nãaŋmɩn gãn chãa pɔ, bojũu Nãaŋmɩn mãɛ lɛ ir wiil ba.

eng: Ever since the creation of the world, Gods invisible qualities—Gods eternal power and divine nature—have been clearly seen, because they are understood through the things God has made. So humans are without excuse.
local: Bojũu, an yi a lɛ daar jaa a Nãaŋmɩn na mɛ a wɛr, nɩbɛ na tʊ̃ɔ bɔ̃ɔ ɩka a Nãaŋmɩn kpɛ̃ɔ ba tara baarfʊ ɛ, ka wʊ ɩ Nãaŋmɩn mãɛ nɩ a bonsɩ ala wʊn maal, a lɛ jũu, tu yifʊ jie ba lɛ be be go ɛ.

eng: Although they knew God, they didnt honor God as God or thank him. Instead, their reasoning became pointless, and their foolish hearts were darkened.
local: Bojũu, ban sɔɔ bɔ̃ɔn a Nãaŋmɩn a nɩtɔ̃nɔ̃, tɩ baa kʊ wʊ tɩɩr ɛ, bɩɩ dãna wʊ ɛ, tɩchɛ a ba tɩɛrsɩ ɩn burbur, ka a ba jɔl nyãn ala pãa lii.

eng: While they were claiming to be wise, they made fools of themselves.
local: Ban sɔɔ 'yɛr ɩka yɛ̃ bɔ̃ɔnbɔ lɛ ba ɩ, ba ɩn jɔlɔ.

eng: They exchanged the glory of the immortal God for images that look like mortal humans: birds, animals, and reptiles.
local: an ɩ ba lɩɛb a tɩɩr 'lɔ ban taa kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn nyã na ba kpire ɛ, na ba kʊ a bʊtɩbɛ ala ban pɛ̃n, ka a kaara a nɩbɛ kaar na kpire, nɩ lubil, nɩ dʊ̃n, nɩ dʊ̃gbɛɛ anaar jaa bʊrɔ.

eng: So God abandoned them to their hearts desires, which led to the moral corruption of degrading their own bodies with each other.
local: Ala lɛ so ka Nãaŋmɩn ir nũu tɩchɛ ba bar be a ba vɔldɛɛr pɔ, ka ba bʊ̃nɔ̃ gãna nɩ taa, sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ taa nɩ a ba ãgãma.

eng: They traded Gods truth for a lie, and they worshipped and served the creation instead of the creator, who is blessed forever. Amen.
local: Ba den a Nãaŋmɩn yelmãɛ tɛɛn jire, na ba puoro tɩ tʊ̃nɔ̃ kʊrɔ a bonsɩ ala a Nãaŋmɩn na maal, tɩ bar a Nãaŋmɩn na ɩ a ma maala 'lɔ a pɛɛfʊ na sɛɛn bɩbir jaa. A ɩ a lɛ.

eng: Thats why God abandoned them to degrading lust. Their females traded natural sexual relations for unnatural sexual relations.
local: A anyãna jũu, Nãaŋmɩn de ban bar be a vã vɔlaa pɔ. Ka a ba pɔbɔ gba ma lɩɛb gãna nɩ a ba pɔɔ taa ba.

eng: Also, in the same way, the males traded natural sexual relations with females, and burned with lust for each other. Males performed shameful actions with males, and they were paid back with the penalty they deserved for their mistake in their own bodies.
local: A lɛ nɩ wʊ ju lɛ a dɔɔr mɩ bar a pɔbɔ bɔfʊ, ka a vɔlaa vɛ̃ ka ba lɩɛb bʊɔrɔ a ba taa dɔɔr. Dɔɔr na lɩɛb tʊ̃nɔ̃ a vã tʊ̃mɔ̃ nyãna nɩ a ba taa dɔɔr ba nyɛn a ba tʊ̃dɛɛr toor tɔɔfʊ.

eng: Since they didnt think it was worthwhile to acknowledge God, God abandoned them to a defective mind to do inappropriate things.
local: Baa nyɛ a Nãaŋmɩn bɔ̃ɔfʊ a wʊ sɛɛn ba ɛ, ka a Nãaŋmɩn bar ba a be a ba tɩɛr sɔ̃ɔnaa pɔ, ka ba maala a ala na ba sɛɛn maalfʊ ɛ.

eng: So they were filled with all injustice, wicked behavior, greed, and evil behavior. They are full of jealousy, murder, fighting, deception, and malice. They are gossips.
local: Ba sɛɛn naa putuo jaa bʊrɔ, bier ɩfʊ, bɔgar dẽme, nɩ bier maalfʊ. Nyuur sɛɛn ba naa, na ba ɩ nɩkʊrbɔ, bʊɔrɔ jɛɛr, na ba bɛlɛ taa, na ba tɩɛrɛ taa sɔ̃ɔfʊ. Ba ɩn gagarsɩ.

eng: they slander people, and they hate God. They are rude and proud, and they brag. They invent ways to be evil, and they are disobedient to their parents.
local: sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ nɩ taa yoe, 'laar Nãaŋmɩn, kpɛrɛ taa, mãɛ 'mɔɔfʊ nɩ yobɔyele. Ba bɔ̃ɔn sɔɩ ban ma tu maal bier, baa turo a nɩbɛ bala na dɔɔ ba nɛɛ ɛ.

eng: They are without understanding, disloyal, without affection, and without mercy.
local: Baa tara tɩɛr ɛ, baa tara yelmãɛ ɛ, baa tara nʊ̃fʊ ɛ, baa kaara nɩrɛ nɩbaal ɛ.

eng: Though they know Gods decision that those who persist in such practices deserve death, they not only keep doing these things but also approve others who practice them.
local: Ban sɔɔ bɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn toraa nyãna binfʊ, ɩka a nɩbɛ bala na maala a anyãna sɛɛn nɩ kũu, bala mãɛ tɛɛ ba lɛ maala a anyãna ɛ, tɩchɛ ba ma sɔɔ naa a bala na tʊ̃nɔ̃ a anyãna.

eng: So every single one of you who judge others is without any excuse. You condemn yourself when you judge another person because the one who is judging is doing the same things.
local: Yɩn a lɛ jũu, yaa mɩ tara tu yifʊ jie ɛ, a yɩn bala na ma tɔɔrɔ a bamɩne 'yɛr. Yele jaa pɔ fʊn tɔɔr a fʊ tɔ sʊɔ 'yɛr fʊ mɩ 'yɔ̃ɔn fʊ mãɛ 'yɛr, bojũu fʊ mɩ ɩn a yele 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ a fʊ tɔ sʊɔ na ɩ, a fʊ tɔɔr a wʊ 'yɛr.

eng: We know that Gods judgment agrees with the truth, and his judgment is against those who do these kinds of things.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã, sɩ bɔ̃ɔ naa ɩka Nãaŋmɩn na tɔɔr naa a bala na ɩrɛ a yelsɩ anyãna 'yɛr tun yelmãɛ sɔr pɔ.

eng: If you judge those who do these kinds of things while you do the same things yourself, think about this: Do you believe that you will escape Gods judgment?.
local: A lɛ a fʊ̃ʊ nɩsaal mɩ na tɔɔrɔ nɩbɛ 'yɛr, tɩ na fʊ mɩ maal a yele ala nɩ a ju, fʊ tɩɛr ɩka fʊn pɔl yi a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ pɔ ɔ?.

eng: Or do you have contempt for the riches of Gods generosity, tolerance, and patience? Dont you realize that Gods kindness is supposed to lead you to change your heart and life?.
local: Bɩɩ yɩ nyɛ naa Nãaŋmɩn yɛ̃ bɔ̃ɔn yɔɔ jũu, nɩ a wʊ maalvɩla, nɩ a wʊ kãnyir, na yaa bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a Nãaŋmɩn maalvɩla wile yɩn tɩɛr lɩɛb fʊ sɔr ɛ?.

eng: You are storing up wrath for yourself because of your stubbornness and your heart that refuses to change. Gods just judgment will be revealed on the day of wrath.
local: Tɩchɛ a yɩ nyãkpãɛn nɩ yɩn ba lɩɛb a yɩ tɩɛr jũu, yɩ guoro naa a Nãaŋmɩn sutuo bine kʊrɔ a yɩ mãɛ guro nɩ a wʊ suur bɩbir 'lɔ wʊn na tɔɔr nɩrɛ jaa 'yɛr nɩ torfʊ.

eng: God will repay everyone based on their works .
local: Nãaŋmɩn na yab nɩrɛ jaa lɛ wʊn tũ jã.

eng: On the one hand, he will give eternal life to those who look for glory, honor, and immortality based on their patient good work.
local: Bala na mar ara na ba tʊ̃nɔ̃ vɩla bʊɔrɔ tɩɩr, nɩ 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ, nɩ a ala na ba pʊ̃ɔnɔ̃ ɛ, wʊn kʊ ban nyɛvʊr na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ.
