eng: But on the other hand, there will be wrath and anger for those who obey wickedness instead of the truth because they are acting out of selfishness and disobedience.
local: Tɩchɛ bala na bʊɔrɔ a ba mãɛ bɔb, na ba tɔr a yelmãɛ tɩ turo dɛɛr, Nãaŋmɩn suur nɩ a wʊ kpãafʊ gãn kʊ ba.

eng: There will be trouble and distress for every human being who does evil, for the Jew first and also for the Greek.
local: Wõm nɩ tuo difʊ na ben be kʊ nɩsaal jaa na ɩrɛ yelbier. An piel naa a Juu nɩbɛ sɛ̃, tɩ mɩ lʊ̃ɔ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ.

eng: But there will be glory, honor, and peace for everyone who does what is good, for the Jew first and also for the Greek.
local: Tɩchɛ tɩɩr nɩ 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ nɩ nyã'maar na ben be kʊ nɩrɛ jaa na maala vɩla, a Juu nɩbɛ nɩ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ.

eng: God does not have favorites.
local: Bojũu Nãaŋmɩn ba tara kaa gar taa ɛ.

eng: Those who have sinned outside the Law will also die outside the Law, and those who have sinned under the Law will be judged by the Law.
local: Bala jaa na ba bɔ̃ɔ a Mosesɩ nɛɛ tɩ tʊ̃ dɛɛr, mɩn wɛ naa ban ba bɔ̃ɔ a nɛɛ jũu, ka bala jaa mɩn bɔ̃ɔ a Mosesɩ nɛɛ tɩ tʊ̃ a dɛɛr Nãaŋmɩn na den a nɛɛ nyã tɔɔr a ba 'yɛr.

eng: It isnt the ones who hear the Law who are righteous in Gods eyes. It is the ones who do what the Law says who will be treated as righteous.
local: Bojũu a ba ɩ a bala na wõnõ a nɛɛ lɛ tor a Nãaŋmɩn niem ɛ, tɩchɛ a bala na turo a lɛ a nɛɛ na 'yɛr bala lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na 'yɛr ɩka ba tor naa.

eng: Gentiles dont have the Law. But when they instinctively do what the Law requires they are a Law in themselves, though they dont have the Law.
local: Sɩrɛ jaa a bala jaa na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, na baa tara a Mosesɩ nɛbinãa ɛ, ba gba ma ɩ naa a yelsɩ a nɛɛ na 'yɛr, bojũu bala mãɛ ɩn a nɛɛ ban sɔɔ ba tara a nɛɛ ɛ.

eng: They show the proof of the Law written on their hearts, and their consciences affirm it. Their conflicting thoughts will accuse them, or even make a defense for them.
local: Ba wiil ɩka a lɛ a nɛɛ na bʊɔrɔ sɛb 'yɔ̃ɔ be a ba nyãa pɔ, ka a ba tɩɛr mɩ di dãasɩɛ ma gɔ̃nɔ̃ ba bɩɩ 'yɛrɛ pʊɔrɔ ba.

eng: on the day when, according to my gospel, God will judge the hidden truth about human beings through Christ Jesus.
local: A anyãna na ɩ naa a daar 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ a Nãaŋmɩn na tu a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ na wʊ tɔɔr nɩrɛ jaa yelsɔɔlsɩ nɩtãa lɛ ɩn 'yɛrɛ a n 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ.

eng: But, if you call yourself a Jew; if you rely on the Law; if you brag about your relationship to God.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã a fʊ̃ʊ, ala ɩ fʊ bʊɔlɔ na a fʊ mãɛ Juu nɩrɛ, na fʊ dɩɛl a Mosesɩ nɛɛ na fʊ jɩrɛ lɛ fʊ̃ʊ nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn na tara nɛbʊ̃'yen.

eng: if you know the will of God; if you are taught by the Law so that you can figure out the things that really matter.
local: ala ɩ fʊ bɔ̃ɔ naa a wʊ bɔfʊ na fʊ bɔ̃ɔ a 'lɔ na gar a jaa bojũu a nɛɛ lɛ wiil fʊ.

eng: if you have persuaded yourself that you are: a guide for the blind; a light to those who are in darkness.
local: ala ɩ fʊ chɛn nyãa ɩka jɔ̃tɔɔrɔ lɛ fʊ ɩ, na fʊ ɩ chãa chaara a bala na be a libe pɔ.

eng: an educator of the foolish; a teacher of infants.
local: na fʊ wile a jɔlɔ, na fʊ wile a bibiir, bojũu a Mosesɩ nɛɛ pɔ lɛ fʊ nyɛ a bɔ̃ɔfʊ nɩ a yelmãɛ.

eng: So whats the advantage of being a Jew? Or whats the benefit of circumcision?.
local: Bo tɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ a Juu nɩrɛ tara, bɩɩ bo tɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ be be a yʊɔr ŋmaafʊ pɔ?.

eng: Plenty in every way. First of all, the Jews were trusted with Gods revelations.
local: Sɔr jaa pɔ, ala na de nie lɛ ɩka bala lɛ Nãaŋmɩn de nie kʊ a wʊ 'yɛrbie.

eng: then why dont you who are teaching others teach yourself? If you preach, No stealing, do you steal?.
local: Fʊ̃ʊ nyã na wile a fʊ taaba, faa wiil a fʊ mãɛ ɛ? Fʊ̃ʊ nyã na ma 'yɛrɛ ɩka jufʊ ba ɩ ɛ, fʊ ma ju naa ɔ?.

eng: What does it matter, then, if some werent faithful? Their lack of faith wont cancel Gods faithfulness, will it?.
local: Bo lɛ na ɩ ala ɩ bamɩne ba tara sɔɔfʊ ɛ? A ba ba sɔɔfʊ na tʊ̃ɔn sɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn yelmãɛ bar bɩɩ?.

eng: Absolutely not! God must be true, even if every human being is a liar, as it is written: So that it can show that you are right in your words; and you will triumph when you are judged. .
local: Kai, sɔr ba be be ɛ! Vɛ̃ a Nãaŋmɩn ɩ yelmãɛ sʊɔ, tɩchɛ ka a nɩrɛ jaa ɩ jir ŋmara. Nɩtãa lɛ an sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ, Lɛ lɛ na wiil ɩka fʊ tor naa a fʊ 'yɛrbie pɔ, na fʊ tʊ̃ɔ a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ jie.

eng: If you say, No adultery, do you commit adultery? If you hate idols, do you rob temples?.
local: Fʊ̃ʊ na ma 'yɛrɛ ɩka nɩbɛ ba ta sɛ̃nɛ ɛ, fʊ ma sɛ̃n bɩɩ? Fʊ̃ʊ nyã na kpɛrɛ a tɩbɛ tɩ fʊ ma juro naa a tɩbɛ der bonsɩ bɩɩ?.

eng: If you brag about the Law, do you shame God by breaking the Law?.
local: Fʊ̃ʊ nyã na jɩrɛ nɩ a Mosesɩ nɛɛ nyã, fʊ ma 'yɔ̃ɔn Nãaŋmɩn vã fʊn sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a nɛɛ ɔ?.

eng: As it is written: The name of God is discredited by the Gentiles because of you .
local: Nɩtãa lɛ an sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ, A yɩ jũu lɛ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a n yuor.

eng: But if our lack of righteousness confirms Gods justice, what will we say? That God, who brings wrath upon us, isnt just ?.
local: Tɩchɛ ala ɩ a sɩ ba torfʊ vɛ̃n ka Nãaŋmɩn torfʊ gã chãa pɔ, sɩ lɛ 'yɛr bo? Sɩ 'yɛr ɩka Nãaŋmɩn ba tor ɛ, wʊn vɛ̃ ka a wʊ sutuo dire sɩ ɔ? Nɩsaal nɛchɩrɛ lɛ a nyãna.

eng: Circumcision is an advantage if you do what the Law says. But if you are a person who breaks the Law, your status of being circumcised has changed into not being circumcised.
local: Yʊɔr ŋmaafʊ tara na tɔ̃nɔ̃ ala ɩ fʊ turo naa lɛ a nɛɛ na 'yɛr, tɩchɛ ala ɩ fʊ̃ʊ baa turo a nɛɛ ɛ, fʊn kaara fɩka faa ŋmaa yʊɔr ɛ.

eng: Absolutely not! If God werent just, how could he judge the world?.
local: Sɩrɛ jaa ba lɛ ɛ. Ala ɩ lɛ lɛ, ŋmɩnɛ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na ɩ tɔɔr a wɛr nɩbɛ 'yɛr?.

eng: But if Gods truth is demonstrated by my lie and it increases his glory, why am I still judged as a sinner?.
local: Nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ na chɩrɛ na, Ka ala ɩ a n ba torfʊ wiil naa ɩka a Nãaŋmɩn ɩn yelmãɛ sʊɔ ka a wʊ tɩɩr pʊɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ, bãa 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na lɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ ma 'yɛr ɩka yelbe-ɩrɛ lɛ n ɩ.

eng: So if the person who isnt circumcised keeps the Law, wont his status of not being circumcised be counted as if he were circumcised?.
local: Ala ɩ a bala na ba ŋmaa a yʊɔr ɛ, turo na lɛ a nɛɛ na bin, ba kʊ̃ kaara fɩka yɔŋmarba lɛ ba ɩ ɛ?.

eng: Why not say, Lets do evil things so that good things will come out of it?.
local: Bo lɛ so ka sɩ kʊ̃ 'yɛr nɩtãa lɛ a yosɔ̃ɔnbɔ na 'yɛr ka a bamɩne mɩ 'yɛrɛ ɩka sɩ 'yɛr ɩka, Yɩ ɩ a sɩ maala dɛɛr tɩ ka a lɩɛrɛ vɩla? A ba sɔ̃ɔfʊ sɛɛn ba naa.

eng: The one who isnt physically circumcised but keeps the Law will judge you. You became a lawbreaker after you had the written Law and circumcision.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ba ŋmaa a yʊɔr a ãgãn pɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ na wʊ turo a nɛɛ jaa nɛ nɛ nɛ na 'yɔ̃ɔ yɩn 'yɛr, a yɩn bala gba na tara a nɛbinsɩ gãn na fʊ ŋmaa a yʊɔr tɩ faa turo a nɛbinsɩ ɛ.

eng: So what are we saying? Are we better off? Not at all. We have already stated the charge: both Jews and Greeks are all under the power of sin.
local: Bãa lɛ sɩn pãa de baar? Sɩn lɛ sa ba bɩɩ? Kai, sɔr ba be be ɛ. Sɩ den nie 'yɛr ɩka a Juu nɩbɛ nɩ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, ba jaa nyɛntaa na ba be a yelbier pɔ.

eng: As it is written, There is no righteous person, not even one. .
local: Nɩtãa lɛ ban sɛb a ba yele 'yɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ, Nɩtoraa jaa ba be be ɛ, nɩbʊ̃'yen gba ba be be ɛ.

eng: It isnt the Jew who maintains outward appearances who will receive praise from God, and it isnt people who are outwardly circumcised on their bodies.
local: Nɩrɛ na ɩ a Juu nɩrɛ ba ɩ a ãgãn nyã yele tɛɛ lɛ ɛ, bɩɩ a yʊɔr ŋmaafʊ na ɩ a ãgãn yele ɛ.

eng: Instead, it is the person who is a Jew inside, who is circumcised in spirit, not literally. That persons praise doesnt come from people but from God.
local: Kai, wʊ ɩn a Juu nɩrɛ na be a pʊɔm, ka a wʊ yʊɔr ŋmaafʊ ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na ŋmaa a wʊ nyãa yʊɔr a ba yi a nɛbinsɩ gãn pɔ ɛ. A nɩrɛ nyãna pɛɛfʊ ba yire nɩbɛ sɛ̃ ɛ, tɩchɛ a yin Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃.

eng: There is no one who understands. There is no one who looks for God. .
local: Nɩbʊ̃'yen jaa ba be be bɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ 'yɛr par ɛ, nɩbʊ̃'yen jaa ba yʊɔrɔ bʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn ɛ.

eng: They all turned away. They have become worthless together. There is no one who shows kindness. There is not even one. .
local: Ba jaa lɩɛb puor, ka ba jaa lɩɛb ɩ nɩsɔ̃ɔnsɩ. Ɩ̃sʊɔ jaa ba maala vɩla ɛ, nɩbʊ̃'yen gba ba be be ɛ.

eng: Their throat is a grave that has been opened. They are deceitful with their tongues, and the poison of vipers is under their lips. .
local: A ba kɔkɔbɔɔr yuon fɩka yar, ka a ba jãlbie bɔ̃ɔ bɛlfʊ. Dʊ̃pãn lɔɔ jɛlaa a ba nɛgãma.

eng: Their mouths are full of cursing and bitterness. .
local: Pɔ'yɔ̃ɔfʊ nɩ 'yɛr bitor lɛ ma yire a ba nɛɛr pɔ.

eng: Their feet are quick to shed blood; .
local: A ba gbɛɛ ma pɔɔrɔ naa jãɩ kpaarfʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: destruction and misery are in their ways; .
local: Sɔr jaa ban de, sɔ̃ɔfʊ nɩ dɔɔyɛ lɛ ma ɩ a jãnɛ.

eng: and they dont know the way of peace. .
local: Baa bɔ̃ɔ nyã'maar sɔr ɛ.

eng: There is no fear of God in their view of the world. .
local: A ba nɩbie ba joro Nãaŋmɩn dãbãɛ ɛ.

eng: Now we know that whatever the Law says, it speaks to those who are under the Law, in order to shut every mouth and make it so the whole world has to answer to God.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã, sɩ bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka lɛ jaa a Mosesɩ nɛɛ na 'yɛr, wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a bala na be a nɛɛ pɔ ɩka, a kpal nɩrɛ jaa nɛɛ, ka a wɛr nɩbɛ ba jaa wa man a ba nɛɛ 'yɛr kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: It follows that no human being will be treated as righteous in his presence by doing what the Law says, because the knowledge of sin comes through the Law.
local: A lɛ jũu, nɩrɛ jaa kʊ̃ ɩ nɩtor a Nãaŋmɩn niem wʊn turo a nɛɛ jũu ɛ, an ɩ a tun a nɛɛ nyã pɔ, ka sɩ bɔ̃ɔ bier.

eng: But now Gods righteousness has been revealed apart from the Law, which is confirmed by the Law and the Prophets.
local: Tɩchɛ pʊ̃pãanyã, a chaar naa ɩka a torfʊ na yi a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃, ba yi a Mosesɩ nɛɛ pɔ ɛ, ala lɛ a nɛɛ nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ di dãasɩɛ.

eng: Gods righteousness comes through the faithfulness of Jesus Christ for all who have faith in him. Theres no distinction.
local: A torfʊ nyã na yi a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃, ɩn a sɔɔfʊ na tu a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ, kʊ a bala jaa na sɔɔ de. Tɛɛ taa jaa ba be be ɛ.

eng: All have sinned and fall short of Gods glory.
local: Bojũu nɩrɛ jaa maal dɛɛr na baa ta a Nãaŋmɩn tɩɩr ɛ.

eng: but all are treated as righteous freely by his grace because of a ransom that was paid by Christ Jesus.
local: A Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ jũu, wʊ vɛ̃n ka sɩ tor na wʊ 'yɛrfaa sɩ pɔrɔ tu a faafʊ 'lɔ na yi a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto sɛ̃.

eng: Through his faithfulness, God displayed Jesus as the place of sacrifice where mercy is found by means of his blood. He did this to demonstrate his righteousness in passing over sins that happened before.
local: Nãaŋmɩn de wʊn a wʊ ɩ a bɔɔrlo bon, a wʊn de a wʊ jãɩ pɛɛn nɩrɛ jaa tu a sɔɔfʊ jũu. Nãaŋmɩn maal a anyã na wʊ wiil a wʊ torfʊ, bojũu a wʊ kãnyir vɛ̃ naa ka wʊ bar a ba yeldɛbɛ ala ban dɔ̃ɔ maal gefʊ.

eng: during the time of Gods patient tolerance. He also did this to demonstrate that he is righteous in the present time, and to treat the one who has faith in Jesus as righteous.
local: Wʊ maal naa lɛ na wiil a wʊ torfʊ a pʊ̃pãanyã, na wʊ ma vɛ̃ ka nɩrɛ jaa na sɔɔ de a Yesu yele, tor.

eng: What happens to our bragging? Its thrown out. With which law? With what we have accomplished under the Law?.
local: Nyɩnɛ lɛ a yobɔyele lɛ be? A ba pʊɔ be ɛ. Yelbuor jũu? A Mosesɩ nɛɛ ban turo jũu bɩɩ? Kai, sɔɔfʊ jũu lɛ.

eng: No, not at all, but through the law of faith. We consider that a person is treated as righteous by faith, apart from what is accomplished under the Law.
local: Ala lɛ so ka sɩn chɛnɛ sɔɔ ɩka nɩrɛ yele ma tor naa wʊn tara sɔɔfʊ a ba ɩ wʊn turo a nɛbinãa jũu ɛ.

eng: Or is God the God of Jews only? Isnt God the God of Gentiles also? Yes, God is also the God of Gentiles.
local: Nãaŋmɩn ɩn a Juu nɩbɛ tɛɛ Nãaŋmɩn bɩɩ? Waa ɩ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ mɩ Nãaŋmɩn ɛ? Ɔ̃ɔ, wʊ ɩn a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ mɩ tʊɔr.

eng: Since God is one, then the one who makes the circumcised righteous by faith will also make the one who isnt circumcised righteous through faith.
local: An ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn bʊ̃'yen tɛɛ lɛ be be, 'lɔ lɛ na vɛ̃ ka a yɔŋmarba yele tor sɔɔfʊ jũu, na wʊ mɩ vɛ̃ ka a bala na ba ŋmaa a yʊɔr ɛ, yele mɩ tor a sɔɔfʊ jũu.

eng: Do we then cancel the Law through this faith? Absolutely not! Instead, we confirm the Law.
local: Sɩ pãa den a sɔɔfʊ nyã sɔ̃ɔn a nɛbinãa bar bɩɩ? Kai, sɔr ba be be ɛ. Sɩn lɛ pãa bɔ̃ɔ turo wʊ.

eng: Therefore, since we have been made righteous through his faithfulness, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Wʊn ben vɛ̃ a sɩ tor a sɩ sɔɔfʊ jũu, sɩ tara na nyã'maar Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ tu a sɩ Soro Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: So what are we going to say? Should we continue sinning so grace will multiply?.
local: Sɩ pãa 'yɛr ɩka bo? Sɩ chɛnɛ ɩrɛ a yelbier tɩchɛ ka a Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ pʊɔrɔ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃?.

eng: So what are we going to say? Are we going to find that Abraham is our ancestor on the basis of genealogy?.
local: Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ sɩn pãa 'yɛr a sɩ sãakpã Abraham 'yɛr, a yele nyãna pɔ?.

eng: We have access by faith into this grace in which we stand through him, and we boast in the hope of Gods glory.
local: A wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ sɩ tu nyɛ sɔr a sɩ sɔɔfʊ jũu kpɛ a Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ nyã pɔ sɩn ben ara a pʊ̃pãanyã, na sɩ chɩlɛ a Nãaŋmɩn tɩɩr tɩɛrtɔ nyã jũu.

eng: Absolutely not! All of us died to sin. How can we still live in it?.
local: Kai, sɔr ba be be ɛ! Sɩ kpin kʊ yelbier, ŋmɩnɛ lɛ a ɩ a sɩn lɛ chɛnɛ be a pɔ?.

eng: But not only that! We even take pride in our problems, because we know that trouble produces endurance.
local: A ba ɩ lɛ tɛɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ sɩ mɩ chɩlɛ na a sɩ dɔɔyɛ pɔ, bojũu sɩ bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka a dɔɔyɛ lɛ na waan kãnyir.

eng: Because if Abraham was made righteous because of his actions, he would have had a reason to brag, but not in front of God.
local: Ala ɩ sɩrɛ jaa Abraham yele dɔ̃ɔ tor a wʊ tʊ̃mɔ̃ jũu, wʊn taa tara na yele kɔ̃ɔ bʊɔrɔ nɩ yuor, tɩchɛ a kʊ̃ naa ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn niem lɛ ɛ.

eng: endurance produces character, and character produces hope.
local: ka a kãnyir wiil a lɛ fʊn be, ka a lɛ fʊn be mɩ waan tɩɛrtɔfʊ jie.

eng: Or dont you know that all who were baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death?.
local: Bɩɩ yaa bɔ̃ɔ ɩka a sɩn bala jaa na so a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ, wʊ pɔl sɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto pɔ, a sɩ lɔ̃ɔ kpin wʊ nɩ a wʊ kũu pɔ ɛ?.

eng: What does the scripture say? Abraham had faith in God, and it was credited to him as righteousness .
local: Bo lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn gãn 'yɛr? Wʊ 'yɛr ɩka, Abraham sɔɔn de Nãaŋmɩn ka a lɛ vɛ̃ ka wʊ ɩ nɩtor.

eng: This hope doesnt put us to shame, because the love of God has been poured out in our hearts through the Holy Spirit, who has been given to us.
local: Ka a tɩɛrtɔfʊ jie kʊ̃ 'yɔ̃ɔ sɩ vã ɛ, bojũu Nãaŋmɩn kpaar naa a wʊ nʊ̃fʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ a sɩ nyãn pɔ, tu a wʊ Sɩɛ 'lɔ wʊn de kʊ sɩ.

eng: Therefore, we were buried together with him through baptism into his death, so that just as Christ was raised from the dead through the glory of the Father, we too can walk in newness of life.
local: A lɛ jũu, ba ũu sɩ naa a kʊ̃ɔ pɔ, a kũu kaar, ka a lɛ a Kɩrɩsɩto na dɔ̃ɔ ir a kũu pɔ, tu a Sãa tɩɩr pɔ, ka sɩ mɩ na be be tara nyɛvʊr paalaa.

eng: Workers salaries arent credited to them on the basis of an employers grace but rather on the basis of what they deserve.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã nɩrɛ wa tʊ̃tʊ̃mɔ̃, a wʊ sãyar ba ɩ kʊfʊ ɛ, tɩchɛ a wʊ tʊ̃mɔ̃ sãyar lɛ.

eng: While we were still weak, at the right moment, Christ died for ungodly people.
local: Yaa nyɛ, a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ na sɛɛ lɛ, sɩn sɔɔ chɛnɛ ba tara kpɛ̃ɔ ɛ, tɩ Kɩrɩsɩto kpin kʊ a bala na ba puoro ɛ.

eng: If we were united together in a death like his, we will also be united together in a resurrection like his.
local: Ala ɩ sɩn nɩ 'lɔ 'mantaa a wʊ kũu pɔ, sɩn nɩ 'lɔ na 'mantaa ir a kũu pɔ.

eng: But faith is credited as righteousness to those who dont work, because they have faith in God who makes the ungodly righteous.
local: Ala lɛ so nɩrɛ 'lɔ an ba ɩ tʊ̃mɔ̃ lɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ wʊn sɔɔ a Nãaŋmɩn 'lɔ na ma vɛ̃ ka a putudẽme yele tor, a wʊ sɔɔfʊ lɛ ma vɛ̃ ka wʊ ɩ a nɩtor.

eng: It isnt often that someone will die for a righteous person, though maybe someone might dare to die for a good person.
local: An kpɛ̃mɛ naa ɩka nɩrɛ na sɔɔn kpi kʊ nɩrɛ yele na tor, amɩne nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ na tʊ̃ɔn sɔɔ kpi kʊ nɩrɛ na ɩ nɩsʊ̃.

eng: But God shows his love for us, because while we were still sinners Christ died for us.
local: Tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn wiil a wʊ mãɛ nʊ̃fʊ a nɩtɔ̃ kʊ sɩ, a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ sɩn chɛnɛ ɩ yelbe-ɩrbɛ, Kɩrɩsɩto kpin kʊ sɩ.

eng: This is what we know: the person that we used to be was crucified with him in order to get rid of the corpse that had been controlled by sin. That way we wouldnt be slaves to sin anymore.
local: Sɩ bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka a sɩ ãgãn koraa pʊɔ naa a Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ, a ba kpan wʊ a be a daa ju, ka a yelbier ãgãn ba lɛ tara kpɛ̃ɔ a sɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ, saa lɛ ɩ a yelbier gbãgbaar ɛ.

eng: So, now that we have been made righteous by his blood, we can be even more certain that we will be saved from Gods wrath through him.
local: An ben ɩka pʊ̃pãanyã wʊ ben vɛ̃ a sɩ tor a wʊ jãɩ jũu, ŋmɩnɛ lɛ an pãa kaara wʊn faa sɩ yin a Nãaŋmɩn suur pɔ.

eng: because a person who has died has been freed from sins power.
local: Bojũu nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na kpi, wʊ yin yelbier pɔ.

eng: In the same way, David also pronounces a blessing on the person to whom God credits righteousness apart from actions.
local: A Nãa David mɩ dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr a 'yɛr bir nyãna nɩ wʊ ju, na wʊ 'yɛr ɩka, bɔɔsʊ̃n sʊɔ lɛ a 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn na vɛ̃ a wʊ ɩ nɩtor ka tʊ̃mɔ̃ ba pʊɔ ɛ.

eng: If we were reconciled to God through the death of his Son while we were still enemies, now that we have been reconciled, how much more certain is it that we will be saved by his life?.
local: Bojũu ala ɩ a lɛ sɩn dɔ̃ɔ ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn dɔ̃dẽme, ka a wʊ Bie kũu wa 'maal wʊ kʊ sɩ, a lɛ wʊn ben 'maal sɩ, wʊn faa sɩ naa tu a wʊ nyɛvʊr pɔ.

eng: But if we died with Christ, we have faith that we will also live with him.
local: Ala ɩ sɩn nɩ Kɩrɩsɩto lɛ 'mataa kpi, sɩ sɔɔ naa ɩka sɩn nɩ 'lɔ lɛ na 'mataa be be tara a nyɛvʊr.

eng: Happy are those whose actions outside the Law are forgiven, and whose sins are covered. .
local: Bɔɔsʊ̃n dẽme lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na vɛ̃ a ba bɔrfʊsɩ kʊ ba, ka a ba yelbebe ala pɔɔ.

eng: We know that Christ has been raised from the dead and he will never die again. Death no longer has power over him.
local: Bojũu sɩ bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka a Kɩrɩsɩto na ir a kũu pɔ, wʊ kʊ̃ lɛ kpi go ɛ, kũu ba lɛ tʊ̃ɔ wʊ ɛ.

eng: And not only that: we even take pride in God through our Lord Jesus Christ, the one through whom we now have a restored relationship with God.
local: A ba ɩ anyã tɛɛ lɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ sɩ chɩlɛ naa a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ tu a sɩ Soro Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ, tu a 'lɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ sɩn ben nyɛ a 'maalfʊ a pʊ̃pãanyã.

eng: Happy are those whose sin isnt counted against them by the Lord .
local: Bɔɔsʊ̃n lɛ a 'lɔ a sɩ Soro na ba lɛ sɔrɔ a wʊ yelbier dɔɔlɔ wʊ ɛ.

eng: He died to sin once and for all with his death, but he lives for God with his life.
local: A kũu wʊn kpi, wʊ kpin kʊ yelbier gbɛbʊ̃'yen kʊ nɩrɛ jaa, tɩchɛ a wʊ nyɛvʊr pɔ wʊn be, wʊ ben be kʊ Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: Just as through one human being sin came into the world, and death came through sin, so death has come to everyone, since everyone has sinned.
local: Nɩtɔ̃ jũu lɛ a yelbier kpɛ a wɛr pɔ tu nɩbʊ̃'yen 'yɔ̃ɔ, ka a kũu mɩ tu a yelbier 'yɔ̃ɔ mɩ wa, sɔr nyã lɛ a kũu mɩ tu pɔɔ nɩrɛ jaa, bojũu ba jaa maal yelbier.

eng: Is this state of happiness only for the circumcised or is it also for those who arent circumcised? We say, Faith was credited to Abraham as righteousness.
local: A bɔɔsʊ̃n nyãna ɩn a yɔŋmarba tɛɛ bon bɩɩ, a mɩ ɩn a bala na ba ŋmaa a yʊɔr ɛ, bon? Sɩ 'yɛr ɩka Abraham sɔɔfʊ vɛ̃n ka wʊ ɩ nɩtor.

eng: In the same way, you also should consider yourselves dead to sin but alive for God in Christ Jesus.
local: A lɛ nɛ nɩ wʊ ju mɩ lɛ ɩka, yɩ kaa a yɩ mãɛ ɩka, yɩ kpin kʊ yelbier, tɩ be be kʊ Nãaŋmɩn, Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto jũu.

eng: Although sin was in the world, since there was no Law, it wasnt taken into account until the Law came.
local: Sɛrɛ tɩchɛ ka a Mosesɩ wa bin a nɛɛ, yelbier den nie be ka a wɛr pɔ, tɩchɛ lɛ a nɛbinãa na ba dɔ̃ɔ be be ɛ, ba maa de yelbier maal bon ɛ.

eng: So how was it credited? When he was circumcised, or when he wasnt circumcised? In fact, it was credited while he still wasnt circumcised, not after he was circumcised.
local: Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ anyãna dɔ̃ɔ ɩ kʊ wʊ? Wʊn ŋmaa a yʊɔr baar lɛ bɩɩ wʊn ba ŋmaa sɛrɛ lɛ? A ba ɩ wʊn ŋmaa baar ɛ, tɩchɛ wʊn dɔ̃ɔ ba ŋmaa sɛrɛ lɛ.

eng: So then, dont let sin rule your body, so that you do what it wants.
local: A lɛ jũu, yɩ ta sɔɔ ka yelbier lɛ di nãa a yɩ ãgãn nyãn na kpi ka yɩ turo a wʊ dɛɛr bɔfʊ ɛ.

eng: But death ruled from Adam until Moses, even over those who didnt sin in the same way Adam did—Adam was a type of the one who was coming.
local: Tɩchɛ kũu din nãa yi a Adama sɔ̃ɔ tɩ tãn a Mosesɩ sɔ̃ɔ, na wʊ gba mɩ tʊ̃ɔ a bala na ba maal a yelbier sɔ̃ɔ a nɛɛ a Adama kaar ɛ, Adama lɛ dɔ̃ɔ ɩ a 'lɔ na wa wa gãagiel.

eng: He received the sign of circumcision as a seal of the righteousness that comes from the faith he had while he still wasnt circumcised. It happened this way so that Abraham could be the ancestor of all those people who arent circumcised, who have faith in God, and so are counted as righteous.
local: Nãaŋmɩn wa bɩɛr man wʊ a yʊɔr ŋmaafʊ yele, ka a ɩ jãnɛ ɩka a wʊ yele tor naa a wʊ sɔɔfʊ jũu wʊn dɔ̃ɔ ba ŋmaa a yʊɔr sɛrɛ ɛ. Lɛ lɛ wʊ pãa ɩ a bala jaa na sɔɔ de sãa, tɩchɛ na baa ŋmaa a ba yɔɩ ɛ, ka a Nãaŋmɩn vɛ̃ a ba ɩ nɩtorsɩ.

eng: Dont offer parts of your body to sin, to be used as weapons to do wrong. Instead, present yourselves to God as people who have been brought back to life from the dead, and offer all the parts of your body to God to be used as weapons to do right.
local: Yɩ taa ɩrɛ wa diere a yɩ ãgãn jie ãsʊɔ jaa kʊrɔ yelbier maalfʊ ɛ, ka a ɩ putuyele tʊ̃tʊ̃bomo ɛ. Tɩchɛ yɩ sɔɔ de a yɩ mãɛ kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn, nɩtãa nɩbɛ na yi kũu pɔ tara nyɛvʊr, na yɩ sɔɔ de a yɩ ãgãn jiir kʊ wʊ, ka a ɩ yeltorsɩ tʊ̃tʊ̃bomo.

eng: But the free gift of Christ isnt like Adams failure. If many people died through what one person did wrong, Gods grace is multiplied even more for many people with the gift—of the one person Jesus Christ—that comes through grace.
local: Tɩchɛ a kʊfʊ ba kaara a bɔrfʊ kaar ɛ, bojũu ala ɩ nɩyɔɔ kpi naa nɩbʊ̃'yen bɔrfʊ jũu, Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ nɩ a wʊ kʊfʊ ala na wa tu a nɩbʊ̃'yen Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto wãɛfʊ jũu, na yaar kʊ nɩyɔɔ gar a lɛ.

eng: He could also be the ancestor of those circumcised people, who arent only circumcised but who also walk in the path of faith, like our ancestor Abraham did while he wasnt circumcised.
local: Wʊ mɩ ɩ naa bala jaa na ŋma a yɔɩ sãa. A ba ɩ yʊɔr ŋmaafʊ tɛɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ ban tara a sɔɔfʊ na ba turo nɩ a lɛ a sɩ sãa Abraham na dɔ̃ɔ turo tɩ wa ŋmaa a yʊɔr.

eng: The gift isnt like the consequences of one persons sin. The judgment that came from one persons sin led to punishment, but the free gift that came out of many failures led to the verdict of acquittal.
local: A Nãaŋmɩn kʊfʊ gba ba nyɛtãan a nɩbʊ̃'yen yelbier 'lɔ ɛ. 'Yɛr tɔɔrfʊ tun a yelbe bʊ̃'yen 'lɔ na wʊ waan sɔ̃ɔfʊ, tɩchɛ a kʊfʊ mɩ tun a bɔrfʊsɩ yɔɔ pɔ, naa waan torfʊ.

eng: Sin will have no power over you, because you arent under Law but under grace.
local: Bojũu, yelbier ba lɛ ɩ a yɩ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ ɛ, bojũu yaa chɛnɛ be a Mosesɩ nɛbinãa pĩlem ɛ, tɩchɛ yɩ ben a Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ pĩlem.

eng: If death ruled because of one persons failure, those who receive the multiplied grace and the gift of righteousness will even more certainly rule in life through the one person Jesus Christ.
local: Bojũu ala ɩ a nɩbʊ̃'yen bɔrfʊ jũu kũu din nãa tu a nɩbʊ̃'yen 'lɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ, ŋmɩnɛ lɛ a bala na nyɛ a Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ nɩ a kʊfʊ 'lɔ na ɩ a torfʊ, na din nãa a ba nyɛvʊr pɔ tu a nɩbʊ̃'yen Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: The promise to Abraham and to his descendants, that he would inherit the world, didnt come through the Law but through the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: A ba ɩ a nɛbinãa jũu lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn dɔ̃ɔ tɩr a nɛɛ kʊ a Abraham nɩ a wʊ yɔn ɩka bala lɛ na so a wɛr ɛ, tɩchɛ wʊn ɩ nɩtor a wʊ sɔɔfʊ jũu lɛ.

eng: So now the righteous requirements necessary for life are met for everyone through the righteous act of one person, just as judgment fell on everyone through the failure of one person.
local: An ɩ nɩbʊ̃'yen bɔrfʊ jũu lɛ waan sɔ̃ɔfʊ wa 'yɔ̃ɔ a nɩbɛ ba jaa, lɛ lɛ so nɩbʊ̃'yen mɩ na maal ka a tor, mɩ lɛ vɛ̃ ka a nɩbɛ ba jaa tor na ba nyɛ nyɛvʊr.

eng: If they inherit because of the Law, then faith has no effect and the promise has been canceled.
local: Ala ɩ a bala na turo a nɛbinãa lɛ so a wɛr ɛ, sɔɔfʊ ba lɛ tara tɔ̃nɔ̃ ɛ, ka Nãaŋmɩn nɛɛ wʊn tɩr mɩ ɩ pɔrɔ.

eng: So what? Should we sin because we arent under Law but under grace? Absolutely not!.
local: Ka bo go? Sɩ chɛnɛ ɩrɛ a yelbier bojũu sɩn ba lɛ chɛnɛ be a nɛɛ pĩlem ɔ, tɩ be a wãɛfʊ pĩlem bɩɩ? Kai, sɔr ba be be ɛ!.

eng: Many people were made righteous through the obedience of one person, just as many people were made sinners through the disobedience of one person.
local: Nɩtãa lɛ a bawõno bʊ̃'yen na vɛ̃ ka a nɩyɔɔ ɩ yelbe-ɩrbɛ, lɛ mɩ lɛ a tu nɩbʊ̃'yen na tu Nãaŋmɩn nɛɛ mɩ vɛ̃, ka a nɩyɔɔ mɩ ɩ nɩtorsɩ.

eng: Dont you know that if you offer yourselves to someone as obedient slaves, that you are slaves of the one whom you obey? Thats true whether you serve as slaves of sin, which leads to death, or as slaves of the kind of obedience that leads to righteousness.
local: Yaa bɔ̃ɔ ɩka ala ɩ fʊ wa den fʊ mãɛ kʊ nɩrɛ na fʊ turo a wʊ nɛɛ gbãgbaa kaar, fʊ ɩn gbãgbaa kʊ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ nɛɛ fʊn turo, ka yelbier gbãgbaa lɛ fʊ ɩ a 'lɔ na chiine a kũu pɔ, bɩɩ a Nãaŋmɩn nɛɛ tufʊ 'lɔ mɩ na chiine nɩ fʊ a yeltorsɩ jie.

eng: The Law brings about wrath. But when there isnt any law, there isnt any violation of the law.
local: Bojũu a nɛbinãa waara nɩ naa Nãaŋmɩn suur. A jie 'lɔ mɩ a nɛbinãa na ba be ɛ, a nɛɛ bɔrfʊsɩ mɩ ba be be ɛ.

eng: The Law stepped in to amplify the failure, but where sin increased, grace multiplied even more.
local: A nɛɛ pʊɔn 'yɔ̃ɔ, ka a bɔrfʊsɩ mɩ waa. Tɩchɛ jie 'lɔ a yelbier na pʊɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ, Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ ma pʊɔn 'yɔ̃ɔ gar a lɛ.

eng: But thank God that although you used to be slaves of sin, you gave wholehearted obedience to the teaching that was handed down to you, which provides a pattern.
local: Tɩchɛ n puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan, yɩn dɔ̃ɔ gba ɩ a yelbier gbãgbaar, tɩ yɩ wa sɔɔn naa a yɩ nyãa wʊ jaa na yɩ de a yɩ mãɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ a wiilfʊ nyã pɔ.

eng: Thats why the inheritance comes through faith, so that it will be on the basis of Gods grace. In that way, the promise is secure for all of Abrahams descendants, not just for those who are related by Law but also for those who are related by the faith of Abraham, who is the father of all of us.
local: A lɛ jũu, a nɛtɩraa wa naa a sɔɔfʊ jũu, ka a Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ na sʊ̃ʊ a Abraham yɔn ba jaa, a ba ɩ a bala na turo a nɛɛ tɛɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ a bala na tara a Abraham sɔɔfʊ, 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a sɩ jaa sãa.

eng: The result is that grace will rule through Gods righteousness, leading to eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord, just as sin ruled in death.
local: Nɩtãa lɛ a yelbier na di nãa a kũu pɔ, lɛ mɩ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ na di a nãa tu a yeltorsɩ pɔ, na wʊ waan nyɛvʊr na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ, tu a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto na ɩ a sɩ Soro 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: Now that you have been set free from sin, you have become slaves of righteousness.
local: Nãaŋmɩn faa yɩn yin a yelbier pɔ a yɩ so yɩ mãɛ, na yɩ lɩɛb yeltorsɩ gbãgbaar.

eng: As it is written: I have appointed you to be the father of many nations. So Abraham is our father in the eyes of God in whom he had faith, the God who gives life to the dead and calls things that dont exist into existence.
local: Nɩtãa an sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ, N maal fʊ naa a fʊ ɩ a paal yɔɔ sãa. 'Lɔ lɛ ɩ a sɩ sãa a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃, a 'lɔ wʊn sɔɔ de, a Nãaŋmɩn nyã na ma vɛ̃ ka kũn ɩ vʊɛ, na wʊ ma bʊɔl bonsɩ ala na ba dɔ̃ɔ be be ɛ, ka a be be.

eng: Once, you offered the parts of your body to be used as slaves to impurity and to lawless behavior that leads to still more lawless behavior. Now, you should present the parts of your body as slaves to righteousness, which makes your lives holy.
local: Nɩsaal 'yɛr lɛ n de man a yɩ ãgãma yɩn ba tʊ̃ɔnɔ̃ nyɔɔrɔ jũu. Nɩtãa lɛ yɩn dɔ̃ɔ ma de a yɩ ãgãn jiir ka a ɩ gbãgbaar kʊ a ala na ba vɩɛl ɛ, naa ma pʊɔrɔ putuyele 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃, pʊ̃pãanyã 'lɔ, yɩ de a yɩ mãɛ ka a ɩ yeltorsɩ nɩ chɛchɛ ɩfʊ gbãgbaar.

eng: Brothers and sisters, Im talking to you as people who know the Law. Dont you know that the Law has power over someone only as long as he or she lives?.
local: Yaa be bɔ̃ɔ ɛ, yɛɛr, n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ a bala na bɔ̃ɔ a nɛbinãa ɩka a nɛbinãa ma tʊ̃ɔn nɩrɛ na chɛnɛ nɩ nɩbir ɛ?.

eng: When it was beyond hope, he had faith in the hope that he would become the father of many nations, in keeping with the promise God spoke to him: Thats how many descendants you will have. .
local: Tɩɛrtɔfʊ jie ba lɛ be be ɛ, tɩ Abraham tara tɩɛrtɔ jie, na wʊ sɔɔ de ka a lɛ vɛ̃ ka wʊ ɩ a paal yɔɔ sãa, nɩtãa lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Lɛ lɛ a fʊ yɔn na wa be.

eng: When you were slaves of sin, you were free from the control of righteousness.
local: Yɩn dɔ̃ɔ ɩ a yelbier gbãgbaar, a yeltorsɩ ba so yɩ ɛ.

eng: So now there isnt any condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.
local: A lɛ jũu pʊ̃pãanyã, yele ba pɔɔrɔ a bala na be a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: A married woman is united with her husband under the Law while he is alive. But if her husband dies, she is released from the Law concerning her husband.
local: De man pɔɔ na kul sɩrɛ, a nɛɛ lũ wʊn mar a sɩrɛ ka wʊ chire dayuo na gãn wʊ, a sɩrɛ na chɛnɛ ba kpi ɛ, tɩchɛ a sɩrɛ wa kpi, a kul taa nɛɛ 'lɔ ba lɛ lũ wʊ go ɛ.

eng: What consequences did you get from doing things that you are now ashamed of? The outcome of those things is death.
local: Tɔ̃nɔ̃ buor lɛ yɩ nyɛ yin a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ a yelsɩ ala na ben ɩ vã a pʊ̃pãanyã? A bomo ala baarfʊ daar ɩn kũu.

eng: So then, if she lives with another man while her husband is alive, shes committing adultery. But if her husband dies, shes free from the Law, so she wont be committing adultery if she marries someone else.
local: 'Lɔ wa tɩ ir dayuo a sɩrɛ na chɛnɛ vʊrɔ, ban 'yɛr naa ɩka wʊ sɔ̃ɔ naa. Tɩchɛ ala ɩ a sɩrɛ kpi naa, a nɛɛ ba lɛ lũ wʊ go ɛ, ala ɩ wʊ gba na kul dayuo, waa sɔ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: The law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus has set you free from the law of sin and death.
local: A tun a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ jũu, a nɛɛ 'lɔ na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ nyɛvʊr faa man yin a nɛɛ 'lɔ na ɩ yelbier nɩ kũu pɔ.

eng: Without losing faith, Abraham, who was nearly years old, took into account his own body, which was as good as dead, and Sarahs womb, which was dead.
local: Waa bal a wʊ sɔɔfʊ pɔ ɛ, wʊ mãɛ nyɛn bɔ̃ɔn a wʊ ãgãn na kor jʊɔl, bojũu yuom kɔbaa lɛ wʊ nyɛ ka a Saara mɩ ab.

eng: But now that you have been set free from sin and become slaves to God, you have the consequence of a holy life, and the outcome is eternal life.
local: Tɩchɛ pʊ̃pãanyã, Nãaŋmɩn na ben faa yɩ yin a yelbier pɔ a yɩ ɩ a wʊ gbãgbaar, a tɔ̃nɔ̃ yɩn nyɛ ɩn chɛchɛ ɩfʊ ka a baaraa ɩ nyɛvʊr na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ.

eng: God has done what was impossible for the Law, since it was weak because of selfishness. God condemned sin in the body by sending his own Son to deal with sin in the same body as humans, who are controlled by sin.
local: Bojũu a bon 'lɔ a Mosesɩ nɛɛ na ba dɔ̃ɔ tara kpɛ̃ɔ na maal ɛ, a sɩ yelbier ãgãn na bal bar, Nãaŋmɩn maal naa na wʊ tʊ̃ a wʊ mãɛ Bie a wʊ nyɛtãan a sɩn na maala a yelbier kaar a wʊ wa ɩ a yelbier bɔɔrlo bon. Lɛ lɛ wʊ sɔ̃ɔ yelbier a nɩsaal pɔ bar.

eng: Therefore, my brothers and sisters, you also died with respect to the Law through the body of Christ, so that you could be united with someone else. You are united with the one who was raised from the dead so that we can bear fruit for God.
local: Lɛ lɛ, n yɛɛr yɩ mɩ kpin kʊ a nɛbinãa tu a Kɩrɩsɩto ãgãn pɔ, ka yɩn be be kʊ nɩyuo, a 'lɔ na ir a kũu pɔ ka sɩn tʊ̃ɔ wɔ̃ wɔ̃mɔ̃ kʊ Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: He didnt hesitate with a lack of faith in Gods promise, but he grew strong in faith and gave glory to God.
local: Tɩchɛ Abraham ba tara baal a wʊ sɔɔfʊ pɔ, nɩ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na tɩr a wʊ nɛɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ wʊ nyɛn kpɛ̃ɔ a wʊ sɔɔfʊ pɔ, na wʊ de tɩɩr kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: The wages that sin pays are death, but Gods gift is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.
local: Bojũu yelbier sãyar ɩn kũu, tɩchɛ ka a Nãaŋmɩn kʊfʊ ɩ nyɛvʊr na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ, tu a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto na ɩ a sɩ Soro 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: When we were self-centered, the sinful passions aroused through the Law were at work in all the parts of our body, so that we bore fruit for death.
local: Bojũu a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ a yelbier ãgãn na so sɩ, ka a yelbier vɔlaa ma de a nɛbinãa tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ tʊ̃mɔ̃ a sɩ ãgãn pɔ a sɩ wɔ̃nɔ̃ kũu wɔ̃mɔ̃.

eng: He did this so that the righteous requirement of the Law might be fulfilled in us. Now the way we live is based on the Spirit, not based on selfishness.
local: ka a lɛ a Mosesɩ nɛbinãa na bʊɔrɔ ka a sɛɛ a sɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ, a sɩ kʊ̃ lɛ turo a lɛ a yelbier ãgãn na bʊɔrɔ ɛ, tɩ turo a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na bʊɔrɔ.

eng: He was fully convinced that God was able to do what he promised.
local: Wʊ sɔɔn de nuur ayi ɩka Nãaŋmɩn tara na kpɛ̃ɔ na maal a lɛ wʊn tɩr a wʊ nɛɛ.

eng: People whose lives are based on selfishness think about selfish things, but people whose lives are based on the Spirit think about things that are related to the Spirit.
local: A bala na turo a ba yelbier ãgãn, ba den a ba tɩɛr 'yɔ̃ɔ a ala na ɩ a ãgãn vɔlaa pɔ, tɩchɛ a bala mɩ na turo a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na bʊɔrɔ, mɩ den a ba tɩɛr 'yɔ̃ɔ a ala a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na bʊɔrɔ.

eng: But now we have been released from the Law. We have died with respect to the thing that controlled us, so that we can be slaves in the new life under the Spirit, not in the old life under the written Law.
local: Tɩchɛ pʊ̃pãanyã, sɩ kpin kʊ a 'lɔ na dɔ̃ɔ lũ sɩ, sɩ son sɩ mãɛ yi a nɛbinãa 'lɔ pɔ, na sɩ pãa tʊ̃nɔ̃ a sɔpaalaa nyã pɔ na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na ba ɩ a sɔkoraa ban sɛb 'yɔ̃ɔ a gãn pɔ ɛ.

eng: Therefore, it was credited to him as righteousness.
local: A anyãna lɛ so ka a Nãaŋmɩn, vɛ̃ a wʊ ɩ nɩtor.

eng: The attitude that comes from selfishness leads to death, but the attitude that comes from the Spirit leads to life and peace.
local: Yelbier sʊɔ tɩɛr ɩn kũu, tɩchɛ a tɩɛr 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na so ɩn nyɛvʊr nɩ nyã'maar.

eng: So the attitude that comes from selfishness is hostile to God. It doesnt submit to Gods Law, because it cant.
local: A yelbe-ɩrɛ tɩɛr ma 'laar Nãaŋmɩn, wʊ maa siir wʊ mãɛ kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn nɛɛ ɛ, wʊ mɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ ɩ ɛ.

eng: People who are self-centered arent able to please God.
local: Bala na turo a lɛ a ba ãgãn na bʊɔrɔ, kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ pɛl Nãaŋmɩn pʊɔ ɛ.

eng: But you arent self-centered. Instead you are in the Spirit, if in fact Gods Spirit lives in you. If anyone doesnt have the Spirit of Christ, they dont belong to him.
local: Yɩn 'lɔ ba lɛ turo a lɛ a yelbier ãgãn na bʊɔrɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ yɩ turo naa a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na bʊɔrɔ, ala ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ ben a yɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ. Ala ɩ ãsʊɔ jaa ba wa tara a Kɩrɩsɩto Sɩɛ ɛ, waa ɩ a Kɩrɩsɩto tʊɔr ɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, my hearts desire is for Israels salvation. Thats my prayer to God for them.
local: N yɛɛr, lɛ a n nyãa na bʊɔrɔ ka n sʊɔrɔ nɩ Nãaŋmɩn lɛ ɩka, a Isɩral nɩbɛ ba nyɛ faafʊ.

eng: Im speaking the truth in Christ—Im not lying, as my conscience assures me with the Holy Spirit.
local: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ a Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ, n ba ŋmara jirsɩ ɛ, a n tɩɛr mɩ din dãasɩɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ pɔ.

eng: I have great sadness and constant pain in my heart.
local: Nɩbaal kpɛ man tɩ põn bar, ka wʊʊr sɛɛ ma a nyãa.

eng: But the scripture that says it was credited to him wasnt written only for Abrahams sake.
local: A 'yɛrbir na 'yɛr ɩka, Nãaŋmɩn vɛ̃n a wʊ ɩ nɩtor, a ba sɛb a 'lɔ tɛɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: If Christ is in you, the Spirit is your life because of Gods righteousness, but the body is dead because of sin.
local: Tɩchɛ ala ɩ Kɩrɩsɩto ben a yɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ, a yɩ ãgãn kpi naa a yelbier jũu, tɩchɛ ka a yɩ sɩɩr tara nyɛvʊr a yeltorsɩ jũu.

eng: So what are we going to say? That the Law is sin? Absolutely not! But I wouldnt have known sin except through the Law. I wouldnt have known the desire for what others have if the Law had not said, Dont desire to take what others have .
local: Bo lɛ sɩn lɛ 'yɛr go? A nɛbinãa ɩn yeldɛɛr bɩɩ? Kai, sɔr ba be be ɛ! Waa ɩ yeldɛɛr ɛ. Sɩrɛ jaa n kʊ̃ naa bɔ̃ɔ yelbier ɛ, ala ɩ a nɛbinãa ba be be ɛ. Bojũu n kʊ̃ naa bɔ̃ɔ jɩɛlfʊ ɛ, ala ɩ a nɛbinãa ba ta 'yɛr ɩka, Taa jɩɛlɛ nɩrɛ bon ɛ.

eng: I wish I could be cursed, cut off from Christ if it helped my brothers and sisters, who are my flesh-and-blood relatives.
local: An taa nʊ̃mɔ̃ ma naa ɩka n ben pɔ'yɔ̃ɔfʊ pɔ naa ba lɛ pʊɔ a Kɩrɩsɩto pɔ ɛ, a n yɛɛr Isɩral nɩbɛ jũu, bala lɛ ɩ a n mãɛ nɩbɛ.

eng: It was written also for our sake, because it is going to be credited to us too. It will be credited to those of us who have faith in the one who raised Jesus our Lord from the dead.
local: tɩchɛ a mɩ ɩn a sɩ bon, Nãaŋmɩn mɩ na vɛ̃n a sɩ yele ka a tor, sɩn bala jaa na sɔɔ ɩka, 'lɔ lɛ sãɩ a Yesu na ɩ a sɩ Soro a kũu pɔ.

eng: He was handed over because of our mistakes, and he was raised to meet the requirements of righteousness for us.
local: A sɩ yelbebe jũu lɛ ba dɔ̃ɔ kʊn wʊ, tɩchɛ ka a Nãaŋmɩn sãɩ wʊ a kũu pɔ a wʊ ɩ vʊr, na wʊ vɛ̃ ka sɩ tor.

eng: I can vouch for them: they are enthusiastic about God. However, it isnt informed by knowledge.
local: Bojũu ɩn tʊ̃ɔn di a ba dãasɩɛ ɩka ba tara na nɩbijɩɛ nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn yele, tɩchɛ bɔ̃ɔfʊ lɛ ba pʊɔ a ba nɩbijɩɛ pɔ ɛ.

eng: But sin seized the opportunity and used this commandment to produce all kinds of desires in me. Sin is dead without the Law.
local: Tɩchɛ a yelbier far kpɛ na wʊ nyɛ sɔr a nɛbinãa sɛ̃ vɛ̃ ka n wɔ̃ jɩɛlfʊ jaa bʊrɔ vɔlaa. A nɛbinãa baa be be ɛ, yelbier ɩn bʊ̃kũu.

eng: They are Israelites. The adoption as Gods children, the glory, the covenants, the giving of the Law, the worship, and the promises belong to them.
local: A Isɩral nɩbɛ bala lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn de ka ba ɩ a wʊ bibiir, na wʊ kʊ ba a wʊ tɩɩr nɩ a nɛtɩrsɩ wʊn pɔn ba, na wʊ kʊ ba a nɛbinãa, nɩ a lɛ ban na puoro wʊ a wʊ puorfʊ yir nɩ a nɛɛr wʊn tɩr kʊ ba.

eng: If the Spirit of the one who raised Jesus from the dead lives in you, the one who raised Christ from the dead will give life to your human bodies also, through his Spirit that lives in you.
local: Ala ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ 'lɔ na sãɩ a Yesu a kũu pɔ, ben a yɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ, 'lɔ na sãɩ a Kɩrɩsɩto a kũu pɔ, na kʊn a yɩ ãgãn kũn nyɛvʊr tu a wʊ Sɩɛ pɔ, a 'lɔ na be a yɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: They dont submit to Gods righteousness because they dont understand his righteousness, and they try to establish their own righteousness.
local: Ban ba bɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn yeltorsɩ ɛ, tɩ na ba bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba bin a ba mãɛ yeltorsɩ, baa siir ba mãɛ kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn yeltorsɩ ɛ.

eng: So, brothers and sisters, because of Gods mercies, I encourage you to present your bodies as a living sacrifice that is holy and pleasing to God. This is your appropriate priestly service.
local: A lɛ jũu, n sʊɔrɔ yɩ naa yɛɛr, a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbaal nyã jũu, yɩ de a yɩ mãɛ na ɩ bʊ̃nɩbie lon bɔɔr kʊ Nãaŋmɩn. A ɩn chɛchɛ naa pɛlɛ a Nãaŋmɩn pʊɔ. Lɛ lɛ ɩ a puorfʊ na tara par.

eng: So I ask you, has God rejected his people? Absolutely not! Im an Israelite, a descendant of Abraham, from the tribe of Benjamin.
local: N pãa sʊʊr naa, a Nãaŋmɩn ben tɔr a wʊ nɩbɛ bar bɩɩ? Kai! A maa mãɛ ɩn a Isɩral nɩrɛ, naa ɩ a Abraham yɔ̃ɔ yi a Benjamin bʊrɔ pɔ.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, we have an obligation, but it isnt an obligation to ourselves to live our lives on the basis of selfishness.
local: A lɛ jũu n yɛɛr, sɩ din sãn, tɩchɛ a ba ɩ a sɩ yelbier ãgãn nyã lɛ ɛ, a sɩn turo a lɛ wʊn bʊɔrɔ ɛ.

eng: The Jewish ancestors are theirs, and the Christ descended from those ancestors. He is the one who rules over all things, who is God, and who is blessed forever. Amen.
local: Bala lɛ ɩ a sãakpãmɩne, a ba pɔ lɛ ba bɩɛr a Kɩrɩsɩto mɩ na ɩ a nɩsaal yi, na wʊ ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn kaara a bomo a jaa, 'lɔ lɛ ban pɛɛrɛ bɩbir jaa. A ɩ a lɛ.

eng: I used to be alive without the Law, but when the commandment came, sin sprang to life.
local: Ɩn dɔ̃ɔ be be ba bɔ̃ɔ a nɛbinãa ɛ, tɩchɛ a nɛbinãa na wa wa, yelbier faan a nyɛvʊr, ka a kũu kʊ ma.

eng: Christ is the goal of the Law, which leads to righteousness for all who have faith in God.
local: Kɩrɩsɩto lɛ ɩ a nɛbinãa tɛɛfʊ jie, ka nɩrɛ jaa na sɔɔ de wʊ, wʊn vɛ̃n ka a nɩnyɩnɛ yele tor.

eng: God hasnt rejected his people, whom he knew in advance. Or dont you know what the scripture says in the case of Elijah, when he pleads with God against Israel?.
local: Nãaŋmɩn ba tɔr a wʊ nɩbɛ bar ɛ, bala lɛ wʊ de nie bɔ̃ɔ. Yaa bɔ̃ɔ a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn gãn na 'yɛr, lɛ a Elaija na dɔ̃ɔ sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn waala dɔɔlɔ a Isɩral nɩbɛ.

eng: But its not as though Gods word has failed. Not all who are descended from Israel are part of Israel.
local: A ba ɩ fɩka a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr ɩn faa ɛ, bojũu a ba ɩ a bala jaa ban dɔɔ a Isɩral nɩbɛ pɔ, ba jaa lɛ ɩ a Isɩral nɩbɛ ɛ.

eng: Dont be conformed to the patterns of this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your minds so that you can figure out what Gods will is—what is good and pleasing and mature.
local: Yɩ taa lɛ wa lɩɛrɛ maala a lɛ a wɛr nɩbɛ na maala go ɛ, tɩchɛ yɩ maala a lɛ na lɩɛrɛ yɩ a tɩɛr paalaa nyã yɩn nyɛ. Yɩn pãa tʊ̃ɔn bɛɛr na yɩ bɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn bɔfʊ, a ala na ɩ vɩla naa pɛlɛ a wʊ pʊɔ naa ɩ chɛchɛ.

eng: Moses writes about the righteousness that comes from the Law: The person who does these things will live by them. .
local: Nɩtɔ̃ lɛ a Mosesɩ man a lɛ a nɛbinãa na ma vɛ̃ ka nɩrɛ yele tor, A nɩrɛ 'lɔ na maala a lɛ a nɛbinãa na 'yɛr, na tara na nyɛvʊr.

eng: Not all of Abrahams children are called Abrahams descendants, but instead your descendants will be named through Isaac. .
local: A ba ɩ baa yi a wʊ par pɔ ɛ, ba jaa baa ɩ Abraham bibiir ɛ, tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn dɔ̃ɔ tɩr nɛɛ kʊ a Abraham ɩka, Isaakɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ a fʊ yɔn na yi.

eng: If you live on the basis of selfishness, you are going to die. But if by the Spirit you put to death the actions of the body, you will live.
local: Bojũu ala ɩ yɩ turo naa a lɛ a yɩ yelbier ãgãn na bʊɔrɔ, yɩn kpi naa, tɩchɛ ala ɩ yɩ den a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ kʊ̃n a ãgãn ma maal bidɛbɛ bar, yɩ ben a nyɛvʊr sɔr pɔ.

eng: and I died. So the commandment that was intended to give life brought death.
local: Be lɛ n nyɛ ɩka a nɛbinãa 'lɔ naa waan nyɛvʊr, kũu lɛ wʊ bar tɩ waan.

eng: Because of the grace that God gave me, I can say to each one of you: dont think of yourself more highly than you ought to think. Instead, be reasonable since God has measured out a portion of faith to each one of you.
local: A Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ nyã wʊn wãɛ ma jũu, n sʊɔrɔ naa a yɩ ãsʊɔ jaa ɩka, ta tɩɛr a yɩ mãɛ tɩ põn bar a lɛ yɩn taa tɩɛr ɛ, tɩchɛ yɩ ma tɩɛr a yɩ mãɛ 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ nɩbijɩɛ jaa jã a sɔɔfʊ 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn na kʊ yɩ.

eng: But the righteousness that comes from faith talks like this: Dont say in your heart , Who will go up into heaven? .
local: Tɩchɛ a yeltorsɩ ala na yire a sɔɔfʊ pɔ 'yɛr naa, Ta tɩɛr a fʊ nyãa pɔ sʊʊr ɩka, An lɛ na do a saaju? Ka a lɛ wiil ɩka, tɩ tara a Kɩrɩsɩto suu waan.

eng: Lord, they have killed your prophets, and they have torn down your altars. Im the only one left, and they are trying to take my life. .
local: N Soro, ba kʊn a fʊ 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ, na ba wɛl a fʊ bɔɔr lofʊ jiir bar. Maa tɛɛ lɛ chɛ, tɩ a ba bʊɔrɔ a n kʊfʊ.

eng: But what is Gods reply to him? I have kept for myself seven thousand people who havent bowed their knees to Baal. .
local: Bo lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr kʊ a Elaija? Wʊ 'yɛr naa, N chɛnɛ na tara nɩbɛ tur ata kɔɔr anũu na ba gbur dumo puor a tɩɩ Baal ɛ.

eng: All who are led by Gods Spirit are Gods sons and daughters.
local: Bojũu bala jaa a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na chiine, bala lɛ ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn bibiir.

eng: That means it isnt the natural children who are Gods children, but it is the children from the promise who are counted as descendants.
local: A ba ɩ a ãgãn vaar bibiir lɛ ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn bibiir ɛ, tɩchɛ a bibiir bala a Nãaŋmɩn na tɩr a nɛɛ a wʊ dɔɔ, bala lɛ wʊ kaa a ba ɩ Abraham bibiir.

eng: or Who will go down into the region below? .
local: Bɩɩ sʊʊr a fʊ mãɛ ɩka, An lɛ na chen a kpĩi tẽe? Ka a lɛ wiil ɩka, tɩ ir a Kɩrɩsɩto a kũu pɔ waan.

eng: We have many parts in one body, but the parts dont all have the same function.
local: Nɩtãa lɛ a sɩ ãsʊɔ jaa na tara ãgãn tɩ ka a tara jidẽdẽme, a jidẽdẽme anyãna ba tʊ̃nɔ̃ tʊ̃bʊ̃'yen ɛ.

eng: So also in the present time there is a remaining group by the choice of Gods grace.
local: Lɛ mɩ lɛ a dɩna, nɩbɛ bamɩne chɛ naa ka Nãaŋmɩn na ir ba a wʊ wãɛfʊ jũu.

eng: The words in the promise were: A year from now I will return, and Sarah will have a son .
local: Bojũu nɩtɔ̃ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn dɔ̃ɔ tɩr a nɛɛ 'yɛr, A daar wa ta, ɩn lɩɛb wa ka a Saara dɔɔ dabile.

eng: Not only that, but also Rebecca conceived children with one man, our ancestor Isaac.
local: A ba ɩ anyã tɛɛ lɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ a Worbɛka dɔɔn dabil ayi ka ba sãa ɩ bʊ̃'yen tɛɛ na ɩ a sɩ sãakpã Isaakɩ.

eng: When they hadnt been born yet and when they hadnt yet done anything good or bad, it was shown that Gods purpose would continue because it was based on his choice.
local: Sɛrɛ tɩ ka wʊ wa dɔɔ a yɩbɛ banyãna, baa mɩ maal yele jaa vɩla bɩɩ dɛɛr ɛ, tɩ nɩtɔ̃ lɛ a ɩ ɩka a Nãaŋmɩn bɔfʊ pɔ lɛ wʊ ma tu ir a nɩrɛ.

eng: It wasnt because of what was done but because of Gods call. This was said to her: The older child will be a slave to the younger one .
local: A kʊ̃ ɩ a fʊ tʊ̃mɔ̃ jũu ɛ, tɩchɛ a 'lɔ na bʊɔl fʊ. Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr kʊ a Worbɛka, A yɛkarma na tʊ̃nɔ̃ na kʊrɔ a yɛbile.

eng: As it is written, I loved Jacob, but I hated Esau .
local: Lɛ mɩ lɛ a sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ, N nʊ̃n Jekɔb tɩchɛ naa 'laar a Esau.

eng: So what are we going to say? Isnt this unfair on Gods part? Absolutely not!.
local: Bo lɛ sɩn pãa lɛ 'yɛr? Nãaŋmɩn ɩn nɩfɩlefɩle bɩɩ? Kai. Sɔr ba be be ɛ!.

eng: He says to Moses, Ill have mercy on whomever I choose to have mercy, and Ill show compassion to whomever I choose to show compassion .
local: Bojũu wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a Mosesɩ, Nɩrɛ 'lɔ ɩn na kaa nɩbaal lɛ ɩn na kaa nɩbaal, naa maal vɩla kʊ a 'lɔ ɩn na maal vɩla kʊ.

eng: So then, it doesnt depend on a persons desire or effort. It depends entirely on God, who shows mercy.
local: A lɛ jũu a ba ɩ lɛ nɩrɛ na bʊɔrɔ ɛ, bɩɩ na tʊ̃nɔ̃ lɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ a yin Nãaŋmɩn nɩbaal kaafʊ pɔ.

eng: Scripture says to Pharaoh, I have put you in this position for this very thing: so I can show my power in you and so that my name can be spread through the entire earth. .
local: Bojũu Nãaŋmɩn dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr kʊ a Ijipitɩ nãa Faaro ɩka yaa, Maa lɛ bin fʊ a yele nyãna jũu, ɩka n wiil a n kpɛ̃ɔ a fʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ, ka ban bʊɔl a n yuor a wɛr wʊ jaa.

eng: So then, God has mercy on whomever he wants to, but he makes resistant whomever he wants to.
local: A lɛ jũu, Nãaŋmɩn ma kãan nɩrɛ 'lɔ wʊn bʊɔrɔ nɩbaal, a 'lɔ wʊ mɩn bʊɔrɔ, wʊ ma vɛ̃n ka a wʊ nyãa kpɛ̃mɛ.

eng: So you are going to say to me, Then why does he still blame people? Who has ever resisted his will.
local: Ɩ̃sʊɔ na sʊʊr ma naa ɩka, Bo lɛ so ka a Nãaŋmɩn na de yele dɔɔl sɩ? Bojũu nɩrɛ jaa kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ tɔr a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na bʊɔrɔ ɛ.

eng: You are only a human being. Who do you think you are to talk back to God? Does the clay say to the potter, Why did you make me like this? .
local: Tɩchɛ fʊ̃ʊ mɩ nɩ ana, nɩsaal na fʊn 'yɛr lɩɛr Nãaŋmɩn? Laa na tʊ̃ɔn sʊʊr a 'lɔ na mɛ wʊ ɩka, Bo lɛ fʊ mɛ ma a nɩtɔ̃ ɔ? .

eng: Doesnt the potter have the power over the clay to make one pot for special purposes and another for garbage from the same lump of clay?.
local: A lamɩɛrɛ ba tara sɔr ɩka wʊ de a yɔɔr bʊ̃'yen 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ mɛn bʊ̃ʊdʊʊ tɩchɛ mɛn ladiraa ɛ?.

eng: What if God very patiently puts up with pots made for wrath that were designed for destruction, because he wanted to show his wrath and to make his power known?.
local: Bo lɛ, ala ɩ Nãaŋmɩn lɛ kaa ir ɩka wʊ wiil a wʊ suur na wʊ vɛ̃ a ba bɔ̃ɔn a wʊ kpɛ̃ɔ, tɩ na wʊ chɛnɛ tara kãnyir nɩ a bonsɩ ala wʊn chɔɔr gun a sɔ̃ɔ barfʊ?.

eng: You didnt receive a spirit of slavery to lead you back again into fear, but you received a Spirit that shows you are adopted as his children. With this Spirit, we cry, Abba, Father.
local: A ba ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ wʊn kʊ yɩ, waa maala yɩ gbãgbaar ka a dãbãɛ kʊrɔ yɩ go ɛ, tɩchɛ wʊ kʊ yɩn a wʊ Sɩɛ a yɩ lɩɛb a wʊ bibiir. A Sɩɛ nyã lɛ sɩ ma de bʊɔlɔ wʊ, N Sãa Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: The same Spirit agrees with our spirit, that we are Gods children.
local: A Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ mãɛ din dãasɩɛ nɩ a sɩ sɩɩr ɩka sɩ ɩn Nãaŋmɩn bibiir.

eng: Sin seized the opportunity through the commandment, deceived me, and killed me.
local: Bojũu a yelbier nyɛn sɔr a nɛbinãa na kʊ wʊ, a wʊ far kpɛ na wʊ bɛl ma, ka a tu a nɛbinãa nyã pɔ, ka a kũu kʊ ma.

eng: So the Law itself is holy, and the commandment is holy, righteous, and good.
local: A lɛ jũu, a nɛbinãa ɩn chɛchɛ, ka a nɛbinãa 'yɛr mɩ ɩ chɛchɛ, naa tor ɩ vɩla.

eng: So did something good bring death to me? Absolutely not! But sin caused my death through something good so that sin would be exposed as sin. That way sin would become even more thoroughly sinful through the commandment.
local: Bon 'lɔ na ɩ bʊ̃vɩla pãa lɩɛb kũu wa kʊ ma? A ba ɩ lɛ lɛ ɛ. Tɩchɛ n bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka a ɩn yelbier, na a lɩɛb kũu kʊ ma tu a bon 'lɔ na ɩ vɩla pɔ. A tu naa 'yɛr binãa pɔ, ka bier na ɩ yelbier sɩrɛ jaa.

eng: We know that the Law is spiritual, but Im made of flesh and blood, and Im sold as a slave to sin.
local: Sɩn bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka a nɛbinãa ɩn Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ bon lɛ, tɩchɛ ka a maa ɩ ãgãn yelbier na da ka n ɩ a wʊ gbãgbaa.

eng: I dont know what Im doing, because I dont do what I want to do. Instead, I do the thing that I hate.
local: N maa bɔ̃ɔ a lɛ ɩn maala ɛ, bojũu a ala ɩn bʊɔrɔ ɩka n maal n maa maal ɛ, tɩchɛ a ala ɩn 'laar lɛ n ma maala.

eng: But if Im doing the thing that I dont want to do, Im agreeing that the Law is right.
local: Ala ɩ n ma maala naa ala ɩn ba bʊɔrɔ ɩka n maala ɛ, n sɔɔ naa ɩka a nɛbinãa ɩn vɩla.

eng: But now Im not the one doing it anymore. Instead, its sin that lives in me.
local: An ben be a lɛ, a ba ɩ maa mãɛ lɛ bɔ̃ɔn tɩ ɩrɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ a yelbier 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ na be a n pɔ lɛ ɩrɛ.

eng: I know that good doesnt live in me—that is, in my body. The desire to do good is inside of me, but I cant do it.
local: N bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka yelvɩla jaa ba be a n 'yɔ̃ɔ ɛ, a maa yelbier ãgãn tɛɛ lɛ. Bojũu n ma bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka n maal vɩla tɩ naa maa tʊ̃ɔ maal ɛ.

eng: I dont do the good that I want to do, but I do the evil that I dont want to do.
local: Bojũu a ala ɩn maala ba ɩ a vɩla ala ɩn bʊɔrɔ a maalfʊ ɛ. Kai, a bier ala ɩn ba bʊɔrɔ a maalfʊ lɛ, ala lɛ n ma maala.

eng: But if I do the very thing that I dont want to do, then Im not the one doing it anymore. Instead, it is sin that lives in me that is doing it.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã, ala ɩ n maala naa a ala ɩn ba bʊɔrɔ ɩka n maal ɛ, a ba lɛ ɩ maa lɛ lɛ maala go ɛ, tɩchɛ a yelbier 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ na be a n 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ maala.

eng: So I find that, as a rule, when I want to do what is good, evil is right there with me.
local: Lɛ lɛ, n nyɛn a lɛ a nɛbinãa na tʊ̃nɔ̃, maa wa bʊɔrɔ ɩka n maal vɩla, a yelbier lɛ ma tɔɔ gbʊr ma jaa yɛ̃ yɛ̃.

eng: In the same way, though there are many of us, we are one body in Christ, and individually we belong to each other.
local: Lɛ mɩ lɛ a Kɩrɩsɩto pɔ, a sɩn bala na ɩ yɔɔ tɩ lɔ̃ɔ ãgãn bʊ̃'yen pɔ, a jidẽdẽme anyã a jaa 'mantaa tʊ̃nɔ̃.

eng: But what does it say? The word is near you, in your mouth and in your heart .
local: Tɩchɛ bo lɛ a 'yɛr a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ? A 'yɛr gbʊr fʊ naa, a ben a fʊ nɛɛ pɔ, naa be a fʊ nyãa pɔ. Ala lɛ ɩ a sɔɔfʊ 'yɛr nyã sɩn mʊɔlɔ.

eng: But if we are children, we are also heirs. We are Gods heirs and fellow heirs with Christ, if we really suffer with him so that we can also be glorified with him.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã, ala ɩ sɩ ɩ naa Nãaŋmɩn bibiir, sɩn lɛ so a bʊ̃sʊ̃n ala a Nãaŋmɩn na tɩr a wʊ nɛɛ, 'matãan a Kɩrɩsɩto. Ala ɩ sɩrɛ jaa, sɩ lɔ̃ɔn wʊn di a wʊ dɔɔyɛ, lɛ lɛ sɩ mɩn 'matãan wʊ põ a wʊ tɩɩr.

eng: But if it is by grace, it isnt by whats done anymore. If it were, Gods grace wouldnt be grace.
local: Ala be ɩ a wãɛfʊ jũu lɛ, a ba lɛ ɩ tʊ̃mɔ̃ 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ go ɛ, ka lɛ ba lɛ ɛ, a wãɛfʊ kʊ̃ lɛ ɩ wãɛfʊ lɛ go ɛ.

eng: So what? Israel didnt find what it was looking for. Those who were chosen found it, but the others were resistant.
local: Bo pãa lɛ? A ala a Isɩral nɩbɛ na bʊɔrɔ nɩbijɩɛ baa nyɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ a bala a Nãaŋmɩn na ir lɛ nyɛ. Ka a bala na chɛ kpɛ̃mɛ a ba nyãa.

eng: We have different gifts that are consistent with Gods grace that has been given to us. If your gift is prophecy, you should prophesy in proportion to your faith.
local: Sɩ jaa tara na kʊfʊsɩ na tɛɛ taa, tu a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ wʊn wãɛn sɩ. Ala ɩ a ãsʊɔ kʊfʊ ɩn a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr 'yɛrfʊ lɛ, wʊ ma 'yɛrɛ jã a lɛ a wʊ sɔɔfʊ na ta.

eng: I believe that the present suffering is nothing compared to the coming glory that is going to be revealed to us.
local: N man nyɛ a sɩ dɩna dɔɔyɛ ka waa jã bon ɛ, ɩn de man a Nãaŋmɩn tɩɩr 'lɔ na wa nyɩr a sɩ sɛ̃.

eng: What if he did this to make the wealth of his glory known toward pots made for mercy, which he prepared in advance for glory?.
local: Bo lɛ, ala ɩ wʊ maal a nɩtɔ̃ ɩka a bonsɩ ala na ɩ a wʊ nɩbaal bomo, ka a bɔ̃ɔn a wʊ tɩɩr tɔ̃nɔ̃, a bala wʊn de nie chɔɔr gun a wʊ tɩɩr.

eng: I gladly agree with the Law on the inside.
local: N bʊɔrɔ naa Nãaŋmɩn nɛbinãa nɩ a n pʊɔ wʊ jaa.

eng: As it is written, God gave them a dull spirit, so that their eyes would not see and their ears not hear, right up until the present day. .
local: Nɩtãa lɛ an sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ, Nãaŋmɩn vɛ̃n ka ba ɩ jaa bũrtũm wa tãn a dɩna. A ba nɩbie ala maa tʊ̃ɔ nyɛ bon ɛ, ka a ba tobo ala maa tʊ̃ɔ wõ 'yɛr ɛ.

eng: Because if you confess with your mouth Jesus is Lord and in your heart you have faith that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.
local: Ala ɩ fʊ haan a fʊ nɛɛ chiir a fʊ pʊɔ 'yɛr ɩka, A Kɩrɩsɩto lɛ ɩ a Soro, na fʊ sɔɔn a fʊ nyãa wʊ jaa ɩka, Nãaŋmɩn ir wʊn a kũu pɔ, fʊn nyɛn faafʊ.

eng: If your gift is service, devote yourself to serving. If your gift is teaching, devote yourself to teaching.
local: Ka ala ɩ tʊ̃mɔ̃ tʊ̃fʊ mɩ lɛ, vɛ̃ ka wʊ ma tʊ̃nɔ̃, ala ɩ wiilfʊ mɩ lɛ, vɛ̃ ka wʊ ma wile.

eng: The whole creation waits breathless with anticipation for the revelation of Gods sons and daughters.
local: A bʊ̃maalsɩ a jaa nyʊnyʊlɔ naa chɩlɛ nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn bibiir wʊn wa vɛ̃ a ba nyɩrɛ.

eng: Creation was subjected to frustration, not by its own choice—it was the choice of the one who subjected it—but in the hope.
local: Bojũu a bʊ̃maalsɩ a jaa ben a ãgãn sal pĩlem an ba ɩ a ba mãɛ bɔfʊ ɛ.

eng: that the creation itself will be set free from slavery to decay and brought into the glorious freedom of Gods children.
local: tɩ ɩ a 'lɔ na maal ba bɔfʊ, na wʊ chɛnɛ tara ba nɩ tɩɛr sʊ̃, ɩka a Nãaŋmɩn na wa faa ban yin a gbãgbaa 'lɔ na sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ kpɛ a mãɛ sofʊ 'lɔ na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn bibiir tɩɩr pɔ.

eng: We know that the whole creation is groaning together and suffering labor pains up until now.
local: Bojũu sɩ bɔ̃ɔn naa a bʊ̃maalsɩ a jaa na hʊ̃nɔ̃, ka a ɔɔrɔ ba fɩka pɔɔ na dɔɔrɔ yi a lɛ daar jaa wa tãn a dɩna.

eng: And its not only the creation. We ourselves who have the Spirit as the first crop of the harvest also groan inside as we wait to be adopted and for our bodies to be set free.
local: A ba ɩ lɛ tɛɛ lɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ a sɩn mãɛ lɛ tara a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ kʊfʊ bidɔ̃ɔ, mɩ ʊrɔ a sɩ pʊɔm na sɩ nyʊnyʊlɔ chɛlɛ nɩ wʊn wa lɩɛb sɩ a wʊ bibiir, nɩ a faafʊ wʊn wa faan a sɩ ãgãma.

eng: We were saved in hope. If we see what we hope for, that isnt hope. Who hopes for what they already see?.
local: A tɩɛrtɔ nyãna jũu lɛ sɩ nyɛn a faafʊ. Tɩchɛ bon fʊn nyɛn nɩbir, fʊ maa lɛ tara a wʊ tɩɛr go ɛ. An lɛ ma tɩɛrɛ bon wʊn tara baar?.

eng: But if we hope for what we dont see, we wait for it with patience.
local: Tɩchɛ ala ɩ sɩ tɩɛrɛ na bon sɩn ba tara sɛrɛ ɛ, sɩ ma din kãnyir chɛlɛ.

eng: In the same way, the Spirit comes to help our weakness. We dont know what we should pray, but the Spirit himself pleads our case with unexpressed groans.
local: A lɛ nɩ wʊ ju mɩ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ ma sʊ̃nɔ̃ sɩ a sɩ balfʊ pɔ. Saa bɔ̃ɔ a lɛ sɩn taa ma sʊɔr a Nãaŋmɩn ɛ, a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ mãɛ lɛ ma de hʊ̃ʊnfʊ 'lɔ sɩn kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ man a 'yɛrbie ɛ, na wʊ ma sʊɔrɔ kʊrɔ sɩ.

eng: The one who searches hearts knows how the Spirit thinks, because he pleads for the saints, consistent with Gods will.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ma chɛl a sɩ nyãa bɔ̃ɔn a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ tɩɛr, bojũu a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ nyãna lɛ sʊɔrɔ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊrɔ a wʊ nɩbɛ nɩ a lɛ wʊn bʊɔrɔ.

eng: We know that God works all things together for good for the ones who love God, for those who are called according to his purpose.
local: Sɩ bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka a yele a jaa pɔ, Nãaŋmɩn ma tʊ̃n ka a ɩ sʊ̃ kʊ a bala na nʊ̃ wʊ, a bala wʊn tu a wʊ tɩɛr pɔ bʊɔl.

eng: We know this because God knew them in advance, and he decided in advance that they would be conformed to the image of his Son. That way his Son would be the first of many brothers and sisters.
local: Bojũu bala a Nãaŋmɩn na de nie bɔ̃ɔn na wʊ chɛl ir ka ba lɩɛb kaara a wʊ Bie kaar, ka wʊn ɩ a yɛɛr ba jaa pɔ Bidadɔ̃ɔ.

eng: but I see a different law at work in my body. It wages a war against the law of my mind and takes me prisoner with the law of sin that is in my body.
local: tɩchɛ n nyɛ naa a nɛbinãa ãsʊɔ na tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ a n ãgãn jiir amɩne. Wʊ jɛɛrɛ nɩ naa a n tɩɛr na wʊ maal ma ka n ɩ a yelbier nɩpɔɔraa a n ãgãn jiir pɔ.

eng: We are the ones God has called. We dont come only from the Jews but we also come from the Gentiles.
local: a sɩn gba wʊ mɩ na bʊɔl, na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ tɛɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ na wʊ bʊɔl a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ mɩ?.

eng: If your gift is encouragement, devote yourself to encouraging. The one giving should do it with no strings attached. The leader should lead with passion. The one showing mercy should be cheerful.
local: Ala ɩ ãkpɛ̃ɔ 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ lɛ, vɛ̃ ka wʊ ma 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a ãkpãɛn. Ka ala ɩ a wʊ kʊfʊ lɛ nɩbaalsɩ sʊ̃ʊfʊ, vɛ̃ ka wʊ ma sʊ̃nɔ̃ ba nɩ pʊpɛl. Ala ɩ nɩdiere tʊ̃mɔ̃ lɛ, vɛ̃ a wʊ ma mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir nɩ a tʊ̃mɔ̃. Ala wa ɩ nɩbɛ 'maalfʊ tʊ̃mɔ̃ lɛ, wʊ ma maala a lɛ nɩ chɩlfʊ.

eng: Love should be shown without pretending. Hate evil, and hold on to what is good.
local: Nʊ̃fʊ wʊ ɩ yelmãɛ nʊ̃fʊ. 'Laar bon 'lɔ na ɩ dɛɛr, tɩ 'mar bon 'lɔ na ɩ sʊ̃.

eng: Love each other like the members of your family. Be the best at showing honor to each other.
local: Yɩ chɛlɛ taa yele, na yɩ de a yɛɛr nʊ̃fʊ nʊ̃n taa. Yɩ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a yɩ taaba gar a yɩ mãɛ.

eng: Dont hesitate to be enthusiastic—be on fire in the Spirit as you serve the Lord!.
local: Yɩ ta ɩ nɩ tũtũlsɩ ɛ, tɩchɛ yɩ vɛ̃ ka a nʊ̃mɔ̃ yɩ a yɩ mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir nɩ a sɩ Soro tʊ̃mɔ̃.

eng: Be happy in your hope, stand your ground when youre in trouble, and devote yourselves to prayer.
local: Yɩ chɩlɛ nɩ a yɩ tɩɛrtɔfʊ jie, na yɩ tara kãnyir a yɩ wõm pɔ, na yɩ tara yelmãɛ nɩ a yɩ Nãaŋmɩn sʊɔrfʊ pɔ.

eng: Contribute to the needs of Gods people, and welcome strangers into your home.
local: Yɩ ma lɔ̃ɔ põn a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ bala na ba tara bon jaa ɛ, na yɩ ma diere sãanba a yɩ yie pɔ.

eng: Bless people who harass you—bless and dont curse them.
local: Na yɩ 'yɛrɛ nɛvɩla 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a bala na gere yɩ, na yɩ 'yɛr nɛvɩla 'yɔ̃ɔ ba, yɩ ta pɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ ba ɛ.

eng: Be happy with those who are happy, and cry with those who are crying.
local: Yɩ chɩlɛ nɩ a bala na chɩlɛ, na yɩ mɩ pʊɔ a bala na kõnõ na yɩ kõnõ.

eng: Consider everyone as equal, and dont think that youre better than anyone else. Instead, associate with people who have no status. Dont think that youre so smart.
local: A yɩn nɩ a bala na kpɩɛrɛ, yɩ tara tɩɛr bʊ̃'yen. Yɩ ta kaara ba pɔrɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ yɩ sɔɔ 'matãan a nɩbɛ bala na ba jã yɩ ɛ. Yɩ ta kaa ɩka yɩn ɩn yɛ̃ dẽme ɛ.

eng: Dont pay back anyone for their evil actions with evil actions, but show respect for what everyone else believes is good.
local: Yɩ ta de bier yãan bier sãn ɛ. Yɩ bɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ sʊ̃, na yɩ maala a ala na vɩɛlɛ nɩrɛ jaa niem.

eng: If possible, to the best of your ability, live at peace with all people.
local: Ala ɩ an tʊ̃ɔ naa, an ɩ yɩn mãɛ lɛ, yɩ tara nyã'maar nɩ a bala jaa yɩn lɔ̃ɔ kpɩɛrɛ nɩ.

eng: Dont try to get revenge for yourselves, my dear friends, but leave room for Gods wrath. It is written, Revenge belongs to me; I will pay it back, says the Lord .
local: N barmɩne, yɩ taa ɩrɛ wa yaara taa sãn ɛ, tɩchɛ yɩ baraa kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn suur. Bojũu a sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, Maa lɛ so a sãn yaafʊ, ɩn lɩɛb yaa a sãn. Lɛ lɛ a Soro 'yɛr.

eng: Instead, If your enemy is hungry, feed him; if he is thirsty, give him a drink. By doing this, you will pile burning coals of fire upon his head .
local: Lɛ an na ɩ lɛ ɩka, Ala ɩ kɔ̃ kpɛ̃n a fʊ dɔ̃sʊɔ, kʊ wʊ bʊ̃diir a wʊ di. Ala ɩ kɔ̃nyũur wa kpɛ wʊ naa, kʊ wʊ kʊ̃ɔ a wʊ nyũ. Fʊ̃ʊ be maala a nɩtɔ̃, an kaara fɩka bũu saalbie lɛ fʊ mʊ̃ɔ dɔɔl wʊ a jum.

eng: And David says, Their table should become a pitfall and a trap, a stumbling block and payback to them for what they have done. .
local: A Nãa David mɩ dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr a ba 'yɛr a wʊ gãn pɔ, Vɛ̃ ka a ba bʊ̃diir difʊ jie ka a lɩɛb bʊ̃nyɔɔrsɩ nɩ chɛɛr, ka a ɩ a ba bãr lofʊ jie nɩ a ba sãyar.

eng: Trusting with the heart leads to righteousness, and confessing with the mouth leads to salvation.
local: Bojũu a fʊ nyãa 'lɔ fʊn sɔɔ den lɛ na vɛ̃ ka a fʊ yele tor, ka a fʊ nɛɛ 'lɔ fʊn haan 'yɛr lɛ na faafʊ.

eng: As it says also in Hosea, I will call my people those who arent my people, and the one who isnt well loved, I will call loved one. .
local: Nɩtãa lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na vɛ̃ ka a wʊ 'yɛ'yɛrɛ Hosea sɛb a wʊ gãn pɔ, Ɩn bʊɔl ban a maa nɩbɛ, a bala na ba ɩ a n nɩbɛ ɛ, naa bʊɔl wʊ a n nɩ nʊ̃ naa, 'lɔ na ba dɔ̃ɔ ɩ a n nɩ nʊ̃ naa ɛ.

eng: Im a miserable human being. Who will deliver me from this dead corpse?.
local: Nɩjarma lɛ n pãa ɩ! An lɛ na faa ma yin a yelbier ãgãn nyã na chiine ma a kũu jie?.

eng: Those who God decided in advance would be conformed to his Son, he also called. Those whom he called, he also made righteous. Those whom he made righteous, he also glorified.
local: A bala wʊn de nie ir, bala lɛ wʊ bʊɔl, a bala wʊn bʊɔl, bala lɛ wʊ vɛ̃ ka a ba yele tor, a bala mɩ wʊn vɛ̃ ka a ba yele tor, bala mɩ lɛ wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ tɩɩr.

eng: Their eyes should be darkened so they cant see, and their backs always bent. .
local: A ba nɩbie a jɔ̃ɔ, a ba kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ nyɛ jaa ɛ, na ba kab gʊ̃mɔ̃ kʊ̃ lɛ tʊ̃ɔ dɔl ɛ.

eng: And in the place where it was said to them, You arent my people, there they will be called the living Gods children .
local: Ka wʊ sɛb a nɩtɔ̃nɔ̃ an ɩ a jie 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ wʊn 'yɛr ɩka, Yaa ɩ a n nɩbɛ ɛ, bala naa lɛ ban lɛ bʊɔl a Nãaŋmɩn vʊr bibiir.

eng: But Isaiah cries out for Israel, Though the number of Israels children will be like the sand of the sea, only a remaining part will be saved, .
local: Isaiya mɩ dɔ̃ɔ mʊɔl a Isɩral nɩbɛ na wʊ 'yɛr, Ala ɩ a Isɩral nɩbɛ gba waan fɩka man nɛɛ bire, a bãlãa bala na wa chɛ, lɛ na nyɛ faafʊ.

eng: because the Lord does what he says completely and quickly. .
local: Bojũu a sɩ Soro na pɔɔn naa a wʊ 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ na wʊ ŋmaa a ju.

eng: As Isaiah prophesied, If the Lord of the heavenly forces had not left descendants for us, we would have been like Sodom, and we would have become like Gomorrah. .
local: Isaiya dɔ̃ɔn de nie 'yɛr, Ala ba ɩ a sɩ Soro na ɩ a gãdaa jaa gãdaa sʊɔ lɛ dɔ̃ɔ bar a sɩ par ŋmaa ɛ, sɩn taa mɩ nyʊɔl kaara a Sodom nɩ a Gomora tẽn kaar.

eng: So what are we going to say? Gentiles who werent striving for righteousness achieved righteousness, the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Bo lɛ sɩn pãa lɛ 'yɛr? A bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, na ba dɔ̃ɔ turo a yeltorsɩ ɛ, bala lɛ be tara yaa. A ba yele tor naa ban tara sɔɔfʊ jũu.

eng: But though Israel was striving for a Law of righteousness, they didnt arrive.
local: Tɩchɛ a Isɩral nɩbɛ na turo a yeltorsɩ ba tara yaa ɛ.

eng: Why? Its because they didnt go for it by faith but they went for it as if it could be reached by doing something. They have tripped over a stumbling block.
local: Bo lɛ so? Bojũu baa turo nɩ sɔɔfʊ ɛ, tɩ turo nɩ tʊ̃mɔ̃, na ba bãr a kʊsɩr bãraa.

eng: As it is written: Look! Im putting a stumbling block in Zion, which is a rock that offends people. And the one who has faith in him will not be put to shame. .
local: Nɩtãa lɛ na sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ a wʊ 'yɛr, Nyɛ, n bin a kʊsɩr be a Jiyon ka nɩbɛ na bãra nɩ a pie 'lɔ na vɛ̃ ka ba loro, ka nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na sɔɔ de wʊ, vã kʊ̃ kpɛ wʊ ɛ.

eng: Thank God through Jesus Christ our Lord! So then Im a slave to Gods Law in my mind, but Im a slave to sins law in my body.
local: Nãaŋmɩn son puorfʊ tu a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto na ɩ a sɩ Soro 'yɔ̃ɔ! A maa mãɛ a n tɩɛr pɔ, n ɩn a Nãaŋmɩn nɛɛ gbãgbaa, tɩchɛ a n yelbier ãgãn mɩ ɩ gbãgbaa kʊ a yelbier nɛɛ.

eng: The scripture says, All who have faith in him wont be put to shame .
local: Nɩtãa lɛ an sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ, Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na sɔɔ de wʊ, vã kʊ̃ kpɛ wʊ ɛ.

eng: So what are we going to say about these things? If God is for us, who is against us?.
local: Bo lɛ sɩn pãa 'yɛr nɩ a yele nyãna? Ala ɩ Nãaŋmɩn ben a sɩ sɛ̃, an lɛ na tʊ̃ɔ jɛb sɩ?.

eng: So Im asking you: They havent stumbled so that theyve fallen permanently, have they? Absolutely not! But salvation has come to the Gentiles by their failure, in order to make Israel jealous.
local: N lɛ sʊʊr go, ba ben bãr na lo na ba kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ ir jaa ɛ? A ba ɩ lɛ ɛ. A ba maal bɔrfʊ jũu, a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, nyɛn faafʊ ka nyuur na kpɛ a Isɩral nɩbɛ.

eng: Dont be defeated by evil, but defeat evil with good.
local: Yɩ ta vɛ̃ ka bier tʊ̃ɔ yɩ ɛ, tɩchɛ yɩ de vɩla tʊ̃ɔn bier.

eng: There is no distinction between Jew and Greek, because the same Lord is Lord of all, who gives richly to all who call on him.
local: Bojũu tɛɛ taa ba be a Juu nɩrɛ nɩ a 'lɔ na ba ɩ a Juu nɩrɛ ɛ. A Soro bʊ̃'yen 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ ɩ ba jaa Soro, na wʊ sʊ̃nɔ̃ a bala jaa na bʊɔlɔ a wʊ yuor.

eng: But if their failure brings riches to the world, and their defeat brings riches to the Gentiles, how much more will come from the completion of their number!.
local: Tɩchɛ ala ɩ a ba maal bɔrfʊ vɛ̃n ka a wɛr wʊ jaa le, ka a ba bɔrfʊ mɩ le a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, a bala mãɛ wa pʊɔ ŋmɩnɛ lɛ an kaara!.

eng: All who call on the Lords name will be saved. .
local: Bojũu a sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, Bala jaa na bʊɔlɔ a Soro yuor ɩka wʊ sʊ̃ʊ ba, na nyɛn faafʊ.

eng: Im speaking to you Gentiles. Considering that Im an apostle to the Gentiles, I publicize my own ministry.
local: Yɩn na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ, an ben ɩka maa lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn ir tʊ̃ a yɩ pɔ, ka n ɩ a nɩtʊ̃nãa a yɩn na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ pɔ. N mʊ̃ɔn nɩbir nɩ a tʊ̃mɔ̃ nyã.

eng: in the hope that somehow I might make my own people jealous and save some of them.
local: A maa tɩɛr lɛ ɩka nyuur wʊ kpɛ a maa mãɛ nɩbɛ ka n tʊ̃ɔ a bamɩne faa.

eng: If their rejection has brought about a close relationship between God and the world, how can their acceptance mean anything less than life from the dead?.
local: Bojũu ala ɩ a ba tɔr barfʊ ɩn 'maalfʊ kʊ a wɛr, ka a Nãaŋmɩn lɛ wa de ba, ŋmɩnɛ lɛ an kaara, a kʊ̃ kaara fɩka nɩrɛ na yi kũu pɔ tara nyɛvʊr ɛ?.

eng: But if part of a batch of dough is offered to God as holy, the whole batch of dough is holy too. If a root is holy, the branches will be holy too.
local: Ala ɩ fʊ den a paanʊ̃dɔ̃ɔ kʊ Nãaŋmɩn, a paanʊ̃ɔ ala na chɛ, a jaa ɩn chɛchɛ. Ala ɩ fʊ den a tɩɛ nyɩɩr kʊ Nãaŋmɩn, a tɩɛ wilsɩ mɩ ɩn chɛchɛ.

eng: If some of the branches were broken off, and you were a wild olive branch, and you were grafted in among the other branches and shared the root that produces the rich oil of the olive tree.
local: Ala ɩ ba 'mɛl a tɩɛ mãɛ wile ãsʊɔ bar tɩ ŋmaa a yɩn na ɩ a mʊɔ pɔ tɩɛ wile wa mar ka yɩ 'mataa tɔɔrɔ nyuro a kɔ̃bʊ̃'yen yire a tɩɛ ãgãn pɔ nʊ̃ɔnɔ̃.

eng: then dont brag like youre better than the other branches. If you do brag, be careful: its not you that sustains the root, but its the root that sustains you.
local: taa tɩɛr ɩka fʊ sa naa a wilsɩ ala ɛ. Fʊ̃ʊ wa 'mɔɔrɔ fʊ mãɛ, lɩɛb tɩɛr kaa ɩka a ba ɩ a fʊ̃ʊ na ɩ a wile lɛ tuo a nyɩbɛ ɛ, tɩ a nyɩbɛ lɛ tuo a fʊ̃ʊ na ɩ a wile.

eng: You will say then, Branches were broken off so that I could be grafted in.
local: Yɩn 'yɛr yɩn pãa na 'yɛr nɩ nyã, Ba 'mɛl a wilsɩ ala bar tɩ pãa na de ma mar.

eng: Fine. They were broken off because they werent faithful, but you stand only by your faithfulness. So dont think in a proud way; instead be afraid.
local: A lɛ ɩn yelmãɛ, tɩchɛ ba dɔ̃ɔ 'mɛl ba bar a ba ba sɔɔfʊ jũu, tɩchɛ ka a yɩn ara nɩ sɔɔfʊ. Yɩ ta bʊɔrɔ yuor ɛ, tɩchɛ yɩ joro dãbãɛ.

eng: If God didnt spare the natural branches, he wont spare you either.
local: Bojũu ala ɩ Nãaŋmɩn ba dɔ̃ɔ bar a tɩɛ mãɛ wilsɩ ɛ, wʊ kʊ̃ bar a yɩn mɩ ɛ.

eng: So look at Gods kindness and harshness. Its harshness toward those who fell, but its Gods kindness for you, provided you continue in his kindness; otherwise, you could be cut off too.
local: Yɩ tɩɛr kaa lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na ɩ a maal vɩla sʊɔ ka a wʊ yele mɩ kpɛ̃mɛ, a wʊ yele kpɛ̃mɛ na kʊ a bala na lo, tɩchɛ na wʊ maal vɩla kʊ a yɩn, ala ɩ yɩ chɛnɛ na chiine a wʊ maal vɩla pɔ. Ka lɛ ba lɛ ɛ, wʊn ŋmaa yɩn bar.

eng: And even those who were cut off will be grafted back in if they dont continue to be unfaithful, because God is able to graft them in again.
local: Ala wa ɩ ba tɛɛn a ba baal bar, Nãaŋmɩn na tʊ̃ɔn de ba lɛ mar a tɩɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ, Nãaŋmɩn na tʊ̃ɔn lɛ de ba mar a lɛ go.

eng: He didnt spare his own Son but gave him up for us all. Wont he also freely give us all things with him?.
local: A 'lɔ na ba mɔ̃ɔ sɩ a wʊ mãɛ bie ɛ, tɩchɛ na wʊ sɔɔ bar sɔr ka ba kʊ wʊ a sɩ jũu, bo lɛ na ŋmaa wʊ sɔr ka wʊ kʊ̃ de a wʊ wãɛfʊ 'matãan a bomo a jaa kʊ sɩ ɛ?.

eng: Who will bring a charge against Gods elect people? It is God who acquits them.
local: An lɛ na tʊ̃ɔ 'yɛr 'yɛr dɔɔl a bala a Nãaŋmɩn na ir? Nãaŋmɩn mãɛ lɛ maal ba a ba tor.

eng: Who is going to convict them? It is Christ Jesus who died, even more, who was raised, and who also is at Gods right side. It is Christ Jesus who also pleads our case for us.
local: An 'lɔ lɛ na tʊ̃ɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ ba 'yɛr? Nɩrɛ jaa ba be be ɛ. A Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto nyã na kpi, 'lɔ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn mɩ sãɩ ka wʊ ir ɩ vʊr na wʊ ara Nãaŋmɩn nũsʊ̃ɔ jie, 'lɔ lɛ sʊɔrɔ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊrɔ sɩ.

eng: Who will separate us from Christs love? Will we be separated by trouble, or distress, or harassment, or famine, or nakedness, or danger, or sword?.
local: An lɛ na tʊ̃ɔ tɔɔ sɩ yin a Kɩrɩsɩto nʊ̃fʊ nyã pɔ? Wõm, bɩɩ wʊʊr, bɩɩ gefʊ, bɩɩ kɔ̃, bɩɩ sal'mʊɔr, bɩɩ dɔɔyɛ, bɩɩ ban na kʊ sɩ nɩ sʊɔ?.

eng: As it is written, We are being put to death all day long for your sake. We are treated like sheep for slaughter. .
local: Nɩtãa lɛ an sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ, A yɩ jũu, sɩ ma tuor kũu bɩbir jaa. Ba ma kaa sɩn fɩka piir ban chiine nɩ kɔkɔɛ ŋmafʊ jie.

eng: But in all these things we win a sweeping victory through the one who loved us.
local: Kai, a anyãna a jaa pɔ, sɩ ɩ naa tʊ̃tʊ̃ɔnbɔ tu a 'lɔ na nʊ̃ sɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: Im convinced that nothing can separate us from Gods love in Christ Jesus our Lord: not death or life, not angels or rulers, not present things or future things, not powers.
local: Bojũu n bɔ̃ɔn sɩrɛ jaa ɩka kũu, bɩɩ nyɛvʊr, bɩɩ malakasɩ, bɩɩ a sɩdɛbɛ, bɩɩ ka dɩna lɛ, bɩɩ nɩdaar, bɩɩ ka kpãɛn dẽme lɛ.

eng: or height or depth, or any other thing that is created.
local: ka dofʊ lɛ, bɩɩ suufʊ lɛ, bɩɩ bon jaa na be a bʊ̃maalsɩ pɔ lɛ, ãsʊɔ jaa kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ tɔɔ sɩ yin a nʊ̃fʊ a Nãaŋmɩn na nʊ̃ sɩ a sɩ Soro Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: So how can they call on someone they dont have faith in? And how can they have faith in someone they havent heard of? And how can they hear without a preacher?.
local: Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ ban ɩrɛ wa bʊɔl a Nãaŋmɩn 'lɔ ban ba sɔɔ ɛ? Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ ban ɩ wa sɔɔ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ ban ba wõ a wʊ 'yɛr ɛ? Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ ban ɩrɛ wa wõ a wʊ 'yɛr ka nɩrɛ kɔ̃ɔ ba wa mʊɔl a wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba ɛ?.

eng: If you were naturally part of a wild olive tree and you were cut off from it, and then, contrary to nature, you were grafted into the cultivated olive tree, wont these natural branches stand an even better chance of being grafted back onto their own olive tree?.
local: Yɩ kaa, ala ɩ a yɩn ban ŋmaa yin a mʊɔ pɔ tɩɛ pɔ wa mar a yir tɩɛ ban sɛl, an lɛ ɩn tuo a yir tɩɛ wilsɩ ala ban dɔ̃ɔ 'mɛl bar marfʊ bɩɩ?.

eng: And how can they preach unless they are sent? As it is written, How beautiful are the feet of those who announce the good news. .
local: Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ ban ɩrɛ wa mʊɔl ka baa tʊ̃ ba ɛ? Nɩtãa lɛ an sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ, Nyɛ lɛ a bala na waara nɩ a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ gbɛɛ na vɩɛlɛ ka.

eng: But everyone hasnt obeyed the good news. As Isaiah says, Lord, who has had faith in our message? .
local: Tɩchɛ a ba ɩ a Isɩral nɩbɛ ba jaa lɛ lɩɛb kaa a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyã ɛ. Bojũu Isaiya dɔ̃ɔ sʊʊr, Sɩ Soro, an lɛ sɔɔ de a sɩ 'yɛr.

eng: I dont want you to be unaware of this secret, brothers and sisters. That way you wont think too highly of yourselves. A part of Israel has become resistant until the full number of the Gentiles comes in.
local: N ba bʊɔrɔ ɩka n vɛ̃ ka a yelsɔɔlaa nyã sɔɔl a yɩn na ɩ a n yɛɛr tɩ ba ɩ a Isɩral nɩbɛ ɛ, ka yɩ taa bʊɔrɔ yuor nɩ a yele nyã ɛ. A Isɩral nɩbɛ tara na a nyãkpãɛn a lɛ nɛ na tɩ tãn a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ a bala na ba ɩ a Isɩral nɩbɛ ɛ, na wa tɩ nyɛ a faafʊ baar.

eng: In this way, all Israel will be saved, as it is written: The deliverer will come from Zion. He will remove ungodly behavior from Jacob. .
local: Lɛ lɛ na ɩ tɩ a Isɩral nɩbɛ ba jaa pãa nyɛ faafʊ nɩtãa lɛ na sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ, Fafaara na yin be a Jiyon wa, na wʊ wa lɩɛb tɩɛr jaa na ba vɩɛlɛ ɛ, yin a Jekɔb bal nɩbɛ pɔ bar.

eng: This is my covenant with them, when I take away their sins .
local: A nɛtɩraa lɛ a nyãna ɩn na tara nɩ ba, a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ ɩn wa de a ba yeldɛbɛ a jaa bar.

eng: According to the gospel, they are enemies for your sake, but according to Gods choice, they are loved for the sake of their ancestors.
local: Fʊ̃ʊ kaa, a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyã vɛ̃n ka ba ɩ Nãaŋmɩn dɔ̃dẽme a yɩn na sɔɔ de jũu. Tɩchɛ ala ɩ a irfʊ pɔ mɩ lɛ, bala lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nʊ̃ yɔɔ a ba sãakpãmɩne jũu.

eng: Gods gifts and calling cant be taken back.
local: Bojũu Nãaŋmɩn kʊfʊ nɩ a wʊ bʊɔlfʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ lɩɛb jaa ɛ.

eng: So, faith comes from listening, but its listening by means of Christs message.
local: Sɩrɛ jaa lɛ ɩka 'yɛr fʊn ma wõ pɔ lɛ fʊ ma sɔɔ, ka a 'yɛrbie ala ban wõ ɩn a Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɛr.

eng: But I ask you, didnt they hear it? Definitely! Their voice has gone out into the entire earth, and their message has gone out to the corners of the inhabited world. .
local: Tɩchɛ ka n sʊʊr, a mɔ̃ baa wõ naa ɛ? Sɩrɛ jaa lɛ ba wõ naa naa. A ba kɔkɔr yin de jie wʊ jaa, ka a ba 'yɛr tɩ ta a wɛr tɛɛfʊ jie.

eng: Once you were disobedient to God, but now you have mercy because they were disobedient.
local: Nɩtãa lɛ a yɩn mɩ na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, na yaa dɔ̃ɔ wõnõ a Nãaŋmɩn toor ɛ, tɩchɛ pʊ̃pãanyã yɩ nyɛn a wʊ nɩbaal a ba bawõfʊ jũu.

eng: But I ask you again, didnt Israel understand? First, Moses says, I will make you jealous of those who arent a people, of a people without understanding. .
local: N lɛ sʊʊr kaa go, a mɔ̃ a Isɩral nɩbɛ ba bɔ̃ɔ a par ɛ? Mosesɩ dɔ̃ɔ de nie 'yɛr, A bala na ba ɩ paal ɛ, ɩn maal ba naa ka nyuur kpɛ yɩ, naa lɛ maal a paal 'lɔ na ba bɔ̃ɔ bon jaa ɛ, ka suur kpɛ yi.

eng: In the same way, they have also been disobedient because of the mercy that you received, so now they can receive mercy too.
local: Lɛ lɛ a bala mɩ na ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ, ba mɩ lɩɛb ɩ bawõnsɩ, na ba pãa mɩ na nyɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbaal a pʊ̃pãanyã, nɩtãa lɛ a sɩn mɩ na nyɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbaal.

eng: God has locked up all people in disobedience, in order to have mercy on all of them.
local: Bojũu Nãaŋmɩn pɔɔn a nɩbɛ ba jaa a bawõnsɩ die pɔ, na wʊn tʊ̃ɔ kaa ba jaa nɩbaal.

eng: And Isaiah even dares to say, I was found by those who didnt look for me; I revealed myself to those who didnt ask for me. .
local: Ka Isaiya mɩ ba dɔ̃ɔ jo dãbãɛ ɛ, tɩ 'yɛr ɩka, A bala na ba dɔ̃ɔ yʊɔrɔ bʊɔrɔ ma ɛ, ba nyɛ ma naa, n wiil naa n mãɛ kʊ a bala na ba dɔ̃ɔ sʊrɔ ma ɛ.

eng: Gods riches, wisdom, and knowledge are so deep! They are as mysterious as his judgments, and they are as hard to track as his paths!.
local: Nyɛ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn lefʊ na suu jã. A wʊ yɛ̃ nɩ a wʊ bɔ̃ɔfʊ ba gbʊr ɛ! A wʊ 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ ba be bɔ̃ɔfʊ ɛ. A wʊ sɔɩ mɩ ba be bɩɛrfʊ ɛ!.

eng: But he says about Israel, All day long I stretched out my hands to a disobedient and contrary people. .
local: Tɩchɛ a Isɩral nɩbɛ 'lɔ, nɩtɔ̃ lɛ wʊ 'yɛr, Bɩbir jaa lɛ n ma tur a n nuur bʊɔlɔ a bibawõnsɩ nɩ a nɩdɛbɛ, na turo a sɔɩ ala na ba vɩɛlɛ ɛ bʊɔrɔ a ba mãɛ tɩɛrsɩ bʊ̃bʊɔrsɩ.

eng: Who has known the Lords mind? Or who has been his mentor? .
local: An lɛ dɔ̃ɔ bɔ̃ɔ a sɩ Soro tɩɛr? Bɩɩ an lɛ mɩ dɔ̃ɔ ɩ a wʊ 'yɛr sʊ̃nɔ̃.

eng: Every person should place themselves under the authority of the government. There isnt any authority unless it comes from God, and the authorities that are there have been put in place by God.
local: A yɩ ãsʊɔ jaa wʊ ma siir a wʊ mãɛ kʊ a paal nɩbɛrɛ, bojũu kpɛ̃ɔ jaa ba be be ka a ba ɩ Nãaŋmɩn lɛ bin ɛ. Kpɛ̃ɔ sʊɔ jaa ba be be ka Nãaŋmɩn ba lɛ bin wʊ ɛ.

eng: So anyone who opposes the authority is standing against what God has established. People who take this kind of stand will get punished.
local: A lɛ jũu, nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na chɩrɛ nɩ a kpãɛn dẽme ba nyãna, a Nãaŋmɩn na bin ba lɛ wʊ chɩrɛ nɩ, ka a bala na ɩrɛ a lɛ na 'yɔ̃ɔn ba mãɛ 'yɛr.

eng: Or who has given him a gift and has been paid back by him? .
local: An lɛ dɔ̃ɔ de nie kʊ Nãaŋmɩn bon, ka wʊ pãa wa yara a sãn.

eng: All things are from him and through him and for him. May the glory be to him forever. Amen.
local: Bojũu wʊ jie lɛ a bomo a jaa yi. 'Lɔ lɛ maal a jaa, na wʊ so a bonsɩ a jaa. 'Lɔ lɛ so a tɩɩr bɩbir nɩ bɩbir jaa. A ɩ a lɛ.

eng: The authorities dont frighten people who are doing the right thing. Rather, they frighten people who are doing wrong. Would you rather not be afraid of authority? Do whats right, and you will receive its approval.
local: Bojũu a kpãɛn dẽme maa diere bier dɔɔlɔ a bala na maala vɩla ɛ, tɩchɛ a bala na maala a dɛɛr lɛ ba ma de yele dɔɔlɔ. Fʊ bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka fʊ ta joro a kpãɛn dẽme dãbãɛ bɩɩ? Fʊ pãa maala a lɛ na vɩɛlɛ, ban dãna fʊ naa.

eng: It is Gods servant given for your benefit. But if you do whats wrong, be afraid because it doesnt have weapons to enforce the law for nothing. It is Gods servant put in place to carry out his punishment on those who do what is wrong.
local: Bojũu wʊ ɩn Nãaŋmɩn tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ ɩka wʊ maal fʊ vɩla. Tɩchɛ fʊ̃ʊ wa maal dɛɛr 'lɔ, vɛ̃ ka dãbãɛ kʊrɔ fʊ, a ba ɩ pɔrɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ wʊ tara a sʊɔ ɛ. Nãaŋmɩn tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ lɛ wʊ ɩ na ara kʊ yeltorsɩ ɩka wʊ kpãana a bala na maala dɛɛr.

eng: Welcome the person who is weak in faith—but not in order to argue about differences of opinion.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ sɔɔfʊ na ba jã bon ɛ, yɩ ma sɔɔ de wʊ na yɩ ta tɔɔrɔ a wʊ yelbil 'yɛr ɛ.

eng: That is why it is necessary to place yourself under the governments authority, not only to avoid Gods punishment but also for the sake of your conscience.
local: A lɛ jũu, a tara na sɔr ka yɩ siir yɩ mãɛ kʊ a kpãɛn dẽme, a ba ɩ ban na kpãa yɩ tɛɛ jũu ɛ, tɩchɛ an ɩ a yɩn mãɛ mɩ bɔ̃ɔ nyɔɔfʊ jũu.

eng: You should also pay taxes for the same reason, because the authorities are Gods assistants, concerned with this very thing.
local: A anyãna jũu lɛ so yɩn yara lõpoo kʊrɔ a kpãɛn dẽme na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ na de a ba sɔ̃ɔ wʊ jaa tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ.

eng: So pay everyone what you owe them. Pay the taxes you owe, pay the duties you are charged, give respect to those you should respect, and honor those you should honor.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa sãn fʊn wa di, yab wʊ. Ka ala ɩ lõpoo lɛ faa yab ɛ, yab. Ka tẽmaal libie yabfʊ lɛ bɩɩ, yɩ ma yab, na yɩ joro a 'lɔ a jofʊ na sɛɛn, tɩ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a 'lɔ mɩ a 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ na sɛɛn.

eng: Dont be in debt to anyone, except for the obligation to love each other. Whoever loves another person has fulfilled the Law.
local: Yɩ taa pɛ̃nɛ taa libie ka sãn ma gã a yɩ chara pɔ ɛ, a sãn 'lɔ tɛɛ na gã a yɩ chara pɔ lɛ, nʊ̃fʊ yɩn na nʊ̃ nʊ̃ taa, bojũu nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na nʊ̃ a wʊ tɔ sʊɔ tun a lɛ a nɛbinãa na 'yɛr a jaa.

eng: The commandments, Dont commit adultery, dont murder, dont steal, dont desire what others have, and any other commandments, are all summed up in one word: You must love your neighbor as yourself. .
