local: Ka a fʊ yɛbɛ nyã a wʊ sɔɔfʊ na ba kpɛ̃mɛ ɛ, 'lɔ a Kɩrɩsɩto na kpi kʊ, fʊ sɔ̃ɔ wʊ naa nɩ a fʊ bɔ̃ɔfʊ nyã bar.

eng: Judge for yourselves: Is it appropriate for a woman to pray to God with her head uncovered?.
local: Yɩn mãɛ yɩ pɛkaa. A sɛɛn ka pɔɔ na sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn tɩ ba pɔɔ a ju bɩɩ?.

eng: You sin against Christ if you sin against your brothers and sisters and hurt their weak consciences this way.
local: Fʊ̃ʊ wa maal a bier nyã tɔ kʊ a fʊ yɛɛr, fʊ 'yɔ̃ɔn a ba tɩɛrsɩ baal, Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto mãɛ lɛ fʊ maal a bier a lɛ nɛ.

eng: Lets not test Christ, like some of them did, and were killed by the snakes.
local: Yɩ taa vɛ̃ nɛ ka sɩ wa bɛɛrɛ a sɩ Soro kaara nɩtãa lɛ a bamɩne na dɔ̃ɔ ɩ, a wʊ vɛ̃ ka a waba dʊ̃ ba kʊ ɛ.

eng: Doesnt nature itself teach you that if a man has long hair, it is a disgrace to him.
local: A befʊ nyãna mɩ ba wiil yɩ ɩka ala ɩ daba mɩ tur jukɔɔl, a ɩn vã kʊ wʊ ɛ?.

eng: This is why, if food causes the downfall of my brother or sister, I wont eat meat ever again, or else I may cause my brother or sister to fall.
local: A lɛ jũu, ala ɩ bon ɩn na di na vɛ̃n ka a n yɛbɛ lo yelbier pɔ, n kʊ̃ lɛ 'wɔb nʊ̃n jaa ɛ, na n taa wa vɛ̃ ka wʊ lo ɛ.

eng: Lets not grumble, like some of them did, and were killed by the destroyer.
local: na yɩ taa vʊ̃ nɛɛr 'yɔ̃ɔ Nãaŋmɩn ɛ, nɩtãa lɛ a bamɩne na dɔ̃ɔ vʊ̃n wʊ a nɛɛ ka wʊ vɛ̃ ka kũu malaka wa sɔ̃ɔ ba jaa.

eng: but if a woman has long hair, it is her glory? This is because her long hair is given to her for a covering.
local: Tɩchɛ ala ɩ pɔɔ lɛ wa bar a wʊ jukɔɔl ka a wooro a ba ɩ wʊ danʊ̃ɔ lɛ ɛ? Bojũu Nãaŋmɩn lɛ kʊ wʊ a jukɔɔl ka a ɩ a wʊ bʊ̃pɔɔraa.

eng: These things happened to them as an example and were written as a warning for us to whom the end of time has come.
local: A yele anyãna pɔɔ ba naa, naa ɩ kpãafʊ gã, ka ba sɛb bin ka a na ɩ kpãafʊ kʊ sɩ. Bojũu a ber baaraa gbʊrɔ sɩ naa.

eng: So those who think they are standing need to watch out or else they may fall.
local: A lɛ jũu, ala ɩ fʊ tɩɛr ɩka fʊ bɔ̃ɔn ara, bɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ sʊ̃, na fʊ taa lo ɛ.

eng: No temptation has seized you that isnt common for people. But God is faithful. He wont allow you to be tempted beyond your abilities. Instead, with the temptation, God will also supply a way out so that you will be able to endure it.
local: Bɛɛrkaa tuo jaa ba wa a yɩ sɛ̃, ka a ba ɩ a ala mɩn ma wa a nɩrɛ jaa sɛ̃ lɛ ɛ. Nãaŋmɩn ɩn yelmãɛ sʊɔ, wʊ kʊ̃ sɔɔ ka a bɛɛrkaa tuo 'lɔ yɩn kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ ɛ, wa nyɔɔ yɩ ɛ. Tɩchɛ bɛɛrkaa wa nyɔɔ yɩ, wʊn wiil yɩn a sɔr ka yɩ tu yi, na yɩ tʊ̃ɔ ara.

eng: So then, my dear friends, run away from the worship of false gods!.
local: A lɛ jũu n taa Yesu turbo, yɩ taa ɩrɛ wa puoro tɩbɛ ɛ.

eng: Im talking to you like you are sensible people. Think about what Im saying.
local: N bɔ̃ɔn naa yɩn ɩ yɛ̃ dẽme ka n 'yɛrɛ a nɩtɔ̃ kʊrɔ yɩ, yɩn mãɛ yɩ pɛkaa a lɛ ɩn 'yɛr.

eng: Isnt the cup of blessing that we bless a sharing in the blood of Christ? Isnt the loaf of bread that we break a sharing in the body of Christ?.
local: A ŋmele 'lɔ sɩn ma de puor a Nãaŋmɩn yãan, a Yesu kũu bʊ̃diir sɔ̃ɔ ma vɛ̃n ka sɩ lɔ̃ɔ põ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto jãɩ. Ka a paanʊ̃ɔ 'lɔ sɩ mɩn ma ŋma, ma vɛ̃ ka sɩ lɔ̃ɔ põ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto ãgãn.

eng: Since there is one loaf of bread, we who are many are one body, because we all share the one loaf of bread.
local: Bojũu paanʊ̃ɔ bʊ̃'yen tɛɛ lɛ, sɩn sɔɔ waa tɩ sɩ ɩn ãgãn bʊ̃'yen, sɩ jaa lɛ a paanʊ̃ɔ bʊ̃'yen 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ ma ta.

eng: Look at the people of Israel. Dont those who eat the sacrifices share from the altar?.
local: Yɩ tɩɛr kaa a Isɩral nɩbɛ pɔ. A ba ɩ a bala na ma di a Nãaŋmɩn bɔɔrlo bomo ma pʊɔn a bɔɔr lofʊ jie ɛ?.

eng: What am I saying then? That food sacrificed to a false god is anything, or that a false god is anything?.
local: N pãa 'yɛrɛ na ɩka a bomo ban lon bɔɔr kʊ a tɩɩ ɩn bon bɩɩ a tɩɩ ɩn bon?.

eng: But if someone wants to argue about this, we dont have such a custom, nor do Gods churches.
local: Ala ɩ nɩrɛ wa chɩrɛ na a yele anyãna, sɩn nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn puorbo ba lɛ tara yele jaa go ɛ.

eng: Now I dont praise you as I give the following instruction because when you meet together, it does more harm than good.
local: A ala na tu ka ɩn wiil yɩ, tɩ n kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ dãn yɩ ɛ, bojũu a yɩ lɔ̃ɔ taa ma maal yɩn dɛɛr gar a vɩla.

eng: First of all, when you meet together as a church, I hear that there are divisions among you, and I partly believe it.
local: A yeldɔ̃ɔ nɩ nyã, n wõn ɩka yɩn ma wa lɔ̃ɔ taa na puor, yɩ ma ooro nɩ taa, ka n sɔɔ de ɩka, a lɛ ben be sɩrɛ jaa.

eng: Its necessary that there are groups among you, to make it clear who is genuine.
local: A sɛɛ naa ka tɛɛ taa na be a yɩ pɔ, ka a wiil buor sʊɔ na sɔɔ Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: So when you get together in one place, it isnt to eat the Lords meal.
local: Yɩn ma wa lɔ̃ɔ taa, a ba ɩ a Yesu kũu bʊ̃diir lɛ yɩ ma dire ɛ.

eng: Each of you goes ahead and eats a private meal. One person goes hungry while another is drunk.
local: Bojũu yɩn ma wa dire, ãsʊɔ jaa ma bʊ̃n dire na yɩ maa gu taa ɛ. Ka a bamɩne ma wa jɛ̃n kɔ̃, tɩchɛ ka a bamɩne mɩ ma nyũ buu.

eng: Dont you have houses to eat and drink in? Or do you look down on Gods churches and humiliate those who have nothing? What can I say to you? Will I praise you? No, I dont praise you in this.
local: A mɔ̃ yaa tara yie yɩn na ma di tɩ nyũ a be ɛ? Bɩɩ yɩ kaara naa a Nãaŋmɩn puorbo pɔrɔ na yɩ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a bala na ba tara bon jaa vã? N 'yɛr bo kʊ yɩ? A mɔ̃ n dãn yɩ nɩ a yele nyã ɔ? N kʊ̃ ɩ lɛ ɛ!.

eng: I received a tradition from the Lord, which I also handed on to you: on the night on which he was betrayed, the Lord Jesus took bread.
local: Bojũu a wiilfʊ 'lɔ ɩn nyɛ yi a sɩ Soro sɛ̃, lɛ mɩ lɛ ɩn wiil yɩ. A tãsɔɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ jaa ban na gar yɔ̃ɔ a sɩ Soro Yesu, wʊ den a paanʊ̃ɔ, tɩ puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan.

eng: After giving thanks, he broke it and said, This is my body, which is for you; do this to remember me.
local: Wʊn wa puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan baar, wʊ ŋmaan a paanʊ̃ɔ tɩ 'yɛr, Nyã lɛ ɩ a n ãgãn na wʊ kʊ yɩ, yɩ ma maala a nɩtɔ̃nɔ̃ tɩɛrɛ nɩ ma.

eng: He did the same thing with the cup, after they had eaten, saying, This cup is the new covenant in my blood. Every time you drink it, do this to remember me.
local: A lɛ nɩ wʊ ju lɛ ban wa di baar, wʊ den a ŋmel nyuraa na wʊ 'yɛrɛ, A dãa na be a ŋmele nyãna pɔ lɛ ɩ a n jãɩ a Nãaŋmɩn na de bin a nɛtɩr paalaa, yɩ ma maala a nɩtɔ̃nɔ̃ yɩn ma wa nyuro na yɩ tɩɛrɛ nɩ ma.

eng: Every time you eat this bread and drink this cup, you broadcast the death of the Lord until he comes.
local: Sɔ̃ɔ jaa yɩn ma wa ɔɔrɔ a paanʊ̃ɔ nyãna, na yɩ nyuro nɩ a ŋmele nyãna, yɩ mʊɔlɔ 'yɛrɛ a sɩ Soro kũu 'yɛr tɩ tãn a wʊ waa daar.

eng: This is why those who eat the bread or drink the cup of the Lord inappropriately will be guilty of the Lords body and blood.
local: A lɛ jũu, nɩrɛ nyã na ɔɔrɔ a paanʊ̃ɔ nyã, na wʊ nyuro nɩ a ŋmele nyã nɩ ba bãma, wʊ maal yelbier nɩ a sɩ Soro ãgãn nɩ a wʊ jãɩ.

eng: Each individual should test himself or herself, and eat from the bread and drink from the cup in that way.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa wʊ ma bɛɛr a wʊ mãɛ kaa tɩ pãa 'wɔb a paanʊ̃ɔ nyã, na wʊ nyũn a ŋmele nyã.

eng: No, but this kind of sacrifice is sacrificed to demons and not to God. I dont want you to be sharing in demons.
local: Kai, tɩchɛ a bɔɔrlo bomo ala a tɩbɛ dẽme na ma lon a ba bɔɔr, a sɩdɛbɛ lɛ ba ma lon a bɔɔr kʊ a ba ɩ Nãaŋmɩn lɛ ɛ, ala lɛ so n ba bʊɔrɔ ɩka yɩ 'matãan a sɩdɛbɛ ɛ.

eng: You cant drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons; you cant participate in the table of the Lord and the table of demons.
local: Yɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ lɔ̃ɔn a sɩ Soro nyũn a wʊ ŋmele, tɩ lɛ lɔ̃ɔn a sɩdɛbɛ nyũn a ba ŋmele ɛ. Yɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ lɔ̃ɔn a sɩ Soro a wʊ bʊ̃diir difʊ jie, tɩ lɛ 'mataa lɔ̃ɔn a sɩdɛbɛ bʊ̃diir difʊ jie go ɛ.

eng: Or should we make the Lord jealous? We arent stronger than he is, are we?.
local: Sɩ bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka a ɩrɛ a sɩ Soro nyuur bɩɩ? Bɩɩ sɩn lɛ tara kpɛ̃ɔ gar wʊ?.

eng: Those who eat and drink without correctly understanding the body are eating and drinking their own judgment.
local: Bojũu nɩrɛ jaa na ɔɔrɔ na wʊ nyuro tɩ ba bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a sɩ Soro ãgãn lɛ ɛ, wʊ 'wɔb nyũn 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ a wʊ mãɛ.

eng: Everything is permitted, but everything isnt beneficial. Everything is permitted, but everything doesnt build others up.
local: Yɩ ma 'yɛr ɩka, saa chire yele jaa maalfʊ ɛ. Tɩchɛ a ba ɩ yele a jaa lɛ vɩɛlɛ maalfʊ ɛ. Yele jaa ba chire ɛ, tɩchɛ a ba ɩ yele jaa lɛ sʊ̃nɔ̃ ɛ.

eng: No one should look out for their own advantage, but they should look out for each other.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa wʊ ta bʊɔrɔ a wʊ mãɛ tɛɛ vɩla ɛ, tɩchɛ wʊ mɩ ma bʊɔrɔ a wʊ taaba vɩla.

eng: Eat everything that is sold in the marketplace, without asking questions about it because of your conscience.
local: Nʊ̃n jaa ban daara a daa pɔ, fʊ̃ʊ wa da ɔɔrɔ ta sʊʊr ɛ, ka a taa ɩ fʊ tɩɛr ɛ.

eng: Because of this, many of you are weak and sick, and quite a few have died.
local: Ala lɛ so ka a yɩ yɔɔ bal na yɩ bɩɛrɛ, ka a yɩ yɔɔ jie mɩ kpire.

eng: The earth and all that is in it belong to the Lord .
local: Bojũu a sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, A sɩ Soro lɛ so a tẽe nɩ bon jaa na be a pɔ.

eng: But if we had judged ourselves, we wouldnt be judged.
local: Ala ɩ sɩ tɔɔr a sɩ mãɛ 'yɛr, a sɩ Soro kʊ̃ lɛ tɔɔr a sɩ 'yɛr go ɛ.

eng: However, we are disciplined by the Lord when we are judged so that we wont be judged and condemned along with the whole world.
local: A sɩ Soro wa tɔɔr a sɩ 'yɛr, toor lɛ wʊ tɔɔ sɩ, na wʊ taa wa lɔ̃ɔ sɔ̃ɔn sɩ nɩ a bala na ba puoro ɛ.

eng: For these reasons, my brothers and sisters, when you get together to eat, wait for each other.
local: A lɛ jũu, n taa Yesu turbo, yɩn ma wa lɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ taa ɩka yɩ di, yɩ ma gu taa.

eng: If some of you are hungry, they should eat at home so that getting together doesnt lead to judgment. I will give directions about the other things when I come.
local: Ala ɩ kɔ̃ kʊrɔ na ãsʊɔ, wʊ ma di be a wʊ yir, ka a yɩn wa lɔ̃ɔ taa dire, a ta wa ɩ Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ kʊ yɩ ɛ. Maa wa tʊ̃ɔ tɩ, ɩn man yɩ naa a ala na chɛ.

eng: If an unbeliever invites you to eat with them and you want to go, eat whatever is served, without asking questions because of your conscience.
local: Ala ɩ nɩrɛ ba ɩ Yesu turo ɛ, wa maal bʊ̃diir bʊɔl fʊ, a fʊ bʊɔrɔ a chenfʊ, fʊ̃ʊ wa chen, bon jaa wʊn wa bin a fʊ niem, dire, ta sʊʊr ɛ, ka a taa ɩ fʊ tɩɛr ɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I dont want you to be ignorant about spiritual gifts.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã, n yɛɛr, n ba bʊɔrɔ ɩka a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ kʊfʊsɩ yele a sɔɔl yɩ ɛ.

eng: But if someone says to you, This meat was sacrificed in a temple, then dont eat it for the sake of the one who told you and for the sake of conscience.
local: Tɩchɛ ala ɩ nɩrɛ wa 'yɛr kʊ fʊ ɩka tɩɩ nʊ̃n lɛ, ta lɛ di a bʊ̃diir ala ɛ, a fʊ̃ʊ nɩ a nɩnyɩnɛ tɩɛr jũu.

eng: Now when I say conscience I dont mean yours but the other persons. Why should my freedom be judged by someone elses conscience?.
local: A nɩrɛ 'lɔ na 'yɛr kʊ fʊ tɩɛr lɛ n 'yɛrɛ, a ba ɩ fʊ̃ʊ tɩɛr ɛ. Bãa 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ nɩbɛ na jɛ̃ kporo ma nɩ a maa mãɛ so fʊ?.

eng: You know that when you were Gentiles you were often misled by false gods that cant even speak.
local: Yɩ bɔ̃ɔn naa a lɛ daar yɩn dɔ̃ɔ ɩ a tɩbɛ dẽme, a tɩbɛ anyãna na ba tara nyɛvʊr ɛ, tɔɔ yɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ bɛlfʊ pɔ.

eng: If I participate with gratitude, why should I be blamed for food I thank God for?.
local: Ala ɩ n puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan 'yɔ̃ɔ a bʊ̃diir ala ɩn dire, bãa 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ nɩrɛ na lɛ kporo ma a bon ɩn puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan tɩ dire?.

eng: So I want to make it clear to you that no one says, Jesus is cursed! when speaking by Gods Spirit, and no one can say, Jesus is Lord, except by the Holy Spirit.
local: A lɛ jũu, n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, nɩrɛ jaa ba be na wa 'yɛrɛ nɩ Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na wʊ 'yɛr ɩka Yesu lɛ wʊ pɔrɔ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ɛ, nɩrɛ jaa mɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ 'yɛr ɩka, Yesu lɛ ɩ a sɩ Soro ka a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ ba be a wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: So, whether you eat or drink or whatever you do, you should do it all for Gods glory.
local: A lɛ jũu ka yɩ nyuro na bɩɩ yɩ dire naa, bon jaa yɩn maala, yɩ maala a jaa kʊrɔ Nãaŋmɩn tɩɩr.

eng: There are different spiritual gifts but the same Spirit.
local: A kʊfʊsɩ tɛɛn taa, tɩchɛ a jaa yin a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ sɛ̃.

eng: Dont offend either Jews or Greeks, or Gods church.
local: Yɩ taa wa maala ka ãsʊɔ jaa wa bãr lo ɛ, ka Juu nɩrɛ lɛ bɩɩ waa ɩ Juu nɩrɛ ɛ, bɩɩ a Nãaŋmɩn puorbo ɛ.

eng: and there are different ministries and the same Lord.
local: Tʊ̃tʊ̃nsɩ mɩ tɛɛn taa, tɩchɛ a jaa tʊ̃nɔ̃ na kʊrɔ a sɩ Soro.

eng: This is the same thing that I do. I please everyone in everything I do. I dont look out for my own advantage, but I look out for many people so that they can be saved.
local: Lɛ gba lɛ n mʊɔrɔ naa maala pɛlɛ nɩrɛ jaa pʊɔ yele a jaa pɔ. Bojũu n ba bʊɔrɔ a maa mãɛ vɩla ɛ, tɩchɛ a lɛ mɩ na ɩ vɩla kʊ a nɩyɔɔ ka ban tʊ̃ɔ nyɛ faafʊ.

eng: and there are different activities but the same God who produces all of them in everyone.
local: Tʊ̃tʊ̃nsɩ tʊ̃fʊ mɩ tɛɛn taa, tɩchɛ a Nãaŋmɩn bʊ̃'yen nyã lɛ tʊ̃nɔ̃ a jaa a nɩbɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: A demonstration of the Spirit is given to each person for the common good.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã a Nãaŋmɩn den a wʊ Sɩɛ kʊ ãsʊɔ jaa a wʊn tʊ̃ɔ de sʊ̃ʊn a wʊ taaba.

eng: A word of wisdom is given by the Spirit to one person, a word of knowledge to another according to the same Spirit.
local: Ɩ̃sʊɔ a Sɩɛ bʊ̃'yen nyãna lɛ ma kʊ wʊ a yɛ̃ 'yɛr, na wʊ mɩ kʊ ãsʊɔ yɛ̃ bɔ̃ɔfʊ, a Sɩɛ bʊ̃'yen 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ kʊrɔ a jaa.

eng: faith to still another by the same Spirit, gifts of healing to another in the one Spirit.
local: A Sɩɛ bʊ̃'yen nyã lɛ ma kʊ ãsʊɔ sɔɔfʊ, na wʊ mɩ kʊ ãsʊɔ kpɛ̃ɔ, a wʊ sãana nɩ baalsɩ.

eng: performance of miracles to another, prophecy to another, the ability to tell spirits apart to another, different kinds of tongues to another, and the interpretation of the tongues to another.
local: Na wʊ kʊ ãsʊɔ kpɛ̃ɔ, a wʊ mɩ tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃, na wʊ kʊ ãsʊɔ a wʊ tʊ̃ɔ 'yɛrɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr, na wʊ mɩ kʊ ãsʊɔ jʊ̃mɔ̃ a wʊ bɔ̃ɔ a 'lɔ na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ nɩ a 'lɔ na ba ɩ ɛ, na wʊ mɩ kʊ ãsʊɔ a wʊ ma tʊ̃ɔ dãgɩl a 'yɛrsɩ, na wʊ chɛnɛ kʊ ãsʊɔ mɩ ka wʊ ma tʊ̃ɔ lɩɛb a 'yɛr dãgɩlsɩ anyãna.

eng: All these things are produced by the one and same Spirit who gives what he wants to each person.
local: A anyãna a jaa ɩ naa a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ bʊ̃'yen tʊ̃mɔ̃ lɛ, a wʊ de kʊ ãsʊɔ jaa a lɛ wʊn bʊɔrɔ.

eng: Christ is just like the human body—a body is a unit and has many parts; and all the parts of the body are one body, even though there are many.
local: A ãgãn ɩn bʊ̃'yen, bʊ̃yɔɔ na sɔɔ mar mar, a bʊ̃marsɩ anyã na sɔɔ waa, tɩ ãgãn bʊ̃'yen tɛɛ lɛ wʊ ɩ. Lɛ lɛ a mɩ kaara a Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: We were all baptized by one Spirit into one body, whether Jew or Greek, or slave or free, and we all were given one Spirit to drink.
local: Bojũu sɩ jaa son a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ, nɩ a wʊ Sɩɛ bʊ̃'yen 'yɔ̃ɔ a ãgãn bʊ̃'yen pɔ, ka Juu nɩrɛ lɛ fʊ ɩ bɩɩ, faa ɩ Juu nɩrɛ ɛ. Ka gbãgbaa lɛ fʊ ɩ bɩɩ, sumɩne lɛ fʊ ɩ, Nãaŋmɩn den a wʊ Sɩɛ bʊ̃'yen nyã kʊ sɩ jaa.

eng: Certainly the body isnt one part but many.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã a ba ɩ jibʊ̃'yen lɛ a ãgãn tara ɛ, tɩchɛ jiyɔɔ lɛ wʊ tara.

eng: If the foot says, Im not part of the body because Im not a hand, does that mean its not part of the body?.
local: Ala ɩ gbɛr wa 'yɛr naa ɩka, n ba ɩ nũu ɛ, a lɛ jũu n ba pʊɔ a ãgãn pɔ ɛ. A lɛ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ vɛ̃ a waa ɩ a ãgãn jie ãsʊɔ ɛ.

eng: If the ear says, Im not part of the body because Im not an eye, does that mean its not part of the body?.
local: Ka ala ɩ toor mɩ 'yɛr naa, n ba ɩ nɩbir ɛ, a lɛ jũu n ba pʊɔ a ãgãn pɔ ɛ. A lɛ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ vɛ̃ a waa ɩ a ãgãn jie ãsʊɔ ɛ.

eng: If I speak in tongues of human beings and of angels but I dont have love, Im a clanging gong or a clashing cymbal.
local: Ala ɩ n ma tʊ̃ɔ 'yɛr a nɩbɛ kɔkɔ bʊrsɩ a jaa, naa tʊ̃ɔ 'yɛr a malakasɩ kɔkɔɛ, tɩchɛ naa ba tara nʊ̃fʊ ɛ, ɩn kaara naa fɩka kur ban ŋmiere a wʊ pɔɔrɔ gɔ̃mɔ̃.

eng: If I have the gift of prophecy and I know all the mysteries and everything else, and if I have such complete faith that I can move mountains but I dont have love, Im nothing.
local: Ala ɩ n tara naa a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr 'yɛrfʊ kʊfʊ, naa bɔ̃ɔ a yelsɔɔlsɩ a jaa, naa bɔ̃ɔ yɛ̃ jaa bʊrɔ, ka ala ɩ n mɩ tara na sɔɔfʊ naa ma 'yɛr tɔ̃n ka a ir, tɩchɛ naa ba tara nʊ̃fʊ ɛ, n ɩn bʊ̃pɔrɔ.

eng: If the whole body were an eye, what would happen to the hearing? And if the whole body were an ear, what would happen to the sense of smell?.
local: Ala ɩ a ãgãn wʊ jaa ɩn nɩbir, ŋmɩnɛ lɛ wʊn taa ma ɩ wõ 'yɛr? Ka ala ɩ a ãgãn wʊ jaa mɩ ɩn toor, ŋmɩnɛ wʊn ɩ mɩ wa wõ nyũu.

eng: If I give away everything that I have and hand over my own body to feel good about what Ive done but I dont have love, I receive no benefit whatsoever.
local: Ala ɩ n den a n bʊ̃tarsɩ a jaa põ nɔ̃n dẽme, naa sɔɔ ɩka ba de a n ãgãn 'yɔ̃ɔ bũu 'lɩraa pɔ, tɩchɛ naa ba tara nʊ̃fʊ ɛ, n ba tara tɔ̃nɔ̃ ɛ.

eng: But as it is, God has placed each one of the parts in the body just like he wanted.
local: Tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn lɛ choo taa a jidẽdẽme anyãna ãsʊɔ jaa a ãgãn pɔ, a lɛ wʊn bʊɔrɔ ba.

eng: Love is patient, love is kind, it isnt jealous, it doesnt brag, it isnt arrogant.
local: Nʊ̃fʊ ma tara na kãnyir, nʊ̃fʊ ma maala na vɩla, waa jɩɛlɛ nɩrɛ bon ɛ, waa bʊɔrɔ yuor ɛ, waa 'mɔɔrɔ wʊ mãɛ ɛ.

eng: If all were one and the same body part, what would happen to the body?.
local: Ala ɩ a jaa ɩn bʊ̃'yen, nyɩnɛ lɛ a ãgãn mɩn taa be?.

eng: it isnt rude, it doesnt seek its own advantage, it isnt irritable, it doesnt keep a record of complaints.
local: Nʊ̃fʊ ba tara nɛtuo ɛ, waa bʊɔrɔ wʊ yõ tɛɛ bomo ɛ, wʊ maa nyɛ suur fɔɔ ɛ, waa 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ nɩrɛ pʊ̃ɔm ɛ.

eng: But as it is, there are many parts but one body.
local: Jiyɔɔ lɛ be a ãgãn pɔ, tɩchɛ a wʊ ɩ ãgãn bʊ̃'yen.

eng: it isnt happy with injustice, but it is happy with the truth.
local: Nʊ̃fʊ ba nʊ̃ yelbier jie ɛ, tɩchɛ wʊ ma nʊ̃n yelmãɛ jie.

eng: Love puts up with all things, trusts in all things, hopes for all things, endures all things.
local: Nʊ̃fʊ ma gun taa lɛ sɔ̃ɔ jaa, wʊ ma sɔɔn yele a jaa, waa baala ɛ, nʊ̃fʊ ma sɔɔn ka a wõ wʊ.

eng: Love never fails. As for prophecies, they will be brought to an end. As for tongues, they will stop. As for knowledge, it will be brought to an end.
local: Nʊ̃fʊ maa ɩ faa ɛ, Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr 'yɛrfʊ na wa ŋmaa naa, ka a kɔkɔ dãgɩlsɩ 'yɛrfʊ mɩ wa tɛɛ, ka a bɔ̃ɔfʊ mɩ wa po chen.

eng: We know in part and we prophesy in part.
local: Bojũu a bɔ̃ɔfʊ kʊfʊ ɩn ŋmaa, ka a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr 'yɛrfʊ mɩ ɩ ŋmaa.

eng: but when the perfect comes, what is partial will be brought to an end.
local: tɩchɛ a bʊ̃mãɛ wa wa, a bʊ̃gãagiel na bɔr naa.

eng: When I was a child, I used to speak like a child, reason like a child, think like a child. But now that I have become a man, Ive put an end to childish things.
local: A lɛ daar ɩn ɩ a bibile, bibi'yɛr lɛ n ma 'yɛrɛ, naa tara a bibiyɛ̃ naa ma tɩɛrɛ a bibitɩɛr. Tɩchɛ ɩn wa ɩ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ baar, n lɔb a bibiyele a jaa bar.

eng: Now we see a reflection in a mirror; then we will see face-to-face. Now I know partially, but then I will know completely in the same way that I have been completely known.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã 'lɔ, chãana pɔ lɛ sɩ kaara jaa tũfuru, tɩchɛ sɩn wa tuor taa nɩ gbie. Pʊ̃pãanyã 'lɔ ŋmaa lɛ n bɔ̃ɔ, tɩchɛ ɩn pãa wa bɔ̃ɔ wʊn baar nɩtãa lɛ wʊ mɩn bɔ̃ɔ ma.

eng: Now faith, hope, and love remain—these three things—and the greatest of these is love.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã a yelsɩ ata anyãna lɛ ba tɛɛrɛ ɛ, sɔɔfʊ, tɩɛrtɔfʊ jie nɩ nʊ̃fʊ. Tɩchɛ a anyãna a jaa pɔ, nʊ̃fʊ lɛ gar a jaa.

eng: So the eye cant say to the hand, I dont need you, or in turn, the head cant say to the feet, I dont need you.
local: Ala lɛ so ka a nɩbir kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ 'yɛr kʊ a nũu, n ba bʊɔrɔ fʊ ɛ. A ju mɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ 'yɛr kʊ gbɛr, n ba bʊɔrɔ fʊ ɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want to call your attention to the good news that I preached to you, which you also received and in which you stand.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã, n yɛɛr, n bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka n lɛ jɛɔ yɩ a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ ɩn dɔ̃ɔ mʊɔl kʊ yɩ a yɩ sɔɔ de na yɩ be ara a pɔ.

eng: Instead, the parts of the body that people think are the weakest are the most necessary.
local: Yɩ kaa, a ãgãn jiir ala na ba kpɛ̃mɛ ɛ, ala lɛ sɩ ma mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir kaara.

eng: You are being saved through it if you hold on to the message I preached to you, unless somehow you believed it for nothing.
local: A 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyãna ɩn mʊɔl faa yɩ naa ala ɩ yɩ chɛnɛ na ara a pɔ, ka lɛ ba lɛ ɛ, a yɩ sɔɔfʊ ɩn pɔrɔ.

eng: The parts of the body that we think are less honorable are the ones we honor the most. The private parts of our body that arent presentable are the ones that are given the most dignity.
local: A jiir ala sɩn ma tɩɛr ɩka a ba tara 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ ɛ, ala lɛ sɩ ma bɔ̃ɔ kaara. Ka a jiir ala mɩ na ba be yõo yi fʊ ɛ, ala lɛ sɩ ma bɔ̃ɔ chɔɔr.

eng: I passed on to you as most important what I also received: Christ died for our sins in line with the scriptures.
local: Bojũu a 'yɛr ɩn dɔ̃ɔ nyɛ lɛ n mɩ de nie man kʊ yɩ ɩka, a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto kpin a sɩ yelbier jũu, nɩtãa lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn gãn na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr.

eng: he was buried, and he rose on the third day in line with the scriptures.
local: ka ba ũu wʊ, a wʊ lɛ ir a kũu pɔ, a ber ata daar nɩtãa lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn gãn na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr.

eng: He appeared to Cephas, then to the Twelve.
local: Wʊ vɛ̃ naa ka Pita nyɛ wʊ, na wʊ lɛ vɛ̃ ka a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ pie nɩ ayi mɩ nyɛ wʊ.

eng: and then he appeared to more than five hundred brothers and sisters at once—most of them are still alive to this day, though some have died.
local: A anyã puor, wʊ vɛ̃n ka a yɛɛr na gar kɔɔr anũu mɩ 'mataa nyɛ wʊ, bamɩne na sɔɔ kpi, tɩchɛ a ba yɔɔ jie lɛ chɛnɛ.

eng: The parts of our body that are presentable dont need this. But God has put the body together, giving greater honor to the part with less honor.
local: Ka a ala mɩ na be a yõo yifʊ, sɩ maa chɔɔr a ala ɛ. Tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn lɛ de a jiir anyãna 'mataa a ãgãn pɔ, na wʊ de 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ kʊ a jiir amɩne na ba dɔ̃ɔ tara ɛ.

eng: so that there wont be division in the body and so the parts might have mutual concern for each other.
local: Ka a lɛ na vɛ̃ a dẽdẽme ɩfʊ kʊ̃ lɛ be a ãgãn pɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ ka a jaa ma sʊ̃nɔ̃ taa.

eng: Then he appeared to James, then to all the apostles.
local: Wʊ vɛ̃n ka a Jemesɩ nyɛ wʊ, na wʊ lɛ vɛ̃ ka a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ ba jaa nyɛ wʊ.

eng: If one part suffers, all the parts suffer with it; if one part gets the glory, all the parts celebrate with it.
local: Ala ɩ a ãgãn jie ãsʊɔ wa bɩɛrɛ naa, a ãgãn jiir a jaa mɩ ma bɩɛrɛ naa. Ala ɩ a ãgãn jie ãsʊɔ jaa mɩ wa nyɛn 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ, a ãgãn wʊ jaa mɩ ma chɩlɛ naa.

eng: and last of all he appeared to me, as if I were born at the wrong time.
local: ka a baaraa jaa ka maa mɩ na kaara fɩka nɩrɛ ban dɔɔ kpalka ka wʊ ir a wʊ mãɛ wiil ma.

eng: You are the body of Christ and parts of each other.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã yɩn lɛ lɔ̃ɔ taa ɩ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto ãgãn, ka yɩ ãsʊɔ jaa ɩ a jie ãsʊɔ.

eng: In the church, God has appointed first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then miracles, then gifts of healing, the ability to help others, leadership skills, different kinds of tongues.
local: A pupuorbiir banyãna pɔ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn de nie ir a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ, na wʊ lɛ tu ir a wʊ 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ, na wʊ lɛ tu ir a wʊ wiwiilbe, na wʊ lɛ ir a bala mɩn na tʊ̃nɔ̃ a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃, na wʊ ir a bala mɩn na sãana baalsɩ, na wʊ ir bamɩne ka ba sʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩbɛ, na wʊ lɛ ir bamɩne na kaara a tʊ̃mɔ̃, nɩ a bala mɩ na bɔ̃ɔ a kɔkɔɛ dãgɩlfʊ.

eng: All arent apostles, are they? All arent prophets, are they? All arent teachers, are they? All dont perform miracles, do they?.
local: A mɔ̃ ba jaa lɛ ɩ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ bɩɩ? Bɩɩ ba jaa lɛ ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ ɔ? Ba jaa lɛ ɩ a wiwiilbe ɔ? Ba jaa lɛ ma tʊ̃ɔ tʊ̃ a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ bɩɩ?.

eng: All dont have gifts of healing, do they? All dont speak in different tongues, do they? All dont interpret, do they?.
local: Ba jaa lɛ wʊ kʊ a baal sãafʊ bɩɩ? Ba jaa lɛ ma tʊ̃ɔ dãgɩl a ba kɔkɔɛ ɔ? Bɩɩ ba jaa lɛ ma tʊ̃ɔ a kɔkɔr dãgɩlaa lɩɛb ɔ?.

eng: Use your ambition to try to get the greater gifts. And Im going to show you an even better way.
local: Tɩchɛ yɩ mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir bɔ a kʊfʊsɩ 'lɔ na gar a jaa. Pʊ̃pãanyã, ɩn wiil yɩn a sɔr 'lɔ na gar a jaa.

eng: Im the least important of the apostles. I dont deserve to be called an apostle, because I harassed Gods church.
local: Bojũu maa lɛ ɩ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ ba jaa pɔ bʊ̃bile naa ba sɛɛn ɩka ba gba bʊɔl ma a nɩtʊ̃nãa ɛ, bojũu n gen a Nãaŋmɩn puorbo.

eng: I am what I am by Gods grace, and Gods grace hasnt been for nothing. In fact, I have worked harder than all the others—that is, it wasnt me but the grace of God that is with me.
local: Tɩchɛ a Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ wʊn wãɛ ma lɛ so ka n ɩ a nɩtʊ̃nãa a dɩna. Ka a Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ ba ɩ pɔrɔ ɛ. Yelmãɛ jaa n tʊ̃n gar a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ ba jaa, tɩ a ba ɩ maa mãɛ lɛ tʊ̃ ɛ, tɩchɛ a Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ 'lɔ wʊn de wãɛn ma lɛ.

eng: So then, whether you heard the message from me or them, this is what we preach and this is what you have believed.
local: Ka maa lɛ bɩɩ bala lɛ, a 'yɛr lɛ anyã sɩn mʊɔl, ka anyãna lɛ yɩ mɩ sɔɔ de.

eng: Pursue love, and use your ambition to try to get spiritual gifts but especially so that you might prophesy.
local: Yɩ turo a nʊ̃fʊ sɔr, na yɩ mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir bʊɔrɔ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ kʊfʊsɩ, na yɩ kaa ir a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr 'yɛrfʊ kʊfʊ, 'lɔ lɛ ɩ.

eng: Concerning the collection of money for Gods people: You should do what I have directed the churches in Galatia to do.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã n bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka n 'yɛr a chɩɛrfʊ yɩn bʊɔrɔ ɩka yɩ chɩɛr sʊ̃ʊn a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ. Yɩ mɩ maal a lɛ ɩn man kʊ a Galatiya paal pupuorbiir gbulsɩ ɩka ba maal.

eng: So if the message that is preached says that Christ has been raised from the dead, then how can some of you say, Theres no resurrection of the dead?.
local: Tɩchɛ ala ɩ yɩ wõn a mʊɔl ɩka a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto ir naa a kũu pɔ, ŋmɩnɛ lɛ a yɩ bamɩne 'yɛrɛ ɩka kũu pɔ irfʊ ba be be ɛ?.

eng: This is because those who speak in a tongue dont speak to people but to God; no one understands it—they speak mysteries by the Spirit.
local: Bojũu nɩrɛ 'lɔ na bɔ̃ɔ a kɔkɔr dãgɩlaa 'yɛrfʊ nɩrɛ ba lɛ wʊ 'yɛrɛ nɩ ɛ, tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn lɛ wʊ ma 'yɛrɛ nɩ. Yelmãɛ nɩrɛ jaa maa bɔ̃ɔ a lɛ wʊn 'yɛrɛ ɛ, Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ lɛ wʊ ma de 'yɛrɛ nɩ a yelsɔɔlsɩ.

eng: On the first day of the week, each of you should set aside whatever you can afford from what you earn so that the collection wont be delayed until I come.
local: A Pɛ̃nfʊ divɩɛ bio daar jaa, a bɩbir 'lɔ na de nie ãsʊɔ jaa wʊ ma ir a libie jã a lɛ wʊn ma nyɛ na yɩ bine, ka maa wa tɩ a ta lɛ ɩ chɩɛrfʊ go ɛ.

eng: If theres no resurrection of the dead, then Christ hasnt been raised either.
local: Ala ɩ a kũu pɔ irfʊ ba be be ɛ, ka a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto mɩ gba ba ir a kũu pɔ ɛ.

eng: Then when I get there, Ill send whomever you approve to Jerusalem with letters of recommendation to bring your gift.
local: Maa wa tɔ̃ɔ ta n de a gãma kʊ a nɩbɛ bala yɩn mãɛ na ir naa tʊ̃n nɩ a libie ala yɩn chɩɛr ka ba chiin a Jerusalɛm.

eng: Those who prophesy speak to people, building them up, and giving them encouragement and comfort.
local: Tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na 'yɛrɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr, nɩbɛ lɛ wʊ ma 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ ka ban kpɛ̃mɛ, na wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ ba ãkpãɛn na wʊ 'maala ba.

eng: If Christ hasnt been raised, then our preaching is useless and your faith is useless.
local: Ka ala ɩ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto ba ir a kũu pɔ ɛ, a sɩ mʊɔl ɩn pɔrɔ, ka a yɩ sɔɔfʊ mɩ ɩ pɔrɔ.

eng: If it seems right for me to go too, theyll travel with me.
local: Ka ala wa ɩ fɩka a maa mãɛ sɛɛn mɩ na chen go, ba kʊ̃ bɩɛl ma.

eng: People who speak in a tongue build up themselves; those who prophesy build up the church.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na ma 'yɛrɛ a kɔkɔr dãgɩlaa sʊ̃nɔ̃ naa a wʊ mãɛ tɛɛ, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ na 'yɛrɛ Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr mɩ sʊ̃nɔ̃ naa a Nãaŋmɩn puorbo a ba baa.

eng: We are found to be false witnesses about God, because we testified against God that he raised Christ, when he didnt raise him if its the case that the dead arent raised.
local: A sɩ pãa ŋmara jirsɩ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ Nãaŋmɩn, bojũu sɩ din dãasɩɛ ɩka Nãaŋmɩn sãɩn a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto a kũu pɔ. Tɩchɛ waa sãɩ wʊ ɛ, ala ɩ yelmãɛ jaa kũusɩ ba ire ɛ.

eng: Ill come to you after I go through Macedonia, and because Im going through Macedonia.
local: Maa wa chen tɩ yi a Masedonɩya baar, ɩn wan a yɩ sɛ̃, bojũu be lɛ ɩn tu.

eng: I wish that all of you spoke in tongues, but Id rather you could prophesy. Those who prophesy are more important than those who speak in tongues, unless they are able to interpret them so that the church might be built up.
local: Maa bɔfʊ lɛ ɩka a yɩ jaa yɩ ma tʊ̃ɔ 'yɛrɛ a kɔkɔr dãgɩlaa nyã, tɩchɛ lɛ ɩn bʊɔrɔ yɔɔ lɛ ɩka yɩ jaa yɩ 'yɛrɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr. 'Lɔ na 'yɛrɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr, gar a 'lɔ na 'yɛrɛ a kɔkɔr dãgɩlaa, ala ɩ nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ ba be be na tʊ̃ɔ lɩɛb na wʊ wiil a par kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn puorbo ka a sʊ̃ʊ ba ɛ.

eng: I may stay with you or even spend the winter there in Corinth so that you can send me on my way to wherever Im off to next.
local: Amɩne sɛ̃ ɩn jɛ̃n a yɩ sɛ̃ kor bãlãa, bɩɩ ka n jãnɛ a be ka a uon wa baar, ka yɩ sʊ̃ʊ ma ka n chiine a n chenfʊ jie.

eng: I dont want to make a quick visit to you, since I hope to spend some time with you if the Lord lets it happen.
local: N ba bʊɔrɔ ɩka n tɔ̃ɔ tɩ nyɛ yɩ a lɛ nɛ a pʊ̃pãanyã tɩ po ɛ, n tɩɛr naa ɩka n jɛ̃ a yɩ sɛ̃ kor ala ɩ a sɩ Soro sɔɔ naa.

eng: After all, brothers and sisters, if I come to you speaking in tongues, how will I help you unless I speak to you with a revelation, some knowledge, a prophecy, or a teaching?.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã, n yɛɛr, ala ɩ n ben wa a yɩ sɛ̃, wa dãgɩl a kɔkɔr baa tɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ yɩn nyɛ? Ka yelwiilaa kɔ̃ɔ ba lɛ n waan ɛ, bɩɩ bɔ̃ɔfʊ kɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr, bɩɩ Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr, bɩɩ kpãafʊ 'yɛr kɔ̃ɔ lɛ ɛ?.

eng: If the dead arent raised, then Christ hasnt been raised either.
local: Bojũu ala ɩ a kũu pɔ irfʊ ba be be ɛ, ka lɛ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto mɩ gba ba ir a kũu pɔ ɛ.

eng: Ill stay here in Ephesus until the Festival of Pentecost.
local: Tɩchɛ ɩn jɛ̃n a Efesus tẽe pɔ tɩ tãn a Pentikosɩ bɩbir.

eng: Likewise, things that arent alive like a harp or a lyre can make a sound, but if there arent different notes in the sounds they make, how will the tune from the harp or the lyre be recognized?.
local: De man a bʊ̃ŋmiersɩ kõn, ka wele lɛ bɩɩ kõrõjom lɛ, ŋmɩnɛ lɛ nɩrɛ na ɩrɛ wa bɔ̃ɔ a sʊkpa nyɩnɛ wʊn ŋmiere bɩɩ pɛɛlɛ ala ɩ a ba tara nɛkpɛ̃ɛ nɩ kɔkɔbile ɛ?.

eng: If Christ hasnt been raised, then your faith is worthless; you are still in your sins.
local: Ka ala ɩ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto ba ir a kũu pɔ ɛ, a yɩ sɔɔfʊ ɩn pɔrɔ, na yɩ chɛnɛ be a yɩ yelbebe pɔ.

eng: In spite of the fact that there are many opponents, a big and productive opportunity has opened up for my mission here.
local: Bojũu sɔr yuon kʊ ma ka ɩn tʊ̃ɔ tʊ̃ tʊ̃mɔ̃, tɩchɛ ka a nɩyɔɔ mɩ kpɛrɛ ma.

eng: and whats more, those who have died in Christ are gone forever.
local: Ka a bala mɩ na pãa sɔɔ de a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto tɩ kpi, mɩ wɛ.

eng: If we have a hope in Christ only in this life, then we deserve to be pitied more than anyone else.
local: Ala ɩ a dɩna tɛɛ tɩɛr lɛ sɩ tara a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ, nɩbaal nɩbɛ lɛ sɩ ɩ, na sɩ gar a nɩbɛ ba jaa.

eng: But in fact Christ has been raised from the dead. Hes the first crop of the harvest of those who have died.
local: Tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn sãɩn a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto a kũu pɔ yelmãɛ jaa, ɩ a bir dɔ̃ɔ, ka a wiil ɩka a bala na kpi mɩ na ir a kũu pɔ.

eng: Since death came through a human being, the resurrection of the dead came through one too.
local: Bojũu kũu tun nɩbʊ̃'yen 'yɔ̃ɔ wa, ka a kũu pɔ irfʊ mɩ tu a nɩbʊ̃'yen 'yɔ̃ɔ mɩ wa.

eng: In the same way that everyone dies in Adam, so also everyone will be given life in Christ.
local: Adama jũu, nɩrɛ jaa na kpi naa, lɛ mɩ lɛ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto jũu, nɩrɛ jaa na ir tara nyɛvʊr.

eng: Each event will happen in the right order: Christ, the first crop of the harvest, then those who belong to Christ at his coming.
local: Tɩchɛ ãsʊɔ jaa nɩ wʊ irfʊ daar lɛ, Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto lɛ ɩ a bir dɔ̃ɔ, 'lɔ pãa wa lɩɛb waara, ka bala jaa na sɔɔ de wʊ mɩ ir.

eng: and then the end, when Christ hands over the kingdom to God the Father, when he brings every form of rule, every authority and power to an end.
local: Ka a baaraa pãa wa, a wʊ de a yele a jaa 'yɔ̃ɔ a Sãa Nãaŋmɩn nũu pɔ, an ɩ wʊ tʊ̃ɔn a tʊ̃ɔfʊ dẽme, nɩ a kpãɛn dẽme ba jaa.

eng: And if a trumpet call is unrecognizable, then who will prepare for battle?.
local: Ala ɩ baa wa pɛɛl wɛraa a jɛɛr 'yɔ̃ɔ ɛ, ŋmɩnɛ lɛ nɩrɛ na ɩrɛ wa bɔ̃ɔ ɩka jɛɛr lo na ba chɔɔr chen?.

eng: If Timothy comes to you, be sure that he has no reason to be afraid while hes with you, because he does the work of the Lord just like I do.
local: Ala ɩ a Timoti wa tɩ naa, yɩ kaa ka yele jaa taa kʊrɔ wʊ dãbãɛ ɛ, bojũu a sɩ Soro tʊ̃mɔ̃ lɛ wʊ tʊ̃nɔ̃ a n kaar.

eng: So dont let anyone disrespect him, but send him on in peace so he can join me. Im waiting for him along with the brothers and sisters.
local: Yɩ taa vɛ̃ ka a ãsʊɔ jaa wa kaa wʊ pɔrɔ ɛ. Yɩ bar wʊ sɔr nɩ nyã'maar ka wʊ lɛ lɩɛb wa a n sɛ̃. Maa nɩ a yɛɛr jaa chɛlɛ wʊ naa.

eng: Its the same way with you: If you dont use language that is easy to understand when you speak in a tongue, then how will anyone understand what is said?.
local: Lɛ lɛ yɩn mɩ, a yɩ jãlbir ba wa tʊ̃ɔ 'yɛrɛ a 'yɛrbie ala na tor ɛ, an lɛ na tʊ̃ɔ bɔ̃ɔ a lɛ yɩn 'yɛrɛ par? 'Yɛr vor lɛ yɩn 'yɛrɛ.

eng: It is necessary for him to rule until he puts all enemies under his feet .
local: Bojũu a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto lɛ a sɛɛ ɩka wʊ di a nãa lɛ̃ɛɛ ka a Nãaŋmɩn wa de a wʊ dɔ̃dẽme ba jaa bin a wʊ gbɛɛ pĩlem.

eng: It will be as if you are speaking into the air! There are probably many language families in the world, and none of them are without meaning.
local: Baal jaa ba be be ɛ, kɔkɔ bʊrsɩ lɛ ba 'yɛrɛ ka a wɛr pɔ, tɩchɛ ãsʊɔ jaa ba be be na ba tara par ɛ.

eng: So if I dont know the meaning of the language, then I will be like a foreigner to those who speak it, and they will be like foreigners to me.
local: Ala ɩ nɩrɛ wa 'yɛrɛ nɩ ma naa a wʊ 'yɛr ka n ba wõ naa ɛ, n ɩn sãan a wʊ sɛ̃ ka wʊ mɩ ɩ sãan a n sɛ̃.

eng: The same holds true for you: since you are ambitious for spiritual gifts, use your ambition to try to work toward being the best at building up the church.
local: Lɛ lɛ a yɩn mɩ, yɩ ben mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir bʊɔrɔ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ kʊfʊsɩ, yɩ mɔ̃ na yɩ nyɛ a kʊfʊsɩ ala na sʊ̃ʊ a Nãaŋmɩn puorbo ka ba baa.

eng: Therefore, those who speak in a tongue should pray to be able to interpret.
local: A yele nyãna jũu, nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ma tʊ̃ɔ 'yɛr a kɔkɔr dãgɩlaa nyã wʊ sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn a wʊ sʊ̃ʊ wʊ, a wʊ tʊ̃ɔ lɩɛb wiil a lɛ wʊn ma 'yɛr.

eng: If I pray in a tongue, my spirit prays but my mind isnt productive.
local: Ala ɩ n dãgɩlaa a kɔkɔr sʊɔrɔ nɩ Nãaŋmɩn, a n sɩɛ mɩ sʊɔrɔ naa tɩchɛ a n tɩɛr a wʊ ɩ pɔrɔ.

eng: What should I do? Ill pray in the Spirit, but Ill pray with my mind too; Ill sing a psalm in the Spirit, but Ill sing the psalm with my mind too.
local: N pãa ɩ ŋmɩnɛ? Maa nɩ a n sɩɛ na lɔ̃ɔn sʊɔr a Nãaŋmɩn naa lɛ 'matãan a n tɩɛr mɩ sʊɔr. Maa nɩ a n sɩɛ na 'mataa yiel a Nãaŋmɩn yiel naa mɩ lɛ 'matãan a n tɩɛr yiel.

eng: After all, if you praise God in the Spirit, how will the people who arent trained in that language say Amen! to your thanksgiving, when they dont know what you are saying?.
local: Ala ɩ Nãaŋmɩn lɛ yɩ dãna nɩ a yɩ sɩɛ, ka a jɛ̃gumo mɩ wa lɔ̃ɔn yɩ na waa wõnõ a lɛ yɩn 'yɛrɛ ɛ, ŋmɩnɛ wʊn ɩrɛ wa 'yɛr ɩka, a ɩ a lɛ, yɩn dãna Nãaŋmɩn, wʊn ba bɔ̃ɔ a par a lɛ yɩn 'yɛrɛ ɛ?.

eng: You may offer a beautiful prayer of thanksgiving, but the other person is not being built up.
local: A yɩ Nãaŋmɩn dãnfʊ na vɩɛlɛ na jaa kper, tɩchɛ a ba sʊ̃ʊ a yɩ tɔ sʊɔ 'lɔ ɛ.

eng: I thank God that I speak in tongues more than all of you.
local: N puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan an ɩ n ma tʊ̃ɔn 'yɛr a kɔkɔr dãgɩlaa nyã gar yɩ jaa.

eng: But in the church Id rather speak five words in my right mind than speak thousands of words in a tongue so that I can teach others.
local: Tɩchɛ maa wa kpɛ a Nãaŋmɩn puorbo pɔ, n ma 'yɛr 'yɛrbie anũu ban na tʊ̃ɔ bɔ̃ɔ a par gar ɩn na 'yɛr 'yɛrbie turo pie ban kʊ̃ bɔ̃ɔ a par ɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, dont be like children in the way you think. Well, be babies when it comes to evil, but be adults in your thinking.
local: N yɛɛr, yɩ tɛɛ a bibitɩɛr bar. Ala ɩ yelbier maalfʊ lɛ, yɩ ɩ bipɩlɛ, tɩchɛ ka a yɩ yɛ̃ pɔ 'lɔ nʊ̃ɔ bɩ nɩbɛrɛ.

eng: In the Law it is written: I will speak to this people with foreign languages and foreigners lips, but they will not even listen to me this way , says the Lord.
local: A sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, Ɩn den kɔkɔr sãan naa tu a sãanba nɛɛ pɔ 'yɛr 'yɛr kʊ a nɩbɛ banyãna, tɩchɛ ba gba kʊ̃ chɛlɛ a lɛ ɩn 'yɛrɛ ɛ. Lɛ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr.

eng: Death is the last enemy to be brought to an end.
local: A dɔ̃ baaraa wʊn na sɔ̃ɔ lɛ ɩ a kũu.

eng: Concerning Apollos our brother: I strongly encouraged him to visit you with the brothers and sisters, but he didnt want to go now. Hell come when he has an opportunity.
local: A sɩ yɛbɛ Apolosɩ, n sʊɔr wʊn gbɛɛ a ŋmɩnɛ jaa ɩka wʊ tɩ a yɩ sɛ̃ 'matãan a yɛɛr bamɩne. Tɩchɛ waa sɔɔ a be tɩfʊ a pʊ̃pãanyã sɛrɛ ɛ, 'lɔ wa nyɛ sɔr wʊn tɩ naa.

eng: So then, tongues are a sign for those who dont believe, not for those who believe. But prophecy is a sign for believers, not for those who dont believe.
local: Ala lɛ so, a kɔkɔr dãgɩlaa 'yɛrfʊ ba ɩ jãnɛ kʊ a Yesu turbo ɛ, tɩchɛ a ɩn jãnɛ kʊ a bala na ba ɩ a Yesu turbo. A Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr 'yɛrfʊ, a bala na ɩ a Yesu turbo mɩ lɛ so, a ba ɩ a bala na ba ɩ a Yesu turbo ɛ.

eng: Stay awake, stand firm in your faith, be brave, be strong.
local: Yɩ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ jʊ̃mɔ̃, na yɩ ara chãchã a yɩ sɔɔfʊ pɔ, na yɩ ɩ nɩbɛ na tara nyãa na yɩ kpɛ̃mɛ.

eng: Everything should be done in love.
local: Na yɩ ma maala yele jaa nɩ nʊ̃fʊ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I encourage you to do something else. You know that the people in Stephanas household were the first crop of the harvest to come from the mission to Achaia. They have dedicated themselves to the service of Gods people.
local: Yɩ bɔ̃ɔn naa a Sɩtɛfanas nɩ a wʊ yir dẽme, bala lɛ dɔ̃ɔn a puorfʊ be a Akaya paal pɔ, na ba mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ. N sʊɔrɔ yɩ naa yɛɛr ɩka.

eng: So accept the authority of people like them and of anyone who cooperates and works hard.
local: yɩ siir yɩ mãɛ kʊ a nɩbɛ banyãna taaba nɩ nɩrɛ jaa na pʊɔ ba bala, a ba kaar.

eng: Im so happy that Stephanas, Fortunatus, and Achaicus have arrived; theyve made up for my missing you.
local: Nʊ̃ɔ kpɛ ma naa ɩn wa nyɛ a Sɩtɛfanas nɩ Fortunatus nɩ a Akaikus na wa ta a n sɛ̃, bojũu bala lɛ sʊ̃ʊ ma yɩn ba be ka ɛ.

eng: Indeed theyve provided my spirit and yours with a much-needed rest. Therefore, give them proper recognition.
local: Bojũu bala lɛ 'maa ma ãgãn nɩtãa lɛ ban mɩn 'maa a yɩ ãgãn, a nɩbɛ banyãna sɛɛn pɛɛfʊ.

eng: since he has brought everything under control under his feet. When it says that everything has been brought under his control, this clearly means everything except for the one who placed everything under his control.
local: Bojũu a sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, Nãaŋmɩn den a yele a jaa bin a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto gbɛɛ pĩlem. Wʊn ben 'yɛr ɩka, a yele a jaa, a Nãaŋmɩn na de a yele a jaa bin a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto gbɛɛ pĩlem ba pʊɔ ɛ.

eng: But when all things have been brought under his control, then the Son himself will also be under the control of the one who gave him control over everything so that God may be all in all.
local: Nãaŋmɩn wa ɩ a nɩtɔ̃ baar, a 'lɔ na de a yele a jaa bin a wʊ gbɛɛ pĩlem, a Bie mãɛ mɩ na siir wʊ mãɛ kʊ wʊ, ka Nãaŋmɩn pãa so a jaa.

eng: Otherwise, what are those who are getting baptized for the dead doing? If the dead arent raised, then why are they being baptized for them?.
local: Nɩyɔɔ ba sɔɔ ɩka a bala na kpi na lɛ ir tara nyɛvʊr go ɛ, tɩchɛ bamɩne sɔɔ naa na ba ma so Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ kʊ ba.

eng: And what about us? Why are we in danger all day every day?.
local: Ka a sɩn mɩ, bãa 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ a dɔɔyɛ ma pɔɔ sɩ sɔ̃ɔ jaa?.

eng: So suppose that the whole church is meeting and everyone is speaking in tongues. If people come in who are outsiders or unbelievers, wont they say that you are out of your minds?.
local: Ala ɩ a pupuorbiir wa lɔ̃ɔn taa ka ba jaa dãgɩl a kɔkɔɛ, na ba 'yɛrɛ, ka a jɛ̃gumo ãsʊɔ be a yɩ pɔ bɩɩ nɩbɛ na ba ɩ a Yesu turbo ɛ, wa kpɛ nyɛ a lɛ yɩn 'yɛrɛ, ba kʊ̃ 'yɛr ɩka yɩ gele naa ɛ?.

eng: But if everyone is prophesying when an unbeliever or outsider comes in, they are tested by all and called to account by all.
local: Tɩchɛ ala ɩ nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ na ba ɩ a Yesu turo ɛ, bɩɩ nɩrɛ kɔ̃ɔ mɩ na ba bɔ̃ɔ a par ɛ, 'lɔ wa kpɛ nyɛ ka a ɩ yɩ jaa 'yɛrɛ naa a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr a wʊ wõ naa bɔ̃ɔn wʊn ɩ yelbe-ɩrɛ, a ala wʊn wõ na tɔɔr a wʊ 'yɛr ka wʊ lɩɛb a wʊ tɩɛr.

eng: The secrets of their hearts are brought to light. When that happens, they will fall on their faces and worship God, proclaiming out loud that truly God is among you!.
local: Wʊn chiir a wʊ pʊɔ a ba niem tɩ lo kpa tẽe puor Nãaŋmɩn, tɩ 'yɛr ɩka yelmãɛ jaa Nãaŋmɩn ben a yɩ sɛ̃.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I swear by the pride I have in you in Christ Jesus our Lord, Im facing death every day.
local: Yɛɛr, bɩbir jaa lɛ n ma tuoro kũu, jɩɩrfʊ kpɛ ma naa a yɩ jũu. A nyɛvʊr sɩn tara a sɩ Soro Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ vɛ̃ ka n 'yɛrɛ a nɩtɔ̃nɔ̃.

eng: What is the outcome of this, brothers and sisters? When you meet together, each one has a psalm, a teaching, a revelation, a tongue, or an interpretation. All these things must be done to build up the church.
local: N pãa lɛ 'yɛr bo, n yɛɛr? Yɩn ma wa lɔ̃ɔ taa, ãsʊɔ jaa ma tara naa yiel na yiel, ka a ãsʊɔ mɩ tara kpãafʊ 'yɛrbir na 'yɛr, ka a ãsʊɔ mɩ tara yelwiilaa na yi Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ na wʊn 'yɛr, ka a ãsʊɔ tara a kɔkɔr dãgɩlaa na 'yɛr ka ãsʊɔ mɩ tʊ̃ɔ lɩɛb wiil a par. Yɩ maala a anyãna a jaa ka a sʊ̃ʊ yɩ a yɩ kpɛ̃mɛ a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ pɔ.

eng: The churches in the province of Asia greet you. Aquila and Prisca greet you warmly in the Lord, together with the church that meets in their house.
local: A pupuorbiir gbulsɩ ba jaa na be a Asɩya paal pɔ puor yɩ naa. Akuila nɩ a Pɩrɩsɩla mɩ den a sɩ Soro yuor puor yɩ, nɩ a pupuorbiir bala na ma lɔ̃ɔ a ba yir, mɩ puoro yɩ naa.

eng: All the brothers and sisters greet you. You in turn should greet each other with a holy kiss.
local: A yɛɛr ba jaa na be a ka mɩ puor yɩ naa. Yɩ mɩ puor puor taa nɩ pʊpɛl.

eng: Here is my greeting in my own handwriting—Paul.
local: Maa Pɔɔl mãɛ nũu lɛ n sɛb a puorfʊ nyãna.

eng: A curse on anyone who doesnt love the Lord. Come, Lord!.
local: Ala ɩ ãsʊɔ jaa baa nʊ̃ a sɩ Soro ɛ, pɔ'yɔ̃ɔfʊ ben a wʊ sɛ̃. Pa sɩ Soro, wa.

eng: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with you.
local: A sɩ Soro Yesu wãɛfʊ a be a yɩ sɛ̃.

eng: My love is with all of you in Christ Jesus.
local: A n nʊ̃fʊ wʊ be a yɩn bala jaa na be a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ. A ɩ a lɛ.

eng: From a human point of view, what good does it do me if I fought wild animals in Ephesus? If the dead arent raised, lets eat and drink because tomorrow well die .
local: Ala ɩ n jɛb naa a mɔdʊ̃ bier a ka Efesus nɩsaal jũu, bo tɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ n nyɛ? Ala ɩ a kũu kʊ̃ lɛ ir ɛ, nɩtãa lɛ a nɩbɛ na ma 'yɛrɛ. Yɩ vɛ̃ ka sɩ di ti nyũ, bojũu bio sɩn kpi naa.

eng: If some speak in a tongue, then let two or at most three speak, one at a time, and someone must interpret.
local: Ala ɩ nɩrɛ na wa 'yɛrɛ naa a kɔkɔr dãgɩlaa, ba ɩ ayi bɩɩ ata, ba ta gar ata ɛ, na ba 'yɛrɛ tɩ chɛrɛ kʊrɔ taa. Ka nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ mɩ be be ma tʊ̃ɔ lɩɛb wiil a par.

eng: However, if there is no interpreter, then they should keep quiet in the meeting. They should speak privately to themselves and to God.
local: Ala ɩ nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ ba be be na tʊ̃ɔ lɩɛb na wʊ wiil a par ɛ, a nɩnyɩnɛ wʊ ɩ jaa gbul a puorfʊ jie, tɩ bʊlɔ kʊrɔ wʊ mãɛ nɩ Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: In the case of prophets, let two or three speak and have the rest evaluate what is said.
local: Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ ba ɩ ayi bɩɩ ata na ba 'yɛrɛ, tɩchɛ ka a bala na chɛ jʊrɔ kaara a lɛ ban 'yɛrɛ.

eng: Dont be deceived, bad company corrupts good character.
local: Yɩ ta vɛ̃ ka nɩbɛ bɛl yɩ ɛ. Fʊ̃ʊ lɔ̃ɔn nɩgãlsɩ ba ma sɔ̃ɔn a fʊ tɩɛr vɩla bar.

eng: And if some revelation comes to someone else who is sitting down, the first one should be quiet.
local: Ka ala wa ɩ Nãaŋmɩn ir yele wiil a bala na jãnɛ, a 'lɔ na de nie 'yɛrɛ wʊ mɩ pɔɔ a wʊ nɛɛ.

eng: Sober up by acting like you should and dont sin. Some of you are ignorant about God—I say this because you should be ashamed of yourselves!.
local: Yɩ lɩɛb de a yɩ yɛ̃, na yɩ tɛɛ bier ɩfʊ bar, bojũu a yɩ bamɩne ba bɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn ɛ, ka n 'yɛr a nɩtɔ̃ ka vã kpɛ ba.

eng: You can all prophesy one at a time so that everyone can learn and be encouraged.
local: Yɩn 'yɛrɛ tɩ chɛrɛ kʊrɔ taa an kpãa yɩ naa tɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ yɩ ãkpãɛn.

eng: But someone will say, How are the dead raised? What kind of body will they have when they come back.
local: Tɩchɛ nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ na sʊʊr naa, Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ a kũu na ɩrɛ wa ir? Ɩ̃gãn buor lɛ ban lɛ iru.

eng: The spirits of prophets are under the control of the prophets.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na 'yɛrɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr wʊ nyɔɔrɔ wʊ mãɛ.

eng: Look, fool! When you put a seed into the ground, it doesnt come back to life unless it dies.
local: Nyɛ dãbol ka. A ala yɩn ma bʊr a ba ɩ a ma bulaa tɩ a bʊ̃koraa pʊ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: God isnt a God of disorder but of peace. Like in all the churches of Gods people.
local: Bojũu Nãaŋmɩn ba ɩ nyɔ̃ nyɔ̃ɔfʊ Nãaŋmɩn ɛ, a wʊ bʊɔl sɩ a sɩn kpɩɛrɛ nɩ nyã'maar nɩtãa lɛ an kaara a pupuorbiir gbulsɩ ba jaa jie.

eng: What you put in the ground doesnt have the shape that it will have, but its a bare grain of wheat or some other seed.
local: Yɩn wa bʊr, a ba ɩ a vaar lɛ yɩ bʊr ɛ, tɩchɛ a bʊ̃bir tɛɛ lɛ yɩ ma bʊr. Ka chi lɛ bɩɩ bon bir lɛ.

eng: the women should be quiet during the meeting. They are not allowed to talk. Instead, they need to get under control, just as the Law says.
local: Pɔbɔ ba ma ɩ jaa gbulo a puorfʊ jie, baa tara sɔr na haa nɛɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ ba siir ba mãɛ nɩtãa lɛ a Mosesɩ nɛbinãa na 'yɛr.

eng: God gives it the sort of shape that he chooses, and he gives each of the seeds its own shape.
local: Tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn lɛ ma kʊ a ãsʊɔ jaa ãgãn 'lɔ jaa wʊn bʊɔrɔ. Bʊ̃bir ãsʊɔ jaa nɩ wʊ vaar lɛ.

eng: If they want to learn something, they should ask their husbands at home. It is disgraceful for a woman to talk during the meeting.
local: Ala ɩ ba bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka ba sʊʊr yele kɔ̃ɔ kaa, ba kul a yir tɩ sʊʊr a ba mãɛ sɩrbɛ kaa. Bojũu a ɩn vã pɔɔ na 'yɛr 'yɛr a puorfʊ jie.

eng: All flesh isnt alike. Humans have one kind of flesh, animals have another kind of flesh, birds have another kind of flesh, and fish have another kind.
local: Ɩ̃gãn nʊ̃nɔ̃ a jaa baa ɩ bʊ̃'yen ɛ. Nɩrɛ ãgãn nʊ̃n tɛɛ naa nɩ dʊ̃n ãgãn nʊ̃n, ka a lubil nʊ̃n be wʊ yõ, ka a jãmɛ nʊ̃n mɩ be a yõ.

eng: Did the word of God originate with you? Has it come only to you?.
local: Yɩn sɛ̃ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr de nie yi bɩɩ? Bɩɩ yɩn tɛɛ lɛ ɩ a nɩbɛ a 'yɛr na de nie ta?.

eng: If anyone thinks that they are prophets or spiritual people, then let them recognize that what Im writing to you is the Lords command.
local: Ala ɩ nɩrɛ tɩɛr ɩka wʊ ɩn a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr 'yɛrɛ bɩɩ tara a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ kʊfʊsɩ, vɛ̃ ka a nɩnyɩnɛ bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a anyãna ɩn sɛb ɩ naa lɛ a sɩ Soro na bin.

eng: There are heavenly bodies and earthly bodies. The heavenly bodies have one kind of glory, and the earthly bodies have another kind of glory.
local: Saaju ãgãma mɩ ben be, ka a tẽe ãgãma mɩ be be. A saaju ãgãma vɩɛl ben a yõ, ka a tẽe ãgãma vɩɛl mɩ be a yõ.

eng: If someone doesnt recognize this, they arent recognized.
local: Ala ɩ nɩrɛ ba wa sɔɔ anyãna ɛ, yɩ mɩ ta sɔɔ wʊ ɛ.

eng: The sun has one kind of glory, the moon has another kind of glory, and the stars have another kind of glory .
local: A mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ vɩla ben wʊ yõ, ka a ŋmɛraa vɩla mɩ be wʊ yõ, ka a ŋmɛrbir ãsʊɔ vɩla mɩ tɛɛ a ŋmɛrbir ãsʊɔ.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, use your ambition to try to get the gift of prophecy, but dont prevent speaking in tongues.
local: A lɛ jũu, n yɛɛr, yɩ mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr 'yɛrfʊ, tɩ na yɩ taa tɔrɔ a 'yɛr dãgɩlaa 'yɛrfʊ bara ɛ.

eng: Its the same with the resurrection of the dead: a rotting body is put into the ground, but what is raised wont ever decay.
local: Lɛ lɛ an be a kũn wa ir a kũu pɔ. A ãgãn 'lɔ ban ũu ɩn bʊ̃sɔ̃ɔ naa, tɩchɛ 'lɔ wa ir a kũu pɔ, waa lɛ pʊ̃ɔnɔ̃ go ɛ.

eng: Its degraded when its put into the ground, but its raised in glory. Its weak when its put into the ground, but its raised in power.
local: Bala wa ũu a kũu wʊ ma ɩn bʊ̃dɛɛr ba lɛ tara kpɛ̃ɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn wa ir wʊ a kũu pɔ, wʊn vɩɛlɛ naa na wʊ tara kpɛ̃ɔ.

eng: Everything should be done with dignity and in proper order.
local: Tɩchɛ yɩ ma maala yele jaa na sɛɛ naa ɩ nɛ nɛ nɛ sɔr jaa pɔ.

eng: Its a physical body when its put into the ground, but its raised as a spiritual body. If theres a physical body, theres also a spiritual body.
local: A tẽe ãgãn nyã lɛ ba ũun wʊ, 'lɔ wa ir a kũu pɔ, a sɩɛ ãgãn lɛ wʊn tara. Nɩtãa lɛ a tẽe ãgãn na be be, lɛ mɩ lɛ a sɩɛ ãgãn mɩ be be.

eng: So it is also written, The first human, Adam, became a living person, and the last Adam became a spirit that gives life.
local: Nɩtãa lɛ an sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ, Adama a Nãaŋmɩn na dɔ̃ɔ de nie mɛ ɩn bʊ̃nɩbir. A Adama bɩɛraa sʊɔ lɛ ɩ a sɩɛ.

eng: But the physical body comes first, not the spiritual one—the spiritual body comes afterward.
local: A sɩɛ sʊɔ ba lɛ de nie ɛ, tɩchɛ a ãgãn sʊɔ lɛ, ala puor ka a sɩɛ sʊɔ pãa wa.

eng: The first human was from the earth made from dust; the second human is from heaven.
local: A nɩdɔ̃ɔ yin ka a tẽe pɔ, Nãaŋmɩn na mɛn a tã-uul, tɩchɛ a nɩbɩɛraa 'lɔ yin saaju.

eng: The nature of the person made of dust is shared by people who are made of dust, and the nature of the heavenly person is shared by heavenly people.
local: Bala na yi ka a tẽe pɔ, kaara na fɩka, a 'lɔ ban mɛn a tã-uul, ka bala mɩ na yi a saaju mɩ kaara na, fɩka a 'lɔ na yi a saaju.

eng: We will look like the heavenly person in the same way as we have looked like the person made from dust.
local: A lɛ nɛ nɩ wʊ ju sɩn ben nyɛtãan a nɩrɛ nyã a Nãaŋmɩn na mɛn a tã-uul, lɛ mɩ lɛ sɩn su a ãgãn 'lɔ nyɛtãan a 'lɔ na yi a saaju kaar.

eng: This is what Im saying, brothers and sisters: Flesh and blood cant inherit Gods kingdom. Something that rots cant inherit something that doesnt decay.
local: N yɛɛr, n bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka n 'yɛr chã kʊ yɩ ɩka, a ãgãn nɩ a jãɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ kpɛ be a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ ɛ. Bɩɩ ka a bʊ̃sɔ̃ɔ naa pʊɔ a 'lɔ na ba sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ pɔ ɛ.

eng: Listen, Im telling you a secret: All of us wont die, but we will all be changed.
local: Yɩ chɛlɛ ka n 'yɛr 'yɛr sɔɔlaa kɔ̃ɔ kʊ yɩ. Sɩ jaa baa na kpi ɛ, tɩchɛ sɩ jaa lɛ na lɩɛb.

eng: in an instant, in the blink of an eye, at the final trumpet. The trumpet will blast, and the dead will be raised with bodies that wont decay, and we will be changed.
local: fɩka a nɩbir na ma wa va a pere kaar, ka a iil baaraa wa pɛɛl. Bojũu a iil wa pɛɛl a bala na kpi na ir naa, ba kʊ̃ lɛ kpi go ɛ, ka a sɩn bala na ba kpi sɛrɛ ɛ, mɩ lɩɛb.

eng: Its necessary for this rotting body to be clothed with what cant decay, and for the body that is dying to be clothed in what cant die.
local: Ka a ãgãma anyã na sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃, ka a lɩɛb de a ãgãma ala na ba sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ su, ka a 'lɔ na kpire kʊ̃ lɛ kpi go ɛ.

eng: And when the rotting body has been clothed in what cant decay, and the dying body has been clothed in what cant die, then this statement in scripture will happen: Death has been swallowed up by a victory .
local: A bʊ̃sɔ̃ɔ naa wa de a 'lɔ na ba sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ɛ, su, ka a 'lɔ na kpire kʊ̃ lɛ kpi go ɛ, ka a lɛ na sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãma pɔ ɩ yelmãɛ, Kũu a fʊ tʊ̃ɔfʊ baar naa.

eng: Where is your victory, Death? Where is your sting, Death? .
local: Kũu, nyɩnɛ lɛ a fʊ tʊ̃ɔfʊ be? Kũu, nyɩnɛ lɛ a fʊ fɩlɩlɩlɩ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ na ma kʊ be.

eng: Deaths sting is sin, and the power of sin is the Law.
local: A kũu fɩlɩlɩlɩ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ na ma kʊ ɩn a yelbier maalfʊ, ka a yelbier maalfʊ mɩ nyɛ a wʊ kpɛ̃ɔ yi a Mosesɩ nɛbinãa pɔ.

eng: Thanks be to God, who gives us this victory through our Lord Jesus Christ!.
local: Tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn son puor fʊ, wʊn kʊ sɩ tʊ̃ɔfʊ tu a lɛ a sɩ Soro Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto na maal.

eng: As a result of all this, my loved brothers and sisters, you must stand firm, unshakable, excelling in the work of the Lord as always, because you know that your labor isnt going to be for nothing in the Lord.
local: A lɛ jũu, n yɛɛr, yɩ ara chãchã, yɩ taa vɛ̃ ka a bon jaa wa dam yɩ ɛ. Yɩ mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir nɩ a sɩ Soro tʊ̃mɔ̃, yɩ bɔ̃ɔ ɩka a ala yɩn tʊ̃nɔ̃ kʊrɔ a sɩ Soro, kʊ̃ wa ɩ pɔrɔ ɛ.

eng: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by Gods will, and Timothy our brother. To Gods church that is in Corinth, along with all of Gods people throughout Achaia.
local: Maa Pɔɔl na ɩ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto nɩtʊ̃nãa Nãaŋmɩn na chɛl ir nɩ a sɩ yɛbɛ Timoti, lɛ sɛɛrɛ a gãn nyã kʊrɔ a pupuorbiir na be a Korɩntɩ tẽe pɔ nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ ba jaa mɩ na be a Akaya paal pɔ.

eng: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and from our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Wãɛfʊ nɩ nyã'maar na yi a sɩ Sãa Nãaŋmɩn nɩ a sɩ Soro Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto sɛ̃ a be a yɩ sɛ̃.

eng: May the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ be blessed! He is the compassionate Father and God of all comfort.
local: Pɛɛfʊ wʊ ta Nãaŋmɩn na ɩ a sɩ Soro Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto Sãa, a Sãa nyã nɩbir na bal nɩ Nãaŋmɩn nyã na tara a 'maalfʊsɩ a jaa.

eng: Hes the one who comforts us in all our trouble so that we can comfort other people who are in every kind of trouble. We offer the same comfort that we ourselves received from God.
local: 'Lɔ lɛ ma 'maal sɩ a sɩ wõm a jaa pɔ, ka sɩ mɩ na tʊ̃ɔ 'maal a bala mɩ na be wõm jaa pɔ, nɩ a 'maalfʊ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ sɩn nyɛ yin a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃.

eng: That is because we receive so much comfort through Christ in the same way that we share so many of Christs sufferings.
local: Nɩtãa lɛ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto dɔɔyɛ difʊ na yaar dɔɔl sɩ jaa, lɛ mɩ lɛ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'maalfʊ yaar dɔɔl sɩ jaa.

eng: So if we have trouble, it is to bring you comfort and salvation. If we are comforted, it is to bring you comfort from the experience of endurance while you go through the same sufferings that we also suffer.
local: Ala ɩ sɩ ben wõm pɔ, a yɩ 'maalfʊ nɩ a yɩ faafʊ jũu lɛ, ala ɩ sɩ mɩ nyɛn 'maalfʊ a mɩ ɩn a yɩ 'maalfʊ lɛ, a yɩn tʊ̃ɔ ara nɩ kãnyir a dɔɔyɛ bʊ̃'yen 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ pɔ sɩn mɩ na dire.

eng: Our hope for you is certain, because we know that as you are partners in suffering, so also you are partners in comfort.
local: Sɩ bar tɩɛr a yɩ jũu, bojũu sɩ bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka a lɛ yɩn lɔ̃ɔn sɩ dire a dɔɔyɛ, a lɛ nɩ wʊ ju mɩ lɛ yɩn pʊɔ sɩ a sɩ 'maalfʊ pɔ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we dont want you to be unaware of the troubles that we went through in Asia. We were weighed down with a load of suffering that was so far beyond our strength that we were afraid we might not survive.
local: N yɛɛr, saa bʊɔrɔ ɩka sɩ sɔɔl yɩ a wõm 'lɔ tɔ sʊɔ na dɔ̃ɔ pɔɔ sɩ be a Asɩya paal pɔ bar ɛ. Wõm jaa jel lɛ dɔ̃ɔ pɔɔ sɩ naa gar a sɩ kpɛ̃ɔ, saa lɛ dɔ̃ɔ tara baarfʊ tɩɛr ɛ.

eng: It certainly seemed to us as if we had gotten the death penalty. This was so that we would have confidence in God, who raises the dead, instead of ourselves.
local: Sɩrɛ jaa a sɩ tɩɛr pɔ, a kũu lɛ sɩ tɔ̃ɔ kaara a tãɩɩ a lɛ nɛ. Tɩchɛ a nɩtɔ̃ ɩ naa ka sɩ ta dɩɛl a sɩ mãɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ sɩ dɩɛl Nãaŋmɩn nyã na sãnɛ a kũn.

eng: God rescued us from a terrible death, and he will rescue us. We have set our hope on him that he will rescue us again.
local: A Nãaŋmɩn lɛ faa sɩ yin a kũu tuo nyã pɔ, 'lɔ lɛ chɛnɛ faara sɩ. 'Lɔ lɛ ɩ a sɩ tɩɛrtɔ jie ɩka wʊn chɛnɛ na faara sɩ.

eng: since you are helping with your prayer for us. Then many people can thank God on our behalf for the gift that was given to us through the prayers of many people.
local: nɩtãa lɛ yɩn sʊ̃ʊ sɩ nɩ a yɩ Nãaŋmɩn sʊɔrfʊ. Yɔɔ mɩ puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan a sɩ jũu tu a wʊ maal vɩla 'lɔ wʊn maal sɩ na wʊ sɔɔ a yɩ yɔɔ sʊɔrfʊ.

eng: We have conducted ourselves with godly sincerity and pure motives in the world, and especially toward you. This is why we are confident, and our conscience confirms this. We didnt act with human wisdom but we relied on the grace of God.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã a sɩ nyãa chɛfʊ jie lɛ a nyã. A sɩ tɩɛr din dãasɩɛ a lɛ sɩn be a wɛr dẽme sɛ̃, nɩ a lɛ a sɩn nɩ a yɩn mɩ na be taa, chɛchɛ ɩfʊ pɔ, nɩ nɛ nɛ tufʊ pɔ, ka a jaa yi a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃. A ba ɩ a wɛr nyã bɔ̃ɔfʊ lɛ sɩ maal a lɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ a Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ lɛ.

eng: We dont write anything to you except what you can read and also understand. I hope that you will understand totally.
local: Bojũu saa sɛb bon jaa kʊ yɩ yɩn kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ kar ɛ, bɩɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ bɔ̃ɔ a par ɛ. N tɩɛr ɩka.

eng: since you have already understood us partly. Understand that in the day of our Lord Jesus, we will make you proud as you will also make us proud.
local: nɩtãa lɛ yɩn wõ a sɩ 'yɛr ŋmaa na yɩ bɔ̃ɔ a par, yɩn wa wõn a jaa na yɩ bɔ̃ɔ a par, na yɩn tʊ̃ɔ chɛ nyãa a sɩ jũu nɩtãa lɛ sɩn mɩ na chɛ nyãa a yɩ jũu a sɩ Soro Yesu bɩbir daar.

eng: Because I was sure of this, I wanted to visit you first so that you could have a second opportunity to see me.
local: Bojũu ala lɛ so ka n bar tɩɛr, naa guor ɩka n tɩ piir yɩ kaa ka yɩ nyɛ tɔ̃nɔ̃ gbɛɛ ayi.

eng: I wanted to visit you on my way to Macedonia, and then come to you again on my way back from Macedonia, at which point I was hoping you would help me on my way to Judea.
local: N guor ɩka maa wa chiine a Masedonɩya n yi a yɩ sɛ̃, maa wa lɩɛb waara ka n lɛ yi a yɩ sɛ̃ go, ka yɩ pãa sʊ̃ʊ ma ka n chen a Judiya.

eng: So I wasnt unreliable when I planned to do this, was I? Or do I make decisions with a substandard human process so that I say Yes, yes and No, no at the same time?.
local: Ɩn guor a anyã, n mɩ 'yɛr bar bɩɩ? Bɩɩ a wɛr nɩbɛ guorfʊ kaar lɛ? Ka a nɛbʊ̃'yen 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ ma 'yɛr, ɔ̃ɔ, ɔ̃ɔ nɩ kai, kai?.

eng: But as God is faithful, our message to you isnt both yes and no.
local: Tɩchɛ sɩrɛ jaa a sɩ Nãaŋmɩn na ɩ a yelmãɛ sʊɔ, a sɩ 'yɛr sɩn 'yɛr kʊ yɩ ba ɩ ɔ̃ɔ nɩ kai ɛ.

eng: Gods Son, Jesus Christ, is the one who was preached among you by us—through me, Silvanus, and Timothy—he wasnt yes and no. In him it is always yes.
local: Bojũu a Nãaŋmɩn Bie Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɛr a maa nɩ a Silasɩ nɩ a Timoti na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr kʊ yɩ ba ɩ ɔ̃ɔ nɩ kai ɛ, tɩchɛ a wʊ pɔ, a ma ɩn ɔ̃ɔ.

eng: All of Gods promises have their yes in him. That is why we say Amen through him to the glory of God.
local: Bojũu a Nãaŋmɩn nɛtɩryɔɔ ala wʊn tɩr ɩn ɔ̃ɔ a Kɩrɩsɩto pɔ. A tun a wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ, a ma 'yɛrɛ ɩka, a ɩ a lɛ, kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn yuor tɩɩr.

eng: God is the one who establishes us with you in Christ and who anointed us.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã a Nãaŋmɩn nyã lɛ maal sɩ nɩ a yɩn a sɩ ara kpɛ̃ɔ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto pɔ, na wʊ oo sɩ.

eng: God also sealed us and gave the Spirit as a down payment in our hearts.
local: Wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ sɩ naa a wʊ sofʊ jãnɛ, na wʊ de a wʊ Sɩɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ a sɩ nyãa pɔ, wile sɩ a yele ala na waara.

eng: I call on God as my witness—I didnt come again to Corinth because I wanted to spare you.
local: N bʊɔl Nãaŋmɩn a wʊ ɩ a n dãasɩɛ sʊɔ ɩka a tara na sɔr ka n bar yɩ, naa ba lɩɛb wa be a Korɩntɩ ɛ.

eng: It isnt that we are trying to control your faith, but we are working with you for your happiness, because you stand firm in your faith.
local: n ba bʊɔrɔ ɩka a ɩ wõm kʊ yɩ ɛ. A ba ɩ sɩ bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka sɩ fɩr yɩ a yɩ sɔɔfʊ pɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ a sɩ 'mataa tʊ̃n yɩ a yɩ wõ nʊ̃ɔ, bojũu a yɩ sɔɔfʊ vɛ̃n ka yɩ ara kpɛ̃ɔ.

eng: So I decided that, for my own sake, I wouldnt visit you again while I was upset.
local: N tɩɛr ɩka n kʊ̃ lɛ tʊ̃ɔ wa piir yɩ kaa, ka a ɩ tuo kʊ yɩ ɛ.

eng: If I make you sad, who will be there to make me glad when you are sad because of me?.
local: Bojũu maa sɔ̃ɔ a yɩ pʊɔ an lɛ na maal ka n pʊɔ pɛl, ka a yɩn tɛɛ ba lɛ ɛ?.

eng: Thats why I wrote this very thing to you, so that when I came I wouldnt be made sad by the ones who ought to make me happy. I have confidence in you, that my happiness means your happiness.
local: Ala lɛ so ka n dɔ̃ɔ sɛb a lɛ ɩka maa wa tɩ, n pʊɔ taa sɔ̃ɔn a bala na maal ma ka nʊ̃ɔ kpɛ ma ɛ. N bar tɩɛr ɩka yɩ jaa na lɔ̃ɔn man chɩlɛ.

eng: I wrote to you in tears, with a very troubled and anxious heart. I didnt write to make you sad but so you would know the overwhelming love that I have for you.
local: Bojũu n sɛb a anyã kʊ yɩ nɩ pʊsɔ̃ɔ nɩ hʊ̃ʊnfʊ nɩ a n nyãa nɩ nɩbikʊ̃ɔ yɔɔ ɩka n taa sɔ̃ɔ a yɩ pʊɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ ka n vɛ̃ ka yɩ bɔ̃ɔ ɩka n nʊ̃ yɩn yɔɔ jaa.

eng: But if someone has made anyone sad, that person hasnt hurt me but all of you to some degree .
local: Ala ɩ nɩrɛ maal ka a chɛ, a ba ɩ maa tɛɛ pʊɔ lɛ wʊ sɔ̃ɔ ɛ, yɩ jaa pʊɔ mɩ sɔ̃ɔ naa ka a lɛ na tu lɛ ɩka n taa vɛ̃ ka a ɩ tuo 'yɔ̃ɔ jaa kʊ wʊ ɛ.

eng: The punishment handed out by the majority is enough for this person.
local: A toor tɔɔfʊ 'lɔ wʊn nyɛ yi a yɩ yɔɔ jaa jie sɛɛ wʊ naa.

eng: This is why you should try your best to forgive and to comfort this person now instead, so that this person isnt overwhelmed by too much sorrow.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã 'lɔ, a sɛɛ naa ɩka yɩ vɛ̃ kʊ wʊ, na yɩ lɛ 'maal wʊ ka a wʊ pʊsɔ̃ɔ taa chen niem ɛ.

eng: So I encourage you to show your love for this person.
local: A lɛ jũu n sʊɔrɔ yɩ naa ɩka yɩ de wʊ na yɩ wiil a yɩ nʊ̃fʊ kʊ wʊ.

eng: This is another reason why I wrote you. I wanted to test you and see if you are obedient in everything.
local: A lɛ jũu ɩn sɛb kʊ yɩ lɛ ɩka n kaa, yɩn sɔɔn tu a nɛɛ a yele a jaa pɔ bɩɩ.

eng: If you forgive anyone for anything, I do too. And whatever Ive forgiven , I did it for you in the presence of Christ.
local: Ala ɩ yɩ wa vɛ̃n kʊ nɩrɛ, n mɩ vɛ̃n kʊ a nɩnyɩnɛ. A ala ɩn vɛ̃ kʊ a nɩnyɩnɛ, ala ɩ amɩne chɛn ka n na vɛ̃ bar, n vɛ̃n kʊ a nɩnyɩnɛ, a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto niem a yɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: This is so that we wont be taken advantage of by Satan, because we are well aware of his schemes.
local: Lɛ an na ɩ ka a Satan taa ŋmɩɩn sɩ 'mataa ɛ, bojũu sɩ bɔ̃ɔn a wʊ guorfʊsɩ.

eng: When I came to Troas to preach Christs gospel, the Lord gave me an opportunity to preach.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã, a lɛ ɩn chen a Toroyas tɩ mʊɔlɔ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ, n nyɛn lɛ a sɩ Soro na yuo sɔr kʊ ma.

eng: But I was worried because I couldnt find my brother Titus there. So I said good-bye to them and went on to Macedonia.
local: n ba chɛnɛ tara ãgãn 'maar sɛrɛ ɛ, bojũu n ba nyɛ a n yɛbɛ Titusɩ a be ɛ. Lɛ lɛ n puor ba tɩ po chen a Masedonɩya.

eng: But thank God, who is always leading us around through Christ as if we were in a parade. He releases the fragrance of the knowledge of him everywhere through us.
local: Tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn son puorfʊ wʊn ma tɔɔ sɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ tʊ̃ɔfʊ pɔ yi a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto pɔ, tu a sɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ ka a yɛ̃ nyũunʊ̃ɔ yaar de a jie wʊ jaa.

eng: We smell like the aroma of Christs offering to God, both to those who are being saved and to those who are on the road to destruction.
local: Bojũu sɩn lɛ ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn nyũunʊ̃ɔ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ kʊ a bala na nyɛ faafʊ, nɩ a bala na wɛrɛ.

eng: We smell like a contagious dead person to those who are dying, but we smell like the fountain of life to those who are being saved. Who is qualified for this kind of ministry?.
local: Ɩ̃sʊɔ nyuro na kũu nyũu, ka a ãsʊɔ mɩ nyuro nyɛvʊr nyũu. Tɩ an lɛ na tʊ̃ɔ a tʊ̃mɔ̃ nyã tʊ̃?.

eng: We arent like so many people who hustle the word of God to make a profit. We are speaking through Christ in the presence of God, as those who are sincere and as those who are sent from God.
local: Sɩn ba kaara bamɩne kaar ɛ, saa maal a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr tɔ̃nɔ̃ bon ɛ. A Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ, sɩ 'yɛrɛ naa a Nãaŋmɩn yelmãɛ an ɩ 'lɔ lɛ tʊ̃ sɩ.

eng: Are we starting to commend ourselves again? We dont need letters of introduction to you or from you like other people, do we?.
local: Sɩ piel naa ɩka sɩ dãn sɩ mãɛ go bɩɩ? Bɩɩ sɩ bʊɔrɔ na gãma nɩbɛ na sɛb ka a dãna sɩ yi a sɩ sɛ̃ bɩɩ yi a yɩ sɛ̃?.

eng: You are our letter, written on our hearts, known and read by everyone.
local: Yɩn mãɛ lɛ ɩ a sɩ gãn, na sɛb 'yɔ̃ɔ a sɩ nyãa pɔ ka sɩ bɔ̃ɔ yaa, ka nɩrɛ jaa mɩ kara.

eng: You show that you are Christs letter, delivered by us. You werent written with ink but with the Spirit of the living God. You werent written on tablets of stone but on tablets of human hearts.
local: Yɩn lɛ wiil ɩka yɩn lɛ ɩ a gãn na yi a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto sɛ̃, na yɩ ɩ a sɩ tʊ̃mɔ̃ tɔ̃nɔ̃, a ba ɩ ba den a tãɩ sɔɔlaa sɛb ɛ, tɩchɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ lɛ sɛb. A ba ɩ a kʊsɩr pɛ̃rsɩ pɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ a sɛb 'yɔ̃ɔ a nɩsaal nyãn pɔ.

eng: This is the confidence that we have through Christ in the presence of God.
local: A tɩɛr nyãna sɩn bar ɩn a sɩ bon tu a Kɩrɩsɩto pɔ, a Nãaŋmɩn niem.

eng: It isnt that we ourselves are qualified to claim that anything came from us. No, our qualification is from God.
local: A ba ɩ fɩka sɩ ɩn yɛ̃ dẽme na sɩn tʊ̃ɔ tɔɔr a lɛ sɩn maal 'yɛr ɛ, tɩchɛ a sɩ yɛ̃ yin a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃.

eng: He has qualified us as ministers of a new covenant, not based on what is written but on the Spirit, because what is written kills, but the Spirit gives life.
local: 'Lɔ lɛ sʊ̃ʊ sɩ a sɩ mʊɔl a nɛtɩr paalaa nyã 'yɛr, a ba yi a ala ban dɔ̃ɔ sɛb jie ɛ, tɩchɛ a yin a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ sɛ̃. A sɛbie ala ɩn kũu, tɩchɛ ka a Nãaŋmɩn kʊrɔ nyɛvʊr.

eng: The ministry that brought death was carved in letters on stone tablets. It came with such glory that the Israelites couldnt look for long at Moses face because his face was shining with glory, even though it was a fading glory.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã, ala ɩ a 'yɛr bie ala ban sɛb 'yɔ̃ɔ a kʊsɩr pɛ̃rsɩ pɔ waan nɩ kũu tɩ 'matãan Nãaŋmɩn tɩɩr, ka a tɩɩr a Isɩral nɩbɛ na ba tʊ̃ɔnɔ̃ kaara a Mosesɩ nie a Nãaŋmɩn tɩɩr jũu, a wʊ tɩɩr gba na wa baar.

eng: Wont the ministry of the Spirit be much more glorious?.
local: A Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ 'yɛrbie tɩɩr kʊ̃ gar a lɛ ɛ?.

eng: If the ministry that brought condemnation has glory, how much more glorious is the ministry that brings righteousness?.
local: Ala ɩ a 'yɛrbie ala na 'yɔ̃ɔ a nɩbɛ 'yɛr tɩɩr bɛrma naa, a 'yɛr bie ala na waara nɩ a torfʊ, ŋmɩnɛ lɛ wʊn jã!.

eng: In fact, what was glorious isnt glorious now, because of the glory that is brighter.
local: Bojũu a Mosesɩ nɛbinãa ba lɛ tara tɩɩr a pʊ̃pãanyã ɛ, fʊ̃ʊ de man a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ, wʊ gar wʊ naa.

eng: If the glory that fades away was glorious, how much more glorious is the one that lasts!.
local: Ka ala ɩ a 'lɔ gba na wa baar waan na tɩɩr, ŋmɩnɛ lɛ a tɩɩr 'lɔ na ba baaraa ɛ, na jã!.

eng: So, since we have such a hope, we act with great confidence.
local: A lɛ jũu, a sɩ tɩɛrtɔ nyãna lɛ vɛ̃ ka saa joro dãbãɛ ɛ.

eng: We arent like Moses, who used to put a veil over his face so that the Israelites couldnt watch the end of what was fading away.
local: Sɩn ba kaara fɩka a Mosesɩ na dɔ̃ɔ vil a fɔbaa pɔɔ a wʊ nie ka a Isɩral nɩbɛ ta kaa ɛ, na ba nyɛ a nyɩlfʊ 'lɔ na baar ɛ.

eng: But their minds were closed. Right up to the present day the same veil remains when the old covenant is read. The veil is not removed because it is taken away by Christ.
local: Tɩchɛ a ba nyãa pɔɔ naa wa tãn dɩna, a fɔpɔɔraa chɛnɛ naa bala wa kara a nɛtɩr koraa jie. Baa for bar sɛrɛ ɛ, bojũu Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto tɛɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ wʊn tʊ̃ɔ for.

eng: Even today, whenever Moses is read, a veil lies over their hearts.
local: A dɩna gba, bala wa kar a Mosesɩ nɛbinãa yele, a fɔbaa ma pɔɔn a ba nyãa.

eng: But whenever someone turns back to the Lord, the veil is removed.
local: Tɩchɛ ãsʊɔ jaa wa lɩɛb wa a sɩ Soro sɛ̃, wʊ ma for wʊn a fɔbaa 'lɔ bar.

eng: The Lord is the Spirit, and where the Lords Spirit is, there is freedom.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã, a sɩ Soro lɛ ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ, ka a jie 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na be, mãɛ sofʊ mɩ ben be.

eng: All of us are looking with unveiled faces at the glory of the Lord as if we were looking in a mirror. We are being transformed into that same image from one degree of glory to the next degree of glory. This comes from the Lord, who is the Spirit.
local: Ka a sɩn bala nie na ba pɔɔ ɛ, pãa nyɩlɛ a sɩ Soro tɩɩr, naa lɩɛrɛ ka a tɩɩr pʊɔrɔ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ na yire a sɩ Soro sɛ̃ na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ.

eng: We know that if the tent that we live in on earth is torn down, we have a building from God. Its a house that isnt handmade, which is eternal and located in heaven.
local: Sɩrɛ jaa, sɩ bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka a sɩ ãgãma anyãna kaara naa fɩka a tãn yie anyãna sɩn kpɩɛrɛ, ala wa kpi, sɩ tara na yir Nãaŋmɩn na mɛ, yir nyã na be a saaju an ba ɩ nɩrɛ nũu lɛ mɛ ɛ.

eng: We groan while we live in this residence. We really want to dress ourselves with our building from heaven.
local: Sɩ ʊrɔ na bʊɔrɔ ɩka, a saaju yir 'lɔ pɔ, sɩ tɩ kpɛ a be.

eng: since we assume that when we take off this tent, we wont find out that we are naked.
local: Sɩn wa kpɛ a be, sɩ kʊ̃ lɛ ɩ sal'mʊɔr ɛ.

eng: Yes, while we are in this tent we groan, because we are weighed down. We want to be dressed not undressed, so that what is dying can be swallowed up by life.
local: Bojũu a ãgãn tãn nyã pɔ sɩn be, sɩ ʊrɔ na ka a ɩ tuor kʊ sɩ, bojũu saa bʊɔrɔ ɩka sɩ ɩ sal'mʊɔr ɛ, tɩchɛ a sɩ de a saaju jikpɩɛraa 'lɔ su pɔɔ, ka a 'lɔ na ɩ a bʊ̃kũu ka a nyɛvʊr vɔl wʊ.

eng: Now the one who prepared us for this very thing is God, and God gave us the Spirit as a down payment for our home.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã, a Nãaŋmɩn mãɛ lɛ maal sɩ a lɛ a yele nyãna 'yɔ̃ɔ, na wʊ kʊ sɩ a wʊ Sɩɛ, ka a ɩ yelmãɛ jaa lɛ an na wa.

eng: So we are always confident, because we know that while we are living in the body, we are away from our home with the Lord.
local: A lɛ jũu, sɩ ma bar tɩɛr, na sɩ bɔ̃ɔn ɩka sɩn chɛnɛ be a ãgãn pɔ, sɩ jãan naa a sɩ Soro.

eng: We live by faith and not by sight.
local: A nyɛvʊr sɩn tara, sɔɔfʊ lɛ, a ba ɩ nyɛfʊ lɛ ɛ.

eng: We are confident, and we would prefer to leave the body and to be at home with the Lord.
local: Sɩ bar tɩɛr, ka a mɩ ɩ sɩ bɔfʊ ɩka sɩ yi a ãgãn nyã pɔ, na sɩ chen tɩ kpɩɛrɛ a sɩ Soro yir.

eng: So our goal is to be acceptable to him, whether we are at home or away from home.
local: Ala lɛ so ka sɩ mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir pɛlɛ a wʊ pʊɔ, ka a ãgãn nyã pɔ lɛ sɩ be, bɩɩ ka sɩ yin a wʊ pɔ.

eng: We all must appear before Christ in court so that each person can be paid back for the things that were done while in the body, whether they were good or bad.
local: Bojũu a sɩ ãsʊɔ jaa na wa ara na Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ dakɔɔ par, ka wʊ kʊ ãsʊɔ jaa lɛ wʊn de a wʊ ãgãn tʊ̃n, ka vɩla lɛ fʊ tʊ̃, bɩɩ dɛɛr lɛ.

eng: So we try to persuade people, since we know what it means to fear the Lord. We are well known by God, and I hope that in your heart we are well known by you as well.
local: A lɛ jũu, an yi a lɛ daar jaa sɩ bɔ̃ɔn a lɛ a sɩ Soro dãbãɛ jofʊ na be, a sɩ pãa mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir ɩka sɩ lɩɛb a nɩbɛ tɩɛr. Nãaŋmɩn bɔ̃ɔn lɛ sɩn be, ka a yɩ tɩɛr gba mɩ bɔ̃ɔ a lɛ sɩn be.

eng: We arent trying to commend ourselves to you again. Instead, we are giving you an opportunity to be proud of us so that you could answer those who take pride in superficial appearance, and not in what is in the heart.
local: Saa bʊɔrɔ ɩka sɩ dãn a sɩ mãɛ kʊ yɩ go ɛ, tɩchɛ a sɩ kʊ yɩ sɔr ka yɩ chɛ nyãa a sɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ, na yɩ tʊ̃ɔ 'yɛr a bala na ma chɩɛrɛ a nyãa nɩ a ala ban nyɛn nɩbir, tɩ bar a ala na be a nyãa pɔ.

eng: If we are crazy, its for Gods sake. If we are rational, its for your sake.
local: Ala ɩ sɩ gele naa, Nãaŋmɩn jũu lɛ. Ala ɩ saa lɛ gele mɩ ɛ, a yɩ jũu lɛ.

eng: The love of Christ controls us, because we have concluded this: one died for the sake of all; therefore, all died.
local: Bojũu a Kɩrɩsɩto nʊ̃fʊ lɛ fɩrɛ sɩ, bojũu sɩ bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka a nɩbʊ̃'yen lɛ kpi kʊ a nɩbɛ ba jaa, a lɛ jũu nɩrɛ jaa lɛ kpi.

eng: He died for the sake of all so that those who are alive should live not for themselves but for the one who died for them and was raised.
local: 'Lɔ lɛ kpi kʊ ba jaa, ka a bala na chɛnɛ a nɩbir, ba kʊ̃ lɛ be be kʊ a ba mãɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ ban ben be kʊ a 'lɔ na kpi kʊ ba na wʊ lɛ ir ɩ vʊr go.

eng: So then, from this point on we wont recognize people by human standards. Even though we used to know Christ by human standards, that isnt how we know him now.
local: An yi a dɩna chiine, saa lɛ kaara nɩrɛ a wɛr nɩbɛ kaafʊ kaar ɛ. Lɛ nɩ wʊ ju lɛ sɩ mɩ dɔ̃ɔ kaa a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto, saa lɛ chɛnɛ maala a lɛ go ɛ.

eng: So then, if anyone is in Christ, that person is part of the new creation. The old things have gone away, and look, new things have arrived!.
local: A lɛ jũu, ala ɩ nɩrɛ jaa wa ben a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ, wʊ lɩɛb nɩpaalaa, a nɩkoraa chen naa, tɩchɛ ka wʊ lɩɛb nɩpaalaa.

eng: All of these new things are from God, who reconciled us to himself through Christ and who gave us the ministry of reconciliation.
local: A anyãna a jaa yin a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃, 'lɔ lɛ 'maal sɩ kʊ a wʊ mãɛ tu a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ, na wʊ pãa kʊ sɩ a 'maalfʊ 'yɛrbie.

eng: In other words, God was reconciling the world to himself through Christ, by not counting peoples sins against them. He has trusted us with this message of reconciliation.
local: Nãaŋmɩn 'maal a wɛr kʊ a wʊ mãɛ tu a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ. Waa lɛ sɔrɔ a nɩbɛ yeldɛbɛ ban maal ɛ. A 'maalfʊ 'yɛrbie anyã lɛ wʊ de tʊ̃n sɩ.

eng: So we are ambassadors who represent Christ. God is negotiating with you through us. We beg you as Christs representatives, Be reconciled to God.
local: A lɛ jũu, sɩn lɛ ara kʊ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto ka a wɛr pɔ, sɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn tu sʊɔrɔ yɩ. Sɩ den a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto yuor sʊɔrɔ nɩ yɩ ɩka, yɩ tɔɔ wa gbʊr a Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: God caused the one who didnt know sin to be sin for our sake so that through him we could become the righteousness of God.
local: Nãaŋmɩn den a 'lɔ na ba maal dɛɛr jaa ɛ, ka wʊ lo a sɩ dɛɛr bɔɔr kʊ sɩ, ka a wʊ jũu a sɩ lɩɛb nɩtorsɩ a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃.

eng: This is why we dont get discouraged, given that we received this ministry in the same way that we received Gods mercy.
local: A lɛ jũu, an ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbaal lɛ wʊ kʊn sɩ a tʊ̃mɔ̃ nyã sɩn tʊ̃nɔ̃, a lɛ jũu saa baala ɛ.

eng: Instead, we reject secrecy and shameful actions. We dont use deception, and we dont tamper with Gods word. Instead, we commend ourselves to everyones conscience in the sight of God by the public announcement of the truth.
local: Tɩchɛ sɩ lɩɛb puor 'yɔ̃ɔ tãsɔɔ yele nɩ vã yele, saa tara bɛlfʊ ɛ, bɩɩ lɩɛrɛ a Nãaŋmɩn yelmãɛ ɛ. Sɩ ma man a Nãaŋmɩn yelmãɛ ka a gã chãa pɔ, na sɩ wiil a sɩ mãɛ ka nɩrɛ jaa tɩɛr bɔ̃ɔ ɩka yelmãɛ dẽme lɛ sɩ ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn niem.

eng: And even if our gospel is veiled, it is veiled to those who are on the road to destruction.
local: Ala ɩ gba a sɩ 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ sɩn 'yɛr sɔɔl naa, a sɔɔl kʊ a bala na wɛrɛ.

eng: The god of this age has blinded the minds of those who dont have faith so they couldnt see the light of the gospel that reveals Christs glory. Christ is the image of God.
local: A ŋmɩn 'lɔ na be be a dɩna jɔ̃ɔn a bala na ba sɔɔ de a sɩ 'yɛr ɛ tɩɛrsɩ, ka ba kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ nyɛ a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ 'lɔ chãa na ɩ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto tɩɩr ɛ, na wile a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn mãɛ na be ɛ.

eng: We dont preach about ourselves. Instead, we preach about Jesus Christ as Lord, and we describe ourselves as your slaves for Jesus sake.
local: Bojũu a ba ɩ sɩ mãɛ 'yɛr lɛ sɩ dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛrɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ sɩ 'yɛrɛ naa a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto na ɩ a sɩ Soro, a sɩn mɩ ɩ a yɩ tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ a wʊ jũu.

eng: God said that light should shine out of the darkness. He is the same one who shone in our hearts to give us the light of the knowledge of Gods glory in the face of Jesus Christ.
local: A Nãaŋmɩn 'lɔ na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr, Vɛ̃ ka a chãa chaar libe pɔ, 'lɔ lɛ maal a chãa chaar 'yɔ̃ɔ a sɩ nyãn pɔ, na wʊ kʊ sɩ a bɔ̃ɔfʊ chãa, na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn tɩɩr a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto niem.

eng: But we have this treasure in clay pots so that the awesome power belongs to God and doesnt come from us.
local: Tɩchɛ sɩn kaara naa yɔɔraa dʊʊr kaar Nãaŋmɩn na de a bʊ̃sʊ̃ 'yɔ̃ɔ, ka a kpɛ̃ɔ wʊ jaa yi Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃, a ba yi a sɩ sɛ̃ ɛ.

eng: We are experiencing all kinds of trouble, but we arent crushed. We are confused, but we arent depressed.
local: Yele ma chɩm sɩ naa a lõbobo a jaa ayi, tɩchɛ a maa ŋmɛr sɩ ɛ, ka a ma ɩ sɩ vãre vãre, tɩchɛ a sɩ tɩɛr maa yɛ̃yɛl ɛ.

eng: We are harassed, but we arent abandoned. We are knocked down, but we arent knocked out.
local: Ba ma ge sɩ naa, tɩchɛ a Nãaŋmɩn ma ben a sɩ sɛ̃, na ba ma ŋme sɩ gaal tẽe, tɩchɛ baa sɔ̃ɔ sɩ ɛ.

eng: We always carry Jesus death around in our bodies so that Jesus life can also be seen in our bodies.
local: Bɩbir jaa lɛ sɩ wõnõ a Yesu kũu tuo a sɩ ãgãma pɔ, ka a nɩbɛ mɩ na tʊ̃ɔ nyɛ a Yesu nyɛvʊr a sɩ ãgãma pɔ.

eng: We who are alive are always being handed over to death for Jesus sake so that Jesus life can also be seen in our bodies that are dying.
local: Ka a sɩn banyã na vʊrɔ be kũu nũu pɔ, a Yesu jũu, ka a nɩbɛ tʊ̃ɔ nyɛ a wʊ nyɛvʊr a sɩ ãgãma anyã na kpi pɔ.

eng: So death is at work in us, but life is at work in you.
local: Ala lɛ so ka a kũu tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ sɩn, tɩchɛ ka a nyɛvʊr tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ yɩn.

eng: We have the same faithful spirit as what is written in scripture: I had faith, and so I spoke. We also have faith, and so we also speak.
local: A sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, N sɔɔn de ala lɛ so ka n 'yɛr. A Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ nyã lɛ sɩ mɩ tara nɩ a sɔɔfʊ na sɩ mɩ 'yɛrɛ.

eng: We do this because we know that the one who raised the Lord Jesus will also raise us with Jesus, and he will bring us into his presence along with you.
local: Bojũu sɩ bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka a 'lɔ na sãɩ a sɩ Soro Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto a kũu pɔ, mɩ na sãɩ sɩ naa nɩ a Yesu 'matãan a yɩn a sɩ wa ara a wʊ niem.

eng: All these things are for your benefit. As grace increases to benefit more and more people, it will cause gratitude to increase, which results in Gods glory.
local: A anyãna a jaa ɩrɛ naa a yɩ sʊ̃ʊfʊ jũu, ka a wãɛfʊ 'lɔ na tara a nɩyɔɔ, na lɩɛb dãnfʊ kʊrɔ a Nãaŋmɩn yuor tɩɩr.

eng: So we arent depressed. But even if our bodies are breaking down on the outside, the person that we are on the inside is being renewed every day.
local: Ala lɛ so ka saa baala ɛ. A sɩ ãgãn vaar gba na suoro, tɩchɛ a sɩ sɩɩr 'lɔ, bɩbir jaa lɛ a ma lɩɛrɛ bʊ̃paalaa.

eng: Our temporary minor problems are producing an eternal stockpile of glory for us that is beyond all comparison.
local: Bojũu a wõm anyãna sɩn nyɛrɛ ɩn dẽfere naa waara nɩ a nyɛvʊr 'lɔ na ɩ tɩɩr gar a jaa wa kʊrɔ sɩ.

eng: We dont focus on the things that can be seen but on the things that cant be seen. The things that can be seen dont last, but the things that cant be seen are eternal.
local: Lɛ lɛ, saa fur nɩbir 'yɔ̃ɔ a ala sɩn nyɛrɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ sɩ fur nɩbir 'yɔ̃ɔ a ala sɩn ba nyɛrɛ ɛ. A anyã sɩn nyɛrɛ, a kʊ̃ kor ɛ, tɩchɛ anyã sɩn ba nyɛrɛ ɛ, ala lɛ na kor.

eng: Since we work together with him, we are also begging you not to receive the grace of God in vain.
local: An ɩ sɩn nɩ Nãaŋmɩn lɛ 'mataa tʊ̃nɔ̃, sɩ sʊɔrɔ yɩ naa ɩka yɩ ta kaa a Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ pɔrɔ ɛ.

eng: He says, I listened to you at the right time, and I helped you on the day of salvation. Look, now is the right time! Look, now is the day of salvation!.
local: Bojũu a sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, A n maal vɩla sɔ̃ɔ, n wõn a fʊ 'yɛr, ka a faafʊ bɩbir daar, n sʊ̃ʊ fʊ naa. N 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, a pʊ̃pãanyã lɛ ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn maal vɩla sɔ̃ɔ, pʊ̃pãanyã lɛ ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn faafʊ bɩbir.

eng: We dont give anyone any reason to be offended about anything so that our ministry wont be criticized.
local: Saa ŋmaara nɩrɛ jaa sɔr ɛ, ka ba taa wa kpɛrɛ a sɩ tʊ̃mɔ̃ ɛ.

eng: Instead, we commend ourselves as ministers of God in every way. We did this with our great endurance through problems, disasters, and stressful situations.
local: Tɩchɛ yele jaa pɔ, sɩ ma maal a sɩ mãɛ wiil ɩka Nãaŋmɩn tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ lɛ sɩ ɩ. A anyãna lɛ a mãɛ nyɔɔfʊ pɔ, wõm nɩ tuo nɩ nɩbaal yele pɔ.

eng: We went through beatings, imprisonments, and riots. We experienced hard work, sleepless nights, and hunger.
local: ba ma ŋme sɩ naa nyɔɔr sɩ pɔɔ tɩ ma dam nɩbɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ sɩ, ka sɩ ma tʊ̃ tʊ̃kpãɛn na sɩ gal gʊ̃ɔ tɩ jɛ̃n kɔ̃.

eng: We displayed purity, knowledge, patience, and generosity. We served with the Holy Spirit, genuine love.
local: a sɩ chɛchɛ ɩfʊ nɩ a sɩ bɔ̃ɔfʊ nɩ a sɩ kãnyir nɩ a sɩ maal vɩla, nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ wʊn kʊ sɩ pɔ, nɩ a nʊ̃fʊ sɩn tara sɩrɛ jaa.

eng: telling the truth, and Gods power. We carried the weapons of righteousness in our right hand and our left hand.
local: na sɩ ma 'yɛrɛ yelmãɛ nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn kpɛ̃ɔ, torfʊ ɩn a sɩ tãjɛbon na ma be a sɩ nũsʊ̃n nɩ a gʊʊr pɔ.

eng: We were treated with honor and dishonor and with verbal abuse and good evaluation. We were seen as both fake and real.
local: na ba ma kʊ sɩ tɩɩr tɩ lɛ kpɛ sɩ, na ba ma bʊɔl sɩ yovɩla nɩ a yodɛɛr. Sɩ ɩn yelmãɛ dẽme tɩ ba maal sɩ nɩbɛlbɛ.

eng: as unknown and well known, as dying—and look, we are alive! We were seen as punished but not killed.
local: Sɩ ɩn nɩbɔ̃ɔnsɩ tɩchɛ a ba maal sɩ fɩka baa bɔ̃ɔ sɩ ɛ. Sɩ ma kpire naa tɩchɛ na sɩ ma chɛnɛ vʊrɔ. Ba ma ŋme sɩn nɛ nɛ nɛ tɩ baa kʊ ɛ.

eng: as going through pain but always happy, as poor but making many rich, and as having nothing but owning everything.
local: Nɩbaal yele lɛ ba ma maal sɩ, tɩ sɩ chɛnɛ chɩlɛ. Nɔ̃ɔ ma kʊrɔ sɩ naa, tɩ sɩ maala nɩyɔɔ a ba lere. Saa tara bon jaa ɛ, tɩ so bomo a jaa.

eng: Corinthians, we have spoken openly to you, and our hearts are wide open.
