local: Saa 'mʊr 'mʊr yele jaa bar a yɩn Korɩntɩ dẽme sɛ̃ ɛ. Sɩ yuon a sɩ nyãn kʊ yɩ.

eng: There are no limits to the affection that we feel for you. You are the ones who placed boundaries on your affection for us.
local: Saa ɩ nɩbikuon dẽme kʊ yɩ ɛ, tɩchɛ yɩn lɛ nɩɩ ɩ nɩbikuon dẽme kʊ sɩ.

eng: But as a fair trade—Im talking to you like you are children—open your hearts wide too.
local: A pãa kaara naa fɩka bʊʊr, naa 'yɛrɛ nɩ yɩ fɩka n bibiir. Yɩ yuo a yɩ nyãn mɩ.

eng: Dont be tied up as equal partners with people who dont believe. What does righteousness share with that which is outside the Law? What relationship does light have with darkness?.
local: Yɩ taa ɩrɛ wa gbɛɛrɛ a bala na ba ɩ a Yesu turbo ɛ, ŋmɩnɛ lɛ a yeltorsɩ nɩ a putuyele na ɩrɛ wa 'mataa? Bɩɩ ŋmɩnɛ lɛ chãa nɩ libe na ɩrɛ wa 'mataa be jibʊ̃'yen?.

eng: What harmony does Christ have with Satan? What does a believer have in common with someone who doesnt believe?.
local: Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto nɩ a Satan na ɩrɛ wa tara guor taa? Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ a nyã na ɩ a Yesu turo, nɩ a 'lɔ na ba ɩ a Yesu turo ɛ, na ɩrɛ mɩ wa tara man taa?.

eng: What agreement can there be between Gods temple and idols? Because we are the temple of the living God. Just as God said, I live with them, and I will move among them. I will be their God, and they will be my people. .
local: Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ fʊn ɩrɛ wa nyɛ tɩbɛ a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir? Bojũu sɩn lɛ ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn nyã na ba kpire ɛ, puorfʊ yir. Nɩtãa lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na 'yɛr, Ɩn ben a ba sɛ̃ 'mataa chiine nɩ ba, naa ɩ a ba Nãaŋmɩn, ka ba mɩ ɩ a n nɩbɛ.

eng: Therefore, come out from among them and be separated, says the Lord. Dont touch what is unclean. Then I will welcome you. .
local: Ala lɛ so ka a sɩ Soro 'yɛr, yɩ tɔɔ yi a ba pɔ, tɩ chɛ ba bar, na yɩ oo yɩ yõ. Yɩ taa ɩrɛ wa sɩrɛ bʊ̃dɛɛr jaa ɛ, ɩn de yɩ naa.

eng: I will be a father to you, and you will be my sons and daughters, says the Lord Almighty .
local: Ɩn ɩ naa a yɩ Sãa, ka a yɩn mɩ ɩ a n dabil nɩ a n pɔɔbil. Lɛ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na ɩ a Tʊ̃ɔfʊ Sʊɔ 'yɛr.

eng: My dear friends, since we have these promises, lets cleanse ourselves from anything that contaminates our body or spirit so that we make our holiness complete in the fear of God.
local: An ɩ sɩn lɛ be tara a nɛtɩraa nyãnaa jũu, n barmɩne, yɩ ɩ a sɩ pɔl sɩ mãɛ yin yele jaa na sɔ̃ɔ a sɩ ãgãma nɩ a sɩ sɩɩr pɔ, na sɩ mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir na sɩ ɩ chɛchɛ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ nɩ Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: Make room in your hearts for us. We didnt do anything wrong to anyone. We didnt ruin anyone. We didnt take advantage of anyone.
local: Yɩ yuo a yɩ nyãa kʊ sɩ. Saa maal a yɩ ãsʊɔ jaa dɛɛr ɛ, saa fɔl a yɩ ãsʊɔ jaa mɩ ɛ, saa mɩ ŋma a yɩ ãsʊɔ jaa ɛ.

eng: Im not saying this to make you feel guilty. Ive already said that you are in our hearts so that we die and live together with you.
local: N ba 'yɛrɛ a anyãna ɩka n tʊ yɩ ɛ, n dɔ̃ɔ de nie 'yɛr kʊ yɩ ɩka yɩ ben a sɩ nyãa pɔ, ka ala ɩ nyɛvʊr lɛ, bɩɩ kũu lɛ tɩ sɩn nɩ yɩn lɛ.

eng: I have every confidence in you. Im terribly proud of you. Im filled with encouragement. Im overwhelmed with happiness while in the middle of our problems.
local: N bar tɩɛr ka a 'yɔ̃ɔ ma ãkpɛ̃ɔ, a sɩ wõm a jaa pɔ, tɩ nʊ̃ɔ kpɛ ma tɩ põn bar.

eng: Even after we arrived in Macedonia, we couldnt rest physically. We were surrounded by problems. There was external conflict, and there were internal fears.
local: Bojũu sɩn ben wa ta a Masedonɩya, a sɩ ãgãma anyãna ba tara pɛ̃nfʊ ɛ, tɩchɛ sɩ tuor dɔɔyɛ ka jɛɛr ma be a yõo ka a dãbãɛ mɩ ma nyɔɔ sɩ.

eng: However, God comforts people who are discouraged, and he comforted us by Titus arrival.
local: Tɩchɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nyã na ma 'maala a nɩtɔr barsɩ, wʊ 'maal sɩ naa a lɛ a Titusɩ na wa wa a sɩ sɛ̃.

eng: We werent comforted only by his arrival but also by the comfort he had received from you. He told us about your desire to see me, how you were sorry, and about your concern for me, so that I was even happier.
local: A ba ɩ a wʊ waa tɛɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ a lɛ yɩn 'maal wʊ. Wʊ mɩ man kʊ sɩ a lɛ an dɔ̃ɔ kpɛ yɩ ka yɩ pʊɔ sɔ̃ɔ, nɩ lɛ mɩ yɩn tara a n tɩɛr, ala lɛ so ka nʊ̃ɔ pãa kpɛ ma tɩ põn bar.

eng: Even though my letter hurt you, I dont regret it. Well—I did regret it just a bit because I see that that letter made you sad, though only for a short time.
local: Ala ɩ a gãn ɩn sɛb kʊ yɩ, sɔ̃ɔn yɩ pʊɔ gba, tɩ maa pʊɔ ba sɔ̃ɔ ɛ. A sɔ̃ɔn n pʊɔ 'lɔ ɩn wa nyɛ ɩka a gãn ɩn sɛb kʊ yɩ sɔ̃ɔn a yɩ pʊɔ bãlãa.

eng: Now Im glad—not because you were sad but because you were made sad enough to change your hearts and lives. You felt godly sadness so that no one was harmed by us in any way.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã 'lɔ, n pʊɔ pɛl naa, a ba ɩ a yɩ pʊɔ na sɔ̃ɔ jũu ɛ, tɩchɛ a yɩ pʊsɔ̃ɔ na vɛ̃ ka yɩ lɩɛb a yɩ tɩɛr. Bojũu a yɩ pʊsɔ̃ɔ ɩn a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na bʊɔrɔ, ala lɛ so ka yelbier ba pɔɔ yɩ tu a sɩ sɛ̃ ɛ.

eng: Godly sadness produces a changed heart and life that leads to salvation and leaves no regrets, but sorrow under the influence of the world produces death.
local: Nãaŋmɩn pɔ pʊsɔ̃ɔ ma waan na tɩɛr lɩɛbfʊ chiine faafʊ jie, a ba ɩ waalfʊ ɛ. Tɩchɛ a wɛr pɔ pʊsɔ̃ɔ ma waan na kũu.

eng: Look at what this very experience of godly sadness has produced in you: such enthusiasm, what a desire to clear yourselves of blame, such indignation, what fear, what purpose, such concern, what justice! In everything you have shown yourselves to be innocent in the matter.
local: Yɩ nyɛ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn pɔ pʊsɔ̃ɔ na waan a yɩ sɛ̃. Nyɛ lɛ yɩn ɩ nɩbijɩɛ, na yɩ ma 'yɛr wiil a yɩ mãɛ, nyɛ lɛ ban 'yɛrɛ dɔɔl yɩ, ka a dãbã chɛlma ŋme, nyɛ lɛ yɩn bʊɔrɔ taa, nyɛ lɛ yɩn tara taa tɩɛr, na yɩ chɔɔr gu ɩka yeltorsɩ a ɩ. Sɔr jaa pɔ, yɩ wiil yɩ mãɛ ɩka yaa tara chɛfʊ ɛ.

eng: So although I wrote to you, it wasnt for the sake of the one who did wrong, or for the sake of the one who was wronged, but to show you your own enthusiasm for us in the sight of God.
local: N gba na sɛb a nɩtɔ̃ kʊ yɩ, a ba ɩ a 'lɔ na maal a dɛɛr 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ bɩɩ a bala na pʊɔ wʊ ɛ, tɩchɛ a Nãaŋmɩn niem yɩn mãɛ yɩ kaa nyɛ lɛ sɩn ma ɩrɛ yɩ a vɔlaa.

eng: Because of this we have been encouraged. And in addition to our own encouragement, we were even more pleased at how happy Titus was. His mind has been put at rest by all of you.
local: A anyãna a jaa lɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ sɩ ãkpãɛn, naa pʊɔ a sɩn mãɛ ãkpãɛn sɩn dɔ̃ɔ tara. A nʊ̃mɔ̃ sɩ naa a lɛ sɩn wa nyɛ a Titusɩ ka wʊ pʊɔ pɛl, bojũu yɩ jaa lɛ sʊ̃ʊ wʊ ka a wʊ nyãa 'maa.

eng: If Ive bragged about you to him in any way, I havent been embarrassed. Instead, our bragging to Titus has also been proven to be true, just like everything we said to you was true.
local: N dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr a yɩ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, ka yaa mɩ su ma vã ɛ. Tɩchɛ a lɛ jaa sɩn dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr a yɩ 'yɛr mɩ ɩ yelmãɛ. Ka a lɛ sɩn dɔ̃ɔ dãn yɩ kʊ a Titusɩ a wʊ tɩ nyɛ ka a ɩ yelmãɛ jaa lɛ.

eng: His devotion to you is growing even more as he remembers how all of you were obedient when you welcomed him with fear and trembling.
local: Ka a wʊ bɔfʊ wʊn bʊɔrɔ yɩ pãa pʊɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ, 'lɔ wa tɩɛr a lɛ yɩn wõnõ a wʊ toor na yɩ de wʊ nuur ayi nɩ dãbãɛ nɩ mĩifʊ.

eng: Im happy, because I can completely depend on you.
local: Nʊ̃ɔ kpɛ ma naa ka n pãa bar tɩɛr a yɩ jũu.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we want to let you know about the grace of God that was given to the churches of Macedonia.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã, n yɛɛr, sɩ bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka yɩ bɔ̃ɔ a wãɛfʊ nyã a Nãaŋmɩn na wãɛ a Masedonɩya pupuorbiir gbulsɩ.

eng: While they were being tested by many problems, their extra amount of happiness and their extreme poverty resulted in a surplus of rich generosity.
local: A bɛɛr kaa tuo na nyɔɔ ba, ka ba ɩ nɔ̃n dẽme, tɩ a chɛnɛ na nʊ̃mɔ̃ ba, ka a nɔ̃ɔ kʊrɔ ba, a ba kʊ tɩ põn bar.

eng: I assure you that they gave what they could afford and even more than they could afford, and they did it voluntarily.
local: Bojũu maa mãɛ din dãasɩɛ ɩka ba kʊn a lɛ ban na tʊ̃ɔ, ba gba kʊn tɩ põn bar. Bala mãɛ wʊɔ pɔ lɛ a yi.

eng: They urgently begged us for the privilege of sharing in this service for the saints.
local: Ba sʊɔr sɩn yãayãabɩlɛ ɩka, sɩ de a kʊfʊ anyãna tɩ kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ ka ɩ ba mɩ nyɛn sɔr pʊɔ a tʊ̃mɔ̃ pɔ.

eng: They even exceeded our expectations, because they gave themselves to the Lord first and to us, consistent with Gods will.
local: Baa maal a lɛ a sɩn mãɛ na dɔ̃ɔ tɩɛr ɛ, tɩchɛ ba den nie de a ba mãɛ kʊ a sɩ Soro nɩ a sɩn lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na bʊɔrɔ.

eng: As a result, we challenged Titus to finish this work of grace with you the way he had started it.
local: Ala lɛ so a sɩ sʊɔr a Titusɩ an ɩ 'lɔ lɛ dɔ̃ɔ piel, wʊ baar baar a wãɛfʊ tʊ̃mɔ̃ nyã a yɩ jie.

eng: Be the best in this work of grace in the same way that you are the best in everything, such as faith, speech, knowledge, total commitment, and the love we inspired in you.
local: Tɩchɛ nɩtãa lɛ yɩn ma maal yele jaa tɩ põn bar, a yɩ sɔɔfʊ pɔ, a yɩ 'yɛr pɔ, nɩ a yɩ bɔ̃ɔfʊ pɔ, nɩ a yɩ nɩbijɩɛ pɔ, nɩ a lɛ yɩn nʊ̃ sɩ, yɩ kaa na yɩ mɩ kʊ tɩ põn bar a wãɛfʊ nyã pɔ.

eng: Im not giving an order, but by mentioning the commitment of others, Im trying to prove the authenticity of your love also.
local: N ba fɩrɛ yɩ ɛ, tɩchɛ n bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka n bɛɛr a yɩ nʊ̃fʊ kaa naa mɩ de man a bamɩne mɩ maal vɩla yele kaa.

eng: You know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ. Although he was rich, he became poor for your sakes, so that you could become rich through his poverty.
local: Bojũu yɩ bɔ̃ɔn naa a lɛ a sɩ Soro Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto wãɛfʊ na be. Wʊ dɔ̃ɔ ɩ taraa sʊɔ, tɩchɛ a yɩ jũu, wʊ wa lɩɛb nɔ̃ɔ sʊɔ, ka yɩn tu a wʊ nɔ̃ɔ pɔ, mɩ lɩɛb taraa dẽme.

eng: Im giving you my opinion about this. Its to your advantage to do this, since you not only started to do it last year but you wanted to do it too.
local: A lɛ ɩn tara na 'yɛr a yele nyãna pɔ nɩ nyã, dɩyɛ̃, yɩn lɛ ɩ nɩbɛ na de nie na ba ɩ a kʊfʊ tɛɛ pɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ ka a ɩrɛ yɩ vɔlaa ɩka, yɩ ɩ a lɛ.

eng: Now finish the job as well so that you finish it with as much enthusiasm as you started, given what you can afford.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã yɩ baar tʊ̃, ka a lɛ na ɩ a yɩ bɔfʊ ɩka, yɩ maal ka a tu taa a yɩ tɩ baar jã a lɛ yɩn tara.

eng: A gift is appreciated because of what a person can afford, not because of what that person cant afford, if its apparent that its done willingly.
local: Bojũu ala ɩ a kʊfʊ vɔlaa ben be, Nãaŋmɩn ma sɔɔn de bon jaa wʊn tara, a ba ɩ a bon 'lɔ wʊn ba tara ɛ.

eng: It isnt that we want others to have financial ease and you financial difficulties, but its a matter of equality.
local: A sɩ bɔfʊ ba lɛ ɩka bamɩne ba pɛ̃nɛ, tɩchɛ ka a bamɩne 'lɔ bala ɛ, tɩchɛ jãtaa a be be.

eng: At the present moment, your surplus can fill their deficit so that in the future their surplus can fill your deficit. In this way there is equality.
local: Ka a dɩna a yɩ bʊ̃yɔɔ na sʊ̃ʊ ba a lɛ ban bʊɔrɔ, ka a lɛ mɩ a ba bʊ̃yɔɔ mɩ na lɩɛb sʊ̃ʊ yɩ, ka a jãtaa pãa be be.

eng: As it is written, The one who gathered more didnt have too much, and the one who gathered less didnt have too little. .
local: Nɩtãa lɛ an sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ, A 'lɔ sʊɔ na wob yɔɔ, wʊ maa tara yɔɔ ɛ, ka a 'lɔ mɩ na wob bãlãa maa tara bãlãa ɛ.

eng: But thank God, who put the same commitment that I have for you in Titus heart.
local: N puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan, 'lɔ lɛ de a tɩɛr bʊ̃'yen 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ ɩn tara kʊ yɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ a Titusɩ tɩɛr mɩ pɔ.

eng: Not only has he accepted our challenge but hes on his way to see you voluntarily, and hes excited.
local: Bojũu a Titusɩ ba sɔɔ de a lɛ sɩn 'yɛr tɛɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ wʊ mãɛ mɩ dɔ̃ɔ kaa ir ŋme nyãa waara a yɩ sɛ̃.

eng: We are sending the brother who is famous in all the churches because of his work for the gospel along with him.
local: Sɩ mɩ tʊ̃n a yɛbɛ ãsʊɔ ka wʊn pʊɔ ka ba 'mataa tɩ, 'lɔ a pupuorbiir gbulsɩ ba jaa na dãna wʊ, a wʊ Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr 'yɛrfʊ pɔ.

eng: In addition to this, he is chosen by the churches to be our traveling companion in this work of grace, which we are taking care of for the sake of the glory of the Lord himself, and to show our desire to help.
local: Ala lɛ so ka a pupuorbiir gbulsɩ ir wʊ ɩka wʊ pʊɔ sɩ, a sɩ 'mataa tuo a kʊfʊ anyãna chiin, na sɩn tʊ̃ɔ 'yɔ̃ɔn a sɩ Soro mãɛ na sɩ mɩ wiil a lɛ sɩn bʊɔrɔ ɩka sɩ sʊ̃ʊ.

eng: We are trying to avoid being blamed by anyone for the way we take care of this large amount of money.
local: Saa bʊɔrɔ hũhũfʊ nɩ a kʊfʊsɩ anyãna yele ɛ.

eng: We care about doing the right thing, not only in the Lords eyes but also in the eyes of other people.
local: Sɩ sɔɔ naa ɩka sɩ maal a lɛ na tor, a ba ɩ a lɛ na tor a sɩ Soro niem tɛɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ a lɛ mɩ na tor a nɩbɛ niem.

eng: We are sending our brother with them. We have tested his commitment in many ways and many times. Now hes even more committed, because he has so much confidence in you.
local: Sɩ mɩ lɛ sʊɔr a sɩ yɛbɛ ãsʊɔ sɩn jʊʊr kaa a wʊ tara yelmãɛ, sɩ tʊ̃nɔ̃ wʊ naa ka ban 'mataa tɩ, wʊ mɩ gba bar tɩɛr a yɩ jũu.

eng: If there is any question about Titus, he is my partner and coworker among you. If there is any question about our brothers, they are the churches apostles and an honor to Christ.
local: A Titusɩ 'lɔ, n tʊ̃tʊ̃tɔ sʊɔ lɛ, ka sɩ 'mataa tʊ̃nɔ̃ a yɩ pɔ. Tɩchɛ a sɩ yɛɛr banyã 'lɔ, a pupuorbiir gbulsɩ lɛ ir ba tʊ̃nɔ̃ ka a ɩ 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ kʊ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto.

eng: So show them the proof of your love and the reason we are so proud of you, in such a way that the churches can see it.
local: A lɛ jũu yɩ maal a yɩ nʊ̃fʊ wiil ba, nɩ a lɛ jũu sɩn ma chɩɛrɛ nyãa a yɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ, ka a pupuorbiir gbulsɩ na be jie jaa ka ba nyɛ.

eng: I, Paul, make a personal request to you with the gentleness and kindness of Christ. Im shy when Im with you, but Im bossy when Im away from you!.
local: Maa Pɔɔl den a Kɩrɩsɩto hɛ lɛ lɛ lɛ nɩ a wʊ 'maar ɩfʊ lɔb nũu kʊ yɩ. N maa 'lara ɛ, maa wa tuor yɩ nɩ gberaa tɩ maa mɩ baa be a yɩ sɛ̃ ɛ, n ba ɩ lɛ ɛ.

eng: I beg you that when Im with you in person, I wont have to boss you around. Im afraid that I may have to use that kind of behavior with those people who think we live by human standards.
local: N sʊɔrɔ yɩ naa ɩka maa wa wa, n taa wa ɩ a ba ɩ lɛ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ a nɩbɛ bamɩne jie ɛ, ba tɩɛr man taa a wɛr nɩbɛ kaar lɛ sɩ be.

eng: Although we live in the world, we dont fight our battles with human methods.
local: Bojũu sɩn nɩ a wɛr nɩbɛ lɛ 'mataa kpɩɛrɛ tɩ saa ŋmaara jɛɛr a ba kaar ɛ.

eng: Our weapons that we fight with arent human, but instead they are powered by God for the destruction of fortresses. They destroy arguments.
local: A sɩ tãjɛ bomo ba ɩ a wɛr nyã tãjɛbomo ɛ, a sɩ tãjɛbomo tara na Nãaŋmɩn kpɛ̃ɔ sɩn ma de wɛl a gãdaar jiir bar.

eng: and every defense that is raised up to oppose the knowledge of God. They capture every thought to make it obedient to Christ.
local: Sɩ ma sɔ̃ɔn a ba nɛchɩrɛ nɩ tɩɛr dɛɛr 'lɔ jaa ban ma de vara nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn bɔ̃ɔfʊ bara, na sɩ ma 'yɛr nyɔɔr nɩbɛ tɩɛrsɩ ka ba lɩɛb wõnõ a Kɩrɩsɩto toor.

eng: Once your obedience is complete, we are ready to punish any disobedience.
local: Sɩ mɩ chɔɔr naa ɩka sɩ kpãa a bawõnsɩ, yɩn wa ba gbɛr baar.

eng: Look at what is right in front of you! If anyone is sure about belonging to Christ, that person should think again. We belong to Christ just like that person.
local: Yɩn kaara a anyã tɛɛ yɩn nyɛn nɩbir. Ala ɩ ãsʊɔ bar tɩɛr ɩka wʊ ɩn a Kɩrɩsɩto tʊɔr, wʊ mɩ lɛ bɔ̃ɔ tɩɛr kaa ɩka a sɩn mɩ ɩn a Kɩrɩsɩto tʊɔr a wʊ kaar.

eng: Even if I went on to brag about our authority, I wouldnt be ashamed of it. The Lord gave us that authority to build you up and not to destroy you.
local: Bojũu maa gba sɔɔ chɩɛrɛ nyãa nɩ a lɛ, a sɩ Soro ba kʊ sɩ a kpɛ̃ɔ ɩka sɩ wɛl yɩ bar ɛ, tɩ sɩ guol yɩ. Vã ba kʊrɔ ma nɩ a yele nyã ɛ.

eng: I dont want it to seem like Im trying to intimidate you with my letters.
local: N ba bʊɔrɔ fɩka a n gãma a kʊrɔ yɩ dãbãɛ ɛ.

eng: I know what some people are saying: His letters are severe and powerful, but in person he is weak and his speech is worth nothing.
local: Bojũu bamɩne 'yɛr ɩka, A Pɔɔl gãma 'yɛrbie tɩrɛ naa, naa ɩ 'yɛrbikpãɛn, tɩchɛ a 'lɔ mãɛ wa be a sɩ sɛ̃, wʊ ma bal naa ka a wʊ 'yɛr mɩ maa ɩ bon ɛ.

eng: These people need to think about this—that when we are with you, our actions will show that we are the same as the words we wrote when we were away from you.
local: A nɩbɛ bala, yɩ vɛ̃ a ba bɔ̃ɔn ɩka, a lɛ a sɩ 'yɛrbie na kaara a gãma pɔ, sɩn ba be a yɩ sɛ̃ ɛ. Lɛ lɛ a sɩ tʊ̃mɔ̃ na kaara sɩn wa be a yɩ sɛ̃.

eng: We wont dare to place ourselves in the same league or to compare ourselves with some of those who are promoting themselves. When they measure themselves by themselves, and compare themselves with themselves, they have no understanding.
local: Sɩn ba 'lara diere a sɩ mãɛ mana nɩ a bamɩne na ma pɛɛrɛ a ba mãɛ ɛ. Ba ma den a ba mãɛ na ba man a ba mãɛ, baa tara yɛ̃ ɛ.

eng: We wont take pride in anything more than what is appropriate. Lets look at the boundaries of our work area that God has assigned to us. Its an area that includes you.
local: Sɩn 'lɔ, sɩ kʊ̃ sɔɔ dãn a sɩ mãɛ a lɛ tɩ põn bar ɛ, tɩchɛ sɩn nyɔ̃ɔn sɩ mãɛ jã a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na 'yɛr ɩka sɩ tʊ̃, ka sɩ gba na tʊ̃ tãn a yɩ sɛ̃.

eng: We arent going out of bounds, as if our work area doesnt extend as far as you. We were the first ones to travel as far as Corinth with the gospel of Christ.
local: Sɩ kʊ̃ dãn a sɩ mãɛ chiin tɛɛr ɛ, ka a na kaara fɩka saa wa a yɩ sɛ̃ ɛ, bojũu sɩ tãn naa a Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ a be a yɩ sɛ̃.

eng: We dont take pride in what other people do outside of our boundaries. We hope that our work will be extended even more by you as your faith grows, until it expands fully .
local: Bɩɩ dãn sɩ mãɛ tɩ põn a tʊ̃mɔ̃ a nɩbɛ bamɩne na tʊ̃ ɛ. Sɩn tɩɛr lɛ ɩka, a yɩ sɔɔfʊ na ben nʊ̃ɔnɔ̃, a sɩ tʊ̃mɔ̃ mɩ na yal 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: We hope that our work grows even to the point of the gospel being preached in places beyond Corinth, without bragging about what has already been done in another persons work area.
local: ka sɩ pãa tʊ̃ɔ mʊɔl a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ be a paalsɩ ala na be a yɩ niem. Bojũu saa dãna a sɩ mãɛ nɩrɛ kɔ̃ɔ paal pɔ, ban tʊ̃ baar ɛ.

eng: But, the one who brags should brag in the Lord. .
local: Tɩchɛ, nɩtãa lɛ an sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, Vɛ̃ ka a 'lɔ na dãna a wʊ mãɛ, wʊ de a sɩ Soro dãna nɩ a wʊ mãɛ.

eng: It isnt the person who promotes himself or herself who is approved but the person whom the Lord commends.
local: Bojũu a ba ɩ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na pɛɛ a wʊ mãɛ lɛ ɩ a nɩsʊ̃ ɛ, tɩchɛ a 'lɔ a sɩ Soro na pɛɛ lɛ.

eng: Its unnecessary for me to write to you about this service for Gods people.
local: A ba lɛ sɛɛ ɩka n lɛ sɛb gãn kʊ yɩ a lɛ sɩn na sʊ̃ʊ a Nãaŋmɩn puorbo go ɛ.

eng: I know about your willingness to help. I brag about you to the Macedonians, saying, Greece has been ready since last year, and your enthusiasm has motivated most of them.
local: Bojũu n bɔ̃ɔn a lɛ yɩn bʊɔrɔ ɩka yɩ sʊ̃ʊ, n pɛɛ yɩ naa na n kʊ a Masedonɩya dẽme, naa 'yɛr kʊ ba ɩka, dɩyɛ̃ jaa lɛ be a Akaya yɩ chɔɔr ɩka yɩ kʊ, ka a yɩ vɔlaa dam nɩbɛ yɔɔ jaa 'yɔ̃ɔ ka ba mɩn ɩ.

eng: But Im sending the brothers so that our bragging about you in this case wont be empty words, and so that you can be prepared, just as I keep telling them you will be.
local: Tɩchɛ n tʊ̃nɔ̃ naa a yɛɛr ba nyãna ka a lɛ sɩn chɩɛrɛ a nyãa a yɩ jũu, taa lɩɛb 'yɛr pɔrɔ ɛ. Tɩchɛ ka a ɩ yɩ chɔɔr baar nɩtãa lɛ ɩn 'yɛr ɩka yɩ ɩ naa.

eng: If some Macedonians should come with me and find out that you arent ready, we would be embarrassed as far as this project goes.
local: Bojũu ala be ɩ a Masedonɩya nɩrɛ ben pɔɔ ba tɩ, na wʊ tɩ nyɛ a yaa chɔɔr ɛ, sɩn be lɛ 'yɛr a yɩ 'yɛr, an ɩn vã a lɛ sɩn ma bar tɩɛr a yɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: This is why I thought it was necessary to encourage the brothers to go to you ahead of time and arrange in advance the generous gift you have already promised. I want it to be a real gift from you. I dont want you to feel like you are being forced to give anything.
local: A lɛ n nyɛn an tara sɔr ka n sʊɔr a yɛɛr ka ba dɔ̃ɔ nie tɩ piir yɩ kaa, na ba mɩ kaa a yɩ chɔɔr baar a yɩ pʊtɩɛr kʊfʊ yɩn tɩr a nɛɛ. Ka a pãa ɩ pʊpɛl kʊfʊ naa ta ɩ fɩrfʊ kʊfʊ ɛ.

eng: What I mean is this: the one who sows a small number of seeds will also reap a small crop, and the one who sows a generous amount of seeds will also reap a generous crop.
local: Yɩ tɩɛr a anyã ɩka, nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ba 'yɔ̃ɔ bʊ̃bʊrɔ yɔɔ a bɔɔ pɔ ɛ, wʊ maa chɛ yɔɔ ɛ. Tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na bʊr yɔɔ, wʊ mɩ na chɛn yɔɔ, nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa mɩ na bʊr vɩla kʊfʊ, a vɩla kʊfʊ mɩ lɛ wʊn wa chɛ.

eng: Everyone should give whatever they have decided in their heart. They shouldnt give with hesitation or because of pressure. God loves a cheerful giver.
local: Nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ jaa wʊ ma kʊ lɛ a wʊ nyãa na tɩɛr ka a ta wa ɩ pʊsɔ̃ɔ, bɩɩ ŋmʊrfʊ ɛ, bojũu Nãaŋmɩn nʊ̃n nɩrɛ na kʊrɔ nɩ pʊpɛl.

eng: God has the power to provide you with more than enough of every kind of grace. That way, you will have everything you need always and in everything to provide more than enough for every kind of good work.
local: Nãaŋmɩn na tʊ̃ɔn vɛ̃ ka a wʊ wãɛfʊ ɩ yɔɔ kʊ yɩ, ka a lɛ a yele a jaa pɔ, nɩ sɔ̃ɔ jaa ka yɩ ma nyɛrɛ a ala jaa yɩn bʊɔrɔ na yɩ ma tʊ̃n tʊ̃sʊ̃n jaa tɩ põn bar.

eng: As it is written, He scattered everywhere; he gave to the needy; his righteousness remains forever. .
local: Nɩtãa lɛ an sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ, Wʊ ma yaar a jiir a jaa kʊ a nɔ̃n dẽme, ka a wʊ torfʊ ba tara baarfʊ ɛ.

eng: The one who supplies seed for planting and bread for eating will supply and multiply your seed and will increase your crop, which is righteousness.
local: A 'lɔ na ma kʊ a kʊɔraa a bʊ̃bʊrɔ na wʊ ma vɛ̃ ka a bʊ̃diir waa, wʊn kʊ yɩ naa na wʊ vɛ̃ ka a bʊ̃bie binfʊ jie waa, na wʊ vɛ̃ ka a yɩ torfʊ ŋmafʊ bɛrmɛ.

eng: You will be made rich in every way so that you can be generous in every way. Such generosity produces thanksgiving to God through us.
local: Nãaŋmɩn na maal yɩ naa ka yɩ ɩ taraa dẽme ka yɩ ma kʊrɔ nɩ pʊpɛl sɔ̃ɔ jaa, naa tu a sɩ sɛ̃ ka a yɩ pʊpɛl kʊfʊ lɩɛb vɛ̃ ka nɩbɛ puoro Nãaŋmɩn yãan.

eng: Your ministry of this service to Gods people isnt only fully meeting their needs but it is also multiplying in many expressions of thanksgiving to God.
local: A yɩ tʊ̃mɔ̃ nyã yɩn tʊ̃ a ba ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ an wõnõ tɛɛ lɛ a sʊ̃ʊ ɛ, tɩchɛ a yaar ta nɩrɛ jaa, ka ba puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan.

eng: They will give honor to God for your obedience to your confession of Christs gospel. They will do this because this service provides evidence of your obedience, and because of your generosity in sharing with them and with everyone.
local: A tʊ̃mɔ̃ nyã yɩn tʊ̃ jũu wiil a lɛ a yɩn mãɛ na be, nɩbɛ pɛɛn Nãaŋmɩn, bojũu a yɩ nɛɛ tufʊ 'mantãan a lɛ yɩn sɔɔ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ, nɩ a yɩ pʊpɛl kʊfʊ 'lɔ yɩn lɔ̃ɔ põn ba nɩ nɩrɛ jaa.

eng: They will also pray for you, and they will care deeply for you because of the outstanding grace that God has given to you.
local: Ka a ba Nãaŋmɩn ban ma sʊɔrɔ kʊrɔ yɩ, a ba tɩɛr wʊ jaa ma ben a yɩ sɛ̃, bojũu a Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ bɛrmɛ naa na be a yɩ sɛ̃.

eng: Thank God for his gift that words cant describe!.
local: Nãaŋmɩn son puorfʊ nɩ sɩn kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ man a wʊ kʊfʊ wʊn kʊ sɩ ɛ.

eng: I hope that you will put up with me while I act like a fool. Well, in fact, you are putting up with me!.
local: N tɩɛr ɩka yɩn tara nɩ ma naa a n dãbol yele pɔ bãlãa, tɩchɛ yɩ gba den nie ɩ a lɛ.

eng: Im deeply concerned about you with the same concern that God has. As your father, I promised you in marriage to one husband. I promised to present you as an innocent virgin to Christ himself.
local: N tara na nɩ yɩn nyuur a wʊ ɩ Nãaŋmɩn nyuur. N tɩr nɛɛ kʊ yɩ ɩka yɩ kul dabʊ̃'yen, na ɩ a Kɩrɩsɩto, ka n na de yɩ fɩka pɔɔsarbile na ba ta daba ɛ, na n kʊ wʊ.

eng: But Im afraid that your minds might be seduced in the same way as the snake deceived Eve with his devious tricks. You might be unable to focus completely on a genuine and innocent commitment to Christ.
local: Tɩchɛ dãbãɛ kʊrɔ ma naa, nɩtãa lɛ a waa na dɔ̃ɔ de a wʊ yɛ̃ yele bɛl a Hawa, wʊ taa mɩ wa lɩɛb a yɩ nyãa yin a yelmãɛ yɩn tara turo nɩ a Kɩrɩsɩto dẽ dẽ dẽ pɔ ɛ.

eng: If a person comes and preaches some other Jesus than the one we preached, or if you receive a different Spirit than the one you had received, or a different gospel than the one you embraced, you put up with it so easily!.
local: Bojũu ala ɩ nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ wan wa mʊɔl 'yɛr Yesu yuo 'yɛr kʊ yɩ, bɩɩ ka yɩ nyɛ a sɩyuo na ba ɩ a 'lɔ sɩn mʊɔl na ba ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ 'lɔ yɩn nyɛ ɛ, bɩɩ 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ yuo na ba ɩ a 'lɔ yɩn sɔɔ ɛ, yɩ ma sɔɔn jaa kayaan.

eng: I dont consider myself as second-rate in any way compared to the super-apostles.
local: Tɩchɛ n ba tɩɛr ɩka n ɩn pʊʊr a nɩtʊ̃n kpãɛn bala jie ɛ.

eng: But even if Im uneducated in public speaking, Im not uneducated in knowledge. We have shown this to you in every way and in everything we have done.
local: Amɩne sɛ̃ n ba jãn a 'yɛr 'yɛrfʊ ɛ, tɩchɛ n tara na bɔ̃ɔfʊ. Sɩ ma vɛ̃n ka a anyã chaar kʊ yɩ yele jaa pɔ.

eng: Did I commit a sin by humbling myself to give you an advantage because I preached the gospel of God to you free of charge?.
local: N maal dɛɛr a lɛ ɩn siir a n mãɛ naa mʊɔl a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyã kʊ yɩ a pɔrɔ bɩɩ?.

eng: I robbed other churches by taking a salary from them in order to serve you!.
local: N faan a pupuorbiir bamɩne ban dɔ̃ɔ sʊ̃ʊ ma ka n tʊ̃ɔ tʊ̃ a yɩ jie.

eng: While I was with you, I didnt burden any of you even though I needed things. The believers who came from Macedonia gave me everything I needed. I kept myself from being a financial drain on you in any way, and I will continue to keep myself from being a burden.
local: Ka a lɛ ɩn dɔ̃ɔ be a yɩ sɛ̃, n bʊɔrɔ na bon, tɩ n ba ɩ tuor kʊ yɩ ɛ, bojũu a yɛɛr bala na dɔ̃ɔ yi a Masedonɩya wa ba kʊ man bon jaa ɩn bʊɔrɔ. N ba vɛ̃ ka n yele ɩ tuor kʊ a yɩ ãsʊɔ jaa ɛ, n kʊ̃ wa vɛ̃ ka a n yele dɔɔl yɩ jaa ɛ.

eng: Since Christs truth is in me, I wont stop telling the entire area of Greece that Im proud of what I did.
local: A lɛ a Yesu yelmãɛ na faa be a n sɛ̃ baar, nɩrɛ jaa ba be a be a Akaya paal pɔ, na ŋmaa ma sɔr nɩ a n nyãa chɛfʊ nyã pɔ ɛ.

eng: Why? Is it because I dont love you? God knows that I do!.
local: Baa 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ n tʊ̃nɔ̃ a lɛ? Bojũu n ba nʊ̃ yɩ bɩɩ? Nãaŋmɩn bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩn nʊ̃ yɩ.

eng: But Im going to continue to do what Im doing. I want to contradict the claims of the people who want to be treated like they are the same as us because of what they brag about.
local: Ɩn chɛnɛ na maala a lɛ ɩn ben maala naa tʊ̃ɔ ŋmaa a yobʊɔrbɔ sɔr, bala na bʊɔrɔ sɔr ɩka ba de a ba mãɛ mana nɩ sɩ.

eng: Such people are false apostles and dishonest workers who disguise themselves as apostles of Christ.
local: Bojũu a nɩbɛ ba nyãna ɩn jir ŋmarba nɩtʊ̃nsɩ na ba ɩ tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ na bɛlɛ nɩbɛ, na ba maal a ba mãɛ nɩtãa a Kɩrɩsɩto nɩtʊ̃nsɩ.

eng: And no wonder! Even Satan disguises himself as an angel of light.
local: A ba ɩ nɛ'maa yele ɛ, bojũu a Satan mãɛ mɩ na tʊ̃ɔn maal a wʊ mãɛ fɩka, a chãa malaka.

eng: It is no great surprise then that his servants also disguise themselves as servants of righteousness. Their end will be what their actions deserve.
local: A mɩ kʊ̃ ɩ nɛ'maa yele ɛ, ala ɩ a wʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ ãsʊɔ mɩ pãa maal a wʊ mãɛ a tʊ̃tʊ̃ toraa kaar ɛ. A ba baarfʊ daar, ban nyɛn a ba sãyar lɛ ban tʊ̃.

eng: I repeat, no one should take me for a fool. But if you do, then allow me to be a fool so that I can brag like a fool for a bit.
local: Ɩn lɛ maal 'yɛr go. Yɩ ta vɛ̃ ka a ãsʊɔ jaa bʊɔl ma jɔl ɛ. Tɩchɛ ala ɩ yɩ gba wa bʊɔl man jɔl, yɩ chɛnɛ sɔɔ de ma fɩka lɛ yɩn na de a jɔl ka a n mɩ tʊ̃ɔ bɔ yuor bãlãa.

eng: Im not saying what Im saying because the Lord tells me to. Im saying it like Im a fool. Im putting my confidence in this business of bragging.
local: N bar tɩɛr nɩ a n yobɔyele nyã pɔ, n ba 'yɛrɛ fɩka nɩtãa lɛ a sɩ Soro na ma 'yɛr ɛ, tɩchɛ nɩtãa lɛ jɔl na ma 'yɛrɛ.

eng: Since so many people are bragging based on human standards, that is how Im going to brag too.
local: Yɔɔ jie jaa lɛ bʊɔrɔ yuor a wɛr nyã pɔ, n mɩn bɔn a yuor.

eng: Because you, who are so wise, are happy to put up with fools.
local: Yɩ ma sɔɔn de jɔl vɩla jaa, yɩn ɩ yɛ̃ dẽme jũu.

eng: You put up with it if someone enslaves you, if someone exploits you, if someone takes advantage of you, if someone places themselves over you, or if someone hits you in the face.
local: Yelmãɛ, yɩ gba ma den nɩrɛ na maal yɩ gbãgbaa bɩɩ na yɩ ɩ tɔ̃nɔ̃ ka wʊ dire, bɩɩ dara ba mãɛ yire nɩ a yɩ niem bɩɩ ma 'mara yɩ a tʊkpala pɔrɔ jaa.

eng: Im ashamed to say that we have been weak in comparison! But in whatever they challenge me, I challenge them .
local: Vã kʊrɔ ma naa ka n 'yɛr ɩka sɩn bal naa nɩ a yele nyã. A ala a nɩbɛ bamɩne na ma de bʊɔrɔ nɩ yuor, maa 'lɔ ma 'yɛr naa jɔl kaar, n mɩ na pãa 'lan bɔ yuor.

eng: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they descendants of Abraham? So am I.
local: Baa ɩ Heburu nɩbɛ ɛ? Lɛ lɛ n mɩ ɩ a ãsʊɔ. Baa ɩ Isɩral nɩbɛ ɛ? N mɩ ɩn a Isɩral nɩrɛ. A ba ɩ Abraham bal nɩbɛ lɛ ba ɩ ɛ? Lɛ lɛ n mɩ ɩ a ãsʊɔ.

eng: Are they ministers of Christ? Im speaking like a crazy person. What Ive done goes well beyond what theyve done. Ive worked much harder. Ive been imprisoned much more often. Ive been beaten more times than I can count. Ive faced death many times.
local: Baa ɩ a Kɩrɩsɩto tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ ɛ? N sʊrɔ naa anyãna fɩka nɩrɛ na ba tara tɩɛr ɛ. N ɩn a tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ gar ba. Naa tʊ̃ tʊ̃kpãɛn gar ba jaa. Ka ba ma pɔɔrɔ ma die pɔ jaa tɔ̃tɔ̃. Ba ma ŋme man kpɛ̃ɛ gar ba. N ma nyuro na kũu bɩbir jaa.

eng: I received the forty lashes minus one from the Jews five times.
local: Gbɛɛ anũu jaa lɛ a Juu nɩbɛ fɔb ma nɩ a ba kpaara nɛɛr lɩjaayi for bir .

eng: I was beaten with rods three times. I was stoned once. I was shipwrecked three times. I spent a day and a night on the open sea.
local: Gbɛɛ ata jaa lɛ ba pɔb ma nɩ dakʊrsɩ, na ba lɔb ma nɩ kɔsɩbɛ gbɛbʊ̃'yen. Gbɛɛ ata mɩ lɛ a gbor kpɛ̃ɛ ŋmɛr wɛl sɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ be a man kpɛ̃ɛ pɔ. Tãsɔɔ gbul nɩ a mɔ̃tɔ̃gbul jaa lɛ n ma be a man kpɛ̃ɛ pɔ.

eng: Ive been on many journeys. I faced dangers from rivers, robbers, my people, and Gentiles. I faced dangers in the city, in the desert, on the sea, and from false brothers and sisters.
local: Ɩn ba gala a yɔfʊ ɛ, n bɔ̃ɔn lɛ a põn kʊ̃ɔ dɔɔyɛ na be, naa tuor nãnyisɩ, ka a maa mãɛ paal nɩbɛ ge ma, ka a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ mɩ ge ma. N din dɔɔyɛ a tẽe pɔ. Naa di dɔɔyɛ a mʊ̃ɔ pɔ. Naa di dɔɔyɛ a man pɔ. Naa di dɔɔyɛ a yɛɛr bala na ŋmara a jirsɩ sɛ̃.

eng: I faced these dangers with hard work and heavy labor, many sleepless nights, hunger and thirst, often without food, and in the cold without enough clothes.
local: Tʊ̃kpãɛn nɩ wõm pɔ, naa ma gal gʊ̃ɔ. N bɔ̃ɔn kɔ̃ nɩ kɔ̃nyũur naa ma gãna kɔ̃ tɔ̃tɔ̃. Ka ɔɔr ma kpɛ ma ka n ɩ sal'mʊɔr.

eng: Besides all the other things I could mention, theres my daily stress because Im concerned about all the churches.
local: A anyã a jaa puor, bɩbir jaa lɛ a pupuorbiir gbulsɩ ba jaa yele ma ɩ ma tɩɛr.

eng: Who is weak without me being weak? Who is led astray without me being furious about it?.
local: An lɛ bal, ka n mɩ ba bal ɛ? A bala na faa maal yelbier, a ba lɩɛb bũu dire ma bɩɩ?.

eng: If its necessary to brag, Ill brag about my weaknesses.
local: Ala ɩ n mɩn bɔn yuor, ɩn bɔn yuor nɩ a yelsɩ ala na maal ma ka n bal.

eng: The God and Father of the Lord Jesus, the one who is blessed forever, knows that Im not lying.
local: Nãaŋmɩn nyã na ɩ a sɩ Soro Yesu Sãa, 'lɔ lɛ a pɛɛfʊ wʊ ta bɩbir jaa, bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka n ba ŋmara jirsɩ ɛ.

eng: At Damascus the governor under King Aretas was guarding the city of Damascus in order to capture me.
local: A be a Damaskus tẽe pɔ, a nãa 'lɔ na turo a nãkpɛ̃ɛ Aretas, wʊ vɛ̃ naa ka ba gu a Damaskus tẽe wʊ jaa ɩka ba nyɔɔ ma.

eng: but I got away from him by being lowered in a basket through a window in the city wall.
local: Ka ba 'mɔɔ ma 'yɔ̃ɔ pɩɛ pɔ tur bar tokoro pɔ ka n fʊɔl yi a wʊ nuur pɔ.

eng: It is necessary to brag, not that it does any good. Ill move on to visions and revelations from the Lord.
local: N kʊ̃ wa bar a yobɔyele nyã jaa ɛ, ala ɩ n gba kʊ̃ nyɛ tɔ̃nɔ̃ ɛ. A nyɛfʊsɩ nɩ a yelwiilsɩ ala ɩn nyɛrɛ yire naa a sɩ Soro sɛ̃.

eng: I know a man in Christ who was caught up into the third heaven fourteen years ago. I dont know whether it was in the body or out of the body. God knows.
local: N bɔ̃ɔn nɩrɛ kɔ̃ɔ na sɔɔ Kɩrɩsɩto a wʊ yuom pie nɩ anaar lɛ a dɩna, Nãaŋmɩn 'mɔɔ wʊn don be a saaju 'lɔ na tu a ata sʊɔ pɔ. Ka wʊ chɛnɛ na tara ãgãn bɩɩ waa lɛ tara ãgãn ɛ, n ba bɔ̃ɔ ɛ. Nãaŋmɩn tɛɛ lɛ bɔ̃ɔ.

eng: I know that this man was caught up into paradise and that he heard unspeakable words that were things no one is allowed to repeat. I dont know whether it was in the body or apart from the body. God knows.
local: N bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka a daba nyãna ɩn ben 'yɛrɛ, ka wʊ ben ãgãn pɔ, bɩɩ waa be ãgãn pɔ ɛ, n ba bɔ̃ɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn bɔ̃ɔ naa.

eng: I know that this man was caught up into paradise and that he heard unspeakable words that were things no one is allowed to repeat. I dont know whether it was in the body or apart from the body. God knows.
local: Nãaŋmɩn 'mɔɔ wʊn don be a wʊ tẽe. Be lɛ wʊ wõnõ yelsɩ wʊn kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ man nɛɛ ɛ, yelsɩ nɩrɛ na ba tara sɔr ɩka wʊ man ɛ.

eng: Ill brag about this man, but I wont brag about myself, except to brag about my weaknesses.
local: A nɩrɛ nyãna tɔ sʊɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ n bʊɔrɔ a yuor, n kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ bɔ a n mãɛ yuor ɛ, ka maa balfʊsɩ tɛɛ ba lɛ ɛ.

eng: If I did want to brag, I wouldnt make a fool of myself because Id tell the truth. Im holding back from bragging so that no one will give me any more credit than what anyone sees or hears about me.
local: Maa ɩka ɩn bɔn yuor gba, n kʊ̃ ɩ jɔl ɛ, bojũu yelmãɛ lɛ ɩn 'yɛrɛ. Tɩchɛ n bar a lɛ, ka nɩrɛ taa tɩɛrɛ a n yele gar a ala ɩn maala bɩɩ a ala ɩn ma 'yɛrɛ ɛ.

eng: I was given a thorn in my body because of the outstanding revelations Ive received so that I wouldnt be conceited. Its a messenger from Satan sent to torment me so that I wouldnt be conceited.
local: Lɛ an na ɩ ka n pɔɔ a n mãɛ 'mɔɔfʊ sɔr, a yelwiilbɛrɛ anyã jũu, ka a pãa kaara fɩka gʊɔ na chɔɔrɔ ma a ãgãn pɔ, ka a kaara a Satan 'yɛ'yɛrɛ kaar, ka a ge ma.

eng: I pleaded with the Lord three times for it to leave me alone.
local: Gbɛɛ ata jaa lɛ n sʊɔr a n Soro ɩka wʊ vʊ̃ɔ a gʊɔ nyã bar ma.

eng: He said to me, My grace is enough for you, because power is made perfect in weakness. So Ill gladly spend my time bragging about my weaknesses so that Christs power can rest on me.
local: Tɩchɛ ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ma, A n wãɛfʊ sɛɛ fʊ naa, bojũu a n kpɛ̃ɔ ma sɛɛn kʊ a nɩrɛ na bɔ̃ɔn ɩka wʊ bal naa. A lɛ jũu, ɩn bɔn yuor gar bon jaa a n balfʊsɩ pɔ, ka a Kɩrɩsɩto kpɛ̃ɔ pãa pɔɔ ma.

eng: Therefore, Im all right with weaknesses, insults, disasters, harassments, and stressful situations for the sake of Christ, because when Im weak, then Im strong.
local: Ala lɛ so a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto jũu, n pʊɔ pɛl naa, a n balfʊsɩ pɔ, tʊbie pɔ, tuo pɔ, gefʊ pɔ, nɩ wõmsɩ a jaa pɔ. Bojũu maa wa bal, lɛ lɛ n pãa ma kpɛ̃mɛ.

eng: Ive become a fool! You made me do it. Actually, I should have been commended by you. Im not inferior to the super-apostles in any way, even though Im a nonentity.
local: N maal n mãɛ jɔl, tɩchɛ a yɩn yele lɛ tɩɛ ma 'yɔ̃ɔ. Yɩ gba sɛɛn nɩ naa bʊɔlɔ ma yovɩla, bojũu n ba ɩ pʊʊr a nɩtʊ̃n kpãɛn bala sɛ̃ ɛ, ala ɩ n gba ba ɩ bon jaa ɛ.

eng: The signs of an apostle were performed among you with continuous endurance through signs, wonders, and miracles.
local: A yelsɩ ala na ma ɩ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ jãnɛ lɛ jãnsɩ, tʊ̃fɛrsɩ nɩ nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃. A anyãna lɛ n mɩ dɔ̃ɔ tara kãnyir tʊ̃n a yɩ sɛ̃.

eng: How were you treated worse than the other churches, except that I myself wasnt a financial burden on you? Forgive me for this wrong!.
local: Pupuorbiir gbulsɩ ba boo lɛ dɔ̃ɔ gar yɩ, ka a ba ɩ ɩn ba dɔ̃ɔ ɩ tuor kʊ yɩ lɛ ɛ? Yɩ vɛ̃ a n dɛɛr nyã kʊ ma.

eng: Look, Im ready to visit you a third time, and I wont be a burden on you. I dont want your things; I want you. It isnt the childrens responsibility to save up for their parents but parents for children.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã, n chɔɔr baar ɩka n tɩ piir yɩ kaa, a gbɛɛ ata sʊɔ lɛ a nyã, n kʊ̃ tɩ ɩ tuor kʊ yɩ ɛ. Bojũu a ba ɩ a yɩ bʊ̃tarsɩ lɛ n bʊɔrɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ yɩn mãɛ lɛ n bʊɔrɔ. Fʊ̃ʊ kaa bibiir maa bɔ gun a ba sãamɩne ɛ, tɩchɛ sãamɩne lɛ ma bɔ gun a ba bibiir.

eng: I will very gladly spend and be spent for your sake. If I love you more, will you love me less?.
local: A lɛ n pʊɔ na pɛl naa ka n sɔ̃ɔ bon jaa ɩn tara a yɩ nyɛvʊr jũu, 'matãan a maa mãɛ wʊ jaa. Ala ɩ n nʊ̃ yɩn yɔɔ jaa, yɩn 'lɔ na nʊ̃ man bãlãa bɩɩ?.

eng: We all know that I didnt place a burden on you, but in spite of that you think Im a con artist who fooled you with a trick.
local: A lɛ an be, lɛ a chɛnɛ be, n ba dɔ̃ɔ ɩ tuor kʊ yɩ jaa ɛ. Tɩchɛ ɩn ɩ yɛ̃ sʊɔ jũu, n den a yɛ̃ yele nyɔɔn yɩ.

eng: I havent taken advantage of you through any of the people I sent to you, have I?.
local: N den a yɩ bʊ̃kɔ̃ɔ tu a nɩbɛ bala ɩn dɔ̃ɔ tʊ̃ a ba wa a yɩ sɛ̃ bɩɩ?.

eng: I strongly encouraged Titus to go to you and sent the brother with him. Titus didnt take advantage of you, did he? Didnt we live by the same Spirit? Didnt we walk in the same footsteps?.
local: N sʊɔr Titusɩ ɩka wʊ tɩ a yɩ sɛ̃, naa 'mataa tʊ̃n a sɩ yɛbɛ ɩka ba 'mataa tɩ. Titusɩ den a yɩ bon kɔ̃ɔ bɩɩ? A ba ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩbʊ̃'yen 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ sɩ jaa tara na sɩ tʊ̃nɔ̃ a tʊ̃bʊ̃'yen ɛ?.

eng: Have you been thinking up to now that we are defending ourselves to you? Actually, we are speaking in the sight of God and in Christ. Dear friends, everything is meant to build you up.
local: Yɩ tɩɛr man tãa sɩ 'yɛrɛ na 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a sɩ mãɛ a yɩ sɛ̃ bɩɩ? Nãaŋmɩn niem lɛ sɩ 'yɛrɛ na sɩ be a Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ, bon jaa sɩn maala, sɩ barmɩne a yɩ ãkpãɛn 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ jũu lɛ.

eng: Im afraid that maybe when I come you will be different from the way I want you to be, and that Ill be different from the way you want me to be. Im afraid that there might be fighting, obsession, losing your temper, competitive opposition, backstabbing, gossip, conceit, and disorderly conduct.
local: Bojũu dãbãɛ kʊrɔ ma naa ɩka maa wa tɩ, n kʊ̃ tɩ nyɛ a yɩ be a lɛ ɩn bʊɔrɔ ɛ. Ka yɩ mɩ kʊ̃ nyɛ ma ka n be a lɛ yɩn bʊɔrɔ ɛ. Dãbãɛ chɛnɛ na kʊrɔ ma ɩka, jɛɛr, nyuur, suur nyɛfʊ, ootaa, yobɔyele, gagar yele, mãɛ 'mɔɔfʊ nɩ buburfʊ be a yɩ pɔ ɛ.

eng: Im afraid that when I come again, my God may embarrass me in front of you. I might have to go into mourning over all the people who have sinned before and havent changed their hearts and lives from what they used to practice: moral corruption, sexual immorality, and doing whatever feels good.
local: N lɛ joro na dãbãɛ ɩka maa wa lɩɛb waara, Nãaŋmɩn wʊ maal ma ka n siir n mãɛ a yɩ niem. Ka n tara pʊsɔ̃ɔ nɩ a nɩyɔɔ bala na dɔ̃ɔ maal a yeldɛɛr tɩ ba lɩɛb a ba tɩɛr yin a yele anyãna pɔ ɛ. Ban bʊ̃nɔ̃ gãna nɩ taa nɩ a ba pepe yelsɩ pɔ.

eng: From Paul, an apostle who is not sent from human authority or commissioned through human agency, but sent through Jesus Christ and God the Father who raised him from the dead.
local: Maa Pɔɔl na ɩ a nɩtʊ̃nãa, a ba ɩ nɩsaal lɛ ir ma bɩɩ nɩsaal kpɛ̃ɔ pɔ lɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ a yin a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto nɩ a Sãa Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃, na sãɩ wʊ a kũu pɔ.

eng: and from all the brothers and sisters with me. To the churches in Galatia.
local: A yɛɛr bala na be a ka a n sɛ̃, sɩ jaa lɛ sɛɛrɛ a gãn nyã kʊrɔ a pupuorbiir gbulsɩ bala na be a Galatiya paal pɔ.

eng: Grace and peace to you from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Wãɛfʊ nɩ nyã'maar na yi a sɩ Sãa Nãaŋmɩn nɩ a sɩ Soro Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ a be a yɩ jie.

eng: He gave himself for our sins, so he could deliver us from this present evil age, according to the will of our God and Father.
local: 'Lɔ lɛ de a wʊ mãɛ tɩr a sɩ yelbier jũu ɩka wʊ faa sɩ a dɩna yelbier pɔ tun a lɛ na ɩ a sɩ Sãa Nãaŋmɩn bɔfʊ.

eng: To God be the glory forever and always! Amen.
local: Wʊ nyɛrɛ tɩɩr bɩbir nɩ bɩbir jaa. A ɩ a lɛ.

eng: Im amazed that you are so quickly deserting the one who called you by the grace of Christ to follow another gospel.
local: A 'maa man nɛɛ nɩ a lɛ yɩn lɩɛb puor 'yɔ̃ɔ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na bʊɔl yɩ tun a Kɩrɩsɩto wãɛfʊ pɔ, tɩ na yɩ lɛ lɩɛb turo 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ yuo.

eng: Its not really another gospel, but certain people are confusing you and they want to change the gospel of Christ.
local: A 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ yuo jaa ba lɛ be be go ɛ, tɩchɛ bamɩne nyɔ̃nyɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ naa a yɩ tɩɛr na ba bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba nyɔɔ a Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ lɩɛb ka wʊ be wʊ yõ.

eng: However, even if we ourselves or a heavenly angel should ever preach anything different from what we preached to you, they should be under a curse.
local: Ala sɔɔ ɩ sɩn gba lɛ, bɩɩ ka malaka lɛ wa yɩ saaju wa mʊɔl kʊ yɩ a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ 'lɔ na ba tu a lɛ sɩn mʊɔl kʊ yɩ ɛ, a nɩnyɩnɛ 'lɔ wɛ naa!.

eng: Im repeating what weve said before: if anyone preaches something different from what you received, they should be under a curse!.
local: Nɩtãa lɛ sɩn dɔ̃ɔ de nie 'yɛr kʊ yɩ, pʊ̃pãanyã n lɛ bɔ̃ɔ 'yɛrɛ go. Ala nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ jaa wa mʊɔlɔ na 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ yuo kʊrɔ yɩ, na ba tu a lɛ sɩn mʊɔl kʊ yɩ a yɩ sɔɔ ɛ, a nɩnyɩnɛ 'lɔ wɛ naa.

eng: Am I trying to win over human beings or God? Or am I trying to please people? If I were still trying to please people, I wouldnt be Christs slave.
local: Nɩsaal lɛ n bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ kʊ ma sɔr bɩɩ Nãaŋmɩn? Bɩɩ nɩsaal pʊʊr lɛ n bʊɔrɔ ɩka n pɛl? Maa ta chɛnɛ bʊɔrɔ ɩka n pɛlɛ nɩsaal pʊɔ, n kʊ naa ɩ a Kɩrɩsɩto tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ ɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want you to know that the gospel I preached isnt human in origin.
local: N yɛɛr, n bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka yɩ bɔ̃ɔ a par ɩka a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ 'lɔ ɩn mʊɔl kʊ yɩ ba yi nɩsaal sɛ̃ ɛ.

eng: I didnt receive it or learn it from a human. It came through a revelation from Jesus Christ.
local: A ba ɩ nɩsaal pɔ lɛ n tu tɩ nyɛ wʊ ɛ, bɩɩ nɩsaal lɛ jãn ma ɛ, tɩchɛ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto lɛ ir wiil ma.

eng: You heard about my previous life in Judaism, how severely I harassed Gods church and tried to destroy it.
local: Yɩ wõn a lɛ ɩn dɔ̃ɔ chɛnɛ puoro a Juu nɩbɛ puorfʊ, naa mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir gere a bala na turo a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto, naa bʊɔrɔ ɩka n sɔ̃ɔ ba bar.

eng: I advanced in Judaism beyond many of my peers, because I was much more militant about the traditions of my ancestors.
local: N bɔ̃ɔ gar a Juu nɩbɛ bala ɩn 'mataa pɔl, tɩchɛ ka a ɩ ma nɩbijɩɛ nɩ a n sãakpãmɩne puorfʊ.

eng: But God had set me apart from birth and called me through his grace. He was pleased.
local: Tɩchɛ an ɩ Nãaŋmɩn lɛ dɔ̃ɔ de nie kaa ir ma tɩchɛ ka ba wa dɔɔ ma, na wʊ bʊɔl ma tun a wʊ wãɛfʊ pɔ, a ɩ wʊn pʊpɛl.

eng: to reveal his Son to me, so that I might preach about him to the Gentiles. I didnt immediately consult with any human being.
local: ka wʊ ir a wʊ Bie wiil ma, ka na mʊɔl a wʊ 'yɛr a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ pɔ. N ba sʊʊr nɩrɛ jaa kaa ɛ.

eng: I didnt go up to Jerusalem to see the men who were apostles before me either, but I went away into Arabia and I returned again to Damascus.
local: bɩɩ do be a Jerusalɛm a bala na dɔ̃ɔ ma nie ɩ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ sɛ̃ ɛ, tɩchɛ n tɔ̃ɔ tor chen Arabɩya a dẽdẽe a lɛ nɛ, a lɛ puor lɛ n pãa lɩɛb chen a Damaskus.

eng: Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to visit Cephas and stayed with him fifteen days.
local: Yuom ata na wa po, n pãa don be a Jerusalɛm ɩka n tɩ bɔ̃ɔ a Pita, naa jɛ̃ a be a wʊ sɛ̃ maal ber pie nɩ anũu .

eng: But I didnt see any other of the apostles except James the brother of the Lord.
local: Tɩchɛ n ba nyɛ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ ãsʊɔ jaa ɛ, ka Jemesɩ na ɩ a sɩ Soro yɛbɛ tɛɛ ba lɛ ɛ.

eng: Before God, Im not lying about the things that Im writing to you!.
local: Yelmãɛ jaa lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ a Nãaŋmɩn niem ɩka, a yele nyã ɩn sɛɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ba ɩ jir ɛ.

eng: Then I went into the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
local: A lɛ puor, n lɛ chen Siriya nɩ Sɩlɩsɩya paal pɔ.

eng: but I wasnt known personally by the Christian churches in Judea.
local: A pupuorbiir gbulsɩ bala na be a Judiya a Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ, ba bɔ̃ɔ ma ɛ.

eng: They only heard a report about me: The man who used to harass us now preaches the faith that he once tried to destroy.
local: A n yele tɛɛ lɛ ba wõ ɩka, a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na dɔ̃ɔ gere sɩ, pʊ̃pãanyã 'lɔ mãɛ lɛ pãa mʊɔlɔ a 'yɛr 'lɔ wʊn ta dɔ̃ɔ bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ sɔ̃ɔ bar.

eng: So they were glorifying God because of me.
local: na ba pɛɛ Nãaŋmɩn yuor a maa jũu.

eng: Then after fourteen years I went up to Jerusalem again with Barnabas, and I took Titus along also.
local: Yuom pie nɩ anaar na wa po, maa nɩ Banabasɩ lɛ don be a Jerusalɛm naa tara Titusɩ mɩ pʊɔ.

eng: I went there because of a revelation, and I laid out the gospel that I preach to the Gentiles for them. But I did it privately with the influential leaders to make sure that I wouldnt be working or that I hadnt worked for nothing.
local: N don be a Jerusalɛm bojũu Nãaŋmɩn lɛ ir wiil ma ɩka n tɩ man kʊ ba a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ 'lɔ ɩn mʊɔl kʊ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ a bala na ɩ a nɩdierbe tɛɛ lɛ n dɔ̃ɔ sɔɔl man a yele anyãna kʊ. A n dãbãɛ lɛ ɩka n taa wa jo pɔrɔ ɛ.

eng: However, not even Titus, who was with me and who was a Greek, was required to be circumcised.
local: Tɩchɛ a Titusɩ gba mɩ na dɔ̃ɔ be a n sɛ̃, na waa ɩ a Juu nɩrɛ ɛ, baa fɩr wʊ ɩka wʊ ŋmaa a yʊɔr ɛ.

eng: But false brothers and sisters, who were brought in secretly, slipped in to spy on our freedom, which we have in Christ Jesus, and to make us slaves.
local: A yele nyã lo naa bojũu a yɛɛr bamɩne na ɩ jir ŋmarba fʊɔl kpɛ ɩka ba tɩ jʊʊr kaa a lɛ sɩn so a sɩ mãɛ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ, ka ba na maal sɩ ka sɩ ɩ gbãgbaar.

eng: We didnt give in and submit to them for a single moment, so that the truth of the gospel would continue to be with you.
local: Saa sɔɔ ba bãlãa jaa ɛ, ka a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyã na ɩ a yelmãɛ na be a yɩ sɛ̃ sɔ̃ɔ jaa.

eng: The influential leaders didnt add anything to what I was preaching—and whatever they were makes no difference to me, because God doesnt show favoritism.
local: A bala a nɩbɛ na tɩɛr ɩka bala lɛ ɩ a nɩyɔ̃ɔnsɩ, tɩchɛ a lɛ ban be maa ba nyɛ a ba tɛɛ taa ɛ. Nãaŋmɩn ba kaara nɩsaal nie tɩ tɔɔrɔ a wʊ 'yɛr ɛ. A nɩdierbe bala ba 'yɛr 'yɛr jaa lɛ pʊɔ a maa 'yɛr 'lɔ pɔ ɛ.

eng: But on the contrary, they saw that I had been given the responsibility to preach the gospel to the people who arent circumcised, just as Peter had been to the circumcised.
local: Tɩchɛ ban wa bɔ̃ɔn an ɩ yelmãɛ ɩka Nãaŋmɩn lɛ de a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyã kʊ ma ɩka n mʊɔl kʊ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, nɩtãa lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na de a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyã kʊ a Pita ɩka wʊ mʊɔl kʊ a bala na ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ.

eng: The one who empowered Peter to become an apostle to the circumcised empowered me also to be one to the Gentiles.
local: Nãaŋmɩn lɛ tʊ̃ a wʊ tʊ̃mɔ̃ tu a Pita pɔ maal wʊ ɩka wʊ ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ nɩtʊ̃nãa, 'lɔ lɛ mɩ tʊ̃ a wʊ tʊ̃mɔ̃ tu a maa pɔ tʊ̃ ma ɩka n chen a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ pɔ.

eng: James, Cephas, and John, who are considered to be key leaders, shook hands with me and Barnabas as equals when they recognized the grace that was given to me. So it was agreed that we would go to the Gentiles, while they continue to go to the people who were circumcised.
local: A Jemesɩ nɩ a Pita nɩ a Jɔɔn ban dɔ̃ɔ bɔ̃ɔn fɩka lue, ban wa nyɛ bɔ̃ɔn a wãɛfʊ 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn na kʊ ma, ba de sɩn nuur ayi nɩ nɛbʊ̃'yen maa nɩ a Banabasɩ, na ba wiil ɩka sɩ tara na nɛbʊ̃'yen, na ba sɔɔ ɩka sɩ chen a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ pɔ tɩchɛ ka ba mɩ chen a bala na ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ pɔ.

eng: They asked only that we would remember the poor, which was certainly something I was willing to do.
local: Yele bʊ̃'yen ban 'yɛr kʊ sɩ lɛ ɩka sɩ tɩɛrɛ a nɔ̃n dẽme. A yele nyã nɩ wʊ ju mɩ lɛ n mɩ mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir ɩka n maal.

eng: But when Cephas came to Antioch, I opposed him to his face, because he was wrong.
local: A Pita na wa tɩ wa a Antiok, n gɔ̃ wʊ̃n a nɩyɔɔ niem bojũu wʊ maal bɔr chãa pɔ.

eng: He had been eating with the Gentiles before certain people came from James. But when they came, he began to back out and separate himself, because he was afraid of the people who promoted circumcision.
local: Sɛrɛ tɩchɛ ka a nɩbɛ bamɩne yi a Jemesɩ sɛ̃ wa, 'lɔ nɩ a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, ma 'mantaa dire. Tɩchɛ ban wa wa baar, wʊ oon wʊ mãɛ yin a ba pɔ, bojũu wʊ joro na a bala na ɩ a yɔŋmarba dãbãɛ.

eng: And the rest of the Jews also joined him in this hypocrisy so that even Barnabas got carried away with them in their hypocrisy.
local: A Juu nɩbɛ bamɩne pʊɔ wʊ naa a nɛɛr ayi yele nyãna pɔ, ka a Banabasɩ gba tɩɛr mɩ bũbur a ba yele nyãna jũu.

eng: But when I saw that they werent acting consistently with the truth of the gospel, I said to Cephas in front of everyone, If you, though youre a Jew, live like a Gentile and not like a Jew, how can you require the Gentiles to live like Jews.
local: Ɩn wa nyɛ bɔ̃ɔn ɩka baa maala a lɛ na tor nɩ a yelmãɛ 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ nyãna ɛ, n 'yɛr kʊ a Pita a ba jaa niem, A fʊ̃ʊ ɩn Juu nɩrɛ, tɩ na fʊ kpɩɛrɛ fɩka a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ kaar ɛ. Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ fʊn ɩ fɩr a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, ka ba kpɩɛrɛ fɩka a Juu nɩbɛ?.

eng: We are born Jews—were not Gentile sinners.
local: A sɩn ban dɔɔ ka sɩ ɩ Juu nɩbɛ, saa ɩ nɩyor na ɩ yelbe-ɩrbɛ kaar ɛ.

eng: You irrational Galatians! Who put a spell on you? Jesus Christ was put on display as crucified before your eyes!.
local: A yɩn Galatiya jɔlɔ dẽme. An lɛ bɛl yɩ ka a nʊ̃mɔ̃ yɩ? A yɩ nɩbir lɛ ba nyɔɔ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto yin a nɩyɔɔ niem kpa a daa ju.

eng: I just want to know this from you: Did you receive the Spirit by doing the works of the Law or by believing what you heard?.
local: Yele bʊ̃'yen lɛ n bʊɔrɔ ɩka n bɔ̃ɔ yi a yɩ sɛ̃. Yɩ nyɛn a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ a nɛbinãa yɩn turo bɩɩ a lɛ yɩn wõ na yɩ sɔɔ?.

eng: Are you so irrational? After you started with the Spirit, are you now finishing up with your own human effort?.
local: Yɩ lɛɛ bɔ̃ɔ yɛ̃ ɔ. Yɩn piel a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ, yɩ pãa bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka yɩ baar yɩn kpɛ̃ɔ pɔ bɩɩ?.

eng: Did you experience so much for nothing? I wonder if it really was for nothing.
local: A yɩ dɔɔyɛ difʊ ba lɛ tara tɔ̃nɔ̃ bɩɩ? Yelmãɛ a ba ɩ pɔrɔ ɛ.

eng: However, we know that a person isnt made righteous by the works of the Law but rather through the faithfulness of Jesus Christ. We ourselves believed in Christ Jesus so that we could be made righteous by the faithfulness of Christ and not by the works of the Law—because no one will be made righteous by the works of the Law.
local: Sɩ bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka, nɩrɛ yele kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ tor wʊ̃n turo a nɛbinsɩ jũu ɛ, tɩchɛ a wʊ yele na tor a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto wʊ̃n sɔɔ de jũu. Lɛ lɛ a sɩn mɩ gba sɔɔ de a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto ka a sɩ yele tor, ka a kʊ̃ ɩ a nɛbinãa sɩn turo jũu ɛ. Ala ɩ a nɛbinãa tufʊ jũu lɛ, nɩrɛ jaa yele kʊ̃ tor Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ ɛ.

eng: So does the one providing you with the Spirit and working miracles among you do this by you doing the works of the Law or by you believing what you heard?.
local: Nãaŋmɩn kʊ yɩn a wʊ Sɩɛ tɩ tʊ̃ nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ a yɩ pɔ, yɩn sɔɔ a nɛbinãa jũu bɩɩ yɩn sɔɔ a lɛ yɩn wõ?.

eng: But if it is discovered that we ourselves are sinners while we are trying to be made righteous in Christ, then is Christ a servant of sin? Absolutely not!.
local: Ala ɩ sɩ mʊɔrɔ naa ɩka a sɩ yele a tor a Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ, tɩchɛ ka a ɩ yelmãɛ ɩka a sɩn mãɛ ɩn yelbe-ɩrbɛ, ka ala be a lɛ, a Kɩrɩsɩto pʊɔrɔ naa a yelbier 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ bɩɩ? Yelmãɛ jaa, a ba ɩ lɛ lɛ ɛ!.

eng: If I rebuild the very things that I tore down, I show that I myself am breaking the Law.
local: Ala ɩ n lɛ mɛn a bonsɩ ala ɩn sɔ̃ɔ bar, n maal a lɛ wiil ɩka n ɩn a nɛbinãa sɔ̃sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃.

eng: I died to the Law through the Law, so that I could live for God.
local: Bojũu a tu naa a nɛbinãa pɔ, ka n kpi kʊ a nɛbinãa, ka a lɛ na sʊ̃ʊ ma ka n be be kʊ Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: Understand that in the same way that Abraham believed God and it was credited to him as righteousness, .
local: De man a Abraham kaa, wʊ Sɔɔn de Nãaŋmɩn, ka a lɛ vɛ̃ ka wʊ ɩ nɩtor.

eng: I have been crucified with Christ and I no longer live, but Christ lives in me. And the life that I now live in my body, I live by faith, indeed, by the faithfulness of Gods Son, who loved me and gave himself for me.
local: Ba 'mantaa kpa maa nɩ a Kɩrɩsɩto a daa ju. A ba lɛ ɩ maa lɛ lɛ be be go ɛ, tɩchɛ a Kɩrɩsɩto lɛ be a n 'yɔ̃ɔ. A nyɛvʊr nyã ɩn pãa ben a ãgãn pɔ, n ben be nɩ a sɔɔfʊ ɩn sɔɔ a Nãaŋmɩn Bie, 'lɔ lɛ nʊ̃ ma, na wʊ de a wʊ mãɛ kʊ ma.

eng: those who believe are the children of Abraham.
local: Bɔ̃ɔ a par ɩka a bala na sɔɔ Nãaŋmɩn lɛ ɩ a Abraham bibiir.

eng: But when it saw ahead of time that God would make the Gentiles righteous on the basis of faith, scripture preached the gospel in advance to Abraham: All the Gentiles will be blessed in you .
local: A Nãaŋmɩn gãn dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr ɩka Nãaŋmɩn na 'yɛr faa a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, a ba sɔɔfʊ jũu. Nãaŋmɩn den nie 'yɛr a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ kʊ a Abraham ɩka, A paalsɩ ba jaa na nyɛn sʊ̃ʊfʊ tu a fʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: I dont ignore the grace of God, because if we become righteous through the Law, then Christ died for no purpose.
local: N ba 'yɛr ɩka a Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ ba ɩ ɛ, bojũu ala ɩ nɩrɛ na tʊ̃ɔn 'yɛr faa a wʊ mãɛ a nɛbinãa jũu, ka lɛ a Kɩrɩsɩto kũu ba tara tɔ̃nɔ̃ jaa ɛ.

eng: Therefore, those who believe are blessed together with Abraham who believed.
local: A lɛ jũu, a bala jaa na sɔɔ de na nyɛn sʊ̃ʊfʊ pʊɔ a Abraham pɔ. 'Lɔ lɛ ɩ a nɩrɛ na tara sɔɔfʊ.

eng: All those who rely on the works of the Law are under a curse, because it is written, Everyone is cursed who does not keep on doing all the things that have been written in the Law scroll .
local: Bala jaa na dɩɛl a nɛbinãa tufʊ ben pɔ 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ pɔ, bojũu a sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, Pɔ 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ turo na a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ba turo a yelsɩ a jaa a nɛbinãa gãn pɔ ɛ.

eng: But since no one is made righteous by the Law as far as God is concerned, it is clear that the righteous one will live on the basis of faith .
local: A ɩn yelmãɛ ɩka nɩrɛ jaa kʊ nyɛ faa fʊ a Nãaŋmɩn niem a nɛbinãa 'yɔ̃ɔ ɛ, bojũu A nɩrɛ 'lɔ na tara sɔɔfʊ tor naa, wʊn tara na nyɛvʊr na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ.

eng: This is the third time that Im coming to visit you. Every matter is settled on the evidence of two or three witnesses.
local: Gbɛɛ ata nɩ nyã ɩn ben tɩrɛ a yɩ piir kaa jie. Nɩtãa lɛ na sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ, Nɩbɛ ayi bɩɩ ata dãasɩɛ ɩn yelmãɛ.

eng: The Law isnt based on faith; rather, the one doing these things will live by them .
local: A nɛbinãa ba be sɔɔfʊ pɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na maala a lɛ a nɛbinãa na 'yɛr, na tara na nyɛvʊr.

eng: When I was with you on my second visit, I already warned those who continued to sin. Now Im repeating that warning to all the rest of you while Im at a safe distance: if I come again, I wont spare anyone.
local: A lɛ ɩn dɔ̃ɔ tɩ a gbɛɛ ayi sʊɔ, n dɔ̃ɔ gɔ̃ yɩ. A 'yɛr bʊ̃'yen 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ n chɛnɛ gɔ̃nɔ̃ yɩ ɩn ba be a yɩ sɛ̃ ɛ. Maa wa lɩɛb tɩ 'lɔ, n kʊ̃ bar a bala na dɔ̃ɔ maal a yelbier bɩɩ a bala na wa bɩɛr maal ɛ.

eng: Christ redeemed us from the curse of the Law by becoming a curse for us—because it is written, Everyone who is hung on a tree is cursed .
local: Kɩrɩsɩto faa sɩ naa a nɛbinãa pɔ 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ pɔ, na wʊ lɩɛb ɩ pɔ 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ kʊ sɩ, bojũu a sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, Pɔ 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ turo na a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na yɔɔl tɩɛ ju.

eng: He redeemed us so that the blessing of Abraham would come to the Gentiles through Christ Jesus, and that we would receive the promise of the Spirit through faith.
local: Wʊ faa sɩ naa ka a sʊ̃ʊfʊ ala a Abraham na nyɛ, ka a bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ mɩ na nyɛ tu a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ, ka a sɔɔfʊ jũu, ka sɩ nyɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ nɛtɩraa.

eng: Since you are demanding proof that Christ speaks through me, Christ isnt weak in dealing with you but shows his power among you.
local: bojũu yɩn ben bʊɔrɔ ɩka yɩ bɔ̃ɔ ɩka a Kɩrɩsɩto lɛ tu a n 'yɔ̃ɔ 'yɛrɛ. Waa bal na wʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ maal yele jaa faa a yɩ jie ɛ, tɩchɛ wʊ tara na kpɛ̃ɔ a yɩ pɔ.

eng: Certainly he was crucified because of weakness, but he lives by the power of God. Certainly we also are weak in him, but we will live together with him, because of Gods power that is directed toward you.
local: Bojũu yelmãɛ jaa lɛ, ban dɔ̃ɔ kpa wʊ a daa ju, wʊ bal naa, tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn kpɛ̃ɔ lɛ vɛ̃ naa ka wʊ nyɛ nyɛvʊr. Lɛ mɩ lɛ, sɩn mɩ bal naa a wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ, tɩchɛ a Nãaŋmɩn kpɛ̃ɔ jũu, sɩn ben be na sɩ tʊ̃nɔ̃ kʊrɔ yɩ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, Ill use an example from human experience. No one ignores or makes additions to a validated will.
local: N yɛɛr, vɛ̃ ka n de man a bɩbir jaa nyɛvʊr pɔ, nɩrɛ wa faa 'yɛr 'yɛr bin baar, nɩrɛ jaa maa lɛ 'yɛr 'yɛr dɔɔl ɛ, bɩɩ ŋmaa bar ɛ. Lɛ nɩ wʊ ju mɩ lɛ a nɛtɩraa.

eng: Examine yourselves to see if you are in the faith. Test yourselves. Dont you understand that Jesus Christ is in you? Unless, of course, you fail the test.
local: Yɩ bɛɛr a yɩ mãɛ kaa ɩka yɩ ben a sɔɔfʊ pɔ? Yɩ jʊʊr a yɩ mãɛ kaa. Yaa bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto ben a yɩ pɔ ɛ? Ka ala ba ɩ yɩ ɩn faa ɛ!.

eng: The promises were made to Abraham and to his descendant. It doesnt say, and to the descendants, as if referring to many rather than just one. It says, and to your descendant, who is Christ.
local: Nãaŋmɩn dɔ̃ɔ tɩr a wʊ nɛɛ kʊ Abraham nɩ a wʊ bir. A Nãaŋmɩn gãn ba 'yɛr ɩka bʊ̃bie ɛ, naa wiil fɩka biyɔɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ wʊ 'yɛr ɩka, A fʊ bir, Nɩbʊ̃'yen tɛɛ lɛ, 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a Kɩrɩsɩto.

eng: But I hope that you will realize that we dont fail the test.
local: N bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka yɩ bɔ̃ɔn naa sɩn ba ɩ faa ɛ.

eng: Im saying this: the Law, which came four hundred thirty years later, doesnt invalidate the agreement that was previously validated by God so that it cancels the promise.
local: Maa 'yɛr nɩ nyã ɩka a nɛbinãa na wa a yuom kɔɔr anaar dɔɔl lɩjɛr nɩ pie puor ba sɔ̃ɔ a nɛtɩraa 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr ɛ, na wʊ sɔ̃ɔ a nɛtɩraa ɛ.

eng: We pray to God that you dont do anything wrong, not because we want to appear to pass the test but so that you might do the right thing, even if we appear to fail.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã, Nãaŋmɩn lɛ sɩ sʊɔrɔ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka yɩ taa maala yeldɛɛr jaa ɛ. A ta ɩ ka a nɩbɛ nyɛ fɩka sɩn ba tara chɛfʊ ɛ, tɩchɛ ka a ɩ yɩn lɛ maala a lɛ na tor, ala ɩ gba fɩka nɩbɛ tɩɛr ɩka sɩ ɩn faa.

eng: If the inheritance were based upon the Law, it would no longer be from the promise. But God has given it graciously to Abraham through a promise.
local: Ala ɩ a bonsɩ ala sɩn wa nyɛ a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ ɩ naa a nɛbinãa tufʊ jũu lɛ, a ba lɛ ɩ a nɛtɩraa yele lɛ go ɛ, tɩchɛ an ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ lɛ wʊ de tɩr nɛɛ kʊ a Abraham.

eng: We cant do anything against the truth but only to help the truth.
local: Bojũu sɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ maal yele jaa tɩ põn a yelmãɛ bar ɛ, tɩchɛ a yelmãɛ yele tɛɛ lɛ sɩ ma maal.

eng: So why was the Law given? It was added because of offenses, until the descendant would come to whom the promise had been made. It was put in place through angels by the hand of a mediator.
local: Bo lɛ so Nãaŋmɩn na wa bɩɛr tɩr a nɛbinãa? Wʊ pʊɔn 'yɔ̃ɔ ban ba sɔɔ jũu tɩ tãn a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ a Bir 'lɔ wʊn tɩr a nɛɛ na wa. A malakasɩ lɛ waan a nɛbinãa nyã, tɩchɛ Mosesɩ na ɩ a 'ma'maala lɛ be a ba chara pɔ.

eng: We are happy when we are weak but you are strong. We pray for this: that you will be made complete.
local: Sɩ pʊɔ pɛl naa, a sɩn na bal, tɩchɛ ka a yɩn kpɛ̃mɛ, na sɩ sʊɔrɔ a Nãaŋmɩn kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka yɩ ta tara chɛfʊ ɛ.

eng: Now the mediator does not take one side; but God is one.
local: 'Ma'maala maa ara kʊ gbul bʊ̃'yen ɛ, tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn 'lɔ ɩn bʊ̃'yen tɛɛ.

eng: This is why Im writing these things while Im away. Im writing so that I wont need to act harshly when Im with you by using the authority that the Lord gave me. He gave it to me so that I could build you up, not tear you down.
local: A anyãna jũu lɛ so ɩn ba be a yɩ sɛ̃ ɛ, naa sɛɛrɛ a yele nyã kʊrɔ yɩ. Maa wa tɩ, ka n taa ɩrɛ wa de a n kpɛ̃ɔ tãna nɩ yɩ ɛ, a kpɛ̃ɔ a n Soro na kʊ ma, wʊ ɩka n de sʊ̃ʊn yɩ waa 'yɛr ɩka n de sɔ̃ɔn yɩ bar ɛ.

eng: So, is the Law against the promises of God? Absolutely not! If a Law had been given that was able to give life, then righteousness would in fact have come from the Law.
local: Ka a lɛ wiil ɩka a nɛbinãa sɔ̃ɔn a Nãaŋmɩn nɛtɩraa ala bar bɩɩ? Kai. Ala ɩ a nɛbinãa kʊrɔ na nɩrɛ nyɛvʊr, nɩrɛ jaa na ɩn nɩtor tu a nɛbinãa jũu.

eng: Finally, brothers and sisters, good-bye. Put things in order, respond to my encouragement, be in harmony with each other, and live in peace—and the God of love and peace will be with you.
local: Yɛɛr, yɩ chɩlɛ, na yɩ mʊɔrɔ chiine niem. A baaraa lɛɛ. A n sʊɔrfʊ ɩn sʊɔrɔ yɩ nɩ nyã. Yɩ tara tɩɛr bʊ̃'yen, na yɩ kpɩɛrɛ nɩ nyã'maar. Ka a Nãaŋmɩn na ɩ a nʊ̃fʊ sʊɔ nɩ a nyã'maar sʊɔ, na ben a yɩ sɛ̃.

eng: But scripture locked up all things under sin, so that the promise based on the faithfulness of Jesus Christ might be given to those who have faith.
local: Tɩchɛ a Nãaŋmɩn gãn 'yɛr ɩka a yelbier nyɔ̃ɔn a wɛr nɩbɛ ba jaa pɔɔ die pɔ, ka a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na tɩr a nɛɛ kʊ a bala na sɔɔ de a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto na nyɛ.

eng: Say hello to each other with a holy kiss. All of Gods people say hello to you.
local: Yɩ puor puor taa nɩ pʊpɛl.

eng: Before faith came, we were guarded under the Law, locked up until faith that was coming would be revealed.
local: Sɛrɛ tɩchɛ ka a sɔɔfʊ wa wa, sɩ dɔ̃ɔ ɩ a nɛbinãa nɩpɔɔrsɩ, naa pɔɔ sɩ tɩ tãn a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ a sɔɔfʊ na wa nyɩr.

eng: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, the love of God, and the fellowship of the Holy Spirit be with you all.
local: A Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ ba jaa puoro yɩ naa.

eng: so that the Law became our custodian until Christ so that we might be made righteous by faith.
local: Nãaŋmɩn kʊn a nɛbinãa sɔr ka wʊ na de sɩ nie chiin a Kɩrɩsɩto sɛ̃, ka sɩ na nyɛ faafʊ tu a sɔɔfʊ pɔ.

eng: But now that faith has come, we are no longer under a custodian.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã a sɔɔfʊ na wa tɩ wa, saa lɛ chɛnɛ be a nɛbinãa pĩlem ka wʊ kaara sɩ ɛ.

eng: You are all Gods children through faith in Christ Jesus.
local: Yɩ jaa ɩn Nãaŋmɩn bibiir yɩn sɔɔ Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto jũu.

eng: All of you who were baptized into Christ have clothed yourselves with Christ.
local: bojũu ba so yɩn a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ a Kɩrɩsɩto pɔ, ka wʊ pɔɔ yɩ fɩka fɔbaa.

eng: There is neither Jew nor Greek; there is neither slave nor free; nor is there male and female, for you are all one in Christ Jesus.
local: Ka bamɩne ba lɛ ɩ Juu nɩbɛ bɩɩ baa ɩ Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, ka bamɩne mɩ ɩ gbãgbaar bɩɩ ka bamɩne ɩ sumɩne ɛ, ka bamɩne ba lɛ ɩ dɔɔr bɩɩ mɩ ɩ pɔbɔ ɛ. Bojũu yɩ jaa ɩn bʊ̃'yen a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: Now if you belong to Christ, then indeed you are Abrahams descendants, heirs according to the promise.
local: Ala ɩ yɩ ɩn a Kɩrɩsɩto sofʊ, yɩ pãa ɩn a Abraham bir, na yɩ pʊɔ a Abraham na di a Nãaŋmɩn nãbonsɩ ala wʊn tɩr a nɛɛ kʊ wʊ.

eng: Im saying that as long as the heirs are minors, they are no different from slaves, though they really are the owners of everything.
local: A maa 'yɛr nɩ nyã, a bʊ̃dire bie gba wʊn ɩ bibile, tɩchɛ waa be wʊ yõ nɩ a gbãgbaa ɛ, an sɔɔ ɩ 'lɔ lɛ so a bonsɩ a jaa.

eng: However, they are placed under trustees and guardians until the date set by the parents.
local: Gugurbo nɩ a kpãɛn dẽme ma pire naa bie nyãna tɩ tãn a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ a sãa na 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: In the same way, when we were minors, we were also enslaved by this worlds system.
local: Lɛ nɩ wʊ ju mɩ lɛ, sɩn dɔ̃ɔ ɩ a bibiir, sɩ dɔ̃ɔ ɩ a wɛr gbãgbaar be kpãɛn bonsɩ pĩlem.

eng: But when the fulfillment of the time came, God sent his Son, born through a woman, and born under the Law.
local: Tɩchɛ a sɔ̃ɔ na wa ta, Nãaŋmɩn tʊ̃n a wʊ Bie ka pɔɔ dɔɔ wʊ tun a nɛbinãa pɔ.

eng: This was so he could redeem those under the Law so that we could be adopted.
local: ka wʊ na faa a bala na be a nɛbinãa pĩlem, ka sɩ tara sɔr ɩ a wʊ bibiir.

eng: Because you are sons and daughters, God sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts, crying, Abba, Father.
local: Yɩn ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn bibiir jũu, Nãaŋmɩn tʊ̃n a wʊ Bie Sɩɛ ka wʊ wa kpɛ a sɩ nyãn pɔ a sɩ ma bʊɔlɔ wʊ, sɩ Sãa.

eng: Therefore, you are no longer a slave but a son or daughter, and if you are his child, then you are also an heir through God.
local: A lɛ jũu, yaa lɛ chɛnɛ ɩ gbãgbaar ɛ, tɩchɛ yɩ ɩn a wʊ bibiir. Yɩn ɩ a wʊ bibiir baar, yɩ ɩn a wʊ bʊ̃dirbe.

eng: At the time, when you didnt know God, you were enslaved by things that arent gods by nature.
local: A kõro yɩn ba dɔ̃ɔ bɔ̃ɔ Nãaŋmɩn ɛ, yɩ dɔ̃ɔ ɩ gbãgbaar kʊ a tɩbɛ ala yɩn ba bɔ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: But now, after knowing God , how can you turn back again to the weak and worthless world system? Do you want to be slaves to it again?.
local: Tɩchɛ pʊ̃pãanyã yɩn bɔ̃ɔ Nãaŋmɩn bɩɩ Nãaŋmɩn na wa bɔ̃ɔ yɩ baar, ŋmɩnɛ lɛ a lɛ ɩ ka yɩ lɩɛb a yɩ tɩɛr 'yɔ̃ɔ a bonsɩ ala na bal ka a ba turfʊ ɩ pʊ̃sɔ̃ɔ? Yɩ chɛnɛ na bʊɔrɔ ɩka yɩ lɛ ɩ a ba gbãgbaar bɩɩ?.

eng: You observe religious days and months and seasons and years.
local: Yɩ tara na bɩbir pɛ̃nsɩ, nɩ chuur pɛ̃nfʊsɩ nɩ sɔ̃ɔsɩ nɩ yuomo pɛ̃nfʊsɩ.

eng: Im afraid for you! Perhaps my hard work for you has been for nothing.
local: Dãbãɛ kpɛ ma naa, ka n tɩɛr ɩka a maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ ɩn tʊ̃ a yɩ pɔ ɩn pɔrɔ.

eng: I beg you to be like me, brothers and sisters, because I have become like you! You havent wronged me.
local: N yɛɛr, n sʊɔrɔ yɩ naa, yɩ be a lɛ ɩn be, bojũu n mɩ dɔ̃ɔ be a lɛ yɩn be. Yaa maal ma yelbier jaa ɛ.

eng: You know that I first preached the gospel to you because of an illness.
local: Yɩ bɔ̃ɔn naa an ɩ baal jũu lɛ so ka n dɔ̃ɔ nie mʊɔl a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ kʊ yɩ.

eng: Though my poor health burdened you, you didnt look down on me or reject me, but you welcomed me as if I were an angel from God, or as if I were Christ Jesus!.
local: A maa baal gba na dɔ̃ɔ ɩ bɛɛr kaa kʊ yɩ tɩchɛ yaa tɔr ma bar ɛ, yɩ sɔɔn de ma Nãaŋmɩn malaka kaar, fɩka Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto.

eng: Where then is the great attitude that you had? I swear that, if possible, you would have dug out your eyes and given them to me.
local: Bo lɛ ɩ a yɩ chɩlfʊ? Ɩn tʊ̃ɔ 'yɛr yelmãɛ ɩka ala ɩ yɩn taa tʊ̃ɔ maal a lɛ, yɩn taa loor a yɩ nɩbie kʊ ma.

eng: So then, have I become your enemy by telling you the truth?.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã n ɩn a yɩ dɔ̃sʊɔ ɩn 'yɛr a yelmãɛ kʊ yɩ bɩɩ?.

eng: They are so concerned about you, though not with good intentions. Rather, they want to shut you out so that you would run after them.
local: A nɩbɛ bala na bʊɔrɔ ɩka yɩ wa a ba sɛ̃, a ba tɩɛr ba vɩɛl ɛ. A lɛ ban bʊɔrɔ lɛ ɩka yɩ ŋmaa yi na yɩ ɩ sʊ̃ʊfʊ kʊ ba.

eng: However, its always good to have people concerned about you with good intentions, and not just when Im there with you.
local: A vɩɛl na ɩka yɩ tara sʊ̃ʊfʊ sɔ̃ɔ, ala ɩ a lɛ yɩn tɩɛr vɩɛlɛ na, na yɩ ma maala a lɛ sɔ̃ɔ jaa, tɩchɛ ata ɩ fɩka maa wa be a yɩ sɛ̃ tɛɛ ɛ.

eng: My little children, Im going through labor pains again until Christ is formed in you.
local: N yɛɛr, a yɩ yele wõnõ ma naa fɩka pɔɔ na dɔɔrɔ bie kaar, tɩ ta a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ yɩn be a lɛ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto na be.

eng: But I wish I could be with you now and change how I sound, because Im at a loss about you.
local: N ta bʊɔrɔ naa fɩka n be a yɩ sɛ̃ a pʊ̃pãanyã naa ta lɩɛb a n kɔkɔr bojũu a yɩ yele wõnõ ma naa.

eng: Tell me—those of you who want to be under the Law—dont you listen to the Law?.
local: Yɩ 'yɛr kʊ ma, a yɩn bala na bʊɔrɔ ɩka yɩ ɩ a nɛbinãa turbo, yaa bɔ̃ɔ a lɛ a nɛbinãa na 'yɛrɛ bɩɩ?.

eng: Its written that Abraham had two sons, one by the slave woman and one by the free woman.
local: A sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, Abraham tara na bibiir ayi, a gbãgbaa pɔɔ lɛ dɔɔ a ãsʊɔ, ka a sumɩne mɩ dɔɔ a ãsʊɔ.

eng: The son by the slave woman was conceived the normal way, but the son by the free woman was conceived through a promise.
local: A bie 'lɔ na ɩ a gbãgbaa pɔɔ bie, ba dɔɔ wʊn tu a lɛ nɩsaal na guor, tɩchɛ a 'lɔ mɩ na ɩ a sumɩne bie, ba dɔɔ wʊn tun a Nãaŋmɩn nɛtɩraa pɔ.

eng: These things are an allegory: the women are two covenants. One is from Mount Sinai, which gives birth to slave children; this is Hagar.
local: Sɩn tʊ̃ɔ de a pɔbɔ banyãna ayi man nɛtɩrsɩ ayi. Haga lɛ dɔɔ a gbãgbaa bibiir. 'Lɔ lɛ ara kʊ a nɛtɩraa 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn na maal a Sinai Tɔ̃ɔ ju.

eng: Hagar is Mount Sinai in Arabia, and she corresponds to the present-day Jerusalem, because the city is in slavery with her children.
local: Haga ara na kʊ a Sinai Tɔ̃ɔ na be a Arabɩya fɩka a dɩna Jerusalɛm, bojũu 'lɔ lɛ dɔɔ a wʊ bibiir ka ba ɩ a nɛbinãa gbãgbaar.

eng: But the Jerusalem that is above is free, and she is our mother.
local: Tɩchɛ a Jerusalɛm 'lɔ na be a saaju son wʊ mãɛ, 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a sɩ ma.

eng: Its written: Rejoice, barren woman, you who have not given birth . Break out with a shout, you who have not suffered labor pains; because the woman who has been deserted will have many more children than the woman who has a husband .
local: Bojũu a sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, Tara pʊpɛl, a fʊ̃ʊ pɔɔ abbaa, na ba dɔɔ bibiir ɛ. Yiele ŋmiere nɩ chɛlsɩ, fʊ̃ʊ nyã na ba wõ bie dɔɔfʊ tuo ɛ. A pɔɔ nyã na ba dɔ̃ɔ dɔɔ ɛ, bojũu a wʊ bibiir waan gar a pɔɔ 'lɔ na kul sɩrɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, you are children of the promise like Isaac.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã, yɩn a n yɛɛr, yɩ kaara fɩka a Isaakɩ na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn nɛtɩraa bibiir.

eng: But just as it was then, so it is now also: the one who was conceived the normal way harassed the one who was conceived by the Spirit.
local: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ ban dɔɔ a bie 'lɔ a ãgãn pɔ, wʊ ge naa a bie 'lɔ ban dɔɔn a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ kpɛ̃ɔ. Lɛ mɩ lɛ a pʊ̃pãanyã.

eng: But what does the scripture say? Throw out the slave woman and her son, because the slave womans son wont share the inheritance with the free womans son .
local: Tɩchɛ bo lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn gãn 'yɛr? Dii a gbãgbapɔɔ nɩ a wʊ bie bar, bojũu a bie 'lɔ na ɩ a gbãgbapɔɔ bie kʊ 'mataa din a Nãaŋmɩn nãbonsɩ nɩ a bie 'lɔ a sumɩne na dɔɔ ɛ.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, we arent the slave womans children, but we are the free womans children.
local: A lɛ jũu n yɛɛr, saa ɩ a gbãgbaa pɔɔ bibiir ɛ, tɩchɛ sɩ ɩn a pɔɔ sumɩne bibiir.

eng: Christ has set us free for freedom. Therefore, stand firm and dont submit to the bondage of slavery again.
local: A Kɩrɩsɩto faa sɩ naa, ala lɛ so ka sɩ so sɩ mãɛ. A lɛ jũu, yɩ pãa ara kpɛ̃ɔ na yɩ ta lɛ de yɩ mãɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ gbãgbaa tʊ̃mɔ̃ pɔ ɛ.

eng: Look, I, Paul, am telling you that if you have yourselves circumcised, having Christ wont help you.
local: Yɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ a n 'yɛrbie jãnɛ. Maa Pɔɔl lɛ 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka ala ɩ yɩ ŋman a yɩ yɔɩ, a Kɩrɩsɩto kʊ tara tɔ̃nɔ̃ kʊ yɩ ɛ.

eng: Again I swear to every man who has himself circumcised that he is required to do the whole Law.
local: N lɛ bɔ̃ɔ 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ yelmãɛ jaa ɩka nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na ŋmaa yʊɔr tara na sɔr ɩka wʊ sɔɔ a Juu nɩbɛ nɛbinsɩ a jaa.

eng: You people who are trying to be made righteous by the Law have been estranged from Christ. You have fallen away from grace!.
local: A yɩn bala na bʊɔrɔ ɩka yɩ nyɛ faafʊ tu a nɛbinãa 'yɔ̃ɔ, ŋmaan yi a Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ, yɩ ŋmaan yi a Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ pɔ.

eng: We eagerly wait for the hope of righteousness through the Spirit by faith.
local: Tɩchɛ a sɔɔfʊ jũu, sɩ chɩlɛ na nɩbijɩɛ ɩka sɩn nyɛn a Sɩɛ tɩɛrtɔ jie yeltorsɩ.

eng: Being circumcised or not being circumcised doesnt matter in Christ Jesus, but faith working through love does matter.
local: A Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ, ka nɩrɛ ŋmaan yʊɔr bɩɩ ka waa ŋmaa yʊɔr ɛ, a ba wiil yele jaa ɛ. A yele 'lɔ na fɛr lɛ ɩka sɔɔfʊ na wile wʊ mãɛ tun nʊ̃fʊ pɔ.

eng: You were running well—who stopped you from obeying the truth?.
local: Yɩ dɔ̃ɔ joro jovɩla, an lɛ ŋmaa yɩ sɔr ɩka yɩ ta sɔɔ a yelmãɛ ɛ?.

eng: This line of reasoning doesnt come from the one who calls you.
local: A sʊɔrfʊ nyã bʊrɔ ba yi a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na bʊɔl yɩ sɛ̃ ɛ.

eng: A little yeast works through the whole lump of dough.
local: Fɩka dãbɩl bãlãa na ma vɛ̃ ka jɔ̃ vʊ̃, lɛ lɛ yele jaa pʊʊr bile ma sɔ̃ɔ yelyɔɔ.

eng: Im convinced about you in the Lord that you wont think any other way. But the one who is confusing you will pay the penalty, whoever that may be.
local: N tara na sɔɔfʊ a sɩ Soro 'yɔ̃ɔ ɩka yɩ kʊ tɩɛr tɩɛr yuo ɛ. A nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na vɛ̃ ka a yɩ tɩɛr va vire na nyɛn gefʊ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, if Im still preaching circumcision, why am I still being harassed? In that case, the offense of the cross would be canceled.
local: N yɛɛr, ba kʊ̃ ge ma ɛ, ala ɩ n 'yɛr yʊɔr ŋmaafʊ 'yɛr. A lɛ wiil ɩka a Yesu daa suur difʊ baar naa.

eng: I wish that the ones who are upsetting you would castrate themselves!.
local: Ɩn naa bʊɔrɔ na ɩka a bala na ba vɛ̃ ka a yɩ tɩɛr gãna jibʊ̃'yen nɩ a ba yʊɔr ŋmaafʊ yele ɛ, ba ŋme a ba mãɛ lãma.

eng: You were called to freedom, brothers and sisters; only dont let this freedom be an opportunity to indulge your selfish impulses, but serve each other through love.
local: Yɩn a n yɛɛr Nãaŋmɩn na bʊɔl ka yɩ so yɩ mãɛ, tɩchɛ yɩ ta de a yɩ mãɛ sofʊ nyãna maala nɩ yelbier ɛ, tɩchɛ yɩ de a mãɛ sofʊ nyã, na yɩ tʊ̃nɔ̃ kʊrɔ taa nɩ nʊ̃fʊ.

eng: All the Law has been fulfilled in a single statement: Love your neighbor as yourself .
local: A Nãaŋmɩn nɛbinsɩ a jaa, ba den sɛb 'yɛrbibʊ̃'yen tɛɛ, Nʊ̃ a fʊ tɔ sʊɔ a fʊ mãɛ kaar.

eng: But if you bite and devour each other, be careful that you dont get eaten up by each other!.
local: Yɩn maa wa sɔɔ dʊ̃nɔ̃ taa na yɩ ɔɔrɔ taa, yɩ bɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ sʊ̃, na yɩ taa sɔ̃ɔ taar ɛ.

eng: I say be guided by the Spirit and you wont carry out your selfish desires.
local: A lɛ jũu, n 'yɛrɛ na kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka yɩ kpɩɛrɛ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na wile, na yɩ ta maala a lɛ a ãgãn na bʊɔr ɛ.

eng: A persons selfish desires are set against the Spirit, and the Spirit is set against ones selfish desires. They are opposed to each other, so you shouldnt do whatever you want to do.
local: Bojũu bon 'lɔ a ãgãn na bʊɔrɔ ba ɩ a bon 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na bʊɔr ɛ. Bon 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ mɩ na bʊɔrɔ ba ɩ a bon 'lɔ a ãgãn mɩ na bʊɔr ɛ. A ma jɛɛrɛ nɩ taa na a na ŋmaa yɩ sɔr ka yɩ kʊ tʊ̃ɔ maal a lɛ yɩn bʊɔr ɛ.

eng: But if you are being led by the Spirit, you arent under the Law.
local: Tɩchɛ ala ɩ Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ ben a yɩ pɔ, yaa lɛ chɛnɛ ɩ a nɛbinãa gbãgbaar ɛ.

eng: The actions that are produced by selfish motives are obvious, since they include sexual immorality, moral corruption, doing whatever feels good.
local: A ãgãn bʊ̃bʊɔrsɩ ma gãn chãa pɔ, ala lɛ a anyãna sɛ̃fʊ, nɩ dɛɛr maalfʊ, nɩ jɔl dãɛn.

eng: idolatry, drug use and casting spells, hate, fighting, obsession, losing your temper, competitive opposition, conflict, selfishness, group rivalry.
local: nɩ tɩbɛ puorfʊ, nɩ sɔyele, nɩ 'laarfʊ, nɩ nɛchɩrɛ, nɩ nyuur, nɩ suur, nɩ mãɛ 'mɔɔfʊ, nɩ ootaa.

eng: jealousy, drunkenness, partying, and other things like that. I warn you as I have already warned you, that those who do these kinds of things wont inherit Gods kingdom.
local: nɩ nyuur, nɩ dãa buufʊ, nɩ dãnyugbulo, nɩ a ala na nyɛtãan a anyãna. N kpãana yɩ naa nɩtãa a lɛ ɩn dɔ̃ɔ de nie kpãa yɩ ɩka, a bala na maala a anyãna, kʊ̃ kpɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ ɛ.

eng: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness.
local: Tɩchɛ anyã lɛ ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ wɔ̃mɔ̃, nɩ nʊ̃fʊ, nɩ chɩlfʊ, nɩ nyã'maar, nɩ kãnyir, nɩ nɩbaal kaafʊ, nɩ vɩla maalfʊ, nɩ yelmãɛ.

eng: gentleness, and self-control. There is no law against things like this.
local: nɩ jomm ɩfʊ, nɩ mãɛ nyɔɔfʊ. Nɛɛ jaa ba be be gar a anyãna ɛ.

eng: Those who belong to Christ Jesus have crucified the self with its passions and its desires.
local: A bala na be a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ kpan a ba ãgãn nɩ a wʊ vɔlaa a daa ju.

eng: If we live by the Spirit, lets follow the Spirit.
local: Ala ɩ sɩn nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ lɛ 'mataa kpɩɛrɛ, yɩ vɛ̃ ka sɩn nɩ a 'lɔ 'mataa chiine.

eng: Lets not become arrogant, make each other angry, or be jealous of each other.
local: Yɩ ta vɛ̃ ka sɩ wile sɩ mãɛ na sɩ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ taa suur, tara taa nɩ nyuur ɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, if a person is caught doing something wrong, you who are spiritual should restore someone like this with a spirit of gentleness. Watch out for yourselves so you wont be tempted too.
local: N yɛɛr, ala ɩ ãsʊɔ wa maal yelbier bɔr a sɔɔfʊ sɔr pɔ, fʊ̃ʊ nyã na tara a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ, tɔɔ wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ a sɔr pɔ nɩ mãɛ siirfʊ. Tɩchɛ gu a fʊ mãɛ na fʊ mɩ taa lo a bɛɛr kaa pɔ ɛ.

eng: Carry each others burdens and so you will fulfill the law of Christ.
local: Yɩ tuoro taa a yelwõnsɩ. Yɩn wa ɩrɛ a lɛ, yɩ turo naa a Kɩrɩsɩto nɛɛ.

eng: If anyone thinks they are important when they arent, theyre fooling themselves.
local: Ala ɩ nɩrɛ wa tɩɛr ɩka wʊ ɩn bon kɔ̃ɔ a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ wʊn ba ɩ bon jaa ɛ, wʊ mãɛ lɛ wʊ bɛlɛ.

eng: Each person should test their own work and be happy with doing a good job and not compare themselves with others.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa wʊ ma bɛɛr a wʊ mãɛ tʊ̃mɔ̃ kaa tɩ na wʊ pãa chɩɛrɛ a nyãa na wʊ ta de a wʊ tʊ̃mɔ̃ mana nɩ a wʊ taaba tʊ̃mɔ̃ ɛ.

eng: Each person will have to carry their own load.
local: Bojũu ãsʊɔ na ma tuon a wʊ mãɛ tuor.

eng: Those who are taught the word should share all good things with their teacher.
local: A bala ban wiil, ba mɩ põ a ba bʊ̃tarsɩ kʊ a bala na wile ba.

eng: Make no mistake, God is not mocked. A person will harvest what they plant.
local: Yɩ ta bɛlɛ yɩ mãɛ ɛ, nɩrɛ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ la Nãaŋmɩn ɛ. A bon 'lɔ fʊn bʊr, 'lɔ lɛ fʊn ŋma.

eng: Those who plant only for their own benefit will harvest devastation from their selfishness, but those who plant for the benefit of the Spirit will harvest eternal life from the Spirit.
local: Fʊ̃ʊ nyã na bʊr a ala na ɩ a ãgãn vɔlaa, fʊn ŋman bʊ̃dɛbɛ. Ala ɩ fʊ bʊr a ala na pɛlɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ pʊɔ, na tun a Sɩɛ pɔ ŋma nyɛvʊr na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ.

eng: Lets not get tired of doing good, because in time well have a harvest if we dont give up.
local: Yɩ ta vɛ̃ ka sɩ bal nɩ vɩla maalfʊ ɛ, bojũu sɩn ŋma naa a ŋmafʊ sɔ̃ɔ, sɩn baa wa tɛɛ bar ɛ.

eng: So then, lets work for the good of all whenever we have an opportunity, and especially for those in the household of faith.
local: A lɛ jũu, sɩn wa nyɛ vuo, yɩ vɛ̃ a sɩ de maal vɩla kʊ nɩrɛ jaa, na sɩ mɔ̃ maal yɔɔ jaa kʊ a bala na be a sɔɔfʊ pɔ.

eng: Look at the large letters Im making with my own handwriting!.
local: Maa wa sɛɛrɛ nɩ a maa mãɛ nũu, nyɛ lɛ a sɛbie na ma ɩ a bɛrɛ jã.

eng: Whoever wants to look good by human standards will try to get you to be circumcised, but only so they wont be harassed for the cross of Christ.
local: A bala na bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba wile a ba mãɛ a ãgãn pɔ, bala lɛ bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba fɩr yɩ ka yɩ ŋma a yɔɩ. A yele 'lɔ yɔɔ ban ɩrɛ a lɛ, baa bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba nyɛ gefʊ a Kɩrɩsɩto daa jũu ɛ.

eng: Those who are circumcised dont observe the Law themselves, but they want you to be circumcised, so they can boast about your physical body.
local: A bala gba mɩ na ŋma a yɔɩ ba turo a nɛbinãa ɛ, tɩchɛ ba bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka yɩn yɩ ŋma a yɩ yɔɩ ka ba na chɩɛrɛ nyãa ɩka bala lɛ ŋma yɩ.

eng: But as for me, God forbid that I should boast about anything except for the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ. The world has been crucified to me through him, and I have been crucified to the world.
local: N taa ɩrɛ wa chɩɛrɛ nyãa bon jaa 'yɔ̃ɔ ka ala ba wa ɩ a sɩ Soro Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto daa ba lɛ ɛ. A lɛ jũu lɛ so, ka a wɛr nyã ju ba tara tɔ̃nɔ̃ kʊ ma ɛ, ka mɩ ba ɩ bon jaa kʊ a wɛr ɛ.

eng: Being circumcised or not being circumcised doesnt mean anything. What matters is a new creation.
local: Ala ɩ nɩrɛ ŋmaan yʊɔr bɩɩ ka 'lɔ ba ŋmaa yʊɔr ɛ, a ba wiil yele jaa ɛ, tɩchɛ a lɛ na sɛɛ lɛ ɩka nɩrɛ wʊ nyɛ dɔɔ paalaa.

eng: May peace and mercy be on whoever follows this rule and on Gods Israel.
local: A bala jaa na turo a sɔr nyãna, nyã'maar nɩ nɩbaal kaafʊ wʊ be a ba sɛ̃. Ba la lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn kaa ir.

eng: From now on, no one should bother me because I bear the marks of Jesus on my body.
local: An yi a pʊ̃pãanyã chiine, nɩrɛ jaa wʊ ta lɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ ma yele go ɛ, bojũu a Yesu fɛlsɩ ben a n ãgãn pɔ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, may the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.
local: N yɛɛr, ka a sɩ Soro Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto wãɛfʊ, a be a yɩ sɩɩr pɔ. A ɩ a lɛ.

eng: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by Gods will. To the holy and faithful people in Christ Jesus in Ephesus.
local: Maa Pɔɔl a Nãaŋmɩn na sɔɔ ka n ɩ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto nɩtʊ̃nãa lɛ sɛɛrɛ kʊrɔ a yɩn na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ be a Efesus na yɩ sɔɔ de a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto.

eng: Grace and peace to you from God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Wãɛfʊ nɩ nyã'maar na yi a sɩ Sãa Nãaŋmɩn nɩ a sɩ Soro Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto sɛ̃ a be a yɩ sɛ̃.

eng: Bless the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ! He has blessed us in Christ with every spiritual blessing that comes from heaven.
local: Pɛɛfʊ wʊ ta a Nãaŋmɩn, 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a sɩ Soro Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto Sãa. Wʊn de a Sɩɛ yelsʊ̃n a jaa na be a saaju na wʊ sʊ̃ʊn sɩ, sɩn be a Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ jũu.

eng: God chose us in Christ to be holy and blameless in Gods presence before the creation of the world.
local: Bojũu wʊ dɔ̃ɔ ir sɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ a Kɩrɩsɩto pɔ, tɩ na wʊ wa maal a wɛr ka sɩ na ɩ chɛchɛ na sɩ kʊ̃ tara chɛfʊ a wʊ niem ɛ, a wʊ nʊ̃fʊ jũu.

eng: God destined us to be his adopted children through Jesus Christ because of his love. This was according to his goodwill and plan.
local: Nãaŋmɩn den nie ir sɩ ka sɩ ɩ a wʊ bibiir tu a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ. A ɩ naa a wʊ bɔfʊ naa pɛl a wʊ pʊɔ.

eng: and to honor his glorious grace that he has given to us freely through the Son whom he loves.
