local: Nɩrɛ ba wa tara sɔɔfʊ ɛ, an ɩn tuo wʊn na tʊ̃ɔ pɛl Nãaŋmɩn pʊɔ, bojũu nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ jaa wa waara a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃, wʊ sɔɔn ɩka wʊ ben be, na wʊ mɩ ma yara a bala na mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir bʊɔrɔ wʊ.

eng: By faith Noah responded with godly fear when he was warned about events he hadnt seen yet. He built an ark to deliver his household. With his faith, he criticized the world and became an heir of the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Sɔɔfʊ lɛ vɛ̃ ka a Nowa tu a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ tɩ ba nyɛn nɩbir ɛ, tɩ na wʊ de dãbãɛ maal a daka bulomtum faan a wʊ yir dẽme nɩ a wʊ mãɛ. A wʊ sɔɔfʊ vɛ̃ naa ka wʊ 'yɛr nɛtuo 'yɔ̃ɔ a wɛr nɩbɛ ba jaa ban tɔr a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr jũu, tɩ na wʊ lɩɛb a yeltorsɩ bʊ̃dire a wʊ sɔɔfʊ jũu.

eng: By faith Abraham obeyed when he was called to go out to a place that he was going to receive as an inheritance. He went out without knowing where he was going.
local: Abraham sɔɔfʊ jũu, a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn na dɔ̃ɔ bʊɔl wʊ ɩka wʊ yi chen a jie 'lɔ na wa ɩ a wʊ sofʊ daar kɔ̃ɔ, wʊ tu naa a Nãaŋmɩn nɛɛ na wʊ chen, wʊn gba na ba bɔ̃ɔ a jie wʊn chiine ɛ.

eng: By faith he lived in the land he had been promised as a stranger. He lived in tents along with Isaac and Jacob, who were coheirs of the same promise.
local: Sɔɔfʊ vɛ̃ naa ka wʊ tɩ kpɩɛrɛ a nɛtɩraa tẽe 'lɔ pɔ ɩ sãan a be a paal 'lɔ pɔ. A gãma sɛɛr ala pɔ lɛ wʊ dɔ̃ɔ 'mataa kpɩɛrɛ nɩ a Isaakɩ nɩ a Jekɔb, na ba mɩ pʊɔ wʊ na wa so a jie 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn na tɩr a wʊ nɛɛ.

eng: He was looking forward to a city that has foundations, whose architect and builder is God.
local: Bojũu wʊ dɔ̃ɔ kaara niem ɩka wʊ nyɛ yir par ban sʊ̃ɔ, ka a Nãaŋmɩn mãɛ ɩ a mɩmɩɛrɛ.

eng: By faith even Sarah received the ability to have a child, though she herself was barren and past the age for having children, because she believed that the one who promised was faithful.
local: Sɔɔfʊ lɛ vɛ̃ ka a Abraham na dɔ̃ɔ kor ɩ danyɔ̃ɔ, ka a wʊ pɔɔ Saara mɩ ab, tɩ ba chɛnɛ na dɔɔ bie. Wʊ tara na sɔɔfʊ nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn nɛɛ wʊn tɩr kʊ wʊ.

eng: So descendants were born from one man . They were as many as the number of the stars in the sky and as countless as the grains of sand on the seashore.
local: Ka a dabʊ̃'yen nyã na kor bʊ̃tul ta kũu, tɩ wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔn par ka ba waa fɩka a ŋmɛrbie na be a saaju, na ba kaara man nɛɛ bire kaar fʊn kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ sɔr ɛ.

eng: All these people died in faith without receiving the promises, but they saw the promises from a distance and welcomed them. They confessed that they were strangers and immigrants on earth.
local: A nɩbɛ banyãna sɔɔn Nãaŋmɩn tɩ tãn a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ ban kpi. Baa nyɛ a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na dɔ̃ɔ tɩr a nɛɛ kʊ ba ɛ, nɩdaa lɛ ba gbã nyãa tɩ sɔɔ de, na ba sɔɔ ɩka sãanba nɩ nɩyʊɔrsɩ lɛ ba ɩ ka a wɛr pɔ.

eng: People who say this kind of thing make it clear that they are looking for a homeland.
local: A nɩbɛ banyãna na 'yɛr a anyãna, wiil ɩka ba kaara na bʊɔrɔ a tẽe 'lɔ na ɩ a ba mãɛ tʊɔr.

eng: If they had been thinking about the country that they had left, they would have had the opportunity to return to it.
local: Ala ɩ ba dɔ̃ɔ tɩɛrɛ a paal 'lɔ ban yi, ban taa tʊ̃ɔn lɛ lɩɛb kul a be.

eng: But at this point in time, they are longing for a better country, that is, a heavenly one. Therefore, God isnt ashamed to be called their God—he has prepared a city for them.
local: Tɩ lɛ ba lɛ ɛ, ba dɔ̃ɔ tɩɛrɛ a paal 'lɔ na gar a be, na be a saaju. Ala lɛ so ka a vã ba kʊrɔ wʊ wʊn ɩ a ba Nãaŋmɩn ɛ, bojũu wʊ chɔɔr a tẽe kʊ ba.

eng: By faith Abraham offered Isaac when he was tested. The one who received the promises was offering his only son.
local: Sɔɔfʊ vɛ̃ naa ka Abraham, a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn na bɛɛr wʊ kaa ka wʊ sɔɔ de a wʊ bidaba Isaakɩ ka wʊ ɩ a bɔɔrlo bon a wʊn lon a bɔɔr. 'Lɔ lɛ dɔ̃ɔ sɔɔ de a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na tɩr a nɛɛ kʊ wʊ, tɩ na wʊ sɔɔ de a wʊ bidabʊ̃'yen tɛɛ nyã na lon a bɔɔr.

eng: He had been told concerning him, Your legitimate descendants will come from Isaac. .
local: Nãaŋmɩn gba na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ ɩka, Isaakɩ lɛ na 'yɔ̃ɔ a fʊ par, lɛ ɩn tɩr a nɛɛ kʊ fʊ.

eng: He figured that God could even raise him from the dead. So in a way he did receive him back from the dead.
local: Abraham tɩɛr bɔ̃ɔn ɩka Nãaŋmɩn na tʊ̃ɔn sãɩ kũu, ka a nyɛtãan fɩka wʊ nyɛn a Isaakɩ na yi a kũu pɔ.

eng: By faith Isaac also blessed Jacob and Esau concerning their future.
local: Sɔɔfʊ vɛ̃ naa ka Isaakɩ 'yɛr nɛvɩla 'yɔ̃ɔ Jekɔb nɩ a Esau a ba nɩdaar kɔ̃ɔ yele.

eng: By faith Jacob blessed each of Josephs sons as he was dying and bowed in worship over the head of his staff. .
local: Sɔɔfʊ lɛ vɛ̃ ka a Jekɔb na ta a kũu, wʊ 'yɛr nɛvɩla 'yɔ̃ɔ a Josefʊ bibiir ba jaa tɩ na wʊ guun lan a wʊ dãgbal diiraa tɩ puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan.

eng: By faith Joseph recalled the exodus of the Israelites at the end of his life, and gave instructions about burying his bones.
local: Sɔɔfʊ vɛ̃ naa ka a Josefʊ kũu na wa gbʊrɔ, tɩ wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a Isɩral nɩbɛ a Ijipitɩ tẽe pɔ yifʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, na wʊ man ba a lɛ ban na ɩ nɩ a wʊ kɔbɔ.

eng: By faith Moses was hidden by his parents for three months when he was born, because they saw that the child was beautiful and they werent afraid of the kings orders.
local: Sɔɔfʊ nyã vɛ̃ naa ka a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ ban dɔɔ a Mosesɩ a wʊ dɔɔrbɔ sɔɔl wʊn bar ŋmɛrsɩ ata. Bojũu ba nyɛn wʊn ɩ bivɩla, na baa joro a nãa binfʊ ɛ.

eng: By faith Moses refused to be called the son of Pharaohs daughter when he was grown up.
local: Sɔɔfʊ nyã vɛ̃n a lɛ a Mosesɩ na wa nʊ̃ɔ, waa sɔɔ ka ba bʊɔlɔ wʊ a nãkpɛ̃ɛ Faaro bipɔɔ bie ɛ.

eng: He chose to be mistreated with Gods people instead of having the temporary pleasures of sin.
local: Wʊ kãan ir ban na 'mataa gen wʊ nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ gar wʊn na di a yelbier nʊ̃ɔ bãlãa tɛɛ.

eng: He thought that the abuses he suffered for Christ were more valuable than the treasures of Egypt, since he was looking forward to the reward.
local: Wʊ nyɛn wʊn na di dɔɔyɛ a Kɩrɩsɩto jũu ka a tara tɔ̃nɔ̃ gar a Ijipitɩ tẽe pɔ nãbomo. Bojũu daar kɔ̃ɔ sãyar lɛ wʊ mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir kaara.

eng: By faith he left Egypt without being afraid of the kings anger. He kept on going as if he could see what is invisible.
local: Sɔɔfʊ vɛ̃ naa ka a Mosesɩ yi be a Ijipitɩ tẽe pɔ, tɩ ba joro a nãa suur ɛ. Wʊ tara na kãnyir, bojũu wʊ nyɛn a 'lɔ a nɩbir na maa nyɛ ɛ.

eng: By faith he kept the Passover and the sprinkling of blood, in order that the destroyer could not touch their firstborn children.
local: Sɔɔfʊ lɛ wʊ de tun a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na 'yɛr a Gõl Bar Bɩbir 'yɛr, na wʊ dɔ̃ɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ ka ba bol a jãɩ a ba dɔ̃dɔ̃ ɛ, ka a sɔ̃ɔfʊ 'lɔ na waara ba tʊ̃ɔ sɩɩr a Isɩral nɩbɛ bidadɔ̃n ɛ.

eng: By faith they crossed the Red Sea as if they were on dry land, but when the Egyptians tried it, they were drowned.
local: Sɔɔfʊ vɛ̃ naa ka a Man Jɩɛ var lɩɛb sɔr, ka a Ijipitɩ dẽme mɩ na wa ta nyɛ a lɛ na ba kpɛ ka a man di ba jaa.

eng: By faith Jerichos walls fell after the people marched around them for seven days.
local: Sɔɔfʊ lɛ vɛ̃ ka a Jeriko dachin kpɛ̃ɛ 'lɔ wʊ jaa gel lo, a lɛ a Isɩral nɩbɛ na wa gʊɔr a tẽe wʊ jaa a ber ayopõi tuor.

eng: By faith Rahab the prostitute wasnt killed with the disobedient because she welcomed the spies in peace.
local: Sɔɔfʊ lɛ dɔ̃ɔ faa a sɛ̃sɛ̃ pɔɔ 'lɔ ban bʊɔlɔ a Worahab, bojũu wʊ dɔ̃ɔ sɔɔ de a bala ban tʊ̃ ɩka ba tɩ jʊʊr a tẽe kaa. Waa dɔ̃ɔ pʊɔ a bawõnsɩ pɔ kpi ɛ.

eng: What more can I say? I would run out of time if I told you about Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets.
local: N lɛ man bo go? N ba tara jaa na man lɛ a Gɩdɩyɔn nɩ Barakɩ nɩ Samson nɩ Jefeta nɩ a Nãa David nɩ Samuwɛl nɩ a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ na tʊ̃ 'yɛr ɛ.

eng: Through faith they conquered kingdoms, brought about justice, realized promises, shut the mouths of lions.
local: bala lɛ de sɔɔfʊ jɛb na ba tʊ̃ɔn nãmɩne, tɩ maal a lɛ na sɛɛ, nyɛn a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na tɩr a nɛɛ. Bala lɛ mɩ dɔ̃ɔ kpal a jubɛrɛ nɛɛr.

eng: put out raging fires, escaped from the edge of the sword, found strength in weakness, were mighty in war, and routed foreign armies.
local: na ba kpĩin a bũu dagel, tɩ na ba jo faa a ba mãɛ a sɔbɛrɛ ala ban taa kʊn ba. Bala lɛ dɔ̃ɔ ɩ a nɩbaalsɩ, na ba lɩɛb nɩkpãɛn, na ba lɩɛb gãdaar a jɛɛr pɔ tʊ̃ɔ a ba dɔ̃dẽme.

eng: Women received back their dead by resurrection. Others were tortured and refused to be released so they could gain a better resurrection.
local: Pɔbɔ mɩ lɛ nyɛn a ba nɩbɛ na dɔ̃ɔ kpi tɩ lɛ lɩɛb ir ɩ vʊɛ. Ba gen bamɩne putuger. Bala ta sɔɔ, ba kʊ̃ naa ge ba ɛ, tɩ ba tɔr tɩ chɛnɛ ara a ba sɔɔfʊ bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba wa nyɛ a kũu 'lɔ pɔ irfʊ na ɩ gar a nyã.

eng: But others experienced public shame by being taunted and whipped; they were even put in chains and in prison.
local: Ba lan bamɩne na ba fɔb ba, tɩ lũn bamɩne mɩ nɩ bɔ̃jɔrsɩ tɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ pɔɔfʊ die pɔ.

eng: They were stoned to death, they were cut in two, and they died by being murdered with swords. They went around wearing the skins of sheep and goats, needy, oppressed, and mistreated.
local: Ba lɔb bamɩne nɩ kusɩbɛ kʊ, tɩ ma chɛ bamɩne mɩ var a yi, tɩ kʊn bamɩne mɩ naa sʊɔ. Piir gãma nɩ bʊʊr gãkpoor lɛ bamɩne mɩ ma dɔ̃ɔ su yʊɔrɔ nɩ na ba ɩ nɔ̃n dẽme, ka ba tara ba gere tɩ sɩlɛ ba a pɔrɔ jaa a lɛ nɛ.

eng: The world didnt deserve them. They wandered around in deserts, mountains, caves, and holes in the ground.
local: Ka a wɛr nyã ba ta sɛɛ ɩka wʊ tara a nɩbɛ banyãna ɛ, ka ba bãra yʊɔrɔ be a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ nɩ a tɔ̃n jur, kpɩɛrɛ a tɔ̃n bɔɔr pɔ nɩ a tũlotuursɩ bɔɔr ala ban ya a tẽe.

eng: All these people didnt receive what was promised, though they were given approval for their faith.
local: A nɩbɛ banyãna ba jaa nyɛn dãasɩvɩla a ba sɔɔfʊ jũu, tɩchɛ a bonsɩ ala a Nãaŋmɩn na dɔ̃ɔ tɩr a nɛɛ ba ãsʊɔ jaa ba nyãa ɛ.

eng: God provided something better for us so they wouldnt be made perfect without us.
local: Nãaŋmɩn guor yelvɩɛl na gar a anyã na wʊ gun sɩ, ka wʊ na de sɩ 'matãan ba maal yele a jaa ka a jãtaa.

eng: So then, with endurance, lets also run the race that is laid out in front of us, since we have such a great cloud of witnesses surrounding us. Lets throw off any extra baggage, get rid of the sin that trips us up.
local: A lɛ jũu, a nɩyɔɔ banyãna na ŋmaa jil sɩ a julõjuur kaar dire a dãasɩɛ, yɩ ɩ a sɩ lɔb bon jaa na ŋmaa sɩ sɔr bar nɩ a yelbier ala na maa yaara tɩ bãr sɩ lɔb ɛ, tɩ na sɩ mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir joro nɩ kãnyir tɩ ta a jie ban tʊ̃ɔ gun sɩ.

eng: and fix our eyes on Jesus, faiths pioneer and perfecter. He endured the cross, ignoring the shame, for the sake of the joy that was laid out in front of him, and sat down at the right side of Gods throne.
local: Yɩ ɩ a sɩ fur a sɩ nɩbie 'yɔ̃ɔ a Yesu na sɩ kaara wʊ jaa tãɩɩ. 'Lɔ lɛ de a nie be a sɩ sɔɔfʊ sɔr pɔ, tɩ baar. 'Lɔ lɛ a pʊpɛl 'lɔ na be a wʊ niem, wʊ din kãnyir kpi be a daa ju, na wʊ sɔɔ a vã. Wʊ pãa tɩ jɛ̃n be a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ a nũsʊ̃ɔ jie.

eng: Think about the one who endured such opposition from sinners so that you wont be discouraged and you wont give up.
local: Yɩ tɩɛr a wʊ yele a lɛ wʊn dɔ̃ɔ tara a kãnyir, nɩ a lɛ a yelbe-ɩrbɛ na dɔ̃ɔ jɛɛrɛ nɩ wʊ, yɩ tɩɛr a lɛ, ka a yɩ ãgãma taa sal a yɩ tɩɛr sɔ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: In your struggle against sin, you havent resisted yet to the point of shedding blood.
local: A yɩ mɔ̃fʊ pɔ, yɩn jɛɛrɛ nɩ a yelbier, yaa jɛb ta a jie 'lɔ sɛrɛ a yɩ jãɩ na yi ɛ.

eng: and you have forgotten the encouragement that addresses you as sons and daughters: My child, dont make light of the Lords discipline or give up when you are corrected by him, .
local: Yɩ ta ĩin a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr 'yɔ̃ɔ yɩ a ãkpãɛn nɩtãa a wʊ bibiir ɛ, N bie, ta de dekpele 'yɔ̃ɔ a sɩ Soro kpãafʊ pɔ ɛ, na fʊ ta vɛ̃ ka a fʊ pʊɔ sɔ̃ɔ a lɛ wʊn gɔ̃nɔ̃ fʊ ɛ.

eng: because the Lord disciplines whomever he loves, and he punishes every son or daughter whom he accepts. .
local: bojũu a sɩ Soro ma kpãana na a bala wʊn nʊ̃, na wʊ ma tɔɔ ãsʊɔ jaa toor wʊn sɔɔ de maal a wʊ bie.

eng: Bear hardship for the sake of discipline. God is treating you like sons and daughters! What child isnt disciplined by his or her father?.
local: Yɩ tara kãnyir a kpãafʊ nyã pɔ. Nãaŋmɩn maala yɩ naa a wʊ bibiir. Bojũu bibuor lɛ be be, ka a wʊ sãa maa tɔɔ wʊ toor ɛ?.

eng: But if you dont experience discipline, which happens to all children, then you are illegitimate and not real sons and daughters.
local: Ala ɩ ãsʊɔ jaa ba wa nyɛ a toor tɔɔfʊ a wʊ bibiir kaar ɛ, ka lɛ sãa yir bibiir lɛ ba ɩ.

eng: Whats more, we had human parents who disciplined us, and we respected them for it. How much more should we submit to the Father of spirits and live?.
local: Sɩ jaa lɛ tara a sãamɩne ka ba ma kpãa sɩ, tɩ sɩ chɛnɛ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ba. Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ a sɩ kʊ̃ pãa siir sɩ mãɛ kʊ a 'lɔ na ɩ a sɩ sɩɩr nɩ a nyɛvʊr Sãa gar a lɛ ɛ!.

eng: Our human parents disciplined us for a little while, as it seemed best to them, but God does it for our benefit so that we can share his holiness.
local: A sɩ sãamɩne ma kpãa sɩn jaa bãlãa tun a lɛ ban tɩɛr ɩka a ɩ naa. Tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn ma kpãa sɩ naa, ka sɩ ɩ nɩsʊ̃n, a sɩn nɩ 'lɔ 'mataa põ a wʊ chɛchɛ.

eng: No discipline is fun while it lasts, but it seems painful at the time. Later, however, it yields the peaceful fruit of righteousness for those who have been trained by it.
local: Kpãafʊ ba be be na ɩ nʊ̃ɔ a pielfʊ daar ɛ, tɩchɛ a ma ɩn tuo. A puor lɛ a ma pãa wɔ̃ torfʊ, nɩ nyã'maar kʊ a bala na sɔɔ tu.

eng: So strengthen your drooping hands and weak knees!.
local: A lɛ jũu, yɩ ir ãgãn sal yin a yɩ nuur nɩ a yɩ gbɛɛ pɔ bar.

eng: Make straight paths for your feet so that if any part is lame, it will be healed rather than injured more seriously.
local: Tɩ na yɩ chɔɔr a sɔr a wʊ jaa jãtaa a yɩ tufʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ, ka a gbɛrsɩ sa na ba tʊ̃ɔ chiine.

eng: Pursue the goal of peace along with everyone—and holiness as well, because no one will see the Lord without it.
local: Yɩ mɔ̃ lɛ jaa yɩn na tʊ̃ɔ ɩ, ka yɩn nɩ nɩrɛ jaa kpɩɛrɛ nɩ nyã'maar, na yɩ ɩ chɛchɛ. Nɩrɛ ba wa ɩ chɛchɛ ɛ, wʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ nyɛ a sɩ Soro ɛ.

eng: Make sure that no one misses out on Gods grace. Make sure that no root of bitterness grows up that might cause trouble and pollute many people.
local: Yɩ kaara sʊ̃, ka a yɩ ãsʊɔ jaa taa vɩɛ a Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ bar ɛ, bɩɩ ka a nyɩɩr tuo kɔ̃ɔ taa tɔ yi na wʊ dam yele 'yɔ̃ɔ nɩbɛ ka a ba yɔɔ jie taa sɔ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: Make sure that no one becomes sexually immoral or ungodly like Esau. He sold his inheritance as the oldest son for one meal.
local: Yɩ kaara ka a yɩ ãsʊɔ jaa taa ɩ sɛ̃sɛ̃yele ɛ, bɩɩ kaara fɩka a Esau na ba dɔ̃ɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr ɛ, na wʊ de a wʊ kpɛ̃ɛ ɩfʊ nɩ a wʊ difʊ jie tɛɛn bʊ̃diraa gbɛbʊ̃'yen tɛɛ na wʊ di.

eng: You know that afterward, when he wanted to inherit the blessing, he was rejected because he couldnt find a way to change his heart and life, though he looked for it with tears.
local: A ala puor, yɩ bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka wʊ lɛ wa bʊɔrɔ naa a wʊ difʊ jie, ka a sãa tɔr wʊ bar, a wʊ mɩ kʊ̃ lɛ tʊ̃ɔ lɩɛb go ɛ. Wʊ gba wa kõn nɩbikʊ̃ɔ nɩ a yele nyã, tɩ waa lɛ nyɛ a sʊ̃ʊfʊ go ɛ.

eng: You havent drawn near to something that can be touched: a burning fire, darkness, shadow, a whirlwind.
local: Yɩn 'lɔ ba wa a tɔ̃ɔ nyã par yɩn na tʊ̃ɔ sɩɩr ɛ, a 'lɔ na ma dɔ̃ɔ lɩrɛ a bũu, bɩɩ ka a jie ma sɔb lii ka a sajakpuro furo.

eng: a blast of a trumpet, and a sound of words that made the ones who heard it beg that there wouldnt be one more word.
local: bɩɩ ka ɩlɛ pɛɛlɛ 'yɛrɛ nɩ a nɩrɛ kɔkɔr, bɩɩ ban wõ a kɔkɔr nyã, ba sʊɔr naa ɩka a 'yɛrbie a ta lɛ 'yɛr go ɛ.

eng: They couldnt stand the command, If even a wild animal touches the mountain, it must be stoned. .
local: Bojũu baa dɔ̃ɔ tʊ̃ɔnɔ̃ tara a lɛ a 'yɛr na kpãkpãana ba 'yɛrɛ ɩka, Ala ɩ dʊ̃ɔ gba wa sɩɩr a tɔ̃ɔ, ba lɔb wʊ nɩ kʊsɩbɛ kʊ.

eng: The sight was so frightening that Moses said, Im terrified and shaking.
local: Ka a jie dɔ̃ɔ ɩrɛ dãbãɛ wʊ jaa, ka Mosesɩ wa tɩ 'yɛr, N mɩrɛ naa nɩ dãbãɛ.

eng: But you have drawn near to Mount Zion, the city of the living God, heavenly Jerusalem, to countless angels in a festival gathering.
local: Tɩchɛ, yɩn 'lɔ wa naa a Jiyon Tɔ̃ɔ par na ɩ a saaju Jerusalɛm, be lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbir nyã kpɩɛrɛ. Yɩ wa lɔ̃ɔn naa a malakasɩ turo turo na lɔ̃ɔ taa nɩ pʊpɛl.

eng: to the assembly of Gods firstborn children who are registered in heaven, to God the judge of all, to the spirits of the righteous who have been made perfect.
local: nɩ a pupuorbiir bala na ɩ a bidɔ̃ɔ, bala yoe lɛ sɛb a be a saaju. Yɩn wa naa a Nãaŋmɩn 'lɔ na tɔɔr a nɩbɛ ba jaa 'yɛr sɛ̃, nɩ a 'lɔ na maal a torfʊ dẽme sɩɩr ka a ɩ chɛchɛ.

eng: to Jesus the mediator of the new covenant, and to the sprinkled blood that speaks better than Abels blood.
local: nɩ a Yesu na ɩ a nɛtɩr paalaa nyã 'ma'maala na mɩr a wʊ jãɩ ka a 'yɛrɛ naa gar a Abel tʊɔr.

eng: See to it that you dont resist the one who is speaking. If the people didnt escape when they refused to listen to the one who warned them on earth, how will we escape if we reject the one who is warning from heaven?.
local: Yɩ kaara sʊ̃ na yɩ taa tɔr a Nãaŋmɩn na 'yɛrɛ ɛ. Ala ɩ a bala wʊn dɔ̃ɔ kpãa faa ka a wɛr pɔ ba chãɛ ɛ, ŋmɩnɛ an kaara kʊ a sɩn, ala ɩ sɩ lɩɛb puor 'yɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn na kpãana sɩ yire a saaju?.

eng: His voice shook the earth then, but now he has made a promise: Still once more I will shake not only the earth but heaven also .
local: A sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ a Nãaŋmɩn kɔkɔr dam a tẽe wʊ jaa, tɩchɛ pʊ̃pãanyã 'lɔ wʊ tɩr nɛɛ ɩka, Daar kɔ̃ɔ go, a ba ɩ a tẽe lɛ ɩn dam ɛ, tɩchɛ ɩn dam a saaju wʊ jaa.

eng: The words still once more reveal the removal of what is shaken—the things that are part of this creation—so that what isnt shaken will remain.
local: A 'yɛrbir nyã wʊn 'yɛr ɩka, daar kɔ̃ɔ go, a par lɛ ɩka wʊn ir a bonsɩ ala na ɩ a bʊ̃maalsɩ na dam bar, tɩchɛ a ala na kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ dam ɛ, ka ala ara.

eng: Therefore, since we are receiving a kingdom that cant be shaken, lets continue to express our gratitude. With this gratitude, lets serve in a way that is pleasing to God with respect and awe.
local: A lɛ jũu, sɩn na ben tara a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ nyã na ba tara damfʊ ɛ, yɩ vɛ̃ ka a nʊ̃mɔ̃ sɩ, a sɩ dãna Nãaŋmɩn lɛ wʊn bʊɔrɔ nɩ 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ nɩ dãbãɛ.

eng: because our God really is a consuming fire.
local: bojũu a sɩ Nãaŋmɩn nyã ɩn bũu na dire.

eng: Keep loving each other like family.
local: Yɩ mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir na yɩ nʊ̃nɔ̃ taa nɩtãa yɛɛr.

eng: Dont neglect to open up your homes to guests, because by doing this some have been hosts to angels without knowing it.
local: Yɩ taa ɩrɛ wa ĩin sãanba defʊ bar ɛ, bojũu nɩbɛ bamɩne dɔ̃ɔ de sãanba maal ba vɩla, tɩ baa bɔ̃ɔn ɩka malakasɩ lɛ ɛ.

eng: Remember prisoners as if you were in prison with them, and people who are mistreated as if you were in their place.
local: Yɩ tɩɛr a bala ban nyɔɔr pɔɔ a die pɔ fɩka yɩ mɩ pʊɔ ban be a pɔɔfʊ jie, nɩ a bala ban gere fɩka yɩ mɩ pʊɔ ban dire a dɔɔyɛ.

eng: Marriage must be honored in every respect, with no cheating on the relationship, because God will judge the sexually immoral person and the person who commits adultery.
local: Yɩ jaa, yɩ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a kultaa, ka a yɩ gaa jie taa tara chɛfʊ ɛ, bojũu Nãaŋmɩn na tɔɔr a bala jaa na sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a kultaa nɩ a sɛ̃sɛ̃nbɛ 'yɛr.

eng: Your way of life should be free from the love of money, and you should be content with what you have. After all, he has said, I will never leave you or abandon you .
local: Yɩ tɔɔ yɩ mãɛ yin a libie nʊ̃fʊ pɔ, na yɩ vɛ̃ ka a bãlãa jaa yɩn tara sɛɛn yi, bojũu Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr naa a wʊ gãn pɔ ɩka, N kʊ̃ wa bar fʊ jaa ɛ, n kʊ̃ wa tɔr fʊ jaa bar ɛ.

eng: This is why we can confidently say, The Lord is my helper, and I wont be afraid. What can people do to me? .
local: Ka a sɩ pãa na 'la 'yɛr ɩka, A n Soro lɛ ɩ a n sʊ̃nɔ̃, ka n kʊ̃ joro dãbãɛ ɛ. Bo lɛ nɩrɛ na ɩ ma.

eng: Remember your leaders who spoke Gods word to you. Imitate their faith as you consider the way their lives turned out.
local: Yɩ tɩɛrtɔ a yɩ nɩdierbe na dɔ̃ɔ man a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr kʊ yɩ. Yɩ tɩɛr a lɛ ban dɔ̃ɔ be tɩ na yɩ jãn a ba sɔɔfʊ.

eng: Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever!.
local: Lɛ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto na be a jãa, lɛ lɛ wʊ chɛnɛ a dɩna na wʊn chɛnɛ a lɛ nɛ bɩbir nɩ bɩbir jaa.

eng: Dont be misled by the many strange teachings out there. Its a good thing for the heart to be strengthened by grace rather than by food. Food doesnt help those who live in this context.
local: Yɩ taa ɩrɛ wa vɛ̃ ka a wiil sãan jaa wa tɔɔ yɩ yin ka yɩ tɩ bɔr ɛ, a vɩɛlɛ naa kaa sɩ nyãa nyɛ kpɛ̃ɔ yi a Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ pɔ, ka a ta ɩ a bʊ̃diir difʊ na ba tara tɔ̃nɔ̃ kʊ a bala na dire yaa ɛ.

eng: We have an altar, and those who serve as priests in the meeting tent dont have the right to eat from it.
local: Tɩchɛ sɩn tara na a bɔɔr lofʊ jie, be a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlorbo bala na tʊ̃nɔ̃ a be ba tara sɔr na di ɛ.

eng: The blood of the animals is carried into the holy of holies by the high priest as an offering for sin, and their bodies are burned outside the camp.
local: A ba bɔɔrlo nɩkpɛ̃ɛ ma den a dʊ̃n ala jãɩ kpɛ̃n be a dinie sʊɔ na ɩ a Chɛchɛ Wʊ Jaa Chɛchɛ Jie ka a tɩ ɩ a yelbier vɛ̃ kʊfʊ bon, tɩchɛ ka ba de a nʊ̃nɔ̃ yin a tẽe dãpuor tɩ chʊm bar kaa di.

eng: And so Jesus also suffered outside the city gate to make the people holy with his own blood.
local: Lɛ mɩ lɛ a Yesu di a dɔɔyɛ a tẽe yõo dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ, ka a wʊ jãɩ maal a wʊ nɩbɛ ka ba ɩ chɛchɛ.

eng: So now, lets go to him outside the camp, bearing his shame.
local: Yɩ mɩ ɩ a sɩ yi tɩ pʊɔ wʊ be a yõo na sɩ di a vã 'lɔ wʊn dɔ̃ɔ di.

eng: We dont have a permanent city here, but rather we are looking for the city that is still to come.
local: Bojũu a tẽe nyã sɩn kpɩɛrɛ kʊ̃ kor ɛ, tɩchɛ a sɩ kaara a tẽe 'lɔ na wa.

eng: So lets continually offer up a sacrifice of praise through him, which is the fruit from our lips that confess his name.
local: Tu a Yesu nyã jie, a lɛ jũu yɩ ɩ a sɩ ma pʊrɔ a wʊ yuor na ɩ pɛɛfʊ ma yire a sɩ nɛgãma pɔ, naa ɩ bɔɔrlo bonsɩ kʊrɔ Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: Dont forget to do good and to share what you have because God is pleased with these kinds of sacrifices.
local: Yɩ taa ɩrɛ wa ĩin a vɩla maalfʊ bar ɛ, na yɩ ma lɔ̃ɔ põn a yɩ taaba, bojũu a bɔɔrlo bonsɩ anyãna lɛ pɛlɛ a Nãaŋmɩn pʊɔ.

eng: Rely on your leaders and defer to them, because they watch over your whole being as people who are going to be held responsible for you. They need to be able to do this with pleasure and not with complaints about you, because that wouldnt help you.
local: Yɩ turo a yɩ nɩdierbe nɛɛ, na yɩ siir a yɩ mãɛ kʊ ba. Bala lɛ kaara yɩ, na ban wa man a yɩ yele kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn. Yɩ turo a ba nɛɛ ka a ba tʊ̃mɔ̃ ɩ nʊ̃ɔ naa ta ɩ tuor kʊ ba ɛ, naa ta mɩ ɩ pɔrɔ a yɩ sɛ̃ ɛ.

eng: Pray for us. Were sure that we have a good conscience, and we want to do the right thing in every way.
local: Yɩ sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn kʊrɔ sɩ, sɩn sɔɔ naa ɩka yele jaa ba be a sɩ tɩɛr pɔ ɛ, a sɩ bɔfʊ lɛ ɩka sɩ kpɩɛrɛ a lɛ na tor sɔr jaa pɔ.

eng: Im particularly asking you to do this so that I can be returned to you quickly.
local: Maa 'lɔ, n sʊɔrɔ yɩ naa ɩka yɩ sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn kʊrɔ ma ka n nyɛ sɔr naa tʊ̃ɔ lɩɛb fɔɔ tɩ a yɩ sɛ̃.

eng: May the God of peace, who brought back the great shepherd of the sheep, our Lord Jesus, from the dead by the blood of the eternal covenant.
local: A Nãaŋmɩn nyã'maar sʊɔ na tu a jãɩ ala wʊn de tɩr a nɛtɩraa na ba tara baarfʊ ɛ, na wʊ sãɩ a sɩ Soro Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto a kũu pɔ, 'lɔ na ɩ a pɛchiin gbɔɔraa.

eng: equip you with every good thing to do his will, by developing in us what pleases him through Jesus Christ. To him be the glory forever and always. Amen.
local: wʊ sʊ̃ʊ yɩ nɩ vɩla jaa bʊrɔ ka yɩ tʊ̃ɔ maal a lɛ wʊn bʊɔrɔ, na wʊ mɩ tũ a sɩ pɔ a lɛ na pɛlɛ a wʊ pʊɔ tu a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto. 'Lɔ lɛ so a tɩɩr bɩbir nɩ bɩbir jaa. A ɩ a lɛ.

eng: I urge you, brothers and sisters, to put up with this message of encouragement, since Ive only written a short letter to you!.
local: N yɛɛr, n sʊɔrɔ yɩ naa ɩka yɩ tu a n kpãafʊ 'yɛrbie anyãna, bojũu a 'yɛrbie anyã ɩn sɛb ba ɩ yɔɔ ɛ.

eng: You should know that our brother Timothy has been set free. If he comes soon, we will travel together to see you.
local: N bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka yɩ bɔ̃ɔn ɩka ba yuon a sɩ yɛbɛ Timoti bar, 'lɔ wa tɔ̃ɔ ta fɔɔ, ɩn tara wʊn 'mataa tɩn ka yɩ tɩ nyɛ wʊ.

eng: Greet your leaders and all of Gods holy people. The group from Italy greets you.
local: Yɩ puor a yɩ nɩdierbe ba jaa nɩ a pupuorbiir ba jaa jaa. Bala mɩ na yi a Itali mɩ puoro yɩ naa.

eng: May grace be with all of you.
local: A wãɛfʊ wʊ be a yɩ jaa sɛ̃.

eng: My brothers and sisters, when you show favoritism you deny the faithfulness of our Lord Jesus Christ, who has been resurrected in glory.
local: N yɛɛr, yɩn sɔɔ de a sɩ Soro Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto tɩɩr sʊɔ, yɩ taa turo nɩbɛ nʊ̃nɔ̃ ɛ.

eng: Imagine two people coming into your meeting. One has a gold ring and fine clothes, while the other is poor, dressed in filthy rags.
local: Ala ɩ nɩbɛ ayi wa kpɛ naa a yɩ lɔ̃ɔfʊ jie, ka a ãsʊɔ pãɛ na wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔn sɛlma mʊtʊɔ, tɩchɛ ka a ãsʊɔ mɩ ɩ nɔ̃ɔ sʊɔ su bʊ̃jarsɩ.

eng: Then suppose that you were to take special notice of the one wearing fine clothes, saying, Heres an excellent place. Sit here. But to the poor person you say, Stand over there; or, Here, sit at my feet.
local: Ka yɩ lɩɛb 'yɛr kʊ a 'lɔ na pãɛ, Jãnɛ ka a dakɔɔ ju, tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a 'lɔ mɩ na su a bʊ̃jarsɩ, Ara ka bɩɩ wa jãnɛ ka a tẽe a n gbɛɛ par.

eng: Wouldnt you have shown favoritism among yourselves and become evil-minded judges?.
local: Ala ɩ lɛ lɛ, a ba ɩ yɩ turo naa a nɩbɛ bamɩne nʊ̃nɔ̃ na yɩ de tɩɛr dɛɛr tɔɔrɔ nɩ a ba 'yɛr ɛ?.

eng: My dear brothers and sisters, listen! Hasnt God chosen those who are poor by worldly standards to be rich in terms of faith? Hasnt God chosen the poor as heirs of the kingdom he has promised to those who love him?.
local: N yɛɛr yɩ chɛlɛ, Nãaŋmɩn ir a bala a wɛr nɩbɛ na nyɛ ka ba ɩ nɔ̃n dẽme ka ban tara sɔɔfʊ tɩ kpɛ̃n a wʊ nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ wʊn tɩr a nɛɛ kʊ a bala na nʊ̃ wʊ.

eng: But you have dishonored the poor. Dont the wealthy make life difficult for you? Arent they the ones who drag you into court?.
local: Tɩchɛ yɩ ma kpɛrɛ na a nɔ̃ɔ sʊɔ. A ba ɩ a taraa dẽme lɛ ma ge yɩ ɛ. A ba ɩ bala lɛ ma nyɔɔ yɩ chiin a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ jie ɛ?.

eng: Arent they the ones who insult the good name spoken over you at your baptism?.
local: A ba ɩ bala lɛ sɔ̃ɔ a yovɩla 'lɔ ban bʊɔlɔ yɩ ɛ?.

eng: You do well when you really fulfill the royal law found in scripture, Love your neighbor as yourself. .
local: Ala ɩ yɩ tu naa a nɛɛ 'lɔ na sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, Fʊ nʊ̃ a fʊ tɔ sʊɔ a fʊ kaar, yɩ maal vɩla.

eng: But when you show favoritism, you are committing a sin, and by that same law you are exposed as a lawbreaker.
local: Tɩchɛ yɩn wa turo nʊ̃nɔ̃, yɩ maal dɛɛr, ka a nɛɛ 'yɛr ɩka yɩ bɔr naa na yɩ ɩ a nɛɛ sɔ̃sɔ̃ɔnbɔ.

eng: Anyone who tries to keep all of the Law but fails at one point is guilty of failing to keep all of it.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na turo a nɛɛr a jaa tɩ wa 'mab bʊ̃'yen, wʊ sɔ̃ɔn a jaa.

eng: The one who said, Dont commit adultery , also said, Dont commit murder . So if you dont commit adultery but do commit murder, you are a lawbreaker.
local: A 'lɔ na 'yɛr, Taa sɛ̃nɛ ɛ, 'lɔ mɩ lɛ 'yɛr, Taa kʊrɔ nɩrɛ ɛ. Fʊ̃ʊ ba sɛ̃ ɛ, tɩ kʊ nɩrɛ, fʊ sɔ̃ɔn a jaa.

eng: In every way, then, speak and act as people who will be judged by the law of freedom.
local: Yɩ 'yɛrɛ tɩ maala bɔ̃ɔn ɩka Nãaŋmɩn na tɔɔr a yɩ 'yɛr nɩtãa lɛ a nɛɛ 'lɔ na ma vɛ̃ ka nɩrɛ so a wʊ mãɛ na 'yɛr.

eng: There will be no mercy in judgment for anyone who hasnt shown mercy. Mercy overrules judgment.
local: Bojũu a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ daar, fʊ̃ʊ nyã na ba dɔ̃ɔ tara nɩbibaal jofʊ ɛ, Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ mɩ jo a fʊ nɩbibaal ɛ. Nɩbibaal jofʊ tʊ̃ɔn 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ.

eng: My brothers and sisters, what good is it if people say they have faith but do nothing to show it? Claiming to have faith cant save anyone, can it?.
local: Vɩla buor nɩ nyã n yɛɛr, ala ɩ nɩrɛ 'yɛr naa ɩka wʊ tara na sɔɔfʊ, tɩchɛ ba lɔ̃ɔn tʊ̃mɔ̃ ɛ, a sɔɔfʊ nyã na tʊ̃ɔ faa wʊ ɔ?.

eng: Imagine a brother or sister who is naked and never has enough food to eat.
local: Ala ɩ yɛbɛ ɩn ãgãn kpãakpol tɩ ba tara bʊ̃diir ɛ.

eng: What if one of you said, Go in peace! Stay warm! Have a nice meal!? What good is it if you dont actually give them what their body needs?.
local: ka a yɩ ãsʊɔ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, Chiine nɩ nyã'maar, a ɩ vɩla kʊ fʊ ka fʊ nyɛ di tɩɩ, tɩchɛ na faa sʊ̃ʊ wʊ a ãgãn bʊ̃bʊɔrsɩ ɛ, bo vɩla lɛ a ɩ?.

eng: In the same way, faith is dead when it doesnt result in faithful activity.
local: A lɛ jũu, sɔɔfʊ ka tʊ̃mɔ̃ ba pʊɔ ɛ, wʊ ɩn bʊ̃kũu.

eng: Someone might claim, You have faith and I have action. But how can I see your faith apart from your actions? Instead, Ill show you my faith by putting it into practice in faithful action.
local: Tɩchɛ nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ na 'yɛr naa, Fʊ̃ʊ tara na sɔɔfʊ ka maa mɩ tara a tʊ̃mɔ̃. Wiil ma a fʊ sɔɔfʊ ka tʊ̃mɔ̃ ba pʊɔ ɛ, tɩ n mɩ wiil fʊ a n sɔɔfʊ nɩ a tʊ̃mɔ̃.

eng: Its good that you believe that God is one. Ha! Even the demons believe this, and they tremble with fear.
local: Fʊ sɔɔ naa ɩka Nãaŋmɩn bʊ̃'yen tɛɛ lɛ be be. A ɩn vɩla. A sɩdɛbɛ gba mɩ sɔɔn de a lɛ, tɩ na ba mɩ joro dãbãɛ.

eng: Are you so slow? Do you need to be shown that faith without actions has no value at all?.
local: Fʊ̃ʊ jɔl nyã, sɔɔfʊ tʊ̃mɔ̃ na ba pʊɔ ɛ, faa bɔ̃ɔ ka bʊ̃kũu lɛ ɛ?.

eng: What about Abraham, our father? Wasnt he shown to be righteous through his actions when he offered his son Isaac on the altar?.
local: A ba ɩ a sɩ sãakpã Abraham wiil a sɔɔfʊ nɩ a tʊ̃mɔ̃, wʊn dɔ̃ɔ de a wʊ bie Isaakɩ ɩka wʊ lon bɔɔr ɛ?.

eng: See, his faith was at work along with his actions. In fact, his faith was made complete by his faithful actions.
local: Faa nyɛ a sɔɔfʊ nɩ a tʊ̃mɔ̃ na 'mataa ɛ, ka a wʊ sɔɔfʊ sɛɛ nɩ a lɛ wʊn tʊ̃.

eng: So the scripture was fulfilled that says, Abraham believed God, and God regarded him as righteous . What is more, Abraham was called Gods friend.
local: Ka a lɛ na sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩ yelmãɛ ɩka, Abraham sɔɔn de Nãaŋmɩn, ka a lɛ vɛ̃ ka wʊ ɩ nɩtor. Ka Nãaŋmɩn bʊɔl wʊ wʊ baraa.

eng: So you see that a person is shown to be righteous through faithful actions and not through faith alone.
local: Yaa nyɛ, nɩrɛ na ɩn nɩtor Nãaŋmɩn niem a lɛ wʊn tʊ̃, a ba ɩ a wʊ sɔɔfʊ tɛɛ ɛ.

eng: In the same way, wasnt Rahab the prostitute shown to be righteous when she received the messengers as her guests and then sent them on by another road?.
local: A lɛ nɩ wʊ ju lɛ ba dɔ̃ɔ kaa a sɛ̃sɛ̃ pɔɔ ban bʊɔlɔ Worahab ka wʊ ɩ nɩtor, wʊn dɔ̃ɔ de a tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ wiil ba sɔyuo ka ba lɩɛb kul.

eng: As the lifeless body is dead, so faith without actions is dead.
local: A lɛ a sɩɛ na ma wa yi a ãgãn pɔ ka wʊ ɩ bʊ̃kũu, lɛ mɩ lɛ a sɔɔfʊ ka tʊ̃mɔ̃ ba pʊɔ ɛ mɩ ɩ bʊ̃kũu.

eng: My brothers and sisters, not many of you should become teachers, because we know that we teachers will be judged more strictly.
local: N yɛɛr, yɩ jaa yɩ ta bʊɔrɔ ɩka yɩ ɩ wiwiilbe ɛ, bojũu yɩ bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka a sɩn bala na ɩ a wiwiilbe na nyɛn 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ gar nɩrɛ jaa.

eng: We all make mistakes often, but those who dont make mistakes with their words have reached full maturity. Like a bridled horse, they can control themselves entirely.
local: Sɩ jaa ma maal 'mab. Ala ɩ nɩrɛ jaa ba 'yɛr 'mab ɛ, wʊ ɩn nɩtor na wʊ nyɔɔrɔ a wʊ mãɛ.

eng: When we bridle horses and put bits in their mouths to lead them wherever we want, we can control their whole bodies.
local: Sɩ ma 'yɔ̃ɔn a kur a wiisɩ nɛɛr pɔ, ka a ma tu a sɩ nɛɛr, ka sɩ ma lɩɛb chiin jie jaa sɩn bʊɔrɔ.

eng: Consider ships: They are so large that strong winds are needed to drive them. But pilots direct their ships wherever they want with a little rudder.
local: Bɩɩ yɩ kaa a gbor bɛrɛ ala a man kpɛ̃ɛ pɔ a sɛsɛkpɛ̃ɛ na ma furo chiine, tɩchɛ a gbor mɔ̃nɔ̃ ma den a vuur mɔ̃ chiin jie jaa wʊn bʊɔrɔ.

eng: In the same way, even though the tongue is a small part of the body, it boasts wildly. Think about this: A small flame can set a whole forest on fire.
local: Lɛ mɩ lɛ a jãlbir na ɩ jaa pʊʊrbile a ãgãn pɔ tɩ ma fere. Wʊ kaara fɩka bũu bir na lo tuu kpɛ̃ɛ pɔ di wʊ jaa.

eng: The tongue is a small flame of fire, a world of evil at work in us. It contaminates our entire lives. Because of it, the circle of life is set on fire. The tongue itself is set on fire by the flames of hell.
local: A jãlbir mɩ ɩn bũu na ɩ a wɛr dɛɛr na be a sɩ ãgãn pɔ na wʊ sɔ̃ɔ a ãgãn wʊ jaa bar, na wʊ chʊmɔ̃ a ãgãn nɩ a bũu dagel 'lɔ na ba kpĩine ɛ.

eng: People can tame and already have tamed every kind of animal, bird, reptile, and fish.
local: Nɩrɛ ma tʊ̃ɔn guol mɔdʊ̃ɔ jaa bʊrɔ, nɩ lubil jaa bʊrɔ, bʊ̃vuuraa jaa bʊrɔ, nɩ a man pɔ bonsɩ.

eng: No one can tame the tongue, though. It is a restless evil, full of deadly poison.
local: Tɩchɛ nɩrɛ maa tʊ̃ɔ a wʊ jãlbir ɛ, wʊ ɩn bʊ̃bier na ba pɛ̃nɛ ɛ, na wʊ mɩ ɩ lɔɔ na kʊrɔ.

eng: From James, a slave of God and of the Lord Jesus Christ. To the twelve tribes who are scattered outside the land of Israel. Greetings!.
local: Jemesɩ, na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn nɩ a sɩ Soro Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto gbãgbaa, puoro naa a Isɩral bala pie nɩ ayi na yaar a wɛr wʊ jaa.

eng: With it we both bless the Lord and Father and curse human beings made in Gods likeness.
local: A jãlbir nyã lɛ sɩ ma de pɛɛn a sɩ Sãa na ɩ a sɩ Soro, na sɩ lɛ de ma pɔn 'yɔ̃ɔ a bala a Nãaŋmɩn na mɛ ka ba kaara a wʊ kaar.

eng: My brothers and sisters, think of the various tests you encounter as occasions for joy.
local: N yɛɛr, yɩ tara pʊpɛl, ala sɔɔ ɩ yelyɔɔ wõnõ yɩ naa.

eng: Blessing and cursing come from the same mouth. My brothers and sisters, it just shouldnt be this way!.
local: A nɛbʊ̃'yen 'lɔ pɔ lɛ a pɛɛfʊ nɩ pɔ'yɔ̃ɔfʊ ma yire. N yɛɛr a ba tor ɛ.

eng: After all, you know that the testing of your faith produces endurance.
local: Yɩ bɔ̃ɔ ɩka yɩn jãn naa tara kãnyir a yɩ bɛɛr kaa sɔ̃ɔ.

eng: Both fresh water and salt water dont come from the same spring, do they?.
local: Kɔ̃vɩla nɩ kɔ̃dɛɛr na tʊ̃ɔ yire bul bʊ̃'yen pɔ bɩɩ?.

eng: Let this endurance complete its work so that you may be fully mature, complete, and lacking in nothing.
local: Yɩn jãn tʊ̃ɔ tara kãnyir, a lɛ na vɛ̃ naa ka yɩ tʊ̃ɔ baa, tɩ na yɩ taa wa faa bon jaa ɛ.

eng: My brothers and sisters, can a fig tree produce olives? Can a grapevine produce figs? Of course not, and fresh water doesnt flow from a saltwater spring either.
local: N yɛɛr, kɔ̃kɔ̃ɔ na tʊ̃ɔ wɔ̃ dãbãɩnãa bɩɩ dãbãɩnãa na tʊ̃ɔ wɔ̃ kɔ̃kɔ̃ɔ ɔ? Lɛ mɩ lɛ, kɔ̃vɩla nɩ kɔ̃dɛɛr kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ 'mataar yire bul bʊ̃'yen pɔ ɛ.

eng: But anyone who needs wisdom should ask God, whose very nature is to give to everyone without a second thought, without keeping score. Wisdom will certainly be given to those who ask.
local: Ala ɩ yɩ ãsʊɔ jaa bʊɔrɔ naa yɛ̃, wʊ sʊɔr yi a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ na kʊrɔ nɩrɛ jaa pɔrɔ, wʊ ɩn nɩmãɛ, wʊ kʊ̃ mɔ̃ɔ fʊ ɛ.

eng: Are any of you wise and understanding? Show that your actions are good with a humble lifestyle that comes from wisdom.
local: A yɩ ãsʊɔ tara na yɛ̃ nɩ bɔ̃ɔfʊ bɩɩ? Vɛ̃ ka a wiil a wʊ maalfʊ pɔ, nɩ lɛ wʊn siir a wʊ mãɛ maala nɩ yɛ̃.

eng: Whoever asks shouldnt hesitate. They should ask in faith, without doubting. Whoever doubts is like the surf of the sea, tossed and turned by the wind.
local: Tɩchɛ yɩn wa sʊɔr bon jaa, yɩ tara sɔɔfʊ na yɩ ta baala ɛ. Nɩrɛ jaa na baala kaara nɩtãa man kʊ̃ɔ sɛsɛbɛ na furo dãdaala.

eng: However, if you have bitter jealousy and selfish ambition in your heart, then stop bragging and living in ways that deny the truth.
local: Tɩchɛ ala ɩ a yɩ nyãa pɔ, nyuur dɛɛr nɩ tuo, nɩ yobɔyele ben be, yɩ ta wile yɩ mãɛ ŋmara jirsɩ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a yelmãɛ ɛ.

eng: This is not the wisdom that comes down from above. Instead, it is from the earth, natural and demonic.
local: A yɛ̃ nyã bʊrɔ ba yi saaju wa ɛ, tɩchɛ wʊ ɩ naa a wɛr nyã yɛ̃ nɩsaal na tara ka wʊ yi a sɩdɛɛr sɛ̃.

eng: Wherever there is jealousy and selfish ambition, there is disorder and everything that is evil.
local: Bojũu jie 'lɔ nyuur, nɩ tuo, nɩ yobɔyele na be, be lɛ guor taa ba be ɛ, ka tʊ̃ dɛɛr jaa bʊrɔ be be.

eng: What of the wisdom from above? First, it is pure, and then peaceful, gentle, obedient, filled with mercy and good actions, fair, and genuine.
local: Tɩchɛ a yɛ̃ 'lɔ, na yi saaju ɩn chɛchɛ na wʊ tara nyã'maar nʊ̃fʊ, nɩ sʊ̃ʊfʊ, nɩ mãɛ siirfʊ, nɩ vɩla maalfʊ, na wʊ joro nɩbaal, na wʊ wɔ̃nɔ̃ bisʊ̃n, na waa ooro ɛ, bɩɩ tara nɛɛr ayi ɛ.

eng: Those who make peace sow the seeds of justice by their peaceful acts.
local: A bala na ba bʊɔrɔ jɛɛr ɛ, na bʊr nɩ nyã'maar, na ŋman yeltorsɩ.

eng: People like that should never imagine that they will receive anything from the Lord.
local: Ala ɩ fʊ ɩ naa a nɩrɛ nyã tɔ sʊɔ, ta tɩɛr ɩka, fʊn nyɛn bon jaa yi a sɩ Soro sɛ̃ ɛ.

eng: They are double-minded, unstable in all their ways.
local: A nɩrɛ na tara tɩɛr ayi, wʊ maa ba gbɛr ara yele jaa pɔ wʊn ɩrɛ ɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters who are poor should find satisfaction in their high status.
local: Nãaŋmɩn puoro nɔ̃ɔsʊɔ wʊ chɩlɛ, bojũu wʊ do naa.

eng: Those who are wealthy should find satisfaction in their low status, because they will die off like wildflowers.
local: Tɩchɛ a taraa sʊɔ mɩ wʊ chɩlɛ a wʊ suufʊ pɔ, bojũu wʊn suor naa fɩka mɔpuul.

eng: The sun rises with its scorching heat and dries up the grass so that its flowers fall and its beauty is lost. Just like that, in the midst of their daily lives, the wealthy will waste away.
local: Bojũu a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ ma nyãa naa ka a tʊl ŋme ka a ko suor, a vapuul ma suor naa ka a vɩla baar. Lɛ lɛ a taraa sʊɔ kaara, 'lɔ wa tʊ̃nɔ̃ a wʊ yar tʊ̃mɔ̃.

eng: Those who stand firm during testing are blessed. They are tried and true. They will receive the life God has promised to those who love him as their reward.
local: Pʊpɛl sʊɔ lɛ a 'lɔ na di kãnyir ara a bɛɛr kaa pɔ, tɩ na waa lo ɛ. Bojũu a nɩrɛ 'lɔ lɛ na nyɛ a nyɛvʊr nãa jupil 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn na tɩr a nɛɛ kʊ a bala na nʊ̃ wʊ.

eng: No one who is tested should say, God is tempting me! This is because God is not tempted by any form of evil, nor does he tempt anyone.
local: Bɛɛr kaa wa wa nɩrɛ sɛ̃, wʊ ta 'yɛr ɩka Nãaŋmɩn lɛ bɛɛr wʊ kaa ɛ. Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ de bier bɛɛr nɩrɛ kaa ɛ, a wʊ mãɛ ba bɛɛrɛ nɩrɛ kaara ɛ.

eng: Everyone is tempted by their own cravings; they are lured away and enticed by them.
local: Tɩchɛ nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ jaa yelbier vɔlaa lɛ ma bɛɛr wʊ kaa na a nyɔɔ wʊ.

eng: Once those cravings conceive, they give birth to sin; and when sin grows up, it gives birth to death.
local: A vɔlaa anyãna lɛ na tara pʊɔ naa dɔɔ bier, ka a bier nʊ̃ɔ dɔɔ kũu.

eng: Dont be misled, my dear brothers and sisters.
local: N yɛɛr nʊ̃nʊ̃nbɔ, yɩ ta vɛ̃ ka ba bɛl yɩ ɛ.

eng: Every good gift, every perfect gift, comes from above. These gifts come down from the Father, the creator of the heavenly lights, in whose character there is no change at all.
local: Bʊ̃vɩla nɩ kʊfʊ vɩla jaa yire naa a chãa Sãa na be a saaju sɛ̃. Libe ba be a wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ ɛ, waa lɩɛrɛ ɛ.

eng: He chose to give us birth by his true word, and here is the result: we are like the first crop from the harvest of everything he created.
local: An wa ta a lɛ wʊn bʊɔrɔ, wʊ dɔɔ sɩn nɩ a wʊ yelmãɛ 'yɛr ka sɩ ɩ birdɔ̃ɔ a bʊ̃maalsɩ a jaa pɔ.

eng: Know this, my dear brothers and sisters: everyone should be quick to listen, slow to speak, and slow to grow angry.
local: N yɛɛr nʊ̃nʊ̃nbɔ, yɩ bɔ̃ɔn a anyãna yɩ ma chɛlɛ fɔfɔɔ tɩ na yɩ ta ma 'yɛrɛ fɔfɔɔ ɛ, bɩɩ nyɛrɛ suur fɔfɔɔ ɛ.

eng: This is because an angry person doesnt produce Gods righteousness.
local: Bojũu nɩrɛ wa nyɛ suur, wʊ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ maal yelvɩla ala a Nãaŋmɩn na bʊɔrɔ ɩka a ɩ ɛ.

eng: Therefore, with humility, set aside all moral filth and the growth of wickedness, and welcome the word planted deep inside you—the very word that is able to save you.
local: A lɛ juu, yɩ bar yele jaa na ɩ dɛɛr bɩɩ bier, tɩ na yɩ siir yɩ mãɛ de a 'yɛrbir 'lɔ na bʊr a yɩ nyãa pɔ, ala lɛ na faa a yɩ sɩɩr.

eng: You must be doers of the word and not only hearers who mislead themselves.
local: Yɩ ta bɛlɛ a yɩ mãɛ a 'yɛr yɩn chɛlɛ tɛɛ ɛ, tɩ yɩ ɩ a 'yɛr mamaalba.

eng: Those who hear but dont do the word are like those who look at their faces in a mirror.
local: A nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na wa wõ a 'yɛr tɩ ba sɔɔ de ɛ, wʊ kaara nɩtãa a 'lɔ na chaar a wʊ mãɛ kaa chãana pɔ.

eng: They look at themselves, walk away, and immediately forget what they were like.
local: wʊn wa yi baar wʊ ĩin a lɛ bar wʊn kaara.

eng: But there are those who study the perfect law, the law of freedom, and continue to do it. They dont listen and then forget, but they put it into practice in their lives. They will be blessed in whatever they do.
local: Tɩchɛ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir turo a nɛbinãa na ba tara chɛfʊ ɛ, na ma vɛ̃ ka fʊ so a fʊ mãɛ, Nãaŋmɩn na sʊ̃ʊ wʊ naa nɩ bon jaa pɔ wʊn maal, ala ɩ wʊ chɛl tɩ sɔɔ, tɩ a ba ɩ wõ tɩ ĩin ɛ.

eng: If those who claim devotion to God dont control what they say, they mislead themselves. Their devotion is worthless.
local: Ala ɩ nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ tɩɛr ɩka wʊ ɩn Nãaŋmɩn puorbo tɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ nyɔɔ a wʊ jãlbir ɛ, wʊ bɛlɛ na wʊ mãɛ, ka a wʊ puorfʊ ɩ pɔrɔ.

eng: True devotion, the kind that is pure and faultless before God the Father, is this: to care for orphans and widows in their difficulties and to keep the world from contaminating us.
local: Puorfʊ 'lɔ na pɛlɛ a Sãa Nãaŋmɩn pʊɔ na wʊ ɩ chɛchɛ na ba tara dɛɛr ɛ. Sʊ̃nɔ̃ a bala na ɩ a kpɩɩr, nɩ a pɔɔkɔɛ, a ba dɔɔyɛ pɔ, tɩ nyɔɔ fʊ mãɛ ka a wɛr ta sɔ̃ɔ fʊ ɛ.

eng: What is the source of conflict among you? What is the source of your disputes? Dont they come from your cravings that are at war in your own lives?.
local: Nyɩnɛ lɛ a jɛɛr nɩ nɛchɩrɛ yi be a yɩ chara pɔ? A ba ɩ a yi naa a vɔlaa 'lɔ na be a yɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ jɛɛr ɛ?.

eng: You long for something you dont have, so you commit murder. You are jealous for something you cant get, so you struggle and fight. You dont have because you dont ask.
local: Yɩ ma bʊɔrɔ na bon kɔ̃ɔ tɩ yɩ maa nyɛ ɛ, na yɩ ma kʊrɔ taa. Na yɩ ma tara nyuur nɩ bomo, tɩ na yɩ maa nyãa ɛ, na yɩ pãa tara jɛɛr nɩ nɛchɩrɛ, tɩ maa nyãa ɛ, bojũu yɩ maa sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn ɛ.

eng: You ask and dont have because you ask with evil intentions, to waste it on your own cravings.
local: Yɩn wa sʊɔr a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃, yɩ maa nyɛ ɛ, bojũu yɩ sʊɔr naa nɩ pʊtɩdɛɛr na de maal a lɛ na pɛl a yɩ pʊɔ.

eng: You unfaithful people! Dont you know that friendship with the world means hostility toward God? So whoever wants to be the worlds friend becomes Gods enemy.
local: Yɩn sɛ̃sɛ̃ nɩbɛ ba nyã, yaa bɔ̃ɔ ɩka nɩrɛ nyã na ɩ a wɛr baraa 'laar Nãaŋmɩn ɛ? Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na ŋmaa a wɛr nyã baraa ɩn Nãaŋmɩn dɔ̃ sʊɔ.

eng: Or do you suppose that scripture is meaningless? Doesnt God long for our faithfulness in the life he has given to us?.
local: Ta baala a Nãaŋmɩn gãn na 'yɛr ɩka, yelmãɛ jaa lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn kaara a Sɩɛ 'lɔ wʊn de 'yɔ̃ɔ sɩ.

eng: But he gives us more grace. This is why it says, God stands against the proud, but favors the humble .
local: Tɩchɛ Nãaŋmɩn ma kʊ sɩ naa a wʊ wãɛfʊ, ala lɛ so a Nãaŋmɩn gãn na 'yɛr, Nãaŋmɩn ma tɔr naa bala na bʊɔr yuor, tɩ na wʊ ma wãɛ a bala na siir ba mãɛ.

eng: Therefore, submit to God. Resist the devil, and he will run away from you.
local: A lɛ jũu, yɩ siir a yɩ mãɛ kʊ Nãaŋmɩn, na yɩ tɔɔ yɩ mãɛ yin a sɩdɛɛr sʊɔ sɛ̃, wʊn bar yɩn tɩ jo.

eng: Come near to God, and he will come near to you. Wash your hands, you sinners. Purify your hearts, you double-minded.
local: Yɩ tɔɔ gbʊr Nãaŋmɩn, wʊ mɩ na tɔɔn gbʊr yɩ. Yɩn yelbe-ɩrbɛ, yɩ pɛɛ a yɩ nuur na yɩ lɩɛb a yɩ tɩɛr, yɩn tɩɛr ayi dẽme.

eng: Cry out in sorrow, mourn, and weep! Let your laughter become mourning and your joy become sadness.
local: Yɩ waala, na yɩ tara pʊsɔ̃ɔ, na yɩ kõnõ. Yɩ vɛ̃ ka a yɩ laar lɩɛb kõn, ka a yɩ pʊpɛl lɩɛb pʊsɔ̃ɔ.

eng: Humble yourselves before the Lord, and he will lift you up.
local: Yɩ siir a yɩ mãɛ a sɩ Soro niem, wʊn 'mɔɔ yɩn ka yɩ do.

eng: Brothers and sisters, dont say evil things about each other. Whoever insults or criticizes a brother or sister insults and criticizes the Law. If you find fault with the Law, you are not a doer of the Law but a judge over it.
local: N yɛɛr, yɩ ta sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ taa yoe ɛ. Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a wʊ tɔ sʊɔ yuor, wʊ tɔɔr a wʊ 'yɛr, wʊ tɔɔrɔ naa a nɛbinãa 'lɔ 'yɛr. Fʊ̃ʊ wa tɔɔrɔ a nɛbinãa 'yɛr, faa turo a lɛ a nɛbinãa na 'yɛr ɛ, tɩ ɩ a wʊ 'yɛr tɔɔrɔ.

eng: There is only one lawgiver and judge, and he is able to save and to destroy. But you who judge your neighbor, who are you?.
local: Nãaŋmɩn lɛ bin a wʊ nɛɛ kʊ sɩ, na wʊ ɩ a 'yɛr tɔɔrɔ, 'lɔ lɛ na tʊ̃ɔ faa bɩɩ sɔ̃ɔ bar. Tɩchɛ fʊ̃ʊ mɩ nɩ ana na fʊ tɔɔrɔ a fʊ tɔ sʊɔ 'yɛr?.

eng: Pay attention, you who say, Today or tomorrow we will go to such-and-such a town. We will stay there a year, buying and selling, and making a profit.
local: Yɩ chɛlɛ, a yɩn bala na ma 'yɛr ɩka, Dɩna bɩɩ bio sɩn chen tẽkpɛ̃ɛ pɔ tɩ maal yuon, da bomo, da nyɛ libie.

eng: You dont really know about tomorrow. What is your life? You are a mist that appears for only a short while before it vanishes.
local: Yaa bɔ̃ɔ lɛn na ɩ bio ɛ, bo lɛ a yɩ nyɛvʊr? Wʊ kaara fɩka ul na lo tɩ wɛl ajienaa.

eng: Heres what you ought to say: If the Lord wills, we will live and do this or that.
local: Yɩ ta ma 'yɛr ɩka, Ala ɩ Nãaŋmɩn sɔɔ naa ka sɩ tara nyɛvʊr, sɩn maal nyã bɩɩ anyãna.

eng: But now you boast and brag, and all such boasting is evil.
local: Tɩ lɛ an be nɩ nyã, yɩ bʊɔrɔ na yuor, na yɩ wile yɩ mãɛ, ka a mãɛ wiilfʊ nyã ɩ yelbier.

eng: It is a sin when someone knows the right thing to do and doesnt do it.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa na pãa bɔ̃ɔ vɩla tɩ ba maal ɛ, wʊ ɩn bier.

eng: Pay attention, you wealthy people! Weep and moan over the miseries coming upon you.
local: Yɩ chɛlɛ, yɩn tara dẽme, yɩ kõnõ ŋmiere chɛlsɩ a yele 'lɔ na waara a yɩ ju.

eng: Your riches have rotted. Moths have destroyed your clothes.
local: A yɩ nãbomo pʊ̃ɔ naa, ka yɛlɛ mʊʊ a yɩ fɔɔr.

eng: Your gold and silver have rusted, and their rust will be evidence against you. It will eat your flesh like fire. Consider the treasure you have hoarded in the last days.
local: A yɩ sɛlma nɩ a libie lon sɔrɔ, ka a ba sɔrɔ lofʊ wiil ɩka an di yɩn bũu kaar. A baarfʊ ta naa, tɩ yɩ chɛnɛ bin a nãbomo.

eng: Listen! Hear the cries of the wages of your field hands. These are the wages you stole from those who harvested your fields. The cries of the harvesters have reached the ears of the Lord of heavenly forces.
local: Yɩ nyɛ a bala yɩn de ka ba tʊ̃nɔ̃ a yɩ por pɔ, tɩ yɩ mɔ̃ɔ ba a yar ka ba kõnõ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ yɩ, ka a yɩ bʊ̃ŋmarba kõn ta a sɩ Soro na ɩ a Tʊ̃ɔfʊ Sʊɔ.

eng: You have lived a self-satisfying life on this earth, a life of luxury. You have stuffed your hearts in preparation for the day of slaughter.
local: Yɩ din nʊ̃ɔ ka a wɛr pɔ lɛ yɩn bʊɔrɔ. Yɩ guol naa yɩ mãɛ a lɛ kʊfʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: You have condemned and murdered the righteous one, who doesnt oppose you.
local: Yɩ ma 'yɔ̃ɔn a bala na tor 'yɛr, na yɩ kʊ ba, tɩ baa maal bon jaa ɛ.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, you must be patient as you wait for the coming of the Lord. Consider the farmer who waits patiently for the coming of rain in the fall and spring, looking forward to the precious fruit of the earth.
local: A lɛ jũu, yɛɛr yɩ tara kãnyir tɩ tãn a sɩ Soro waa daar. Kaa nyɛ a lɛ a kʊɔraa na ma tara kãnyir chɛlɛ nɩ a bonsɩ wɔ̃fʊ daar, na wʊ tara kãnyir chɛlɛ a sadɔ̃ɔ nɩ a sabaaraa.

eng: You also must wait patiently, strengthening your resolve, because the coming of the Lord is near.
local: Yɩn mɩ, yɩ tara kãnyir na yɩ ara kpɛ̃ɔ, bojũu a sɩ Soro waa gbʊr naa.

eng: Dont complain about each other, brothers and sisters, so that you wont be judged. Look! The judge is standing at the door!.
local: Yɛɛr yɩ ta hũhũnõ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ taa na yɩ taa nyɛ 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ ɛ. A 'yɛr tɔɔrɔ ara naa a dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, take the prophets who spoke in the name of the Lord as an example of patient resolve and steadfastness.
local: Yɛɛr, yɩ kaa a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ bala na de a sɩ Soro yuor 'yɛr 'yɛr, din dɔɔyɛ tɩ tara kãnyir.

eng: Look at how we honor those who have practiced endurance. You have heard of the endurance of Job. And you have seen what the Lord has accomplished, for the Lord is full of compassion and mercy.
local: Ala lɛ so ka sɩ bʊɔlɔ ba bɔɔsʊ̃n dẽme. Yɩ wõn a Job kãnyir wʊn tara na yɩ mɩ nyɛ a lɛ a sɩ Soro na maal kʊ wʊ a baaraa. A sɩ Soro ɩn nɩbaal joro, na wʊ maala vɩla.

eng: Most important, my brothers and sisters, never make a solemn pledge—neither by heaven nor earth, nor by anything else. Instead, speak with a simple Yes or No, or else you may fall under judgment.
local: N yɛɛr, ala na chɛ lɛ a anyã, yɩ taa pɔrɔ nɩ saaju ɛ, bɩɩ a tẽe ɛ, bɩɩ bon jaa ɛ, tɩ yɩ vɛ̃ ka yɩ ɔ̃ɔ, ɩ ɔ̃ɔ, ka yɩ kai, mɩ ɩ kai. Ka yɩ taa kpɛ 'yɛr pɔ ɛ.

eng: If any of you are suffering, they should pray. If any of you are happy, they should sing.
local: Wõm pɔɔn a yɩ ãsʊɔ bɩɩ? Wʊ sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn. Nʊ̃ɔ kpɛ̃n a yɩ ãsʊɔ bɩɩ? Wʊ yiele yiel dãna nɩ Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: If any of you are sick, they should call for the elders of the church, and the elders should pray over them, anointing them with oil in the name of the Lord.
local: A yɩ ãsʊɔ bɩɛrɛ na bɩɩ? Wʊ bʊɔl a pupuorbiir nɩbɛrɛ ka ba wa jɛ wʊ kãa nɩ a sɩ Soro yuor, na ba sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn kʊ wʊ.

eng: Prayer that comes from faith will heal the sick, for the Lord will restore them to health. And if they have sinned, they will be forgiven.
local: A Nãaŋmɩn ban sʊɔr nɩ sɔɔfʊ na sa naa nɩnyɩnɛ baal, a sɩ Soro na nyɔɔ wʊ naa ka wʊ ir. Ala ɩ wʊ mɩ maal dɛɛr, Nãaŋmɩn na vɛ̃n kʊ wʊ.

eng: For this reason, confess your sins to each other and pray for each other so that you may be healed. The prayer of the righteous person is powerful in what it can achieve.
local: A lɛ jũu, yɩ 'yɛrɛ a yɩ maal dɛbɛ kʊrɔ taa, na yɩ sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ taa, ka wʊ sãana yɩ. Nɩtor Nãaŋmɩn sʊɔrfʊ tara na kpɛ̃ɔ na maal bon.

eng: Elijah was a person just like us. When he earnestly prayed that it wouldnt rain, no rain fell for three and a half years.
local: Elaija ɩn nɩrɛ a sɩ kaar, wʊ sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn nɩbijɩɛ ɩka saa wʊ ta wa ɛ, ka war ŋmaa yuomo ata nɩ ŋmaa.

eng: He prayed again, God sent rain, and the earth produced its fruit.
local: Wʊ lɛ sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn go ka a saa wa, ka a bonsɩ bul a tẽe naa wɔ̃.

eng: My brothers and sisters, if any of you wander from the truth and someone turns back the wanderer.
local: N yɛɛr, ala ɩ a yɩ ãsʊɔ 'mab naa a yelmãɛ sɔr pɔ, ka a yɩ ãsʊɔ lɩɛb wʊ waan.

eng: recognize that whoever brings a sinner back from the wrong path will save them from death and will bring about the forgiveness of many sins.
local: yɩ bɔ̃ɔn a anyã ɩka, nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na lɩɛb yelbe-ɩrɛ yin a wʊ yelbier pɔ, wʊ faa wʊn yin kũu pɔ, na wʊ mɩ pɔɔ yelbebe yɔɔ jaa.

eng: Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ, To Gods chosen strangers in the world of the diaspora, who live in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia.
local: Maa Pita na ɩ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto nɩtʊ̃nãa lɛ sɛɛrɛ a gãn nyã kʊrɔ a yɩn a Nãaŋmɩn na chɛl ir a yɩ ɩ sãanba yɛ̃yɛl ka a wɛr pɔ, na yɩ be a Pontus, nɩ Galatiya, nɩ Kapadosiya, nɩ Asɩya, nɩ a Bitiniya.

eng: God the Father chose you because of what he knew beforehand. He chose you through the Holy Spirits work of making you holy and because of the faithful obedience and sacrifice of Jesus Christ. May Gods grace and peace be multiplied to you.
local: A Sãa Nãaŋmɩn den nie bɔ̃ɔ yɩ, ka a wʊ Sɩɛ oo yɩ, a yɩ ɩ chɛchɛ, yɩn wõnõ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto toor jũu, ka wʊ mɩr yɩ a wʊ jãɩ. Wãɛfʊ nɩ nyã'maar na dɔɔlɔ yɩn taa.

eng: May the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ be blessed! On account of his vast mercy, he has given us new birth. You have been born anew into a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead.
local: Pɛɛfʊ wʊ ta a Nãaŋmɩn na ɩ a sɩ Soro Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto Sãa! A wʊ nɩbaal bɛrma naa, ka wʊ dɔɔ sɩ dɔɔ paalaa, na wʊ kʊ sɩ tɩɛrtɔ vʊr tu a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto wʊn sãɩ a kũu pɔ.

eng: You have a pure and enduring inheritance that cannot perish—an inheritance that is presently kept safe in heaven for you.
local: nɩ a nãbomo ala wʊn bin gun sɩ be a saaju, a ba wɛrɛ ɛ, a ba sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ɛ, bɩɩ jʊɔlɔ ɛ.

eng: Through his faithfulness, you are guarded by Gods power so that you can receive the salvation he is ready to reveal in the last time.
local: A tu naa yɩ sɔɔfʊ pɔ, ka a Nãaŋmɩn Kpɛ̃ɔ ger pɔɔ yɩ, tɩ tãn a bɩbir baaraa 'lɔ a faafʊ 'lɔ na chɔɔr baar na wa nyɩr.

eng: You now rejoice in this hope, even if its necessary for you to be distressed for a short time by various trials.
local: A anyãna pɔ lɛ yɩ chɩlɛ yɔɔ jaa, an sɔɔ ɩ ɩka yɩn din dɔɔyɛ bɛɛr kaa bʊrɔ bʊrɔ pɔ.

eng: This is necessary so that your faith may be found genuine. Your genuine faith will result in praise, glory, and honor for you when Jesus Christ is revealed.
local: A anyãna wa naa ka a yɩ sɔɔfʊ na kpɛ̃mɛ gar a sɛlma na ɩ bʊ̃sɔ̃ɔnãa ban ma dʊʊl bũu pɔ ir a dɛɛr bar. A yɩ sɔɔfʊ na lɩɛb ɩ pɛɛfʊ, nɩ tɩɩr, nɩ 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto na wa nyɩr.

eng: Although youve never seen him, you love him. Even though you dont see him now, you trust him and so rejoice with a glorious joy that is too much for words.
local: Yɩ gba na ba nyɛ wʊ ɛ, tɩ na yɩ nʊ̃ wʊ. Yaa gba nyɛ wʊ a pʊ̃pãanyã ɛ, tɩ sɔɔ wʊ, na yɩ sɛɛn a pʊpɛl tɩɩr 'lɔ nɩrɛ na kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ man ɛ.

eng: You are receiving the goal of your faith: your salvation.
local: Bojũu yɩ nyɛrɛ naa a yɩ sɔɔfʊ bon na ɩ a yɩ sɩɩr faafʊ.

eng: The prophets, who long ago foretold the grace that youve received, searched and explored, inquiring carefully about this salvation.
local: A faafʊ nyã 'yɛr lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr a wãɛfʊ nyã na wa a yɩ sɛ̃. Ba vɩɛr naa a faafʊ nyã na ba chɛ̃pɛ̃ wʊ jaa nɛ nɛ.

eng: They wondered what the Spirit of Christ within them was saying when he bore witness beforehand about the suffering that would happen to Christ and the glory that would follow. They wondered what sort of person or what sort of time they were speaking about.
local: Ba mʊ̃ɔn nɩbir ɩka ba bɔ̃ɔ a sɔ̃ɔ nɩ a yelsɩ ala a Kɩrɩsɩto Sɩɛ na be a ba sɛ̃ na wile ba, a lɛ wʊn 'yɛr a dɔɔyɛ a Kɩrɩsɩto na di nɩ a tɩɩr ala na turo.

eng: It was revealed to them that in their search they were not serving themselves but you. These things, which even angels long to examine, have now been proclaimed to you by those who brought you the good news. They did this in the power of the Holy Spirit, who was sent from heaven.
local: Nãaŋmɩn wiil naa a wʊ 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ banyãna ɩka a ba ɩ ba mãɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ ba tʊ̃nɔ̃ a lɛ ɛ, tɩ chɛ yɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ. A lɛ ban 'yɛr a nyãna yɩn ben wõnõ a pʊ̃pãanyã a bala na mʊɔlɔ a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ tu a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ wʊn tʊ̃ a wʊ yi a saaju. A malakasɩ mɩ gba ta bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka ba muul kaa a anyãna.

eng: Therefore, once you have your minds ready for action and you are thinking clearly, place your hope completely on the grace that will be brought to you when Jesus Christ is revealed.
local: A lɛ jũu, yɩ chɔɔr a yɩ tɩɛr gun tʊ̃mɔ̃, na yɩ nyɔɔrɔ yɩ mãɛ. Yɩ de a yɩ tɩɛrtɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ a wãɛfʊ 'lɔ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto na kʊ yɩ a wʊ waa daar.

eng: Dont be conformed to your former desires, those that shaped you when you were ignorant. But, as obedient children.
local: Yɩn wõnõ a Nãaŋmɩn toor jũu, yɩ taa lɛ maala a kõro mamaalbie ɛ, a lɛ yɩn ba dɔ̃ɔ bɔ̃ɔ ɛ.

eng: you must be holy in every aspect of your lives, just as the one who called you is holy.
local: Tɩchɛ nɩtãa lɛ a 'lɔ na bʊɔl yɩ na ɩ chɛchɛ, yɩ mɩ ɩ chɛchɛ a yɩ yelmaalsɩ a jaa pɔ.

eng: It is written, You will be holy, because I am holy. .
local: Bojũu a sɛb ɩka, Yɩ ɩ chɛchɛ, bojũu n ɩn chɛchɛ.

eng: Since you call upon a Father who judges all people according to their actions without favoritism, you should conduct yourselves with reverence during the time of your dwelling in a strange land.
local: Yɩn ma bʊɔlɔ wʊ a yɩ Sãa, 'lɔ lɛ na tɔɔr nɩrɛ jaa 'yɛr, na waa turo ɛ, yɩ kpɩɛrɛ a yɩ kpɛfʊ nɩtãa sãanba, na yɩ joro wʊ dãbãɛ.

eng: Live in this way, knowing that you were not liberated by perishable things like silver or gold from the empty lifestyle you inherited from your ancestors.
local: Bojũu yɩ bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka a ba ɩ bʊ̃sɔ̃ɔnãa fɩka libir, bɩɩ sɛlma lɛ Nãaŋmɩn de yab den yɩ yin a yɩ sãakpãmɩne yelpɔrsɩ ala na lũ yɩ pɔ ɛ.

eng: Instead, you were liberated by the precious blood of Christ, like that of a flawless, spotless lamb.
local: tɩchɛ a ɩ naa a Kɩrɩsɩto jãɩ vɩla ala na ɩ a pɛraabile na ba tara chɛfʊ ɛ, bɩɩ jãnɛ jaa ɛ.

eng: Christ was chosen before the creation of the world, but was only revealed at the end of time. This was done for you.
local: Nãaŋmɩn ir wʊ naa tɩ wa bɩɛr maal a wɛr, tɩchɛ wʊ vɛ̃n ka wʊ nyɩr a sɔ̃ɔ baaraa a yɩ jũu.

eng: who through Christ are faithful to the God who raised him from the dead and gave him glory. So now, your faith and hope should rest in God.
local: A wʊ jũu lɛ yɩ sɔɔ de a Nãaŋmɩn na ir wʊ a kũu pɔ na wʊ kʊ wʊ tɩɩr, ka a yɩ sɔɔfʊ nɩ a yɩ tɩɛrtɔ na be a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: As you set yourselves apart by your obedience to the truth so that you might have genuine affection for your fellow believers, love each other deeply and earnestly.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã yɩn chɔɔr a yɩ mãɛ yɩn turo a yelmãɛ jũu, yɩ nʊ̃nɔ̃ naa a yɛɛr sɩrɛ jaa. Yɩ nʊ̃nɔ̃ taa nɩ a yɩ nyãa wʊ jaa.

eng: Do this because you have been given new birth—not from the type of seed that decays but from seed that doesnt. This seed is Gods life-giving and enduring word.
local: Bojũu yɩ nyɛn dɔɔ paalaa 'lɔ na ba ɩ bʊ̃kpiraa ɛ, tɩchɛ wʊn ben be, na ba tu a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr na ɩ vʊr, na be be bɩbir jaa.

eng: Thus, All human life on the earth is like grass, and all human glory is like a flower in a field. The grass dries up and its flower falls off, .
local: Bojũu, Nɩsaal kaara naa fɩka mʊɔr, ka a ba tɩɩr wʊ jaa kaara fɩka a mɔpuul. A mʊɔr wa jʊɔl, a puul mɩ ma suor naa.

eng: but the Lords word endures forever. This is the word that was proclaimed to you as good news.
local: Tɩchɛ a sɩ Soro 'yɛr 'lɔ ben be bɩbir jaa. A 'yɛr nyãna lɛ ba mʊɔl kʊ yɩ.

eng: Therefore, get rid of all ill will and all deceit, pretense, envy, and slander.
local: A lɛ jũu, yɩ ir yɩ mãɛ yin a yosɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr, bɛlfʊ, nɛɛr ayi tarfʊ, nyuur, nɩ kporfʊ jaa bʊrɔ pɔ.

eng: Instead, like a newborn baby, desire the pure milk of the word. Nourished by it, you will grow into salvation.
local: Yɩ kaara fɩka bipɩlɛ, na yɩ bʊɔrɔ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr ka ɩ yɩ vɔlaa a bɩr kaar, a yɩn tʊ̃ɔ nʊ̃ɔ a yɩ faafʊ pɔ.

eng: since you have tasted that the Lord is good.
local: bojũu an ɩ yɩ lɛm kaa lɛ a sɩ Soro na nʊ̃mɔ̃.

eng: Now you are coming to him as to a living stone. Even though this stone was rejected by humans, from Gods perspective it is chosen, valuable.
local: An ɩ yɩ wa naa a wʊ sɛ̃ na ɩ a Kʊsɩr vʊr a nɩsaal na tɔr bar, tɩchɛ ka a Nãaŋmɩn ir wʊ a wʊ ɩ bʊ̃sʊ̃.

eng: You yourselves are being built like living stones into a spiritual temple. You are being made into a holy priesthood to offer up spiritual sacrifices that are acceptable to God through Jesus Christ.
local: yɩn mɩ na ɩ fɩka a kʊsɩvʊɛ a ba mɛn yɩ, nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn yir ka yɩ ɩ a wʊ bɔɔrlo chɛchɛsɩ, na yɩ loro a wʊ bɔɔr wʊn sɔɔ tu a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: Thus it is written in scripture, Look! I am laying a cornerstone in Zion, chosen, valuable. The person who believes in him will never be shamed. .
local: A sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, Nyɛ, n bin a kʊsɩr be a Jiyon tẽe pɔ, a kʊsɩr vɩla 'lɔ ɩn kaa ir ka wʊ ɩ a kʊsɩr ju, ka nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na sɔɔ de wʊ, vã kʊ̃ di wʊ ɛ.

eng: So God honors you who believe. For those who refuse to believe, though, the stone the builders tossed aside has become the capstone.
local: Pʊ̃pãanyã a yɩn bala na sɔɔ de wʊ, wʊ ɩn kʊsɩr sʊ̃. Tɩchɛ a bala na ba sɔɔ de wʊ ɛ, A kʊsɩr 'lɔ a mɩmɩɛrbɛ na tɔr bar, 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a kʊsɩr ju.

eng: This is a stone that makes people stumble and a rock that makes them fall. Because they refuse to believe in the word, they stumble. Indeed, this is the end to which they were appointed.
local: na, Wʊ ɩ a kʊsɩr 'lɔ a nɩbɛ na ŋmiere a gbɛɛ, na wʊ ɩ a pie 'lɔ ban bãra loro. Ba ŋmiere naa ba gbɛɛ bojũu ba tɔr naa a 'yɛr 'lɔ 'yɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn na dɔ̃ɔ bin ba.

eng: But you are a chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a people who are Gods own possession. You have become this people so that you may speak of the wonderful acts of the one who called you out of darkness into his amazing light.
local: Tɩchɛ yɩn 'lɔ, Nãaŋmɩn lɛ ir yɩ a yɩ ɩ a wʊ bɔɔrlo nãmɩne, na yɩ ɩ nɩchɛchɛsɩ, na yɩ ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn sofʊ, ka yɩ na pɛɛrɛ wʊ, an ɩ 'lɔ lɛ bʊɔl yɩ yin a libe pɔ waan a wʊ chãa herete pɔ.

eng: Once you werent a people, but now you are Gods people. Once you hadnt received mercy, but now you have received mercy.
local: Yaa dɔ̃ɔ ɩ a wʊ nɩbɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ pʊ̃pãanyã yɩ ɩ naa a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ. Yaa dɔ̃ɔ tara a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbaal ɛ, tɩchɛ pʊ̃pãanyã yɩ tara wʊ naa.

eng: Dear friends, since you are immigrants and strangers in the world, I urge that you avoid worldly desires that wage war against your lives.
local: N barmɩne, n sʊɔrɔ yɩ naa nɩtãa nɩyʊɔrsɩ nɩ sãanba ka a wɛr pɔ ɩka, yɩ ir yɩ mãɛ yin a ãgãn vɔlaa na ma jɛɛrɛ nɩ a yɩ sɩɩr.

eng: Live honorably among the unbelievers. Today, they defame you, as if you were doing evil. But in the day when God visits to judge they will glorify him, because they have observed your honorable deeds.
local: Yɩ kpɩɛrɛ vɩla a bala na ba puoro sɛ̃, ban sɔɔ gba kpɛrɛ yɩ ɩka dɛɛr lɛ yɩ maala, bala wa nyɛ a yɩ vɩla maalfʊ ban 'yɔ̃ɔn Nãaŋmɩn tɩɩr a wʊ waa bɩbir.

eng: For the sake of the Lord submit to every human institution. Do this whether it means submitting to the emperor as supreme ruler.
local: A sɩ Soro jũu, yɩ siir yɩ mãɛ kʊ a tʊ̃ɔfʊ dẽme, ala sɔɔ ɩ a nãkpɛ̃ɛ 'lɔ lɛ.

eng: or to governors as those sent by the emperor. They are sent to punish those doing evil and to praise those doing good.
local: bɩɩ a nãbil bala wʊn ir bin a tẽn tẽn ka ba kpãana a bala na maala dɛɛr tɩ pɛɛrɛ a bala mɩ na maala a lɛ na tor.

eng: Submit to them because its Gods will that by doing good you will silence the ignorant talk of foolish people.
local: Bojũu Nãaŋmɩn bɔfʊ lɛ ɩka yɩn maala vɩla na yɩ pɔɔ a ba bɔ̃ɔ dẽme na ɩ a jɔlɔ nɛɛr.

eng: Do this as Gods slaves, and yet also as free people, not using your freedom as a cover-up for evil.
local: Yɩ kpɩɛrɛ nɩtãa sumɩne, tɩchɛ yɩ taa de a mãɛ sofʊ nyã pɔɔn yelbier ɛ, tɩ yɩ kpɩɛrɛ fɩka Nãaŋmɩn gbãgbaar.

eng: Honor everyone. Love the family of believers. Have respectful fear of God. Honor the emperor.
local: Yɩ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ nɩrɛ jaa na yɩ nʊ̃ a yɩ taa Yesu turbo, na yɩ joro Nãaŋmɩn dãbãɛ, tɩ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a nãa.

eng: Household slaves, submit by accepting the authority of your masters with all respect. Do this not only to good and kind masters but also to those who are harsh.
local: Gbãgbaar, yɩ siir yɩ mãɛ kʊ a yɩ tʊ̃nbɔ nɩ 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ, a ta ɩ a bala na bal nɩbir maala yɩ vɩla tɛɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ a bala mɩ na kpɛ̃mɛ a nɩbir.

eng: Now, it is commendable if, because of ones understanding of God, someone should endure pain through suffering unjustly.
local: Bojũu a Nãaŋmɩn na maal vɩla kʊ nɩrɛ 'lɔ na tara tɩɛr vɩla, na wʊ di dɔɔyɛ na ba tara sɔr ɛ, bojũu wʊ tara na Nãaŋmɩn tɩɛr.

eng: But what praise comes from enduring patiently when you have sinned and are beaten for it? But if you endure steadfastly when youve done good and suffer for it, this is commendable before God.
local: Tɩchɛ bo lɛ na ɩ a yɩ tɔ̃nɔ̃, ala ɩ ba ŋme yɩ naa yɩn tʊ̃ dɛɛr jũu? Tɩchɛ ala ɩ yɩ din dɔɔyɛ yɩn maal vɩla jũu, an ɩn tɔ̃nɔ̃ kʊ yɩ Nãaŋmɩn niem.

eng: You were called to this kind of endurance, because Christ suffered on your behalf. He left you an example so that you might follow in his footsteps.
local: A anyãna jũu lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn bʊɔl yɩ, bojũu Kɩrɩsɩto mɩ dɔ̃ɔ di dɔɔyɛ, na wʊ maal wiil yɩ ɩka yɩ turo a wʊ pala.

eng: He committed no sin, nor did he ever speak in ways meant to deceive.
local: Ka waa dɔ̃ɔ maal dɛɛr jaa ɛ, bɩɩ ka bɛlfʊ yi a wʊ nɛɛ pɔ ɛ.

eng: When he was insulted, he did not reply with insults. When he suffered, he did not threaten revenge. Instead, he entrusted himself to the one who judges justly.
local: A lɛ ban dɔ̃ɔ tʊrɔ wʊ, waa mɩ tʊ yaa a sãn ɛ. A dɔɔyɛ na pɔɔ wʊ, waa jo dãbãɛ ɛ. Tɩchɛ wʊ den a wʊ mãɛ kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn na tɔɔrɔ a 'yɛr jaa dẽ dẽ.

eng: He carried in his own body on the cross the sins we committed. He did this so that we might live in righteousness, having nothing to do with sin. By his wounds you were healed.
local: A wʊ mãɛ lɛ tuo a sɩ yeldɛbɛ a wʊ ãgãn pɔ a daa ju, ka sɩ mɩ na kpi kʊ yelbier tɩ be be kʊ yeltorsɩ. A wʊ fɛlɛ ala jũu lɛ sãa sɩ.

eng: Though you were like straying sheep, you have now returned to the shepherd and guardian of your lives.
local: Na yɩ kaara fɩka piir na dɔ̃ɔ bɔrɔ, tɩchɛ pʊ̃pãanyã yɩ lɩɛb wa a pɛchinɛ na kaara a yɩ sɩɩr sɛ̃.

eng: Wives, likewise, submit to your own husbands. Do this so that even if some of them refuse to believe the word, they may be won without a word by their wives way of life.
local: Pɔbɔ, a lɛ nɩ wʊ ju mɩ lɛ, yɩ sɩɩr yɩ mãɛ kʊ a yɩ sɩrbɛ. Bamɩne baa sɔɔ puor ɛ, tɩchɛ a yɩ befʊ na tʊ̃ɔn lɩɛb a ba tɩɛr a ba puoro.

eng: After all, they will have observed the reverent and holy manner of your lives.
local: bala wa nyɛ a lɛ a yɩ maalfʊ na ɩ vɩla, nɩ lɛ yɩn 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a yɩ mãɛ.

eng: Dont try to make yourselves beautiful on the outside, with stylish hair or by wearing gold jewelry or fine clothes.
local: Yɩ ta vɛ̃ ka a yɩ vɩla ɩ a yõo pãɛfʊ tɛɛ ɛ, nɩtãa a ju wobfʊ, sɛlma bɔ̃jɔrsɩ yarfʊ, nɩ a fɔnɩbikpãɛn sɛfʊ ɛ.

eng: Instead, make yourselves beautiful on the inside, in your hearts, with the enduring quality of a gentle, peaceful spirit. This type of beauty is very precious in Gods eyes.
local: Tɩchɛ yɩ vɛ̃ ka a yɩ pãɛfʊ be pʊɔm na ba jʊɔlɔ ɛ, na ɩ vɩla, hɛ lɛ lɛ lɛ, nɩ jomm, ala lɛ ɩ vɩla a Nãaŋmɩn niem.

eng: For it was in this way that holy women who trusted in God used to make themselves beautiful, accepting the authority of their own husbands.
local: Bojũu nɩtɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ a mama pɔɔsʊ̃n bala na tara a Nãaŋmɩn tɩɛr ma maal a ba mãɛ vɩla. Na ba sɔɔ turo, na ba sɩɩr ba mãɛ kʊ a ba sɩrbɛ.

eng: For example, Sarah accepted Abrahams authority when she called him master . You have become her children when you do good and dont respond to threats with fear.
local: Lɛ mɩ lɛ a Saara na dɔ̃ɔ wõnõ a Abraham toor na wʊ ma bʊɔlɔ wʊ a wʊ soro. Yɩn lɛ ɩ a wʊ bibiir, ala ɩ yɩ maala na a lɛ na vɩɛlɛ na yɩ kʊ̃ joro dãbãɛ ɛ.

eng: Husbands, likewise, submit by living with your wife in ways that honor her, knowing that she is the weaker partner. Honor her all the more, as she is also a coheir of the gracious care of life. Do this so that your prayers wont be hindered.
local: Sɩrbɛ a lɛ nɩ wʊ ju mɩ lɛ, yɩ tɩɛrɛ a yɩ pɔbɔ yɩn lɔ̃ɔ kpɩɛrɛ nɩ. Yɩ kaara ba nɩ 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ, bojũu baa tara kpãɛn jã yɩ ɛ, tɩ na ba pʊɔ yɩ a Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ kʊfʊ 'lɔ na ɩ nyɛvʊr, ka bon jaa taa var a yɩ Nãaŋmɩn sʊɔrfʊ ɛ.

eng: Finally, all of you be of one mind, sympathetic, lovers of your fellow believers, compassionate, and modest in your opinion of yourselves.
local: A 'yɛr baaraa lɛ a anyã, yɩ jaa yɩ tara tɩɛr bʊ̃'yen, na yɩ kaara taa nɩbaal, na yɩ nʊ̃ taa maala vɩla kʊrɔ taa nɩ mãɛ siirfʊ.

eng: Dont pay back evil for evil or insult for insult. Instead, give blessing in return. You were called to do this so that you might inherit a blessing.
local: Yɩ ta de yelbier yãan yelbier sãn ɛ, bɩɩ tʊbir yãan tʊbir sãn ɛ, tɩchɛ yɩ 'yɛr nɛvɩla 'yɔ̃ɔ ba, bojũu a anyã 'yɔ̃ɔ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn bʊɔl yɩ ɩka yɩn nyɛn sʊ̃ʊfʊ.

eng: For those who want to love life and see good days should keep their tongue from evil speaking and their lips from speaking lies. .
local: Bojũu, Ala ɩ nɩrɛ nʊ̃n nyɛvʊr na wʊ bʊɔrɔ bɩbivɩɛlsɩ wʊ ir wʊ nɛɛ yin 'yɛrdɛɛr nɩ bɛlfʊ pɔ.

eng: They should shun evil and do good; seek peace and chase after it. .
local: Wʊ lɩɛb puor 'yɔ̃ɔ yelbier tɩ maala vɩla, na wʊ bʊɔrɔ nyã'maar joro turo a wʊ puor.

eng: The Lords eyes are on the righteous and his ears are open to their prayers. But the Lord cannot tolerate those who do evil. .
local: Bojũu a sɩ Soro kaara naa a bala na tor, na wʊ wõnõ a ba sʊɔrfʊ. Tɩchɛ a sɩ Soro pʊɔ ma sɔ̃ɔ naa nɩ a bala na ma maala yelbier.

eng: Who will harm you if you are zealous for good?.
local: An lɛ na ge yɩ ala ɩ yɩ mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir maala vɩla?.

eng: But happy are you, even if you suffer because of righteousness! Dont be terrified or upset by them.
local: Tɩchɛ ala ɩ yɩ gba din dɔɔyɛ yɩn maala vɩla jũu, Nãaŋmɩn na sʊ̃ʊ yɩ naa. Yɩ ta joro dãbãɛ nɩ a ba vuurfʊ ɛ. Yɩ taa joro dãbãɛ ɛ.

eng: Instead, regard Christ the Lord as holy in your hearts. Whenever anyone asks you to speak of your hope, be ready to defend it.
local: Tɩchɛ a yɩ nyãn pɔ, yɩ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a Kɩrɩsɩto na ɩ a yɩ Soro. Na yɩ ma chɔɔr sɔ̃ɔ jaa ala ɩ nɩrɛ wa sʊʊr yɩ naa a tɩɛrtɔ 'lɔ yɩn tara, tɩchɛ yɩ ma man naa jomm nɩ 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ.

eng: Yet do this with respectful humility, maintaining a good conscience. Act in this way so that those who malign your good lifestyle in Christ may be ashamed when they slander you.
local: na yɩ vɛ̃ ka a yɩ tɩɛr chaar, ka a bala na sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a yɩ yuor yɩn turo a Kɩrɩsɩto jũu, ka vã na kpɛ ba, nɩ a ba yosɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr.

eng: It is better to suffer for doing good than for doing evil.
local: An ɩn vɩla ala ɩ Nãaŋmɩn bɔfʊ lɛ, yɩn di dɔɔyɛ vɩla maalfʊ jũu, gar yɩn na di dɔɔyɛ dɛɛr maalfʊ jũu.

eng: Christ himself suffered on account of sins, once for all, the righteous one on behalf of the unrighteous. He did this in order to bring you into the presence of God. Christ was put to death as a human, but made alive by the Spirit.
local: Bojũu Kɩrɩsɩto kpi naa a yɩ yelbier jũu gbɛbʊ̃'yen tɛɛ, a 'lɔ na tor kʊ a bala na ba tor ɛ, ɩka wʊ waan yɩ Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃. Ba kʊ naa a wʊ ãgãn, tɩchɛ ka a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ sãɩ wʊ a kũu pɔ.

eng: And it was by the Spirit that he went to preach to the spirits in prison.
local: a Sɩɛ nyã lɛ a Kɩrɩsɩto chiin a kpĩi tẽe na wʊ tɩ mʊɔl kʊ a kpĩisɩ bala na be a pɔɔfʊ die pɔ.

eng: In the past, these spirits were disobedient—when God patiently waited during the time of Noah. Noah built an ark in which a few lives were rescued through water.
local: A banyãna lɛ tɔr a Nãaŋmɩn nɛɛ, tɩchɛ ka wʊ di kãnyir ka a Nowa wa maal a daka bulomtum baar. A nɩbɛ bãlãa na ɩ aniin tɛɛ wa nyɛn faafʊ a kʊ̃ɔ pɔ.

eng: Baptism is like that. It saves you now—not because it removes dirt from your body but because it is the mark of a good conscience toward God. Your salvation comes through the resurrection of Jesus Christ.
local: A kʊ̃ɔ nyã nyɛtãan fɩka a Nãaŋmɩn kʊ̃ɔ sofʊ na faa yɩ mɩ a pʊ̃pãanyã, a ba ɩ ãgãn dɛɛr pɛɛ barfʊ lɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ a nɛɛ yɩn tɩr kʊ Nãaŋmɩn nɩ pʊtɩvɩla. Yɩ nyɛn faafʊ tu a Kɩrɩsɩto kũu irfʊ pɔ.

eng: who is at Gods right side. Now that he has gone into heaven, he rules over all angels, authorities, and powers.
local: 'Lɔ lɛ do a saaju tɩ jãnɛ, a Nãaŋmɩn nũsʊ̃ɔ jie ka a malakasɩ, nɩ a tʊ̃ɔfʊ dẽme, nɩ a kpãɛn dẽme ba jaa turo a wʊ nɛɛ.

eng: Therefore, since Christ suffered as a human, you should also arm yourselves with his way of thinking. This is because whoever suffers is finished with sin.
local: A lɛ jũu, a Kɩrɩsɩto na di dɔɔyɛ a wʊ ãgãn pɔ, yɩ mɩ su a tãjɛbonsɩ ala gun a tɩɛr 'lɔ̃nɔ̃, bojũu a 'lɔ na di dɔɔyɛ a wʊ ãgãn pɔ ba lɛ pʊɔ yelbier pɔ ɛ.

eng: As a result, they dont live the rest of their human lives in ways determined by human desires but in ways determined by Gods will.
local: Ala lɛ so, wʊ ta lɛ kpɩɛrɛ a wɛr pɔ nɩ a ãgãn vɔlaa ɛ, tɩchɛ wʊ kpɩɛrɛ a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na bʊɔrɔ.

eng: You have wasted enough time doing what unbelievers desire—living in their unrestrained immorality and lust, their drunkenness and excessive feasting and wild parties, and their forbidden worship of idols.
local: Bojũu a kõro jaa, yɩ mɩ sɔ̃ɔn sɔ̃ɔ na yɩ maala a lɛ a bala na ba puoro ɛ, na maala, na baa tʊɔrɔ a ba mãɛ nyɔɔrɔ ɛ. Ka a ãgãn vɔlaa, nɩ dãburo, nɩ a pepe dib, na yɩ pʊɔ a dãnyurbo gbul pɔ, na yɩ maala tɩbɛ na sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a Nãaŋmɩn nɛɛ.

eng: They think its strange that you dont join in these activities with the same flood of unrestrained wickedness. So they slander you.
local: Ban tɩɛr naa ɩka yelsãan lɛ, yɩn ba lɛ pʊɔ ba a befʊ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ pɔ ɛ, ka ba pãa piel tʊrɔ yɩ.

eng: They will have to reckon with the one who is ready to judge the living and the dead.
local: Tɩchɛ ban man a ba nɛɛ 'yɛr kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn na wa tɔɔr a vʊɛ nɩ a kpĩisɩ 'yɛr.

eng: Indeed, this is the reason the good news was also preached to the dead. This happened so that, although they were judged as humans according to human standards, they could live by the Spirit according to divine standards.
local: A anyãna jũu lɛ so ka ba gba mɩ mʊɔl a 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ kʊ a kpĩisɩ, ɩka ban tɔɔr a ba 'yɛr nɩtãa lɛ ban na tɔɔr nɩrɛ jaa 'yɛr, tɩchɛ ka ba kpɩɛrɛ a sɩɩr pɔ Nãaŋmɩn kaar.

eng: The end of everything has come. Therefore, be self-controlled and clearheaded so you can pray.
local: A bonsɩ a jaa baarfʊ tara naa, a lɛ jũu yɩ mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir na yɩ kpãa a yɩ mãɛ tʊ̃ɔ sʊɔrɔ Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: Above all, show sincere love to each other, because love brings about the forgiveness of many sins.
local: A ala na gar a jaa lɛ a anyã, yɩ chɛnɛ vɛ̃ ka a nʊ̃fʊ be a yɩ pɔ, bojũu nʊ̃fʊ ma pɔɔn yeldɛbɛ yɔɔ.

eng: Open your homes to each other without complaining.
local: Yɩ ma diere taa a yɩ yie tɩ ta ma ɩrɛ gbʊrɔ̃ gbʊrɔ̃ ɛ.

eng: And serve each other according to the gift each person has received, as good managers of Gods diverse gifts.
local: Ɩ̃sʊɔ jaa wʊ de a Nãaŋmɩn kʊfʊ wʊn nyɛ tʊ̃nɔ̃ kʊrɔ nɩ a wʊ taaba nɩ yelmãɛ nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn wãɛfʊ a jaa.

eng: Whoever speaks should do so as those who speak Gods word. Whoever serves should do so from the strength that God furnishes. Do this so that in everything God may be honored through Jesus Christ. To him be honor and power forever and always. Amen.
local: Ala ɩ nɩrɛ jaa 'yɛrɛ na, wʊ 'yɛrɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛrbie ala. Ala ɩ wʊ ɩn tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃, wʊ tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ a kpɛ̃ɔ 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn na kʊ wʊ, ka a yele a jaa pɔ, ka Nãaŋmɩn nyɛ tɩɩr tu a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto pɔ. 'Lɔ lɛ so a tɩɩr nɩ kpɛ̃ɔ bɩbir bɩbir jaa. A ɩ a lɛ.

eng: Dear friends, dont be surprised about the fiery trials that have come among you to test you. These are not strange happenings.
local: N yɛɛr nʊ̃nʊ̃nbɔ, yɩ ta vɛ̃ ka nɛɛ 'maa yɩ nɩ a bɛɛr kaa tuo na vulo yɩ fɩka yelsãan kɔ̃ɔ lɛ ɩrɛ yɩ ɛ.

eng: Instead, rejoice as you share Christs suffering. You share his suffering now so that you may also have overwhelming joy when his glory is revealed.
local: Tɩchɛ yɩ chɩlɛ an ɩ yɩ pʊɔ naa a Kɩrɩsɩto dɔɔyɛ difʊ pɔ, ka nʊ̃ɔ na kpɛ yɩ a wʊ tɩɩr wa nyɩr.

eng: If you are mocked because of Christs name, you are blessed, for the Spirit of glory—indeed, the Spirit of God—rests on you.
local: Ala ɩ ba tʊ yɩ naa yɩn bʊɔlɔ a Kɩrɩsɩto yuor jũu, Nãaŋmɩn na sʊ̃ʊ yɩ naa, bojũu Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ ben a yɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: Now none of you should suffer as a murderer or thief or evildoer or rebel.
local: Ala ɩ yɩ din dɔɔyɛ, a ta ɩ fɩka nɩkʊrɔ, bɩɩ nãnyie, bɩɩ nɩdɛɛr, bɩɩ tɔ'yɔ̃ɔnbɔ lɛ yɩ ɩ ɛ.

eng: But dont be ashamed if you suffer as one who belongs to Christ. Rather, honor God as you bear Christs name. Give honor to God.
local: Tɩchɛ ala ɩ a yɩ ãsʊɔ jaa wa dire na dɔɔyɛ wʊn turo Kɩrɩsɩto jũu, wʊ ta vɛ̃ ka vã kpɛ wʊ ɛ, tɩchɛ wʊ pɛɛrɛ Nãaŋmɩn a yuor nyã wʊn di jũu.

eng: because its time for judgment to begin with Gods own household. But if judgment starts with us, what will happen to those who refuse to believe Gods good news?.
local: Bojũu a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ sɔ̃ɔ ta naa, ka Nãaŋmɩn na piel tɔɔrɔ a wʊ nɩbɛ 'yɛr. Ala ɩ sɩn lɛ wʊn piel, ŋmɩnɛ lɛ an kaara kʊ a bala na tɔr a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr nʊ̃ɔ?.

eng: If the righteous are barely rescued, what will happen to the godless and sinful?.
local: A sɛb ɩka, Ala ɩ an ɩn kpɛ̃ɔ kʊ a nɩtor, ŋmɩnɛ lɛ an kaara kʊ a bala na ba puor ɛ, nɩ a yelbe-ɩrbɛ.

eng: So then, those who suffer because they follow Gods will should commit their lives to a trustworthy creator by doing what is right.
local: A lɛ jũu, vɛ̃ a bala na dire a dɔɔyɛ a Nãaŋmɩn bɔfʊ jũu, de ba mãɛ kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn yelmãɛ sʊɔ na mɛ ba, tɩ mʊ̃ɔ nɩbir maala vɩla.

eng: Therefore, I have a request for the elders among you. I urge the elders.
local: A maa na ɩ a nɩdiere naa nyɛ a Kɩrɩsɩto dɔɔyɛ nɩ nɩbir, naa mɩ ɩ nɩrɛ nyã na pʊɔ a tɩɩr 'lɔ na wa nyɩr, n 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ na a nɩdierbe na be be a yɩ pɔ ãkpãɛn.

eng: Like shepherds, tend the flock of God among you. Watch over it. Dont shepherd because you must, but do it voluntarily for God. Dont shepherd greedily, but do it eagerly.
local: ɩka ba kaara a Nãaŋmɩn piir a wʊn de 'yɔ̃ɔ a yɩ nuur pɔ, na yɩ tʊ̃nɔ̃ kaara nɩ ba, a ta ɩ fɩrfʊ ɛ, tɩchɛ a ɩ yɩ bɔfʊ, bojũu lɛ lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn bʊɔrɔ. A ta ɩ libie 'yɔ̃ɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ ka a nʊ̃mɔ̃ yɩ a tʊ̃fʊ.

eng: Dont shepherd by ruling over those entrusted to your care, but become examples to the flock.
local: Yɩ ta pirpire a bala yɩn kaar ɛ, tɩchɛ yɩ ɩ jãnɛ kʊ a piir.

eng: And when the chief shepherd appears, you will receive an unfading crown of glory.
local: A pɛchiin gbɔɔraa wa wa, yɩn nyɛn a sãyar na ɩ a tɩɩr na ba jʊɔl ɛ.

eng: In the same way, I urge you who are younger: accept the authority of the elders. And everyone, clothe yourselves with humility toward each other. God stands against the proud, but he gives favor to the humble.
local: Lɛ nɩ wʊ ju mɩ lɛ a yɩn bala na ɩ a pɔlbil, yɩ siir yɩ mãɛ kʊ a yɩ nɩdierbe. Yɩ jaa jaa yɩ ma siir yɩ mãɛ na yɩ ma tʊ̃nɔ̃ kʊrɔ taa, bojũu a sɛb ɩka, Nãaŋmɩn 'laar a bala na 'mɔɔrɔ a ba mãɛ, tɩ ma sʊ̃nɔ̃ a bala na ma siir ba mãɛ.

eng: Therefore, humble yourselves under Gods power so that he may raise you up in the last day.
local: A lɛ jũu, yɩ siir yɩ mãɛ a Nãaŋmɩn kpɛ̃ɔ sʊɔ nuur pɔ, ka wʊ 'mɔɔ yɩ a yɩ do a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ wʊn bʊɔrɔ.

eng: Throw all your anxiety onto him, because he cares about you.
local: Yɩ de a yɩ yelwõnsɩ a jaa kʊ wʊ, bojũu 'lɔ lɛ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a yɩ tɔɔ.

eng: Be clearheaded. Keep alert. Your accuser, the devil, is on the prowl like a roaring lion, seeking someone to devour.
local: Yɩ gu yɩ mãɛ na yɩ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ jũmɔ̃, a yɩ dɔ̃sʊɔ tɩɩ kaara naa jukpɛ̃ɛ kaar, na uoro yʊɔrɔ na wʊ pãa kaara bʊɔrɔ nɩrɛ ɩka wʊ nyɔɔ 'wɔb.

eng: Resist him, standing firm in the faith. Do so in the knowledge that your fellow believers are enduring the same suffering throughout the world.
local: Yɩ var wʊ sɔr, na yɩ ba gbɛr ara a yɩ sɔɔfʊ pɔ, bojũu yɩ bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka a yɩ taaba na be a wɛr wʊ jaa, mɩ dire na a dɔɔyɛ nyãna tɔ yɩn dire.

eng: After you have suffered for a little while, the God of all grace, the one who called you into his eternal glory in Christ Jesus, will himself restore, empower, strengthen, and establish you.
local: Yɩn wa di a dɔɔyɛ nyãna bãlãa a Nãaŋmɩn na ɩ a wãɛfʊ sʊɔ a 'lɔ na bʊɔl yɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ a tɩɩr nyã na ba tara baarfʊ pɔ a Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ, wʊ mãɛ na maal yɩ naa na wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ yɩ ãkpɛ̃ɔ tɩ na wʊ vɛ̃ a yɩ ara kpɛ̃ɔ kʊ̃ dãm ɛ.

eng: To him be power forever and always. Amen.
local: 'Lɔ lɛ so a kpɛ̃ɔ bɩbir nɩ bɩbir jaa. A ɩ a lɛ.

eng: I have written and sent these few lines to you by Silvanus. I consider him to be a faithful brother. In these lines I have urged and affirmed that this is the genuine grace of God. Stand firm in it.
local: Silasɩ na ɩ a yɛbɛ yelmãɛ sʊɔ lɛ sʊ̃ʊ ma ka n sɛb a gãn bile nyãna, naa 'yɔ̃ɔn yɩ ãkpãɛn din dãasɩɛ ɩka anyãna lɛ ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn sɩrɛ wãɛfʊ. Yɩ ara kpɛ̃ɔ.

eng: The fellow-elect church in Babylon greets you, and so does my son Mark.
local: A yɩ taaba pupuorbiir na be a Babilon Nãaŋmɩn na 'mataa ir yɩ, puor yɩ naa, ka a n bidaba Maakɩ mɩ puoro yɩ.

eng: Greet each other with the kiss of love. Peace to you all who are in Christ.
local: Yɩ puor puor taa nɩ nʊ̃fʊ. Nyã'maar wʊ be a yɩn bala na be a Kɩrɩsɩto 'yɔ̃ɔ.

eng: From Simon Peter, a slave and apostle of Jesus Christ. To those who received a faith equal to ours through the justice of our God and savior Jesus Christ.
local: Maa Simon Pita na ɩ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto gbãgbaa nɩ a wʊ nɩtʊ̃nãa lɛ sɛɛrɛ a gãn nyã kʊrɔ a yɩn na tu a sɩ Nãaŋmɩn yeltorsɩ pɔ, nɩ a sɩ Fafaara Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto na yɩ nyɛ a sɔɔfʊ 'lɔ na kpɛ̃mɛ a sɩ kaar.

eng: May you have more and more grace and peace through the knowledge of God and Jesus our Lord.
local: Wãɛfʊ nɩ nyã'maar a dɔɔlɔ yɩ taa tu a bɔ̃ɔfʊ yɩn bɔ̃ɔ Nãaŋmɩn nɩ a sɩ Soro Yesu.

eng: By his divine power the Lord has given us everything we need for life and godliness through the knowledge of the one who called us by his own honor and glory.
local: A Wʊ kpɛ̃ɔ lɛ wʊ kʊn sɩ bon jaa sɩn bʊɔrɔ a nyɛvʊr nyã jũu nɩ Nãaŋmɩn puor vɩla tu a sɩ bɔ̃ɔfʊ sɩn bɔ̃ɔ a 'lɔ na bʊɔl sɩ, nɩ a wʊ mãɛ tɩɩr nɩ a wʊ maal vɩla.

eng: Through his honor and glory he has given us his precious and wonderful promises, that you may share the divine nature and escape from the worlds immorality that sinful craving produces.
local: Tu anyãna pɔ lɛ wʊ kʊn sɩ a bon na bɛrma nɩ a bʊ̃sʊ̃n wʊn tɩr a nɛɛ ɩka, a tu a pɔ, ka yɩn pʊɔ a befʊ 'lɔ na ba sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ɛ, na yɩ jo yi a wɛr nyã a yelbier vɔlaa na sɔ̃ɔ.

eng: This is why you must make every effort to add moral excellence to your faith; and to moral excellence, knowledge.
