local: Ajienaa kaa ɩ Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ de ma naa ka n nyɛ a nãa dakɔɔ a be a saaju nɩrɛ kɔ̃ɔ na jãnɛ.

eng: The one seated there looked like jasper and carnelian, and surrounding the throne was a rainbow that looked like an emerald.
local: Ka a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na jãnɛ a nãa dakɔɔ ju nyɩlɛ jaa pala pala fɩka gar kʊsɩr, nɩ kʊsɩr wolo wolo, ka a sagõgoraa na nyɩlɛ ŋmaa jilʊ̃ a nãa dakɔɔ.

eng: I saw a powerful angel, who proclaimed in a loud voice, Who is worthy to open the scroll and break its seals.
local: N nyɛn a malaka kpɛ̃ɔ sʊɔ 'lɔ na de nɛkpɛ̃ɛ sʊʊr, An lɛ sɛɛ ɩka wʊ 'lɔɔr a jãnsɩ tɩ yuo a gãn.

eng: But no one in heaven or on earth or under the earth could open the scroll or look inside it.
local: Tɩchɛ nɩrɛ jaa ba be a saaju, bɩɩ a wɛr pɔ, bɩɩ a tẽe par na tʊ̃ɔ yuo a gãn kar ɛ.

eng: Twenty-four thrones, with twenty-four elders seated upon them, surrounded the throne. The elders were dressed in white clothing and had gold crowns on their heads.
local: Dakɔɔr lɩjɛr nɩ anaar mɩ ben be jilʊ̃ a nãa dakɔɔ, ka a nɩbɛrɛ lɩjɛr nɩ anaar mɩ jãnɛ a ju, na ba su bʊ̃pɩɛl na ba sun sɛlma jupile.

eng: So I began to weep and weep, because no one was found worthy to open the scroll or to look inside it.
local: Ka n kõ nɩbaal kõn bojũu nɩrɛ jaa na ba be be sɛɛn na yuo a gãn kar kaa ɛ.

eng: From the throne came lightning, voices, and thunder. In front of the throne were seven flaming torches, which are the seven spirits of God.
local: Saa nyɩɩrfʊ nɩ gɔ̃mɔ̃ na tãna yire naa a nãa dakɔɔ par. Ka a fãtɩnɛ ayopõi mɩ 'lɩrɛ a nãa dakɔɔ par, anyãna lɛ ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɩr ayopõi.

eng: Then one of the elders said to me, Dont weep. Look! The Lion of the tribe of Judah, the Root of David, has emerged victorious so that he can open the scroll and its seven seals.
local: Be lɛ a nɩbɛrɛ bala ãsʊɔ pãa 'yɛr kʊ ma, Ta kõnõ ɛ. Nyɛ a Jukpɛ̃ɛ 'lɔ na yi a Juda bʊrɔ pɔ, na ɩ a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ David yɔ̃ɔ na jɛb tʊ̃ɔ. 'Lɔ lɛ sɛɛ na tʊ̃ɔ 'lɔɔr a jãnsɩ ala bar tɩ yuo a gãn.

eng: Something like a glass sea, like crystal, was in front of the throne. In the center, by the throne, were four living creatures encircling the throne. These creatures were covered with eyes on the front and on the back.
local: A nãa dakɔɔ niem bon ben be kaara man kʊ̃ɔ kaar jaa wel wel wel, na wʊ nyɩlɛ chãana kaar. A bʊ̃nɩbie anaar jil naa a nãa dakɔɔ tara nɩbie a jiir a jaa a puor nɩ a niem.

eng: Then, in between the throne and the four living creatures and among the elders, I saw a Lamb, standing as if it had been slain. It had seven horns and seven eyes, which are Gods seven spirits, sent out into the whole earth.
local: N pãa lɛ nyɛn Pɛraabile ka wʊ nyɛtãan bon ban dɔ̃ɔ kʊ ka wʊ ara a nãa dakɔɔ par, ka a bʊ̃nɩbie anaar nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ ara jilʊ̃ wʊ. Wʊ tara na ɩlɛ ayopõi nɩ nɩbie ayopõi, ala lɛ ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɩr ayopõi wʊn ma tʊ̃ a wɛr pɔ jiir a jaa.

eng: The first living creature was like a lion. The second living creature was like an ox. The third living creature had a face like a human being. And the fourth living creature was like an eagle in flight.
local: A bʊ̃nɩbir 'lɔ na de nie nyɛtãan jukpɛ̃ɛ, ka a ayi sʊɔ mɩ nyɛtãan narbile, ka a ata sʊɔ mɩ nyɛtãan nɩsaal, ka a anaar sʊɔ mɩ nyɛtãan tuu.

eng: He came forward and took the scroll from the right hand of the one seated on the throne.
local: 'Lɔ lɛ tɩ de a gãn a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na jãnɛ a nãa dakɔɔ ju nũsʊ̃ɔ pɔ.

eng: Each of the four living creatures had six wings, and each was covered all around and on the inside with eyes. They never rest day or night, but keep on saying, Holy, holy, holy is the Lord God Almighty, who was and is and is coming.
local: A bʊ̃nɩbie anyãna ãsʊɔ jaa tara naa pɩbɛ ayʊɔb, ka a nɩbie pɔɔ a ãgãn wʊ jaa naa pɩbɛ pɔ. Ba ma yiele nɩ yiel mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ nɩ tãsɔɔ ɩka, Chɛchɛ, chɛchɛ, chɛchɛ 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a sɩ Soro Nãaŋmɩn nɩ a kpɛ̃ɔ jaa sʊɔ. 'Lɔ lɛ be be, 'lɔ lɛ ɩ, 'lɔ lɛ na wa.

eng: When he took the scroll, the four living creatures and the twenty-four elders fell down before the Lamb. Each held a harp and gold bowls full of incense, which are the prayers of the saints.
local: Wʊn wa de a gãn ka a bʊ̃nɩbie anaar bala nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ lɩjɛr nɩ anaar bala, ba jaa gãn kpa tẽe a Pɛraabile niem, ka ãsʊɔ jaa tara kõrõjom nɩ sɛlma lapʊɛ na sɛɛn a tãɩ nyũu vɩla, ala lɛ ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ sʊɔrfʊsɩ.

eng: Whenever the living creatures give glory, honor, and thanks to the one seated on the throne, who lives forever and always.
local: Sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ jaa a bʊ̃nɩbie ala na ma wa puoro pɛɛrɛ dãna nɩ a 'lɔ na jãnɛ a nãa dakɔɔ ju, 'lɔ lɛ be be jaa kpo kpo.

eng: They took up a new song, saying, You are worthy to take the scroll and open its seals, because you were slain, and by your blood you purchased for God persons from every tribe, language, people, and nation.
local: Bala lɛ yiele a yiel paalaa na ba 'yɛrɛ, Fʊ̃ʊ lɛ sɛɛn ɩka fʊ de a gãn na fʊ 'lɔɔr a jãnsɩ, bojũu fʊ̃ʊ lɛ ba kʊ, ka fʊ de a fʊ jãɩ yab den nɩbʊrɔ, 'yɛr bʊrɔ, balsɩ ba jaa nɩ a paalsɩ a jaa kʊ Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: You made them a kingdom and priests to our God, and they will rule on earth.
local: Fʊ̃ʊ lɛ maal ba ka ba ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ nɩbɛ nɩ a bɔɔrlorbo tʊ̃nɔ̃ kʊrɔ a sɩ Nãaŋmɩn, bala lɛ na wa ɩ a wɛr nyã wʊ jaa nãmɩne.

eng: the twenty-four elders fall before the one seated on the throne. They worship the one who lives forever and always. They throw down their crowns before the throne and say.
local: a nɩbɛrɛ lɩjɛr nɩ anaar bala gãn kpa tẽe tɩ puor a 'lɔ na jãnɛ a nãa dakɔɔ ju, 'lɔ lɛ na be be jaa kpo kpo. Ba yãan a ba nãjupile bin a nãa dakɔɔ par tɩ 'yɛrɛ.

eng: Then I looked, and I heard the sound of many angels surrounding the throne, the living creatures, and the elders. They numbered in the millions—thousands upon thousands.
local: Be lɛ n pãa kaa nyɛ malakasɩ turo turo fʊn kʊ̃ bɔ̃ɔ a ba nɛɛ ɛ, ka ba ara jilʊ̃ a nãa dakɔɔ nɩ a bʊ̃nɩbie ala nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ bala.

eng: You are worthy, our Lord and God, to receive glory and honor and power, because you created all things. It is by your will that they existed and were created.
local: A sɩ Soro nɩ a sɩ Nãaŋmɩn, fʊ̃ʊ lɛ a sɛɛn ɩka fʊ de a tɩɩr, 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ nɩ a kpɛ̃ɔ. Bojũu fʊ̃ʊ lɛ maal a bomo a jaa, a fʊ bɔfʊ pɔ lɛ a jaa maal naa be be.

eng: They said in a loud voice, Worthy is the slaughtered Lamb to receive power, wealth, wisdom, and might, and honor, glory, and blessing.
local: Ba yiele naa nɩ kɔkɔkpɛ̃ɛ 'yɛrɛ, A Pɛraabile 'lɔ ban dɔ̃ɔ kʊ lɛ sɛɛn ɩka wʊ tara a kpɛ̃ɔ nɩ a nãbomo, nɩ a yɛ̃, nɩ a fɔ̃ɔ, nɩ a 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ, nɩ a tɩɩr, nɩ a dãnfʊ.

eng: And I heard every creature in heaven and on earth and under the earth and in the sea—I heard everything everywhere say, Blessing, honor, glory, and power belong to the one seated on the throne and to the Lamb forever and always.
local: Be lɛ n wõ a bʊ̃maalsɩ a jaa na be a saaju, nɩ a tẽe, nɩ a tẽe par, nɩ a man pɔ, nɩ bomo a jaa na be a ba pɔ, na ba yiele yiel 'yɛrɛ, A 'lɔ na jãnɛ a nãa dakɔɔ ju, nɩ a Pɛraabile lɛ so a dãnfʊ, nɩ a 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ, nɩ a tɩɩr, nɩ a kpɛ̃ɔ, bɩbir jaa.

eng: Then the four living creatures said, Amen, and the elders fell down and worshipped.
local: Ka a bʊ̃nɩbie anaar bala 'yɛr, A ɩ a lɛ, ka a nɩbɛrɛ bala gbur dumo puor a 'lɔ na jãnɛ a nãa dakɔɔ ju nɩ a Pɛraabile.

eng: Then I looked on as the Lamb opened one of the seven seals. I heard one of the four living creatures say in a voice like thunder, Come.
local: Ka n kaa naa nyɛ a Pɛraabile na 'lɔɔ a jãnsɩ ayopõi ala ban mar a gãn 'lɔ na de nie, be lɛ n wõ a bʊ̃nɩbie anaar bala ãsʊɔ kɔkɔr fɩka saa na tãn, ka wʊ bʊɔl, Wa.

eng: So I looked, and there was a white horse. Its rider held a bow and was given a crown. And he went forth from victory to victory.
local: Be lɛ n kaa nyɛ ka a ɩ wii pɩla lɛ, ka a 'lɔ na do, jã tam a ba de nãa jupil kʊ wʊ ka wʊ yin darfʊ an ɩ tʊ̃ɔfʊ sʊɔ lɛ wʊ ɩ.

eng: When the Lamb opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature say, Come.
local: A Pɛraabile na lɛ 'lɔɔ a jãnsɩ ayi sʊ̃ɔ, n wõn a bʊ̃nɩbie ala ayi sʊɔ na 'yɛr, Wa.

eng: Out came another horse, fiery red. Its rider was allowed to take peace from the earth so that people would kill each other. He was given a large sword.
local: Ka a wii jɩɛ wa yi, a 'lɔ na do ba kʊ wʊn kpɛ̃ɔ ɩka wʊ faa a nyã'maar tɩ vɛ̃ ka jɛɛr ir a wɛr wʊ jaa, ka a nɩbɛ kʊrɔ taa. Ka ba kʊ wʊ a jɛɛr sɔkpɛ̃ɛ.

eng: When he opened the third seal, I heard the third living creature say, Come! So I looked, and there was a black horse. Its rider held a balance for weighing in his hand.
local: A Pɛraabile na lɛ 'lɔɔ a jãnsɩ ata sʊɔ, n wõn a bʊ̃nɩbie ata sʊɔ na 'yɛr, Wa! Ka n kaa ka a ɩ wii sɔɔlaa lɛ, ka a 'lɔ na do tara bʊ̃mansɩ a nũu pɔ.

eng: I heard what sounded like a voice from among the four living creatures. It said, A quart of wheat for a denarion, and three quarts of barley for a denarion, but dont damage the olive oil and the wine.
local: Be lɛ n wõ a bʊ̃nɩbie anaar bala chara pɔ fɩka kɔkɔr na 'yɛrɛ, Kɔ̃ lo naa, chi bʊ̃manãa nɛbʊ̃'yen na da naa nɩbʊ̃'yen bɩbir gbul sãyar. Ka a kamaana mɩ nɛɛr ata mɩ da nɩbʊ̃'yen bɩbir gbul sãyar, tɩ taa ɩrɛ wa sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a kãa nɩ a dãa bara ɛ.

eng: When he opened the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living creature say, Come.
local: A Pɛraabile na lɛ 'lɔɔ a jãnsɩ anaar sʊɔ, n wõ naa a bʊ̃nɩbie anaar sʊɔ na 'yɛr, Wa.

eng: So I looked, and there was a pale green horse. Its riders name was Death, and the Grave was following right behind. They were given authority over a fourth of the earth, to kill by sword, famine, disease, and the wild animals of the earth.
local: Ka n kaa nyɛ wii ul a nɩdaa. Ka a 'lɔ na do yuor di kũu, ka a kpĩisɩ tẽe mɩ turo a wʊ puor. Ba kʊ ban kpɛ̃ɔ ɩka ba de sɔkpɛ̃ɛ, nɩ kɔ̃, nɩ baal, nɩ mɔdʊ̃n kʊn a wɛr nɩbɛ ala ɩ ba põn a tẽe a wʊ jiir anaar.

eng: When he opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar those who had been slaughtered on account of the word of God and the witness they had given.
local: Wʊn lɛ 'lɔɔ a jãnsɩ anũu sʊɔ, n nyɛn a nɩsɩɩr bala ban dɔ̃ɔ kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr nɩ a wʊ dãasɩɛ jũu ka ba be a Nãaŋmɩn bɔɔr lofʊ jie pĩlem.

eng: They cried out with a loud voice, Holy and true Master, how long will you wait before you pass judgment? How long before you require justice for our blood, which was shed by those who live on earth.
local: Ba tãna naa nɩ nɛkpɛ̃ɛ 'yɛrɛ, A Tʊ̃ɔfʊ sʊɔ, nɩ chɛchɛ sʊɔ, nɩ yelmãɛ sʊɔ, a wa ta dabor ka fʊ tɔɔr a wɛr nɩbɛ 'yɛr na fʊ yab a sɩ jãɩ sãma.

eng: Each of them was given a white robe, and they were told to rest a little longer, until their fellow servants and brothers and sisters—who were about to be killed as they were—were finished.
local: Be lɛ ba kʊ ba jaa bʊ̃pɩɛl tɩ 'yɛr ɩka ba di kãnyir bãlãa ka ba baar kʊ a ba tʊ̃tʊ̃taaba nɩ a yɛɛr a ba kaar ka a ta.

eng: I looked on as he opened the sixth seal, and there was a great earthquake. The sun became black as funeral clothing, and the entire moon turned red as blood.
local: N kaara naa ka wʊ lɛ 'lɔɔ a jãnsɩ ayʊɔb sʊɔ. Ka a tẽe mĩi wʊ jaa, ka a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ sɔb fɔsɔɔlaa kaar, ka a ŋmɛraa mɩ lɩɛb jãɩ kaar jaa kpãn kpãn.

eng: The stars of the sky fell to the earth as a fig tree drops its fruit when shaken by a strong wind.
local: ka a ŋmɛrbie mɩ yi a saaju pɔrɔ loro a tẽe pɔ fɩka tɔ̃ɔ kara sɛsɛkpɛ̃ɛ na fu lɔb.

eng: The sky disappeared like a scroll being rolled up, and every mountain and island was moved from its place.
local: Be lɛ a saaju pilʊ̃ fɩka sɔ̃gãnãa ban pil, ka a tɔ̃n a jaa nɩ a dõdolsɩ a jaa mɩ ir a ba jiir.

eng: Then the kings of the earth, the officials and the generals, the rich and the powerful, and everyone, slave and free, hid themselves in caves and in the rocks of the mountains.
local: Ka a tẽe nyã nãmɩne, a tẽe nyã nɩbɛrɛ, a fɔ̃ɔ dẽme, taraa dẽme, a gãdaar, nɩ a gbãgbaar ba jaa, nɩ a sumɩne ba jaa pãa sɔɔlɔ a tɔ̃n bɔɔr pɔ nɩ a tɔ̃n kʊsɩbɛ pɔ.

eng: They called to the mountains and the rocks, Fall on us and hide us from the face of the one seated on the throne and from the Lambs wrath!.
local: Ba pãa bʊɔlɔ na a tɔ̃n naa kʊsɩpãnsɩ, Yɩ lo pii sɩ ka sɩ taa lɛ nyɛ a 'lɔ na jãnɛ a nãa dakɔɔ ju ɛ, na sɩ ta mɩ lɛ nyɛ a Pɛraabile suur ɛ!.

eng: The great day of their wrath has come, and who is able to stand.
local: A ba sukpɛ̃ɛ bɩbir vɩɛ naa, an lɛ na tʊ̃ɔ ara a ba niem.

eng: After this I saw four angels standing at the four corners of the earth. They held back the earths four winds so that no wind would blow against the earth, the sea, or any tree.
local: A anyã puor, n nyɛn a malakasɩ anaar ka ba ara a tẽe lõbobo anaar, na ba nyɔɔ a sɛsɛbɛ taa lɛ ɩka wʊ taa furo a wɛr pɔ, naa man pɔ, bɩɩ tɩɛ ju ɛ.

eng: I saw another angel coming up from the east, holding the seal of the living God. He cried out with a loud voice to the four angels who had been given the power to damage the earth and sea.
local: Be lɛ n nyɛ a malaka ãsʊɔ ka wʊ yi a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ purfʊ jie waara na wʊ tara a Nãaŋmɩn vʊr sʊɔ jãnɛ. Wʊ bʊɔl naa a malakasɩ anaar bala nɩ nɛkpɛ̃ɛ ban kʊ sɔr ɩka ba sɔ̃ɔ a tẽe wʊ jaa naa man 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ ba.

eng: He said, Dont damage the earth, the sea, or the trees until we have put a seal on the foreheads of those who serve our God.
local: Yɩ taa ɩrɛ wa sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a tẽe, naa man, naa tɩɩr ɛ, a sɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ a sɩ Nãaŋmɩn tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ a jãnsɩ a ba gbersɩ pɔ baar.

eng: Then I heard the number of those who were sealed: one hundred forty-four thousand, sealed from every tribe of the Israelites.
local: N pãa wõ naa a nɩbɛ bala ban 'yɔ̃ɔ a jãnsɩ nɛɛ ka ba ɩ, nɩbɛ turo kɔbaa lɩjaayi nɩ anaar na yi a Isɩral balbʊrsɩ pɔ.

eng: From the tribe of Judah, twelve thousand were sealed; from the tribe of Reuben, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Gad, twelve thousand.
local: A bala na yi a Juda bʊrɔ pɔ ban 'yɔ̃ɔ a jãnsɩ ɩn nɩbɛ turo pie naa yi , ka a Wuruben bʊrɔ mɩ a nɩbɛ turo pie nɩ ayi , ka a Gad bʊrɔ mɩ ɩ a nɩbɛ turo pie nɩ ayi .

eng: from the tribe of Asher, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Naphtali, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Manasseh, twelve thousand.
local: ka a Aserɩ bʊrɔ mɩ ɩ a nɩbɛ turo pie nɩ ayi , ka a Naftalɩ bʊrɔ mɩ ɩ a nɩbɛ turo pie nɩ ayi , ka a Manasɛ bʊrɔ mɩ ɩ a nɩbɛ turo pie nɩ ayi .

eng: from the tribe of Simeon, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Levi, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Issachar, twelve thousand.
local: ka a Simeon bʊrɔ mɩ ɩ a nɩbɛ turo pie nɩ ayi , ka a Levi bʊrɔ mɩ ɩ a nɩbɛ turo pie nɩ ayi , ka a Isakar bʊrɔ mɩ ɩ a nɩbɛ turo pie nɩ ayi .

eng: from the tribe of Zebulun, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Joseph, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Benjamin, twelve thousand were sealed.
local: ka a Jebulun bʊrɔ mɩ ɩ a nɩbɛ turo pie nɩ ayi , ka a Josefʊ bʊrɔ mɩ ɩ a nɩbɛ turo pie nɩ ayi , ka a Benjamin bʊrɔ mɩ ɩ a nɩbɛ turo pie nɩ ayi .

eng: After this I looked, and there was a great crowd that no one could number. They were from every nation, tribe, people, and language. They were standing before the throne and before the Lamb. They wore white robes and held palm branches in their hands.
local: A anyã puor, n lɛ nyɛn nɩyɔɔ nɩrɛ na kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ ba sɔr ɛ, ka ba yi a paalsɩ a jaa pɔ, balbʊrsɩ, nɩbʊrsɩ, 'yɛrbʊrsɩ a jaa pɔ, na ba ara a nãa dakɔɔ nɩ a Pɛraabile niem. Ba sun fɔpɩɛl tɩ tara bɛ wilsɩ a ba nuur pɔ.

eng: They cried out with a loud voice: Victory belongs to our God who sits on the throne, and to the Lamb.
local: Bala lɛ chɩlɛ 'yɛrɛ, A faafʊ yi naa a sɩ Nãaŋmɩn na jãnɛ a nãa dakɔɔ ju nɩ a Pɛraabile sɛ̃.

eng: All the angels stood in a circle around the throne, and around the elders and the four living creatures. They fell facedown before the throne and worshipped God.
local: Ka a malakasɩ bala na ara jilʊ̃ a nãa dakɔɔ, nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ, nɩ a bʊ̃nɩbie anaar bala gãn kpa a nãa dakɔɔ niem puoro a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛrɛ.

eng: saying, Amen! Blessing and glory and wisdom and thanksgiving and honor and power and might be to our God forever and always. Amen.
local: A ɩ a lɛ! Nãaŋmɩn son pɛɛfʊ nɩ tɩɩr, nɩ yɛ̃, nɩ dãnfʊ, nɩ 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ, nɩ kpɛ̃ɔ nɩ fɔ̃ɔ. A ɩ a lɛ.

eng: Then one of the elders said to me, Who are these people wearing white robes, and where did they come from.
local: Be lɛ a nɩkpɛ̃ɛ ãsʊɔ sʊʊr ma, A bala na su a bʊ̃pɩɛl, ba boo lɛ, nɩ nyɩnɛ lɛ ba yi.

eng: I said to him, Sir, you know. Then he said to me, These people have come out of great hardship. They have washed their robes and made them white in the Lambs blood.
local: Ka n 'yɛr ɩka, Nɩkpɛ̃ɛ, fʊ̃ʊ ba lɛ bɔ̃ɔ ba. Ka wʊ 'yɛr, A banyãna, bala lɛ yi a dɔɔyɛ kpɛ̃ɛ 'lɔ pɔ. Ba pɛɛn a ba fɔɔr nɩ a Pɛraabile jãɩ.

eng: This is the reason they are before Gods throne. They worship him day and night in his temple, and the one seated on the throne will shelter them.
local: Ala lɛ so ka ba ara a Nãaŋmɩn nãa dakɔɔ par tʊ̃nɔ̃ kʊrɔ wʊ mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ nɩ tãsɔɔ a wʊ puorfʊ yir. A 'lɔ na jãnɛ a nãa dakɔɔ ju na 'yɩɛr pɔɔ ba.

eng: They wont hunger or thirst anymore. No sun or scorching heat will beat down on them.
local: Kɔ̃ kʊ̃ lɛ kpɛ ba go ɛ, kɔ̃nyũur mɩ kʊ̃ lɛ kpɛ ba go ɛ. Mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ kʊ̃ lɛ ŋme ba go ɛ, tʊl kʊ̃ lɛ kʊrɔ go ɛ.

eng: because the Lamb who is in the midst of the throne will shepherd them. He will lead them to the springs of life-giving water, and God will wipe away every tear from their eyes.
local: A Pɛraabile na ara a nãa dakɔɔ sãlsɔɔ lɛ na chãnɛ ba. Wʊn ma ŋmaa ban chiin a nyɛvʊr kʊ̃ɔ par, ka Nãaŋmɩn pãa 'yɩɛr a ba nɩbikʊ̃ɔ a jaa bar.

eng: Then, when the Lamb opened the seventh seal, there was silence in heaven for about half an hour.
local: Be lɛ a Pɛraabile 'lɔɔ a jãnsɩ ayopõi sʊɔ ka a saaju wʊ jaa ŋmaa vʊʊr jaa chɩr chɩr chɩr kor bãlãa.

eng: Then I saw the seven angels who stand before God, and seven trumpets were given to them.
local: Ka n nyɛ a malakasɩ ayopõi bala na ara a Nãaŋmɩn niem ka ba kʊ ba ɩɩl pɛɛlsɩ ayopõi.

eng: Another angel came and stood at the altar, and he held a gold bowl for burning incense. He was given a large amount of incense, in order to offer it on behalf of the prayers of all the saints on the gold altar in front of the throne.
local: A malaka ãsʊɔ na tara a sɛlma lapʊr, wa ara naa a bɔɔrlo bomo chʊmfʊ jie. Ba den a tãɩ nyũu vɩla ala kʊ wʊ ɩka wʊ de pʊɔ a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ ba jaa sʊɔrfʊsɩ a sɛlma bɔɔrlo bomo chʊmfʊ jie kʊ a 'lɔ na jãnɛ a nãa dakɔɔ ju.

eng: The smoke of the incense offered for the prayers of the saints rose up before God from the angels hand.
local: A tãɩ nyũu vɩla jʊʊr yire naa a sɛlma lapʊr 'matãan a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ sʊɔrfʊ yi a malaka nũu pɔ, duoro a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃.

eng: Then the angel took the incense container and filled it with fire from the altar. He threw it down to the earth, and there were thunder, voices, lightning, and an earthquake.
local: Be lɛ a malaka lɛ mʊ̃ɔ bũu bie a sɛlma bɔɔrlo bomo chʊmfʊ jie sɛɛ a lapʊr na wʊ lɔb bar ka a wɛr pɔ ka tãnfʊ, nɩ gɔ̃mɔ̃, nɩ saa nyɩɩrfʊ ka a tẽe wʊ jaa dam.

eng: Then the seven angels who held the seven trumpets got ready to blow them.
local: Ka a malakasɩ ayopõi bala na tara a ɩlɛ, go a pɛɛlfʊ.

eng: The first angel blew his trumpet, and hail and fire mixed with blood appeared, and was thrown down to the earth. A third of the earth was burned up. A third of the trees were burned up. All the green grass was burned up.
local: A malaka 'lɔ na de nie pɛɛl naa a wʊ ɩɩl, ka a sakpʊlõbie nɩ bũu na 'matãan jãɩ loro a wɛr pɔ. Ka a tẽe jiir ata jibʊ̃'yen di wʊ jaa, ka a tɩɩr mɩ jiir ata jibʊ̃'yen mɩ di lɔ̃ɔn a mɔ'yɔlɔ a jaa.

eng: Then the second angel blew his trumpet, and something like a huge mountain burning with fire was thrown down into the sea. A third of the sea became blood.
local: Ka a malakasɩ ayi sʊɔ mɩ pɛɛl a wʊ ɩɩl, ka ba 'lɔb bon na nyɛtãan tɔ̃kpɛ̃ɛ na nyɔɔ bũu 'yɔ̃ɔ a man pɔ. A man jiir ata jibʊ̃'yen lɩɛb jãɩ.

eng: a third of the creatures living in the sea died, and a third of the ships were destroyed.
local: ka a bʊ̃nɩbie mɩ jiir ata jibʊ̃'yen kpi, ka a gborbɛrɛ ala mɩ jiir ata jibʊ̃'yen mɩ wɛ.

eng: Then the third angel blew his trumpet, and a great star, burning like a torch, fell from heaven. It fell on a third of the rivers and springs of water.
local: Ka a malakasɩ ata sʊɔ mɩ pɛɛl a wʊ ɩɩl, ka a ŋmɛrbir kpɛ̃ɛ na 'lɩrɛ fɩka bũu yi a saaju lo a põnsɩ nɩ a bulsɩ jiir ata a jibʊ̃'yen pɔ.

eng: The stars name is Wormwood, and a third of the waters became wormwood, and many people died from the water, because it became so bitter.
local: A ŋmɛrbir yuor din, Bʊ̃tuo. Ka a kɔ̃n jiir ata jibʊ̃'yen be tuo, ka a nɩbɛ bala na nyũ a kɔ̃tuo ala, ka yɔɔ jie kpi.

eng: Then the fourth angel blew his trumpet, and a third of the sun was struck, and a third of the moon, and a third of the stars so that a third of them became dark. The day lost a third of its light, and the night lost a third of its light too.
local: A malakasɩ anaar sʊɔ pɛɛl naa a wʊ ɩɩl, ka ba ŋme a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ jiir ata jibʊ̃'yen nɩ a ŋmɛraa jiir ata jibʊ̃'yen naa ŋmɛrbie ka ba jiir ata jibʊ̃'yen ɩ libe. A bɩbir jiir ata jibʊ̃'yen ba tara chãa ɛ, ka a ŋmɛraa naa ŋmɛrbie mɩ ba chaar a tãsɔɔ jiir ata jibʊ̃'yen ɛ.

eng: Then I looked and I heard an eagle flying high overhead. It said with a loud voice, Horror, horror, oh! The horror for those who live on earth because of the blasts of the remaining trumpets that the three angels are about to blow.
local: Be lɛ n kaa naa wõ tuu na ɔɔrɔ be a saaju 'yɛrɛ nɩ nɛkpɛ̃ɛ, Nɛbier! Nɛbier! Nɛbier! Yele na wa naa nɩsaal jaa na be a wɛr pɔ, ala ɩ a malakasɩ ata bala na chɛ wa pɛɛl naa a ba ɩlɛ.

eng: Then the fifth angel blew his trumpet, and I saw a star that had fallen from heaven to earth, and he was given the key to the shaft of the abyss.
local: A malakasɩ anũu sʊɔ na wa pɛɛl a wʊ ɩɩl, ka n nyɛ a ŋmɛrbir 'lɔ na dɔ̃ɔ yi a saaju lo a tẽe. A ŋmɛrbir nyã lɛ ba de a bɔɔ fulom pãn bir kʊ.

eng: He opened the shaft of the abyss; and smoke rose up from the shaft, like smoke from a huge furnace. The sun and air were darkened by the smoke from the shaft.
local: Wʊn wa yuo a bɔɔ fulom nyã nɛɛ, ka jʊʊr yire fɩka bũu kpɛ̃ɛ jʊʊr. A jʊʊr anyãna na yire a bɔɔ fulom nyãna pɔ liin pɔɔ a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ naa tẽe wʊ jaa.

eng: Then locusts came forth from the smoke and onto the earth. They were given power like the power that scorpions have on the earth.
local: A jʊʊr anyãna pɔ lɛ soor yi yaar a tẽe wʊ jaa ka ba kʊ ba lɔɔ fɩka lɔ̃n lɔɔ.

eng: They were told not to hurt the grass of the earth or any green plant or any tree. They could only hurt the people who didnt have the seal of God on their foreheads.
local: Ba 'yɛr kʊ ba ɩka ba taa ɩrɛ wa ɔɔrɔ mɔɩ bɩɩ bʊ̃bul nɩ a tɩɛ vaar ɛ, tɩchɛ a dʊ̃nɔ̃ a nɩbɛ bala tɛɛ na ba tara a Nãaŋmɩn jãnɛ a ba gbersɩ pɔ ɛ.

eng: The locusts werent allowed to kill them, but only to make them suffer for five months—and the suffering they inflict is like that of a scorpion when it strikes a person.
local: Baa kʊ ba sɔr ɩka ba kʊrɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ ba ge a nɩbɛ ŋmɛrsɩ anũu. Ka a ba dʊ̃fʊ ɔɔr kaara fɩka lɔ̃ɔ na dʊ̃ nɩrɛ ɔɔrɔ.

eng: In those days people will seek death, but they wont find it. They will want to die, but death will run away from them.
local: A ber anyãna pɔ nɩbɛ na bʊɔrɔ na kũu tɩchɛ ba kʊ̃ nyɛ wʊ ɛ. Ban bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka ba kpi tɩchɛ a kũu kʊ̃ lɛ kʊ ba gba ɛ.

eng: The locusts looked like horses ready for battle. On their heads were what seemed to be gold crowns. Their faces were like human faces.
local: A soor nyɛtãan wiisɩ ban pãɛ jɛɛr 'yɔ̃ɔ, ka a jur kaara fɩka sɛlma jupile ban su, ka a ba nin nyɛtãan nɩsaal nie.

eng: their hair was like womens hair, and their teeth were like lions teeth.
local: A ba kɔɔl kaara fɩka pɔbɔ jukɔɔl, ka a nyɩmɛ mɩ kaara fɩka jukpɛ̃ɛ nyɩmɛ.

eng: In front they had what seemed to be iron armor upon their chests, and the sound of their wings was like the sound of many chariots and horses racing into battle.
local: Ba tara na nyãa pɔɔrsɩ ka a kaara fɩka kue, ka a pɩbɛ dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ fɩka torkosɩ a wiisɩ na tɔɔrɔ joro chiine jɛɛr jie.

eng: They also have tails with stingers, just like scorpions; and in their tails is their power to hurt people for five months.
local: Ba tara na jʊɛ dʊ̃nɔ̃ nɩ lɔ̃n kaar, a jʊɛ ala tara na fɔ̃ɔ na dʊ̃n nɩbɛ ge ba ŋmɛrsɩ anũu.

eng: Their king is an angel from the abyss, whose Hebrew name is Abaddon, and whose Greek name is Apollyon.
local: Ba tara na nãa na kaara ba, 'lɔ lɛ a malaka 'lɔ na kaara a bɔɔ fulom 'lɔ. Heburu nɩbɛ bʊɔlɔ wʊ naa, Abadɔn, ka a Greek nɩbɛ mɩ bʊɔlɔ, Apolyɔn. A par lɛ, sɔ̃sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃.

eng: The first horror has passed. Look! Two horrors are still coming after this.
local: A nɛbier 'lɔ na de nie lɛ a nyã na tal, tɩchɛ ka a ayi sʊɔ chɛnɛ be puor waara.

eng: Then the sixth angel blew his trumpet, and I heard a voice from the four horns of the gold altar that is before God.
local: Ka a malakasɩ ayʊɔb sʊɔ pɛɛl a wʊ ɩɩl, n wõn kɔkɔr na yire a sɛlma tãɩ nyũu vɩla chʊmfʊ jie kpʊ̃kpʊɛ, be lɛ ba ma chʊm a kãnyũu na be a Nãaŋmɩn niem.

eng: It said to the sixth angel, who had the trumpet, Release the four angels who are bound at the great river Euphrates.
local: A kɔkɔr 'yɛr kʊ a malaka ayʊɔb sʊɔ 'lɔ na tara a ɩɩl, For a malakasɩ anaar bala ban lũ a be a Yufɩrɩtɩ põn kpɛ̃ɛ pɔ.

eng: Then the four angels who had been made ready for that hour, day, month, and year were released to kill a third of humankind.
local: A wʊ for a malakasɩ anaar ba nyãna ban dɔ̃ɔ lũ gun a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃, a bɩbir 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ nɩ a chuu 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ naa yuon 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ ɩka ba kʊ a nɩsaal jiir ata jibʊ̃'yen bar.

eng: The number of cavalry troops was two hundred million. I heard their number.
local: Ka a bala na do a wiisɩ ba bʊɔl naa a ba nɛɛ fʊn kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ ba sɔr ɛ.

eng: And this is the way I saw the horses and their riders in the vision: they had breastplates that were fiery red, dark blue, and yellow as sulfur. The horses heads were like lions heads, and out of their mouths came fire, smoke, and sulfur.
local: A wiisɩ nɩ a bala na do ɩn nyɛ a jãna pɔ, a nyãa pɔɔrsɩ mʊ̃ɔ naa fɩka bũu, ka amɩne mɩ ɩ bufulo, ka amɩne mɩ ɩ bʊ̃dʊɔr. Ka a wiisɩ jur nyɛtãan jubɛrɛ jur, ka a bũu jʊʊr nɩ jerbɩ mɩ yire a ba nɛɛ pɔ.

eng: By these three plagues a third of humankind was killed: by the fire, smoke, and sulfur coming out of their mouths.
local: A gefʊ ata ala na yi a wiisɩ nɛɛ pɔ lɛ, bũu, nɩ jʊʊr, nɩ jerbɩ, ala lɛ ba kʊn nɩbɛ jiir ata jibʊ̃'yen.

eng: The horses power is in their mouths and their tails, for their tails are like snakes with heads that inflict injuries.
local: A wiisɩ anyã fɔ̃ɔ be naa a ba nɛɛ pɔ naa jʊɛ pɔ, a ba jʊɛ kaara fɩka waba naa tara jur, ala lɛ ba de gere nɩ a nɩsaal.

eng: The rest of humankind, who werent killed by these plagues, didnt change their hearts and lives and turn from their handiwork. They didnt stop worshipping demons and idols made of gold, silver, bronze, stone, and wood—idols that cant see or hear or walk.
local: A nɩsaalba bala na chɛ, na baa kpin a gefʊ nyã ɛ, ba sɔɔ bar a tɩbɛ ala ban maal nɩ a ba nuur ɛ. Ba chɛnɛ na puoro a sɩdɛbɛ, nɩ a bʊtɩbɛ ban maal a sɛlma, nɩ a kur pɩɛl, nɩ a kur jɩɛ, nɩ a kusɩbɛ, nɩ a daar na ba wõnõ 'yɛr ɛ, ba kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ chen ɛ.

eng: They didnt turn away from their murders, their spells and drugs, their sexual immorality, or their stealing.
local: Baa mɩ lɩɛb a ba tɩɛr bar a nɩkʊyelsɩ, nɩ sɔyelsɩ, nɩ sɛ̃sɛ̃yelsɩ, nɩ nãnyiyelsɩ ɛ.

eng: Then I saw another powerful angel coming down from heaven. He was robed with a cloud, with a rainbow over his head. His face was like the sun, and his feet were like fiery pillars.
local: N lɛ nyɛn a malaka kpɛ̃ɔ ãsʊɔ na yi a saaju suro waara. Wʊ vaar naa julõjuur ka a sagõgoraa dɔɔl a jum. A nie kaara fɩka mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ, ka a gbɛɛ mɩ kaara fɩka bũu lɩraa.

eng: He held an open scroll in his hand. He put his right foot on the sea and his left foot on the land.
local: Wʊ tara na gãn bile na yuo a wʊ nũu pɔ, na wʊ de a nũsʊ̃ɔ gbɛr 'lɔr a man pɔ tɩ a gʊbaa gbɛr dɔɔl a gogoro ju.

eng: He called out with a loud voice like a lion roaring, and when he called out, the seven thunders raised their voices.
local: tɩ na wʊ pãa tãn kpɛ̃ɔ jaa fɩka jukpɛ̃ɛ na uor. Wʊn wa tãn a lɛ ka a kɔkɔɛ ɩ ayopõi mɩ tãn satanaa kaar.

eng: When the seven thunders spoke, I was about to write, but I heard a voice from heaven say, Seal up what the seven thunders have said, and dont write it down.
local: An wa tãn a gbɛɛ ayopõi a lɛ, ka n ta bʊɔrɔ ɩka n sɛb tɩchɛ n wõn kɔkɔr na yi a saaju 'yɛr, A tãnfʊ ayopõi ala na 'yɛr sɔɔl bar, ta sɛb bin ɛ.

eng: Then the angel I saw standing on the sea and on the land raised his right hand to heaven.
local: Be lɛ a malaka 'lɔ ɩn nyɛ na 'lɔr a nũsʊ̃ɔ gbɛr a man pɔ tɩ dɔɔl a a gʊba gbɛr a gogoro ju, jɛɛn a nũsʊ̃ɔ a saaju.

eng: He swore by the one who lives forever and always, who created heaven and what is in it, the earth and what is in it, and the sea and what is in it, and said, The time is up.
local: Wʊ pɔn naa a Nãaŋmɩn nyã na be be jaa kpo kpo, 'lɔ lɛ mɛ a saaju, nɩ a tẽe, nɩ a man, nɩ bomo a jaa na be a ba pɔ, A kʊ̃ lɛ kor go ɛ!.

eng: In the days when the seventh angel blows his trumpet, Gods mysterious purpose will be accomplished, fulfilling the good news he gave to his servants the prophets.
local: Tɩchɛ a bɩbir 'lɔ a malakasɩ ayopõi sʊɔ na wa pɛɛl a wʊ ɩɩl, a Nãaŋmɩn yelsɔɔlaa na pãa maal baar nɩtãa a lɛ wʊn man kʊ a wʊ gbãgbaar 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ.

eng: Then the voice I heard from heaven spoke to me again and said, Go, take the opened scroll from the hand of the angel who stands on the sea and on the land.
local: Be lɛ a kɔkɔr 'lɔ ɩn dɔ̃ɔ wõ a saaju lɛ 'yɛr kʊ ma go, Chen tɩ de a gãn bile 'lɔ na yuo a malaka 'lɔ na ara a man pɔ naa gogoro ju nũu pɔ.

eng: So I went to the angel and told him to give me the scroll. He said to me, Take it and eat it. It will make you sick to your stomach, but sweet as honey in your mouth.
local: Ka n chen a malaka sɛ̃ tɩ sʊɔr wʊ ɩka wʊ kʊ ma a gãn bile. Ka wʊ 'yɛr, De 'wɔb, wʊn mire naa a fʊ pʊɔ pɔ tɩ nʊ̃mɔ̃ a fʊ nɛɛ pɔ sɩkãa kaar.

eng: So I took the scroll from the angels hand and ate it. And it was sweet as honey in my mouth, but when I swallowed it, it made my stomach churn.
local: N den a gãn bile a malaka nũu pɔ 'wɔb. Ka wʊ nʊ̃mɔ̃ a n nɛɛ pɔ a sɩkãa kaar, tɩchɛ ɩn wa vɔl baar, ka wʊ mire a pʊɔ pɔ.

eng: I was told, You must prophesy again about many peoples, nations, languages, and kings.
local: A ba lɛ 'yɛr kʊ ma go, 'Yɛr 'yɛr kʊ a nɩbʊrɔ, nɩ paalsɩ, nɩ 'yɛrbʊrsɩ nɩ a nãmɩne.

eng: Then I was given a measuring rod, which was like a pole. And I was told, Get up and measure Gods temple, the altar, and those who worship there.
local: Be lɛ a nɩrɛ kɔ̃ɔ de dãgbal na kaara a damanaa kaar kʊ ma tɩ 'yɛr, Chen tɩ man a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir nɩ a bɔɔr lofʊ jie na fʊ sɔr a nɩbɛ bala na puoro a be.

eng: But dont measure the court outside the temple. Leave that out, because it has been given to the nations, and they will trample the holy city underfoot for forty-two months.
local: Tɩchɛ taa ɩrɛ wa mana a yõo ɛ, bojũu be lɛ a bala na ba ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ ɛ, na be 'mu 'mulo a tẽchɛchɛ nyãna ŋmɛrsɩ lɩjaayi nɩ ayi.

eng: And I will allow my two witnesses to prophesy for one thousand two hundred sixty days, wearing mourning clothes.
local: Ɩ̃n kʊ naa a n yelmãɛ dãasɩɛ nɩbɛ ayi ka ba su fɔjãrsɩ tɩ mʊɔlɔ na ba 'yɛrɛ ber tur dɔɔl kɔɔr ayi nɩ lɩjaata .

eng: These are the two olive trees and the two lampstands that stand before the Lord of the earth.
local: A dãasɩɛ dẽme ayi bala ɩ naa a kãa tɩɩr ayi nɩ a fãtɩnɛ dɔɔlfʊ jie jiir ayi, ba ara naa a Soro nyã na ɩ a wɛr wʊ jaa niem.

eng: If anyone wants to hurt them, fire comes out of their mouth and burns up their enemies. So if anyone wants to hurt them, they have to be killed in this way.
local: Ala ɩ nɩrɛ bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka wʊ maal ba bier, bũu ma yi naa a ba nɛɛ pɔ di a bala na ɩ a ba dɔ̃dɔ̃mɔ̃sɩ. Nɩtɔ̃ lɛ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na tɩɛr a ba sɔ̃ɔfʊ ma kpi.

eng: They have the power to close up the sky so that no rain will fall for as long as they prophesy. They also have power over the waters, to turn them into blood, and to strike the earth with any plague, as often as they wish.
local: A nɩbɛ bala tara na kpɛ̃ɔ na nyɔɔ saa a saaju ka a saa kʊ̃ wa a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ ban mʊɔlɔ ɛ. Ba mɩ tara na kpɛ̃ɔ na lɩɛb a kɔ̃n a jaa ka a ɩ jãɩ, na ba mɩ na tʊ̃ɔ vɛ̃ ka yelbier wa a tẽe pɔ sɔ̃ɔ jaa ban bʊɔrɔ.

eng: When they have finished their witnessing, the beast that comes up from the abyss will make war on them, gain victory over them, and kill them.
local: Ala ɩ ba wa baar naa a dãasɩɛ difʊ, a mɔdʊ̃ɔ 'lɔ na yi a bɔɔ fulom 'lɔ pɔ na ŋmaan ban jɛɛr na wʊ tʊ̃ɔ ba na wʊ kʊ.

eng: Their dead bodies will lie on the street of the great city that is spiritually called Sodom and Egypt, where also their Lord was crucified.
local: Ka a ba ãgãma gã be a tẽkpɛ̃ɛ 'lɔ sɔkpɛ̃ɛ ju. A tẽkpɛ̃ɛ nyã lɛ ba lɔɔrɔ sʊkpa bʊɔlɔ nɩ Sodom bɩɩ Ijipitɩ, be lɛ ba kpa a ba Soro a daa ju.

eng: And for three and a half days, members of the peoples, tribes, languages, and nations will look at their dead bodies, but they wont let their dead bodies be put in a tomb.
local: Nɩbʊrɔ, nɩ 'yɛrbʊrɔ, nɩ balsɩ ba jaa, nɩ paalsɩ a jaa na kaara naa a ba ãgãma ban tɔr a ũufʊ bɩbie ata nɩ bʊ̃ŋmaa.

eng: Those who live on earth will rejoice over them. They will celebrate and give each other gifts, because these two prophets had brought such pain to those who live on earth.
local: Nʊ̃ɔ na kpɛ̃n a nɩbɛ na kpɩɛrɛ a wɛr pɔ ka ba chɩlɛ kʊrɔ taa bonsɩ, bojũu ba kʊ naa a 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ ayi banyãna na dɔ̃ɔ ge a wɛr nɩbɛ.

eng: But after three and a half days, the breath of life from God entered them, and they stood on their feet. Great fear came over those who saw them.
local: Tɩchɛ a bɩbie ata nɩ a bʊ̃ŋmaa ala na wa po, Nãaŋmɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ ban nyɛvʊr ka ba lɛ ir ɩ vʊɛ, ka a dãbãɛ kpɛ a bala na nyɛ ba.

eng: Then they heard a loud voice from heaven say to them, Come up here. And they went up to heaven in a cloud, while their enemies watched them.
local: A 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ ayi banyãna pãa wõn nɛkpɛ̃ɛ na yi saaju 'yɛr, Yɩ do wa ka. Ka ba tu a julõjuur pɔ duoro a saaju ka a ba dɔ̃dɔ̃mɔ̃sɩ kaara ba.

eng: At that hour there was a great earthquake, and a tenth of the city fell. Seven thousand people were killed by the earthquake, and the rest were afraid and gave glory to the God of heaven.
local: Be lɛ ajienaa ka a tẽe mii ka a tẽkpɛ̃ɛ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ yie jiir pie jibʊ̃'yen lo naa kʊ nɩbɛ tur ayopõi ka a dãbãɛ kpɛ a bala na chɛ a ba pɛɛrɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nyã na be a saaju.

eng: The second horror is over. The third horror is coming soon.
local: A nɛbier ayi sʊɔ lɛ a nyã na po, tɩchɛ a ata sʊɔ mɩ turo naa.

eng: Then the seventh angel blew his trumpet, and there were loud voices in heaven saying, The kingdom of the world has become the kingdom of our Lord and his Christ, and he will rule forever and always.
local: A malakasɩ ayopõi sʊɔ pɛɛl naa a wʊ ɩɩl, ka a kɔkɔyɔɔ 'yɛrɛ be a saaju, A wɛr nyã nãalʊ̃ʊ ɩ naa a sɩ Soro nɩ a wʊ Nɩ-iraa nãalʊ̃ʊ, 'lɔ lɛ na di a nãa jaa kpo kpo.

eng: Then the twenty-four elders, who were seated on their thrones before God, fell on their faces and worshipped God.
local: A nɩbɛrɛ lɩjɛr nɩ anaar bala na jãnɛ a ba nãa dakɔɔr ju a Nãaŋmɩn niem gãn kpa tẽe puor a Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: They said, We give thanks to you, Lord God Almighty, who is and was, for you have taken your great power and enforced your rule.
local: tɩ 'yɛrɛ, Sɩ puor naa a Soro Nãaŋmɩn na ɩ a Tʊ̃ɔfʊ Sʊɔ, 'Lɔ lɛ ɩ, nɩ 'Lɔ lɛ dɔ̃ɔ be be, bojũu wʊ den a wʊ kpɛ̃ɔ piel kaara nɩ a wɛr wʊ jaa.

eng: The nations were enraged, but your wrath came. The time came for the dead to be judged. The time came to reward your servants, the prophets and saints, and those who fear your name, both small and great, and to destroy those who destroy the earth.
local: A paalsɩ vʊ̃n fʊn nɛɛ ka fʊ suur mɩ dire. A sɔ̃ɔ ta naa ka fʊn tɔɔr a kũn 'yɛr, na fʊn yab a fʊ gbãgbaar na ɩ a 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ lɔ̃ɔn a fʊ nɩbɛ naa bala na 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a fʊ yuor, a bil naa bɛrɛ ba jaa, na fʊn sɔ̃ɔ a bala na sɔ̃ɔ a wɛr.

eng: Then Gods temple in heaven was opened, and the chest containing his covenant appeared in his temple. There were lightning, voices, thunder, an earthquake, and large hail.
local: Ka a Nãaŋmɩn yir na be a saaju pãn pãa yuo ka ba nyɛ a nɛtɩraa daka be a yir pɔ. Ka saa nyɩɩrfʊ, gɔ̃mɔ̃, nɩ tãnfʊ, ka a tẽe wʊ jaa mii, ka a sakpʊlõbibɛrɛ loro.

eng: Then a great sign appeared in heaven: a woman clothed with the sun, with the moon under her feet and a crown of twelve stars on her head.
local: A nɛ'maa yelbɛrɛ na yi a saaju ka a pɔɔ vaar mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ fɩka fɔbaa tɩ dɔɔl a gbɛɛ a ŋmɛraa ju, ka a ŋmɛrbie pie nɩ ayi ɩ nãa jupil ka wʊ su.

eng: She was pregnant, and she cried out because she was in labor, in pain from giving birth.
local: Pʊɔ lɛ wʊ tara ka wʊ dɔɔfʊ ta ka wʊ kõnõ nɩ.

eng: Then another sign appeared in heaven: it was a great fiery red dragon, with seven heads and ten horns, and seven royal crowns on his heads.
local: Be lɛ ka a nɛ'maa yele ãsʊɔ lɛ yi a saaju kaara fɩka dajɩl na ɩ jɩɛ na wʊ tara jur ayopõi nɩ ɩlɛ pie ka a nãa jupile be a jur pɔ.

eng: His tail swept down a third of heavens stars and threw them to the earth. The dragon stood in front of the woman who was about to give birth so that when she gave birth, he might devour her child.
local: A wʊ jʊʊr pɩɩr naa a ŋmɛrbie jiir ata jibʊ̃'yen a saaju 'lɔb a tẽe. Tɩ ara gu a pɔɔ 'lɔ na bʊɔrɔ a dɔɔfʊ ɩka 'lɔ wa dɔɔ wʊ vɔl a bie.

eng: She gave birth to a son, a male child who is to rule all the nations with an iron rod. Her child was snatched up to God and his throne.
local: A pɔɔ dɔɔn a bie ka wʊ ɩ dabile, 'lɔ lɛ na kaara a paalsɩ a jaa nɩ la kpɛ̃ɔ 'lɔ na kaara a kur dãgbal kaar. Ba jãan a pɔɔ bie nyã don a Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ ka wʊ tɩ be a wʊ nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ.

eng: Then the woman fled into the desert, where God has prepared a place for her. There she will be taken care of for one thousand two hundred sixty days.
local: Tɩ ka a pɔɔ mɩ jo kpɛ a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ na wʊ tɩ be a jie Nãaŋmɩn na chɔɔr bar ka ban kaara wʊ maal ber tur kɔɔr ayi dɔɔl lɩjaata .

eng: Then there was war in heaven: Michael and his angels fought the dragon. The dragon and his angels fought back.
local: Ka a jɛɛr lo be a saaju. Maɩkɩl nɩ a malakasɩ jɛɛr nɩ naa a dajɩl, ka a dajɩl nɩ a wʊ malakasɩ mɩ lɩɛb jɛɛrɛ nɩ a Maɩkɩl nɩ a wʊ malakasɩ.

eng: but they did not prevail, and there was no longer any place for them in heaven.
local: Tɩchɛ ba tʊ̃ɔ naa a dajɩl nɩ a wʊ malakasɩ, dii ba bar a be a saaju.

eng: So the great dragon was thrown down. The old snake, who is called the devil and Satan, the deceiver of the whole world, was thrown down to the earth; and his angels were thrown down with him.
local: Ba lɔb a dajɩl kpɛ̃ɛ nyã bar a yõo, a kõr waa 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ na ɩ a dɛɛr sʊɔ, nɩ a Satan, 'lɔ lɛ bɛlʊ̃ a wɛr nɩbɛ ba jaa. Ba lɔb wʊn bar ka a wɛr pɔ, nɩ a wʊ malakasɩ.

eng: Then I heard a loud voice in heaven say, Now the salvation and power and kingdom of our God, and the authority of his Christ have come. The accuser of our brothers and sisters, who accuses them day and night before our God, has been thrown down.
local: Be lɛ n wõ nɛkpɛ̃ɛ na 'yɛr be a saaju, Pʊ̃pãanyã, a faafʊ bɩbir vɩɛ naa, a sɩ Nãaŋmɩn wiil naa a wʊ kpɛ̃ɔ, nɩ a wʊ nãalʊ̃ʊ, nɩ a wʊ Nɩ-iraa kpɛ̃ɔ. Bojũu a 'lɔ na bɛrɛ a sɩ yɛɛr nɛɛ, 'lɔ lɛ bɛrɛ ba a nɛɛ a Nãaŋmɩn niem mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ nɩ tãsɔɔ, ba daa wʊn lɔb.

eng: They gained the victory over him on account of the blood of the Lamb and the word of their witness. Love for their own lives didnt make them afraid to die.
local: Ba tʊ̃ɔ wʊ naa nɩ a Pɛraabile jãɩ nɩ a ba dãasɩɛ 'yɛrbie. Ba sɔɔn kũu na baa de a ba nyɛvʊr ka wʊ ɩ bon ɛ.

eng: Therefore, rejoice, you heavens and you who dwell in them. But oh! The horror for the earth and sea! The devil has come down to you with great rage, for he knows that he only has a short time.
local: A lɛ jũu, saaju naa bala na kpɩɛrɛ a be, yɩ chɩlɛ! Tɩchɛ nɛbier ben a yɩn na be a wɛr nɩ a man pɔ, yɩn din dɔɔyɛ, bojũu a dɛɛr sʊɔ suun a yɩ sɛ̃! Ka a wʊ suur dire, bojũu a wʊ ber ba waa ɛ.

eng: When the dragon saw that he had been thrown down to the earth, he chased the woman who had given birth to the male child.
local: Lɛ a dajɩl na wa nyɛ ka a ɩ tẽe lɛ wʊ lo, wʊ dire naa a pɔɔ 'lɔ na dɔɔ a dabile.

eng: But the woman was given the two wings of the great eagle so that she could fly to her place in the desert. There she would be taken care of—out of the snakes reach—for a time and times and half a time.
local: Ka ba kʊ a pɔɔ tuu kpɛ̃ɛ pibɛ ayi, ɩka wʊ ɔɔ chen a jie ban chɔɔr bar be a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ, be lɛ ban tɩ kaara wʊ yuom ata nɩ bʊ̃ŋmaa ka a waa taa pɔɔ wʊ ɛ.

eng: Then from his mouth the snake poured a river of water after the woman so that the river would sweep her away.
local: Be lɛ ka a waa chɩr kʊ̃ɔ a nɛɛ pɔ põn kʊ̃ɔ kaar, ɩka a di a pɔɔ.

eng: But the earth helped the woman. The earth opened its mouth and swallowed the river that the dragon poured out of his mouth.
local: tɩchɛ ka a tẽe sʊ̃ʊ a pɔɔ haa a nɛɛ nyũ a kʊ̃ɔ ala a dajɩl na chɩr a nɛɛ pɔ.

eng: So the dragon was furious with the woman, and he went off to make war on the rest of her children, on those who keep Gods commandments and hold firmly to the witness of Jesus.
local: Ka a dajɩl pãa nyɛ suur 'yɔ̃ɔ a pɔɔ na wʊ chen tɩ ŋmaan a pɔɔ bibiir bala na chɛ jɛɛr. A bala na turo a Nãaŋmɩn nɛɛ na ba mɩ tara a Yesu 'yɛr yelmãɛ.

eng: and I saw a beast coming up out of the sea. It had ten horns and seven heads. Each of its horns was decorated with a royal crown, and on its heads were blasphemous names.
local: Ka a dajɩl ara a man gogoro nɛɛ. N nyɛn mɔdʊ̃ɔ na yire a man pɔ na wʊ tara jur ayopõi tɩ bul ɩlɛ pie ka a nãa jupile su a ɩlɛ ala pɔ. A jur ala pɔ yoe ben be na fere sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ Nãaŋmɩn yuor.

eng: The beast I saw was like a leopard. Its feet were like a bears, and its mouth was like a lions mouth. The dragon gave it his power, throne, and great authority.
local: A mɔdʊ̃ɔ 'lɔ ɩn nyɛ nyɛntãan gbɔ̃gbɔ̃ɔ tɩchɛ ka a gbɛɛ kaara fɩka pɛɛlaa gbɛɛ ka a nɛɛ mɩ kaara fɩka jukpɛ̃ɛ nɛɛ. Ka a dajɩl de a wʊ kpɛ̃ɔ nɩ a wʊ nãa dakɔɔ nɩ a wʊ tʊ̃ɔfʊ kʊ a mɔdʊ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃.

eng: One of its heads appeared to have been slain and killed, but its deadly wound was healed. So the whole earth was amazed and followed the beast.
local: A mɔdʊ̃ɔ ju ãsʊɔ kaara fɩka bon ban chɛ ɩrɛ na kʊ tɩchɛ ka a chɛfʊ jie 'lɔ 'mɔ̃ pɔɔ. Nɛɛ 'maan a nɩrɛ jaa na be a wɛr pɔ tɩ na ba turo a mɔdʊ̃ɔ 'lɔ.

eng: They worshipped the dragon because it had given the beast its authority. They worshipped the beast and said, Who is like the beast, and who can fight against it.
local: Ka nɩrɛ jaa pãa pɛɛrɛ a dajɩl bojũu 'lɔ lɛ de a wʊ kpɛ̃ɔ kʊ a mɔdʊ̃ɔ 'lɔ na ba mɩ pɛɛrɛ a mɔdʊ̃ɔ 'lɔ 'yɛrɛ nɩ tɩ sʊrɔ, An lɛ kaara a mɔdʊ̃ɔ nyã kaar? Nɩ an lɛ na tʊ̃ɔ ŋmaan wʊ jɛɛr.

eng: The beast was given a mouth that spoke boastful and blasphemous things, and it was given authority to act for forty-two months.
local: A mɔdʊ̃ɔ nyã tara na nɛɛ na 'yɛrɛ ɛ jãjɩɩr 'yɛr na wʊ fere, wʊ tara na sɔr ɩka wʊ 'yɛr a wʊ yobɔ 'yɛr maal ŋmɛrsɩ lɩjaayi nɩ ayi .

eng: It opened its mouth to speak blasphemies against God. It blasphemed Gods name and his dwelling place .
local: A wʊ 'yɛrbie chɩrɛ nɩ naa Nãaŋmɩn na wʊ fere sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a Nãaŋmɩn yuor nɩ a jie 'lɔ wʊn be nɩ a bala na kpɩɛrɛ a saaju.

eng: It was also allowed to make war on the saints and to gain victory over them. It was given authority over every tribe, people, language, and nation.
local: Ba kʊ wʊn sɔr ɩka wʊ jɛb a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ na wʊ tʊ̃ɔ ba na wʊ mɩ tʊ̃ɔ a nɩbʊrɔ, nɩ 'yɛrbʊrɔ, nɩ balbʊrɔ, nɩ paalsɩ.

eng: All who live on earth worshipped it, all whose names hadnt been written—from the time the earth was made—in the scroll of life of the Lamb who was slain.
local: A bala ba jaa yuor na ba dɔ̃ɔ sɛb a Pɛraabile ban dɔ̃ɔ kʊ a nyɛvʊr gãn pɔ ɛ, tɩ a wɛr wa mɛ, na gbur dumo puor a mɔdʊ̃ɔ nyãna.

eng: Whoever has ears must listen.
local: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na tara toor, wʊ chɛlɛ.

eng: If any are to be taken captive, then into captivity they will go. If any are to be killed by the sword, then by the sword they will be killed. This calls for endurance and faithfulness on the part of the saints.
local: Ala ɩ ãsʊɔ jaa nyɔɔfʊ lɛ ban nyɔɔ wʊ, ban nyɔɔ wʊn chiin. Ala ɩ sɔkpɛ̃ɛ mɩ lɛ ban kʊn ãsʊɔ, a sɔkpɛ̃ɛ lɛ ban kʊn wʊ. A lɛ jũu a bala na ɩ Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ, ba tara kãnyir nɩ yelmãɛ.

eng: Then I saw another beast coming up from the earth. It had two horns like a lamb, but it was speaking like a dragon.
local: N pãa lɛ nyɛn a mɔdʊ̃ɔ ãsʊɔ na yire a tẽe tara ɩlɛ ayi fɩka pɛraabile tɩ 'yɛrɛ fɩka a dajɩl.

eng: It exercises all the authority of the first beast in its presence. It also makes the earth and those who live in it worship the first beast, whose fatal wound was healed.
local: Wʊ yin de a mɔdʊ̃dɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ kpɛ̃ɔ na wʊ wiil wʊ mãɛ a mɔdʊ̃dɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ niem tɩ fɩr a nɩbɛ ba jaa na be ka a wɛr pɔ ka ba gbur dumo puor a mɔdʊ̃dɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ chɛfʊ jie na 'mɔ pɔɔ.

eng: It does great signs so that it even makes fire come down from heaven to earth in the presence of the people.
local: Wʊ tʊ̃n nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃sɩ na wʊ gba ma vɛ̃ ka bũu yi saaju suu wa a tẽe ka nɩbɛ ba jaa nyɛ.

eng: It deceives those who live on earth by the signs that it was allowed to do in the presence of the beast. It told those who live on earth to make an image for the beast who had been wounded by the sword and yet came to life again.
local: A wʊ nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ anyãna jũu, ba kʊ wʊn kpɛ̃ɔ ka wʊ de a mɔdʊ̃dɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ jie, na wʊ bɛl a wɛr nɩbɛ. Ka wʊ 'yɛr ba ka ba mɛ bʊtɩbɛ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ nɩ a mɔdʊ̃ɔ 'lɔ ban dɔ̃ɔ chen a sʊɔ tɩ wʊ 'mɔ pɔɔ.

eng: It was allowed to give breath to the beasts image so that the beasts image would even speak and cause anyone who didnt worship the beasts image to be put to death.
local: Ba kʊ wʊn sɔr ɩka wʊ kʊ a bʊtɩɩ 'lɔ nyɛvʊr wʊn mɛ ka wʊ nyɛtãan a mɔdʊ̃dɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ, ka wʊ na tʊ̃ɔ 'yɛr ka ba kʊ a bala na tɔr a bʊtɩɩ puorfʊ.

eng: It forces everyone—the small and great, the rich and poor, the free and slaves—to have a mark put on their right hand or on their forehead.
local: Wʊ mɩ fɩr nɩrɛ jaa, ka bile lɛ wʊ ɩ bɩɩ kpɛ̃ɛ lɛ, taraa sʊɔ bɩɩ nɔ̃ɔ sʊɔ, sumɩne bɩɩ gbãgbaa, ka ba 'yɔ̃ɔ ba jaa jãnsɩ a nũsʊ̃ɔ nũu pɔ bɩɩ a gberaa pɔ.

eng: It will not allow anyone to make a purchase or sell anything unless the person has the mark with the beasts name or the number of its name.
local: Nɩrɛ jaa kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ daara bɩɩ da bon ka baa 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ a jãnɛ na ɩ a mɔdʊ̃ɔ yuor bɩɩ a wʊ jãnɛ 'lɔ na ɩ a wʊ yuor ɛ.

eng: This calls for wisdom. Let the one who understands calculate the beasts number, for its a human beings number. Its number is six hundred sixty-six.
local: Ka 'lɔ yɛ̃ 'yɛr lɛ, ala ɩ nɩrɛ tara na bɔ̃ɔfʊ, wʊn tʊ̃ɔ sɔr a mɔdʊ̃ɔ sɛbie an ɩ nɩsaal sɛbie lɛ. A sɛbie ɩ naa kɔɔr ayʊɔb dɔɔl lɩjaata nɩ ayʊɔb .

eng: Then I heard a loud voice from the temple say to the seven angels, Go and pour out the seven bowls of Gods anger on the earth.
local: N wõn nɛkpɛ̃ɛ na yi a be a puorfʊ yir 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ a malakasɩ ayopõi bala, Yɩ chen tɩ kpaar a Nãaŋmɩn suur a laar pɔ bar a wɛr.

eng: Then I looked, and there was the Lamb, standing on Mount Zion. With him were one hundred forty-four thousand who had his name and his Fathers name written on their foreheads.
local: Be lɛ n kaa nyɛ a Pɛraabile ka wʊ 'mataa ara nɩ nɩbɛ turo kɔbaa lɩjaayi nɩ anaar a Jiyon tɔ̃ɔ ju ka wʊ yuor nɩ a Sãa yuor sɛb 'mar a ba gbersɩ pɔ.

eng: So the first angel poured his bowl on the earth, and a nasty and terrible sore appeared on the people who had the beasts mark and worshipped its image.
local: Ka a malaka dɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ chen tɩ kpaar a wʊ laa bar ka a tẽe ka a natɩdɛbɛ na bɩɛrɛ tɛ a nɩbɛ bala na tara a mɔdʊ̃ɔ 'lɔ jãnɛ tɩ mɩ gbur dumo puor a bʊtɩɩ.

eng: I heard a sound from heaven that was like the sound of rushing water and loud thunder. The sound I heard was like that of harpists playing their harps.
local: N wõn kɔkɔɛ a saaju na gɔ̃nɔ̃ fɩka kɔ̃kpɛ̃ɛ na joro bɩɩ saa na tãn. A gɔ̃mɔ̃ mɩ kaara fɩka kõrõjomsɩ ban ŋmiere.

eng: The second angel poured his bowl into the sea, and the sea turned into blood, like the blood of a corpse, and every living thing in the sea died.
local: A malaka ayi sʊɔ mɩ kpaar a wʊ laa bar a man pɔ, ka a kʊ̃ɔ lɩɛb fɩka kũu jãɩ, ka a bʊ̃vʊʊrsɩ a jaa na be a man pɔ kpi.

eng: They sing a new song in front of the throne, the four living creatures, and the elders. And no one could learn the song except the one hundred forty-four thousand who had been purchased from the earth.
local: Ba yiele naa yiel paalaa a nãa dakɔɔ niem nɩ a bʊ̃nɩbie anaar bala nɩ a nɩbɛrɛ bala niem. Nɩrɛ jaa ba be be na tʊ̃ɔ a yiel jãn ɛ, ka a nɩbɛ turo kɔbaa lɩjaayi nɩ anaar bala tɛɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na faa ka a wɛr pɔ ba lɛ ɛ.

eng: The third angel poured his bowl into the rivers and springs of water, and they turned into blood.
local: A malaka ata sʊɔ mɩ kpaar a wʊ laa 'yɔ̃ɔ a põnsɩ nɩ kʊlsɩ pɔ, ka a kɔ̃n mɩ lɩɛb jãɩ.

eng: They werent defiled with women, for these people who follow the Lamb wherever he goes are virgins. They were purchased from among humankind as early produce for God and the Lamb.
local: A banyãna lɛ ba sɔ̃ɔ ba mãɛ nɩ pɔbɔ ɛ, bojũu ba gun ba mãɛ ɩ chɛchɛ, na ba ma turo a Pɛraabile jie jaa wʊn chiine, bala lɛ ba da de kʊ Nãaŋmɩn fɩka bʊ̃dɔ̃ɔ ban de kʊ Nãaŋmɩn nɩ a Pɛraabile.

eng: Then I heard the angel of the waters say, You are just, holy one, who is and was, because you have given these judgments.
local: Be lɛ n wõ a malaka 'lɔ na kaara a kɔ̃n 'yɛr, Fʊ̃ʊ lɛ ɩ a Torfʊ Sʊɔ, a fʊ 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ nyã tor naa, fʊ̃ʊ lɛ ɩ, fʊ̃ʊ mɩ lɛ dɔ̃ɔ be be Chɛchɛ Sʊɔ a yelsɩ anyãna fʊn tɔɔr a 'yɛr jũu.

eng: They poured out the blood of saints and prophets, and you have given them blood to drink. They deserve it.
local: Bojũu a putudẽme bala lɛ kʊ a fʊ nɩbɛ nɩ a fʊ 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ a fʊ kʊ ba a jãɩ ɩka ba nyũ, a lɛ sɛɛn ba naa.

eng: No lie came from their mouths; they are blameless.
local: Baa haa nɛɛ ŋmaa jir ɛ. Baa tara chɛfʊ bãlãa jaa ɛ.

eng: And I heard the altar say, Yes, Lord God Almighty, your judgments are true and just.
local: Be lɛ n wõ kɔkɔr na 'yɛr be a bɔɔr lofʊ jie, Ɔ̃ɔ, n Soro Nãaŋmɩn na ɩ a Tʊ̃ɔfʊ Sʊɔ, a fʊ 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ ɩn yelmãɛ tɩ mɩ tor.

eng: Then I saw another angel flying high overhead with eternal good news to proclaim to those who live on earth, and to every nation, tribe, language, and people.
local: N pãa lɛ nyɛn a malakasɩ ãsʊɔ na ɔɔrɔ be a saaju tɩ tara a 'yɛr kpo kpo bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a bala na be ka a wɛr pɔ, nɩ paalsɩ a jaa, nɩ balbʊrsɩ a jaa, nɩ 'yɛrbʊrsɩ a jaa, nɩ nɩbʊrsɩ a jaa.

eng: The fourth angel poured his bowl on the sun, and it was allowed to burn people with fire.
local: A malakasɩ anaar sʊɔ kpaar a wʊ laa 'yɔ̃ɔ mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ ka wʊ tara kpɛ̃ɔ vuul a nɩbɛ nɩ bũu.

eng: He said in a loud voice, Fear God and give him glory, for the hour of his judgment has come. Worship the one who made heaven and earth, the sea and springs of water.
local: Wʊ tãn na nɩ nɛkpɛ̃ɛ, Yɩ joro a Nãaŋmɩn dãbãɛ na yɩ kʊrɔ wʊ tɩɩr, bojũu a wʊ bɩbir vɩɛ naa ka wʊn tɔɔr a nɩbɛ 'yɛr. Yɩ puor wʊ, 'lɔ lɛ maal a saaju, nɩ a wɛr, nɩ a man nɩ a kʊ̃ɔ yifʊ jiir.

eng: The people were burned by intense heat, and they cursed the name of the God who had power over these plagues. But they didnt change their hearts and lives and give him glory.
local: A mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ tʊl vuul ba naa tɩ ba chɛnɛ tʊrɔ a Nãaŋmɩn yuor an ɩ 'lɔ lɛ tara a gefʊ anyãna a jaa kpɛ̃ɔ na baa lɩɛb ba tɩɛr kʊ wʊ tɩɩr ɛ.

eng: Another angel, a second one, followed and said, Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great! She made all the nations drink the wine of her lustful passion.
local: A malakasɩ ayi sʊɔ mɩ tun wa 'yɛrɛ, Wʊ wɛl naa, wʊ wɛl naa, a Babilon tẽkpɛ̃ɛ nyã 'lɔ lɛ maal a paalsɩ a jaa a ba nyũ a wʊ sɛ̃sɛ̃yele dãa ala na tɔɔ Nãaŋmɩn suur waan.

eng: The fifth angel poured his bowl over the beasts throne, and darkness covered its kingdom. People bit their tongues because of their pain.
local: A malakasɩ anũu sʊɔ kpaar a wʊ laa 'yɔ̃ɔ a mɔdʊ̃ɔ nãa dakɔɔ ju, ka a wʊ nãalʊ̃ʊ wʊ jaa lɩɛb libe, ka a wõnõ a nɩbɛ ka ba ɔɔrɔ jãlbie.

eng: Then another angel, a third one, followed them and said in a loud voice, If any worship the beast and its image, and receive a mark on their foreheads or their hands.
local: Ka a malakasɩ ata sʊɔ mɩ tu wa 'yɛrɛ nɩ kɔkɔkpɛ̃ɛ, Ala wa ɩ ãsʊɔ jaa puor naa a mɔdʊ̃ɔ nyãna naa bʊtɩɩ nyãna ban mɛ a wʊ kaar, na wʊ sɔɔ ka ba 'yɔ̃ɔ wʊ a jãnɛ a gberaa pɔ bɩɩ a nũu pɔ.

eng: and they cursed the God of heaven because of their pains and sores; but they didnt turn away from what they had done.
local: tɩ chɛnɛ tʊrɔ a Nãaŋmɩn nyã na be a saaju a ba natɩɛ nɩ an bɩɛrɛ ba jũu. Tɩchɛ baa lɩɛb a ba tɩɛr bar a ba tʊ̃dɛbɛ ɛ.

eng: they themselves will also drink the wine of Gods passionate anger, poured full strength into the cup of his wrath. They will suffer the pain of fire and sulfur in the presence of the holy angels and the Lamb.
local: wʊ mɩ na nyũn a dãa ala na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn suur, ala lɛ ba pʊr 'yɔ̃ɔ a ŋmele pɔ na ɩ a suur. Ban ge wʊ naa nɩ a jerbɩ bũu na dire a malaka chɛchɛsɩ bala naa Pɛraabile niem.

eng: Then the sixth angel poured his bowl on the great river Euphrates. Its water was dried up so that the way was ready for the kings from the east.
local: A malakasɩ ayʊɔb sʊɔ mɩ kpaar naa a wʊ laa 'yɔ̃ɔ a Yufɩrɩtɩ põn kpɛ̃ɛ pɔ, ka a kʊ̃ɔ sɩr chɔɔr a sɔr bar kʊ a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ purfʊ jie nãmɩne.

eng: The smoke of their painful suffering goes up forever and always. There is no rest day or night for those who worship the beast and its image, and those who receive the mark of its name.
local: A ba gefʊ jʊʊr na uro ba tara ŋmaafʊ ɛ. Pɛ̃nfʊ ba be be kʊ a bala na gbur dumo puor a mɔdʊ̃ɔ nɩ a bʊtɩɩ 'lɔ ban mɛ a wʊ kaar mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ bɩɩ tãsɔɔ ɛ, bɩɩ ãsʊɔ jaa na sɔɔ de a jãnɛ na ɩ a mɔdʊ̃ɔ yuor ɛ.

eng: Then I saw three unclean spirits, like frogs, come from the dragons mouth, the beasts mouth, and the mouth of the false prophet.
local: N lɛ nyɛn a sɩdɛbɛ na kaara pãtɩɛ kaar ka a yire a bon 'lɔ na kaara a dajɩl kaar nɛɛ pɔ, naa lɛ yire a mɔdʊ̃ɔ 'lɔ mɩ nɛɛ pɔ, naa mɩ lɛ yire a jir ŋmarba 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ nɛɛr pɔ.

eng: This calls for the endurance of the saints, who keep Gods commandments and keep faith with Jesus.
local: A lɛ jũu, a bala na ɩ Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ na ba turo a wʊ nɛɛ na ba mɩ ara nɩ yelmãɛ kʊ a Yesu ba tara kãnyir.

eng: These are demonic spirits that do signs. They go out to the kings of the whole world, to gather them for battle on the great day of God the Almighty. .
local: A sɩɩr anyã ɩ naa a tɩɩ sɩɩr na tʊ̃nɔ̃ nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃sɩ. Ba chen naa a wɛr wʊ jaa nãmɩne sɛ̃ tɩ lɔ̃ɔ taa na ŋmaan a Nãaŋmɩn Tʊ̃ɔfʊ Sʊɔ jɛɛr a wʊ bɩbir kpɛ̃ɛ 'lɔ daar.

eng: And I heard a voice from heaven say, Write this: Favored are the dead who die in the Lord from now on. Yes, says the Spirit, so they can rest from their labors, because their deeds follow them.
local: N pãa lɛ wõn kɔkɔr na yi a saaju 'yɛrɛ, Sɛb a anyãna an yi a dɩna chiine, pʊpɛl sʊɔ lɛ a 'lɔ na kpi a Soro sɔr pɔ. Ɔ̃ɔ, lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ 'yɛr, Ban pɛ̃n naa a ba dɔɔyɛ pɔ. Bojũu a lɛ ban dɔ̃ɔ tʊ̃, na turo ba naa.

eng: Then I looked, and there was a white cloud. On the cloud was seated someone who looked like the Human One. He had a gold crown on his head and a sharp sickle in his hand.
local: N lɛ kãan nyɛ julõjupɩla, ka a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na jãnɛ a julõjuur ju kaara fɩka nɩsaal bie na wʊ su sɛlma jupil a ju pɔ, tɩ na wʊ tara bʊ̃ŋma sɔkpɛ̃ɛ na dire a nũu pɔ.

eng: Another angel came out of the temple, calling in a loud voice to the one seated on the cloud: Use your sickle to reap the harvest, for the time to harvest has come, and the harvest of the earth is ripe.
local: Be lɛ a malaka ãsʊɔ mɩ yi be a Nãaŋmɩn yir wa bʊɔlɔ 'yɛrɛ kpɛ̃ɔ jaa kʊrɔ a 'lɔ na jãnɛ a julõjuur ju, De a sɔkpɛ̃ɛ na fʊ piel tʊ̃mɔ̃ bojũu a tʊ̃mɔ̃ sɔ̃ɔ ta naa, bojũu a wɛr nyã ban na ŋma mʊ̃ɔ naa.

eng: So the one seated on the cloud swung his sickle over the earth, and the earth was harvested.
local: Lɛ lɛ a 'lɔ na jãnɛ a julõjuur ju jaal a sɔkpɛ̃ɛ bar a wɛr pɔ na wʊ ŋma a wɛr wʊ jaa.

eng: Then another angel came out of the temple in heaven, and he also had a sharp sickle.
local: A malaka ãsʊɔ mɩ na yi a be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir a saaju na wʊ mɩ tara a bʊ̃ŋma sɔkpɛ̃ɛ diir ãsʊɔ.

eng: Still another angel, who has power over fire, came out from the altar. He said in a loud voice to the one who had the sharp sickle, Use your sharp sickle to cut the clusters in the vineyard of the earth, because its grapes are ripe.
local: Ka a malaka ãsʊɔ mɩ 'lɔ lɛ kaara a bũu ka wʊ wa yi a be a tãɩ nyũu vɩla chʊmfʊ jie bʊɔl a 'lɔ na tara a bʊ̃ŋma sɔkpɛ̃ɛ diir kpɛ̃ɔ jaa tɩ 'yɛr, De a fʊ bʊ̃ŋma sɔkpɛ̃ɛ diir 'lɔ na fʊ ŋman a tɩɛ wɔ̃mɔ̃, a wɛr lɛ ɩ a tɩɛ ka wʊ wɔ̃mɔ̃ mʊ̃ɔ.

eng: So the angel swung his sickle into the earth, and cut the vineyard of the earth, and he put what he reaped into the great winepress of Gods passionate anger.
local: A malaka jaal a wʊ sɔkpɛ̃ɛ bar ka a wɛr pɔ ka wʊ ŋmara a wɔ̃mɔ̃ kpaara 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a be ban ma nɛɛrɛ ŋmɛrɛ, na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn suur.

eng: Look! Im coming like a thief! Favored are those who stay awake and clothed so that they dont go around naked and exposed to shame.
local: Nyɛ ɩn wan nãnyie kaar. Bɔɔsʊ̃n sʊɔ lɛ a 'lɔ na ba gur ɛ, tɩ mɩ tara a wʊ bʊ̃sursɩ jɛn na wʊ kʊ̃ wa chiine nɩ sal'mʊɔr ka a ɩrɛ wʊ vĩ ɛ.

eng: The spirits gathered them at the place that is called in Hebrew, Harmagedon.
local: Be lɛ a sɩɩr lɔ̃ɔtaa a nãmɩne a jie 'lɔ a Heburu nɩbɛ na bʊɔlɔ Amagedɔn.

eng: Then the seventh angel poured his bowl into the air, and a loud voice came out from the temple, from the throne, saying, It is done.
local: A malakasɩ ayopõi sʊɔ kpaar naa a wʊ laa 'yɔ̃ɔ sɛsɛbɛ pɔ, ka kɔkɔr yi be a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir a nãa dakɔɔ par 'yɛrɛ kpɛ̃ɔ jaa, A maal naa.

eng: There were lightning strikes, voices, and thunder, and a great earthquake occurred. The earthquake was greater than any that have occurred since there have been people on earth.
local: Be lɛ ka saa nyɩɩrfʊ, nɩ gɔ̃mɔ̃, nɩ saa tãnfʊ wa, ka a tẽe dam wʊ jaa miin kpɛ̃ɔ. An yi a lɛ daar jaa, nɩsaal na be ka a wɛr pɔ a tẽe ba mii a kpɛ̃ɔ a lɛ ɛ.

eng: The great city split into three parts, and the cities of the nations fell. God remembered Babylon the great so that he gave her the wine cup of his furious anger.
local: Ka a tẽkpɛ̃ɛ 'mar jiir ata, ka a paalsɩ tẽbɛrɛ mɩ lo lo. Nãaŋmɩn chɛnɛ na tɩɛr tɔ a tẽkpɛ̃ɛ Babilon na wʊ gbʊ̃m wʊ a dãa na sɛɛ ŋmele na ɩ a wʊ suur.

eng: Every island fled, and the mountains disappeared.
local: Dõdolsɩ a jaa bɔr naa ka fʊ kʊ̃ lɛ nyɛ tɔ̃ɔ go ɛ.

eng: Huge hailstones weighing about one hundred pounds came down from heaven on the people. They cursed God for the plague of hail, because the plague was so terrible.
local: ka a sakpʊlõbibɛrɛ na tɩrɛ yire a saaju loro ŋmiere a nɩbɛ ka ba tʊrɔ a Nãaŋmɩn a sakpʊlõbie gefʊ nyã jũu.

eng: Then the winepress was trampled outside the city, and the blood came out of the winepress as high as the horses bridles for almost two hundred miles.
local: Ba nɛɛrɛ ban a be a nɛŋmɛrfʊ jie a tẽkpɛ̃ɛ 'lɔ yõo, ka a jãɩ joro a ŋmɛrfʊ jie ɩrɛ na ta mɛl kɔɔr ayi ka a wii mɩ ma kpɛ ka a nɛɛ 'lɔr.

eng: Then I saw another great and awe-inspiring sign in heaven. There were seven angels with seven plagues—and these are the last, for with them Gods anger is brought to an end.
local: N nyɛ naa a nɛ'maa yelwiilaa jãnɛ ãsʊɔ a saaju, malakasɩ ayopõi na tara wõmsɩ ayopõi, ala lɛ ɩ a baaraa na vɛ̃ ka Nãaŋmɩn suur lo.

eng: Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls spoke with me. Come, he said, I will show you the judgment upon the great prostitute, who is seated on deep waters.
local: A malakasɩ ayopõi na tara a laar ayopõi, ãsʊɔ wan wa 'yɛr kʊ ma, Wa, ɩn wiil fʊn a lɛ a pɔɔ baa nyã yuor na yi na jãnɛ a mansɩ nɛɛ 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ.

eng: The kings of the earth have committed sexual immorality with her, and those who live on earth have become drunk with the wine of her whoring.
local: 'Lɔ lɛ a wɛr nãmɩne gãn ka a wɛr nɩbɛ mɩ buu nɩ a wʊ sɛ̃sɛ̃yele.

eng: Then I saw what appeared to be a sea of glass mixed with fire. Those who gained victory over the beast, its image, and the number of its name were standing by the glass sea, holding harps from God.
local: N lɛ nyɛn bon na kaara fɩka man kʊ̃ɔ jaa wel wel nyɩlɛ chãana kaar lɔ̃ɔtãan bũu ka nɩbɛ ara a wʊ gogoro par. Bala lɛ jɛb tʊ̃ɔ a mɔdʊ̃ɔ nɩ a wʊ bʊtɩɩ nɩ a yuor jãnɛ. Nãaŋmɩn kʊ ban kõrõjomsɩ.

eng: They sing the song of Moses, Gods servant, and the song of the Lamb, saying, Great and awe-inspiring are your works, Lord God Almighty. Just and true are your ways, king of the nations.
local: ka ba yiele nɩ a wʊ gbãgbaa Mosesɩ nɩ a Pɛraabile yiele 'yɛrɛ, A Soro Nãaŋmɩn na ɩ a Tʊ̃ɔfʊ Sʊɔ, a fʊ tʊ̃mɔ̃ 'maan nɛɛ tɩ põn bar, fʊ ɩn nɩtor nɩ yelmãɛ sʊɔ, fʊ̃ʊ lɛ ɩ a nãmɩne ba jaa Nãa.

eng: Then he brought me in a Spirit-inspired trance to a desert. There I saw a woman seated on a scarlet beast that was covered with blasphemous names. It had seven heads and ten horns.
local: Be lɛ, a malaka 'mɔɔ man a Sɩɛ pɔ chiin a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ. Be lɛ n tɩ nyɛ a pɔɔ na jãnɛ a mɔdʊ̃jɩɛ ju. Ka a mɔdʊ̃ɔ nyã ãgãn wʊ jaa pɔɔn yoe na fere nɩ Nãaŋmɩn na wʊ tara jur ayopõi nɩ ɩlɛ pie.

eng: The woman wore purple and scarlet clothing, and she glittered with gold and jewels and pearls. In her hand she held a gold cup full of the vile and impure things that came from her activity as a prostitute.
local: A pɔɔ vaar naa fɔjɩ ile ile na wʊ pãɛn sɛlma nɩ kʊsɩsʊ̃n nɩ lɛrbikpãɛn. Wʊ tara naa a sɛlma ŋmele a wʊ nũsʊ̃ɔ pɔ a wʊ tʊ̃ kakaba nɩ a wʊ sɛ̃sɛ̃yele na sɛɛ.

eng: Who wont fear you, Lord, and glorify your name? You alone are holy. All nations will come and fall down in worship before you, for your acts of justice have been revealed.
local: An lɛ kʊ̃ joro a fʊ̃ʊ Soro dãbãɛ na wʊ kʊ a fʊ yuor tɩɩr ɛ? Bojũu fʊ̃ʊ tɛɛ lɛ ɩ chɛchɛ. A paalsɩ a jaa na wa gãn kpa tẽe a fʊ niem, bojũu nɩrɛ jaa nyɛ naa a fʊ tʊ̃torsɩ.

eng: A name—a mystery—was written on her forehead: Babylon the great, the mother of prostitutes and the vile things of the earth.
local: Yuor sɛb mar a wʊ gberaa pɔ, Fʊn kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ bɔ̃ɔ a par ɛ, a Babilon na yi yuor, pɔɔ baar ma, nɩ a wɛr tʊ̃ kakabsɩ ma.

eng: After this I looked, and the temple in heaven—that is, the tent of witness—was opened.
local: Bojũu a anyã puor, n kãan nyɛ a saaju ka a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir a jichɛchɛ 'lɔ na ɩ a dãasɩɛ jie ka wʊ yuo.

eng: I saw that the woman was drunk on the blood of the saints and the blood of Jesus witnesses. I was completely stunned when I saw her.
local: N nyɛn a pɔɔ na nyũ a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ jãɩ buu, a bala na di a Yesu dãasɩɛ. Ɩn wa nyɛ wʊ a lɛ, nɛɛ 'maa ma naa.

eng: The seven angels, who have the seven plagues, came out of the temple. They were clothed in pure bright linen and had gold sashes around their waists.
local: Ka a malakasɩ ayopõi yi a jichɛchɛ nyãna na ba tara a Nãaŋmɩn suur wõmsɩ ayopõi sun bʊ̃pɩɛl na nyɩlɛ ka a sɛlma bʊ̃lunsɩ bãbã a ba nyãn pɔ.

eng: Then the angel said to me, Why are you amazed? I will tell you the mystery of the woman and the seven-headed, ten-horned beast that carries her.
local: Ka a malaka sʊʊr ma, Ŋmɩnɛ a nɛɛ 'maa fʊ? Ɩn wiil fʊ naa a pɔɔ nɩ a mɔdʊ̃ɔ wʊn do na tara jur ayopõi nɩ ɩlɛ pie yelsɔɔlaa par.

eng: Then one of the four living creatures gave the seven angels seven gold bowls full of the anger of the God who lives forever and always.
local: Be lɛ ka a bʊ̃nɩbie anaar bala ãsʊɔ kʊ a malakasɩ ayopõi bala sɛlma laar ayopõi na sɛɛr nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn nyã na be be jaa kpo kpo suur.

eng: The beast that you saw was and is not, and is about to come up out of the abyss and go to destruction. Those who live on earth, whose names havent been written in the scroll of life from the time the earth was made, will be amazed when they see the beast, because it was and is not and will again be present.
local: A mɔdʊ̃ɔ 'lɔ fʊn nyɛ, wʊ dɔ̃ɔ be be, pʊ̃pãanyã waa lɛ be be go ɛ. Wʊn yi naa a bɔɔ fulom pɔ na wʊ chen a sɔ̃ɔfʊ jie. A wɛr nɩbɛ bala yoe na ba sɛb a nyɛvʊr gãn pɔ ɛ, yi a wɛr maalfʊ daar ɛ, bala wa nyɛ a mɔdʊ̃ɔ nyã, nɛɛ na 'maa ba naa bojũu wʊ dɔ̃ɔ be be na waa lɛ be be ɛ, tɩ na wʊn lɩɛb wa.

eng: The temple was filled with smoke from Gods glory and power, and no one could go into the temple until the seven plagues of the seven last angels were brought to an end.
local: Ka a jʊʊr sɛɛ a Nãaŋmɩn puorfʊ yir yi a Nãaŋmɩn tɩɩr nɩ a wʊ kpɛ̃ɔ pɔ, ka nɩrɛ jaa kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ kpɛ a puorfʊ yir ka a malakasɩ ba wa baar a Nãaŋmɩn suur wõmsɩ ala baar ɛ.

eng: This calls for an understanding mind. The seven heads are seven mountains on which the woman is seated. They are also seven kings.
local: A ka ɩ naa tɩɛr na tara yɛ̃ 'yɛr. A jur ayopõi ɩ naa a tɔ̃n ayopõi a pɔɔ na jãnɛ, nɩ a nãmɩne ayopõi.

eng: Five kings have fallen, the one is, and the other hasnt yet come. When that king comes, he must remain for only a short time.
local: A ba anũu kpi naa tɩ ka bʊ̃'yen chɛnɛ, bʊ̃'yen mɩ ba wa sɛrɛ ɛ. Tɩchɛ 'lɔ wa wa, wʊn kor bãlãa.

eng: As for the beast that was and is not, it is itself an eighth king that belongs to the seven, and it is going to destruction.
local: A mɔdʊ̃ɔ 'lɔ na dɔ̃ɔ be be na waa lɛ be be go ɛ, wʊ ɩ naa a nãmɩne aniin sʊɔ na pʊɔ ayopõi bala pɔ na chiine a sɔ̃ɔfʊ jie.

eng: The ten horns that you saw are ten kings, who havent yet received royal power. But they will receive royal authority for an hour, along with the beast.
local: A ɩlɛ pie ala fʊn nyɛ ɩ naa nãmɩne pie na ba di a nãa sɛrɛ ɛ, tɩchɛ ban kʊ ban kpɛ̃ɔ ka ba ɩ nãmɩne lɔ̃ɔn a mɔdʊ̃ɔ sɔ̃ɔ bãlãa.

eng: These kings will be of one mind, and they will give their power and authority to the beast.
local: Ba jaa tɩɛr ɩn bʊ̃'yen, ka ba de a ba kpɛ̃ɔ nɩ fɔ̃ɔ kʊ a mɔdʊ̃ɔ.

eng: They will make war on the Lamb, but the Lamb will emerge victorious, for he is Lord of lords and King of kings. Those with him are called, chosen, and faithful.
local: Ban ŋmaan naa a Pɛraabile jɛɛr, tɩchɛ ka a Pɛraabile na tʊ̃ɔ ba, bojũu 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a sorbo ba jaa Soro nɩ a nãmɩne ba jaa Nãa. A bala wʊn bʊɔl na wʊ chɛl ir ka ba turo nɩ yelmãɛ, na pʊɔ wʊ naa.

eng: Then he said to me, The waters that you saw, where the prostitute is seated, are peoples, crowds, nations, and languages.
local: Ka a malaka 'yɛr kʊ ma, A kɔ̃n ala fʊn nyɛ be a pɔɔ baa 'lɔ na jãnɛ, ɩ naa a nɩbʊrsɩ, nɩyɔɔ, paalsɩ nɩ 'yɛrbʊrsɩ.

eng: As for the ten horns that you saw, they and the beast will hate the prostitute. They will destroy her and strip her bare. They will devour her flesh and burn her with fire.
local: A mɔdʊ̃ɔ nɩ a ɩlɛ pie ala fʊn nyɛ ban 'laar naa a pɔɔ baa nyãna na ba tɔɔ wʊ chɩɛr tɩ chɛ wʊ sal'mʊɔr. Ban 'wɔb wʊ naa tɩ chɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ bũu pɔ ka wʊ di.

eng: because God moved them to carry out his purposes. That is why they will be of one mind and give their royal power to the beast, until Gods words have been accomplished.
local: Bojũu an ɩ Nãaŋmɩn lɛ de 'yɔ̃ɔ a ba tɩɛr pɔ ka ba na ɩ a wʊ bɔfʊ, ka ba jaa tɩɛr ɩ bʊ̃'yen ka ba sɔɔ de a ba kpɛ̃ɔ kʊ a mɔdʊ̃ɔ ka wʊ di nãa ka Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr wa maal baar.

eng: The woman whom you saw is the great city that rules over the kings of the earth.
local: A pɔɔ fʊn nyɛ ɩ naa a tẽkpɛ̃ɛ 'lɔ nãa na tʊ̃ɔ a wɛr nãmɩne ba jaa.

eng: After this I saw another angel coming down from heaven. He had great authority, and the earth was filled with light because of his glory.
local: A anyã puor, n nyɛn a malaka ãsʊɔ na yi a saaju suro waara. Wʊ tara na kpɛ̃ɔ. A chãa na be a wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ chaar naa a wɛr wʊ jaa.

eng: He called out with a loud voice, saying, Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great! She has become a home for demons and a lair for every unclean spirit. She is a lair for every unclean bird, and a lair for every unclean and disgusting beast.
local: Na wʊ tãn kpɛ̃ɔ nɩ nɛkpɛ̃ɛ 'yɛrɛ, Wʊ wɛl naa! A Babilon tẽkpɛ̃ɛ 'lɔ wʊ wɛl naa! Wʊ lɩɛb dɛɛr dẽme gaa jie, nɩ sɩdɛbɛ, nɩ lubil dɛbɛ nɩbɛ na ba ɔɔrɔ ɛ kpɛfʊ jie.

eng: because all the nations have fallen due to the wine of her lustful passion. The kings of the earth committed sexual immorality with her, and the merchants of the earth became rich from the power of her loose and extravagant ways.
local: Bojũu an ɩ a paalsɩ ba jaa buu naa nɩ a wʊ sɛ̃sɛ̃yele, ka a nãmɩne na be ka a wɛr pɔ gãn wʊ. A yayarba na be a wɛr pɔ len tu a wʊ bʊ̃yɔɔ pɔ.

eng: Then I heard another voice from heaven say, Come out of her, my people, so that you dont take part in her sins and dont receive any of her plagues.
local: Be lɛ n lɛ wõ kɔkɔr ãsʊɔ na yi a saaju 'yɛr, Maa nɩbɛ, yɩ wa yi a wʊ pɔ na yɩ taa ɩrɛ wa pʊɔ a wʊ dɛɛr pɔ ɛ, bɩɩ na yɩ pʊɔ a wʊ wõm pɔ ɛ.

eng: Her sins have piled up as high as heaven, and God remembered her unjust acts.
local: Bojũu a wʊ maal dɛbɛ dɔɔl taa tɩ ta a saaju, ka Nãaŋmɩn tɩɛr tɔ a wʊ dɛɛr wʊn maal.

eng: Give her what she has given to others. Give her back twice as much for what she has done. In the cup that she has poured, pour her twice as much.
local: Yɩ kʊ wʊ nɩtãa lɛ wʊn kʊ yɩ. Yɩ yab wʊ tɩ lɛ dɔɔl wʊ. Yɩ kʊ wʊ a dãa nɩ a wʊ ŋmele nɛɛr ayi ka wʊ nyũ.

eng: To the extent that she glorified herself and indulged her loose and extravagant ways, give her pain and grief. In her heart she says, I sit like a queen! Im not a widow. Ill never see grief.
local: Ge wʊ ka wʊ waal, a yuor nɩ a nʊ̃ɔ wʊn bʊɔrɔ kʊrɔ a wʊ mãɛ. Wʊ ma chɛn nyãa 'yɛr, N ɩn pɔɔ nãa jãnɛ, n ba ɩ pɔɔ kʊɔr ɛ. Nɩbaal kʊ̃ wa kpɛ ma jaa ɛ.

eng: This is why her plagues will come in a single day—deadly disease, grief, and hunger. She will be consumed by fire because the Lord God who judges her is powerful.
local: A lɛ jũu, bɩbir bʊ̃'yen lɛ a wõm na wa a wʊ ju, kũu, nɩ kõn, nɩ kɔ̃. Bũu lɛ ɩ a di wʊ an ɩ a Soro Nãaŋmɩn nyã na tɔɔr a wʊ 'yɛr ɩn tʊ̃ɔfʊ sʊɔ.

eng: The kings of the earth, who committed sexual immorality with her and shared her loose and extravagant ways, will weep and mourn over her when they see the smoke from her burning.
local: Ka a wɛr nãmɩne na dɔ̃ɔ gãn wʊ na ba 'mataa din wʊ a nʊ̃ɔ, bala wa nyɛ a lɛ wʊn dire ka a jʊʊr uro, an ɩrɛ ban nɩbaal ka ba kõnõ kʊrɔ wʊ.

eng: They will stand a long way off because they are afraid of the pain she suffers, and they will say, Oh, the horror! Babylon, you great city, you powerful city! In a single hour your judgment has come.
local: Dãbãɛ kpɛ ba naa a ger nyã jũu, ban ara na tɛɛr jaa tɩ kõnõ 'yɛrɛ, Nɛbier, nɛbier, a fʊ̃ʊ tẽkpɛ̃ɛ nyã, pa Babilon, a fʊ̃ʊ nyã na tara a kpɛ̃ɔ, bɩbir bʊ̃'yen lɛ a sɔ̃ɔfʊ be wa a fʊ ju.

eng: The merchants of the earth will weep and mourn over her, for no one buys their cargoes anymore.
local: A yayarba na be a wɛr pɔ na kõnõ na tɩ waala bojũu nɩrɛ ba be be na da a bonsɩ go ɛ.

eng: cargoes of gold, silver, jewels, and pearls; fine linen, purple, silk, and scarlet; all those things made of scented wood, ivory, fine wood, bronze, iron, and marble.
local: A sɛlma, kpãna, kʊsɩsʊ̃n nɩ a lɛrbikpãɛn, nɩ fuu pɩɛl, nɩ fuujɩ ile ile, nɩ fuu saalsɩ, nɩ a fuujɩ kpãn kpãn, nɩ a daar ala nyũu na nʊ̃mɔ̃, nɩ bomo ala jaa ban de wab chɛlɛ maal. A daar ala daa na be tuo, a kurjɩɛ, nɩ kursɔɔlɔ, nɩ a kʊsɩkpũlo bie.

eng: cinnamon, incense, fragrant ointment, and frankincense; wine, oil, fine flour, and wheat; cattle, sheep, horses, and carriages; and slaves, even human lives.
local: A kãnyũu vɩɛl ala, nɩ a dãn, nɩ a kãn, nɩ a jɔ̃n, nɩ chir, nɩ a niin, nɩ a piir, nɩ a wiisɩ, nɩ a wiisɩ torkosɩ, nɩ a gbãgbaar.

eng: The fruit your whole being craved has gone from you. All your glitter and glamour are lost to you, never ever to be found again.
local: A bʊ̃vɩɛlsɩ ala na ɩrɛ fʊ a vɔlaa, a jaa chen naa. A fʊ nãbomo naa fʊ yobɔyele a jaa bɔr naa. Fʊ kʊ̃ lɛ nyãa go ɛ.

eng: The merchants who sold these things, and got so rich by her, will stand a long way off because they fear the pain she suffers. They will weep and mourn, and say.
local: A yayarba na da a bomo kʊ wʊ na ba le, nɛɛ na 'maa ba naa a wʊ ge fʊ nyã pɔ, ka ba sɛr yi tɛɛr jaa tɩ kõnõ waala.

eng: Oh, the horror! The great city that wore fine linen, purple, and scarlet, who glittered with gold, jewels, and pearls.
local: 'yɛrɛ nɩ, Nɛbier, nɛbier, a fʊ̃ʊ tẽkpɛ̃ɛ nyã, a fʊ̃ʊ na ma su a fɔpɩɛl, nɩ a fɔjɩ ile ile, nɩ a fɔkpãn kpãnsɩ. Na fʊ ma pãɛn a sɛlma, nɩ a kʊsɩsʊ̃n, nɩ a lɛrbikpãɛn.

eng: in just one hour such great wealth was destroyed. Every sea captain, every seafarer, sailors, and all who make their living on the sea stood a long way off.
local: Mɔ̃tɔ̃ bʊ̃'yen lɛ a fʊ nãbomo anyãna be wɛ a jaa. A bala na kaara a gborbɛrɛ ala a man kpɛ̃ɛ pɔ, nɩ a nɩbɛ bala na be be, nɩ a bala jaa na tʊ̃nɔ̃ dire be a man pɔ, na ara na tɛɛr.

eng: They cried out as they saw the smoke from her burning and said, What city was ever like the great city.
local: Bala wa nyɛ a jʊʊr ban kõnõ na 'yɛrɛ nɩ, Tẽbuor lɛ be ka na wʊ ta a tẽkpɛ̃ɛ nyã.

eng: They threw dust on their heads, and they cried out, weeping and mourning. They said, Oh, the horror! The great city, where all who have ships at sea became so rich by her prosperity—in just one hour she was destroyed.
local: Ban ɔɔn tãna 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a jur pɔ tɩ kõnõ tɩ waala 'yɛrɛ nɩ, Nɛbier, nɛbier, a fʊ̃ʊ tẽkpɛ̃ɛ nyã. A jie nyã a bala jaa na tara a gborbɛrɛ yʊɔrɔ nɩ a man pɔ, ba jaa len tu a fʊ jie, tɩ mɔ̃tɔ̃ bʊ̃'yen a fʊ be wɛ a nɩtɔ̃.

eng: Rejoice over her, heaven—you saints, apostles, and prophets—because God has condemned her as she condemned you.
local: Saaju, chɩlɛ a wʊ jũu, yɩn Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ nɩ a nɩtʊ̃nsɩ nɩ a 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ yɩ chɩlɛ. Nãaŋmɩn tɔɔr a wʊ 'yɛr nɩtãa a lɛ wʊn maal yɩ.

eng: Then a powerful angel picked up a stone that was like a huge millstone and threw it into the sea, saying, With such violent force the great city of Babylon will be thrown down, and it wont be found anymore.
local: Be lɛ ka a malaka kpɛ̃ɔ pãa 'mɔɔ kʊsɩr kpɛ̃ɛ na jãtãan nɩɛr lɔb 'yɔ̃ɔ a man pɔ tɩ 'yɛr, Nɩtɔ̃ lɛ ɩn lɔb fʊ bar, a fʊ̃ʊ Babilon tẽkpɛ̃ɛ nyã ka nɩrɛ kʊ̃ lɛ nyɛ fʊ jaa go ɛ.

eng: The sound of harpists and musicians, of pipers and trumpeters, will never be heard among you again. No craftsman of any kind will ever be found among you again. The sound of the hand mill will never be heard among you again.
local: Ba kʊ̃ lɛ wõ kõrõjom ŋmefʊ, nɩ yiel yielbe, nɩ daa pɛɛlbɛ, nɩ ɩlɛ pɛɛlbɛ jaa go ɛ. Tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ jaa bʊrɔ kʊ̃ lɛ be be go ɛ, ba kʊ̃ lɛ wõ jɔ̃ nãɛnbɛ dɔ̃ɔn go ɛ.

eng: The light of a lamp will never shine among you again. The sound of a bridegroom and bride will never be heard among you again because your merchants ran the world, because all the nations were deceived by the spell you cast, and because.
local: Fãtɩn bũu kʊ̃ lɛ chaar be go ɛ. Ba kʊ̃ lɛ wõ pɔɔ paalaa nɩ wʊ sɩrɛ kɔkɔr go ɛ. A fʊ yayarba lɛ dɔ̃ɔ yi yuor a wɛr pɔ. A fʊ tãsɔɔ yele lɛ fʊ de bɛl a paalsɩ jaa ka ba bɔr.

eng: the blood of prophets, of saints, and of all who have been slaughtered on the earth was found among you.
local: A wʊ pɔ lɛ ba nyɛ a 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ jãɩ nɩ bala ba jaa ban dɔ̃ɔ kʊ a wɛr pɔ.

eng: After this I heard what sounded like a huge crowd in heaven. They said, Hallelujah! The salvation and glory and power of our God!.
local: A anyã puor, n wõn gɔ̃mɔ̃ fɩka nɩyɔɔ na tãna be a saaju 'yɛrɛ nɩ, Dãnfʊ wʊ ta Nãaŋmɩn! Faafʊ nɩ tɩɩr nɩ kpɛ̃ɔ ɩ naa a wʊ tʊɔr.

eng: His judgments are true and just, because he judged the great prostitute, who ruined the earth by her whoring, and he exacted the penalty for the blood of his servants from her hand.
local: bojũu a wʊ 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ ɩn yelmãɛ tɩ mɩ tor. Wʊ 'yɔ̃ɔn a pɔɔ baa na 'yɛr, 'lɔ lɛ sɔ̃ɔ a wɛr nɩbɛ nɩ a wʊ sɛ̃sɛ̃yele. A wʊ gbãgbaar wʊn kʊ, wʊ yab wʊn a sãn.

eng: Then they said a second time, Hallelujah! Smoke goes up from her forever and always.
local: Ba lɛ tãna na go, Dãnfʊ wʊ ta Nãaŋmɩn. A wʊ jʊʊr na uro duoro ba tara ŋmaafʊ ɛ.

eng: The twenty-four elders and the four living creatures fell down and worshipped God, who is seated on the throne, and they said, Amen. Hallelujah.
local: Ka a nɩbɛrɛ lɩjɛr nɩ anaar nɩ a bʊ̃nɩbie anaar bala gãn kpa tẽe puor a Nãaŋmɩn na jãnɛ a nãa dakɔɔ ju pɛɛrɛ wʊ 'yɛrɛ nɩ, A ɩ a lɛ. Dãnfʊ wʊ ta a Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: Then a voice went out from the throne and said, Praise our God, all you his servants, and you who fear him, both small and great.
local: Be lɛ ka a kɔkɔr yi be a nãa dakɔɔ par 'yɛrɛ, A yɩn na ɩ a Nãaŋmɩn gbãgbaar na yɩ joro wʊ dãbãɛ. Ka bil bɩɩ bɛrɛ lɛ, yɩ pɛɛrɛ a sɩ Nãaŋmɩn.

eng: And I heard something that sounded like a huge crowd, like rushing water and powerful thunder. They said, Hallelujah! The Lord our God, the Almighty, exercised his royal power!.
local: N pãa lɛ wõn dɔ̃ɔn fɩka nɩyɔɔ na gɔ̃nɔ̃, bɩɩ fɩka kɔ̃kpɛ̃ɛ na joro, bɩɩ saa na tãna kpɛ̃ɔ jaa, Dãnfʊ wʊ ta Nãaŋmɩn! Bojũu a sɩ Soro Nãaŋmɩn na ɩ a Tʊ̃ɔfʊ Sʊɔ, lɛ di a nãa.

eng: Let us rejoice and celebrate, and give him the glory, for the wedding day of the Lamb has come, and his bride has made herself ready.
local: Yɩ ɩ a sɩ chɩlɛ ka sɩ ãgãn 'maa a sɩ kʊ a Nãaŋmɩn tɩɩr an ɩ a wʊ Pɛraabile pɔɔ kulfʊ bɩbir vɩɛ naa, ka a pɔɔ mɩ chɔɔr a wʊ mãɛ baar.

eng: She was given fine, pure white linen to wear, for the fine linen is the saints acts of justice.
local: Wʊ sun bʊ̃pɩɛl na vɩɛlɛ tɩ nyɩlɛ. A bʊ̃pɩɛl anyãna lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ yeltorsɩ.

eng: Then the angel said to me, Write this: Favored are those who have been invited to the wedding banquet of the Lamb. He said to me, These are the true words of God.
local: Ka a malaka 'yɛr kʊ ma, Sɛb a anyãna, An ɩn pʊpɛl kʊ a bala ban bʊɔl a Pɛraabile pɔɔ kulfʊ lɔ̃ɔ di jie. Ka a malaka mɩ 'yɛr pʊɔ, A Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr anyãna ɩn yelmãɛ.

eng: Then I fell at his feet to worship him. But he said, Dont do that! Im a servant just like you and your brothers and sisters who hold firmly to the witness of Jesus. Worship God! The witness of Jesus is the spirit of prophecy.
local: Ka n gã kpa tẽe ɩka n puor wʊ, tɩchɛ ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ma, Ta maal a lɛ ɛ. N mɩ ɩ naa a gbãgbaa nɩtãa a fʊ̃ʊ nɩ a fʊ yɛɛr kaar, bala lɛ tara Yesu dãasɩɛ. Puor Nãaŋmɩn! Bojũu, a Yesu dãasɩɛ lɛ ɩ a Sɩɛ 'yɛr.

eng: Then I saw heaven opened, and there was a white horse. Its rider was called Faithful and True, and he judges and makes war justly.
local: N nyɛn a saaju na yuo, ka a ɩ wii pɩla lɛ, ka a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na do yuor di, yelmãɛ sʊɔ nɩ nɩtor. Yeltorsɩ lɛ wʊ ma tɔɔr a wʊ 'yɛr tɩ ŋmaan wʊ jɛɛr.

eng: His eyes were like a fiery flame, and on his head were many royal crowns. He has a name written on him that no one knows but he himself.
local: A wʊ nɩbie 'lɩrɛ naa fɩka bũu, ka a nãjupil yɔɔ su a wʊ ju pɔ. Yuor sɛb mar wʊ nɩrɛ jaa na ba bɔ̃ɔ ɛ, ka 'lɔ tɛɛ ba lɛ ɛ.

eng: He wore a robe dyed with blood, and his name was called the Word of God.
local: A fuu wõo wʊn su ɩn jãɩ wʊ jaa ka a yuor di, Nãaŋmɩn 'Yɛr.

eng: Heavens armies, wearing fine linen that was white and pure, were following him on white horses.
local: A saaju sojasɩ sun bʊ̃pɩɛl don wii pɩɛl turo nɩ wʊ.

eng: From his mouth comes a sharp sword that he will use to strike down the nations. He is the one who will rule them with an iron rod. And he is the one who will trample the winepress of the Almighty Gods passionate anger.
local: Ka a sɔkpɛ̃ɛ diir yi a wʊ nɛɛ pɔ, 'lɔ lɛ wʊ de chɩɛrɛ nɩ a paalsɩ ba jaa, Wʊn kaara ba jaa nɩ a kpɛ̃ɔ 'lɔ na kaara a kur dãgbal kaar. Wʊn nɛɛrɛ ban ŋmɛrɛ be a tɩɛ wɔ̃mɔ̃ nɛŋmɛrfʊ jie nɩ a Nãaŋmɩn na ɩ a Tʊ̃ɔfʊ Sʊɔ suur.

eng: He has a name written on his robe and on his thigh: King of kings and Lord of lords.
local: Yuor nyãna lɛ be a wʊ fuu wõo na suu a gbɛkpɛ̃ɛ pɔ ɩka, A NÃMƖNE BA JAA NÃA, NƖ A SORBO BA JAA SORO.

eng: Then I saw an angel standing in the sun, and he called out with a loud voice and said to all the birds flying high overhead, Come and gather for Gods great supper.
local: N nyɛn malaka na ara a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ ju tɩ bʊɔl a lubil a jaa na ɔɔrɔ be a saaju nɩ kɔkɔkpɛ̃ɛ, Yɩ lɔ̃ɔ taa wa a Nãaŋmɩn lɔ̃ɔ di jie.

eng: Come and eat the flesh of kings, the flesh of generals, the flesh of the powerful, and the flesh of horses and their riders. Come and eat the flesh of all, both free and slave, both small and great.
local: Lɛ lɛ, yɩ wa dire a nãmɩne ãgãma nɩ a fɔ̃n dẽme nɩ a kpãɛn dẽme, a wiisɩ nɩ a ba duorbo, gbãgbaar nɩ sumɩne, a bil nɩ a bɛrɛ, a nɩbɛ banyãna ba jaa ãgãma.

eng: Then I saw that the beast and the kings of the earth and their armies had gathered to make war against the rider on the horse and his army.
local: Ka n nyɛ a mɔdʊ̃ɔ nɩ a wɛr nãmɩne nɩ a ba sojasɩ lɔ̃ɔn taa ŋmaan a 'lɔ na do a wii nɩ a wʊ sojasɩ jɛɛr.

eng: But the beast was seized, along with the false prophet who had done signs in the beasts presence. The two of them were thrown alive into the fiery lake that burns with sulfur.
local: Tɩchɛ ba nyɔɔn a mɔdʊ̃ɔ lɔ̃ɔn a jir ŋmara 'yɛ'yɛrɛ na tʊ̃ a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ a mɔdʊ̃ɔ niem. A nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ anyãna lɛ wʊ de bɛlʊ̃ a bala na tara a mɔdʊ̃ɔ jãnɛ tɩ puor a bʊtɩɩ. Ba lɔb ba jaa ayi bʊ̃nɩbie 'yɔ̃ɔ a jerbɩ bũu na dire pɔ.

eng: The rest were killed by the sword that comes from the mouth of the rider on the horse, and all the birds ate their fill of their flesh.
